text:
This review specifically applies to plumbing department. Heather Garcia was the best but she left a few years ago. The guy that is there now is nothing like her.

Pros: Can't beat their prices
Cons: with who you have there now, too many cons to list. My advice, do all the research yourself down to the product number and all accessory parts! That's the only thing that saved me. I literally had to walk him through every item, giving him the product number,  making sure he entered to correct color! Not a joke. He was not at all comfortable using the computer. He even told me he had a client with a $10K order and the order was messed up and he. Lamed it on the computer system. No dude-  it was you! You had a difficult time entering my stuff- don't blame it on the computer.

If you choose DW because of the price, just realize you will need to do all the work yourself. Go to other places where they really know their stuff (Ferguson, Capital Hardware). Don't expect that kind of knowledge from DW plumbing department. Unless Heather Garcia comes back.

Good luck!

Oh man, this hurts. I love (or loved, to be most correct) iTap but my most recent experience reminded me that indifferent service will probably result in a dispassionate experience. And when the majority of patrons you're encountering are passionate about their beer, well let's pour some passion on their sugar. Know what I mean?

Def Leppard-like references aside, love bites because sometimes, it ends. When my brother asked for the Boulevard Tank 7 saison beer (my recommendation), I noticed he didn't seem to be enjoying it as much as I expected he would. "Try this," he implored my cousin. Two seconds later, my cousin admitted he didn't care for the beer as he's 'not into hops.' Hops? What? My beloved Tank 7 is a saison. As in, low in IBUs (international bitterness units). It's dry, light in color but full in flavor and straight up DELICIOUS. But hoppy? That, it's not.

I decided to get to the bottom of things and without seeing the bottom of the pint glass, instantly realized my brother received either the wrong pour or maybe the keg had just been switched and we were tasting the previously-parked beer. In any case, my brother informed the bartender and she tried the beer. She then had the other bartender try the beer. After several minutes of deliberation they arrived at the conclusion that indeed, the beer did not taste like Tank 7. "And? Can I please get another beer since it isn't what I asked for?" Long pause. "I'll have to talk to the manager to see if it can be taken off your bill."

Just get the kid a beer. If you're having problems with the lines and can't deliver the product in the quality as intended, (nicely) offer him a replacement beer. On the house. Instead, the attitude was free and the replacement beer turned out to be a pain in the ass to get.

Sorry, but you can keep the attitude and I'll take my friends, money and love for great craft beer somewhere else.

Customer service is hit or miss. One morning in the drive thru I had ordered a tall coffee in a grande cup with extra milk, which is pretty much my standard order at every Starbucks I go to in the colder months and it has never ever been an issue. The barista gave me a really hard time about it and wanted me to pay for a grande even though I only wanted 12 oz of coffee. She told me the policy was to charge for more than 4 oz of milk. I worked at Starbucks for a few years and this was never something I had heard of. Regardless, I wasn't asking for 4 oz of milk, maybe 3 at the most if it was filled to the standard amount in a grande cup, which I can tell you is not actually 16 oz. I ended up just getting the tall black coffee since clearly she couldn't be bothered to talk to me about it anymore. 

When they do have nice people working, it's a great experience. They usually make my iced coffee perfectly.

Hmmm tough resort to rate... All the good reviews and bad reviews are right ..

Location 5 stars
Beach Access 5 stars
Cabana Bar 4 stars bartenders know how have fun, they do close early though ..
Rooms 3 stars (We had a gulf view) you can tell they have upgraded the kitchen and bathrooms, everything else dated and a little run down
Hallways, Stairs, elevators 1 star kind of nasty and very loud at all hours.. (Especially stairwells-scary).

Kind of place if you're young or on a budget you can over look a lot. If you are accustomed to above average accommodations you should avoid. Reminds me of the Flamingo in Vegas.

Business photos are highly misleading.... Good Luck

Save yourself the headache and humiliation!

Made a reservation for a Saturday night. Turns out he doesn't stock bottled water- problem already... I ordered a veal dish, and my wife linguine with clams. While sitting and waiting for the meal the owner (that's right- the owner is a waiter there) never once refilled our beverages, never cleaned the table of all the crumbs. When the soup finally came out, it was bland veggie soup that anyone could get from a can. When finally after quite some time our entree's came, I saw my wife picking out what I thought were pieces of clams... but NO! they were giant cloves of garlic that were so abundant, she could barely eat the pasta at all. When I called this jerk over, he said (and I'm quoting here)- "what do you want? you're in an Italian restaurant!" with an attitude like I've never encountered when eating out. They he proceeded to add "you know- I don't put this much in, people complain, I put this much in, people complain- you know it's Italian". At which point I was vexed and fired back- "That's your explanation? You've got to be kidding me"- Grinningly he replied- It's how we make it. 

What a dick. Never again. Plus the food sucked- I make better at home.

I Called at 1:00 am for a ride, was told it was on it way( i live 7 minutes from Ardmore). I called back at 1:30 to find out when the cab would arrive, was put on hold for 7 minutes.  I hung up and called back- no answer.
At 2:47 am I called to the cancel the cab, the dispatcher said" not a problem, I've got you covered." I don't think that means what you think it means....  Awful, Awful, Awful.

When you want to be ignored, this is your place! I honestly have never been ignored like that in my life. I walked in with my mother and right away someone greeted me and asked me if someone was helping me. I kindly said no and she said someone will be with me. I was happy and I proceeded to look around. For about 15 mins I waited and again someone else asked if I was being helped and I said no. The only thing this lady said was tell the stylist to take you upstairs to where the clearance dresses are. WOOOOW. I didn't know that just because I was Hispanic that I didn't have money to splurge. I was crushed. So I sat with my mom on a couch that was in the front of the store and all I saw were people walking in the store and straight to the back like as if there was a party back there. Everyone was being helped right away and I felt like I was missing out on something. My mother and I sat on that couch for more than 20 mins and I felt like I wasted my time. I walked out not only without a dress that I possibly could've gotten, but angry and as livid as a Hispanic can be.

We had irrigation repair done by Action Lawn & Landscape. Their work was great and they were able to solve our problem. I would love to be able to give them 5 stars for their work. Their office staff were super helpful and gave answers easily.
 
It is only 3 stars because of the customer service with the employee we worked with. He promised us quotes and completion of work and did not follow through without us messaging and calling. In the end, we were without water for 24 hours with no prior notice and were expected to pay more than was on our original invoice. He said that we were "told" about the water being shut off and the additional fees. These were both communicated at the last second without any notice. Poor customer service on his part is why I am giving 3 stars and am not recommending this company to other people.

-Written by Victoria Kenney

Located right in the middle of the CBD is this little GUMBO-like convenience store/mini-mart. Hot chicken, which is what they're famous for, alcohol, beer, bleach, water, toothpaste, cooking oil... I mean if you need it, it can probably be found here. 

The location of this convenience store is great, especially for hotel guests. I mean how many times have you checked into a hotel & realized you needed something the hotel didn't provide? Well if you're near this mini-mart, you can grab it go. That's a plus. And they're open 24 hrs, so you can get it whenever. 

And considering they're in the heart of the CBD, I don't find their prices to be as high as I thought they would be.

Tale of two visits: first time service was quick and food was great. The enormous cinnamon roll was particularly tasty. Second time we were waiting about 30 minutes for scrambled eggs, toast, and bacons and just had to leave. They had not started making it yet when I asked to pay for coffee and tea. Very disappointed.

Came here for a romantic dinner with my husband....I will start with the highs, really good chocolate dessert and yummy lobster Mac and cheese. Now for the lows.....1)ambiance is terrible inside (it's extremely loud and nearly impossible to hear from across the table without speaking super loud)...2)our cioppino was extremely salty  with most of my mussel shells being empty, this experience was topped with 3)an inattentive server. Waited about 15 minutes before the server came over to the table after we were seated. No warm greeting or apology for the delay. He didn't have any recommendations for us nor did he go over the chef's special. He comes back in another 10 min and we get stale tasting bread with olive oil/seasonings served to us :( Absolutely terrible...and inedible. Our dishes come out in a reasonable time. The server never came back to ask us how our meal was or to check if we needed anything. He comes over with the check and I asked to also order a dessert to go (we didn't want to spend anymore time inside than we had to). For a nicer restaurant option, I was definitely expecting excellent service :( hopefully this was an off night and not a reflection of Hyatt's standards.

DESPICABLE!

My husband was referred to this office for a root canal. He had his Member ID card in hand but he was also asked for his Social Security Number. My husband told them no, he would not provide his SSN. 

The office staff informed him that this was their policy and if he wanted treatment, he would have to PROVIDE HIS SSN OR PAY IN FULL OUT OF POCKET. 

He was in a lot of pain and didn't want to deal with the hassle of finding another provider, so he paid $974.00 out of his own pocket. 

Advanced Endodontics then FILED THE CLAIM with the insurance for their TOTAL fee of $1,425. The provider discount was $439 leaving a balance of $986, patient portion being only $226. They now owe us $748. As of today, they claim to have mailed it.

What kind of office "policy" forces a patient who is in pain and has PROOF of insurance, to forgo their desire to protect their personal information or PAY IN FULL.

They should be ashamed of themselves.

This place has a serous problem with estimated wait times. I was told I would be seen in 30 minutes or so and it's now been 2.5 hours and they have no idea how much longer. The staff does not seem interested in trying what so ever to give you an estimated time and their sympathy feels forced, if they do not know they should not say 30 minutes. You are given a pager but truly if you are at urgent care it's not likely you feel like browsing around town window shopping or what not though as the only positive I thought I should mention it. The nurse taking vitals was nice too.

This place was horrible I ordered the veal cutlet Poe boy & it was like 2 week old fried shoe leather I took two bites and threw it out. Unfortunately I couldn't ask for a refund because we picked up take out . Neve again

A bit of a wait since we had a party of 8 and it was Easter. We didn't mind and they were super apologetic.
Had a delicious grilled cheese (Sara Joe's), tried the beer biscuit, pancakes, everyone at the table was happy. Owner is super friendly, good prices, took a bunch of pies for later. If you are in the area definitely stop by for breakfast or lunch.

This is an upscale artisan market with restaurant/coffee shop inside.  The ambiance is great.  Staff are helpful.  It is counter order style and then they bring you your food to your table.  There is indoor and outdoor seating which is nice.  The courtyard is shaded and it is nice to sit there in the mornings.

I had the tamale breakfast which was a deconstructed tamale.  This was delicious and not too heavy.  The poached egg was a nice combo with it.  The avocado was fresh.  A very tasty breakfast for sure.  

 Good place for breakfast.

Now I'm not sayin it was....and I'm not saying it wasn't.....but I ate at Mixto on Tuesday afternoon and had a stomach bug something mighty fierce on Tuesday night. Add that to what I thought before my face was in the toilet (atmosphere = good, waitress = attitude, food = blah), and I surely won't be a repeat customer.

Pretty darn tasty! gf and i went today for lunch around 1 or a little later. It was super empty, only one other table, so the service issues other people have mentioned were not in play. it's cute and clean and i like the low key decor, such as it is.  The server was very nice and friendly. We ordered a rice and a sesame naan to share, and got a chicken tika masala, aloo gobi (potato/cauliflower) and bhindi (okra). they were all really good, and the bhindi was awesome -- i am not always an okra fan but this was delicious. there's just enough left over for small lunch tomorrow, bill was $22. will definitely be back for more!

WHAT AWFUL CUSTOMER SERVICE. at all cost- do not ever get the home delivery- we're now on 4 days of no couch and customer service has done nothing but kept us on hold for hours at a time and had no answer for us to where our couch is or when it's being delivered after false promises of same day delivery. Very upsetting.

I try to give this place a chance because it's across the street from my job. However I've never had a good experience with the food. My egg is either burned, or the biscuit isn't cooked all the way. This time there was MOLDDDD on my biscuit. 

I called to let them know and they were happy to give a refund.

Great service, great coffee, but food definitely needs more work/attention.

We went there today for lunch and the hostess seemed confused and was unaware where the men's bathroom was located.  She then walked us down to the entrance of the restaurant to "get ya as close as possible" but we were in the way of the servers bringing drink orders to the tables.  I'm hoping she was just a new employee. We were brought to our table fairly quickly and provided a list of the specials.  The restaurant filled up quickly so I hoped it was a sign the food was good but I was mistaken.  I asked the waiter if the crab cake melt was good and he said it was very good as he had tried it.  I ordered that with a side salad.   When the food arrived it was less than pleasing and barely warm. I regret now not taking a picture to show the presentation.  It was two English muffin halves topped with crab and then they stuck a big uncooked slab of tomato on top which cooled off the crab melt and was not an attractive top to the meal.  Additionally, I couldn't find the cheese that would have made it a melt.  I also ordered a ginger ale which wasn't carbonated.  Really not a great meal.
Finally, there was a woman who evidently worked at one of the stores who walked around the tables smiling and turning her head slowly to attempt to make eye contact so she could perhaps draw you into her store.  She carried a sign that had the store name on it.  The only problem was that she made 3 passes slowly walking by and it became a nuisance rather than something that draws people into a restaurant.  It reminded me of the people that are at the entrance to the food store every weekend trying to engage you in conversation for a donation.... except these people are inside while I'm tying to eat.  I get it, peddlers village..trying to peddle their goods but I'm just trying to eat my barely warm crab melt and get out of there.  
The waiter was new and was training but he was very nice. Even though the food was terrible, I gave him a good tip.

Dizengoff was a great place for an afternoon snack/lunch. Their interior reminds me of a sidewalk eatery you'd see in a European/Asian country. The interior is quite dark with its intentionally shabby looking interior, and picnic-table types. They had a mosaic on the wall with different colored tiles, and a menu board that reminded me of the writing on old-style movie theaters. They also had a free jar of bazooka bubble gum at the counter, which really brought out the eatery vibe. Here, you paid as you ordered at the counter then they would bring you out your food, once it was prepared. 

On this really hot day, we decided it would be a great idea to order a Frozen Lemonada. It had a very sharp and tart taste to it, as well as a very lemon taste to it. It reminded me a lot of San Pellegrino with a hint of lime, in the sense it tasted more like sparkling water than it did of actual lemon. 

We also ordered lamb hummus, which costs around $12.5 dollars. The hummus itself is reminiscent of what you'd expect in Zahav, wherein it was extremely creamy, smooth and fluffy. The lamb, which came with some herbs, added a nice salty and herby taste to it, which the hummus was able to balance out extremely well. The pita was also great, as it was extremely warm, and soft, making it much easier to eat with the hummus. There were two of us, but we split the hummus and the pita, and still felt like we had more than enough. 

The cucumber salad was really fresh and crispy. The herbs were also a nice touch to it, even if it was a bit on the spicier side. The pickles and onions were also great additions to help cleanse the palate. 

I'd definitely come back again, if I am ever craving hummus. I'm just not sure if I'd ever get a Frozen Lemonada again.

I hate to give a local restaurant this low rating, but was so disappointed that I probably won't go back.   Spent $100 for two meals and an appetizer.   Appetizer (calamari) was so so. It was very tough and chewy to me.    Had the pasta'e'fagioli and it was awful.  Had an off taste.  Then onto chicken francese which was cold, overcooked, tough and probably the worst chicken francese I have ever had.....and that includes catered meals.   We paid our bill and my friend still had not finished her wine.   The manager came up and asked us to leave because he needed the table for someone who had requested a bar table (note there were other vacant bar tables). His attitude was what I would term smarmy...The tables overlook a wall so its not like our particular table was the best in the house or anything.... I have gone there twice previously and had good food and service so I was really surprised.  This has never happened to me in a restaurant before but I think I will choose another restaurant before I go there again.

Usually I don't write much reviews simply because I'm too lazy. But I couldn't resist myself from writing one because the food was SO bad. We ordered Lamb Roganjosh where the lamb was too chewy and dry, Kaju Paneer Makhni's gravy was good but Paneers were really hard, and lowest on the chart was Boneless Chicken Biriyani which is the worst biriyani I have ever had in my life. Why would you prepare a dish with boiled chicken breast pieces in a yellow masala rice and sell it as Chicken Biriyani?!

I wish I had an option to rate 0 stars. 

PS. Who puts so much sugar in Raita?

Loved this restaurant. Not far from the Airbnb we are staying at. Ordered the chicken pasta wasn't in the mood for seafood. It was delicious. Got the conch fritters and they were good too. The Caesar salad was small but good. Drinks were delish. Krista was my server. She was awesome. So attentive and sweet. Everyone was so nice from beginning to end. They even opened the door and everyone wished us a good evening. We are visiting and really enjoyed our visit. Def Check them out. Good parking in the front. Clean and love the decor. We had a nice time there and would come back if in the area.

Never been here before and definitely never coming back! I received a few gifts from here for Christmas gift from my work. Today I came to just exchange a sweater that didn't fit and was planning to shop (I.e. SPEND MORE MONEY than what the sweater cost) and the girl working said "we have a 14 day policy so sorry." Are you kidding me?! 14 days from a Christmas gift that you wouldn't allow me to spend the $85 the sweater was worth plus more to have a great experience and become a loyal customer? I shop at certain boutiques because of the customer service, then the quality of merchandise. This place seems to have cute but expensive clothes, definitely never going to have me as a customer here. Horrible policy's and "customer service!"

A gem of a boutique in the Mall, amazing team that works there.  Great clothing options for all sizes and styles.  Check in often as you will see new items on the shelves and hanging elegantly for you to pick.
Expensive dining in for lunch - and rude servers made me not go back - food used to be great -front end staff too - but at the end they acted like they were doing me a favor. I went elsewhere

I absolutely loved this place. I could tell lotsa folks who work at the hotels and casinos go here on their days off. I saw two dealers and a concierge the day I went. I felt it was mostly just locals. I ordered a country fried steak with gravy and a side of eggs and hash browns. It was delicious, and came with a side of cantaloupe, too. The service was very friendly, and although it was packed, I didn't feel rushed out. It's definitely worth walking across the river from the casino scene to enjoy a breakfast or brunch here.

They scammed my friend and she lost $90 because of it since she had prepaid. These people are HORRIBLE. Don't stay here. There was urine all over the toilet and burn marks on the beds. There were pubes in the bed, and in the sink. Also, there were holes in the wall and the mirror was cracked. Probably bed bugs. Just pay the extra money to stay at a nice hotel. This one is a sack of crap. My friend will be talking to a lawyer. We're college kids who visited Philly, and we wanted to stay at a place on a budget. I did not appreciate the catcalling from the meth heads, nor did I appreciate the staff peeking into our room.

The hot corned beef sandwich was nicely presented, but had way too much salt. It tasted like it might have been from an industrial source--do they have thinly sliced corned beef in cans?

Our waitress was very friendly and helpful. It's a shame the food wasn't any good. The 8-page menu should have warned me off--how can you possibly make so many different things well?

I've know I have had Fresco's before tonight, unfortunately, it wasn't very memorable. So this review is about tonight's meal. I called in my order around 8:45 pm, well after the dinner rush. It still took over an hour for the food to get to me. Funny how it arrived 10 minutes after I called. Just the amount that it takes to get from Frescos to my house. And when I got my food, it was cold. You know how cheese hardens up on your when its cold? Yeah, it was that cold. $12 for cold pizza? And to add insult to injury? The chicken was super dry. Next time, I'll pass.
Had a great dinner here tonight. The shrimp appetizer was very good as was the pot roast and chicken and waffles. Cant wait to go back

I was excited to come here after reading about the fresh donuts in all kinds of fun flavors. It was worth the beautiful morning drive to get here at 0800 opening on a Saturday morning. I went right at opening thinking the place would be crazy packed, but surprisingly it wasn't during the time I was there.

I believe their menu might change a bit with the seasons but don't quote me on that. I just thought I saw/read they had Christmas selections out but don't recall any flavors being particularly for Christmas. Maybe I overlooked it or misunderstood. But either way, so happy with the flavors and quality and friendly service too!!

Something has happened to the Eegee's quality. 
My Eegee's Fizz lacked the fizz and was full of lemon seeds at the bottom of the drink. My husband's Tea-Eegee's lacked the tea flavor and the Eegee's slushy consistency. Disappointed after waiting in the drive through line for 25 minutes. I'll wait until they get their stuff together to try again.

The Smiling Elephant possibly has the best Thai food in metro Nashville. I ordered the somtum green papaya salad and their house pad Thai with glass (rice) noodles (it should be noted, I did not request these and they are an option. I think the kitchen may have made a mistake). The ambience is one of my favorite features of this establishment. The warm, wooden-ceilings, floors, and tables complement the spotless, white walls perfectly. Gilded picture frames with elephants and royalty fit nicely with elements of greenery spotted throughout. The noise was raucous and joyful. 

The staff here is no-nonsense and speedy. Don't feel too offended if they are rushing, they just have so many patrons to serve and get through. That being said, hospitality could be improved. When I asked for a customer receipt, the hostess stared at me for a few full seconds before printing one out and tossing it at me wordlessly. Our waitress also served a table which arrived after us, leaving us to ourselves for an hour without food on a Tuesday night from 7-8pm. The food is good, but not that good. Our entire table felt slighted, and we were not alone. All around us, patrons were asking for receipts, requesting service, and requesting boxes. While the waitstaff succeeded in getting people glasses of water, that fulfilled the minimal requirement. The lines were exorbitant for a Tuesday excursion, and the service did not warrant the hype previously given.

The parking is atrocious. People will meander around the lot because they don't want to stand in the crowded, claustrophobia-inducing line at the entrance. There is a private lot down a long, sloping hill which is near impossible to get out unless you grit your teeth and say a few prayers. Yikes. I will try again via takeout, please. 

Not the best Thai place due to location by a long shot, but you could do worse than the Smiling Elephant. Plus, they make a MEAN, green somtum salad. That alone should entice you through the parking lot from hell.

Perfect for special occasion (read: $$$$).  Candle-lit, chic black chairs and thick oak tables, well-dressed waiters and diners make for an intimate ambiance and upscale experience.  The focus here is tapas and wines (obvi), but feasts include a whole pig roast, lobster paella, and special occasion pre fixes.   

I recommend splitting a pitcher of the white sangria, and adorning it with pulpo, mussels, and an ensalada verde (as large as an upside-down cereal bowl).   

It's Garces, so good cheeses and meats as well.  If you are into that .

stephanie the owner of wags is zany and fun ,,, she has a huge following due to her love of animals and her abilities with people ,,,,,he service is both affordable and convenient !!!! i recommend several dog walkers to my clients and she is definitely at the top of the list !!!!      prices are reasonable and competitive and she is very flexible !!!!!     please check out her services !!

Absolutely love the IMS. Same shit every year but it never gets boring. Be prepared to spend so much money but it's fun in the process. Food is over priced and alcohol is never strong. Good thing is you can bring in your own bag or cooler (look up size limits before going). When they have big events going on there is tons of free stuff. Carb day was amazing this year. Around race time the IMS is the place to be!

I enjoyed the food, the staff, & the ambiance I really like the black & white look. it was loud it made it hard to hear the waiter poor guy had to repeat himself, i think its a combination of the music that was playing in the restaurant and the live music playing at the next door night club, & parking is tricky. However food was great. I've had the coconut soup, fried rice, drunken noodles, spare ribs, & of course thai tea and Everything I've had is delicious price per entree is around $10-&15. I will definitely be back!!

We love the bar and all the food they offer.
We are trying everything, and so far, it's all great!
Heather and Brandie are great hosts, and keep your glass full.
I had the inside out quesadia special yesterday, and just had left overs for my lunch.
My wife had the fish and chips, and they were also great. At 8 bucks it's a real deal.
We go every week and will continue to...the beer selection is real nice.
My wife and I love coming to splash island.  We got in easily and there is so much to do once inside!

This isn't a review of the food because I never got the far. This is a comment on the stuck up nature of the hostess when we went on 2/21/18 around 8:30. We had been to the area during the day to walk around and have a beer and everyone was nice. So we figured it'll be a good place to have dinner. It was a Wednesday and it's a big place so I assumed no reservation were necessary. Anyway when I walked in with my wife and mother-in-law she looked at me with a weird face "like, why are you here?" After an awkward few seconds she forced a smile. I ask for a table for 3. She replied, "Yeah at this point in the night we're not taking walk-ins." She said you can try the bar which was clearly full while at the same time Not looking in our direction. She didn't offer anything along the lines of an apology, can I reserve something for you on another night or this is a better time if you don't have reservations. We weren't too casual it was a nice summer night so I had khaki shorts a nice T-shirt etc. The ladies also looked respectable.  Maybe she doesn't like Latinos. 
It a shame because it is a nice place but I guess you have to look a certain way.

WARNING! THEIFS & WILL BREAK YOUR PROPERTY! Do not hire these people. Not only did they break my 55" TV on move in, they stole my property (of all things my underwear bin.) they showed up 2 hours late to unload my truck, and when I asked about the theft and breaking of my TV, I was slandered, cursed at, and told "Eat 2 Dicks" and "Your a horrible excuse for a human being." Not only do they have terrible customer service, but they are unprofessional and unaccountable.

I would've love to give zero stars, but it won't allow it. I called and set up an appointment with this Orkin branch. "Technician" was scheduled to come here between 12 & 2. 2:20 I called and was told they tried to contact him and his phone went directly to voicemail. He must be on the way. 3:30 I call again. Again the technician is called and no answer! I called back at 4:30 to see what to expect. Another phone call to the technician hours unanswered! I asked to have a supervisor call me. No response from anyone! I finally called back the next day and was told I could be rescheduled the following day! Very unprofessional business and will NEVER call again! I recommend Todd's Pest Control!

The starbucks was closed when we got here so I tried their latte and chai tea latte. Their menu is very limited but they has enough to satisfy your coffee addiction and please your sweet tooth. There was only one lady working the cash register and coffee machine so be prepared to wait if there's a long line. Not a bad way to get your coffee fix though. The service was also very good. Friendly staff and good coffee. Better than starbucks to be honest. I recommend coming here than Sbux if you want good service and a good cup of joe!
Pretty decent custard and the nice boy behind the counter was super sweet and helpful. I'll be back again tomorrow before I skip town! :)

Couples Prenatal Yoga with Stacy Broussard proves to be creative, calming, and easy for a beginner like me or challenging for my intermediate level yoga mate. Her voice is soothing and really takes you deep into relaxation. Highly recommend Urban Breath and Stacy.
Food was great, had the wings and they make some of the best wings I have ever had. Great small environment where I got to enjoy watching the NFL draft eating wings.

I think this place is ok, or maybe it was what I ordered. 

I had the pecan crusted chicken atop a salad. Honestly I didn't taste any pecans, but it was definitely a nicely fried piece if chicken. Now the mustard dressing was good, it kind of reminded me of Outback's honey mustard dressing. 

Would I return?  Sure. Will I order the same?  Nope.

For $14 for a salad I expected a whole lot more.
I love this place so much, the only place I trust with my pups. Thank you for your service & patience.

I had to move into an apartment very last minute and they did everything they could to make my move-in process quick and easy. They were helpful in all aspects of the move-in including movers, making sure everything was right in the apartment, and sending dimensions to my designer. Great experience!

It was only okay, especially since we waited over 40 minutes for a table. It's pretty crowded inside and we were pretty much 4 inches away from another table. The server was fine but didn't seem friendly and wasn't trying to make sure our experience was good. It very much seemed like it they were trying to get us out as soon as they could. 

The gumbo and jambalaya was good but they were super salty.

Came over here on my trip to New Orleans. Needed a sports bar to watch college football and came on a friend's suggestion. Definitely a dive bar. Good amount of TVs for the many football games that were on (came after the LSU game). 

Had their catfish po-boy, which was excellent and a good price. They also have a really good beer selection, including local beers.

Place gets 3 stars as my experience was tainted by a "bus" of bros/sausage fest that came in around halftime of my game. They also need to have their restroom cleaned more often.

Bleh...what a disappointment!  I was expecting a lot more out of this place.

I went for the lunch buffet with 2 coworkers. Maybe we should have known something was up when we showed up and there were 4-5 people standing around bored and waiting to greet you. There was no lunch crowd at this place. It was not packed at all. Some of these greeters should have been in the back cooking instead! Then they proceed to seat us at the furthest table away from the buffet. By the way, there are stairs at this place mind you.. So you must be able to balance and carry strange "tray" plates as you go up and down the stairs to your table.

As for the food.... it seemed like it was basically good quality food. But, there was not a whole to choose from. There is a very limited selection of choices. There was a sign offering salmon with  a crawfish tail sauce, but the pan was empty. I asked someone if they would be bringing more soon. She said she would check. When she got back to me she told me that they would have some more out shortly. Well, after waiting 10 minutes, the cook puts out sausage instead. (!) He proceeds to leave the salmon sign up still. I asked him when the salmon would be up and he told me they were all out. (!) So much for waiting around wasting my time for that. (By the way, I don't like sausage...so couldn't eat that.) Since there wasn't much else to eat, I got myself a bowl of the strange looking light colored gumbo. I've never seen a gumbo that looked like white gravy before.. Even though it didn't look right, it tasted fine. One of my coworkers gave me a piece of her beef since I was not able to get any meat. We all agreed that the beef tasted good, but it was cold. Cold food..? O...K...

We felt bad for the people at the table next to us. They kept asking to speak to a manager because they had been waiting over 30 minutes for their meals that they ordered off the menu. For some reason, no one could get a manger to them and when she finally did arrive and they told her their story, all she offered was a free dessert. Kind of strange that it would take so long for their food to arrive when the place was empty.. (Like I said... send the greeters to the back to get cooking...geez...)

I don't think I'll be coming back here anytime soon. They have a lot they need to work out still. Also, drinks are not included with the buffet price. I figured as much... but just a warning to those that are used to drinks being included with your buffet meal.
My favorite place in Nashville for meat & three and hashbrown casserole.

I recently called to inquire about making an appointment, while the representative that I spoke with seemed friendly enough, all I wanted was a basic cleaning and my insurance does cover cleanings and X rays they were "mandating" some sort of imaging that the insurance would not cover, I informed her that I could not afford this she told me that I pretty much didn't have a choice. I do fully understand the importance of dental health but I do not appreciate being told that I have to pay for something or I would not be seen. There was no compromise, such as paying installments or being billed said amount and that feels unreasonable. It definitely made me feel as though all they wanted was my money. I highly doubt I will be visiting this establishment.

I love chipotle, but this location is just terrible. I've gone a few times hoping maybe it was just a fluke.  Overall the food at this location is poor quality and doesn't taste as good as others

Staff is very friendly, however.

i got 2 appetizers and 2 entrees. i paid $40. for chinese food carry-out. i thought "well it must be amazing...."  so i gave it a try. yes, the food was good but the portions were so small! usually when i've gotten chinese food, I have left overs for lunch. not here. there was barely enough as a dinner entree. it was too little for that much money.

Been back a couple times for drinks. Mixed drinks are good but a little pricy. Two of those times it took 1/2 hr to get served and we were there an hour before we got food and it was not very busy. Food has been hit or miss.

Food (7.5/10) = Tried the Korean BBQ Short Rib Beef Tacos, Thai Basil Chicken Tacos, and the Edamame Tacos.  The best were the bbq short ribs and chicken tacos, although they didn't leave a lasting impression.  The edamame was boring for my taste.  I'v heard about this korean-mexican fusion food in magazines and TV, so I was looking forward to trying it out for the first time.  Unfortunately, it didn't have the "wow" factor I had hoped for.   It wasn't worth the $8.  Also ordered the thai tea....it was good but not worth the $3.  

Ambiance (8.0/10) = It's a food truck so not exactly an appropriate category to comment on.  It is a colorful truck

Service (9.5/10) = Very friendly.  

Overall = Eh, this place was ok.  If I worked around the area, or was a Penn student needing food on the go, then I would return to this place.  It seems like the OG Dog and steamed pork buns are popular choices based on previous yelp reviews.  If for some reason I ever stop by this place again for food, I'll try the other items on the menu, and maybe it will warrant a change in my ratings.
Excellent bread and great tasting meat! You can't beat their neat. ;-) crowded and a line,  it well worth the wait.

Wonderful people to work with, they genuinely care about their customers.  Rarely can you say there is a business you can trust and rely on... but this place is top notch!  Brought my elderly dad's laptop in, and these folks fixed it and treated us with respect in a speedy fashion.  If you want to support the local economy this is as good as it gets!

Came here looking for crawfish, and found out that this was one of the few places that offered it when it was not high crawfish season.  It was  $6 a pound, and we got four pounds.  Almost got five pounds and glad we didn't when I saw how much crawfish was included.  We also ordered the sausage.

The crawfish was pre cooked.  I don't know if it was fresh or frozen crawfish since it was already cooked.  The crawfish has a good amount of flavor from the spices.  The sausage was big.  We weren't able to finish it since we had a meal before this aiming with the four pound of crawfish.  

Parking is limited.  There is only one restroom.  Space is small, but they have a few picnic tables outside.

I have had many meals at Doc B's over the last few years and was completely disappointed in my to-go order this evening. I ordered the steak Wok out bowl which I have ordered numerous times. The steak was so burnt and there were large black crispy chunks all throughout my meal. The full meal tasted burnt that picking around the meal was not an option. It was NOT edible. Highly disappointed! It's one thing for food to taste a little off, or different, but for the chef to knowingly see this and still prepare it this was is what is most frustrating. Laziness. I don't write reviews, and actually had to download Yelp this evening to write this. Gross!

Small restaurant in a local strip mall. Disappointing experience.

Cons:
-breading on Halibut was too thick
-Halibut had a "fishy" taste (fresh fish should never taste "fishy")
-Trout had too much dill weed sauce, hard to tell what species it was

Pros:
-Good waitstaff and prompt service
-reasonable prices
-clean

Awesome pizza! Best "gourmet" and GF in Carpinteria. Service s.l.o.w...understaffed,
so sip on a variety of local beer or wine.

A little cluttered but it has all the things you don't need but want. I liked how the staff were totally laid back. They greeted me, but didn't pester. I was able to sort of peruse the store. I bought some nice mugs and finally finished off my set.

Good cookies!

I bought some of these cookies for my wife and she and the kids enjoyed them very much. The only downside is that each cookie cost over $1.35 a piece which can really add up fast.

I. Love. Cadiz. Period.

I was already impressed walking in. I really loved the outdoorsy feeling the restaurant gave me. The brick interior walls, the wicker chairs, the trees inside... It was all very thoughtfully planned. 

The food was amazing (probably because the chef is one of the top 250 chefs in the world!). I loved everything we ordered. The San Sebastian cocktail drink was very delicious too. I couldn't taste any of the alcohol at all! My absolute favorite was the oysters!!! If you love oysters, you HAVE to try the oysters here. They're to die for! The home-made yogurt with guava was pretty scrumptious too! So was the margherita flatbread... and the wild boar ribs... Mmmm... 

Drinks were super cheap - $5! The oysters were $2 a pop. We had SO MUCH food!! $140 for 2 people - and it was well worth it (we splurged a bit with all the dishes). 

This place gets 5 stars because of the ambiance, the food, and the service. The other thing is that it's probably the only place on State St. of it's kind! I wish I lived closer to this place. Love it and can't wait to come back!

Before we begin this review, I need to state that I am a tea snob and that reviews may be skewed!

So I had high expectations for this place as I thought it would be a 7 leaves or an authentic boba tea shop but I was pretty disappointed. I come from the SGV in LA, the mecca of all things asian and tea drinks. I ordered a sea salt jasmine green tea, a boba milk tea, and a rose green tea with boba. Besides the price, which was way too overpriced for my opinion, the actual drinks didn't really impress me. The boba was not very chewy, the tea wasn't too flavorful, and it didn't have a strong taste. I would compare this to a lollicup if yelpers have been there and if you have you know for the price of this place (~$5-6 for a small) you would normally pay ~$1-2 for a regular. If I think in terms of an average person from SB, I would say this place is decent but still a bit overpriced for a drink. 

I haven't experienced the karaoke, and it seems like one of the few in SB so I would give it a try. For those thinking about trying the drinks here, I say do it and form your own opinion as it was decent but I don't think I will be back. 

- J

My boyfriend and I were up in the air between don pablos and olive garden tonight. We chose olive garden, boy what a mistake. 

Our meal started out with our obnoxiously loud waitress telling us what a bad day she was having and how she couldn't wait for it to be over (which she would repeat at least 3 more times throughout the night), especially since the "table over there" (Yes, she was actually pointing at the table and its occupants) didn't leave her a tip.

We ordered our salad (mine) and soup (boyfriends) along with some alfredo dipping sauce for our breadsticks. 

Soup, salad and breadsticks came out, lettuce was overdressed and very wilted. Dipping sauce came out about 5 minutes later at which time we asked for more breadsticks. We were told "oh, it's going to be awhile".  By the time the breadsticks were out the dipping sauce was a solidified bowl of cold glop.

Our meals came out (smoked mozzarella chicken dine and take one home special) and they looked and felt like they had been under the heat lamp for way too long. The sauce on top had dried to the pasta and turned it into one gelatinous glob. When I mixed it the pasta underneath was bone dry. 

Our waitress was nowhere to be found, we finally tracked her down and asked for some extra sauce to which her reply was "it will be a few minutes, I have to go greet my new table".  Ten minutes later we had sauce and cold dinners. I couldn't even eat mine; boyfriend was starving and ate his. We waited 20 more minutes until she came back around, spilled 4 large waters on the floor (and my feet) and asked if we wanted our check. 

I've waitressed before, if you go to a table and someone's meal is untouched you ask if everything was ok, don't just say I'll wrap it up for you.

I was so ready to get out of there I didn't even bother asking to talk to a manger. I will not be back. Ever.

It amazes me how people can bring themselves to rate this place 4 or 5 stars especially the one who eat Pho!! Omg! I had to stop after the 2nd bite and drive 15 minutes to my regular spot to order another bowl of pho to eat!! I haven't done this before. No one deserves to eat this kind of food really. You can clearly tell they don't give a damn about what they serve to you. I truly think this place need an honest review so just in case people who read reviews can avoid paying for food that they couldn't even eat like me. And after you read this, if you still want to eat at this place, all power to you! Don't say I didn't warn you!
Not very accommodating, not a people oriented business, competitive rates but poor customer service.

Visiting St Louis from New York, we were disappointed. I'm not sure why most ppl are saying the calamari was delicious. We ordered it, it tasted like eating paste or chalk, is this consistency you tasted?  They gave us another dish of calamari, and it was even more calamari, it was 5% better, but it still wasn't good. Not sure what it was, but being from New York we would consider that calamari gross. 

Please tell me it was a bad batch or off season; that should not have been served.  

And this is not a case of New York food is better, it was just bad we were stunned.

My boyfriend and I decided to get a late night dinner and Dante's Fire was always a place that always showed up on Yelp, so we decided to give it a try!

Boyfriend: Upon pulling up to the parking lot, I thought I might see my black sheep cousin since it looked like a strip club. But to my surprise (and slight disappointment) it was not a strip club. We really enjoyed how late the restaurant stays open and the service was prompt and courteous.

Before putting in our order, the waitress gave us some house made bread with two different types of butter. The bread was not bad, but both of us thought the butters were slightly salty. For the appetizer, we ordered a bowl of Clam Chowder to share. It was really good with a unique flavor and plenty of clams. I ordered the Tuna Tataki for my entree and thought it was pretty good, but a touch salty as well. Nevertheless, it was a nice spin on the traditional Tuna Tataki.

Boyfriend: I ordered the smoked pasta gambare. It was delicious and creamy. Not very spicy though and some bites were a tad salty. The portion appeared small but it was deceivingly filling.

Overall, we thought Dante's Fire was pretty good. The service and food were both great! The price was fair as well.
The girl at the front door has a power trip!!!! My party of six was turned down because she thinks she runs the restaurant..

Charming, cute and well appointed room in a location that is walking distance to downtown. 

Check in was straightforward and easy. 

Biggest downside was the extra guest in the bed.

We enjoyed our meal here - we arrived from out of town and every other place nearby required a reservation. There was a long wait to get a table, but the staff moved so efficiently that it wasn't too bad - operations deserves a hand. The food was good - not great.  Both apps (poblano corn empanadas and lobster tostadas) were incredibly flavorful, my margarita was so tasty - not too sweet, not too sour- and the rim was the perfect match. For dinner we ordered the burger and the fish tacos - both were ok. The burger was good quality meat and cooked to temp but it was bland. The fish tacos were a no from me, dog. Just don't. Not horrible; but not delicious either - they were tolerable.  Our service was really great; some staff smiled less than others, but everyone worked really hard, seemed to work as a team, and as busy as they were, they made it all look so effortless. I might try this place again; wouldn't rate it as an absolute must visit restaurant, though.
FPP is good / service is very Nashville. Buy the dough and go home to make it in your own oven. It's so goood.

This was my second visit to the restaurant since it became the Beijing Duck House and I was happy to find that décor had been updated and the food much improved.  The dishes we ordered were delivered quickly and were quite tasty.  The staff was attentive and the menu interesting.  Worth a try.
Very good seafood and drinks. Gets pretty crowded even on a weekday so be prepared to stand or wait forever for a seat.
I had a wonderful experience here with a cashier who was very kind and helpful. I have always had good experiences with customer service here and good meals too.

This pizza joint lured me in mainly because their commercials rave that they were started by an actual Italian man that knew what he was doing.  Having loved the pizza we had in our trips to Italy, I thought I would give it a try.  The verdict was nothing more than another fast pizza joint that was certainly no better than a Pizza Hut, Dominos or Papa Johns.  In fact, it was greasier than most of the others.  The highlights of the pizza were the old world pepperoni that actually curls up in cooking and tastes great!  The only down side to these little guys is that they are extremely salty so for me, it made the pizza a little too salty as a whole.  I did like that you can flavor your crusts just like you can at Hungry Howies and the parmesan garlic crust was certainly tasty.  Overall, I doubt I will be going back here.  I would rather drive down to Best NY Pizza and get a decent pizza than to waste money on yet another pizza chain like this.

Deja vu!!! house turned restaurant, extended wooden porch seating, with a down home vibe. I've been somewhere east in Tampa who has a similar concept except its only available on Sundays... Hmmm...
Why countdown til Sunday, when you can fill your southern eats craving on any given day!!
 What is southern food? To most, the first thought that comes to mind is fried goodness. But in reality, it's more than that. It's comfort food... Food that reminds you of Sunday dinner at a family members house. Food that makes you feel good. Recipes that have names you can pronounce made with ingredients you keep on hand.

Southern fresh gives you and inviting vibe of home away from home. The menu is quite limited, offering two appetizers,  two soups, two salads, four entrees and two dessert selections the day I dined. They do provide and adequate beer/wine list.

My thought process would be if the menu is this simple, every dish, side item etc. should be spot on. On a side thought, of a menu this limited, why didn't this establishment go a fast casual route?

Nonetheless, lets talk food:

Starters:

-Grilled chicken wings: Bang!!!! Spot on flavor profile. The dish comes with six whole wings. They are seasoned, smoked and grilled off. When they come, you bite into tender chicken who is smokey down to the bone, on the outside is its dry rub seasoning balanced and flavorful and subtle charred bits on the edges that resonate smokiness. It is also served with a sweet and tangy barbeque sauce which works well with this chicken. 

Entrée:

-Fried Chicken: I was highly recommended and noted worth the wait. That being said, it is made to order, so expect about a 20 minute turn around time. My limited vision of the kitchen noted literally a small 4 burner stovetop with cast iron skillets ready to go for each fried chicken order. You can order your pieces white, dark or mixed. Testing how technically sound the fried chicken was, I decided to order white meat.  On first bite seemed solid, crispy/crunchy outside and moist/meaty chicken breast interior. I must say however, the chicken didn't seem to have a flavor build meaning I tasted some simple flavors on the outer coating but reaching down to the bone there was a lost for flavor. Now, my grandma didn't make me chicken or southern food as a kid, but the new skool in me would say where was the flavor build profile on this chicken in contrast to the love put into the grilled chicken I started off with as an appetizer.

Sides: Mash potato and a side of gravy: Mash was ok, a little peppery on some bites but nothing that yells comfy potatoes to me. The Gravy was also basic, maybe a chicken stock based gravy but nothing yelling unique gravy.

-I was told if you are putting more than 5 ingredients into your collard greens then you are using and doing too much. Their collard greens seems like something if I had a grandma this would feel like down home simplicity. The greens were leafy and not overprocessed, the note of vinegar was not overpowering and the small bits of a curated meat added a smokey and salty note.

Dessert:

Banana Pudding: I'm not a big fan of custard desserts, but how could I not end a meal on a sweet note. I did like the bits of banana that were really sweet throughout the pudding. The vanilla being somewhat soggy on the top was a miss for me, yet I'm not sure this may be the traditional approach. Being a fan of texture contrast I would have preferred the wafer crumbled and sprinkled on top leaving its baked crunch intact.

Southern fresh overall has a great concept of "down home" cuisine. I do wish for more "down home" menu items like mac n cheese or meatloaf speaks to my southern comfort. The prices are reasonable and the hospitality was hosted with charm. Yall give em a try now..,.  Hopefully, one day I will return to find a fuller menu that screams  "down home".
Not to impressed by the service or the food. There was a hair on my plate and the sandwich was cold. Went more then once and each time and there was an issue.

Soooo. Where do I begin. First the Manager/ Owner Maria is straight up rude and disrespectful to customers. When I called in to order an item from the menu she told me that I could not order it because the menu  was printed wrong and she was aware of it. I simply asked her to meet the expectation based on the menu in my hand and she told me to order somewhere else. She then said that she is not just the manager but also the owner because her husband owns it. I simply disconnected the call. As a consumer spending my money there is just too much competition to spend money with someone who admits to false advertisement then holds the customer accountable for understanding its a typo on their menu. DO NOT PATRONIZE THIS RESTAURANT the owner is egregious and disrespectful. Not today. In 2019 customer service is paramount and honoring what is printed on your menu is a No Brainer!

  The issue is I attempted to order the eggplant platter that states it comes with fresh fries AND with spaghetti or ravioli. When I called in she stated it only comes with one or the other. Read it for yourself and tell me.

Good evening,

Visited here for the 1st time since my very critical review of a year ago. Very happy to say this visit (Dec29 during football games) was a very good experience. Good food, attentive service & I love the TVs at the booths. Highly recommend the pulled pork sandwich

A hole in the wall never sounded so good

I always forget about Chatterbox, until I'm in a "live music" mood. Despite how long it's been around, this little spot remains one of Indy's best kept secrets. Nestled on the well-loved Mass Ave, I'm surprised by how often it's overlooked. 

Chatterbox offers live jazz seven days a week, with Sunday-Thursdays free and a minimal entry fee of $8 on Saturdays and Sundays. The bar isn't super extensive, but this isn't exactly the spot for craft cocktails. There are light snackables available, if the mood should hit you.

If you're on the hunt for some of Indy's best musical talent, The Chatterbox just might be for you.
Incredible donuts. The apple fritter was the best and largest we have ever seen.  The glazed donuts were great as well. We got the half a dozen and split them up not a failure in the bunch .

Ahhh.   Well we came in on our visit to NOLA and we ordered oysters as an appetizer.    We also ordered food.    Our food came out first and oysters like ten min later.   There was 6 people 3 couples they brought one check said we can't spilt it.    We were like yes you can it took a manager to get a spilt check and it's been 20 min and we are still waiting for it to come back split......edit.   A member of our group left an umbrella and we went back to get it and the manager wouldn't let them.back in and said they didn't find a purple umbrella.....

Poor service. Took 10 minutes to get two beers and we were literally only guests sitting at a table. When food arrived it was not good. Warm at best and tiny portions. Glad we used Groupon because full price would have been terrible. The crab spinach dip was about the size of three table spoons of food. The shepherd pie was 99% cold mashed potatoes and flavorless. The fish and chips were very cold and we saw the server 2 times entire meal once to take order and once for bill. Very disappointed

I went in for an oil change, which was quick and nice. However the inspection report shows that they are no different from those maintenance shops that are trying to rip you off. So basically the report says that my car is gonna fall apart: all four tires, air filter, spark plugs, serpentine belt, valve cover, ALL need to be replaced! There's also brake fluid, coolant, and power steering fluid ALL need to be replaced and flushed. Things like the belt, air filter, and tires are very easy to inspect so I don't know how can they tell me everything needs to be replaced when everything looks good. They also didn't do tire rotation because my tires were "too dangerous to put back on" LOL! The service was ok but I wouldn't believe a word on their inspection report.

I stopped here one night to grab a pizza and wings. I hadn't called ahead so I knew I'd have to wait a little while which is fine. They told me 10 minutes. After about a half hour, I asked if I could get an idea of how much longer it would be, the girl rolled her eyes and told me it's coming out of the oven now. AN HOUR after I got there, when I finally told them to forget it, I'd just go, they had my order. And shockingly, it's cold. 

I thought I got them on an off night. Turns out, that was a super night of service for them. I gave them a second chance recently to eat in with my son. They told me to wait to be seated because they had to bus some tables. Every single table was covered in trash, except the two occupied tables. So we get seated finally and enjoy the lovely symphony of the entire staff screaming at each other while we wait 45 min for our one cheese pizza. So that's fun when we're sitting next to the kitchen and I'm trying to talk to my three year old over the profanity. Half the stuff from the salad bar is empty, the restaurant is filthy and freezing (we had to wear our coats), there's brown chunks floating in our drinks and the waitress is in the corner rubbing her feet (blech!) while all the tables remain covered in trash the entire hour we're there.

I'm really not quick to complain and I'm completely understanding of off nights which is why I never wrote a review of our first visit but this place is insane and completely disgusting. I couldn't find a dirtier restaurant if I tried. The waitress was nice to us and the pizza was good the second time, I'll give them that and end on the only positives.

Honestly one of my favorite places to get my nails done. They go into so much details on your nails without it taking so long. You can literally go with anyone there and you will be happy with your nails. 

They also are very current with designs and stuff. I just dont do the deep designs but from what I've seen they are good at it.

Wow. I'm surprised this place was rated as well as it did. I just didn't get it. The biscuits were just as dense as the ones I'd ordered at any other diner across the US. We had the eat nasty, southern Benny, and hummingbird family preserve and all dishes were very average. I'd say more but this review comes a few days after our visit and nothing was memorable about the place besides the nice "Nashville" sign.

Great place if you are looking for happy hour specials! I came here on a Thursday night and they had some really good deals. As a table, we ordered fried pickles, ranch pretzel knots, and buffalo wings. Everything was so good. The fried pickles came with a honey mustard and aioli sauce for dipping. The ranch garlic knots were life-changing, seriously, I could have eaten all four. They were seasoned perfectly and the ranch sauce was a nice touch. The buffalo wings were also very delicious. I feel like you can never go wrong with wings, but they came with baby brussel sprouts on time and I thought that was a great topping choice. Needless to say, everything was devoured in minutes. 

My drink choice that night was something they had on special. I ordered the watermelon mojito and it was really tasty. The only thing I would add is that I wish it had a little more of the watermelon flavor to it. Otherwise, I thought it was the perfect summer drink. Between the six of us, our bill was about $15 each. I didn't think that was bad for all the food and drinks we ordered.

Paid them $300 to reopen Air returns that were closed when they installed all new Hvac and air duct system in my home in Largo.  I have plenty of complaints about that, the largest being that they threw away a 1 year old UV light in the previous system which could have been used.   Back to the point of air duct reopening; I live out of town and when I came to town and find all the returns closed off, filled with insulation and board.  They however had called me to collect the money saying the job was done.  Now 3 voice mails are not returned.  I am not yelling, but asking them to come out and complete the job.

Absolutely terrible experience! I've lived in philly for a year now and this is probably the worst experience I've had at any establishment! Fire that racist waitress who doesn't acknowledge black customers. I will never go back and I assure you I will make sure everyone knows how terrible this experience was. Shame on you!

let me start by saying i've been here multiple times and it's normally good. they're slightly pricier than others in the area, but i was willing to pay it. but this time... my pedicure was so short and half hearted. it's the first of the season so i expected a lot of scrubbing and cleaning, but there was close to none. the whole thing was over in about 15 mins. i took a pumice stone to my own heels when i got home and took a shower because they still badly needed it. my eyebrow wax was the worst i've ever had. she pulled the wax off so slowly and lazily, and didn't bother doing any scissor trimming, which seems to be standard these days. when i got home and got a good look, i was legitimately angry i had paid for the service. i started tweezing to get everything that was left, but there's so much that i gave up and i'm going to pay for ANOTHER wax somewhere else tomorrow and hope my skin doesn't freak out. so disappointed. got halfway out of the parking lot before realizing she didn't ask me for my punch card... couldn't bear to go back. they're usually good so i'm going to try them again, hopefully my experience will be better with a different tech.

I went here for the lunch buffet today. When I arrived at 11:59am, there were NO pizzas or bread sticks out. So I ate a salad while waiting. We waited 11 minutes for the first pizza to come out. The pizza was not "hot out of the oven" either. The half pepperoni-half hawaiian was dry, hardly any sauce at all. Next pizza was a full hawaiian, then a chicken type pizza. Those were all gone very quickly, and yet we waited again, about 5 mins for another pizza to be put out. The pepperoni was good, hot, and had a nice amount of sauce on it.  Lots of people waiting around to eat. Not good on your lunch time. Place was clean though.

I went in to babies r us to get a gift. Staff was pretty nice, prices are reasonable and a good selection. The cashier was not at all happy. She got annoyed when I asked about a price of something. When she handed me my bag she pushed it into my hands. I hope the next time I go in there they have happier employees working.
So yummy! Got 3 asada with onions yum! Easy parking. Would highly recommend to go on an empty stomach. Soooo delicious
Not fresh at all they took the juice that was already made out of the fridge. The server was rude. Overall I would not recommend this place.

We ordered with the help of our server off of the 2 for $20 menu clearly intending to get that deal. She told us we had to get an appetizer that we didn't originally plan on so that we could get the discounted deal. When the bill came, she didn't give us the 2 for$20 price. When we talked to her about it, she took the bill to her manager to see if they could do something. The manager refused to help...wouldn't even come talk to us. Dad even got up to find a manager and none was found. They wouldn't give us the deal...we ended up paying an extra $10+. 
-the manager sat in the corner watching us, clearly seeing us unhappy and just stated refusing to help or speak with us. Our server lead us to believe we were getting the deal, then didn't receive it. Very dishonest and a complete lack of customer service.

First, the parking situation is awful. Second, when I walked in I had to wait in a long line (35 minutes) for my meal. After waiting that long, I waited a ridiculous amount of time for my food to get here. Also, who else thinks it's crazy they don't bring food out to you? Rude. When i finally got my food, i was disturbed to find bugs in my salad. That is extremely insanitary and I will never come back here again. Also it is expensive. I must say, the McAlister's deli not too far down the road is a much better option than this establishment.

Only giving them Two because of the casino and the dealers. Dealers here are great makes it a great place to play. As for the hotel part of this place, it has gone down hill so bad. I have never been so disappointed with a hotel ever. We used to stay here monthly back when the old owner and managers where there and it was great. Now, they didn't have us reserved for the right month, then insulted us, gave us the wrong room, never vacuumed for the four days we stayed, then charged us full price after being told they would comp two nights and give us a discount. I WILL NEVER BE BACK! They just lost a long time customer!

Yes, it's a chain. Let's get that out of the way first.

Our recent visit here though was anything other than what you would find at a comparable fast casual chain restaurant. The staff interacted with us and behaved like we were at a higher-end restaurant. The cashier was friendly and personable, the dining room guy was always over asking us if we needed anything, and the manager came over and talked to our kids.

Later, the kids (who are 5 and 3) went up to buy chocolate chip cookies, and the manager came back to tell them that he was so impressed with how grown-up they were that he wanted to also give them a rice krispies treat. This is the kind of experience I would expect to get paying 2 to 3 times what I paid for this meal. It may be a chain restaurant with chain food, but I don't think you'll get a friendlier experience than this Noodles & Co. location from another chain restaurant.

Why is anyone eating this mess?  Burger devoid of flavor, covered in chalky shredded cheese, mushy tuna & frozen sides.  Literally 11people walked in and ordered water and food.  Not here to drink, so i assume food is the attraction. Quote of he night:  oops, i forgot your cheesefries in the microwave - nuff said.  Blech, yuck and forget this joint.

I live right down the road from this car wash and there aren't many options on places right on this stretch of 19. I've gone here multiple times but it appears things don't improve with time. I purchased their Superior Wash last visit, which runs around $26, plus a tip for attendants. You'd expect an awesome job for a price that steep. They did a decent job on the inside, but don't make much effort on nooks and crannies. The outside was a total disaster. I had to ask for the towel to finish the job myself. I'll be driving further out next time to a place that can deliver.

I wasn't impressed, i think the stuff just sounds really fancy but the taste is bland to me. I don't think it should be priced so high for what you get. I got the peruvian burger which based on the description seemed great. I ended up with a small burger with a pattie, some spicey sauce on it and a few crumbles of gorgonzola cheese. The lemonade was good,,,,,but I've had way better. $13 and didn't even eat all that expensive boring sandwhich

My husband and I visited on our trip from DC.  This place is fabulous.  Very clean with a hip atmosphere, modern decor.  The food was fresh and delicious.  Great menu selection.  Friendly, attentive staff.  Highly recommended!!!!

The level of customer service is disappointing and below par of the minimum requirements it takes to serve at a coffee shop. First I was looked at like I was from another planet when I asked if I could pour my own creamer into my coffee, which resulted in me having to say "when" while an employee poured it for me. I get if they have some weird food handling restrictions, but maybe don't look at me like I'm insane for being an adult and fully capable of doing it myself. To top off my experience, I asked for a refill (my second cup)  in my "bottomless" cup of joe, and was promptly forgotten about for 10 minutes while they had casual conversations with an incoming customer while the cafe was almost totally empty. I had to pack up all of my work and leave. I regret tipping for the hopes of having a routine coffee shop experience. Never going back!

Kinda mixed feelings about this place...I'll get to the skinny of it all.

The BBQ is decent.   The ribs are tender and good, the brisket is on the dry side, and the sides are OK.  I enjoyed the food.   They don't pull the membrane off the ribs, which is BBQ 101, from where I come from.   

The employees are indifferent at best.   They didn't care all that much... just chop a meat and get it out.   You can tell when people are just mailing it in.   

The BBQ sauce is stellar.   They have a few different, really good options.  You can't hardly screw that up with a good recipe to follow.   A good sauce, hides a multitude of BBQ sins.   

If you're hungry, and want some BBQ you'll probably love it.  

It's OK.

I had the fajita combo with chicken and steak. It was awesome. So much food! It was all very fresh and tender. Presentation was awesome. Everything came out sizzling. Would definitely order this again. Not the biggest fan of the salsas they offer but not a big deal because the food doesn't really need it.
pizza was not spectacular, but decent. The employees were friendly.  Service was quick and efficient.  I was only there passing through, but probably would look for another pizza joint to try

I went to Iron Hill for a faculty lunch. I was a first timer.. I really can't say it was memorable. Just regular franchise food. Would I go again..? Probably but I won't rush there either.. Just meh..

I've been going to Lancers my entire life, and that ended firmly and quickly today. Been out of town for a long time, but always made a point to stop in for a diner breakfast whenever we're back. When you arrive to a middle-school poster print out of the laminated Constitution taped to the door, an eye-roll from the person seating you (apparently for wearing masks) and a table selling copies of a Tucker Carlson endorsed book about how Fauci is the devil, it's clear that they not only don't want our business, but they are hell-bent on actively putting the health and safety of every person who enters their establishment at risk. Seeing as most of the clientele I saw there today was in the 55+ range, they're putting PA's most vulnerable at an even higher risk. Having now spoken about this with many of my friends in the area, this has been a long time coming - I've heard that many people have also had some pretty terrible racial interactions over the years on top of everything else.

Save yourself the trouble - Red Lion is just a bit down the road and when we ate there a week ago they were working hard to protect the health and safety of their workers and patrons.

I live in the area so I figured I had to give this place a try.  I got a large Pepperonius Monster pizza.  The one mistake (and probably biggest) I made was getting the pizza to go.  While it was decent pizza, it would have been better eating at the restaurant.  The pizza box was flimsy and had caved in on the pizza by the time I got it home.   The inside of the restaurant is very nice and they even have what appears to be a very large outdoor patio in the back.  For the price (about $25 for a large pizza) I would suggest eating at the restaurant and not risking taking it to go.

Food was decent but felt a little awkward when our waitress asked my 10 year old daughter if she wanted "soy sauce " for her steak then immediately corrected herself by asking if she wanted steak sauce instead.  She must have assumed all "Asians " use soy sauce with our meals.  Not PC.

the typos on the website should give you a clue as to how together the group is. I went with a friend for a girls' day. we both got massages (they were quite curt when asking if we had coupons for these - we didn't but if you don't like coupons, stop offering them), she got a wrap and I got a facial. we arrived quite early and yet had to remind them that we were there twice. while we both got the worst massages we had ever had, our other treatments were SUPERB. We then sat in the sauna for a bit and the hot tub and just hung around. the setting was a bit too open to groups and even kids who were "just looking" (who wants to be gawked at by pre-teen boys and groups while attempting to while scantily clad and attempting to relax).

This was one of the only places I made it to multiple times while on my trip to NOLA. Loved the atmosphere, and the bartender is amazing. She was very friendly and professional. Great drinks, and the bartender even remembered my friend and I's drink orders when we came back in the second time. This place is a relief from the craziness of Bourbon Street and I definitely recommend it.

THIS RESTAURANT IS 100% DIRTY AND DISGUSTING! I JUST ORDERED FOOD FROM HERE FOR THE FIRST TIME THROUGH GRUBHUB AND YOU KNOW WHAT I FOUND? 

I found a ROACH in my food! A medium-sized, dead roach! And to make matters WORSE, I found ANOTHER dead bug in the food! There's no telling WHAT ELSE is in this food. That is filthy, trifling, BEYOND ANYTHING. This restaurant is about to get SHUT DOWN. I'm reporting this place and I have pictures as evidence. There's no telling how many bugs are being consumed in this place on a daily basis!

****
UPDATE 09-25-18: I have to finish telling you what happened last night. So, basically, I posted this review first, posted it on GrubHub, then I called the restaurant. I don't appreciate how the person on the phone had this "it's no big deal" kind of tone in his voice. It was as if he was used to this thing happening all the time and it was just routine. The nonchalant man sent the driver back out to get the food. I used my fork and put the second bug right back where I found it and gave the food back. (I had wiped the roach on the inside of the white plastic bag the food came in when I was first examining it to be sure I wasn't losing my mind when I SAW that it was a bug in my food)! When the driver came, he had the NERVE to call himself questioning me like he had anything to do with the situation and I was lying or didn't know what I was talking about. He asked to see the bugs. When I showed him all my crisp, high-quality photos taken on my phone, he got all quiet then backed down like "Oh, I'll let the owner know and if he decides to refund you, that's between you two." Yeah, OKAY. Don't question me like you're involved when you're just the DRIVER.  Anyway...he took the food back. In the midst of all this, I was on an online chat with a GrubHub rep and I'm telling him everything that was happening in real time. The restaurant cooperated with me for the most part while I reported to GrubHub BUT here is where they lose all respect from me. They LIED to me. I caught them in a lie. How? Because when I first told the nonchalant man on the phone the situation he said that the restuarant gets complaints all the time about bugs but that's because customers always mistake the variety of Indian spices for bugs. Okay. I CAN understand that, especially since Indian dishes are very versatile with the seasonings and sauces and whatnot. BUT, when the restaurant called me back he said that they looked through the food and couldn't find the bugs I said, " Oh trust me, they're in there. I put it back." So then Nonchalant asks if I can send the pictures I showed the driver because HE wants to make sure it's not just spices. Okay. Not a problem. I took down his number then sent the pictures. Then he texts me and basically tries to compare the bug on the fork to an UNCOOKED CARDAMOM!!! NO! Then he said I'm the first person ever to complain of bugs being on the food and blah blah blah.
