(But I thought people complained of bugs all the time because of "mistaken spices?" 

Mind you, NONE of the people I've talked to so far was even the owner of the restaurant...in fact, I never DID speak with the owner.

So after that back and forth in text, they realized I wasn't playing with them and they called GrubHub with the QUICKNESS to refund my money. 

The GrubHub chat rep I was online with was still working but I told him that the restaurant already called and said to refund me. The GH rep pulled up the info and verified it. And I was refunded. 

I know some folks out there might think I should be satisfied I got my money back and I am but...NO. That's a HEALTH HAZARD. They LIED to me, and also, they NEVER addressed the roach. I cannot....don't EVER go to that place if you can help it. EVER.

Second time disappointed. Ordered dozen at 8am so I thought it would be fresh. Most were stale and had to throw out after a day,  the French toast bagels. Couldn't cut them. Texture it's very spongy to eat. Plain ones were ok though.  Wanted to give it a second chance.

Amazing pizza we love it here. The sauce is where all the magic and makes it different the all other pizza. The Hawaiian pizza with the add a bacon was so good, especially when you see the owner even in the shop working. Always go back I moved a little further and I still go back and buy my pizza there. I love that they have an incentive when you buy so much pizza they give you one. They have so many different and special types of Premium pizza. When you're all done the sauce and cheese in the tasty pizza, the crust is a pleasant finish, it's nice and soft and taste as well.

WOW. What a cute place! I came here for brunch with my boyfriend and we really enjoyed our food! 

We came on a Sunday morning around 1030 and ended up waiting close to an hour for our table which was to be expected. When we were seated our waitress was friendly and got our drink orders right out. I received some burnt coffee that never got refilled and my boyfriend got orange juice and water that never got refilled. 

I ended up ordering the vegetable skillet which was appetizing, however, i waited about 15 minutes after getting my food to finally get the attention of a server to get some ketchup. By the time I did receive my ketchup my food was nearly cold. I still enjoyed it though! My boyfriend ordered a sandwich that he enjoyed but communicated that the bread was a smidge hard. 

I would give a higher review for the excellent food, however, the long wait and lack of service shortly after arrival was a bit of a drag. I'll have to come back on a less busy day.
I've been going here for about a year now, the owner is a very friendly and personable guy. The food is also extremely reasonable and very good quality. I highly recommend the General Tsos meal!

This place is located on the second floor of West Edmonton mall on the east side near the bridal shop.  There is an upper parking lot which is a bit odd to figure out if you've never been here before.  This is a good place for games for kids and adults since there's nothing else close by.  I don't play games but have eaten here and enjoy listening to the bands playing here.

My friends and I have been here a few times but mainly to listen to the band, the dance floor is a good size however not that many people dance so they usually have at least two tables set up there.  I invited a few of my friends to celebrate my birthday and reserved a large table which was on the dance floor.  Since we brought a cake, they kindly offered to put my cake in the fridge.  The food prices are average and I had a burger with fries which was good.  The drinks are a little pricey I thought and we have tried a few different cocktails on the drink menu but none would we order more than once.  They do have the more common drinks like a Caesar, paralyzer, Malibu and pineapple juice if you ask as they are not shown on the menu.  They gave us little bags of popcorn to enjoy before our food arrived which I thought was a great gesture!  Who wouldn't like a little snack while watching the band?  Everyone seemed to enjoy their food, the service was great that night however once the band started playing, it was way too loud.  So loud we had to move our entire party further back.

The very last time our group went to watch the band and dance, we received very poor service.  As we walked by four staff members standing near the bar, we seated ourselves nearby.  No one acknowledged us for a while.  When a server did come over, we asked for water as we were waiting for a few other friends to join us.  We received water but no drink or food menu so had to ask for them.  When we ordered a drink, I asked about the popcorn since we got some all the other times we have been there.  I was told they usually bring some out after we order appetizers but then she did bring us some right away.  We had the butter tarts with ice cream, you get three to an order and they were delicious!  I also ordered a Caesar and it came with a pickle, slice of salami and half a pickled egg on top.  Boy did I enjoy that!  I'll be ordering this drink every time!!

I find the service is hit or miss but it's a great venue for music since it's separated from the games area and there is lots of seating so no one needs to stand all night.  They also have other events like Name that Tune every Wednesday, karaoke and various bands throughout the month.  The events are posted on their website.

I

I usually live by the rule of staying away from anything that glorifies mustaches (dear world, please stop printing "I mustache you a question" phrases on everything), but Prime Stache gets a pass in my book.

We ordered the honey sriracha wings and the Ron Burgundy. The wings were bomb! I was anxious to see what their honey sriracha sauce tasted like, but turns out to be a great combination - not too sweet, not too spicy. I ordered it for my meal and it was definitely plenty enough for me. The Ron Burgundy was also pretty good and hefty. There's an overflowing amount of pork in the sandwich so I found it hard to stay classy, San Diego.  

I don't know how to put this, but our server - Alex, was kind of a big deal. Someone give this server a raise because that was one of the best service I've had at a restaurant in 2016.

My husband took me here for my birthday this past weekend. We adore F. Scott's and look forward to always going...they always have amazing jazz music which provides such a wonderful atmosphere. The martinis are great (well the sakeatini my brother-in-law got wasn't great he said...tasted like a watered down drink). I had the French Raspberry martini and it was amazing! We got there an hour early to sit in the lounge to take in the live band, but it was full, so they opened up their "overflow" room which was actually really nice that way our party was able to sit and chat and enjoy our martinis. We had a wonderful server who really knew her stuff! They had the table decorated with confetti for my birthday.  I had a salad with the lemon vinaigrette and it is by far my favorite...others at my table had the Caesar salad and said it was exceptional. Most of us had the New York strip and it was bursting with flavor. It was on a bed of garlic mashed potatoes that were wonderful! We also had their delicious vanilla bean creme brulee'. It was such a fantastic dinning experience! We can not wait to go back!

Decent little place, the service is always a bit slow, but the food is very tasty. I got steak tacos and they were great authentic tacos with corn tortillas, cilantro and onions. Their homemade salsa is really good, which is one of the most important components for a taco shop! The red salsa is MAJORLY hot so use caution (ten cuidado). My husband got the banana smoothie which was pretty gross - basically frothy banana milk that wasn't really cold. So go for the tacos and skip the smoothies.

A few friends of mine and I are It's Always Sunny In Philadelphia fans, and we decided to take a road trip to Philadelphia to go to the tavern and explore Philadelphia. The service was very disappointing. As former reviews have stated, the bartender and server were both very rude. The server forgot several of our requests and had to keep going back to the kitchen (Ex. I added shrimp to my salad-no shrimp) We were the only ones in the restaurant, and the server walked past our table several times without making eye contact with us. We had to flag her down to correct our orders and then it appeared we were bothering her.  The only saving grace was the Macs Iced Tea. I'm glad we all got to see it, but if you're a huge fan of the show, there's not much memorabilia in the restaurant and the service wasn't great.

Very average- I have given this place a second chance the other weekend and that is the last it gets. The nachos looked so good based on the menu description but when they came, it was luke warm with tons of chunky non-melted cheese with corn. It seemed like they poured a can of creamed corn with cheese all over the nachos. The steak fajitas were a huge disappointment- the steak was over marinated and there was hardly any steak but tons of onions and peppers. Not a fan and wouldn't go back.

We had three great meals here. The staff was attentive and the slow smoked barbeque was quite good. The decor and interior are attractive. The mixed drinks that we had, were perfect. 

The sides really impressed me. Most barbeque places don't make an effort with sides, but the gin salad, purple slaw and the blue cheese slaw were outstanding and uniquely flavored. 

Having said that, our fourth visit was not as impressive as the sides were suddenly tasteless and watered down.  I hope that management takes notice and corrects this.  Another suggestion would be to heat the metal serving plates that are used, so that the barbeque is better than luke warm when delivered.

Next visit - We went again and the plates were warmer. That was an improvement. It was lunchtime and the music was really too loud to enjoy a conversation. I asked the waiter if they could turn it down a bit and he was able to do this.  A lot of Florida restaurants don't understand the difference between dining music levels and bar levels. The purple slaw was much better this time. Unfortunately, the gin salad was apparently prepped hours before lunch and had soured.  With an acidic salad like this, it has to be made frequently. It can't sit in a bowl for hours and break down. The pulled pork was good, but the brisket was dry as a bone. It looked like the brisket had been cut in advance for portions, then reheated. That's a "no no" with brisket. I really want to like this restaurant, but they need to try a little harder.

That was unexpected! I'm a fan now. Cute little place tastefully done. Ingredients are fresh and creative. I had a really difficult time selecting a menu item, a true sign of a good place. The shrimp and artichoke pizza w pesto was really good, and for $8.75 a fair price. Next time I'll bring the fam and have a glass of wine too. No dessert though? C'mon!

My vanilla latte was on point, perfect as it was!
I normally like my coffee milky and sweet, since this was my first time here I decided try a vanilla latte and leave it as it is, nothing extra. It was absolutely perfect.  They only have 1 standard 12oz cup ($5)
And their cold brew is pretty good and comes in a cool glass bottle!

Way too crowded, so I had to check out the other locations & I was not disappointed. Whenever you see a long line of people waiting for food, you know its gotta be good! If you are going to try this partucular location I recommend arriving very early & making a reservation if possible. The beignets were great & very affordable. Check them out, you won't be disappointed!

Rode the hop on hop off city sightseeing tour bus in NOLA with my elderly parents.  It was a nice way to get an overall view of the city.  We bought a 3 day pass, but only used it once.  ONE HUGE PROBLEM....the tour guide with the microphone.  A "little" man with big political and social opinions who took advantage of his "stage" and pelted us with never ending comments and ideology that was nothing short of outrageous and out of line.  We were beyond appalled and my 85 year old frail father got so upset he wanted to punch him when we (the riders) were shamed for being Americans if we had ancestors from the South.  He insulted us when he dumped on our President and numerous other politicians and made it impossible to enjoy the NOLA narrative.

I really don't understand how they don't have more bad reviews... Roughly 5 years ago i was searching for my first car at 16. I had saved up my money and had a good amount to put down on a car. I was looking for a small four door used car. Salesman was very nice but unfortunately he knew what he was selling to me and didn't say a word. He knew i was a high school student and needed this car to get me through college. The whole reason i saved up to get a decent car... Ignorant to say the least. How can you sell a car to a STUDENT knowing the issues it has. 2014 ford focus 2 YEARS OLD when i bought it... apparently ford has a lawsuit against them for these cars because of the transmission issue that is "unfixable" mind you they've had this issue for years before i even was looking at cars. My warranty covered it until 100,000 miles.. so i didn't think much of it until i heard its an on going problem meaning i would have to come back EACH YEAR to get the clutch replaced. I hit 100,000 miles and they refuse to pay for it let alone help me figure out what to do!!! They told me to contact ford.. wtf?!!! U are ford and if u have to contact someone higher up these employees should be doing it for me! My car would stall and have issues accelerating after stopping at stop signs and lights so pretty dangerous if u ask me. Eventually, after it got to a point i felt seriously unsafe driving this anywhere.. we called ford they told us about the lawsuit and we could file a claim. They said we would see an email in 10-15 days and to follow the instructions. After 20 days still no email.. we started calling at least twice a day.. all we got was rude and disrespectful customer service and was told we would get it. My car then started to get even worse so we called with the same disrespect we were given and they said we had to pay the 2,000 dollars and then "call back" to get reimbursed.. ABSOLUTELY RIDICULOUS!! As a college student this was such a stressful first car. I loved the vehicle literally everything about it but the trans was great. But the fact they sold this to a 16 year old and not a word about this issue really rubbed me the wrong way. We were going to pay it and wait for the reimbursement bc i was in the process of saving for a new vehicle and wasn't ready to buy yet. Unfortunately i was in a car accident so I never got the chance to see if they'd actually pay me back. I currently own a brand new Nissan rogue 2021. They cared about the fact i was a college student and told me about any recalls and past issues they've had with this vehicle. I will never NEVERRRR buy a ford again. Let me just add here while I'm at it.. i went in for service a few times and they tried taking advantage of a female teenager. My mom would call have everything set up for me bc she works and cant come every time.. With the most disrespect and ignorant attitude i was left crying and having my mom call them because i didn't know how to deal with them. I will always remember this girls name 'Jessica Kuntz' very rude and made me so upset.. again i was a minor. I am now 21 and speaking up because that service is not acceptable. Id stay farrr farr away from fords. 
Sincerely - your lost customer Kylie Tripp

[$8] for students w/ valid ID. 

This is basically a place where you take pretty pictures of the mix of glass and concrete and then take pretty pictures of yourself with the glass and concrete and you walk here and there trying to justify the $8 you paid to get in (this all takes a total of 20 min max) and then you leave.

The art installation is all within a small enclosed space, so there's really not much else to do. They have more art inside, but looking around everything including the art inside doesn't take long. 

I showed my yelp check-in, so I received a free postcard. The photos on the postcards are blurry/not high quality, so don't expect too much.

This is a good place to go once in your life though, seeing as how your friends' instagram pictures of this garden will make you envious until you finally do go and snap a photo of it with your own camera and upload it to your account.

Ok, first I have to say that I'm a sushi/sashimi afficianado, so I'm very picky about my fish being fresh. I've been to some places througout the SF Bay area who "claim" to be sushi restaurants, who serve plain crap that a true sushi chef from Japan wouldn't dare serve.  

Granted that Reno is in the middle of the desert so I wasn't expecting the fish to be freshly plucked from the ocean, plus it was for an all you can eat special, so I knew the bulk of the stuff had to be frozen. I came in with the preconcieved notion that it wasn't going to be that good. I was pleasantly surprised. 

We opted for the all you can eat sashimi for $33. We didn't bother with the all you can sushi, even if it was $10 cheaper. C'mon folks you're not here to fill yourself up with rice and maki-rolls! Go for the good stuff. And go for the good stuff we did - unagi, ikura, tobiko, hotate, tako, ika, hamachi, tai, maguro, ebi, amaebi. We skipped the cheap stuff and dove straight in for the high ticket items. I think the restaurant  may have broken even with us. They ran out of tako and ika, and I saw they were running low on amaebi.  It was actually pretty good, considering it used to be, flash frozen fish.

Our sushi chef was Angel. He was very attentive and kept asking us if we wanted anything more. Well, duh!

The selection as others have pointed out, is limited. On the other hand, it's freshly cooked and tastes great. Fresh cooked, great tasting food, can't ask for anything more. The surroundings just add to the charm. This is probably my favorite place for Middle Eastern (Kurdish) food in Nashville.

Impeccable service, we dined in a private room and fellas Joe and Kevin catered to us. They were so wonderful. Being in the service industry myself in LA I so appreciated their time and dedication with my family while we celebrated my mom's 60th over a long wkd just before Thanksgiving. My parent's can be pretty tricky when it comes to dining out! We were just a few feet from viewing ability to the world renowned incredible wine cellar. The only reason I'm not giving 5 * is because the grub could use a little fine tuning, not my favorite of all the restaurants we dined at during the weekend but the awesome feel of being in a place so historic that survived such a disaster coupled with the stellar service made up for that. Definitely a place to go and tour and at least grab a drink!

Love love love this nature sanctuary. It's actually open 7 days a week, early morning to sunset, with a great education center and trails. Parking is ample, and free!, and they do a good job of posting maps around the park and at several stations throughout trails. I haven't seen too many animals, yet, but it's a beautiful place to go for a walk.
Food was good, hot and served very quickly. Chips were hot and not greasy or oily. Margaritas were so, so good.

There is too much great competition for this restaurant to be the best one in that area. I often found the condiment bottles and jars on the tables to be in need of a good wipe down between guests. The spring rolls were just OK, not nearly as good as others in the same area. The peanut dipping sauce was mediocre. The only positive for me is that they accept credit cards and the food is generally OK.
Great old school Drive-In! Food was better than I expected, very pleased.

For awhile after the original Minar Palace closed, New Delhi was my main source for Indian food. Don't get me wrong, it's perfectly fine, but I am very glad that Tiffin, Ekta and Minar Palace 2.0 exist now.

This was a cool environment. The food was okay. The service was lack-luster. 

This is a really cool environment. Things to note: You can only order a fountain beverage from the bar - aka, if you'd like to order a coke from the soda gun you have to go to the bar. Otherwise, if you get a soda at your dining table, then it comes in a bottle. The food was good, but the timing of the food along with the temperature made for a less than amazing experience. My friend ordered their chicken wings, and I ordered the Bibimbap with spicy chicken (pictured). Her wings came out about 10 minutes before my food. Which I didn't mind, but because mine came in a sizzling hot bowl, it took forever to cool to a point where I could actually eat it. 

About the food - a great fresh taste for the Bibimbap. The spicy chicken is pretty spicy. It was much spicier than your standard hot sauce. My friend had the plate of ten bone-in wings with the mixed flavors. She said they were delicious, and that the spicy half definitely had a kick.
Food is subpar, coffee is pretty good but low rating because how do you open a coffee shop but not open before 8?

Square Donuts are the cat's meow in donuts. They finally got here from Terre Haute. For years we would stop in Terre Haute on our way West, pick up a dozen or so and  eat them all the way to Denver. The raised and jelly-filled are our favorites. The former are light and tasty. The latter are generously filled with jelly and make eating just one (at a time) a challenge. I would usually be done with mine by the time we reached WaKeeney, KS...well short of Denver.

I've been a customer since It was purchased in 2003. Daytimes are a friendly working crowd. Cheers but a mixed crowd. Bar food is good. All drinks are free pour, and a double for $3 more, always. Wings are excellent.

Soooo where do I start. You get Amazing Fried Chicken & Fish with Cajun style sides not to mention hot fresh biscuits. While your getting great food and decide you want a drink there is a Full Margarita Bar with a variety of flavors. I HAVE NEVER SEEN Chicken and Margaritas at the same counter! This MUST BE a NOLA thing!! The food was beyond good and the owner must have OCD because the public restrooms have a code from your receipt that you must utilize to access the rest room! Never seen that either, but I appreciate a clean public bathroom. So I really had to go.... had to use the men's and it was SUPER CLEAN that was a first!

Gosh, I really don't know what to make of this place...

The staff is so unbeliveably nice and accomodating, but I left feeling as though the brunch was not worth the price tag...Unlimited Mimosas and Brunch for just over $40 (inclusive of tax & nice tip)

Let's start with the dessert table...everything that has Strawberry Mousse should be done away with. The taste of fake strawberry is just not my thing.  Can't say that there was anything that was exceptionally good on the dessert table, which was pretty disappointing, because there were so many options.

In terms of the food...there were a lot of options, but nothing really stood out as being "great".  The bacon was good. Everything else was pretty reminiscent of a hotel buffet.  I totally get that they are trying to appeal to a broad range of tastes and due to the sheer size of the restaurant they should, however I'd rather see them do better with fewer items than just okay with so many items.  Some of the specialty item sounded amazing, but after trying them they missed the mark some way or another, be it lack of flavor, temperature or texture. I shouldn't go to a place like this and only be looking forward to a good omelette!

I also would not order the bottomless mimosas again, which had more orange juice than champagne...every glass brought over total looked and tasted as if it was pure orange juice. I am usually good for about two at any other restaurant, but I literally had five and gave up!

Again the staff is nothing short of amazing, but the food could use a little fine-tuning.

Please let me begin with the point that this is my local shop and I have been a  patron this store countless times. Personally I have managed several facilities within customer service for twenty years, and have significant experience in handling customer relations. I am shocked and sad to say I've never worked with such an astoundingly poor example of a location by such a well run parent chain. This isn't a rant or name calling post, just an informative recommendation and a review of my own individual experience with over a dozen plus dealings with this particular location. 

After today, I will not patronage this Firestone store again. It's not the products, nor the firm, but the employees and spokespeople who have ruined my experience and trust in their service and ethics. Over my four past transactions with them, they presented themselves as smug, condescending, and drastically appear to under appreciate educated auto clients. On what is now multiple instances, they have burned me on several transactions and I continued to give them the chance to rekindle and earn my business. Until now. I own six cars and routinely require maintenance to keep them performing properly. I am Auto savvy, highly knowledgeable in auto care, and was even a former Firestone employee. 

After this store kept one of my vehicles off the road for two months due to their technician's mistake during an annual inspection, today, I called ahead to ask them if they would replace my own tires on one of my vehicles. After confirming, and taking the time to make the arrangements to drop off the tires, then commuting to pick up the proper vehicle, upon my return the serviceman then informed me that they do not install tires for customers unless they sold the tire. Their spokesman also told me customers own tires need to have the original new stickers and new rubber whiskers on them. 

The tires I dropped off were new, previously mounted for a display, had the new whiskers on them, and all measured greater than 14/32 of an inch in tire life. I understand business policy and the ethical treatment of business clients better than most, have mounted and dismounted hundreds of tires in my life, and have in-depth experience in tire care. I wasted an hour plus for no logical reason, effectively communicated my request prior to arriving the initial time, and will never again allow for the employees of this location to negatively risk my time, and/or quality of repairs to any of my vehicles. Ordinarily I provide multiple chances for firms to offset poor performance and lower than expected customer satisfaction levels. This experience today was the final straw, and I will now avoid this store to find the quality of service and ethical treatment as an auto customer. 

I feel as if I was intentionally managed out from this location repeatedly by the behavior of their service spokes people; perhaps because I have auto comprehension, arrive with detailed and concise requests, and the location does not up sell me on their higher profit goods and services.

BL has very long lines to get in. Once in you place your order, you seat yourself. I was in Nashville for a few days so woke up early to beat the crowds. I ordered pork and eggs which are eggs, pork chops, grits and biscuit. The coffee was strong but not in a good way. I ordered the Bonuts also. If you have party of 4 place a order. One Bonut per person is enough. They are very sweet. The porkchops were tough and the butter knife is not enough. I would not discourage you from eating here, please try it. But if your looking for a good hearty fulfilling breakfast, Cracker Barrel is best.

My motto used to be, if you can't say nothing nice, say nothing. However, I have rethought my motto. Businesses need to know where they have to improve . So with that said, I was disappointed with the service. My friend and I was initially sat at a barbecue table but were moved once we decided to order off the dinner menu. Being moved wasn't the issue, the issue was the waitress neglected to inform us that it was going to take 45 mins to an hour to have the kitchen prepare our food. We were waiting and waiting for service after
being moved and it literally took 20 minutes to get a server, which was not our initial waitress, to come to our table. Their saving grace was the dumplings; they were amazing! I can't say much else for the rest of our food because it simply lacked luster. I ordered the Soon Tofu. My soup had a big piece of tofu on top that disintegrated as soon as I put my spoon in and the seafood was over cooked, chewy. 
I supposed I probably would have had a better experience if I would have just ordered the barbecue. I hear it's good and have won awards. Perhaps the Cafe' should stick to their claim to fame. I'm just saying.

So my sister and I were too lazy to leave the house for food but didn't really want regular pizza place food. Luckily for us Nick's Roast Beef showed up on Grubhub so we were able to get delivery. 
In my estimation the price was a little steep. Coming in at $38 for chicken fingers, two orders of gravy friend, and a brisket sandwich. But it was really good and definitely a nice change up. 
Since I paid I tried one of my sister's chicken fingers, and they were legit the best chicken fingers ever. Not like fried run of the mill mass produced pieces of crap. They were more like chicken cutlets. My brisket sandwich was very good, and I especially loved the barbecue sauce that accompanied it. While the sandwich was good it was a bit dry, but thankfully I had ordered extra gravy, forward thinking person that I am, so it all worked out. And last but not least, those gravy fries! They were delicious and artery clogging I'm sure. The order was huge and we definitely could have gotten away with only ordering one between the two of us. 
The food was quite good and delivery was fast and friendly. Highly recommended! Just don't forget your side of gravy y'all's.
sadly, although the flyer we received in the mail said they delivered, they refused to deliver to our home because it wasn't a large order. guess we will never know how good they actually are.

I like that you don't need an appointment to be serve however doesn't feel like a real beauty salon when no one make a personal connection with you. I'm use to being greeted by name and introduce to my stylist but not here. They will wash you and get you going quickly. The stylist, I've used twice that's I don't know her name does a good job getting you in and out.

I haven't had dinner. Just popped in for appetizers after going to a movie. Server was fabulous. Suggested a nice bottle of wine. Wasn't annoyed that we weren't ordering a big tab. All 5 of us were very pleased with our selections. Calamari and scallops especially. Trendy decor. It gets a bit noisy in there and also a lot of food aromas that permeate your cloths.

The absolute worst customer service I've experienced in a long time. If you mess up my order don't spend five minutes arguing with me about how you got my order right?? At no point am I going to say oh yeah you're right this is what I ordered. Then you tell me you're about to close & your delivery driver is too busy to bring me the correct order. So you offer me a 2$ credit for next time? Sorry no deal I'm on my way for my full refund.

If you're spending the money to go to an amusement park - don't come here. We spend our first two hours here in line for rides that stopped working when we were almost to the front and spent 45 min already in line. Then we rode the Seikra and it stopped and we were stuck on it! Spent about 25 min in the blazing sun at a 90 degree angle as an alarm was sounding, which really doesn't help the atmosphere up there. My brother was crying and my mom and I were about ready to have a panic attack. All we got out of this was ONE fast pass. The managers and park really didn't care. We looked up the Sheikra online later and this seems to happen a lot, maybe they need to take better care of their park. Pretty sketchy - especially since multiple rides had to close for malfunctions. 
The park is somewhat hard to navigate and it's hard to find all the animals because of how poor it's set up. But the animals are interesting to look at, but might as well just go to the zoo since they're probably treated better. 
Then it started pouring and everything was shut down, they didn't even keep open the animal pavilion so everyone had absolutely nothing to do and they don't have any sort of compensating weather policy so if you do come, come when there's for sure no rain
We were planning on finding a manager to discuss these issues and how disappointing it was to spend $700 on a crappy day. But by the end of the day we were so sick of it and done we just went home.

My husband and I just spent an hour and a half going in and out of rv's and fifth wheels in the lot. We passed many service people and no one ever asked if we needed help. My husband stopped one and asked a question. She answered the question over her shoulder as she kept walking. Very sad. Why did I stop here. Back to My Comfort for us

Got the Impossible burger this time and was it was extremely salty, other than that, the food has always been amazing to me ! Service is very friendly and considerate. I was served by Brandon and he was great. Will definitely come back.

This location gives nothing but cold food every time I go here. The only one I've gone to that I have been dissatisfied about ever. I'd rather go all the way from Tampa to Wesley chapel/ Lutz for a fresh juicy sandwich. Not coming back even if it's 2 mins away from my house.

Your milage may vary (YMMV) 

Case 1: Wife requested specific massage therapist which provided moreso of a sports therapy deep tissue massage.  When this therapist was no longer there, the next 'certified' RMT rubbed oil on her sore back.  


Case 2: On a Sunday morning, we were in a hurry to get a gift certificate.  Being there right at 10am as per the store hours, no one was there.  10:30am... still no one.  And no, it wasn't a long weekend.

Will we go back?  No.
Should YOU go? ... roll the dice and tread lightly.

We just had breakfast here and found everything to be solid.  It's your standard diner food but it was good and affordable.  I had the sausage gravy with biscuits (not the best I've had but wasn't upset to eat it). Service was quick and attentive, place is small but could be a little cleaner.  Overall happy with our visit and we'll definitely be back!

Went to this restaurant last Friday night. We had a reservation, and were seated right away. The service was good but very confusing as we had two different servers and others who delivered the food. The Ahi Tuna salad was ordered by two in our party, and both sent it back. It appeared, and tasted like it had been covered with course sea salt. When we complained, our waitress explained that it was a new seasoning that they had recently added. It was so late in the dinner service that both parties declined to have another one prepared. I'm about 10 minutes, a manager came over and delivered replacements. He was most gracious, and offered to remove the charges for the salads. I would still recommend this restaurant, but not the Ahi Tuna at all.
This restaurant is consistently clean, has really nice employees, and the food tastes great! What more do you need at a McDonald's?!

Sometimes when a restaurant is so busy the people who prepare your food don't really listen (or care).  Like when you're asked if you want sour cream AFTER they've already placed sour cream on your burrito... yea, that happened...

Been here several times over the years. Large wine and beer selection - main draw of the place. Definitely a solid 4 stars. 

Parking: street. Although there is a small lot behind the building. It is really for other businesses during the day, but in the evenings it seems ok to park there.

Music: background music playing, sometimes live music. One time we left it was so bad, another time they were really good and enjoyed it. The playlist is varied.

Inside: color pallet is purple walls, wood tones, with browns and ambers all around. Very dark inside even on a bright sunny day. One pool table, one night there is free pool. They have local restaurant menus you can order food from if you get hungry, you just have to walk and pick it up yourself. Couches are fine with two people, but not the greatest for large groups to kick back and relax on as you may bump heads with the person behind you; it becomes very intimate with strangers in this way. it can get really warm inside. When its a full room it gets really loud. The crowd seems working class or upperclassmen in college/grad students. If the seating was more comfy, and was quieter when so full of people I would give it a five.

One amenity people often like is the free popcorn they offer. Sometimes they have other items like cookies or veggies and dip at the bar. Sometimes they let you taste the wine before a you purchase a glass or bottle. This is a nice way to develop ones own palette and make sure you like what you are getting. 

Outside: Nice back patio with two seating areas, one is covered. I like to sit back there only when there is no smoking. I discount a star for fostering smoking. Get there when they open if you want the outside seating for a group. 

Bathrooms: Appear well kept and clean. Secure door that locks. Hot and cold water. Paper towels.

One evening our group moved from inside to outside when it opened up and then it got late and everyone dispersed. The open tab and the card were left there overnight. Went back the next day to close the account and get the card.

My one bad experience: I gave it three stars and not four because the bartender tacked on his/her own tip onto the credit card when the tab was left open and the card was left overnight. 40% tip onto of the drink charges. I normally tip in cash as it is, so this was an unpleasant surprise when I picked up my card. I will only bring cash from now on.

Still a good laid back place to grab a drink with friends after work.

My go to place for deserts when I am in NOLA! You feel bad eating the deserts because the presentation is always soooooo pretty! And they are really good too! They claim that they have the world's best eclairs but I think they are so much more than that! Try their seasonals! The only reason why this place is a 4 star is because they usually run out of their seasonal options pretty quick and the only option would be macarons...

There's a reason I haven't stayed at a Best Western since 2001.  This place reeks of class; from the tube TVs to the wall mounted bottle opener next to the shower.   The only positive of this motel is the staff were very friendly. This place is probably ok if you are looking for a place to crash after a night of debauchery.  But if you are looking for a place to relax after a long day at the office I would suggest going elsewhere.

As someone who's been to Italy and tasted real, genuine gelato, I've got to say that this place is pretty legit because it truly reminded me of my time there. It's so smooth and creamy. And the flavor is wonderfully rich. What's nice about Capogiro is that each day they change up their flavor list, so every time you go you'll likely be surprised with seeing a flavor you've never had before. And there's always a good variety to choose from! 

The place itself has a really nice ambiance and a good amount of space. The staff members have always been very friendly, and don't mind giving you a sample or two to taste. I love that it's open pretty late, as I usually come here for a late night snack. My only con is that it can be a tad pricey, even though it is excellent quality. Thankfully, I can usually be satisfied even with a small size because the gelato is so rich. 

If you're a fan of gelato, I'd definitely recommend you come here!

I went to Molly Goodheads twice within two weeks. Both times we had a great experience. Service and food were both excellent. They make one of the best grilled grouper sandwiches ive ever had. Same for the grouper cheeks. Flavorful, juicy and cooked perfectly. Molly Goodheads has been around for a very long time and that experience shows in the way they run their restaurant. This is an easy recommendation for anyone looking for quality seafood in a casual relaxed environment.
The Alaskan White Fish dinner on Fridays is a must!

Whitney apartments have roaches. ALL the buildings are infested! I moved here three years ago based off the nice experience my brother had when I was younger. To date I have had repeated issues with pest. They moved me to another apartment in an effort to escape the infestation, however they raised my rent by $250.00 due to market value for the same apartment. I threw out all my old furniture and appliances to now have roaches again. All the buildings are infested and the manager doesn't care. They ignore complaints and don't even greet residents when in their company. Rather avoid you and walk by with their head down. They send an exterminator that sprays in your apartment in less then 60 seconds and this is what I was told legally all they have to do. 


Can't wait until my lease is up 
Unhappy current tenant 2018

Everytime I go here I like it less and less. Not relaxing (despite the attempts at a hot stone massage), aggressive cuticle and callus work, expensive pedicures. This place is just not worth it for the extra $$. Despite the fact I keep returning (serious short term memory)!! I say try a cheaper place or splurge and try a fancy pants place like rescue rittenhouse..
Amazing!  So happy to find 'Good Vibes Juice'  Just might be my new favorite place!  Great vibe & excellent service...& absolutely delicious product!

If I only considered the food, I would give it 4 stars and possibly five.  The food is excellent, the service very good, the people very nice.  Unfortunately, there are some drawbacks.  First, the smoke in Peppermill, particularly when you are in line, is unbearable.  I am not anti-smoking but there is a time and place for it.  The smoke was so dense that people were leaving to go outside or elsewhere just to be able to breathe.  Yes, I know they have put small air purifiers wherever they could which tells me that they are making the effort and that they are aware of the problem.  Nevertheless, this does not obviate the fact that anyone who brings children to this location should be prosecuted for child abuse and, as for adults, there were so many bad comments and complaints that it offsets all of the good points of this restaurant. It would seem to me that they could, and must, update their main air conditioning system to purify the air or else make it a non-smoking location.  It smelled, you are looking through a cloud, and as much as I enjoy the restaurant, it's just not worth breathing that stuff while you are on line or inside which, fortunately, was not nearly as bad. 
    
The other two points are (a) they tell you it is a 45 minute wait when it was an hour and 45 minutes.  Second, one major reason for the looong wait is that they do not have enough busboys working.  The servers also bus tables.  There were too few of them and so while people waited on line outside, there were about 7-8 tables at any given time that were just waiting to be cleaned off and sat there empty until that occurred.  Poor management.  Look at it this way.  You have, let's say, 50 tables. It takes people about an hour to an hour and a quarter to eat and leave.  In the hour and 45 minutes I was in line all of those tables should have been turned at least once and yet, despite the fact that there were only about a dozen people ahead of us waiting for tables, we waited all that time.  It makes no sense.  Even at an hour and a half per table they should have turned 50 tables in the time they turned maybe 15-20 - if that many.  When I asked the very nice lady manager why it was taking so long, she said that too many people sit there playing with their cell phones.  That may be a contributing factor but you had to be blind not to see that tables were empty and could have been turned.  

This should be rated ***** and not *** but it takes more than good food to run a successful restaurant.

My friend and i stumbled upon this place while exploring St Pete. Needless to say, we were very disappointed. My friend and i both work and have worked in many restaurants that specialize in seafood. I myself am from Maryland & have lived in Florida for the past 4 years. I ordered the shrimp dinner and requested it grilled. I had a choice of two sides so i chose the veggie of the day which was broccoli and then French fries. When my order came, I'm not lying when i say the shrimp was tiny & Luke warm. It was not grilled at all. It was exactly like the frozen shrimp you buy at Walmart that you put in shrimp salad. They were that small. My broccoli was cold & also frozen as well. I could tell because the color was very pale green and it was cut in a way that made it look mass produced and frozen. The french fries were standard restaurant quality fries that are quickly thrown in the fryer. Again, luke warm. They wanted $15 for this meal... All in all very disappointed.
 Our server was great and very sweet. The service was fine however i felt compelled to save people from coming here and getting ultimately disappointed with this seafood place.

Last Thursday, June 18, 2020 I asked for a quote for lawn services for a rental home. I only looked into McGuire's because of the great service I received from them in past. I clearly explained that I had Bahia grass.  They told me I would have someone go there within the next couple of days.  I received a call this morning, telling me they would be able to service my lawn and a quote was given to me.  There was no mention of any issues of my grass being Bahia.  Later in the afternoon, I received another call that the quote I received had to be modified because the fertilization service could not be included because my grass was Bahia, but that they could still provide lawn maintenance.  No problem.  A couple of hours later I get ANOTHER call.  This time, they leave a voice mail telling me they will NOT service the lawn at all because it is Bahia grass.  "I am new and this is a new policy I was not aware of" was the excuse. Had I been informed of this 5 days ago, I would not have had a problem.  I have essentially lost 5 days in which I could have found a lawn service provider.  This was handled very poorly.

Pizza is generally ok for the area but you would think a pizza maker would know how to properly cut it. Didn't realize how much it matters till my recent order.  Taste and quality has also been steadily declining over the last couple of years.

During covid this place has taken measures that ensures the safety of customers. The tables are actually spread out 6 ft unlike most restaurants that do outside seating. It was out first time here and we got the house special Pad Thai and the Pineapple fried rice. Both dishes were really good. The service is okay due to the fact that they sat inside the store and did not check up on us at all. I'm not sure if that's their policy but occasionally checking on guests is not a bad idea.

Decorated in linoleum and lit by fluorescent lights, this long-standing original is always full of authentic, hungry locals. The food is solid but most of all, it's CHEAP. One cool thing is if you can score a table, you can order your meal for dine-in and also order extra items to go. They will have the bahn mi or whatever you asked for waiting for you at the counter when you go up to pay.

Oh yea, how to pay? You will not be presented with a bill. Instead, note down your table number and go up to the register and let him know when you are done. They'll confirm what you ordered and rattle off the total. Cash makes the transaction go by faster but they do accept plastic.

As for the food, we thought the pho was good but not ultimately spectacular. I was pleased the pho ga included more flavorful dark meat rather than generic white meat. It was also clear they had prepared the chicken in house, rather than using prepackaged white meat which is generally flavorless. 

In terms of dollars per taste, it's a winner.

The service at this location was fantastic. Jay was a great salesperson & was so wonderful to work with (as well as the manager on staff).
 I will say however, their warehouse service is by far the absolute worst I have ever experienced. The amount of uninformed employees topped off with miscommunication among each person we dealt with makes us never want to return. Nobody seemed to understand policies or have a handle on delivery dates. They couldn't even return a phone call correctly. I am so dissatisfied with the warehouse that this is my first and last purchase with Ashley Furniture.

Scheduled an appointment 3 days in advance to see a vehicle and explained I would be traveling from two hours away. Showed up and the entire interior of the vehicle was filthy. The car had a bad shake and pulled hard due to an alignment and tire balance issue. The salesman explained I could pay to have that fixed, they weren't correcting the issue. Completely wasted my time. I've never had an experience like that before when purchasing a vehicle. Had the car been cleaned and had no mechanical issues, I would've purchased the vehicle.

Good coffee, good pastries. However, it's the rudeness I received from the cashier that is the reason for the 2 star rating. The vacant, irritated stare she gave me as I ordered without even attempting to greet me after I gave her a warm smile and good morning, was very off putting. So was the homeless man who asked me for money  in the middle of my order. The cashier made no apologies to me, she said nothing and continued staring at me. After he left, I asked, nicely and out of curiosity, if soliciting was legal in Philadelphia. I was not being passive aggressive. She just says "well I was gonna ask him to leave but I thought I'd finish the line first, k?" The "k" was accompanied by a smile that lasted only for .5 seconds and fell away immediately after she said it. I almost said something, and I almost left. I just am not used to this level of rudeness. Having worked in retail and the service industry, I know that my managers would have given me a few strikes or maybe even terminated me for adopting that kind of a behavior with a customer. I never received that kind of rudeness and indifference even when I was living in Madrid. Needless to say, I will not be coming back to Joe's anytime soon. Not as long as they continue to employ people who treat customers as if their business is an inconvenience to them. Not as long as they continue to employ people who refuse to give a genuine (or at least a convincing fake) smile.

I have been a Vanguard client for 30+ years, and I have had it with their poor customer service.  I am moving all my Vanguard accounts to another financial institution.  They give you the run around anytime you want information.  Most recently I was calling to respond to an online note on my wife's account that the account was about to be turned over to the state.  When I called with my wife on the phone they locked her account so she is now unable to access the account online.  The rep on the phone was very unhelpful, and not at all sympathetic to our situation.  I have dealt with a number of financial institutions, and Vanguard is the worst when it comes to customer service.  They put you through hoops to make small changes to your account.  I am so disgusted with their attitude that I am willing to pay more at another institution that cares about my business.  If you are looking for convenience, Vanguard is not your provider.  If you want access to the low cost funds they offer, I suggest you buy their ETF's through a discount broker.

Drinks were good and the waitress was quick on empty glasses. App - FANTASTIC chicken quesadilla with some kinda sauce thats was great with all the fresh veg. Chilled salad was great not wilted or weak. Entree jerk chicken very moist with a kick cooked perfectly. The coconut rice for a side was good didnt care for the mashed potatoes -no flavor. I originally ordered the cuban -DONT MAKE THAT MISTAKE :( But I will say I was able to send it back and get the new entree without delay. Overall great service good atmosphere.

This is a classic example of a diner that's open from breakfast to dinner. It has a parking lot and nice tables and booths, and very well kept. 
I stopped for dinner and ordered the small plate of fried green tomatoes. The remoulade  sauce with horseradish was outstanding!
The shrimp and grits is a big meal with cheese grits, blackened shrimp, bacon, cheddar,scallions, and an egg on top. There is not much you can eat after that!
The menu has something for everyone.

So I went to the "hood" in Tampa Florida and stopped by Bar-B-Que King. The ribs are meaty but too much bone and the sides aren't fresh. 
I went to Tubby's in Zephyrhills Florida and OMG! Bayyybeee!! Yasss!!! Get you some! Fresh sides meaty ribs not too much bone. Fresh and hot off the grill and you can taste that it was smoked and grilled to perfection! Homemade iced tea! Southern hospitality at its finest and beautiful tables to eat at! 
I never believed in food being the way to someone's heart but I found out today I was wrong! Tubby's Q has my heart and my husband's heart and belly. It's the place to eat with just the right music and ambiance. We will be back God willing

I apparently had a bout of amnesia and forgot this establishment severely overcooks their chicken wings.  The bones in the wingettes are brittle and break when you bite into the meat.  The meat is dry and the skin has the texture of sandpaper.  It is very disappointing for a sports bar.

I had really been looking forward to trying Athens, and they let me down... I love Greek food, and I thought I could start a steady relationship with this place, especially considering it's a only a couple miles from home, but the first date didn't go so well.

First of all, they really need to work on their first impression. The floors and tables were dirty, the salt and pepper shakers were grimy and the air vents on the ceiling are surrounded by mold. 

We ordered the hummus and tzatziki for appetizers. The hummus was so-so, but the tzatziki was downright gross. It tasted like there was mayonnaise in it, and there wasn't even a hint of cucumber. 

My husband got a burger which he said was not all that great, but he did enjoy the fries. My Greek salad came with a thick, creamy dressing. Not at all what I was expecting with a Greek salad, so I asked for vinaigrette. The server brought out a bottle of balsamic vinegar and a bottle of oil. Both were very low quality. 

This place left a lot to be desired, and I won't be going back.

5th  and last time coming here to eat. the food is just not good, i have only eaten here because it is a convenient location. crab legs are good, mongolian bbq is ok, but the chinese food is horrible. how to you make a bad crab rangoon or egg roll? the staff is never friendly , our waitress never refilled our drinks or delivered any napkins. they charged me for my 3 year old when they said she was free. im not one to complain at the restaurant, i'm the type that just never goes back. i recommend eating elsewhere. please there must be a good chinese buffet here in reno!

Very attentive service in a pretty dining room.  However, an overall sense of OK, not wow.  An encyclopedic wine list, but they were out of my first choice.  They suggested a substitution of a Barbera di Asti for the Rhone blend I had chosen.  It was a nice wine, but at over $50 for a wine that retails for $12, not exactly a value.  A nice appetizer of mussels in a lemon grass scented curry sauce.  Would have enjoyed some bread to soak up the broth, but none was offered after the initial serving.  The duck was rather ordinary.  Would have preferred a crisp skin and the breast not as well done.  I asked to sub the Mac and cheese for the rice, it was flavorless. The pickled vegetables were a nice ouster point to the richness of the duck.  My companions enjoyed their chicken and swordfish.  On the whole there is more interesting food in NOLA and at better prices.

Held a baby shower here recently for my daughter .  Marissa was very helpful in the planning and I think everyone enjoyed themselves.  We used the main dining room and bar area.  The brunch food we chose was plentiful and mostly yummy although the grits were a bit bland .  We had sampled the shrimp and grits which were very good when scouting the place but the cheesy grits I had on that date
Did not compare - however the rest of the food - including sausage , bacon , eggs and pancakes was yummy .  I wish their pastries included some kind of iced cinnamon roll mostly because that's a favorite of mine but also because it would have been more festive than the small muffins which were a
Bit dry - the glazed donut/ roll was good though .  The only real issue was that the drink station - serve themselves coffee and hot water in thermal vessels and juices plus champagne to make mimosas - was not out until about 30 minutes after guests arrived -- and the juice carafes were very small - almost like what you'd place on one table so kept needing refill ( I think when it appears like there is only a small amount guests will be influenced to not tAke much out of politeness so should look plentiful ) also twice when I went for coffee it was empty -- although they did refill it when asked .  
The staff was very pleasant - the bartender was helpful and good about our taking up his entire bar top .  
I'd highly recommend this as a party venue or just as a good brunch place

I've been here 3 times now, I get the same drink every time. Local honey latte + lavender, it is amazingggg! So delicious! I really love this hidden gem, parking isn't the best but there's always the library's parking lot! Keep in mind it is a small shop so you'll have to squeeze through to order and what not. The food options are a plus also! I come a few times a week so if you're thinking about trying them out, I'd say go for it! 


* heard about this place through my cousin, I've also seen it float through social media
Needs more staff but otherwise handles the customer flow very well. Very small store so it gets crowded. Would opt for Dunkin' Donuts if you don't want to deal with the line (I did)

The food is delicious, always has been. However, the staff is rude. One of the owners, was drunk and obnoxious and couldn't care less about how he behaved in front of the patrons. Fortunately they detract from his behavior by allowing a delightfully entertaining reenactment of the hunger games played out by the cock roaches and mice amply running from the kitchen to the salad bar. I think their future patrons should include the board of health.

Located near many restaurants and hotels. Came in and found what I needed. It was a little hard to find what I needed as the descriptions of what is on the aisles weren't that great, but I eventually figured it out. It's your regular dollar tree. I'd attend again to catch a bargain.

I was on a 3 day ride on my VTX 1800 and heading home when I had a tire stem issue. I needed an emergency repair so I could make it 3 hours home before dark. Jeff was awesome! He put a priority on the work order and I was on the road in an hour! They saved me big time!

After my third visit to Memphis Taproom, I am officially hooked.

This is a quaint little place. I love that it's not too big and not too bright. It's almost like a cool little dive bar that only locals know about, only better. There is a nice bar in the front and tables in the back for lunch/dinner. It looks really small, but I've never had any problems getting a table right away here- the last time I was with a party of 7 and we were seated right away, at 9 o'clock on a Friday!

My favorite part of MTR is the staff. They are so knowledgeable about the beer list and are always eager to make recommendations based on your tastes. I've had some of the best beers of my life just by letting our server pick my beer for me. They have great beers on tap, but I prefer to go for some of the more obscure imported bottles they have.

Now, the food. OMG THE FOOD. First of all, I am obsessed with their wings. I fancy myself something of an authority on wings, and I'll tell you that these are some of the best. They're pilsner-brined, so they come out extra plump and juicy. They're always perfectly cooked and the sauce is really good too. I could stand it to be a little hotter, but now I'm just nit-picking. 

I've also gotten the smoked coconut club all three visits. Listen. I know what you're thinking. I can tell you, as an avid meat eater, I too was afraid to try a tofu and smoked coconut sandwich, but I am SO glad I did. The flavor is wonderful and the sandwich is filling enough without making me feel like I need a nap. Also, since I have had it three times now, I can confirm that it is as consistent as hell. I have also had a few bites of the roast pork sandwich and the Port Richmond platter- both fantastic. The table also went crazy for the beer-battered dill pickles that a friend ordered and while I didn't taste them myself, they definitely looked delicious.

I really love this place. I can't wait till the weather warms up and I can check out the attached beer garden, but I promise I will try something different to eat next time...maybe...

I think that this place needs to be cut a little slack. It is fairly new and when I went I LOVED it. I am not a natural fan of German food but I love food in general and I was VERY pleasantly surprised by how much I loved it. The service was not good, some of the food was not as hot as I would have thought it should be but overall for a new place, I thought it was great. I think that the management should take a look at thee reviews and adjust their style. I think it has a promising future if the staff can kick it up a notch.

Nothing particularly wrong with this spot, but let's be honest, we all came here because the line at Biscuit Love was too long. While my group mostly had breakfast, I had our waiter's recommended favorite the Welsh Dip. Not bad, not great either, just an average meal at an average place I'm afraid. Wish I had more to say about this place, but that was my impression. I'll completely forget that I ever went to this restaurant a year from now when I'm remembering my Nashville trip.

Bf and I decided to come here for fancy Christmas dinner in between going to some friends' for a quiet dinner. I absolutely love this hotel during the holidays. The lights that are set up in the lobby are quite stunning and really get you in the holiday spirit. 

The meal itself for the holiday was from a set menu with choices of soup (turtle vs butternut squash), salad (beet or some sore of iceberg lettuce), mains (rack of lamb, snapper, or something I don't remember), and dessert (chocolate mousse or cheesecake) for $80 per person. On a normal basis, I would absolutely not want to pay that much for a meal, but we got some money as a gift and decided to spurge. All of the food was delicious except for the crab served on top of the snapper (tasted fishy, like it had been left out). 

Something that irked me: they asked us to move our reservation up by 15 minutes, so we did. Then they sat us more than 20 minutes late. To be fair, things in New Orleans rarely run on time. However, we were on time - so I feel like they should have either at least acknowledged that or comped drinks. 

Hints: there's usually free parking on the street in the nearby area. The 2 hour pay to park spots are free after 6pm. The bar was very accommodating and can make things off menu. Ex - they kindly served me a quite strong brandy milk punch. I still think Dante's Kitchen has the best one in the city, but this was a close second.

Food was great, service was great, ambience was great.

The only thing lacking with the waiter's ability to sell. We left still hungry because we didn't know that our order was only enough to feed a small child. Our bad

First time here. 1) take too long for the food to come out. 2) the shrimp and veggie tempura are not hot. 3) sushi : the rice is not fresh. They do not have the low sodium kind of soy sauce. 4) the server is nice. Overall, only the drunken fish will come back here.

This place was recommended to me by an employ at a plant I was working in. The business is kind of hidden in an old strip mall type building. Yelp map shows it to be a little bit down the road from its actual location. It sits behind the Exxon gas station. It's kind of rough looking on outside and the inside. The lady working the register as well  taking orders has her act together and is keeping things moving swiftly out front and in the back as well. She was very friendly and seems to have a people person personality. The food was really good and the prices are very reasonable. From the number of people coming in and out while in there, they must have a well established business and reputation. I would recommend this place to anybody that was looking for a place to eat lunch. The hot roast beef Po'Boy is good, the bacon cheese burger is good and the daily lunch special I've ate are good also. They just need fresh made sweet tea and it would be complete deal for me.

Warmest beer in town. Typical selection of whatever the distributor tells them to have on the shelves. Luke warm beer and a terrible selection.  Prices are comparible to everyone else but apparently the distributor won't let them turn their coolers on.
Great food and good service. Love the rustic atmosphere and it really gives you that country home feeling.

A fire distroys a restaurant- it's a story we're all fairly acquainted with.... and a fishy one at that.  I mean sure, restaurants have all sorts of fire hazzards, but the biggest in my opinion is the owner.  When I heard Lorenzos burned to the ground earlier this year, my knee jerk reaction was that the owner had 'had it'... and I was devistated that I'd never be able to say a fair goodbye to the cheezy no-frills XL slice of deliciousness that had sustained me through many of drunk college nights and pre-sporting-event drive-bys.  

ALAS- I went on Saturday night for my Welcome Back Lorenzo's late night drunk slice.  I got there around 1:45a... just in time for mayhem.  I waited in line, turned the corner, and got my first glimpse of the new digs- it's awful.  It was never a nice sight prior to the fire... but at least it wasn't trying.  Now, it IS trying... and it looks like New York.  Pre--fire Lorenzos had a little area in the back where you could put a plate and a drink down, and stand to eat your pie.  Nothing fancy... but it got the job done.  Post fire Lorenzos built the same little area out of marble/granite (drunk?) in the front of the store.  Meh... What happened to my crappy pizza?

I kept an opened mind, and ordered 2 slices in a box... 1 for me, 1 for the boyfriend.  Total = $6.25!!!!!  That's $3/ slice + $.25 for the box  OK people I understand that the slices are huge... and that the pizza is delicious, but in college less than 10 years ago, I was paying $1/slice... then $1.25... so we're talking about a 140% cost increase!  When this happened to the cost of a gallon of gas, people were OUTRAGED!   The cost went all the way up to $2.25 before the fire.  The original increases was due to some tough econamic times... cheese and flour were more expensive... but now we're almost back to normal... yet the price continues to increase.  

And that would be ok if the pizza was as-good post fire as it was pre-fire.  It's close... but somethign was missing... the grit... the rudeness... the sense of joy when you got a huge-mungo slice, or the sense of reality when you got one of the smaller slices of the pie... the ever-changing staffers behind the counter taking on the crowd... 

Lorenzo's is back... that's the good news.  The bad news is its sort of like a former fat-kid that lost a lot of weight- its got a fancy new facade, and a cockey confidence that makes it kind of a dick.  I'll still swing by for the late night drunken slice, but the spot it had in my heart is narrowing...

First time here. I had the beef brisket. The twice baked potato was excellent. I would personally recommend this exact order. The pita bread had olive oil smothered around it. Total plus. They also give you a bun to make a sandwich. 10/10 would go back

I wish I could give a higher rating. I'm hoping I will upon my next visit. However, I really loved many things about this place, and I'm pretty darn picky when it comes to dentists: 
1. The Dr. Mednick was great! She was extremely thorough and explained everything she was doing in layman's terms. Very nice.
2. The staff was very friendly
3. You have a front row seat of the Catalina's while you wait in the dental chair. 

I few things went wrong for me with this place:
1. After giving my insurance information 3 separate times over the phone, they asked me once again for it when I came for my visit
2. I scheduled a cleaning, and ended up being put down for just a check-up. The next appointment was not for another month and a half, even though I'm way over due for a cleaning. 
3. I was there for 2 hours for only a check up. Granted I needed new x-rays, but, still quite a while to wait. 

Overall I really like this place, and am looking forward to returning and re-reviewing. Maybe the first visits are always a bit hectic.

This place is a true representation​ of what to expect while visiting NOLA. When you walk in you can feel the place pull you in I am not going to say that this is stuff that I have around my life but wanted to pay my respects to see another way of life in a culture practiced by many. I would not want to label all I had seen but some did blow my mind. I have only seen a place like this in movies or TV shows. I kid you not I felt like Vampires and Ghosts could be near and I can't say that I pay any mind. We enjoyed stopping in and being in awww of such trinkets and can appreciate the history of NOLA and how this is a way of life for most that live here. I would recommend stopping in and looking around.

Never, never, never never, never again!!! My car steering wheel was wobbling at speeds at 45-50.   The wobbling was noticeable of concern.  I took the car to Chapman on 05/11/2018 at ~0800.  I got there, and the "greeter girl" (for lack of a better name), came up to me, I explained the issue and she told me that "I would have to wait" because they are "short staffed".  For 10 minutes, I waited for service--in my car, and NO service representative came up to get my information.  Five minutes passed by, and I saw the "greeter girl" again, and she just glared at me.  I continued to wait for another five minutes.  Finally, I had enough of waiting in my car, and I drove off--it was clear that I was not going to get service that day.  The sad thing is, because I did not wait for a service rep, I am not in the computer to be questioned about their quality (perhaps there is a camera in the bay).  That is why I am writing this review.  After two horrific break downs, I will NEVER buy another Honda...after dealing with Chapman and trying to get an extended warranty, I will NEVER buy another car from Chapman Automotive.  In fact, the only thing Chapman had going for it WAS the service.  And, now that is GONE!  I needed my car checked out because I was going to go on a trip to Phoenix on Wednesday, and I wanted to get this issue checked out.  I honestly do not know what to do--I guess I will have to go to Dobbs(???) or perhaps an actual tire place.  Regardless, I am "out" with Chapman and Honda!  DONE!!!

I have mixed feelings about this pet care company. 
Pros: Easy to reserve online, first night free for new customers & they have no problem giving you a tour of the kennels. Also, a live feed for daycare which I didn't use but the idea is nice. It was nice to call and get an update too.
Cons: They only give you an hour to get your pet otherwise you are charged for another night. 11-12 pickup is not great for people that are traveling. And...they don't leave the pets collar on them which made me feel uneasy. 

All said and done I would use this kennel again but will probably stick to my regular kennel
I got a catfish poboy and red beans and rice with sausage. Overall edible but rather bland.  Even the tea was watered down.

We came here for my sons birthday over a year ago while we were in town. We had made reservations for 8:45 and  we were told we had to wait 30 to 45 to be seated, even with a reservation. We were told  they were short staff and behind on reservations. We went to the bar to grab drinks while we waited, which was overall disorganized. After waiting 30 minutes we got a message that our reservation had been canceled because we didn't show up????? 

Despite the way our evening started. The food and our waitress were great. The steaks don't disappoint and the grouper was delicious. 

Hopefully they got new management as my son does not stop talking about the steak he enjoyed here. 

Best of luck!

I am here on a Monday night at 9:30pm.  The first three items that I attempted to order were unavailable.  Perhaps it is just me, but I find that to be unacceptable whether there was a convention in town or not.  Add in a homophobic waiter (Alex according to my receipt), and it was not a pleasant experience.

Customer service is beyond top notch. Zach made sure I got the things I needed to get it done in time. Walked in knowing nothing of what I wanted/needed to purchase as a Christmas gift and Zach went above and beyond to help me, never once trying to oversell or convince me to get something more expensive than what I needed. Every question asked was answered without him ever having to go look it up or ask someone else. This guys knows his stuff.

I only stayed for a few hours, but I thought it was very nice and upscale compared to most hotels in the area. I felt more comfortable here and the rooms were nice and clean. I did get free parking because I stayed barely 24 hours.

Disappointed by every single dish of food we ordered. The service is decent , but when it comes to the food taste as an experienced Asian, I don't think I could like it. Most of the food is way Americanized and has very salty taste. Don't get it why this place is so overrated. Definitely not coming back again.

Mahi Mahi is really tasty and Short Ribs are bomb but for some reason i dont know why i really hate the rice taste? But i can see why people love it but to me rice it tastes different from anywhere or how i cook it just not what i like. Everything i liked was those two meals and others are ok not bad for those dishes.

I had the special of the day - swordfish with broccolini. It was great! Someone at my table had ordered the lobster roll and the bun was too dry. The lobster wasn't that special either. The food is overpriced and they have small serving sizes. The service is great there.
This place has a nice atmosphere and service is good but food is just average and overpriced. Seems like they put this place in this area just to overcharge for mediocrity.

Awful.  

We looked up the menu before heading in & things looked tasty.  Read the reviews & most said that the food was good, but that it was overpriced.  Normally, dinner for two for $22 is a good deal...but not when the food is bad.  

I had wanted a pulled pork baked potato, as seen on the online menu, but the girls at the counter treated me like I had two heads when I asked for it.  So we ordered two pulled pork sandwiches, baked beans & mac n cheese (all to go).

When I got home I realized they charged me for two large sides, but gave me one small.  The beans tasted like canned crap.  The mac n cheese was a chalky bland mush of soggy noodles.  The pork was so dry we had to smother it in sauce...which was mediocre, at best.  The only difference between the original BBQ sauce & the spicy is crushed red pepper.  

AND their sweet tea was bitter....HOW DO YOU MESS UP SWEET TEA?!  It's just tea & sugar.  Not hard.  

Luckily for us, Riverview has a BBQ truck on every block.  Next.

My go-to place will always be Pepperfire, but my family and I enjoyed our first try of Hattie B's  tenders at the Hot Chickenrama! Festival. I look forward to trying out the store experience some time.

I must be insane. I keep on expecting something different from this branch which is near me. But it's still the same place that has horrible service. This used to be my go to for everything- 9 yrs ago I moved to Tucson and since coming here was always a nightmare, I am so glad Lowe's is nearby. This store is full of employees but don't seem concerned about being of service. You can never find anyone to help you. I needed some capstone and 2 employees, a man and a woman chatting, couldn't help me, fortunately a young man was nearby to help. I was not surprised to  hear a much older woman shouting in a loud voice that she was there waiting for someone to help her, who never showed up even if they supposedly told her someone was coming. Today I went to return plants that had died with the heat, had my receipt, the pots and whatever was left of the veggies and the customer service person, had to call Tony, assistant manager, who questioned me about the plants, and my ability to grow them '" my ability is not a requirement in your return policy."  By the way, I also had an online order of which part was for pick up here and when I was about to get it, found out they cancelled the order, returned it to shelf because it wasn't being picked up, the online support had to call them and tell me that was what they did. Horrible store- no wonder reviews are bad for this branch. I am insane to expect things to change in this branch.

My mom and I visited this place and I found a very pleasant girl behind the counter. She told us about their teas and that they were custom blended. She also told us about a hands on tea blending class that they have.

My mom and I both ended up ordering the same Apricot & Cream tea latte. Definitely could taste the apricot. We really enjoyed these drinks. They were at the right temperature and in a large ceramic mug just perfect for holding in your hands and drinking tea.

We also had two Macarons each. I did think these were on the expensive side at $3 each at their regular price. They did taste great. Macarons are tricky to make at home. If you have never tried them you should try them when you make it to this store. 

I am sure that the next time we are in the Broad Ripple area we will stop in for another cup of tea.

One star for booking high profile EDM acts from around the world - it's about time.  One additional star for a good sound system and crisp lights and effects.  That is where the positive review ends.

As all the other reviews state, no liquor license.  Tickets aren't cheap so it's ludicrous to have to pay additional money to bring in your OWN booze (which you already paid for once).
