They won't let you bring in any bottles that are already open, either, so I witnessed people in line chugging a mostly-full bottle of Johnny Walker - because that always ends well.  

If you've never been to a better venue for dance music, you might think this is okay.  If you have been to better venues, you will hate this.  I went to see one of my favorite acts but couldn't find my groove at all because literally every 30 seconds someone was shoving into me trying to get closer to the 'stage' area or to another point across the venue.  This is partially a people problem, as many didn't seem to realize that there were other people present who also wanted to dance.  But it's also a layout and crowd management problem.  They need to remove the tables from the middle of the dance floor AND they need to rope off specific areas to allow people to move freely to the bathrooms and smoking area without cutting through the entire crowd.  This is not rocket science.

I went to a 21+ show.  I can conclusively say I would never, ever go to an all ages show here even if Armin van Buuren himself invited me.

As for these rumored 'cool jets', they never manifested, with the end result being that it felt (and smelled) like dancing in a swamp.  I expect to sweat at this type of show, but as I said before, I was barely grooving compared to my usual and was still dripping sweat.  Oh, and need to get some air?  Well, it better be in the smoker's area or else they won't let you back in - hope you like the overwhelming stench of cigarettes.

This place has a lot of potential but right now they are not maximizing it.  I love who they book, but I probably wouldn't come back here unless they made substantial improvements.  What a tease.
I just had my dog-hair-filled Acura SUV cleaned by Dr Detail and it looks amazing!  they did a great job!  thank you Dr Detail, I feel like I have a new car.

Went in, place was empty. Nobody at the counter, rang the bell. Minute later guy comes 0ut of the back om the phone and told us to wait while he continued to tslk on the phone and do a little clean up. This went on so we walked out.
Caught a very random show here during one of my trips to New Orleans.  Wasn't too excited about the band, but it's a cool, intimate venue and I'd go back...

Allow me to be the first to review the newest pizza shop in Downingtown... "Welcome to Downingtown Spatola's!!!"

This place has been open for about a month now, and like all new restaurants in the area I was curious to see what they had to offer. I must say though, I was a little hesitant to give this place a try because of how consistently good the pizza is from our local go-to pizza shop (Coco's). Nonetheless, some neighbors had tried the pizza here and said it was good so we figured it would be worth giving it a try. My wife was hungry for Sicilian pizza tonight, so we decided to order from Spatola's and see what they had to offer. With that said, this review is based solely on the Sicilian pizza. When I arrived to pick up the pizza, I was impressed by the renovated decor and cleanliness. This is definitely a place I would bring the family to for lunch or dinner. I eagerly drove home to get my hands on a slice of deliciousness that I had smelled all the way home. After sinking my teeth into the first bite, I am happy to say that Downingtown now has another option for good pizza! Spatola's delivered on all three elements of a good pizza: 1) Crust with a nice crunch on the bottom and edges, yet chewy, 2) Sauce that's great tasting with all of the right spices, and 3) Quality cheese that doesn't leave a puddle of grease on your plate. I ate 6 slices (atypical for me!), so anything less than a great review wouldn't be fair. At $15.75, they are a touch higher priced than most local places but I am always willing to pay for quality eats. 

Spatola's, well done. You have lured me in with your Sicilian pizza, so I will most certainly be back to try the traditional pizza and other menu items. Keep it up, and I will be sure to update my review with an earned fifth star! 

If you are hesitant in trying this place because it's new, just try it out and please Yelp your experience. There have been many mediocre pizza places that have turned over at this location, so good local places like this one will only thrive with support and positive reviews.

**** 4 solid stars from me ****

Well, figured we would give another chance, this will be the last time except to go for wings and get my growlers filled.  The head chef really needs to step up with the menu.  We ordered a southern chicken sandwich for $10 which I could easily go to Wendys and buy for $5 with fries and a drink(pathetic).  Frozen patty, lettuce and mayo on a bun for $10??? really?  Burgers are just as bad, frozen 6oz patties.  Onion soup for $8- bread was stale, cheese was in a huge lump at the bottom, tasted like beef broth from a can.  Daily specials are more of a revolving weekly thing, depending on what the chef wants to cook/not cook I assume.  How long can you keep a Pittsburg meat sandwich on the special and charge $12 for it?  You are frying some meat and putting it on bread!  Look at the Downingtown menu and the specials are more diverse and fresh.

Stick with the beer and maybe the wings. I have been here over 12 times, it has not changed.  Overpriced food...but good beer.  Without the beer, this place would not exist.

Anyone who gives this place 5 stars obviously has not been to other restaurants in the area, or is paid to post them. Half moon, Kennett brewing company, Giordanos, Two Stones pub, are all better food options.

I'm not very familiar with the Philly restaurant scene (I've lived in DC for 7 years and currently live in NJ)  but I was expecting more. I came here for dinner with a friend and while the staff and service was great, the food not so much. For instance, I ordered a small salad as an appetizer ($18) which looked like it it could have been made at a local diner. For the main, I ordered the sweet potato gnocchi which was fine, but the sauce overpowered the flavor of the gnocchi itself. Also, portion size was not very generous as I expected the main dish to be a little larger than the size of my fist ($26). I typically have a major sweet tooth and was really looking forward to dessert, but by the end, I was so unimpressed with the dishes that I decided to skip it and head on over to a local bakery across the street. Overall, the place was decent but not memorable.
Love this place and the women that work here! Very friendly! I am glad they found a nicer and better location! It's the only place I will get my eyebrows threaded at! They do the best job!

I bought a fire stick that didn't work. When I went to return it the lady helping me answered the phone in middle of my transaction. Then when she asked me what's wrong I told her that the fire stick wouldn't stay connected to the wifi and suddenly stop working. She then look at the receipt and told me if I had the same debt card. I told her no and she refused to give me back my money. So I've learned to never buy anything from hhgregg ever again and they don't stand by there products.

This place has been just horrible. They charged us almost 100$ for half an hour and 3 cocktails that were not great either. They also charged 20$ per person included in the check. Overall very bad experience. I do not recommend . Better go to South. Better vibe, nicer place and straight forward hostes.

PERFECTED‼ I had one of the best pedicure and manicure services I've ever received and I go all over the world! Titi was a master craftsmen the way she took the time to execute my services! I've been with my previous manicurist for 10 years, but I'm switching to this salon. The options for services are prestigious for the Midwest. The ambiance, music selection, customer service and results are FABULOUS! I've waited for the Midwest to open a salon of this magnitude. I had premiere birthday services and I'm so grateful for TiTi brightening my day!

I normally stay away from strip mall dining but I do not live in St Louis & this was near my Mom's house. We were able to sit right away at a bar table. The rest of the restaurant was full. The service was great & we were given the most delicious bread for an appetizer & a free donut desert with bacon & cream for my Mom's birthday.  The food was awesome... potstickers, spinach salad, shrimp, tasting board. It is a must try! 
I even let the manager know how impressed I was with the place. 
Good wine selection too.

Was really hoping I would like this place.

It kinda reminded me of BurgerFi, the decor was hip and there was a variety of burgers.

Though I was sadly disappointed. My wife and I ordered the mushroom burger along with an order of fried green beans. The mushrooms and onions on the burger were fine, but the patty tasted like it was something that I would buy frozen from a grocery store. The fried green beans needed some salt, there wasn't really any taste to them. Just tasted like batter basically with a small hint of green beans. (Oh well at least I got some vegetables in on that meal :P)

The place looks cool, but I wouldn't recommend it.

This place really confused me; it's seems like it's trying to be very glitzy and fancy, but its so old fashion and out of date, its failing. Like when my grandma would get all gussied up in her polyester pants and clip on earrings. She had all of her finest duds on, but they were all 25 years old....
Right off the bat, I knew the meal was going to be questionable. They brought out "fresh baked" bread and butter, and as an Italian and bread connoisseur, I was insulted. It was dry and sort of stale-ish and just awful! 
Very little on the menu appealed to me, and what did, seemed ruined by odd ingredient pairings. In the end I just got a bowl of gumbo as my meal. The BF was more ambitious, ordering the pork chop and spinach salad. My soup came first and I ate a few bites while waiting for the BF's entrée. It was overly fishy and paled in comparison to the out-of-this-world gumbo I had at K Paul's Kitchen. I soon discovered why when I bit into what I thought was chicken but was in reality was an oyster. The BF seemed to enjoy his pork chop and, despite my resistance in trying it, we both enjoyed his spinach salad. The BF ordered the white chocolate bread pudding for dessert which was really weird. Its not your typical with hunks of bread, but more like a smooth soufflé. I learned its one of their specialties...from like 1806 or something. It comes cut in a triangle and has zero white chocolate flavor. On the other hand I had very little of it because the soup was already making me nauseous, so... 
One cute thing though, they give you a recipe card for the dessert you order when you leave. If it had been any good I would have been charmed. 
As a side note, that gumbo made me sick all night:(

This place breaks my heart. We were very friendly with previous owner who suddenly passed away. As far as I know this is new ownership. Its clear to us they are not using the previous cook because the food has taken a step back. My wife is from Vietnam and this used to be our go to place for her. Its still acceptable.

2 stars because the top shelf margarita was good. Everything else about my evening there was enough for me to know I don't need to go back. There are plenty of other Mexican restaurants I'd rather spend my time and money. 

First of all, we were a large group so I know that has its own challenges. The place was mostly empty tho and they wouldn't seat us until they could sit us in a section with no other tables so they could dedicate a server to us. Whatever. I think they also stuck a new waitress with us which totally sucked for her and they shouldn't have done that. It took at least 15 min for her to bring us waters.. another 10 min before drink orders.. then another 15 min or more before drinks showed up. Then another 15 min before she came and took food orders.. so what are we at, about 45+ min before we place food orders? She also didn't know the menu so couldn't answer many questions.. and then I get the wrong tacos.. but I don't want to wait 15 min before she shows back up to let her know. She also added an extra item onto our order and charged us for it despite us never having ordered it. They did resolve this when we paid our check. 
The food wasn't very good anyway. The house made flour tortillas were raw, I couldn't eat them so I just ate my taco filling with a fork. Several others in my group wanted to order a torta but at 6:30 on a Saturday night they were totally out of bread for the rest of the evening. That's an entire section of the menu you can't order from! Our server was sweet but definitely could've used some extra training before throwing her out to the serve tables on a Saturday night. Unless you live walking distance to this place, don't put in the effort. The parking lot is also a paid lot after 1 hour and since we had to wait 40 min before they'd seat us in their empty dining room, well.. parking wasn't free either.

Use to have the best service and salads in town. They have really gone down hill. We ate there this Saturday and my wife's chef salad was so sad. She had to send it back because they forgot to put tomatoes in it. When she did get it back they had taken some half assed extremely thin slices of tomatoes that they use for their sandwiches and threw them on top. We had also paid for 4 Brooke dessert brownies that they did not bring to the table. The turkey and ham is just some thin sliced sandwich meat that they throw in with the mounds and mounds of greens. We remember before the pandemic every time we came in the manager would come to our table and ask how was the food and did we need anything. NO SIGN OF A MANAGER. Soooo disappointed!!

The staff was very nice the menu has plenty to choose from.The food was excellent the complementary chips and dip was a nice touch and also at the end of your amazing meal you get a another complementary desert, not to mention the bill was very reasonable. I Will definitely be coming back  here on another cheat day, so worth it.
Ribs and steak fries makes for a great meal. They aren't on the menu, but they are awesome.

great customer service they're always so nice when my company orders lunch there. they bag them up separately & put the names on them!!! they're food is always fresh and AMAZING ITALIAN FOOD!!!!!!! haven't had a bad experience yet!!! will continue to order from them and take friends from out of town to them!!!

It bothers me to have to give this place 2 stars because there is so much potential here to be an awesome place. Food is excellent (when it comes hot), beers are on point, and the overall vibe of the place is really cool.

I have been here a half a dozen times and every single time the food and the menu is awesome,but the service process is absolutely terrible. I was served a cold meal for the last time and I will never be back. Thanks Ala Spina for taking what could be an awesome addition to the neighborhood and ruining it with your mis managed poor service process.

Only giving two stars for the food- 
my dinner and crab crakes were delicious. Service was poor, our waitress seemed to disappear after giving us our drinks and appetizers. Service is everything to me, I won't be rushing back anytime soon

I really wanted to like Yen Ching a lot. From what I had read the food was great. I found my lunch to be very Americanized Chinese, not the Chinese experience I was looking for. Food was OK, but even the spicy Szechuan Pork I ordered was pretty bland. On the good side, there was plenty of pork in the dish. The Egg Drop Soup I haven't had in years and was also very bland I wish I'd been offered a choice of maybe Hot and sour Soup also, but I wasn't told about it and it did not say anything about choices on the menu. 

Also, I have to say---places that only have forks and not chopsticks are only telling customers they think no one in Boise knows how to use chopsticks. At least if they have them at the table in a container I wouldn't have to every time go through asking if I can have a pair. Would I go back? If I was downtown, maybe for lunch. But it certainly does not excite me like some of the other choices of food in Downtown Boise.

This is why I told the conference planners that I wanted to fly southwest or airtran. No snacks??? That's ur first no no! Where they do that at??.. The gate waiting area was tiny and uncomfortable. The plane was tiny and uncomfortable with not enough overhead baggage storage.  The flight attendants were rude, cold, and did everything but "attend" as their job title describes.  NO SNACKS!! I know I was only going to Atlanta from Philadelphia but I was banking on snacks :( my heart weeps...

Affordable place for brunch or lunch.
Sandwiches as well as breakfast options and of course the beignets.

I came here today to mail an international package to my friend in Switzerland . I really didn't have any problems at all   I came here about 2:30 in the afternoon on a Thursday and there was hardly a line   I filled everything out. The lady was quite helpful. She informed me of my different options. Made my selection paid the price and now my gift package is on the way

This place is the worse! I order from Dunkin everyday and when I came here it tasted nothing like it. First she forgot the mocha and sugar. She says oh yeah I did and giggles. Got her to remake it and there's still no sugar I give up on this place. At night it's just young girls working not answering the phone or doing anything. DO BETTER! I wish I could give this place no starts. This is the second time I've been here and the first time was almost a year  ago I thought something might have changed, but no.

Went here for the 1st time and doesn't really have good management. Only 1 employee serving the food to everyone. She was running everywhere to get the food and drinks to customers. We waited more than an hour to get 2 bagels with eggs! When we went to pay we were outraged about the price! $40 for 2 bagels,  2 drinks and a cookie! Insane! Will not be returning due to high prices and the food is not really that great either!

Definitely a "get what you pay for" gym. 

Costs: It's $150 enrollment fee, though they do have specials regularly;for instance, New Years is generally $0 enrollment fee, Valentines Day they had half off so $75, and just a couple weeks later for March Madness had 4 days of $0 enrollment fee, damn it! So if you're interested, sign up for emails and wait it out for a $0 enrollment fee promotion, they come around often and you'll kick yourself for wasting money on any enrollment fee here. Note there is an annual fee of $40, making it around $13-$14/ month in reality. 

Atmosphere: This is my first real gym experience and it seems most everyone minds their own business. A lot of various "types" of people. Relatively clean, lots of paper towel and spray stations. Locker rooms seem to be kept pretty clean, but I haven't used the showers. But in the evenings IT'S PACKED! Often it can be hard to complete your routine/program due to waiting on others. A lot of people tend to be on their phones too holding up machines, I've had to ask people to move so I could use the machine. 

Not many broken machines that I've seen either. The pool is pretty cloudy so I don't plan on using it. 

Overall, the cheapness comes through, but it allows you to add other stuff to your workouts since it is so cheap. They offer free fitness assessment, but I won't do that b/c I don't want to be talked into something unknowingly.

"We only serve potato soup on Mondays and Wednesdays...and on Tuesdays we wear pink!" 

OK, I made that last part up, but what gives with the totally random soup schedule? I came here on a cold day after thinking about how good a cup of potato soup would have been all afternoon, but unfortunately my craving and the cold weather arrived on a Sunday. Why is potato soup rationed to two totally random days during the week, while I see the same other lame-o soups up on the menu every time I walk through the door? 

I should note, all Panera's I've been to seem to play this game of musical soups, but I don't have the time or patience to keep up with each location's schedule. Maybe the company need's some sort of soup tracker app, or they could simply not continue to disappoint their customers by offering their full menu every day of the week. What a novel idea, eh?

Dinner here today was a positive experience.  The restaurant was fairly busy but we were greeted promptly.  We were seated and greeted by our waiter quickly.  Some Steak 'n Shakes tend to have (very) slow service but that was not the case here tonight.  Good service.  Rapid delivery of our meal.  Overall a good experience.

Awesome place!! Went last night and had a very nice time. Food was amazingly delicious and the waiting staff was very pleasant and courteous. Darrick, our waiter, was very nice making us feel welcomed and treated each one of us with respect and always with a smile. He was friendly and even made our 10 year old feel very special!! 
If ever in NOLA, gotta come to this place and try their Gator Bites and Crabmeat Cakes...Delicious!! Hope you are lucky enough to meet Darrick on your visit!

We winter in St. Pete and go to Moon Under Water every two weeks or so.  Poor service?  Lousy attitude?  Yes.  Yelpers have written about this problem, and here's another example.  My husband and I recently ordered the normally tasty chicken pot pie. We didn't ask him, but the waiter told us that it should be out very quickly.  We waited and waited (not a problem) and the meals were finally delivered and served (at tableside, they pour the pie filling into the mile-high flaky pastry).  First bite and we found the pie to be just barely warm.

I didn't plan to say anything because it was edible--and because our waiter was already in an eye-rolling snit about problems with the people at the table next to ours:  they were trying to tell (convince!) him that the tonic water in their drinks was flat.  His all-too-typical-these-days response:  they had been serving tonic water all day from that dispenser and they hadn't had any complaints about it.  Pathetic.  PLEASE, Mr. Server, do not argue with customers, especially about an issue like that.  One of the dissatisfied customers finally took charge and firmly asked him to tell the bartender about the problem.  Anyway, I knew he would not take well to a "complaint," so I decided to tell him that I had a "comment" about our meals.  I did not expect him to have the audacity to reply; I thought he would thank me for the feedback and assure me he would make the kitchen aware of the problem.  His response?  I should have told him earlier because he could have done something about it, "tossed it into the microwave oven or something."  (I don't like to think that I go to a restaurant to have microwaved food - especially a flaky pastry.  I know it happens, and that's fine, but why not keep up the façade that the food is warm from the oven?).

This brings me to my point, which definitely applies to Moon Under Water, and to more than one server we've had there:  serving staff work for tips, and today's general attitude is that wait staff take an order, send it to the kitchen, and deliver the order when it's ready.  To be truly doing their jobs - and EARNING their tips - they should check the details to be certain the order is properly prepared, presented, and otherwise ready to be served before plunking it down on the table (preferably without having to ask first, "Who ordered the...?").  At Moon Under Water It's usually the kitchen or the customer that's wrong, never the server.  Why waste time deflecting the blame?  Just make it right from the beginning and - voilà! -  no irritating little problem customers to waste their time.

The food was great, however the service was not our waiter was visibly annoyed even though we were very sweet, nice, and cordial to her. She gave us a limit on out bottomless mimosas saying depending on how we act is how she would determine if we got a bottle. She was not like that to other tables even when one of her tables broke a glass, we also had to keep asking for juice as she never once came to our table asking if we need anything and when we did ask for more juice she just gave us dirty looks, she also continuously kept coming to our table trying to take our plates even though we were not finished and gave us our check halfway through our brunch and when we asked for another round of champagne for our mimosas and more juice she told us that the champagne we did get would be our last one ( even though we were not rowdy in the slightest) and that she would refill our juice when it was empty ( she didn't by the way we had to wait for her to pass by and hold our hands up like little kids). All in all the server ruined the experience which is sad because I really love that place and have been there before, would recommend, but if the server is still there I will not be going back. She really needs a lesson on how to be a good server to people who have done her no wrong except for trying to have a good time.

Food is good, service needs some work. I didnt want what was on the actual menu.  Just wanted to couple slices of mozzarella prosciutto and bruschetta.  He looked at me like i was odd.  I explained i just wanted something light.

I got 2 of the 3.  No topping on the bread.

The mozzarella was very light and tasty and the prosciutto was cut perfectly thin and had a great taste that was not overly salty.

I travel a lot for work (75-90% surprising for a guy who isn't in sales) and stay at a lot of hotels.  My favorite by far is Marriott and a majority of my stays are in Marriott branded hotels.  Granted I'm use to staying at the JW Marriott but when they aren't around I will stay at a normal Marriott.  This one is similar to all the others where you get a good clean room at a reasonable price.  The service was fine - not over the top but certainly not lacking.  

My dissatisfaction with this hotel had to do more with infrastructure items.  The toilet backed up every morning with material from other rooms.... flushed it and it was fine.  Toliet operated fine other than that and it didn't run over onto the floor.  I was there for only two nights and didn't feel like moving rooms so I just lived with it.  The other thing was that you couldn't get the water much hotter than lukewarm.   I spend 15 minutes playing with it and that is the best I could do.  Turn it all the way up and it was actually just room temperature - back it down a few inches and you could get lukewarm.  Wanted hot water but lukewarm will do in a pinch.  At least it wasn't cold.  

Getting my room serviced also took quite a bit of time.  When I pulled into the parking lot late the evening before there were perhaps 20 cars so I knew the hotel wasn't full yet when I returned early (2 pm) the next day the room still had not been serviced.  I had to call and ask that it be done.  

As far as the staff goes - they were very good and helpful.  I was greeted warmly and when I asked for something they jumped to get it for me right away.  I have no complaints there.  

Would I go back......  Probably not.  There is another Marriott property further down from here that is much nicer (had dinner there during this stay).  That one is on the bay and in a much more interesting part of town.  Stayed here because it was closer to where my meetings were going to be - will know for next time though.  Don't get me wrong though as this wasn't a "bad" place.  Lord knows I have stayed in bad places over the years.  This one just isn't as nice as those around it and if given a choice I'll stay in the nicest place I can.

They have a great menu with a huge selection of choices. If you bring a large party here everyone will be able to find something that meets their taste. The wait staff is extremely helpful and friendly.

I love their rice pudding.

Called an inquire about specials, was told it's all you can eat snow crabs.  I asked what time it ends tonight and the person said at 9pm tonight when the kitchen closed. 7 mins later, I'm here and seated and the waiter advised no more crabs. Very disappointed because I just called!  According to the dining area..it's a light night. I probably won't come back.

This is the first time and the last time we will order or eat the food. The burgers were not what we ordered. We ordered fries with cheese on the side and the put it on the fries. Also, there is no malt flavor at all in the milkshake. If your staff can't read the order, your are not going to last long. Mediocre food, incorrect orders and worse milkshakes than what I can make myself. This is a terrible first impression.

Not a great experience. The place looked pretty good and I saw coconut shrimp on the menu, so I went in. The band was good and the place is new and clean but the food is not at all good and I was not too happy with one of the servers. 

So, the woman who took our drinks order asked what my party wanted. I'd already had a drink elsewhere that was pretty strong and didn't want alcohol. I was ordering food, so I forgot I was in a bar, not a restaurant, and said, "just water for me." I don't hear so well and the music was pretty loud, so I turned my attention back to the musicians when the server started yelling, "Ma'am, Ma'am!" at me. When that got my attention, she informed me there was a one drink minimum. Fine. I ordered a soda water with a squeeze of lime. The waitress didn't have to take that tone.

Needless to say, I was less than happy when the shrimp came. The guy who brought the shrimp seemed nice enough, unlike the drinks waitress, but then there was the food. It was FIVE, count them, FIVE shrimp which were undoubtedly frozen, not fresh, extremely greasy, and with a very thick, overcooked crust on them. This cost over $10.  I will not be darkening the door of this establishment again and take it as a sign of the deterioration of Frenchmen Street, which has become Bourbon Street Jr. Why two stars? The decent band and the clean bathroom.

Ok, I admit I've never been in here for their drinks. Maybe because each time I end up going out.. I always go straight to Sky Bar, only because I'm cheap, and I'm a creature of habit. BUT this review is not for their drinks or nightlife bar scene, in fact its for their amazing burgers. Bumsted's burgers are MUCH better than Lindy's on 4th.. and also much more affordable. Did I mention they are bigger too? I came here 2 weekends or so ago, and I still remember the delicious  burger I ordered. *Ok I don't remember the name, but it was the portobello, bacon burger.. instead I substituted the portobello for some ground beef.. and voila.. my "creation" was mouth watering deliciousness. Oh and let me tell you the service here was phenominal, the waiter even offered us to get us a to go icetea refill without even asking! how amazing is that?

Lithia Springs Park- Lithia, FL (Hillsborough County Park $24/night)

WARNING: do NOT STAY at Lithia Springs Park!!

We checked in two weeks ago with our new Southwind Class A and were given a site map that was wrong. We were then directed to site 29, which only had 30a power (we had not yet gotten an adapter so had to run the generator). 

We finally moved to a beautiful site by the river and wow - this place is an outdoor homeless shelter. We saw a TV being stolen (reported it and nothing was done), we had people walking into the park who weren't staying there who tried to steal one of our outdoor tables, we had neighbors who were up all night cursing loudly at each other. We were even told by rangers to "watch out for site 35 and that he is ok if he's on his meds". Broken down campers everywhere and worst of all the lady at the front station is VERY rude. Told my wife that we HAVE to leave by 11am tomorrow - meanwhile they had allowed a tent camper to squat on site 45 for DAYS past their departure date and didn't do anything about it. If you are a good and decent camper they will harass you otherwise they can't do anything with the druggies that abound in this park. 

Stay away!!

I've given this place multiple chances because I live in the neighborhood, but every time I go here, the bartenders are so rude. Last night I asked if they had White Claw (I know, how basic of me) and the bartender laughed in my face and walked away without taking my order. She was so incredibly rude. Thankfully, the other bartender took my order after I stood there for a minute waiting for someone to come back. I will say, that their sandwiches are incredible, but please teach your bartenders how to be decent.
Friendly staff and decent food for the money. Very close to the tourist zone but at least they make an effort with the food.

Disappointed with the jambalaya supreme. Very dry. Sausage pieces all charred. Rabbit pieces dry. 
Taste plate had great fried chicken and red beans and rice. 
The beer was cold.

It felt very touristy.. I went during a show and it was.. eehhh.. and the food.. well I've had SO MUCH BETTER.. so many other places.. and it wasn't cheap for what I got.. it only received 3 stars bc of the historical building..

Literally right around the corner from the only other tiki bar in the quarter.  Neat place with good atmosphere and decor and nice cool air conditioning (essential in August).  Decent selection of tiki drinks available along with Red Stripe beer in a bottle.  We had a poor experience with one of the staff members but the owner advises that it has been addressed.   Recommended and check out the one around the corner too!
Eh, this place was okay for pho, but they took FOREVER! Seriously long wait. Broth was kind of plain. But they gave a ton of noodles.

To say they are the best in town would be a fib; cause I haven't experienced all the other roofers:)  My experience w/Schneider has me as a repeat customer which means I will not be experiencing another company as long as they continue to give quality, reasonable service.  I am a shut-in and unable to visit the job site.  I request pictures of the finished project and I also went sneaky...I had another roofing company inspect the roof for possible problems to which they responded "everything was fine and in tack", my tenant concurred. The other roofer couldn't even fake a mistake.  I have hired Schneider for repairs to my home roof and replacement for a rental and have been very satisfied with their skills and customer service experienced from the office.  GREAT COMPANY....no joke...I call it like I experience it:)

I ordered the Surfer, a hashbrown, and a bottle of Coke. This was without a doubt the best sandwich I've had in my entire life and the best meal I've had in 7 years of living in Philadelphia. I'm pretty sure I brused my pisiform bone trying to open the Coke but it was all worth it. Great service, great food, and even a great Yelp review.

Love the food. I have ordered many times. Fantastic chicken and sides. However, terrible service. I was at the bar with colleagues, my coat on a hanger nearby. When i went to leave my coat was gone. I asked the bartender who told me someone in my party took it. That was unlikely and i said as much. He said to ask the front if it was in lost and found. I went up and asked and they told me they didn't have my coat. At that point i asked what the hell happened to it, and they got a manager. The manager immediately came up and threatened to call police on me for asking for my coat. I told him to fuck off and either give me my coat or tell me they didn't have it. He then produced my coat from the back. No idea why they took my coat in the first place or then held it from me and threatened to call the police simply for me asking for my coat and telling them to fuck themselves. Not polite, i will admit, but they took my coat and then threatened to call the police on me for asking for my coat and telling them to fuck off.

Well for starters the food was cold when it arrived but the entire blame is probably not on just the restaurant. But let me tell you what is, poor food. I had brisket and ribs and actually just ribs because my brisket was all fat. My ribs only had a little meat on them and being from St Louis, I know good ribs. My wife's brisket was not much better and her chicken was very dry. The sides were not good either because they were very seasoned or salty. The best thing really was the bread . So with a $38 dollar tab I will not be eating from here again. But this is just me and my wife's opinions

My wife and I just wanted side salad's, and they both came with the wrong dressing. She wanted no onions on her salad which came with onions on it. We both were supposed to have ranch dressing however one had blue cheese and the other had Caesar. They were not that busy the wait staff was great just a mess up by the kitchen

If you a trim or something simple and straight forward at a bargain, this is the perfect place. I went in to have an inch cut off and maintain my hair a little, and the women did just that. She was very diligent with every snip and even went on her knees to examine my ends at eye level, then proceeded to snip a few bits off. 

However, if you want a trendy and complicated haircut, I would go elsewhere. But with $20 for a haircut with hair as long and as thick as mine, that's a steal!

The sushi was decent but expensive.  We ordered a large bottle is Sake and were brought a small in-house glass.  We also ordered edamame , some of which was warm and some cold.  We were hoping once the sushi arrived that would change things but unfortunately we received very little sushi for the cost.  Maybe they can charge these prices for the portions because they're in Montecito.  My sense, not worth it.

The great Philly cheesesteak wars rages on but there's on one originator of it all: Pat's

Geno's cheesesteak is literally right across the road but I had to try originator first being my first visit to philly. 

It was amazing!!! I ordered the cheesesteak with onions and cheese whiz. You can choose without onions or provolone or sharp American cheese. The default is cheese whiz. The greasy melts cheesy mess was amazing as you bite into it and the flavors explodes in your mouth. The savory steak mingles well with the sweet onions and melts cheese all sopped up in the bread. 

The fries were regular. I didn't get it with cheese but will next time. 

Tips:
- CASH ONLY: be sure to bring cash. 
- meal total: cheesesteak with coke and fries will run you $20-$25 depending on extras
- separate lines for cheesesteak and another line for fries and drinks
- limited outdoor seating
- lines are long but moves very quick

Really disappointed.  We had been to the Fellini's in Media and loved our experience and were sadly disappointed with the sister location in Newtown Square.  We had reservations for 7pm and were not seated until 7:25.  We brought a bottle of Pino Grigio, which they never offered to chill for us (instead a glass of ice was brought to the table).  Our waitress was very sweet, and equally scattered.  While she was taking our order she was waving to people in the parking lot... 3 out of our 4 dinners were wrong when they eventually came out to the table.  I had asked for a subsitution of alfredo sauce on my pasta special, which instead came out as a super oily butter sauce that was tasteless.  The silver lining of my meal were the mussels and clams; they were great and super fresh.  However, I doubt we'll ever be back.

First I would like to pay tribute and respect to Chef Paul. He was a brilliant chef who set standards in the food industry and he will be missed. I have waited a very long time to experience the cusine of K Paul. I was in New Orleans and had the opportunity to dine in several excellent establishment. Unfortunately K-Paul was not one of them. The food was of average quality, and to say the dining room staff had an attitude is a gross understatement. The Gumbo was outstanding but it was followed by some badly over cooked Shrimp. The staff was indifferent and even rude at time. During 
 dinner, a large party of diners came into the dining room and we were seated across from them. When they were finally seated, a young lady proceeded to yell the specials out over the crowd. I would consider this appropriate at a bowling alley. However I was surprised to find this at K-Paul. I did waste my time speaking to the manager who dismissed my complaint without an apology. Considering the expense and the experience we had I will not be dining there again.

Paid for the dinner and had to get a refund because of the poor selection of food and all of it looked old and not appetizing whatsoever. Only bright side was that the manager was nice to give us our refund.

The burger was okay but found out owners changed twice and the recipe changed when they sold it.  As a matter of fact they don't even carry or offer the Iranian Juice anymore.  The owners took the Iranian Juice recipe with them.  

I would pass on this joint just to many burger spots with more flavorful burgers.  Now don't get me wrong therr was nothing wrong with the burger and it had flavor but I was looking to taste that Iranian Juice do it just didn't hit my taste spot.

I may be a bit demanding when it comes to American Cuisine there are so many burger joints you got to rise above average.   So if you want a lot of beef get the 27 oz. Deluxe Cheese Burger.

Came to New Orleans for the first time this week for work. Started off funky with delayed flights and intense traffic from airport to hotel.  St Patty's day on bourbon street was way worse than I could have imagined. Basically it comes down to this, I wanted to roll out of town as soon as we got downtown because it's a shithole, literally a shithole down there but the rest of the city grew on me and it was alright.  Get the hell out of the drunken haze that is the canal, bourbon, and royal Street areas. I mean you see some cool shit for sure but its filthy and smells like a giant piss was taken over the whole area. Piss isn't really that awesome.  

The garden district, St Charles area, and Freeman Street are cool. Go across Canal and you get into some much calmer waters with less overpriced bullshit. 

Restaurants are good for sure and a lot of them. Just look around and find one.  Be prepared to wait in line though, nothings quick for the most part. 

I'll come back for sure. It took a few days to get to not hate the dump and to sorta like it.

It's just ok Asian food - just satisfy my short craving. I ordered BBQ wonton soup. It's not terrible and it's not that great either. Service is just average. Nothing fancy. I ordered at the counter and waited at my table until my number was called. The portion is good. They seems to run out of people to clean tables - dishes from previous customers still on the tables for 10 mins or so. 

I personally wouldn't recommend this place unless you are in a craving mode for any mediocre Asian food. Having a low expectation is a key to avoid disappointment.

Bottom Line:  Great place to have a romantic meal

    Since my wife and I had our son, we have only been able to get out once or twice and month for a date night so these events are pretty special to us.
     My wife ordered the New York steak and i ordered the kind of sampler plate for the fish.  
     The steak plate was a nice generous size, cooked as close to medium rare and one would expect and it was a pretty good steak and I would hope so for what they charge.
     The sampler plate was similar in that all of the things on the plate were well prepared and tasted good but for that price, I would certainly hope so.
  
     There was a little hang up because we used an Access card which clearly states that you can get one 50% off meal here but when we tried to use it they told us its not applicable on Fridays and weekends.  There is nothing on the site or the app that says anything about that.
     So instead they comped our drinks which brought the price down to what it would have been with the discount.  This was a classy work-around from the staff and we were satisfied for sure.

  All in all we liked this place.  The service was good, the food was pretty good and it was very quiet and romantic for our date night.  We will be back!
As a realtor, I work with a lot of people in the industry. These guys are OVER priced and I do not recommend them. Do your research.

It was an off Saturday - meaning the Cardinals were not in town - so it wasn't very busy.  Arriving around five, there were not any people there.  Maybe there were 6 total people in the place when we arrived.  There were six people working, and I'm using that term very loosely. Now, you'd think that with six people "working" you'd be able to get drinks, some refills, and  your check.  No.  No you'd be wrong.  

Front of house here is a disaster.  Nobody seemed to understand what their role was, and it was hard to get a drink. 

And then we ordered food. 

We got some pickles and a little nosh and what came out was stale and tasteless.  There were fruit flies everywhere.  Our intention was to stay for dinner, we quickly surmised that if the apps were this bad, what would follow wouldn't be an improvement.  It took us forever to get our check as everyone scurried behind the bar trying to look busy without doing anything.  

Three stars only because the beer selection is good.

Had a long work lunch here and it's a solid choice for a group midday meal. I dislike coming here at night because it's so dark and hard to maneuver around but during the day, the natural light really helps.

We ordered the bacon grease popcorn as an appetizer and it was delicious. I wish I knew how they seasoned every kernel of popcorn so evenly! The plate came away orange from the bacon grease but just shut your eyes and keep shoving popcorn in your mouth.

I had the roast beef poboy with debris gravy and it was exactly what I needed on that chilly day. Definitely a knife-and-fork sandwich. It was layers of roast beef with a spread of mayo, tomato, and the lightest sprinkling of shredded lettuce. I decided to go with onion rings for my side but didn't realize that their onion rings aren't your typical ones that come frozen in giant boxes. It's more of a flaky coating on still-crunchy onions. 

Overall, if you're not a beer drinker, you'll still find things you like about Khyber Pass.

New Years Eve, had the 6 oz steak and it was one of the best steaks I have ever had.  It was cooked to perfection and had the best flavor.  It was so good that I could have eaten two of them.  Huge shout out to Rose the owner who recently purchased Cabana and re named it Cabana Taps.  Great music, great food and great location.  You must visit Cabana Taps!  Also, Cabana Taps follows all Covid rules.

It took a lot for me to give this place a not so good review. The wait time was amazing, given the fact they were packed. Once seated, the waitress greeted us and drinks were brought. We were not quite ready to order when she came back, but it did take a little longer than we had hoped for her to come back to get our order, but again, the place was busy so I did not think much on it. The appetizer and salads came out in good time, but the entrees took far too long. When they did come out, one of the orders was incorrect. The waitress admitted she entered the order in wrong, and the manager came over promptly to find out what was wrong, even though the waitress already knew what needed to be corrected. The manager offered us fresh fries and drink refills while we waited for the entree to be remade, which neither came. The refire of the entree took just as long to come out the second time as the first,  which makes me believe the kitchen did not order it on the fly, but put the order back in queue.  The entree was a turkey burger and fries, so it should not have taken as long as it did. I would have thought since the waitress said she messed up the order, that maybe our appetizer would be comped or the entree that was incorrect, but the bill was for full price.

Don't listen to the positive reviews on the Santa Barbara store. I doubt they are real. I bought two tires from Firestone and one developed a slow leak. I asked them to take a look and see what was going on and they said I was out of warranty because it had been four years even though I only had 20K miles on a 40K mile warranty. Worst of all they said if they took the tire off to look at it they could not put it back on so I would have to buy a new tire at a much higher price than the one I originally bought. This is a horrible way to run a business. I took the tire over to Big Brand who had no problem taking it off and seeing if they could fix it. They didn't try to sell me a new tire. Firestone should definitely look at the buisness practices of the people running their Santa Barbara store.

OK, I'm not the type that gives binary reviews, all one- or five-stars. I'm giving this place one star and I'd give it less if it were possible.

My two kids and I decided to try Pollo Feliz out, and boy are we sorry. I got a bad feeling about the place as I scanned the interior while walking along the building to get to the front door. The placed looked dingy, but my kids wanted chicken and I didn't want KFC or Popeye's, and so we went in.

My son ordered the three-piece meal, my daughter the two-piece kid's meal, and I got the chicken salad.

The pieces of chicken that my kids received were very small, and looked like they had been sitting under a heat lamp for a week. My son said they tasted like it as well. The chicken was dry and leathery, without much flavor other than burnt flesh. We went to the condiment bar looking for something to put on the chicken to improve, or at least re-hydrate it, but that was a non-starter. The bar was mostly empty, and where it was missing containers you could see that there wasn't any ice under them, just water. It was nasty looking, as if the stuff had been sitting out all day un-refrigerated, and I didn't want to chance my kids getting food poisoning, so we skipped it.

My salad was the typical taco salad one gets at Taco Bell, and other fast food chains. The shell tasted like it had been fried in year-old oil that hadn't been anywhere near a filter. The chopped chicken looked and had the consistency of jerky. This would have been alright it there had been some flavor to it beyond charcoal. Lastly the beans had zero seasoning.

My daughter needed to use the restroom, but before we could even open the door we were assaulted with a foul stench. I was afraid she'd lose her dinner if she went in, so I decided she could hold it until we got home. 

The whole interior of the place was dingy and dirty; the floors looked like they hadn't been properly mopped this decade, and many of the "clean" tables had flecks of matter stuck to their surfaces.

I dearly wish I had thought to check if there were any El Pollo Loco locations in Tucson, but it didn't occur to me until we got back to our lodgings. It turns out there's a location only 3.5 miles away. 

To sum it up, this place is a dingy hell hole with foul food that I wouldn't purchase to feed my dog. Stay far, far away from this place.

Deee-licious.  They've raised the prices for the all-you-can eat dim sum to $12 per person, but that's okay because I'm pretty sure my friends and I ate half the restaurant today.  (Hey, you bike from West Philly to Chinatown at lunchtime and see how hungry YOU are!)

Kingdom of Vegetarians scoffs at the peons who may confuse its all-you-can eat offerings with some sort of dim sum buffet.  Here, you get the menu and your pick of it for $12.  For a party of four, we literally covered three small tables in food.  I'm pretty sure I eat something new (in addition to my standbys) every time I go.  Speaking of standbys, the sesame chicken has been hit-or-miss lately, but the pork buns are stellar, as always.  The proprietor also offered us their special blend of iced tea with our meal (he must have noticed how exhausted I looked when I arrived) - it was delicious!

The only things holding this place back from five stars are the atmosphere (it's dark and bland inside, with smooth jazz playing on the stereo), and the fact that you have to pay two extra dollars for this experience nowadays.

Just entered the store and said hi to the supposed Client Experience Manager (redhead) and she ignored me. I repeated Good Morning and then she said hi without even looking at me. She also made a client feel dumb at the cashier. The Client Experience Manager should learn how to do Client Experience. Not good.

It is worth a try but after all of the wonderful reviews, I think my expectations were a little too high. With that being said, be aware this is on the pricy side. The food was fresh and well prepared but none of our party found it exceptional. The fried green tomato caprese salad was very good. I had a half rack of lamb which was $40. The chops were more rare than medium as ordered and bled into the sides. The side veg was broccoli and squash, nothing special other than not over cooked which I appreciated and smashed potatoes. The steak pasta was very good, the steak was tender and tasty. The shrimp creole was much spicier than expected but good. The meatloaf was ok, but nothing special.  The service was good and people were friendly. So if you set the right expectations it can be an enjoyable dinner.

Alaska roll it's amazing!!! All rolls are good but The Alaska one becomes my favorite, u guys should try it with eel sauce!!  Great service Tiphani rocks!!! The Prices are really fare!! I love the atmosphere it's really quiet and clean!! I can say it's a real sushi house!!! Finally!! 

Greetings from Sarasota!
The Charleston Shrimp and grits were great!! As soon as we walked in the door the service was amazing. This will be one of my all time breakfast favs

They could be more attentive to stock on the floor -- there are sometimes empty bulk bins and I had to use a large skewer package to reach a jar of sauce that was all the way back. 

Speaking of, each item for the 11.99 chicken special wasn't nearby and I had to go across the store for each item in the bundle. When I asked a floor attendant about missing sides, he seemed puzzled and the person he referred me to was only slightly more knowledgeable. Based off of other stores (and the cashier's reaction) I don't think I even got the right "sides" for the deal, but different stores/policies maybe? 

Asked another question another day, got a puzzled look and another redirect. A bit awkward again but oh well. 

The cashiers are on point - always knowledgeable and friendly to talk to. Their produce is pretty much fresh all the time too, which is especially nice when paired with their ads. Manager's special meats are hidden gems.
this place is not clean go to pima county health dept restaurant rating web site and see your self. bad service. go some place else

We ordered off the late-night menu for delivery after a long flight. The food came quick but the food was low-quality college food at best. The full menu may be better but the hot dogs and wings were not worth the price at all. We order 3 hot dogs, 2 orders of wings, and one order of french fries for $70. The wings were oily and lacked flavor and the hot dog was dry. The fries came with an unknown brown dipping sauce that looked like it had been left out (and something Gordon Ramsey would not have been happy about). Sweet potato fries were fine but we wouldn't come back.

Food is wonderful and the staff as friendly and welcoming as they always have been (former Lee Garden buffet but same owners and staff) They still have good Chinese items on the menu but the Vietnamese food is great! Love the vegetarian Bahn Mi and tofu spring rolls. Had the vegetable soup with egg noodles last visit and wow! The broth was so good, not over salted, great flavor. My husbands favorite is the grilled chicken noodle bowl. And the avocado smoothie with boba is the best I have had, not too sweet. My In-laws had been coming for the buffet for so many years, they are happy to still have their favorites on the new menu, but we are getting them to try Vietnamese food too! ( no idea why this is posting as location California, we live in Tampa)

Really great way to beat the oppressive heat for a couple of hours. There's a lot of memorabilia and the layout is done nicely. They rely on a little too much video. It would have been great to have a live guide, but there's a lot of interesting information regardless.

I'm here once a month or so because my kids (3 and 5) love to make their own pizza.

For what it is (a chain pizzeria), it's not too bad.  I tend to not get pizza,, rather they have a decent Chicken Milanese and OK wings (though a bit pricey for 9 wings).

Service is usually OK - ask for Joyce.  She is great with kids.  And again - keep in mind - I avoid the pizza for my wife and I.  Go to a real pizza place for that.

Great pizza right in downtown Franklin.  A great deal at $8.95 for unlimited toppings.  We had a gluten free as well which was really good.  The crust is really crisp and the pizza cooks really fast.  We will be back for sure.  Check this place out!

Overall, I had an okay experience. That's it, nothing more and nothing less and it's not because I am being petty but because I am honest. Let's begin!

I booked a microdermabrasion facial and a 2 hour massage because it was nearby where I lived and the prices were great. I had booked an appointment for Saturday at 9:00am so when I showed up, I was told it was at 10:00am. Miscommunication much? I know for a fact it was set at 9:00am, FACT. So of course I was already irritated and went home until my appointment. There is no call/text/email reminder when your appointment is so you can see where the communication went. Something they can definitely upgrade to.

Facial: 1/5
I had my facial appointment with Candi and she was a wonderful lady, however, when it came to the facial, I knew almost instantly that I either have someone who is new or someone who definitely needed more training. She was a nice and kind lady but her service was definitely not up to par with the other spa facials or microdermabrasions that I've had. Why, you may ask? As the specialist, as you are steaming someone's face, while you wait for it to finish, the spa specialist either gives you a hand massage or a scalp massage but all she did was sit and wait so you can imagine the awkwardness that happened in between. You can just tell when someone knows what they're doing and when someone is doing the bare minimum. This was a bare minimum visit.

Massage: 4/5
As for my two hour massage with Kevin, he was wonderful and spent every minute of the two hours with me if not more. He used different techniques and said they were Hawaii massage techniques which is why that massage was definitely different. I'm an athlete and work out junkie so he started out with a few stretches for me to do and mind you, they were very different and some uncomfortable but when I left, my body felt so good. He spent the extra time focusing on the areas that I wanted him to and he was just incredible. I definitely enjoyed it and he used just the right pressure. Definitely would be the only reason I return because everything else is bleh.

Result: 2.5/5 Stars

Drive thru wait is way too long even when i was the only car waiting. I thought maybe the wait is worth it.... What a disappointment the burger was cold, lettuce and tomatoes were wilted. Old fries but at least they were warm. One star for the cold sweet tea.

I have not been back to eat in the restaurant but I have to update my review based on my less than stellar delivery I have experienced lately with this restaurant. With so many sushi places in Philly, I do not recommend ordering from this one.

First off, be careful if ordering through their website. The prices are not up to date. Despite ordering and paying through the website, when I received my deliver the receipt was for almost $8 more. Just an FYI, they have raised their prices. 

Now to the sushi, it has been very blah the last few times I have ordered. To the point I was unable decipher what was salmon vs tuna. Seemed to be poor quality fish. The rolls fall apart very easily and are very small for the price.

Locale in pieno stile delle paninoteche del posto, maxi schermi, grande bancone x le bibite, noi ci siamo andati per pranzo ed era praticamente vuoto, meglio andarci di sera ... Fanno spesso musica dal vivo. Per i panini, nulla di che, la Pigna colada invece era davvero buona.
Great sushi, BYOB, very intimate atmosphere.  Make sure you make reservations for a busy night

Took my family to Circles for a family Bday celebration. We were greeted warmly for our reservation and seated immediately. Tonya our waitress was a rockstar as she was  great. Everyone ordered different entrees and the entire group loved everything. The seafood is fresh and local so that is highly recommended. We will be back.
Best cioppino I have ever had, service was great, go early as it fills up fast.

I had the catfish po boy and my friend had the blackened chicken po' boy. Both of which were delicious. Loved every bit of it. Add a hurricane and what more could you ask for. Just wish I had room for dessert.

Simple barber shop with a nice earthy decor. 

The barbers and patrons of the shop were pleasant and easy to get a long with. 

Haircut styles seems limited. Everyone had either Dillinger based hairstyle or one of long top shaved sides under cut deal. 

My haircut was fine it was simple and quick. 

Although there are some price discrepancies. I was told by someone who has been a regular there for quite some time at he pays $20 a cut. 

Online it says $22. 

And at the locations it's $25. 

It's not a huge deal but consistency is nice. 

Other than being ridiculed in the parking lot near the barber shop. All was fine.

I came to this Philly brunch institution for the first time last weekend.  It was packed when we arrived around 11:30, but the wait was no more than five minutes (despite being given an estimate of 20).  I had the buffalo chicken "cobb" salad, which was delicious.  I had the small size, which was reminiscent of salads at cheesecake factory, so the large must be seriously insane.  Despite being called a cobb salad, this version had no bacon or egg, subbing about 1/4 of a well-ripened avocado, black beans, and tortilla strips.  Nevertheless, it was delicious and very filling.  I could have done without the tortilla strips, which seemed a little stale, but I am not a fan of bready things on salad in general.  

Service was good, especially considering how busy it was.  Our water glasses were constantly filled to the max.  We came with a baby, who received her own glass of water with no ice in a lidded cup with a straw, which she was much to little to drink out of, but was a nice touch nonetheless.  This place is BYOB (and offers carafes of the usual juice offerings at a reasonable price).  Altogether, four stars, would definitely come again.

My suggestion after living here more than a year, RUN! Run and never look back. I've had mold problems since week 1 and they have yet to do something about it. Also, this is the kind of place you'll be looking forward to living at: when I used my free carpet cleaning for renewing, I was told by the carpet cleaner that I had done a pretty good job considering the previous tenants. I asked him what he meant and he let me know that he was told to come and clean the carpet after the previous tenants left and apparently they had to clean it 4 times after having to tell the manager to change the carpets bc they could not remove feces. Yes, you read that correctly FECES. When I was moving in,  they knew I was pregnant and the manager had to be told to change the carpet (which apparently wasn't happy about it). Also, when I moved in everything was dirty and I still am trying to work towards cleaning it up. It is so disgusting though....yuck! If you have kids, also know that there are ants everywhere. And I mean EVERYWHERE. I'm not a dirty person at all and I even have my carpets cleaned at least twice a year and I still have this problem....I have a little girl so please just try to imagine what kind of horror I am living in trying to give my daughter a clean home...but I can't. Bc I have mold, bugs and previous disgusting tenants. Just look at the pictures I have posted of the mold. It has been over a year and they have only done something about it once. I just about had it that I decided to write this review and also, I have now asked neighbors to please write me letters of how disgusting the previous tenants were in case I have to leave and break my lease due to the mold and all this nastiness. Don't come here. Seriously, this is a horrible place.

BEFORE YOU ORDER, READ THE REVIEWS !!!
The worst food I've ever ordered. It was so gross/ unpresentable that it couldn't even be eaten. It amazes me this place is still in business and can operate serving the disgusting food they serve. You guys suck!!!!!

This review will address only the quality of the food, as the service and cleanliness of the restaurant were perfectly fine.

The dearth of quality Chinese restaurants in New Orleans always has me seeking new places.  After reading the reviews for Dragon King, I was eager to check it out.

The hot-and-sour soup was good, but the steamed dumplings were only so-so.  Though the large size of the dumplings was certainly a plus, there was little in the stuffing mixture to balance the overwhelmingly meaty, greasy pork flavor.  Yes, too much umami can be a bad thing.  Perhaps a light, ginger or citrus-infused dipping sauce would offset the intensity of the dumplings.  Sadly, the sauce was little more than soy sauce with a few green onions thrown in (and perhaps a little sesame oil).

The fried rice that was served with our plates was very tasty, with perfectly seasoned and cooked bits of pork boosting the flavor (the egg rolls were pretty decent too).  The centerpieces of our plates were, however, lackluster.  I had the General Tso's chicken with white meat.  The sauce had a good flavor (ask for extra spicy) and the breading was not excessive, but the chicken was horribly overcooked.  Perhaps I deserve leathery chicken for asking for white meat at a Chinese restaurant, but it's basic culinary knowledge that white meat cooks more quickly than dark.

My SO had the chicken with garlic and broccoli.  Like the General Tso's, the accompanying sauce and vegetables were good.  Unfortunately, the chicken was less than spectacular.  The ultra-thin slivers of chicken tasted more of fat and connective tissue than meat, resulting in a gooey texture that was slightly off-putting.  Perhaps the chicken was sliced the wrong way?

 My feeling is that Dragon King is similar to every other Chinese restaurant in the city in that it offers a small handful of great-tasting items hidden within a vast menu of mediocrity.  The food is inexpensive and the portions generous (standard for Chinese restaurants of this kind), but nothing about it sets it apart from the competition.  I will likely give Dragon King another try, but for now, my quest for excellence in the New Orleans Chinese scene sends me elsewhere.

I lowered my rating from 5 to 3 stars. The smallest filet is now 8 oz's instead of 6. Also, they ADD a 12% tip on the bill without telling you which is just wrong. The grass fed steaks are just not tender. For the money, it's just not worth going.

UPDATE:  after hearing from Berns, the 12% EXTRA is that customers must PAY the salary that Berns won't pay the employees and then you add a tip. So, if that's how it works you are expected to pay 30% I guess. Also, they will cut your filet to whatever oz you want, they just don't tell you. 
For those that don't mind all of this, great!!  I'm out. Redlands Grill is better in my opinion ‍.
Love this place! They have really good rotating taps. I love the Korean Chicken and their burgers Tuesday is burger night. Get the Hawaiian if you like pineapple!

My experience with Dr. Herrema has been nothing short of incredible. He did a hip replacement on my right hip in June 2018 and I was immediately walking without much pain. In fact, I was able to get off pain meds within 24 hours. I was pain free after 5 years of extreme discomfort. 

Rehab was fast. PT at home and a quick recovery. I was even driving in 13 days.

Well, I knew I needed my left hip done as well and same thing, super fast recovery and back to work in under 2 weeks.

Wow. All I can say is this man is gifted, very caring , and just brilliant. I have referred a friend who also got phenomenal results and is extremely happy.

I cannot say enough good things about Dr. Herrema and his staff. They took such good care of me.
Loved it. Green flat enchilada and fruit salad are the way to go.

I was completely and utterly dissatisfied. Our waiter was amazing but the food was terrible. We waited for over an hour for our food. The majority of the steaks were under cooked. We asked if we could talk with the chef and he was incredibly disrespectful. I will never be back here, and highly recommend you don't go to this restaurant.

I grew up in Reno and both my sister and I had "Lammies" from Motor Sheep in our cribs when we were babies! When I had kids, guess what we bought for them and from where... Good service, knowledgable staff, and great products. I live in Vegas now, but when my mother-in-law wanted seat covers a couple years ago we took her car on one of our trips to Reno to visit family and had them installed by Motor Sheep. Highly recommended.

It's good food but it's odd. Huge plates of food but 50% of the plate is lettuce and jicama and tomatoes. Kinda weird.

The Mole was way too sweet.

The sample platter was pretty good. All good clean food. I don't think this place is quite as deserving as its reviews.

Not to mention the price. It's very pricey for what it is, which is fancy Mexican food.

Also Margarita was good but $15 and small!!
Incredibly small blackened chicken sandwich that was mediocre. Plug added for scale. Go elsewhere

Be very careful when you shop this store in the near department. When they run a manager's special you digital coupon does not work on this. The price for ground beef today for fresh is 1.99 per pound. The price for the managers special is 2.25 a pound for meat grounded days before. If noticed a couple of times with their pricing. Be very careful what they ring up.

This place should not be patronized whatsoever! Waited about 10 minutes for a table while the staff didn't acknowledge us whatsoever just continued to wipe tables down and ignore us. Finally the two female workers bickered back and forth about where they would seat us with an attitude after they heard me say something about seating. If you don't want to do your job don't got to work. The one finally comes over doesn't say hi, just says "3" and sits in a corner outside. Never asked what our preference was. I don't like to bring race into the mix but we were non Caucasian and they were and it was very clear they didn't want to serve us. We left needless to say.

I went for lunch with three friends, lowest price bowl of noodles or rice, was $10. The strips of pork that I got in my bowl of rice was about 75% fat and the pork that I pulled off, had very little flavor. 

The grunge music was so loud had to scream to talk at the very small table, with wood stump stools.  Since the table was so small, drink glasses were very small, or maybe just to up their profit.

The servers screamed something every time new customer walked in, because of loud music, could not understand what they screamed.

Parking lot was very small.  Had to park on the street. 

I guess you could say I wont be going back.  

Was very curious why the bartender wore black latex gloves.  I don't think it was to keep her germs off anything, since I saw her push her hair behind her ears 3 times while waiting to be seated.

They have tons of colors to
Choose from and are very affordable (for the most part) Its $28 for a full set of acrylics with regular polish. I picked out my nail color and asked the lady to paint them in regular polish because I simply can't afford the fancy gel polish. She obviously didn't care what I wanted and gave me gel polish anyways without telling me she was. Second, after asking me the shape I want and the length and doing it she then tells me that she's charging me for shape and length. When obviously it's to late to change my mind because she already shaped and cut. If she would have told me before it wouldn't have been a problem. So instead of paying $28 for acrylics WITH REGULAR POLISH. I payed $50 for gel acrylics. If you want to get your nails done here they charge $5 for shape and $5 for length, thought you should know before they surprise you. My nails are cute but not $50 worth cute.

I called for a Togo order and just wanted one crab Rangoon that is $5 worth and then asked how many pieces that comes with it and the girl straight up said No. I'm like oh okay, there is so no good customer service in here~ the cream cheese inside was very sweet cheesy and has an off color~ definitely not made fresh everyday. Their sweet and sour sauce looks very face and artificially made, made my stomach hurt so I stopped eating~
Looking for a good drink? Stop by and see Eddie the bartender - his drinks are kickass!!

Chris and his team always give you a good job at a fair price
The work is done rite the first time
I would recommend him to anyone

I visited the market this week. The place is huge with a variety of items. ATM was down and transactions were cash only. The produce looked wilted. The meat in the freezer had freezer burn. 

The HEALTH INSPECTOR must pay them a visit ASAP!!!!! They bake all the Arab bread and sweets but no one wears gloves and they work on an open surface which does not look sanitized! My bubble was burst when I walked in & was excited about seeing unique breads my mom used to make & my eye caught the gentleman making it including the surface area! 

This place is off my list!

This place isn't bad, but it's a bit pricey for salad and self-serve beverages. The restaurant is clean and most people seem to use it for take out, at least at night. It's not spectacular, but there's nothing particularly bad about it.

The ingredients are fresh and it's in a convenient location. It's usually easy to get a parking spot right in front of the store.

We really enjoyed our stay here. We had a King suite close to the elevator. King bed and sofa sleeper. The COVID restrictions were all posted, and it was easy to social distance in the lobby/eat in area because it is very spacious.
Breakfast was provided for us, two options for a warm breakfast sandwich - you could ask for it to be prepared, or take it to your room and prepare (all suites have a kitchenette with a microwave, fridge and dishwasher) along with cereal, yogurt options, fruit and bagels/cream cheese. Various juice options and milk / coffee were also available.

Pool was open and in use, with a reservation.

The suite had nice amenities - Neutrogena products in the bathroom (shampoo pump and body wash pump in the shower) coffee maker and dishes in the room was a nice plus. 

Check in was fast and friendly - easy parking. We would stay here again!

A friend said Friendly's has the best fried food so of course I had to try some. Outside the restaurant it smelled great so that already drew me in. It's set in the cute-as-a-button Zionsville downtown so once you walk in, it's a hole in the wall tavern type look that seems a bit out of place but it was packed so it definitely belonged. I decided to choose my fried food wisely so I ordered fried mushrooms; I love fried mushrooms but so often they're bland and poorly done so I decided to test taste her fried food comment with those. It was an excellent choice, they were the best I'd had in a long time. I'd go back just for those to be honest.
Wow! Great people. Great service. Great donuts. Can't get enough.

Good spot for music and tequila! It's off broadway so it's less crazy, which means you can breathe and enjoy your time. They typically have good live music inside. Happy hour is a plus, the margaritas are good. Plenty of tequila to choose from.. food is pretty good!

Came here because everyone raving about food. Limited menu : chalk board in stairwell. This is literally a hole in the wall almost under the stairwell. You place your order at a tiny window and wait.  Actually some of the seating is under the stairwell.  There's a counter facing the walll, one facing the window and one outside facing in the window.

I opted for the lamb wrap and water.  Choice of lamb, chicken or falafel wraps with veggies and choice of sauce. I chose a mild sauce.  Make a few specialty teas... see blackboard photo.  We also tried fried chick peas...interesting.

The lamb wrap ($10) was huge and delicious.  I would definitely get this again... only I'd get on days when I want to take it home or to the park which is just a block away.  We ate at bench facing the window, and it was okay..imagine it would be hot or cold, depending on the weather...today the door was open and it was a great day.

Definitely should give it a try.

Stone crabs are good
Oysters good

Don't waste your money on cedar key clams
So tiny I had to use my glasses to find them
Lol

A waste at $11 fir six
Robbery

Really good food, very very fair prices and our waitress was extremely helpful and friendly. This is a great spot to go to if you have a group of people who want to eat different things. They have great gyros, pizza, paninis, burgers you name it they probably have it. Also, they have a good amount of seating out side. Overall, cute neighborhood place to grab some grub.   Only reason I didn't give this place 5 stars is because parking was a little bit of a hassle (street parking) and the desert was OKAY.

Ate there a few weeks ago with the girlfriend and our meal was excellent.

We got spinach cakes and fried green tomatoes as starters. Both were very fantastic. I've never had spinach cakes before, but we were both fans after tasting these.

She got the philly cheesesteak and mac and cheese. I got the shrimp and grits. Her meal was excellent (I stole a few bites). Mine was very good, as good as any shrimp and grits I've ever had in Tennessee. My only complaint, is that they should be careful as to the juices from the sauteed veggies on top of the grits, as they became a little too loose. Flavors were spot on.

Beer selection looked decent and the menu is pretty diverse.

I'd give this 5 stars but I just didn't feel it was quite there. I wish I could give 4 1/2.

Maybe on a return visit I can bump it up.
Ordered meatloaf. Was ice cold! What Bullsh_t! Why? Sent it back, and they microwaved the heck out of it! How tasty.. NOT!

Dr. Sanders prescribed my 11 year old brother codeine cough syrup (the FDA banned it for kids). He's been out of school for two weeks and he didn't even have to be. Dr. Sanders was frankly awful. He was uninterested and didn't even examine my little brother. Both my mother and I are totally unimpressed. Nancy Favata is absolutely fabulous, and so is every other doctor besides Sanders. However, if you're planning on taking your child in I wouldn't. A pediatrician is a better idea.
