We came for brunch on our last day in NOLA. Three of us had bottomless Bloody Mary's (peppery and perfect). The Herb Roasted Duck Bowl was amazingly good! The Eggs Apolline was good, but the Duck Bowl was better. We also started with the orgasmic Cinnamon Bacon! This was most def worth the trip from The Quarter. We'll be back!

We were super excited to find an Italian Deli near Indian Rocks Beach, this review COULD have been so much higher but when we ordered our sub we asked for a roast beef, extra red roasted peppers, extra fresh mozzarella and Mayo. The young man taking our order told that because we wanted a crunchy bread that sandwich would be $14 we were like fine. We also requested that part of the sandwich have just a little mayo and ham for our child. He said he would make 3/4 with roast beef and 1/4 with ham with very little mayo, said perfect. He asked if we wanted tomatoes and lettuce we said no thank you.  He hands me the sandwich with lettuce and tomato, so we said we asked no tomatoes no lettuce- he started to reply that wasn't the case. We explained one of us is allergic to tomatoes could he please remake the sandwich. He did but this why I am giving this deli 1 star if I could I would lower it!!! He obviously just wiped the lettuce and tomatoes off the sub. When someone is allergic to something they can't ingest it. So now we are on the beach and I paid over $21.47 for a sandwich that has been fingered to get the items off it and given back to us. The ham has a ton of mayo not light mayo so now I need to find a new meal to order from since we can't take the risk of an allergic reaction. This was very disappointing! I know we are specific on what we want but there are reasons why we are so specific. This sandwich is not a difficult one to make especially for an Italian Deli! Note to management / Owner you need to train your deli staff to take allergies more seriously at this place.

I used Rockstar over President's Day Weekend 2012 to go Wine Tasting in Santa Barbara and our group had an absolute blast! Our driver Alton (or as we called him Al) not only got to our destination early but was willing to wait for us to finish our breakfast before we left! 

Maybe they did not have top of the line COMPLIMENTARY champagne ( yes it was Andre like another pointed out but come on... It is complimentary and free! If you don't like it, don't drink it!), maybe the cheese was not gourmet (once again... ITS FREE!!! Get over it), and maybe the yogurt covered raisins were from Trader Joes (ONCE AGAIN... FREE!!!!).

However, the service was amazing and that is what you are paying for in a LIMO Service... Not what they offer as food and drinks! Bring your own top shelf beverages and gourmet cheese if you can't handle free stuff INSIDE the SERVICE you are paying for.

Al was very knowledgeable about the wineries we should go to and managed to even squeeze in four in 5 hours!

"Al" was a Rockstar and so was this LIMO SERVICE!

I brought my Nissan Versa in May 2015. I went to the dealership to just look at cars and see what they had. However, the car sales man was very pushy and told me I didn't qualify for the type of car I wanted. I should have left then. It is partly my fault I went alone as a woman who didn't know what she really wanted. I was not prepared. 
I got horrible financing though the dealership and I am stuck with a car I never wanted. 
Like seriously Nissan who makes a car with no automated windows or locks, but has blue tooth and aux? 

I'm stuck with the car, fine. However, I took the car to get the free first oil change that they give their customers when you get a car. The car was smooth when I first got it. It made no noises at all. After the oil change I got in December of 2015 and whatever else they did the car, it now makes a gurgling sound. It's so annoying because a new car like that should not being making any type of noise. Someone told me to take it back to the dealership to have them fix it, but honestly they might just make it worst and charge another arm and leg for maintenance. From this experience I know to never go to a dealership alone and have financing beforehand. Plus, the car salesman was just so sketchy and rude. For that alone I would never buy another Nissan or even step foot in a Nissan dealership.

Has nice finds but god so expensive you may as well buy something brand new. but i say if you are looking for maybe one of kind things (not usually clothes, maybe an album, book, furniture maybe yes) but everything else is reallllly expensive--for a thrift store
Expensive.  Unfortunately I did not like my choice of food. Too much sauce on the Lombardi Linguine and too little pasta. Don't go out of your way to come here.

I would given a zero star if I could have. After waiting for the cashier to get off th phone for 5 mins, then paying 44 dollars for 2 people to skate, I had the pleasure of waiting at the skate window for 10 minutes to get skates. My daughter ended up with the wrong size, so we had to wait again for another 5 mins. No exaggeration!!! The customer service was the worst. Use your money elsewhere.

We just ate at the Loft at Traders Point Creamery. What a disappointment. Either the kitchen or the servers could not seem to get our order correct. We ordered appetizers of panzanella, cup of mushroom soup, radish torte and the cheese and meat tray along with a few non-alcoholic drinks. I had initially ordered a strawberry smoothie but was later told they could not find the strawberries, I asked the waiter "did the cooks know that strawberries were red and speckled?" He did not appreciate my question. It then took a while to get the drinks and a little while longer to get sweetener for my alternative drink of iced tea. When the panzanella came it was missing half the ingredients. We then waited a while for the radish torte (which was not that great) and soup. When they finally brought the soup it was not even mushroom. The meat and cheese tray was okay. We ended up sending the panzanella back to get all the ingredients that were supposed to be in the dish and also sent back the soup which was some sort of boiled green soup. We never did get the mushroom soup but the panzanella was finally brought back correct. We were asked if we wanted more bread and we said yes, however no more bread was brought until the table was cleared and we were trying to work out the bill. The initial bill had 2 items we did not get on it...the non-available smoothie and soup. We were told by the waiter that the manager had taken some off our bill but then charged us for 2 items we never had! He finally got our bill fixed and credited us for the panzanella. Worst service ever, so shocking for this establishment. Not impressed by the food either. The manager never came to speak to us and apologize for the errors either. We then went down to the farm store to pick up some cheese etc. and then when paying the bill my husband noted that he was charged for 4 items when he only bought 3. The cashier corrected her error and admitted it was her fault but then also over charged on another item by $2.00 and was willing to give us the $2 back in cash before we noted the 4th unknown item. She ended up getting the manager and voiding the bill and re-ringing it correctly. I am not sure what the problem was today but I will not be going back anytime soon , especially on a Sunday! What a disappointment. I gave this a one because there is no lower rating.

The people here were really nice! Make a few jokes with them and it can really change your experience. 

I shared a cheesesteak with provolone and hot peppers and it was more grease than anyone should be eating before noon. It was definitely greasy goodness. My heart stopped a little, but then i looked up at the wall and realized that Mr. T ate here once and that if Mr. T can do it, so can I! Digressing.

Anyhow, it was tasty. The lines are usually out the door and around the block. So, we came early to avoid the lines. 

Anyhow, good cheese steak.

Glory Days in Palm Harbor, FL is the old Leroy Selmon's location on US19. It's basically a sports bar -- millions of large-screen TVs, a big bar area, and food that pretty much reflects the sports bar genre.

Like Selmon's before it, it rates a so-so -- not terrible, but not a place that offers much that will have you salivating to return.

To be fair, the place has only been open a little while, and it's theme and location means lots and lots of customers, even on a week night. All that traffic so soon after opening means teething problems; spotty service and a slow kitchen among them. We'll try it again in a while to see if they've addressed those problems.

But the experience was not very good from the start. The hostess, who looked like she was in junior high school, didn't have a clue and left us standing in the lobby a lot longer than she should have while she conversed with other employees. Once seated, someone took our drink order and then disappeared for a long time. The food was very slow coming out of the kitchen. When it finally arrived, the food was, well, just okay.

Beth's burger, ordered medium-rare, was well-done. My steak rice bowl, supposedly a signature item, was okay but unremarkable -- a strip of yellow rice with a few small tomato chunks for color, a strip of steak pieces that were too grisly (and very rare rather than the ordered medium-rare), and then a strip of very ordinary salad greens.

Our server was a very young man who did a pretty good job but who acted like a robot. Every word and action seemed to be rehearsed, and there was no way he was capable of spontaneous conversation.

Average-to-below-average food. Average-to-below-average service. Not a formula for success. 

Leroy Selmon's in this location started out great and just slowly declined until it closed. This place is in danger of the same fate if they don't get their stuff together. But we'll give them another shot in a few weeks, and hope things improve.

We were robbed! Where do I begin? I've waited my whole life to have an "Al's" experience. The food was terrible, the pretentious owner/manager was condescending and disrespectful. He came to our table of five and told us it was too much trouble for him to split the check and that we could do it ourselves and " that it better add up to the total of the bill."
As far as the meal... I don't even know where to start.
I've never felt the need to write a review but if I can save anyone from wasting your hard earn money and time, it's worth it!
Go to 801, Stoney River, Ruth's Chris but DON'T go to AL's!!!
CUTE ENVIRONMENT GREAT SERVICE VERY GOOD BURGERS AND MALT SHAKES... this location is better than the Eagle one!

One star might be too many. Just got home from eating there. Had the fried shrimp with mashed potatoes and slaw. The shrimp were good, the slaw was very, very, dry and the so called MP had to come from a box. All of this and a beer and two glasses of wine for two of us and the total bill with tip was $128. Wow...never again. Use to just love the place but man it has gone down hill....

I came here with friends after work because we usually hit up Cheesecake Factory or another chain spot but we wanted something different. I found this place on yelp with almost 5 stars so we tried it out. BOY WAS I PLAYED. The place was empty which was fine but .. weird. We ordered drinks. My friend got a vodka club - she receives it and takes a big sip. It was tequila and club. She takes back to bar and they apologize and he starts to make a drink and is pouring the vodka in and says "say when". 

We ordered nachos - really plain chips with some shredded melted cheese and a huge dollop of sour cream. Not great at all- but they're chips. 

This is where is gets scary. We all ordered the chicken thumbs; my friend takes a bite and says "does this look cooked?" I immediately think she's being dramatic and I take her nuggets to look. I cut in half and oh my god, it's literally raw. Like clear chicken where you can see the lines and it's jelly and slippery like you just pulled it out of your fridge to cook. We instantly lost our appetite and showed the waitress. She says "that's fucking disgusting let me get my manager". She comes back over and says THE MANAGER REFUSES TO COME TO THE TABLE. Now granted we are all store managers of the same company different locations so we are appalled because we never in a million years could imagine not coming to a customer when they had a problem. The waitress looks defeated because he won't come take care of the situation. She says she's so sorry and takes off all of our food and says she again agrees it's absolutely disgusting. Props to the waitress for being an adult and shame on the incompetent "manager" of this business. My review is coming here because my friend has been sick for days. Avoid this place at all costs.

I'm Vietnamese - born in Vietnam and grew up in Houston.  My wife and I were in Nashville for the first time, and she found this Vietnamese restaurant on Yelp.  It had pretty good reviews, so we drove way over to the east side of town by the airport to check it out.  My wife tries to find Vietnamese restaurants when we travel because we both like Vietnamese food, it's usually cheap and it's fun to try different places. We've eaten Vietnamese in Boston, Sacramento, Houston, Oahu, Chicago, Washington DC, Salt Lake City, Little Rock, Westminster, San Jose and Los Angeles.  

All that said, I have no hesitation in saying that this is the worst Vietnamese food I've ever eatn.  I ordered bun bo hue, and I swear the base was canned tomato soup, but more dilute - completely flavorless. The meat was strange - I can't even tell what kind of meat it was.  First time I've ever not even eaten half of my meal at a restaurant.  We also got the spring rolls, which were awful - stuffed with mostly iceberg lettuce without the typical herbs and flavors you usually find in goi cuon and bi cuon.   The grilled squid was good, but don't go there.  Please, just don't do it.  I'm hoping to find better Vietnamese in Nashville when we return.

i fucking love this place. they are even nice to the craft cocktail "cocktail" bitches who came to be bartenders but cearly by their attitude....not so much!!!!!!! best potato salad and customer servious!!!! id love to know where that last commenter works causes apparently shes a bartender..... please tell us.

Cool little spot on Mass Ave with live Jazz music regularly. The decor is unapologetically quirky; the walls are full of memorabilia, memories & fun. Pitstop here for a drink or three before a show at The Old National Center then stop back in after your show for a Chatterbox encore! 

Limited food options; good selection of cocktails, beer, wine. Patio seating available! 

Free entry Sun-Thursday; Music charge Fri-Sat.

The food was great! I enjoyed the Jambalaya, cabbage and potato salad. Both my kids ate it too! 
Good prices, easy ordering, very causal.

Yes, this place is cheap but honestly I don't care how cheap it is but I do not like paying for someone to mess up my hair. The first time I went I came for my friend because she went to school there and she gave me highlights and did great! But then everytime I tried to get my hair done, she wasn't available so o kept going to different people. I have never liked my hair 100% but I never hated it...until last week. I came to get my hair platinum and I had already had light brown hair with blonde highlights. The girl that did my hair was super nice but he turned my hair green! She was freaking out so I remained calm and tried not to be mean about it. They told me to come back the next day for free so they could fix it so I did. the girl that had "fixed" it was a different girl from before and she kept telling me over and over that it will look awesome. At the end result I was not pleased. My hair is not Orange on top and bright yellow all over. I would NOT recommend this place if your looking for a cheap color. They do pretty good on cuts but definitely don't go in excited for your hair to turn out exactly like u want because it will not.

Going to this Dairy Queen is always an adventure. The employees there aren't always the friendliest or hard working. I went there one night and it took almost 10 minutes to get my ice cream when it wasn't even busy. The cashier didn't really know what he was doing and that took another 5 minutes. Since this one is closest to me I decided to go there but making an extra 10 minute trip would be worth it to go to another one.

Truly tasty pies, but mediocre service and higher-than-average prices leave something to be desired.

We ordered a small pizza for delivery. Initially we thought we'd order 2 (since we asked for their smallest size), but after being quoted almost $50, we decided to just do a single pie and go half and half. 

They estimated 40 minutes for delivery, which we thought was a little long considering how close we live but understanding that maybe it was just busy, we waited. After 45 minutes passed, we called to check in and learned that they had accidentally put us in for carry out and estimated another 40 minutes. The pizza finally did arrive and we thought it was actually pretty good. 

They definitely use quality ingredients and their crust is stellar (slightly thicker with a nice chew but crispy on the outside). That said, the lady we dealt with wasn't the most courteous (barring the mixup and the long delivery time) and the prices are comparatively pretty high for what you get. Will likely return as a carry out customer with a coupon...3.5 stars.

Best Breakfast  In Florida!
Only 7 miles from Treasure Island  Beach 
WORTH The Drive !!!

We ate here 2 mornings in a row !
Delicious  
Super Friendly Staff 
Excellent Food Quality 
Farm To Table Wonderful 
They sell Their Own Cook books and Ground Coffee.
Can't Wait til they open one where we live .
Its a buffet.  Don't expect gourmet food.  It is wut it is.  Only fat people eat here.  Literally.

Do not use this company. Our pod was supposed to be delivered on a Thursday in the PM. Called to confirm, they told us it would be there../never showed. They called to say there was a technical issue, and guaranteed 8-12 delivery on Sat. It is now 8:20 pm and no delivery. They can't tell us where the pod is. Driver called me at 12:10 to say an hour-hour and a half...no show. Do NOT deal with them! Have already filed a complaint w BBB and am calling them hourly about this. Zero accountability and zero help. STAY A


Update: the resolution was that we rented a U-Haul, drove to their warehouse in another state and retrieved our belongings from the warehouse. The worker there admitted that they normally have four drivers, but are down to three, and that the company keeps booking appointments that they know can't be kept. This was on a Sunday. On Monday I had to fight with them for nearly an hour, but they did eventually cave and give us our money back. Never again will I use this company.

I decided to try out Amped via my insurance company . The front desk staff is amazing,super sweet and very friendly ,that's about the only thing I Iike about this gym. 

They set me up with a trainer to chat with, which I thought would be someone to show me around the gym and how to use their equipment . Nope, no one did that. What happened was pressure to use one of their personal trainers . I wasn't a fan of the tactics used, trying to guilt me to care more about myself which meant getting a trainer  and to move money around so I can afford it.That tatics reminded me of use car salesman. It kind of felt like a quota needed to be met. Not saying she wasn't nice, she was but her sales tatics were not good. 

I did decide to sign up for 3 months training  because she said I would get work out plans nutritional plan and someone to check in with which I actually needed. I went to my first training session , we did talk about nutrition and goals and set up a week but she said I knew what I should eat(she's right). Next a quick evaluation , lunge set, squats , a plank and standing angles set of push ups.  I didn't leave with any type of workout plan for the next week. Also no measurements so there's no plan and no way to measure success. 

Here's the next problem , at the original meeting I let them know that the in two week I couldn't meet in the morning and I wanted a female trainer. I was given the first available trainer but she's not available at night so now I the next session will be a different trainer and they said she doesn't have much availability. 

A positive is the other trainer was great,  friendly and informative. There was also another trainer that helped me on his off time. When I recieved the training plan, I had no idea how to do half of the exercises,  so I asked if someone could explain them and he took to the free weights and showed me how to do the exercises , which was really nice.

Beyond the front desk, the atmosphere is not for me. So I'll be turning in my written letter to end my personal trainer session once my  3 months is up. Save yourself the trouble, if ypu don't want personal training skip the meeting.

I recently bought a house with soft water and purification systems previously installed by Indy Soft Water.  I had no experience with either system but knew some annual maintenance and filter changes were due so I scheduled an appointment. The technician was very prompt, almost before the scheduled time, and thoroughly walked me thru both systems, how they worked and how they should be maintained. He was also very persistent as it required some extra effort to get the remote to sync up with the purification system. I was very happy with the service.

Don't come here if you're in a hurry! A group of four of us came in for dinner on a Saturday night and ended up waiting for about 45 minutes. We were finally seated in a bar area with lots of TVs on the walls. 

When we finally got seated, the service was quick enough. Our table ordered Homemade Chips and Bacon Dip, a Three Amigo Taco Salad, Claus' Sausage Sampler, and a Chubby Burger. I had the burger and it was very good, using the same bacon dip that comes with the chips. 

I also tried the Alabaster Belgian White Ale, which was nice and light with subtle citrus flavors. 

Overall, I would come here again, but only during a less busy time. While the bacon dip is indeed delicious, I don't know if it's quite worth the wait.

Very nice place, nice and bright. First, they bring out the most wonderful buttery rolls ... To die for. Our server Daniel, was great. Timed our courses just right.

Had the mussels to start then the lobster on lobster with mashed potatoes and green beans - awesome! No room for desert!

We will definitely be back.

Place is small, and reservations is a must because they fill up quick. Price is a little steep. Caesar salad is a small portion for a full price and swimming in dressing. The salad is not chopped. Chicken and biscuits are overpriced, salty, very spicy because of the red sliced peppers sprinkled. The biscuits are not the fluffy ones. It is flat and dry. It took almost an hour for the main course to arrive. It is not handicap accessible, and  only metered parking in front.
A bit overpriced and the ordered stew had less meat than rice. The staff was slow during pick up and in the restaurant.

Journey is a high end buffet wanna be. They're a little cheaper than your usual Todai, Nori Nori, and Korean buffets, but the food is not nearly as good.

For some reason my parents love this place, and whenever I go home to visit, they take me there. Sure, I can tolerate eating there, the food isn't THAT bad, but in my opinion there are so many better places to go to! They have a mix of western and Asian dishes. They have pizza, fries, chicken fingers, cold seafood, fruits, desserts, teriyaki, fried rice, spring rolls.... etc. You can probably find any of your common buffet dishes here. But they're all just OKAY.

The decor is not too bad, the place is spacious, and service is good.

The one thing that I enjoy at Journey's is the melted chocolate fountain where you can dip strawberries in. :]

It was an okay experience, they were picky about donations and used  furniture costs were higher than what I would pay. I couldn't find a salesperson that could help me for an unusually long time. When I did he did not have the authority to negotiate prices. As I said just okay.

This place really hits the spot. There's nothing better than a deliciously home cooked meal, and that's what you'll get here. Unfortunately I got a groupon to try this place out, and normally that would be a great thing as the food was incredible, but I feel kind of bad bc it was THAT good for a decent discount. But, I guess the process of groupon works, because I will be back!! Thanks Chef Dan.
Unsanitary as hell. Watched a busboy repeatedly take untouched bread rolls from customers plates after they left and reuse them with new customers. Sickening. Will never eat here again.

Where do I start? Every once and while my fellow Yelpers and I disagree . For the most part I've found the reviews reliable - so much so that my team has learned to use yelp as a resource and that's how they picked up on the Stockyard for a group dinner

That said, I could not disagree more strongly with the other reviews on this one. The Stockyard is a classic case of a restaurant surviving purely on its historic reputation.  We had a large group - so let me begin at the beginning...

While I expect a high-end steakhouse to be pricey - I also expect to "get what you pay for". The wine list was not a good start. The wines I recognized were priced 4-6X retail. When we have a significant size group I try to target wines in the 50-80$ range. Thats a real challenge at the stockyard. Precious few decent wines in this range. We went with a Malbec that I know sells for about $20 a bottle retail - $80. Also got a white -- which was a bit more reasonably priced - $65 and surprisingly good.

We did the "seafood" appetizer - again, family style due to group size. I was surprised that they didn't cut the fried lobster tails into bite size portions  a bit awkward to share. No real complaints here, but it wasn't superb either. At this point i am still thinking "expensive but average steakhouse". 

But then we got to the main course - and the hustle. Our main waiter launched into a description of the available entrees to the group, including - much to my dismay the description of limited number of American "kobe" filets. In a group this size, I'd want a heads up that he was offering $90 filets BEFORE people start to order. This was followed by an offer to each table (unknown to me) of a 2lb lobster add-on for each. He later claimed he asked first on this - but he did not come to me and i was clearly acting in the capacity of host. Talk about cranking up the bill...

All of this - while disconcerting and annoying - is still somewhat "forgivable" when you get a really outstanding meal and service. Nothing could be further from the truth. 

To a man (or woman) our group was disappointed in the quality and preparation of the entrees - including the kobe which was described as bland and a bit chewy. My porterhouse was undercooked, non-prime beef and clearly not the size stipulated on the menu. I am no steakhouse novice. I know what to expect and this was clearly far below my expectation at a third of the price --  I could have gotten better at Outback. Based on conversation, I was clearly not alone in this opinion. Additionally, a number of people had to wait extended time for entrees while others ate. Lobsters - while large, should have been pre-cracked so inexperienced diners wouldn't struggle so much (and not overcooked).

Overall, our price per person was outrageous, the food average, and the approach to draining our pockets insulting. 

If you want a good meal in Nashville look to the west end near Vanderbilt. Some great places... but Stockyard is the kind of place that makes "overrated"  seem like a compliment.

I went on a busy Sunday Easter morning but I'm glad there was an option to sit at the bar. I ordered a glass of rosé and huevos rancheros and my friend got a mimosa and crawfish and grits. 

I didn't realize my dish came with a runny egg (which I absolutely don't eat) so I had the dish sent back and remade with just an egg white. There was no fuss about this which I appreciated. My friend didn't enjoy the crawfish and grits. Very bland and just not appetizing. 

For the price, we weren't expecting such small plates. While I appreciate the great service, I didn't care for the food. That's until I ordered dessert. 

Chocolate chip cookies, vanilla milk and a strawberry pop tart. All were amazing and helped this rating.

We decided to try Osaka for dinner this past Friday evening. I thought the food was good, not great and the presentation was nice.  We were served our dinner before we had a chance to finish our soup/salad.  Definitely had the feeling of being rushed even though the restaurant was not busy. 
We were seated at a back table in the front room, close to the kitchen.  The wait staff repeatedly slammed the faux leather covered menus down as they were passing from the front room to the back room...a loud popping sound over and over again, it was distracting, annoying and completely unnecessary.  

I would not recommend this restaurant, and more importantly the wait staff needs training on the basic courtesies of customer service.
Food was delicious! Everything you would expect and want from a Jamaican Restaurant. The flavors were authentic and right on point. Awesome ambiance and music which pairs perfectly with your meal!

We came here the other night for our anniversary. When we came in they were expecting us and sat us at a table that we had reserved. It was nice and even better that they allowed us to bring our own bottle of wine. They sat us down and the waitress was great extremely nice and friendly. They got us our drinks quickly and we placed our orders for food as well. When the food arrived it tasted great, flavorful and both plates looked great. The one downfall it had all been sitting for some time waiting for the fries to be done the food was barely warm minus the fries of course. The food tasted great but the one thing wrong is that it wasn't hot. But I understand because we were the only people in the restaurant. Other then the food being cold it was a wonderful experience and we will be going back. Thank you! :)
Don't bother. They turned down our party of seven when we said we only wanted wine. Went across street and started with wine and built up nice bill. Guess a.kitchen does not like to serve.

Came in on thanksgiving morning with my family of five. We were seated quite fast,however That was the only positive side of our experience. Our food took longer than half an hour, granted it was busy but the issue here was that the food was definitely sitting on some heat lamp for all that time because when it came out to us it was cold and stale. The waiters only excuse was that it was busy! He only brought our food out when we had to ask another sever why it was taking so long. The manager Chris was no help at all they need to train him and his staff on customer service! Instead of resolving the issue he continued to argue with us as if we did something wrong. Other tables got their food way quicker than ours even when they ordered 15 to 20 minutes later! I remember him saying that he didn't want to argue with was because it was thanksgiving! However all we wanted us the empathy to make sure it doesn't happen again not only to us but too any other customer of IHOP in downtown Santa Barbara that staff really needs a new training program!! Starting with their incompetent managers!

Let me start out by saying this review is for a delivery order and not in the restaurant. 

I'm usually not a patron of too many chain restaurants but this is one that has historically been good. Portions are huge which is great for leftovers....fresh ingredients....etc. 

I haven't eaten here in a bit but we ordered from them for delivery on Friday night. We live less than 10 minutes away. We both ordered the Steak Diane and Chicken Madeira combo. First, ice cold.  If we lived further away, perhaps i would understand. Portions have drastically shrunk. Not sure if it's the same in the restaurant or this is a delivery thing. Lastly, it wasn't even good. I've had both these meals many times over the years and this was NOTHING like what I've had in the past. 

The saving grace was the dessert. It was 5 stars. 

I'll try eating here again someday but definitely not for delivery.

Love the concept and their menu, but I'm sad to say that the food was somewhat underwhelming. For the price to size and quality of portions, I was a little disappointed. I hope maybe with this being the first year they've had the truck, hopefully they'll soon be working out some kinks. I hope the pulled pork won't be as dry in the future and the flavors come together better for the chicken and brie wrap. Also, the couple times I've had their tots, they've been on the soggy/chewy side, rather than the usual thick crispy crust I'd expect even to get from the cafeteria.

I'm certainly going to give them another go around, and I'm hoping that next season when they're around again, I'll be much more excited about their food.

Really disappointed with this place after spending $48 on a basic mani pedi (No add ons). The lady I had flew through my mani and pedi and I was done at least 15 minutes earlier than the two other people I came with. My nails look grown out and shabby after $48!!! Never going back.

I ventured into this gem in my search for unique places to see art and I was not disappointed. This is fine art in miniature. Miniatures are reality reduced to scale. Someone may use this in design or movies I assume, but these are collections of hobbyists. The time and detail that goes into this is amazing. The details are astonishing, from tiny telephones, to art on the walls, drapery, chandeliers, stairwells to tiny pets and even food. It is memorizing. Here are just a few I could capture well since it is difficult to take a picture of something behind glass. My favorite house was the replica of the museum. One particular  artist strategically places a tiny mouse in all of her creations as a signature. It's fun trying to find it.

My friends and I had so much fun at the bar! The atmosphere was great but the music was SO LOUD. We could barely hear each other speaking and had to repeat ourselves so many times. There were 4 of us and 2 of us had seats. At one point people offered us there seats and we said no because we wouldn't be able to hear each other if we were all sitting. Please lower the music.

Let me be honest. Dr. Kesari did a crown and they helped me do the cleaning. Before taking up service i did mention to them that my colleague did warn me to check the prices as he experience a increase in his liability when the final settlement came from insurance.
Therefore i was a bit careful, mentioned the same to the staff and Dr. Kesari. They assured that they would provide me the correct quote after checking with the insurance company. Only when i was assured the cost that would be borne by me,i agreed to take up the service. There was no intimation to me towards the increase in cost. After four months i get a call from collection department stating my dues towards the clinic.
I feel cheated at this point as the practice by the team at the clinic is do the service and leave the rest onto the mercy of the customer. I feel like a scapegoat so beware of them. 
Today when i called them and was explaining my situation, the staff Krystal went rude and banged the phone. This shows how genuine and true they were.
I am sorry to say that, though the Dr. is very reliable, if the staff would have been true then i would have avoided some unnecessary service that i took which was not urgent.
Visit them only for the doctor. No complains for the treatment, but no praises to the staff who checks the details and getting a higher cost onto me.
The staff puts the blame on the insurance company.

Jamy has been my saving grace! I am so incredibly thankful and lucky to have her as my hair stylist. She brought me back to blonde with a bright bayalage and NO damage!! My hair has never felt or looked better!! Even better, she's the SWEETEST person. I look forward to all my appointments with her so we can chat and catch up on what's going on in our lives! She always makes me feel special! I highly highly recommend her to everyone.

PLEASE READ THE FOOD SAFETY REPORTS on FALLS TAPROOM BEFORE DECIDING TO GO TO THIS ESTABLISHMENT. (http://www.phila.gov/health/environment/FoodSafetyReports.html)

I wish I had before coming here. Had I had known that there were mouse/ feces problems and dangerous food preparation methods, I would not have ordered the pierogies and the sweet potato fries (which were tasty at the time of eating them). After enjoying my food, my boyfriend and I noticed a mouse run past our table. I got up to tell the bartender (who was the only one working that night) that we had seen a mouse, and he proceeded to tell me that there were mice because was there was construction going on next door. I would have believed him, however a kitchen worker then drug a large bag of trash across the floor and out the front door. I was so disgusted that I got my boyfriend and we promptly left after asking for our check. I immediately checked the health & safety reports when I got home and to my horror I had read NUMEROUS things that made my skin crawl, clearly showing that the owner had done nothing to fix anything that was on the report in past years. Only go to Falls Taproom for the beer (hopefully the draught lines aren't contaminated too).

Came here for dinner on a Thursday evening. Pretty quiet, but the city's been pretty quiet anyway as of late. 
Sat outside on the side; awesome view of the Delaware. 

We ended up bringing our own wine, which they immediately popped open for us. (Cool thing about that place, if you call ahead and ask, they don't mind at all. At least from what I experienced. Who knows on another night if that won't be the case.)

Got the Taleggio and Fig flatbread to start. A little lackluster. I love Taleggio cheese, and I guess the promise of the fig lead me to believe that it would be jammy and luscious and figgy-ness everywhere. Not the case. (I'm sure they weren't) but the figs had the same quality as slightly underripe raw figs; it gave more of a texture rather than a flavor to the flatbread. The wilted arugula it was presented on helped when heaped on top to give some sort of flavor. But, on to dinner!
Got the cioppino; so much seafood, so delicious. What threw me was the pasta with the dish. It might be because I'm used to cioppinos coming in just a broth, like a bouillabaisse, but it just didn't need the pasta. Plus it was angel hair. I feel like even Mr. Batali would have an issue with cooking angel hair pasta. Duck confit looked great though, didn't try any, but did sample the poached pear, and it was wine-soaked and awesome. 
Did get the brussel sprouts with CRANBERRIES!? Genius. So so delicious. Not over or under done, but just right; I would have had to fight Goldilocks off, they were so just right. 

No dessert, no time. They were closing up, and who likes to be the last table anywhere  anyway, keeping the servers there all night? Plus service was friendly and great.

If you are looking for traditional Mexican food Carlitos is not your place. Upscale, fancy ,  Americanized,  Mexican food. A burrito is around $16. If I want real Mexican food I go to a Taqueria and there are plenty of great ones here in Santa Barbara but I love Carlitos. Their vegetable tostada is to die for. Warm corn tortillas with a layer of black beans piled high with a fabulous selection of grilled vegetables and  topped with guacamole. This is served  with a spectacular dressing. Large enough for two people. They have really great ceviche, maybe the best ceviche in Santa Barbara. My largest complaint with ceviche is the over salting of it which is something they don't do. Nice outdoor patio with plenty of shade across from the Arlington Theater. A great place to sit on a Sunday afternoon and they are dog friendly as long as you sit on the outdoor patio. I have eaten their burritos , Ceviche, guacamole, and  roasted vegetable tostada. So far I have loved everything I've eaten there. Service is good and people are friendly. Don't go there if you want traditional Mexican food. A great date place although a bit pricey but the portions are good. All in all I give it 4 stars.

Place was recommended by my hotel but I will not go back and I travel to Nashville frequently. Did a good job, but the guy who did my nails spoke hardly any English, the manicure table was filthy, tech told another employee he couldn't wait to get home while giving me a pedicure, and then didn't even say thank you when I gave him a 20% tip.

Saturday night visit near the hotel we were staying at. Definitely a mistake. Food was poor quality. I ordered a roast beef sandwich. The beef was extremely dry and bordered on the inedible. My husband's medium-well burger had a red center. 
They are very disorganized.
I gave it an extra star for location.
Restaurant closed several weeks ago. Used to be  a good place.  Hope  someone  else will  take  over  the  location. Farewell  to  Mimmo.

Beware. Wish I would have read this first.   Guy quotes 65 dollars to come out.  He spent literally three minutes putting a stick in the drain and that's it. So he charges an additional twenty bucks "labor".  Just be honest up front and I would have no issue.  Never will be using them again.   There are plenty of honest people out there.

This is a cute place and prefect to bring the family.  I read the reviews about the service and I think if the owners trained the waitstaff a bit, they would see a huge improvement.  To me, it seemed as though the waitstaff is very inexperienced.  That's fixable.

I've been here twice and although our servers both times were inexperienced they still got the job done.  

The food is very good.  I have no complaints there.  

As long as we don't have any major problems with the waitstaff we will continue to go back.

I would give it more stars if it wasn't so damned difficult to flag down staff. It was packed for lunch on a Sunday. The place is new so the floors and furniture didn't stick to you (like the other Pho places). Our party of 6 including a 5 yr old ordered Pho, Bun, and Chicken Fried Rice (no kids menu here). I had the Pho #25. Digging in, I knew I would have trouble with the noodles. It's those noodles that are too long for one slurp. The soup looked perfect but heavy on MSG.  The meats and tripe in the dish was delish and tender (MSG helped). The neat thing is Cafe Banh Mi is attached so there were some cool authentic Vietnamese desserts to end the meal. The banana leaf yellow bean wrap was spot on. I had wanted to get some Banh Mi to go but the lack of a menu board discouraged me. I also didn't want to flag down staff again. It was an experience trying to get the waiters' attention maybe I will return in the summer.

Absolutely l, a GREAT place to stay.  Service is great.  Morning buffet in the hotels restaurant, Restaurant 211, was fantastic.  Clean. Service was excellent.  This is where I will stay if I have to spend time in Tampa again.

A decent sports bar located in a hotel area right off the highway and near several regional sports facilities. They are used to accommodating groups, so this makes it a good option for your teams to gather between or after tournament games. Our team stopped in for a late dinner after a day on the soccer fields. The restaurant put up a good energy-building pasta buffet to help prepare us for they next day. The facility is spacious, with a bright atmosphere and plenty of view screens to watch your favourite sports. They have a few arcade games and such for the kids to spend their time and bond together on. Service was quick and friendly. Food was decent and fresh. Recommended if you are in the area to catch a game on television or wind down after an event.

Congratulations Fox & Hound you just made my shit list. I came in to watch the game last night and only stayed long enough to finish my drink. The place was kind of empty but the tables were dirty. Ordered a drink.... went to take a piss and as soon as I entered the restroom I got hit with the nastiest stench I've ever smelled in an American restroom. It smelled like a sewer in that bitch. It was at that moment that I knew there was no way in hell I could eat in this establishment.  On the plus side the waitress was really nice so I'll give them two stars. We finished the drinks and went across the street to watch the game.

Turned away after seeing they were OPEN ON CHRISTMAS ... because we had small children. Other people were getting in without reservation but we were given the 'need to have a reservation' excuse. Even with mutiple open tables. Horrible customer service. Not family friendly at all, unfortunately.

I've had sushi all over St. Louis and Tokyo Sushi is definitely my favorite so far.  It just so happens to be closest to my house which is a nice bonus. The staff is consistently courteous and efficient.  (We have eaten there and taken it home several times)

All of the seafood is always very fresh.  I have tried most of their rolls and the Las Vegas is the BEST!

I haven't had such bad food since I was in college and short on cash. First and foremost the delivery was an hour later than expected.
The Quesadillas de Camaron y Jaiva read stale quesadillas smothered with cheese, scattered with some prawns and no hint of crabmeat.
And then for the Burrito de Chuleta Ahumada; it left a bad taste in my husband's mouth. It was the first time I have seen my husband not finish his food let alone not eating even half of it.
All I can say is I'm glad we didn't order any dessert!

I have been coming here for over 18 years bring in my previous and current shelty service dogs. In fact Obe is there now. I have written only two of these comments for anyone, this being the second. 
I will not go anywhere else for service. My dog comes back happy, well groomed and clean. If you know anything about shelty coats they can be a handful. 
The de-shedding bath is great especially for someone who is also allergic to dogs.

Simply put, the customer service here is great.  I called once to see if they had the latest issue of The Walking Dead (any a you comic nerds out there need to check it out if you haven't already) and they had one copy left which he automatically put on hold for me, before I could even ask.    I suppose that's not too extraordinary, but it got my attention.  It's a great place for graphic novels, and current issues..  but as far as I could tell, not so great for back-issues.  They've also got figurines, toys, RPG stuff and used comics for those of us penny-pinchers.

Good taste of food. Twas disappointing when were there yesterday for dinner. First, I ordered diet coke and I repeatedly said it to our server so she could understand it clearly but she gave me lemonade same thing happen during our first visit in this location. I mean what's in your lemonade that you kept serving it to customer/s?! Doesn't make any sense. Second, my partner ordered sushi with mayonnaise on the side, again, we repeatedly said it to our server couple of times and they served us sushi with mayonnaise on it and not on the side. And when we called our server yet she offered to replace it but it seems that they just took off the top part of sushi with mayonnaise on it and served us the same. We might not going back to this location again. It was unfortunate because they have decent taste of food.

The spätzle and cucumber salad were fantastic. My friend said his golosch was good, but my mushroom stew was meh. You shouldn't be using canned mushrooms in a dish where mushrooms are the star (or ever).

Salmon was delicious but baby back ribs weren't even baby backs. ... if u know what I mean then you've had below moderate BBQ. Oh well. This was their catering so maybe their restaurant is better I hope it is but I won't give it another try.
Too pricey for its taste.  Too overrated, there's lots of more authentic tasting Filipino Restaurants to go to. Do only dish that I like is their Chicken Sisig.

Careful. The dancers, the show, the band...great. The establishment itself, no way. We had to wait in the alley on a Friday night. We got there at 9:30 or so,  and didn't mind waiting. The door man outside was very pleasant, very interactive with the people in line. Good guy overall. We didn't think we would actually get in, but the door man (and forgive me for not getting his name...because he was solid and good at his job), actually got us in after a relatively short wait (30 mins). We entered, and as straight forward as I can word this, never encountered an employee that was pleasant, nice, inviting, helpful after that. The first employee we met was Dave ( I would assume he was the manager). He and his cravat were about as unfriendly and as uninviting that an employee can be. If I were the owner, I would have serious issues with this man in my business, let alone running my business. We were lucky enough to get a spot along the wall for the burlesque show. I am told these are the premier spots. The problem is, there are no cocktail servers for the people along the wall. We (my party of 8) asked several servers as they passed by if we could order a drink. Each one told us no. So, we had to walk to the bar to get a drink. Two problems with that. At the bar, the crowd was three to four deep. Only three bartenders for 100-200 people or so. Second problem, patrons were 3-4 deep, anxious, angry, and unable to get a drink. That aside, back along the wall, when you finished your drink, there was no place to set your empty glass/bottle. The servers walking by wouldn't take it from you, and if you set it on the counter/dance floor in front of you, Dave was all over you threating to kick you out. So, now you have now drink, no way to get a drink, and nothing that you can do with your empty glass.  Unbelievable. I'm not a youngster, but at the same time, I am totally adaptably, yet there was nothing about this situation that would make me come back. To ensure that I wasn't  just being persnickety, I looked around. The 20-30 somethings were getting frustrated and leaving too. Dave continually made every situation worse as I watched him make most of the customers uncomfortable.. No problem, except for the fact that people started leaving. By the time my wife and I walked out it was just after midnight, and the crowd of many had dwindled noticeably. The show was great. The music was good. The fact that you couldn't get a drink, the staff was so unwelcoming and the entire atmosphere was not conducive to having a good time, emptied the place long before it should have and probably cost the girls, the band, the servers, and the OWNER, money. Better change things, or the status quo will shut you down because you wont get repeat customers.

We popped over on a whim on opening day. Everyone else was having Mexican on Cinco De Mayo and I figured Ramen and Poke would be deliciously different than the norm!

Upon entering, you are presented with iPads for ordering and then a nice modern dining room with shared and personal tables. Waiters later come out with food and wonder which table holds the associated diners. The only clue is your small number on the receipt. 

Our Poke bowls were actually rice based which is different than the Hawaiian style we've grown accustomed to. The fish, rice and veggies were fresh but severely under seasoned. I asked for extra Poke sauce and that helped. Although I ordered mango as a topping, they didn't have any and offered strawberries instead. I went for it!

The ramen was also under seasoned which is unusual for ramen. Usually, it's got the salt quantity to last you a week salt-free!

We did enjoy Vietnamese coffee which surprisingly was free likely due to a iPad menu glitch. 

Overall, the meal was filling and fresh and fun but a little short on flavor. Sriracha saved the day!

My husband and I went on a Wednesday night around 6:30pm. They don't take reservations, but found some room at the bar. I suggest with all the happy hour food and drink specials they have during the week Monday-Thursday, and Sunday (see picture) you should not come during peak dinner time. Expect to have a least a 30 minute wait. 

Good selection of local beers on tap. Nice selection of wine and spirits. Option to have your red wine infused with smoke! 

We ordered the buffalo cauliflower. Good portion size for 2-4 people to share. Great flavor, served on the medium side of spicy.  Nice selection of specials. I had the chicken pot pie empanadas!  Great flaky pocket crust with chicken filling on a bed of pickled daikon radish salad. My husband ordered (off the menu) the Southwestern flatbread. Crispy thin bread topped with black beans, avocado, cilantro and a zesty creamy drizzle. Yum! The flat breads are a generous portion, enough for two people to share.

Even though we were full we had to try the Oreo Cheesecake. And it was worth it! Layers of chocolate, cheese case and cake-like brownie. 

Will definitely be back and have put this place on my favorite list!

I understand dentistries are businesses, but this one is more like a scamming business. I've never been OVER-CHARGED in my life for dental care until I came here. The front office people are nice to you until they need to squeeze their overly price fees out of you, then the become vicious.  I'm not sure if the actually dentists are aware of the borderline financial scams the office manager is encouraging her staff to practice. This is the worse dentistry I've ever been to--be not deceived by the fancy office and the fake smile when you first arrive. I was literally told that I had some form of "gum disease" and I needed some special treatment. For the record, I've never had a gum disease as an adult, as I practice excellent dental hygiene. Well, I visited another dentist just to be sure and...voila!...Stacy Johnson's dentistry were lying when they said I had a "gum disease." BTW, they were charging thousands of dollars to help cure it. 

If you go on Google Plus, they have fake reviews for themselves. So despicable.

I really used to love this place.. after all it was the first place I tried sushi at 10 years ago. But over the years the service has dwindled and the quality of the sushi too.. I loved coming here because their unagi sauce was phenomenal and they had the best deep fried rolls hands down. But now the unagi sauce even tastes different. They say they make it in house I don't know for sure if that is true or not but it definitely doesn't taste the same anymore. 

I didn't want to post any negative reviews because like I said I have been coming here for over 10 years now. I know that they have been going through a lot of changes as far as turnover, new staff, new management and/or new ownership so I wanted to give them a break. However the last five or six times that I've been there my orders have been incomplete or messed up, and the service has been subpar (to put it nicely). 

My husband also had his first taste of sushi at Sushi Pier Midtown and he was in love with the place. He isn't hard to please when it comes to food but even he now  prefers a different sushi place. So the only reason why we have come here at all within the past year was at my request. Prior to today, the last time I had come in was on May 4th. I came in with my sisters, my mom, my daughter, and a friend from out of town (party of 6) and our orders were messed up so many times. We never got any refills on our drinks, we tried asking but every time the server came by they quickly left before we could ask for anything. It seemed like our server had a bit of a attitude. However, because we had a party of six we had the mandatory tip added to our bill. I love to tip people but for quality service. I've tipped pizza delivery drivers $25 before because I received excellent service. I really loved this place and hopefully things will turn around in the future.

Love this place.  Clean and very calm.  Everyone is friendly and professional.  I was a first timer for a dip manicure.  It looked great after a month!  I am a hard working restaurant manager who does not get to pamper her hands. Now I am treating myself to pretty hands!

0 stars if I could. Left soap stain residue all over my car. I went to a self service afterwards and it still wouldn't come out so I had to go and buy wax to try and cover it. Also had them clean the interior and still sand and dust everywhere. Possible that they didn't even touch the inside.

This has to be the worst post office station in the world.  The lines are long. There is only  one teller working in the front lobby and on a daily basis they close early. Like 3 pm during the week day and even earlier during the weekend. This has to change and it's been like this for years.
Stopped for breakfast. Had been there before for snacks and drinks, never considered breakfast. Glad I did! Food was hot, fresh, large portions, everything including service PERFECT

Party of 6 last night, first time at the Melting Pot. Our 8:45 reservation had us seated at 9:15. We waited by the bar and had a drink while we waited. The bartender was amazing...she was run off her feet though, mixing drinks, serving tables etc. 
Our waitress was unimpressive, somewhat sarcastic at times and did not offer us anymore drinks once we were seated??? She kinda ran thru the menu options and did not explain things very well. We ran out of cheese fondue and still had a lot of bread and veggies left to dip...we had two pots of cheese for 6 people...in my opinion it wasn't enough. The booth was crowded and only had two burners. The salad was good and the main course was not too bad. 5 out of 6 of us had the 4 course experience. 
The chocolate fondue was the best part. 
I would not return...except maybe for a drink at the bar.

The name "Dim Sum Garden" is given somewhat erroneously given because this place does not serve a significant amount of Dim Sum staples. Instead, it should really be called Xiao Long Bao, because that's really all they serve -- though they do serve it well. 

During my last visit, I had the xiao long bao at the insistence of my friend, and the scallion pancakes. Both of these were delightful, nothing too spectacular, but when I was hopeful to order a Char Siew Pao (a must-have at any dim sum restaurant, otherwise you shouldn't bother call it dim sum) I was very disappointed. "I don't see any Char Sie--", "We don't have that". The restaurant also failed to provide a wider variety, proving that they're more of a Xiao Long Bao Parlour, rather than some Dim Sum Garden. This restaurant doesn't sell Dim Sum in a traditional sense, but an assortment of many other Chinese dishes, and limited Dim Sum. 

I won't go so far as to call this a shitty restaurant -- because it isn't -- but it's not the place to go if you want Dim Sum, authentic or not. A proper Dim Sum restaurant would have the carts being rolled around, and a pretty standard inventory of dishes, larger than this one. It seems like Dim Sum Garden tries hard to cater to a wider audience (predominantly non-Chinese) presumably because of the very traditional and authentic Dim Sum restaurant right next door.

I walked in and was helped immediately. I have natural hair and wanted a wash, blow dry and flat iron. I don't like sitting under the dryer with rollers, but prefer an actual blow dry. The stylist listened and did what I asked. I've been to a couple Dominican salons in Brandon and so far, she got my hair the straightest and it lasted the longest. However, I had to tell her multiple times to stop pulling my hair so hard when she was detangling and also when she was blow drying. Not sure why this is common in SOME Dominican salons. They have to know that all that pulling is not good for your hair and also it hurts!! While I may return, I will definitely ask for someone else to take over if I go through that again. I do not want to, and shouldn't have to tell someone to stop pulling my hair so hard.

O, Whole Paycheck (I mean Foods) Market... how I simultaneously love and yet loathe thee. I love thee for thy vast selection of high-quality foods and produce, yet I loathe thee for thy prices. What's a body to do? 

...Make more money, I guess, so we can continue to fork it over in the name of nutrition!

We have been enjoying this wine bar for many years, even before moving here. However, our last visit 3/23 was disappointing. There was no California Chardonnay to offer, and the red wine selection was Cabernet, Cabernet and Malbec. We never had reason to question the pour, and this time learned it's 5 oz. Normal is 6. Very enjoyable atmosphere on the balcony, however.

This place is an awesome addition to downtown Indianapolis! Prices are great, kids are welcome and there's such great nostalgia going on with all of the pinball machines. The ONLY reason I'm not all in for 5 stars is the beer list. It could be better and I'm sure that it will get better over time. We'll definitely be heading there again soon!

Do not go to these people. Darren was very professional and cordial to me and my mother last year. This year he is unprofessionally and inconsiderate I dropped off my taxes for him to complete . I explained that I was sick and was prepping for a procedure and needed the taxes to be done as soon as possible. I called and my mother called and stopped in the office and each time he said he was working on it and should be done in a few days. After 3 weeks I asked for my taxes back and nothing was done. He lied to me for weeks. Darren claimed he called me once after I complained to his partner about the situation. I told him I gave him my updated home number with my taxes. I never received a call from him. I am glad I did not let him do it because he lost papers I had given him to do my taxes. A CPA ADVISED ME THE CORRECT WAY TO PROCESS MY TAXES SINCE AN ESTATE TAX WAS INVOLVED TOO. Do not use this person Darren and I called Mark about this and he was no help since they are partners. I would never recommend these dishonest people.
its a good philly cheesesteak, but it's a little pricey. they don't offer water, which to me is just weird. the cheese fries were delicious but also too much.

Nice spot. Although I prefer the ambiance of the restaurant, Sabour, it is still a nice addition to the place. The food was great and the waiter was very knowledgeable. It's a little secluded so you really can't see anyone when you are there - so if you want a private conversation it will work well. If you want to see people, you'd be best to go up into the restaurant.

Visited the Golden Valley branch tonight to have some dinner with friends. The place wasn't busy at all -- 3 tables of customers. Walked in and waited to be seated and no one welcomed us. I'm a very understanding man but the place wasn't busy and they were certainly not understaffed -- in the back by the drive thru window, 3 were just standing there and having a ball - laughing like hyenas, 1 at the bar, and 1 serving the tables.

We decided to leave after a few minutes and no one in there seemed to care at all. Glad we didn't stay -- especially after reading all the bad reviews. I even wanted to write to the head office about it, but it seems like all the reviews are bad about this chain, not just in Reno.

We are not coming here again. We'll spend money elsewhere.

Blaze over all is a great place to eat very friendly but pizza is just decent.  Blaze has a very nice atmosphere and is an interesting place to be as soon as you walk in. It is very bright and open. The staff make it seem very inviting and fun. The service here is always great. The employees are always super friendly, try to make you laugh, and would do anything to help you.  The presentation of the meal is interesting and very unusual for a pizza place. The pizzas are just a size for you to eat but the food is always sub par and very pricey but all around great place to eat!

I could have done these better if I did them myself. They didn't even clean around the edges and so many air bubbles. and when I asked them to fix it they were extremely annoyed and told me that it was my fault my nails weren't smooth.

Since this seems to be the new "it" place for registries, I find myself going here much more than I would like. I always make sure to either take pictures of my phone if the registry number and the items I want on my phone or print out what I'm looking for. Their website is much better than the store.

If you'd like to order something online, it would be much easier on you. When I go in and ask a sales associate a simple question such as "where is this" they NEVER know. They aren't friendly and hardly ever smile. Customer service here always gets an F. 

The only reasons they have any stars is because they are usually tidy and organized. This store carries a few items that cannot be found anywhere else and they also have great deals. I love their coupons.

My friend and I ended up at Something Sweet since we our favorite restaurant was closed one night, and I've been wanting to try it out. Pretty bad decision, it turns out.

We arrived and were seated within a few minutes, which was nice. That was the end of the good service. We waited 20 minutes until the waitress brought us water, then another 15 until she came back for our order. My friend got the mushroom soup and salad, and I got the Caprese salad. He says his Italian dressing tasted like sour pickle juice, and my Caprese was alright. Not the best I've had, but I enjoyed it.

Another 30 minutes or so passed, and the waitress took our dessert orders. My friend ordered some type of chocolate cake, but they were out, then switched to the Chocolate Obsession Cake (I believe). I got the creme brulee. His cake was entirely bland. You're probably better off going to Safeway and buying a cake for cheaper, since I'm pretty sure what's what they do here. My creme brulee, again, was mediocre -- nothing wrong with it, but I've had a lot better from places that DIDN'T supposedly specialize in desserts.

Overall, I won't be returning. I just got the vibe that they're buying their food somewhere else and just reheating it before serving it. The service was horrible -- we took over two and a half hours to get dinner and dessert. I definitely do not recommend this place.

In comparison to all the other places around it Conshohocken, Plymouth meeting, Norristown etc this place is mediocre. I've never been really happy with anything I've gotten there (except the pork, but thats a given). But, I've also never been really unhappy. I say you try it and make your own opinion!
This is our favorite restaurant in the Nashville area.  Food & service are incredible.  I'm so sad they are temporarily closed but we will be anxious to go back when they re-open!

Horrible,  poor customer service skills, poor work,  poor service.  Only difference between them and robbers is they came to my house without a gun and a mask. Total Protection will lose a lot of customers if they keep dealing with these guys.  #2thumbsdown

No clue how to make a chai latte, or possibly any latte. Really? A teabag in some hot milk that I have to squeeze honey into is a latte??? Wrong. And charging $4+ for it is outrageous. I won't even go into the milk issue.

Heard they had decent burgers.  Tried the double burger double bacon.  Tasty, but expensive.  $16 seemed a bit excessive for what I got.  Fries were mediocre as well.  Expected them to be seasoned.

Ordered a beer and it was flat.  Service was average.

May try them again.

Cafe Fulya is one of my favorite things in Queen Village. 

The coffee is a bold brew.
Oj is fresh squeezed.
The omelets are perfect every time. 
Bf loves the Turkish breakfast platter.
The coconut strawberry cake is amazing (although the last few times we've had it, it seems that the recipe for the cake has changed).
Last but not least - the baklava. You HAVE to have a piece of baklava!

Other goodies like free wifi, chill music, and nice folks make this place definitely worth the visit.

Very much enjoyed our visit to Gelato di Riso. Really cool atmosphere and vibe inside, and of course the gelato is phenomenal. Can't go wrong picking anything- I will say that the salted caramel is fab and probably my favorite out of all them. Made for the perfect trip to The Hill after stopping for pasta earlier!

They only did not listen to me while there , but  when I called 8 days later got labs sent out results , they said "apparently there was a problem with your sample." 
Never did they call to tell me this , nor did they let my primary doctor know anything about my ER VISIT 

Umm... no , you guys messed up the labels even before sending

Tim and the team at Nashville Warehouse Tire were so professional and helpful. My Jeep's transmission died and they inspected it and let me know what was going on. They were friendly and responsive, and they gave me a VERY competitive price for the repair. You can tell they really care about their customers. Had I not had a powertrain warranty through Chrysler, I would have definitely used their services. I have found my Nashville mechanic for sure! A big thanks to the team at Nashville Warehouse Tire!

We love the P&M's on Kolb/22nd but the one on Tanque Verde is awful. The food is bland and the last time I was there my husband and mother both ended up with food poisoning. 
We stick to the Kolb location now-

The overall wait for a walk in was very quick and easy but the girl who took my blood mid 40s 50s black woman shoved the shit of a needle hard as hell in my arm for no freaking reason. Like Bitch it's skin, not fucking rubber!!! That shit hurt and pissed the the fuck off!!! Then she had to do it twice cause she couldn't find the vein. If she's there to take your blood. RUN!! Go to a different location

The interior design is pretty cool, it's a pretty hip vide. The coffee was OK, just typical coffee. The service was pretty bad. It was a bunch of high school boys who seemed pretty clueless as to what they need to do and very slow service.

I have been going to this clinic for several years now to see Dr. Talanian, and I have had good experiences so far. Dr. Talanian listens well and takes your opinion and feelings into account when recommending treatments/next steps. Like most physicians, she is short on time, which can be annoying, but that's our medical system.
I was more impressed by the service I received when I was in the process of getting medical paperwork filled out to attend school, in Spring 2017. I had to go back and forth to the office more times than I can count to get the TB tests and vaccines done, and the paperwork all filled out to the school's satisfaction. However, the office staff, Dr. Talanian, her MA, and one of the NPs were fantastically accommodating to help me read through the complicated paperwork, answer my questions, advocate for me, and make sure I got everything done on time. In the midst of all this, I was even out of the country for five weeks, and they worked around that too. 
At one point one of the medical providers ordered the wrong blood test, which was frustrating. However, they re-ordered the correct test promptly when I noticed and let them know. I'm surprised to read so many poor reviews, since overall the accommodation and service made a strong impression on me and I was so thankful to them for lightening my stress load.
Yeah return call within 3 hrs NOT !!!! I've been trying to make an appointment for the past 3 day with no return call whatsoever so NO I will not be using them for my repairs.

I've been going to this Walmart for two years and they continue to have the same problem. No matter when I go there are never enough lines and they seem to be short stocked on supplies. Sunday nights are the worst. Week after week they know Sunday afternoon crowd wipes them out yet they never plan a head to keep up their stock. No excuse.

Chill vibe, friendly staff but had to give 1 star due to I felt like I was lied too. If you go there for food do not pay any attention to the online menu. Prices are 3 times the online menu (especially the wing prices). Once I received the bill I question the price and was told it was an old menu online. Biggest issue is they don't even have a menu inside the bar to provide you what they currently have and the current prices.

We very seldom visit Chain Q places but decided to try Dave's outside of the usual one we stop by in Orland Park, IL. We don't like Dave's up north so decided to try this one hoping for better. But it was even worse than up north. 

I got the burnt end sandwich which was burnt sauce over bad meat. Absolutely terrible. My wife got a small sauced rib and we took a chicken sandwich with us. All were so salty we threw I everything out. The Chicken sandwich was also so salty we threw it out too. 

Management there admitted to the saltiness but offered no refund or a new order.  He said sometimes the kids in the back don't shake the rub mixture enough while putting it on the meat and has to remind them from time to time. Not a good explanation so we will never go there again or the one in Orland Park.

Love going to this theater since they remodeled and updated to the premium seating. It's nice to be able to recline and get comfortable during a long movie. The concessions were also upgraded to include more hot food choices and they also serve alcohol options as well. We went to the Star Wars Fan Appreciation event last week which included popcorn with our ticket that was easy to obtain even though they were very busy and there was a girl at the entrance to the theater to greet us with a collector's pack for the premiere. This is the most convenient AMC location in town to my house and has a large parking lot as well so I never have to leave too early to get to the movie. However you have to walk down a dark walkway in between buildings to get from the parking lot to the entrance which makes me a little uncomfortable when I'm alone. I have to be honest that sometimes I skip this theater and drive out to the West Olive location just because they offer the dine in experience for the same ticket price. But if I'm not attending a movie during a meal time I go the to the Esquire every time.
Nothing special about my visit here - the food was only ok, it seemed a bit over oily and not very fresh. I'm in no hurry to go back..
Tastes great and the fruit was very fresh. They were a bit on the thinner side, good nonetheless. The prices are a little steep as well considering the size of the crepe and the lengthy wait.
The view is amazing. The staff is friendly. I was there with a large group. Our server was not the best but he tried. The food was ok. It's was a long drive for ok food.

Dr. Pervais is just amazing.  Competent, caring, and really works with her patients.  The staff is super friendly and tries to be helpful as well, particularly with our odd insurance plan.  I recommend this practice highly.  You will not be disappointed.
Definitely do your self a favor and stop by and get the all that jazz sandwich. Soooooo good down to the very last bite!!!

Actually, according to their website, the SubStop is only temporarily closed and is planning on opening in a new location in the near future.  So I would just continue to check their website, periodically, to see when they have re-opened.  I deeply miss their amazing dark wheat turkey and cheese sandwiches.  I hope they will still be in walking distance from my office, but I'm not going to press my luck.

This location needs to be cleaned up on the exterior. I'm actually scared to see what the inside looks like. Surprisingly, the food wasn't bad. I had a large order and the entire order was correct. I wasn't missing items nor was the food cold or nasty. The fries were hot and so was the chicken. The employees also were very courteous making sure that I had everything I needed. I just hope the owner of this location fixes the exterior of the building. Looks pretty bad.

Reasons to stop by here:

* Sit by the large wide open windows and see the Nashville street life unfolding in front of you. Like being outside, but better!
* Bartenders, they are fast and friendly.
* Margaritas! 
* Live music
* Crisp coconut shrimp.... Yum!
* Their hot chicken wings are really, really good. (#wingpicky)

We also reserved a king and got two queens.  Walls were so thin we could hear peeing in other rooms.  Car alarms were going off all night.  The couple next door started fighting at three in the morning and then at 5 am.  We heard some of the foulest language we've ever heard including our 5 year old.  It was supposed to be nonsmoking and the fighting couple smoked both days we were there.  After finally falling asleep after the fighting, the maid decides she needs to clean our room at 7;30 am on a Sunday!!!!!  What a nightmare.  I left an email and never heard back.  You'd"d think with business hurting they would be extra vigilant but this establishment will never get our business again.

Very very pricey. I'm all for gastropubs, but their alcohol and menu seemed very pricey. We tried the elote appetizer and it was really good but the chips were almost fried too long. The menu seemed very pricey, especially when you have to buy your sides separately for a burger. I'm all for nice food and paying when it's worth it, but that place didn't really give me that vibe. Also, our server disappeared for a very long time, not to mention the fact that the place seemed pretty dead.

Everyone told me this was the greatest nice restaurant in New Orleans, hands down. I thought it was 'just decent,' and i'm the over optimistic person who always gives 4's and 5's.

Numerous people told me to get the alligator with chili garlic aioli, it was ok. I guess I'm not a huge fan, tasted like chewier, smokier chicken.

The liver pate's weren't great either.

The braised cochon special pork dish was tasty. The pork was tender, like wagyu beef. I'll give that one 4.5 stars.

The BBQ oysters were pretty good, but very pricey.

I guess the $40 per person price with drinks (moonshine was strong and unique) for the very limited portions didn't make great sense to me. 

I had better local Cajun homecooked food.

I have to say we were really excited about Lucky's coming to St. Petersburg. We have visited numerous times since they opened. As a culinary professional I can say the last 3 times we went to get prepared food was a disappointment. Our chicken tasted like it had been in the case a day past tossing time.  The deli sandwiches were without flavor and had only about 2 slices of meat. For the price you pay for their prepared meals you would expect much better. The fresh breads we have purchased we also low on the flavor scale. Produce, Coconut Water, Bulk foods and sweets are great however many items are out of stock. Hopefully some changes are coming.

This is our first time here.  I ordered the redfish blacken.  It was decent.  The fish was very charred on one side. Almost inedible. There was no sauce to accompany the fish.
