I had to take my piece of lemon from my water and add more flavor to it.    My potatoes were tasty and so was the green string beans.  My husband got the New Orleans sampler.  It was three bowls of different types of jambalaya or gumbo.  Not as feeling as we thought.  We received a free banana foster's cheesecake for dessert.  At first, we were not sure what it was.  It had two scoops of cheese cake on the left and right side of a piece of pumpkin/banana spiced bread slice.  It was engorged in a thick syrup that was sweet and cinnamon flavor.  there were small slices of very thin mushy bananas.  It was interesting to say the least.  Our bill was a little over $50 with tips.

The nail polish was lay on so thick that even if the upper layer was dry, the bottom layers were not; I stayed there to dry for at least 30 minutes.  Even after 6 hours my nail polish is still not fully settled and still messing up. 5 nails look messed up already.   I love the color but very disappointed with the outcome.

the staff is so nice when you go in to browse.  they're incredibly helpful and friendly.  i have purchased a few things that i really love from this store.

unfortunately i would not recommend that anyone make any large purchases from this store.  i ordered (and paid up-front in full) something to be made custom that was supposed to be in their store in two weeks.  i got very concerned when a month later i hadn't heard from them.  i could not get a straight answer for another month and a half as to where my order was.  their customer service is a nightmare!  

beware their post-purchase customer service:  don't buy anything there you're not walking out with that day!

If I could give this place a negative number I would.  Stayed one night, June 19, 2015 prior to an early flight out the next day.  The place was like a free for all.  People in the halls and rooms yelling and screaming well into the wee hours.  Supposed class reunion going on among other things.  Will NEVER stay at this or any other Embassy Suites again.  Pull out couch still had prior guest's linen on it.  Bathroom dirty upon arrival.

Our family's favorite breakfast spot. We go at least once a month and the staff is always nice, quick and super helpful. Even when it's busy we never have to wait too long, even though we're a party of 6. Coffee is good, pancakes are delicious and the French toast is perfect. Can't wait to go again next weekend!

I love this club so much it truly is a playground , safe haven , and slice of heaven all in one the only thing is the security guys are the worst inside sometimes this one security guy kept hitting on us and we consistently politely declined. Which was annoying but hey it comes with the territory of clubbing I guess.Other than that the bouncers outfront are always super great it's literally just to bad seeds out of the group.Everyone is so loving and amazing and I'm truly grateful for this Tampa gem.

ANDROIDS ON THEIR ANDROIDS
I was hopeful that OCH's Church Alley would be a reasonablly priced, ethnically diverse neighborhood eatery like say Freret Street Poboys or Frady's in the 9th. But alas, it's yet another bourgey coffee shop, the type that was trendy in wealthier more legit cities than ours 10 years ago. The patrons included a klatch of bullet-coiffed vulturish harridans (realtors bottom feeding?) and blasé tableting Gap-sters (prey for those Remaxx-i-trixes?).

NO YELLOW, BLUE OR PINK PACKETS
My $4 petite sized iced coffee was painfully bitter. Seriously I winced and needed milk. But there's no creamer or sweetner station, just a silly sideboard with raw sugar and honey - how adorable...

86'D MY BREW AND SASHAYED TO FRENCH TRUCK
After a short walk and $0.50 cents more I feasted on their sublime "New Orleans Iced". Not only is it larger but it's sourced from their adjacent roasting facility and served with a creamy shaved ice blend. To me it's the best in town.

Good stuff.  Small place, but what a great place to eat.  I went for lunch and got the Sampler breakfast.  Wow, what a huge meal.  Also had at the table was the philly cheesesteak sandwich and biscuits and gravy.  Both were excellent.  Can't wait to go back.

As a visitor to the area last weekend, I'm probably not as qualified to review this place as a local or a regular, but I have to say I had a blast! Coming from Seattle, where we have a serious lack of roof-top party decks (or any outdoor space at all in bars, really) I enjoyed moving up there after we had a bite to eat and hanging out on the deck. 

The atmosphere is fun and, yes, kind of "faux-beachey," but the old building is neat and there was even a Rebelution-esque band setting up while we ate. Overall, I would definitely come back. Even with a group of about eight people we had attentive service throughout the whole night, so no bad experience there as others have mentioned.

If you're visiting the "hip scene" of downtown Boise, check this place out!

Tip: The Long Islands were strong and tasty!
At first, I did not know what to order. It was suggested by one of the hosts that I try the crawfish etouffe. This was 4 days ago and I am still dreaming about it!
No masks, no business from me! You are literally killing people by not requiring masks of your staff or customers! F-

This place has some facinating and tasty latin fusion appitizers. We had the bruchetta tostones and the maduros relleno to start. both had an unusual and pleasant flavor but the texture of the tostones bruchetta left something to be desired. The maduros relleno fell apart while I was trying to eat it but I didn't really care. 

For the meal my companion had the vegtable paiella, absolutely delicious. I added a bit of salt but she thought it was perfect. There was a wide variety of vegtables all perfectly cooked and the flavors blended well while not getting muddied together at all. I had the bandeja tipica, it was SO delicious. My mouth is literally watering at the memory of it! My only slight critique is that it was a tiny bit dry, but that is what the side of beans is for. Pouring that on and mixing it in really brought the dish together. The server was very plesant and appropriately attentive. she did make a mistake with our order but apologized and fixed it promptly. 

We were so stuffed we couldn't possibly eat dessert but we came back several hours later. I had a hot chocolate made fresh with milk and real cocoa, I am very picky about hot chocolate and this held up to my high standard. My companion was pleased with her coffee. Dessert is where this place fell a bit short. We ended up tasting almost every dessert on the menu through a sampler dish and they were all disappointing in one way or another, mostly texturally. But the creme brule was decadent and just amazing. Highly highly recommended!

Very disappointed with the bartenders that start at 9. I understand that they don't know me but after 20 minutes and asking the bar back to there get there attention... and no response.   I will never be back agian

Arranged A/C maintenance two weeks ahead of time - technician never arrived.  I called and they had no record of the appointment.  I rescheduled for three days later - technician never arrived.  When I called the next day to inquire, I was told, "Oh, It's been crazy.  We're down a tech and they didn't get to your appointment."  But not even a courtesy call? Unprofessional - find someone else.

I'm not even a customer yet and already hate them.  I'm moving to an apartment soon and I have to pay for gas.  I tried looking up many different companies, but Spire just bought all of them!  I've tried getting an appointment for 2 weeks now, but they keep telling me online my address doesn't exist in their system.  IT'S AN APARTMENT COMPLEX WITH ALMOST 1,000 PEOPLE LIVING THERE!  My manager said almost everybody uses Spire.  How does my address, which so many other people have with a different unit number, not exist in your systems?  I tried reaching out to Facebook for help, and the same thing.  This is exhausting and just plain stupid.  Again, I would literally go with anyone else had they not taken them over.  Unbelievable!

Sorry Soulshine, I wasn't impressed with you.

Coming in on a Friday night, I was surprised to get a table immediately and had high hopes after a good start. They were busy inside, but not packed and I really liked the big open restaurant and nice patio. Our waitress started off friendly but when it took a while to get our drinks and order, I should have known we were in trouble. She was very frazzled early on; not sure if she was just new or had too many tables or what, but she came over in a panic saying she couldn't take our order because she set down her book somewhere. Um, OK?  

Once we were able to order food, we ordered the tamales and hummus to snack on, then the mushroom pizza as an entree. The tamales were ground beef and spicy, and the best thing we ate. The hummus was decent but had an olive tapenade that overpowered the hummus. I didn't love it. The pizza was middle of the line, OK, but not amazing either. 

I would probably have given them an OK rating, but trying to get the bill and get it correctly was a CHORE. We were still enjoying our drinks and our bill was brought to the table. Um... we hadn't asked for the check and were going to use a ScoutMob coupon for half off. Plus our waitress added on a drink that wasn't ours. When we asked to use the coupon, our waitress gave us an attitude and said "it's going to take me a minute to redo it." Um, we get that but we would have told you about the coupon if you had asked if we were ready for the check. Sigh. The "minute" turned into 20. We waited and waited for our check, and when we FINALLY got it, (and it was still incorrect) we just paid and bolted for the door. It wasn't worth trying to fix.

I'm not sure I'll be back to Soulshine. If the food was better I might be tempted, but with middle of the line food I think I'll pick somewhere else next time.
This place is overpriced and overrated.  I am very disappointed with the flour tortillas.  Will never go back again.

Seems like a pretty nice place, bartender was friendly. But a few mistakes like charging for a 20oz pour when I got a 16. Happened to a couple of others at the bar as well. Brought the check after the first round so I felt as if I couldn't order another drink. Overall good though.

This gets one star only because a zero is impossible. The bus never showed up period. When 3 of the seven stranded customer call they were told three different answers. The bottom line, you are SOL. Greyhound has no respect for their company and zero respect for the customer. If you choose to use Greyhound, good luck, you're gonna need it.

Staff was very courteous and friendly.  Dr. Pollard was condescending in how he explained out of pocket costs for deeper cleanings and not taking easy way out.  Previous dentist did deep cleanings for a lot less. Telling someone they have jacked up grill but it'll take a ton of money to pay for isn't very helpful at all. Update 2/13/15: Dr Pollard did reach out to provide initial service to get teeth back on the right check.

Did not feel welcome here, felt more like a burden. There was a man and woman working. The guy did all the work while the woman sat in the back of the restaurant on her phone. Woman came up to help him for a few seconds but the faces she was making was extremely rude.  Looking forward to eating here for the first time but my first experience of this place wasn't great at all.

Wow. Look at these reviews. Not surprised by the experience I just had. Went there about midnight. Ordered two cheeseburgers, no onion. You guessed it, onion on both. Cold as f*ck, and one had two "tops" for a bun, not to mention the bread was stale as sh*t on both. Holy crap, you get out of life what you put into it. For these workers, obviously that is very little. Smartin up! Please, for humanity's sake!!!

The inside was so cute, small but very cool. We loved the bao buns but the ramen was meh... me and my boyfriend both got food poisoning after so unfortunately as good as the bao buns were the meat was probably off. Wouldn't come again

Stopped here for the first time last Friday. Excellent variety. Sturgeon, ahi tuna, grouper, swordfish , mussels, oysters and more. Reasonably priced for the quality and freshness you get. Friendly staff and plenty of parking. Well worth a visit
Excellent take on blue-collar-but-local sourced burger vibe. Fresh, high quality ingredients, including grass-fed burger beef.  Expect a line at peak lunchtime.  With it!

i'm pretty tough to please when it comes to BBQ. These guys have brought the "texas" style retail BBQ experience to PA. Buy it by the lb. and have yourself a meatfest. From the ribs to the brisket it all rocked. I will definitely be a repeat customer... great food at fair prices that's keto friendly? color me a fan.

I picked out a color for a regular manicure and the nail tech just didn't paint my nails and still charged me to full price. I didn't have time for them to redo the service once I realized I wasn't getting my nails painted. 

There are lots of nail places in STL and I will not be returning to pay full price for partial services.

I am taking a short intermission from my carry out meal from Cocina Aqui so I can write this review. I wish I could give it more than five stars. My husband and I enjoyed this dinner so much, we want to go meet the owner. We are so truly in awe over this delicious food. I am stuffed after finishing two thirds of my dinner, but I will not let the rest become leftovers. I am merely taking a break so I can finish this amazing dinner later. Not only is it the best Southwestern style food either of us has ever had, it may be the best meal we can remember....ever.

I had the lobster enchiladas. I am so in love with this dish I want to stick my face in it. It has the perfect blend of spices and flavor. I would chop off your arm if you tried to take it from me. 

The chips are some of the best I've had anywhere. They are fresh, crisp, and seasoned just right. I usually review Mexican restaurants based on the quality of their guacamole, and this is right on! 

My husband had the pork enchiladas and he felt the same way about his meal. Even the refried beans and rice were a fantastic! A little spicy, but not too much. They are usually left on my plate, but not this time! My husband finished his, and now he's trying to fight for mine.

We are on vacation, but if I lived here, I would be a regular - no doubt. My God.

The boyfriend loved it but I was not a fan of their thin crust.  I call this kind of pizza cracker pizza because that is what it tastes like.  They have won some accolades though, so maybe I am the pizza fool.  The sauce and toppings were good though.  I am sure they will only get more popular when Sabrina's opens up.

Before my own review:  please see all of the other negative reviews at this company's previous address on Patterson.  There are many others voicing the same complaint. 

Let me be the first to say it here: stay as far away from this "practice" as you can.  

They charged me a sneaky co-pay for a regular physical --not something that is legal for preventative care. When I was called on the phone by the office staff they were aggressive and seemed angry that I couldn't speak with them there and then.

I walked into the office a few days later to see what the issue was and asked what they were billing me for.  They initially told me it was for a referral.  Not legal either.  The front desk staff were snide, argumentative and bordering on aggressive.

 "...He asked you about your hearing and you responded. Therefore it is diagnostic..."

I paid the bill because they they threatened me with collection and I couldn't be bothered with the legal headache.

These people are deceptive and should be ashamed of the way they are duping patients into extra billing.  They should inform patients about costs up front.

If you do have to go there, make sure to ask your doctor to tell you when they cross that line between "preventative" and "diagnostic",

but really, go to Samsum or some other place where they have bit of respect for clients.

This place used to be the best in the area, but it's gone downhill. Maybe because Christie herself isn't there as often.  Last time I was there, I had my gel manicure soaked off with remover, and instead of using a metal tool to scrape it off, the technician used her own dirty fingernails. And they were very dirty underneath! She was also miserable and unfriendly. She was really annoyed that I wasn't sure what color I wanted. Sighing and rolling her eyes. And I swear I took 30 seconds, not ages and ages. I just wanted to to compare TWO bottles. TWO.  I wanted to look inside, because sometimes the color is different than the label, and these two colors were similar.. But that was too much trouble.   Also, last two times I had a pedicure, they left me sitting there for ages while they started work on someone else. This place used to have high standards - now I fear it's just the same as any other nail salon, perhaps worse. Their prices are high but it used to be worth it, because of their attention to detail. Not any more.

I took my son's car for a last minute oil change at Maggiano's before he left for school. I figured an oil change was going to be easy and inexpensive. When they called to let me know the car was done I was told that they also found a screw in the tire and they plugged it for me. They had already done this without calling and I was now being charged $58. I was annoyed that I didn't  get a call before they did it but thankful that they found it. When I went to pick it up I was also told that the tires were dry rotted. 

This past weekend some additional work had to be done. Luckily I have a friend who works for a dealer and is on the up and up. Come to find out the oil filter was
seeping. The oil was just changed two weeks ago by Maggianos!!! And the tires
are good for at least another year. I feel like they are are a complete ripoff and like to "find" things wrong with your car so they can jack up the price. It's a shame because I like to support local businesses but I won't be supporting them again.

We dined here recently on a slow night. Let me start off by saying we have ate at other Mitchell's locations previously, so we know the standard. This location has fell behind in food and service. Pros: the shrimp, bread, steak, and lobster were good. Cons: salmon didn't have any flavor to be Asian style, the rice was so mushy a baby with no teeth could swallow, and the spinach wasn't good quality it tasted funny. Also, our waitress, Lindsay, was HORRIBLE!!. She had only been there a month or two but the manager should have been coaching her more to ensure the level of service didn't fall thru the cracks. I could tell other servers were doing an excellent job with their tables. Our waitress had 1 table majority of the time and couldn't get things right. I want to say Jessica stepped in to help when our waitress went missing for extended periods at a time. GREAT JOB JESSICA!! Next time, don't get Lindsay and order the salmon. Overall, only time will tell if we come back to Mitchell's. We don't want one bad location to spoil the bunch.

Great quality, delicious food, low prices, huge portions. Service from our server, Kurt, was horrible. Took a while to take our order, asked what I wanted and then proceeded to just stare at my boyfriend with wide eyes as if to say "you gonna order?" But instead said nothing so it was just awkward. Brought us our bill without asking if we wanted boxes with a lot of food still on my plate. We had to quickly ask for a box before he got too far away and he didn't even turn to acknowledge the question.  He just said "yeah sure." As he was walking away. That's why we only left a $2. The only thing he did right was bring us our food.

We've stayed in this hotel a couple of times, and I've always been impressed.  The service is great and the rooms are clean and lush.  The beds are fantastic!  I did forget my hairspray on my last trip, and called concierge to see if they had any.  Of course, they had some available for sale in the gift shop.  So, I go down there and ended up paying $4 for a travel size of low-end hairspray.  What a rip-off.  I understand prices will be raised a bit for some things, but I guess they know they've got you over a barrel on some things, too.  Otherwise, the stay was enjoyable, and the area around the hotel are great as well.  I'm excited to go back and check out the park across the street next time.

Had the Asian Beef & Broccoli w/ Lo Mein & 2 Eggrolls.  The beef was tasty & tender but the noodles were served cold, good otherwise.  The eggrolls were veggie filled, nice w/ soy sauce drizzled down the core...love anything fried.

Total tab w/ small coffee was $20 after 15% discount for Gold Annual Pass.

We ordered a $1400 sectional about 14 months ago and from day one the cushions began to lose firmness.  The springs gave out so much you could feel the frame underneath when you sat down.  Guests we had over commented on how uncomfortable it was and difficult to get up from.  Since my couch was still under the warrenty they are going to replace it.  However when we ordered new furniture they said it would take 6-8 weeks!  I called to check in on the status and the salesmen told me they had the sofa in the warehouse and were waiting on the chair.  I asked if I could come pick it up to which he replied that would be fine.  However he forgot to press the hold button when asking the female associate about it and she snapped "they are getting their couch for free so they can just wait."  
I am pregnant and the sooner we could get our uncomfortable couch replaced for a sturdy one the better.  Also $1400 is not being refunded to my checking account so it is not free but an exchange.  I had friends tell me previously about the rudeness of the staff and now I understand firsthand.  Buy at your own risk!

Dec. 2017
Disappointed again.  We went back the other day for the early bird special, all was nice, but the quality of the food was not a good as it should be.  I doubt we'll ever be back.

Mar. 2016
We went back today and we were highly disappointed....

1.  Upon being seated, we observed that the place was almost full AND the noise-level was so loud, that it was nearly impossible to have a conversation without shouting at each other.  There is no sound absorption anywhere in the restaurant.....very annoying.

2.  We were advised that the Tuesday all-day "Early Bird" special has been discontinued.....so much for saving a buck.

3.  We both ordered the Blackened Chicken Alfredo,  The chicken was cubed and hardly blackened, also very bland with no taste.  It had an excessive amount of Alfredo sauce.

4.  We were offered garlic bread, but the waitress never mentioned that it was an additional charge.  Four pieces of garlic bread was $3.90.  A rip....

We don't expect to be back anytime in the future.

Previous review from Dec. 2015....
We did the Early Bird dinner specials of Meat Loaf, mashed pot., and salad......it was well worth it.
A fun place.  Live music outside and a piano bar inside.
Great, prompt, friendly service.

Nice product, worst business practices ever. Their Facebook page says: 

"Happy summer!
The shop will be closed the next two weeks for vacation! Friday August 18th thru Sunday August 27th. Thanks!"

I show up the next weekend, Saturday September 2nd, during business hours, after having ordered product online days before for a dinner party. Sign on door reads 'Closed for Vacation". 

This has happened to me before. I preordered product to ensure availability, only to show up and they tell me they never got the order, and they have no goods to sell me. All of this while the two owners are having a family fight right in the store.

I went across the street to McMillans, bought a cake, and went home.

Never again.

Restaurant side is horrible for service. It's  never ever busy in there, but it takes forever to get served and forget refills for coffee or water, you'll never get it unless you find them. My mother after giving enough if the service asked about the wait for everything. They said they were busy. Place wasn't even half full. I've worked in a restaurant. That was not busy. Food is gross too. We only go to the bar side now service is better there and we only get the wings since it's the only good thing

The staff was super friendly and my coffee was never empty. The food was ok but I wanted to sub avocado for my hash browns, and they won't let you do that. If they let you sub avocado, I'd for sure come back. Squeeze in allows for subbing avocado so I'll just continue going there.

I was there last night. I have ordered medium premium veggie garden pizza. The woman who take my order was weird, she didn't understand the order even if I pointed out in the menu, she stood out for 20 seconds by looking at the menu. 

The reason for 1 star is that "Hair" came out from the pizza. That would happen, I'm not that cruel but when I told it to the server, he said he will talk to manager and after 10 mins, nobody even offer to change my pizza, nobody even came to my table. I couldn't eat the rest. Also when I go to the cashier, same server took my payment and he just said that he was sorry about the "hair thing". Actually I was expecting not to pay anything because I just ate 2 pieces of that filthy pizza.

I'm so done with Pizza Hut, I suggest everyone to stay away from these guys.

The staff and owner are extremely rude. They also wouldn't let me bring in my camel pack, and presumed to charge me $4 for water. Way to accommodate for the rave community. I doubt I'll ever come here again, regardless of the event.

My husband and I stayed here when attending a wedding in St. Pete. It was a wonderful experience. We were here on a weekend and it was very quiet - we had the restaurant, pool, and bar pretty much to ourselves with the exception of one or two other guests who came and went. The hotel is very clean, the staff are courteous and professional. The LUNA restaurant is fabulous - breakfast was exceptional. Nice outdoor pool, hot tub and walkway. The room couldn't have been more beautiful and comfortable. Definitely recommended. Side note: as we checked out (Monday morning) we noticed the parking lot was now full - this hotel definitely gets busier during the week with business travelers.

gorgeous. great deal.  rooftop deck!  booked it through hotwire, so only paid $100. split between 4 ppl- hellz yeah!  the lobby was fancy, everyone was polite, and the room was clean and comfy. it was in the perfect location for our one-night stay in new orleans before heading back to the cold after our cruise. we were able to wander around the french quarter all night and they didn't even laugh at us when we called at 3:30 a.m. asking for a wake up call at 4. well, maybe they did.

Had a rocky start with Raquel ( at Santa Barbara store). Wouldn't print out any of the designs for me to show my husband or contractor or designer. Hard to get appointments with her. Then she took a leave of absence and nobody told me and I had to track down the reason i wasn't hearing back from her. Should have had an automatic reply saying she was out and who to contact. Thank goodness Angela Beardon became my new rep. She was fantastic. Helped me with the design, easy to reach, responsive wanted to make sure I was happy. Fixed a few things that weren't right. She was top-notch. Installers were good at what they did but didn't care for their language or music, both of which were foul! I had to ask them to turn down their rap music with mature lyrics. They need to be aware when the homeowner is present, to watch their language amongst themselves and use headphones, turn down their music, or ask the homeowner if they care. Overall experience was rocky to start but the final product is wonderful.

Good for four hours a day.  

No, I'm not referring to Lindsay Lohan's daily time of sobriety.  In case you good people don't get out during that magical time between the end of work and the beginning of dinner, there is this thing called happy hour.  To connect this bit of ubiquitous restaurant/bar marketing ploy to this review, I say this:  Old Pueblo Grill is really only a decent place to visit during happy hour.  In full disclosure, I have been to this place one time, and it was during....  you guessed it:  happy hour.  

Now, quite a number of places in the Old Pueblo offer half-off appetizers during happy hour, and Old Pueblo Grill is no different.  On this occasion, I sampled the Firecracker Shrimp, decent-sized shrimp, crusted and served in a spicy chili sauce.  As much as I am loath to eat seafood in the middle of the desert, the quality wasn't all that bad.  The sauce had a nice spicy kick with a slight tanginess to it.  And for half-off, it wasn't a bad value.  Would yours truly pay a full $10 for the same appetizer in the near future?  Maybe.  Maybe not.   Nothing else on the starter's menu really struck my fancy.

So I've established that the apps, or one app to be precise, is doable.  How about the drinks you ask, since we are talking happy hour?  Three words:  one dollar off.  Not enough to tear it up, in my alcohol-soaked mind.  But in all fairness to Mr. McMahon, he of the McMahon chain of restaurants that Old Pueblo Grill is part of, the drinks aren't in the over-the-top price range.  If I recall, the beers on tap are $5 normally.  

I wish I could say the same for the rest of the menu.  Okay, maybe I shouldn't characterize the dinner menu prices as over-the-top.  At or near the top is more like it.  But the place is a fairly fancified, up-scale kind of joint.  (Think McMahon chain.)  One where you wouldn't really expect to encounter groups of teachers from two different schools.  I know that because once our bartender overheard our conversation, and immediately figured out that we were complaining about students, she freely told us that the group a few feet away out in the patio were teachers also.  

The bartender was one of the most cool, personable, and friendly ones I've had in a long while.  But as the place the filled up, she and the one other server in the bar area seemed quite overwhelmed handling the mostly AARP crowd.  Who knew that pouring 7 and 7s required that much time?

Despite the fairly overall negative impression I came away with on my initial visit, I'd be more than willing to give Old Pueblo Grill another shot.  They do offer an impressive list of tequila.  In fact, the menu promotes a tequila cellar with numerous types of different tequila in stock.  And they have margaritas the size of Thirstbusters.  Well, they're 42 ounces suckers according the menu.  

I'll just need to make sure that I come on a pay day.  Or have an arm and leg to offer.

We ordered our food and 50 minutes later the manager asked us if we ever ordered! About 20 minutes later, the manager asked us who took our order. He came back and said he would discount our order because of the long wait. Ten minutes later we had our food. My turkey burger was slightly above room temperature, but my fries were ice cold and limp! The other people in my party said their beef burgers and onion rings were on par with the other premium burger places. We walked in at 2pm on a Monday with very few people seated and expected a better overall experience. I am not sure if I will ever return, but the rest of my party was not receptive to the idea. On the positive side, their prices are lower than the other premium burger places in Nashville.

Heard about this place from a friend and was so excited to try a place thats not as far as Charlotte Pk. Interior was really nice; loved the modern vibe it gave off. Staff were super friendly.  However, the main entree of pho was sadly a disappointment. The noodles were still kind of hard which meant it wasnt cooked fully. The broth was the main thing that settled everything. It was tasteless, no flavor at all. I had to wing every sauce i could just for it to taste like a basic pho. I always use about a tbs or 2 of housin sauce for pho. But i think i added  6 tbs to get it to taste like something. Other than that, appetizer of the shrimp and pork  dumpling were fantastic! And the special fried rice was amazing as well! Just hope they can improve on the broth soon!

They have been out to my home twice within a 1 week period. It took 2 weeks to get them out in the first place.   On the second visit the technician left my house without a word.  He left a voice mail message saying the part was the wrong one.  When I finally got a hold of him and asked "why the wrong part was ordered, there is only 1 front lock, and how much longer I would be inconvenienced without a washer?".  His response to me was, "How about this Kim, call someone who gives a SHIT".

Yup, that's right, I was shocked that someone is the service industry would talk to a customer like that.  This even gets better, the owner Keith Tyson has yet to return the 8 messages that I left him. 

I don't know how they stay in business with customer service like this.  

If this is the business that Whirlpool contracts with I will certainly never buy that brand again.
Fantastic!  Chicken with garlic and lemon is delicious.  We also had the ham croquette.  Quaint setting with Cuban influence.  Cuban coffee too!

Somewhere on my journey to find that next experience, a friend mentioned a restaurant that I would probably love called Tres Carnales.  Indeed. (Thanks Jake!)  I have had a love affair with the food of the three gentlemen that own the eatery.  They keep it simple with great, fresh ingredients.  The restaurant is casual with great sugar skulls, calaveras and luchadors everywhere.  The food served is what you would find at a Mexican street vendor while browsing in a market.

The boys know how to promote themselves and have won many awards for their food.  The restaurant is usually very busy and it can sometimes be hard to find a table.  I do love their fish tacos!

Yelp has been helping me find some great new places to try out.  One of my fellow Yelpers suggested that if I liked Tres Carnales, I should try a little place called Cafe del Sol.  I always do my homework before I try a new place now.  I read the reviews and see what the menu looks like.  I have learned that everyone's palate is different and to always take the reviews and average them out.  They looked good!

The hours are different.  Please phone ahead or visit the website if you are going for the first time.

The cafe is located in a tiny strip mall with a Husky gas station.  Don't blink, you will miss it!  There is free parking out front.

When you go inside there are several small tables.  I am guessing the restaurant will hold about 50 people.

When we were seated, the decor reminded me a lot of what my Grandmother's home felt like. (If my Grandmother were Mexican) Plastic tablecloths draping the tables and Mexican knicknacks and decorations on the walls.  Comfortable.

The menu at Cafe del Sol is completely different from Tres Carnales.  You cannot even compare them.  They both have tacos, that's about it!  This is a restaurant where Mama is in the back doing all of the cooking and her two daughters are in the front taking care of the guests.

They have Mexican Cerveza and the Jarritos line of sodas.  Beer goes so nicely with Mexican food, helps calm the heat!

I wanted to try something that I had never tasted before so I chose the Beef Birria ($15 Tender pieces of Angus beef slow cooked in a broth of tomatoes and a selection of traditional Mexican spices and chilies)
Cafe del Sol Beef Birria

As you can see in the pics below, it is served with some mildly spiced Mexican rice and beans.  The beef was tender as described and not overly spicy but does have some heat. The rice was also spiced perfectly for me. The beans were something that I had not experienced before, great smooth texture.

My husband had the Chicken Enchiladas with Mole Sauce ($15 - rich  slowly simmered sauce using various chilies, nuts,seed, dried fruit and finished with chocolate)

Thankfully he let me have a taste of the Mole, it happens to be a favourite of mine that is hard to come by in Edmonton.  This Mole had a distinct cinnamon and chocolate flavour.  It is sooo good!  I think this will be my choice for my next visit!

We finished off our first visit with an order of churros. They were served with traditional cinnamon sugar and chocolate sauce to dip them in.  Sadly they were re-heated so the texture was not the best but they were still super tasty!

This was a wonderful meal in an environment that was cozy and made you feel at home.  I made sure to let one of the daughters know that I really enjoyed the food.  She brought Mama out for me so that I could thank her myself.  She smiled, and said "Thank you for coming to my home".  It really does feel like you have been invited into someone's home for a meal.  Comfort food, Mexican style!

I now have another favourite gem to add to my collection: Cafe del Sol Many thanks again to the family for the wonderful food and welcoming environment!
Very eclectic little place with amazing food and some very creative brunch specials. Definitely worth the drive outside the quarter.
Everything about this place is amazing! My favorites: Brisket Salad, 4 taco combo with plantains and the Jamaicanmecrazy Cocktail.

The sushi burrito was good overall. It was tasty. The burrito can be rolled a little tighter. It was falling apart while I was eating it. It was also a bit too wet from too much sauce being put on it.

Beer was good. But I tried to watch a football game of two top twenty teams and they wouldn't turn it on. They have like 30 TVs   The channel that was on was sports center   No one was watching it. Food was bad

I have been here 2 times now.  The second visit with friends was equally good.  One of the things I like about this place is the live jazz they have during dinner.  It's total reminiscent of a Jazz Club with the u-shaped leather booths if you're lucky to snag one.  But overall, the music can be heard from any place in the restaurant.

Be sure to book reservations on line at open table!  What's nice about this place is three areas of seatings.  Up front near when the band performs are small club leather chairs.  Towards the side is the bar, and high top seating.  And then lastly Towards the back is the dining area.  They also have an outdoor patio as well.  Given that pleasant weather in October you could've easily at outside and have dinner al fresco.

Not to be missed appetizers are the fried Brussels sprouts and Boudin Scotch Egg.  These were an absolute hit for our crowd.

Although it's not an expansive main entree list.  They hit one of everything.  Fish, chicken, steak and seasonal favorites.  Our choices amongst 8 people were across the board.  But all thought there dish was delish.  I had my fav fall specialty.  Short Ribs.   down it was a winner.

Between apps and main entree we ended up passing on dessert.  But I know they have a few offerings as well.

For a nice evening out with the spouse or significant other with live music and good food, be sure to check this place out.

I have been going to this car wash for many years. This is the first time they failed me. Since I have been thier for many years, I assumed it would be a 100% job in the past. That is the reason for not checking before leaving.

After I got home, thier was dirt marks on the doors and areas of the inside was not vacuum. The open point between the seats was dirty. 

I paid for the highest level of clean and extra for the floor mats. This what I get for my money. This place to me it is not worth your money.

I tended to like this place more than the other reviews. The staff was very nice and I got a spicy pork taco which was great with rice and a side salad. We also got nachos which were some of the best I've had in Nashville. Maybe a bit pricey, but I'd come back.

Ate here for a second time with my son. I love the atmosphere. The owners are truly Italian and it's humorous to hear then banter back and forth in Italian. If ya close your eyes you would think you were in Italy. We split a Caesar salad. Dressing was home made and ingredients very fresh. We chose the short rib pizza and it was very tasty. Only reason 4 star is for the price,  the pizza is kinda small. My son is 14 and is a growing boy with a teen appetite but still... kinda small for the price. We had no left overs.

two broken implants,much pain,errors upon errors,he wanted to keep digging but grew weary and had concerns about his ability to get it done. plus I need my jaw ( I'm funny about that ).
after the second break began referring to mentor with a passion,maybe should have found his mentor and let him/her perform the work. out 4000 and no tooth.....

Nashville hot chicken YUM! I ordered the 4/5 hotness and should have ordered the 5-"what the cluck"hot. The 4 "damn hot" was still really, really good. This is really a 5 star review but 4 stars only because of the extreme casual, super loud atmosphere. The line is out the door but worth the wait. Plus the people in Nashville are so friendly, the time flies by! Oh-PS-If you are a wine drinker--no wine--just beer. Still a top notch local flavor experience!!!
Service was good, staff very friendly. The food was overpriced for what we received. The risotto had very little flavor. Would not recommend ordering that.

Some of the reviews on here are a little harsh. I personally have a different experience. I used to go to the Beer Seller quite a bit back in my single days. Now I'm married with three kids, so I don't go to bars all that often. However, when my wife and I do get a night out with our friends, this is always the place we want to go.

The new ownership took the concept of the Beer Seller and improved it to what is now the Yard Line. I think their menu is unique and edgy, granted, if you are dieting, then you might want to eat before you go, because I can only imagine the calories in the Craig Burger! They have several different beer choices, and the staff has always been very helpful, and quick with what you are requesting. 

It's a fun environment. The bar has pool tables, shuffle board tables (my personal favorite), and various arcade games. They also have karaoke nights, which is a great way to hear people try to sing, then tell them they are actually good, and hope to see the American Idol judges rip them some day. 

I enjoy going there. It's one of the few bars in the Castleton area that hasn't turned completely ghetto, and where I don't fear getting shot!

MaGerk's seems like it would shine as a fun place to watch a game, so when I went for dinner I wasn't expecting anything more than some decent American bar/pub food. However, no one was happy with what they ordered for dinner. The burger with bacon jam was actually pretty bad- it tasted way too sweet. Despite being hungry, myself and the other person that ordered it in our party didn't even finish it. I've had maple bacon jam quite a few times before but this was so sweet I couldn't see how anyone would like it. On the upside, the nachos were good. the craft beer they had on special was extremely discounted, it's a nice venue, and the service was friendly.

prices went up on food AND drinks so we've been looking around to find another go-to neighborhood spot, trying a few new places.  I'll still go here for special occasions (went today for a birthday) but it will no longer will be our regular stop.  Jonathon is STILL the best, nicest and cuutest bartender ever!
Typical cheap low quality chinese food. Some people are looking for that but I prefer Asian food from a bit more high end establishment like Umami.
Great little place in a great little neighborhood with tasty crepes and pretty people.  I ate the crepe. I bought the t-shirt.  I'll go back.

Absolutely love the coffee here at Ren. There are so many other delicious-sounding beverages to choose from - but the coffee here has stolen my heart! I just have one request - please offer an organic whole milk option - If you had this, your coffee shop would be perfect!

Never had a bad experience at the airport with Enterprise. Lines are often shorter than others, people are, if anything, too happy and smiley. Maybe I'm just grumpy since we go down there its February when its sucky up here and lovely in Florida. Suggestion: if you book a car, go back online and do it a second time...the prices change based on what cars they have available now...not later. That's weird. Make sure you get a map from them before you leave the airport, and don't let them up sell you to a bigger car.
This place is really good. Had the special shrimp PO boy-awesome. I enjoyed the red beans, bread pudding and etoufee sides. Very cool place.

Finally went in after too many people recommended the place. Totally lives up to the reviews. Excellent tasting meat - and it is just as people describe, meat simply on tortillas that you add toppings to. Authentic, local - but not intimidating. The guy behind the register was totally helpful taking time to describe the different meats (eyes, tongue, cheek...). They prepared a to-go for me and included everything - no fail! Need to go back on the weekend to get the tripe.

Things I like about Sue's Produce Market:
1. Great Location, esp right next to Rittenhouse Square and close to Walnut Street shopping
2. Some great deals:
   a. fresh cilantro leaves for $1.00!!
   b. Jalapeno pepper for $0.08!!
3. Great variety of nuts, dried berries, fruit, and most veggies
4. Very friendly staff

Things I don't like: 
1. High prices for milk, eggs, yogurt, among other things
2. Small place
3. Only open til 6pm 
4. Accepts cash only

Quality  vs. Quantity...which do _you_ prefer?   My choice is clear.

The only reason they get a star at all is because I have to provide at least one to get this posted. 

Called cab to get from hotel in Carmel to the airport. Cabbie pulls up 10 mins later than promised, jumps out to "use the bathroom" and comes strolling out eight minutes later with the hotel's free breakfast in his hands! Apologizing, he then stuffs the breakfast in his mouth and starts plugging the airport into his GPS. THE AIRPORT. Seriously. 

I tell the driver, again, that I am in a very large hurry and am in real danger of missing my flight. So the GPS has him taking side streets, and I ask him, "Do you know how to get to the airport? Why are we taking side streets?" If there was traffic, sure - but it's early Saturday morning. Driver was about to make a right turn, and instead, he jerks the car across four lanes of traffic and makes a U-turn through the tailend of a yellow light and heads towards the highway.

Finally, we pass the hotel again, heading back the way we came, and I asked him if he was going to keep the meter running since he couldn't figure out where to go... he reset it but didn't say a word. 

I was in the back of the cab as I started to write this. It was all I could do to distract myself from the flight I was about to miss. 

This was also the ONLY cab I'd been in all week not working on the flat rate system that ALL cabs in Indianapolis and the surrounding areas had in effect through Super Bowl week/weekend.

Had nothing but great experiences with Indy cabs that entire week. But that was a mess. AVOID.
This Kroger is always busy no matter what time we go but they keep it well stocked and clean. They keep the checkout lines running smooth.

Came here mainly for the Dim Sum I would said it was worth the try and overall the food was "okay" the service I would say needs to better with having a continuous speed of food coming around so if your expecting food being forced in your face with variety this will only give you half of that.

Went here on a Thursday night. The location is a bit sketchy next to an old motel and the restaurant itself is dated but I LOVE the food! Service was friendly and prompt. There is a fairly large selection of cooked AND raw food so there's something for everyone. On top of your typical sushi dishes there is wonton soup, BBQ ribs, calamari, shrimp, teriyaki beef, etc. 

They also offer ice cream at the end! I know that sounds disgusting on top of fish but it actually isn't as bad as it sounds. 

Overall, I will return. Don't be scared to give this place a try!

I hate leaving bad reviews but today was just unacceptable. I went in at 1256 and placed my order. The people working here had no idea what was going on, they all looked lost. I ordered 2 chicken sandwiches, a bacon cheese burger 2 smash fries and a regular fries as well. I waited 30 minutes for my order and when I got home, the food was not up to quality. the bacon cheeseburger was burnt, I mean crispy burnt. my chicken sandwiches were cold and my 2 smash fries had so much oil on them they were not eat able. paying 32.26 for food that's not good sucks. the apologised for the wait and gave me a $2 off coupon like it would make up for it.here are some pics of the oily fries

I'm not sure why this place is always out of control crowded. I just do not understand the CRZY lines at the place.  isn't it just regular coffee?  their hot chocolate is un sweetened and just ok. They obviously have good coffee but I'm just not sure it's worth the CRZY lines all the time.  The best part of this place it their water machine that dispenses carbonated chilled water. I can come all day just for their water.
How many times do you have to say I only want mayonnaise on the burger, before they understand it and get it right? About 7. The food wasn't too bad, but not worth the money.
Bangles is a refreshing find when eating in Indian restaurants had become a sort of boring. The Kozhi milagu rasam is a classic not to be missed. Overall experience was awesome at a great price.

What can I say, Rosebud loves it! Very clean with staff that actually appear to love their job and the dogs. Great choice of extra activities, pool, socializing with the other pups, etc. They provide you with a report of your dog's stay at checkout...behavior, eating, potty, etc. And are happy to provide an update over the phone. My friend from Boston called to check on Rosebud when I was out of the country, only to be jealous bc she had just come in from the pool. Also greatly appreciate special pricing discounts to make affordable. And they are vigilant about the dogs shot records.

Everyone is very friendly, and personable. They don't look like 'hate their job' so to speak. Not to mention their attention to detail and quickness. If I ever have to pawn something again, that's my first choice!

Yemanja Brasil is a great, eclectic eatery tucked in the center of the historic Benton Park neighborhood. It's down the street from Venice Cafe, The Filling Station, and Fraser's, but in a mostly residential district. Yemanja is in a historic building and the decor is old and new together.  Very eclectic and colorful.  They have a great patio.

Because Yemanja is off the beaten track and a wee bit pricey, it's been awhile...  enter Groupon... 

I've had the Brazilian caipirnhas (rum and sugar cocktail) before, but on my recent visit, we had the drink special of the night (Cadillac Caipirnhas), OMG! Fantastic!! It was more like a sangria/margarita inspired caipirnhas with fresh fruit and a little triple sec added to the Brazilian rum mojito. Two of those (each) at $8 each was the most expensive part of our meal. So yummy and buzz-worthy...  ;-) 

Most entrees were a tip over the $20 mark, so we opted to go after the appetizer menu and treat it like tapas. We almost ordered several apps separately, but the waitress kindly informed us that they had a sampler platter that was off of the menu. It was $11 and had an empanada, some fried yucca w/ dipping sauces, and some of the most amazing bruschetta that I've ever had. We also ordered the mussels ($9). The yucca and empanadas were a bit overcooked, but still yummy. The sauces were delish and the mussels w/unlimited bread for dipping was a treat. Honestly, anything would have tasted good with those drinks... We were happy customers! 

Even though they are a bit pricey with their entrees, I love the shrimp entree with coconut milk . They also have phenomenal risottos and stews. Most can be split.   A few of the stews are bland to American standards, but they are typical Brasilian dishes, so you can't fault the restaurant!  

Definitely worth a try!!
My favorite Thai place in Indy! The food is always amazing! Their prices are also pretty reasonable!

Long review! Went here for a late dinner/celebration with some friends. We "researched" the website extensively for menu, parking info (their map is very useful!), and hours. We had a large party so I tried to make a reservation for 15. I was told that I needed to put down a credit card for such a large group and to ensure the minimum food/drink purchase of $15/per person. I decided not to make a reservation and just walk-in (I was not going to put down a card for that much)!

Worked out fine for us though. We arrived around 10:15 pm with about 8 people. I could see why they needed the credit card reserved because the  inside of the restaurant isn't very large. Luckily a table left so instead of an hour wait, we got seated real quick. We had a large table and when more of our friends showed up (at random times), we just added more chairs. The table easily accommodated everyone and our server was nice and was not flustered one bit with all the additional people.

We missed the live music that night but there was a DJ playing a variety of music (some old school thrown in too). The music was a little too loud though and I would have preferred it turned down a notch to make conversation easier. 

On to the food! Their dinner menu is still served until 11pm and then they switched to lounge menu. We made it in time for dinner. Their food is tapas style with a few dishes that are larger. I ordered the Shrimp & Grits and Authentic Nachos with Cuban roast pork added. 

Shrimp & Grits: not what I expected. There was a scoop of grits in the middle of the bowl cradled by a smothering creole sauce.The menu called it étouffée.  There were large shrimps and andouille sausage bites. Not a bad dish but just not as flavorful as I thought. 

Authentic Nachos with Cuban roast pork ($4 extra): This was HUGE! We actually didn't finish it. The roast pork was flavorful and there was a lot of it! The nachos were evenly layered so that each bite had toppings. I'm actually surprised that I liked the nachos over the Shrimp & Grits. 

I tried some of my friends' dishes including the truffled mac & cheese (overrated and kind of dry for a mac & cheese. You can't just throw truffle in there and expect it to be amazing on it's own). There were two orders of this at the table and both were left unfinished. The Roasted Garlic Frites (fries) were good. I don't like potatoes but I liked these especially with the malt vinegar aioli (unique flavor) and chipotle ketchup. The Spicy Tuna Tartare wasn't bad. The wonton chips were humongous and the tuna had a nice sesame flavor. 

After dinner, we headed upstairs to the rooftop bar. The rooftop bar is definitely way smaller in person than in the pictures! Fly Bar kind of has a hipster, trendy vibe but the people are casual. There were tall tables and some benches for seating. Bar wasn't too crowded at the time. The view was okay... just a lot of buildings blocking anything pretty to look at. Some friends had a few drinks but then we got bored so we left for other adventures. 

Overall, not a bad place. I want to come again maybe earlier in the evening to catch the live music.

This place has a great local vibe and everyone is always really nice.  I fun place to catch a drink, watch a game, and the food is good.  The lunch stuff is all solid like chicken cheese steak, the burger, or crab cake sandwhich.  At dinner I have had salmon and tuna and loved them both.
Lousy OVERPRICED food & drink, but usually great music. Whether it's worth waiting in line online to purchase tickets or outside the club to get in is still to be determined........

What happened to our favorite breakfast spot!??? We have been coming here for 20 years and this most recent visit was so disappointing. The ran out of croissants at 10:00???? The quiche crust was tough. The pastries were more like rolls!!! We are broken hearted!!!
The dessert here is really delicious and the staff are very friendly. I will go there again

This review is for the drinks only...the new old fashion is very good...wife had the white sangria...but come on...that doesn't count...the new old fashion is actually a well made old fashioned using Jamison instead of a bourbon...and let's face it...if you're drinking old fashioned  you're just interested in the alcohol anyway'

Compared to other high end department stores (Saks, Ogylvies, Harrod's, gallery Lafayette) this place sucks. While I am often tempted into buying more than a few beautiful clothing items by Ogylvies when I'm in Montreal, I haven't purchased anything at this store in years. The service is horrid. Truly bad. You can walk around browsing for hours and no one will ever acknowledge you. I've visited when I'm decked out in designer duds and when I'm just schlepping around the downtown, it makes no difference, I'm never acknowledged. If you ever did get one of the ice queens to acknowledge you, there's nothing to buy. The plump section doesn't seem to be there any more. The lingerie section hosts about 3 bras and a single package of stockings. The scarves are humdrum and the shoes, while some are lovely, never come in my size. The makeup section is so-so. They have some unique brands but make no mistake, this isn't Sephora, you can't try things out easily.  One big plus, Holts is great at inspiring me to save my cash for for vacation shopping.

I'm always looking for a good Korean restaurant in the area because it's a rare gem to find. I heard this place is better than Koreana but that it's still just okay. After coming here with a friend, I agree with the rumors I've heard. It's just OKAY Korean food. I think it's a little expensive to be honest for the quality of the food but if I was a student in the area craving for Korean food, I could see myself coming here often. I got a typical traditional dish, kimchi jjigae. Not the best I've had but not bad either. It's medium spicy, wish it had more meat in it for the $9.99 price. I could see a lot of college students getting their Korean fixin here.

It's a PCA church. Rock band for worship and watered-down spiritual songs. Theology was reformed and about what I expected, but their new secondary pastor (this is 2018) made inaccurate references in his sermon (Abraham didn't take Isaac to the Temple in Jerusalem). We'll probably visit again when come back to the area.

This review is for DAN! Wow!!! Found out it was his first week and he really did SUCHHHHH a good job. Attention to detail, timeliness, super friendly, and sooo great. What a good addition to this location!!! Super happy with the service. Would come back and request dan!

Easy to find and enough parking in the plaza. It has a good amount of choices for food but it's mainly seafood. So if you never been to Chickie's & Pete's and you're from out of state then you need to try the crab fries. They are the best fries in the city probably even the state just go and enjoy yourself.

We visited on opening night; perhaps it's not fair for me to review NYAJ quite yet since there were a few minor glitches you'd expect the first few hours of business.  But, we really loved the place. First the oops: Our waitress up on the second level had obviously never served tables before: 

-she didn't know how take customers in order, 
-grab drinks from the bar promptly (we watched as they sat on a tray at the bar for more than ten minutes while managers and the bartender walked by the warming glasses and failed to ask whom they belonged to), 
-she had no clue how to work the tablet order-taking system (she explained she was not tech-savvy), which made ordering slow and frustrating, 
-we had to flag her down to get refills of empty water glasses.  
      
Although she was a dud, we noticed every other waiter seemed efficient; many of them from the Glen Mills and Boston locations on hand to train.

     Now, the good things about NYAJ: this brand new restaurant was a delight.  A pair of happy hostesses pushed open the doors to greet us.  We were seated right away, having made our reservation on Open Table.  The maitre d' was light hearted and fun.  The industrial space is glorious, two floors of dining with great looking bars on each floor. Snippets of famous movies projected on a giant brick wall for both stories of customers to see. It was hard to believe that only a few years ago, we were in the same building buying teen clothes for our girls at an American Eagle (and, before that, it was a movie theatre). Our food was great - there wasn't a bad meal in the bunch.  It was actually difficult to decide to to order, there were so many nice choices.  The only B grade went to the pizza: it was 'just alright' and the crust was not crunchy as promised.

     The renovated/re-styled St James on the pedestrian street was supposed to open the same night; we heard it did not, perhaps to allow NYAJ to do its thing.  Those of us on this section of the Main Line with slim dining pickins are lucky to have this addition.

Baked bread appetizer was excellent. Farm house salad also very good. Pizza, meh. We will come back and try a pasta dish or sandwich. Would come back but not for the pizza. If you like the artisan style of pizza you will probably enjoy it, I prefer a traditional crust. Staff was nice and friendly.
They never have my daughters formula! Never! I have to go to Walmart or the target in Brentwood. Most staff here aren't friendly either

Excellent customer service and work quality. Had to have a drain pipe replaced underneath our shower. Had several quotes and all were around the same price point. We liked the folks from Morton the best. The job ended up taking slightly longer than they thought but the price stayed the same and the end result was perfect. We have recommended them to my inlaws and we would definitely use this company again. I think the family business aspect came through again after the work was done when we received a thank you card. That was a nice touch.

I was surprised, I heard so many great things about the pizza here, but I left disappointed.  I would compare the pizza with Mac and Manco's (or whatever they are calling themselves now), though not as good.  Now, Mac and Manco's isn't really the style of pizza that I am big on anyway, so take it how you will.  

The place is a nice enough bar, I really dig the astetics.  Service is friendly, but slow.  Small working class town bar.  I think it is cash only, which didn't really both me because this was the one day that I happened to have cash on me.  Prices weren't too bad.  The two stars are mostly for the pizza, nothing really wrong with the bar.

Had dinner there last night. They were very accommodating of our party of 14. The food was great and the portions were big. Chicken wings and kick ass fries were a big hit. We then topped it off with the best bread pudding I have ever had, and I have had a lot. Service was great even with our large party. I will go there again. prices were very reasonable.

Fresh-tasting sushi at an affordable price makes for an easy dinner decision that I'll repeat during my next work trip to Nashville.

Zushipoke had everything packaged for pick up, and on a Wednesday evening at 6 pm, my to-go order was placed and ready by the time I drove over from the hotel (app 20 min).

It's the perfect choice for when you're in the mood for sushi but not wanting to spend a ton nor resort to grocery store sushi that's been sitting out all day. I had a couple rolls and enjoyed both!

The burgers here are so tasty and there's always something yummy on the beer list.  The folks working at lunch time are great. It can take a bit of time for your food to come out, so it's probably not the best lunch stop if you're in a hurry.

It was my birthday and I was looking for a more exclusive spot to enjoy some distinguished drinks with some fabulous people...boy did this place let us down. 

First we waited in line-which is to be expected, as the establishment is trying to give off a certain aura about the place, no problem we were prepared...even though it was raining. The bouncer said 10-15 minutes, no big deal. He then proceed to text and talk on his phone...and when 8 people emerged from the basement establishment, he proceeded downstairs...myself and 4 friends followed. (Keep in mind this was all of our first times there). We walk into a small loungy atmosphere and find a table that hasn't been cleared yet, but we aren't picky and sat down.

Next thing you know the server is asking the bouncer what the deal is with our table...he takes no time to start screaming obscenities in front of us saying he didn't let us in and he didn't know what the f we were doing. I kindly interrupted asking if there was a problem, and he said "YEA YOU NEED TO GET THE HELL UP SO WE CAN DO OUR JOBS"

Wow what a way to be greeted at a "classy lounge" our first time AND on my birthday. Now granted we didn't know we were suppose to wait upstairs, the bouncer didn't pull himself away from his phone long enough to ask us to wait or even make eye contact before he proceeded down the stairs. We had no idea, there were no rules prefaced.

At this point I wanted to leave, because honestly you don't talk to myself or my friends that way regardless if we didn't follow the unspoken expectations..however my friends reminded me to not let him ruin my night. I decided to ignore the ridiculous behavior and at least give it a chance. 

There are quite a few cocktail options from the prohibition era which were a nice mix of many types of specialty liquors, I had a punch which was decent, but nothing great-not really even worth the $13 I paid for it. The server seemed annoyed and only apologized for the bouncers behavior after he delivered our check. 

At one point we saw 2 tables open up and a group of 4 minorities come in who asked to be seated, the bouncer literally told him it would be a 45 minute wait...now I don't want to judge, but that just didn't seem right.

Honestly it takes a lot to make me mad, but this place is trying WAY TOO HARD to be something it no longer is. I found the rich history to be lost in a place supposedly full of it. Honestly I left there disappointed in the service mostly, and in the lack of historical validity, and it's sad because none of my friends nor I will ever be back. 

There are so many more exclusive speak-easy's in the city in much better locations, with better cocktails, and more of an exclusive feel. The only reason I even went to this place was because of the good reviews, but take this review seriously, it's just a wannabee.

We stopped by Earl of Sandwich tonight and truly enjoy our visit. Delicious Food and nice Restaurant .  I had the Holiday Turkey Sandwich  Stuffing and Cranberry ..  Mouthwatering !! And my wife had a chicken wrap .. She loved it as well ..  Great  Iced Tea and Iced Coffee as well .. 2 Thumbs up !!  Thanks EOS ..
Ordered the  fish since it was Friday fish fry. It was awful. It tasted and looked like frozen fish. The fries were under cooked. Chef needs to check out The Tridents fish
A bit threadbare but always safe and a good experience overall. Free wifi is slow, but you can upgrade to higher speeds. Staff is ALWAYS friendly, eager, and helpful.

I've eaten at this place back in the 90s and it was way better. I believe its new management as the flavors were not the same. Pizza seemed cheap yet the price was of a higher end pizza. This pizza is no better than pizza hut but without the chain. I would actually rather had Pizza hut at the time.  This was my pizza while growing up but now its just a hole in the wall going the wayside of out of business.  Not able to recommend.
Now here is a place that makes a good cheesesteak with large portions!  You definitely get your money's worth. I highly recommend!!

Nice outdoor seating
Great salsa, the perfect blend of seasoning and spice
Excellent ceviche
Awesome margaritas
And great tuna tostada salad

Fabulous hotel but horrible customer service! The staff here doesn't greet you if you have a question. The staff at Mila restaurant are much nice and provide great customer service. Very disappointed with the customer service at the hotel. I would provide 5 stars if the staff was friendly. Hotel is situated a few blocks away from Bourbon street. If you're looking for a great place to eat Domenica in the Roosevelt hotel is delicious.

Le daría cero estrellas, pase una mala experiencia el día de mi cumpleaños no dejaron sentar a los invitados ya que faltaban solo 2 personas por llegar, esta prohibido cantar cumpleaños, terminamos comiendo en el restaurante de al lado llamado Kuchi Sushi  (perdieron una gran venta éramos 10 personas ) no volveré

Mike represented us with the listing and selling of our house. We had never met Mike but someone we trusted referred us to him. We were very pleased with Mike and his results. He always looked and presented himself in a very professional manner. The price he suggested listing our house at was more than we thought we would get. He was right. Our house sold in 5 days for $2000 less than our asking price. If we ever get crazy and think about selling our new house I would definitely use Mike again.

Let me start by saying I feel bad for the darn polar bear who got stuck living at a zoo in Tucson AZ. I don't know what he did to land that gig, but everytime I see him all I can think is: poor, poor bastard.
Our little zoo is a really great way to spend a few hours. During warmer months I would recommend going in the morning. The animals (for obvious reasons) are not as active when it's 110 outside. Go figure right? Reid Park Zoo is great for families too. Because of it's smaller size it is not an all day exhausting ordeal. There is even a water play area for the little ones if they get cranky and need to cool off. 
Along with the obligatory gift shop full of stuff nobody really needs, there is a cafe with lunch options and snacks. The food is overpriced and nothing to write home about. There are also several snack stands throughout the zoo in case you enjoy munching on Dippin Dots while viewing the animals. 
If you like to visit the zoo often, they do have memberships which are reasonably priced. Military members and their families can get a discount off of a yearly membership. Daily tickets however are not discounted.

We went here for lunch yesterday. We waited over an hour before our food came out. Apparently our  server -Noy. She made no apology but her staff did. This Noy apparently takes everyone's orders and then has her staff deliver drinks and food. I would suggest that they have at least two order takers so this doesn't happen again. 
Food wasn't very good either. We won't be recommending or returning to this restaurant.

My dentist in Omaha recommended this place to me as the best burgers he's ever had.  He apparently makes it a point to eat at O'Connell's at least once every time he's in town. 

So when my dad came to town, I brought him here with some friends to test the dentist's assertion.  They WERE good burgers, and good onion rings, and good St. Louis atmosphere.

However, the burgers were just pretty good, definitely not the best I've had.  That award goes to Seamus McDaniel hzandzdown.  

At any rate, give this place a go.  I will again.

-------

EDIT:  I went back.  The burgers are only average.  I'm not especially impressed, except by the prices.  I'm not nuts about their house dressing, and their refusal to carry other dressings is kind of annoying.  

And paper plates?  Really?  I guess it keeps clean-up easy and cheap, but really?  

However, those gooey butter cookies are fantastic.  They're no better or worse than what you could find at the grocery store or elsewhere, though, so not worth a special trip.

Overall, I have no major complaints about O'Connell's, but I don't have much praise either.  If you want the best burger of your life, keep looking (go look in Dogtown...).  I'm all for supporting local institutions, and this seems to be one of them; however, I doubt I'll be craving what Paul G. rightly calls "pretty so/so bar fare" from O'Connell's very often at all.
Hands down the best sushi place! You must go to Charlie's and try it! The salmon melts like butter in your mouth.

I picked up an order of brisket it was super soft and tasty highly recommend. The grits were really special really different than the way it is served everywhere, I recommend it. I was not convinced on the banana pudding it tasted like they added too much whipping cream like the one they already sell made at supermarkets.

It's good, but can't beat Prince's. The chicken and shrimp is fantastic, but skip the mac and cheese...tastes like frozen mac and cheese with spicy chicken seasoning thrown in and it is disgusting. And unfortunately, I have to agree with the common opinion here that the owner is very rude. Ask him questions about the food, menu or prices and you'll get a free side of attitude with your order. Another downside: they don't have a soda fountain, only bottles.

The food is a little bit tasty ( i have ordered steak) and the qaulity of meat and sushi is very good , also the service is perfect. I went there after was disappointed from Hachi (they dont accept walk ins). We got the warm greeting from the hostess, they are so friendly and especially our server, he is so attentive to us, i would come again.

Came here with a Bachelorette party (called ahead for reservations). Got put in such a cute private room, we were all very excited (12 of us).  The food was great! We had pizzas, pastas, salads.  The service was a bit lack luster - in a 3 hour dinner we only got 1 round of drinks bc the server was no where to be found.. and one of the girls got charged for NINE pizzas (we didnt even have nine all together....but wouldn't the server look at the bill and realize that one person couldn't possibly eat NINE pizzas?!) Anyways, if the service had been better, I'm sure we would have loved this place!

This place has a great beer selection.  A good food/snack selection.  The atmosphere is your typical industrial feel which is nice.  I walked in the door, and there was a group of people 5-6 and a cat sitting on the table.  Then another cat came out.  Needless to say I didn't order any food and I'm not sure I will go back.  #rearend. #catsdintbelongontables

Oki Maki shares a space and an entrance with Pirate Crab (newly opened restaurant). Upon walking in, we didn't know where to look first. The friendly staff appears to be in-tune with this, as they witnessed our perplexed expressions and asked if we were there for Oki Maki or Pirate Crab.  

As referenced by other reviewers, the ordering process is very disjointed. My Love was in the mood for maki and I was in the mood for ramen. The main menu for Oki Maki is listed on the wall above the food assembly space, however, the appetizer menu is listed on the back wall near the utensils and napkins. Very easy to miss. You are unable to order ramen at the counter, rather, you have to order via an iPad along the same wall that leads to Pirate Crab. He ordered his maki/sushi burrito and I was instructed to visit the iPad area to order my ramen. I wished there was a way to be alerted of this ahead of time, as my ramen came out a bit after he already received his maki. There was a sign on the floor under the "iPad bar" that provided some ramen ordering instructions, but again, it was very easy to miss. And the ramen sign referenced the "Ramen Room", which is now the space for Pirate Crab. I think a new sign is in order...just saying. 

A receipt is printed out for your ramen order and you bring it to the register to pay. But what if there is a long line for the non-ramen orders? What if people are taking forever to decide upon their meal (food-assembly style)? Do you go all the way at the end of the line and wait behind the people who are placing their orders, or do you just go right up to the register to pay since you're not actually ordering anything at the counter? But if you DO head right to the register, then you're cutting the line. Again, very disjointed. 

The ramen was good (albeit a little overly-salty) and he felt his sushi burrito was "okay". 

The flow of the space, the limited seating, and the ordering process felt super clunky to us. "Confusion" was definitely the theme of the night among us and the other patrons.

The food and my waitress were great! I was there with my young daughter, we are only in town for one night and really wanted to check this place out. She asked about playing skeeball the whole time we were eating. Loaded up my card for the arcade.. it's Tuesday at 7pm and we were the only ppl at the games but 2 balls into our first game, a girl came over and made us leave because it was reserved for a party. Every area in the place was reserved apparently or they just didn't want us there. So I had to carry my crying child out and now I have a $20 game card I'll never use. I could care less about getting kicked out but Breaking my kid's heart is enough to make me furious. Will easily never return here and I'll make sure to spread the word on how rudely we were treated.

Sunday brunch buffet is great here. There's so much to choose from. My sisters and I sat on the terrace over looking the ocean while a Jack Johnson type of musician played on the side. This has to be one of the best Sunday brunches I have been to. Great service! The he'd also came out and talked to us to chat for a bit and asked us how our food was. They only valet here.

You can't possibly think this is real Mexican food. Nope. Not at these prices. I get the vibe and it isn't the least bit authentic. This is total template cuisine, made in a corporate boardroom somewhere. This could be Greek, so make some tzatsiki sauces and chop cucumbers; this could be Mediterranean so make some hummus and chop up some ----well. cucumbers. It just so happens that someone discovered how to blend dried chile peppers and make some fairly decent salsas and made up a menu with vaguely Mexican or Latin dishes.

First up is the service. Whoa, dude. Chill. It's in your face, almost yelling (it's noisy) but they do try to be officious. Ordered the salsa mexicana ---which comes with chips of course and it better if it's all costing $6. You didn't think chips and salsa were free, did you? I passed on the guac, which is all of $14 because while I like it. I couldn't finish it by myself.

Ordered the carne asada, which was a hanger steak, so not even the best cut. I really wish it hadn't come with the "creamy poblano corn rice" because that was a mound of glop. This was something that Zarela Sanchez made at her old Zarela's restaurant in NYC (now defunct), but this one was far inferior. Why was rice by itself not an option? Why add a creamy version that has flavorless poblano pepper slices mixed in with corn? It didn't work.
