There were no beans on the plate, so I had to make tacos out of very-small tortillas and the pre-sliced beef. Pet peeve: why do you have to slice my meat for me?

I admit the pineapple margarita was pretty delicious, but not all that strong.

To finish the meal and to prove that this really was a Mexican experience, the dessert was the El Vez special. Get this: a slice of flourless chocolate cake, pieces of banana, some peanut butter, some fudge and a scoop of vanilla ice cream. Looked nice too. Ah, the memories of my Mexican mom making this for us when we were just mere chirrun! NOT!

Final q: El Vez is the name of a performer who dresses like Elvis Presley. Is this place named after this kitschy performer?

These guys are FANTASTIC. I was in and out in less than 20 minutes. Told a friend how fast it was and ended up taking her car for her since I had the day off. These guys are highly recommended in my book!
Thank you for swift and accurate service!

Killer cocktails, great food, and a pleasantly-relaxed vibe. Chef Elmi is two for two with ITV.
The bartenders here are on point. I've had dinner, drinks, and dessert here in every possible permutation of those three options and they've consistently delivered. It's an enjoyable time every time.


One of the best late-night snacks in the city: Death Star + Beef Fat Biscuit.
The tacos are great! The yellow salsa is so good - the other 2 are mediocre. Margs are good. Always have great service. Thanks!

Terrible experience!!! I called and they don't take orders over the phone, they rush you and they argue with you. The lady that answered the phone was just restocking the place ! Apparently that's more important,

Had the Chile Nogada and Empenadas. 

Empenadas were decent. Could have been a bit less doughy but the contents were tasty. The portion was almost too large. 

The Chile Nogada was meh. The sauce was both too creamy and a bit overwhelming. The contents were an interesting mix of flavors but lacked balance in the seasoning. 

The free chips and salsas were actually the highlight of the meal. The salsas were fantastic, with a decent touch of heat. 

This restaurant warrants a second visit to explore other items on the menu at some future date.

I would give this place ten stars if I could. I am not sure if this place cares what kind of review you give them. Not because they don't value what their customers think, but mostly because they know their food is good.  They do not come up with dishes to impress others and maybe that is the reason why their food comes out to be so delicious.  When you read the menu, you are like hmmmm okay what's the big deal about this sandwich; but when you get the food you know exactly what the hype is about.  I was surprised that the place was not packed, it could be because we went around their closing time and beat the lunch hour rush.  We had to have the collard green sandwich, and when you take the first bite your mind goes wait this is something green and it still taste this good.  So, you take another bite to make sure your taste buds didn't deceive you, then another and another because you just can't believe how good this thing is.  Try to decipher what's in the sauce but I couldn't.  All I know it was tangy, salted perfectly, the collard greens were soft but not mushy and tofu so firm.  Right there was cabbage in it too, now that I think of it such a healthy sandwich.  Then you make a mistake and ask your friend to take a bite, that is when half your sandwich disappears.  We also had the chicken pot pie, which was like an empanada but their version, their lamb neck roti was warmly seasoned and covered in cool yogurt sauce.  This place satisfies your soul and if you are in New Orleans your trip is incomplete without eating here.  Highly highly recommend it, not only because the food is good but also because they cook from their heart and that love, and warmth shows in their food.  I would have stopped by before heading to the airport again but sadly my flight was close to the time they open; but this will be my first  stop when I go back.  Now stop reading the review and just go and experience it for yourself.

I just had dinner here with my husband and sister, one word-DELICIOUS! This was the best restaurant I've been to in a while. There is a tasteful menu, with something for everybody. We tried the beef brisket, lobster pot pie, pasta, and margarita pizza. It was all mouth watering delicious. Good portions, not too big, rich, and flavorful. We ate on the second story outdoor patio, the atmosphere was great, the service was fantastic. We didn't have to ask for a thing, they brought us extra napkins, and kept our water glasses full. The waiter was friendly and helpful, I would recommend this restaurant to everyone!

WORST customer service ever. There was a huge line starting, to wait for whatever of the 3 registers were open next and SO many people cutting in front just not caring. I said something to someone and the guy said "sorrry" and just stayed where he was. He cut the entire line. And when I finally got to check out I told the lady at the register ( HALLD on my receipt ) and she had the audacity to say " this is my last week so I don't really care". Thankfully I only came here for a costume for Halloween. Never again 

HAHAH like are you joking. This place is a joke.
Love their fresh cheap tofu and Thai ingredients. Sad to see my favorite corner store closing it's doors

Consistently rude employees that blame the customer for lost packages. I've never been anything but nice to them and they still ignore all my requests. I only do pick-ups there and try to have all I can mailed to work to avoid that. All mailings I go to 4th street.

Thankful for having the BEST mailman possible on our route so I don't have to deal w/ this terrible location often.
Why is planned parenthood more expensive then a regular gynecologist.  This is ridiculous.   They don't cover most things and charge more then a regular doctor.  This is just stupid

Perfect for what ever your looking for. Whether you're looking for a delicious healthy veggie version that's dairy and gluten free, or a grub worthy bbq this is your place! Both my girlfriend and I absolutely love this place and it's easily our favorite pizza place in SB. They also have a selection of beers, some on tap, and a nice little selection of wines. Don't pass this place up, you'll be sorry you did.

What a small friendly local bar. No athletes found here, but lots of drinking enthusiasists! Long bar top, pool table and small eating area in the back. Small table outside.

5 stars for their fried, crispy not oily chicken wings. You can get a combo of flavors for the wings, too. Pretty large pieces of wings. The skin was crispy, not soft or mushy. Got the Orleans style which tasted like a peanut butter hint of a thai type of sauce. The dry rub was awesome with a spicy parmesian crispy topping.

Small variety of items on menu but quality char-hamburgers and tacos. Lots of parking.

The only time I go to McDonalds is for the shamrock shakes. This location is the worst at actually having them.  They have been advertised for the last week, including new flavors, and they don't have them.  Same thing last year it was like every 3rd trip they would actually have them. Oh the shake machine is down or we don't have the mix.

Took my cat here for breathing issues, the vet said his lungs sounded "harsh" that was it. 

I took my dog here for routine blood work and 3 hours later she was vomiting from then until the next day. The vet combined 2-3 vaccines into one and caused an adverse reaction that almost killed my dog. Luckily fluids helped her. I don't recommend this place to anyone. Not to mention the time when a cat was stuck in their walls and they neglected to let anyone in to help.

Wow the most unprofessional contractor I have ever dealt with.  I was closing on a house and needed the items corrected that the inspector noted in his report. Eddie told me he was going to Disney until Monday  for five days but would take care of the wiring problems before closing. Monday morning came and no Eddie. Called him and he assured me they would be there in the afternoon and start work. The afternoon came and he called and said he can't do the job because it was to hard for him. They waited five days to tell me they didn't want to bother with the job leaving me scrambling to find another electrician to clean up his mess three days before closing. I did find an electrician and after inspecting the previous work they did they suggested I file a complaint and get their license revoked because of the illegal and not to code work they did. If he didn't quit I never would have found out how incompetent and dangerous their previous work was.

First of all when you pull into the parking lot it looks like you are pulling into a dark alley. However, the inside is actually quite nice and very big. And then you get to the buffet....

There isn't much of a selection it's mostly just western food. I think that everything on my plate was cold and it almost felt like the "hot roast beef" came out of the fridge. I was not impressed and won't be going back. Oh and by the way it costs almost 25$ plate. Save money and go somewhere nice where you can actually get a hot meal!

Overrated food and dated setting. Went there for a family dinner and the meals seem like they are pretty on brand for a place called "Concordville Inn". However I would say if you are 65+ the rating would be more in the 3-4 star range.

Good pizza and cheese steaks in the D-town area. Kind of hard to locate, it is on a back street, but a lot of guys at work recommended Sam's hands down when I moved to the area (work in Coatesville).

If I'm grabbing pizza or a few sandwiches for a party, Sam's is typically the place I hit up unless someone has another request. Usually try to call ahead because the wait can be a bit as they get busy on weekends.
Mediocre food for a very high price. Over $13 for a half lb buger, fries and a soda. And, like so many have said, they screwed up the order. I wont be going back. Try a burger from Uncle Vinnies!
rice and chicken bowl is delicious for its simplicity, a very welcoming and underrated place, really good service.

LOVE THIS DELI!!! The krakowska is DELICIOUS! No one has EVER not liked it that has tried it. Real Polish sausage! No, it's it like the stuff at Publix, this is the real s**t! And they have pickled mushrooms and peppers! Some crusty bread and it's I'm back in Poland!
My sister told me about this place so she took me down there is the best hoagie I ever had , it does look like an apartment mall

Was in town meeting a friend for lunch and decided to eat here since there was plenty of places to park. We arrived about 30 minutes after opening for lunch and the place was mostly empty. The server  informed us that they were out of the first 3 or 4 things we attempted to order. Though the server was nice and apologetic, it was frustrating that they were out of so many items just 30 minutes after the restaurant opened. We ordered a salad to share which was brought out with a stack of dirty plates which we requested be replaced with clean ones. The server, again apologetic, agreed that the plates were "gross" and brought out a new stack that were equally as dirty. The good news is, the margarita pizza was tasty.

On February 2010 I bought a new set of uniroyal tires I used these tires for about 3 years they still had tread on them I tried to call the store on June 2013 I have been noticing dry rot on the tires even though I still have tread on them I tried to file a mileage warranty claim on the tires and they said dry rot and weather cracking is not covered under the mileage warranty I bought 60000 mile tires and only got 35000 miles out of them I rotated them and checked the air all the time and they still did not last the mileage warranty. I no longer own the tires I only have one tire left from that set and it is a spare which is dry rot from now on I will only buy used tires if I have to keep buying a tire every 6months or a year because of dry rot.

I have visited Crepesody twice now and have had the same experience both times.  I want to say that the Crepes are very very good but the service level is 2 star.  The location is great, the concept is great, the food is great but the level of customer service is lacking.  In the morning, most people want to be in and out by a decent amount of time.  Both times I went there I had to wait 20 min to get 2 crepes.  I think they would benefit heavily to have an extra employee scheduled for the morning rush.  Also the girl that is usually taking the orders needs some training on customer service and speed.  She is definitely friendly but they need to understand the customer base that is coming in to get a quick coffee and breakfast.  There needs to be a little more sense of urgency and attentiveness.   I think that they could get alot more sales if they put people through alot quicker.  I will say I will try to go one more time because the Crepes were so delicious and light.  It is what is making me go back.
Absolutely horrible service. Went for lunch on a Friday. Left 2 hours later.  Not that busy.  Food was fine and beer obviously was great but the service was dreadful.

Just ok. Really nothing special about this place. I've tried Gondolier a couple times and every time I leave a little disappointed. The food is just a bit plain and kinda boring. If anything, the service has always been good here. But I have yet to try anything that made me say Wow!!! If there's no wow then there's something missing.  

For me it's all about the Pizza, give me a nice crust with some good quality tomato sauce and of course great mozzarella and you have my attention. Unfortunately Gondolier falls short on these areas. It's just average, not bad and far from great. 

I'm willing to try a few more items from their menu, perhaps some pizzas are better than others. I really want to like this restaurant, once you've had good pizza there's a certain standard that must be met. 

I'll update my review if they prove me wrong, I'm ok with them making me eat my words. But so far the Yelp gang seems to be on my side on this one.

I have ordered from this place ALOT, and almost every single time they somehow disappoint me. They consistently give me terrible quality food that when it actually IS the right order (which is rare), it still ends up being a huge disappointment with how crappily the food was prepared. Things like getting half the amount of a large pizza or getting a cheesy bread that only fills a quarter of the box and no cheese. They mess up my order almost every single time like giving me bbq wings when I always order hot wings and never giving me any sauce even when I pay extra for extra sauce. The amount of times I've had to call to tell them they got the order wrong or they forgot something that I've had to specifically ask for is ridiculous. Never mind the fact that I live less then five blocks away and still have to wait almost an hour ever time I order from there. Seriously, this place is never worth it. You'll just be treated rudely by the employees and be disappointed in every aspect

I wouldn't waste your money - 

We went here for my Wife's Birthday for lunch today - bad mistake.

Hummus portions were huge and very good, I ordered a Caesar Salad, My wife had a chicken and pasta with red sauce.  at lunch on a Wednesday - it was 40 minutes between the time they put down the hummus and my entree's came. (I literally watched the table before me get served, eat and pay their check before my food came)

in both cases our chicken was rubbery, the red sauce was runny, my salad was much smaller than advertised (I had asked the waiter for an approximation - he gave me the bowl size (including a sizable rim), not the salad size. 

All told I wasted almost an hour and a half of my life that I will never get back - and I'm now stuck at work late on my Wife's birthday because you could care less about the food you serve.

Been there once, really liked it. The Slides were icy and closed... And lethal if you fell but the rest of the hill is a lot of fun. Very high base elevation nearly gave me vertigo riding up the chairlift and I have skied many places in 50 years. Nice layout and nearly vacant especially at the end of the day. I really enjoyed skiing there and want to go back.

I'm a cheap date.  I don't believe in paying more than $5 for a movie (unless it's a very special occasion).  We go to the movies during the $5 window - before 6pm Mon-Thurs or before noon Fri-Sun.  Thus we generally skip the crowds, the obnoxious teens, and (usually) the drunks.  Occasionally we will be in a theater with someone who should be barred from all quiet activities and have to hike down to the customer service office to get our money back and come another day, but for the most part things are good before 6.  

The seats are awesome.  They're comfortable fabric seats with arm rests that can be raised so you CAN comfortably stop blood flow to the arm of the person next to you when there are scary things on the screen.

They sell alcohol here, and it's more reasonably priced than the soda.  The popcorn is rarely more than cold.  If you're coming here to watch a movie, you'll be comfortable.  If you're coming here for the rest of the theater experience, you might be less pleased.

DEFINATELY...the worst property management company I have ever encountered.  As a disabled combat veteran with 22 years of service and having lived all over the states if befuddles me on the complete lack of communication and attention to detail that this company provides.

I would HIGHLY recommend that if your a property owner and/or possible renter you forgo this company and look for someone else.

I've never waited over 2 hours for a party of six. The waitress was rude throughout the whole night, not placing the entire order in. We got half the food an hour and a half after getting there, spoke to the manager about it (no apology, from anyone, not once) and then waited another 30-40 minutes for the rest of our food. The only reason I'm giving them 2 stars is because the food was tasty overall and we did get the extremely late meals taken off.

Every single time I visit this business on Canal St., the service is great.
The frozen yogurt is no different than any other Pinkberry - it's delicious, as usual.

I just have to say that I'd rather drive the extra 3 miles from mid-city to go to an establishment with impeccable service.

I stopped in to Steve's Cafe for a quick breakfast the place was busy but I got seated with no problems. My server was very friend and suggested the Stuffed French Toast which I tried, it was very tasty and filling. I enjoyed my meal and was out the door in no time. I will definitely return the next time I'm in town.

We were in town for vacation and ordered takeout from gyro shack. Very tasty gyros. Only complaint is my husband ordered and paid for extra sauce and we didn't receive it. Also the drinks that come with the combos are tiny. Like kids meal tiny. Get two if you are regularly thirsty.

I don't think they are 4 stars. The most is 3.5 stars. The soup base is a bit blend. Their best dish is Tonkutsu black garlic lover. After that all dish are just average. I give them half stars additional due to pandemic and at least reasonable pricing. 

The soup base is not total pork bone base. The soup is at the light side now t too creamy. The meat just ok for their charshu. Veggie is not heavily use. The porch egg probably the item worthy. I would give their ramen C grade. 

The appetizers fried shiu mai is good enough even they are not in the great category. The fried calamari is just average. Food at least is fresh to have. 

The place is big enough for hosting a lot of customers. Service is good may be we are the only customer. I would said they are like the take out restaurant quality. If I am hungry a bowl of soup during winter, I may think to visit. There are a lot can improve on the ramen.
This is a classic burger joint. The boys are in the kitchen whippin it up and lookin good while 'doing it. Casual place where you can spend less than $ 20 and get a good "full all day" type of meal.

I popped into this new Chop Shop one day after work. I was in desperate need of a new cut, and I figured for $10 anything would be good. A friend had a previous bad experience with the shop on South Street, but I was feeling risky.

The shop was dead, so I was seen right away. The stylist who did my cut was Magoli. She was very friendly and thoroughly went over what I wanted. She asked about my normal styling methods and definitely took that into consideration before starting the cut.

She was quick, but not careless. She didn't talk to much (which I love), but she was still very friendly. The only thing I didn't like is that she didn't show me the back of the cut when she was done, but that might have been because two other girls walked in and distracted her. Not sure, but really not a biggie.

She told me that the $10 was just a promo for their Grand Opening, and the cuts would be $20 in the future. I don't think I'll go back for that reason, because if I'm going to spend $20 I might as well spend $45 at a salon where I can expect a little bit more. All in all I am happy and have received a lot of compliments on the new 'do.

3.5 stars: ordered the tofu pad Thai, tom yum noodle soup, and shrimp rolls. Shrimp rolls were not that great...oddly long and not enough shrimp in the rolls...literally half of the roll contained shrimp and the other half empty. Pad Thai was a bit spicy for 3 stars but tasted okay. Tom yum soup was bland...had to add pineapples for flavoring. Prob will try the fried in the future.
It was an outstanding dinner. The Warm Water Sea Bass on Risotto was perfect. Everyone at the table loved their food!

Man, it has been a long time. My first encounter with this pub was back in 1991 in my Navy days. Some friends of mine and I were cramming nachos and margaritas at Los Amigos (which I am not sure exists still), and being silly sailors at the bar. He sits next to us and he invites our rowdy foursome for a drink on him at his place down the way, Brownies. He had us squids at "drink on him".

Decor was comfy and easygoing, and I see it hasn't changed. Service was friendly and fun. Drinks we paid for were good and not too pricey. We came back several times and always left pretty happy. Maybe someday soon I will darken Brownie's door and relive them good times.
Great yogurt options and toppings! Cool vibe inside.

I can't believe I'm writing a review for an auto tags store, but this place is exceptional for an auto tags store.

I actually enjoyed working with Carol, who is 500% friendlier than anyone I have ever met in an auto tags store, and is patient with questions. Clean, bright and well-kept office, not dingy like most places. Highly recommend.
Rug came back destroyed and smelled absolutely disgusting. When asked if even cleaned. Manager was rude as hell.

My grown daughter and I Love this place.  So many pieces of pottery to choose from and the girl working there was very helpful.  They had nice background music playing and I found it very relaxing.  It was a great afternoon.

Stopped here for some take out the other night. We ordered 2 cheese steaks, onion rings and fries.  The cheese steaks were a little small but also cheaper than most places but really good and really cheesy.  It was nice and mixed in and the roll was fresh.  The onion rings were also good. Fries tasted kind of old and were nothing special. Would skip them next time.

Was at your restaurant 9/6 at 6pm.

The manager was nice. 

The hostesses were rude, unprofessional and unhelpful. 


Such a shame, Erin is great but I will not return. 


Thought I would share; who wants to not be greeted by smug hostesses? I'll pass.

I have rented from Enterprise in the past however the last few times I did, it was from the Sunset & US 19 location. The staff there take the word professional to another level. 
Javon, the branch manager and his staff are professional, friendly, always greet you with a smile and most importantly, they deal with you first. 
They are always striving to be better than what they already are by asking  what can they do to be better next time and it's not a robotic question, they REALLY want to know. 
I have been to other rental places and other Enterprise locations and they all could learn something from the staff at this location. They forever have my rental business and reccomendation to others. Keep up the great work Javon, you definitely have your hand on the pulse of great customer service!!!

It was okay....though I'd be willing to pay more to stay at another hotel during my next stay in Philly.

Great location.  Nice lobby.  Check-in was a breeze.  The front desk employee was nice.  Yummy cookie (per the chain's usual quality), but the complimentary lemonades were horrid.  Fast elevators.  Clean common areas.

My Queen suite was huge, with a very comfortable bed, and soft sheets.  However, there was visible dust and cobwebs on the sprinkler above the bed, and the light switch in the bathroom was broken [read: would click on by itself, as it was half way out of the fixture].  Nice design.  Nice desk area.  TV was a little subpar for a Hilton line.  But very awesome that housekeeping left a bag of fresh towels and bath products on my door when my DND sign was up!

The internet was a disgrace.  The webpages needed to charge my room worked just fine.  After that, I was on the phone with IT for a good ten minutes.  He wound up giving me a complimentary multi-day promo code.  I thought I'd got the better end of that deal, until it became clear that I wouldn't get enough bandwidth to be consistently connected on my laptop and mobile at the same time, and was going to constantly be kicked off, and asked to re-enter the code [read: stymied in the middle of my work, and having to repeat functions due to the inefficient internet].

The walls between rooms didn't seem to be too thin....though nose from the hall was tragically loud.  Also, my suite was in a corner at the end of a hall (standard) with the ice machine AND staff service door RIGHT outside of the door.  Poor placement for a suite.

Check out was a breeze.  Pleasant staff.  And valets waiting to hail you a cab.  And the charge to my card came through as expected.

I'd stay again if there was nothing else on this level within my price point.  But I'm not jumping at the bit for a repeat stay.

Without a doubt the worst course in the city. Only a couple times were there actually fairways even cut and the greens were the length of normal fairways.

The clubhouse was OK and the guy was polite but all the stuff for the hotdogs at flies flying around it and literally inside of the container.

Several holes didn't have tee off markers. They let people tee off wherever they want. 

If it was free I probably would not play there again.

The thing I like best about Bubble House is the decor and ambiance... perhaps I haven't been to enough "good" Asian restaurants, but I find it very relaxing and pleasing. My friend and I sat in a tiny booth in the dining area that was very cozy and secluded.

The food, as others have said, was definitely overpriced for what we got, but the food itself was still good. 3 stars, for defs. We shared the duck samosas and pan fried noodles with shrimp, and we both got vietnamese coffee and then a pot of "Buddhist Finger" (???) oolong tea. The samosas had cheese in them (again, ???) but they were still good. The pan-fried noodles were tasty, but nothing incredible. The coffee was strong (yes!) and the tea enjoyed a temporal moment of goodness, which was eclipsed before by weakness and after by bitterness. We lacked the foresight to remove the tea filter from the pot.

The bathrooms were jarringly different in style from the rest of the restaurant and a lil' shady. The dessert menu was very disappointing... I would expect at an Asian-y restaurant you would have light, perhaps even non-dairy desserts, but all of the desserts were like, chocolate cake and cheesecake. Weird.

I would come back here again but not regularly.

We came to Theo's to celebrate my Dad's birthday. Everything was on point.  The food was great we enjoyed the cheesy stixx, the Caesar Salad was garnished with strips of Parmesan Cheese. We had 2 pizzas everyone enjoyed. But the staff was wonderful they kept our glasses full and helped with the cake we brought it. They all made us feel special. Hats off to this location and their staff. Great food and super service!!

I can't tell you how disappointed I am.  We are new to town so we wanted to explore and found this all you can eat sushi place. I don't even know where to start, the tasteless food, horrible customer service of the fact that we got the receipt without asking for it while we were still eating  30 mins into our arrival. DO NOT waste your money here. Cheap cheap cheap restaurant. NOT GOING BACK!

I don't do book stores any longer.  I used to.  I used to be the ideal customer: buy plenty of books, buy the crap/cheapo books, but the accessories, etc.  No longer.  I buy nothing in these places.  I read entirely digitally now.

But my children enjoy real book stores and I am willing to cater to them to ensure they are readers.  To that end, this is a fine experience.

But the thing is, the place really only handles bestsellers.  Nothing niche.  My daughter likes manga, and B&N has manga, but only the manga everyone begins with.  You want something else, it's not in the store.

My son likes graphic novels.  Same deal there.  Want mysteries?  Really only the "top" books.

And the rest of the store is replete with crap.

It is what it tries to be, but it doesn't suit this customer at all.

I'll try not to be too harsh but originally I was going to give this place only one star. 

Ok, I've just finished dinign at Mumbai Bistro where they sell Indian food for $4.95 a pound. The menu changes everyday and today we were greeted by four vegetarian dishes and two chicken dishes. My problems with the place is as follows:

1. The location used to be a sushi place and it was relatively cool (temperature-wise), now that they are an Indian restaurant with a hot food kitchen in the open and a hot bath setting to keep the food hot. That place is a sauna, the ventilation is non-existent and eating inside is quite insufferable. 

2. Six items today, four of them are vegetarian, three of them are masalas that taste very much alike. By the time I get to the chicken masala, there were only two cubes of chicken left and I was forced to just get one cube so my friend can have some. Of the twenty minutes we were inside, they didn't think to replenish the food, so I guess customers who came after us won't get a taste.

3. We got each a light plate, two sodas, two chutneys and shared one slice of onion naan and it cost us $19. With the selection so few and every item cost extra, you might as well pay a few more bucks and walk two more blocks to the larger all-you-can-eat buffets. 

The food didn't taste bad, in fact it's nicely spiced but the heat is just too uncomfortable (maybe I wouldn't complain as much when its cold out), the value was just not good enough to make me go back unless I was famished and I'm too lazy to walk the two blocks then I might go back for take out.

On a late weekday afternoon, the lazy sociable clientele and new money were relaxed and measured in their breezy discussion, while starched white linens flagged the corners of each table like a detente.  I was watching a single Asian gentleman in a fedora pipette eye drops, pausing to create a plaintive scene, before he was dutifully picked up by his wife. Only because the hamburger with Roquefort came out dry, the sliced bread wasn't freshly sliced (were they reusing sliced bread during a pandemic?), and a couple at a neighboring table had the audacity to attempt to pay for me without us having spoken a word, do I give this establishment 2 out of 5 stars. I refused the inexplicable courtesy from the gentleman patron, and paid for my own meal (over $30), with the instruction that if the gentleman thought he had paid for anything having to do with me, I had expressly rejected his offer, and that he should be comped the next time he was patronizing them. He had left without a word. I had overpaid for my meal.

Meet some friends up here for a late lunch. They had salsa ,chips and the black bean soup. They loved  both. I got there and ordered the Shrimperita . It was very disappointing, then I got the bill and realized it was an 18.00 disappointing pizza. We threw 3/4 of it away. What a waste. Probably wont be back.

Some of the stylist are great others not so much. The first time I went there I had to wait a while but the girl who did my blow dry was great and knew what she was doing. The second time got someone else and waited for a while a again and the results were below average. i probably could have done a better job myself. I guess you can't have high expectations with students. overall good ambiance and friendly staff. Manager is nice too. I guess if you find someone you like then stick to them :) I would only go again if I can't get in somewhere else and since it's close to me. Otherwise I wont and definitely would ask for the same girl that I had the first time. I think Jessica or Jennifer (sorry been a while). I give first experience a 4 and second 3 stars because she was nice.

Horrible service.   Took over 20 minutes to order drinks.   Then another 15 plus minutes to get them and order food.   We waited over 45 minutes to get our food and it was cold.   And the girl at the hostess counter was not friendly maybe because she was too busy talking to her friends to help customers

Service was fine, atmosphere is fun, food needs some work.  The temperature of food served needs to be improved, cold tacos are not appetizing.  The salsa is tasty.  I think appetizers and drinks is the way to go.  I am not a fan of food that is served below temperature.
Great food and atmosphere.  Smoked brisket was excellent: tender and juicy. They need more waitstaff.

I had absolutely the worst service at Stonehouse Cafe. I was moved twice due to having my dog with me and not sitting in the "correct" section. I observed multiple tables waiting a long time to be acknowledged by the serving staff. I received my food and it was cold and the eggs were undercooked. At that time the owner/manager in the sundress and big hat walked by and wigged out over my dog and said "you're sitting in the wrong section and should not be here with your dog." Even though the staff sat me there and this place is apparently dog friendly. Friendly being the operative word. The owner/manager woman then told me my eggs were clearly cooked right and I was wrong. Shes awful! Meanwhile, I see her gesturing wildly towards her staff about the situation and scolding them. Her poor attitude and lack of customer service trickles down to her wait staff who emulates her behavior and they look clearly miserable, and created an uneasy environment. New managment needed!

This grape slush reminds me of the kool-aid I would make as a child. You know when you out in double the amount of sugar the recipe calls for - so good!!! Not for the faint of heart, because it's eight-kinds-of-sweet!!!

Still love this place but last visit there were fresh jalapeños on my daughters burrito bowl. Wish they would ask or post somewhere that they can put or take this off, she couldn't eat her dinner. I traded with her and although I like heat it overwhelmed the taste of the carnitas. If you do not want fresh jalapeños on your food make sure you say so.

This place has the worse and I do mean worse customer service and management staff I have ever encountered. The food is really good but, the customer service is  piss poor! I went on a Saturday before they opened and stood outside along with several other patrons and when they opened the doors everyone was seated as I try to place a to go order I was then advised that it would take an hour and 45 minutes before I received my order because they have to serve the patrons sitting inside the buildings before they can serve any to go orders.  Today I called at 5:30 to place two go orders and they stated that they do not serve to go orders on Tuesdays because it's too busy, I asked the manager where is that stated on your menu or website and he stated it was not but that's something that we are doing because he's too busy on Tuesdays and then proceeded to hang up the phone, I called back and spoke to someone else and they reiterated the same BS and commits to hang up the phone. I tried to call back to no avail they were fused to pick up the phone.

Cedar Point is a great neighborhood standby with lots of options. I've only been for brunch and late night cocktails, and I've never been disappointed. It's not the most exciting menu, and dishes can be bland, but after a long night, I'm happy with any combination of eggs, toast, coffee, and champagne cocktail I can get. They have tons of vegan and vegetarian options, and the omelet on the specials menu is usually a good bet. There is also rarely a wait, and the patio is lovely. If you're a fan of seitan or buffalo flavor, get the buffalo seitan wings, and get the blue cheese in addition to the horseradish dip.
Service is poor!  Save yourself some time and hit another place in the FQ.
I only have been here once -- but terrible security line and the free internet did not work. The train heading to downtown Philly is cool, but had to wait a long time for it.

good food. but they prioritize groups of people over couples
on the waiting list for over an hour, came in to check in and they were empty. worst service ever

Well I've been trying this place out a couple times , I will now never go back. When doing my nails the woman dug her drill into my fingers it didn't hurt at that moment but once I washed my hands OHH did it sting. Also its been only 2 weeks since my last fill mind you she half assed the entire thing, now one nail is broken and another popped off ! I'll be going elsewhere for now on !

Pepperfire is always good hot chicken. They can be a little inconsistent sometimes with the spice level but over all a good hot chicken. Sides are also very good. They do have a few unique hot chicken meals that you won't find anywhere else in town like the tender royale with a deep fried pepper cheese and the applefire that is waffles, tenders and sweet apples. It's definitely worth give a shot

I happened to be in town over the tail end of Restaurant Week. My Philly host made a reservation for Friday evening. I was expecting a high end experience. It is definitely a nice restaurant but not nearly as intimidating and stuffy as some reviews made it seem. Modern yet comfortable styling, attentive but relaxed staff. Creative but approachable meals. 

Beverages: I had Blood Lust and enjoyed the not-too-sweet orange impact. My friend had a Spice World. We later had Malbec and rose proseco. 

Food: Because of restaurant week, we selected from the pre fix menu. First course: winter lettuces and vegetable tartine. Both were delicious - good balance of flavors and textures. Main Course: gnudi (yum) and lamb burger (fine). Dessert: orange pistachio parfait and cocoa choux (both ok). So some stars and some average performers. But nothing bad. All in all, a good meal!

Walked in. Walked out. OVERPRICED. 

Get boba in LA or SF. $2.99 for a huge one and boba included. Also, made by folks who actually understand how a boba drink should taste.

Went to get them to see my car first and assessment and a quote, ended leaving it there to be tinted.  Dropped off at 0930 and received a call before 1 pm. Great service,  my car looks great and the drive home was so much cooler.  I didn't lose my temper in the traffic and my air-conditioning was working like a champion.  Great price,  excellent service and they even fixed my viper alarm.

Whenever I want to go somewhere small, cozy, comfortable, and yummy-smelling, I head to Raging Sage.  The staff and overall vibe are really friendly (no too-cool-for-school attitude here!) and the pastries rock.  There is even an occasional vegan cake!

It's a great place to spend a few hours, but because it doesn't have lunch food, it's not a good spot to camp out all day.  (I keep fantasizing about them taking over the flower shop next door and turning it into a lunch spot!)

This is a pretty nice little neighborhood festival. It offers a nice setting in the local park, food and drink options, live music, and a bunch of vendors selling their merchandise.

It's nice that this is a family friendly event, and that it is free to attend. I think that the thing that stood out above anything else was certainly the amount of arts and crafts vendors. This was more of a pop-up shopping event for local handmade goods than it was a festival for anything else. If you are into those sorts of things, this would be a great place to stop by as I don't think there are many options for free events that host that many vendors in the same place. There was a stage with bands playing some short sets in the evening, and it was a pretty good selection of bands to spend some time watching.

The lantern part wasn't super impressive, at least not for what I had pictured in my head for it being part of the name of the festival, but they did have paper lanterns strung throughout the area and it was nice in the evening when the sun went down. I did think that it was a little too dark when we were walking around the area at night, which was a little bit of an inconvenience on the grassy area after a bunch of rain. The park is a great setting though, and it's nice with all of the grass and trees. 

As far as food as drink goes, they had beer for sale from a local brewery and a few food trucks. The lines weren't too long in the evening when we went, but I can see them being long with the limited selections if it got much busier. They had some tables and chairs set up to sit at, but they were full when we went. 

I'll probably stop by here again if I am in town and don't have plans, but I don't know that this is as much of a must-attend festival as some of the other local neighborhood events in the city. In the hierarchy of all of the awesome local festivals that we have throughout the year, this one gets an "A-OK" rating from me.

this place is selling illegal cannabis. shipped in from california, low grade not a legal missouri dispensary. dont pay the weed tax.  its a pop up store in brentwood not following the cannabis community rules.   they even gave me a joint to smoke on my way home as i was driving.   stay away no good cannabis.   save youe money a waste if time.  scammers for sure

This review calls for brevity ("The most valuable of all talents is that of never using two words when one will do" -Thomas Jefferson).

Like so many, I'd been waiting on this place to open, seems like the coming soon window-wrap has been there since 1986. When you wait that long for something to open expectations are high and those expectations on the food aspect of this place were so not met.

The service was great! The bartender was amazing and accommodating and the gentleman servicing the guest were very respectful and not overzealous. For a place called the BBQ Saloon I'd expect more than two barbecue sauces (Carolina & St. Louis) and they were just okay. I had a turkey sandwich and the flavor was subtle (tastes like they are cooking for an older crowd - w/ no salt or pepper). The coleslaw was different and added blueberries to the mix. The garnishes were extremely fresh but that cornbread is the worst. It tastes like hot water cornbread were no egg is used and water is the moisture component. This was the densest, driest piece of cornbread I've ever had in my natural born life.

The owners of Wildflower own this as well so they aren't new to the restaurant game. If they just wanted to open a bar they should've done that. Pappy's is way too dang close for their initial showing to be like this. If I were stranded on this corner, BBQ Saloon would be the last place I picked to go eat (but I'd get a drink).
Best "locals" ski resort in the Tahoe area!

I wish I could give this place 10 stars I had pizza lemon pepper wings crawfish bread and fully loaded waffle fries .... so delicious so tasty the lemon pepper wings had so much flavor the crawfish bread I don't have words so gooooood and the pizza is like no other I will return always

My husband and I only had one night in New Orleans, and wanted to find a cool bar in the French Quarter but not as cheesy and crazy as some of the Bourbon Street bars. Erin Rose came highly recommended and did not disappoint. It is a small, divey bar that seemed like the type of place that both tourists and locals would visit. The two bartenders were very friendly and attentive- we even got a free drink near the end of the night! We also had a good time talking to the other patrons at the bar. The frozen coffee was AMAZING! I would recommend Erin Rose to anyone interested in seeing the French Quarter while keeping it low-key, and would definitely return.

Waited 2 hours and 20 minutes for a delivery half a mile down the road.  Driver finally showed, said the food "shouldn't be THAT cold...".  It was ice cold.  Never again.  Chain pizza is a better option.

Excellent spring rolls - large, crispy and tasty!

Pho on the other hand is pretty standard in my opinion. 

Decor is unchanged and old since I can remember but you generally don't go for pho to admire the decor of most places. I don't mind the old decor gives it a bit of character.

Service is fast and efficient. Parking free outside in the parking lot.

You can ask for your bean sprouts to be steamed prior to arriving (that's how I prefer mine)

All in all a decent option for pho when you're craving some in the south side

This is a truly wonderful place.  Friendly staff who clearly love food and love sharing it with others.  I stopped in for lunch today and was so impressed.  Chicken with white rice and beans - delicious!  I had to get the sweet plantains for dessert before racing back to court too!  All this was like $12 with a drink - very reasonable.  And if you need a pick me up?  Get a colada with sugar (double espresso).

Have used WWS for three major home stereo/video projects.  I did not need top of the line products and they found the right quality at a price which was lower than I expected.  Whenever there is a problem, they can either help me by phone or come over to fix it.  They have suggested solutions and worked with my ideas to give me what I wanted.  Mario P. in Montgomeryville is the greatest!  The technicians are pleasant and do clean work.  Have never used for car audio.

SPAradise is the only place I go to have my hair & nails done. Angela is very meticulous and always does a great job, as does all of her staff.I have even brought my niece in for a girls day Mani-Pedi day & She said she felt like a princess!

My husband and I were very impressed with this spot. The wait was an hour to get seated but worth the wait. This was our first time trying the Nashville hot chicken. We ordered the medium spice level which was hot but perfect for us. Any hotter it and it wouldn't have been enjoyable. The beignets were fresh and warm and the chicken was amazing! I also really liked the mac and cheese! I don't think the price was a lot either! I would highly recommend this spot!

I use to love this place slowly going down hill over the last year food just getting down right bad went in today to give them one last shot ordered a simple bacon and eggs they first forgot the eggs then brought me runny scrambled egg then I tried the bacon it was left over reheated from breakfast hours so I sent it back an got ham instead I t to was reheated from breakfatst it was chared and yet barley warm and as far as the casino it to has gone down hill spent over 5 hundred the last two times I was in there and not even a two dollar return just took it every one I talk to has beentreated the same way and yes I know its called gambling  for a reason and no one made me play but im just disappointed in a place that wont even give dicount on food that was that bad done and wont go back ihop and Dennys here I come

3.5 if I could.

The plov and the borscht were great!  The ambiance was good also.  The service was just OK as was the bombe dessert and the fried ravioli was not very good in our opinion.  

The plov and borscht would make a great take out meal for a chilly winter evening - comfort food and tasty too!  

I'd go back if it were closer or I was in the area, but unlike Zaika, I wouldn't drive 40 minutes for this.  If you're in the area, check it out, but stick to things that other yelpers raved about, such as the plov and enjoy!

Loser's 2 is the Cool Springs sister bar to the original Loser's on Division St in Nashville (next to another related bar Winner's).
Went during the first week and lunch was a little shaky.  Of course it normally takes a few weeks to get things worked out it was disappointing to have corn dogs served frozen not once but twice.  Didn't seem to be much of a lunch crowd but I hear the night is pretty busy.  I'll go back in a month or two for lunch and try again, in the meantime grab a beer at night to see how it compares to the Tin Roof 2 down the street.

I really really wanted to love this place and was very excited to eat here. My fiance and I entered the restaurant and were impressed with the delicious smells and cleanliness. The person who brought our food was friendly and kind. Such good people. But we did not enjoy the food. We ordered the nihari (beef curry) and the fried charga (chicken) with 2 tandoori naan. The naan was hot but the nihari was lukewarm and bland. Filled with oil and overpowered with lack of fresh flavor we asked them to take it back to kindly heat it up. They did put it in the microwave. But it did not make a difference. It still was not edible. The charga was a little better but not great.  But the charga appeared freshly prepared.  Perhaps we ordered the wrong thing and should have stuck to the kebabs and rice. The basics. Maybe we would have had a better experience. Three stars because I am giving them the benefit of the doubt that their kebabs are a better gamble than the oily beef stew (nihari) and the bland fried bird (charga).  I won't eat here again. But they are very kind people who serve you.

Took our car in at 12:30 for a 1pm slot inspection as we need to hit road asap and was told by the guy that it won't be a problem. Walked out for 1.5 hrs lunch and came back to find the car still hasn't been work on and was told that they won't be able to look at it for another 2hr.  They skipped our car to work on the bigger jobs! Total waited time 5hrs for an inspection, wasted half a day. Dishonest customers service!
Its an okay place. Expensive and most of the exhibits are broken or really dirty looking. Aimed for smaller children. My 6 year old was rather bored there. Parking is tight too.

Food was just okay. I started with the biscuits, the mango and passion fruit jam was delicious. I am SMUCKERS Strawberry Preserve kinda girl, and I was pleasantly surprised by how good the jam was. For my main course I got the carnitas burrito, it was flavorful and eggs were cooked well, but it was just meh. I wouldn't tell my mom to try it when she comes down to visit. Service was pretty par. This was my first time visiting Dante's, the coffee was room temp. The server was constantly checking on his other tables but hardly ever came to ours. He freely brought refills to other tables, but I had to ask for mine. He was helpful with the menu selections but overall did not meet the service standard other NOLA restaurants have.

When I joined (for $10), I told the people I did not want to renew the membership after the first month, as I was only interested in using the pool. In order to use the pool, you have to pay the highest fee, almost $40 per month. I only went to the pool one time.
Since that time, they have taken fees from my account twice, in spite of the fact that I told them I did not want to renew, and told them twice more in text messages with two of their representatives.
I am a Senior Citizen on a fixed income and cannot afford this amount.  I am not interested in their classes or equipment.
I want them to stop taking my money, and to refund the money they have taken, which was not authorized.
Elaine J.

I LOVE coming here!
I always get the fish tacos, and my boyfriend always gets the shrimp tacos with mango. The corn and the black beans are amazing! Also, all of the salsas... delicious. For a good price too! It's usually pretty busy though.

I was looking around town for a jeweler to get a custom engagement ring. After searching all of Tucson, I came across Trinity Diamonds. Joanne walked me through the pros and cons of the different cuts, the clarity differences, and the price difference between each diamond. She was able to find something around my budget. She ordered the diamond and the following day she took a look at it to make sure it was up to her standards. I came in shortly after to make sure I was happy with the diamond. We looked at different settings/rings and found something that was stunning. She then had everything custom made in a timely manner that I needed the ring in. Overall Trinity Diamonds made a stressful thing like ring shopping, a smooth and enjoyable process.

This is an awesome local shop that provided me several gifts and stocking stuffers for Christmas! You can park in the back parking lot and enter from the rear if you can't find parking on Big Bend. They even have some reserved spots on the far end of the lot, right by the door. The shop is divided into two parts - one with clothes, accessories (jewelry, etc), camping/survival gear and kids stuff (small selection). The other side is the general store, with tons of local food and drink items, plus some cooking/kitchenware. I found a lot of unique things here, which was appreciated by the recipients! I'll definitely be back!

This #CVS on Main St in Dunedin has the loudest, most annoying door alarm ever! I don't know what the manager was thinking?? I've been here for an hour waiting on a prescription and it's been going off constantly - as loud as a smoke alarm! 4x every time the door opens or closes. If I worked here I would quit over this. ‍

It has been 2 years since we have visited this place. Great beer selection as usual, try to hit their happy hour as prices are a little steep. They have added a kitchen and offer smoked sandwiches which were very good. Outdoor patio is wonderful with plenty of seating and is dog friendly as well. No dogs inside, plenty of parking and very clean.

My boyfriend took me here for my 23rd birthday, and it was an all-around amazing experience! The ambiance is beautiful, service was exceptional, food and drinks were absolutely delicious. They made sure to give us the most intimate table in the restaurant, and we had the BEST view of the city. Made my birthday so special. One of our favorite restaurants we've been to and my new personal favorite.

A month ago I ate at KK for the very first time and I loved it! So, this time I came back and brought 10 friends with me. This time around the food was still delish (my Duck Fried Rice was yum yum), but the service wasn't so. Let me be clear, the employees are all friendly, it's the wait to get our food that was horrendous! I realize that KK is a food truck so expect service to be slow, but waiting for 40-45 min to get our food isn't acceptable. And for those who ordered multiple dishes (soup, salad, entrees), each came out at a different time and different order. 

One of my friends ordered 2 entrees, a soup, a papaya salad and sticky rice to eat with it. The entrees came out first, about 35 min after she ordered, the soup came 10-15 min later, the papaya salad came 10 min later, and she reminded the employee of the sticky rice, but that came 20 later. Really, sticky rice shouldn't take that long to come out; it definitely shouldn't be over an hour after ordering.

Okay, fine, whatever, at least the food tasted decent, so when writing a review on Yelp she gave KK a 3-star. The worst part is the response she got from Dennis P, the owner of KK, "Blah, blah, blah, blan, blah, blah. Thank you." How absolutely rude! FYI, this is NOT how you run a business, Dennis P.

Those of you who love the food at KK and will return, find out when the slowest time of the day is and go then, otherwise you will have to wait 30-40 min for your food, or even longer. I, on the other hand, might never be back due to the "owner" being so disrespectful.

5-star for the food
1-star for the rudeness
= 3-star average

We went here last night after dinner. The lady with my bleached purple hair helped me I order mines in a waffle cup she mixed in what I wanted then threw my ice cream in the air and it landed in my cup but not before touching the roof she asked if I wanted her to re make which she did. Once again she threw it in the air and it touched the roof again this time I didn't see it happen ( I was paying) but the people I was with did and the lady behind us did also so I asked her it if touched and she said no. I told her I wanted another one and to NOT throw it !! She then got an attitude and asked the another man to make it. 
WHY WOULD SHE THROW IT AGAIN FOR A SECOND TIME ?!!

My brother said this was the freshest, best fish he had had outside of Tokyo. Plus the interior looks like the rec dec of the original starship Enterprise. The happy hour colored drinks are amazing- I challenge anyone to "drink the rainbow"! You can get 6 yellowfin tuna sushi for 6 bucks, so its a great snack with drinks. I just wish they had better beer- not the same old shitty Asahi. The $7 salads were huge! and get the dressing on the side, it was swimming in it.

BYOB. Cash only.

Opentable will not let you make large reservations for this place because the restaurant is so small that they'd have to move tables around to accommodate parties of 4 or more.

It's a few blocks away from South Street, but the food is fantastic. Not a lot of vegetarian options, but you'll love this place if you love pork. I was disappointed in my scallops only because I didn't get full off such a small portion (5 scallops and some rice, really? What did I just pay for?)
I love their micheladas here! Perfect amount of spice. I wish I knew their recipe they use in the mix.

I really wanted to like this place. The space is bright, inviting, and conducive to productivity, and I was digging the overall atmosphere as soon as I walked in.

What I wasn't digging, though, was my latte. I'm used to my suburban coffee shop whipping up a smooth, silky oat milk latte each and every time. Literally, my only complaint is that it once arrived too hot.

The latte I received here made me miss my neighborhood oasis. Square One's latte was bitter, because the coffee was bitter. I noticed that the barista used Pacific brand oat milk. Not Oatly, but the next best thing, and what my local place uses. This bitter latte also cost me $6.50. Ouch!

I don't think I'd return here for any reason. There are too many coffee options close by, and I have a feeling plenty of them have perfected the latte.

Worst company ever
Called them @ 6 pm was told they'd be to my house in 2-3 hours 
Called BC air wasn't blowing cold 
After 2 hours I called to find out they cancelled my appt BC it would be to late when tech arrived 
And never even called to inform me 
Even though they advertise 24/7 service 
So arranged appt  for next morning 
Tech showed up , was here  less than 10 min charged my landlord 650$, lied about adding freon to my unit 
Charged her 300$ for a 2 lbs of freon ( which they never even did)
And instead of changing fan out BC we used a tool to get it to spin ,tech just replaced the compositor that retails for $25 
BC I googled it
(This part gets burnt out when fan motor isn't working properly kinda like battery in car when alternator is bad )
Landlord is fighting charge 
I would not recommend this company to my worst enemy ever

We came here after our Lyft driver made the  recommendation. We arrived around 12:30 on Sunday and we didn't have to wait. The food was so scrumptious! We shared the brisket sandwich and Steak a la Plancha with a side of the Street Corn. We also tried the Pomegranate mimosa and Bloody Mary. The ambiance was great and the service was friendly. If we make it back to Nashville I'd love to come back!
Nice dinner on the patio.  Food was very goid.  they have a chef rather than a cook. I had the fish special.  Nice and light for summer.  Weight staff very responsive.

I went to this restaurant after seeing it on the news fox13. Had such high hopes, but when I came here in 7/5/21 I was very disappointed .our server Gigi was the best thing out of this experience. The calamari came out way over cooked, and the shrimp did not taste like fresh shrimp. I was told it was fresh not farm raised. And here is my final complaint. Look how dirty this place is. Dust everywhere and it grossed me out. I can't imagine what the kitchen looks like. Here are some pictures

Visited this location to test out the brunch option. It was tasty.a wide variety of meal options. I opted for the waffles sausage and pineapple with an apple juice. The staff was very attentive and quickly address the concern that the heating element had expired making the food a little cold. It was buffet style so you just picked what you wanted. A nice option when you don't feel like cooking and need to catch up with friends.

Things to know:
1. There is plenty of parking at the downtown location.
2. During the summer you can sit out stand it's very relaxing as you look up at the city skyline.

I showed up at the airport in Tampa to pick up the car I had reserved through Priceline, when I got there, there were about 20 people in line waiting. After a few minutes, we were all told that "We have no cars". This reminded me of an episode of Seinfeld, where they didn't have a car for Jerry, he quipped "you are good at taking the reservation, you are just not good at holding the reservation". So, I ended up at national car rental, which was really easy to get the car, but ended up being 4 times the price of what I was quoted by Executive. Save your time and money and use someone else, I know that I will never consider them again!! Too bad I couldn't give them negative stars!!!!

Very average food.
Chipped plate.
Peeling wallpaper. 
Service was good.
Wait time was ok.

Well this is my first time. My first impression was nice clean and well lit inside. The ladies were very polite.  I order chicken wings with cabbage and Mac&cheese.  I really like the Mac&cheese! The chicken and cabbage needed some salt but over all great. I would eat here again

Always good! Sandwiches are a 3 but thr pizza is always a 5. I love the quality you get from the small business, family owned operation. Fresh made dough, homemade sauce, and fresh ingredients with these piping hot ovens make a great pie. Oh and the Alfredo pasta is delicious and the portion size is huge!

I really wanted to love this dry cleaner. More eco friendly dry cleaning is what I prefer for my husband's suits. I was even willing to drive half an hour for drop-off/pick-up. 

The first time I went the owner was at the front counter. While I had researched them before going, I wanted to know if there was anything more about their processes before I dropping off. His manner was gruff and rude. He raised his voice and spoke at a hurried pace, like my questions were a waste of his time, and he suggested I look at the website for more info. He also said it would be about five days to get items back. I didn't know him well, so thought he might be having an off day and decided to give it a go and leave the clothes. They did an ok job with the clothes. 

The next time I went the the owner was there again. Still somewhat gruff, but not as much. I was dropping off a dress of my own as well as my husbands clothes. I also wanted the dress hemmed. He told me he would have to charge me the cleaning price of a gown, and quoted around $30! For a dress, not a gown! His logic was that there were two layers of fabric in the skirt, a liner, and a sheer fabric. So it would "take hours to press". Seriously? Because the dress came in an envelope, squished, and it took twenty minutes to steam at home. Oh, and they no longer offer alterations. Forever the dress then, I just wanted to be lazy. I will clean it myself. 

Upon returning for my husbands clothes a different woman was at the front counter. She was informing all clients that it would be a week or so before they would get clothes back now. Final strike for me. I've researched and found another dry cleaner who has better customer service.

We arrived ready to buy a car. Picked it out & were willing to pay the advertised price. We asked for the advertised 0% financing & were told that the only way to get 0% financing is to pay more for the car. Huh? Doesn't that defeat the purpose of 0% financing? They demanded that we pay an additional $2500 to get the 0% financing. I'm not even kidding. We asked to speak with the sales manager (Richard Mayer) & he informed us that it's in the small print. I asked him to show us where in the small print it states that..... he read all the fine print & couldn't find it. He read it again. And agains. The salesman read it. Nobody could find the small print that stated we weren't eligible for the 0%. I assumed they would recognize that they were wrong & do the honorable thing. Nope! They didn't budge. The icing on the cake is the "courtesy fee" of $1850. That fee is to pay for tint (which we don't want.) rain flaps (which we don't want, wheel locks (which we don't want) & a service on the house (we live an hour away & don't plan to have our car serviced there.) We explained that we don't want any of these features & we were told we don't have a choice. We HAVE to pay that fee even if we refuse the items.  These guys are con artists. No transparency. Blatant bait & switch. We wasted 4 hours of our day dealing with the most unprofessional dealership I've ever witnessed. Would NOT recommend them. BUYER BEWARE!!!!!

Really cool place. Love the decor and the laid back yet upscale ambiance. The place is rather large including a well stocked and long bar. Food was quite good and the presentation was nice. Server was friendly and overall service was pretty good (not perfect though). I'd definitely come back and try it again some time.
Fun dive in downtown alton, cheap drinks, funny crowd, and you can bet any horse track across the country...nothing better then a drunk degenerate...you wont hate this place

Had my driveway sealed 5/2/2013 by Driveway Concepts. Their 3 man crew did a fantastic job. My driveway looks like new!
The crew was polite, friendly and very neat. 
I liked them so much, I talked my next door neighbor into having his driveway done at the same time. He was equally pleased at their work.

Despite the BBB's continued claims that they actually did their job with regard to my complaint last year, please see the Yelp reviews of Vinesse Winery (here, I'll even give you a link: https://www.yelp.com/biz/vinesse-westlake-village). By the way, the Vinesse website still lists themselves as "A BBB Accredited Company" with a rating of an "A" (as of 9/28/16) *despite the fact* that there have been 96 complaints against this company brought to the BBB in the last 12 months alone (and out of 24 reviews, 22 of them are negative). Makes one wonder what it takes to get a lower BBB rating, hmmmm??? 

I really shouldn't have to defend myself against the BBB but I don't want others to learn the hard way. The BBB of Santa Barbara, CA is NOT a trusted source for consumers.

Stopped by this Denny's on Sunday at 2 for breakfast and lunch. Was sat immediately and water was delivered. We quickly ordered a simple breakfast and my partner ordered a burger and fries.  45 min later the server brought our food, apologized for the wait and finally filled our water. This is the second visit with extremely slow service. Never again!
I highly suggest you go anywhere except this Denny's.

Despite an appropriate (if new) ambience for a BBQ joint the dry ribs were extremely disappointing. While the rub was tasty it not extend into the meat which was virtually tasteless. I needed the sauce, which was nothing special, to have any taste. The sweet tea was decidedly unsweet.
Pretty famous/popular establishment, but food here is only okay.

My man and I were on vacation in the area. We wanted a nice view and a good meal so we settled on Endless Summer. We couldn't have been more pleased. We had a decent view of the sunset and of the harbor. Yeah, it could be a little better of a view but it was good enough.

My man had the Thai Sticks with a peanut-type sauce. He couldn't stop raving about them. I had the flat iron skillet steak medium rare. Seriously, THE BEST steak I have ever had to date - and I am pretty picky about things like that. I kept making almost orgasmic noises eating it, I couldn't believe how velvety, juicy, and flavorful it was. We split a slice of mud pie for dessert which was kind of awesome. It was huge! 

While we don't have kids yet, we did get to see how the staff treated babies and little kids. They were very welcoming, accepting and seemed to enjoy having them around.

If you're thinking of buying from House of Beauty, ....just don't.

Apparently the same dismal experience as the rest of these reviewers (with the exception of Nita W below.... did she get paid for the 5 star review??) UPDATE in response to Nita's response to me below.... I DON'T BUY IT NITA, I JUST DON'T BUY IT. YOURS IS THE ONLY 5 STAR RATING AS FAR AS THE EYE CAN SEE AND THERE ARE LITERALLY 5 PAGES OF 1 STAR REVIEWS. And just so everybody knows, I 

I ordered some rollers off their website, selected 3 day shipping. I got an instant, automated confirmation email confirming my order had been received.... but apart from that, that's all I got. So, 3 days passed and my order hadn't arrived so I started to worry. I checked my order status at the provided link, and not only had it not been sent, the order hadn't even been processed, as the fields for shipment status and order status were totally blank. It's like the order never existed... but of course, they took my money. 

I attempted to contact them 5 times, by phone twice, leaving messages that were not returned, email twice, and through paypal. They didn't respond to anything.

I waited 7 more days before taking further action, and then filed a paypal dispute. They still ignored me. Then, a few days later, I decided to escalate the dispute to a claim, with Paypal. 

FINALLY, I get an email from House of Beauty that my item has been shipped, which it actually had, thank god... I guess, though I really just wanted my money back and to not deal with them anymore. 

The items finally arrived after all of that bullshit, and even though I don't really want or need them (because in the mean time I bought the items somewhere else so now I have duplicates) I dare not attempt to send them back and get my money back because I know there's no chance in hell of that. 

Stupid that it took opening a Paypal claim to force them to process my order. What jack offs.

A Fun little joint with a classic diner feel and good food. The wait staff was extremely pleasant and the menu was larger than I expected it to be. 

I had a patty melt and fries and the burger was very good. The fries were crispy and thick. I had a tough time deciding what to eat because they had a number of comfort food items like hot beef sandwiches, Ruben sandwiches, etc. 

I did not opt for a shake or ice cream, but based on the menu that is also a specialty here. 

I will be back to try some of the other items here and suggest if you do too!

Hopefully the owners see this and make it right. I had my racket strung here and they broke the grommet that holds the string. Very disappointed. However the owners are nice I just wish they didn't damage my racket like this.

Fantastic. Great service.  Nice atmosphere.  We would have sat outside but it was a little cool out.  We had them make us a cheese plate which was great.  They were out of calamari.  I had the tomato soup with a side of fried polenta.  Yummy.  My husband had the halibut and he enjoyed it as well.  We did not get dessert we were too full.  Loved it and would go back in heartbeat.
Awesome staff, great food. A  Garden City gem.

Music was cool. Dinner took a very long time to arrive and when it did it was just ok. The ribs were practically flavorless. Unless you soaked them in BBQ sauce it was pointless to eat. The best thing I can say is that it's food.

Took my Nissan in for service due to a no start condition.  Agreed to pay for a complete diagnostic of the engine and determine the problem.  After a week, was told it was showing a code for crank sensor, which the parts department did not have.  I brought in a used one (junkyard), and car started up right away.  Two 10mm bolts, a plug and MAYBE 5 minutes labor.  Got the privilege of paying over $200 for this work, and received NO diagnostic exam of the engine after it was running (needed to address rough run at idle).  Instead, I got a list of high-profit service job recommendations (oil leak, CV boots, tires) that I could have gotten for free at Firestone or Tires Plus.  Took my car there for their Nissan expertise, but only got a sales job on maintenance.  Disappointed.

I thoroughly enjoyed staying at this hotel during our stay in NOLA. The location couldn't have been more perfect, as it's so close to bourbon street but at the same time far enough so it doesn't get too loud at night. 

I booked the room pretty far ahead in advance, so we were able to get pretty good rates. Since I've worked at a hotel before, I called ahead for someone to leave a note on my reservation making sure I got two queen beds and also requested a nicer room. (Pro tip: always call the hotel to double check and make sure you get everything you want!!) We got a room with two queen beds and in a floor fairly high up with a nice view of the pool! 

The bar in the hotel had great happy hour deals when we were there. Unfortunately didn't get to check it out since I had so many other bars on my list I wanted to go to, but the ambience of the bar looked really cool! On a Friday night we saw a singer performing and she was really good; she really reminded me of the classic sort of NOLA music vibes you'd get around the area. 

The other fancier hotels are located on the main strip of the city. I don't remember the name of the street, but walking along it really reminds you of LA. I personally preferred the location of our hotel more because all the tours and restaurants I had bookmarked were so close to where we were! 

I would definitely stay here again and recommend anyone else to do so too!

We have a very small yard, but as we have gotten older it has become too much for us to take care of without outside help. We saw Hillside doing work in our neighborhood so asked them if they would care for our garden. The little work done was quite acceptable if expensive, however promises made by Joe Colacurcio to show up and take of our small garden on an ongoing basis were never fulfilled. Ignoring customers multiple requests is not a good way to run a business.

This dive bar has a sort of rude charm.  The staff isn't outgoing but i think that is their  shtick.  Some of the best burgers I'v ever had.  May I suggest the peanut butter and bacon burger, it sounds odd but tastes amazing, as well as the Bull fighter with salsa, jalapeno and avocado.  Don't be put off by the service, just bring patience and earplugs.  Also, there is not much room to sit.

The food was decent but unfortunately they obviously don't care about any COVID safety, no one was wearing masks the cook, the counter person not even most of the patrons coming in and out of the place !

First buffet since the start of the pandemic, so I was a bit worried about sanitation. To be honest, in Reno, people don't really care/wear a mask so the buffet basically was "normal" (aka pre-pandemic) since no one wore a mask. Would just be cogniscent of that before going. Made a resevation via phone beforehand, because I thought there would be a lot of people for weekend brunch, but actually could have just walked right through. The buffet was around 75% capacity.

Seems like all the food items are back to normal. Here are some of my favorite things at the buffet.
- The pho here is bustin! It's so weird to eat pho at a buffet, but the soup is actually spot on and everything is very customizable down to what kinds of noodles you can get. Honestly, I would pay good money to eat this pho outside of this buffet setting. Honestly giving this place +2 stars just for this pho.
- Passion Fruit Sorbet - I'm a big fan of sorbets, and this passion fruit one hits the spot. I was worried there would have artificial flavors, but the sorbet is awesome.
- Hot crab legs with butter is where its at. The crab legs are so juicy and because you are at a buffet, its unlimited. 
- Get the yogurt with granola and fruits. Seems like the biggest waste of money, but this is probably one of the best yogurt parfaits I've ever had because of the honey they drizzle on top. Yum!

And here are some things to avoid.
- This is honestly so sad, but the prime rib and the rest of the carving station is really not that great. The prime rib doesn't have a lot of flavor and was very tough. The suckling pig was not juicy and also very tough.
- Would avoid the crepes. It's not very thin and the flavor is pretty heavy.
- Would also not get the enchiladas which are quite thick and don't seem like it is cooked through.

Overall, solid buffet. Is it worth $40? Not sure because the food is not mid-blowing other than the pho, but if you find what you like in it, I think its very worth the $$.
The food here was good, I especially like the guacamole. I would try again in the area

Joe from Jaxx has been nothing from a consummate professional.  What a great guy and strong representative to the industry, taking care of the property owner, my vehicle and staying proactive with the insurance company.  Highly recommended

This hotel is lovely - but after staying here twice we started to realize that it's really lacking with the details.   Our room is beautiful but we didn't have soap, or robes. The place for the mini fridge was left empty.  We went out for the day and came back to our room propped open without a housekeeper in sight.  We ordered room service and it came in 60m (even though we were promised 30).  The order was wrong, the coffee pot missing a lid and smelling like it needed a good cleaning. They were generous and comp'd our meal (we didn't want to wait another hour).  Overall not impressed with the details.

Was recommended to come here by the doorman of my hotel, who I will not be tipping when I leave. Ordered six oysters on the half shell and six Mambo oysters. All 12 were old, foul tasting and nauseating. To help I open the package of crackers and even they were stale.
