9 months later, and things are still not right. It takes a minimum of five phone calls to get anything from them. They sent the plasterer back out to look at the sun shelf. They picked up my teak patio furniture up and put it in the pool to see if it was level, didn't see an issue with the rocking, stated that it wasn't their fault, b/c the bubblers were installed to high. Then, they informed me that the only way to fix it would be to empty the pool (again), jack hammer everything out, re-pour it, then replaster it. They were rude and abrasive.  The contractor was there working on the auto fill at the time, and said that wasn't right, they'd get someone else. I've heard nothing since. I am stuck with that shelf.  
    The auto fill was installed post-build b/c they forgot it, and was done incorrectly. It was piped wrong. They claimed it was repaired twice, and finally "fixed" it last week. Last night, I turned it on. This morning, I woke up to my pool being 2" below the water line, the top has blown off my filter, water is spraying everywhere, and Challenger Pools?  They're not answering their phones. 
    Bottom line?  I'm $20k in out of pocket expenses to fix shoddy work. They will not pay for their errors, and quite frankly, since they get final payment before the plaster, they don't care. Do yourself a favor, steer clear of this nightmare.
If I could put a negative star I would. Do not rent from this management company!!!
I was shocked at home good this place was, given its location. Also, not sure why it's tagged as $$, as my girlfriend and I each got tea, an appetizer, soup and an entree for about ten bucks each.

Came here for a bachelorette party over the weekend. We were really excited to try Evangeline but I was unimpressed.

FOOD
A few girls and I ordered several different items to split and get a taste of everything... 
Fried Oysters - pretty good
Fried Alligator - the best thing we got
Seafood Martini - the shrimp was plain and didn't taste like it was seasoned, everyone else loved the crab concoction with it
Pasta Evangeline - good sauce!
I tried my friends red beans and rice which was really good.

BATHROOMS
The bathroom was gross. Pictures for proof. Nothing to say... this is a restaurant not a bar!

SERVICE 
They cleared our plates without asking if we were finished so several girls who were in the bathroom came back to their food already taken.

Decent NY style pizza in the West End, good salad to go with it. Pizza crust was a bit thin and crispy, at least on my slices, but i'll take that over too chewy.  Good ingredients on the pizza, multiple offerings.

We went here with a Living Social deal and WOW we were impressed! We had a pulled chicken sandwich with Mac and cheese as a side and a 2 meat plate with brisket, pulled pork, also Mac and cheese and fries as a side.  The meat was absolutely delicious! All were cooked perfectly, tender, moist and well seasoned.  I would say that it edges Brushfire by a bit, which is also delicious. The sides were really fresh and delicious too. This is a good thing because a lot of BBQ themed restraints seem to regard sides as an afterthought.  If you want delicious BBQ meats with sides that are above the typical slaw and beans you would get at other places, this will do it. Salad and homemade crutons a plus as well. We will be back.

If you go to one place for dinner in New Orleans, let it be Doris.  This place is a value for the service and quality of food.  A lovely and unpretentious space which attentive and fun staff.  My mom and I shared the tuna tartar (AMAZING) and lamb chop (also really good) appetizers and then split an 18 oz 31-day aged ribeye which had us in heaven.  It was perfectly prepared and so, so, so flavorful.  We also shared a side of salad and asparagus gratin.  Our drinks were really great too. My mom asked for a gin and peach schnapps drink but because they didn't have the schnapps, the bartender made a custom drink for her using special teas and cherries...it was really tasty and thoughtful.  My mom liked it so much she ordered one for the walk home (when in NOLA...)

The next time I'm in New Orleans this will be my first reservation after the plane ticket.

I gave the Livery another try and had a better experience. Service was still great and food was better. Perhaps I came on an "off night"
last time, because everything was on point. The cauliflower appetizer is amazing. I could have ordered and eaten three.

Frigidaire gave me their name when I called about a warranty issue. They asked me questions about my refrigerator. Asked me if it was in the garage. Told them yes. Sent repairman. He immediately told me it was a $20 part that I needed because it was in the garage - something about the temperature in the garage causes the freezer to kick off. He said he would order the part. Asked him if I needed to pay him the $20 now. He said no - I could pay when it was put on. Told me the service call was paid for under warranty. They came to put on part. Then told me I owed them $140 something! I told them to take the part out as that was not what I was quoted by "their" employee. He refused to acknowledge that the person that cam was their employee - have a feeling that person no longer works there! He left the part in. I sent them $20 that I was quoted. I have been in sales for 42 years...when I quote a price, I stand by it...that is why I have been in business that long. If I didn't stand by my quotes, I would have been out of business a long time ago. He asked me - if he called Frigidaire and they told me the service call was not covered, would I pay it? I said no because I was quoted $20.
Brennan's was a nice place for a formal meal, but it wasn't outstanding for the price, just really nice.

I love St Louis Kitchen. It's delicious, classic St. Louis Chinese food. It's close to my house and I love ordering pick up! I've always been treated well by staff!

Until last night, I decided to try and call to order delivery. It was a success! We were within the delivery area, and I was excited for the food that was now on its' way.

When the delivery guy got to the door, he saw my boyfriend and his friend asleep in front of the door and began rudely yelling at them, "Foods' here!! FOOD HERE!!" 

Meanwhile, I was getting up from the couch saying, "Hang on a sec, I'm coming out!" But he was so busy yelling and shouting into my house that he didn't hear me. He kept shouting that the food was here even though I was trying to confirm to him that I was on my way to the door.

It was abrasive and rude. I hope that St. Louis Kitchen can strive towards educating their employees on proper customer service and how to greet guests. I feel as though there should have at least been some sort of pause to listen. But he was just standing there, rolling his eyes and shouting.
Always good food and great service. Wild Boar Parpadelle and Black Spaghetti was our choice.  We were not disappointed.  Katie's is on our go-to list.
Best pulled pork in Philly! Fantastic mac and cheese. Desserts to perfection! Khyber is great. It can get super noisy though, but it's totally worth many visits.

We've been back several times for dinner, and gotta say it's been great every time. And now, no more ordering at the counter- it's real menus and full table service. Menu is still kept small but ever-changing. Sometimes I look at the menu and think "...that's all?" but menu descriptions don't always due justice. Tonite we had, among other things, the avocado steamed bun and kanaage .? Waa? But the steamed folded Chinese style bun with the hot avocado, coarse salt and spicy pickled cucumber was surprisingly good ( even better than the pork belly bun, I thought!). And the crispy chicken thigh nuggets on seasoned rice with picked onions and cilantro was also great. This has actually become one of our fallback date nite dinner places. It's never too crowded but the small dining room is intimate.

I used to love this store up until about a year ago. Customer service has completely went downhill. They have closed all the express lanes forcing customers to scan their own groceries. This is always a challenge since I buy lots of produce, equipment is sensitive and always requires that I wait for an employee to push a button to allow me to continue scanning. They don't seem to care any longer about their customers. This store has become so crowded that I tend to go later on Sundays. By then, many of the products I'm looking for are stocked out. I'd rather spend a few dollars more and go to Albertsons!!

The people who work there are friendly and the place is clean. Overall, I've been disappointed. My Mother-In-Law went there on our wedding day and told me that the girl hadn't listened to her and has been disappointed with her hair in all of our pictures. 

Despite her opinion, I read reviews and decided to have a very expensive treatment done to my hair. The treatment didn't take and my hair is no different than it was before. I scheduled a consult with them to find out if the treatment had been done incorrectly or if I had done something wrong with it- All I got was two of them trying to convince me that my hair was much better than it had been before the treatment. They reluctantly offered to do the treatment again but quickly insisted that it wouldn't work if it hadn't the first time. I left, disappointed. I was mostly disappointed because I've consulted with 2 other hair stylists who told me that the treatment clearly hadn't taken and that I should  really contact the people who did it because most salons would want to know and they would fix it right away. Maybe another salon would have....

Tmom's scores big on bar food. The burgers are awesome because they use potato buns, the fries are awesome because they're waffled and the corn dogs are awesome because they're corn dogs. Mom's also has a vegetarian/vegan menu to boot! There's almost always a drink and food special, so you're never really missing out.

2 pool tables upstairs and a back room where you can smoke. At Tmom's, you can do drunks 'n' crafts and they let you graffiti on their walls. It's a throw'em back establishment and they typically play great music (The Smiths meets crust punk). It's a weekly refuge for me.

Very good experience, great service, BYOB is welcome!   The lamb vindaloo at spice 7 level is just fine,  the chicken tikka masala at 7 was a bit mild, however still tasty.  Garlic naan was excellent, still thinking how good those were!    as out of town visitors we welcomed this excellent meal, and as Indian's we know these guys know how to cook!

I've been going to this salon to get my nails done for a year now. And I made an appointment to 2days prior to get a full set. Arriving to my appointment time the nail techs were late & also didn't have the supplies needed to do my nails. I had a class to get to & my nails were half done. Due to the fact that this was the first time there was a problem, I told them I would come back after my class. I reached out to the nail tech before going back to the salon & she informed me she hadn't gone to get what she needed. Appointments are made for a reason. Due to the complete lack of professionalism & not caring to keep a loyal customer, I will not be returning to this establishment anymore.

I gave Sagami 2 stars but that didn't mean I had a bad experience.  I'm just fortunate that there are some of the best sushi places around near wear I live.  Blue Fin & Umai

First the good points.  The service was fantastic from start to finish.  They provided a free pot of tea and pickled vegetable / noodle combo.  I thought the interior was very warm and friendly.  Wood paneling rapped the walls and it works at this place.  The ceiling is very low as others have mentioned.  The prices are very fair.

Since I haven't been to Japan yet I can't really comment on how authentic the food is.  I know that here in the states the sushi is more over done then in Japan.  Sagami's food is simple and that is how I would envision it in Japan.  The rolls are very small and don't have a lot of extra things to add flavor.  

For my entree I ordered the Chirashi.  In hind site i should have just got the Sashimi dinner because I only ate a very small amount of rice that came with the Chirashi.  The main turn off for me was the fish selection in the Chirashi.  Obviously it had tuna and salmon.  There was some shrimp, octopus and I forget the last fish. 

I'm not an Octopus fan just because it takes so long to chew it.  I feel that  putting shrimp in this meal is kind of taking the easy way out.  I would rather have another fish to sample.  

I drove from Montgomery County,PA to try Sagami because of the reviews on yelp.  I have to say that I was not impressed and think the extremely high ratings are a bit inflated.

I had the mushroom. It was ehhhh. Subpar to say the least. I wasn't impressed at all. Nothing much to say. I thought the restaurant was cute and intimate, but that's about it. 

I was visiting St. Pete on vacation and wanted to try the place. Let's just say it was a try.

Ambience: 
Outdoors right next to a batting cage/range. You order food from a window and eat at the tables outside. 

Food:
- Ordered the "Triple Play" tacos. Had the shrimp, carne asada, and ahi tacos. Shrimp taco literally fell apart as soon as I picked it up out of my basket. Was very disappointed and had to eat it with a fork. Carne tacos were good--a little salty. Ahi tacos were my favorite--could have used more wasabi aioli but were overall very well balanced. 
- Also tried to guacamole and chips. In my opinion, there was too much lime (which my partner liked) so I didn't eat very much of it. 

Other than that, line goes through fast and food comes out fast as well. Would considering coming back for ahi tacos, but felt the food was "all right", overall.

As Starbucks go this one is pretty small, and although the staff is very nice here, the lines are often too long and frankly, it's just a Starbucks and there are other choices not far away. the parking lot is very small and the drive thru makes the entire retail experience a bad one.

It's the same bagels and menu as the other locations but there's something just kind of off about this location. It's really dark and it looks like none of the employees want to be there. Got a veggie sammy and the avocado was dark and sour. I love their bagels though and will keep going.

After a day of bar hoping, we decided to grab something to eat and happened upon this place.  I was surprised they sat our group of 8 right away and someone came to take our drink order.  The Margarita was average, nothing to write home about.  

We were all starving so we put the food orders in ASAP.  We ordered the hot honey wings and were disappointed when they didn't have any heat.  I ordered the 1/4 bbq chicken with greenbeans and it's was OK, but I wasn't all that impressed.  Two of the guys ordered the Kona burger with pineapple and there are no words for how disgusting it was.  The burger ordered medium rare came out gray and the medium well was like a hockey puck.  Personally I think it sounded bad on the menu(coffee crusted hamburger - no thanks!).  There wasn't much coming back from the debacle of those burgers.  

They weren't all that busy, especially for a Saturday night, but we came in around 9pm so maybe we missed the rush.  That being said, the service was on the slow side...maybe they were trying to give you an authentic southern experience??  

The night we visited also happened to be the hottest day of the year, hitting the triple digits.  I'm not sure if the AC was broken, or what the problem was, but it was atleast 80+ degrees in there. Additionaly,  I couldn't ignore the giant buffalo head wearing a sombrero hanging on the wall.  It was like the Mona Lisa, it's eyes following me everywhere.  I don't think we will be visiting again, the buffalo will need to make a new friend.

Eh! Spinach and artichoke dip was pretty nasty, was a thick blob of paste for $12. The mojito's were also way over priced for the tiny glass with not much flavor and too much ice. No one wore masks and it gets way too loud and crowded even at 6pm on a Friday. No thanks.

Sales Rep RICHARD J MILLER admittedly selling a 2016 Yamaha FJ with KBB book value of $6500 for $8400 bc it's a "seller's market" is a dirtbag move in my book. 
Additionally brand new bikes are going for $8000. 

Buyers beware, know your book values before making a purchase.

As a fan of the prohibition style establishments I must say this one takes first place in presentation at the door. From the inconspicuous man taking names to the gentlemen who escorts you downstairs and onto your table. Ambiance is great as it is well crafted with a long but narrow room, dark wood, subtle lighting and the bar all the way at the end. The drink list is quite extensive. It fulfills both the romantic vibe or a more casual evening with friends. My mint julep was overflowing with fresh mint so I was incredible happy. Tables are a little too close together and it can feel like you are sitting on top of one another especially in winter time when everyone is wearing and a winter coat that they then have to pile up on the booth or themselves (maybe I missed a coat rack). Unlike Hop Sing cell phones are allowed and picture taking can become annoying. Heads Up: they don't serve vodka. Be a patron, if not for the superb drinks then for the history throwback.

I finally tried this place recently after I got tired of hearing about it from everyone else. I had the beef martini with the coconut cupcake. First of all, do not be confused by what the does a beef martini mean. It is deliciousness in a sandwich. Heavy on meat and mushrooms with savory sauce that will leave both your palate and stomach satisfied. The coconut cupcake was also a 5/5. It tasted like natural coconut and not like the artificial sugar kind. Would definitely recommend. Can't wait to try their other cupcake flavors

I was loged into my password-secure Amazon account. A pop-up appeared offering an google chromebook for $199.00 with 6 mos to pay, interest free if I signed up for an Amazon Card. "I Agreed." A month later I received the back-ordered chromebook. A month passed. No Credit Card, No information about the transaction on my Amazon Account. I ordered some dog wormer, it didnt arrive. I emailed Amazon and asked if they wanted me to pay for the laptop. Amazon could find no information about the transaction on my account. I called Amazon, they had no idea and said go to Chase.com. Chase.com requires a credit card number to access so I couldn't get in I. Called Amazon. They gave me telephone number for Chase. Chase said I had a Chase card, I said I never contracted for a chase card. I signed up for an Amazon card. 
A week later the credit card and a bill arrived....for almost 400.00 and included late fees and interest.
I never received that credit card or information about the card --because Chase, without my knowledge or consent changed the billing information and mailing address in my password-secure amazon account. I never received the dog wormer...Chase sent it to an address of their choosing.  I filed a complaint with THE CONSUMER FINANCIAL PROTECTION BUREAU but they did very little.

Two days after I filed the cojmplaint I was at our local food mart (where I have shopped for 20 years) I tried to pay for my $35.00 purchase with a personal chech (which I have done for atleast 20 years) and it was declined because a company called TELECHECK (which was/or partially is a subsidy of Chase) said I was a risk. I have excellent credit and have never bounced a check in my life (and I am not that young.) I think by signing on with Chase, Amazon has become a monster. I use to love Amazon but I don't think I will purchase through them again.
Also....I bet it won't be long before we see a big drone in the sky with Mr.Chase Incorporated  painted on it.

I went there and saw a car I liked the price was great but was met by nate and other sales agents they started changing the price on me and i told them what I can afford on payments and they kept going higher and higher . I was there for hours back and forth and many promises which they failed to meet. I purchased the car and was promised a certain time frame to pay and now the time frame is shorter than what they said. Evryone there washes there hands 
Horriable service!!!!! Do not recommened stay away

The lone employee was nice (she was overwhelmed-there really should have been more than one person working there). The sandwiches were below par. I ordered the rotisserie chicken sandwich and it had barely any chicken on it. The roll edges touched for lack of chicken! Definitely not as pictured on the menu! My spouse ordered an Italian BMT and it had one thin slice of ham, but plenty of salami. We've given this place a few shots as the other poster did, but we won't be returning either.

This place is so... Posh. So hip. So... Trendy. I imagine I'd feel at home here if I was a 20-something naive girl who just moved to Nola to pursue a career in library science or baking. And maybe I'd wear oversized glasses and a plaid scarf with skinny blue jeans and a messy bun. But alas I am none of those things. However, I can appreciate this place for what it is.

Initial impressions :
-small menu. Love small menus.
-plaid shirt and blue jeans uniform. Love uniforms.
-open spaces with huge windows for prime Instagram posts.
-technology on iPad for seating and organizing the crew.

The good :
- food is delicious. Well prepared eggs and
 very appealing dishes.
-coffee on point.
-corn bread hot and butter not pre-packaged. I hate pre-packaged butter.
-nice staff. Seem to be very efficient and not too nice. I don't trust people that are too nice or people that say "trust me". No thanks, I won't.

The bad:
-they ran out of juices at 10:30 am? The fuck??
-prices kind of steep. 

Overall a good place if you're into posh, trendy, or fetch things.

The food portions were very generous. My garlic chicken wings were so so. The pub was loud and crowded. Our friends said their drinks were watered down. It made me dock off a star. The place was clean. My favorite highlight in this place was the jukebox. The appetizer of bread and bacon cheese dip seemed delicious. Everyone ate it at our table except me. The service was friendly and gave military discount. The location was easy to find and had a lot of places I wanted to visit and see.

I really want to give Central Table a stunning 5 star review because the service I had last week was outstanding. Not only was my service outstanding--I could see the few people around me were getting the same Grade A service I was getting. 

I also want to say I have heard rave reviews about Central Table's brunch selection. One friend said their breakfast pizza is stuff of hangover-cure legend. When Alive magazine gave them the A-List award for best, "Crowd-Pleasing" menu, I was all in. 

I went there on July 5th. Yes, of this year. When it was beautiful out. While everyone was getting ready for round 2 of fireworks, my husband, friends, and I decided to head indoors for some dinner. 

The place was empty. I always feel the rush of blush cover my face when I go into an empty restaurant and say, "I have a reservation." I don't know why, but it's like going to a party and realizing  you are the only guest who showed up. Now this could very well be because most St. Louisians were out doing more season-appropriate things. Whatever the case, we were empty nesters. 

After we got over the sound of hearing our own echo, we order from their vast menu. Now, as foodie law states, if a menu is too diverse it is usually a sign of trouble. I hate that rule. I want to be able to go to a place--be adventurous and order the bone marrow and have my dinner guests be able to order a burger or pizza. Welp, throw in a sushi and pasta menu and that is exactly what you can get at Central Table. 

Unfortunately, there really wasn't anything wowing about the menu.The fact that the place is on the pricey side only made the blandness of the foodmore noticeable. I will say the toast that came with the bone marrow was amazing. It was the first time I ordered it and had no idea how to eat it. Our waitress was so nice and helpful. 

Overall, I will definitely return for brunch and give another shot at dinner as well.

I had never thought about trying a pineapple and pork taco so I went in. Very prompt service. Very friendly family environment. I will definitely go back, hopefully to try something different but those pineapple and pork tacos were the bomb!
Fun spot to hang out. Didn't have music the night I was there, but seemed like a good funky venue.

Great Philly joint to review for my 300th!  Probably the coolest place I've ever had a drink.  Won't find it if you're not looking for it, which just adds to the experience.  Walked through the first door and it was dead quiet, which made me question what I was walking into, but walking through the second door and into the Ranstead room I knew I was somewhere special.  Bartenders look like they're straight out of the Mad Men days.  Took a seat in one of the booths and one of the two bartenders immediately came over with glasses of water and a drink menu.  Drinks go for $12, but you're getting a quality cocktail.  I had two of the bulldog coolers and they were delicious.  If you're really into fine drinks and a bit adventurous, I definitely recommend going for the bartender's choice.  Tell him what types of alcohol you enjoy and they'll whip something pretty unique up for you.  Such a great place to just go and chill after a night out in the city. Dark, cozy, great drinks, great company.  Can't beat it.

We had the chocolate crepe and the strawberry melange, they taste not bad, however we were sitting outside but then a friend joined and we asked to sit inside and ordered one more dessert for him, that's when they became unfriendly. I was a little disappointed as we were here for family vacation. I wish they value us as customers
Like it's sister restaurant in Twin Oaks, very good breakfast food with high quality ingredients. I particularly like the omelettes and skillets.

Disaster. More harm was done to our home. I cannot emphasize how much I regret them entering our home.

We wanted to fully ground our first floor and kitchen. We received an estimate from Gen3 for 12 outlets that simply needed the grounding wires to be attached, which was relatively simple and cheap. They also recommended updating the grounding system by installing two grounding rods in the basement floor and bonding the whole system to the water meter and gas meter. We agreed to this work, quoted at $1000.
The electricians that came out were pleasant and nice, but they didn't understand what the job entailed. The electrician that changed out the outlets didn't know that we wanted all outlets grounded; as it turns out only 4 of our 12 outlets had grounding wire in them - he should have noted this immediately. We were told in our original estimate that all 12 had grounding wire. So, great, now we have 4 random outlets grounded (none in the kitchen where they matter most). We didn't discover this until the end of the work, when the grounding rods were already placed and bonded to the water meter. Very unfortunately for us, for their work cause a 1-2 liter per hour leak in our lead pipe feeding the home from the city water main. (As an aside, we were interested in replacing this pipe but had the water tested the day before and found lead levels well below the EPA recommendation - so decided not to replace the pipe). Had we known the true situation of our house's electrical system (4 outlets with grounding wire instead of 12), we never would have had them out in the first place. I should have done the work myself!
The cost to replace the lead pipe was $4500 (Goodman Plumbing I heartily recommend). I spoke with Debbie, one of the Gen3 owners, who refused to take any responsibility of the faulty original estimate and avoided any ownership for a pipe that her employee damaged. She only offered to waive the cost and pay $85 for the fee the plumbers changed to come out the following day.
She committed to sending us the $85 2 months ago. After much prodding from my realtor to Debbie, we are still waiting for the check.
___________________________________________
After my 9/16/16 review, one of the Gen3 owners reached out to me and made good on her commitment. Though I still would not recommend their business, she did what she thought she should do to make a disaster-situation right.

It does look very sketchy and shady on the outside. I'd drive by so many times wondering what was inside. Finally decided to go it and...

It's very nice. The customer service was wonderful. I was greeted as I entered, the lady behind the counter was pleasant and helpful....blahblahblah...on to the food.

I went with the dumplings in hot oil as recommended by another reviewer. Let me tell you....AYE-MAY-ZING!!!! Also got two egg rolls (nicely done. Not greasy or gross looking. Tastes great) and General Tso Chicken (big pieces of chicken...not overly fried...tasty).

Guess I found my new restaurant for take out!
Got the white pizza and wasn't impressed. The ricotta was good but that's all I could taste. Went to pick up one of the super tiny slices and all the toppings fell off. I doubt I'll be going back
Food was really tasty. Atmosphere was great for the entire family and staff was very friendly.

Second time here. Didn't know you cannot get any happy hour - priced drinks in the restaurant area. Only in the bar area. And this was the Friday night of Cinco de Mayo weekend. Rediculous! This was not stated when they seated our party of two in the restaurant area to eat dinner. We were only asked if we wanted to eat inside or out. So much for happy hour prices!

Cash only, long lines no matter when you show up, limited menu. But: delicious. So delicious. We went there with a friend on his birthday- it's all he wanted to do on his day- and oh man. I sort of haven't stopped thinking about it since then. Huge portions, low cost.  Yes please !

Don't waste your time and money.  The inside looks okay and the service is decent but the food is AWFUL!!  My husband and I both ordered pasta and it was disgusting - both dishes completely lacked flavor!  His seafood pasta, in his words, "tasted like skim milk - it didn't even taste like seafood."  My spaghetti was covered in a ridiculous amount of tomato paste, that's right, tomato paste.  It was clearly right out of a can with no seasoning whatsoever - it even had the texture of tomato paste.  Do yourself a favor and go anywhere else in the neighborhood.

Honestly, this is my favorite restaurant.  The sushi is fantastic!  My favorite maki combo: yellowtail jalepeno, salmon avocado, and spicy scallop.  I love the crispy beef (sounds corny but is really good), the general tso's chicken, the green curry, the honey walnut shrimp, the hot and sour soup, and the lemon grass soup.  Really, it's all fantastic.  Wendy is a lot of fun.  

I probably eat there twice/week.

Paid $17+ dollars for a crooked burger with lettuce on one side and meat on the other (actually tasted pretty good), soggy fries and a flat soda. Restaurant also seemed short on staff. Pretty sure I will never come here again.

Unfortunately for me it was not a good experience my pad Thai was burnt. I think the cook scrapped all the burnt food from the pan and dumped in my plate. I didn't say anything since no one bother to ask me how was my food. Honestly they have to have known what they were serving because when the waiter put down the plate you could smelled the burnt onions.

I really liked this place. The restaurant was clean and smelled amazing, the food was ridiculous (fresh, authentic Italian) and the server that helped us was very attentive.

The only thing that sucks is parking. It took us forever to find a spot....but it is Center City, so maybe I shouldn't be whining so much about this. 

Will be coming back the next time I am in Philly.....

Why did I wait so long to finally check out this world-famous place and sink my teeth into a signature "Danny Burger?" Danny's only been around for 10 years but, better late than never.

This is such a fun spot to visit and a tasty spot to eat. The inside is filled with pennants, pictures and all sorts of Major League Baseball memorabilia that it is well worthwhile to squeeze into the small, standing-room-only space to take a look. Warning: You may emerge still smelling - and smelling of - the tantalizing aroma of freshly grilled beef.

Just about any time you go, it will be busy but the orders are taken and filled efficiently, people dining there make quick work of their food once they sit down at one of the outdoor tables and the whole process seems to move along surprisingly well and fairly fast.

The menu is lengthy so be prepared. Lots of items are named for pro baseball players and there are specials so enjoy finding famous names and then figuring out the ingredients for each namesake item.

Parking is across a fairly busy street so be cautious when crossing. The owner, Danny, is a delightful person who may, if he has time, show you a special map displayed in the back of this landmark dining spot. Each pin on a map of the U.S. and each name penned on the wall signifies where folks come from to check out Danny's All American Diner and Dairy Bar. It has become a favorite spot to stop for visitors from across the country, and around the world, especially since Food Network star Guy Fieri taped a segment there and created an addition to the menu, the Guy Fieri's Triple D Triple Play burger. It's a mouthful that features a burger topped with mojo pork, pastrami, Swiss and American cheese, tomatoes, jalapeno peppers, French fried onion rings and mustard on sourdough bread.

Buffets are a hit and miss, much more often a miss than a hit. And King Buffet falls squarely in that "miss" bucket. 

There are several things that I did not like about my experiences here and it is hard to know where to begin. Maybe I will start off by noting that the vast majority of things we tried were either overcooked or over-seasoned (or both). Also, while there is a wide assortment of things relative to a typical Chinese buffet, they fall *far* *far* below what one would expect at any decent restaurant. Yes, there were "crab cakes" on the menu but I swear to God, that if you could find any chunks of crabmeat in those "crab cakes", you have a promising career as a detective at a forensic lab! Oh yes, there was sushi but if you took even one look at the wasabi, you would know that the pale green paste they had out there was anything but wasabi. I could go on and on about other aspects of the food too. 

As far as it comes to service, the restaurant falls short as well. We came in at around 10 p.m. on a Saturday but even though they are supposed to be open until 11 p.m., I found quite a few stations empty. I  had to point that out to at least 3 employees before one of them offered help and decided to replenish some of the empty stations. Of course, conveying things is harder if employees don't speak English which seemed to be the case with quite a few of them. Then again, even though they were supposed to be open until 11, they gave us our check at 10:45 and started clearing out the food at around 10:50. It's also worth mentioning that I did not get any refills on my water. Lastly, as my picture of the restroom attests to, the trash bin was a mess but no one seemed to care. I even saw an employee who used the restroom and left, without bothering to bring the mess to anyone's attention. I guess he just didn't care.

If all of this very mediocre food and indifferent service came with rock-bottom prices, somewhere in the neighborhood of say, $10 it may have been acceptable but prices here are higher at $14/ person. Both my wife and I thought that was too high for the suburbs given what they were offering.

If I have one suggestion for the restaurant, it would be that they can have fewer items on their menu but try doing a better job of cooking the items and not bringing standards down to the absolute lowest common denominator. And perhaps putting a bit of effort into the ambience of the restaurant - cleaning up the bathrooms as they get dirty over the night, keeping the ice cream station cleaner, preventing unruly kids from running amok, and maybe even teaching their staff basic English. But sadly if I could discern anything about the attitude of the management, it was - we couldn't care less and we couldn't be bothered. 

Unless I am out of my mind, this is the last time I have stepped foot inside King Buffet.

We got to the Southwest terminal early in morning and wanted food and coffee. Great American Bagel was on the side of the terminal where no gates were open yet. We were probably the first ones there. I got a jalapeno bagel with egg and cheddar and my husband got the Asiago Omelet. We were both very happy with our choices. The coffee was average but still got us going. There were two ladies behind the counter and they were both very nice and service was quick. But we were also the only ones there.  If we are in this terminal again at breakfast time, we will surely stop by.
Asian noodles, taste good, fast, and cheap. Enough said.

My husband and I ate here for Valentine's Day. I had seen this restaurant on open table a few times and had been really excited to try it. We both felt like we were at a wedding. They had live music for the holiday and we were sat right next to the "band" so when they sang we were unable to talk because it was so loud that i couldn't hear anything my husband was saying.  There were all tables for 2 set up facing the band/dance floor and most couples were slow dancing, which really isn't our scene, so that's why we felt like we were at a wedding. 

They had a prefix menu for Valentine's Day. I had the salad as a first course and it was just okay. It was spring mix with maybe 3 or 4 candied walnuts. Not much dressing so it was very dry. My main course was the islandic cod and I thought it was great. It came with vegetables and they were okay. My husband had the calamari (very good) and filet (was only okay). We are not dessert eaters. but it came with the meal and that was good. We had the cheesecake and chocolate trilogy.

The service started off good but then went downhill. I know we went on a busy day, but it took 30-40 minutes from when we received our dessert to receive our check, and that was only because I had to chase down the server. Other tables around us were also waiting just as long. Another table had the manager come by because he had ordered a drink and it was 15 minutes later and still hadn't received it.

My husband and I would have been fine staying that long (dinner ended up being 2 hours) if we were being checked on/drinks being refilled, but no one ever came back to check on us. We really were only checked on when each course came out. But never in between to see if we wanted another round of drinks or anything.

I'm glad I tried it here, but don't think we would come back. I also read a review that it's an older crowd and would agree. My husband and I are in our mid-30s and we definitely felt like we were 20+ years younger than everyone else.

Bill Pearce Honda service department has the worst customer service I've ever experienced at a dealership (usually you pay a premium price for a premium product, not the case here). I took the Honda in for an oil change and they tried to upsell me $600 worth of work, most of which I could do myself in an afternoon with a screwdriver, all to stay in a tire program that's utterly worthless. The tires don't even have a warranty, so if you get a flat before they're completely bald you have to pay for a new tire out of pocket anyway! To top everything off, the service rep was condescending and not knowledgeable about the services she was attempting to sell me. When I asked to talk to a manager, a man came over to our desk and threw his iPad down. I told him that it was unprofessional and I didn't feel comfortable. I had to speak to two more people before I could even get my keys back, including the *actual* manager who seemed concerned that they didn't get him when I asked for him. Anyway, while I appreciate the nice job the body shop did with the Mazda, I don't appreciate what I experienced today. If it wasn't for the body shop they would be getting one star.

No frills restaurant with good food, great staff

I dined here with two friends and we had good food at a reasonable price. I  had the wild caught Louisiana shrimp served with moque choux and rice.  It was tasty, not greasy, and filling. I thought about dessert, but didn't order it because of the small selection which the waiter seemed hesitant to even recommend.  This is more of a beer place and the wine selection is just okay.  I would definitely recommend this restaurant if you're looking for a casual dining experience with good food in a clean, unfussy setting.

Okay people reading these comments.....please get that this is a Vietnamese spot not Chinese. I think they offer Chinese because so many think everyone Asian is Chinese.

If u live in the quarter there is very little good cheap food and this place is good. There is no Vietnamese food for miles so we love this pace. I am the type that saves up for Commanders or Galatoires and I love it at moon wok and usually have them deliver....it makes me feel real, healthy and full. 

Do not expect a classy dining experience just basic , healthy and tasty. If you don't know Vietnamese get brave and try it. Anything with a pork chop in it is good.

Delicious espresso, they pull great shots and are super nice.  Discount if you ride your bike there... cute!  Really nice outdoor seating area.  And homemade ice cream.  I only wish one of the coffee places in Eastie had good pastries instead of muffins from costco.  But yeah, sip is delightful.  Much less harried than the other Eastie coffee shops.

The old Crabshack was good eats, drinks and music and atmosphere, but the owners were arrogant pr___s! We had a restaurant.com coupon and had to talk/beg them into honoring it for cryin out loud! They signed up for it! NOW the owners are personable and accommodating!
The clam strips are HUGE! Never seen them that size before! Love them! The fried fish leaves something to be desired, twice my hubby was not pleased, and he really isn't a picky guy. Maybe the owners aren't all that experienced in restaurants, but they try and they are open to suggestions. You got to start somewhere and I'm certainly willing to support their efforts! Overall, good food, good service, good music and atmosphere! Really cool place to go, even if it's raining, you can still enjoy the outdoors under their huge thatched roof with the big oak (?) tree growing through the middle! I DO recommend this restaurant! " Read less
Will never return here.  The employees have no idea what they are doing.  Was misinformed about what can be bought on the spp and dull trying to get my $60 back.

Delicious food in a very stylish and clean restaurant located in the Epicurean hotel across the street from Bern's Steakhouse. Can't go wrong here during weekend brunch.. food has been great and cocktails are delicious!
Ordered a seafood dish, they left off some ingredients.  Taste ok. Food was cold. Not impressed.  New Orleans native hard to find good restaurants now.

We always drove by and always wanted to try this restaurant.  So we finally did.
It was really packed and really big restaurant. The service was not as good,  we expected more attention since the price of the food was alot. We had bottle of wine,  the liver rigatoni had a weird after taste I didn't like it.  I only ate the pasta. 
The grilled octopus salad was good.  I had the steak,  and I didn't like it.  How can you go wrong with a steak? Mine just tasted blah and it was cold!  
My boyfriend had the lamb,  and it was very gamy. . He felt sick afterwards.

Over all,  I feel like this restaurant is over hyped and over priced.  We didn't have a good experience here,  and is too big so you don't get the service that your deserve for the price you're paying.  We spent $250 on dinner just for us two,  and we wouldn't have minded if it was at least good.
Padthai was ehh. Not al lot of flavor. Tom Kay guy soup has some extra veggies in it that don't normally come with it. I wouldn't go back.

I ordered Uber Eats from here and was not happy with my tacos. They were actually the worst tacos I've ever eaten in my life. No where on the menu did it say that they came with slices of avocado and guacamole on the. The avocado was so mushy that it was impossible to remove it all from my tacos. The tacos also came with a very little amount of cheese and no lettuce or sour cream. So I could only take a bite before I had to stop eating because the taco was completely overrun by avocado. I would definitely not recommend going here for tacos.

Tacos are more than delicious. Had the steak and eggs and pulled pork. The steak and eggs is by far one of the best tasting tacos I have had to date. You can't beat 2.00-2.50 a taco for the size. The sides are good portions also. Only thing that fell short was the chips we ordered came out in very small smashed pieces. If it wasn't for that it would have been 5 stars.

for those of you who don't know, this place is also green eggs cafe 8am-4pm! we saw the green eggs cafe sign when we passed by once but couldn't find it on yelp, thought it was weird so we came back today and we're told it's two restaurants sharing one place! 
one thing to have in mind is green eggs cafe is a cash only place, they do have an atm inside the store but they do charge you some fees if you withdraw so if you can have some cash prepared beforehand that'll make things easier! 
place is clean, simple and lots of natural lighting. food came out pretty fast and is delicious!! we got "the kitchen sink" and "creme brûlée french toast".  
the kitchen sink is a bunch of savory breakfast goodies all in a skillet. there are eggs,potatoes,sausages meat, cheese topped with gravy and biscuits.  
i loved their creme brûlée french toast a bit more to be honest, it's just the right amount of sweetness with lots of berries!!
Pizza is pretty tasty but not our favourite. Don't particularly like their pizza sauce; too acidic. And they don't use a lot of cheese. The topping are good and fresh though.

I called on a late Saturday morning to see if they could fit me in for an eyebrow wax and was happy to hear that they could just an hour later! I walked because I live on the canal. The girl who did my eyebrows was awesome and gave them all of the extra attention they needed.

The only con is that the room is really cramped, and I think no matter how great the service is, $20 is a lot for an eyebrow wax. That being said, if I ever need an emergency eyebrow wax again, I'm sure I will be calling The Naked Monkey.

Fabulous job!! Preston responded quickly to my inquiry, scheduled around our needs, and then did a superb job on our two cars...he was assisted by a buddy and they worked continuously until the cars inside and out were immaculate.

Price was very fair and the quality was excellent.

Best of all, he introduced himself with a handshake and exited with a thank you handshake...very impressive.

Thanks, guys!

I ate here for lunch once.  I almost didn't come in, but the photographs of the specials in the window looked interesting and enticing.

I didn't then order any of those specials, which might have been a mistake.  I got ordinary chicken with eggplants, and I have no complaints.  The food arrived quickly and was quite hot--it's surprising how often restaurant food isn't really hot--and tasted good. So I found nothing unusual or remarkable about this restaurant, just an excellent execution.  I was completely satisified, and I look forward to coming back and trying something new.

I hope three stars does not seem too few, because I find no fault with Red King's.

Will not be going back.  I know it is expensive as I have been there a few times which I could deal with if they got the order right but I got a salad and when I got home realized there was no steak on it.  I put my dogs away, drove back to the store and told them I had not gotten any steak.  The 4 people at the counter looked at me and the cashier said she had not charged me.....ok, I have no problem paying if I had not been charged.  She never bothered to check my ticket and I had not saved my receipt. I got back to my house after the fiasco and realized I also did not get 2 other ingredients so I was basically given a bowl of lettuce, carrots cucumbers and cilantro for whatever high price they charged me and I haven't even looked at my cc to see if I paid twice for the steak.

This place may be one of my favorites in the Philly area. My husband loves Creole food and this place is fabulous! We are lucky enough to live right down the street. Brunch or dinner, takeout or dine in, it is always fabulous. The staff is always friendly and John makes amazing food! We just left Easter brunch which was beyond tasty and filling! The pesto for bread dipping (served with dinner) is a favorite of mine, among basically I've ever had there. I highly recommend this quaint, awesome restaurant.

I've only been here once, after shopping at nearby Marisa's (which is an absolutely amazing dress shop. If you are looking for a dress, go here first. I've followed her for more than 10 years. She always has a unique selection of of-the-moment and classic styles at amazing prices).  When we asked at Marisa's for good local places, Sycamore was one of the places they recommended. 

The food was really, really good.  The food was of the caliber I tend to expect of restaurants in larger cities. Oysters Rockefeller the best I've had, the steak and crab special the best I've ever had out side Baltimore (we know our crab!), and my mom's chicken parm was also tasty.  The garlic mashed potatoes are ridiculous, the vegetable sides nice and crisp.  Our server was attentive and quick - you could easily see why the place was packed. 

I'm looking forward to eating there again the next time I'm in town. If you haven't been in, you should go.

We've lived in the area for over a year now, & there's nowhere else we'd rather order pizza from. The prices are reasonable, service is friendly, and we've always gotten exactly what we ordered. Tonight we ordered a large pepperoni pizza, extra crispy fries, & boneless buffalo chicken bites. You know how sometimes places forget the sauce or don't really make your fries crispy? Well we got extra sauce and the fries were actually crispy! If you live in the area, definitely try this place out.

Not the cleanest facility in the world. (The wet sauna in particular was dank and mildewy, and could have stood a serious bleach party. Or four.)

Staff was friendly enough, though, and the lockers were very reasonably priced.

Just "meh."

Came for their wing special that they advertise on their website but they no longer have it. We ordered wings anyway and they messed up our order. We ordered 1lb buffalo and 1 lb parm garlic. We got two orders buffalo. That being said, they were just ok. About half were a bit freezer burnt and chewy. We did enjoy the fresh made chips.

Bathroom was disgustingly dirty and it's only 3pm on a weekday. When I pulled my pants back up, they were wet from in front of the toilet. 
Barista Brittany I think with bleached blonde and black roots made my mobile order wrong and when I asked about it, she admitted to making it wrong but said "I asked for many customizations" and she had a friend laying on the pick up area girl talking.  I waited several minutes longer than I should have for my drink. I noticed later the light ice was not lite, in addition to missing my Carmel drizzle. 
Maybe the health department should check this place out. 
Maybe they need some customer service training.

I have been here 3 times now the first two times breakfast nothing special chunky milk and cold I went for lunch had Mexican burger it was good but cold the fry where so cold good service I won't go back tho 3 time cold food each time

This is the second time we have stayed at this hotel.  It's quiet, close to all the French quarter fun, and a beautiful hotel.  Bed is very comfortable, staff is friendly and accommodating, and the rooms are smallish (it's the French Quarter) but comfortable and clean.  The in-house bar and restaurant are decorated La Belle Époque style and romantically charming.  The courtyard is beautiful...day or night. The food is just okay, somewhat overpriced, but mood of the restaurant sort of makes up for it.  We paid $122.00 a night and was reasonable for Twelth Night celebrations.

I would and probably will stay here again because I love the mood and decor so much.  Nothing like enjoying a late night cocktail amid gas lamps and Creole cottages as a back drop.

Really enjoyed this place! My friend and I both got pizzas and split them and it was pretty tasty. I enjoyed being able to sit outside and there was no wait when I went (on a Thursday night). I'm giving 4 stars cause I thought some of the menu items were a little pricey, but overall I had a good experience.

If you enjoy partaking in the sweet sipping of fermented grape juice, you simply must speak to Quinn. This gentleman has a most peculiar and spectacular knowledge of the substance, and is sure to satisfy any and all inquiries concerning the matter. Please, my dear friends, do yourself a favor and broaden your pallets with the help of an exquisitely talented future sommelier. 

In all seriousness, Quinn is the man and this place is amazing. Highly recommend
This was an ok bar. I did not notice this was a smoking bar. The service was decent. The drinks were blah... there was no music playing. They have drink specials but nothing that was all that great.

I went here to redeem my Groupon. First of all, this was the last day to redeem the Groupon yet there were only 2 people on staff and a massive line up. Second, they charged us for tax. We redeemed 2 Groupons that day at different locations. The first one we went to (Namao) had great service - one of their employees was handing out free samples to everyone that was waiting in line. But more importantly, we weren't charged for tax at that location in addition to the Groupon. We explained this to the employee at the Marble Slab on Jasper Ave but she was making a fuss about having to pay tax and not marking our stamp cards (the Namao one marked our stamp cards). I realize that each location is a franchise but clearly there was poor communication as to what the Groupon entails. Lastly, the flavours we got were Birthday Cake and Coconut... but BOTH tasted like Sweet Cream! In fact, when I was sampling other flavours before ordering, they all tasted like Sweet Cream. This definitely isn't the case at other Marble Slabs I've been to. I don't know if each location mixes each ice cream flavour on-site, but I was really disappointed in the ice cream we got at the Jasper Ave location. I'm not going to stop buying ice cream from Marble Slab, but I likely won't return to the one on Jasper Ave.
Great tacos great price loved the al pastor. We were able to place a larger order and pick up in a short time.
Great atmosphere. Fair price.  Menu is limited but everything we've tried is delicious.  Repeat for sure.

As I come from The Netherlands, where we have a huge variety of Eastern European food, I expected a little more from this place. Food was OK, but I have had better, both in Florida and Europe. I had the chicken shawarma and my husband ordered the kufte kebab. We would love to see them do great, because we need some more variety in this area. I will come back and try them out again, hopefully I will be pleasantly surprised.

My family came here Mother's Day and it was an over the top experience. Everything was on point, From the moment you step foot inside of this restaurant you get the whole home vibe feel and then as soon as you put the food up to your mouth you feel like you've been reborn. This is a must try restaurant for any occasion. So stop wasting time figuring out what to eat and go to Backfin now and I promise you'll be super satisfied. Keep up the good work guys much love.
Didn't honor my reservation. Walked me to a hotel with a rollaway bed. Bad place. Will not try again.

Update:  They offered me a free cut to make up for the mess that other stylist did to my hair. Had to wait Months for it to grow back at a reasonable length n was still Very Leary about going back but I got Jade n she did exactly what I asked. Thank You Jade ! Your the best they Have and Not EVER Did I see you do the "Eye Roll" at someone who was speaking to you.  But Great Clips  ....  I'm Never Coming back.

Bobo Lahori. 
Ate here for lunch with my best friend.
We had a nice meal and would like to come back.

I really want to check out their Saturday Buffet.Regarding the food selection; I love seeing uncommon items on the menu. One item that stood out of the menu was goat brain. I want to take my husband here so we can try it together. 

The people there were friendly and could help you with the menu if Pakistani food isn't something you are familiar with.

After a second trip I am revising my review to three stars. I visited the buffet, the food wasn't very hot and there was a goat dish I didn't care much for. It seemed very fatty. My spouse was put off by the loud speaking guests at a nearby table. Food was still mostly good and service was up to par and friendly.
Great shop to get a Philadelphia souvenir.  Sweet lady than runs/owns the place.  She gave us several suggestions on places to see while here and where to go for a great Philly cheesesteak.

Our experience started off very positive. Friendly staff and a great homecoming dress. However we were fooled...

We specifically asked about "prom promise" or "hoco promise" in this case. This is the promise that the dress you purchase has not already been purchased by someone else for the same event, and will not be sold after you've bought it. Nothing worse than two girls in the same dress. We were assured that we were fine.

FOOLED!!  Not only did my daughter and another at her school have the same dress, they were both purchased at Esmeralda's.  A 3rd girl had it in another color, also bought there.

I posted a negative review on FB, and got a response from the owner. She promised to give me a new dress for another event for free - more than I was looking for. But fooled again! Every time I've tried to reach her since SHE HAS NEVER RESPONDED! Prom season is here and we got our dress elsewhere!

Avoid this store! I am still saddened that we were lied to. And angry that the owner never made good on her promise to fix the situation. She won't even call me back! Will make sure I share this everywhere!

Heard about Smees from my sister. She had the wings the first time she went. Loved them. Said food and service went down hill from there. My daughter and I are foodies. Decided to try for ourselves. Along with my sister giving them another try. My sister ordered hers online. My daughter ordered ours. My sister went to pick up. She got there order was incomplete. She ordered a Cesar salad. They had ran out of romain lettuce so they made her nothing. They put her artichoke and spinach dip in a styrofoam cup. They had not put cheese on the italian beef i had requested in the note box and it was made with shredded beef that was tough to eat not sliced beef and they had not made my daughter sandwich at all. My sister had to wait there almost 30 min for them to prepare and reprepare our food. My daughter asked for no mushrooms on her philly steak. Sandwich came full of mushrooms and no flavor. My sisters blue cheese dressing for her buffalo salad she had to reorder was runny like melted sour cream. We both asked for extra dressing neither got any. My side salad looked like someone had chewed some and put back in container. They only offered my sis water to drink for her wait. First time and last time. Sorry not sorry to say.

The next time you visit the women's shoes section at Dillard's International Mall, take a look at the carpeting. It's been that way for about 10 years now: the filthiest, nastiest sales floor I've ever seen. I wouldn't allow my wife or my mother to shop there. Dillard's in general has become something like Marshall's--just not as clean, and much more expensive.
The turkey grinder literally put one slice of turkey on it and was 80% lettuce. I've been customer for years but I'm over it. They literally just sent me a lettuce sandwich

I reviewed this place awhile ago and just want to say it's amazing as ever! They are now open for brunch on the weekends. The huevos compadres is probably the best breakfast I've ever had in my life!!! The chilequiles (I think that's how it's spelled) is amazing as well.. Try it.. Fried tortilla strips with sauce and cheese... Brilliant! Also the owner's friendly and good humored personality completes the experience. I just love señor taco so much! I only wish they had a place near downtown as there is a serious need for a restaurant like this!

This place has a lot of potential. Quaint location in the perfect neighborhood for its appeal. However, the price of the dinner entrees doesn't match up with reality. 25-30$ signals exceptional which is far from what they deliver for the dinner entree. The brunch is reasonably priced but the dinner entrees surprisingly are not nearly the level of the pastas or appetizers (I love the gazpacho and risotto but the entrees are not nearly as good for twice the price). Some of the dishes are great which keeps me coming back. Ambiance and neighborhood won't keep steady business unless the dinner service is revamped.

I'm in Hawaii, but bought my Certified Pre-Owned 2018 BMW X5 eDrive from Bert Smith BMW in Florida.  Keith Sanders was the sales guy, with Jerry Cardine in finance.  Both guys were very professional and patient with my wife's negotiation on price.  My wife is a price fighter, but Keith and Jerry never got upset with her battles.  After the price battle was settled, Jerry and Keith helped set up for shipping and out-of-state registration and title change.  The car is excellent, better than expected.  I'm very happy with the price.  I highly recommend Bert Smith BMW.

Go hang out the Bar with Ink he will steer you in the right direction for drinks and food selection.  This place is a hidden Gem.  If your feeling frisky try the "Slurricaine" frozen goodness in a glass.  Also the other frozen drink wild berry lemonade.   Garlic Parmesan fries with the Chipotle Ranch is a must try for an appetizer.  Right across the street from Ascend amphitheater.

We stayed at the Residence Inn recently and enjoyed our stay. It was rather expensive, but the hotel was very clean, and the beds were comfortable. Since it was a suite, it had a refrigerator, sink, stovetop, bar, etc. The bathroom was nice and spacious. My only bathroom complaint was there wasn't a place to hang a hand towel next to the sink, so you had to lay it on the sink or hang it over by the tub.  There is not a ton of parking, and if you are using a luggage cart, you have to use the front door because it is the only door with a ramp and without steps. Keep that in mind if you or your family member is handicapped or can't step up or down without assistance. If you are visiting Opry Mills, the Grand Ole Opry, or wanting to stay close to Nashville but not downtown, I would recommend this hotel.

As a fellow vegan, I go to every vegan restaurant in town to try what they offer. Came here twice, and left just as disappointed each time. The food isn't good, and it's kinda expensive. My food looked like they made it last week. Service is rude. We had to wait for the food 45+ min. Defitely won't come back. Restaurant is in a great location in the Gulfport area.

Just ate there and there were cockroaches everywhere. Do not eat here! I am from out of states and I have eaten in multiple places in Louisiana. I can tell when a place is a dump tourist attraction. AVPID AT ALL COSTS. believe me not worth your money or time

Very disappointed and reduced the Kimpton brand in my estimation (in fact I just cancelled my next Kimpton stay as a result)

We were booked in to a "King Deluxe," the room was tiny. So small that it felt like the bathroom was in the room. In fact it was only separated by a sliding door that opened at the foot of our bed so that when opened its literally felt like the toilet was in your bedroom. Besides being shockingly small, the bedroom felt worn out, dated, and while maybe not dirty....not fresh and clean either. The bathroom was reasonably nice although it felt cheap and minimal.

There were a range of other subpar elements.
 -- the light switch on the wall and lamps didn't work well, constantly frustrated trying to turn on lights! 
--  the lighting itself was crappy, all old style compact flourcents in what seemed like a wide array of color temperatures. 
-- The black out curtain was obstructed from closing by cables. 
-- There was an ironing board but no iron. We had to call three times to get one (each time explaining that we were in a rush). 

The front desk staff was fine, not especially pleasant or warm, just sort of unconcerned. We ask for advice once and they both spent 5 minutes seeming confused before they tried to find someone else to advice (the questions was, a good place to eat quickly near by). The two instances in which we had the help of other hotel staff (delivery of the iron, front door) they were super nice and friendly.   

The location was good for our purposes right near Rittenhouse Square.

My daughter and I came recently for lunch on the recommendation of a local shop owner. My cucmber mojito was refreshing. The salad was typical. The ropa vieja was great. The tres leche cake was superb. The service was fast, a little too fast, as we were not finished with one course before the next. The music was okay and the atmosphere was nice. Lunch was about $40 including tip, which is typical for New Hope. We would return.

I was contacted by a rep of the company.  We were eventually able to get on an auto pay plan.

I've upped it to 3 stars because they did reach out to me and address my main concern.  But I didn't go higher because it still took too much work to get on the autopay, some of the fees I had to pay were not made clear to me and seem a bit excessive, and (before I got on autopay) it wouldn't let you do a prepayment or schedule one... so you had to stay on top of it every month or face some headaches.

They only have 13 cocktails on their menu. Three are listed as "new." I ordered one of the other 10. There were two women behind the bar. Neither knew how to make it. One of them looked at the same menu I had, saw the ingredients listed on the menu, and poured some of them into a glass with ice. She handed it to me and said, "You better stir that."

It was lousy. Of course it was.

Really bad staff and staff training at this place.

This hotel is in a prime location and the beds are A+.  With that being said, I don't think it merits the top dollar it charges.

I spent one night here, which cost $600 plus a resort fee.  For that price, we got a "suite" which includes a small section of the room with a loveseat and an extra tv.  

Pros:  the location can't be beat if you want to be downtown.  You're walking distance to everything, including the funk zone and all the shopping on state street.  They have a very cute rooftop but it can be hit or miss.  

Cons:  our room was on the 2nd floor overlooking the valet entrance, and we could hear road noise.  There was no luggage rack in the room when we walked in, so I had to request one.  We hung around the room for about 30 minutes, and the luggage rack hadn't arrived by then.  

For breakfast the next day, we ordered room service.  Bagel and cream cheese is $4 and they gave us very little cream cheese.  Also, we were woken up several times throughout the night by drunk people running through the hallway.  The TV offerings are also super limited, as we couldn't even access Netflix.  I haven't encountered this in the last five years, and I travel quite a bit for fun and for work.  What high-end hotel doesn't have a smart tv with Netflix access?  

Overnight parking is $38.  Also, I couldn't even use the makeup mirror as it had a huge blurry stain spot in the middle, rendering it totally useless.  

Overall, I don't know why I would ever spend $600 to stay here.  Come if you get an amazing deal or something, but for that price, you can find me at the Bacara or Belmond.
4 out of 6 times i order from here, there rude , makes you not wanna eat there. Rather wait for pizza on 12th there always friendly!

Love coming to this place. The owner John is super cool. Went in today for a inspection and walked out full from a couple slices of pizza, inspection done, and a new tail light. I highly suggest this place especially for inspections.

I came here on my birthday and ordered takeout with some friends. This restaurant has very strict COVID protocols, so dining in was not an option. They were more than happy to fulfill a takeout order in person though. 

We tried what seemed like the gamut of the menu, and were not disappointed. Our orders included lo mein, a tofu dish, beef and broccoli, crab puffs, egg rolls, a spicy chicken meal, among others.  

As a rule, whenever we go to a Chinese restaurant, we make sure to eat the meal family style, so that everything ordered is shared amongst the group. The general consensus among everyone was that while the food was tasty, many of the dishes needed more sauce, especially those that had a meat like chicken or beef in them. 

It's very rare when I complain that a Chinese restaurant has too little sauce. Many places I go provide too much sauce, to the point where everything seems like it is drowning in it. However, this place was the opposite. The portions were very generous given what we paid for. 

Maybe a little too generous in our case given the sauce situation.

I can't speak about the restaurant, but the bar was fun and lively, and barkeep, Phyllis, was a helpful cutie. She recommended fun drinks for us  (hurricanes) and was terrific to chat with. I will say I saw a couple of the dishes: they looked very good, and the couple I talked to about the food said they enjoyed it very much. I'd come back for more.

Oh my goodness I died and went to Heaven. The biggest, fluffiest, most delicious pancakes I have ever tasted. Seriously, you must try them. My hubby got the pancake special of the day which has Reese's pieces, Hershey pieces and something like a caramel sauce on top. My hubby sounded like a zombie because he kept moaning at its sugary goodness. I got the corned beef with two eggs. It was good in its own right, but not at all what I'm used to. It had a mayonnaise, mustard like glaze, caramelized hash browns and shredded corn beef. Again, good, but not something I'd like to order again. I do love this place though, so all is forgiven. Will definitely, definitely come again.

I had panzarotti's here for the first time. However, they were way to oily. They do not compare to the consistently good panzarotti's I have gotten from Sal's Pizzeria in Marlton, NJ. The place is spartan, with security windows. However the service was friendly. Not much room to eat here.

Absolutely delicious!! The marble cake is not dry at all and the slices were huge. The icing is incredible. Chad was so great at answering all my questions and they made the cake exactly how I wanted it. Turned out beautiful and so tasty. It is so worth the money and I will recommend it to anyone looking for a cake for any occasion! Thank you so much!

Consistently good quality food served up quickly. I love trying out the new offerings and I'm rarely disappointed. From the Coastal Med Bowl to the salads to the kabobs, I almost always leave satisfied. Even the burgers are delicious!

It strikes me odd when I give one of our "regulars" only 3 stars but continue to frequent a place for 20 years!  That translates into "I'm satisfied with the things most important to me and accepting of their short comings".

The wait can be long at dinner hour on weekends.  They accept reservations early in the day and have expanded with a second dinning room next door;  the entrance is through the Pizzeria.

Portions are huge and you will leave with dinner for the following night.  All meals come with a decent house salad, warm bread and butter.  Drinks are served in tall plastic cups (one Of the small things I don't like).  Service is fast an efficient.

I have tried a few disappointing choices on the menu so I stick with my favorites. The eggplant is sensational and my usual but chicken parm, clams or mussels over linguini and chicken and spinach are good.

This is worse than a tourist trap.  I came here for its coveted Cuban history and left severely unimpressed and ashamed this establishment is touting to be "Cuban".  I am basing this review from a Cuban-American perspective, a perspective with roots in Miami.

I saw the recommendation to go to this locale from one of the "visitors guides" during my stay in Tampa.  I should have known better  but I figured, this establishment has deep roots in this community, predating Miami's Cuban scene by at least 50 years.  Situated near the downtown/Ybor neighborhood, it wasnt hard to find.

When I walked in, I decided to order my usual staples...Without looking at the menu I ordered (in Spanish nonetheless) a pastelito de guayaba, a pastelito de carne (cashier said "we dont have any"), my initial thought was...no pastelito de carne??..ok next, a croquetta de hamon (we dont have any)..that should have been my warning sign btw..next, dos cortaditos bien oscuro and a tostada.  The cashier rang me up ($10....wtf) and asked for my name.  A few minutes later I saw the girls making coffee asking another employee what a cortadito was...I decided not to keep warching and keep my expectation high.  After about 5-6 minutes, my tostada was ready, the pastelito and then the coffee came.  They decided to make me a cafe con leche sized cortadito.  It did not come with sugat premixed (a no-no for a Cuban establishment), the coffee tasted like ass (it wasnt Pilon, Bustelo  La Llave, it was more water than coffee actually...it tasted lile what Colombians call Tinto (with milk)...yuck!

I stirred in some sugar packets and then tried tasting...yuck!!..Instead of complaining, I threw it out.  The pastelito was very heavy on the bread.  Cuban pastries in Miami are very airy and fluffy, this was a brick with guava paste soldered inside.  In the garbage with the coffee.  The tostada was actually good, but the bread was a little different than the Cuban bread produced in Miami.  I noticed that Cuban bread in Tampa is of a darker color, kinda like a light french bread but not as doughy.  I ate half and threw it away.

In all, I understand that the neighborhood is transient  may cater to mostly non Cubans, and selling to those people, yes this concept may work.  Do not come here from Miami and expect to be blown away.  Kind of like eating Italian food in the US, go to Europe and protest that its not your interpretation of Italian.  Either way, I wont throw out my interest completely until I visit Qest Tampa at Teresitas or somewhere near there for a second opinion.

I averaged my stars for this review: 2 for ambiance and 4 for food and customer service.  This location has about the same menu as the location in Newtown Square, and I think that the food selections are very good.  A little something for everybodies' tastes.  

I ordered the Portobello Burger which came with grilled portobello, roasted red peppers, aged provolone, and baby arugula on a potato bun.  I was very satisfied with the meatless option.  The mushrooms were plentiful and tasty and I really enjoyed the aged provolone it was a great touch of flavor.  Instead of fries I opted for the side salad option and was not disappointed.  Very LARGE side salad and it was probably more filling than having a side of fries.  Would definitely recommend this choice on the menu. Our server was great and very cheerful.  Not too overbearing but noticed when we needed some attention.  

Although ambiance should not always factor in how a place is rated especially if you are happy with the food and service, I think this place could use a refresher.  I understand it is the local town pub and some people like the same old, but when you look at their newer menu style and how great their website looks, the actual physical location just does not match anymore.  Newtown Square had a recent facelift and it looks a little more updated, but I think I heard a rumor they no longer have the same owners.

Overall as far as the food and service goes I would recommend but I would let people know to not expect anymore than a basic pub even in Berwyn.
Food was good, but not the best. The fries were great. I ordered the fish po boy which was just two pieces of small fish on a sandwich. I wasn't impressed.

1st time trying, totally accidental too. Tried my "known" pizza spots, no answer as its after 10pm. Well THAT was the best thing to ever happen, because I found this place. DAVES IS NOW MY SPOT. It has surpassed quality and flavor the top rated on yelp in my area. The texture of crust is superb, sauce/gravy perfectly savory and sweet, cheese is quality, toppings so good. They were quick,efficient and even tho we ordered 15mins to closing they didn't turn us away.  I'm loyal to Dave's now and as long as I'm in delivery distance

Stopped by for lunch. Was initially shocked at how small it was! When I got there, only bar seating was available...but since I was alone, it was perfect. Ordered the gyro (comes with fries), added feta, and got a can of Sprite. With tip, it was over $14. Steep for lunch. 
Food was...fine. Nothing bad about it. Nothing outstanding. I was a little annoyed that I paid $1.45 for a small ramekin of feta. I'm not cheap when it comes to money, but that just seemed like a lot for a little feta!
My meal was fine but honestly, I probably won't return. Saltus has better fries (the Greek fries are next level), Original Gyro Joint has more bang for your buck, and Adel's has better service, fountain drinks, and better flavor. I will admit that I like their tzatziki here better than other places. Hope this place does well, but I'll have to get my gyros from somewhere else.

Worst experience ever. Was told the heat was broke and has been for a week. It was freezing, ate with our coats and gloves on! Outside of the sushi, You could get the same food at Joe's Crab Shack.  Out of seaweed salad that night too. I wouldn't go back and tons of other places downtown have working heaters.
Pretty ridiculous to have a reservation and still be waiting outside in the cold 45 minutes after the reservation time.

Had a chicken salad hoagie and their potato salad on the side. Seriously some of the greatest potato salad I have ever had. Their service was also very quick, I only had to wait in a short line to place my order and wait. The prices were very reasonable too!

We came for restaurant week and I'm glad we did! We were seated immediately at a cute little table outside.
