Appetizer - I had the salad with farro and it was delicious 
Entree- I had the pear purse pockets which was highly recommended by our waitress. It was just ok, I had originally picked another pasta entree but she really pushed it so I went with her recommendation. After I wished I had stuck with my original pick 
Desert- we got a trio with ice cream, lava cake and a baklava bread pudding. The ice cream and lava cake were tasty but the bread pudding was weird. 

Over all Great wine list, great atmosphere, good food. Will definitely be coming back.

Fairly typical Walmart. One big problem here seems to be a lack of employees. I know that all retail stores leave closed lanes for checkout, but this particular Walmart seems to never have more than 8 lanes open, if that.
The kibby is amazing and the pita is to DIE for. I love that combo and it is now my staple fair. I am not a falafel fan but they make a falafel that I can eat.

Have been here for 1.5 hours trying to buy an iPhone. I forgot that I have a lock on my credit report for safety. So after 1/2 hour wait, plus another 1/2 hour in the purchase process, they refused to finance my phone as part of the Apple Care program. After I figured out what happened, and unlocked my credit report, I am trying once again to buy the phone. I explained to Daryl, the sales person who helped me initially, that the problem was fixed and that I wanted to proceed. His response was to rudely brush past me and say that I needed to wait my turn. Apparently I am back on the end of the line. Standing here another 1/2 hour, still no help.

The place is being remodeled. That said they no longer offer a pho boat. No points were deducted for the remodel which had all the decor down but my bowl of pho was only lukewarm when I got it. The rare round streak didn't really cook in the broth because the broth was too cool and the bean sprouts were also mostly raw because of this. I might give it another try if I'm in Tampa again but I'd recommend checking the temperature of your food before you did in, it was an unpleasant surprise. Oh and their service was also remarkably slow.

I tried Martin's for the first time, and it will likely be my last time.

I ordered the pulled pork plate, which was $12 (over $13 after tax).  I consider that to be a high price, but I expected that the portions would likely justify the price.  I was wrong.  I found the portions to be very stingy.  I only had a few ounces of pork, and my two sides were in tiny, tiny Styrofoam cups.  To boot, my potato salad was very bland.  I felt like the flavor of the pork was fine, but I've definitely had better barbecue.

Also, the layout of the restaurant was rather ignorant.  You stand in line to order, but that line completely blocks the entrances to the bathrooms.  I had to wedge my way through people in line as I went to and from the bathroom.  There was no avoiding it.  The restaurant also featured gigantic ceiling fans, which made the dining room drafty and they were blowing my napkin and receipt away unless I secured them somehow (you need to keep your receipt out for when the call your number, otherwise I would have put it away).  Another annoyance is the restaurant workers calling out order numbers the whole time.  There was a particularly shrill girl who was squawking out order numbers the entire meal and it was very unpleasant.

So, the bottom line is:  Martin's is very overpriced for what you get.  The food is mediocre at best, and the atmosphere and layout of the restaurant are poor.  I know of much better barbecue places in Nashville where I could get much better food, much better portions, and even be able to afford a beer with my meal and pay about the same price.  Even the toothpicks were cheap and flimsy.  I will not be returning to Martin's.  Unfortunately, it cost me $13 to learn that lesson.

Lower your prices.  Serve bigger portions.  Get rid of the big ceiling fans.

I've been coming to prep for many years now. Their quality and attention to detail are extraordinary. This morning I ordered a chickpea scramble and it was overcooked and on top of that, the avocado was rotten. I will twice before ordering from them.

This place is pretty bad. The quality of the food is very low and it came out luke warm. The flavors aren't there and the ingredients don't seem fresh. The decor is nice and seems authentic, but the food is definitely not. Many better Mexican choices in the area. Service is also poor

On a Friday night, karaoke. It was packed. A mixed crowd. 

Bouncer Alex is funny. 

Manager called a customer fat while they were kicking her out, unnecessary.

Also, I know it was my own risk to play the jukebox near the end of the night, but the manager also turned it off after I put 6 bucks in that would've played because I asked for it to be turned on and I heard most of them before close but the first one I missed while it was off.

I have been here many times over the years and love the feel of this indie mostly used bookstore. The place is giant. The front of the store is split into the typical sections: children's, history, fiction, and more. They also have music at the front. The staff member was friendly and helpful- I advised I was looking for a biography and she didn't know if they had it but told me where to look. She had small conversations with us while we were there and it was nice. The back of the store is the clearance section which is overflowing. There were books on the floor and a bookshelf of books that went allllll the way to the ceiling. I actually spied a couple of cobwebs but that was kinda part of the vibe. We didn't end up getting anything this trip but I have gotten things in the past.

i have not returned to this olive garden (clearwater us 19) in over a year because of there shitty service. i always order togo and every single time whether i am speaking to the manager or an employee they have some sort of attitude and are very condescending. i spoke with the manager the other day about redoing my food order which had been messed up. she proceeded to make it seem like i was lying to get free food. she stated they have new management and she would never just give me free food is the order was messed up then proceeded to tell me to have a nice day and then hung up. i am a waitress and i feel this manager should be replaced and her staff extremely evaluated. learn what customer service means.

Seemed dirty. The food was nothing special and came out lukewarm. My hot cocoa was topped with so much whipped cream I couldn't drink it without making a mess all over the table, however our server was friendly and polite. Most likely won't be back.
Brisket was tender and flavorful. Loved the spicy BBQ sauce. Slaw was fresh and full of flavor. I was just passing through town but will definitely be back for this!

There were quite a few snafus with the service. (One person in our party didn't receive her food... 3 of eight of us didn't get our drinks. Asked- still didn't get them.). Coworker went to get ice water for us. 

Anywho, the inside of this place is cool- it's an old jail and if you're familiar with Mad Art Gallery, that's still inside!

You can sit inside of a jail cell for a really cool experience. (See photo). 

I tried several of the sauces and I really liked the blackberry, which is surprising because I don't normally like sweet thinks.

You all in the STL yelp arena know how I feel about Cuban sandwiches. I have an emotional response to that flavor profile. 

The Cuban sandwich here is really, really, good- and while it's not "Cuban bread" it is very close to a light crisp, pressed, bread that works nicely for the Cuban. Nice crispy pickles. The mustard is present but not overpowering and their meats are genuinely tender and delightful. They didn't try to do anything silly like sub the mustard for their own "house sauce". Thankfully. Other places put cuts of meat on their Cuban that are stringy and fatty in order to save a buck, thus they end up being pulled off of the sandwich when you bite into it; these are not like that. Tender and delicious and definitely moving itself up into the top three for Cuban sandwiches for me!

Plan on spending at least $15 per person with a drink, assuming you don't get any premium sides and billy goat chips are your jam.

Will return!

My BF needed a suit dry cleaned for a wedding.  We took it, along with 3 other articles of our clothing, in on a Thursday afternoon.

At the time we dropped it off they said it would be ready the next day... which was exactly what we needed. We were excited about the quick turn around and the convience of this place being right in the mall!

On Friday, when I went to pick it up I was told it would be another 15-20 mins. I gave them 30.  When I picked up my order I checked to see if everything was there- and they were in fact MISSING my blazer.  The clerk tried telling me that we had only brought in 3 items, instead of the 4 I knew we had.  

Granted I was annoyed, but the blazer wasn't all that expensive.  As I said "forget it" and started to leave- they found my blazer... ON THE FLOOR.  By the way their floors are gross and looked like they hadn't been vaccuumed in months.

The said they'd have my blazer cleaned and ready for the following day (which I couldn't do becasue of the previously mentioned wedding). So I asked a friend to pick it up for me the following Tuesday.  Which was a circus in of itself.

I can't speak to their alteration services, but I definately will not be using these folks again for dry cleaning.
Delicious food, very hard to find great vegetarian options in this city. They very quickly corrected a missing drink order and gave us an extra drink. Thank you!
Good dim sum for America, good xiao long bao for America. That's it!

This place is held very near and dear to my heart.  The classes (children and adult) are incredibly creative and educational, free or  fairly cheap for what you get out of it.  The classes are taught by professional artists/teachers that definitely know their stuff and have a few tricks up their sleeves.  The faculty and staff are amazing people!  It is highly recommended you sign up to become a member because that helps them support classes being free or cheap in the future.

There are three galleries located in the main building and another one in the Works on Paper building.  The gallery spaces hold really great shows, and the Challenge exhibition is a juried competition held every year with artists from the surrounding area submitting work in hopes of being included in the final twelve.  

The main building has been recently renovated (last year), adding an elevator to make the building handicap accessible, there is also a Romanesque church sanctuary still intact, and amazing to work in or hold events.  There is a seperate Works on Paper building that houses the etching, litho, and photo studios.
I love dessert and baked goods, and Cairo Cakes certainly delivers!  Yummy!

Well I feel kind of guilty for giving this place 3 stars, as almost everyone has given it 5 stars, which leaves me a bit confused. I've been to a lot of hibachi places, and this has definitely been the least impressive by far. And no...I don't need all the spinning, onion volcanoes or rice 'heart beats' either...but I do expect friendly service and a good meal. I understand this is a small family owned place, and there are only 2 people for 4 tables, but then it would lead me to assume they would have perfected the whole thing by now. First, we did not get drink orders, and just got water handed to us which is fine, but there were a couple people who did not want water and had to ask a couple times for their sodas. I do like that the menu is tiny and only has a few options...makes it easy...except if you are vegetarian. FYI they cooked the meats and vegetable right next to each other, on the exact same spot on the grill...even I felt bad for the vegetables at that point. Also, the vegetarian option is the same veggies everyone gets (mushrooms, zucchini and onions) so if you don't like those options...o well. They didn't even have vegetable tempura available or anything else for the vegetarian, other than the 'meaty' vegetables. Next, they do NOT do fried rice...first place I've ever seen that does that. Usually the rice is the best part. Instead we got a THIRD of a bowl of white regular rice...not even kidding. The salad was very average with cold, wet lettuce and shredded carrots. The dressing was OK. I must say the shrimp was delicious, the chicken not so much, it was dry and completely flavorless. A couple people had to ask for salt and soy sauce because their meals lacked so much flavor. The whole time the chef was cooking he barely talked or smiled at us, and could tell was pretty much done with the day (we were the last table of the night though). The sad finale, was freezer burned vanilla, chocolate or orange sherbet small scoop of ice cream. Everyone left a bit perplexed and disappointed considering they were raving about this place from how they remembered it from like 10 years ago. So if this was any other place I'd give it 2 stars but because of the great prices and it being a small business, 3 stars will do. Anyway, I just could not relate to any of the 5 stars reviews and do not think they represented this place at all. I would not want anyone to go to this place with such high expectations. Sorry!!!
Food was great! Atmosphere was fun. Our server deina was amazing!! We had a great time.

The State Fair is always a good time. Whether you're there to see the animals, watch any of the competitions, ride some carnival rides, or just eat a ton of junk food, I promise you'll have a good time. It's wise to try to come on a discount day ($2 Tuesday, Foodie Friday, etc.) to save some coin, but be warned that it's also busiest on those days. Luckily, the fair is big enough that most things aren't crazy crowded. You'll have to wait in line for certain things, but never for too long. One thing that I really hate, however, is the parking. No matter what, getting in and out of here is a nightmare. But don't let that dissuade you, the fair is still a lot of fun and certainly worth checking out.

Average place, but good value for price.  This isn't a trendy hipster sub shop.  It is an old folks' diner.  Big pork tenderloin for $5.  Generous quantity, not necessarily quality.  Corn flake breading was an interesting touch.
Great place to stop before or during road trips.  Perfect meal on the go.
Good food. I was very surprised and pleased. To put it another way: It didn't suck.

I go a different place every Saturday to try a breakfast burrito (I'm on a quest to find my favorite) and i was pleasantly surprised by this one! Great price, for a HUGE burrito. The taste was overall great, the tortilla, the burrito itself and I really enjoyed both salsas. The girl at the counter was super friendly and my burrito came out fast and hot! The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars was because the horchata was gross. I get horchata everywhere I go, and this was my absolute least favorite. But everything other than that was fine.

The Getaway used to be our go to for the view and good food. The view still cannot be beat.

We invited friends to the Getaway after a year long wait. We bragged about the ocean paradise. What a mistake! 

We ordered our drinks, appetisers, and meal through an absolutely terrible app called TAP UP.

We ordered 6 drinks, two which showed up, one incorrect beer, the others never showed up.  Off the the bar for the other drinks, self service, hmm not what we were hoping. 

Appetizer order same story, one order, calamari was great, but we we the only couple that got our order. Eating while others waited is so wrong. Off we go after 20 minutes to ask for the other two couples orders. Came 15 minutes later. Chips and salsa, chips and Queso. Tough order to fill.  Excuse from servers the TAP UP APP, screwed up the order as it always does. So sorry, we do not like the app either, wrong orders occur and our tips suffer. USA servers for the most part are the best in the world. Not their fault. During the course of the meal we were asked if we ordered nachoes, fish tacos, salads, beers, none were ours. Clearly the app was wrong. 

Main Course same story, we got our wraps, boring, no sauces, asked for them had to find a server. Our friends no meals. 20 minutes later asked where they were, 5 minutes later they showed up cold. No one finished their meal and one of our friends cancelled their order because it was cold and tasteless.

Manager Ravi tried to get her a new meal but since everyone was fed up and wanting to go she declined. His offer. Ok no charge for you. Others sorry, but no offers.

We will not be back, would not recommend, suggest they drop the App, and the servers get a job from a place where the manager tries to make things right so they can earn the tips they deserve.

On the way out, went to the bathroom. Out of order, sorry. Summa up the evening.

Do better guys. You have a paradise, but it is lost.

JCP is my department store of choice. This location is clean, well organized, and free of clutter. My favorite departments besides clothing would be small appliances, and housewares. I appreciate they offer coupons that can be used for a variety of items unlike Macys which exclude almost everything which makes the coupons not beneficial.
The burgers and shakes here are some of the best I've ever had! It's a small cozy restaurant with extremely nice employees and locally sourced ingredients!
It's okay place for breakfast. Fast service. Appears fancy and clean but nothing really stood out when it came to food. Will try other breakfast place when I come back to NOLA.

Very cute. My mom and I went in and were able to relax and read. The couple who own this place are sweet and helpful. They make you feel like the place is your home. Lots of locals stoping by. My mom got a coffee and it was super smooth and full bodied flavor. She also got the turkey sandwich which was definitely worth the money. I got the eggs Benedict which tasted as good as they looked. Plenty of yummy pastries to choose from and free wifi. Definitely want to come back. Unique twist? The decor is also for sale!

Slow service...the worst part was my wife found hair inside her roll which she took a bite out of and hair she almost ate but caught between her teeth and the waitress replied everyone has short hair? Disgusting I understand it's a all you can eat restaurant but the quality tasted old and the rice was hard like completely smashed...

I'll be honest, I am addicted to Hot Box Stix!  I can't deny it any longer.  Their pizza I can do without, I don't find it any better than the average chain-type pizza.  Oh, but those breadstix!!  I always get them with ranch and cheese sauce.  Dip the stix in cheese 1st, then into the ranch.  Wonderful.  

I typically head there for the "Box Lunch"  which is a choice of 2 of 3 items: a slice, a salad and stix.  As you might have guessed, I skip the slice for the salad and stix.  It's a great deal.

I haven't had any of their specialty pies yet, but I can't really see them being any better than their regular pizzas.

Customer service here at Saks Fifth Avenue on city line  is down right garbage. I have been on the phone with these people since 1pm trying to get connected to someone in the men's department. I AM STILL GETTING CONNECTED AS WE SPEAK. Then when I try to tell these operators that nobody is answering they completely disregard what I say and connect me to an extension that's clearly not working. If your workers cannot manage answering the phones why have phones period? This is absurd and horrible customer service. Never in my years traveling in the Army have I seen this. Now they dropped my call and is now closed how convenient. If customer service is like this I can imagine how everything else is

I used to live near Criniti and I have to say, it is an absolute privilege to be so close to such a great establishment.  They make the best Alfredo sauce ever.  It's a little expensive and they do have weird-ish hours (I mean, am I spoiled to expect a restaurant to be open later since I'm a late eater?) but its really good and it doesn't really matter, I suppose.

I went there for a date and it was a pretty good date restaurant.

Excellent customer service. Your wait time is pretty short and when they do get to you the customer service is very good. Did some comparisons on the work we needed and they were way below the price of everyone else as well as completed the work much quicker. 

Their attention to what you need is great as they truly listen and don't try to sell you a product or service you don't want or need.

Highly recommend them for any lock related services as well as making fobs for your vehicle.

Incredibly subpar & bland tasting for Italian food. Our lasagna simply tasted like noodles, cheese, and some watery tomato paste. Comparable to my high school cafeteria's lasagna back in the day. The "mini meatball" appetizer was just crushed meatballs in a bowl of watery sauce; but the bread they came with was delicious, probably the best tasting item of our dinner! 
Very cozy interior of the restaurant & kind staff, for what it's worth. However not cozy or kind enough for us to go back.

(Note: reuploaded and edited years-old review due to it being pulled for originally being unintentionally "sexually offensive.")

Of course I want matzo balls larger than my skull taking up all of my soup bowl and half of my table!

Indeed, I'd love nothing more than an ungodly amount of meat shoved between two dwarfed slices of bread that takes a knife, a fork, a backhoe, a post hole digger, and a steam shovel to eat!

Go ahead and feel free to drain my wallet with ridiculous prices for your samitches and everything else you may be serving at any one moment! Who cares? I can't eat half of this stuff, so please let me pay triple for it!

Sure, I'd love to try some dessert! Oh wait, I'm already full from the feeble attempts at trying to even start what I ordered for dinner. Dammit, I don't have another eight bucks to throw your way for pastries, either!

And please, feel free to make your seemingly dry looking floors so amazingly slippery that I feel I might break my neck with every step in your eatery! After all, what's a few thousand more dollars for a hospital visit? I already spent a fortune for nearly the same experience with the food!

We have dined here twice recently. The place is a mixed bag. The happy hour specials are enjoyable and a decent value. My steak the first night had a lot of inedible fat. Mom's orange chicken tonight had a kerosene-like flavor. Nothing has been horrible and we would consider going back.

Several things I rated only three stars:
There is a pool. But it's outdoor. 
The wifi is kinda slow for videos. 360p is the maximum without stopping. 
The room I stayed in had hot water issue. Takes about 5 mins to get real hot water at night. 
The furnitures are old and flimsy. 

Good thing is there is a hoop in the back, remember to bring basketball.

Delicious soup dumplings. Not a traditional dim sum place but still tasty nonetheless. Recommend the Pan Fried Pork Soup Dumplings. Don't recommend the crabmeat dumplings - there's no crab meat.

Outdoor seating is packed but service is still quick!

Subsequent visits to this place have gone better. For example, they are serving their red and white beans in a bowl now, so you get a much better portion. People are polite and food has been better overall.

This place.  Whoa.  Great f*cking pizza and great selection for meat and non-meat lovers.  Went in for a going away lunch deal for a co-worker, and I was pleasantly surprised.  I only wish it were closer to the home front.

The food was great - the pizzas....I couldn't begin to decide which ones to try.  They have one that is solely for mushroom lovers - believe it has 3-4 different types of shrooms on it.  Whoa!  The Edgar Allen Poe was amazing.  They also have clever names for their pizzas, which is a good time in itself.  In addition - the salads are pretty darn amazing as well.  

Great beer selection on tap.  Great service and atmosphere.  Quick for carry out - unfortunately no delivery, but we can't have it all now can we?

If in or around Kirkwood and looking for something simple yet delicious - pop in and give Dewey's a shot.  Good stuff!

I came to Sarah after a long stretch of terrible experiences with many other salons in Reno. I have a TON of hair, but it was no problem for Sarah. She is super friendly and does an amazing job from start to finish. I recommend her to everyone, and everyone has been extremely pleased with her work. If you need a stylist she is awesome and very reasonably priced.

Robin and I hit the new location of Beaujolais last night. It was pretty damn amazing.

We tend to like to sit at the bar whenever we can, especially on the first visit to a place to scope out the kitchen, the staff, and the vibe. We got immediate, friendly service and ordered some drinks. The bar staff was trained by Duncan at Chapel and it shows. The cocktails they were serving were uniformly excellent. 

We split the Chacuterie plate, a butter lettuce and crab salad, and the Steak Frites. The chacuterie was traditional but tasty and I'll be dreaming about the duck rillette for a long time.

The butter lettuce salad was served with a heaping serving of delectable crab. Nothing awe inspiring but solid and oh-so tasty.

The Steak Frites were clearly the star of the show. The steak had that lovely crusty sear on the outside, the fries were top notch, and the bernaise was a work of art. Top shelf, all the way.

The new location is lovely inside. The staff is attentive, friendly, knowledgeable, and...oddly...seemed genuinely nice. 

Look, this isn't a cheap meal. It's just not. But it's worth every penny. Save up some dough and head on down to Beaujolais. You won't be sorry.

Dirty, slow, most stale disgusting food , sliced cheese in my taco ! Pathetic ass taco ! Told me to pull into parking lot NEVER brought my food out absolutely rude ! Needs to be shut down ASAP! never have condiments!!!! Workers don't wear gloves or hair nets !

This is an excellent place for gluten-free food.  One of the best places my wife and I have had with food that typically are not offered gluten-free like chicken tenders.  We were in town for the weekend so we had dinner here and decided to come back for brunch.  The ambiance is fantastic with the rear garden patio and bar area a real treat.  We almost forgot we were outside in the city.

Hey I'm vietnamese (asian) 
& proud ... this asian target worker was truly targeting me!!
 sad but true
Earth Day Horror!
I was attacked by one of their employee (she was asian) for getting a free RED earth day bag, I've talked to the store manager - he was right there when it happened. Unbelievable? 
Believe it. so next time Earth day roll arounds I would stay clear of this store. "Chris" the on duty store manager apologize but seriously. come ON ! I have never experience such a ordeal to act like I was stealing.

I have been couple of times to Cajun Kitchen at Goleta but it was not much satisfying as compared to one at Santa Barbara. I haven't tried all the menu at this place but I do eat chicken or salmon salad every time I get there. 

To be specific their quality is same but the problem is with quantity.  Cajun kitchen at Santa Barbara servers really good quantity on their various salads such as chicken, shrimp, and salmon. 

At Goleta I have experienced that the quantity is almost half of what they server at SB such as small piece of salmon, chicken, etc. I think they should improve their standards because being a franchise there shouldn't  be any difference in quality and quantity at any of their restaurants.

They just raised the prices even more, most of us go out to lunch for a quick inexpensive lunch, but their price are a little too hot for my wallet, they could also use a smile here and a smile there, cleanliness could use a little more love also.

Rocky

If need home cooking, this is your place. The restaurant chef prepare the meal from beginning. A lot of work. The dish that I have need a lot of time to prepare. I have the OX tail that was cook for long time to make it soft. It cook in the Caribbean spice and has a lot of Indian influence. The tail must be slow cook to make it separate from the bone. This dish accompanied with cabbage and the bean rice. You can have the choice of white rice. 

I also have the flower drink - Sorrel. They prepared it and put in the cup and store in their refrigerators. Sweet treat. They have some other food please check it out. They also have fried fish. You can call ahead to have your fish prepare before you arrive. It will take some time to fried. The owner of the restaurant is very friendly. I like about this mom and pop shop.

Beautiful landmark in SB in the old victorian section of town.
Take a blanket and lunch for a good relaxing time!

Overall a good experience. Solid 3.5 stars but the tie always goes to the runner and the server was good. 

My biggest complaint about this location is that the host acted like it was a huge problem for a lone diner to have a table instead of sit at the bar. I sat near the door and they were steady but didn't go on a wait the entire time I was there. The bar filled up. 

I had a "slam-gria" which is mostly Barefoot pinot grigio. The two sushi rolls I had were good. Nothing exciting, but the roll and the fish appeared fresh. I didn't notice a sushi chef or bar so I assume they're made alongside the other kitchen items. They had a pretty diverse menu. 

Alexa was my server. She was great. She didn't have the same attitude to lone diners. She was friendly and prompt. Without her service, this review would probably be a 3.
A must-visit if you like beer! The draft list is amazing and the vibe is very chill! Friendly bartender. An awesome place to grab a cold brew!
Gone down hill - all bounce houses were sticky and dirty. Food, drink spills on floors - needs a makeover - good for young kids if they cleaned bounce houses and sticky floors.

This website should be quite easy but it's a mess.  All it wants first is my location. I give that and then it takes me to events at nearby Barnes and Noble store. There is no place, at least in the iPhone to search for a book. One simple thing I expect to do at a bookstore web site. Some people still want to read books so heads up. I didn't need the complaints about your cafe in the store.  Just where are the books.  Some businesses no matter how much going for them, are destined to die.  Sad to lose the last book store.

Unfortunately, up until this evening, I would have given them 4 or 5 stars. We have eaten in the restaurant 2 or 3 times and have ordered delivery many times. The service in the restaurant has always been slow, but not terrible and the delivery was fine; never super fast, but not slow either.

Tonight, we ordered a late dinner online around 8. Around 8:45, our food wasn't here, so I tried to call the restaurant to find out when the food would arrive. The phone just rang and clicked over to a voice mailbox. I tried calling multiple times with the same result. I guess they decided to close early tonight and didn't shut off their online ordering service, so we'll have to find something else for dinner.

I don't necessarily have an issue with a restaurant closing early, but they should not allow someone to place an order, if they are not open. I'll likely never eat here again.

Wonderful Thai Restaurant tucked away in the center of Westchase. I took my daughter here for lunch. Great service. Lunch specials with small salad, entree and dessert. Smaller portions but still filling. Dinner is regular priced and larger portions. Love this little spot.

Food was ok.  Cheese fondue was missing a kick.  They don't use Kirsch anymore so it was sort of bland.  The ahi tuna was gamey. The bread for dipping in cheese wasn't very fresh.  The wine prices were good.  Our waitress was pleasant.  Will probably not eat there again. The Melting Pot in other cities has been much better in our experience. Kind of a bummer considering we were looking forward to a yummy fondue dinner.

I didn't write a bad review the last time we went. Maybe I should have and maybe it wouldn't have happened AGAIN! We go here for the chocolate fountain for my kids. Table of nine. You do the math. I would pick soemhere else to eat anyway, but my kids love the fountain! Second time now they have shut the chocolate fountain down an hour or more before closing. Arrived to eat at seven, at 7:45 they were taking it down. This exact thing happened to us the last time and my kids were very disappointed. I even said something and the people that worked there could have cared less. They put out a bowl of chocolate. Luckily I had taken a plate of strawberries by the salad bar while getting dinner. I guess I should have had my kids eat that before their salad and dinner. No reason to shut down the dessert bar an hour plus before closing. You've lost our business and I won't go back.
Easy in and out. The food was fine no surprises. The place could have used a good cleaning. Prices were reasonable. Will stop again on my way home from work.
WORSE CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER!!!! Do not eat here! They are terrible! Very Very RUDE with very bad attitudes.

I'm going to start off my saying I know this review will be biased. My friend is the pastry chef here. 
But omg
We were brought small amounts of everything to try and honestly it was all good. The grit soufflé was amazing. The steak was amazing. The desserts, though I'm biased, are actually unreal.
This is THE place if you want to have a special night.

I don't know what the previous reviewers were thinking, but the flavor of the tacos and ramen were amazing. Ambiance is relaxed and comfortable, staff were very nice. I was so happy to find birria in NOLA! My only suggestion would be homemade tortillas a little crispier. The tacos had a good amount of cheese and sauce had great flavor. I will definitely be back! Thank you to the owner and staff for providing a great experience!

My boyfriend and I liked the looks of Harry's Plaza Cafe, so we walked in and asked to see a menu. That was one quick way to learn they don't appreciate people asking for menus. Apparently, the "consummate" service they tout on their web site includes being openly haughty to potential customers.

If Harry's really takes pride in its service, they need to reconsider how they treat walk-ins. I'm taking my money elsewhere.

This place is not a drive by. The Fried Oreo Martini was a great starter. Tried the Buffalo Wings and the Beef on Weck. Both were excellent and the next best thing to going home to Buffalo. I'll be bringing my wife back for the Beef on Weck as this is a favorite of hers. I would go out of my way to hit this place, it's worth it.

3 stars for now. They just opened with new staff so it'll take some time for them to figure themselves out. 

Killer B's opened in the space that used to be occupied by California Crisp Cafe, and most recently Kozmo's. A minor remodel and new attitude here, and beer. I did notice that the plates they used are the same plates they had since it was California Crisp. . . 

A group of us checked it out for lunch and ordered pulled pork sandwiches and brisket sandwiches. I got mine with a side of fried mac and cheese. 

Overall the flavor of the meats are pretty good. The BBQ sauce was nice. I really enjoyed my sandwich. 

Although i hated the fried mac n cheese, theirs didn't work. :( Prices are more or less in line with other food choices in the area. 

At the end of the day, this will be in the lunch rotation. Check it out, it's a nice casual place to grab a sandwich and maybe even 5 beers. The service staff here is basically all college girls, cant go wrong with that!

Get yourself to the Voom Room. I had a cut with Kori today, and she did a phenomenal job, especially with attention to detail and communication, which are really important to me. While I was there, I saw other patrons come and go for a men's cut, women's curly cut, color, and eyelash extensions - everyone looked great and seemed happy with their service, with stylists also making a clear effort to run on time. Also, Kori (the owner) sets a great tone in terms of her interactions with clients and the stylists - everyone was pleasant and fun, with a good sense of humor and no drama. They also create a relaxing experience from start to finish - with a nice greeting from reception right off the bat, drinks, a head massage during the shampoo, and great-smelling products. Kori was also very understanding about the fact that I have a new baby and willing to be flexible to accommodate us. A lovely experience all around, and I'm looking forward to making this my salon home. Check them out!

Drove down from Seattle to Orlando, dropped off all my loads. Next pickup was Plant City, Florida.

Showed up at Plant City, parked my truck, and within moments I started smelling the sweet and pleasant aroma of fried fish lingering through the humid air. Fried fish? Naw. Plant City? Naw.

Started breaking my neck, looking all around trying to identify the source of the sweet smell, all I saw were a bunch of old warehouses and randomly parked trucks. I know I smelled the sweet aroma of fried fish, but from where? Hmmm... kept looking...

After looking around for over an hour, out of the corner of my eye I saw some people congregating around a building about 250 feet away. Walked over there, and the smell got stronger and stronger. Bingo! What do you know! Fried fish. 
Walked inside, was immediately greeted and offered a cozy table in the corner near a window. Lunch buffet with delicious fried catfish and other mouth watering goodies. Omg, I was in heaven. Amazing. Great food, great service. Five stars.

Came here for dinner and the place was pretty shabby. Our waitress was fantastic but that was about it. The man who brought us our meals gave us attitude when we asked about our appetizer since it had yet to be brought out. The bang bang shrimp was underwhelming and the snow crab was over salted. My fiancée got the seafood pasta which was alright but nothing spectacular.
DO NOT GO HERE only one technician there and she rushes through manicures. I got a gel manicure and it lasted 3 days. She didn't take time for cuticles or anything. Definitely not worth the money!

Wilmington, DE
The buffet is pretty watered down for me because I like the really spicy places. A little disappointed. ;-/

I had to check what I rated the other yum yums (a 3) before voting this one. This one is sub par in comparison to the warminster location. It's small and cramped and their parking lot is equally small and cramped. During covid they had stupid in and out doors which the staff sternly told you about as if you're an idiot for not knowing - sorry I'm not a regular . The donuts at this location are much much smaller in comparison to warminster. I only stopped because I happened to be up by this way and I won't be back. Back to warminster I go with bigger donuts, more parking spaces, one door and slightly nicer employees.

I am a low maintenance tenant. I do not have much interaction with maintenance nor do I go to the office much.  However, when I have dealt with them they have been courteous and responsive. 

I have lived here about two years. It's quiet and I haven't had any significant issues.  The parking is plentiful, the location is convenient to shopping and entertainment. The traffic backs up a little from Earhart to Dickory around rush hour.

The only complaint I really have is that the inside of the apartments are in dire need of an update. Basically, the 80's called and they want their kitchen and bathroom back.  There is a roach problem, but I suspect that comes with LA territory. My cat took care of most that issue.

I don't know what has happened to customer service these days . If you buy something to support a local neighborhood business you expect a certain level of respect when you go back to tell them the product is damaged or broken . Why would anyone purposefully buy something , break it and try to return it? The fact is you have to stand by what you sell, how you package it when it goes out the door and make it right when something goes wrong due to a misstep by an employee, possibly the owner. This type of lack of service is why these businesses come and go. You can't build loyalty if you don't do right by your customers . It's Christmas time for cripes sake . Don't curse at customers when they are trying to get an item exchanged or soon you won't have any.

Had a late Sunday brunch here. The wait was acceptable as we did not have reservations and it was Sunday after all. I do not mind waiting for quality food and had no specific events on my schedule for the rest of the day. After an hour wait, we were seated in the courtyard. 

The courtyard was lovely, but the tables are a basket weave iron and made for uneven surfaces to set your drinks. Coasters help! The band had been moved to the courtyard to make room for more seating inside, so we had our own personal musical performance. They were mindful not to play too loudly in the small, enclosed space. 

There was a considerable delay in taking our order and several tables either complained or left. Again, I was with good company and had no particular schedule for the day so we enjoyed the courtyard and some good conversation. I ordered the fried oyster mushrooms and the Eggs Hussarde. 

It took 25 minutes to get our starters. We were feeling sorry for the staff at this point because obviously the kitchen was in the weeds as well. The mushrooms were nice. A delightful flaky breading and not greasy at all. 

After another 40 minute wait our entrees arrived. The Eggs Hussarde was a bit of a disappointment. The steak was flavorful but I had ordered it cooked medium and it was really black and blue. I could have sent it back but we had already waited over an hour to be served. The tomatoes were not seasoned at all and the eggs were overcooked with no ooey gooey yolk to enjoy. I loved the fried oysters that garnished the plate. Not chewy or greasy although, they too could have used a little seasoning. 

Would I return to Tableau? Maybe. Sadly, with so many excellent choices within walking distance I suspect it will be awhile before I try this place again.

This place is very good. We usually get this to go, drunken chicken sandwich is great so is the steak sub. Can't go wrong with the burgers or chicken sandwich either. We've been back here a handful of times so far and we haven't been disappointed yet.

Went to Legacy for the fist time this past week.  Julia did my acrylic nails, pedicure and a wax.  I spent a total of 1 hour and 45 minutes there. She did an amazing job!! She was so professional and super fast.  She did the best set of nails I've ever had.  Perfection is all I can say.  Do not hesitate to go to Legacy Nail and if Julia is available then better yet!!

Ah, the disappointment.  ... the ahi was pulverized and it wasn't even spicy.

the hamachi was so very fishy.

Thank god the green tea was good, washed everything down.  

The chef looked cute tho.  Nobu.  He's very friendly and looks like a Japanese Mario bros.  Too bad I don't think I'll ever go back here for sushi.
This place is a classic example of an awesomely crazy and quirky thrift store. I always find something cool here, it just might not be what I came in looking for.

Gave this place one star because the service was terrible. The waitress sat down to take our order, which was kind of weird. We ordered two iced lattes with our meal. She later came out & told us their espresso machine wasn't working. Then we opted for a frappe, but the waitress claimed they didn't make those. Mind you this was right after we saw them listed on the menu she just gave us. Lol. So then we asked if we could get lavender lemonade instead; also on the menu. She acted clueless & said she needed to check if they had that. She came back & told us they didn't have that to serve either. So we ordered iced coffees. Our food finally came. Our waffles were less than lukewarm. We asked for butter & the waitress brought out cold, refrigerated butter. The eggs Benedict was a little warmer than the waffles, but still not the desired temperature. Unfortunately our iced coffees finally came after we were halfway finished with our meal. 

On top of that all, the place is so small that when staff is socializing at one table all but five feet away, it becomes distracting. Staff altogether was extremely unfriendly & unprofessional. Overall made for a weird environment. Never mind that tables & seats needed to be cleaned as we had to wipe crumbs from them before we even sat down. 

The food wasn't anything special. I suggest going to First Watch or Kiki's for breakfast & coffee. You'll be much happier, get better service, pay the same or maybe even less, & just have an all around better experience than you would at this place.

I rarely write reviews unless I receive excellent or exceptionally poor customer service.. In this case it falls to the latter... I ordered an $7,700 refrigerator from these guys and the only time they could deliver it was the 4 hours of the week that I wasn't available (despite only being 34 mi away). I was having knee surgery that day and I told them I would be unavailable until 12pm and rather than try to accommodate they proposed the end of the following week. We let them know that we were available anytime prior to their window but would be out of town the new proposed day. They refused to budge and ultimately said "there's no money in this deal for us (their fault, not mine as I was willing to pay more from them due to the great sales service) so we're cancelling this sale..I'm in sales so anyone in this field knows that people buy from people they like, especially when the product is available from other retailers at a lower price. I'm sympathetic to any small business during Covid but I don't think my ask was unreasonable. It all worked out though. I was able to find the exact fridge from another retailer for $700 less and I'm getting the delivery 1 day earlier! At the risk of a seemingly biased review of the competitor, I won't mention the other retailer.

Went there for my grandson's birthday. It was not a busy day. We were the only one birthday party going on that afternoon. The staff was friendly and staff member that came out as Chuckie Cheese was great with the kids. 

Service was slow and the pizza was not very good. The kids did have a great time. I would not give a high recommendation. Feel it was definitely over priced and food was not worth it.

Cafe Beignet was the first stop on my foodie tour in NOLA.  The ONLY things that should be on their menu are coffee and beignets as this is what they are known for and excel at.  

On the otherhand, their omelets were terrible, bland without seasoning. I actually loved their beignets better than Cafe Du Monde because I prefer light and fluffy beignets. Cafe Du Monde's beignets were too dense/tough for me.

I wasn't feeling to good because of my neck and hip. So I reached out to her and she got me in on short notice.  She was friendly and patient and listened to my concerns. Her massage was awesome. I left feeling good. I would recommend her to anyone.

We had a Venti pizza that tasted good but the crust was burnt. This charred taste turned me off to the food a little. I like bbq and burnt ends but I don't like burnt bread.  

The wings we got were a little burnt too but were tasty. The fire oven did something magical to the wings' fat making them succulent and juicy. The seasoning on the wings seemed minimal. 

I like the pizza toppings available; so we also got a pepperoni, mushroom, caramelized onion, and arugula pizza. There seemed to be good combos but I wasn't dying to order a specific one; we ended up with the Venti and quite liked the ricotta meatball combo.

The person that served me was not very nice. I waited 4 minutes for her to finish her smoke before she came in to help us. The food was ok. I've had better at other subways. I would not recommend this location.

My family and I went here for my mother's birthday. We had heard great things about the restaurant and wanted to try it out!!! The band that was playing was great!! They played a lot of oldies,which was great!! Our service was probably about the worst. It took 10 min for the waiter to greet us, 15 min to take our order, and forever on our food. He never even came to check on us after we got our food. Also, my mom and I ordered a pitcher of margaritas to split, we got 3/4 of the way done and realized we were not feeling it at all. At the beginning we tried so hard to taste a hint of tequila. But there was nothing. We waved down the waiter and he said they put 6 shots in it, there's no way, my mom and I are light weights and definitely would have felt it. So we paid 22 dollars for a slushy with no alcohol and a ton of calories. The food was okay.

Great spot to grab a quick lunch if you are ever in the area. Stopped here after a trip to the D-Day Museum (or before I can't remember now)

Anyway I got the Rico (an open faced Brazilian sandwich) which was phenomenal. I love how simple the menu is also, and I don't mean simple in a bad way. They know what they are and they don't need a 30 page menu full of stuff nobody's going to to order.

I don't understand why this location received such favorable reviews. I merely wanted some tasty frozen yogurt and I was disappointed. For starters, you can only sample their self serve yogurt if you ask an employee for a sample. They trust their customers to use the machines to serve themselves cups of yogurt but not samples. Ultimately, it probably doesn't matter too much because all of the flavors (except chocolate) taste like vanilla with a slight hint of another undetectable flavor. The shop is clean but not a great place to hang out because it is terribly loud. If you're used to Yogurtland or Pink Berry, you will be disappointed.

This is a great little sandwich shop that is a local favorite, not a tourist hangout.  We ordered two of the alligator poboys (not on the regular menu--look for the specials to the right of the register) and a Friday special, which is fried shrimp and catfish. 

All the main entrees were very good.  It was exactly what we wanted to start our New Orleans vacation.  The only item we didn't care for was the potato salad salad that came with my Friday special.  It was particularly bland.  Try ordering a different side.  However, the jambalaya, my second side, was really good.

I definitely recommend this venue.  A tip: Order ahead by phone, which I'm pretty sure you can do before the door open at 11:00am.  We got there right when they opened, and even though we were only the third party to order, it took about 20 minutes to get our food.  While we were waiting, several people came in to pick up their phone orders. 

Oh yeah, one more thing: CASH ONLY.  Just a heads up.

If you're going to be ill this is a great place to get treatment. The entire staff who are called the smile crew were attentive friendly and experienced. I was seen by a doctor Alexis Hanson who not only was professional and informative but had the best disposition ever. If possible to have her as my primary I would switch in a heartbeat. How refreshing to experience this kind of attention and expertise. Obviously I would highly recommend this clinic.

First let's be clear,  besides the name this place has nothing in common with the original. And it is most definitely not an Irish pub. It's a sleazy strip mall bar. Pretty plain, bland and greasy food. Mediocre service? Lousy bands on weekends. None of that is the worst part.  It seems they don't care about attracting decent customers. The place has a makeshift patio in the strip parking lot that really serves as a smoking/bud light party area for all of "Pasco County's finest" . Just trying to get in the front doors of the place is nasty, creepy and uncomfortable. You have to walk a gauntlet of spitting, cussing, loud drunks. Stay away!
Cheap on the treats, poor quality. New management has driven this location into the ground.
Honestly, not as good as i expected. It kinda tastes fake and artificial. I would recommend going to Bango Bowls instead.

The Jalapeno bread and the pumpkin chocolate chip bread are good. However, they don't offer baguettes, which was why I went in there. The service is okay and seem annoyed when you ask for a sample and the samples they give are huge when you just want a small bite to try. 

They have normal biz hours during the week 6-6 and limited hours during the weekend.

The place would be 5 stars if the service was a little better, offered quality crusty baguettes, and the prices were a little lower (they are higher than at the Great Harvest Bread Company).

This McDonalds use to be top quality regarding customer service but has since gone downhill in the last 2 years.  I don't know if its management or that the McDonald's entity has gone south nationwide but they can't get an order right to save their life or change the drinks out on a timely basis.

We heard about this place from a friend & wanted to try it. There were so many mixed reviews we thought we'd give it a shot & form our own opinion.  Short story - the food quality & quantity was not so great.  Cocktails were subpar.  
On the other hand - service was good.  Our server, Caroline was great!  Save yourself a trip & go somewhere else.
Awful.  Didn't show up on time, left trees behind and never came back to clean up.  Broke numerous items.  Why didn't we read the reviews sooner!!!

Made a trip especially to buy walnuts only to have manager check and explain they were no longer carrying them.  He had 3 people check.  On-line for same store, shows they DO carry them.  One week later, called their CS and was told (when she checked the item #) that they have over 900 bags of product I want.  Annoying to get mistaken information and be required to make another trip.

Also, while sometimes seemingly understaffed (nobody around to help locate a product or answer a question witho

It was wonderful service here but when we revived are burger  the bun was moldy that was really gross but all beside it's my favourite restaurant but I hope this won't happen again I will definitely come back here again for chicken fingers

My family love this place! We came here for Friday brunch buffet, the selection was great, from plain white rice to jambalaya, from white bread to corn bread. There's also turtle soup, sausages, shrimps and crawfish. Everything I tried was up to my expectation. 
There's a salad bar that's filled with cheese, vegetables, fruit, cold salad, tuna, salmon, egg, and chicken salad. I really like the salmon salad! The dessert selection is probably my favorite :-p, they have Oreo cakes, lemon cakes, brownies, apple cobbler, King cakes, banana foster, and bread pudding. Yum. I will definitely come back for their brunch buffet. It's only $30 per person! Drink is not included but the price is super reasonable. I love it :)
Came in for an oil change, left with my oil changed. This place has a sketchy vibe but it's cool. Decent prices and pretty quick service, I'll be back.

I've been here a few times. Although parking is a struggle, I find the pastries to be pretty good. 

However, one person there is a bit unpleasant. Every single time I've been there I noticed that she's rude to me but not her "customers"...whatever that means (I assume she doesn't want any more "customers" or regulars.) For that reason alone, I will no longer return and won't recommend them to my friends and family, which is a shame because their pastries are great. With that said, there are many other bakeries in Philadelphia.
Food and service were quite good on a busy Saturday night

Stopped by for breakfast over Memorial Day weekend.  The menu has a lot of tempting choices, so it was hard to decide.  My husband ended up ordering the Redneck Benedict - egg and sausage patty on a biscuit topped with sausage gravy.  I went with the Cajun Crab Benedict (egg and crabcake with the traditional hollandaise sauce).  We each ordered the homefries as our side.  Both dishes were delicious.  The biscuit on the Redneck was a little on the dry side.  My dish didn't have a lot of Cajun flavor, but was tasty all the same.  The potatoes were well seasoned.  I would definitely recommend this place to others.
Ill re-review it when I FIND it. The location directions are terrible I've been wandering for an hours after commuting from NJ for these. Wow.

This place is disgusting, the wait times are horrendous all the time even if it is not busy. The food is ok but not anything out of this world. The bar is the worst in town, if you want to have a good night out this is NOT the place to go. 
I would like to share a little bit of information about the type of ownership this establishment has. The owner of the place thinks that it's acceptable to not only have bar fights outside his establishment but thinks that it is ok to JOIN IN!! The owner is pathetic and I refuse to give any business to people who condone such acts!!! You sir are the most horrendous pos I have ever laid eyes on. I would like to add that I witnessed this with my own eyes and it was disgusting some of the gentleman left with blood dripping down their faces and the owner was apart of this horrific scene.

Amazing. I went during lunch time and ordered one of their specials. It was 2 courses for only $9, and there was plenty of food. The spices were excellent and everything felt homemade. One of the best tasting meals I've had in Indianapolis. I highly recommend their beef-lamb dishes.

Great workout!  Your legs will shake, you will sweat, and you will be sore the next day. 
The facility is nice and clean, with all of the supplies you will need for your workout.  
My one star deduction is the lack of friendliness or inclusion from the other attendees.  Unless you're a member who goes at the same time every week, it is highly unlikely that anyone will talk to you.

The beauty of beer and breweries is that you once you try a brewer's beer selection and you feel like there really is nothing of theirs you care for they come back and surprise you. Never write off a brewer for their current beer selection! On a quick beer tour we stopped by the Upland Tasting Room and tried their Tripel, aah-mazing! Yum, Yum! Consider me reformed!

Beer: 4/5 
Service: 5/5 - These guys are super friendly
Location: 4/5 - So comfy, cozy!

This location is in front of a Walmart.  Very very busy. Staff isn't super friendly. Found the hosts to be standoffish. Staring down customers. Very unsettling. If I were easily Intimidated it would have bothered me. Not a fan! 

Our waitress was awesome. Never gave her name. But she was great!

Circles is surprisingly a romantic place to eat at. The interior is very modern and classy. The servers wear this suit (I'm assuming it's some traditional Thai costume) that makes the place more "Asian". 

Another compliment, which I wish I have known before I got there, Circles is BYOB. So if you are looking for a Thai BYOB restaurant, I def recomment this place. 

I got the duck dish which was probably one of my top 5 best duck dish I had. The meat just fell off the bone, and the portion was huge. Generous amount of sizzled sauce on the duck, but balanced out w/ the side vegetables. I shared 2 appetizers with a friend-the Thai rolls and Crab&Pork dumplings. Both of us were surprised to see the portion size of these apps. We had a hard time finishing even one dish b/c our entrees were so big. ( The entrees came out at the right timing but we were taking our time w/ the app and wanted to leave some room for the entrees). 

I guess I would give Circles. Circles was a modern (contemporary) Thai restaurant and it does a good job mixing the two authentic and modern taste. The manager goes around the tables and makes sure you are enjoying your time and food. I would give 3.5 start just b/c I havent rly tried Asian fusion food enough, so I don't know how better it can get if it can. Is it worth checking out this place? I think so, and remember to bring your bottle.

I wanted to get my pets washed.
Has a moron answering phone.
Lady told me he is going to be on my side of town once in the year 2018.
And I could only make an appointment on that 1 day!
Who knows how good he is at pet grooming,... guess he won't be getting my money ever.
Tired of speaking with ding bats, like this.
Good luck with your business

I've been a huge sushi fanatic for years and this place takes the top spot! Visiting New Orleans from Baton Rouge and I had to stop by here based on the reviews. The Tiger Roll was so good, best sushi I've ever had. The bathrooms are really clean, and the service was excellent. Will definitely be coming back here next time around.

If you are ever in reno please go support the homie  Kinan and his family business. Their hot chicken sandwiches are poopin and he gives great conversation while you wait for your food. The chicken is very tender and delicious. Its a must stop! The amount food is worth the price you pay. Support the local businesses everywhere you go.

Only thing I ate yesterday was Eegees and I got food poisoning last night so that was super cool. The only time I've gotten food poisoning from an eegee's. Also I ordered a meatball sandwich for a coworker and they told me it was literally black and burnt? Like who serves a sandwich like that? That's really messed up because I can guarantee the person who made it would be upset and ALSO wouldn't want to eat a sandwich that looked like that. But they went ahead and wrapped it up anyway. Im incredibly upset I'd give zero stars if I could. I'll go to literally any other eegee's from now on thanks for the food poisoning and serving food literally no one wants to eat

Beautiful studio, but the classes were not to par. I have taken pole fit classes for about a year and a half and was bored out of my mind. I understand each studio does things differently, but the option to move to the next level was blown off when I inquired. If your looking to try pole fitness this could be a good start. If you have some experience and want to build on your skills I recommend another studio.

Had breakfast here for the past two days.  We sampled the pancakes, egg whites, salmon hash, and fruit breakfast and all were excellent.  Service was very friendly. Coffee is black ink and not for the feint of heart. Well done!
Fairly normal diner food.  Always looks a little "dirty" but could be due to age and older furniture.   Not our first thought when thinking diner.
I just stopped in for some soft pretzels and pomegranate tea.  I also tried their chicken salad. It is amazing! Friendly fast service with excellent food - if you can't beat it!

High quality steakhouse. We are in town for a conference and had a reservation for 10. They had our table ready early, which was nice. Large menu selection and some wonderful specials available. 

To start We ordered fried mozzarella and calamari for the table.i wasn't impressed with the calamari but the mozzarella was great.

Next came soup/salad which I skipped but everyone's looked amazing, especially the French onion soup! Huge crock, lots of cheese, lots of onions! 

For my main dish, I ordered the tuna (based on the recommendation of our server). It was HUGE! I only ate 1/2 of it. My coworkers got the sea bass with the Teresa finish (breading and white wine sauce I believe?) which was amazing. The steaks looked good too. Sides were good, especially the gouda mac and cheese! 

Desserts were had only by 3 people...a key lime pie (excellent), a fruit and ice cream (ok) and a banana flombe (good). 

My only complaint is the time it took from salads to main course took a while. With 10 people, this was a 2.5 hour dinner. LOL!

I found myself hungry around noon in Doylestown today, and decided to check out Hickory Kitchen for an early lunch. 

First impression was that this was an efficient, sunny, Main Street bistrot serving everything from soups to burgers to BBQ, with a large vegetarian option.  I almost went for the Special Bison Burger after having just had a Yelp discussion about buffalo burgers, but had eaten enough meat yesterday, and just didn't want more.  So, I went for the Grilled Veggie Quesadilla and home brewed Sweet Peach Iced Tea.  The chef and server got busy at the grill, and produced my meal in about 10 minutes. It came out on a small aluminum baking tray (clever), with Sour Cream, Salsa, and a bag of chips (?). 

The Quesadilla was very good, and I could not finish it. It was full of chunky veggies and cheese.  The Iced Tea was fine, just lightly sweetened. Each table had three bottle rockets with three varieties of BBQ sauce - mustard, maple, and tomato based. I tried each of them on the quesadilla in place of the jar based salsa.  I liked them more than the salsa, and think HK is onto something as an alternative to the traditional accompaniment for Mexican dishes.  The mix of Mexi - BBQ could be a big hit (but, it is not Tex-Mex).

This review will likely be updated when I return to taste the BBQ options here. Most of the raves seem to be for the ribs, so I will get back for them. 

All in all, a good first bite, but I can't say that I am drawn enough to return promptly that being a fan suggests.  A nice effort with potential.
Mac and cheese pancakes should give this place 4 stars hands down. Very creative at this place and a lot of comfort food to take care of that long night fuzzy head the morning after.
Still would not eat the menu if I could avoid it, but it's the perfect place to enjoy a margarita and queso before eating real dinner.

Not sure what to say about this place. We came for karaoke and we sang all night!! It is not the usual place I go to however my friend recommended it for karaoke. We had a beer and picked our songs and that's exactly what we did. Sang our hearts out to Celine Dion and Alana Myles........come on down and have a beer or two and chill for a bit.

We were looking for some good takeout. We have never been to Greene Turtle before so we decided to give it a try because they had a great menu! We were NOT disappointed! I got the blackened chicken wrap, and it was perfect. The spinach tortilla was delicious! My husband got the cheeseburger, and he loved it (he's picky with burgers)...it was cooked perfectly! My boys each got a kids chicken finger meal...they cleaned their plates! The fries were so crispy, which is a must! We also got the crab pizza app which was absolutely to die for! We will definitely be ordering from there again.

One of my favorite memories of the Peristyle was dancing across it, just spinning and spinning...of course I was just a tot when I did that, although yes I would do that again...as soon as it gets a little cooler. Yesterday when I passed by the entire pavilion was full of tables and chairs as caterers were setting up for some sort of to do. 

When it was built, in 1907 it was designed for open air dances with a band on one side and a dance floor on the other. I love the neo-classical colonnade and I miss the melty faced lions. Who knew our weather would be so brutal on their faces but those were "renovated" in 2012 and look more lion-y now. 

You can rent the space for your event from City Park, hence the catered event yesterday, but if it's not booked you can have your own dance party/photo session/impromptu soiree in it's environs and backed by beautiful Bayou Metairie.

Had two bad experiences here over the past weekend.  I was here on Saturday with some girlfriends for midday drinks, and my sister and her boyfriend came with us to get something to eat.  It was about 3pm when we showed up, and the only food items that people could order were pizza or salads.  We were also told that the kitchen would shut down completely at 4pm.  When we were there, however, we noticed other people eating burgers, sandwiches and other items aside from just pizza and salad.  Then, on Sunday, I made the mistake of coming back here with my family for lunch.  Let me just say, all of the bartenders, on both Saturday and Sunday, were 100% friendly and approachable.  They couldn't have been nicer.  However, the hostesses were completely rude and unfriendly.  We didn't have reservations, and I got the sense that the hostess was trying to dissuade us from eating there.  She said it would be a 30 minute wait, which was fine, but when I asked if we could sit outside (because it was a gorgeous spring day) she rolled her eyes and said it would take much longer.  We took a seat at the bar but ultimately decided to spend our money somewhere else where the staff were happy to have our business.  The attitude was so unreal that I doubt I'll ever go there again.

His just might be my new fav for pizza and they deliver! Super friendly service, unlimited toppings for a flat price. Def can finish one pie on your own. Nice thin crust but not flimsy so your toppings don't fall off. You can choose everything from
Goat cheese, bacon, sausage, olives, spinach! Great place def check it out

Seriously the best AMC theatre in the Tampa area. The seats make this place the absolute best. They are so comfortable and recline. Everything was clean and they stagger the seating so no ones head is blocking your view and you don't have anyone resting their feet on the back of your chair. 

The staff are very friendly and helpful, even when we had a technical issue with booking our seats. They even reminded us to check our AMC Stubs rewards and we ended up with free drinks for the showing!

It is about a 20 minute drive from downtown, but it is worth the drive for the better experience.

This place is pretty cool. I always get an omelet and its always a hit or miss. But all around its great diner and pretty much the best around. I would really recommend this place if your looking for some where to relax and have a good bite

I just...keep...coming...back... I love Maggiano's Little Italy. The vibe is great. Very family oriented and always busy, but in a good way. Just a fun environment. 

Ihad the mozzarella sticks for the first time and wow, dank. Very saucy and good. Had also the pesto pasta for the first time. Also very good. I got it with the whole wheat penne to be healthier and I enjoyed it. The bread and oil is terrific as always, and service top notch. 

It's not the best of the best, but as far as chains go, I highly recommend :)

Arrived and did not realize it was opening day. A line of people waiting to get their names on the list and the waiting area was kept full all morning.  The wait was about thirty minutes to be seated. Drinks were on the table within minutes. With a full house, the staff stayed friendly and positive. A few guests, not so much. 

Food - Slow in coming, however, expecting quick service with every table occupied, well that makes you a special kind of person. 

Drinks - apparently they didn't have enough glasses for their mimosa demand.  AA might have been canceled this morning? Either way, the taste was good and the drink strong. 

Staff - They did great!

Their wings have been featured on at least one foodie show.  Once upon a time, the Jug was a great spot for wings and beer.  Now, its just a tired local watering hole with even more tired staff.  Too many other options to bother going out of my way to hit the ol' Jug anymore, because it really isn't anything special.

Immaculate, clean room! Modern! Great service at check in! Hotel was large and had a great bar ! Room didnt have a fridge? Pillows were hypoallergenic, and bed was so soft and luxurious. Comforter was really high quality and I slept very well! Weird to have a full length mirror aiming at you while you are using the toliet. I would recommend it and would be back. Room service was really delish and abover average and delivered with a smile. Was I rude not to tip with a 5.99 delivery fee--I think not. All and all it was a great place and I would be back and recommend it to all! Check out is 12 noon! Nice! For 134.00 a night (triple A discount) - I will be back !! The no fridge in room thing was unsetttling tho.. 2 stars removed!

This place is fantastic!  Warm inviting atmosphere, experienced staff that always make you feel welcome, don't rush you, & of course a menu with many interesting things to choose from. 

Place is very roomy & the kitchen is open so you can smell all the deliciousness coming from there!

Steaks are fantastic here & you definitely need to order the side of onion straws with it - sooooo good!   Seafood is top notch as well & the salads are always good.    Nice bar with a great selection of alcohol.

This place does not stay open too late so be aware if you are looking to stop in to have a drink around 10 pm or later.
Got a crappy haircut from an intern and the razor lounge a did not make good on it. Full price for a student,, full price to get it fixed. Nice customer sat.

Love thier pizza, just an old school pizza parlor. Pizza and soda. They are very family friendly and a great place to meet up with friends.
Sometimes its good to call your order in ahead of time.
Quite a nice find! Good prices and tasty food (ordered the beef fried rice). The staff are really attentive and friendly. Would recommend!

The food was great... The tiny sides will likely keep me from returning. The pictures on the Rackz websites menu show a generous portion, but the reality was they came in Dixie cups Little larger than a shot glass.  Very disappointed. A half a rack of ribs and two sides for over $20... the sides should be more than three bites. How much do beans and coleslaw cost a restaurant anyway?
Nice rooms (we were on 19th floor), thought food was OK at all places we ate, really a large resort. Didn't find the gambling too rewarding.
Pizzaa was completely burnt and salad was all of 2 bites with vinegar and no oil. Lazy.

Looking to do a progressive dinner at some new spots in St Pete. This place has tapas and sounded really good. 

Nice place. Sat ourselves and service was right on point. Had the pork belly bao buns and egg rolls. Both were amazing. Awesome flavors in the bao and a nice slow heat.  

Decent beer selection that goes really well with the food. 

We will be back here.

Have you ever had someone in your car driving, risking not only your health, but everyone else on the road trying to play Pokémon on their phone? I have. There's probably a Poke place being built downstairs in my building as I type this...I just gotta catch em all now. Saw one on my home from work....this place was recommended by a friend. 

I think this place used to be just Chinese takeout, next to another Chinese takeout place. So that's your inside. We're so spoiled in Tampa. But there's no indoor seating for the times. You can order inside or call ahead. Friendly service. 

What got me was the unlimited toppings. So you can choose your fish/protein and pile on toppings like seaweed or avocado. The combinations are endless. 

Fish was decently fresh. Everything tasted alright. Price point is around $10 and up for a burrito. Maybe I should of asked for more toppings or extra protein. From what my friend said, one burrito is enough and possibly not able to be finished in one sitting. For me, I was still hungry after 1. Maybe a burrito with a free drink could be better. Overall great place for a quick, and portable sushi fix. Don't forget the sauce.

This is just what Affton needed! What a great addition to the neighborhood! There is so much positive to say about 9 mile. First off, the food trucks - the rotating options for lunch and dinner have offered more food choice variety to the area. I love having all these delicious options. 

Next up, beer - all local craft beer options! You can get cans of beer outside at the tent or walk up to the Canteen for a looong list of beer choices. They also offer locally sourced cocktails. Staff are friendly and service is fast! I also love the set up of the Canteen - many tables inside and large garage doors that open to the outdoor patio. 

There are many places to sit at 9 mile - tables, adirondack chairs, grass and rock tiers. You can also bring your own chairs along. The bathrooms are also great especially during COVID times. No doors and you go around a wall to the stalls keeps the fresh air moving! The sinks, soap and towels are outside and sinks operate with a foot pedal - perfect! 9 mile also has a stage for bands, a big screen for movies and sports games. 

Things to know before you go-  check out their social media pages to find out what food trucks will be there that day and it also will tell you the lunch and dinner hours. The only downside that I have found is that if you go during the day in the hot summer sun there is not much shade, so bring sun protection.

What can we say.... the food....the beverages... the atmosphere...but most of all Megan, our bartender. Amazing! She made the experience here one we appreciated and won't forget. I highly recommend Kingfish. Yumm.

I love going to this place. It's a cozy place to go for the winter, and a relaxing place for the summer. Lots of beer on tap paired with good food equals a pleasant experience. Most of the beers on tap are micros, with many of them local and regional. The beer is reasonably priced also. 

I had a New York steak, which was above average. Nothing fancy was on the plate. My wife got the Alfredo, and it blew us away. It was the best Alfredo I have ever had. It had a good blend of garlic, lemon, and a little cheese. 

I look forward to going here again.

Couldn't tell you what the food or service or ambiance is because there was no parking for the restaurant on a TUESDAY night!!!!  The only parking available was at a ridiculous gauging rates because the restaurant is located on "broadway".  If you've visited Nashville before you know it's the most popular tourist trap in Nashville.  

Maybe next trip out here I'll give it another try (without a car!!!).
We were in the area looking for a late night snack...and a warm cookie sure hit the spot!

I give them 2 stars because the quality of the product is great. The customer service is awful. I've had two bad experiences here. I blew off the first one, but after today 12/23, I will not shop there anymore and I will stop suggesting Penzeys to my friends. Liz, the manager was very rude and unapologetic. The store was not busy but I was clearly an inconvenience. They had a "Free Gift with $5 purchase" promotion via email that I wanted to take advantage of. The promo was released  on 12/13, 4 days before I made my purchase on 12/17. I brought my receipt and the email print out but she wouldn't look at it. After asking why she wouldn't redeem it twice, she threw up her hands and said, "FINE FINE we will do it!" I had spent $30 on a gift set for my girlfriend but hadn't seen the email.  My request to redeem it seemed logical. I've done this before at other businesses, big and small. It was two days before Christmas and the "leader" in the store had very little cheer. Its very unfortunate because I know this doesn't reflect the entire Penzeys organization, but both incidents left an awful taste in my mouth.
