What I don't understand is why give a customer such hassle if you're giving something away for free? Why not look at the receipt? Who knows how much money we'd spent in that store already? I'm obviously a loyal customer if I receive the email promotions. Why risk losing a customer at all?
I own a small business and have worked in customer service for years. I was not rude and I kept my cool, but this kind of treatment is just awful.
After giving my girlfriend her gift, I told her what happened. She had already had a bad experience there herself so she's now decided to return the free gift and not shop there anymore. I hope Penzeys responds to the email I sent them.
If you are into Beers this is the place, but the food has really been hit or miss for me. I am not a beer guy so it really been about the food. And if my kids are not happy, so goes the saying....

If you're like me and your heater breaks the Saturday before Christmas on one of the coldest days of the year, call Donnelley's but only if you're in a pinch. 

They've got a receptionist every day of the week. I'm not talking about an after hours/weekend emergency number. I'm talking about a dedicated person who is more than happy to schedule you ASAP or for a specific time, despite if it's an emergency or not. They even text you to confirm and remind you of appointments! Most other HVAC places tell you to call back during normal business hours (M-F) unless you're willing to pay the emergency fee (typically around $100). Then on Monday, you're stuck waiting in queue because everyone and their grandma are trying to call in.

Their repairs are higher than other HVAC companies, but they're reliable. Or at least James W, the HVAC guy who came out to repair my furnace, is. He was honest and knowledgeable. He was able to explain the issue to me in a way I could understand. He pointed out the newer parts and explain to me how he diagnosed the issue when I asked. The last HVAC guy I had simply told me my furnace was old and he recommended I replace the whole thing or pay $700 to "maybe" fix the issue. 

They charged me approx $300 to replace a flame igniter sensor. This is pretty pricey considering google indicates the part costs somewhere between $50-$100. The remaining $200 is the "installation" fee (literally a few screws and a wire) and this doesn't even include the $85 diagnostic fee. I would've done it myself, but this same part has burned out twice in one year already and Donnelley's included a 1 year warranty on their work.

tldr; extremely pricey, but reliable and generally good customer service...
Great food for the chicken lover but we go for the Impossible veggie  burger.  Craft soda,  herbal teas, great sides and of course you have to try the mini donuts!
Appreciate that they are particular about what they accept for consignment.  Have done business with them for years and they have always been friendly, fair and prompt.  Thank you to all the staff.

There are not any places in SB that do pastor on a roasting spit, so I saw this and had to try it. Total let down, meat was bland, salsas were bland and the homemade tortillas even were disappointing. It was also pricey... $2 for a taco? Other comparable places in town are still under $2 and larger portions with better quality meat. 

Service was also lackluster, only 2 tables when I went there and they were slow and unmotivated.

Stopped in on a Thursday evening with my parents and two sisters because my mom had some kind of gift card for them.

Among the five of us, we just ordered about 10 or so appetizers for us to all share. We shared the cheesy bites, quesadillas (one with the meat, two we requested to be prepared vegetarian), onion rings, the chips and spinach dip, etc. The service overall was pretty good, although it did take a little while before any of the food was brought out- even the bread and butter, which is brought out only if requested.

The main reason for the low rating is that they put shrimp in the vegetarian quesadillas- to which my sister nearly vomited upon discovering. Being picky myself, I'd dissected them before eating them so I didn't accidentally eat any, but it was pretty gross to find shrimp in the food that we'd requested to be made vegetarian. The order of vegetarian quesadillas that were made properly were pretty good though.

The bread and butter was actually pretty good, probably the best tasting food item we had. The cheese bites were good but the sauce had left a little to be desired (it tasted better dipped in the pico de gaio salsa that came with the quesadillas). The chips and spinach dip was okay. The onion rings are the size of donuts and have a little coconut in them, and I wasn't really crazy about them as I'm not really a fan of coconut.

We left full and took some leftovers with us. Experience was dampened by the surprise shrimp, and I don't think we'll make any plans to return.

while in Philadelphia i stumbled upon Insomnia cookies.. and i am so happy i did. The moment you step into the store the smell transport you to cookie heaven!. I purchased a box that sampled all of there cookies. They were all equally delicious warm and gooey!

Ribs R Us has great customer service. That was the best part of the experience for me. The food was decent, but not impressive. the sides and the cornbread were pretty inconsistent as far as flavor and texture. Of the three cornbread ordered, only 1 was moist and not dry. The bbq sauce was just all right. It had a certain level of sweetness but the vinegar was very prominent. This could have been what they were going for but it was not what I was expecting or seeking. 

I can't say I would not give them another chance, however, it would not be my first selection.

Step back into the 70s with the food you remember, exotic drinks with little umbrellas and pagoda lanterns from the ceiling. 

This is a sit down Chinese restaurant from an era gone by and not many left. Complete with zodiac placemat. You know the one. 

The fried rice is proper fried rice. Excellent flavor. 

I only wish the Chinese noodles were the flat sort. Duck sauce could be thicker. But it's minor compared to the freshness and memory when there many like these and no fake take out joints. 

Enjoy Zom Hee!

Really good food but not great service. Waitress told us our rolls would be 8 piece but they were 6, and another staff member would not allow more than 1 mint to be taken from the mint bowl... it's a mint!
great local chinese food place in Kennett.  Crab Rangoon is fantastic.  Orange chicken is consistently great and good selection at affordable prices.

After me and my date sat at the bar it took approximately 15 minutes for the staff to clean up the previous customer's plates. When I ordered my drink there was debris and insects in my glass. I ordered a pulled pork sandwich and found inedible pieces of raw onion. When we were finally able to get our server's attention he was polite but it was almost impossible to be attended to throughout our meal. There is obvious egregious mismanagement, questionable ingredients as well as sanitation issues. After paying over $60 for nothing more than macaroni and cheese and a simple sandwich I do not plan on returning. It is not worth the price of admission unless things drastically change.
Really good pizza, but the service is always terrible. If the people there weren't so rude, I'd probably order from them more often

I'm somewhat underwhelmed with this place. Actually I'm underwhelmed with the food choices in general offered by the Philadelphia international airport. I searched and searched and then finally settled in this local establishment. I didn't want much. Just looking for a burger, fries, a glass of wine. And I got that here. I wanted to fee like I was in a restaurant not a fast food joint. I'm not impressed with the plastic everything. Even my glass of wine. The my waitress was nice. She warned me about the bottled water vs tap. When she brought the bottle of Nestle Water ( yuck! - taste like dirt), I asked her if they had anything else and then declined to drink the bottle of $2.00 water, that is available at Walmart for $0.17. But I'm not made about the mark up. Just next time you only offer bottled water can I get a dasni?  My food was okay. The burger patty was very dry, which is surprising because it was medium well. Again, all served on plastic. I wasn't impressed. Avoid if you can. And for the wine drinker, their premium wines ($8.75/glass) is Beringer. You can almost buy an entire bottle of the stuff for that amount. IMO, their Pinot Grigio didn't strike me as premium. On my way home, I'll remind myself not to stop here, I'll just look for a fast food place. Cheaper and I'm sure the food is the same quality.
Food was average, prices were not. I do not recommend  this place  for foodies. The chicken breading had no flavor.

Ok, i have eaten here numerous times. Food was always good and a nice amount. After work Sunday night I decided to call in my usual order of the chicken wings, onion rings and side of Mac& cheese. When I got home with my almost 30$ order I was very very VERY disappointed! The amount of food they gave me wasn't enough to feed a child! 5 wing parts are 2 and 1/2 full wings. Now every time I eat there my plate was overflowing w wings as was the Mac & cheese. I paid the same amount as when I eat in so, WHY DIDNT I GET THE SAME AMOUNT OF FOOD!

I was excited to try Cask, it had been several years since I had last been and now remember why I waited so long. Inside seating is extremely loud. With the loud music and all the chatter, it's hard to hear yourself think. Our drinks were good (spicy Bloody Mary and Bubble drink) the kitchen was slow and it took awhile before our appetizer came out. Our menu items came out about 15 mins later. My husband ordered a burger with an egg but the egg was missing and I had to ask for more hollandaise for my Benedict. My daughters had pancakes which were yummy. The food is good but nothing to write home about. I feel my filet Bennie could have been executed better (meat was clunky and not tender).

I'm up for any food that is homemade and local with natural ingredients and I will pay premium price for it. I was really disappointed when I found out that Woodside use high fructose corn syrup as the sweetener instead of sugar! Whether the controversy that high fructose corn syrup is worse for you than sugar is true or not, HFCS is definitely cheaper and found in many commercially processed foods I try to avoid. Even in the ice cream aisle in the grocery store, the cheap $2/half gallon ice creams (e.g Friendly's, store brand, etc) are sweetened with HFCS. More expensive premium brands are sweetened with sugar (Breyer's, Haagen Daz, etc). With that said, I cannot justify paying premium prices for Woodside's ice cream when it is made with HFCS.

Ultimately, I have an real issue with the false image that this company puts up. They have you thinking that since their store front is located on a nice little pasture in Hockessin--sometimes even with a picturesque cow grazing in the field nearby--that their ice cream is made with wholesome ingredients but in reality they add artificial ingredients and high fructose corn syrup to cut costs. I'm beginning to wonder where the ice cream is even made. Do they ship it off to a out of state facility like UDairy does? The ice cream tastes good, but really I haven't had ice cream that I didn't like (I'm a sucker for ice cream). I just don't like how I'm paying for the image that this ice cream shop is local, homemade, or even small business. 

I'm not giving this place a completely awful 1-star review because I do the farm setting where the store is located is indeed charming and quaint. It is a nice place to hang out with friends. Other ice cream shops do not have such a nice view with picnic tables where you can sit and chat. Come here for the experience but don't buy the overpriced pints or quarts to take home with you! If you want all natural, local ice cream, you're better off going to an upscale grocery store.

Not the greatest haircut, and slightly higher prices than you can find elsewhere. Shop ambiance felt strained and forced. Conversation was... odd.

It's an OK haircut in a pinch, but you can find better for your regular shop.

Very Disappointment with Nissan of Turnersville. I purchase an extended Warranty when I purchased my vehicle. I purchased a used Nissan Altima from Tony, which was an amazing experience. Previously I had bought an Xterra from Tony that's why I came back. After the purchase is when things went down hill. You would think that the sales department were the difficult ones but the service department is. 

I brought my car in for an oil change. While I was there I told them I was having some issues with the A/C. They told more its would be $170 to diagnosis the problem then  they would know how much it would be to fix. I asked if it was covered under warranty. They told me they wouldn't know until they look at it. Really!?!?!?! I bought that car 7 Months ago. Why would the air to covered? Great job standing by the product you sold me. I could understand if  it was 2 or 3 years from purchase. After questioning why I was being charged $170.00 when there is a warranty I was told the same thing again. 

The worst part is the people in the service department are very nice which makes this difficult to write. Thats the only reason they were 2 Stars rather than 1.

Would highly consider not shopping here. I havent decided whether I would ever buy a Nissan again but it's not looking good. What I can say is I will never be back to Nissan of Turnersville. If I need to go to a Dealership I'll be going to Cherry Hill in the future.

I didn't think anyone in this world could mess up guacamole alas Taco Mama did.. that was the most unseasoned and unflavorful guac my friends and I ever had. Even the salsa was a bit watery and had no kick to it. The chips were bland and had no salt on them.

While I only tried the chips and guac, my friends who ordered entrees were also very disappointed. Very bland shrimp tacos and the Mac n cheese was made with penne noodles and also disappointed the crowd. The corn also had no flavor to it.

Overall, I wouldn't recommend this restaurant if it was the last one standing in Nashville. Maybe it was a bad day... but still.. go anywhere but here. I beg of you.
It was ok.  There are a ton of better food options in the loop, but Fitz's is good a good bet with kids.  I have be a number of times and everything I have had is solid but nothing really stood out.

Interesting concept, but not real "training".  More concerned with keeping the participant in a "range" rather than form or function.  No care for making sure that participants are doing the exercises properly which can lead to injury.  Expensive, for most of this exercising you can do on your own, the motivation here is a yelling "trainer" and I will use that term loosely.   

Try to cancel this membership and they will exercise you again.  You actually have to go to fill out a form, WAIT WHAT CENTURY ARE WE IN???  When you do the staff treats you like a villain and they were actually quite rude.  And guess what I am still awaiting a call from the manager, since I cancelled.  I requested the staff to have him call me and 6 days later, nothing. 

Good luck with Orange, it is not the new black, but did make me Blue, :-(

We went here last night before a movie at Moolah with 2 other couples. The place was generally empty, so we were seated quickly and a round of house margaritas were ordered. The chips and salsa arrived as we were seated and I was pleasantly surprised at the spiciness of the salsa. The chips were fried perfectly and tasted great (with a little salt added). The drinks arrived quickly and they were delicious!! You could actually taste the tequila, and at the $3 happy hour price (until 6:30p) you can't beat it! Dinner was quickly ordered and the three couples all took advantage of the 1 appetizer, 2 entrees, 1 dessert for $20 deal. The hit of the night was the steak "Austin" and there were no complaints with any of the other food. My chimichanga was delicious, crisp and filled with shredded pork, plus it was large enough to be full last night and take the rest home for lunch today as well! My husband and I ordered the sopapilla for dessert and it arrived warm and delicious! The fried ice cream looked fantastic as well, and nobody had trouble finishing their plates! We had a fantastic server who made the evening very enjoyable as she seemed to know exactly when we needed something and stayed away when we didn't. Overall, I feel the food was good enough to come back for, and the price can't be beat!

It is no surprise that this is one of my favorite and go-to restaurants. I've been here multiple times and regardless of the day or time, it's always packed. The place is clean and the staff is always smiling. There's nothing more that I look forward to after a night of drinking than a big bowl of pho from Thinh An. I also love their mi sate, hu tiu bo kho, and com tam ga nng. My mouth is watering just thinking about it! Their pastries and banh mi are out of this world! It's a must try!

I was excited to come and try this place and I felt very disappointed. It's a big restaurant, they don't take reservations which is okay so if you want to eat here plan ahead. I love egg Benedict's and it's my absolute favorite and I have had quite a lot of egg Benedict's to know good from bad. I also know that egg Benedict's shouldn't be OVERCOOKED! My water cup smelled like eggs which was disgusting! There was a table next to me that was empty and I took the glass from there and it smelled the same way. I understand there are a lot of cooking eggs but I think hygiene is very important! I tried not to drink water because I was disgusted. Definitely not a place I would recommend and not for me.

I think they have the best bagels in town but they get two stars because THEY ARE ALWAYS OUT OF BAGELS!!!! If you don't come in as soon as they open they are out and they refuse to make more. It's bad business in my eyes and it's unfair to the people who are coming to the store.

Stopped in here for breakfast and went with the steel cut oatmeal with cinnamon crunch topping and strawberries along with an orange scone and a caramel latte. I would have liked my oatmeal to have been a little more moist, but it wasn't too bad. The orange scone and caramel latte were delicious. Downside for my wife is that there aren't many gluten-free options for breakfast. Even the soufflé, which would normally be a GF option is not here, so she had to stick with the oatmeal and fresh cut fruit. Decor inside was nice with plenty of seating and staff was friendly.

The NOLA Burger was good, but the beef patties are very salty!! The Caramel Milkshake was excellent. The staff are friendly and the tables are cleared quickly and promptly. The atmosphere is very industrial with the stainless steel and concrete set-up like a Chipotle restaurant.

Tutti frutti was good but not as good as sweet endings. The candy/fruit choices were limited and the yogurt lacked texture (if that makes any sense to you). I also noticed fruit flies flying around the cut up fruit which i thought was gross. 

I tried the following yogurts: 
Peanut butter-yuck 
Birthday cake- okay, if your into that 
Chocolate-Very good 
Original-The best of all the yogurts. 

All in all, I would probably come here again considering the fact that they have a nice seating area and open space.
Showed up at 9:20pm and was told they were closed.  Call ahead before you take the drive and don't go by the hours you see listed here.

What a joy this place is! I mean you're going to have a good time anytime you go for frozen yogurt, but this beat out expectations. I was just delighted at the many flavor options and selection of toppings.

The best thing of all, though, was MK's amazing hospitality.   He made us feel so welcome and was very kind to my 10-year-old while at the same time being respectful and careful not to entice him and go past his mom's opinion on how much was allowed. 

We played the game of guess the price (if you get it down to the penny, your yogurt is free!) and then my dog got a little snack on the house too!  A snack he really needed since we had just come over from the vet and have an ouchy on our foot.  

This was so great, we will be back soon!

My wife and I often stay at the Gold Ranch RV Resort adjacent to the Gold Ranch Casino. The Sierra Cafe is located inside the casino. We find the food to be good. We were amazed at the $2.22 breakfast special expecting "low grade" food or service but got good, tasty food and good service too. They also have a half off special from 2 - 5pm, M-F if you are 55 and older. Tuesday is half off until 9pm if you have a players card. It's not just food at a bargain but tastes good too.

A few months back I tried to get some help with determining if a hard drive was salvageable. Over the phone the tech was argumentative and didn't want to listen enough to actually be helpful. 
When I actually went in, the help didn't get much better. I can't tell you if they are any good at repairs because I was too off-put to use them.
How do you not have a website/menu on yelp?  Yet another pizza place that will not get new business.
Would never stay.  After unauthorized charges were placed on my card Motel 6 refused to reverse the charges.  I've had to dispute it with my cc.

This will be the last time I take a car to this location. When I dropped truck off I explained to them I wanted them to fix what was broken. Later I was told because of my trucks age I needed more done. To make matters worst I was told they were unable to find my original problem. I hope they enjoy my money because I will not return to this location.

You know those University or Hospital dining halls. This is pretty much the quality of food they serve. Filling, large portions, overly salted or seasoned, and utterly unmemorable. The food placement even looks ugly to begin with (not that I care that much about it), but looks like they just throw stuff together. 

On the bright side you get lots of options (maybe they try to do too much) and surf and turf at ridiculous prices... but if your date probably wouldn't give you a second chance after trip to this place... unless she really really like surf and turf.

New Year's Eve. I get it , not a night to review BUT: the smell at the bar of a dirty mop, a server who should seek another profession, and the denial of a simply request to break a bill to pay valet simply killed the evening. 
Food C-, atmosphere is always good, service sucked. 
No excuses. I'll stick to their brunch from time to time.

IN 2013, Dodge Moving delivered items to our address in error that should have gone to other destinations. We called several times and were told they didn't have any trucks coming our way and that we would have to make arrangements ourselves to have the items delivered to the correct address and Dodge would reimburse us.  Why did this become OUR problem when Dodge made the mistake and had been paid for the proper delivery of the items?  

Is this the kind of customer service you provide?????

Still a great cheesesteak after several more visits over the past couple of years.  

The BIG STORY is the availability of a credit card processor that saves the time and expense of bringing enough cash or using the captive ATM.  

The sandwiches come to ten bucks with a drink, so it pays to be ready for the splurge.

The sushi is ok. It's room temperature. You can get decent deals here. But my family seem to like it bcuz it's super close to home. 

Crab puffs are delicious just wish they had sweet and sour sauce. Calamari was delicious with lemon and their sauce they give to you!

I absolutely hate giving this place three 1/2- four  stars because I feel like I must have came on a bad night or something! We ordered the chicken wings - great! My brother didn't like the really crispy top layer but I did. We then got the cornbread - biggest let down. The cornbread was hard as a rock and the butter was too! I then got the chicken sandwich was pretty happy with it!
Wish I could give it zero. There is nothing good about this location. Between only accepting cash almost all the time and saying they "don't serve breakfast." Worst location.

We had a 7 PM dinner reservation at the restaurant for Saturday Nov 9th. When we arrived, we found large crowd of people with 7 PM reservations waiting inside and outside in the cold with hostess nowhere to be found. We had to track down the hostess to put our name on the list even though we had confirmed reservation. There was no proactive communications. It was extremely frustrating for all waiting with confirmed reservations but with no information on when they may be seated. Many left after waiting for 45 minutes to an hour. We finally left after 70 minutes of waiting. We had never experienced this sort of mismanagement in any other restaurants. The management is clueless about customer service. It is hard to understand how this restaurant can have any positive recommendations and be in business for this long. Confirmed reservations mean nothing for this restaurant. I would neither visit nor recommend this restaurant.

It was alright, but nothing special.  Decent size tables, free wi-fi and pretty well placed plugs.  They only take cash and don't have a huge selection on the menu, but it did the job and gave me some decent people watching on the street outside.

I really wanted to give less stars but Jeff from Service earned the last star single handedly. My vehicle was in for service for the past 2 years 3x for the same issue (noise on passenger front wheel). A year ago, struts and sway bar links were replaced. Apparently the sway bar links needed to replaced again this time around, ok fine. I pick up my vehicle drive around the block and pull right back into the lot  because the noise was definitely not as intense BUT it was still occurring. Jeff had a tech ride with me and the tech agreed he heard it as well. I made an arrangement with Jeff to bring my vehicle back and he would have enterprise ready with a rental for me. I didn't even walk into the service area and Jeff was on the phone with enterprise, great customer service Jeff! And yes my car is finally noise free! Until next year...

My wife and I shared a stuffed potato and a devil crab..we were stuffed to the gills. Yes, they were wonderful. The devil crab was FULL of this outrageous amount of lump crab meat..amazing. We also took a cuban to go and it was the best we've ever had.We live in Jersey City and purposely made the trip there; it was def worth it! Great food and service.

Went to boca  other day, once i got there i was  seated, asked for a michelada but the waitress didnt ask me what kind of beer i wanted with my bev she came back 10 mins later asking me what kind of beer i wanted, so i got my drink,( which was really good), and the waitress took my menu without asking if,  i wanted to order food, she came back and asked me,  are u good? And i asked her if i can order food,  she brought back the menu, i asked the waitress, whats in the " caldo de camarones?" Besides camarones? She said i dont know, she didnt  bother telling me, i will ask  the cook if u want?, this place is a   disaster! save ur time and money and go to a diff place
Pizza was decent.  The Brussel Sprouts were the bomb diggity!  Good dessert.  Fast service.

Food and service is variable. Had some great wings the first time, then the wings the second time around I called in an order they said it would take 30min and when I showed up they said it will be out soon and waited another 30min. Just ordered a pizza and they said it would take 30 min the pizza was cold when I showed up. I'm not sure what's going on with the kitchen but there seems to be a lack of communication.

**Tire center review* I typically go here for groceries and am in and out because of the Scan and Go feature which I love. Using it, I don't have to deal with the employees much; however, the tire center has to get it together. Walk-ins are always 3-5 hours wait time because they don't have a lot of people working and to schedule and appointments are always a week away, at the minimum. I don't mind to wait, but there isnt anywhere to sit! Love the prices, hate the experience.

Ate there yesterday during Outfest, place was not crowded. Ate there several times in the past. Our waiter was cold and had the attitude of Joan Crawford on acid. Drinks were watered down and food was so- so.
A couple beside us received a dirty glass and when they brought it to the waiters attention his reply was in abitchy attitude was ... " We'll see what we can do !", after several minutes of no response they left. When the waiter returned he just said " tough / next ! " . And three guys on the other side of our table that the one received a wrong salad. They took his wrong salad back to the kitchen and never returned with the correct one.
I ate there in the past when the place was packed and better waiters and no issues.
But to eat there and the place is a little over half full and this happens ... no excuse!
I worked in restaurants and bars and this is unacceptable.
Never going back ... I'll spend my rainbow dollars at a better establishment.
Beautiful old place, it looks and feels like your dining in Mexico. The waitress we had was awesome, couldn't have been better.  The food was great! It's a must try

It was OK. I ordered the shrimp po boy and my husband ordered the alligator po boy.

Fries were EXCELLENT! :) 

The shrimp was over breaded and you couldn't even taste it. Along with that, the bread was so hard and flakey it was almost too hard to eat.

The gator had good flavor, but again because of the bread it was masked.

Our friends tried the blackened catfish po boy and she said it was super good, another friend tried just the tuna fillet and they over cooked it and another friend tried the gumbo and said it was excellent. Personally I wouldn't go back - it does not offer authentic southern food but not many restaurants in the French Quarter do.

Avoid this viva location. Long waits with no one else in drive through. When you ask for extra sauce they act like it's coming out of their paycheck and their kids aren't going to eat tonight. Breakfast burritos stuffed with potatoes, very little egg or meat. Carne Asada is chewy with hard spots.
Love their food but not always consistent on the amount of sauce on the tacos that can be frustrating. Love how quick it is and the simple atmosphere.

Really bummed out. I've been having issues with them for a while but they're near my house and a small business and I wanted to support them. Today, however, I saw that they're selling Ruellia Simplex, an invasive species classed Category 1 (so invasive it's dangerous to the native ecology) and when I asked them if they would consider ceasing to sell it, they told me it was a native plant. This is not the first time they have told me bad information about a plant. I don't know if they lied because they didn't know and didn't want to look foolish, or because they simply didn't care. Either way, it's the last time I'll be going there.

I bought a printer on line and I recieved A USED one.
Imagine that. Called them and they said they will call the next day to see if I still want it after a $10 discount.... They never called back... I really needed it since I could not go out because of quarentine. I printed less than 10 pages and the ran out of ink.... what a bad deal...

Finally. 
I gave in to all of the hype surrounding this little Thai spot on 8th Avenue. 
So happy that it lived up to my expectations although their idea of "Thai Spicy" left a little to be desired!

There is nothing spectacular about the location or interior of this little dining gem but the service and healthy menu are worth a trip back!

Dinner for 2 set me back around $25 and included a refreshing Pomegranate juice ($2) and Smiling Elephant Pad Thai dish ($10.95) that consisted of stirfried green bean noodles with tofu, bean sprouts, ground peanuts and a choice of meat. I doubled up on the tofu and was very happy with the texture and flavor although when I requested Thai Spicy, I was expecting more punch? The server even warned that it would be extra spicy but it was underwhelming on the spice meter for me.

This is a great spot for lunch or dinner but be warned that no wine or other booze is served here! I got over that quickly as the food was truly delicious.

Super cool place and of course the sushi is crazy good. I mean i am very picky about sushi since have been to lots of 5 star sushi places. Their fish is FRESH. Will come me back for sure. Our server Keithy(or Kathy) was great, friendly and  of course beautiful

Awful......awful....awful.  this place has the worse service.  I ordered 2 quarter pounded with mustard and onions only.   As I'm driving off, I check my order.  2 quarter pound with pickles and ketchup in the bag.  When I go inside, the lady helping and taking order argues with me that I had cheese on my sandwich.  I tell her I did not want cheese.  "But the other two had cheese on them", she says.  Exactly.  I did not want cheese...not pickles, or ketchup.  After twenty minutes of listening to some other non customers at the counter cursing and harassing other customers, she finally brings me my order.  I checked.  OK.  Order right.  Driving down the highway.....taste sandwich....dry, and awful. How does this place stay open.

We love melting pot and have had great experiences...for the most part. 
We called ahead and asked how long the wait was from downtown Tampa at 8:15. We were told we could probably be sat in 20 minutes but it would be 8 pm at the latest. We were on a time crunch but still decided to take the 20 minute detour to drive out there. We checked in at 7:32 and were told it might be another 20 minutes. No problem. 
8 o clock came and went. I checked with the host stand and they said there were two tables ahead of us and that it might be another 10 minutes. At 8:10 they said there was only one other table ahead of us. By this time we realized we probably would be late if we waited. We decided to leave. I went up to let a manager know because I believe in letting companies know when and why they are losing my business. He told me there was only 1 table left and that he was "sorry" it was "8 minutes late" (in a nice first world country problem tone). I told him we drove out this far because we were told it would be 8pm "AT THE LATEST". Then in a condescending tone he informed me that "this is a restaurant" and that "it is Saturday night". I talked it over with my wife and once we realized it was a restaurant we felt much better. We were no longer hungry. Oh wait. 
Maybe he should have informed his hostess about that so they can give more accurate quote times. I dunno. We left. 
Ps. We ended up at a seafood joint .8 miles up the street called "shells seafood" and some guy opened the door for us and they had us sat in 20 seconds. 3 minutes later we're eating "award winning" clam chowder and sipping on a margarita that we got for free for checking in on yelp.

So, I'm from Florida visiting family and just had to include a trip to Fast Eddie's in there somewhere. Never a disappointment here. Great food and service always. They also get some killer bands here. For those that haven't been here, bring cash and only over 21 years old are allowed in.
$17.99 for July 4th Special all you can eat crab legs. Hibachi area. Pretty clean buffet. My kids enjoyed it.

Disappointed today. My husband and I went for lunch during our work at home lunch hour. The person serving us was not wearing her mask properly and dropped off her nose and mouth frequently. When we got home we realized she had not put my soup in the take out bag, so my husband had to use the rest of his lunch hour to go back. I tried to call the store, kept going to voicemail which said the box was full. I was hoping she could have the soup ready for pick up, but couldn't even make that work, only saw 1 person taking care of orders. Addendum: my husband went back & got my soup, but she didn't put the bread in that we ordered & offered nothing for our inconvenience. Customer Service has gone way, way down sine this store first opened.
Greatest cantina in Tampa good food friendly staff! Location is close to home plenty of parking!
Good food but they treat there customers really poorly they yell at them and talk to you like you are stupid very disappointed like food but hate going there

Pizza has become in a sorts, traditional, its classic, easy and very filling. What it has not been, is fast and for those of us willing to invest 20-30 minutes of our day waiting for it to come to us, not a quick lunch. The blaze veers away from traditional and makes pizza more fast food friendly while maintaining pizzas traditional roots. 180 seconds! I was skeptical at first, something must be sacrificed to make it this fast, but it's an awesome tasting pizza. Is it Casanova's? No! But it's a hands down winner over commercial pizza places, with a price that's pocketbook friendly! I will admit the build your own section is a bit confusing with all the choices and be warned, if you're OCD watching them create it, will be nerve racking but well worth it. The blood orange lemonade completes the meal and 17$ for two, might be worth a shot for date night. I'm a fan!
Really happy with Dr Phelan. Thoughtful, patient, took his time to explain how to deal with our kitten's cold. Wish we were still in the area so we could keep going here!

My son is really into trains so we decided to give it a try. First, tickets were expensive and I thought at least kids would be free, since my son is only three but it was almost full price for him!

When we arrived parking was an additional
$20! The gentleman at the parking booth gave as misinformation saying that the train had left when I knew that the train was at 4pm because I had purchased e-tickets. Why did this individual not know the train schedule? Seemed to be someone who maybe didn't want us there or was poorly informed. 

So the train ride was a bit boring. The kids lost interest quickly. This was definitely not worth the money at all! Do not recommend this outing!

I went to pick up our order. Noticed none of the cooks were wearing face masks. I asked why. The teenager at the counter gave me a blank stare and gestured at the cook closest to me. I again asked why the cooks weren't wearing masks. He grunted and pointed at the plexiglass barrier at the counter, as if that was protecting the food. I said "but you're handling our food," He grunted and went back to his work. No one spoke to me except to ring up my order. "I'm not coming back," I said loudly. No response. 
The food has always been good, but their customer service has been barely acceptable. This was the last straw. I'm angry at myself for not refusing our food altogether. That was my mistake. Oh, and also thinking that this company had the slightest concern for their customers. I saw their "97 out of 100" health score. From four years ago! We will never use them again. I strongly urge you to avoid them.

I wanted to like this nail salon so bad but I was disappointed. I will say that the young gentleman that gave me my pedicure did a good job. But I have never had a pedicure that takes over an hour and a half. I was at the salon for 2 hours & 30 minutes+ for a pedicure and a dip manicure. Nothing special, no nail art. I just thought that was an over the top amount of time for two simple services and I have never been to a nail salon that takes that long. 

The man who did my dip manicure did a mediocre job. I couldn't tell how bad of a job he did in the lighting when he finished, but as soon as I walked outside and saw my nails in person, I was disappointed. He did a poor job of removing my cuticles/dead skin, my nails were cut/filed unevenly and he buffed off some of the dip color after putting on a top coat. It's only been 3 days since I had these services and my manicure looks a week+ old. They have already begun to chip and the top coat is looking dull. $85 for the service I received was definitely not worth it. Had they done a better job, it would have been worth every penny. As nice of a salon as it is and as many employees as they have (which is a total plus), I expected more. I won't be back.

Slow, slow, slow service from the blonde, white sushi chef.  He seemed unable to remember an order.  We felt that he'd rather be elsewhere as he would drift into a stare and not move for several minutes.  It seemed as if we did not exist to him.  Other wait-staff was efficient and expedient.  Spoke to the owners  next door.  They did not care.  They told us he'd been there 2 years, almost as if they were defending him..  They Acted as if they were dumbfounded at my request to test the chef for drug use.  After speaking to the owners about our experience, it seems they must make enough money off the ignorant fools that eat here, and they don't need nor want our money, or recommendation.  No tip- for certain.  I can't believe a friend would recommend this place, but being a sushi aficionado, this place leaves much to be desired- like an actual MEAL.  The waitress was asking us if we wanted dessert!  What?!  I hardly had anything to eat and I'm as small as a bird!  I told her "no", that I was still trying-trying- to get enough "dinner"!  and we were the only patrons, the 2 of us- at the sushi bar and there were 3 sushi chefs working on food for  a couple tables- maybe 8 people at most.  DO NOT GO HERE- you will leave hungry and your wallet will be empty.
This is the place we go to for salsa nights at the piazza. Always good food and fun times. No complaints. Sucks that it started raining. Ur we still have fun.

In a word...TERRIBLE. Not only are we still waiting for a room at 4:05pm after asking for early check in, we have now missed all of our planned events. We booked through Expedia, and they are just at fault as the hotel. The hotel explained that early check in is not guaranteed, but I guess regular check in isn't guaranteed either. We have a ghost tour scheduled for 10pm, hopefully we'll be in a room by then, but judging by the line out of the building and the poor customer service, that's only wishful thinking at this point.

Overall the museum was what I would have expected. It's not for everyone and there seem to be some repeat exhibits... for example, several different fetus specimens and a lot of skulls. 

The understanding and being able to view the medical profession from the 19th century was what I really took away from this museum and how healthcare was viewed. 

When we first arrived and purchased our tickets for $18 each (general admission price), we were not told a private event would be held in an hour and the entire medicinal garden would be closed (hence the 3 stars). We really wanted to view the garden area and the medicinal species they had so we saved it for last. By the time we made it to the gardens at 1:30pm on a Tuesday the gardens were completely closed off for a 'private event.' To say the least we were very disappointed as no signs were ever posted notifying anyone that this area would be closed at a specified time. Then when we left the museum and looked through the gate/fence to see what we could see of the medicinal gardens there were only two people back there joking around not even really 'setting up' for a private event! 

We tend to spend a moderate amount of time looking through exhibits and our entire time at the museum was probably at most 2 hours. They are VERY strict on the no photos policy. I sat on a bench to take a break from walking and checked my emails. Then all of a sudden from one of the exit doors a lady popped out and just starred me down. It wasn't until I put my phone away that I realized she was watching me the whole time to see if I would take any pics. After I put my phone away, she disappeared through the same door she popped out of. 

I would say this museum would be a little difficult with younger children. The display cases are original and fragile with very little room to move around others that are spending more time looking/reading the exhibits.

This bar has a great personality. Although aesthetically similar to when it was Loser's, now the ambience is better, the staff is way better and the food is tasty. I don't particularly like the out-of-towners that stop in from hotels, but it's cool ‍

We came on a Tuesday. No Red Beans n Rice?!?! WTH?  Hubster is loving the Boudin balls!  Gator bites are meh. They are served with some sort of Orange sauce that we passed on, cause, well...they're gator bites. She offered a small ramekin of the sauce we passed on and...well...honestly it was pretty revolting...almost tasted fermented.  

The ginger soda was really good and the fruit punch/ginger combo was good too.  At 7 pm, the couple who were eating when we arrived had paid their bill and left. We were alone in the dining room. The server was reading something on her laptop while she waited for our food to be done. Her computer is really slow. Fortunately, they have a tv which is good because the food comes out of the kitchen very slowly.

Dinner for me was a bowl of chicken and sausage gumbo followed by a selection of side dishes: ettoufe,  jambalaya, brie grits and corn choux. I would have added Red Beans and rice, but they were out.

The gumbo was dark, spicy and a little salty. The ettoufe was also spicy and flavorful. The jambalaya was Smokey and delicious. The corn choux was a chunky dish of corn, bacon, onion and red and green peppers. Very good. The brie grits were very satisfying, although, tasted nothing like brie and the cheese shavings atop the ramekin were most certainly not from brie.

Hubster had the jambalaya pasta and said, "it was pretty damned good and filling".

I would order the jambalaya again and the brie grits.

Food comes out of the kitchen very slowly. Dessert was plain beignets. They don't look like the beignets I am used to, but, hey...its fried dough...how can you go wrong with that?!?

We never would have found this place were it not for the Groupon coupon. Not sure I will ever trust Groupon again. They did add an automatic 15% gratuity. Not sure if that is a Groupon thing or not, but heads up!

We went there for lunch, very excited to try new location. I ordered a egg omelette but noticed it was very sweet like a crepe mentioned it to an very friendly service person. She checked came back and said it must of been prepared on top of a pancake residue. Upon further dissection of plate the so called egg mixture flacked like pancake mix. Brought it to the attention of owner and she looked at me like I was crazy! I was treated with disrespect and all I was trying to tell her it was a funny mistake. After being embarrassed by her behavior she took the order of check.

Not impressed. We had been here twice before and had good meals. So we took out of town guests here this wknd. The reservation was for 7 and they shoved us in a booth meant for 6. Our waiter was not friendly and odd. The fish in the fish and grits was super bland. The only redeeming thing was the awesome (NOT healthy though, warning!) southern burger. We won't be going back.

I've been here several times during work lunches and they never disappoint! Outdoor dining is spacious and it's a very cute restaurant with great service! Wide arrange of food and everything was extremely delicious. Highly recommend visiting this place!

We were walking around downtown Tampa looking for food before attending the Tampa Bay Margarita Festival this weekend. I had heard several friends rave about Bavaro's. So, when I saw the sign I knew that we were eating there. 

The restaurant is pretty tiny, but it is comfortable. It definitely has that intimate, family owned restaurant feel. They even had these adorable recesses built into the brick walls that contained family pictures. So cool. 

We were in the mood for pasta. The menu is mostly pizza and only contains a few pasta selections. The sauces also do not have descriptions on the menu. Luckily, we had an amazing server. He explained everything to us and gave us recommendations. 

For a starter, my Husband ordered the garlic bread with cheese added. This "garlic bread" was more of a flatbread wood-fired pizza. It was delicious, just not what we expected. 

I ordered the Homemade Ravioli al Formaggio (cheese ravioli) with Arrabbiata sauce. My Husband ordered the Homemade Ravioli Emiliani which was basically a meat ravioli with a delicious meat bolognese sauce. Both dishes were served with two small pieces of bread. Both dishes were delicious, but a little monotone. I wish mine had some meat in it. My Husband's tasted great to me, but he said it got a little old after a few bites. 

Now, to the real reason for 3 stars and the reason I left a little confused. This meal was $50 (before tip!). We had water to drink, garlic bread that was basically a small pizza and cost $9 with the cheese and our entrees were $18 and $19. We left full, but not really satisfied. This meal was good, but not $60 good. 

While we liked Bavaro's environment, service and the food was pretty good, we will not be back. We have even better homemade pasta for MUCH cheaper prices at our favorite local Pinellas Park restaurant. No hard feelings and I wish Bavaro's success. It is just not for our cheaper blood.

I've been to this place a few times. And they never disappoint. The employees are very nice and the shop is clean. They do a great job on my manis and pedis every time and their prices are reasonable!
I also was excited to see they offer SNS dipping powder. It looks wonderful and feels strong!

My technician didn't talk to me once, despite my numerous attempts to stimulate conversation. When she was done, she simply said to go sit at the light, no thank you or smile. Plus, sloppy job. She didn't remove existing polish completely and left noticeable gaps with new polish.

Therefore, I will not be returning. One star is for the gentleman working that greeted me when I walked in.

Food, eh. 
I could have gotten the same meal for half the price at Cedars Cafe in Frazer. 

Two dinners, chicken on one, ground lamb on the other, 2 coffees and two desserts, all for the low low price of $72.

I went to this establishment tonight with my family. i go here a lot it's literally my favorite and all the workers are so nice but tonight was a little different. When we walked up to the window the girl that was helping us was super rude her name is Riley and she talked to us with a disgusting tone, she talked to us like we were bugging her.

Really... second hand French fries??? re-frying old fries??? Ridiculous!! Dancing behind the counter and just enjoying your friends besides cooking a good burger and fries for your customers?? You should be ashamed, however,  you are obviously NOT!! DO NOT BUY FOOD FROM HERE!! Terrible!!

Dinner at Spice 28 was a whole lot of ok. The food was alright but nothing to write home about, and the specialty mojito was definitely not worth the price tag. Likewise, we were served by not one but three servers, which made for a less personal experience. Coming to Spice 28 for dinner was still a much better experience than going there for Philly Sips. While most other restaurants offer full-size appetizers for half the price, Spice 28 uses the promotion to serve one tiny egg roll on a plate for $2.50. True story. No exaggeration.

Ok, so apparently I have a new "go-to" pizza place in town. Sometimes after a long day, it's nice for someone else to make a quick dinner... Hmm...I should really use a different word than quick because "quick delivery" usually doesn't happen..lol! The last time my 45 minute wait time turned into over an hour 1/2. I definitely was upset, but at least the food was hot when I received it. Of course this is chain pizza, so it's not like Chicago pizza (back home), but it's always had a good flavor every time we've ordered. The subs are pretty decent as well. (Chicken parm is usually pretty good - tonight we're going to try the philly.) The employees are friendly and always smiling. Really, the only negative would be the long delivery time, but other than that I'd MUCH rather have Roma Pizza than Dominoes, Pizza Hut, Papa Johns, or some other type of chain pizzeria. I'm satisfied with Roma being my "go-to" place.

We sat on the patio, music was a good volume, we could talk just fine. Waitress was great  We ordered the calamari for a starter after seeing it mentioned in a few reviews. Unfortunately it was a sad disappointment. Ours was very very bland. The fish tacos were good. Big battered fish portions in each, good of that's what you like, thank goodness we shared! Upgraded to yam fries, they were very good, perfectly cooked and large portion! Breeze on the patio was sooo nice!

A long-time Wash Ave Rosalitas fan, I called in advance to book a party of 15 at the new location. I was told I would receive a confirmation from a manager and waited for a response. Two days later I called to confirm the reservation (after not hearing from a manager) and was told that they'd get back to me immediately. A day later (today) I called again, heard the same story, asked to talk to a manager and could be placed on hold. The manager then explained they were "booked solid for two weeks" and that the hostess has no authority to make a reservation for my group. I called Hacienda, they booked my group for tonight, and I received great customer service. I will no longer patron Rosalitas.

We had a great experience here. When we checked in there was no line. Then we went to security, and there was only 1 person ahead of us. Easy, quick and painless. I'll fly in and out of this airport any day.

First time ever going in this hospital. Worst experience ever. Came in at 5pm didn't leave until 11:45pm not because I was attend but I left myself. As to why I left knowing i would risk my child. Let me explain more I have a 3 week old baby who had trouble breathing around so many people in a small waiting room area. When I tell you everyone was sick everyone was sick. The staff was uncaring and took others before us and we been there first. It wasn't that important to them or severe. A staff member had told me. But to me it was that's why I was there. I was told by the staff more then once that there was two people in-front of us. Hours had past and I still was waiting. I keep asking how long the wait and it was always later two more people in-front. They got tired of me asking so they sent me to the back area to wait. More people there coughing and cold area for a newborn to be in. I decided to leave and go to another place with my baby. If she's already having trouble breathing why would I allow her to stay in an environment with sick people and a cold area. That's not even the worst part a women came in carrying her child because she passed out and even they we're dismissed. They only asked what was wrong and didn't even tell her mother anything else or took them in right away. why Not take them in and have someone else fill out the paperwork after wards or during. Their healh was more important. Honestly would have Zero stars with me.
We found Rustic Blue print by accident and are glad we did. Love our new custom table.  James and his teaM exceeded our expectations.

Are you kidding me !! Ordered the warm lobster roll with mango and jalapeño peppers and a traditional roll . The lobster was fishy and tasted like it was just defrosted . The roll was so overwhelming that it almost didn't matter how bad the lobster was !! To make it worse was the 16.95 price each ... what a rip..Was going to go back to get reimbursed but I was so upset I threw the sandwich in the garbage so I couldn't prove it to as inedible!
Great place to stop on your way to the airport. Nice craft beer selection and excellent service. Didn't order the pizza but it looked and smelled fantastic. Will definitely go again.

This was my second visit to Monell's at the Manor for breakfast...the first was 5 months ago.  We went on a Sunday morning.

I'll begin with what I didn't like:
The alleged gravy was more of a flour/water mix with no discernable flavour whatsoever. As a restaurant claiming to bring "the South to your mouth", they should be embarrassed about this substance.  
The scrambled eggs were so overcooked that they were in one hard lump in the bowl when brought to the table.  I don't require them runny, but this was way over.  
The fruit preserve at the table was not reminiscent of any fruit I'm familiar with.  It tasted of nothing more than sorghum.
The fried chicken, while cooked well, lacked any seasoning.
The country ham just didn't seem very country.  It was tasty, though.  
The breakfast potatoes were inconsistent.  The original serving was very good.  The bowl emptied quickly and another was requested.  The second bowl was quite lacking...no seasoning whatsoever.  
The grits also could have benefitted from basic seasoning.  

What I liked:
The bacon was nice...cooked just right for me, between floppy and crispy, in that firm and chewy area.
The country ham, while not very "country", was still tasty. 
The potatoes, if seasoned, were the best thing they served on this morning.
The corn pudding was well prepared and delicious.  

After the first visit, I was ready to return.  All in all, this visit was not enough to make we want to return again.

Had my French manicure and red color pedicure . Had to make improvements, as the French had too thick white tips and too far from cuticle. 

Update: had to take away stars , cause second experience was awful: Pam finished ladie's pedi and didn't wash her hands tried to start my manicure like that , I asked her to sanitize hands and she wasn't very glad about it . After she did gel cover , she went to do next customer's nails and I was sitting with sticky nails waiting somebody removes that layer . I am glad that another girl made pedicure , cause Pam always in a hurry , she wants for one hour to take three customers . For me hygiene and relax are very important. And here is a big lack of it 

I'd rather be with my natural nails , not having any more this kind of experience until I find a  good quality place .

I had two people clean our place after moving in. They were very polite, dressed professionally and heard my requests. I needed service mainly to my floors (is what I told them). After attempting to clean them myself, I kept noticing my efforts weren't enough. Countless swiffer wipes kept turning up with dirt. I thought by having them do a  "deep clean", that they might do something different. But, I'm sad to say my swiffer mop still came up with dirt when they left. One person vacuumed, the other mopped. But I suppose the mopping hadn't been repetitive in its rinsing. And that's not what I was looking for.  I have to say that aside from my floors, they do know how to make your place look very nice.

I never write reviews. I seriously went out of my way to download Yelp to warn people of how appalling this car wash has become. They used to be great. I'd come once a week. Their vacuums used to work great, now they barely suck anything up. They had a power wash, which was needed because their machines doesn't take everything off, and they took it away. I used to tell everyone I knew how spectacular this car wash was, now the only thing I'm going to do is urge them to find a new car wash place with a power wash and strong vacuums..

Been recommended the place by a couple people, especially for the lunch specials. 

Came to try for dinner and made a reservation through yelp. When we got there around six there weren't a lot of people but by the time we left it was definitely filling up! 

The place is nicely decorated with a couple of tall tables in front, two seaters by the bar and larger tables in the back.  

Service was quick and we got our food fairly quickly.

I had been hearing about the short rib bourginon skillet which definitely isn't traditional Korean, but had good recommendations. The meat was melt in your mouth tender and extremely delicious. We also got the soondubu, which wasn't as spicy as I liked but my mom loved the level of spiciness. There was also quite a bit of seafood in it. 

I liked the banchan variety, but wish there was kimchi in there. The ones included were daikon, eggplant, spicy pickles, and a mix of soybeans, corn, and carrots. 

There are also nice lunch specials for 9.99 on weekdays. It includes a banchan side dish along with a main dish with rice.

I love everything about how The Bar Method classes make me feel!

I love the challenging and shaky feeling I have that reminds me how hard my body is working. I love how the ballet stretching makes me feel elegant. I love the positive encouragement and the productive feedback I get from the instructors. I love that the fat roll above my knees has disappeared (how did I even get that?). I love the cool down vibe at the end that feels like a reward after all that shaking. Lastly I love how well maintained the studio is. It always seems to smell like fresh laundry which I keep meaning to ask about but am too forgetful to ask at the end of the class :).

Been missing out for multiple years.  Live just around the corner and always passed this by.  Finally made plans to stop in and what a great surprise.   The place was beautifully laid out but unpretentious.  Stefano and his team were amazing. Excellent service.   The veal piccata and side of fra diavalo pasta was on point.  $20 corkage fee was very reasonable.   Great place and definitely headed back soon

My first time here.. I got a breakfast burrito and a hash-brown to go... unfortunately the hash-brown was burnt and so rock hard that it was completely inedible. 

I'd give them another try as I am hoping it's just a fluke.. but was highly disappointed

Bandit has a great working space, with spacious tables and good natural lighting. They have a great playlist as well. However, I did not like their expresso. It was the most acidic expresso I've ever had. I couldn't even finish it because its taste wasn't pleasant and enjoyable at all. 
I haven't tried any other drinks there because, in my opinion, if the expresso, which is the base of other drinks,  didn't taste good, I don't know what to expect from the other drinks.

This was the best place for lamb! The wait was bad but I was just hungry. The decorations was awesome, they were very friendly. And I got really full! I will most definitely be coming back! Thank you again!

DO NOT STAY HERE, RUN FOR YOUR LIFE*********

Wish I could give it zero stars...this was the second worst hotel stay of my life, after one that my parents chose back in 1996 for a ski trip in upstate New York...they were greedy and thought it would be cool to book FOUR wedding blocks in the SAME weekend this past weekend, while construction was going on (the bride and groom told us the hotel never told them about construction that would be happening during the height of the travel season in our country um hello August, why not do the construction in freaking January or February?), with ONE elevator working which moved at the speed of smell and took you up when you asked it to take you down, NO FREE BRUNCH?!?!?!, servers were rude and nasty and I had to eat with my hands and wipe them on my jeans since there were no utensils or napkins and no one was bussing the tables, the hallways needed vacuuming as I literally could see with my naked eye piles of actual dirt in the corners on the carpets!, our room smelled massively strongly of hardcore cleaning agents, fantastic for people with autoimmune diseases and allergies...my husband was the best man for one of those weddings and we didn't even get to check in until almost midnight, and now we see that they double-charged us (the lady at the desk insisted that I present my credit card, even though my husband had earlier and the room was booked in his name?).  Half of our friends didn't have towels or soap, let alone were placed near each other Christ on a cracker.  This is why Philadelphia just is not as together as New York City.  I have worked for Hilton in midtown and even though the hotel there was kinda dirty and outdated, at least they have customer service down.

Not really like the old Dos Gringos, and the food much more expensive.  Had the chiles rellenos and my son had the chicken tacos, three shells like taco bell, with very little chicken and rice and beens, not worth what you pay for and they say it is the same it used to be????  Where is the free cheese sauce they used to give??????

We had a gift certificate, otherwise I wouldn't typically go to a pizza chain.  Salad was lame--100% assembled Sysco ingredients.  Stromboli was fine, but the calzone left lots to be desired.  The best part is the beer list, which is varied and decent.  Don't be fooled, though, when Old Chicago says they have 101 beers, they include Coors, Coors Light, and Bud, Bud Light and etc. in the list.

I have only lived in Tampa for 2 weeks and have already been here twice.  You can order a whole pizza or can order by the slice. At $3 a slice it is well worth it!  The slices are HUGE and they have about 10 different pizzas to choose from.  They also have garlic knots and cal zone type entrees.  The crust is thin and very crispy on the bottom.  I like to hear a good crunch when I bite down, and I definitely hear it with this pizza.  The place is small, and my stroller wouldn't fit down the isle very good so we sat outside under the awning.  I have a feeling this will be our go-to pizza place.
Always delicious tapas and drinks, especially cocktails. Cool atmosphere. We liked the back patio but not the smokers.

I guess going to this place after Thai esanse and after coming back from Thai land I didn't really like the food that much the best thing was the red curry. I ordered a hot pot of tum yum soup and it tasted very different then the one I tired in other Thai restaurants and Thailand. It was really sweet but usually it's a very lemony soup. The drunkin noodles were okay and the spicy mango chicken was okay. The best thing was the chili paste shrimp appetizer.

Been here twice and both times was not impressed. Nice staff and atmosphere but the food underwhelmed. I live right down the street and the place is always packed but I have no interest in going back.

Brought s party of 12 here , which was prearranged by the NOPSI hotel.  When we arrived it took them 30 minutes to deliver our first round of drinks . I finally got up to retrieve the final drink ( which they simply forgot)so I could make a toast and when I approached the bar a bartender named Rob scowled at me and said "what's your problem it hadn't been that Long "
The  waiter Giovanni did his best all night long to give us incredible service and quite frankly he is the only Gem at the little Gem.  The food was ultimately mediocre and they struggled to get it out in a timely manner.  My travel coordinator called the next day to complain and the owner was unpleasant and stated that she was not present and couldn't speak for me ( also mentioned we tipped big so we must have been happy ...the tip was for Giovanni who did his best to save them from themselves). My advice to the little Gem is to fire Rob the bartender and hire a great manager who understands good food and service. Avoid this place ... easy to do much better in NOLA!

So this place is delicious. I had the fish burger and sweet potatoes fries which were other great. The staff was super nice, and the service was good. The only complaint I have is about the owner, who came in and very rudely spoke to his employees about how they should've called him (they were having electrical issues, and their credit card machine wasn't working- which did not affect business at all according to one employee). Not only was he rude to them, but he was rude to them In front of customers, which was awkward to say the least. He also go in the way of the cash register, which delayed customers from paying for the meals.

Overall the place does have some really great food though

I was pretty impressed considering the size of this little museum. Although we specifically came for the Vishnu exhibit I was pretty much taken away by the building itself. It's an awesome example of 1930s Art Deco and used to be a post office. We're very lucky to have such a building and an art museum in the middle of downtown. I'll definitely be coming back for other exhibits.
This place is not a good place to get your hair done! They totally messed up my hair and they charged me way over the price they quoted! Never go here!

Sad to say, I won't be going back. Been once before and food was just okay. I liked the concept of this place being a family run restaurant so I decided to give it another try, assuming I maybe just ordered the wrong thing. Everything I ordered on the 2nd visit was unpleasant tasting. So much so, I couldn't finish. But service was beyond bad. I mean, really, really bad. As in, absent. Very few check ins on us, no drink refills, and incorrect tab. No excuse for bad service when a restaurant is this tiny. Also be warned, the prices have either increased or there's a discrepancy on their website. Menu prices are more than you see online. And by that I mean, overpriced.
The food is solid.  The pasta, pizza, and raviolis all were tasty and served well.  The eclectic funky atmosphere is the same as always.  I wish I could stay awake til 3am to watch closing time.

Refreshing concept. The beer selection is awesome. Maybe it is because it was the first week of opening, but it got busy quick. We were asked by an employee to give up our table while we were still finishing our beer. =/

Pizza is alright...not out of this world.
The Curry chicken is dry and flavorless, the macaroni is pasty, and the rice is unseasoned. Also, expect to wait up to 40 minutes for your food
Potent menu selections, perfect portions, right pricing, and great chow!  The elderly owner still shows up and serves...js

I spent a sublime couple of days at the El Encanto this month. Not only is it a stunningly beautiful hotel but the service was extraordinary. I was able to relax and enjoy the spa, the wonderful food and the environs and my stay was made extraordinarily special with the assistance of Macy Weiser who was particularly helpful, facilitating a truly memorable experience. I am fortunate to be able to travel a great deal, but my stay at El Encanto was delightfully memorable  in every way.

So this is a great space to get work done and the coffee is good. HOWEVER, the food leaves so much to be desired. I ordered a breakfast sandwich and paid an additional $1 for avocado on it. When the breakfast sandwich arrived, it was cold (cold eggs are gross) and the eggs made up about 95% of it. In addition, there were 3 PAPER THIN slices of avocado on each size that could not have totaled more than 10% of an avocado when taken together, about 5 pieces of spinach thrown on, 5 tiny slices of red pepper and then small slice of mozzarella cheese. For a $7.50 sandwich, it was incredibly lackluster. And it will be the last time I order food there. Sadly, this is not an isolated experience. Another time, I ordered a breakfast sandwich (this was before I had learned my lesson) and sat and waited. And waited. And waited. I noted a staff member chatting with someone after she had delivered their food--she clearly knew the person--and I watched them chat for at least 15 minutes. In my head, I'm thinking that there is no way this person is responsible for making my food because I'm sitting there watching her chat while I'm waiting. I went up to the counter after 20 minutes and very kindly asked about my sandwich (mind you there were only 3 other people in the place and no one had ordered food since me). I watched the guy then make eye contact with the staff member who was chatting. She casually sauntered back to the kitchen area about 3 minutes after that. About 5 minutes later, I was presented with my order. No avocado on it, even though I had asked for it and on the wrong type of bagel. Not a huge deal but after waiting nearly 30 minutes for it, I would have expected the order to be right. The girl who gave me the food (and who kept me waiting) did not even apologize--the other staff member was the one who did so. For the prices they charge, the service and the food should be better.

Great hang out and bar. Went here with a company event and the food is pretty good. I ordered the Cuban sandwich which was a great  size and flavor. I think the beers on tap are a little limited so was disappointed there but found one I enjoyed and it would up all good. I'm not sure if there is space inside or not to eat because the event was outside but man it was hot and hard to enjoy ourselves with the Florida weather. However, this may have been the companies choice and not red mesa. Overall I'd go again and give it another try.

This was a well rated restaurant for authentic Southwest breakfast eats. The Mac and cheese waffle southwestern Benedict was delicious, my husband had the Triple berry waffle special which he loved but there was no maple syrup on the table and he wanted that as well with the bacon he ordered and he waiter told him the berry syrup should be enough. I don't think he understood the syrup was for the bacon and not meant to adulterate the breakfast dish. I enjoyed mine as well but I did not like the coffee-it tasted a little over brewed or like it sat on the burner too long. Overall it seemed like a great place for families and offered play tables for kids and the atmosphere was upbeat.
This is wonderful...but be prepared to wait.  Food is always good and I have enjoyed Sabrina's each time I have visited.

Have ordered from here twice on ubereats. First time, my roommates and I ordered the Black and Blue Burger, the BBQ Bacon Burger, and the Buffalo Chicken Burger. The food was prepared quickly and it was so good. We were taken aback by how delicious it all was. We ordered the same thing last night and again it all was so tasty.

The burgers were flavorful and the buns were fresh. Everything was warm and cooked exactly how we like. 

Fries are the size of mcdonalds fries but not as crispy. Would prefer crispier, as they are a bit floppy, but they were still a great side for the burgers. Appropriately salty.

Plan on ordering from here whenever I want a burger fix. Cant wait to try it in person when everything opens up again!

I visited this restaurant with my family yesterday after reading some really positive reviews. We had reserved a table and were promptly seated. The buffet counter was open and we thought of trying the buffet. I am a vegetarian and was hoping to find some good food here. There were a couple of appetizers, there was samosa and mushroom. I opted for the samosas it was very oily and the filling was not good. The mushroom seemed less appealing, I gave it a pass. There was soup but we were told its not vegetarian. There was idly and sambar which was ok. Then there was channa masala, punjabi kadi and paneer. Kadi and paneer were not good, channa masala was ok. There was no dal which is something that we can expect in a decent buffet. There were very limited option for vegetarian and whatever was available was not good. The items dint work well together which is important in a buffet. There were 4-5 meat\fish items in the buffet and they seem to be more popular. Naan was served on the table which was good. Onto the dessert, there were a couple of items both looked gooey. My wife had the mango pudding (I guess) and she liked it, there was another pudding which we dint have. I was hungry but had enough of this restaurant. This buffet was priced at $17 and definitely not worth it. The service and decor was better than most indian restaurants. But unfortunately service and decor cannot beat taste and hunger, I went to a drive through and had a veggie burger which was unusually more satisfying. 2 stars for service and decor @Bangles.

This is a great place doing great work and under different circumstances, I'd give it four or five stars. 

Clearwater Marine Aquarium is a rescue and rehab hospital. They take in and treat injured sealife and release them if able. As a facility, it's pretty similar in size to other rehab hospitals we've visited in the past. 

You can see up close how the staff trains and rehabilitates the animals and it's clear that they really love the work that they do. The animals definitely seem appreciative as well, looking and behaving happily.

My biggest issue is that despite the name, this is NOT an aquarium. It's a hospital facility and one's expectations should be adjusted accordingly. Don't expect the huge walk through tanks, large number of animals or extensive educational opportunities that you would find at any normal aquarium.

What you WILL find is essentially a shrine to Winter and the unforgettable classic family movie: Dolphin Tale. To be fair, the movie was about one of the dolphins rehabilitated and housed there - and much of the movie was filmed there. Still, I'd rather see some more educational signage instead of notes about how the director would watch filming through a certain tank window. 

The last gripe - and reason for taking the review down to 2 stars from 3  is the price of admission. Yes, the money is going to a great cause, but $20 is incredibly excessive for this venue. $10 would be much more appropriate.

This place looks beautiful inside but the problem with the restaurant is they do not let you veer from the menu. For instance, if you want tacos they are served with received beans and rice, NO EXCEPTIONS. When you get salsa with you chips, you only get a tiny bowl of salsa that can satisfy only one small child. When I asked for more, they literally brought me a shot glass of salsa. I thought it was a joke. 

Remember when I said you cannot veer from the menu? Well, when I ordered the chicken taco they told me it was different than the menu and it now had radishes on it. I'm confused, so I can't change the menu but you can? Okay cool. I won't come back.

P.S. I gave it two stars because at least I got to drink a beer.

We loved this place!  My daughter and I enjoyed dinner on Saturday night; she ordered the vegan mixed platter (six vegan dishes chosen from a menu of seven dishes) and I had the meat and veggie mixed platter (2 meats and 4 veggies), both served on injera.  Everything was delicious---wonderful variety of flavors.  Our waiter was attentive and helpful and the atmosphere was warm and friendly.  I was only sorry that there were not more customers at this little gem--I want this place to succeed so that we can enjoy another meal there the next time we are in Indy.

Me,my sister and nephew went to Dolly's today and I must say we totally enjoyed the food. Everything was right on point. The pholorie and doubles were so good. They were made fresh and that's a plus.The sizes of the plates were very good also. I will most definitely be going back.

Called about a home we recently purchased and most notably, the issues with the lawn sprinkler and drip system. They system was in a box in a hole dug by the faucet. The controller was cheap and not designed for what was needed for sprinkler and drip zones.
