Enter Masters Tree and Lawn Service! What a difference it makes when you have a competent professional performing lawn sprinkler/drip system maintenance and installation. We now have a new drip/sprinkler system control box mounted on the side of our house. The new box  controls the sprinklers and the drip zones on our property with ease and efficiency. Their service professional even set our new system to water on the days TMWA directs us to water.  Five stars plus for a timely response to our service request, as well as a professional, competent installation of our new sprinkler drip control box. Many thanks

Ok first of all I NEVER leave reviews but I felt the need to do so because EVERYTHING was absolutely wonderful and above my expectations honestly. I rarely eat downtown in New Orleans because I'm a native and know that most restaurants cater to the palette of tourist , Jimmy J's is not one of those restaurants. Everything we ate was seasoned to perfection, ESPECIALLY the pork chop and  fried green tomatoes omg I need another right now. The drinks were delicious and our server BECA(probably short for Rebecca was the bestttttt! We will be back!

For lunch, it was great! Friendly service and available staff.

Sashimi lunch hit the spot. Perfect amount of good and no coma to follow (I say this as someone who is particularly sensitive to sleepiness after lunch, which is actually why I usually skip the meal altogether). On an iced cold day, the hot tea was enough! But the fish was great and affordable. I'll be back.

Food is average at best.  Had loose stool immediately after here...I'm not certain if it was directly because of their food so I can't entirely blame them for it.  But again the food was average at best.  Drinks and ambience is pretty good.  Service is above average.
The Gorgonzola ravioli with beef tips was way too garlicky! But I heard good things about the salmon.

Nothing compares to the carrot cake pancakes here. By far my favorite breakfast food there is, to top it off the pancakes are huge. The breakfast hashes are also great, plus since they're made to order you can substitute out anything you don't like. Overall the food is great, it's a little pricey but the service at this location it not too great.
This is my wife's and I favorite restaurant in Palm Harbor. We go once a week. She likes the salads and fish and I like everthing else. I highly recomnend it.

So I have been here several times, giving them lots of chances to improve, but it just falls flat every time, no matter what time of the day.  The delivery of drinks is very unorganized whether you are getting it Takeout or not.  They don't have enough room at front to wait for drink order, they don't place your name on takeout drink order so you have to ask them if it is yours and with so many of them making different drinks how would each one of them know who it was for if they don't write the name on cup.  They all generally seemed annoyed when you asked them if it's your drink and same attitude when ordering.  They give you a number if you dine, but they have to look all over the place, up and downstairs to find you, so more chances for them to naturally spill your drink.  One thing they do well is their cortado!  However, I am not impressed with their specialty lattes, they barely taste like anything.  Recently, Their maple bourbon latte twice had tasted just like a regular latte with no syrup, very disappointed for the price of the specialty latte!  Also recently they have served my nitro cold brew on ice before, big mistake and they didn't even Apologize when I took it back to be fixed.  Their hot food is usually good but underwhelming.  Their muffins are good.  I'm losing my interest with this company as I have been a loyal customer and they keep decreasing in quality service at the front.
I got ox tails and yellow rice and potatoes for a friend of mine. The portions were large but it was kind of pricey, came to 18.80 with a Gatorade and 15.99 before.

Back in the Lou and thought maybe the bartender from my previous review was just having a bad day; however after reading other reviews since my own...nahh...I'll save my money and head to BPV or Fridays

Came here for the first time and my dolsol seafood bibimbop was good and served on hot stone bowl. My husband had spicy beef soup, he also liked it. They don't have ventilation for cooking meat in the middle of the table yet. I was cold because they opened the door for the smoke. Hope they have it soon. This is good place to gather and eat good korean food.
Love their food and drinks but their customer service is horrible... they never fail to mess up every order we order...

I like the atmosphere of this place.  It is very casual and friendly. The only problem I have with the place is the hashbrowns. I love going out to breakfast and getting fried eggs, hashbrowns and toast. The hashbrowns here are okay, but not great.  I imagine much of the rest of their food is good, but I can't give this place more than 3 stars if the hashbrowns are only average.

I don't travel often, when I do I hope to get my money's worth. I have stayed in some run down hotels, but this place takes the cake! The tub surround has a gap in it I could put my fingers in, the door lock does not work properly, loud guests, very small room, 1 elevator that is very slow..... I paid $100.00 for this room, not much on the grand scale, but about $80.00 too much in this case!
I booked this hotel with Reservations.com, I can guarantee that I will NEVER use Reservations.com again either. I called Reservations.com to see if they have any idea just what a dump this place is, they said I will have to call the hotel, then I'll call you  back. NO THANKS! I don't want any of their thugs to bust the door down, did I mention, the door does lock properly?. Oh yeah, 1 star is more than this place deserves! Marty

We were visiting and staying at nearby hotel and hankering for BBQ. Previous reviews said parking was bad but we arrived before dinner hour and didn't have problem with parking. We loved the atmosphere, and it was only mile from hotel and near historical area of St. Charles. We ordered the chose two meal, with two meats of choice and two regular sides. I had turkey, BBQ pulled pork, sweet potato pie, and potato salad. Pulled pork was so tasty, as well as the sweet potato pie. Potato salad wasn't anything new, turkey was high quality but not added taste as pulled pork was. We are definitely glad we went here and our waitress, Kelly, was so attentive and hospitable to our every need. Shout out to Kelly!

Not too far from the house so why not give it a try. I love hot chicken.  The place is kinda hidden but it's easy to get to. Everyone is very welcoming as soon as you walk in the door. I got the hot chicken sliders in mild and it was good but not my best. It came with one side and I got the jalapeno mac and cheese and also an order of fried pickles. I will have to come back to try out the bbq.

Worst Starbucks. Never fills up drink all the way and gets it wrong half the time. It's close to home but will be driving 3 miles out of the way to avoid this crappy place - and it's a 20 minute way when you come in the mornings - on a good day

I had an existing woodburning firebox and flue pipe removed and a gas unit installed.  The gas unit is made of flimsy metal and the unit makes 3 to 5 loud booming sounds a night.  It creaks and crackles the rest of the time.  The chimney chase was closed up at the top but the fireplace still has a large draft.  The installers expect the homeowners to watch the entire installation in the room or they will assume the homeowners have no interest in the quality of work.  Get an official statement of the work to be done, not just the order form.  Ensure that every work item is clearly listed, and alternate work to be done in place of the stated work.  Ensure the person who writes the order and the installers agree on all the actual work to be done.

Decided to try this place for dinner on a Friday night while I had family in town visiting. Not a great experience. Took almost an hour for our food to arrive and it seemed like everyone around us was getting food (there were only six of us and we all had sandwiches, burgers). Also, my burger ordered medium rare was more like well done. I won't be back soon.

In love with fresh kitchen! You have to try there sweet potato noodles, literally life :) everything is really fresh, delicious and healthy! This place gets extremely busy during there lunch hours though. Definitely worth a try!

First time threaded! A great experience after getting burned by regular wax @ another salon. My hair grows back really fast but after getting my eyebrows threaded, it took my hair 2 weeks to grow back. They even helped to reshape my eyebrows.
We had a flat about two miles away. Austin got us taken care of right away. He was very kind and made the whole experience less stressful.
The lamb & skordalia gyros are fantastic, and the Dark Chocolate cake ( made from scratch) is to die for! My boyfriend just stole a bite while I am typing this...gotta get back to defend my dessert!!

Convienent location right off the interstate. The Ultimate Bacon Cheeseburger was delicious and our entire meal came out perfect. Would have gave five stars had the woman at the drive-how window not have been so rude. Food is great but service could have been better.

Tried this place based on the awesome Yelp rating and it was a mixed bag.

$10 20" cheese pizza is a great deal but they made the crust so thin that it burned. The parts that weren't over cooked had very good flavor. Had it not been burned I think it would have been an upper tier thin crust pizza. 

Bbq chicken pizza was awesome and I was basically stuffed after one slice. Wife got the penne alfredo which was fine, but not world beating. Sauce tasted like something right off the shelf from Publix.

I stopped by the Cork & Bottle at the same time as the Mid City Green Market.  They were having a wine tasting and the wines were mighty tasty.  I was impressed with their stock & the sales people were helpful but not pushy.  

PLUS, they have their bourbon selection bottled by a company in Kentucky.  How cool is that?  And did I mention that it's a mighty tasty bourbon too.

This review is for the service department.

When I bought my Jeep back in 2006, I purchased the lifetime oil change program. So, every few months I come back here to get an oil change for my SUV. It's convenient because I can just schedule an appointment and not have to worry about paying for the service. The con is that I have to go here unless I want to pay for an oil change somewhere else. 

The service department is just ok. The people who work there seem to be indifferent when I walk in. It's never really busy when I'm there, so I'm always surprised when it takes forever for a simple oil change. I've learned to bring something to read or watch Netflix while I wait in their waiting room to pass the time. 

Like a typical car dealership, something needs to be "fixed" or "replaced" each time I'm there. Most recently, I needed a new air filer. I think they are just trying to get a few extra bucks from me. Luckily I have a mechanic friend who was able to replace it for me and I only had to pay for the filter, which saved me money.

One of the worst places I have ever stayed at. The owner had rules, such as no walking on the grass. He came out yelling at my family who was having a family reunion right on the beach... My 81 year old grandmother was even with us. He comes out cursing at the top of his lungs, spit flying from his mouth saying we all need to leave and even called my uncle ugly... None of us gave him any issues. We didn't cause a disturbance or have any issues with the guy. He wants $10 a person that wasn't staying with us. We tried to give him our credit card and he said he was calling the cops. The cops came and even agreed with us that this guy was off his rocker, and the owner ended up kicking us out. He charged us for 2 days when we had only been there one!! This guy was the most unprofessional business owner I have ever come across and embarrassed himself in front of a family that has been coming here for years. Maybe I would understand if we broke some rules or were causing a disturbance but we had just gotten there and were saying hello to each other when the guy lost his mind. I would give this place 0 stars if I could. This place is run by an owner who wants to ruin a good time and to steal your money.
One of my favorite McDonalds!!! Open 24/7 clerks are always nice here. Even remember my name!!!!!

Service was 100% perfect, atmosphere was fantastic, and the food was wonderful. We got the lobster Mac and cheese and the cornbread for appetizers. Both were super tasty. Mac and cheese is served in a skillet and was perfectly cheesy. We loved the cornbread. For entrees we had the ribs with super slaw and red beans and rice. The peach sauce was very tasty and it came with sooo many ribs I had to take some home. The grouper was okay, a little salty but the other spices were spot on. The grit cake that comes with the grouper was unique and crispy. I think the sides are where it's at, I want to try them all.

The Mud House -- or-- my partner and I's new favorite breakfast place.

I've heard the name thrown around a few times and on a whim we decided to give it a try!

We both ordered the American Breakfast. My first choice was the French Toast but I was in the mood for something savory. The food was perfectly seasoned. The bacon stood out to me. just perfect. If you like your bacon burnt then this isn't for you (why people think burnt bacon is standard astounds me). 

My partner wasn't impressed by the coffee and said the blueberry muffin was lacking. However, for the latter I think it's because blueberries aren't in season. 

The atmosphere is fun and hip. The staff are helpful and friendly. Our cashier gave me a crow sign placard just because it matched my outfit.

My friend will be coming to visit me from Portland in a few weeks. Guess where I'll be taking them.

The last time I ate at Yianni's will be the last time I eat there. The sponge docks is a favorite place to visit for me and my family and each time we go we get a bite to eat.  We've stopped at Yianni's on 3 occasions and each time have had a similar experience.  The food has been fine in flavor and presentation, it is average for the area. It's the service that has been lacking every single time.  Though the restaurant gets a steady stream of customers, our visits were never overcrowded, so it's difficult to justify the lack of service (long wait times for everything ie. drinks, order, refills, check, etc.), the miscommunication (incorrectly placed orders resulting in wrong meals being brought out), and the argumentative attitude of the wait staff with each other and when being informed that the food brought out is not what was ordered.  The sponge docks are home to many Greek cuisine restaurants, I suggest picking a different one. They get a solid 2 for the food, but there is good food all along the avenue and better service too.

A good experience. Don't listen to reviews complaining about time... there's a labor shortage right now and high demand for this place. I got a combo #3 with medium hot. It took about 20 minutes. Not too bad. If you're worried about waiting, just order online instead. The food was really good- better than the Cane's. Spice wasn't bad at all and the sauce was really good. People were super nice as well. Fried chicken is still fried chicken- don't expect gourmet. But it's good.
For the price, it was bleh. A lot of money for VERY little return. The exhibits were short and bland. Not a whole lot to look at.

The atmosphere here is rather like in the old sitcom "Cheers" in which everyone is treated like a regular and never forgotten.  The owner and his employees instantly make you feel like you're extended family just for showing up.  I love this place, and the lattes are perfect every time.  Once you go here, you'll never go to Starbucks again.

The spicy wings were good. The spice hits you right away but doesn't linger too long so you're not dying the whole meal.

The soy could use more soy.

Both are much tastier the next day straight out of the fridge!

Took my team here for a holiday lunch and had a great experience! Between us we had the chicken, chopped salad, mac and  cheese, cole slaw, spoon bread, biscuits, and mashed potatoes. The salad was solid but definitely not as memorable as the sides and chicken. That's my fault for trying to go healthy. The honey was delicious but not even remotely spicy, as it's billed to be.

Service was all-around good, but there was just one kind of weird service issue. Our waiter, who was very friendly, tried to talk someone in my party out of the sweet tea. "Are you sure you don't want half unsweet? It's very, very sweet." 

Anyhow, the complaints here about communal dining are strange. It's no big deal and meeting new people won't kill you.

TERRIBLE service - avoid. I bought a dishwasher almost a month ago - and I still dont have it. The fulfillment is the absolute worst I have ever seen. 
And after all the frustration they simply hide behind their "policy" and I am left wondering where my $700 dishwasher is.

Use Home Depot instead - at least you will be heard.
OK bar service, but maitre'd was a jerk. Pass.
Great place for a quick bite to eat. Fast and friendly service...on a budget! Delicious Rock and Roll sushi roll.

I would have awarded $100 to anyone who could have found a single bit of lobster in the lobster mac and cheese I paid 20-something dollars for on my visit. 

Maybe this is a fine place for college kids on a special night out (of which we encountered many), but nothing special if you have even a moderate amount of experience with fine dining.

The place is extremely loud, the food is average, and the service staff seem fairly inexperienced. 

Maybe my expectations were just too high.

I used to bring my family into Sahms once a week. We haven't visited Sahms in several months. Service and food quality had been inconsistent. The burgers used to be amazing. It appears they have changed their supplier and it is no longer worthy of recommending. My kids were really disappointed. The burgers are definitely not the same.  I had the salmon which was average but their rice is similar to something they serve in the cafeteria at my daughter's school.  Their quality of their salads never leave me satisfied so I opted for the steamed carrots with my salmon. Mehhh!  We will go to Chatham Tap next time.
Service was very good. Steaks were ok but for the price I would have expected exceptional. I feel it is overrated and overpriced.

this place seems classy, and we felt it was a bit aloof, but the food was tasty.
we ordered the "happy hour" sweet potatoes and the sliders, and from the regular menu we got the seafood gumbo and shrimp remoulade.

it was surprising to me that everything arrived all at once. 
I expected from a classy place like Tableau to at least *ask* us if we wanted everything at once. 
even though everything we ordered is considered "starters", the fact that we ordered them and nothing else should be a "hint" that we may treat them as separate courses.

I stopped here to get a banana split today with a date. It was not a great experience. First off, on the menu there are three sizes. Apparently, there's really only one. Ok, no problem. So this thing comes with two tiny scoops of ice cream, a banana and two other toppings for $8.50. This is hardly enough for one, let alone two people. I basically bought a $6 banana and two teaspoons of ice cream and some peanuts. Even for center city this played is just over priced and low quality for what you're playing for. 

This is the first negative review I've even written, but I just feel ripped off.

I always walk by this restaurant so I decided to try them out last night. I ordered shrimp in garlic sauce with white rice along with an order of spring rolls. The food was just ok..nothing special about it. The shrimps were big but all of the vegetables took away from the dish. The spring rolls were bland and very small for the price. I may give them another shot and try a different dish. Hopefully the food is more flavorful next time.

FOOD: 2/5
SERVICE: 3.5/5

My Order: Sampler appetizer with fried alligator, tilapia, frog legs, crawfish, calamari, mushroom stuffed inside some gooey bread (??) and an empanada-like-baked good with meat inside.

Could NOT finish everything! I was full and slightly disgusted by this abundance of fried food. The only thing that I really loved was the empanada-like item. The frog legs were chewy and tasted like chicken. The alligator was tough and okay. The crawfish and calamari were not amazing. And, I hated the mushroom item the most!

The service was decent. They had check up on our table several times to see if we needed anything. Water was refilled promptly. Check was given promptly. Although, I think it's because it wasn't busy on a Monday at 11am.

Overall, I would NOT go here again. It was just more for convenience since our hotel was next door and we needed something fast to eat because our taxi was scheduled to pick us up soon.

Such a horrible and awkward experience! We came with family & friends we haven't seen in awhile and were hopeful in having a good time. Drinks took what seemed to be a lifetime, by the time she brought the drinks the first served needed refills.(that she failed to get) literally we sent our kids to the machine to get their own because an hour past after we ASKED for a refill! Then next shift came in and they got into a server fight INFRONT of our whole table! After the manager got involved being just as rude and arguing infront of us, he dismissed our original server and the new shift took over only to share with us... Our order was never placed!

This place charges twice as much for men as it does for women on Saturdays.

This is discrimination, pure and simple. I understand that they obviously want to encourage more women to come and then draw in more men on the prospect of having a lot of women to choose from.

What about gay men? I have no incentive to pay twice as much to attend, and my female friend wanted to attend with me so we could enjoy the evening together.

Are gay men simply not welcome?

Get rid of this messed up outdated policy.

Atmosphere was nice. 
Food was tasty. 

Portions extremely small compared to price. 
$10 shake give me a break. 
Ordered a beer. Didn't come with a glass. 
Side of baked beans was more like soup. 
Waiter seemed overworked but was as attentive as could be. 

Bring prices in line with portions or portions in line with prices and this is easily a 4 star go to place.

I'm very conflicted about this place. On one hand, it's an easy, no nonsense, straightforward gun range. Don't come here expecting any smiles, or pleasantries. These people don't have time for none of your damn politeness. The range is small, but serviceable, and the people seem to mind their own. The cost to shoot is reasonable, and I was in and out with zero fluff. Sign this, "what do you want," bobs your uncle, and boom. 

On the other hand, this place is MAGA central, and is teeming with good ol' boys, replete in their camouflage accoutrements. There's a sign on the door, MAKING YOU REMOVE YOUR MASK; "if you're scared of the virus, don't enter" it reads. The staff is aloof to the point of negligence, as they didn't seem to give a SINGLE SHIT about making my customer experience pleasant. As mentioned above, the range is small, and if you are a new shooter, you're better off going somewhere else, as you are completely unmonitored once you go through the doors. 

I see many reviews mention how expensive everything is, but I didn't find that to be my experience, but I did bring my own gun and ammo. I DID have to buy their target, as they told me the ones I brought were too small, but I was around $20 for a target and an hour of shooting. They also have a small inventory of guns for sale, and I didn't think the prices for those were anything egregious either. They had a Canik TP9, that was about the same price I paid for mine. (What I did find weird is that neither of the guys working seemed to really knew much about guns, as they were unable to answer my questions about magazines, and the one guy didn't even know what gun I was talking about when I asked to see it. Then again, they could've just not cared at all, which also would be on brand for this place)

I'll probably continue to go here, but I always feel like I need to keep my wits about me here; more so than usual at a range. It just feels like at any moment, one of these dingbats will try some "cool trick" they saw on YouTube, or horseplay with a loaded weapon while they argue about who gets to headshot their Barack Obama target first. And the no mask policy just seems like unnecessary tough guy posturing.  

Still, it's close, cheap (for me, bringing my own stuff) and fast.

So the hotel itself is ok.   It's clean enough.   We have stayed here before and actually we're planning to stay one night a week for the next few months because of its conventions to work- however today we were told they didn't do early check in without a fee.  They did end up letting us check in a bit early so it turned out OK.   They were super nice at check in when we had a few issues with our room.   They fixed everything right away.

When we drove out of our way on our return home after a weekend escape, we thought that Biscuit Love was going to be the icing on the cake. 

If we went based off of first appearance we would have both given this a 5 star review. This transformed historic house in Franklin is an amazing setting for a breakfast/brunch location. 

The other thing to note, they have plenty of seating available, especially when you factor in the outdoor space they have (go past the restrooms to find this seating). 

Staff were friendly enough, nothing over the top to make them stand out, but took pretty good care of us while we were there.

Now the disappointing part. The menu was full of things that we wanted to try. In fact we had to take a minute to narrow down the selections. For a starter we had the BONUTS: Fried biscuit dough, tossed in sugar, topped with lemon mascarpone, served over blueberry compote. This was easily the highlight of our meal and would come in with a 4 out of 5 stars. My suggestion, if you are looking for a place to get a cup of coffee and share a dessert with a friend....hard to go wrong with this option. Next up my gf had one of the signature breakfast biscuit sandwiches (S.E.C). The biscuit was really dry and crumbling, making it impossible to eat and the sausage was bland. She tried to spice it up by adding some gravy, didn't work. I went with the BLACKLOCK HASH. Very disappointed! Consisting of Alma's Skillet Potatoes, Smoked Sausage, Peppers, Onions, and 2 Sunny Side Eggs. Bland would be the best way to describe this dish. Now I asked for the egg on the side, but the potatoes weren't seasoned with anything and there were maybe three small bites of sausage in the bowl. 

The BONUTS, Biscuit Sandwich and Hash (plus one Coke) were about $32.....not remotely worth the price or the time to drive out of the way for this experience.

I love Los Betos! If U want good & cheap Mexican food, look no further! My 5 year old daughter loves their bean & cheese burrito! It's huge & she can finish it all! I like their carne asada & lengua taco. They have 3 different kinds of salsa; mild, medium & hot. They also have sliced radishes, pickled carrots & jalapeños! Yumm!

This is the nastiest complex ever the management which is Amanda and Patricia are the worst Patricia kids run around complex writing on complex grounds and Amanda has a truck with expired plates piled with junk she would have anybody else car towed we are old and tired of seeing Patricia kids walking dogs without leases and they don't pick up after the dogs either but they don't care because they stay here rent free and they got the property looking real bad and have the nerve to go up on rent when they say remolded they mean different color paint and that's it,I took pictures and going to send to news2 and corporate because management need to be replaced and maintenance only pick up paper and can't do that right all the neighbors are tired of Amanda and Patricia except they friends that do whatever they want.

Bye

I broke my humerous in 3 places 01/06/19. I saw Dr. Mahoney the day after the emergency room. He was very knowledgable and interested in all aspects of my injury. Between Dr. Mahoney and Andrew Knox his PA I got the best treatment possible. It was a painful, confusing time for me. 
I had never broken a bone. Both my doctor and his PA were always kind, understanding, and took as much time as I needed to answer all my questions. I never felt rushed, or like I was just another patient. It has been five months and I am finally feeling like the trauma is in the past. This is my second experience with TOI and both my experiences with the Doctors and the staff get 5 stars. Mary Colletti Bishop.

I don't use their dog walking services, so I cannot comment, but I do shop there for my dog food and treats (and toys occasionally).  My one dog has horrible stomach issues and usually cannot stay on the same food for more then three weeks.  Rich, the owner, gave me some samples to try, including the Verus fish formula.  We've been feeding this food for what seems like forever at this point.  It is extremely reasonably priced and they always have what I need in stock.  

They carry a variety of foods, but not too many mainstream brands but I love getting the Three Dog Bakery treats for my two and I find their stomachs don't react like they used to to their old treats and food.  I also love the variety of high quality toys they carry.  I find that they last longer and my dogs love them.

I also like the fact that they are eco-friendly.  They email you your receipt which I prefer than paper receipts I just throw away.

Went there for breakfast items stated at &10.99, walked out. That's insane for a diner having no breakfast specials. We ate there 2 weeks ago and the food. & service was just okay, really ad first impression.

Location and staff are top notch - very professional team and exceeded my expectations.

The hotel is older but solid in comfort, safety and convenience.  Wish they had a full size coffee maker though as I travel with my own coffee and those tiny cup makers wear me out as I drink 8 cups first thing in the morning.

Only thing I dislike is the Fitness Center - the treadmills are somewhat dangers as your front feet are prone to hitting the front of the machine and possible losing footing.  The wi-fi also does not perform well in that area which makes an hour run seem longer.

I would stay here again and kudos to the Staff

Literally a roach motel. LITERALLY!! Roaches on the heating unit (which the maintenance people will explain as "seasonal bugs"). And noisy as heck. Stay here if you want to hear the road noise from the interstate, all activity in the room next to you, the hallway noise, and the crappiest most expensive breakfast available (with nasty servers).

This place has come a long way in the last 4 or 5 years. The yeast they used to use made ALL the beers taste like banana. They've seemed to have fixed this and the NCAALe is the way to go. It's the rookie of the year. Everything else on the menu, including food and beer, is the equivalent of "eh, it'll do"

And someone please sweep the floor. It's not old food on it but layer's of dust accumulated over the years in every corner and under magazine racks and the employee counter.

Expensive place but food was good.
On Christmas Eve, my husband n I were in st Louise when we thought of grabbing a quick dinner at whole foods. Since whole foods closed down early on Christmas Eve we found 1764 across the street open and landed having dinner there. Food was yum but I found it on the expensive side.

Not worth the price of admission. I used to go here all the time with my girlfriend when she lived in Hillsboro Village, but that was just because of location. Now that we live on West End we never frequent here. This is a good social gathering place for brunch and breakfast but that is about it. They offer free wifi and a rustic coffee shop setting.

I don't bother to review McDonalds very often, but...

This location really tries to work the speed of their drive through.  They have a double drive through to start, then at rush times they put another person outside to collect cash so they are working two payment windows too.

I went throught at peak breakfast time this week and my car almost never stopped rolling.  I give them a lot of credit for being innovative and trying to keep the wait short!

Multiple times I have had employees be very short with me and not helpful in the beer department.  They are extremely arrogant.  It is a frustrating place to shop.
 The do have decent prices when you have a coupon.
FOOD: sometimes good, sometimes not so good.  SERVICE:sometimes good, sometimes not so good.  Knowing what to expect is like riding a new roller coaster for the first time...blindfolded!

Caveat emptor.  When you get to the register,  you find out about the 3% credit card surcharge. Prices are already premium here, and that amount may exceed the allowable amount according to the CC company's merchant account. 

McCafferys and Simply Fresh are really equal in quality, have better price and selection, without the silly surcharge.
Great local place to go to. Great food!   Awesome Bar tenders. Jimmy is a great bar tender nice friendly and full of energy.

As Germantown residents, my husband and I were thrilled for a more casual option in the neighborhood. We came for lunch the day after their soft opening, and we were very impressed.

The service was incredible. Our waitress was very friendly, and every single manager/owner came over to make sure that we were enjoying our burgers and to introduce themselves. It's always nice to know that they are interested in getting to know you as a customer.

For burgers, I had the special of the day, the Jack Special with the sauce on the side. When they brought my burger, the sauce wasn't on the side, and the cheese was "American cheddar" instead of just cheddar. I have a bizarre aversion to American cheese so they remade my burger without cheese or sauce. I ended up with a hamburger with lettuce, pickle, and onion. It was INSANELY good. I did find it overpriced at $10.00 for what turned into a basic hamburger with no fries. My husband ordered the Greg Brady, which had mac n cheese and barbecue chips on it. He loved it although it was definitely not a light lunch. His was about $8.00 and definitely seemed to be a better value than my choice. My husband also ordered fries, which were the crinkle cut type. They were fine, but nothing to write home about. 

The beer selection is also great. They have a program similar to the Flying Saucer to encourage trying new beers. If you try 100 different beers, you get your own personal mechanic or Western shirt. Granted, these were not very good looking but a fun touch nonetheless. 

One complaint on the decor, all of the stools were hightops with no backs-- for those of us of Hobbit height, these are not comfy... at all. I would love for there to be more options for seating without having to sit outside. 

Another complaint is the lack of healthy options. I understand it is a burger place, but the burgers aren't huge, and I was hungry a few hours after eating. The only current sides are fries and sweet potato fries. I would love to have the option of a side salad or vegetable to lighten up the heavy fare. 

I look forward to returning and becoming more of a regular!
I feel like they changed the salsa from 2 years ago... but it's still good. I'm biased though, love me some mexican food

I was not impressed at all.   This place definitely requires a different palette of taste.  It was 3 people included myself. We had only one bottle of wine and ordered the $55 "Trust in Us" share a plate. Our bill came out to be $251 and I left out of there extremely hungry.   

I am used to eating in share a plate type of settings, but the food here is definitely an acquired taste.   I only came in because the amazing reviews, but it was just not my cup of tea.  The customer service was great though.

They are contracted as the delivery service for the big box hardware store with the orange buckets and aprons, and honestly I would never buy a large item from that store again because of this delivery company.  The delivery service is a scam.  No matter what level of delivery you pay for - front porch, inside the house, or room of choice - they will try to only leave it on your doorstep.  They also call you only minutes before they arrive in an effort to make sure you aren't home to direct them and get the service you paid for.  If I wouldn't have been home they would have left the item in my driveway and been long gone by the time I arrived.  Do NOT use this scam of a delivery company.  And do not buy delivery items from the store the rhymes with "Dome Repo" unless you want these company to try to scam you.

Did an order for delivery. Food was good but portions were a bit small. Also, I think they forgot to give me one serving of rice. Finally, I paid $3.50 for an order of naan and only received ONE piece of naan. Usually in restaurants, I think that'd get you a basket of naan. 

I will not be ordering delivery from here again. Maybe would check out the restaurant in person because the food wasn't bad.

The tech was great, very nice. However, I called them because my pull out hose for my kitchen faucet had sprung a leak. I did some research before he arrived...The quote for a new faucet was 630 - 860. If I bought my own and had them install 310.
Here is the best part....the hose that was leaking that needed to be replaced was FREE from Delta ....the quote to have Midway get the hose and install...$275.00.
I had another company do it...less than a 1/2 hour work. $75.00..

You do the math....never again...rip off company.

It's a nursing home full of drunks basically. If that is your scene, this place is for you.

Now they cut the staff down and say "oh people didn't show up."

Yeah right.
the place to be to get pho. place is often pack with hungry customers. if you come here you'll know why. not very big so seating is a factor but its worth the wait.

Food was good. Would have appreciated a more detailed menu. The store was very dirty. Floors dusty, not just snow dirt. Napkins on floor. Tables not wiped. Drink area messy. Soda sprayed all over my hand when I dispensed it. Store was nearly empty at 1:30 pm and 4 staff members behind the counter with few customers.

We went here for the first time excited about going to an old fashioned diner / soda fountain.  The shakes and other desserts were fine..... until our friend spotted a cockroach crawling near our table on the floor.  You know it's not a clean place when your shoes are sticking to the floor when you're standing to pay the bill. Staff was kind but it's a do not return for us.
Views 5 star, service 4 star. Chowder 4 star, garlic baked clams imho 3 star, a bit soggy but great garlic flavor. I'd go again just for the views!
It took a half hour to get our food. Pico de Gallo was bland and the taco shells tasted store purchased. Disappointed

Decided to order pick up from El Molinitos on 22nd and craycroft (3/19/2020) and I must say........it is the most over-priced mediocre Mexican food I have ever eaten in Tucson. Ordering via phone was damn near impossible as every question I asked could not be answered via the most basic English sentence. For example, I ask "Is the side of rice big enough for 2 people?" and the only reply I got was a confused grunt or 2. I should of hung up on this nasty food place then and there.

Anyway, so I ordered a Chimichanga with Carne Asda, a quesadilla with carne and a side of rice. The total order was $26. Needless to say the food I picked up was NOT very good. My carne Chimichanga was way over cooked, lacking any sour cream, guacamole or cheese, and it actually looked like someone stepped on it (See Picture if you dont believe me). I have never ever had a chimi that looked flat like that. Also, the side of rice came in a teeny tiny Styrofoam cup. I mean.....muy muy pequeño arroz! I dont know what you charged me for the rice.....but total rip off!

El Molinito should be ashamed of itself cooking such awful Mexican food! Especially in Tucson! 

I will NEVER EAT THERE again and I suggest that you never eat there agin too! Yuck yuck yuck yuck!

Wow, I didn't expect much. But the food blew me away. I had the chicken quesadilla which could possibly be the best one I ever had. Their steak taco wasn't bad. What truly shined was their sauces. 5 stars, would come again.

Let me start off by saying, I've been to Michael's ONCE and never again.  Not only did I get thrown out for telling someone to keep their hands off my wife, but, when I wrote a negative review, Michael's cried to Yelp to remove my review.  Poor babies who cannot stand up and be scrutinized and Shame on Yelp for removing a review that didn't contain foul language or names.  Yelp and Michael's are a good fit for public deception.  Unfortunately, you will not have a chance to read an honest review of this business so you'll have to go there and experience the scuzziness for yourselves.

Convenient location where different king cakes from different bakeries are in one spot. This Hub is located inside of the Broad Theater and gives you the opportunity to step out of you king cake comfort zone and see what other bakery's have to offer. My favorite is #haydels, but they did not participate this year. Now to be honest, I didn't really step out of my comfort zone since I purchased a Caywood and Randazzo's king cake. The Randazzo's king cakes are second on my king cake list. I think their bakeries are related in some way, but don't quote me. 

I love the concept. If you are open to trying different king cakes, this is the place for you to come and explore.

Food was delicious, wonderful and light. For three of us, we split a salad and two pizzas and it was just right. However the staff felt pretentious and cold. Even though the place was empty the wait took ages.

Open on Sunday. That's the only reason we ended up here. My daughter had to dress like an "old person" for school on Monday and I forgot about this until Sunday. Being one of the very few thrift stores open on Sunday, Thrift City as on the agenda. 

It's incredibly disorganized. I haven't found a thrift store in NOLA to organize by size (irritating) but at least most of them sort by color... this place doesn't have any sort of organization system that I've been able to see.

Good prices, though. I left with a dress, hat, and wool shawl for about $15.

Nice guys, never had an issue with their service or questioned their honesty. I've brought four vehicles here in the last five years without incident. 

Clean waiting area, beverages provided.

Great BBQ (brisket and ribs) sides are also amazing sweet potato soufflé and Mac and cheese.  and IMHO the best selection of craft brews on tap in st Pete always a great porter or stout. Very friendly staff.

I have fond memories of Cafe Maspero. It is one of the first restaurants I ate in at the French Quarter. Alas, my memories are fonder than my most recent experience. 

We were seated immediately, the place was empty on a Friday night at 6:00 pm. Our server Tracy came right over and took our drink orders. Drinks came right out. We ordered dinner. I got shrimp, Toni a muffelata. Teagan got the seafood platter. 

Salads came out quickly, but nothing special. Mostly iceberg with shredded cheese and a lonely cherry tomato. Next were our dinners. My fried shrimp was excellent. Cooked perfectly, crunchy, not too salty. Delicious without any condiment. The calamari and oysters in Teagan's platter were both over cooked. The calamari shattered when I bit into it. Toni's muffelata was okay. Fresh bread, plenty of meat, served hot. If you didn't have anything to compare it to, you might even say great. The balance of olive salad to meat was off and the olive salad wasn't very good. The fries served with our meals were undercooked and under seasoned. 

The atmosphere at Cafe Maspero is divey but in a great location on Decatur Street. Great place to hang out and people watch. 

Tracy was great. Friendly and attentive. She heard us mention Brittany Spears and shared a story about her Halloween costume. Great fun!!  

If only the food were better. This is my third visit to Cafe Maspero over the course of about 14 years. This is the first time I was disappointed in my food. Maybe it was just an off night. The lack of a crowd on a Friday night suggests otherwise. Thanks for the memories Cafe Maspero.

The food was not just delicious. It's divine and unique. I had ramen with "braised" pork in it. Oh, it was awesome: the noodle was soft and the soup was unforgettably tasty!!! On the top of everything,  the price of wine and dish is so~~reasonable!! The serving staff was kind! I liked the interiors of this spacious restaurant too!!! Two thumbs up!!!
Just amazing.  Everyone needs to try their tatter tots!!
They transferred my info to my new cell phone, which I purchased through Facebook, quickly and no charge.  Great service!
An unexpected good Mexican restaurant amidst chain restaurants. Had the chicken and rice soup and a fish taco. The best sopa de pollo I've had. Taco ok.

Called in an order for pick up. They said it would take 20 min. I got there about 25 min after the phone call, and the first thing they do is make you pay before checking on the food. After I pay, they come back and tell me it's going to be ready in 20 min......... I responded with "another 20 min?!" and the employees just looked at me with an annoyed look, no apologies, no double checking on the order. It was obvious they forgot about my order, but they clearly did not give a shit.

So I waited another 35 minutes and I finally got my sandwich, which they didn't fully make it the way I ordered (wrong cheese, asked for no sprouts). 

Basically, never call in for a pick up, because they lack enough organization to handle pick ups and dine ins.

Last time I went here it was a rainy, chilly Sunday afternoon. I really didn't want to leave my house, but McDonald fries rock! They had a long line, so I went in. Two African Amercan girls with weaves, were digging and scratching their scalps, than touching the counters! Yuk! One had on gloves! I thought she must be cooking the burgers! No way am I eating here! My stomach turned! I called the manager as soon as I got back home. She said the girls had been talked to about scratching their scalps around the food. She said that she has had lots of complaints about them. So they already have been talked to, but continue to ignore the managers reprimand! I won't be ordering from this location as long as those girls work there! That's just unsanitary to be digging into your scalp, while serving people food! Yuk!!!

decided to come here because it's closer than my favorite place.  i read the reviews and gave it a chance.  however every ice cream flavor we tried had very little flavor.  the only thing that was decent was the bear tracks which is most likely due to the added fudge and peanut butter cup.  the vanilla coconut and coffee were bland to say the least.  i don't know how else to explain the flavor profile of the ice cream but bland.  i gave it two stars based on affordability.  i will be heading elsewhere for my ice cream moving forward.

Fantastic tour.  Captain Brent did a nice job explaining what we were seeing and pointed out so many things I would have just looked past.  I went on the pontoon boat and enjoyed it very much. Next time I'll try the airboat.  I've done the airboat tours in Florida so I wanted to try something new.  This tour was by far better than the couple I went on in Florida (one guaranteed alligator sightings...that should have been a red flag...nothing that day).  We saw so many alligators on the Jean Lafitte tour I stopped counting.  Thanks for a great time exploring the swamp!

This is my go-to place when I am craving Mexican food or a margarita. I've never eaten in Mexico but if I had to imagine, it doesn't get more authentic than this! My top 3 favorites on the menu are arroz con pollo, chicken fajitas, and the chicken fajita quesadilla. The quesadilla is loaded with cheese and chicken and veggies. So good. 

I always get the Texas margarita with sugar on the rim. The 27 ounce is a lot with food so I leave stuffed to the max but after a long week it's worth it.

We went here with high hopes of finding a more genuine German restaurant, but unfortunately, did not find what we were looking for.

The restaurant & building are super cute! And decorated in vintage German artifacts! However, the service was similar to that of a diner and the food matched that. The food tasted very cheap, like fast food, and was pretty opposite of German food. The only thing I liked besides the restaurant decor & placemats was the bread appetizer they provide while you wait and the beer selection. (They have a nice list of German beers.)

On top of the sad food options, it was expensive. For two people with a beer each, it was about $50. That's a lot for cheap food. 

We probably will not return, but at least we tried it! Love the idea, just not the execution.

We stopped in Destrito for lunch. Although the entrance was a little camouflaged and not easy to find, the decor and atmosphere inside was really cool. We started off with mushroom Huarache. It didn't ring Mexicano but it rang taste explosion on my palate. I highly recommend it. Next we had the chicken enchiladas... Another hit with the taste buds! Love the combo of habanero with the coconut. Service was very good. Chalk up another great restaurant in Philly.

there is a young gentleman that works at this location in the drive thru pharmacy & although he might know how to do his job, his communication and customer service is the WORST!!!!!  I've been here 3 times and had to get rude with him because he speaks to people in a very unprofessional way. i drove another 20 minutes just  to go to another location because I was about get out of character just to get my medicine when I was sick as a dog. This guy is a Straight asshole. DO NOT GO HERE.

It took us 10 minutes to find this place! You'd think they would put a pretty big sign of the name of the restaurant but NOPE, we circled around so many times trying to find this place. If you're trying to find this place at night, the front window has Christmas lights and its right across from Spaghetti Warehouse. Inside is very cute decor!

Anyway, I came here starving and was hoping I'd get some food fast! However, that didn't happen. For a Saturday night, there was only one waitress and I'm guessing one chef because it took FOREVER for our food to come out. The appetizer came out pretty quick so it sure did increase my appetite but it took a very long time before our entrees came out. 

The pad thai was pretty good. The Kang Dang (chicken curry) and the Panang (beef curry) also. A big problem with the place though? TOO SALTY! While all the dishes had great flavor, everything was overly salty.

The portions were also pretty small and prices were overpriced. The curries were at $11 and $14 respectively. No thanks!

Sadly disappointed. After reading so many good reviews we chose this spot for the hubs first taste of a muffaletta. The olive salad was mostly cauliflower and had a bitter taste. Good meat and bread, however. I guess he will have to wait until I drag him to Shreveport for a real muffy at Fertitta's!

So, I never imagined that this would be anything above a sub-par experience, but my most recent visit to Top Dog (probably my third visit total) was not as bad as expected. 

Top Dog is what I consider SJT (South Jersey Trash). This consists of a bunch of scantily dressed girls, tough guys that want to be like The Situation, and bartenders that do everything in their power to avoid eye contact with you while you wait at the bar for 10 minutes. I came here when I first turned 21 (not too many people in here are that much older) and vowed never to return again. However, a friend who desperately wanted to go brought me a few nights back.

What saved them from complete failure were two things:

1.Decently priced beers-$2 for a Yuengling OK-already a good price and on a Friday night, that is just unheard of unless you go to a dive bar.  
2. Half of our time there was spent on the outdoor deck. The bartenders were extremely nice and were quick to deliver us our drinks. The inside-different story. 

In closing I would not recommend coming here. It's your typical college bar/I just turned 21 and feel like a bad ass kind of place. Don't come here expecting class because there is none to be had in good ole' Top Dog.

Although I did not have the opportunity to eat at Paco's in person to absorb all of its illustriousness, I was lucky enough to utilize the convenience of its expertly placed drive-thru window manned by some of the finest employees I have ever had the pleasure of doing business with. Paco's is located on grant road on the north side of the street. It is painted a beautiful off-white with a light orange almost pink trim (you can't miss it, it is right next to Amor Nails). There is a beautifully handmade billboard that is unparalleled in craftsmanship with a sturdy column hoisting it proudly in the air as if to scream at the city of Tucson that Paco's IS a force to be reckoned with. 

I have had many opportunities to dine at various establishments in the Tucson area but wouldn't consider myself to be a food connoisseur in even the most remote sense. That said, I have had several of the breakfast burritos and to be frank, they all seem to be a little potato heavy. This is good because I love potatoes (I have been told by many that I have an addiction). However, the fish tacos are what warrant my semi-uncanny review. I decided to try the fish tacos by means of a campaign of finding the best in Tucson. My sister and I are very close with a wonderful bond and we do a lot together. If your feel good meal is a fish stick middle school hot lunch slathered in tartar sauce wrapped in a tortilla with cabbage on top then this may be the choice for you, however, I graduated from middle school 36 years ago and the last thing on my mind is returning for the fine cuisine. I believe there to be much greater options within the Tucson area.

With Tucson being a budding young city with major potential in the culinary field, I will not say to Paco's that I won't be back. However, I will not be back for the fish tacos because they left a lot to be desired. As many have noted here, the bathroom is on the side of the building which is great if you're just trying to pop in.

Bad customer service . Decided to refinance our car payment  through pyramid we were helped by Catherine who was very very friendly and made the process nice and easy for us . Only reason why I give it 3 stars is because the tellers specifically "Jamie" came off very rude as she asked me and my fiancé when we first walked in who we were speaking to regarding the loan and he was not sure so his response was im not to sure I just know it was a lady and Jaime very rudely responded "okay well that doesn't help at all" as the teller next to her laughed slightly leaving my fiancé embarrassed . Very bad customer service up front and horrible first impression .

Our son is student at the University of Tampa and this has been his restaurant of choice when we corm to town.
We were recently in town and naturally, went to Bern's. As always, there were at least 4 people working at the hostess stand. As always, they were very dismissive and quite snobby. Unfortunately, this seemed to be the attitude of the evening.!  We had made reservations 2 weeks in advance and still had to wait over 20 minutes for a table, 8:30 reservation. Once seated our waitress was uninterested in serving us, never even got her name. Steak was NOT up to expected standard. We felt rushed and my 18 year old son didn't finish his food and didn't even want to take it with him.
Time to find a new steak restaurant in Tampa!

this week was pierogie week.
there were additional kinds of pierogies which were paired with suggested PBC beers.
it was great-- especially the buffalo chicken!!!
definitely worth the little trip north!!

My first trip to Cinitiolis was back in 1989. I was a Senior in High School, we were starting our own band and needed a PA system. We were referred by a friend's father that had been a loyal customer. We were treated great, and not over sold.

When I returned as an adult to purchase a simple non-powered mixer, the scene was totally different. It seemed much more like a junk shop where washed up cover band leaders go to reminisce about the 1980's and try to trade in their Peavey speakers with the gray carpet finish. Benny is the star of the show and I love that. He's got tons of character but he is as much of a Used Car salesman as you can get. 

I took a small "hot spot" monitor there to be serviced. It didn't work when I got it back and was told by the guy at the cash register that I didn't know how to use it... In fact, I knew how to use it - the thing was still broken. The same guy at the register is the reason that I will never return to Cintitolis. His personality is that of a dying house plant. He tries to be "funny" when he's really not, he's kind of a dick in reality.

Only go here if you're down on your luck and need to buy second hand gear for that 6th attempt at stardom.
The best BBQ I have ever had. So wonderful. I did not even need to add sauce for the ribs. The beans were perfectly balanced. Can't wait to enjoy it again.

I have been with Verizon/Frontier for 6 years and use to praise their service to my friends and family. However, in the last two years I was promised two separate times certain pricing only to find out a month later my bill actually increased from what it was originally and also that I locked myself into a two year contract. I was very pregnant when the price first increased so I just let it keep auto drafting the bill and not worrying about it.  Finally I got tired of overpaying so I put my infant in the car and did circles around a parking lot for 1.5 hours to get it resolved. On that call I was promised certain pricing just to find out my next bill was double the price quoted. I called with a crying baby on my shoulders and was on the phone for 2 hours going from person to person and finally retention who could not help and also let me know I would have to pay $226 to cancel my services. I am beyond annoyed and to the point of tears. This company is an awful bunch of liars and crooks and I would really rethink choosing them as your internet or cable provider!!!

So the positive, it's really nice inside and the Heights really needed a nail place. Unfortunately, I left so disappointed. I had a specialty pedicure with gel - it cost $70 plus tip. So for that price I am expecting the cuticles to be cleaned up and the nails to be shaped to perfection. I ended up having another nail tech come over and try and fixed the botched work. Everyone there was very nice but so upset that I wasted all that time to feel like I need to go to another salon and have them redone. I loved the nail art/manicures that they have posted on Instagram, so I am guessing that's where this place is on point.

We ordered lunch delivery for two people today, and while the food was very good, delivery was slow.  That being said, when I called to inquire if there was a problem, I was told delivery orders always take about an hour.  The food was good enough that we can take that into consideration and live with it.  

We moved from LA less than a year ago and have been hard pressed to find any good Asian food in Boise.  While this place can't compare to Chinatown, it could certainly hold it's own in other parts of the city.  

Very tasty indeed.  Nice portions at a fair price.  We will be back.

Maybe I'm spoiled by the Nordies I had back in SoCal but this one doesn't impress me at all. Small selection. The customer service is nonexistent. I'm used to Nordstrom customer service in CA where people ask if you need help, show you what you are looking for, etc. Not here. Everytime I'm in there I have to go looking for help. I guess this is the reason I don't shop Nordies as often as I used to. If I'm going to fend for myself, I might as well tackle TJ Maxx.

Honestly, the food was excellent. I've come here a number of times, ordered a lot of take out, and it's always delicious. But the staff could do a little bit better. Tonight, despite having almost an hour until they closed, we were "politely" rushed out. The waitress, Natalie, made sure to take one of our plates that clearly had food on it without asking if we were finished. We overheard the table behind us talking about how they felt rushed and their food had been forgotten. Instead the staff was focused on refilling ketchup bottles and picking up silverware from unused tables. As we walked out, the guy at the front sort of opened his mouth like he was going to tell us to have a good night and then just said nothing. In past visits, they almost always forget the correct sauce when I pick up to-go orders. Starting to wonder if it's just a service issue.

Already had another pizza from here since August and went back on 9/24 to try something new. We got calzones and cannolis. Both were delicious!

I love calzones. I got one filled with ricotta, pepperoni, mushrooms and black olives. Hubby's was filled with capicolla and mushrooms. They were both served with sauce on the side. They were great! I ate about 2/3 of mine and saved the rest for lunch.

Then we had cannolis for dessert. Filled with chocolate chips and sweet cream cheese. Perfect ending.

We'll definitely go on eating here. Once it cools off for good, I want to try the cioppino...
They were busy and we had to wait a bit, but Janae understood what we needed and where we needed to be and worked hard to make it happen. Highly recommend!

This spot located right next to Jackson Square is bustling for breakfast. However, that's not necessarily a sign of amazing quality, rather a sign of prime real estate in the infamous French Quarter, where breakfast restaurants are minimal, but people are abundant. 

Truthfully, the place felt like a money-machine...the establishment and servers were simply churning out food and drinks, trying to get people in and out as fast as possible while charging high prices for an admittedly simple menu.

Speaking of the menu, there were some items that seemed exciting. Yet, everything came out and the quality was subpar. The biggest clue to the cutting corners practice came with my latte. I ordered a vanilla latte- something I was pleasantly surprised they offered. When it came out I was confused as to why it was lukewarm- don't get me wong, I usually couldn't care less about the temp of my drinks, and high heat is not something I demand. But this was oddly cool, even though it was brought out almost immediately. I took one peer across the bar where the drinks were coming from and saw a digital coffee machine. The ones that have a billion buttons and can pour out anything from coffee to hot chocolate to a cappuccino. They're physically cannot surpass a certain heat level. 

Between the mediocre food and barely warm coffee, I knew this place was just trying make a quick buck. Even the server was incredibly detached, clearly hustling as fast as he could. The lack of quality and care marked this as a place I would not return to. Location is stellar, but everything else falls as a result.

I've been walking by Bob and Barbara's for a couple of months now and have been meaning to step inside. Then, last night, a couple of friends and I wanted to go dancing and figured this would be the closest and most-chance-for-dancing place in the neighborhood. 

I'd definitely like to go back when it's not super crowded because last night (Saturday), it was precisely that. One thing that was abundantly clear was B+B's dedication to serving PBR and their PBR memorabilia collection: whoah. 

There was a band playing that night who did a couple of feel-good oldies such as "My Girl" -- this was probably the best part of the hour that we were there. Nobody was really dancing like I'd hoped they would. On the other hand, there really was no room to dance. 

The bartender was nice enough. They only take cash, so bring your dollas. 

I'd love to come back on a more low-key night to check out the space and to see how they do drag on Thursday nights. Otherwise, the beer was cheap, the space was fun to look at, and my fellow patrons seemed decent.

During our recent long weekend in New Orleans, I wanted to find a bar that was a bit different, a bit off the beaten track. Found out that the Omni has a rooftop bar/pool area and decided to check it out.

We took the elevator to the top floor and discovered that there is a gate into the bar/pool area with card access for guests. However, the gate was ajar, and we quickly found our way to the bar.

It was a gorgeous day and it was nice to be outside. We were in a covered area right next to the bar, and there were plenty of sofas, tables/chairs, and bar stools to choose from. We grabbed a table and waited for service.

Unfortunately, there was just one server for the entire area, and she was slammed -- taking drink and food orders for everyone in the bar area and parked around the pool.

It took a while to her to get to us -- maybe 15 minutes. She kept telling us she'd get to us as soon as she could, and eventually she did. We ordered a couple margaritas and left it at that. Just ok -- nothing to write home about.

Given the slow service, we decided to cut our losses. We finished our drinks and moved on.

Pro tip: Sit at the bar and order your drinks directly from the bartender.
Ordered a dozen roses to the ritz Carlton. All of the roses were very open in full bloom.  I hope they last a day or two
A bit of a disappointment--food wasn't as great as we thought and we even bought to go for our kids to taste :( KFC is even better

Let me start by saying I'm only giving two stars here because I've eaten here before and it's been good. Let me start by giving the background info. 5:30 pm Monday night, it's pretty dead and we like it here so we brought our two year old with us. She's good at restaurants. 

Our server started out with such an attitude I wanted to get up and leave before our paper menus were handed to us.  He continued to be so stone cold that I regretted coming here. In fact he ruined my meal by making me feel uncomfortable and he didn't say much.

We ordered their $16 burger and $15 meatball pizza. Both have been great in the past.  Tonight the burger was awful.  No flavor not even the cheese.  The pizza was soggy with cold meatballs. Yuck yuck yuck. 

I totally regret coming here and won't be back in the future. I could have gotten a better meal at Toni Roni s and chances are they'd have less snotty service. I ll let my friends know too about this bad experience.

I love Talbot's!  The center-city Philadelphia store is easy to shop in, the sales staff is very helpful and knowledgeable, the prices are fair, the sales are great, and the clothes are classic in style. I'd be totally bereft if there were no Talbot's here!
I love this place!!!  Great spot for dinner or even just drinks and live music.  When I'm in Nashville, this is where I go for guaranteed enjoyable time.

I really regret signing up with Infinity Rentals even though it wasn't too expensive...but I think $34 is too much for what they're giving me.

Every email they've sent me with listings contain 6 of the exact same listings. So they send me an email with 30 listings, but in reality it ends up being about 5 because they're the same listing over and over...I called them with this problem 3 times already and every single time they said it would be different..but it hasn't been. 

The listings that I called were already rented! and one person even said that the listing was really old!

I asked for a refund and they said no.

Another thing, I called them and left them a message cause they didn't pick up and I never got a call back.

This really isn't the way to go if you're looking for a place to rent. It'll just stress you out even more!

They may be open until 6 o'clock but I went at 4:30 on a Tuesday and discovered that the young girls' phones were more fascinating than waiting on mere customers. Gave up without ordering anything. May have to go back at an earlier time of the day when they feel like working.
UPDATE: Gave this place another chance, earlier in the day when other customers were around. Food was very good and authentic tasting. I'll definitely go back again, but I'll watch for some activity first.

I have shopped at Lush for a long time. I've enjoyed their philosophy, customer service and the quality of their products.  However, that has changed. I purchased a set of bath bombs and other products packaged for spring for my child. We attempted to use bath carrots as directed, putting them under the running water. No bubbles appeared for any of the three carrots in the set.   I went into the store to describe the situation, and their reply was that I should've brought the faulty soaps back to the store for them to try them under water ---and if they didn't work for them, they would refund my money.  I was not looking for a refund, but certainly left the experience unsatisfied & not valued as a customer.  With so many forward-thinking companies and environmentally friendly bath products on the market, I will be making future purchases elsewhere.

The worst service ever! We waited over 20 min for anyone to even speak to us and he only said he would bring us water then waited another 20 min for him to come back without waters and then 45 for the food to come out! They were not even busy! Food is mediocre and it is way overpriced. There are too many amazing restaurants in New Orleans to waste your money here.

I made clear when I came in what insurance I had for my son.  I was told it would not be an issue.  Months later I receive a bill for hundreds of dollars with the excuse of "the code we use aren't accepted by your insurance company."  That would have been nice to know so we could have gone elsewhere.  We saw a nurse, got some benedryl and then a outrageous charge for which we are being told, "too bad."  This place is a scam.  Billing aside, we were the only people there, waited forever, and the place is dirty.  DO NOT trust these people with your children or your money.

All the high prices you come to expect of Indian fair, but none of the flavor. My first experience was during the lunch buffet and then I decided to give it another try.. The dinner was disappointing as well. India Grill doesn't belong among the quality local restaurants in St. Pete.
We normally don't go to sandwich shops but this is no ordinary sandwich shop. The bread is fresh and delicious. Well worth a try. We keep coming back for more.

Way over priced for what you get. Went in there on a Saturday, not busy at all. Staff was overly pushy for us to decided what to order. There was no line and once I ordered the guy rang me up before my wife and even kids even ordered. I am not trying to be petty but my  "small" sandwich was a full 3" smaller than my wife's sandwich. Bland bread, bland meat, bland sandwich, overpriced. Jimmy johns is cheaper and WAY more tasty. Save your money.

I recently moved back to St. Louis and was looking for a good BBQ/smokehouse. After seeing all of the very positive Reviews for Bogart's, I decided to give it a try. I drove from St. Charles to the Soulard area to pick up some ribs, and some pulled pork. I tried their potato salad, beans, and pickles. I ordered a to go order because I was short on time. When I arrived back in St. Charles I found that my order was incorrect. I was very disappointed and contacted Bogart's immediately. Niki from their staff contacted me back and apologized for the incorrect order. They are usually very busy and as with any business, this stuff can happen once in a while. Niki told me to come back in when I had time and they would make it up to me. They have more than made it up to me. I cannot praise their customer service enough! They were extremely gracious and took care of me. Now on to the food. I have had their ribs, their pulled pork, their brisket, and their smoked turkey. All of the meats were excellent! Their ribs had a nice smoke ring and were very tender. Their turkey is the best I have ever tasted. I enjoyed their brisket (nice and tender) and their pulled pork. The standouts to me are their ribs and smoked turkey breast. Side dishes that I would highly recommend are their potato salad, and beans. Their potato salad tastes like a mix of grandma's and deviled eggs. Extremely yummy and brings back that nostalgic feeling of summer and great BBQ. The beans are phenomenal!!!! If allowed, I would eat them 4 times each and every day. They would compliment any meal but together with the tremendous BBQ, they will send your taste-buds into orbit. They have a smokey, and meaty sweetness that is pure bean perfection. Do not miss out. I would say that this is definitely the best BBQ restaurant in St. Louis and I will be a customer for life!

Ms. Sylvia was the best!!! She was professional and explained the process in detail. The earring selection is vast and affordable. The location is easy to get to. I would recommend for new parents with babies. You have personal service. Love love love!!!

My husband and I were lucky enough to catch the Fat Noodle truck at one of its stops at Dragoon Brewing Company.  We were BLOWN AWAY by both the quality of the ramen, and the friendliness of the owner.  We'll be keeping an eye out for them in the future!

I can't speak for all David's Bridals but I can say this one is amazing!!!!  I ordered a dress in January for a May wedding.  But covid happened and shipping my dress just went down hill quickly!!!!  The GM Sandy and the other mangers called me and the wear house to try and get my dress for like 6 weeks.  And when my dress unfortunately wasn't going to come on time they already had a back up dress for me!!!  And they got the dress and alterations done in 2 weeks!!  Plus the alteration needs a shout out because I'm a heavy chested girl and didn't want my bra to show and they added wider straps and a back piece!!!  They are amazing!!!  Even in this crazy covid stuff they are rocking it out!!!!

Sorry to say the quality has gone way down hill.  The food we ordered came out like it had been sitting under heat lamps for a while. Dried out.  My enchilada mole was burnt. 

We used to love this place. Looks like we're going to find another restaurant.

This restaurant was quick to seat my party (with no reservation but we were there at 3:30pm).  We sat outside and was greeted pretty quickly by our waiter.  He said he'd be back with a round of water which he brought promptly.  The reason for three stars is because the food was just ok and the food takes forever to come out! 

The tostados were gross I don't recommend them!  The mussels were pretty good but by the time it was brought to the table they were luke warm and two of them were bad because they didn't open when cooked, I know that happens but usually aren't served to the customer.  My friends enjoyed the view and had a good time.  The wait times for food and refills on drinks take a long time so don't go here if you're in hurry.  I think this is more of a date night or tourist spot for people who have time to spare on eating out.

I've been here a bunch of times, mostly for trivia.  It gets very crowded & loud.  Ok food & beverages.  Service is not good though.  Sometimes you'll wait a long time for someone to come to your table.  On our most recent trip, the owner & a waitress both took orders at our table & messed up.  The waitress brought the wrong sauce for my friend's pretzel nuggets & forgot to bring 2 drinks, the bill didn't include 2 other drinks, then they charged those 2 drinks to my boyfriend's card when we were honest about them not being on the bill, even though he only had a tab at the bar for himself for 1 drink.  They refunded his card, but then took our cash to cover the drinks, which was supposed to be our tip.  It was annoying.

I was planning a special birthday for my fiancee and she loves Italian.   I talked with locals and read reviews so made the reservation for Friday night with high hopes.   We Did Not Get Seated Until 30 Mins after our reservation without being kept informed.   However Still Hopeful.   Ordered the chefs board which was good but no separate plates brought so struggling on small table to eat it( perhaps they don't give small plates with it)
I ordered chicken pesto and partner the spaghetti and meatballs.   I had the red sangria and she  a virgin bloody Mary( which she said was amazing)
The Chicken Pesto Was Bland And I found myself adding the garlic and almonds from the chefs board into it to add flavor.   Partner stated hers was good and when i tasted it the sauce was good.   Our Waiter Was Very Inattentive.   I had to ask for bread to be brought and he never came back to see if we wanted anything else .  I had told when i made reservation it was fiancees birthday and they stated she  would get dessert.   I had to tell waiter and he brought a dessert  menu but when she said she was full did not offer to make it to go ( so she did not get one)
The restaurant is noisy but we were able to carry on a conversation.   Overpriced for quality.   Was  worth the try but not a second one.

We ate here tonight. The lettuce on my wife's salad was limp, the chicken was under cooked and the salad was drench in salad dressing. My dinner tasted good but was cold. By the time I got back to our motel room, the bathroom was all that was on my mind. Never again.

Ok, so I have to start this review by saying that the food we received was great.

The reason why I will never return with 
my group was the VERY RUDE server that we had the displeasure of working with last night.

Emma-Jane got off on a bad foot with us when we asked for our party of 6, to have checks split down to couples, which she flatly refused to do.

Next, I asked Emma Jane to put red sauce on the pizza that ordered instead of white.   She literally stood there and screwed her face up, and announced that she wasn't sure if THEY could do that.  I asked her to please talk to the chef, and she responded that SHE wouldn't advise it.  (Good job she is not the one eating it)

Then at the end of the meal, she bought the 1 bill and left us to do the math for each couple.   So when she came back for the credit cards, she huffed and puffed because she had to write down the 3 different amounts for the cards.

Never before have I seen such a rude server.  Especially in a place where we have just spent over $350 on some food.

Emma Jane needs to be let go or retrained before being allowed to work with the public.
