Rant over. 

The quality of the food there was very good though!
Big place, kinda quiet. Lots of TVs. No Absolute Manadrin, No Stoli O.  Very limited bar.  Nice decor.  Nice video games.   Easy to stumble home from
Chris gave me a great haircut, and offered advice for hair care going forward. Also had a complimentary drink during the short wait. 5 stars on a short notice apt.

I love when local places market/promote themselves at my place of business.  We have a huge staff where I work that are always looking for something new to eat.  A crewe of 5 folks from Jimmy John's stopped by today with sandwich samples.  The staff loved them.  I personally tried the veggie sandwich which was so tasty and gourmet it reminded me of living in California.  Great idea, and i will definitely be adding this to my list of places to visit for lunch.

The website looks nothing like the restaurant (www.alibabatucson.com).  We asked the manager and he said that the webdesigner just picked out some other fancy restaurants and used their picture for the website.  That's messed up!

The food was mediocre.  The wait staff was too eager to please.  

You can go elsewhere for good Persian food.

No alcohol.

Stopped in for a late lunch today and had an awesome experience. The food is always great, but our server Bethany was fantastic, she took wonderful care of us and towards the end she even chatted with us a bit. Best experience I've had at a restaurant in a long time.
Best pizza ever and the service and delivery was awesome and really nice people thank y'all so much and the pizza and wings are great
The hype is more than the product! Service was slow and took 1 hr to get my order with 25 or so people in the building. Very very pricey!

I took my 2006 Mustang in for an alignment after recently installing new shocks/struts/springs. I was told my right ball joint was bad and I needed new front lower control arms with new ball joints and was quoted over $1000 for repairs. I said no and upon rechecking the ball joints and control arms found they were in normal working condition. I was told they did as much of an alignment as possible with a bad ball joint. Upon checking that I found they made no measureable adjustments to the front end nor did they center the rear end which has an adjustable panhard bar which would have no impact on adjusting even if the front end did have an issue.

I've been coming to this restaurant for a few years, and while service and food go up and down in terms of quality and quantity, in general it's by far good enough to keep me coming back. As far as food, I'm a fan. I actually haven't been to a lot of Indian lunch buffets, for comparison, but the selection here is good enough to keep me stuffed at one plate. The rice and naan come out fairly often during peak lunch hour (+), but every once in a while they lack a little in veggie dishes(-). During the week they have one dish of rice biryani, one dish of naan, usually at least 3-4 veggie dishes and 4-5 meat dishes with one rotating type of fried appetizer in the middle too. Their masalas are great, and I'm a fan of their really tender goat and lamp curries. I especially love that they have a huge selection of cold items, salads and as much raita and onion chutney as I can ladle on to my plate. In the past, mango drinks (either juice or lassi) have been a la carte or only out on the weekend buffets, but maybe in the last four ish visits it's been out on their buffet permanently as part of the price. Big fan of that. If I had to pick one negative though, it would be the overall heaviness of all the food. Whether veggie or meaty, everything is super saucy/creamy. And when it's served buffet style with the temptation to pile on, my stomach starts to hurt verrry quickly! 

Service has also gotten better. Not in the way that I have any clue what the guy's name is, or even what his voice sounds like really. But I, against my will, seem to drink BARRELS of water when I go out, and not once in the past year or so has my water glass ever been empty! That is more than enough for me at this type of place. Kudos to you guys. Current lunch buffet prices have gone down in the past year, or at least back down to what they used to be after a temporary price hike. 9.95 for weekdays, 10.95 for weekends. Pretty good deal when you also figure in there's really no need to order any a la carte beverages.
Service gets the star. Food was cold and boring.  Go eat Cajun food at a bad Cajun restaurant, this is awful.

All you can eat sushi is a smoking deal for the price and the selection but I'm not crazy about the quality of the sushi itself.  Service and ambiance need an upgrade also.  So it just depends on what is most important to you quantity or quality.
The crust was like unflavored hard bread. I was expecting a delicious deep dish waste of a night in Nashville.
Food was served cold (twice). Was really looking forward to trying the place out with the family but it didn't turn out well.

I'll start off by saying my wife and I sat at the bar for lunch and our bartender was lovely and did everything she could to make up for failures that were out of her control.  My margarita was also delicious!   When we first walked in, it was apparent that the restaurant staff was overwhelmed.  The manager was working the host stand and clearly wasn't skilled in the " Hi how are you?" department.  Our bartender was handling the bar, side bar and several tables.  As I mentioned before, she was extremely pleasant but, the restaurant was clearly short staffed and she had a lot to manage.  We ordered our appetizer which came out in decent time and was delivered by a manager who dropped it off and scurried away before I even knew what happened. This manager did not check to see if we had appetizer plates or roll ups.  We were ready to dig in when we noticed we had no plates, utensils, or napkins.   This can happen anywhere and is generally not a deal breaker for me.   It took a few minutes but we got the bartender's attention and asked for utensils etc.  Several minutes then went by while we stared at our food and it got cold before the manager with poor greeting skills showed up with one set of utensils.  My wife appropriately questioned where the other set of utensils was and the manager responded with a inappropriate and unprofessional comment basically indicating that we were lucky to get one set of utensils.   What?  Well it turns out that Mission Grill had run out of clean silverware.  Seriously!  We looked around and there were others eating with their fingers waiting on utensils.  This is totally absurd and should never happen at any establishment.  I guess the back of the house was as short staffed as the front.  This was a clear display of management issues which was made obvious during our first encounter at the host stand.  I wish I had turned around!    Additionally, the manager did not appear to put forth any effort in helping the servers and bartenders run food, clear plates or tend to any of the needs of the customers given how short staffed they where.  Oh wait... he did bring two people one set of utensils with a "take it or leave it" attitude.    The food was average.  The carnitas  had the most flavor but nothing to rave about and the shrimp appetizer was over cooked.  I'd go back and sit at the bar for margaritas but I highly doubt I will ever eat here again.

This is a little speakeasy inspired place behind the main sports bar called Danny's. It will be a little hard to find at first since there only seems to be a sign for Danny's out front. They're both owned by the same owner but one allows smoking inside and one does not. There's a stark difference in atmosphere and price point between the two places so your point of entry will affect your first impressions. 

Danny's allows smoking inside the bar so if you're not a fan avoid entering through the front and first side door to get to the Boar and Buffalo. Go to the very end of the building and there should be a separate entrance right at the end to get into the speakeasy. When we went they didn't have a sign up so we entered through Danny's instead.

Once inside the atmosphere is very elegant with swing music, barrel tables and chairs and a vast selection of bourbon. I got the Botanical gin cocktail and that was nice. I tried the sweet tea cocktail after that but it wasn't for me. Followed up with a gose. 

The hubs and company had some scotch and whiskey. The selection is very good so snobs will  have a field day. Just note to ask how much a glass is beforehand as there's a huge markup. The hubs got charged $38 for a neat glass of Kilchoman Sanaig which retails for $63 a bottle at Luekens. Ask before hand so you're not blind sided. 

We got the steak and mushroom fries which were freaking delicious for bar food. Try this one out. They also have plenty of other selections including charcuterie.

Atmosphere was good, service was ok (our waitress was a bit distracted, but we got what we needed) and food was yummy!  The Nashville Hot was certainly HOT  yummy but hot!  Our group had Nashville Hot Nachos, Nashville Hot Street Tacos, and Nashville Hot Chicken and Beignets and they were all good. We like heat, but the Nashville Hot was almost too much, so I added extra pickles to try and cool things down. The loaded lemonade an the Cowboy & Hippy brew were both a refreshing way to tame the heat.

This is a place that began back in the day with panache and promise, and now seems neglected and running on fumes. The table was sticky, the bathroom was dirty and out of paper towels, the service was awful (took nearly two hours to get served--they lost our order) and the food, with the exception of the pork dumplings, was bland and unremarkable.

Saudia seems nice but her employees are rude.. I'm from
Outta town so i order over the phone I ordered from her twice & both times her employees were rude they don't speck while making your food I asked them questions about the food just trying to make conversation while waiting & they just look at you.. I will not be going back.. It's like you walk in a store people greet you & you get a good vibe the vibe is bad in that place..The Seafood salad is good though.. Two thumbs up

This place is purely delicious! I was staying with my friend and her husband for a few days in New Orleans. My first night in the city, they took me to Acme Oyster House. This was on a Sunday night, so the place was packed! We waited in line for about 25 minutes but the food was surely worth it. I've never had oysters before and don't necessarily prefer them, however, my friend's husband told me I had had had to try the chargrilled oysters! And boy, was he right! They were grilled, buttery, garlicky, they definitely hit the spot after my flight. I also threw in some hot sauce to spice things up. 

I also ordered a shrimp po'boy. The actual po'boy tasted fine, I just wish they could have spiced it up a bit more without me having to add hot sauce to it. Our server was very polite and when I asked her certain questions about the menu, she was able to give me a clear, honest answer. 

You can see employees on the side chucking fresh oysters. Typical New Orleans vibe! Food tastes and smells great, and it's located right off of Bourbon Street! I would recommend this place to my friends visiting New Orleans! I've already told a bunch of people about the chargrilled oysters!

The selection of beers is quite impressive for a small place.  The decor is rustic and fun, nothing fancy.  They have a canning machine for the draft that I never saw before.

The dogs are fine, though I'm one of those people who doesn't understand expensive hot dogs.

My one disappointment was growler prices.  I wasn't paying attention and asked for a 64oz growler of a relatively pricey beer I guess.  Brought my own growler.  The total was $55.  Major sticker shock.

This is now called Bens paradise restaurant. The food is disgusting they changed everything. The potato salad was disgusting, the Greek salad dressing was sweet. I've lived in Safety Harbor for 30 years and I have never had such a bad experience at this restaurant. It used to be wonderful but ever since the new owner they have changed everything. They ruined my favorite entree, which was a Greek salad with their potato salad. I'm not sure how long it's been under new ownership but I urge you not to even try their food. Also the waitress was very rude and definitely did not want to be there.

We walked in and the place was empty and they told us it would be a 10 min wait just to be seated... the place was empty. Multiple workers looked at us multiple times and kept walking, and the very few people who were seated were trying to find waiters to take their orders while the managers were standing in the corner joking around for 20 mins.. terrible experience

The outdoor patio atmosphere is lovely. The food is ehhhh. I had the lobster roll and it wasn't that great. Heavy on the celery. My friend ordered the turkey w Brie cheese and cranberry mayo on a pressed panini. Sounded so good but the flavor was bland. Both were served w very thick and crunchy potato chips. Not a cheap place either, a little on the pricey side. You can try it for yourself but all in all it was just okay.

My most recent cleaning experience with Melody was fantastic! She had amazing attention to detail, went above and beyond, and was a joy to communicate with. I'm delighted with BLM and really appreciate their services and hard work!

We were in the area for business and then decided to spend the afternoon checking out a few breweries. We walked in and stood at an open spot at the bar for 15 min and no one acknowledged us. I then decided to make a comment about not being served. The patron next to me got a servers attention and told him we needed to be waited on. He said he couldn't and decided to grab a tray of fries and stand at the side of the bar having a French fry snack. There was a female bartender 2 patrons to my left not serving any beer and making conversation about Winston Churchill. At this point we decided our money must not be good enough for this place and decided to go elsewhere. If you can't make conversation and productively work a bar, bartending ain't for you!

Car has been scheduled for 7 AM regular maintenance a week prior for service. I bought the car in and called at 12:30 on my lunch break hoping it'd be done soon or would be done so already. They have given me nothing but the run around saying it'd be done at 3 PM, 5 PM, and before 5 PM. Why does no one know what is going on? Why is the front desk lady who moved my call to service answered me with an attitude? I have been polite, but now I see how they run things. Pain in my ass! And now have to get an uber to get my own car because their shuttle to transport me to my vehicle shuts down at 3 PM when I bought my car in at 7 AM! It was just an oil change and tire rotation. Now it's pure negligence when I scheduled for 7AM hoping I'd be first but they're just handpicking blindfolded cars they WANT to work on.

If I could give this management a half star, I would do this. Currently, I reside in a house that consists of three units that is managed by Bancroft & Associates. The last review that wrote about Bancroft & Associates, is the most bullsh** review I have ever read. They are by far NOT professional and they also lack in communication. The company tries to sugarcoat everything they will do when explaining how they will manage your property--but they do not. I moved into the house and it was an utter mess. They did not clean anything of the unit, even though they promised professional cleaning and pest control. After  frantically calling for help needed to the house, they finally fulfilled my wishes after two weeks (which is a very long time when you need things to work around the house or fix). We were promised air conditioning, it did not say it was a evap-cooler. You can only imagine how furious I was when I was moving in my things as well. Currently it is April, and now they are FINALLY turning on the evap cooler, even though it is about 90 degrees. Contacting the office is also ridiculous because the receptionist is always putting you on hold, and giving an attitude. To be honest, the entire management does not even give two sh*** about your business. If you are honestly trying to look for a reliable management company, I highly recommend you to look the other direction, and do not even use this service. This is a scam, and you will literally be wasting your money on a management company that will not provide for you or your tenant.

Went late at night. Got the pistachio baklava. When we tried it, the ice cream was really good and firm. When we started to dig in, the ice cream was really soft already, which we didn't like. 

Then we opted for the already made choco taco in the freezer. We got the vanilla. It was really really good, got a lot of ice cream. 

Cute place. Would go back for the choco taco.

I had to make a last minute massage appointment and so I went here and it was okay. It wasn't as great as the reviews said it was going to be. 

I did the 60 minute body massage and 30 minute reflexology. But it wasn't deep enough and I was having a lot of shoulder and calf pain. I walked out of the massage feeling most of the same way. I'm a little upset I spent so much money, thinking I was going to feel a lot better afterwards.

Holy Smokes this place is busy! We arrived at 530 on a Sat and we were told there was a 75 minute wait. We were joining friends so we went ahead and waited. It was about  95 minutes later that we were seated. 
Be sure to grab a menu while u wait because it's legit 15 pages! 

When we finally had a server,Hannah was very nice and  had drinks out and orders in quickly. Then we waited another 45 minutes for food. Our orders came out one at a time which is odd if you're in a group. My wife waited another 20 minutes for her meal and by that time my food was cold because I didn't want to eat while she waited. 

All 4 of us really enjoyed our food though... saving grace! All the chicken dishes were really tasty. 

Cheesecake was a must but it's a take home dessert for sure! It's soo heavy after a pretty large meal. 


If you plan to have a meal.. be sure you plan ahead and make this a 3 hour event! I think reservations are needed here sooooooo bad!

I purchased a car for on December 27, 2016 from this auto lot, just over two month later it died with service warnings flashing. I took it to get serviced and have the battery replaced- and being that it hadn't been driven more than 3,000 and it was within 90, they could help me out. They saw my vehicle very quickly and got me a response within a half hour- even though I told them what I needed. They charged me a service fee just for running an electrical test with results that I already knew. Then they wanted to charge me over $400 for a new battery and labor. As a female, I know it doesn't look like I know when I'm getting g ripped off, but I know enough to know a battery is around $120- which means you charge around $280 for labor and tax? I don't know, it may just be me, but if you sell a faulty car that needs to be repaired within that short of time don't you think that you would be able to help your customers out. I'll stick to my South Philly repair shops that value customer service. 





Here's my update 4 months laters. Brakes and rotors just went, so needed to buy new parts for that. Along with needing a new tires and alignment straightened out on it. I have but in more than $1000 just in repairs in under a year of owning it. Thank you Barbera's.

We stopped in here for a quick lunch, despite looking like a very large space it's not super big and there was a bit of a wait.

We started with the jalapeno-bacon rangoons because how could you not? They were as good as they sounded, which made me happy. I went with the Brussel Sprouts Pizza which was delicious and one of the only vegetables I had in Nashville. The pizza was good, but HUGE! I definitely recommend splitting it with a couple of people. 

This place is good, upscale bar food. It's not something I would eat regularly, but is a nice break from heavy Southern food.

Really wanted to check this out to see how their Chinese food is, sadly to say, disappointed. 1/2 roast duck is actually a 1/4. Overpriced. The Mongolian beef, spicy chicken, tofu/veggies were all 2/5 at best.

What a great decision for New Year's Eve! Decades in NOLA and I've never been here - I know, I know, sad. My husband has been trying to drag me here, but having my roots in Cajun cuisine I shy away from these types of restaurants. Why pay to eat out what I can cook at home, right?  BUT they were open for New Year's Eve and we were hungry. Great decision to make this my first visit! 

The atmosphere is fun and eclectic while still feeling authentic New Orleans. To help celebrate the New Year they had all the tables covered in confetti, and a hat for each person. The servers and kitchen staff were all in a great mood (how refreshing in comparison to places where people hate working holidays). We had champagne, along with a lovely meal. We started with the calamari app; cleaned very well, nicely seasoned and crispy. My husband went for the specialty Paneed Rabbit - more food than he anticipated as the meal is served with large portion of rabbit and sides. He was very happy with everything. I ordered the specialty Grilled Grouper with Imperial Crab, and my portion was quite smaller than his, but still tasty. The crab meat was absolutely delectable. Clean and refreshing, with seasoning that did not overpower the seafood. The fish was cooked perfectly as well, and the seasoning again did not overpower the seafood flavor. Overall a great meal and a great way to start 2019.

Observations from a early Friday evening first visit here:

 - $5 Valet - Really? Same price as Bern's....self park for a beach bar is way more appropriate. It's a insult to your customers.

 - The place has a good location, and a nice upscale indoor dining room.
 - The menu is extensive, to the point that it seems like they are trying too hard to please every possible taste. 
 - It is just above moderately priced.

Food: 
 - Had to send back the Oysters Rockefeller, they were cold...should have known, since they were delivered within 5 minutes of the order. They were replaced with a freshly made order that was amazing. HIGHLIGHT OF THE MEAL.
 - Coconut shrimp were a bit warmer, but had also been sitting a while as well, prior to delivery, still, they were good.
 - We ordered a Chicken Club and a Mahi Taco plate for dinner, and left the table for a few minutes to look at the wrestling memorabilia.  Our Drinks disappeared!  They were replaced by the server.

 - Chicken Club, pretty good - should have toasted the bread. Fish Tacos, good, but average.

 ''''French Fries''''' NOT GOOD - overly oily, not crispy, and NOT HOT.....They have a chance to have amazing fries.....Come on guys, BAD french fries at a beach bar? 

We saw a table of 6 pretty young girls arrive, get seated, and wait, and wait, and wait....and then walk out!!!  These were the PERFECT customers for a beach bar like this.....No service for them, and they left!............... It was not busy, the majority of the tables in the inside dining room were empty, and they were overstaffed.  Three to four employees "Hanging out" at the hostess stand at all times, just killing time and talking to each other.

Our server was SO NICE, and tried very hard. but it was obvious that it was out of her hands.

Even their signature drink, "Hogan's Punch" took 10 minutes to arrive, and I assume that the bartenders have this pre-mixed.

Somebody call Gordon Ramsey!!! A huge investment for Hulk, poorly run.

I may return for a cocktail, but honestly, the kitchen is hit or miss, and it showed on every course they delivered. It is OBVIOUSLY A MANAGEMENT PROBLEM.

Mr. Hogan, you are missing your chance at greatness in the restaurant business, because your management isn't paying attention, or simply lacks experience.

My second time here. Busy saturday night but got seated quite quickly. Drinks were delicious. Our waitress (trish?) was WONDERFUL. Very knowledgeable. Had oysters to start and both had burgers which were to die for. 10/10 recommend
Love this new courtyard ok it's a couple years now but new to me.  Across from the Aces ball field.

This is a quiet, cozily decorated place with authentic and delicious food. We enjoyed summer rolls to start and jasmine tea to start. My entree was delicious Drunken Noodles with chicken; the others at my table enjoyed their entrees as well...seafood pad Thai, tofu pad Thai, and cashew chicken. The service was good and prices fair. The only complaint was there was a dime-sized piece of shell from a mussel in the seafood pad Thai. Definitely going back.

The food is good, the service has gotten HORRIBLE. Now I'll qualify this: I have been coming here for years when Mama Maria was behind the counter, and always had a great experience. The brothers are great guys, but yesterday I had a waitress that was angry, pushy, and righteous. I'm not a difficult person, and I appreciate and respect everyone, but I also expect that in return.

I grew up in New Orleans, and at one time this was my favorite restaurant.  No more. On a recent visit to the city my husband and I stopped in for lunch. While the server was efficient in taking orders and delivering our food that's as far as it went. I ordered a cup of gumbo and a roast beef Po'boy. The seafood was nonexistent in the gumbo, there were two pathetically small shrimp and that was it. The roast beef for the po' boy looked a lot like deli roast beef, with a little gravy spooned over it.  My husband had the Creole platter; both the etouffee and jambalaya were bland.  There are too many restaurants in NOLA to settle for this, at best, mediocre meal.  The only reason I give it two stars are my nostalgia for the old Gumbo Shop and the setting.

Taco Tuesday is alive and well at Mex Authentic! Crispy tacos for cheap and they are really good! Great tostadas too! Their chip salsa has just the right amount of heat to gobble down the whole basket of crispy chips! The owners Gabriel and Tori are super friendly and make you feel right at home!

The Quisibella day spa is tucked into the corner unit of a strip mall on upper State Street ... which may, in and of itself, immediately raise a few red flags. Bearing in mind that this was not a trip to the Bacara, I entered the spa with an open mind. The spa has an Asian-fusion-meets-yoga-studio-meets-salon vibe. The foyer was cluttered, with overflowing nail stations on one side and a spa waiting lounge filled with tshotshke (and a clear view of the parking lot...) on the other. The locker rooms were old, giving them a dingy appearance. And things went down hill from there. Despite having a back to back facial and massage, the staff did not want to give me a robe (because getting fresh massage oil on my silk jumper or stretching it over my shoulders for the facial sounds ... great ...). After standing at the counter blinking in confusion for a few moments, I walked back to the waiting area, stunned. I must have given her a look to kill because a robe mysteriously appeared a few minutes later. That said, I have never, EVER been to a spa that didn't provide robes - especially for a back to back treatment. The manager cited high water prices as their reason, but - drought or not - that would be like a hotel saying they only wash sheets once a week because it costs too much. Which ... brings me to the massage tables. While the treatment rooms themselves were nicely appointed, there were visible stains on the massage table sheets. The technician mentioned their all-natural products stained the sheets but that they were, in fact, clean. Eeek! An easy solution here is either a. bleach or b. (if you don't want to use bleach in favor of said all-natural alternatives) get darker sheets that don't show discoloration. There is something remarkably uncomfortable about disrobing and sprawling out on sheets that look unclean. Despite the ambiance, the treatments were very enjoyable. My masseuse clearly knew what she was doing and my esthetician was thorough ... but I was so distracted by the venue that I had trouble relaxing and settling into the treatments and would be very hesitant to recommend this spa in the future.

It's a standard Hilton. Good check in with friendly front deskers who kept it moving. Room was a bit less shiny and newish than a lot of Hilton's it felt like. Rather small work out area, but high quality Precor Equipment. I made a boo-boo and made my reservation for one day less than I thought. Called and fixed it after extending online. 

The front desk agents booked me for my same room without any trouble. I would have stayed at the Doubletree next door since I like things a little more laid back, but DT had those cookies that are so good and I'm trying not to eat sweets.

Excellent experience!  Dr. Agin and his team provided highly customized care, and developed a program for me that improved my overall health and resulted in a 20-pound weight loss.  The diagnosis and treatment was thorough and involved lab work and a customized treatment plan. I feel great!

I wanted to love it here, but aside from the sushi, the yakitori, etc., is simply ok. However, a lively atmosphere and if you're lucky enough to make conversation with sushi master, he's got a great story to tell.

Food was good, however the service is very slow. Waited over ten minutes for the greeting/beverage, then food order was not complete. 1 cook, 1 server and 1 host to staff restaurant for a breakfast on a holiday day - not the smartest.

I bought a mattress while back paid over $2000.00 and was not comfortable, laying on it, feel that we going to fall off, approach Leon's store and was told to approach the manufacturer, when I did They wanted me to mesure the bed across and it's level and What I sent him was fine, I sent him pictures a person seating on the side how the mattress  was sagging so low, but for him it was normal but for me it was not normal when laying on the bed and have to feeling to roll off, He kept on asking all stupid questions to proof that nothing was wrong with I still have the mattress I don't think I will trust a word they say when purchasing something, it's all rosy until something is wrong then the nightmare start

Not impressed with this over priced restaurant.  The kids meals were not good.  The kung pao stir fry sauce was pretty bland.  The egg drop soup was  about flavor-less.  The pad thai tasted close to what I would imagine dog food tastes like.  The only redeeming quality was the server was a nice guy.  All in all I will never go back to this restaurant if I get to choose.

Been again this year and the staff are friendly, the Ice cream is superb!  Seats outside are a welcome relief from the heat of the beach. I had a milk shake and that was sublime. Definitely go to this place for a quality product.
Great down home cooking. The food was very well prepared. Delicious. The smoke was a little strong inside so we had to eat outside. Great service.

Not sure what has happened to Moxie's!? We walked in and only 5 tables were seated. Took 20 minutes to get out drinks. Ordered after 30 minutes. Food was terrible. Cold fries, wilted salad, flavorless pasta and overdone fisher and chips. Used to be so good. Waitress was nice, but new.

This place was hands down the best Chinese food I've had anywhere in this region. The food was fresh tasting, not greasy at all, with robust flavor. I've never had General Tso's as delicious as this one, the chicken was such a better quality than anywhere else. We've since moved and have been on the search for a place that can come in as a close second, but still no place has even come close. Also, the staff were always friendly and professional. Miss this place so much.

Went there for the Irish-italian parade. Interesting crowd- heavy tattoos; both men and women.   Had a shrimp wrap which was pretty good. My 12- year old son had their burger which was quite good. We tried to convince them that he was a 142 year-old leprechaun (it was St Patrixk's Day) not a 12 year-old boy but they weren't buying it.  The green beer was PBR and $2 ($3.50 inside, $2 outside, that kinda pissed us off. )  Bartenders were friendly but clearly catered to the regulars.

April, our waitress, was awesome!  She took our party  of 10 on Sunday morning Father's Day and she handled us like a pro!  Great food and we never got rushed. Definitely recommend this place for an old fashioned hearty breakfast.

My expectations were high because I read they have some of the best margaritas and the margaritas weren't even that great. The food could be better. Don't bother ordering soup, it's not worth it. I was expecting a more authentic Mexican experience with fresh, flavorful and appealing looking dishes and this place fell short. Even the chips and salsa were bland. 

On a positive note, the patio atmosphere is nice and I do appreciate when a restaurant makes and effort to offer a gluten-free menu.

We went for breakfast because I heard it was good and of the good reviews. Unfortunately it was a disappointment.
I ordered a waffle and a piece of scrapple. The scrapple was cooked nice but the flavor was mediocre at best. The waffle... again it looked to be cooked very nice, loaded it up with butter and syrup, I shit you not it was the blandest thing ever!
I couldn't taste the butter, syrup, or the waffle.
I thought maybe my taste buds were messed up but nah I could taste the coffee just fine. 
My buddy got an omelette with a hair in it but said otherwise that and the potatoes were good.
Server was nice but we will not be back.
Also, the place smelled a little weird. Maybe just from starting the gas burners first thing but damn vent that crap.

My family and I will never return to this place ever again. How can you enjoy a meal when a establishment such as Shepard's has untrustworthy employees and management? Our phones were stolen directly from our table. Please be careful and watch your personal belongings!! The security department is also a fraud!! They claim their cameras didn't have a clear view of our table from were our phones were taken;  in addition, servers and a bus boy clean our table literally two minutes after we walked away. Their head of security really needs to be re-train. Management never came to assist us at all that night. From what the police officer stated. "At least 5 other phones were stolen as well that day". Whats really funny is that during the investigation two different servers pretty much were caught providing false statements.. This restaurant is very immune to thefts and not executing preventative actions to re-assure customers a place to enjoy their meal and not have their belongings stolen repeatedly!!!

I love max brenner in NYC, so I figured in Philly it would be the same. It was a mistake...food was ok, I had the flatbread pizza and it was tasty. However, we ordered the fondue for 2 as dessert, and first, after waiting for almost 40 min, the waitress brings the chocolate without the water bath, just the small pot which I could barely reach (she put the fire for toasting the marshmallows right in front) I asked her about the water bath and she just said "we are not doing it today, we are too busy". If the chocolate doesnt have the water bath it gets hard very quickly, so I asked her to set it up anyways.. She gave me a very, very annoyed look and literally, it took her 2 seconds to set it up. Not cool. Besides that, in the pot there was really a smudge of chocolate, we could barely dip anything in it. If you are in a place where the specialty is the chocolate fondue, and they are charging you $25 for it, on valentine's day, it better be good. This one was really dissapointing, and adding up the bad service, its a shame.

This was our first time to this Red Lobster.  We walked in on a Sunday around noon.  There was no wait at all  Not sure how long it has been there, but it looked brand new, and neat as a pin.  Our server, Kendra, could not have been nicer.  She got our drinks right away, and took our orders after making sure we knew what was what on the menu.  I ordered the Bar Harbor Lobster and Shrimp Trio, which is a grilled main lobster tail, garlic grilled shrimp, and a creamy parmesan lobster shrimp bake.  This came with broccoli and red potatoes, and also a dinner salad for around $25.  My hubby had the Surf & Turf, a rock lobster tail and sirloin, with broccoli and red potatoes, and dinner salad, for around $26.  The food came quickly, and was nice and hot.  His sirloin was cooked to perfection, and I had no complaints about any of my dishes.  The broccoli tasted really fresh, no old vegetable taste here.  My grilled garlic shrimp were just right on the garlic - hate it when garlic overpowers whatever you are eating.  Those cheese biscuits are yum, huh?  We were too full for dessert, but saw some going by, and they looked yummy.  Hey, you can pay $25-$30 just for a lobster tail alone, so I think the value here is very good.  The prices are not unreasonable for the amount of good food you are getting.

This is a great restaurant find! We were in to check out Villanova and Mary the owner of this restaurant, actually called me to improve our reservation time. Who does that? Great service, highly personal and simply outstanding food. Chicken Picata, Ribeye and Pasta all some of the best we've had. Thanks for a wonderful meal and adding to our Villanova experience.

I really wanted to try the blackened grouper Reuben due to it being on tv as the best sandwich in America. I'll admit the sandwich was tasty but not the best sandwich I've ever had. I would recommend it to somehow craving a good fish sandwich. I also want to mention there was a cover to sit outside with the live music. A bit rediculous in my opinion but hey to each their own.

In a rush for lunch and stopped in for something quick. The service was good and enjoyed the atmosphere. My food arrived quickly but wasn't impressed with the lunch portion. I'm sorry to say the portions were small and the quality was just average. If I lived closer I may have given it a second try.
Batch. More like Scratch it off your list. Donuts didn't taste fresh. Dry, dense and cold. Coffee was old. Whiskey was good though. Pricier than other places.
Second (and only times) in where wait is an hour.  Only two people working on a Saturday?  Grr.

Was excited to try Root. I won't be back again. There really wasn't any redeeming qualities. The greeter seemed bothered that we came in. It was over 10 minutes before our waiter came. Another few minutes just to get a glass of water. Another 10 before he came back to take our order. While we were eating our appetizers our food came out, they saw that we were eating still and turned around and put it back in the kitchen. We were done with our apps almost 10 more minutes before they brought out our food. They may have microwaved it to heat it back up because that chicken was the rubbery and dry. To close, they gave us a check for $100 more than what it should have been.(we eventually got the correct bill) I hate to write a bad review but we went there because of reading the great reviews about it and I hope my friends don't waste there time and money.

First off let me say the service was amazing and the atmosphere was very unique (in a good way).  The interior is of modern industrial and the food choices look amazing.  

Only the two stars because the food really lacked.  I am Tennessee and Latin so I ordered the Cubano my favorite sandwich in any establishment and with that being said, this one lacked.  I ordered the potato salad as my side and though it looked like it was full of flavor but it was bland ):  I've had way better from a grocery store back home. The cubano was just full of cheese and that's all you could taste.  The lemonade was on point though and you can tell that it's homemade.  Sorry guys.  I really wanted to like this place ):
I tried this out late on a weeknight, I can see what they are going for but in the end I felt it was just OK.

Okay so I originally gave this a good review, but I've become more inclined to update reviews since places in the city can change at the drop of a hat without explanation- this being one of them.  

For one, regardless of how much you like any food item here, it's never going to be worth the price and they seem to value quantity over quality (the sheer enormity of my first plate was probably why I gave it a higher review before).  I've paid the bill here and spent the same amount as I would have at the Industry (and at the Industry I would've gotten a professional ambience and booze with my meal).  Everything here is just too hit or miss to be worth it and the laissez-faire approach leaves little to be desired.

Last time we visited, we luckily went with cash.  Even though the option of paying with a card has now been added, the machine is always conveniently broken. 

We ordered off the new Fall menu and opted for the fish and chips and the chicken pot pie.  My fish and chips, though once again insanely large in quantity (seriously, the whole fish must have been cooked and thrown on the mountain of fries), was bland and the batter was wayyyyy too thick and mealy.  It felt like I was eating a non-sweet funnel cake wrapped around a flavorless fish.  Plus, no tartar sauce or aioli for dipping- just vinegar.  I wasn't even offered the tartar sauce, like some other people mentioned in their reviews.  Plus, this time my mountain of too many fries was overcooked and unenjoyable.  I boxed 3/4 of my fish cause it was so big and I didn't even touch the leftovers.  I think I boxed it out of politeness.

The chicken pot pie we were told would take up to 25-30 minutes since it's scratch-made.  We weren't in a hurry so we didn't mind, but the finished product was not even worth the price nor the 30 minute wait when it was all said and done.  It wasn't even an actual chicken pot pie by definition since there was no bottom or sides to complete the full pie crust- just chicken and veggies with a thin crispy layer of crust on top.  Sure, it was homemade, but it was lacking in flavor and spice and that gooey/bready wonderfulness that usually comes with eating a chicken pot pie.  Completely underwhelming.

The place was full of flies in the summer which I expected.  We just went this past week when it was mild fall weather and the place was STILL full of gnats and files.  I know they come from the outside and they don't have screens for the open windows for whatever reason but it's still an abnormal amount and they all flock to the lights above our food.  It was so bad even the waitress apologized.  Maybe put in some screens, I don't know.

They do try and make you feel like you're an old friend here with the over-casual attitude.  I get it, you want the customers to feel welcomed and keep coming back.  But it becomes kind of awkward and unprofessional when you cross the line from staff to old chum who jokes around and has to comment on everything and completely loses any sort of professional demeanor.  I know this isn't fine dining...and it doesn't need to be a stuffy atmosphere...but the laidback/too cool for school act gets old fast.  I just want to get in, eat, and get out.

After 3 times here, we've decided we're probably not coming back.  If it was super cheap with neighborhood diner prices, maybe it would be worth the trip.  But it's not.  Stick to the many other bar/restaurants nearby.

I booked pedicures for our group to celebrate after finals. Called and booked it. They even sent me an email confirmation and my friend a phone confirmation. We get there and suddenly there is no appointment at all, whatsoever, and no openings for the rest of the day. Several of my friends stayed after their finals just for this. All they did was give me a puzzled look and comment to each other how peculiar this was.

The thing is, I am a patient and understanding person. It takes a lot for me to only give one star; it has to really be deserved. I couldn't stand the incompetence, and I'm done giving the Aveda Institute any more chances.

Drove all the way over there to spend a good amount of money, walk up to the door and they are requiring customers to wear mask! 8-21. Needless to say I turned around and went somewhere else to spend my money. Im not supporting a place that is requiring it.

I absolutely love thai food and have been here with friends before. Their food has been decent as far as I can remember. However, recently, my boyfriend and I went in with a groupon and they were absolutely horrendous. We ordered the duck avocado special and ordered a side of rice because it didn't come with rice. When we received our dish, the avocado was not ripe and tasted like cardboard. They took the dish back and said all their avocados were like that. So we ordered another dish that came with rice. Not only did they NOT apologize, but they refused to comp us for the side of rice we only ordered because of the duck avocado dish that we couldn't even eat. 

We'll never eat at this location again. The point of a groupon is to bring in service so we can go back again...they absolutely failed at that.

I did not know much about this place before we visited a few days ago, but I was pleasantly surprised. We had the duck empanadas as an appetizer and they were delicious. Highly recommended! My husband had the hickory smoked rotisserie chicken and he loved it. I had the sweet chili glaze rotisserie chicken. It was full of flavor and so good! We'll definitely be back!

Went here on a Sunday and ordered at 1:20. Wait staff did their job. We ordered 2 apps, a bowl of chili, 10 wings, and a kids' sliders. We did not have our food at 2:20 so we asked for it to go. Even then  it was 2:35 before we had out food to go. The manager came by with a half hearted apology and a coupon. Coupons are a cowardly compensation. Useless for an out of town person (and this no impetus to improve) and useless for an in-town person who doesn't plan on coming back. Poor kitchen work, poor management, and poor response. The food was fine by the time we got it and ate it in the car.

Lots of things for the kids to do and try but for the price the parents pay - the parents deserve better accommodations while the kids play. Unimpressed with the metal picnic tables, metal tables and the most uncomfortable metal chairs. Bring ear plugs - theres music blasting, TVs on top of that and then screaming kids on top of that.

I recently stumbled on this place and I'm glad I did, sadly I feel not too many people know about it, mainly because of the other two very famous and hyped up  Mexican restaurants down the street. I usually don't write a good review until I've actually tried the food more than 1 time. At this point I've eaten there 5 times and feel confident about my recommendation. The Fajitas were delicious, the carne asada plate was good,the breakfast burrito was amazing!! the carne asada tacos are yummy, the shrimp cocktail was good as well I've tried all the salsas and they are all good and authentic . The place it self is very clean, the service can be a little slow sometimes apparently  but I don't mind it since I'm on Latin time anyway so I suggest ordering a beer and chilling out. I highly recommend giving this place a shot for all the skeptics out there !

I've never had a bad experience here.  Wait staff and bartenders are very attentive and always make you their priority.  Kelly is the best if she is bartending and you are sitting at the bar!  Ken is also great at the bar.  My favorites on the menu include (I don't eat meat so only listing fish and veggie options) mushroom soup, carrot and ginger soup, crab cakes, calamari salad, strawberry salad, mac and cheese, oysters, you cant go wrong with any of the pizzas.  For drinks, they have great wines by the glass.  I personally love the Bee Sting and the red sangria.

If I lived closer this would be "home base" for me. In spite of the distance I've been here twice in the past 2 months. First for a pre-dinner beer and second for the burgers I've seen folks rave about.

First, the service at the bar was quick. It was a Friday night and pretty busy but we had no issue getting the bartender's attention to get a drink or to close out in a rush when we were advised our table at a place around the corner was ready. The best part was the sticker shock... in a good way. A rum and diet + a beer = $9. My boyfriend actually had me double check that it was right! 

When we returned it was after an event at Bok where we needed a little bite between brunch and dinner. I had heard the burgers were great so we split one. They are as close to what you would make at home as I've ever seen out. The meat was fresh and the burger was cooked to a perfect medium. The size was a little small, but so was the price tag. 

Overall great product and service at a solid price. A great value!
In town for work for a week. Stopped by this place twice, and even wanted to go a third time! Their subs and boba are a perfect pair!

The boat ride was okay. But the dinner really sucked. I can't see spending that much money on a bad buffet. I did appreciate that the dinner time cruise allowed me to take day pictures and sunset pictures so for that reason I recommend the evening cruise. Just don't pay for the dinner.

We were weekending in Philadelphia and found this on Yelp. We got there for brunch on a Sunday around 9:50, and while it was crowded, we were still seated immediately (there was a line out the door when we left). Menu has everything from basic breakfasts to brunch specials (sweet potato hash, huevos rancheros). We opted for basic - plenty of scrambled eggs and bacon. Potatoes were crispy but needed salt and pepper. Coffee was lovely and the pastry we took to go was very good.

I gave it 4 stars instead if 5 only because we were 2 people and there was no space between us and another party of 2- it was like we were sitting  at a table for 4. I like a little more privacy.

We were in a little bit of a hurry, and did have to flag down the waiter for the check, but I also got the impression that there was no rush to get people out the door. All in all , a very good experience and we will return.

Portobello sandwich was delish the aoli sauce was great  5/5 $14

Summer raw cheese cake 4/5 more of a mouse but I still enjoyed it- not overly sweet which is perfect $10


Coleslaw 4/5 good - creamy 


Tomato soup - 1/5 WAAAYY to salty! I can't even eat it :( maybe I got a bad batch? Because this is one of there signature dishes I don't understand how it could be so salty....I don't see anyone else complaining so I'm going to assume I got a bad batch or a overly salty part that wasn't mixed properly? 

Customer service 4/5

Speed 3/5

The kids enjoyed the fish tacos and I enjoyed seafood dinner. We were able to enjoy the sunshine state's weather -- by sitting on the patio. 

The minus 2 stars were based upon the waiter who was assigned to us - he was M.I.A. (missing in action) majority of the time until it was time for us to pay. We had to flag down other wait staff to provide us service such as condiments, napkins, refills on the beverages, and the check. 

The tip reflected the service received (or the lack thereof) from that waiter... "pay attention to your customer's and don't forget you are receiving a gratuity based upon your service not the yummy food". 

PS: Call ahead to see if they are busy - specifically on the weekends.

I was surprised to see a negative review of Cambridge School. After having my son at another daycare in the area (that was so-so), we transferred to Cambridge. He's now 3.5 and has been there since he was 15 months. We couldn't be happier! The director and teachers are very thoughtful. There is a turnover of teaching assistants. Our assistant just left, and a new one is supposed to start. But he's had consistent teachers for each schoolyear. Classrooms are very clean, and the outdoor play area is great.
Good service and interesting decor, but not the greatest food. Got the veggie burger with tator tots, and the husband got nachos, both of which were incredibly underwhelming in flavor.

The self checkout person next to the pharmacy is depressed and takes out their hate for living on us Kroger customers.  I would love if everyone in today world could just get along.  Although with this person it is absolutely impossible.  I get that the self checkout machines are a piece of crap.  I get you hate fixing all the stupid simple crap on the machines.  I get you hate living and love taking out your hate on us customers.  I get that you love it and you get lots of pleasure when you bring others down to your level of depression.  I get it.  But please leave us out of your stupid petty everyday drama.

I seriously wish I lived in the area because I would visit the smoothie bar about every day. The Smoothie Bar is located inside of Fit Flavors, so when you're looking for the sign take note. When you arrive to the smoothie bar there is a menu with lots of options, and all of the macros/calories are listed. They use 1st Phorm protein powder, which if you are not a protein powder guru, I'll let you know that it is great tasting and amazing quality. Love that company and all of their products. 

I ordered a 24 oz peanut butter cup smoothie, which was only about 340 calories. When you drink this smoothie you'll be shocked on how it is only 340 calories. The taste is phenomenal and not too sweet either. They also have thick straws so that you're not wasting too much time trying to suck the smoothie up. You can tell that these are quality smoothies that are not pumped with added sugars and unnecessary additives. You'd feel confident in having one for a meal or a post workout with zero guilt.

I can't wait to come back tomorrow and the rest of my time visiting the area to try some more smoothies!
I don't know why this place is so popular.  The owners are great, but the po boys are not good by any standards.

Decided to try a new place for my weekend breakfast and saw that this place was popular on Yelp. Upon arriving it was extremely busy which is a good sign and I was hopeful that the meal would live up to the hype. The meal in itself took a long time to come out but I understand because of the amount of people that were there. My meal however was not what I ordered and because it took so long I didn't want to send it back seeing as I was starving by this point. I am honestly not even sure the name of the food they brought to me but it wasn't enjoyable at all so I can't give the food a fair assessment.
I brought my dog here for grooming and the shops dog peed on me! All I was offered is a paper towel! I had to ask for a disinfectant wipe for my shoes. I doubt I'll go back

Pathetic absolutely pathetic would be an understatement for this piece of trash of a restaurant. Cooks and servers cursing while they're making food. Yelling screaming going on but the worst part is we didn't get anything that we ordered. Absolutely ridiculous this restaurant should be closed down!!!

This is my third time  I've been to Boca. 1st time I went for brunch but I wasn't impressed, I didn't get to order what I had anticipated getting which was the pork breakfast flatbread, because they were not  serving it that day of the weekend. I got the hangover sandwich and it was ok, nothing mind blowing especially for the price. I wanted to like this place so badly, because of the farm to table concept, So I decided to go back for dinner. Went back for dinner and had a better experience. The Rockefeller oysters were amazing and the truffle Parmesan fries were perfectly crisp and not overly soggy and oily. I ordered the sweet potato gnocchi and basically licked my plate clean of all the sauce. It was delicious! I love sweet potato, so this dish was perfect for me. 

I went back this past Friday  with my boyfriend and 2 other friends and I had a mediocre experience. The swine flatbread was light and great appetizer that was tasty but not too heavy. I also ordered the shrimp and grits. The dish was almost cold when I received it, I did not want to send it back as I feared heating it more would overcook the shrimp. That being said, the last time I had shrimp and grits at Roux, they were bursting with flavor and this dish was not. My boyfriend ordered the short rib dish and it was very disappointing. We had looked  at Yelp pics and saw this dish in a photo and when the dish arrived, we were disappointed that the pasta was plain crinkly style pasta instead of large ravioli style pasta. The dish description said there was spinach but there was barely a hint. The goat cheese helped a lil with flavor but it needed a dap more to spread all throughout the dish. The short rib was sooo bland. That being said when we reheated our leftovers the next day we added  some spice and seasoning and the dish was so much tastier.  My friends ordered the tuna appetizer and the staff chicken meal with fried green tomatoes. Chicken was very simple, but the green tomatoes were to die for. I have tried the fried green tomato app and I would recommend that. We ordered desert and opted  for coffee and the bread pudding. I know coffee isn't a speciality here but my boyfriend and I always get coffee with dessert at restaurants. Both were terrible. I had to send these back, this time. The blueberry banana bread pudding was more of a cornbread in a skillet. It was very dry and not how i was accustomed to eating banana bread pudding desserts. The ice cream however was good. The coffee tasted like bad office coffee. Tasted old and watery, and not hot at all! The server offered to remove both items. The service was ok as well. It seems that At Boca you have to be sure to order the right things and just go on a good day. There isn't consistency.

Called this Place and the guy on the phone have 0 patience to answer questions and help with inquiries. 

I ended up not ordering. If this is how they take care of the public I don't care about their food. 

Very upset.

So the food was really good, and the service was good but it wasn't great. The portions were ginormous. You could definitely split your entree if you're getting desert or appetizers.  I had the fish sandwich and it was so big I gave half to my husband. 
I loved the ambience, it was very dark and they had live music. I just wasn't blown away by the service but it could be because it was a Saturday night during peak dinner hours and they were slammed. Definitely check it out:)

Second time the service was incredible. Hope I get this sever when we go back!
Tried this place for the first time and I was very pleased. I love soul food and this spot is very satisfying.

Disappointed is the word for this past Saturday visit to Improv. No, I'm not talking about the comedians, they were fine, even thought we (4) were not a big fan of Pablo Francisco's humor. We got there about 15 minutes before the show started and were sat at a corner table - we wanted to order drinks and food right away, but nobody showed up for 30-40 minutes after we had sat. I asked 3 different waitresses to take our order and none of them did - finally our waitress showed up to take our order and brought our drinks. The food is mediocre and drinks ok. We had a hard time paying attention to the beginning of the show because the waitress was trying to take our order and understand what we wanted. At the end, she gave us the check and push towards me to pay so she could get her 15% tip (required). Next time, I'll probably try another comedy club with a better service that can at least bring us drinks when we want.

I ate here for the first time today and it was awesomeness.  The manager was bustling and we got everything super fast.  At first we just walked in and looked around like we were in a daze- because we had never been there before.  They asked us MULTIPLE times where would like to sit and what do we want to order.  All of the staff was very active, the place was nice & clean.  Our food was served hot & fresh.  But most importantly- the bathroom was so clean you could eat off of the floor!!! Simply amazing experience!!

We were very excited about visiting here for dinner but left unimpressed. Food was pretty good, but service was an issue. The restaurant wasn't even halfway full and it took 15 minutes to order drinks (and no refills) and an hour to get our food. There are lots of options for ranch-lovers but we were never given a list of all the ranch choices. Our waitress just recommended her favorites.

While in Old City, I came in with a group of friends. We had heard a decent amount of hype about this place and it's nitrogen ice cream. We came in to a cute cafe with decent ambiance on a rainy night. There were a variety of dessert options including nitrogen ice cream, waffles, frozen yogurt, smoothies, and more.

The girl working behind the counter was sweet and hard-working. She was pregnant and pretty much running the store alone. My friends had chose their nitrogen ice cream flavors which are made fresh with cream and infused with nitrogen. I went for some frozen yogurt.

Overall the flavor variety for frozen yogurt was pretty lacking. The nitrogen ice cream was cool, being that it was made on the spot, but the taste was nothing extraordinary. 

This place was cute for a visit, but I don't know if I'd come back again.

David's Bridal is always a hit or miss for me. I've been there on multiple occasions: once to try on different styles of dresses, with no intention of purchasing a wedding dress for myself (Let's be honest, their wedding dresses look cheap), once to try on bridesmaid dresses for myself for my sisters wedding (I went with Vera Wang, can't go wrong with Wang!), and on other occasions for flower girl dresses, or to grab their Men's Warehouse tuxedo coupon, lol. The service is usually not great, except for the time when I was paired with a bridal consultant who was in-training... Which to me means, their training likely needs to emphasize quality customer service, since once the employees finish training, they seem to have unnecessary attitudes. Overall, I think David's Bridal is A-OK, and serves it's purpose for me.

This place has good food. I have not been disappointed with the food or the drinks. It is Vietnamese-inspired and they do it well. Love the pork belly bowl, the veggie bowl, pho and of course those killer wings. I hate they I never make happy hour. 

Folks who complain that it is not authentic Vietnamese, I will see you at the authentic spots too. Love the food here!

Tre fratelli USED to be a great place from pizza to pasta.  I'm not sure how many times it has changed ownership over the years, but the last couple of times we've ordered take out the food was not very good at all.  My sister has gluetin allergies and she ordered a gluetin free ham  pizza; it was topped with lunchmeat.  Tonight I ordered penne with the Tre Fratelli sauce, which is ham, peas, and mushrooms in the vodka sauce.  It too was sliced lunch meat and not even edible.  Since when did it become OK to substitute lunchmeat for what should be diced real ham?  I wonder how many of their other meat items are substandard.  Don't think I'll be finding out anytime soon.

The regular pizzas however are still pretty good.   That is the only reason I am even giving it  two (2) stars.

Food could be great? Service could be wonderful? But I wouldn't know! Made a reservation at 3pm in the afternoon. Left my name, number and other standard info but when we got there they were closed for maintenance. Did I get a call? NOPE! And they denied that I even called to make a reservation. Coooooooool!

Go in knowing exactly what you want from your dogs boarding experience, and leave confident that they FULLY understand, and will follow through. Otherwise, assume your dog will sit in his kennel all day, if you haven't taken full advantage of their "a la carte" menu that is probably also misleading.

The BEST frosting I've ever had other than my mom's!!  I had the chocolate cupcake with vanilla frosting.  Service was very nice - we went on a Sunday afternoon and it wasn't at all crowded.  Wish I could have tried the Italian hot chocolate everyone raves about, but I was so satisfied after the cupcake!

The cake was chocolate - moist and flavorful.  The frosting was perfect.  Made of granulated sugar but mixed very well so you didn't get the crunchy granulated taste.  Topped with sugar crystals, it brought me back to my childhood.  I would make the three hour trek to Philly just to get another one!

The cafe was clean, bright, pleasant environment.  Staff very nice and accommodating.  The chocolate truffles and confections beautifully presented.  Great experience.

Got physical mail from Matteo advertising free delivery and multiple coupons for $4, $5 ( and free tiramisu). Called and they told me they don't deliver so the mail we got was 100% false. Still ordered, cost us $15 more. Regardless of how good the food is or isn't not a smart way to conduct business for a new company. Bold move.

Fast, responsive, smart! I'm a business owner in Carmel Indiana. They have saved us many times when we have been in a technical bind. They have saved us lots of time and money because of their expertise in getting us back in working action. uBreakiFix goes above and beyond to help us out!

If you're looking to feed a rabbit or small bird. Then this is the place for you. Here's a picture of the bowl I was given today for my order. Service was also be discourteous. Won't be back. Thanks for the $10 bird food.

Great place to spend several hours sharing food w/friends.  Just make sure you all order the same Level on their All-U-Can Eat menu.  All their sushi is made fresh, so it takes a while to make & arrive at your table.  Just order 3-4 items at a time, then order more immediately when those come.

The place gets crowded, so make a reservation, and go early.  You will be there a few hours anyway.

My family and I are multi-state restaurant connoisseurs so to speak! We enjoy seeing if the value and quality of a particular food chain remains consistent from one state to another. Having been introduced to Five Guys in California and severely enjoying it there, we thought while we're here in the St. Louis area that would definitely eat at Five Guys here. Not only where we pleasantly and happily satisfied, but the staff kept a smile on our faces the entire time we waited for our food. They greeted every customer that came in the door, they answered any questions that we had while we were standing at the counter, and they made sure to get our food ready quickly. I definitely recommend this location because you will be satisfied both with the food that you eat as well as the service. Cheers!

I remember I used to crave Toscana's back when it was actually amazing (about 2-3 years ago). We used to come here on special occasions- birthdays and anniversaries, etc. so we decided to come here for my moms birthday and we were so excited until .... 

The food that we ordered was SUPER bland and honestly I could make better food at home. I wouldn't write a highly critiqued review if this was maybe my first time trying it but I know the potential of this restaraunt and clearly the quality has gone down which is SO sad to say ESPECIALLY FOR A SPECIAL OCCASION. I feel really sad for my mom because it was her special day :/ I used to tell my friends and family that everyone should try it but I no longer hold that opinion. 

As for the service we ordered some waters and my poor Dad didn't get a water. The waitress gave us all water except for him. She then proceeded to go to another table and give them their water -___- why not give us all of ours and then if the other table needs more water then get more ???? My dad was so sad too :/ ugh. I'm really disappointed. To make it worse she didn't even apologize or acknowledge that after we asked to get another cup -____- My mom gave her a big tip because she's a really kind person despite the horrible service 


I finished my duck and I was still hungry. The bread - why can't they heat it up and put it in a toaster or make it warm? The butter was so bad and artificial - not what it used to taste like. The pasta - was like canned pasta I can buy for 4$. 

All in all I would not come her ever again. Too expensive and definitely NOT worth your money for really low quality food. I came for the amazing Italian food that they used to have but that no longer stands. Believe it from someone who tasted their food when it was once amazing

The good: 

CLEAN.  I'm going to say it again: CLEAN.  I know there's a certain charm to the filthy, gritty NOLA venues, but it was SO nice to find somewhere that I didn't feel the need to get a tetanus shot after.  It was brand new when we visited -- signs talked about how they were open while they were finishing construction.  

Music.  The music was good.  I'm no jazz/blues expert, but I was entertained.  

Food/Beer.  We had the Oysters Rockefeller.  Delicious.  And they have a much larger beer selection than most of the other places we tried.  I'm from Chicago, so the prices didn't cause any alarm (as I've read in some other posts.)  

The bad:

They were SERIOUSLY disorganized.  We placed our food order and waited... and waited... and waited... and finally saw the bartender we ordered from frantically put it in about 30 minutes after she realized she never had.  Then no one went and got our food after it was ready.  It took about 45 minutes to get oysters.  Not a great start.  

Also, we heard one couple stop to tell a staff member they were leaving because they had never gotten served.  I believe it.

Lastly, a manager (?? it was hard to know who anyone was) loudly told an employee to "stop standing around" right in front of guests.  

Overall, 3 stars.  I don't live there, but I'd go back.  I want to believe this is a function of the new open more so than a flaw in the business model.

If you're hungry and want some down home food, Penrose is the diner for you.  I had roasted turkey smothered in gravy on top of Apple stuffing.  The mashed potatoes were average but the gravy covered up some of their shortcomings.  I will say the turkey was good and not at all dry.  The menu will take you 15 minutes to get through...so I guess I will have to stop by on a future trip to Philly.

I've been coming to Sake Uptown for years now I'm and off, but I've pretty much only gone during happy hour time. I believe that the nice Uptown/Magazine St. location and reasonable happy hour prices have made this sort of an easy option for post-work eats, but I've honestly never had awesome food nor experiences here. Not bad, but not particularly good either lol. The sushi is just ok as far as quality, and the drinks are never good nor strong, to me. The kitchen food comes out pretty fast but the sushi takes FOREVER; you will have gotten your spring rolls or gyoza, eaten them in 3 minutes (because the quantity of the appetizers and kitchen food isn't awesome) and be waiting 20 minutes and still not have your sushi or rolls. Also, the staff at Sake, to me, has never been welcoming nor friendly...I feel like everyone is always just looking at me when I enter, as in "what are you doing here". Well, I'm there to patronize your establishment. So, with all this said, I have significantly cut back on my visits to Sake; I pretty much only go if I'm with someone else who wants to go.

Have ordered from here several times. 
Wings take a while and are a bit small but that's not an issue. 
What is not good is the cashier taking phone off hook and leaving it. Then hanging it up without helping customer that's called in. Been in several times and witnessed their lack of giving a crap to other customers who have tried to call in orders. Even had comments made about said customers to us from cashiers. If I owned this store I'd be really unhappy with the amount of money these employees are allowing to slip away. * I called to order and was placed on hold. Drove there (8 minute drive not an issue) and walked thru door ( still on hold) and cashier hung phone up that was laying on counter. I said " That was me that you just hung up on. " They were not busy. She walked from back where she was just standing. Didn't even apologize so we left. Will visit the Clearwater store since we've never seen this behavior at that store. Again if I owned this business I'd be fixing that problem immediately.

I was hoping for authentic Mexican when I came in. Decor is great, choice of tequilas is pretty good. As soon as I looked at the menu I knew this place was owned by a gringo. I got a casa Rita with Don Julio, unfortunately the premium tequila didn't mask the awful sour mix. The menu claims it is fresh lime juice, I don't believe that for a second. After the disappointment of the margarita, I couldn't bring myself to order any American food poorly masquerading as Mexican.

Awesome spicy ramen and rolls. Great experience and an awesome environment.  Everyone is truly friendly. Just found out about this place through word of mouth. Been here twice in a mouth and I can definitely recommend this place to others. 

Military Discount? No.

Typical diner, but where I go when I am out late in the West Berlin area.  

I like that they have some traditional greek options on the menu like spanakopita.  A lot of diners I've seen in the area don't have these options.  Breakfast is fine, other than the Greek options it is the same as any other decent diner.

Makes a great late night option for a quick bite.

I have had the same issues, my payment is due on the 25th and no matter when we schedule the payment with the bank bill pay, it is always late. what a racket, you would think they are holding the check and not posting it. I requested a summary of my account to reconcile and was told "No,  I am not giving it to you, I am telling you how to do it." then I had insurance charges that took them 4 months to remove but they did not remove the late fees for the invalid charges. they are very unprofessional. I am suprised they are able to continue in business with those kind of practices.

Very friendly service food was good. Owner even came  and sat down and was very friendly and social. We ordered the Hawaiian burger and it was very good lots of flavour ! I would come back again and recommended everyone try it for your self and make your own decision . I like it  and my partner also loved the food and the gravy was awesome.
Very nice "family feel" to the restaurant. We say outside and they brought the food to us, even though they weren't waiters. Very friendly and excellent food!!!

Unbelievable lack of basic customer service. We walked in to see 4 employees and what appeared to be a manager of sorts (blue button down shirt and a headset. I am drawing a conclusion.) We stood there. We waited. They continued their conversation. Another employee squeezed their way past us. We continued to wait. We said hi. They looked at us. Continued their conversation and giggling. A legitimate 3-4 minutes passed with 5 employees (counting the manager) standing within 3 feet of us. Ignoring us. They finally asked what they can do for us. I said "Never mind" and walked out. As we walked away, they literally all stood there in the window shrugging at us and making confused gestures. Absolute garbage service. I've heard the horror stories of service at the table. I had no idea it would start at the door. The upside? We went to Prasino's right next door. That one is getting a 5 star :) Don't waste your time!

My husband and I came here on a Tuesday night after a friend recommended. We had about a 25 minute wait. My husband ordered the Crawfish Beignet and it was really good, although it had a spicy kick to it. I ordered the Cuban sandwich and it was very dry. I've had much better Cubans from food trucks! I ordered the Frosé and it had an overwhelming taste of cucumber. I could not even drink it. 
Our service was less than stellar. Our waiter was nice, but not very attentive. I went without water most of the meal. 
All in all, it's a cute little place with street parking and so-so food & not so great service.

Lovely little place! The table area was full, and there was a line to order, but it was quick and efficient. They had a great selection of breads, pastries, sandwiches, salads, and whatnot. It had a bit of everything for all kinds of tastes. 
I had a portobello sandwich with basil mayonnaise, and a French silk pie. My daughter had a pastry roll that she had been eyeing and a cheese quesadilla. The prices were very reasonable. The quality of food was very good. The food was very delicious. The service was pretty quick. 
I liked the rustic look of the place, too. It even felt cozy, despite the fact that it was almost like a fast food (but much better!). 
I definitely felt like it was a Southern type of place. The pies looked and tasted. AMAZING.
Poor service, food was just ok. Given the other options in the area it's a tad overpriced for what you get. Not a good value.

Rental staff was misleading, not paying attention or downright misinformed. Full explanation below.

Booked a rental through Kayak at a good rate (~$35/day). We pre-paid through Kayak, and thought the cost was fixed.

When we picked up the car, the customer service agent was clearly overworked, answering multiple phones (desk phone and cell phone - at the same time) WHILE also going through the paperwork with our rental. Not a good first impression.

I was listed as the primary driver and over 25. Because I did not have a credit card at the time, my girlfriend was switched to be the primary driver, she was under 25, and her credit card was used. 

There was to be an under-25 fee for the rental (now in her name), but we were verbally assured (after asking multiple times) that the underage driver fee would be waived because she was a AAA member. Awesome! We also declined the gas fee.

Until we were billed for the underage driver fee anyway. And the gas fee. It came out to $353 in addition to the $345 we already paid through Kayak ($698) - this additional fee made us pay more than double the cost of the original rental through Kayak, and was something we neither planned nor have the budget for. After calling Hertz this afternoon, we were told by the assistant manager for this location that there was nothing he could do. No refund, nothing. So I am escalating this further.

I'm already past the point of declaring Hertz a lost cause and never renting with them again. I would also avoid this branch if you don't like being severely mislead.

We're currently escalating this and I intend to alter this review if Hertz offers anything to help ameliorate this situation.

When you have a 24 hour gem like this, it's quite difficult to find things to complain about. On the surface, it's not in the best of neighborhoods, but even that keeps no one who is truly in the know from coming here. I've known about this place for ages, my cousin would tell me about the prices here all the time and I would not believe him one bit.

It wasn't until I finally moved to Mid City and discovered for myself that he wasn't full of his usual bag of $ h! T,  the prices here are ridiculous, and I mean in the good way.
