I don't understand how one can buy a 32" sandwich for under $10 and it not be skimp or tasteless, or have a single thing wrong with it at all. I don't understand, but I am grateful that the option is available to me.

I've only come in once with incident, and it was only that the kitchen was closed for an hour (3-4am) for cleaning so keep that in mind.

Also, the breakfast here is decent as well, I overheard a few orders for a sausage, egg n cheese sandwich which I'll be sure to get in on. I did have a small plate of grits with eggs, bacon and a slice of toast for like, three bucks. Just be sure to have your salt and pepper ready because there is none in the grits. The grits were also kinda watery, i like mine less soupy, but it got the job done.

Ordered furniture August 10th, paid in full out of pocket and still waiting on second half of furniture. Salesman followed us like a hawk around store for about an hour then after we get to the register we were told the chair has to be put on hold because it's out of stock. Fine, things happen I get it. Then sales guy also started drooling over another client in the middle of checking us out. Really? We Recieved 3 voicemails week of August 26th that furniture would be delivered Saturday....get call Saturday morning that it was never taken off hold and can't be delivered today. Really? Great customer service. Just careless bland apologies and promise of delivery. Last furniture store that delayed delivery too long offered to take money off and speed up delivery. THANK YOU RAYMOUR & FLANIGAN. Will never return to this store. Bobs furniture is GREAT but I will drive to another store or order online next time we purchase from the company. This store will not get our business again.
Food was amazing. Customer Service was ok

After waiting 30 minutes for non service at the establishment on 38th street, decided to try them. I really wanted my taco trio lol. We get to this one and waited 10 minutes while no one came to the speaker of the drive thru. My husband pulls around to head to the drive thru window where a car was already present. Seems there was an altercation between the driver and drive thru clerk. Drove back around to the drive thru, after yelling hello twice, finally ordered food. Once to the window the manager approached cashier register with drive thru clerk, neither acknowledged us waiting to hand cc to pay for order, waited another 10 minutes! No one in plain sight finally the drive thru clerk appeared and got the cc to pay for Order, handed the receipt and disappeared for another 10 minutes! Finally order in hand but asked 3x what drink did we have! Finally on our way home after an hour or so dealing with two different long john silvers!! Horrible! The 1 Star was for the first time trying the trio taco's (salmon, shrimp, and fish) they were very good

Filling and reasonably priced. It's not super fancy, but food is good and the service was prompt and attentive without being overbearing.  The kids grilled cheese was on texas toast and nearly perfect. Next time I'll spring for extra cheese.

Another tourist spot and philadelphia landmark. I usually only eat here if i have guests from out of town that want a down and dirty philadelphia steak. there are definitely better places. come for the experience, not the food. line up, read the sign, memorize how you will order something, pay, go find a free table, eat, and people watch.
Took 45 min for a cheesesteak and baked ziti and they weren't even busy. Never coming back 6/18 5:40 pm

Hate to be negative man. But wow I ordered a chicken Parm to go for my cousin Ronnie, and after waiting at the restaurant for 30 minutes for my food to be done, I get home and Roni's chicken was not cooked properly at all. Half of the chicken filet was barely cooked. The bread rolls were stale. The next morning Ronnie goes on to tell me how he was in agony in the bathroom. Poor guy. I understand everyone has a bad here n there but y'all got my cousin Ronnie ****** up for sure.

Food is priced for high end Mexican cuisine. The preparation is hit or miss. Serving sizes seem small compared to other Mexican restaurants. This is my second time here. They aren't very busy. It took almost 8 minutes before someone came over to take drink orders after being seated. The booth behind us took 6 minutes. Once we got a waiter he was friendly and had no issues. The first experience here was definitely better. Their fountain sodas seem to be out of adjustment as well. Will update after eating here again.

After going to Pats, Gino's and Max's, I stopped at Jim's to see how they compare. Side note: I'm not a fan of pats or Gino's, but Max's is the best in Philly in my opinion.

Jim's was actually really good, definitely not the best by any stretch, but very good. I always get mine with peppers and a little ketchup. Overall I would say this place is good, but when I'm in Philly, I won't be stopping her again unless Max's is closed!

We went for dinner and drinks last night and it was actually quite a disappointment. We were not told by the hostess when we were seated that we had to sit in the bar or the patio to get happy hour prices on drinks. So we sat in the general dining area and thought we were getting happy hour prices, only to find out on the bill that this was not the case. We were excited to find out we could order a burger even though it was not on the menu, but when it came, it was cooked on the medium side in the middle and charred on the edges. I like meat cooked medium rare, but my friend did not appreciate that her burger was still "mooing" on her plate. I hope that other food items are better on the menu, but I'm not sure how excited I would be to return.

They don't understand that if you keep the season passes at a reasonable rate..$99.00 which we've paid for many seasons . More people will buy. My family has bought season passes for six years at that rate.. then they went up and we've never bought or went since then. The joint is fun for kids and adults but raising the price just puts us off. Family of five, that's $500 you'll never get bcuz you think it's best to raise rates. I see there at $112 now, as of 5/28/18. For raising rates by $60 for my family we will not buy,  my kids are older now and it's just not a big deal if we make it to the park anymore,, but at $99 we would buy and spend money on tubes,food,lockers, and whatever else we wanted, keep raising the rates and I'm sure many family's will be in the same mind frame as us?.?. Good luck wild island!!!



P
Called today asked because i had burnt bulbs on the gas gauge on my 93 volvo 850 and a broken odometer gear refused to do the work was told had to go to the dealer.

Great food, service and ambience. Menu has something for everyone. I had the stuffed shrimp..so good. Scallop appetizer was also delicious. I didn't try any of their craft beers but the dirty martini was very good. April was our server and very professional. Everyone who worked there that we interacted with was wonderful. I loved the outdoor area. The rooftop area I hear is nice and I will be back to check that out someday.

I happen to be in this part of Brandon and was in the mood for pizza,  then again I am always in the mood for good pizza.  This place claimed to have NY style and it was close so I figured I would give them a chance.  Its a bit hard to find as it's set back off of Brandon Blvd , as I past it several times.Its a very tiny place on the far right side of a shopping plaza.

.Of course I asked if they had any Sicilian available and they looked at me like I asked for an order General Tso's.            
  If you are going to promote NY Style pizza, you should know what that exactly means and not just something to lure customers in.   

All they had available in the "slices" was neopolitan which was another term they were a bit stone faced about.....  So I ordered 2 slices of reg, their response was " You don't want any toppings on it???" No just str8 up cheese.  Again same stone faced look,       Now I do like pizza with certain ingredients but those are designed that way. Example :  Eggplant Parm pizza I throughly enjoy however the pizza was designed with those "toppings" if you will.     I noticed down here a majority of the pizza places promote pizza with toppings but unfortunately they use that to enhance the taste of their inferior pizza instead of complimenting it.    OK I will get off my Pizza soapbox , I am just very serious about pizza even in its most simplistic form.   

  Of course they didn't have that available , what they did have was a reg slice with pesto.  They said the lunch special was 2 slices and a can of soda for $5 not a bad deal for pizza , well if it was good pizza.   Ok here we go , the pluses are it is similar to NY style in appearance and slightly in taste .  The negatives unfortunately outweigh the +'s, the pizza was extremely oily , now I do like a lil grease if u will in my neo but this had abundant amount to the point I had to take napkins to absorb the oil. The taste very artificial almost plastically , cheese was more rubbery than creamy,sauce was very acidic,  too bitter and overly sweet. The crust wasn't terrible but it didn't have the right amount of crunch and support this oily "pizza" . 
  The owner and I think who was his son was friendly which was a plus but I think if they are going to brand their pizza as NY style you should do your research and know what that entails first. Of course some ppl may say that this is THEIR own interpretation of pizza and I can respect that opinion but I know what pizza is supposed to taste like and this unfortunately is not their best interpretation.

I bought 90min body scarab & Swedish massage, but she ( dawan) gave me only 50min !!! since I came late 10 min & they said that the total massage should be 70 min! And they charged for pregnant fee for extra$15!!!  I hired the nanny to watch my baby and I wanted to relax, but it turned out disappointed & stressful day! Never go back there!!!

Clean and rather fast considering how busy they are. Thanks, Starbucks!  I love the added touch of the beautiful wrought iron chandeliers, and beam ceilings. Lovely.  What's that in the next room? A brass instruments sculpture? So festive! So New Orleans chic! Love it!

The blackened catfish special on cornbread with collard greens was awesome . The abita amber beer a local beer was really a good compliment.
The French bread was good.
The wife enjoyed her Greek salad with blackened chicken. The four ounces of wine served to my wife was marginal.  As was the service, nice kid but just not on top of things as promptly as it should be.
The bread pudding was one of the best I've ever had.  The wife loved her pecan pie.

The place is fairly clean.
The food was highly recommended and did not disappoint.

This was my first time going with a girlfriend. I had the stroganoff burger and fries and she had bratwurst with German potato salad. Gotta say the best pot salad I've ever had and one of the best burgers out there. I really enjoyed sitting outside as well, it had a nice ambiance. Excited to come back and try more things!

Good bar to go to if you're in the area. 
Awesome Bloody Mary. 

Food I can't rave about but it is a bar.

Spicy Shrimp tacos- tasty but not spicy because of the cream sauce. Don't like crispy hard taco shells cause I find it greasy.

Jack Daniels burger- mediocre. You're way too basic to order this. Don't get it. It tastes like an average burger and you slap some bbq sauce on it. Patty tasted frozen and bread was generic/not fresh. Gimme some brioche! But .. again this is a bar.
Try the sushi valley roll and the sesame chicken is the best I ever had!! The owner came out and gave the personal touch! They have lots of non sushi options!

Less and less friendly when it comes to customer service. And don't be a student whose exhausted if you fall asleep for a few moments you will be asked to wake up...irregardless of giving them your money.
wheres the COOK? BRANDI makes the best fried pork chop! please for the love of god reinstate brandi and the kitchen! need my pork chop! fellas, we LOVE this place! need my pork chop!
They are getting 2 Stars only because of the balcony seating; other than that, my husband preferred a pb & j .

Ah! Bertucci's, long time no see! The last time I went to a Bertucci's was several years ago when it was still was in Bryn Mawr. The overall quality of the food was not good and the service (at least I think) was slow.

Anyhow, I ate at the location in Wayne tonight because I was outvoted and someone in my party had a craving for the chicken parm. We started off walking into the restaurant and it was empty and to me,​ it was a little concerning and maybe that had to do with what came next.  Our waiter was rather interesting but sort of struggled with certain aspects. He was a little quirky and struggled with service. I had to ask for a straw 3 times before he accommodated my request. 

I'll start with the drinks. One person in my party enjoyed her Coke, and another person seemed to enjoy his Strawberry Lemonade. I ordered a strawberry lemonade, and let's just say was I knew something tasted off. This was not homemade, instead,​ it tasted ​an awful​l​y lot like Minute Maid, which was not specified on the menu. Next, we ordered the food. One person in my party ordered the Shrimp & Lobster Rossini and he really enjoyed his meal or at least he was positive! The other person ordered the chicken parm and she was happy with her meal. I also ordered chicken parm but my meal tasted microwaved and the marinara sauce tasted like it was factory-made​. In fact, to further prove the point, I had a piece of frozen chicken or chicken that wasn't cooked all the way through.

The good thing were the rolls! The rolls as always are delish. 

I would say $90 (including tip) for 2 chicken parms, 1 Shrimp & Lobster Rossini, and 3 soft drinks is a lot of money. Is this place, really high end or is it trying to be? I don't really know how much longer this place will last but we shall see. It's sad, the chain went downhill​l and just isn't good anymore and is trying to raise prices while cutting both quality and cost.

Hopefully, the right leadership will coming around and turn it around to what it was and maybe lower the prices!

Probably the best Mac and cheese i have ever had and the vegetable medley was cooked perfectly! For three people it was 40 dollars, which is reasonable! The service was friendly and welcoming and made it a good experience. There's tons of menu options as well as specialty Irish items! For bar food this is the place to go if you don't want to spend a big chunk of your paycheck!

I had been looking forward to going to LEX since it has opened & finally made my way on NYE. Despite that I feel the tickets were way overpriced (open bar was only from 8-10 & there was no complimentary champagne for a midnight toast) one of the bartenders doubled her tip on a round of drinks I bought at the first bar you see when you walk in. They were charging $4 for little Grand Sierra water bottles that the resort gives out for free & when they ran out of those, they still charged $4 for a plastic cup of water. The place was a mess with TONS of crap on the floors (popped balloons, glasses, napkins, you name it), I would have thought they would have done a better job cleaning the floors. I witnessed so many slip & falls, it was ridiculous. Besides the venue being nice, I was very disappointed. G-Eazy killed it of course, but as soon as he performed, the club emptied out early & the DJ after his helped emptied the rest.

all I can say is WOW. my expectations bar had not been set or any reviews read prior to our decision to eat here last night. Vicky seated us and gave us great input on what she liked. and we followed her lead. she encouraged us to try machetes instead of the 99c margaritas, and holy moly. it was a fishbowl on a stem huge. and I was feeling the buzz half way thru and was feeling no pain by the time it was emptied. (mind you we skipped lunch)
after half of number two I had to step away. (more like wobble) cannot believe they were only 3bux.
now to the food. had the 5 taco carbon and the 20 dollar 1 pound ribeye. it was amazing, like better than we had eaten since my uncle who is an amazing Mexican chef had cooked for us.
Alex was our waiter. and even when I got a little silly, he was such a gem.......chips in a humongous bowl, two ginormous bowls of a wonderful Smokey salsa, we had a GREAT evening celebrating. and will come back.
YES. the casino looks like you will get mugged just walking to the doors. and it is wicked awful smoky inside, but we found zero smoke in the booth we were sat at after zero wait time at 5pm. I didnot honestly hold out much hope for a quality meal, or even a beverage with evev a hint of alcohol in it, but WOW....... I loved it, it indeed is a recommended stop if you are in Sparks.

This is the second time I've walked out of Doc's disappointed.

First things first, I really wanted to like this place: Its in a great location in West Chester, has outdoor seating, great oyster selection, and is one of the few exclusive seafood restaurants in the area (I love seafood).

We went here in a group of 7 for my good friend's girlfriend's birthday.

Sat at the bar for drinks waiting for the rest of the party, bartender was friendly and beers are beers.

As the other people arrived we were seated immediately in the back corner.

While the waitress was friendly, she was clearly new, and not trained very well. She did not know the specials, or the drink policy of the day (let us order an unavailable drink for that day).

I ordered 2 apps, a pound of sauteed littleneck clams, and half dozen oyster on the half.

First the clams: Portion was skimpy, barely a dozen. Clams were cooked properly but were very gritty, broth was over-reduced and way too salty.

Oysters: The waitress got my order wrong and gave me 2 of the wrong kind. no big deal. But the quality of the oysters were sub-par at best. The guy shucking the oysters had no idea of what he was doing; they were mangled, i mean like he stuck the knife into the oyster, blended it, pried it open, poured the liquor out and threw it on a metal pan, with jarred cocktail sauce, and a lemon. There was barely half the oyster left. At practically $3 a piece, i was disappointed. 

I ordered the crab cake sandwich as a main. The waitress got the order wrong, and I received the crab cake entree. Sent it back, came back to me on a stale bun (the one crab cake from the previous plate), was cold, tasted of being previously frozen, and definitely canned crab meat, and now included a side of frozen french fries.

I shook my head as a got the bill and signed the $70 check (just for my food and drink).

Such a shame, this place has so much going for it, just bad management.

I will not be going back.

Went here on a bingo night with some family and it was not at all what I expected. Always thought of bingo as a boring game but apparently as long as there's a drag queen doing the announcing it's an extraordinary evening.
I tend to get my burgers pretty plain so I can't vouch for much on that front, but it was cooked well and I had no issues. The drinks are pretty good, though.
Only reason I took a star off is because it took a long time (close to 30-45 minutes) to be served. Staff was friendly but seemed a little frazzled on a night that wasn't particularly busy.
I got the Twenty Stuff and it was so good. I thought it was closed and couldn't find it, but was grateful when we did!

good shop very good work
take my hyundai here now used to only go to dealership but joe is great cant ask for anything more 

always come here for anything my car needs

My daughter & SIL are visiting from AL and were meeting friends for dinner. We suggested Sacred Pepper, as we go quite a bit and always enjoy. They did not have the same experience . There was a band playing ( not a big enough place for a band) which was so loud they could not hear each other or be able to have a conversation. Feel really bad for our recommendation, as these are friends they aren't able to visit often.

My wife and I took our daughter and a friend to St. Louis for her birthday a couple weeks ago.  We decided to stay at the Hyatt due to the location right downtown.

The hotel was nice and in a great location.  We had a 2 bed room which was very nicely decorated and clean.  The room was a little smaller than comparably priced rooms but given the downtown location this was understandable.

There really were only two reasons I wouldn't give the hotel a higher rating, one more than the other.  The first, the lesser of the two reasons, is there isn't parking actually at the hotel.  You have to go about a block passed the hotel and park at another property.  It's a very short walk so it really isn't a big deal and the parking was very inexpensive.

The main reason I don't give it a higher rating is that the beds were very uncomfortable.  All four of us awoke the next morning with sore backs and hips from the beds, and nobody got a good night's sleep.

Overall it's a very nice hotel and the staff was exceptionally nice and helpful when we spoke with them.  If the beds were more comfortable this would probably be a regular stay for us in St. Louis.

Our party of 5 arrived at Luke on a Friday before heading off to Jazzfest. We made a reservation the night before but when we entered the restaurant it too the hostess almost 5 minutes to even acknowledge that we were standing in the lobby. There were 2 other groups waiting there as well. 

That beginning wait kind of set the tone for the whole meal. The service was a little slow. The cocktails and food were good. I got the southern breakfast, my friends got the chicken biscuit and shrimp and grits. We all liked our meals. 

If you don't mind a wait then stop on into Luke, where the experience is good but I would not go out of the way to stop in here.

I have taken one class and it was fine. As asked via email from Noorish if I had any feedback or comments. I did. I got an email back telling me I had to correct myself. Now, there are many Yoga studios and Yoga studio managers that are more open to comments and treat their Yoga students with more respect and better. I would suggest going to a Studio where you would receive both.

As per my local friends, Nico's is the go-to place after a drunken night out.  It's open 24 hours and is basically fast-food Mexican.

I came here for breakfast after an early morning hike up Tumamoc Hill.  Way to cancel out a hardcore workout!  I had an egg and cheese breakfast burrito.  For ~$5, you get a really large and filling burrito that tastes decent.  Nothing special.  You can't really eff up egg and cheese, right?

I'm done with this place.  The women are usually good but the men are terrible.  They rush through everything, barely give any massage with the mani and do a rotten job painting my nails.  I had Andy this week, who I think owns or manages the place and he did a terrible job.  And he was doing my mani while trying to manage customers as they came in and he walked away several times with no explanation or excuse to me.  There are too many choices to put up with this crap.  I know it's a running joke but I'm tired of the person doing my nails carrying on conversations with other workers in their native tongue - it is just rude.

I'd give this place no stars if it was possible.

Stains on the carpet, no hot water in the morning, out in the middle of nowhere... a dream come true for someone who plans to sit in bed all day while in Nashville (and the beds are comfy).  But it was a complete nightmare for my trip.  Granted, I was here because of a conference so I got a discounted rate, but it was still not my hotel of choice.  It was totally out in the middle of nowhere (compared to downtown Nashville), but there are shuttles that will pick up you at any location in downtown.  There is also a complimentary shuttle to and from the airport. 

The good: very friendly bar staff and shuttle driver staff (front desk staff.... eh, no thanks),  good drinks, the on-site restaurant is decently priced and has a variety of items, and the soda machines are cheap.  

Overall, I would much rather stay in downtown Nashville!
Honestly, the atmosphere of the place was great.  The service was great.  The food was ok.  The salmon was swimming in barbecue sauce.  The broccoli rab was over garlicky.  The cole slaw was soupy...

Friendly staff and the best breakfast sandwiches in the area! Being a millennial, I am always on the go so it is great that they don't mind when I call my order ahead, then swing by to pick it up without waiting on my way somewhere.

I'd much rather give my business to a local business like Memphis Market rather than a chain like Wawa. I love this local corner store!

Not the best place to stay. The tile work in the room was horrible. Random tiles were sticking out and so it made me trip a few times. The only good thing was they let us check in a little earlier. The room had an odd smell..... the bathroom wasn't the cleanest. The ceiling in the bathroom had questionable stains on the ceilings. Don't stay here if possible.

We should have known not to stay here when we walked in and saw a handwritten sign that said if your room smells like weed, you will be charged a fee. We booked two rooms at this hotel, one for my wife and I, and one for our 18-year-old son. We checked in around midnight and as we were walking to our room, it was hard not to notice the prostitutes and shady characters roaming the halls. They seemed to be watching our every move. When we got to our first (non-smoking) room, we opened the door and it felt like the morning after a Saturday night at a smoky pool hall. I immediately felt like I needed to shower. We also found stains on the sheets and holes in the wall. On to our son's (non-smoking) room.... Exact same experience, smoke-infested, poorly maintained room! We told him to stay in the room and do not answer the door no matter what. Sure enough, within 10 minutes of dropping him off, he had two separate knocks on his door. He called to tell us this and he also mentioned he was having a hard time breathing due to his asthma and the room smelling like smoke. After deliberation, we decided to leave this hotel due to safety and health concerns. We went to the front desk and they told us they understood and they would refund one of the rooms but we need to talk to the manager when they came on duty about a refund for the second. They said given the asthmatic condition, they believed we would get our money back. We left this hotel and went to another hotel not too far away.

Fast forward to regular business hours and us calling the manager to discuss the refund. She said she would not give us a refund because she watched the video surveillance and we wound up staying in one of the rooms. This was a complete and utter lie and we called her out on it. We asked her to show us the video and she got very snippy and told us we are not getting a refund. We are currently disputing this with our credit card company.
Great little spot.  Staff is friendly and knowledgeable.  Menu very limited but everything we ate was excellent.
Love the waffles with bourbon maple syrup.  Best in town.  Chicken moist and tasty.   Vanilla Coke if you don't want beer. Plan to visit often

Hat Date #2: Expensive Restaurant

Favazzas is kind of a tough restaurant to review.  You see, in that narrow maze of streets known as The Hill, Favazzas is probably my favorite "Italian" restaurant.  I won't retread the definition of The Hill Italian vs actual Italian (view my previous reviews of Pietros and Zia's for my little rant), but let's just say there's a reason why Americans keel over from heart attacks at 30 and actual Italians look young until the day they die.

The hard part is that in spite of Favazzas being my favorite Hill restaurant, there's virtually no way I could defend giving it 5 stars.  This is the same stuff you find at every other Hill restaurant, but just better.  Virtually every dish is swimming in some kind of sauce, the pasta reeks of garlic, you wait for 30-45 minutes just to sit down (no reservations taken on weekend nights), and you're crammed in with every other person who thinks they're having a reeeeel fancy meal.

The waiting area frankly stinks.  You put in your name and you pretty much have to mingle around in the lobby area with very little seating and hordes of other people waiting for a table.  There's a small bar that you could sit at if it's not crowded (it virtually always is).  But until your name is called you're pretty much relegated to sharing intimate space with strangers and avoiding the hot/cold air every time the outside door opens.

Ok, but here's the catch.  Everything else is actually pretty good.  The food is a notch above your standard hill cuisine, the service is consistently very good (if not excellent), and prices are reasonable ($15-20 for an entree).  The bread they bring out before the meal is pre-buttered and toasted, and is so completely heavenly that I think I could live on it for weeks at a time if necessary.

This is heavy food, folks.  H-E-A-V-Y.  No light fare here.  Fried meat, cream sauces and mounds of pasta are the rule, and they're light on fresh veggies.  You gotta come with an appetite, and even then you'll still probably leave with a doggy bag.  It's quantity over quality, but not necessarily in a bad way.

See what I mean?  Favazzas isn't really a great restaurant, just a really good one.  I'm not a huge fan of restaurants on The Hill in general, but if someone is in the mood I always suggest Favazzas.  It's the best of the worst I suppose.  But that makes it sound worse than it is.  It really could stand on it's own outside The Hill environment, which I suppose is the ultimate test.  In my opinion, if you want to go to The Hill to eat a decent meal (or someone you know is BEGGING you to go), pick Favazzas and don't look back.  You won't get perfection, but you'll get a damned good meal.

They don't have the prosciutto burger any more. I was told it's not a good sale. Fried mushroom was delicious but fried pickles were too salty. However, I have to agree that their burgers taste average for high dollar. $13 which probably worth $7-8. We have much better burgers around for lower price. The worst thing to me was they added tips to our bill. We had two parties and 7 people together. They added 20% and ask for additional tip. That's what I normally tip, (easier to calculate than 18%), but I don't like to be forced to appreciate their service! Felt slightly insulted!

Came here one night with my friend because there was literally nothing else open past 9:00 in East Nashville (COVID, I guess?). The food was okay, nothing to write home about and a little expensive for bar food/ sandwiches, but it served the purpose of dinner at 9:30 pm. They did have a very extensive beer list (guess that comes with the territory of beer halls), and I enjoyed my cider. Wouldn't be my first choice of place to eat, but I'm mostly writing this so that if anyone else finds themselves yelping a late-ish night bite, they'll have this to go off of as a solid recommendation.

Service was fine, food was not good at all.  Someone burnt the roux for the seafood gumbo which left it tasting bitter and the other items we purchased were mediocre at best. VERY pricey for the quality! Would not recommend.

Wow??? I have been eating at local home sryle resturant and cafes for nealy 46 years and I have to say this resturants breakfast menu is extremely confusing and over priced for a hometown style resutrant with American Classic food. The waitress even agreed that it's isn't very user friendly. 

 This breakfast menu is miserable to try and order from as I'm left woundering what the hell is so far off with this thing. I have been in several time with the hope that somehow I might be able decipher this thing and to order a basic American style breakfast with ease. But this menu is a hodgepodge of over priced nothing. Very little to choose from. A basic 3 omelet starts at almost $8.75, and is eggs only. That's 3 eggs with nothing else for almost $9 bucks!!!! You can buy 4 and a half dozen for that at the local grocery store with change to spare. Then to add what 8most people would consider basoc omelet staples (cheese, ham, mushroom, ext.) they price gouge the living hell out of you foreach ingredient. This menu desperately needs a rework and some Dennys style loving. 

Now this rating dosnt not reflect on resturants quality of food, cleanliness or the staff and their friendliness in the least. As a matter of fact the new girls willingness to help me figure out this maze of the menu has convinced me to bring my original rating from a 2 to a 3

Cleanliness & environment +
Staff efficiency & Friendliness +
Food quality and portion size +

Menu price and value -

But alas as I live in the neighborhood I have decided that l will be driving farther away to the nearest Denny's as I'm not willing to spend 10min scratching my head in hopes to order somthing easly comprehended, constructed and reasonably priced.

My wife and I stayed here for a few nights while visiting family for Thanksgiving.  This is the WORST hotel I've ever stayed at. The rooms are COLD you try setting the thermostat up to warm up the room and nothing. The shower gets water all over the bathroom floor even when using the curtain.  The walls are very thin, you can hear every step and conversation of the people above you.

Then they try to entice you with their peanut M&Ms $8 for a little bag, and the little 1 hit booze bottles for $20. They even got some kinky sex lube too....weird.

Ended up being $500 for 2 nights. Then you go to check out and they're charging a "good life fee" $25 a night


Do NOT waste your money, we would have been better going to motel 6.

All staff at Mambos is very attentive; our server Steven gives you that southern hospitality feeling, the restaurant manager goes to every table to check on you and the host is awesome as well. Great place to visit when you come to New Orleans.

Not only are the cookies super yummy they are amazingly cute! I was fortunate to have some adorable Galentines day cookies sent to me. They were so pretty I didn't want to eat them, but I'm glad I did!
This place is {amazing}! Worth the wait!  And I loved all the items they had for sale in the shop part :)

Whoever gives this airport less than 4 stars does not travel often. Not only was checking in easy and fast but this airport has some of the best restaurants and most restaurants I have seen out of any airport in the United States

I'm usually in here only as a stop either before or after an event downtown in which I am looking for some cheap beers before heading somewhere that is going to cost a lot more. Unless it is right before a big Pacers game or on a day that brings a ton of people downtown, there is usually plenty of space in here early in the evening. It gets more busy later in the night with a little more of a party type crowd. 

The bar itself is pretty large with seating around it. There is also an assortment of tables and booths setup in a way that still provides lots of room to stand and move around the place. I have eaten here a couple of times and think that you probably get what you pay for with the standard bar food. You can fill up here with a greasy meal for $10-$12, but it used to be better prices before they changed their menu. Beer-wise , there is usually a great special for something cheap and in a pint can at about the lowest price you can get a beer anywhere in the downtown area, so that's usually what I'm looking for when I am here.

Service has always been below par in my opinion. Bartenders are good at getting things done efficiently instead of having much customer service, but they do that well and getting a beer quickly is usually all I am looking for here anyway. The waitresses that go around to the tables have never impressed me. It's usually very slow and bare minimum when it comes to customer service. I think it's probably less noticeable late in the evening when they are just running around filling beers, but if you are getting food and wanting to sit around somewhere earlier in the evening, this isn't a place to come looking for good service. 

They have a decent sized stage for live music, which not a ton of places provide in that area. I also know that they have trivia here. There are lots of TVs that always have on sports. Overall, a good place to come grab some cheap beers in a bar setting. Live music is hit and miss, but there are also some good bands to come see here.

I love this place and can't pay full price for sushi ever again! I use to go once a week before I got bz with work. They have more not fried options than the other place across the street. Its the 1 of 2 places where I go to in Bensalem.

It is possible to get a very tasty meal here, but depends greatly on who is behind the counter preparing your order. Don't let the old guy (Bill?) make it. He gives you half as much food as the others and barely puts on any dressing.

purchased a Maytag washer on 6/16/20 from gerhards did first load machine was banging and shaking and the clothes came out covered with soap (white streaks). called for service they sent out a tech.said the machine didn't show any error codes or something to that effect,said it was fine and to use less soap.continued to have problems with banging and shaking soap on clothes and the fill level. I took some video of the machine running sent it to gerhards and they sent a different tech to my house for 2nd service call.this guy was very friendly and explained in detail what we should do to get the best results from this machine,one thing he said was to use 1 tablespoon of soap per load. there are 16 tablespoons in a cup, that's a half ounce per tablespoon. a 37 oz. bottle of tide(high efficiency)  says it does 25 loads.according to gerhards math I should be getting 74 loads of wash from my 37 oz. bottle of tide. well the clothes aren't clean they smell,still have soap streaks on them,and are a twisted up wrinkled mess,and the machine still bangs and shakes we can hear on the second floor and it's in the basement. he went on to tell me other issues with this machine that I don't want to go into now.I emailed Ian at customer service gerhards this past friday he never got back to me, I emailed him again on Wednesday and told him I called the store. he responded by saying he forwarded my email to the someone at the store and service, he also asked who I spoke to at the store. I have emailed him again today and he has not responded.when I called the store on Wednesday afternoon,asked to speak to customer service,they put me hold came back and said no one was picking up. asked for my name and number would call me back, as of close of business thurs. no one has returned my call.
 the number we called is the phone number on their web site I don't know what store that connects with specifically, they have 6 locations, this review isn't about an individual location. it's about gerhards the company.we purchased this machine from the web site and finished the transaction on the phone,we were never in one on their brick and mortar stores. In one of my emails after the 2nd. service call I asked gerhards if they would exchange this machine for another one or we could do an upgrade to a different washer, and still no response.We decided to shop local and support a family run business and this is how we are treated.

Walking by I saw lunch special on a  board  $5.99 so I went in for lunch. Fried chicken wings, Mac  and cheese, greens, all well made and presented. Just  a bar with great lunch at a good price. Was  there just a day so have to return next time in Philadelphia. The usual places were packed with convention  goers, this was close but none discovered  it so it was nearly empty.

The beginning of a wonderful evening.  This new Italian trattoria offers a nice blend of Italian cuisine in that you get a nice bit of Northern Italian combined with the Mediterranean flavors from the south.  

The love of my life and I went last Friday evening. The place was busy but not crowded and lively but not raucous.  I was able to pay attention to her every move and word as she had me completely transfixed.  

It's BYOB and we brought a nice bottle with us to pair with an appetizer of Lamb sausage and Polenta.  Sausage had some very nice spice to it and the polenta was crisp to the outside and soft and warm to the inside.  We continued with a spaghetti dish with eggplant and ricotta for me and tagliatelle with scallops and shrimp for her.  The sauces were perfect in the application and taste.  The pasta was just al dente and perfectly cooked.  Overall the meal was wonderful and highly recommended.  We did not partake of dessert as the night was young and beautiful, just like my wife.  

Buona Sera....
CoVid changes ruined the atmosphere of this famous place. It's now coffee in foam cups and no table service. The beignets were great but now available all over New Orleans.

I'm a vegetarian and I was excited to see a burger place open on my block that sold veggie burgers. Was. As in, past tense. 

Product (Food): The burger itself was adequate. Definitely no need to write home...

Service: The woman behind the register was a brusque but it was okay because she seemed to be doing a lot. I can appreciate hard work so I placed my order and sat down. That is when the rabbit hole opened...the young woman who made my burger sneezed several times. Several. But she didn't change her gloves once. I could not believe it. She then packed up someone's sandwich and sent him on his way. I just KNEW she was going to change her gloves before she proceeded to make my sandwich. Yea. No. She proceeded to make my sandwich without changing her gloves. Even as I write this words still escape me...


Overall: I thought my experience might be unique so I have come in a pretended to look at a menu while I watch them make food. It wasn't a unique experience. The level of cleanliness is unacceptable...especially since I can literally see you making the food. They are litterally across the street from me and I have not and will not go back.
Service?  Eh.  Food?  WOW!  Been here twice and had veal parmigiana both times.  To die for!!!!

Tried to make a reservation for two because the previous week the place had a 2hr wait at 4:30 and there was a big football game again. Was told that they weren't taking any reservations and to call about a half hour before coming. Since there was a game that was going to be on, called 90 min ahead to see if that might help. Again was told they weren't doing reservations but this person added that this was DUE TO THE GAME and they could be sure how busy they will be and to call a half hr before coming to get an idea of the wait time. I've worked in retail and this was horrible. I knew there was the game which is WHY I was calling ahead.

Have only eaten here once,  so possibly a fluke,  but food ranging from mediocre to very poor,  joined by decent service and dismal atmosphere,  made for a forgettable dining experience. There are too many outstanding Vietnamese locations nearby to come here.

Evidently this local chain has found the lock on very enthusiastic and peppy hosts and waiters. It's almost breathtaking how exuberant they are, though it's so much one begins to wonder how much of that is genuine. No matter, the visit is for food. Inside, though the parking lot is very funky, is pretty comfortable and has all the usual trappings you would normally expect for this kind of place.

The food, however...this is the second location I've eaten at and I feel pretty confident in saying that I won't be returning to another by choice. It's not that there's anything wrong, per se, just that one can definitely do a lot better on the Mexican side of things around town. It's not low grade or low rent or anything, but doesn't seem to be reaching for the stars to any great extent, either. It's AN option, somewhere you could hit if you were around, say. 

I played it safe and went with nachos this time and this was not one of the better versions I've had of this or the chorizo that was on it. I have, however, also definitely had lesser versions of both, so I guess this falls in the gap. All in all, everything I've had at both locations has just been kind of "there" more than anything else. It is almost worth going just to see the incredibly upbeat staff, though...not so much for the food, though.

I tried to order from there last night, and they added extra charges that were not listed on the menu to the total. When I asked the manager about the total, he was rude to me, and said "F... you" to me and hung up on me, and the owner apologized, but did nothing else about it. The manager who cursed at me still works there, and never apologized. I ended up ordering from another pizza place down the street, and recieved great customer service.

TLDR: Great place for roast pork sandwiches right in the middle of Reading Terminal Market.

As someone who is not from Philly, I didn't even know that Philly was known for yet another sandwich until very recently. Obviously just about everybody knows about the cheesesteaks, but don't sleep on the roast pork sandwiches. Tommy DiNic's does a really roast pork sandwich and it should be one of the reasons you come and visit Reading Terminal Market.

Tommy DiNic's has been around for quite awhile and is now four generations deep into the business. It's appeared on a variety of food shows and its roast pork sandwich with provolone and broccoli rabe was even called the "Best Sandwich in America" by the Travel Channel.

Located right in the middle of Reading, don't be surprised to see a line when you come here. However, don't worry - the line moves pretty fast. Despite having other sandwiches on the menu, getting the classic roast pork sandwich with provolone and broccoli rabe was the only option. 

It's a fairly large sandwich, and for about ten bucks, I would say it's a really good deal. If you're looking to eat at other places within Reading, splitting this sandwich in half with someone should be more than enough before you move on to another vendor.

After pretty much inhaling the sandwich, I would say that even though it's not the best sandwich I've ever had, it's still a really great one. It may be a matter of personal preference, but I still prefer a cheesesteak. Still, I certainly wouldn't mind having a roast pork sandwich either.  Definitely give it a try!

The sandwich maker was being very skimpy on the vegetables, and I even had to ask for more spinach.  So it was a small sandwich, but my main concern is that it looks like only bologna for the meat.  I didn't ask for cheese, but as you can see, there's no variety in the meat.  So it was so-so.

Bone-in rib eye was ordered medium rare. Half of it came out ok, the other half was not even cooked, only seared. The waiter was very kind and asked us if we wanted a new piece or just to fix it. We opted to fix it and it came but more on the medium side, but at least it was edible this time. Even without the trouble, the steak is not worth the near $50 they charge for it.

The quinoa salad side was a good compliment against the steak. The pork chops were quite delicious. Their side of onion rings came out extremely greasy - so much so that the batter pretty much slid off the onions. Ordered two cocktails too and they came were extremely strong. One wasn't even drinkable.

Very reasonably priced for a tourist centric location and quite delicious. Great service and cozy location. Alligator po' boy was delicious (French bread it was served on was a bit dry) and the red beans and rice were just awesome. 25% public service discount, so make sure you show your military ID! Police badge, etc.

We were heading to another restaurant, but the line was out the door. We opted to try this place. Casual atmosphere and not busy at all. Seated ourselves. I ordered a strawberry margarita on the rocks only to find out they were out of strawberry mix. Our server recommended mango, but it was best frozen. Not a fan of frozen but tried it. Little alcohol in it. There was nice variety on the menu. Typical bar style food with a few non-fried options for entrees. I ordered blackened Mahi with mashed potatoes and cole slaw. After awhile the new owner came out to say they were out of mashed potatoes. Said he just took over the place 2 weeks ago. I understand issues like this can happen. Then our food came out. The quality of my meal was so disheartening! The fish was small and dried out. It reminded me of fish I've bought frozen at Walmart. No flavor, except salt. Certainly not an $18 meal. My only bad experience on this trip. Won't be back unless the reviews are outstanding.

Bayona has never really been to my taste and a recent visit confirmed what I have long thought but kept to myself.  The restaurant is pretty in an old fashioned way and the courtyard is lovely (like so many in the quarter).  However the decor is dated as is the clientele - the average age I saw in the fairly empty dining room was well over sixty. While old-fashioned decor and aging patrons are not necessarily an issue I find that the food and service matches.  Over multiple visits the menu has seemed relatively unchanged and there were a surprising lack of options during my last one.  While dishes like garlic soup and caesar salad are well executed - that I am ordering them indicates that there is not much else to excite me.  Tried the tripletail as a main which was fine but not particularly exciting - just traditional and a little bland.  Service was excruciatingly slow for a small business lunch (party of 2) and even though we tried to move it along it still took well over an hour for two courses in a nearly empty restaurant.  We kept ordering from the busboy to speed things along and as a result the first time we saw our waitress was when she dropped off the check.  While Bayona is better than many options in the quarter it is not for me and certainly avoid if you are looking for a quick meal.

So I'm going to bump up my rating for this restaurant now. It feels like they've improved upon the menu and their service.

The service has improved greatly. The servers are more attentive now than in previous visits, even during their busiest of times. I have to mention again that their server, Terrence, is the absolutely the best and I try to sit in his area whenever I dine here to ensure I get his exceptional service.

I would personally like to see this place update their menu a little bit while continuing to serve their popular items.
Within walking distance to our condo, when we are in town we frequent Salt.  AJ, Natasha and Jordan are all great!  Would like to see them open for breakfast, at least on the weekends!

It's been 4 or 5 weeks since I've been back, so some of my issues may have been addressed already.

Was excited to see this place open. First and second visit found the brisket sandwich portions lacking from their price point, kinda disappointing. Beans were good though, not good enough to bring me back anytime soon.

Drinks need to be self served, without having to wait on an available counter person to refill you while your food gets colder.

They need to figure out how better to offer their sauces than on a table by the register all unmarked to what is what. Would be nice if they had sauce caddies on all the tables so you can sample while you eat at the table.

Like the "Big Ass Fans", and when they're smoking it really gets the area smelling great. But is too noisy/loud in there. Turn down the music so it's background music and you can still talk with your friends and not have to keep saying "what"?

 I'll give it a try in another couple months. Maybe they've worked out some opening kinks by then.

I get take out here on occasion and it's fairly good for a quick lunch.  The service is ok and the food seems to be better now than it was a few months ago.  It seems a bit odd, doughnuts and Pho, but I admire anyone willing to try something different.  I wouldn't say it's the best Pho in town, but it's close to my office and convenient.

Well me and some friends went to this place for a nice birthday dinner...it was the worst experience I have had on a vacation....we arrived and the hostess told us first it will be a 15 to 20 min wait...they said it was because 1 in our party was in a wheelchair...no problem.then after a 40 min wait..I believe they forgot us..cause the started seating people with huge parties before us.. walk-ins....so we get seated. Finally...and ordered  fast cause we were really hungry....the seafood fried platter was advertised as the best in  town....we ordered it....it came I got a combo shrimp and catfish ..no oysters...mine came with oysters...and the taste was totally terrible...very old grease was used..because it all tasted the same...bad! I loved coming to this town..but my friends birthday meal was ruined....the servers were great...too many hostesses that were not trained and people running around like they were lost....the food sucked and was not worth the price of a krystal burger....for your sake and your stomachs...go somewhere else...please!!!

The Pho is great and the customer service is even better! Bubble tea is a little on the pricey end but very good nonetheless.  

*Update* The one thing they're lacking is cleanliness. I accidently dropped my cell phone under the table and was disgusted by the  dirt, mold and other unidentifiable objects living under the table & on the walls. I immediately lost my appetite and haven't been back since....

I came here with my girlfriend because of the good reviews on Yelp! While the decor was inviting and staff cordial, the food menu tempting and drink menu enticing, the former were well worth the experience but the latter quite disappointing.  We ordered chips and guacamole with pico: the dips were combined in the same bowel which was quite small and lacking pizzazz. With high hopes we delved into the fruit, pickled onion and avocado salad but sadly it was topped with a sour and tart oily dressing and fried strips of flour tortilla both of which did not complement the freshness of the fruits, veggies & herbs.  We then reluctantly moved on to the black bean & portobello tacos that were completely devoid of flavor.  The variety of beers on tap were redeeming though so if we go back, that's what we'll stick to.

Great furniture store and even better customer service! Ask for John Alley; he's particularly fabulous!!! He came out to my house to help me figure out the right furniture style and size, spent time helping me figure out right direction on fabrics/color, followed up with me without being pushy or annoying. He has a great eye, listens to the customer and found me a terrific, affordable sofa!

So overrated. The tamales were ok, I have had more authentic tasting tamales, though the corn tamale was fantastic. I wish I could say the same for the beef and chicken tamales. Tried my girlfriend's chimichanga, it was alright. Nothing spectacular. The food was way overpriced. The salsa was not good. The fried ice cream was decent. The cinnamon shell was good. This place reminded me of a place called Hacienda in Evansville. Take that for what it is worth. Most gringos love that sort of thing I guess. If you want a true Mexican experience, look elsewhere. If you are looking for a sanitized, Fisherized, experience in a rough neighborhood so you can tell all your suburban friends how scary it is, this place is for you.

Well... I think this is a 3 strikes you're out kind of situation. The internet, tv cable, and jacuzzi were all out of service at some point during our multiple night stay.

CONS: 
1st WIFI was first day and second night. Tv cable also had issues Sunday morning when watching the news. Jacuzzi was closed the last night due to 'too high of algae'. I guess these things happen, but it is unfortunate when they do. 

Noise is an issue. This is the first hotel I've been in that you can hear 'loudly' the person walking around in the above unit and when someone closes their door it sounds like they are slamming yours. Not remotely sound proof at all. People loud in back parking lot and hallways at night and early in morning. I had to sleep with earplugs. 

I was not greeted when I walked in, lady didn't look up once from her computer. 

Cleaning staff stored carts of trash in hallways near elevators every day of our several day stay. Hallways generally pretty dirty with a "beat up" kind of look. 

Window view from room is a parking lot. 

Questionable white stains on furniture in room and dark stains on floor. 

This is the last time I let me dad book the hotel. Oops, my bad.

PROS:
Jacuzzi was nice and hot at night (my father said it was only warm during the day so I was happy it maybe got turned up at night)

Breakfast in the a.m. was okay. Eggs not good but the rest was fine but carb heavy.

Probably wouldn't have thought about trying a meatball restaurant but I went with a group of friends to celebrate a birthday this past week & glad I did. I tried the 4 balls & got to sample different balls & sauces, all very tasty. Though the balls are the main focus & good, I'm pretty sure my favorite was the Mac & Queso & this banana pudding dessert that I wished we hadn't been sharing! Great customer service & I plan to go back, I'm taking some more friends for another birthday next week!

Well - Don't expect service... Went in - no welcome. Found seat at bar - no service. Finally got menu from seater & said we only wanted drinks - told she didn't do that. Waited & waited - no bar service.  The place isn't busy but the 3x bartenders were to busy talking amongst themselves.
TERRIBLE & refuse to go back. Lot's of nearby options.

Absolutely love this place and have never had a bad meal! I usually get the ahi tuna which has never disappointed. Burgers are amazing as well as the fish and chips. No matter what time of day we are here, and we are the old folks that show up at 2:00 just because we can, the cocktails are awesome. Just seriously wish they served raw oysters!

The food here is just terrific. It's the service that really disappointed me on my last visit. It was a Thursday night. Fairly busy, but we were seated within 15 mins. For the rest of the evening, though, I felt mostly ignored. Did you ever go to a spot where you feel like an outsider, like everyone else is a "friend" but you are a stranger? That's how we felt. I watched waiters dote on other diners--filling water, checking in--but I had to flag someone down from across the room to get a water refill. The worst part, though, was when--in the MIDDLE of our meal--a waiter asked us to move to another table so they could accommodate a big group. Up we went, dragging our plates and wine. I really felt disrespected--as though the other customers were more valuable than we were.

Pasta was great and the broccoli rabe with sausages is excellent. I just wish the service was as expertly prepared.

I saw all the reviews and was thinking, of course these yelpers know what a good doughnut is. The dont! The staple of any doughnut place that you shouldnt get incorrect is the glazed. Had those and they were blah! Texture kind of gummy and the flavor was bad. Had some fritters also and they werent the best either. Maybe the other doughnuts were good but if you cant get the basics right I dont trust anything else

They were missing half of the items on the menu and it took too long. I was also discouraged from making a hat out of the food by other patrons. I wanted to wear my food as I believe is my God-given right but now I am a bad boy! I'm such a bad boy! I took my food home and added it to the food heap on the living room floor. Anyone is welcome to add to the pile. The carpet is behind ruined so I'm not worried about cleaning it up. I asked for complimentary lipstick and no one knew what I was talking about. This restaurant also doesn't have a bad boy menu for bad boys.

I was suprised to look Antoine's up on yelp and see 3 star avg.  With the grade inflation at Yelp I would say that's not a good sign.  Unfortunately I will have to add to that with this review.

The good things about Antoine's are the physical space and the staff. Spacious, cool, not to noisy at lunch.  The staff was inefficient and friendly, not overly gloating or trying to be entertaining but just right.  Good advice on menu too. She actually steered us away from a couple of things she did not like. Rare.

But the food was not so special.  I had the fixed lunch menu, great price at $20.18.  I started with the tomato soup which was very good. earned a star.  

Then came the appetizers which I would not have ordered but you have to dance with them that brung ya.  oysters rockefellar, which they invented I understand. oyster I had was plump and wonderful but too much of everything else around it overpowered in.  Escargo 2 different ways, again snails were very good but too much of everything else on top, oil etc.

I should mention the bread which they were generouns with.  It was very tasty but not the right consistency, definitely not close to real French bread, too chewy.  And the butter was salted. How is it that a famous NOLA French restaurant does not know you do not serve SALTED butter. You can go the the holiest of holes in the wall in Europe and you will never get salted butter.

On to the meal, fried drum. very fresh and well fried. It had a tomato sauce on it that I was not wild about but was good. However, the rice pilaf it was served over was terrible.  I could taste the dame dried spice.  nothing worse than that taste, ruins everything.  its one thing for a chef at a fancy restaurant to use some dry spice instead of fresh but to use too much!  are you kidding me. what are they teaching them in cooking school these days.

others at table had the pompano (too dry they said) softshell crab (really good)

Dessert was bread pudding, nothing special. really amazing that this restaurant cant find someone to bake good bread pudding.  ITS NOT THAT HARD!

sorry Antoine's you are trading on your name and selling inferior food,  like some other restaurants in town. Yeah I am talking about you commanders palace

After a wedding and reception downtown, I decided to treat myself by staying downtown. The Sheraton is a nice hotel. You gotta watch for where to pull in to park or you will miss it.
The rooms are a fair size and everything was in good repair. I paid the extra money to have a river view. If you stay on the downtown side it can get noisy with the street noise.
Overlooking the Hillsborough river is serene and quite tranquil. I enjoyed my short respite, the bed was very comfy, staff could have been friendlier and the room could use some upgrades for the price. Overall it was an enjoyable experience. Parking is a joke and way overpriced.
What's a girl to do? Take a quick break and don't look back.

CHEERY STAFF: I'm beginning to think that Esplanade Avenue is the new "neutral ground" that officially divides the fun, upbeat 20-somethings upriver; from the morose zombies that sling hash downriver.  The servers at Capdeville are interesting, funny and actually enjoy working there.  Further, they have manners: at least 2 or 3 welcomed me when I arrived, just as many actually thanked me when I left!  Sure, the kitchen can be slow, but at least you will have a pleasant time waiting.

TRUFFLE OIL MAC & CHEESE is a must, and when coupled with the the Mayor's Melt (a flavorful mix of Gouda, roasted aioli, red chili ketchup & grilled onion) it's a meal that easily feeds two for just $20!

OTHER EATERIES WITH UPBEAT "NON TREND" 20-ISH STAFF: Cowbell, Artz, Café Amelie, Blue Dot, La Boulangerie, La Casita, Taqueria Guerrero, The Grocery, Carmo, 801 Royal, District Donuts, Slim Goodies, Café Nero, Mister Gregory's, Root, Domenica, Little Vic's, Coquette, Café Reconcile.

Been here once for dinner. It's a small place inside but appeared to have a nice patio bar area with music. We waited longer than expected for a table but once seated and ordered the food came rather quickly. The menu prices are rather inexpensive, unless you order combo platters or build your own, which we did. Next time we may opt for a basket as we had a lot of food. We ordered the fried clam strips as an appetizer. These were huge, lightly fried and not super greasy. Probably the best clam strips I've had anywhere. I also had blackened grouper and scallops. The grouper was good, but I've had better and the scallops were delicious. My husband's surf and turf platter included a steak which was tough and chewy, but let's face it, it's more of a seafood place! After a while it seemed we fell off the radar and were forgotten about. My water wasn't ever refilled and it took forever to get our bill. All in all the positives outweighed any negatives and I would definitely go back. It's a great little seafood place that's casual and so close to home. I would like to try one of the all-you-can-eat fish nights they have during the week.
a pretty average bar environment... with loud folks and smokey air... but, the beer list is definitely worth a trip!  i didn't get a chance to try the food though...

I was unable to even go to eat here because there was 7 people in my party. What restaurant doesn't take a reservation for parties over 6. The manager was supposed to call be back when she came in at 5pm. Never got the call but was told when I called back 3 hours later  she went home early. But they could see my message. 15 minutes later I get a call from the manager to say that she couldn't take my reservation. I guess they don't need the business!

Super hipster place. I felt like I needed to drive a Subaru and have a bike rack to park here. But, with that being said, the food is phenomenal. I got the barbacoa burrito and this thing was huge. And I don't even bother with the rabbit food (lettuce, tomato, onion, etc) and even without all that stuff this was a very hefty burrito. I also got a fish taco just to give it a swing and it was really good. The fried fish was crispy on the outside but very tender and juicy. The service was quick too. When we got there the line was almost out the door, which isn't a huge feat because the place is pretty small, but the line moved very quickly. The prices were also very reasonable, I think it was around $50 for 4 people to eat. I wouldn't hesitate to come here again.

Services requested: toe repolish (smudged work from a different shop) and design on each ring finger.

I will start by saying the shop is cute and they do offer water or wine. I was pressed for time and declined both options but it is a nice touch to offer. I needed just the last three toes on both feet retouched because they were smudged. He wouldn't negotiate the price down and insisted I pay $12 for toe color change. I will say my toes came out nice but my solution could have saved me money and him time. Also they were busy so it was quite a bit of starting and stopping which made the visit longer than it needed to be imo. 
Second I asked for design on two fingers total. I was informed it would be a total of $15 for both fingers and I agreed. Less than 10 hours later the 3-D design has broken off my nail, also some of the stones within the design have fallen off as well. I was so disappointed my money had been wasted like that. Also there is a $2 transaction fee if you use debit or credit card no matter how much you spend. I find that a bit unpleasant. I'm also from out of town so returning to have my nails fixed wasn't an option.

I am a big fan of their Chicken Biryani. Eating there almost every weekend since last two year since my USF days. But in my recent visits, I have observed that they reduced the number of Chicken Pieces (which use to be 3 pieces in a single biryani) to 2 pieces which is not fair for the price. All I got today was 2 pieces (1 medium, 1 small) of chicken with loads of rice. I have also noticed a new option in their menu today to buy extra chicken pieces for the biryani. Not a good way to do business by taking out pieces from which was standard at 3 pieces since a long time and charging customers to pay extra for the pieces. Makes a bad impression on regulars!

We had an amazing experience at Lorusso's!  For appetizers we had the toasted ravioli and calamari fritti, both were very good.  Next time I would like to try the flash fried spinach, the table next to us ordered it and it looked delicious.  We tried the della casa and caprese salads- excellent.  For dinner we ordered the chicken spedini, eggplant lasagna, nanna's stuffed eggplant, and the scampi spinaci...everything was very tasty- great marinara sauce.  For dessert we had the pear cobbler and the tiramisu, awesome!  We will definitely be back!

I took a trip to New Orleans with my extended family the weekend of December 1st for my father's 60th birthday. We booked what we thought would be a "fun" tour, the New Orleans original cocktail tour. Mind you, I have a 7 month old that came on our family vacation. We did our research knowing that we would learn history of New Orleans, and the history of the cocktails and we would see some famous restaurants where these drinks came from. 
When we checked into the tour, there is an elderly gentleman that was the tour guide, came over to my group and shouted disgustingly "I have never taken a baby on my tour, babies are not allowed on this tour". That just gave the premise of what was going to happen next. 
Mind you that everyone in New Orleans I have come into contact with this past weekend was exceptionally nice, except for the Grayline tour representatives, and it disgusted me. 
I had to go back to the ticket window to identify why this now was, with the entire tour and the ticket window representative at Grayline tours being dumbfounded as to why a baby can't attend a walking tour in New Orlenas, because it DID NOT MAKE SENSE, that a sleeping baby in a stroller is not allowed on a history tour of new Orleans, that yes you go into restaurants where they serve drinks???? This went up the ladder very quickly as the tour had to depart. The Director Cheryl then came out to speak with me, and said, a baby has never been on this tour and is not allowed, and this went back and forth for about 5 minutes, with honestly no reasoning. This was very disgusting on their part, did offer any other tours, and then had no availability when we followed up for me and my family for any other tours this weekend for us to go on. Still did not get a refund. 
Taking a step back, why did Grayline tours not allow my family of 6 and a 7month old sleeping baby attend a history tour around New Orleans? Because there was alcohol? Because there was restaurants that serve alcohol? Why couldn't the baby follow the tour and not enter the establishment was what we asked and the Grayline still said your whole group CANNOT go on the tour now. The restaurants or bars should establish if they would let a child enter, not the tour guide not the tour guide company. The elderly tour guide was just terrible how he came off saying "I have never", as opposed to "hi how are you, lets discuss something". Learn hospitality like everyone else we met this weekend. 
Truly disgusting in a tourist environment to do. Grayline, you should really evaluate family tours, and yes this is a family tour if you are wondering, because it's a history tour first, get drunk second. 
Also, I travel and take my child everywhere, there has never been an issue entering an establishment at 2PM in the afternoon and them asking my child for identification.

We've satisfactorily donated here several times and we've satisfactorily purchased here several times and plan to continue doing both.  "Truckee Meadows Habitat for Humanity is part of a global, nonprofit housing organization that builds homes, communities and hope.  Truckee Meadows Habitat for Humanity is dedicated to eliminating substandard housing locally and worldwide through constructing, rehabilitating and preserving homes; by advocating for fair and just housing policies; and by providing training and access to resources to help families improve their shelter conditions.  Habitat for Humanity was founded on the conviction that every man, woman, and child should have a simple, durable place to live in dignity and safety, and that decent shelter in decent communities should be a matter of conscience and action for all.

All are welcome: Truckee Meadows Habitat for Humanity has an open-door policy: All who believe that everyone needs a decent, affordable place to live are welcome to help with the work, regardless of race, religion, age, gender, political views, or any of the other distinctions that too often divide people.  In short, Habitat welcomes volunteers and supporters from all backgrounds and also serves people in need of decent housing regardless of race or religion.  As a matter of policy, Habitat for Humanity International and its affiliated organizations do not proselytize.  This means that Habitat will not offer assistance on the expressed or implied condition that people must either adhere to or convert to a particular faith, or listen and respond to messaging designed to induce conversion to a particular faith."

According to the Yelp ratings' definitions, Three Stars is 'A-OK'.  That to me is an acceptable rating of a business that does day in and day out what they are in business to do.  It is certainly NOT a negative rating.  Four and Five-Stars to me is when a business goes over and beyond what they are in the business to do.  One and Two-Star Ratings are for when a business fails to do what they are in the business to do.

Meat was good - we had to specifically ask to get the filet. Mashed potatoes were dry. Rolls were great and lobster bisque was fabulous. We specifically said we were celebrating a birthday- they never acknowledged. The server, James disappeared several times leaving us empty glasses and never bothered to clear empty plates. Left us waiting 20 minutes or more just to get the bill and the place wasn't even that busy. If you are looking for awesome food and service to celebrate a special occasion- Go elsewhere.

Went there on Tuesday for the first time, food was mediocre and prices high. $88 for dinner for 4. Felt like I walked into a cici's pizza, very poor ambiance. Would go back just to try the pizza but would prolly do carry out. Credit card reader was attached to a smart phone, first time I've used one of those.

I recently visited this spa for a little getaway by myself. From the time I was picked up at the airport until the time I left I was treated like a queen. the room was very comfortable. I had a ball walking around the resort and spa. The food was great and was served in a timely manner. Make sure you take the time to book a treatment at the spa. And make sure you locate the plunge pool and take the plunge into the chilly mineral waters. I reccomend the inclusive spa package! I cannot wait to go back.

Came here on the recommendation of a friend.  Big line when I rolled up and total disorganization. You have to be allowed in to the restaurant to order and to pick up your food.  I ordered and was told cash only. Seeing as I only ordered a cheesesteak I figured it would not take as long as the pizzas other people ordered. Went to wait outside.  Everyone packed around the restaurant, with many saying they had been waiting 45 minutes to an hour.  They call the names of the orders out from inside so if you can't understand exactly what they yelled you are basically stuck trying to guess if your order is ready or not.  Then, once they call out a set of names you have to wait again in line to then pick up your order and pay.

Given how long it was taking and how unorganized things were I should have known better and walked away, but I decided to wait seeing as I was in no rush.  Finally, about an hour later, my order was ready.  After waiting in line to pick up my order and finally paying, I get my cheesesteak, which is warm, not even hot.  On top of that, it cost $13, which is the most expensive cheesesteak I can remember ordering in Philly.

As for the quality, to me, this probably ranked among the worst cheesesteaks I have had in Philly (granted most are great so not saying a lot). The meat and cheese was fine, chopped up and the cheese adequately mixed with the meat.  Where things fell apart for me was the bread. They use a seeded roll more reminiscent of a french bread style with a firmer texture than your standard amoroso roll.  The seeds were far too overpowering of a flavor in the sandwich, which ultimately somewhat ruined it for me.

Many people say the pizza is great, but I did not try it and given the mess their ordering process was, I'm not sure I will be back to try it.

So for the first time ever, I didn't have a horrible experience at this McDonald's. I live pretty close by and come in once every month or two (usually for take-out), and usually there is a combination of some really sketchy people (talking to themselves/possibly high on drugs) and old people (getting free tea refills), coupled with a long line served by young cashiers. Here's a sampling of things that have happened to me in the past: given me medium fries instead of a large and charging me for the large, forgetting to give me the muffin I ordered, didn't give me ANY sauces for my McNuggets (where are you, my sweet and sour??), couldn't count and didn't give me back the correct change, and the worst one - sometime last year, on a busy Saturday around lunchtime, we waited in line for about 10 minutes before overhearing the cashier tell the people in front of us that they had run out of fries. RUN OUT OF FRIES!!! How could they have let that happen?? 
Anyway, this time went as smoothly as it could have gone: no line, I used a coupon without any problem, cashier charged me the right amount, and my food was ready about 1 minute later. Of course, my fries were a little stale, and my Big Mac was, as usual, haphazardly slapped together, but all in all, an acceptable experience.
Oh and in case you ever need a late-night McDonald's fix, this one is open 24/7. Just beware of the people in there at that hour...

Don't do it.  The dolma taste canned.  The hummos is just so average.  The prices are way too high.  If you have ever eaten good Greek food you will be so disappointed.  I don't get how this place stays in business and has been around for so long.  I really love Greek food and this is in my neighborhood.  I wish it were better.  

The regulars (owners?) who sit at the sidewalk tables during the day belch choking cigarette smoke that you can smell from doors away.  However, they are so sweet and super nice to my dog.  Really good seeming people.  I wish I had something nicer to say about the restaurant.
