The first desk lady was super friendly great face to walk into the location .... the refresh of the brand looked nice but the food did not match the new brand concept ..... the food was old school and needing some updating .... but the food was ok same same Shoney's breakfast I had when I was a child over 25 years ago..... the servers weren't that attentive .... a guest was asking for help ... she looked at me and kept talking about her night ..... shoneys you are on your way but the culinary needs some refreshing as well to capture a wider audience.... the marketing team did a great job with the look of the corporate building

I was looking at "nearby" activity in Santa Barbara, and I saw this Viet bakery that serves banh mi.  I also found out they make their own bread, and serve burritos & phô.  Mama's is on Hollister across from Goleta Valley Hospital.

I bought 3 small banh mi w/ ham, meatloaf, & cotto ($6.25), and a cà phê sa đá (no pic).  While I was waiting, I was admiring the racks of freshly baked bread, and goodies in the case.

The banh mi were OK.  There was a fair amount of meat, but the bread was not was not as good as Westminster or GG.  In addition, the price was high:  3 Stars.  However, the cà phê sa đá was really good, and comparable to Westminster or Garden Grove.  It was strong, and sweet the way a good iced coffee should be:  4 Stars.

Regarding the ambiance, it is a hole-in-the-wall, but that adds to the charm.  I saw people running in, and grabbing something to eat on the fly:  4 Stars.  Likewise, the Viet family that owns this place are supah nice.  Their daughter works here too, and she obviously takes after her parents:  4 Stars.

I would return to try the phô, some baked goods, and grab another banh mi (despite the price).  The service is friendly, the cà phê sa đá is good, and its nice to patronize a good Azn biz in a non-Azn enclave.

I only continue to go here out of pure convenience. They do not do the greatest  
Work.. leaving windows smudged and they don't throughly clean when you pay the extra $ to do specific areas.

yes ian in light iof the day and the sadness the food was great and your hospitally was very nice and hope to get down h
there some day to eat again and give you some ideas but wanted to let you know as a person that been involved such as running alacarte places and years also in fine dining you are going in the right directions also it would be a nice questure it you put like a little comp on the table when people come in like cheese port wine and horseradish and the cheddar cheese spead or rattatotie just small plate people like that but just wanted to let you know food was great and deserts were superb keep up good work want to get down there hope door open thank murph michael

The place looked ok upon check in. I was only staying one night then flying out the next morning. They put me on the top floor, 15. Still ok. The room next door had the tv volume wide open. I didn't think much of it at check in as I went on a walk down to the Truckee River. Got back after a beer and dinner. TV still blasting. TV blasted all night still blasting when my alarm went off at 5am. 
Was told the desk was manned 24/7. Stopped at the desk for them to call a taxi to airport since the free shuttle doesn't start until 6:45am. Not what they told me when I booked the room. There was no one at the desk. After 10 minutes, I guess I woke the guy up in the back room. 
He called a taxi and pointed to the door where I should wait. 20 minutes, no taxi. I go back in and ask him to call again. No taxi. I go back in. He had told me to wait at the wrong door. 
What's up with not properly training employees in the service industry??

Been to Liberty Hall probably a dozen plus times since it opened. Always great artisan pizza. Love the casear salad with anchovies. The dressing is well balanced and not too creamy. And best of all, Liberty Hall is BYOB, so one can bring their fav wine or beer. Service is also always fast and friendly. I'll be back!
Major drug use in the building. A lot of partying and folks damaging property. Not safe for families.

They are getting two stars only because the menu looked amazing and I would have LOVED to eat here.  We arrived a little after 8 pm, sat outside because it was very noisy inside and then waited for our server.  After about 10 minutes someone finally showed up to welcome us and take our drink orders.  The drinks took a while to come because they did not have the beer my husband wanted and he had to reorder.  We then placed our order which included a cold appetizer, two salads and a shared plate.  25 minutes later, we were still waiting on our cold appetizer of pickled vegetables with bread.  Needless to say, we canceled everything and asked for the bill as we were on a reasonable time table with our kids coming home.  The excuse we got was that there were two large parties ahead of us, so I guess our money doesn't matter.  They brought the bill without even trying to make it better or even compting us a drink.  I mean come on, your pour us 4 ozs of wine each and you can't even give us one for free after we have the inconvenience of trying to go on a date night and not even getting food.  The final nail in the coffin, is that I go to look at an uncleared table the entire time, a half eaten burger with fries and other trash.  SO disappointing, as we live in neighborhood and really like to support local business.

I really wanted to like this place the first time I went and the second time I hoped that it got better.  I agree with other Yelpers on here that for Boise this is probably pretty good but in a big city it would be pretty average. 

The bread loaf with olive oil and balsamic is out of this world. 

Salad is good with a blue cheese vinaigrette, but nothing out of this world.

Spaghetti was overly salted marinara sauce and too much oregano but the garlic bread was like toasted french bread with butter and no garlic flavor.

Quattro Formaggio was super oily with no flavor which I can't believe because it had bacon in it.  Not sure how that happens. Took 2 bites and decided to take the rest to go for a reheat leftover.  I had to choke it down as a leftover, ate half and threw the rest away, still had no flavor.
 
Berry crisp started out great but as we kept eating still had frozen berries. 

Server asked if we liked our food when we were done.  She did say she would pass on to the kitchen staff the info we gave her.

Overall it seemed like dishes couldn't balance between no flavor and overly seasoned.  Like I said I really wanted to like this place. :-(
Ordered a margarita pizza and cheesy bread. Everything lacked salt, I had to slather the pizza in ranch to give it any flavor. Delivery was fast but next time I will order from somewhere else.

After reading an old Philly.com article about the history of this fine establishment me and my hula girl boogied down for a late lunch, and Poi were we happy! We went with a Mochi Nori fried chicken and a 1/2 and 1/2 of the Spicy Ahi and Shoyu Ahi poke bowls. We began our culinary vacation with the chicken - crisp and tender with a creamy dressing joined by some sticky rice and a mac salad. After scarfing that down we moved onto the poke(mon) bowl as we had to catch em all. The fresh fish was delicious and plentiful for a good price (it can be ordered by the pound to go). This was just the beginning of our 50 First Dates to this island treasure located in the heart of Center City.

I had an inspection done last year by Ely Stokes after a condo in my community had a fire in their fireplace. The inspector told me that my fireplace was unsafe and had a ring that was completely rusted out just about the box going up into the chimney. He showed me pictures of what was supposed to be the inside of my fireplace. I was told that it all needed to be replaced and was unsafe to use. The quote I received was 10,000 to have this all done. I finally had a second inspection done last week and found out that there is nothing wrong with my fireplace absolutely no rust and it doesn't even need swept. I even asked the inspector 5 times to double check before I told him about my previous inspection. He even knew what company it was with out me even telling him. I can't believe a company would be so dishonest and try to take advantage of someone like that. I am not sure if the inspector just saw an opportunity to make money because I am a single women, but I will never recommend anyone to them.

My impression of this restaurant has always been that it was a tourist spot, but not a tourist trap. I heard the food was not bad. So I went to check it out. The atmosphere is like any family restaurant in America. Nothing special. The tables are bunched together. And of course, there's the big flat screen TV everywhere.  I guess the brick walls kind of remind you you're in the French Quarter. The food was either fried or in heavy cream. Nothing really healthy on the menu. The portions were small, the prices were high. The pastas came with no sides, just a couple of small pieces of french bread. On the positive, the food was good. So if you eat here, you will like the food. If you have a healthy appetite, you'll need to order an appetizer and maybe a desert to be full. My main issue with this place is the small portions.

Upon visiting other Winn Dixie's (fuel perks has made me a Winn Dixie connoisseur) I can safely say that this Winn Dixie is bullshit. 
It's marginally  closer than the one on Veterans and so (up until today) I normally went there to be greeted by much of the same: no selection of anything, rude guests, disgruntled employees, wishes I could get everything at Farmer's Markets. 
I don't know why this location is allowed to be open and I really feel horrid for the people who have no choice but to go to this dump

The people who work there are not super helpful and the selection is better at Planet Organic.  However, they have a few delicious flavours of dairy-free ice cream.  Lemon and Mango/Pineapple are the best :)

Location is great, views from the water-side rooms are awesome. Aaaaand that's about as much positivity as I can muster. 
This place was tired in the 90s, and has been continually used and abused since then with little regard for cleanliness and aesthetics. 
So much potential, but the owners have clearly made the decision to put exactly ZERO money into updates or repairs. There's deferred maintenance EVERYWHERE you look. 
It's okay for a boozy weekend with the boys, but the  classy ladies are going to be grossed out by this place.
Just make sure you understand what you're getting into.

This year's pop-up garden on South Street is worth 3 stars out of 5. It just felt way too crowded and packed into a very small space. I enjoyed the food from the Jamaican Jerk Hut, and the dark and stormy cocktail (my absolute favorite), but we couldn't find a seat the entire hour that we were there. Regardless, I loved the decoration, the plants, the music speakers hidden in fake rocks, and more. Can we bring this back to last year's location again? Or another more spacious place next year?

If you're looking for good, authentic New Orleans food this is a must try! Daiquiri's were amazing! They have so many flavors to choose from. The combination platter was delicious. My favorite was the crawfish étouffée. The seafood okra gumbo was good too (only if you like okra) the consistency was very strong. 

Would def recommend and would come back if ever in the area again.

Super fast and efficient. Great portions and nice ambiance

Can't stand this unethical immoral place.  My 80-year old father-in-law and his 72-year-old wife walk in and are "sold" on a 42-inch LG LED TV.  They spent a little over $600 on the TV, so they got a pretty good deal.  That's where the good news ends.  

The snake, jackass of a salesman CONVINCED the elderly people that they HAD TO HAVE some $300+ Panamax home theater management/surge protection system because the horribly unstable electricity in Reno will surely wipe out their new television.  Scared, my in-laws agreed that they needed this stupid thing, so they bought it.  Then they were convinced to purchase a 4-year extended warranty for another $120.

I then later found out that Best Buy offers no interest financing if you spend more than-- you guessed it-- $1000.  So let's do the math:  $600+ for the TV; $300+ for the surge protector; $100+ for the extended warranty.  Just over $1000, how convenient!

Furious, I contacted the store manager, some kid named "Rob."  Completely unsympathetic, he tried to convince me that my in-laws actually got a really great deal and would be very happy with their purchase of the surge protector.  In fact, he suggested that I go on-line and see for myself just how good these products were.  Indeed, one could spend well over $1000 for a really good home theater management system, so according to "Rob," spending a little more than $300 is a great investment.

I told him my in-laws would be returning to the store to demand a full refund for the surge protector.  Suffice to say, this will be the last time any of our family ever steps foot in that horrible store!

If Koch's Deli were any closer to campus, I'd probably die from binge eating their sandwiches.

Seriously, the sandwiches here are incredible. And GIGANTIC. Each sandwich is just layer upon layer upon layer of juicy, delicious meat. I honestly have no idea how so many layers can exist in one humble sandwich. I couldn't even open my mouth wide enough to eat the Penn Special that I bought!

Each sandwich is a bit pricey and takes a while to make, but it's completely worth it! The employees also frequently give out deli meat samples to the people waiting, so it's completely possible that you get your deli meat fix before your sandwich is even done being made.

Such a tiny and unassuming deli. Such mind-blowingly amazing sandwiches.

I am just counting down the hours until I go there again and try another one of their specials.

Bought a car there in May 2018.  They still have not given me the information on the company who put on vinyl stripes.  Do not trust what they say.  The oil was NOT changed and the car had defective tires.   They will tell you anything to get you to buy then after you pay for the car you will get little cooperation.

Somewhere between a diner and an awesome breakfast joint is the BBD.
-
I had an hour+ to kill and was hungry so I decided to dwell and sup at this fine and friendly place.

I had the Steak and Eggs w/bacon. and I have to say I left very happy and satisfied. It was prepared just as I had ordered it, and the staff were excellnet to myself and infant daughter.
Last minute booking with first rate charm, cleanliness and character! Location is fabulous, barefoot to the beach and walking distance to great restaurants , a fabulous wine bar and on the 699

This is one of the better pizza joints in the Warminster area. They have a huge selection of pies that will satisfy your appetite. Compared to the local competition that make an equally good pie they are expensive. Must be the high rent in this dying strip mall. Example: Stopped in for a single slice...$4.00 bucks. Eight slices in a large pie so that's $32.00?? Yes the price is a little lower for a large pie but not by much. They gouge you for a single slice. Other prices on their menu are very expensive. Again it must be the high rent especially after the Acme closed.

Let's look at the close competition. Roman Delight right up the street has a better menu, really good food, equally good pizza with coupons making this a better value.

Uncle Joe's Pizza Shop. Located at Street and York Road. Right behind the Eagle Dinner in the strip mall. This is my favorite for pizza. They have the best tasting pizza at the lowest price. Their menu is extensive. Any possible pizza they can make for you. The other food is equally good at a fair price. They offer fast delivery.

It's your money. Spend it where you get the best value.
The best customer service possible at this location. Always a pleasure seeing Faith at drop off and pick up.

We had a wonderful dinner at Flemings tonight.  The steak and lobster were wonderful, as were the appetizers.  The wine and beer were also great.  Service was perfect, and they even gave us champagne for our birthdays.
Stick to the Pho and the jasmine tea!   The rest is disgusting.  This place is so gross I'm never going back.  You people need taste buds.
Would have given a 1 but the Tampa seane downtown is that Terrible.  The wait staff spent more time looking at there bad reviews on yelp.  Wow.
I got the chicken tikka platter-- decent flavor, large portions, forgotten sauce... oh well I ate it with sour cream instead, which sufficed.

This review is just based off the cinnamon rolls. We ordered a box of twelve and they were all different sizes, some where huge some were tiny but all the same price. The actual taste was good but nothing EXCEPTIONAL. I think it taste like Cinnabon, but obviously with better ingredients.  If you don't eat it right away it's a bit dry. 
Also, we ordered the blueberry scone that looked really good, it was just okay but not spectacular, since it was crumbly and dry. But it has a nice texture on the outside.


However, The lady working was SO nice and sweet.

Skip the food; you can eat better elsewhere for the same price. That's not the reason you came here anyway; If you want a muffuletta, just go to Central Grocery. You want to drink Pimm's Cups and Mint Juleps. Not that you can't get better versions of either a block up the street at The Hermes bar, but you came here for the atmosphere. That's what you came here for. You want to drink coll drinks out in a French Quarter courtyard. So, yeah, do that.

My first time trying Maggie on the move and I have mixed feelings. WE had the falafel gyro, which had great flavor, the veggies were crisp and pita was nice and soft. I don't think the falafel was fried and the texture was a little more mushy than what I'm use to. On the other hand I do like the fact that it's not fried.
It might have needed to cook longer to crisp it up but all the flavors were great. 

We also ordered 2 dolmades, which I love.They are one if my favorite things to eat and they had them without meat which is the way I like them. Unfortunately , the were not good, I only took two bites and threw them away. The rice was hard, undercooked and I  think the leaves weren't  rinsed well. In both bites, it was like I was crunching down on sand.
Looking at the reviews this might have been just an off night so I'll be sure to try them again and update my review.

The boba is good! Especially the black milk tea smoothie. My personal favorite. Their udon is nothing special; tastes like microwavable noodle bowls. But everything else is good, especially for the price. And customer service is good as well.

Crystal Brady did an awesome job handling the sale and purchasing  side of our house. She was always there for us when needed and always made us feel like a priority. We would recommend her to anyone who is serious about selling their house or buying. Because once she gets the job, she will not stop until she can hang the sold sign out front. Great person and realtor! She's the best!!!! We love you, Crystal!!!!

Sweat Fitness on 24th and Walnut is a small gym that makes the most of its minimal footprint.  The gym, which takes up one floor and can be seen almost entirely from the floor to ceiling windows that face out on Walnut Street, is divided neatly into a cardio area and weight area.  Additionally, there's a multipurpose studio which is used for group fitness classes (i.e. yoga, zumba, aerobics, bootcamp) and a smaller studio for spin classes.  There's also one other small room for stretching/abs behind the main weightlifting area.

Pros:
While small, the gym features a lot of equipment.  The cardio area has a great number of treadmills, ellipticals, and stationary bikes.  They also recently got a slate of brand new cardio machines too, which is always a plus.  In the weight area, there are plenty of options for machines and a wide array of dumbbells.  It also never gets overly crowded during peak hours so you'll never have to wait to use any of the equipment.  The trainers are friendly and there's generally a good rapport between the gym regulars and the staff.  Finally, the classes, which are included with the monthly fee, are definitely worth it.  I've taken the spin classes and they are pretty intense but so worth it.  The location is also great for those who live in Rittenhouse/Fitler and commute to University City daily.  Plus, you can see the people walking by on the street as you work out.

Cons:
This gym, overall, feels dirty, and I'm not the only reviewer here that feels that way.  I don't care how often they clean the gym or clean the equipment, but there's a general feeling of dirtiness that is pervasive whenever I go.  The Men's locker room is a joke: small, cramped, and I would never, ever, ever want to shower or change there (usually I leave my belongings locked in a locker and retrieve them after my workout).  The spinning studio is also uncomfortably small.  I also wish that they would upgrade the equipment, and most of all, put in a leg press machine (similar to the leg curl machine they already have).  The membership fee is also a little high considering what the gym offers you in terms of space and equipment (could be lower).  Plus, you can see the people walking by on the street as you work out.

Overall, I am probably only a member of this gym because of the location and they have the basic number of machines I would need to have a good workout.  It's an ok gym, but I will probably choose not to renew my membership when it is up for renewal.

It's Cinco De Mayo so what better place to enjoy tacos, right? Unfortunately not.
They were pretty crowded, not surprised. Never worried about that. Having a packed house is always great. However it isn't so great when your family is seated in a back corner table and forgot about. 
We sat patiently for 20 minutes waiting for someone to order our drinks and food. The worst part is, we watched people get sat after us and have their orders taken and drinks delivered. We even tried to wave our waiter down! How embarrassing. We got fed up and just left. I let a man in charge at the cashier know and he didn't apologize or even try to help the situation. 
Like I said, busy day. I get it. But make sure you have enough people on the floor to serve your paying customers. 
Won't be returning as of right now, sometimes all it takes is one bad experience.

I bought a groupon for an oil change last Saturday on my way to Instant Lube and able to pull right in the lube bay when we got there. Our truck was done in less than 15 minutes. The guys there were very courteous and quick. I will definitely be returning!

Very unhappy with my pizza. I ordered pizza  wings a few nights ago, the wings were very good, but I couldn't even eat my pizza. I ordered it well done, not only was it not well done but it under cooked  and very sloppy. Today was garbage day and most of the pizza went out in the trash.

Mid-priced sneakers, shoes and sandals for men, women and kids.
Decent selection and decent prices - often buy one get one 1/2 off, etc.

Better quality than payless. Not as good as "real" shoe stores.

I can usually snag decent summer sandals here and my husband who favors a "casual" work shoe got 2 pairs here recently.

The Doylestown store is much cleaner and nicer than the one in South Philly.

We're pleased enough to be repeat shoppers, but true fashionistas may balk.

I have always loved Kobe's food, but they cannot answer their phones to save their life. I end up calling their restaurant for an hour trying to order take out and no one ever picks up the phone. It's very aggravating if i'm on a time frame. And the best part is when I get there in person, and there's practically no inside. However their food keeps me coming back.

Zahav has an incredible amount of hype where everyone knows that it is THE top restaurant in Philly and if you want to go you'll need to reserve a spot months in advance. I know because of that people have so high of an expectation that nothing could ever meet it. I say it's definitely worth checking out for yourself because it is as great as people say it is....the hummus is actually some of the best I've ever had that I dream of it. I love how it's more of a family style environment where you get to order a lot of small plates and share with the table so you can have the full experience and try all the sides, meats and desserts. The service was great, the atmosphere was beautiful and it was overall a really great experience.

This one of my favorite restaurants in New Orleans. Love the red beans so much that I ordered one to eat and another to go. Highly reccomend!!! Taylor and Anthony are awesome bartenders. Great service!!!

Our waitress was super! The food was ok! 
The glasses from the bar has lip stick  on them!
Need to learn how to clean the glasses or get a new dishwasher!!

The location is great if you need to be close to the Quarter.  Adorable hotel with a clean pool.  The room was very small, but the bed was comfy.  The cleaning crew decided to clean the walkway outside of the rooms at 7 am on a sat. morning.  After an hour of covering our heads with the pillows, my spouse had to go out and ask them to stop.  Other people were complaining too because it was extremely loud.  The noise from the bars across the street until 3 am is to be expected, but 7 am pressure washing?!  They did not have parking, but there is a lot around the corner.

This restaurant had been open well over a year. Mistakes on every meal ordered, whether pick-up or delivery. Delivery took TWO HOURS
 So, we decide to go simple. We ordered 3 Gyros and each year oh came with a side we ordered one with french fries one with a salad and one with hummus. They all three came with hummus which of course, I did not realize until I was already back home. Then as we sit down to eat we open up the boxes and the food, is refrigerator cold. I mean, they just took it out of the refrigerator and bagged it. I asked for extra tzatziki and was told no and the amount that they give you by the way is not even enough for half a sandwich I mean it's yogurt for goodness sake. Sad as I am to see another business dying in that great location they just need to either pick up the pieces and really get going or just shut the doors because I for sure will never be back and I feel sadly for them.
One last thing: a gyro is a sandwich what we received was rice yellow rice on top of and on top of that was gyro meat with one slice of tomato halved and put on the sand and put on the in the box and a sprinkling of parsley. That's it that's all we did get a side of pita for the homeless but that was it there was nothing to actually eat at as a gyro unless you use these little triangles.

My wife and I got takeout from here for dinner. I had the Beef Pho Special. The broth was excellent. My wife had the Chicken Spicy Lemongrass. She does not like spicy hot foods and they made it mild for her. I would order from here again when in the area.
Best rentals in town!!!!  My boyfriend and I rented bikes from Bike NOLA for Jazz Fest - couldn't have been happier with the bikes and service!!!!  We will definitely rent from Bike NOLA again!!!

Wonderfully service and sweet treats! This cute little spot had some delicious brownies and cookies and gooey bars also a great selection of ice creams we got chocolate and it was delish. Definitely recommend

Visiting my cousin in California who decided to take a day trip to Santa Barbara.  She is a fan of Brophy Bros and I see why.  We sat on the deck and had a great view of the marina. Food was excellent and I loved the chowder!!! 

One concern is that the deck is not accessible if you travel with someone in a wheel chair. The staff was quick to assist my cousin up the stairs who uses a wheeled walker for balance.

There are few family fun values as accessible and consistent as the Edmonton Eskimos empire. Not even CFL fans, we signed up a few years ago out of curiosity and have since upgraded out seats - and love every minute.

Yes, its football. There will be a loud, rowdy, possibly topless man nearby who has clearly had his fill of beer. But stadium staff are efficient at dealing with real problems, and the atmosphere is very family positive.

There is a non-alcoholic family section near the one endzone for those who would like to protect the very impressionable from people with poor decision making skills.

If you are a beer drinker, bring a can cozy as they have switched to wide mouth aluminium bottles this year that warm up fast in your hand.

And remember: holding an Eskimos ticket (any seat) gives you free Edmonton Transit (bus, LRT) for 2 hours before and after each home game. Save those toonies for some mini-doughnuts.

I lived here most of my college career. I always knew people that lived here at some point or another.

The apartments are sort of spacey, other than that, this place is gross/weird.

Yes, there are two bathrooms, but one has the shower (which will be used by EVERYONE) and the other has a tub. Really? Unless you're a chick, you're not gonna be using a tub, so it ends up being used as a storage room.

I feel the rugs under the sinks are just tacky, sorry, my opinion.

The kitchens are always terrible, the dark brown cabinets just make you feel depressed as hell.

The pool is cool (especially the sun-bathing girlies) but you can't drink/BBQ by it, so it gets lame.

Parking is horrible. One parking spot per apt building. People usually take your spot (I did a couple times, so what) And parking outside is always taken up by idiots parking in 2 spots to hold a spot for a roommate, or a motorcycle jerk taking a spot, or a piece of shit car that doesn't run so it's parked right out front and doesn't move for months. Anything else in the immediate vicinity is never available.

At night, they always have night security, but they're always dicks and hate that they have to work till like 6 AM telling kids to shut up.

And that brings me to my final comments, there are soooo many people packed here that it's impossible NOT to hear other people yelling/slamming doors/running/walking. During any sports event, there's always cheering. Don't get me wrong, I partook in this myself.

So. Cheap rent, but you get what you paid for.

P.S. some of those bikes have been parked out there for years, no doubt.

Hmm.... this was my first experience at the EEC when I attended a band's concert.  I thought I had paid money to watch Beirut perform, but to my surprise the headlining band was a bunch of pillars and columns.  Perhaps the band was hidden behind these columns? I don't know - but if there was a band on stage, you really had to be in a strategic position to see them. 

While the acoustics are only sub par and more like a 2-star (again, heavily dependent on where you are in the room), the lackluster yet expensive bar and lovely stench of cheap alcohol, vomit and other sorts of aromas, a potpourri really, gives the feel of a well beaten night club way past its golden years.

Entering this place is the best part. I certainly felt like I was at the Gaza strip border crossing into Israel. Everything was suspicious - the lady that was feeling me up (I suppose the only high point of this process) actually looked into my wallet - what was she looking for? Cyanide?  And of course, things were complicated because I was carrying my routine evening pills. As soon as she started looking into the pillbox with her expert eye that is definitely trained to spot E from a distance, I snatched the box from her and emptied the pills into my mouth.  I was like, 'here this is yours - do what you want with it!' 

I'm not sure what's with the security. For such a cheap atmosphere and experience it is weird that the security is so heightened.  Unfortunately, I'm a slave to the big businesses. If a band that I love plays here again, I will have to swallow that cyanide and watch them here.  Thank you, EEC.

Love a good barbecue and Half Liter knows how to make it right!  As part of the Sahm's family, this establishment knows what customers like.  I love the space, both inside and out.  A great setting for a place to meet friends or just a break off the Monon.  Tvs, games, patio seating, great food, awesome beers.

Seating can be a little confusing.  Several entrances to the building make it a strange space to figure out the first few times.  Half Liter is connected to Liter House is co elected to the Farmers Market space etc.  staff is easy to find and very helpful for any navigation issues.

Menus are bright and exciting.  I suggest getting g a few different things at your table and sharing.  All is good and you can't really go wrong.  

Also, fun stores right next door and in the area.  This is a great place to wonder around and just enjoy the Indy scene.  Hip and trendy.  Fabulous weekend brunch.

Came here for happy hour and am definitely a fan.

Their Sizzle, Swizzle, and Swirl happy hour menu is small but varied enough to accommodate most tastes.  They offer a couple of vodka drinks (cosmo, pomm martini), a gin drink, and a manhattan for the whiskey lovers among us. I ordered their normally $11 Manhattan, which was very large (came served in a large martini glass), very smooth, and very strong (what's not to love?), and at happy hour is only $8 (and is a serious deal given the size).  I also enjoyed the seared ahi tuna, which had a delicate flavor kicked up a notch with a nice (though light, not overly strong) mustard sauce. It is also a mere $8 on the happy hour menu.

The service was friendly and accommodating. We arrived at 4:30 and pretty much had our pick of spots in the bar area--by 6pm, it was packed, so if you're wanting to enjoy a happy hour in the bar, I would definitely get there on the earlier side of that happiest of hours (beginning at 4:30 here).

I haven't been here for a full blown meal--but given the happy hour deliciousness, that must change--soon!  Until then?  As far as the bar/happy hour offerings--definitely a fan!

Get to it!

This Sheraton is decent enough, and it provides a comfortable stay, but it feels kind of aged, and not in a vintage hotel, good way either. The staff is always courteous, and the location is great, but for the price I think I'll send my friends and family to newer hotels that feel nicer in the future.

Maybe I am biased to super nice people, but this Mom and Pop shop is ridiculously accommodating.  While staring down their vegan menu and asking about the choices, the owner Steve said that he would be willing to make anything I suggested if I had an interest in something that wasn't on the menu. Wow.  These people are sweet and genuine folks who want you to be happy with your food- really happy.

As for the food I ordered, it was gobbled up quite quickly. I believe the chik'n is Gardein, but the way they batter and fry and season it for the buffalo options is so tasty! Ciabatta is fresh and perfectly sized for their sandwiches.  The accompaniment of pickled cucumbers is reminiscent of an Asian pickled dish- refreshing.  I wish the sandwich had more veggies, but really, who orders a chik'n sandwich for the veggies?

The place is clean and has a good amount of seating.

The soups are all made from scratch- they didn't have the chipotle sweet potato but the white bean escarole was delicious. I feel most people would love it who like that creamy soup style, but I might have had a bit less salt if possible.

The salad greens and crab cake (vegan) were fresh. I was not a fan of the crab cake, but I have never liked crab cake! They were huge though!  There are several dressings to pick from, too.

I like this place a lot for their willingness to make you whatever you please, and they do it freshly, while you wait. It's good!!!

I've been in here for coffee and for Morning Glory muffins a few times. Whoever makes those Morning Glory muffins and sends them around to all the coffee shops is my hero!! Recently I sat with my friends and had a proper meal. Not the greatest falafel I've had and I wasn't really that WOWed by what my friends got either (a gyro, the Lahmacun - a Turkish pizzetta - and the beef Mousaka dish). I also am not that crazy about the space. I don't know, I just don't get a really welcoming or even cool vibe from there. I will probably go back because it's convenient but I wish the falafel were crispier and not as dense. I do like that they have a jug of water for self serve. I really think that should be mandatory in NOLA, especially in summer. I would like to try some of the other menu items as I am a big fan of Middle Eastern food and I definitely want to check out more FALAFEL options in the area.... Suggestions!?!?

We had a group of 5: 2 people in their 40s, 2 in their 20s, and a teen for Tesla's Mystery. This was definitely challenging, but doable for our team (we made it out with about 10 minutes to spare). The ambiance and details are super well done and interesting. The room requires some ingenuity to solve, so bring your A-game! This was my first escape room experience, and I can see how people get hooked! 2 people in our group had done other escape rooms before and commented that this one is way better. This was an incredibly fun experience, and we will definitely be back to try Secrets of the Abandoned Circus!

We were super excited to try floras knowing the same owners for prep n pastry own it. We went on Father's Day morning and We we were all super disappointed with the food. All Of the food came out cold, the potion size for the price compared to other places was horrible, the water was warm when we were sitting outside in over 105 degree weather.... It's the small things like that that count. Also the waitress started right on our food order and didn't even ask what we wanted to drink... it definitely need some improvement!

I'm the kind of gal that will not put any thought in drastically altering my hair.  I'll get an idea and have to act on it...immediately.  This happened on Friday, when I decided to lop off my elbow length hair to donate.  I called Regis and they were able to see me.

Mason was fantastic!  I told him that I wanted to donate my hair and he did a great job of measuring it, banding it and putting it in a bag for me to send off.  He also did an exceptional job with my haircut.
They were great a mistake was made and they fixed it and gave me a free donut!  Thank you!

I tried this out on a Groupon deal.  I wanna make it clear that this place does NOT feel shady.  I've seen a lot of reviewers post this.  While it's not an "upscale" day spa vibe, it is clean.  I got the ionic soak which to me is just a relaxing foot bath.  I'm not really into the hype with the water change as it's been proven that regardless if your feet are in the water or not, it still changes dark brown.  The massage part was great!  I never thought I would be so excited about someone touching my feet.  I will agree that this is most certainly not reflexology and is definitely more a foot massage, it was still great.  If your someone looking for real reflexology do your homework into local places first.  The only real gripe I had is that the deal is misleading.  It says 60 minutes reflexology and ionic bath. I was left in a room for the foot bath for 35 minutes (I timed it on my phone) and 25 minutes foot massage.  I would go back for a general foot massage in between regular reflexology appointments that I would go elsewhere for.  I would suggest to give it a try.

Came here and the girls are very attractive but remember their goal is to take your money. I disagreed with the number of lap dances that i was being charged for so the bouncer printed me a photo from inside the booth showing my entry time on the corner. Hot dancers and great drinks but if you don't want to have surveillance cameras taking pictures of you in the private booths then don't go into the booths.

At some point on Saturday evening I was reminiscing with a friend about my weekends as a college freshman spent at smelly, dirty, and overcrowded frat parties.

Fast forward a few hours and several drinks later, we stumble into Mad River. It's a frat party for those of us who no longer go to real frat parties! 

$5 dollars at the door, sticky floor covered in mystery substances, ridiculous lines for drinks, vomit smell throughout, crowded dance floor, scantily clad girls, and obnoxious guys.

This won't be my regular weekend spot, but when all I want to do is dance you may find me here.

Emilio I think was the young mans name he was courteous, well spoken and anticipated our needs. I will come back when he is working for sure. Thank you for the excellent customer service. 
-Dorothy

This is my favorite Publix ever!! Growing up in Florida, I have a very strong sense of loyalty to Publix. It's where my parents got my deli meats for my school lunches in middle school, where I go to get birthday cakes for friends' birthdays, and definitely where I go when I want a good sub [must-try: the chicken tenders sub or the ultimate] or bucket of fried chicken. Every Sunday, we'd end up coming to this location.

The deli department here is always well-stocked and pretty fast. I suggest their Ringleader platter if you're looking for something to bring to a party. The bakery department here is always well-stocked too! Their sprinkles or semisweet chocolate chip cookies are a must-try. They also seem to have a greater assortment of cakes here opposed to other Publix locations.

This location is also huge for a Publix, which are normally pretty big to begin with! The organic food section here is larger than most Publix locations and they rarely run out of high-volume items, especially the BOGOs. 

Although there are other Publix locations closer to our house, we always make the trek to Bardmoor because the location is just that good.

I've been going to the Moo location in Ottsville for a couple of years now.  I was happy to find out there was a new location in New Hope - which I travel to more often.

The burgers are really good.  The taste is great (I even eat the onions - I never eat onions), and it's not so giant that you don't have room for fries and a milkshake.  Because you need to save room for their milkshakes....they are my fav!

That being said, I do think it's a tad over priced.  I'm pretty sure our food bill came to about 25 dollars for a moink, veggie burger, and an order of french fries to share.  Add twelve dollars for two milkshakes...and you probably could've taken a family of four out to eat with that cash.  And the only other thing that I found odd is that you can't add tomato to a cheeseburger...because they will not mix dairy and tomato.  It's a veggie burger - let us have tomatoes!
Best food in lambertville.  Excellent service and BYOB.

Dennis Anderson

Not a real good meal, I ordered the crab mac (20 bucks)and was very disappointed. I would expect to find at least a chuck or 2 of crab but the crab was so ground up and fishy that I could not finish it. My wife had the chicken and she said it was dry for the price I would expect better.
     Before I left I used the mens room and the seat was so loose it was almost falling off and there was no TP that I had to use a seat cover for you know what. Very upset and will not be back.
I eat here about twice a week. I like the soups and egg salad sandwich. The coffee is pretty stand up too. I also recommend their catering. Very good service.
This is an average establishment. Service was lacking. Food was subpar. My kid wanted McDonald's and honestly I may have been better off stopping there.
I enjoy coffee and tea from here. And also the owner of the place are absolutely friendly.

It doesn't get any better with a Dentist. He has taken care of our whole family for over 17 years.  Total trust with his recommendations, care and office. My kids who live out of town continue to fly back in and make appointments with him during their vacation visits.  That says it all!

I have been to World Cafe Live twice now (for two completely different types of events), and both experiences have been positive.

The first was a Christine and the Queens show last month. The show was great, and the venue was the perfect size. There is plenty of room to move around (or dance, depending on the type of show!) but it's still small enough that basically any spot has a decent view of the stage. The sound and production seemed great as well, so no complaints there.

The second event that I attended was The Moth's monthly story-slam. If you're unfamiliar with The Moth, definitely check out the podcast and attend a story-slam. It's great to hear real stories from real people right from your own city. Anyway--this one was also a great time but set up more like a dinner theater. My friends and I sat in the balcony, which ended up being a great choice as we had an excellent view of the stage below.

I can't speak to the food yet (maybe on a future visit!), but it's definitely a solid venue and I'm sure I'll be back.

Meh to the Bloody Marys. Rare is it that I'm not compelled to order two, and here, I was not. 

And trust me, with the way I was feeling that day... it was bloody necessary. A bland Bloody Mary is a sin in New Orleans and I hold Bloody Marys up to my city's high standards.

As others mention, the front patio is all what this place is about. And truly, I'd probably give them three stars because I didn't eat and it's hard to judge a place on a single cocktail.

But... a Bloody Mary is a very important thing. Especially on a Saturday at 3pm.

Went there on 3/24/2014 to grab a few beers, food, and some apparel.  I first ordered a flight and a cup of chili, but let the bartender/server know that I will be ordering a meal shortly.  When I asked if I could order the grilled cheese, I was told that the kitchen was now closed.  I was a little disappointed because I told the bartender/server know that I would be ordering food and she never said anything about the kitchen closing (common courtesy).  What irritated me was when I asked what time the kitchen closed, she said 6:30 PM.  I showed her my phone, which clearly showed 6:25 PM.  She went back to the kitchen, came back, and told me that they had already cleaned up.  If I was the owner, I'd be pissed.  If the kitchen closes as 6:30, it closes at 6:30 -- not 6:20 PM.  Whatever, my one experience at Yards Tasting Room wasn't a good one.  If you're going to order food, be sure to order it 30 minutes prior to the kitchen closing, who knows when they're going to actually close.

I won't waste my money here again. The food was just mediocre and they were out of a couple menu items. Service was ok. The Durango mussels were just average flavor, which is disappointing because they sounded so good. The frites are just a bunch of broken fries pieces, but the dipping mayo is good. The sweet potato wedges were undercooked and hard. Basically, don't waste the hassle finding parking for such mediocre food.

We have been to this location before. 
Man has it gone down hill. 
We visited on a Sunday evening around 6pm. 
We were very disappointed in the condition of the dining area. While we were there, any table that vacated was left with dirty dishes and uncleaned. 

The waitstaff was pretty good but clearly overtaxed as there seemed to be too few staff. 

I was surprised that the food was as decent as it was. This was the only redeeming aspect.

Have been to this Iron Hill once before, and the one in Newark MANY times.  Actually, i think this rating got a little bump from my previous Iron Hill experiences, as THIS experience, quite frankly, was not that great!  I had the Jamaican pork chop and sweet potato fries.   Both were just... fine.  Almost at the caliber of good cafeteria food at a nice university dining commons.  (SOME of those are pretty good!)  I had never been upstairs before, and from what I can recall, the atmosphere DOWNstairs is really much nicer.  It was all just pretty much, meh... which surprised me.  

Would I go again?  Sure.  Great to have a micro-brewery in the neighborhood.  Would I tell you to drive long distances in the driving rain and sleet to get there?  Nope.  Wouldn't recommend that!

For the first time in all the visits I've had, my meal was actually hot!
It was so hot, I had to blow on my eggs.
Excellent!

Had I had my paintbrush...
I would have painted a smile on my waitresses face.
She had a scowl the whole morning.
I was afraid to ask her for more coffee.

I think Tiffin has real promise.  I've been there several times, and ordered usually vegetables (e.g. something with cauliflower or potatoes) or chicken.

The one thing that was weird was the lack of ambient music.  It was so odd -- there were only two parties upstairs -- mine and another -- that I took out my phone and set it to play some Indian background music.

I came here a few times and it was ok, but my last trip here made me don't want to come here anymore. I brought my bike in to fix a squeaky brake, but the guy working there wanted to "save" me some money and save him some time so he can work on the next bike so he just twisted the brake pad, took $12 from me for labor fee, and I still have a super loud squeaky brake. I went back and tell them the problem is still there, but, after an hour of waiting in line and fixing the bike, the problem is still there.

Went to Fermentaria for lunch with three friends. Probably would have been better if we drink beer, but none of us do! Two of us had mushroom tacos, which were tasty (but not much there for the price). The peas & carrots salad was okay, but really overpriced at $12. The person having the fish tacos asked for the chipotle mayo on the side, only to be informed that the chef will agree to leave out an ingredient when possible but will not allow anything to be put on the side! So, she got a Seinfeldian "No chipotle mayo for you!" Maybe we could understand if this was a gourmet chef in some very exclusive restaurant, but -- seriously?! The customer can't decide how much or how little chipotle mayo to use? Bad business in my opinion. The chef needs to chill and show some flexibility. PS: My friend didn't like the fish taco dish at all and only ate a small portion of it. The menu is very limited and prices rather high for small plates. Plus side: our waiter, Darren, was terrific!

This place has some set charging almost $4 a slice for that processed crap. It's close to the train station and figured it to be a long 5+ hour trip back to San Diego, so thought I'd grab a quick slice. I love coming to Santa Barbara but next time I'll pass on this place.

I am from out of town I went n for a beer. I recently renewed my drivers liscense and at first he rufused to accept it. Then after I got my beer her never waited on me. I noticed patrons come in and not be greeted. Customer service is really lacking.  I won't b back.

Great Sunday brunch! Dishes feature a Spanish/Mexican touch with tostadas, chorizo, molletes. My husband is in love with the biscuits and chorizo gravy. Fun list of morning cocktails. I went with the raspberry mayamosa.

Tonight my wife and I celebrated our 21st wedding anniversary. We decided to do so at the Kingfisher and brought our 17 year old son with us to enjoy the night with us. I made reservations to ensure we would have a table. We arrived promptly at 6:30 and we were seated. Once we were seated our waitress came over, took our drink order. When our drinks arrived she took our appetizer order and entree order.

That is when things went wrong. My son said that the waitress gave him a derisive look. I also noticed that she seemed to give me a less than pleasant look as well. We are assuming he received it because of his age and I received it because of the full sleeve of ink on my left arm.

Regardless during our stay, my son's drink sat empty for most of his meal and was only refilled after I asked two different busboys to ask the waitress to get him a refill. My wife's wine glass sat empty for half her meal and never was refilled. At no point during our meal did the waitress stop to check on how the meal was going or if we needed anything.

Meanwhile she was checking all the other patrons around us frequently, refilling their beverages and checking on them on a regular basis. She blatantly IGNORED my wife who was sitting patiently waiting for her to finish with another patron while staring at her.

My wife and I both earn a nice living in high end white collar jobs, the Kingfisher is not what we would consider a upscale dining experience for us. To be judged and treated like this based off my son's age and my ink is just stupid and asinine. The food was mediocre at best with small portion sizes and just not good enough to support the rudeness of this staff member.

Oysters were fresh and fried calamari was good. Staff needs some serious retraining if they are going to have bigoted behavior like this.

I love their food, but the service is unreliable at best. They often mess up my order, and they always seem to be mysteriously out of what I want. The employees are kind of a mixed bag. Some have been very helpful, but others are disinterested or rude. I'll probably keep eating here because it's convenient and affordable, but I grit my teeth every time I go in.

I don't know anyone who has ever been here.  So we decided to go over on New years eve assuming it wouldn't be packed.  It was not packed which was fantastic.  This was around 9ish but it never really got what I'd consider packed.   We got the cool little back room space all to ourselves.  Now I don't drink so I can't attest to the goodness of the actual drinks but I did go up to the bar to get a drink for my girlfriend.  It was very hard to get their attention. My friend said that was because the drinks they were making were super special and had to all be hand made.  They were not just mixing shit.  The dead bastard was very popular with my group.  
However it took me about 10 minutes to get one.  I had two people on separate occasion take my order and say they'd get it right away.  Finally the guy all the way at the other end brought it to me.  I believe in an effort to make up for it later in the night we received a complimentary beer.
Over all the bar tenders were very nice though and I realize they were busy.  So the rating is more of a reflection on the actual establishment.  If I had to sit anywhere other than the cool back room I would not have been impressed.  I'd go back if it wasn't busy.  This is not a place I'd ever choose to go to to meet anyone.   BYOF.
Can no one make brisket in the midwest?  Another restaurant where the brisket is dry, dry, dry, no flavor, tasted like it was just baked in the oven.  No smoke flavor and tough!

Took my wife here for brunch, they are redoing the place, which was a turn off. The food was just okay. I had the fish tacos, they were not fresh. I would have thought they would taste like they were right out of the ocean since we are so close but sadly they did not.  On the bright-side, we had the best waitress she kept our glasses full and we never waited for anything.

This Jimmy Johns is exactly like the rest, so no surprises there.
A single sandwich will almost certainly be ready for you before you can even get your wallet back into your purse.
self service fountain drink station and clean tables await you.

I would like to see them bag your sandwiches if you tell them they are to go, instead of me having to reach over to their side of the counter to get my own bag. And bigger bags to fit the chips would be even better.

I miss their rewards card, I wonder why they did away with them.

I want to love Catch 22. I have given this place a few fair shots over the year I've lived in Mt Juliet. The concept is great. The atmosphere is good. The menú is great. But the execution always seems to fail. Every time I've been there is something lacking! Most recently I got the burger that comes on an English muffin. They didn't toast the English muffin!  It was cold and soft, which means it turned into a soggy mess when the burger juice hit it. If it were toasted, it would have stood up to the juice! Also my fries were room temperature, while my husband's were obviously fresh and hot. These types of small things are what prevents us from making this place out go to local date night spot. There is a lot of room for improvement. 

I am giving it three stars based on the great concept, the fantastic beer selection, and the pretzel. The pretzel comes with a wasabi honey mustard and it is amazing. I recommend the sauce on the side to dip into, otherwise they squirt it all over the pretzel and it is so messy and sticky. You'll be happier and cleaner if you ask for it on the side. 
We will be back again because this is a nicer place that is not a chain, but Catch 22, work on the execution of your food! 
Also, there is a coupon in the fundraiser book! Bogo 1/2 off!

Oh trolley stop! You will always be a jem in my eyes! So what if you brought out my appetizer the same time you brought my meal. When it's a slow night that's something you have to deal with. Sorry for my friend complaining.

the ambiance is cool and they have nice outdoor sitting. Had the Cracklin Calamari salad, which was relally good, dressing was yummy, there was a ton of calamari in it, basically calamari appetizer on a bed of lettuce with dressing. Wine selection  was good, service was very good and were seated immediately. we will be back.

Brew Ha Ha has the best coffee and pastries of any coffee shop in the area. They provide a nice atmosphere as well with friendly staff and comfortable seating. My biggest gripe is that their wi-fi scarcely works and there are few outlets around the place. I would love to come here to do work often but the lack of reliable wifi makes me choose other places instead.

White sand yeah!  The hotel is very old, but will do the job if you are there for the beaches. Easy walk to the beach and food. Don't count on much for breakfast as it is pretty simple fare. Some hostess donuts and coffee is pretty much it. The staff were very friendly and helped us out with finding places to eat while I was there. There are many fancier places, but they cost way more. You won't be disappointed with this place.

The calamari and the sauce it has was some of the best I've ever had..... With that said the steak sucks , I had the $15 sirloin and the $10 outback / longhorn special blows it away . I even got
It with the "Oscar" which is crab meat and vegetables and that was not great.  The view is nice and the restaurant has a nice decor and atmosphere however the steak was really sub par

. It was on Monday. The place was packed. Miss Saigon did not just have her frame of nice view, friendly staff, decent decor,  her Pho ,eggrolls, roasted quails ($3.95) & PANCAKE are some of my favorite dishes.  I m more impressed with their Herbal & leave- Tea Menus. I love their Moc Cau Tea (strong and smell good).  I will come back to try o a ther tropical teas.

I visited here with some friends who are locals.  The wait staff knows them well, so they were very friendly and joked with us.  I had the teriyaki bowl, which was served on a plate, but was very good.  I had the chick teriyaki, which came with steamed rice.  They have a pretty good variety of foods. Good for a casino cafe.  It's inside the Rail City Casino in Sparks.

You ever been to an organized event that features a pancake breakfast? Well I have. Most of those instances were including me volunteering to serve at a homeless shelter to serve others. During this instance, I felt like I was on the other end of this exchange, but I had to pay pretty high prices for it. 

We sat on picnic tables, which wasn't super ideal, especially when it's rainy, cold and muddy outside. Maybe this review would be more positive if this experience was inside, but when you put each detail together, it really feels tough to give more than two stars. I wasn't elected to give partiality to organizations that feature breakfast foods. I was elected to bring the citizens of this world the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth. For this who think this is wrong or harsh, please refer to the attached photo.

Went to lunch here on a Saturday afternoon. The server was just fine, polite and friendly. I ordered the strawberry chicken salad. I proceeded to feed my infant daughter some small pieces of chicken out of the salad. Then I started to eat the salad myself. After a couple minutes, a quarter size spider crawled out of the lettuce and onto the edge of the plate. I was so disgusted I pushed my chair back away from the table. The server quickly came over and apologized and took the plate away. She brought me another salad but I wasn't willing to see what else may be hiding in that bed of lettuce. The other diner at my table also had a salad but after that experience she didn't want to continue eating it. The spider salad was not on our bill, but we did not have a manager come over and check on us and see how we were after that ordeal. The server did apologize.

I'm sad to post a review like this for a local business but it's the truth. I won't be dining here again.

Came here for brunch with a group of 5 a couple months ago.  In my opinion, the food was pretty good, but definitely overpriced.  The French Toast and salads were delicious, and the drinks were decent, but the waitress was a bit pushy with the drinks and the prices were definitely way too high.  This isn't Manhattan after all.... if I wanted to pay NYC prices, then I'd go to NYC.  And I'd get NYC quality too. 

Worth a stop-in if you've got money to blow and want to act fancy.  If you're a local from the neighborhood like me, you probably don't go there very much.

0 Stars if I could. Rude, unprofessional and lack of customer service. I went in to buy a car and all the salesmen were just standing there, nobody greeted me. When I said I'm looking for a car, he said someone will be right with you. Nobody ever came out. Take your money somewhere else.

This used be to a great venue back at their old location. Now it is hypocrites pushing a Christian agenda. Allowing certain bands and nixing the ones they " deem inappropriate" and I honestly couldn't tell you based off what criteria. The people who work the snack desks are always polite and friendly, but the security is the most stuck up group of people I have ever met. I've sworn off going to shows here, even if it is my favorite band, I'll pass. Get back to your roots and learn how to run a business. I would give you zero stars if I could.

Prepare for looooooooong wait time. 
Plan ahead for this .... Food is okay.
Tom kha soup is not good. Stay away from it.
Rice was semi boiled in Thai fried rice 
Drunken noodles was good..

Of all the outbacks- this one takes it. Heads above the rest. We've been here plenty in the last two months- every single time steaks, prime rib, perfect. Drinks top notch. Old layout before all the remodeling started, not the mall food court design. 
Staff great, can't say enough.

A Letdown

I've heard lots about Upperline as one of the best restaurants in New Orleans.  On this, our most recent trip to the Crescent City, we finally had a chance to try Upperline and I'm bummed that I was disappointed.

Service was only OK, a bit snobby at times and often unavailable completely.  The menu is a bit cumbersome with the focus being on a 3 course set menu but with many options for a few dollars more here and there.

We started with cocktails and the house drinks were unimaginative.  The good old Sidecar was very good.

For our first course we started with the Fried Green Tomatoes and the Oysters St Claude (listed on the menu with the note: "One of USA TODAY's Top New Dishes in the USA for 2007"--the menu is replete with self-congratulatory bon mots).  The tomatoes were good and the oysters were very good.

For our entrees we had the Shrimp and Grits and the Roasted Half Duckling.  Both were just OK.  Nothing on the plate implied that this was a 5-star (or maybe not even 4 star) restaurant.  The desserts were, again, fine.

I write this review the day after our dinner and I had to go to the restaurant's website menu to refresh my memory on what we had.  A place that boasts so much about itself and charges prices commensurate with that boast, should deliver a memorable dinner.  Ours, sadly, was not.

This restaurant has been around for some time so I had moderate hopes. I was VERY disappointed. The food is overpriced (yeah, yeah, vacation town, whatever). My husband got 6 shrimp on a plate with nothing else for $15. Three of our plates came out then two more came out 15 minutes later and the shrimp came out 7 minutes after that! The food was mediocre at best (and I don't believe it would have tasted much better if we had the chance to eat it hot).  When we asked to keep the food hot while we waited for the rest of our food we were told no. When we complained our waiter got mad. No manager spoke with us, we were not provided even an apology, and we were made to feel like we were wrong for wanting to all eat together. We will not be back and would not recommend this place.

I stopped in for lunch during my recent business trip after reading this was a must visit place in New Orleans!  

Beautiful dining room and spacious patio, professional service and simply amazing food.  I enjoyed the tomato goat cheese bisque and the shrimp and grits appetizer as my meal - so rich, flavorful and downright tasty!

What a lovely place to escape the noise and enjoy a relaxing lunch or dinner in the French Quarter!
The food might be good but the service is terribe. Waited over 30 minutes for catfish and gumbo
better than eurocentric geno's, but still ehh

This place is the Chick-fil-A of oil and lube shops. By that I mean the customer service is top-notch, the place is clean and organized, and they go above and beyond to make sure your experience is comfortable. The standard oil change includes free coffee and a newspaper, they inspect most of the vehicle's components to make sure everything is working properly, and they update you on things that should be worked on. There's a variety of fluid changes they can package in and give discounts on which is really nice. 

If I had a complaint it's the price, it's a little on the high side...but the experience was so pleasant I almost don't mind. I'm a sucker for good customer service and I can see myself coming back for sure. 

They even give a discount if you come back for an oil change before the recommended mileage!

Love the atmosphere and old school classic new orleans style. Unfortunately the rest of or experience wasn't great. The host at the door was rude which set the night off poorly. The food was nothing special we tried maybe 8 or 9 different dishes but nothing was impressive, special or flavorful. It felt like old people food in the style of plating from decades ago.

Really cool vibe and great food, however a giant cluster f**k.  Took nearly 7 minutes after being seated for someone to acknowledge me.        This place is in need of a major overhaul in training.  The food sat at the counter for a long time.  I sat staring at salads that were fresh from the kitchen just sitting there.  I stared at them hoping one of them WASNT mine as several minutes had lapsed. I kept staring and staring.  Then a woman grabs one of them, place a paper on top (receipt?) and walks away.  A minute or so  passes and sure enough she grabs the salad and takes off the paper that she placed on top of the salad and brings it to my table.  UGH, my "grilled" chicken was luke warm at best.  Get it together folks! i

Had high hopes for Roux! But they fell short... granted, I will be back a second time and give them another chance.

But basically, went for brunch last Sunday after church. No reservations, and the placed was packed. So we found sitting on the bar area. Our waiter was the best, very nice and helpful. We ordered 4 grilled Oysters, the Eggs Benedict and my friend ordered the Shrimp Po Boy. We also had the two for one mimosas!

Ginger Mimosa: Okay. Interesting combination that works!
Oysters: Amazing, so tasty. Only downfall is that they were drenched in Parmesan cheese... so much cheese that it just tasted like a ball of cheese (not that there's anything wrong with that :) )
Shrimp Po Boy- Couldn't eat it since Im allergic to shrimp....

NOW, what makes me give them TWO stars, the Eggs Benedict. I am an Eggs Benedict connoisseur. I've ordered the dish at EVERY single place that serves it for the past 8 years. In the U.S., outside of the U.S., American, French, Asian restaurants. You got it, I want it. But what is my favorite part of the dish? The feeling you get when you cut your perfectly poached egg and the gooey yolk comes running down the bread and plate... YUM. Sadly, I didn't feel that at Roux because BOTH of my eggs were overcooked, to the point that they felt like boiled eggs. That's a huge no-no and basically ruined the dish for me... How can you serve eggs benny and not make sure you cooked the eggs properly?????!!!!!
Also, the ham was way too aggressive, I felt like I had half a cow on my plate.The side potatoes were delish and helped cure my sadness. 
In my book, everything can be good, but cooking eggs properly is a basic chef skill at any good restaurant. 
I will be back. I just wont order any dish with eggs on it...

I'm usually happy with the service and the food is good. However today we walked in at 11 when it opened and all went well with drinks and appetizers but we were sitting there for over an hour waiting for our food. We were the first table for a while and  as we waited the tables filled up and the waitress tried her best but it was highly frustrating hearing excuses waiting for over an hour. We gave them the benefit of the doubt anyway but at one point the waitress and even the manager stopped coming around and no food was being served. The manager said it's because everyone came in at once but like I said we were the first order before anyone else came in. We ended up leaving and left enough cash for drinks and an appetizer. We had to guess because no one came around to give us a final excuse or check. Oh well sorry I had to get a hungry 3 year old home for a nap before their afternoon Birthday party. It was frustrating because the staff didn't seem to care or have a clue. We may come back but not until a long hiatus.

Food is good but service is terrible.  Ordered the creole snapper but somehow it showed up without the creole and crawfish sauce?  WTF?!  Are you kidding me?  $26 dish that arrives to the table without the signature sauce.  Asked the waiter if it would be 'taken care of' on the bill, he said he'd have to talk to his manager.  WTF again?!  How about advocating for your customer who is possibly going to tip you.  Anyway, they gave me a $1.20 discount, wow!  Last time for us.  Too bad, food was good but you need to have great service to go with it.
This restaurant is one of the worst TGI Fridays I've been to. It's dirty, service is horrible and the food is terrible. Don't wast your time or your money here!

I live 5 minutes from this Giant but will now travel the extra miles to the Flourtown store after shopping there for the first time today. The difference between these two stores is like night and day. The store (Flourtown) seems cheerier and cleaner and the employees are so much nicer. Very pleasant experience.

I've been to many locations in St. Louis as I am a Massage LuXe member (for about 3 years), however it was my first time here at St, Charles. First, the frontdesk staff was very friendly and helpful. This Massage LuXe, like others, is very clean, and nicely decorated.  

Both my husband and I had massages. He was called 10 minutes before me (right before our appointment time of 3) and I got called back closer to 310. I wasn't stressing because in the past this has happened, but the therapist will always add the extra time at the end. Never have I had anyone short change my 50 minutes. Well, my massage was 45 minutes, at best. "It's only 5 minutes" Welllll, I paid for 50, girl. Unfortunately the massage itself was very poor quality. One thing I really didn't like was she sprayed eucalyptus on a tissue and then draped the saturated tissue across my face, eyes and nose included. I've never had anyone do that (usually they'll just put it in front of your nose for 10 seconds while you deep breathe). Well, she put it in my face and I thought "Well this is different.. Let's give it a try!" until about 90 seconds later when I couldn't breathe anymore, was starting to get a headache and my eyes were watering. I had to take it off my face. I just don't know why this is her practice when nobody, in my years of going to massages, has ever done anything like that. She didn't ask if I wanted it either, just put it on my face.    

She spent a very long time on my feet and felt like no time at all on my back (which I told her was my one problem area). 

My husband didn't have much more luck than me. After asking for more pressure over and over, he finally gave up. Now, usually if one of us gets a bad massage its a fluke, and the other one had a good one.  It's never happened where we both had bad massages at the same time. Well, that was the case here. We both were so disappointed and so unhappy with the quality of our massages we will not be back to this location. I am giving 2 stars because of the positives I listed at the beginning of my review.

Was there about a month ago, the food was good service was slow and even slower getting the and paying the bill, was I a hurry after waiting 15 minutes just pay, I hastily signed my receipt and got out of there, when I checked my receipt later they charged me $37 for two breakfast and coffees. I thought it was very expensive. Was on the the area this morning and again the food was good and as usual our waiter was a little slow, well well we got our check and again the bill was over $37 fool me once shame on me. Two $10.99 breakfasts coffees another $2.99 each and a $9.99 charge for "Bac" questioned our waiter he did not know how that charge go on the bill. I took them another 15 minutes to take the charge off of the bill. BE very careful of your check watch out for that phantom BACON CHARGE!!!!  My last visit to this establishment.

Getting REALLY TIRED of them not having what I wanted to get. I go at all different times of the day and they never fail to disappoint me. Cmon man! You guys are making me dislike wawa.... And I'm from Wildwood where I used to live on wawa.

I would have given it less than one star if I could. I had high hopes for this Wendy's when it first came to the 'hood, now I'm completely hopeless about it. Food is OK, but the SERVICE is unbearable. I don't know why I keep hoping this place will get better because I'm sadly disappointed each time. Last night, there was ONE car in front of me. I waited for in line 10 minutes before I peeled out in a hangry rage--the person ahead of me hadn't even had their order taken yet in all that time! When I went around to the drive-through window to see what the issue could have been to cause the delay -- Armed robbery in progress? Hostage situation? Someone had a medical emergency? -- I see the cashier just standing there, no other cars in sight, just staring off into space. I have never felt the need to leave a Yelp review, especially a negative one, but this place deserves it. Service is abysmal. If I feel the need for food, I'll go across the street to the Burger King which I would give a relatively better two-star review.
They weren't scheduled to come by till 7:30 pm he showed up at like 11 am instead fixed my air conditioner and changed my air filter all while I was at work

Fabulous new find

The service was friendly without being intrusive, the menu has just enough choices and the dishes I tasted were excellent. I especially enjoyed the ambience which is quite novel for Nashville. I will be back with friends!

Run for the hills folks.  My wife went here and came home not wanting to talk about it.  Diane insulted my wife and her friend.  My wife came home told me they use profanity when they interact with people...jump on Yelp, guess what...others say the same thing.  One bathroom upstairs and one outside on the deck...bring your parka or you could freeze heading to to can folks.  Or just don't go...not a good idea unless you really like to be insulted, cold, embarrassed, badgered, and worn-out.  I now need to give my wife a pedicure just to readjust her to normal life.....oh man.

warning veterans...

the home depot's in reno and carson city pick and choose what products they will give the veterans discount. 
do not shop there unless you have no other choice. 
it appears it is only hd in nevada dont give the discounts, all hd in california give veterans discounts.
let''s show them our sacrafic won't allow them to line their pockets.

don't shop at home depot.

Was really disappointed with this stop. The menu and other reviews appeared to be a great place to stop for a quick lunch. Everything we got was somewhat cold, or tasted like fair food. Nothing to write home about. Other than a nice location, or if you are looking to stop in for a beer and people watch, that's kind of it for me here. The food I would pass on in the future.

This is a decent place for a quick work out. Don't expect a big gym with tons of amenities because this place won't deliver that. It doesn't even have enough weight lifting machines and they only carry two kinds of cardio machines: elliptical and treadmill.

It's located in a convenient area and parking is easy. You can come in whenever but they have limited staff hours.

In town for the night looking for late night dinner. Found the New Deck Tavern open and empty. It was a Tuesday night at 9 pm. Service was
fast and friendly. Ordered soups and sandwiches. The Onion soup was ok. The special burger ordered med rare was well done. The only good thing was the place was quite.

Large bar area with lots of space, good music for a sports bar.  The food is nothing to come back for, but there is plenty of space and it does fill up.  Cheap drinks keep the crowd pretty well lubricated, I feel like it's a frat party for recent college grads and mid 30's professionals.

We wanted to move to Verizon and were said that if we port our numbers , the activation fee for the 2 devices will be waived off. But it never happened. We came back to the store and asked about it. They simply said they never said that. Worst response ever. Mobile protection plan was also added without our consent . That's 1 more $45 on our bill. The rep , did not update our address to Delaware address after adding my Virginia address temporarily. Total negligence . We're treated bad when asked when we asked about all these. Avoid taking any new connections from this store. Do not believe anything they say.

Sushi Inc. definitely impressed with its great service, cool yet simple ambiance, and extremely fresh, flavorful rolls. On top of all this, it's reasonably priced, especially considering the excellent quality. 

I had the ginger salad to start, which was actually made with fresh lettuce and one of the better ginger dressings I've had. After my salad, I had the Kat Roll.
