This was my personal favorite because I love avocado and eel sauce. I tried some of the K-Town, a roll wrapped in rice paper with a ton of fresh fish, but it's just not my preference of roll. 

Overall, I was really pleased with Sushi Inc and will definitely be back. Glad to have another sushi spot in the area!

Randomly stopped here for dinner and the food was good, but the service was not. The atmosphere also involved a lot of dogs, belonging to both patrons and passerby, and I was all about it. 

Ok, service... it took a minute for someone to come out to take our drink orders, fine whatever; then gradually the intervals between interactions with our server increased. He promised bread, and it came out with our entrees; he came by well after we had finished our meal and didn't have the check ready; getting the check itself was an ordeal. Also, if I may - he made a snarky comment about how I ordered my steak and I was like, dude, my chef boyfriend already gave me a hard time about it, can you just not... also, he didn't tell us the specials, which we learned when he was telling the people next to us about them. I could have saved myself overcooked steak embarrassment had I known about the grouper special.

The food was good. Bread was warm despite its tardiness. My steak and mashed potatoes were divine in every way. My family had minor gripes about their dishes but finished everything. The desserts looked good - tiny, but good - and we passed only because we had somewhere to be. The menu generally had a lot of delicious-sounding items on it, from fish tacos to (of course) grouper done many ways, to classic shrimp cocktail; sandwiches, pasta, stir fry. Lots of stuff that seems divergent at first glance but actually makes sense.

The price for four people wasn't actually that bad, considering the location near the water.

Let's keep this simple folks: wide selection of booze With live entertainment (the guy waiting on our table was chiming along with Rihanna singing "only girl in the world" and the old guy at the bar was jiving all alone at midnight singing for himself on his birthday...in an empty restaurant.. Not quite sure whether that was funny or pathetic..2 pints of beer and some flatbread down.. We decided that we had enough!
Good, authentic Thai food. Quick and fresh. Nothing fancy. Enjoyed the spring rolls and Pad Thai!

This lager gal loves wheeling a granny cart to SBD to pick up a case of Yuengling Lager.  It's fun to roam the aisles, jam-packed with beer and soda, where it's hard to resist a twelve-pack of San Pellegrino Limonata plus some G&T friendly tonic water.
Reasonable prices and a great experience, if you're visiting florida, you MUST take a trip to mons venus.

Interesting find tucked away in the corner of a strip mall. Very clean with a courteous staff.

However, the food was just sort of meh. I have a feeling after tasting the tap water that the weird taste in the food stems from their water supply. Anything that has to use water (such as ice and rice) had a distinct strangeness to the taste. The salsa was also pretty awful.

On a plus side, happy hour is all day and the drinks are decent. While I don't see myself coming back here, I would not say "stay away" to anyone interested in giving it a shot.

I went here twice/month for a little bit over two years, only leaving unhappy a handful times, strictly due to inconsistency. Sometimes my acrylics were perfect, sometimes different shapes per finger, sometimes extremely thick, sometimes breaking the next day for whatever reason. I won't return because I will not spend my money in an establishment any longer where I may leave happily, or may end up frustrated about why I just wasted money on subpar work and it not be fixed. Ryan is amazing, as well as a few others. Some people's work.... mehhh. I approached the owner three time throughout my time here, expressing my frustrations, at which point she suggested I don't come back. By now I'd already decided this. Then proceeded to back track and told me to come see her specifically at the other location which is not anywhere close to me. I would hope that everyone is good enough at their job for this not have to be the case, but instead I'll bring my money elsewhere where consistency is key. However, I will give them credit for the few people who can do designs and love their amount of options available for colors, glitters, etc.

We went here for brunch on Sunday. I had the picnic chicken sandwich and my wife had the wild truffle  mushroom omelette. The chicken sandwich was good. Unfortunately my wife found a hair in the last few bites of her omlette. My wife is a hairdresser and knows what hair looks like. The server was insincere in her apology and quickly removed the omelette (no chance of taking a picture) . The manager claimed that the hair was a fibre from the mushroom. We felt  the manager treated us like a freeloaders. The manager did not comp the omelette.  Rather she gave us two lattes to go(we already had coffee). Hopefully this review changes the outlook on how the restaurant treats their customers. I will not recommend this restaurant to anyone. On a positive note Kevin one of the Servers is very nice.

food was good, place was clean, waitress super nice, but the hostess had a bit of an attitude. really put me off my experience, by the end I had mostly forgotten about it since the waitress was so nice. the bloody Mary is also very very spicy for some reason. the breakfast bowl was really good, lentils were my favorite part surprisingly

Let me first say that the doctors and technicians are unbeatable and I have absolutely NO complaints about them. Unfortunately, in the last year the wait times have become completely unreasonable (an hour and a half of wait time, divided between the outer waiting room and the inner waiting area where you wait for an exam room) and the new computerized check-in kiosks are an absolute horror, producing long lines and bottlenecks while people fumble through purses and wallets for insurance cards and driver's licenses and try to hunt-and-peck through the numerous screens. I was really shocked, as my previous experiences with Whitson had always been so wonderful, but I'm afraid I will have to find another ophthalmologist who doesn't overbook so badly.

This by far was the most relaxing time I've had in Tampa. It was great to see it in a different way. I liked that I could stand on the bike, being a bigger girl, and not have a fear of toppling over in Hillsborough river. I loved seeing the university of Tampa in a different vantage point. If you're thinking of doing it, just do it. It's wonderful!

This was a really great place for a casual night out. The menu features all of the usual cravings plus some creative spins on popular classics. The food was pretty average but nonetheless very delicious. Only complaint was that lettuce was forgotten on one of the burgers, but other than that it was great! The atmosphere was also nice because it was very Philly sports themed so customers would feel at home in the restaurant.

Literally could not find the theater, it's barely lit. We get there 15 minutes late for the movie, after walking for 20 mins in the 20 degree weather trying to find it, only to be told their registers are shut down so we can't buy tickets. Won't be coming back, horrible customer service.

We used Discount Party rentals for our wedding and they were great.  So easy to work with and they made the whole process a breeze.  We rented cocktail tables from them and they were awesome.  We would recommend them to anyone in the market for party rentals in SB, we talked to all the party rental places in SB and these are the only ones who didn't give us attitude.  We only needed to rent 6 tables and didn't need them delivered, every other place were rude and difficult to work with since our order was small, but Discount Party rentals treated us like we had a huge order, so ill only use them in the future.

They are great and they prove that customer service rules all...  thanks again guys.

I strictly only order take-out from Hong Kong Diner. I have never dined in, and, honestly, probably never will. This is such a great choice when you want to have a night at home binge watching some Netflix. I pick this up typically late at night since they are open until 3am, so I can't really vouch for their daytime hours, but it is my go-to choice when my boyfriend and I don't feel like cooking and also don't feel like putting real pants on. 

I always get a combination plate with fried rice and usually sesame chicken and orange chicken. Pretty basic, and 90% of the time it hits the spot. I do recall a couple times when some of my chicken was just fried gristle, but you can't complain too much when it's 2:30 in the morning.

Service is pretty great too. I will say that it probably helps that one of the guys there can recognize my boyfriend's voice on the phone. Ha! I guess when he was living the single life he lived right behind Hong Kong Diner, and I can only imagine how often Bachelor-Adam picked up food. It's still impressive that he remembers Adam after so long because we only get Hong Kong maybe once every couple months.

Came here for lunch with a big group after helping out with the JHS Thanksgiving Drive, and after seeing the crazy line outside of Parkway.  Anyway, we immediately were able to sit outside in the beautiful weather.  I ordered an Abita Christmas Ale and eggplant fries to start.  The ale was ok, drinkable (I have very steep beer standards) and the fries were delicious.  I offered to bring my SO some food, so I got to order two po-boys!  I got the meatball po-boy and the Katie's cuban (cochon de lait in place of regular sliced pork).  The meatball po-boy was delicious.  The sauce, the cheese, the meat: all excellent.  The Cuban was your typical cuban with that NOLA twist.  Very good, but a Cuban is a Cuban.  And my SO enjoyed bites of both halves that I took home.  
Service seemed a bit slow, but we were a big group and it was a busy day.

All in all, a good experience.

Wanting to call and order a pickup meal from this nearby place, my wife and I both went online and each found a menu on two different local menu sites. I ordered a two-item combo and a tostada. All went smoothly, food was ready quickly, got the "no beans, rice with guac and sour cream on the side" right. But the bill seemed high. "My combo is only $8.95," I said. "No, it's $10.95," the cashier told me. "But the menus online--" said. "Those are out of date," he replied, "We don't use them." I asked if they had their own web site. No, "We're working on it."

I get home and look at the receipt. They'd charged me for a "Tostada Grande." I called the store and asked for the manager. After checking with the manager (I'm sure), the woman on the other end of the line told me, "He's not here. He went to the bank. Can I take a message?"

I said, "I was in earlier--"

"Oh, are you the one who asked about the prices?"

"Yes, but I'm calling to let you know that you charged me for the grande tostada, not the regular."

"We don't have the regular on the menu anymore, just the grande."

"But when I told you--or whoever I placed the order with--that I wanted a tostada, you asked if I wanted the grande. I said no, the regular."

"Well, we don't have the regular on the menu anymore, just the grande, but we still make the regular. It costs the same as the grande."

"Well that's just dumb," I said, and then recalled I'd seen another customer review saying the manager refused to come to the phone when queried about an overcharge. "Put the manager on, please."

"He's not here--"

"He's at the bank, right? At 7:30 on a Friday night?? Tell him I said I will NEVER eat at Rudy's again!"

Sadly, there are a couple of other Rudy's in our area, all, I think, owned by members of the same family, and all will be tarred by brushes like this...

They're delivery service is not professional.  They won't get out the car at night. And there racist. I was over a friends house we order for delivery at 12:30am they came right to the door. We hen I order from my house they don't  to get out the car. I made the last time me order from them Friday. POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE

At best, the thirty minutes my wife and I spent at this hotel was a lesson to refrain from using Priceline to book a hotel room.  I am from Nashville originally and just wanted a place to crash and recharge from family time.  I thought it strange to get a hotel in Metrocenter when I bid on a room downtown.  But our needs were relatively low; we only wanted a clean place to shower and sleep.

We checked in at about 4 o'clock with a slow, unkempt, but polite young gentleman.  We went up to our room and were immediately knocked down by a powerful chemical smell.  Apparently the chemicals were aimed at covering up the mold smell.  There was a pile of salt and food residue on the night stand.  The bathroom door had a hole that looked like it had been punched through.  There was some kind of blue-green gooey substance on the bathroom floor.  The front door was chipped and stained.  The chair was sun-faded, worn out, and dust exploded from it by touching it.  The hotel guide was defaced and falling apart.  The television was about twenty years old with a bad picture--and the best part of our brief stay.  After half an hour of apologizing to my wife and cataloging the innumerable problems, I finally just made a reservation at a reasonably priced hotel with which I am familiar.  Our stay was less than an hour, but the impression will last a lifetime.  I would not be surprised if the hotel is demolished within the decade.

I am not quite sure what a fair price for a room in this condition would be, but I would explore other options if you have at least $30 a night to spend.  The star system used by websites such as Priceline deviates so much from the predominant star-rating system that it should be investigated by consumer protection agencies.

I will never stay at this hotel.  I will never attend a conference at this hotel.  I will never use Priceline again because of the poor quality and misleading terms.  Based on my bad experience and  the overwhelming majority of poor yelp reviews, I would urge you to look elsewhere.  I would also be suspicious of the review authors who gave this establishment high marks.

This place by far is one of the worst places I have ever had breakfast.  I have always heard about this place and I finally had the opportunity over the weekend to try it,  let's just say i will never go back.  I reserved the small room off the side of the actual restaurant for a small party /get together.  I showed up around 1130 and the food was already sitting there.  Not really too sure how long it was sitting there waiting for the guests to arrive.  The food was way overcooked and not one person from the staff came to make sure that everything was ok or if we needed anything.  The only time I saw someone was when they came to refill my pitchers.  The amount of money that was charger was RIDICULOUS.  I am so happy that not all my guests showed up, they were definitely not missing out on anything.

This is the best Escape Room I've been to yet. The decor and props were spectacular, since they have the props from The Darkness haunted house to draw from. There were a few times when the recorded voices could be difficult to hear, but that's only because other people on the team were talking at the same time. The host was very good about offering slight clues and hints when needed, and did so in a way that kept the fourth wall up. Everything was reasonable and solvable. We did the Haunted Hotel scene, really want to do their other settings now. Highly recommended.

No ketchup 
No chicken 
No Coca-Cola 
No dining room ..... no in person ordering 
But they have their masks ‍

Went there tonight to get warm donuts. I asked for two but got three, which was a pleasant surprise. However, the donuts, while warm were delicious, but the novelty flavor came in the sugar dusting on top. I would go there on a cold night for a warm, tasty treat but certainly would not go out of my way to get a donut. Will sample the chicken at a later time....

Alright, what this place needs are really two different ratings: one for their music and one for their staff. First, I'd give the place five stars for their music. This is a go-to place of mine whenever visitors come to town, or I just want to see an amazing show in the round. Forget Bluebird, you'll never get in. Besides, this place is almost just as good except..

The service here deserves a solid one star. The staff here will actively ignore you. They will walk over to you, look you straight in the eye, turn right back around and head to go talk to the bartender. I've honestly been here probably 10 times in the last two years and have only once had good service, and it turns out that was the server's first day working there. 

The food is decent and the beer selection ok, but again beware of the staff. Sit at the bar if you ever want a drink.

It's kind-of funny... I've ate from the Hoss' Loaded Burgers Food Truck many times, and still have yet to actually order a burger.

The first time I "met" this food truck, I wasn't particularly hungry, so I opted for their Blackened Spices french fries. At $2 for a generous portion of fries, it was definitely enough to satisfy me. The flavor was so unique and delicious, almost spicy, but addictive. I was hooked.

Now, every time I am at the Hoss' truck, I order their large fries in either the Blackened Spices or Parmesan Rosemary flavors, and a side of their sorbet of the day. Weird combo, I know. But their sorbet is so delicious and fresh, and paired with the flavors of the fries, it's a delicious small meal! My favorite sorbet is their Tequila Lime. YUM.

Maybe one of these days I'll try one of their burgers, but for now, I'll stick to my favorites!

Went to Terry's for lunch because they have great coffee, I ordered a burger and hash browns, wife ordered eggs and hash browns. Something was wrong with my burger it just wasn't right not sure what is was, I told my wife somethings weird with my burger didn't taste right. I got half the burger down set it down and quit eating my hash browns too and went back to my coffee. I told my wife somethings wrong with my food, she said her eggs were OK but hash browns weren't crispy as she requested instead almost uncooked, we realized our food was half cooked, my burger patty was raw in the middle and hash browns were not cooked long enough. I don't complain about food or send it back and don't enjoy the company of people that do that either,  I'll go back because of the great coffee but will order foods usually not stored frozen like eggs, bacon, English muffins, its just hard to find a place that serves great coffee and Steve's now Terry's has great coffee now needs to work on serving great food.
 Update: Terry's doing better so I upgraded them to 4 stars, great food, GREAT COFFEE!

Great take out
Sides just can't be beat
Got lobster Mac, coleslaw and broccoli salad to go with stuff we had grilled and all were top notch!!

The two stars are for the nice service. Other than that, the food quality is very poor. Beef short rib was edible but not very flavorful. Scallops tasted fishy and the mushroom risotto was not good. The mushrooms that were used were bad, smelled and tasted of bleach.

I did try to purchase a vehicle at this location. The salesperson was pretty good and took me out for a test drive. I did contact a few other dealers in the area to compare prices. I did find two places that had the exact same equipped vehicle but with a lower cost. Since the this dealer is closer to my house I gave them the opportunity to match the other dealers lower price and they didn't.
Last night my car had a flat tire and the portable air pump that comes with the vehicle didn't work. It turned out to be an electrical issue with the car. When I went to pick up the car the air pump was still sitting in the front passenger area, and the cables and cords were laying all over the place. The rear passengers under mat storage areas were also left opened and the mats off to the side. The air pump is stored near that area.
After telling the service writer about the condition her reply was "the guy who did it is gone for the day, but I'll tell him about it on "Monday" when he is in.
I asked her to have someone else take car of it and to wash the car. She did it but was a little huffy and puffy about it.

After believing the claims on their website, filling out the extensive application, and mailing in pictures and payment, Grundy declined to offer me insurance.  Their website boasts: "unlimited miles"  and "It is even OK if occasionally you drive your collector car to work".  Don't believe it.  These are the exact things they declined me for.  After I submitted my application they followed up by sending me one last quick form to sign and asked about the estimated mileage I had submitted on my application.  First, the form they wanted me to sign said "It will not be driven to work...".  I didn't sign it because, per their website, that is one of the reasons I decided to go with their insurance.  Second, I replied telling them the estimated mileage was a guess.  Really, I didn't think much of that.  They offer unlimited mileage, per their website, so it shouldn't matter.
  
The rejection letter said: "declined for: unacceptable usage".  My recommendation: DON'T WASTE YOUR TIME applying if you plan on driving your car.  The application and website are directly contradictory.  

If you're skeptical of my review, yes I submitted info about my modern daily driver car, I have no accidents/tickets, garage kept...I met all the conditions on their website.

Be very careful with these online-only institutions. Read their policies closely and be sure you understand what you're getting into. Also, if you're considering using the credential outside the USA, know that more and more countries and foreign programs are strictly forbidding ANY online work--whether for certificates or degrees. So you might find yourself out of a pretty chunk of change only to be stuck with a credential you can use only at certain places. Worse, there's still significant institutional prejudice here in the USA, too. A University of Phoenix degree simply doesn't carry the weight of the analogous degree from the University of California Berkeley.

Yes, online learning is becoming more common, but therein lies the problem:  as more and more people acquire these convenient degrees, the market is being flooded with credentialed graduates, all competing for jobs, grants, and admissions to stellar terminal programs. Whom do you think employers, universities, and (other) organizations are more likely to be impressed with, a graduate with an online degree from a relatively new company or a graduate with a degree from a world-class historic university? When I spoke to this school's admissions team about a program for one of our family members, the ACE representative pointed out how many of their graduates were employed, having fulfilled state requirements. Yes, in limited educational disciplines. But depending on what you envision for your future, a generic credential may not suffice. After speaking with ACE enrollment, I contacted several graduate schools of education at major research universities. They were direct in pointing out that admission to their universities takes heavily into account the rigor and competitiveness of the prior academic programs of applicants. But every online school claims its program is of the highest academic caliber. There is no "highest" if everyone is the same.

Also, I'm not trying to be snide, but look at ACE faculty. How many graduates from Standford's internationally renowned program do you see? Or Columbia's? Or Harvard's? Faculty networking, opportunities to participate on faculty research and publish, traveling to present data at national conferences under the auspices of well-published faculty and a university with clout--these may be important to you. If so, consider carefully if ACE suits the profile.

Having said all that, ACE is relatively affordable, though if you're considering international travel and work, there are more affordable options abroad (UK, Europe...) whose programs are also "accredited" by international educational boards--and which are awarded from historic brick-and-mortar universities. We weren't impressed with ACE. Its representatives lack the polish and confidence of their counterparts at world-class universities. Call them. Discuss anything with them--from long-term opportunities post-graduation to the lingering effects of anti-egalitarian degree-name (school, program) bias. Then compare their responses with traditional universities'. Decide for yourself. We enrolled our family member in a traditional program that offers all the above perks. More expensive, yes. But, as the saying goes, you get what you pay for.
Super expensive! Still delicious sushi and nice for occasional and special meals. Great ambiance and experience.

I've loved this place since the moment I stepped in. Always a fun time! Yes, it's a dive. Yes, I love dives. Yes, I'm straight. Yes, it's considered a gay bar but from what I've seen, as long as you're cool than it's all good. Music is always varied and of course, so is the clientele as there are themed events at times. 
Drinks are well poured, priced right and staff has always been friendly. 
Word to the wise: don't park in the Adult Expectations parking lot. They're not nice about it. Maybe if they only went across the street for a drink...

We have been to several different stores so it is time for a review. Not a fan at all of this store. 

From the name you would think you would get great bargains. Wrong. You can get the same items for less elsewhere. They are limited as to what is on the shelves. 

On this trip milk and eggs were on the list. Both were priced higher than ALDI, Dollar Tree, and Walmart. 

The store was on the cleanish side. Lots of carts available. Staff seems friendly however we interacted very little with them. 

We won't be returning to this store.

Horrible service from "Vicky" at the front desk! I have always preferred this hotel chain that one time I even lived in another one of their hotels for 6 months, but after this horrible experience with " Vicky" I will NEVER stay here or at another Holiday Inn Express. I've never met anyone as rude and bitter

My eyebrows were in dire straits, but I've been super busy and didn't have time for a wax and the post-wax redness.  My sister had been talking up threading as the way to go and when I discovered Shanti did it and was around the corner and is only $12 I was sold.  I agree with others that its not a warm and fuzzy type of place.  When I got upstairs after some trouble with the door, I was told to go to the room on the left.  I sat down, and waited a few seconds.  I informed the woman (sorry forget who it was) that I had never been threaded before, and made it clear that I just wanted my eyebrows cleaned up.  I had to hold my hands in kind of awkward positions as she did it.  I think it was a little more painful than waxing, but it was super quick.  My brows seem a little thinner than I normally get, but all the hairs I wanted to get rid of are gone.  $12 is a lot cheaper than you'll pay for waxing at any of the Rittenhouse salons/spas, so I think I'll definitely be going back.  Bonus: they have a stamp card to get the 10th wax free.
The food wasn't bad but we ordered the hottest thing on the menu and it was about as hot as Taco Bell Mild Sauce. Weak.
Great location but hotel is old, under construction right now and was very dusty. Very pricy for the service!!!

- D Sauvignon = mangosteen, juicy, 3/5 $40

- Grenache = spice, herbs, fruity, low tannins, fun but needs aerator. $40 4.5/5

- PNO = boring. 2/5

- Syrah = ok 2/5

- Port = very herbal, almost too much. Best when mixed. Not chocolaty made with herbs. Not a real port wine. 4.5/5

- Vermouth = smells like dark raw honey. Taste like bee pollen and honey. Little harsh like bee pollen. Better mixed. Slightly like juniper gin. Needs to be mixed. 3.5/5
Great location.  Clean and safe.  The rooms were a little small, but who cares?  Thanks to Rico for the great restaurant tips!  You're a prince. Breakfast buffet was yummy.  Nice pool area.

Extraordinary flavors from southern India ! Impeccable service, very reasonably priced. I highly suggest you follow the owner's recommendations as they were spot on this evening in the company of two friends! The curries are to die for. I was completely blown away by the quality and fantastic flavors of the food here at 615 Chutney ! Thank you for a lovely evening!

we went here originally because I'm a huge sushi fan and it's right by our loft. They have a happy hour from 4.30 to 6.30 with half price drinks and half price sushi- so it gets pretty crowded. 

the half price sushi drew us in- but also drove us away. the pieces were so small it looked like they just used half the ingredients for the happy hour! not only that, but the rice (on more than one occasion) has been overdone and hard. the meat also tastes a little off. 

my husband tried the curry the last time we went- giving it one more shot. again, the food was sub par, the service was lacking as all 5 servers serve all tables- they constantly forgot to bring drink refills and we felt we were being interrupted. 

We wont be going back.

Enjoyed their sausage pear sandwich immensely. Perfect marriage of delicious flavors. My hubs had something w a lot of meat and Fritos in it and he loved it!

Our soup was barely warm but still good.

I feel like TGIF is the good place to go if you aren't local. I was in town for biz meeting and needed a place to have lunch. I know what I'd get from TGIF so without hesitation we went there. 

Fast service, friendly people serving food, and large parking. Perfect for biz lunch.
Good fun, great music and a wonderful selection of food, most of which (but not ALL) feature the squash like vegetable as an ingredent.
Best part of this place is the vibe. Great way to spend time with friends and have some decent food. Good pricing on pitchers.

if it gets were possible to give negative stars, I would. Comcast is the worst company that I have ever dealt with in my life. I pay 80$$ for their blast internet and I get dial up speed. after speaking with them till I was blue in the face, a rep finally told me they didn't even offer that speed in my area. so, basically they sold me something they can't even offer me. they won't refund my money. I have stood in their lines for hours only to be blown off by a rude rep. I have been told I am stupid by a rep over the phone. unbelievable.  I am stuck with these clowns till at&t is available in my area. :( oh, they constantly mess up my bill and overcharge me, let's not forget that.

decent food served in a modern, lounge setting.  sushi was decent but it didn't blow our socks off.  we were surprised to find bim bim bap on the menu..and they also make pad thai if you request it (non- menu item and very generous serving).  bim bim bap was tasty but was not served in the traditional stone bowl.  when it came to raw fish, we stuck to makis...spicy tuna was ok, not memorable.  Spider roll was prepared nicely.

The bar looked very welcoming...nice spot to have drinks.

We have been going to Santucci's as a date night place for years.  We absolutely love their vegetable pizza.  Every vegetable on top is so fresh and delicious.  We have never been able to find a vegetable pizza anywhere else like it.  Furthermore, the staff is always super friendly.  As for Joe, we can't say enough good things about him.  During our last visit, we were joking with him that we can't get our kids to eat the pizza because the cheese is on the bottom so we save it for date night.  That night he sent us home with all the ingredients we need to cook the pizza at home with the cheese on top.  Not to mention, he sent it home free of charge.  Talk about going out of his way for us.  A well deserved 5 stars here.

I was selling a scooter I had lost the key for. They were to install a new ignition. The folks who send out the ignition told me it goes out within 24 hours. But it took 2 weeks for Maplewood to call to say the ignition was finally in. The repair was to cost about $150. Once they had it, they called and said I need a new battery and the carburetor needed to be cleaned. I authorized the work. When they called with the bill, it was $350! They were to deliver the scooter on a particular day. Never showed up. Later said that we never agreed on that day. Same thing happened a second time. To their credit, when the scooter arrived, it sounded great  - so the mechanic is good. But my helmet and goggles that had been locked in the secured seat storage area had been stolen. I asked them about it and they pretended like they didn't know anything. No apologies. Nothing. Summary - mechanic seems good - but to work with these people you need to keep your wits about you or you can leave there with your pockets picked. IMHO.

I stopped at this place for lunch. 

The building exterior and the interior resembled that of a Don Pablos; I used to like Don Pablos (when they were more common), so this place invoked a bit of nostalgia for me.

We received tortilla chips & salsa; they were good, but its hard to mess up tortilla chips. 

I ordered the Burrito Supremo; I enjoyed it, and it was really filling. I also enjoyed the refried beans & the rice; it's a very simple side dish but it really compliments the meal.

The server was friendly and helpful.

Overall the food was good and I will likely go again.

Okay okay okay, you might be wondering how a Cuban bakery can get three stars....

First let me start by saying, I love Cuban food. Since moving to Tampa, it's held a special place in my heart...and my stomach. However, after coming to La Segunda for a breakfast sandwich, I have come to learn that Cubans are good at baking bread, but not necessarily making a breakfast sandwich with that said bread. 

I ordered a sandwich with cheese, egg, and sausage. The portions are pretty decent for the price. The sandwich itself has enough for two meals and is roughly $5. However, there is a serious lack of seasoning and taste in the eggs. I was very hopeful, but ended up highly disappointed. Stay clear from those breakfast sandwiches, better off picking up a McMuffin. 

They have an assortment of different pastries and other lunch sandwiches that I didn't get to try. From what I hear, they're supposed to be pretty good so I might give La Segunda another shot.

Three of us ate here for the first time.  We ordered four different dishes and each one was absolutely amazing.  Our order consisted of clear noodle soup, sweet sour pork, house special noodles, hot crispy pork.  Every dish was delicious.  We will definitely be back!!!

This place is horrible!!!   They consistently overbook and the wait is always over an hour.   It also seems that they take advantage of the insurance companies.   X-rays are taken at every visit, even after the bone has completely healed.   Too many follow up appointments--especially for workers comp claims.

Don't you just love when you pick a new place to dine at and it's an overwhelming win?  I found such a place in Barbecue and Bourbon.  The BBQ was fantastic.  I decided to go with a four meat platter.  It was part of the Devour menu.  If you are unable to catch them during Devour week, I would still go with a platter for your first visit.  I really enjoyed sampling the ribs, pork, brisket and sausage.  To my surprise, my favorite was the smoked sausage.  Delicious!  
The restaurant is tiny but bursting with personality. The staff was extremely friendly and attentive.  Excellent bourbon options just as the name suggests and I was very happy to see they also served Kentucy  Bourbon Barrel Ale - Beer.  
The only drawback is the size.  The space.  I mentioned earlier, it is tiny.  That is not an exaggeration. It is so small, you will constantly be stepping around your fellow customers and the staff.  But please don't let this discourage you!  Just giving you the 'heads-up' so you won't be as taken aback as I was when I arrived.   All in all, I very much enjoyed my evening there and will be back soonest.

Average Thai/Laos food got the Pad Thai and it was extremely sweet and a lot of sauce! I come here often because it's close to work and if I didn't bring my own food from home, but other then that the food is okay.

Note to owner, if you are going to have floors that require the use of a  5 gallon bottle of bleach to clean them, wait until the place closes to patrons. Bleaching the floors while people are eating definitely tells them to go away and never come back. There are so many other places to get a cheesesteak!

If you're looking for a Sisco truck delivered, uninteresting, painfully loud, and horribly disorganized eatery with sub par, small, chemical-infused wings - this is the place for you!

Food: [2/5]
Buffalo Wild Wings - your entire franchise is founded on the quality, variety, and promise of great wings - how in the world could you possible fall short on the bread and butter of your company?

While you have a great variety of options for flavors, all of the varieties that I have tried taste like they were made in a lab. It seems like you guys did some research to find a common base and then add in some acidic taste combination of God knows what to get a general flavor that in general tastes like a jalapeno. How about using actual jalapenos?

The wings are small and tasteless, save for the chemical base added to them.

We also ordered an app: the soft pretzels with dipping sauce. The sauce container was, at best, half filled. The pretzels were over done, chewy, lacking, and cold upon delivery.

Service: [2/5]

This place has a waiting section that can hold about 10 people in total, where you get your feet stepped on from patrons coming and going the entire time. It's chaotic and cramped and the sound of the TV's is deafening. You can also wait outside, where they are kind enough to have more speakers, duplicating the ear crushing volume as inside. We asked for our order to go and the girl stared at us like we were asking her for answers to quantum physics theories. It's pretty simple: we don't want to eat here because it's setup like a Chuck-E-Cheese filled with douchbags - this means put our food in something we can take with us.

Filling the place with good looking servers may, and likely does, influence some people to stay and eat here - but not I. I want good food, prompt service, and an atmosphere that doesn't feel like the outtakes of Animal House.

You can't miss this place walking on Girard-there's a large vinyl record on the wall! When I was a regular here, it was new and just getting established and was STILL an amazing experience. Their vinyl collection specializes in 60's and 70's funk, soul, R&B and 80's/early 90's Hip-Hop. I am always finding something really cool. I literally have never had a better croissant than this place. It was warm and the perfect balance of soft and crispy. The barista/owner is a totally chill, cool-as-heck dude that is always up for talking about the music he loves and that you SHOULD love.

I ordered the Margherita from Slice on a Sunday afternoon.  The delivery time was great, it only took about 30 minutes which I was shocked about.  The price I felt was a little high but I'm not complaining because it seems like what most pizzerias are getting to now.

I could tell that the pizza was hot before I even opened the box, which is always a plus.  The issue came when I opened the box to dive in.  The cheese had all melted back together so the slices were very hard to pull apart, ended up having to recut them.  Then the pizza was just very flimsy.  You could not pick up a slice and just eat it at all.  The crust was very thin and soaked all the way through.  The flavor was good but overall I wasn't very satisfied with the pizza, may try again eating in their restaurant.

Its always such an artistic and mindblowing experience each time I visit.  This time it was with my daughter.  The Saunders planetarium show is our favorite.  The colors, the scientific details in all things done at mosi never seize to amaze me. We love to see the "one small step" expedition. This replicate the events that took place at the moon landing and emulates what happened. I was like a giggly girl. My daughters eyes lit up as she is a fan of the idea zone. Here she explores and become so fastened on the displays. Shes only 4 and is already so intrigued. I definitely want to try the ropes course next. My last visit was memorable and each exhibit was adventurous as the last. Very informative and well designed. Thanks MOSI.

In terms of retail, this place has a few stores that I like to visit and few that I don't want to visit again. In terms of food, they have an Einstein Bagel inside and upstairs in the Medical Center and they have a few freestanding places out front: Logans, Panda Express, Panera, and Cheeseburger Charlie's. 

Just be careful when walking between the parking lot and the stores, it seems that many of the people driving through are headed to the Medical Center and are drugged up and don't pay much attention. Ha!

I had been looking for new Asian Market when my friend told me about this location. 

The store is large and even has a jewelry store and a home goods store located inside of it! So when I say they have an incredible selection, I'm not kidding. 

Since my husband is a fan of Ramen, I was able to over indulge him for the Holidays and get him a ton of flavors I wasn't aware of. He was happy! 

I will certainly come back here in the future!
Way overpriced for what it is and the food wasn't that great.  Most of the items came out lukewarm.  Ended up spending upwards of $40 and came out hungry.  I can't even remember what I've ordered!

I took advantage of working from home to check out the new BBQ place in Soulard.

Yes, it's carry out only, but I like that.

I also like that they actually care about the food they're serving and go out of their way to find local and sustainable ingredients.

Anyway, I got the pulled pork platter and it was delicious. It came with two corn muffins, and my choice of sides. I chose the mac n ' cheese and the coleslaw. I would have gotten the potato salad, but it had capers in it and I hate capers.

The pulled pork was really really tender, and it had a lot of delicious burnt ends. The portion was extremely generous, and you could easily split a platter with another person.

The mac and cheese is delicious as well, I think it's white cheddar, I'm not quite sure. But it was great. The coleslaw is good too!

I lovedddddddd the corn muffins. They were made with corn in them, and were really dense and moist. 

They have 4 different bbq sauces to choose from, and I chose... all. There's a blackberry ancho, smokey, house sauce, and a mustard sauce. I liked the blackberry ancho and the mustard the best.

It was really, really good, and if you find yourself around Mad Art Gallery between 11-2, do yourself a favor and check it out.

While visiting friends in the St. Louis area, my wife and I needed a battery recharger for her Canon camera and I needed a 8 megabyte memory card, so I asked for a recommendation on a good camera shop in the area and my friend directed me to Schillers Camera, where Sven White assisted us. I had a question or two about a Nikon lens and I appreciated his time and knowledge. I recommend this shop to anyone who wants to deal with a reputable, knowledgeable camera dealer.

I visited Edison on a Tuesday night with a group of eight coworkers. We were traveling to Tampa for work and are always looking to explore some fun, unique and delicious spots. Several members of our groups had been before and it came recommend, especially the desserts. Edison was able to fit a large group in relatively last minute and said they'd even be able to give us separate checks. Always a plus when traveling in a group. 

My car arrived early and we decided to grab a seat at the bar and enjoy a cocktail before the rest of the group arrived. The space is modern and open and definitely plays up to the Edison theme. They definitely seem to pride themselves on their cocktails and the bar tender was very friendly, attentive and knowledgeable about the cocktail menu as well as other liquors, wine and beer served. Their wine and beer list wasn't extensive but definitely top notch as far as what they did offer.  Solid A for cocktails and atmosphere. 

Our group ordered a large variety of food. From cheese plate to seafood items to the fried chick and  several desserts. While everyone was pleased with their food. No one felt overwhelmed and it definitely left something to be desired. Especially for the price and the hype of "lots of flavors" in different dishes. More often than not the group felt like their dish was a bit bland compared to what was described by the waiter or the menu. I shared the cheese plate and was a bit disappointed in the amount and variety of cheese offered in the plate for $17. I've had my fair share of cheese plates and this one was definitely overpriced for only three cheeses and the accompaniments.  

E everyone in our group would agree that dessert was the best part of the meal. I'd definitively save room for dessert and would recommend the chocolate peanut butter bar or the butter cake. Our service was wonderful as well, which I especially appreciate when dining in a large group which I know can be challenging.

My barber recommended this place between the shop and my volunteer gig. Surprised that they weren't on Yelp yet. The place was clean and contemporary inside. The service was wonderful and the servers attentive.

I was in and out within 20 minutes. The food was fine. The toast was a thick slice of Texas style toast. Could have done without the potatoes in my omelette. I'll make sure to let them know in advance the next time.

Great find for the area.
Probably the best pizza in the area. Thin crust and kind of sweet sauce. Parking can be hard at times but worth it.

I went to little nonna's before a night out. 
The outdoor seating is pretty cute for the size! We made reservations 30 minutes before coming and we were able to get seated outside. 

We ordered the garlic bread, the spaghetti and meatballs and the rigatoni a la vodka. 
Firstly, the garlic bread was super burnt and charred. Do it didn't taste as tender and garlicky as I wanted.. 
they give you a whole garlic head on the side that has been baked with olive oil. The garlic wasn't as pungent as I'd like either. I think they over cooked it.

The rigatoni was pretty delicious. The sausage that came with it could have been better. The sauce didn't have the saltiness that comes with the sauce. Other than that, there isn't much that I could complain about. 

The spaghetti.. don't get me started. It was EXTREMELY salty. They skimped out on the salt from the rigatoni and dumped it on the spaghetti. The dish was huge and so were the meatballs. But, if someone is going to eat that much salt with their spaghetti their arteries are going to burst. As much as I love spaghetti.. it's not a hard dish to prtgr

Two stars because I have received a couple facials from her in the past that were decent but the last two times I've tried to schedule something I've been completely ignored. I tried on Facebook and by phone with no response. Even if the answer is no, a response would be the professional thing to do. How do you run a business if you don't even respond to your clients?
The out side service is always so slow!  Just order two beers at a time and don't wast your time on the food because it is so inconsistent.   Good beer.

I am in from out of town and sat in this most wonderful little neighborhood cigar store and enjoyed myself immensely. A really large and diverse selection of cigars, and not all of them too expensive. Fine conversation and character. 

I'll spend time here every time I'm in STL going forward. Thanks for a great cigar outing!

I was so relieved on Saturday night when I stepped into The Irish Pol.  In the midst of Olde City, The Irish Pol is a calm, quiet alternative to the watering holes with frat boys crushing beer cans on their foreheads.  With an extensive draft beer selection, the place is tiny and intimate and not over-crowded.  We spent our evening upstairs in the private room for a wedding after-party.  I'm not sure if that room is usually open to the public, but there is a bar upstairs and some cozy benches around the perimeter of the room.  

If you're looking to have a low-key evening and enjoy an extensive beer selection, onward, ho! to The Irish Pol!

Great pizza, great gelato, and great service. Christino's was a serendipitous find for our group of out-of-towners and they were extremely gracious hosts who offered authentic Italian pizzas and gelato.
Nice facility but body shop service is poor. They didn't even come out to show me the work done.

We ordered take-out so I can't speak to the atmosphere, but the food was sub-par, and, with TONS of other restaurants around, they probably need to step up their game. Here's a tip, go to the Opry House Grill next door. Now that food was amazing

The food was tasty enough, but not necessarily innovative. I had the chicken Cobb salad. Bacon, avocado & blue cheese were plentiful. Rich enough to have without dressing.

Chicken Parm sandwich was flavorful and filling. Portions big enough for one very hungry person or for two slightly hungry people to share.

They have free popcorn!

Outdoor seating is ideal for people watching. Convenient & reasonably priced stop for quick lunch.

Sushi is fine, octopus and white fish Nigiri pieces have a very mild taste. Salmon is my favorite, sweet and fresh. Spicy tuna roll is very good too, and their miso soup. The wine store next door has good selection of sake to match their sushi.

Chirashi was a disappointment. This is normally served in a bowl with a bed of sushi rice, then all sashimi pieces on top. Instead, it was on a plate with steamed rice (not sushi grade) and very lightly seasoned with rice wine.
Salad served with a dressing that looks more like a coleslaw. Ordered their Agedashi Dofu, a bit confused since it was simply thin fried tofu. Typically this is heavily battered in cubes soaked in a broth with mirin & Japanese soy sauce.

Overall, just ok for a sushi place.

Excellent experience today. My sweet friend since 8th grade drove a good bit to have lunch w me and we decided on this restaurant because it's convenient for her in traveling and me too.

We got the table-side guac and ordered medium heat. It was delicious with minced red onions,  lemon juice, garlic powder, cilantro and 3 plump avocados. This was my first experience with table- side guac. It could have used a lot more heat but I really enjoyed the "show" it's quite a production when they wheel the cart out etc. it was delicious. 

My friend ordered lunch portion chicken fajitas at only 10.50. Very delicious eye appeal and they came out sizzling hot. I used to be a server at Casa Maria in KOP and it was very reminiscent of those days gone by. They gave a nice set up of beans, sour cream, lettuce etc. I got a lunch combo that was a Chile relleno, and I thought I ordered the cheese enchilada. What I got was a mini cheese quesadilla and it was good. Not sure what cheese they use in the quesadilla but it doesn't taste like authentic Mexican to me. I really did enjoy the meal and the service was great! I'm sorry I don't know the man's name who served us but he was a pleasure at noon on Sunday 12/30/18. We wanted to chit chat and he did not rush us. I sure did appreciate that.

The server gave us two "secret" envelope things that you bring back to your visit to see if you win a gift card, free appetizer or free dessert on your next visit. It's a fun clever promotion. 

The restaurant itself is very beautiful inside. Please keep up the great work!

Have been here about 10 times over the past 6 months for eyebrow waxing, gel manicures and pedicures. Have never been offered a drink. Recently went with my mother-in-law for a girls night. We opted for regular manicures. When we asked for a glass of wine, we were told it was only for people getting a gel manicure and up. (Note: sign says 1 complimentary glass of wine per visit). They either need to change their sign or comply! We were embarassed and will not be returning.

This is the only market you can find hard to find Chile candies !!  They also have hard to find sodas and chips.  It's convenient for the location and for the building it's in kept pretty clean surprisingly
Wings ribs and beer! So good. The Alabama white sauce on the wings it spectacular. The staff is great too.
Weird atmosphere, horrible decor, totally not a "Grill." Owners smoking cigarettes outside by the windows, food is sub-par. Truly a grotesque experience.

Great new restaurant on Oak street uptown! I came in for happy hour on a Friday around 5 and we had the option of sitting at the bar, at high shareable tables, or outside. We chose to sit at the end of a high shareable table, which allowed us to chat with others but talk on our own when we wanted to. I'd heard about their happy hour ($5 small plates, half off Louisiana draft beer, half off 6 ounce wine pours, and half off well cocktails) so I came determined to try a bunch of different dishes. 

Dishes we ordered:
Fried cornbread -- OH MY GOSH. These were absolutely amazing! First of all, I love love love cornbread so frying it made it even better! Plus it was paired with a goat cheese -- yum! We ended up ordering a second order because we enjoyed them so much
Fries -- really yummy! They weren't quite as crispy as I would have liked but they were flavorful and I really liked the dill za'atar ranch that came on the side
Crab potatoes -- I liked these but the pickled lemon was a little overpowering
Mushroom Boudin balls -- didn't taste like mushrooms at all! The flavor was very smokey and tasted like actual meat (but it is totally vegetarian). I liked them but I didn't love them.
Cauliflower Rillettes -- really liked this! It was served as a dip with toast, which was fun to eat. The flavors were really great, especially the sweetness of the fennel-raisin preserve.
*Yes I know we went very carb heavy but I like carbs and it was happy hour so we treated ourselves.

Overall, very very happy with the food we ordered! The space itself was absolutely beautiful, it seemed very down home chic while still feeling natural in New Orleans (didn't seem too upscale New York/ San Francisco/ etc.). DTB seems like a great spot to go for a date or with a group of friends for cocktails and dinner. While the main dinner plates aren't cheap, I can definitely see myself returning for a nicer dinner or another happy hour. All in all, I'll definitely be back!

Food -- 4.5 stars
Price -- 4 stars normally (5 for happy hour)
Drinks -- 4.5 stars (good selection)
Service -- 4.5 stars
Atmosphere -- 4.5 stars

Service department is far below expectations. I purchased a used 2012 F250 with 53k miles from upstate Pa. I dropped it off here to diagnosis a violent shake in the steering wheel and to give it a multi-point inspection. Fred beans technicians claimed the issue was merely an alignment and tire balance problem. Oddly, they don't have a machine to balance these tires, that are slightly larger then stock. They charged me $74.18 stating the steering and suspension components are all good! I proceed to take my FORD truck to a Toyota dealership who supprisingly has the proper tire balancing machince!? Toyota balances the tires. Problems not fixed. We end up taking it to a local competitor who properly diagnosed the issue as a "death wobble" a term ford knows well. 

Track bar, steering damper and track bar ball joint are the components that were compromised. The Fred Beans service department appear to be inexperienced. It's disappointing I can't trust Ford technicians to work on my Ford truck.

I was excited to try this place. I was in the mood for a good burrito and enchiladas. It was just my luck that they actually had a combo meal with both. I ordered a #19 and it was a Beef Burrito with a Cheese Enchilada, Rice, and Refried Beans.

Well where do I start? Ok, so my Burrito was right on top of the Enchiladas and Beans which made my burrito extremely soggy. The Enchiladas were extremely disgusting! The beans had many pieces that were not fully cooked. It was like I was buying into little pebbles. And the rice was just mussy and had no flavor. My Burrito was supposed to be a Beef Burrito with Peppers and Onions, but it was more like a Peppers and Onions Burrito with Beef. And the Beef was very very dry! If I could give this place zero stars I totally would. I will never go here again! 

Utterly disappointed!!!

Didn't have good serice. No silverware before we started eatting. Eggs were not over hard like I asked for. My friend did not get her double chess like she asked for. Overall service was horrible. I hope they improve for the future.
We just ordered hot dogs this week and found green mold on the hot dog bun.

I've stayed a few times and this time was by far the worst experience.  First, my reservation was lost.  I will say the front desk resolved that issue promptly and gave me a larger room for the same price since it was an error on their side.  The main issue is really the cleanliness of the room.  I found hair on my bed and nail clippings on the floor behind the nightstand.  I am a bit if a neat freak but I would say even this would turn anyone off.  Luckily there were 2 beds so I picked the bed without hair on it.  This is the first time I have experienced this here so I will give it another chance but this visit was not good.

Bad service and food. Basically a diner. Don't come here if you're looking for better quality food than a waffle house, at least for breakfast. Small menu of basically eggs and Omelets, nothing remotely worth coming for. Maybe they have a better lunch or dinner menu but I was not allowed to order from that. Service was incredibly poor - server was grumpy and not accommodating. At first I was told no substitutions, then told I would be charged (which is fine, but I don't know how spinach substituted for a meat, which is more expensive, makes sense for additional charges... But whatever. Don't tell me I can't make substitutions if that's not even the case). It was a $4 charge btw which seems very excessive. He also completely ignored me when I asked a question and informed us he was bringing us our checks the moment he delivered our food. We did not ask for them or say we were in any type of rush. In fact we were drinking mimosas so he did both us and himself a disservice by not leaving our tab open.

Food was decent but service was awful. Not only did we have to ask for drinks more than once but we had to SHARE FORKS! We realized only 3 of 4 of us had forks before we got appetizers and she NEVER bought them even though we basically begged her.  Good thing one of us had a burger and could lend out his fork!

This is the only place I will bring my car, excellent work, professional crew, and great prices. I have a black Camaro that gets dirty quickly, they have been able to restore the like new look of my car every time I go. The hand wax is very thorough and excellent price. I will never take my car anywhere else!!

As you can see from my prior reviews, I enjoyed my dinner at Firebirds down in Gainsville, Virginia. This was my first time here at this location for lunch. My party of four did not have a reservation. We were seated immediately when we arrived around 12:30. I started with my usual Pom Ginger Margarita for $11. I enjoyed my Kobe meatloaf for $14.50.

Caroline served our table needs well. My total bill was $101 less gratuities. I would return here again given the chance or even another Firebirds location.

Who goes to Acme Oyster house and orders a po' boy? Me! Boy, that was a big mistake. The po' boy was a huge disappointment. That bread was SO hard and stale I ended up just eating the protein inside. I think the menu even said it was a "famous" po' boy. Hmmm.

I will say that the charred oysters are TOTALLY worth the wait in the line. The few times I have been, the wait wasn't too bad. Anything longer than 30 mins is too long for me... but we got a table within 20 minutes and the line does move pretty fast here. My friends enjoyed their dishes, but I really think the only thing I would recommend here are the oysters. Don't go to a oyster house and order anything else but oysters. That was my mistake. 

The char grilled oysters come out fresh and piping hot, you can even see the guy making them in one section of the restaurant. Don't forget to dip your french bread into the nice butter afterwards. So good.  Learn from my lesson and just stick to the basics when ordering.  

P.S. The door person will catch some attitude with you. Not sure if it's a happening spot and they felt entitled, but I didn't think it was warranted especially since I was not inebriated like most folks waiting in line.

Read a lot of reviews that mention this is a must see while in Santa Barbara. It certainly was. The architecture and landscaping on the property was beautiful. There is a tower you can climb up to get a 360 view of Santa Barbara. 

There were showing free movies in the evenings.
The best Italian beef and sausage combo outside of Chicago.  Just right amount of spicy peppers.  Crispy bread.  The sandwich is so big it won't fit in your mouth.

Ummm...how come we haven't figured out how to merge gelato and bar drinks here in NYC???

Gelato was only "meh" to me.  But Capogiro scores extra points for 1) SERVING BOOZE and 2) being the place where my baby bro got propositioned by some hot blonde taller than him.  Awesome.

I just returned from Seattle and their mighty Pike Market, therefore I couldn't help but compare. Bottom line, this market has nothing on Pike. It's a place for locals and probably tourists. But when I return to Philly, this will not need a revisit. 

Soap Lady is the coolest spot. Everything else can easily be obtained from other locations.

After a year of waiting to get a new puppy after the loss of our pet, my husband found Playful Pets online. We made an appointment to see a few of the puppies and couldn't have been more impressed. The facility was very clean and spacious and actually a great place to see puppies. 
The staff was also super friendly and knowledgeable. Not to mention patient!! 
Our puppy is very healthy and happy since day one!! We're very happy with the service snd the dog we chose!!

My experience w/AutoNation Port Richey did not go well.  Since I dealt w/them from a distance (east coast of FL vs the west coast) it was was important that I receive accurate and timely information.  Neither was rec'd and I would suggest to any potential customer not to expect good service unless you a local customer.

I literally just walked in from an early evening at Giorgio on Pine. After an afternoon at the Chamber Orchestra, my parents, aunt and uncle and I went for dinner at this homey BYO just two blocks from the Kimmel Center.

Giorgio himself seated us at a table that had our bottles of wine ready and waiting (aunt and uncle had dropped off the goods before the performance). We started with the complimentary bread, olive oil and whole cloves of roasted garlic, which spread onto the crusty bread like butter. We also shared appetizers of grilled vegetables and a prosciutto plate, which were all plated lovingly. My uncle ordered the mushroom soup, which was more like a pate or a spread. So savory, so delicious- I think it just needed to be thinned out a bit more.

I had the special appetizer of polenta-encrusted scallops in a red pepper cream sauce as my entree. SIX scallops- yes, SIX- for a whopping $13. I dare you to find a better bang for your buck on this side of Broad! 

The rest of the table had fresh ravioli, veal melanzane, veal with lemon, and the special fettuccine with crab. The only meal that disappointed was the crab- I think my mother was expecting whole pieces of meat instead of shredded.

We wrapped up with a piece of Tiramisu. There was no corking fee, the waitstaff was polite and authentic (real Italian accents!) and the food was wonderful. The only thing that I found even slightly lacking was the atmosphere. The table and chairs set up seemed a little sterile, despite the exposed brick and otherwise cozy atmosphere. Know what I mean. Regardless, I'll be back with a big group of friends for sure.

Roxy is the most passionate person I have ever met, you can really feel she loves her job and wishes to make her clients happy. I went in with just a vague idea of what I wanted but she got me instantly. When the color didn't quite get to the tone I liked, she did it again and it was perfect. Not only my hair looked great when I left, I also felt I spent some very delightful time chatting with Roxy. I will definitely go back!

First time coming to this restaurant. Intended on trying the avocado toast, that looked so delicious on advertisement, but they were out. Ended up getting the Thai Peanut Salad with chicken and my friend got the catch of the day. The salad definitely needed more flavor- a waste, very bland. The fish portion was so small. Drinks, atmosphere, and service were good. I'd give it another try.
Two hour wait with two families in the lobby.  Walked through dining room and counted six open tables...disappointed.

If you ever want to wait an hour and half for under cooked salmon and then they take it back and totally over cooked it, if you want all these to happen then this is the place. Feel bad for the young management team because they didn't even offer to adjust the bill or anything.
The food was very flavorful, the margaritas tasty, and the service was fantastic!! Highly recommend!

Walked in and we're sort of rudely told to take a seat- and then you have to go order at the bar, wait for your own drinks- return for your own refills. Perhaps this is an issue due to Covid and lack of staff. However, the staff could have been a bit more informative and helpful. Food was just okay. I'd skip this place unless you're staying at the motel next door and just want to belly up to the bar.

My husband and I ordered the meat sampler for 2, each cut was cooked great, moist with lots of flavor. 
It is very reasonably priced, and we got a free glass of malbec which was good, and we ordered a Pinot Noir which was also good. So overall a great place. 
Here is where they need to improve - the atmosphere. If they would only add a white tablecloth it would make the place perfect!! Service was good too, so we will be back. 
Update- returned with my son on a weeknight, the place was empty. If you do not order the sampler for 2, the price is close to what Cooper's Hawk charges, and I'de rather be at Cooper's Hawk. Some of the meat was fatty. Place really lacks atmosphere. Not sure if Ill be back. Did I mention tablecloths would be a great addition?

Mediocre food and terrible service. The good reviews on Yelp totally confuse me.  We got the Burrata with black truffles to start, sounds good but it wasn't.  He got the flat iron steak which came out extra rare; sent it back to get it medium rare and it was well done.  My spaghetti with clams were sooo dry and tasteless; the picture of this dish looks awesome with the fresh clams but I had about 20 clam shells but got about 8 that actually had meat in them.  Even the bread wasn't good.  It's like Sbarro at the mall, all dried out and flavorless. How can an establishment like this stay in business, let alone be popular?  All hype, no substance.  Trust me, don't go here.

This place was kinda disappointing. The first thing I saw was a lot of staff in the front by the entrance. Something about that was irking... I feel like they should have been tending to customers instead of being in the front. 

The place is clean and well decorated. It's got a nice bar with TV's around so if you want to come chill, it's a good place to do so. I sat with my friend at a table and the waitress came. She seemed very young and very shy... Service was a bit awkward but can't complain about her work since she came to check in on me and my friend constantly. 

The food... I ordered the ribs and it was pretty solid. The ribs did come off the bones pretty effortlessly. I got the rice as well which was seasoned a bit too much but it's ok. The steak my friend ordered was a big problem... He ordered the porterhouse medium rare and one side was medium well while the other side was close to being raw. Steakhouse that doesn't cook steaks well.... It was my biggest disappointment. 

I gotta note that the bathrooms are freezing. The temperature difference between the main section of the restaurant and the bathroom is stark... I wonder why?

Overall, the place is ok. Wouldn't really look to come back for steak. Maybe other things?

Ordered a mocha coolata (kinda like a frappachino) it was amazingly delicious!!! The staff was friendly and the place was spotless!! The manager here is doing well. Amy (manager) is doing great with her crew. Last review said it was horrible and after talking to a few people who agreed it was, but now it has done a 360!!! I'm looking forward to coming back!!!

This hotel was fine for a night in Nashville.  It was within walking distance to many restaurants and bars. It's so close to Hattie B's which is reason enough to stay. We arrived a little early and they let us check in early.  We took a lyft from the hotel a couple of times and it was only about $5 to mercy lounge and the gulch.  Great location!

Another great Green place with pathways that are ideal for dog walking or running. The trails wind through the park around hills and large grass fields back through the back woods and around a small pond that is gorgeous for observing the geese and fish.

The sledding hill is great in the winter but expcect long lines as it is a popular park!

Handful of Korean BBQ places in the area, this one stands out a bit because it also offers hotpot and sushi. The interior decor definitely looks more like a Japanese restaurant with the wood rafters. Service is fantastic and friendly, BBQ is pretty standard, and the side dishes are yummy. Whenever I go out for Korean BBQ, I always order seafood pancake (which is great here as well) and Jap Chae (which is not so great here). I like that everyone gets one of those little yogurt probiotic drinks at the end. I haven't tried the hotpot or sushi, but I feel like a restaurant is better when they focus on one style of food instead of trying to encompass multiple styles. I think the best Korean BBQ's in the area only serve Korean BBQ.

pros: clean enough, a nice little parking garage underneath the hotel, good location, indoor pool

cons: unheated very chilly pool, outdoor hot tub, morning breakfast area too small and cannot serve all the people that want to use it, no wifi. 

I probably won't stay here again since there is a Hampton right around the corner and I like them better.

I was very happy to be able to reserve a scooter for my 70+ mother 2 days prior CMA fest! and was told they only had one kind 3 wheels!.  When we arrived for pick up it had 4 wheels and said it was the last one even though I had reserved one already!!  Then on second day battery ran out while my mom was in downtown Nashville.  Bless those that took care of disability transportation, as they made sure they got back to our car!  My sister had to drive back to franklin and get a new battery!! Guy said he should have checked it...YES he should have... also took back a day early and battery had ran out again after about 2 hours limited use the day of return( supposed to be 4 hours non-stop)!!  Because of repeated issues we asked about a discounted rate.. and guy said he would have to see what was wrong first!!! Customer service buddy ...it should not matter, already changed battery once and battery was in red again! We had a faulty unit!.  I do  not normally write bad,   reviews but beware...make sure to pay ahead and get what u paid for..The positive was my mom really enjoyed having a scooter and would not have enjoyed cma fest without it!!

I had such high expectations for clarkville after seeing it written up in many online articles but our experience fell SUPER short. 
 
First, our waitress had an incredibly hard time describing dishes on the menu and I actually had to step in to explain what things were to the other people in our party. 

Second, the food was just okay. The other people in my party liked their salads but the pizzas were blah. We ordered the tomato pie and sals famous. The tomato pie was bland and nothing exciting, Sals famous was extremely salty. 

Third, and this was the worst part- we ordered drinks from our server (I wish I had remembered her name) one young male ordered a beer, he was not carded. I needed some more time so i waited until the waitress came back. When she came back I ordered a glass of wine, and she asks for my ID (flattering at the age of 26) after a LONG day of moving, I had forgotten it. She told me that the restaurant was "strict" on carding. Well my response was- how can you be strict if you didn't card the other person at this table and she was incredibly rude and walked away. I am fine with carding when ordering alcohol, I never get annoyed when asked to prove my age, BUT you can't tell me you're strict on carding when you didn't card the MUCH younger person sitting with me. Needless to say, what if I told you the other person in our party who she did not card was under 21.

If you're "strict" on carding you need to be carding every individual at the table, you can not pick and chose. Im almost positive the Pennsylvania Liquor Control Board would agree with me here. This whole situation would have been a nonissue if she had carded everyone and been pleasant about it. Instead she was rude, smug and unapologetic. 

Overall I will not be returning to this mediocre restaurant. Im sure the food will improve with time, and I hope they will find friendlier people to work there.
Terrible service, they don't manage setting guests during busy times. 45minutes?  Not hardly. Took way to long to get seated and the food was just average.

The customer service sucks and the manager jen gave me a nasty look and rolled her eyes at me when I tried to ask a question. The equipment is just okay nothing special, my college gym is better than this and it's free back at school. Once my membership is done with I'm not going to renew because of the horrible customer service. They need to treat their customers better.

Salads are way over priced. I got the large kickin' cowboy for a total of $14.14. To add chicken it cost $3.50, I got 1 tiny piece of chicken sliced into 5 mini strips. The flavor was great, but for what you get, it should be 8 dollars max. It was not filling what so ever. 

South coast deli is still the best place in town- gigantic salad, tons of chicken for $9.50.
So much fun. Food is fun. Ambiance is fun! Crowd is fun! Great spot to start the night!

We love Three Birds and have gone often.  We take our dogs and sit at the outside patio.

My husband works in hospitality and on Mondays, Three Birds offers a 50% discount off the bill or at least we thought.

We told the waitress that my spouse was in hospitality and she asked me if I was too and I told her I'm not.

We were "schooled" that we need to mention the hospitality aspect at the beginning of the meal.  She was delivering our drinks and we hadn't yet ordered food.

We were then told that my husband would get a discount but I wouldn't.

We didn't make a deal but we're now angry as we're home and realize Three Birds hadn't honored their pledge to the industry.

Not to mention that when we got home, even my husband's drinks were not discounted.  He was charged $3.50 for beer.  Are beers $7.00 or did we get charged full price?

We don't usually go out for hospitality night for various reasons and if we do go in the future, it won't be here again.

Variable.  Many people love this place. We have going it to be inconsistent.  Sometimes the quality of the food and portion sizes are wonderful. Other times it is skimpy portions and bland fare.  It can be good but IMO it can't be counted on.

I was recommended to Molly Maids by my realtor. I needed a cleaning service for my empty condo before listing it. It was a horrible experience! There were several areas that were NOT cleaned properly: the powder room had not been touched, glass doors were not wiped down, granite still had grime on it as if it had not been wiped down, and cabinets were not wiped out like they were supposed to be. After I called and complained, they came back to fix what they had missed but then tried to overcharge me! Gina had explicitly told me that they were there for 2 hours and 59 minutes but then she claimed the system said they had been there for almost 3.5 hours. I did receive a refund to adjust the time but it was still frustrating that I had to call and complain after they didn't do a proper job cleaning in the first place. Oh and then after all of that, they didn't even vacuum the stairs... Horrible service and will not recommend! 

*And a side note, my realtor that had recommended them to me was the one telling me that it was not clean and she said she felt bad for ever recommending them to me in the first place.

I had the garlic crusted drum and it lacked in so much flavor. Especially, salt!!! I was looking so forward to trying this place but was sadly let down. Plus,  the waiter was very nice but just too chatty. Kept over talking when first reviewing the menu.

I normally order Mediterranean food from another restaurant, but decided to give Urban Eat's chicken Mediterranean rice bowl a try. The portion size was great, but I had approx. 5 pieces of chicken in the bowl, 3 spinach leaves/other toppings and hardly any sauce. Therefore it was mostly dry rice... The rice had great flavor but I was expecting more than a bowl of rice. My colleague ordered the same but the Asian style, and her's looked worse. I also wasn't expecting the serving size for the Naan wedges and dip to be so small. It was $5, and we bought it to share. There were only a few pieces of bread, and the dip comes in a 3 oz cup (aka a small condiment cup). Definitely not shareable. I would try it again if the food was made better next time, but likely will go back to regularly ordering from my other go-to.

DO NOT USE PROTURF! They are a bogus company that actually made my lawn worse and over charged for treatments. They said they seeded my yard and not a blade of new grass came up anywhere. This company is a scam!

In some ways I feel bad for writing this review but seriously can't get the experience out of my head.

I went into the salon for my 1st time yesterday on a whim after work for a pedicure. There was a little wait, so I sat on the side distracted by e-mails on my phone & was fine. I was watching the women get nice leg massages during the pedicure & was excited to relax.
