One of the ladies working there felt bad for my wait, which didn't seem that long honestly, & offered to do a free polish on my nails - I thought that was really nice. When it was time for my turn, I was having trouble rolling my tighter pants all the way up to my knees like the lady wanted, so she motioned for me to come into the back with her. She handed me a towel & said to take off the pants & wrap the towel around my waist so she could do the full leg massage. I was a little taken back but tried to look at it as another nice gesture.

During the pedicure, the lady doing my feet became distracted for a couple of seconds & used the scraper thing on the top of my foot as opposed to the rougher bottom. She completely rawed off a piece of skin & cut another part - I was bleeding & have pictures of this. She proceeds to ask me "what happened?" when she comes back to paying attention. I sat dumbfounded, like what else? I haven't left this chair since we started. She used some green/blue liquid stuff to fill the cut & make it stop bleeding. I told her twice how bad it was burning. That was it from there on out, I couldn't focus on the massage because of the burning.

Another lady came over to give me the free polish towards the end of the pedicure but didn't remember that I should probably put my pants back on before that. So I had to walk over to the dryer with wet feet, wet nails & a towel still wrapped around my waist! It was the most awkward, Twilight Zone-ish experience of my life!

When it came time to pay for the service, there was a sign that clearly states "Cash tips only", so I assumed I could slide my card through the Visa machine slot openly sitting on the desk & pay for the total, outside of the tip on my card. Nope, the lady said "Cash is better, there's an ATM".  So $33 for a painful pedicure + $3.50 in ATM fees.

Most of the women in the chairs looked extremely relaxed & it was crowded when I first got there, so I'm sure they have potential to do a great job. That just was NOT my experience at all & I could never talk myself into giving it another go.

So I had to go visit this place as it receives so much hype from the one in New York! And if you're new to Shakeshack, after you order from the register, they give you a little plastic contraption that will light up when they're ready for you to pick up your food so you can seat yourself first instead of standing around waiting for your order. 

Of course, I had the Shack burger. (Being that it's their house burger... and one's house burger should definitely be telling of how good the rest of their food is in general.) It was delicious! The burger patty was juicy, the bread became very flavorful from soaking up the oil and meat juices, and that shack sauce really tasty and ties the taste all together. If I had to describe the shake sauce, it's kind of like their own special mix of mayo with a hint of savory sourness. It tastes good though, trust me. Although I wish the burger was a bit bigger given the price - it's about the same size as a McDonald's burger, except double/triple the price.

I also had a Black and White shake, since I just kind of had to try the shakes from a place called SHAKEshack. It was  a vanilla shake with fudge chunks blended into it. It tasted alright, but it was a little too sweet and way too thick for my liking. Probably wouldn't get a shake again if they're all the same consistency.

Lastly, I have to give an A+++ for their staff, they're definitely very nice and friendly despite the hustle and bustle of 30+ people in line. They also passed out little samples of smores ice cream while people waited in line as to lessen the long wait time impatience of customers. Was not needed, but definitely a very nice and tasty touch!

I'd definitely be up for returning to try more here with friends!

To be completely honest with you, there was nothing there absolutely start out about this pizza except for the size of it. I basically bought it for the photo op on Instagram. You can get this quality type of pizza really anywhere. The restaurant itself wasn't that glamorous the service wasn't that great but the pizza was good and I got a lot of likes on Instagram! I do recommend though for people who are coming to visit Philly for the first time :-) plus who can complain about eating a big ass slice of pizza anyway?

Went to the tasting room. Liked the original vodka. Would have liked to have our choice of flavored vodkas to try. They were out of many of the flavored vodkas to buy. Great idea, seems like it could be a good product all around, except we didn't want to get $30 bottles without tasting them. Would be willing to pay for the tasting experience if we had the option.

I've been back to Soak twice since my original review. The first return trip was excellent - noticing my review, management offered to have me come back in for a re-do. Taylor at the downtown location did a great job on a shellac manicure with excellent attention to detail. 

More recently, I visited the South Reno location for another shellac mani and it was ok. Again, not a ton of attention paid to my cuticles, so my edges looked a little ratty again. Also, the gel started lifting after about a week  and kept snagging on things, so I soaked them off so they wouldn't rip off. I guess I should have tried to go back to see Taylor.

I went once and will never return. I arrived your usual itch-it was sunday and had no beer in the apt-and decided to be experimental and try NP. I had passed by it many times before. It seemed like one of those places that middle class white folk go to be nostalgic about being working class or something. The wood paneling, bricks and dusty bottles all add to the sense of a time past, kinda like the sandlot. This is not to mention the staff. It was your usual restaurant/plantation labor setup: hispanic workers in kitchen popping their heads out every once and a while while your white staff gets cozy with the regulars. 

The one thing that really bothered me about this place was the way the bar smelled, like an old sponge that you just refuse to throw away because you are too lazy or cheap to get a new one. I brought this up with the bartender and he said it was the old septic. Well, I guess I would have to get used to smelling an old sponge while drinking here: I think I would rather get a new one.

Yes! It's open! Get here while you can still get in the door. The gumbo is all dat! The gumbo roux is perfect! 

The shrimp & grits are also very tasty and flavor packed! 

I tried the Crispy Chicken Sandwich and it was delightful. 

They just opened so get in soon!!

WOULD NOT RECOMMEND TO FRIENDS OR FAMILY: We're from Tempe. And lately we've been skeptical about going out to new places cause we just usually get horrible serve. We're not hard to please either. So with that being said. Our server acted like she wanted to have an attitude with us because my partner was seated at another seat than I was at cause I walked in first. Anyways she came to our original table and was like "I wondered where you went" in an attitude. We smiled (we shouldn't have to apologize). Took more than 8 min. To get our water. And another 10 min or so to get our drinks. (It was NOT BUSY). Then we were afraid to ask for more salsa and strWs cause she looked and acted like she was going to have a huge attitude and that we'd be waiting for another extra long time for sides. The food was NOT what we expected. My partner got a carne seca burrito and was NOT impressed. Maybe go over that a bit more with customers? And I got tortilla soup .... NOT what I expected either and I did not enjoy it. Drinks were decent. Waited FAR to long for our check with our dirty plates in our faces. Staff was slow and looked like they would be anywhere but work. A host inside was on her phone charging it the entire time and playing games! . How unprofessional.  ambiance was just as impressive as the experience.... would not go back. ( I will say the host outside was the only enjoyable person. And even while leaving she never said "have a good day." Or any kind thing. Just horrible.
Ordered a breakfast bowl on the go.  They provided no fork. Between that and hash browns being undercooked this is turning into not a good spot for breakfast.

I went yesterday at 4 o'clock to get a Mani/pedi it was dead so I was able to walk in and sit down. I got gels on my nails and a regular polish for my toes. My toes look horrible my husband could do a better job! The whole experience was very uncomfortable. Instead of doing my feet first they did both at the same time. He was very rough and I kept saying ouch hoping he would get it! The woman kept stretching my arms over my body and sticking my hands under the light. Seriously you'd think I had a groupon or something! Horrible place I paid just as much as anywhere else! Don't waste your time or money!

:(  Please see my review for the Hungry Cat Hollywood.  I freakin' adore the Hollywood outlet, and I assumed that I would adore the Santa Barbara location as well.  

Took the (almost) in-laws here for a nice dinner over the weekend.  The food was great--no complaints there.  The service, on the other hand, left much to be desired.  In Hollywood, you can hardly drop a shell outta the bowl without it being picked up ASAP, but in Santa Barbara, dirty napkins, dishes, and various icks were left on the table indefinitely.  We're not talking like the waiter cleaned the mess in 3 trips--we're talking shells still on the table when we paid the bill (after dessert, no less).

Again, drinks and food = excellent.  Just like Hollywood in that respect.  But if I'm paying the $$$ and trying to impress my friends and family--I expect the extra table care.  We'll be sticking with Hollywood.

Every few months I try to give this location another chance when I don't feel like driving to Legends. NEVER AGAIN. They never get anything right. The food always tastes awful. Something is always missing. Just don't even bother going here.

I've had nothing but great experiences in my 3 years as a customer at Shu-Renu. The repairs have been quality, and I've had them do a wide variety of repairs (new soles, fixing soles that are coming loose, heel tips, shine/polish, etc). I was most impressed because I once took a pair of fabric shoes in for cleaning, and while they promised to do their best, they made no guarantees that the oil damage could be undone. When I picked up my shoes they looked 100 times better, but they refused to charge me for the cleaning because they couldn't get the spots out of them. To me it is the sign of a good business that they won't charge for work they can't stand behind.

The other reviews state that orders are not finished on time, and I can't speak to that because I've never needed anything back in a rush or gone in to pick up on the due date. My only complaint is they could be a little better organized.

BLUF:  Best sushi in Mid-City (not many options...but still).  Very friendly service in a small cute little restaurant.  Good menu options with helpful pictures.  

Yesterday was my birthday.  We felt like sushi but were tired of Tokyo (nothing wrong with Tokyo).  Asuka is in a weird location but glad we went.  Just a few tables so we were surprised we didnt have to wait.  One waitress but she was super nice.  Cheap $4 glasses of wine.  

We got 4 rolls between the two of us and it was the perfect amount.  I have no clue what we ordered but they were all amazing.  We got 2 that didnt have rice and they were great.  All of them had substantial amount of fish in them as well which was nice.  Prices were fine.  

Really a great place and we'll be back the next time we want sushi in our neighbhorhood.

THE FOOD WAS GREAT ! My whole table got the Fried Catfish and Shrimp & it was some of the best I have eaten in a while. The only reason I didn't rate this place as a 5 out of 5 was solely based on the ambience. Right next to the table was a heaping pile of trash on the ground... but other than that it was SOOOO GOOD . If you come here during dinner time be prepared to wait... it took us over an hour but THE FOOD was totally worth the wait.

I went last night with friends for the first time. The fried mushrooms were great, the loaded baked potato was delicious, the buffalo wings were delicious however, the steak had a charred taste.  My friend and I had the ribeye and after we ate it , the lingering taste of charred meat hung around. I would probably go again and try the pizza.

I simply cannot say enough good things about this business. Hopefully by the end of reading this review you'll be convinced that you MUST use this company.

I had originally planned to buy the materials elsewhere & just have Chris Grant do the installation. However, they offer a discount if you also purchase the floor from them, so I went in to see what they have. Since they only stock high-quality floors that they stand by, I chose one with a little help & they installed it without problem. Impeccable customer service throughout. Amazingly nice crew. However, as soon as they left, my family and I noticed that the floor we'd chosen was popping and crackling. Chris came over to look at it, and he immediately said, "I wouldn't want to live with this floor, so I wouldn't expect you to, either." PEOPLE, they replaced the floor ON THEIR OWN DIME. If I had purchased the floor elsewhere, I would've been stuck in an endless loop of "it was installed wrong" from the manufacturer and "no, the floor is faulty" from the installer. But when The Floor Store & More says they stand by their products, THEY MEAN IT.

But wait, I'm not done. When they came back to install floor #2 (one that I chose and like even better than the first), they went OUT OF THEIR WAY to make sure the baseboards remained pristine and that we still had a livable space each evening when they left. (We could still cook and eat in the kitchen each night & even sit and watch TV on the couch.) It took them longer than it would have if they'd just ripped it all out in a single day, but they truly treat each home as if it's their own and took the time and care to do it as perfectly as they could. (We have a largish family--six people--so this kind of courtesy made going through the installation a second time so much easier on us.)

The quote I got from The Floor Store & More was not the lowest, but in the long run I saved thousands (not to mention my sanity) by going with them. BTW, they're also the only business I came across that will install, caulk, and paint your baseboards if you want them to. 

Please check out this company; it's rare to find such amazing customer service. Even more amazing to find a company that 100% stands by their products & services, even if it means they lose money on a job.

I'm a fan but with one reservation. My husband and I adore Cafe Provencal's cuisine and we consider it "Our Place" for our anniversary and Valentine's Day.  We've eaten there for years with no complaints until this past Valentine's Day when we witnessed the man who was changing the table linens had placed all of the clean silverware for the table setting ON THE SEAT OF A WOODEN CHAIR!  You would think a restaurant of this calibre would train their employees to use a basket or just  a clean napkin to hold the clean silverware.  We're not sure if we will be returning.  The food is top notch, however.

In my profession I make visits with the guests of this institution.  I can tell you without reservation it is a place you do not want to visit.  There is one good thing about my exposure to this establishment over the past forty years. When I say I am ready to leave, the doors open for me to exit. And I do. Additionally I have never requested to spend a night there.

If you are gluten and dairy free I recommend this place highly. Usually places don't cook the dairy free cheese correct but it was melted perfectly. The only problem was one of my meat options wasn't on my pizza but they apologized and took it off the bill right away. Staff is very kind.

Two of us arrived at 1:00 for a lunch on a Saturday. We were not greeted at all. Eventually, a staff person told us we had to sit inside 'cause the tables outside were full. Fine, we sat inside. 
The same person brought us water, then later a set up, then later the menu. Eventually, the server arrived and took our food and drink order. We ordered the same thing, avocado toast. 
We were chatting and weren't upset, but after 45 minutes, we did not have our food order. The server asked if we received our food. We said no. Shortly after, he brought us cold, tough, terrible croissants, "on the house". As my friend said, "not worth the calories". We didn't eat them. We then waited another 20 minutes till the avocado toast arrived. 
The avocado toast was terrible. The egg was hard boiled and sliced into wedges on top of the avocado. A baby green salad with vinegarette was piled on top of the avocado. Terrible. The toast we received is not what was described on the menu. Then, we were really ready to leave. We tried to catch any of the staff's attention to get our check. they were huddled across the restaurant, chatting. We discussed walking out. We could have, very easily and none of them would have noticed. We could have had a free lunch. But we didn't. We flagged down the unfriendly hostess, who pointed at the server, who was across the room, so we waved out hands/arms again, he noticed and came over.  Another 10 minutes elapsed while he prepared the bill, then he returned to the table and we said, wait, we've got the payment for you. He didn't wait. We waited again to get his attention, gave him the payment an d waited for the receipt. Ridiculous. Not going back. Food terrible, service terrible.

Came here on Sunday afternoon around lunchtime and we were able to get right in which was pretty nice. Our waiter Christian was very nice and also quick which is a plus in my book. We started off with chips and queso for an appetizer and they brought out 3 different types of salsas as well. The queso was the type where it was made in a skillet and was more thick/cheesy compared to a more liquid version at most restaurants in Nashville and with queso I prefer the latter so I was not too impressed with this queso. The salsa was ok as well. They has a cilantro based which I liked, smoky based which I did not like and a spicy one that had a little bit of heat but didn't have any special taste to it. For our entrees I got the Pollo Rojo and Pork Belly Pastor Tacos and my wife got the Short Rib Tortas. We both agreed that all of the food was a bit bland/dry and didn't stand out. Although the place has a good location and cool vibe it's not enough for me to come back.

Strange place.... Good if you are out renting a canoe or kayak for the day, but otherwise, it's a typical dive bar.  Food was actually decent, and they have some unique items like the blue cheese burger.  It's an adventure, and a place to "people watch"... Previous reviews about multiple mullet sightings aren't kidding!

I used the drop of laundry service to have 15 round table cloths pressed for my wedding. I had called around for quotes. When I called Jessica's laundry I gave the measurements of the table cloths, number of items and timeframe in which I needed them. I was quoted a price (lower than other quotes) and happily dropped off my order. The day before I had scheduled pick up I received a call that my order was complete but that I had been misquoted on the price and it was going to be double the original quote to pick up. I was not called prior to the job being done to be informed of a price change so I was not willing to pay the difference and expected to pay what I was quoted. Had I been given the new quote before the work was done I'd have taken my order elsewhere. I was told that the girl was new and gave me the wrong price and that I should have known that that size job would cost more. I am not in the laundry business and have no idea what the costs are! I was then told that the prices are listed on the desk in the store...great, but I got the quote over the phone and expected that the person I spoke to knew what they were talking about! Not my job to figure out the estimate! I went to pick the order up, after receiving the call they were finished and was told that they were taken home by an employee and ironed at their home! I'd never authorized that, but had to make another trip back the next day to get them. Instead of hanging them, like originally agreed, they had recorded them and placed them back in a box, requiring repressing...which we did ourselves after paying. They were incredibly rude and acted like I was at fault and I should have somehow known better. You couldn't pay me to deal with these rude, unprofessional indivi

I'd give a 5 for excellent food but goddam it's hard to get ahold of people! One time I called to place an order & once I arrive the damn place was closed!! I had to settle for McDonald's that night I was so pissed. They need to learn how to answer the phones & stop closing early just because they feel like leaving!

The food is great, service skilled and thoughtful, ambiance pleasantly French, but the restroom is not even minimally accessible. An ADA-height toilet and a grab bar beside it would make a visit to Caribou much pleasanter for people with accessibility problems.  Go and enjoy dining there, but guests need to be prepared not to need the restroom if they need it to be accessible.

I do not give the hotel one star because of the rooms or location, I give the hotel one star because a dress of mine was stolen from the closet.

I was in town for a wedding and had some dresses hung in the closet. I left them there when I checked on by accident and called immediately, before new guests could check in, to let them know I left them and that I would be back to pick them up. I called once and no one answered. I called a 2nd time and I explained what happened and a woman took my information and said my supervisor will call you back. I never got a call back so I called a 3rd time a few hours later and the woman said well our supervisor is gone now so she will call you in the morning and took my information again. I never got a call the next morning so I called AGAIN and was told they had my dress and I could pick it up. I explained that I had 2 dresses hanging in the closet and the woman tried to tell me I didn't have 2, I only had one. I again reassured her there were 2 dresses and that the one that is now gone still had the tags on it. I admitted this as partially my fault since I forgot them there and the woman seemed to have no interest in hearing me out and did not re-assure me at all that she would look into the situation. I do not trust the employees here nor would I feel comfortable leaving my belongings in one of their rooms

SO MANY FLAVORS and options! Great ice cream. The only complaint is the long line at night! But that is to be expected after-dinner hours at a great ice cream place. 

The cash-only thing is annoying.

I paid for a manicure and pedicure. But did not have time to do both so stylist said I could come back the next day and she would do the manicure. I came back a day later and the stylist and the owner acted like all I needed was polish. Rather than argue I left the shop. The pictures say it all. Very disappointed in treament from owner.

Literally half a cup of coffee ! I always get this order and it is a inch maybe an inch and a half less then the cup . This is absolutely ridiculous the line takes forever and not to mention you have to repeat yourself numerous times in order to get what you want. Never coming here again !!

We've been to Elsie's a few times and have never been disappointed.  Their falafel rolls are delicious and work perfectly for our vegetarian, vegan & gluten free family members.  

In addition, the people who work there truly know the definition of quality customer service.  I feel they always go above and beyond to meet the customers needs.  Most impressive!!!
The summer salad is delicious.  The parking isn't great and the area isn't the best for Nashville. However, well be back.  Convenient for business at the airport and friendly staff. Reasonable prices

It was the perfect pairing of sub-par food with sub-par beer. Hopefully that is what they were going for.  Sadly, I have eaten here 4 or 5 times, and attended a birthday party too. The beer has never been that impressive, and the food selection seems to be an afterthought.  Halfway through my meal I always find myself thinking "why didn't I just go to Bosco's?"

We got a garage door, installation included, from this local Home Depot.  The installer didn't show up.  We called the 800 number several times, each time getting a different "supervisor".  These "supervisors" all explained that they didn't do the actual installation, they contracted it to a local guy.  The local guy had some dispute with them and wasn't doing any more installations.  They asked if that didn't seem pretty immature of him.  That wasn't too helpful to us, whether or not it was immature.

Finally one of the supervisors somehow got the installer to come out.  He did a decent job; just a couple of dings in the garage door, cut electrical wires, etc, all the while complaining a lot about Home Depot.

The process took 6 weeks, during which I made 5 phone calls to Home Depot and 3 to the installer. I was left with a garage door, a paid bill, and no desire ever to talk with a Home Depot "manager" again.

Less than average. The drinks were full of mixer. I ordered three Moscow mules and they all tasted like I was drinking lime juice. The plates were extremely small portions for the price. Three pieces of shrimp plus a piece of steak smaller than three fingers will run you +$50. Waitress rarely came by. Overall, super overpriced for mediocrity. Don't recommend. Nashville has better steakhouses

My wife, grandmother and I came as a recommendation from my grandmother. Nice, local sports bar that's been here for quite some time now.
  Very friendly staff. Was a good amount of people there on a Monday night, but it wasn't loud.
  Food was excellent and the drinks were well crafted and quick. Mostly burgers and sandwiches, but they now have a tex-mex menu, but we didn't give it much attention, likely would in another visit. Same goes for their breakfast menu.

The guy who did my hair cut earlier was extremely rude. (Don't even know the name, but probably in his 40s and with tattoo on his right arm, short). He kept using his mouth to blow away the hairs dropped on my neck and face. What's even worse, I am sure he smokes a lot from the awful breath and I don't know if he got a flu cos he kept coughing... 

Before I left, I still tipped out of courtesy but for God's sake, he yelled at me and humiliated me in Mandarin saying it's not enough... Totally shameless... Please avoid this salon

The third star is for the location and ambiance. The last two summers I really enjoyed Morgan's Pier. This year though the food is meh and extremely overpriced, service is even worse than the food, and the drinks are just okay. I'd come here again but just for a few beers after having dinner and drinks elsewhere.

I've been here many times with friends and have to say that they have the best chicken wings I've ever had. I don't know what's in their sauce but holy cow is it good! Staff is super friendly and karaoke on Friday nights is always a great time. Kitchen is open until 2 am! Come here and try the best wings in Tampa!

OXALIS

New bar//restaurant in a familiar building.  We made our first visit, for beer, wine, & burgers. 

Cheeseburgers were surprisingly good.  Soggy, limp fries, though.

 Redecorated, restroom finally modernized with working commode.  

Slow service, very limited menu, cozy atmosphere with neighborhood crowd of drinkers, not diners. Well stocked bar for cocktails, liquors, & liquers; but limited not-so-cold beer & wines. No salt  or pepper shakers. 

Street parking only, typical of 'hood.  Confusing entrance signage; the REAL entrance is just behind an "Entrance" sign pointing away from it; try the second door from corner on Louisa Street side.

I say:  The just across-the-street Booty's menu is better; unless  you really want a burger..  Try "Oxalis" for a drink, first

Ayer les bon mangers !

Rick

Update: Definitely only order in store here. I eat here almost once a week and never have any problems in store. However, I ordered online for the second time (see original review) and my salad came without the meat. I requested another ingredient be left off, which it was, but I wanted the meat. Unfortunately I didn't notice until I got back to the office, and don't have the extra time in my day to go back to correct mistakes. 
------------------------------------------------


Today was my second time eating here. The first time I ordered at the store, and today I ordered online to pick up. Got the salad back to the office, and on the fourth bite, there was a curly black hair right in there with my lettuce. So I had to throw away a $12 salad after 3 bites. Gave it 2 stars instbecause the salad I had the first time was very good, though overpriced.

We went there yesterday and had their fish and kebab.  Kebab's were chewy but fish was good.  Service was ok. The server took a long time to give us bread and when we asked for more she said it will be $1.00 more. Inside was way too loud.  I sat outside.  If they care at all about their customers they should change this so people can sit inside and enjoy in extreme weather conditions.  Sangria was full of sugar no alcohol and my friends martini was just plain alcohol poured out of a bottle.  We paid about $70.00 not including tips but not worth that much money totally overpriced. I would go to orlando and have my Greek food there.  Not going back here again.

Gumbo was delicious albeit a bit salty for my taste but not my hubby's. The drum was rather dry and uninteresting but perhaps the spices seemed bland in comparison to the intensely flavored gumbo. I thought the bill was pretty high given the quality of the food and the atmosphere (clean but very cafeteria-rustic) -- we had a large gumbo, the drum and boudin balls and two glasses of Chardonnay and the tab was $60. 

Staff very friendly and attentive and warranted a 20% tip.

My wife and I ate our New Year's Eve 2012 dinner here.  The restaurant is nice but my wife and I thought it looked a lot like The Cheesecake Factory on the inside.  Our table service was very good.  Our waiter always checked on us but didn't seem very knowledgeable about the menu and desserts.  The food is WAY OVERPRICED!!!  The food is average at best.  The food had very little seasoning and the portions were uneven.  I had the chicken pomadore and I must of had 5 pounds of angel hair pasta and only a few small pieces of chicken.  My wife had the pasta brio which was ok but not great.  I read the other food reviews where people said "Ohhh, the food is so good".  No it's not!  People, grow up!  Just because the restaurant is in upscale Frontenac doesn't mean it's great.  It's not, it's average at best and is no better than Olive Garden.  So if your considering eating here, consider saving yourself a ton of money on average Italian food and go to Olive Garden, or better yet, go to The Hill and eat at a REAL Italian restaurant.  Our meal was over $80.00 dollars for just two people ( ridiculous!).

THIS COMPANY HAS 121 COMPLAINTS WITH THE BBB THEY ARE NOT ACCREDITED AND THE BBB GAVE THEM A F!!! BUYER BEWARE...

July 31, 2018, I contact Spartan Van Lines (Demonic Lies) whose name is actually Moving Systems, to have my furniture moved. I spoke to a dude named​ Daniel who just happens to be a con artist. I went through the regular ritual, you know the drill...The itemized list. I told him everything I have down to the dust on my boxes and bins. 

After Daniel, the con artist documented everything the company would pick up I received an email with all of the items listed. Daniel the con artist quoted me the price to move my items $1589.00 but there was a catch. I had to allow Spartan(Fraud) Van Lines legally known as Moving Systems, to charge my debit card $476.00 towards the move.

Daniel the con artist repeatedly stated the $476.00 dollars would be deducted from $1589.00 leaving a remaining balance $1113.00.
Literally, as soon as I authorized the payment based off of our verbal and written contract I could no longer reach Daniel the con artist.

During the business conversation, I had to correct him when he referred to as sweetie WTF! This was right about the time he had me locked into his scam. I can imagine his eyes rolling back in his head like A Great White Shark getting ready for the kill. Guess that's why he calls me out of my name. He thought he had me. 

Well to make this short as possible which most of the victims on here know that impossible with human waste like these criminals. But here goes...Daniel the con artist had me reach out to an individual who seemed to be quite dissatisfied with his crooked buddy luring him into another scam by these crooks. 

The man was very forthcoming about it. He said, "I've asked Daniel to stop giving out my number to have me involved with this" 
At this point, I was really uncomfortable but they had my $476.00 and they were not answering the phone. So I was forced to go along with it. 

Okay,​ fast forward moving day this rude Indian dude showed up to the crib with some helpless looking young black man with an expression on his face that said "Maam I'm just trying to feed my family" Soooo the Indian​ man walks into my apartment with a clipboard​ in hand, Red Flag and the young fella dragging their drop cloth rags.

The Indian​ dude starts casing my apartment like he was looking for Osama Bin Laden. I'm​ watching this dude like if you even spit out a vowel from the word money in it I will throw you out Ralph Kramden style​

He did and I threw him out and his victim helper. ​To sum it up the man was trying to charge several hundred dollars more. He came to do another estimate/price totally disregarding the price that was promised. I had the exact amount of items as I told Daniel the con artist.

When I'm in that area, I get 2 or 3 dishes from the lunch menu.
     We always get Mongolian Beef, and share it.
The last two times, everyone that tried it, said it tasted funny.
It did not taste, like any M.B. we have ever had. 
   It was OK, but very different. And I have tried M.B. all over town. 
I will try something else, next time.

Service is always good.

I'm glad Grant Rd is completed, I think.

I stopped by here before getting on my cruise since it was close to the port and I had time to kill. I came here with the intentions of just trying the oysters. Must get the charbroiled oysters! It was absolutely AMAZING!!!! Good thing this isn't in NY because I would get a heart attack from eating too much of this.

Less than 2  years ago, Instead of shopping at Home Depot or Lowe's or Gephardt's,  we decided to try a local privately owned-and-  run appliance store. Bought an expensive Maytag which was recommended,  with French doors and bottom freezer and water dispenser for our new home. I was silly enough to trust the Maytag name.
We have had more problems with this than I have ever had with an appliance, and I have bought many of them!

After purchasing AIG insurance on parts (for more than $400...).
1.  The door never closes all the way, it must be pushed hard or it won't close and beeps. A technician said that they are made that way. Very annoying. 
2. Next, one of the chilling drawers broke, after less than a year.
3. Now the level which regulates the ice maker won't stay up, and we have an overflow of ice all the time. Waited more than a day for a technician to call us back. Then my husband was told by the insurer that we have to find our own Maytag technician because there are none in the area. Bucks County, PA! Then they will reimburse us. 
4. Back and forth with the store, they are still waiting for authorization from AIG. Meanwhile, we can't regulate the ice and can't turn it off, so we are stuck at home until its corrected! Unless we want to find ice all over the floor when we return home. I couldn't make this up!

So we shopped local and trusted a name brand, but probably won't again.
If we don't get someone from AIG out by tomorrow, we'll have to call a local tech and pay them. Oh, and I am sure that we can find a tech who repairs Maytag!

If you want to be treated by disgruntled and unprofessional employees, the go to this location. During the pandemic the employees received bonuses and a $1 raise in paycheck. That's a lot! Specially given so many people lost their job. But still they kept the same condescending attitude, you would think they would feel maybe a bit grateful to still have a job? Nahh! 
Seriously if you are not happy with your job then quit! If you are then show it. This was from management to everyone, except an Asian lady, she was very kind and was the only one who smiles and you feel a bit more genuine about. But haven't seen her. 
Natural Grocers step up your game and do your community right.

Sprouts is a Godsend to this part of Goleta. The old Vons was actually pretty darn good but after the Haggens debacle its sooo very nice to have a great store like Sprouts in that location. Sprouts has great produce and a really nice meat section with both fresh-in-the-case cuts as well as pre-packaged ones ready to go.  They've also got a good selection of ready to eat items and a good mix of some hard to find specialty items such as lo carb,  gluten free and diabetic things we used source from 2-3 different places. They only used about 75 percent of the old Vons space but Sprouts still seems spacious, well lit and teeming with shoppers this Holiday weekend. On top of everything else the staff seemed so super friendly and helpful, I was really amazed, they are really working hard to keep customers happy. My only criticismis is that the wine and beer section (especially the wine) is particularly weak with little to no local offerings save for a few which are mass produced and distributed widely. Its a bit of a shame since this is a premier wine growing region with dozens of excellent producers here, why would I want to buy wine produced a million bottles at a time from wine factories when I can get such great local stuff even at Costco?? Hopefully Sprouts will come around on this and realize they'll sell more wine if they get some local wines in there :-) Even if they don't we'll still shop there because everything else is excellent, amazing even :-) Thank You Sprouts.

Snappers has never disappointed.  Though I do recommend going early to avoid the crowd, which seems to arrive at 6.  I could live on their appetizers and wine. Service is efficient if not the friendliest.  
The seared tuna is hands down my favorite dish even though it's an appetizer.  Early bird has a set number of entrees that are markedly less expensive.   I believe it's until 6pm.

Best place to eat in SB !!!!
Looks like a place lots of local go , and Thad's a key indication the food must b above par..... 
We went for breakfast then went BACK for dinner .... 

Very good basic breakfast !!!! Applewood bacon great .....
Dinner time very nice .. Hubby had Rockefeller n sea bass ,, I had range free chicken .... Nicely done 

The location is PERFECT !!!! Love it the restaurant is in it's own little alcove of beach , water n waves everywhere !!!!!!

If your in SB BYPASS state St go directly to cliff drive

We have returned to Pitman Animal Hospital after care received at another clinic in the area failed to meet our expectations.  We recalled our experience at Pitman Animal Hospital fondly where we took our pet for a second opinion in about 2013.  While we don't know if we will stay, we can say they treat us with the utmost respect, and I feel like our pet is part of their family.  They are caring, compassionate and I believe really care for animals.  It's all about the customer service, and at this point, they meet all requirements.  If you are looking for a place to start, start here at the Pitman Animal Hospital.  We went for our first well animal visit last week and we are impressed.  I think they will be hard to beat!!!!!

Food is always hot, tasty and consistent. 
Beer is always ice cold. 

Tvs are on the best games. 

What more could you ask for in a sports bar?

Great vegan food, and great prices! 

I had the Philly steak sandwich (grilled onions & mushrooms, lettuce & tomato, long roll & ketchup) and my friend had the Bistro Bella ( herb glazed grilled portobella, dijon, olive tapenade, tomato, artichokes, red onion & arugula). Both sandwiches are $8.50, which is average for any sandwich joint. More importantly, most sandwiches were delicious. My Philly steak was the best Philly style 'steak' sandwich I've eaten in my life. Granted, I haven't eaten a true steak sandwich in years, but from how I remember it, this tops a standard Philly steak sandwich. The mushrooms were perfectly cooked and seasoned, the roll was great, and the homemade ketchup was surprisingly tasty. My non-vegan friend loved her Bistro Bella. According to her it was 'a really awesome burger' we also split the sweet potato fries with the sriracha dipping sauce (again, delicious!)

For how packed the restaurant was, the service was pretty great. Someone took our oder on a tablet while were in line, so that by the time we arrived at the cashier our drinks were ready and we could pay. We waited maybe 5 minutes for our food. When I say it was packed, I mean the line was out the door packed. 

Overall, great food, great service. I will definitely come back next time I am in the area.

Do you like beer? Do you enjoy trying local brews? Do you peruse beer menus looking for something new and different? If so, this is the spot for you. So many beers! And for a bar located right in the middle of the action, this place is always relatively empty. And it's significantly quieter than all the neighboring bars. Those are two big pluses in my book. The bartender was really friendly and knowledgeable. She was great at figuring out what we like and suggesting other options. A very pleasant experience! And all in a place that has a dive feel.
This place was fantastic. Not too costly, very tasty. Our whole group thought the pickles were amazing.  I had the back yard burger, it had lil smokies built into the patty! Highly recommend.
Very good pho. Terrific service. The only negative we encountered was figuring out how to get there off of 82nd St. It actually is very easy, but the nav system had us driving in circles.

My first experience playing paintball, and I have to thank the whole team at Xtreme for that. It had been drizzling that day, but we arrived. We got started a bit late, but were checked in on time and went through our orientation, talking about safety as far as rules. There were a bunch of people in our group, and a good amount of 10-ish-year-olds. Playing down on the main field was great, it was a pretty tough course but a bunch of fun opportunities to get tactical. Unfortunately our day was cut a bit short by a tornado warning, but we still got a decent amount of play time in. We weren't able to go check out their upper course because of "muddy conditions", but I don't really understand that because that was the whole point of the game haha...

Anyways, I had a fantastic time my first time playing. The only thing that would've made this experience better was if the games were a little bit better organized, and the maintenance on the guns was kept up a little bit. We had multiple gun failures during our session, and a little over halfway through our game, our 'leaders' switched and the next one was kind of spacey and just kind of let everybody talk over each other as *we* decided what to do. It was kind of unorderly and made more people frustrated as the 10 year olds screamed out different ideas haha. Maybe have more control over the game next time. Other than that, I can't wait to play again!

Here at Hertz in Philadelphia airport. 6am. First in line.  Made my reservation weeks ago.  Was just told they have no cars but will put me on wait list.  I took the red eye here bc I have a meeting early this morning.  Now I will be late.  Will wait another 10 min then will have to take a long taxi ride.  Great work Hertz.

Had a delicious lunch with one of my best friends. Loved the bruschetta appetizer. Split a cup of crab bisque and each ordered salads. All were perfect. Great service. Nice ambience. Gets crowded at peak lunch hours.

Solid three stars for this place. It wasn't that the pizza wasn't good, it was just that I was underwhelmed. As a frequent visitor to Blaze pizza, I feel like the selection here was limited. The price was reasonable ($5 plus .50 per extra topping), but I'm not hard pressed to visit here again on my own terms. The owners also stated that they've sold the business and will be moving shortly.

My sister and dad kept raving about this place, and even though it's a chain, I thought I'd give it a shot. I'm sorry I did.

The atmosphere is nice and cozy, and I liked the little "windows" at the end of the table that butted up to the kitchen area where the waitress took our order and delivered our food. But that's about where it stopped.

The hashbrowns were greasy and tasteless and the eggs a little runnier than the over medium I ordered, but still hot and yolk flowing. For four triangles of toast, you are given one small package of jelly. No jam, no preserves. Many other restaurants (every locally owned breakfast place I've been to, anyway) have those nice little holders with a fun selection of jams, jellies, and fruit butters to spread on your toast but here I felt like I was being provisioned and given the benefit of getting one container of grape jelly. Okay. I'm sure I could have asked, but I shouldn't have to.

The coffee was okay. No dairy free creamer which was a disappointment for my lactose intolerance but I ended up using the cream and was no worse for wear in the end, so that was good. 

I don't plan to return and I'm sorry to my family that I didn't enjoy it! I don't often rate places poorly, but we can't all have winners.

Meh. Have ordered take out twice from here and the last time turned me off. Previously tried a couple of the sandwiches, which were good. The other night my boyfriend and I went in to order carry-out, two Caesar salads - one with chicken and the other salmon. From a place that is trying to present itself as quality Italian dining, these salads were pathetic. The salads looked like they came from a high school cafeteria, with a small portion of romaine (we ordered large salads), shaker cheese and no shaved Asiago as presented in the description. The salmon was a normal portion, however the chicken was less than a quarter of a breast. The inside is very charming and we had been meaning to eat in at some point, but probably won't bother after this experience.

I'm writing this review as I am waiting to ship out my package. Normally Keith or 2 other women are working (and they are awesome) but this time it's a woman who wouldn't acknowledge me even while starting my transaction. 

She was upset with me because I was paying cash and she didn't have a till ready in any of the registers. Also she was talking to a customer that came in after me saying she couldn't help him because she had to help me; legit point but the tone was that I was a pain and a nuisance.

Next time I'll wait until Keith or another staff member is available or just come back. Again, hats off to the other staff members. I'm a regular here at this location. 

I finally had some dialogue but it was negative. Apparently they don't use the "ground" slips anymore, which I find odd because the shipping station is stocked with them and nothing else. This concludes my visit. Thanks for reading.

For a place so gorgeous and a menu so outstanding, I'm quite disappointed in the service and responsibility of the staff there. 
My fiancé and I went out on a date for the first time in a very long time (had a huge misfortune happen to us) and I wanted to treat him to something very European (we got engaged in Belgium). He was not feeling so great when we got there but we started with the SMALL Croustille Au Fromage to share. We then got our 4 small pieces of baguette and butter to share. After that, we ordered our entrees, Veal Scallopini for him, Duck Confit for me. Everything tasted all fantastic and I was enjoying myself, until about 1am after and I called Alberta Health Link and they said I had food poisoning. I believe that the duck was possibly not prepared properly and I called the restaurant to notify them that this had happened and to make sure it does not happen again. The manager said that I ate too much and that it had nothing to do with the restaurant. He then said that it was not food poisoning and that he hopes to see us again and to make it up that he will buy us dessert or a bottle of wine. I blatantly said to him that we will not be coming back and to train their staff better on how to cook duck breast. Unbelievable.
mmm I got a Grasshopper cupcake today, and it was delicious! The store was also decorated for Christmas and it looked really cute :)

Multiple trips to different Redstones and this one is just as disappointing. I ordered a club soda, it was flat the bartender tried again it was still flat, he was kind of, oh well. So I asked the female bartender to take it off the bill and give me water, which she did. They were out of many things because the menu is changing the next day. 
Wanted a salad w romaine she said everything is a mix. So I odered ceasar, she said that's a mix too. I ended up ordering 3 buffalo shrimp which are always great. I did notice that they used to have 'Maytag' blue cheese, now its just plain old blue cheese. There is a big difference. 

Maybe because it was after nine, but I ordered a baked potato and I definitely got an old microwaved potato, and I think micro waved asparagus as well. 

I was just very underwelmed. High priced place like this, I expect fresh food.

When I worked over in this area I came here frequently when in the mood for a Chicago dog. The place was always packed and food was always good. The dogs are the best and a must try if you're in the area! The buns are so fresh and soft and the flavor is just awesome!! The fries are just crinkle fries from a bag, nothing fancy so you could probably do without those. The guy that owns the place is friendly as well. A must try!

My Jason's Deli experience yesterday was ehh, ok I guess. If I was solely basing my experience on customer service and portion sizes, this probably would have been 4 stars. Customer service was pretty good. The cashier was courteous and was patient with us as we browsed the menu and tried to make our choices. The server was also good about bringing out the food quickly and clearing away the plates quickly. Portion sizes, Jason's Deli gives way more food than most other places, say like a Panera. When I got the Quarter Muffaletta Special with a cup of soup, I was expecting just a soup and the sandwich. But the quarter sandwich was actually a pretty hefty size, well stuffed with meat, cheese, and olive salad topping; as well as a huge portion of potato chips. It caught me off guard, but in a good way.

But what made this only 3 star worthy? Well, like several previous reviews before mine, this place just has a very off-putting smell. Indeed it does smell like either dirty mop water was used to mop the restaurant or dirty dish rags were used to wipe the tables. Whatever it is, it just made for an unpleasant dining experience. The bathroom too was not maintained very well. Everything was just so wet, the floors were rather nasty, and the soap dispenser was just laying sideways on the bathroom counter; definitely not a good look for bathroom cleanliness or hygiene purposes. Finally, I don't know why, but everything tasted so salty. The seafood gumbo was super salty, the olive salad in my sandwich was super salty, and the chips were super salty. Maybe this is just a one time issue, but the food was definitely over-salted this time around.

So, someone please take care of cleanliness and restaurant maintenance issues. Once these are taken care of, the dining in experience will be that much more enjoyable.

I used to frequent Maggianos alot and it's always been pretty good and the service was always good. I recently visited 6/14/2021 and had the worst experience. 1st- the waitress was horrible, she was not friendly and had a dry personality, I'm an understanding person so I assumed the waitress was tired because it was late around 9ish ( we had very late dinner because we ate after my daughters dance recital) or had a bad day, (but kudos to the hostess, bartender and other staff who were personable and provided good customer service). 2nd we ordered meatballs as an appetizer they were okay, but  i would not order again, they served a little below room temperature. 3rd i ordered the chicken piccata, you could never go wrong with this dish but i was sooo wrong or maybe it was just a bad night. The meal had no flavor the chicken was tough the pasta dry I couldn't believe what i was trying to eat . 4th i customized my daughters meal :butter  pasta noodles with shredded grilled chicken and Alfredo on the side, the order came out wrong because the chicken was not served on the side, i didn't make it an issue my daughter love the chicken but the noodles lacked seasoning so she didn't like them, i asked a couple of time for salt but the waitress never brought out and rarely came to check on the table. 5. the worst part - i saw a small COCKROACH ... i tried to not to make a big deal of it because i didn't want to upset my daughter and her dad was eyeing me because i stood up looking at on the floor making sure it didn't climb up near our feet... i also tried to flag down the waitress to kill it but of course she was no were to be found and the area was somewhat empty and the bug finally scurried off so it was pointless to bring it up since it was no telling we're it went. So since DAD hates when I address things i stayed calm and didn't say anything - because i was beyond disgusted, lost my appetite which didn't matter because my food was awful and it was late and we were tired and really just ready to go home... i could go on and on about this horrible dinning experience but you get the picture.
Great service fun atmosphere! Very busy yet the line kept moving quickly. I wish all Starbucks were as efficient as this one.

I'm seeing a lot of complaints about the garage; for an extra $5 you can have the valet park your car.  No problems with that and worth the extra $5 plus tip that you give to the valet as far as I'm concerned.
We stayed on a Saturday evening through Sunday afternoon.  When we arrived around 5, there was no line to check in and it took about 2 minutes.  Warm cookies given, but they had nuts.  No nut allergy here, but I hate cookies with nuts.  Deduct one star!!  Kidding.
We decided to do room service because we were running low on time to get to a show and figured we'd do that rather than try to find a place to eat on a Saturday night and then rush through dinner to get back.  
If you're traveling with kids, room service is cheap for them.  Kids meals are $5 and are nicely sized.  I got a HUGE crabcake sandwich with an embarrassing amount of fries for $14, which is very reasonable and probably cheaper than what you'd usually pay at a sit down place.  I never expect much from a crabcake outside of B'more but this was great.  Not much filler, good flavor.
The hubster's meatloaf was also nicely portioned and he ordered a side of creamed spinach (eww!).  He said it tasted great though, and it came in a freaking TROUGH.
Gratuity was added to the room service bill and the man who brought our dinner was so  nice.  I asked if gratuity was added and he said yes, and thank you so much for asking.    I kind of feel like ass for not giving him something additional, but they do add something like 20% to the bill so, I'm trusting it gets to him like they say it does.
The room was a good size, very clean, comfortable beds and pillows.  Remote and t.v. were confusing to use, but that's pretty usual for a hotel room.  
Bathroom had a nice deep tub, fancy toiletries that smelled funny, and a hairdryer.  Score.
The next morning we did the hotel buffet.  I've had good luck with hotel breakfast buffets, but this SUCKED.  First of all the person manning the hostess area was pretty miserable, especially when I asked the cost of the buffet.  When she told me $16 I was ready to turn around but for some reason my husband thought it was still a good idea.
The eggs were runny, the potatoes tasted like they'd been sitting there for a day and then reheated.  They did have a do it yourself waffle station with fresh berries and that was nice, but that's about the nicest thing about the buffet.  They did have some other fresh fruit and yogurt, but come to think of it, when I stayed at the Marriott SpringHill Suites once, they offered a FREE buffet with the same items, save for the potatoes and bacon.  They did offer a mimosa made with chambourd.  Probably should have gotten that....  Oh well.  
We wanted to go to do some shopping and we weren't sure if we'd be back before check out so we checked our bags.  It wasn't too obvious where to go to check bags.  There was a closed door that was marked, but no one standing there and there was no one at the concierge stand (I never saw anyone there the entire stay).  Eventually I saw someone come out of the room and they checked our bags.  
Check out easy peasy as well although you were unsure if it actually worked.  You dial something on the phone and then hear some sort of tone and that means you're checked out.  I think they should have some kind of recording that says "you're checked out" but who am I?
In the morning, we got a statement under the door with what would be charged to the room.  Room service/valet was on there.  I noticed online when I checked my bank account that there was a charge for that plus another $50 which is what I suppose they secure when you check in and give them your credit card.  Which I suppose every other hotel in the world does.  IIRC though this was way less than the Marquis in NY which secured what you paid for the room as the deposit or whatever it is that is called.  
Oh by the way, they had dumb WiFi, the kind you have to pay for.  If you're a rewards customer I believe you get it for free.  It kills me when hotels make you pay for internet, but there are a million other places nearby where you can hook up to WiFi.
We'd stay here again if we're ever in Philly.  We got a great deal via expedia/hotels.com for $119/night so I have no complaints there.

I purchased a group on for fox and hound and didn't realize that it expired 2 1/2 months after the purchase. I called to see if they would honor the Groupon anyways and the girl on the phone said yes we realize the expiration was hard to see and such a short duration. We we decided to go in and have dinner. Upon receiving the bill we were advised that we didn't order the minimum of $20 so we ordered the cheesecake ( which was fabulous by the way) so our bill over the $20. The waitress took out Groupon and realized it had expired and said she couldn't honor it and got the manager and he eventually took only $10 off our bill. Knowing this we never would of ordered the desert. 

Over all the service was great and the food was great! It's very upsetting to get the wrong information regarding the expired Groupon.

I would call this place more of a fusion. I had the rabbit which was really good. There were no vegetable options as in side, they did have brussel sprouts with bacon which I really didn't care for. The iron cornbread was more like a cake if anything else. won't really order again and my bf makes 100% better crawfish etoufee (better seasoning and flavor) and he's German Ha!
I will come back because the service was really good, the atmosphere is inviting and I would like to try the rest of the menu

Thanks for helping my parents pup out. Violet has been listening and with a little help from her training tools, she's become aware of going outside to do her duties, and she's cut back on charging at my cats. Granted we still have to use the tools that were given to her. 

Now if only you can train my mom? Lol

Had a pretty good order for delivery.

The jerk chicken was good, the jerk seasoning on the rice was really good.

Really enjoyed the yams and green beans, and my girlfriend enjoyed her fish platter.

Would order again.
Crowded on Sundays but great prices! Got potatoes, onions, lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, grapes and lemons for $13!

It was a wonderful experience at the Cooper's Hawk Winery & Restaurants - Tampa. The entire place has a great vibe. My friend and I shared a Bourbon Lacquered BBQ Pork Chop and few other menu items. Everything was prepared perfectly. Team ensured everything was alright. We will most definitely be back!

This is the hidden pearl of New Orleans. We were walking and came upon a friendly man telling us how great the food was, and they had immediate seating!! (Joey). So we looked at the menu and it was liking to all 4 in our party, so we went inside.

The space was small, quaint, clean, and we were introduced to our waitress (Kathy) and we sit down and went to restroom, all as expected, clean, etc.

The "Bloody Mary" was wonderful!! The food was Great!  Best Calamari in NOLA!! Waitress was very attentive no glass went below half full and she was on top of it!  Great place, wonderful staff: Joey and Kathy especially!!

 We went back the next day for a Mufeletta sandwich! WOW! Huge! And awesome! 

 I highly recommend frank's and it will now be a regular stop on my trips to NOLA.

Thanks

Best Thai place in Tucson. The food is simply awesome and cheap. The restaurant itself is does not look attractive and is bare bones. But the food is finger linking good :)
Our standard order is the yellow curry, the penang Curry, Thai fried rice and papaya salad. It's just so good :)

Though no Pandas are on the menu at this "Gourmet" Chinese restaurant, there is at least a variety of shrimp and chicken styles to appease both my picky palette and the interests of my small charges.  The BBQ pork is tasty, but an unnatural shade of bright red. The server couldn't answer our question about how they got the pig so red...but she was very nice when asked for extra sauces.
I have to admit to being a little disappointed that there is only one type of tea offered and Pepsi products rather than Coke :(
BUT they do have Sobe drinks and I am cutting out soda in my life!

The food was yummy and filling!
I loved the Sweet and Sour chicken best. 
The fried rice and "spicy pepper chicken" were not that spicy - something that made me happy.
^.^
Go forth and support Pandas! Get some non-greasy and some of the freshest chain-store fast food I have yet encountered!

This place is not what it use to be. I remember you could walk around Jackson Square and it was the heart of the city. People would be selling their art, fortune tellers were in abundance. You could go there and get a part of NOLA. Recently I visited and it was close to a ghost town. Only a couple of artist were there. It looked like all the life was gone. The music was still there but it still was missing something. Maybe it was the time of the year, maybe it was the rainy days...I don't know. I dream of the old New Orleans before the soul left.

Very happy with our blinds. Second purchase here and looks like we will be updating our blinds throughout the house. Service was great in store and they were ready in 2 days for pick up! I highly recommend them!

Tonight I ordered wings, fries and pizza. I picked it up, and we waited 10 minutes for the food to be finished, which is fine. However after returning home, we did not have the fries in any of the bags. After calling, they said they would deliver them to my house. Two hours later, no fries and out a few bucks! Didn't receive a follow up call or any information after that.

Ok I know pizza!! I've eaten at tons of Brick Oven/Wood Fired style pizzerias. If Bavaro's wants to remain in Tampa then they need to get it right. Yesterday I stopped in for lunch and ordered the Arugula personal pie. It came to my table soggy and the toppings fell off when I picked it up. Zero flavor. Not what I was expecting. Perhaps RE-Training needs to take place here on how to properly make a Neapolitan Wood Fired Pizza.  Hope it's not like this at the other locations.
I don't know much, but what I do know is that I frikkin LOVE papa John's pizza. Better ingredients better 'za. Tell the papa I said hey. Goodnight!

As someone from out of town, I always hear about how delicious the food is in New Orleans. But tbh I fully did not expect to Come to NOLA to have one of the best vegetarian meals I've ever eaten.  I'm so glad we gave it a try. The food was awesome, and the staff was so pleasant. We started by getting an introduction to the menu from Robert and ordered the ricotta. It was very tasty. It was served with thin wafers, which i didn't expect. We ordered the carrot cake pancakes (that came with this yummy pineapple cream cheese topping), a farro bowl that was so filling, and corn (that came with cheese on top).   I also ordered the broccoli which was roasted and served on top of hummus with a drizzle of this sweet chili sauce. It was by far my favorite way Broccoli has ever been prepared. It was so good!  Overall, I would definitely come back. It's BYOB tho so be sure to come with mimosa in hand.

Typical Reno trying to copy Portland and be 'hip and cool' but falling short. Doughnuts are meh, I can see how if people haven't been to the real deal are impressed but the places they're imitating do it a hundred times better. 


I'll probably go back again because I'm a local, old school Reno degenerate  ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

Awesome local watering hole and late night food place. All the bartenders know your name, and if they are new, they learn it quickly as well as your usual drink or beer of choice.  Chris, the owner, is the most friendly, service oriented, and down to earth bar owner around. He is always up for a good conversation, and his professional demeanor is why this bar succeeds. Oh, and late night food!  Their kitchen is open until 11:30pm Mon-Saturday serving up what in my opinion is likely "competition worthy" wings.  All in all, a great bar, great staff, and fantastic "more than just bar food". Stop in, and you'll see that what I say is beyond true.

no repair shop is perfect, but I think the question that we need to ask is, "What would we do without the Geek Squad?".  If the Geek Squad disappeared overnight, what would our options be?  Very limited, I believe.

  Nonetheless, I have gone to the geek squad numerous times for their mobile car electronics service and have been treated well every time.  They seem to know their onions, too.  And then one time, I needed some service from the upstairs "consumer electronics" Geek Squad and the young lady there was very helpful and very professional.

  Again, I think this is a national asset and should be condemned cavalierly for a random bad experience,, which goes with the territory sort of for electronics and gadgets.

Good luck finding the place with a GPS. We drove around and around and eventually found it largely due to my stubborn nature. It's in the inner loop in the northwest part on the inside in a building that says "365". 

Parking is metered so bring four quarters for an hour. We didn't have any change and the old school meters didn't accept cards or any electronic form of payment. Luckily for us, no ticket was in our future! I'm a risk taker. 

Whew, now for our visit! I was thrilled they serve LavAzza coffee since it's absolutely my favorite. They even sell some bulk packages to go which is good to know since it can be very hard to find in Florida. I still have quite the stash at home so I didn't buy any more this time. 

We had a double espresso and a latte. The espresso was fine with a proper crema. The latte seemed a little weak. They have young girls working there rather than practiced baristas so perhaps that was the reason. 

We also tried some Limoncello gelato which was very smooth and delicious and pricy. 

I'm not sure when I will get out here in the future. But, I will try it again if the opportunity presents itself.

We went there as part of a large group. They were able to serve us and seat us together. Staff was good, but the food was mediocre. Guacamole lacked taste. Salsa had a weird boozy taste. Queso was ok. We ordered tres leches dessert and it was frozen solid. Maybe they forgot to thaw it before serving? Main entree was decent.

Cool place, but they just took a short position to miss out on the long. I am currently looking to transition from my own supply of the green powder (cheaper and tastes better) to purchasing tea. The idea is to make it slightly more difficult to consume. However, customer service is priority to me and they missed the mark today. The woman who served me was courteous and I have no issue with her. The issue is that I had one more punch on the rewards card and they didn't honor it because they don't issue them anymore. The standard that I've experienced everywhere else is that ones that are already issued are grandfathered in. Instead, the manager or owner texted "those are hella old - don't accept it (she didn't tell me - I peeped it)." Well, fella, it's your right not to accept it, but I can just go to the other place right down the street from me and get the same thing. So I hope saving the one free serving was worth the many more you could've sold to me for a profit. Peace out.

Marriott titanium elite member and they would not honor my reservation.   What a dump. Will never stay at a SpringHill suites ever again anywhere.  How horrible.  And the Marriott reservation manager called them  and couldn't believe their response.   Marriott should dump this brand as it makes them look bad. This isn't my first run in with Springhill.  It's a true dump- all of them. Sleep in your car instead.  Went and stayed at a Hilton where I am Diamond and treated like a king.  Never again Springhill- there isn't much more to say. Marriott will hear about this agai.

Oh my gosh these are the nicest employees I have ever met. We came in thinking that it closed in a
Little over an hour and asked to register my daughter. The lady was so kind and helpful.  We walked around for an hour and thought we had five minutes left. Come to find out they were just about to close when we walked in. So kind. Great costumer service.  
I loved all their selection and this is the place to shop for a new mother or mother to be. Thanks ladies in Franklin.
Tammy is the one who primarily helped us as she was amazing.

I want to love this hotel because the location  is perfect.  BUT this trip alone : hotel room wouldn't lock, i had to leave so door was "fixed" while i was gone.  Key stopped working, front desk is a hike from the annex. Returned to hotel today,  door unlocked after maid service.  My key didn't work again,  but  door was unlocked so i got in. Great news since our computers,  jewelry, etc were in the room. The in room safe didn't work, so anyone could have entered our room and stolen  everything. The screaming baby  next door is just the cream on top! 2 nights of this in a hotel that is half empty.  Called front desk. The people next door are checking out tomorrow  we're told.  Omg.  I want our last 2 nights comped.  The kid sounds like she's in our room- it's  awlful.

I was extremely excited when this place moved in to a strip mall nearby.  I waited for MONTHS as the "coming soon" sign loomed large.  As soon as it was open, I was there that day for lunch, and still frequent it though it's not the BEST chinese food I've ever had (that distinction is held by this little hole in the wall place in Edwardsville, IL--but that's a review for another time).

There are several things I love about this place which make it unique to the area: 1) it's open on Sundays.  I can't tell you how many times I've been hungry for Chinese food on a Sunday and none of the restaurants nearby were open.  2) They take credit card.  Again, it seems like a no-brainer, but most of the Chinese places around us are cash only.  3)  They have the best crab rangoon I've tasted in YEARS.  They're HUGE, creamy and very flavorful with a nice, crispy wonton exterior.  4) They have not only cheap lunch options ($4.55), but also 'combo plates' that are available at any time of the day, and they come with fried rice, an egg roll, and a moderate price ($6.75). 5) Vegetables--I haven't found another chinese restaurant that loads up on veggies like their dishes do.  If nothing else, I feel like I'm eating a bit more healthily here than I do at other fast food places. 

Drawbacks:
1) Mei fun= no fun.  I'd never tried it before and tried it here, but found it very bland. 2) if you're a fan of spices,  they're a bit non-spicy.  I'd recommend any of their spicy dishes if you want a good flavor, but not a big "kick".

If you live in St. Ann or Bridgeton, it's a great place to frequent (or get take out from).  It's not spectacular enough though to drive from another state for a taste though :D
Some real good food. A little pricey for a bar albeit a little upscale bar. A little to noisy. But we'll worth a visit now and then, not just on a regular basis.

Zombie friggin' apocalypse.  Events mandated that I return to this store for emergency provisions on a Friday night.  Holy crap.  I am sorry but the clientele that this store now attracts will give you the dystopian retail expectation that a Trump presidency will create.

No fewer than 3 large dogs in the produce and open meat area, because the class of shoppers feel entitled to bring their pit bulls into food stores.  400 pound, ARMED, gear heads in cammos (perhaps to fend off a canine), and people working on their cars in the lot.  Badly illuminated, dirty, and clerks who rightly feel miserable about having to earn a living in this retail environment.  They frequently have cops parked outside for who knows what....

It is really awful.

Spend an extra 10% and head to the Target at the other end of the center.

It was just average.  Bad acoustics inside so we ate outside.  Server was prompt and polite - she earned the two stars.   The food was below average.  I order crab cakes.  Way too much filler, could barely taste any crab.  For $28 I want crab.  The plating and presentation was great but I've honestly had better crab cakes from the frozen food aisle at Giant.  Wife ordered grouper - said it was ok - a little over spiced and too dry.  Our first visit, probably won't return.

it was ok, the staff is not that great with customers, they are ok, i had french gel manicure and the white part crack in all the nails like 3 days after, i believe is because they use almond oil, when your are going to use gel you need your nails as clean and dry as possible. it only last me a week. no happy about it.

First let me say I was very happy to join Giant fitness when it first opened in northeast Philly. All new equipment , pleasant staff ( on day I joined and most that I have interacted with since joining). Place has been kept clean and they have a nice gentleman who is always working to wipe down equipment, Windows etc. who is very pleasant and smiles all the time.  Sounds good, right?? Every day I have gone to the gym I have carried with me a small cross body bag where I carry my phone that I use to listen to music during my workout and my keys EVERY SINGLE DAY SAME BAG, no one has ever said it was not permitted till today. The manager sent a young woman to tell me I couldn't have the bag on me, she was very nice about it. I asked why when I have had it on me EVERYDAY I have been at the gym and she said she didn't know but I couldn't have it in the gym. I went to the front desk to speak to the manager and lo and behold the manager is the same person who has signed me in many times,has walked by me during my work out many times and watched me take the 2 bucks out of this same little bag to hand to him to pay for the bottle of water I purchase EVERYTIME I go to the gym. He informs me that I had to have been told this was not permitted when I signed up. I assured him I was never told this and that maybe they could put a sign up right there at the front desk so that it is seen and he says there are signs and points to these little white signs all the way across the room that are on the big mirrors and says with a tone "there they are, no chalk no bags" . I again said it would be helpful to have one of those signs at the desk and he said " I am the manager and don't deviate from what corporate wants". I then said to him I challenge that attitude and that I am also a manager and GOOD CUSTOMER SERVICE would dictate that he discuss putting a sign up front so that everyone can see it as they come in for their workout eliminating any future problems. He continued to say he just doesn't deviate from what corporate tells him to do. We did go back and forth a couple of times but it was clear he was not capable of really looking at what good customer service is so I left without even finishing my workout.  Clearly this manager needs further training on customer service!  
For me this issue is no longer about being permitted to carry a bag or not, if it is a rule I will abide by it. It is now about the attitude of this manager and the need for as he said "corporate" to ensure that this information is in fact provided to their  customers up front via placing the same sign at the sign in desk as they have on the mirrors where the hand weights are ( I as a 60 year old woman have NEVER used those weights nor use that side of the room). It is a simple fix to a simple problem. They also should consider providing managers and those who are responsible to sign people up for the gym with more in depth training on their policies so that these policies  can be appropriately explained in a manner that is in tune with good customer service. This will eliminate many of the bad complaints I have just read on Yelp and other review sites. Unless of course this organization is just another money grab who just don't care about customer service once they have your signature on the contract.....

This is a little gem of a musem stuffed as only the Victorians knew how to do with incredible bric-a-brac, embellishment and over the top design.  It makes you want to build a turrent bedroom with a flying vault in your own home.   Reading all the items collected about H Plant, his railroads and his resorts opens your eyes to how 'modern' his business was using integrated 'vertical' structure to capture the populace pre-disney. 

Very much worth a stop in Tampa.

Was not impressed with this place at all. Upon walking in, the place was messy and not very clean. I told the woman what type of haircut I wanted and even showed her a picture. I could tell it was not cut the way I asked for. Then when I asked for her to blow it dry she refused since they were closing.
