So I had to leave the salon with wet hair and could not even really see how the haircut came out. When I got home and blow-dryed I could see how bad it was. Crooked in the back and not at all angled like it should have been. So now I have to go to another salon to have it fixed. Oh and it seems they never clean out their hair brushes. It was gross and could definitely spread lice. Should be some sort of violation. I will never go there again.

We must have gone on a bad night, because I don't understand the great reviews. For the price we paid, I expected a lot more from this place. In the end, the food was just ok, more on the level of a diner than a nice establishment. Complete with sloppy plating. 

The steak had to be sent back and was served with a frozen puck of butter on top, the meatloaf had no texture (think spam-like), the mashed potatoes were flavorless (and I'm not even sure why I got them - the meatloaf was supposed to come with some kind of grits), the vegetables were overcooked, mushy and brown, the fries were cold, the lava cake was dry and crumbly, and they just didn't even bring the sweet potatoes that my partner ordered.

We didn't bother mentioning the missing dish, it wasn't on the bill. I'm sure they would have been more than happy to bring it out had we spoke up. Which is why they get 2 stars - the staff and waitress were very pleasant. 

I don't think we'll be giving this place another try.

This was my second visit to Russel's on Mackland.  My first visit was about a year ago for brunch.  It was crazy busy that day and the experience was just okay.  But our experience tonight was tremendous. 

First of all, we had a reservation and were seated promptly.  Our waitress was helpful and eager.  We started with drinks:  Cuban, Sangria, and a few others from our table.  The drinks were tasty.

The dinner plates were excellent.  Our friend enjoyed his crab cakes, but felt the portion was small.  It was in the small plate section ... so there's that.  It looked good and he said the flavor was good.  Two of us had the bacon wrapped meatloaf.  It was out of of this world flavorful.  I had the lemon herb marinated fried chicken, which was sweet and savory.

For dessert, we had death by chocolate cake, a cinnamon chocolate ganache cake, and flourless chocolate cake.  The desserts were spectacular.  We will definitely be back!!!

Disappointing. We went mainly to see the Peter Max exhibit but had planned on spending time looking at the other exhibits. Other than the parachute dresses exhibit and the permanent exhibit of Roman  and Egyptian pottery there was the gift shop and a small cafe. I've seen larger museums in smaller cities.

This place is great...we have been going for years and we have been terrible customers for not reviewing sooner. The only place we ever want to eat in SB/Goleta is Tamira. The food is always fresh, the customer service is exquisite, and it's so satisfying. Perhaps they don't have the biggest variety when it comes to the buffet...but every item is delicious and cooked to perfection. The food doesn't feel heavy either, and we've always been able to walk out perfectly content...being able to combine the options they offer to meet our exact tastes.  The buffet changes daily and we love this! The owners are always there making sure the customers are happy. And when I say the customer service is exquisite, let me elaborate by saying that our cups have not once ever reached empty! You can tell they pride themselves on customer service...it's like they know our needs before we know them. Definitely a place to try :)

Showed up with only one person waiting. The place seems to be undergoing a remodel and is not finished. The staff was talking with each other in the back but no on greated anyone who walked in.  One staff member came up to the front to ring out a customer and still did not acknowledge anyone waiting.  It took over 15 minutes before someone actually talked to me.  By the time all was done and my pedicure failed with 3 days as the paint started chipping off immediately.  Very disappointed!!

The lanterns were beautiful, and should only be viewed when it's dark outside. I was kind of disappointed with everything else, however. The food selection was poor, the stores were tacky, and I couldn't get a good view of the stage performers. Plus, it was pretty crowded.

I travel a ton and it was great to have an opportunity to visit here.   The food was great.  Sweet with a slight southern tang.  Food was amazing. 
To note, I smoke meats all the time.  I think I am really good at it and people often rave on my food.  This place excels in smoking food.  Sides are stellar.  I like their beans better than mine and that's hard to say.  

The place is definitely great, so  much so I ate there two days in a row.   It seriously worth your time and money.  I will definitely be back when I get into town.
Delicious lunch and excellent, friendly service. Fresh, tasty and healthy food. Accidentally stumbled upon this funky place but well worth the stop. Thank you :)

This is what a gym should be like. 

First off, it's a good size. It has a variety of equipment. 

Every piece of cardio equipment has its own TV with 10-20 cable channels (including ESPN, MTV, and all the locals), and then theres 9 additional machines in their movie theater which plays a new movie every day. 

They have a good variety of free weights including: 2 smith machines, 1 free rack, 2 flat benches, an inclined and declined bench and then 3 dumbbell benches which can also be adjusted up or down, 2 full sets of curling bars (which are really convenient so that you don't have to keep switching weights. I haven't seen these at any other gym), one is an ez-bar and one is flat. 

They also have the normal resistance machines that other gyms have, however these machines are very new and have the most fluid range of motion of any machines I've used. They are also very adjustable and can be made to fit my short self easily.

All of these amenities come for only $20/month which is very comparable to other gyms in the area. The staff is also very personable and friendly. Sadly, I haven't been in the area for a while so I had to cancel my membership (which was easy to do), but if I am in the area again and need a gym, this Retrofitness will be my first choice.

Great sandwich shop franchise.  Chipotle may brag about it's organic ingredients, but in terms of objective quality, Primos has them beat.

While you'll never get a bad sandwich from a Primos hoagie shop, you can easily find superior sandwiches if you're in the south Philly area.  If you're out in Jerz or other outskirts, you're probably better off with the somewhat uninspired but consistently above average Primos.

I'm giving this place two stars for mediocre food and poor service. 15 mins for waitress to greet us, 25 mins for the food to come out. 

My four horseman -  meh.  It looked like it was sitting out for awhile because there was a thin layer that formed on top of the sauces.  I like the corn fritters the most which is saying something about the entree itself. 

Tejon burger - soggy bun ruined the burger. 

Very underwhelming.

The crew of guys were really polite. Didn't try to oversell me on anything just more informative. 

It's been a while since I used Jiffy Lube and the pricing seems to be just as high as regular mechanic shops (used to be a much better value).  I won't be making this mistake next time.  Luckily they were running $20 off special so it brought "down" the price. 

My biggest issue was how I was talked to by the "crew chief". He was barking orders at the guys (not too over top). The problem came when they were confirming my oil. It's a new car so I told them just whatever is cheapest my car will take (had no idea if my car takes synthetic or conventional). Explained it's a lease so not worried about getting most mileage. Lol

Well the "chief" think name I heard was Gordon.   Came over to my window and basically scolded and chastised me for selecting conventional oil.   Shoved the cap at me to show me I needed a blend and kept repeating the name I'm supposed to use (lot of good that did cause already forgotten it). That otherwise was no good for my car etc.   He is lucky I maintained my composure and just voicing what I think of my treatment this way. I don't work for him and shouldn't talk to me (or any other customer) like I do (I don't talk to my employees that way but I don't work in a hot sweaty loud environment though - so don't want to be too judge mental)  He then went and scolded the guys about the type of oil (which if they were going to use wrong then go get em). 

Needless to say after this experience I will just take car to dealer (or across the street to one of two other places).  Jiffy Lube wasn't really any faster or much cheaper than I remembered it.

Visited Hardena, which was on the list of places to eat for a long time. Upon entering we noticed a line of people waiting to get to the counter to order. The restaurant is small and most tables have been cleared out to accommodate customers for take out. 

You can order at the steam station where an employee fills a to go container with your choices. The employees were very friendly and helped us pick what to order. We picked a rice box and 3 items. Once we were rung up we went outside to eat on a bench in front of the store. 

The food smelled yummy but sadly it was bland. Out of the three items I ordered, only the tofu tempeh dish stood out. The jackfruit curry (only served on weekends) and the eggplant dishes were just so-so. My friend ordered chicken curry, tempeh fritter, and potatoes. They enjoyed the potatoes and found the curry just ok. The fritter was good but a little too oily. 

Not sure if we just picked the wrong items or had really high expectations. Not sure a future visit is in the cards for the future for now, which results in a 3 star rating. If I do go again in the future, I will update my review.

Things to know:
- expect a line
-online reviews state they are only open for takeout and delivery, however they have on table in the store for 2 people and a bench outside to dine at
-certain items are only available on weekends (example: jackfruit)
-vegan and vegetarian options available 
-the menu varies from day to day, the menu online is out of date
-there is a bathroom and WiFi (but did not use either)
-they have a homemade hot sauce that you can ask for while ordering
-there is a separate to go menu if you want larger quantities of any of the items
-Cc are accepted

This wasn't the best or worst Thai restaurant I've been to.  I wasn't use to the limited options, but I didn't mind. The atmosphere was nice and the service was efficient. I liked the cute hearts that held the straw wrapper together. I'd probably return every so often.
Just drove across town to go there yesterday and they had taken down the 30% off sign and had a sign up that said "Closed for mechanical problems "

Ok, I'll start by saying that even though I live just blocks from Chinatown, I've never really found an asian takeout restaurant I love. That said, I think I've found a new regular in Pagoda Noodle Cafe.

Lunchtime on a Sunday and we were getting pretty hungry... beyond the "let's see what's in the fridge" stage and straight to "if we don't get lunch soon I'm going to turn into the hulk" stage.

We found Pagoda and ordered online. I got the wonton soup, pork dumplings, and scallion and ginger noodles (because I have a serious case of the eyes-bigger-than-stomach syndrome) and he got the general tso chicken (no surprise there). I have to say I was extremely satisfied with all of them. 

The steamed dumplings were different than I expected, but I am no dumpling expert by any means. I still enjoyed them. The wonton soup was flavorful and wontons tender. The scallion and ginger noodles were very flavorful. Jerrett said his chicken was good too, but I didn't have any.

All in all, I would recommend. and we will be back when the hulk inside me needs supressing again.

I had recently attended a Benefit at Paris.  Parking was No Problem with a covered Carport to drop off / pick up guests.  Very nice entrance way and banquet area.  Acoustics are excellent for Live Music.  Dance floor located in the center of room which is nice.  Food was above Average Catered Food.  Promptness and delivery of the food was Excellent!  Service was Excellent!  I would recommend this venue for events.
Chef Gary Curry

I have never been to any restaurant that has offered Huevos Rancheros with meat in it.  Not only that, but it wasn't even listed on the menu that it would be there.  Besides their sub-par coffee the service was mediocre, with one enthusiastic man running around the place being friendly for the rest of the staff.  Another establishment that treats a young face as a second class citizen, we tip when given proper service. Not going back.
Booked the Amtrak Thruway bus to Chicago instead of the early morning train. Turns out it is a damn Greyhound. They subcontract the route though Greyhound. Will never do that again.

My reservation for 4 people was at 5pm. We got there 10 minutes early and waited at the bar. We weren't seated at a table until 5:20pm. After sitting, we waited another 10 minutes for a server, after I had to ask for one and got a nasty look from another female server for asking. For appetizers, we ordered the Pecan Brie and the PB&J Brussels Sprouts. Both were very good. Three of us ordered the fish tacos, which were supposed to be Mahi. The fish was very dry and was quite "fishy" tasting. I would not order it again. For dessert, I ordered the Banana Bread Pudding. Again - very dry. The driest bread pudding I have ever had. I had three bites and did not finish it. It was not moist at all. Our server ended up being quite good and was very attentive (I tipped her well). I would definitely order the PB&J Brussels again.  They had a great Thai peanut sauce on them, which I love. I'm not sure if I would go again, but if I did, perhaps I will just stick to apps.

Quick friendly service, easy to make appointments and professional... I see complaints about wait times but we had no wait and the staff was excellent with my 9 year old... it was our first visit with the office so it may take a few more to really give a good rating but as of now we are happy with the service.
Best Bakery in the city, upside down pineapple cake is unbelievable, you have to try the banana pudding, only problem is that it's hard to decide which flavor all is great!

O literally just spent 4 days eating hospital food and that was better than my $22 meal from this place. For the amount of money I paid for this coconut shrimp and my upgrade to the onion rings for $1.50 more I would expect a much better meal. My onion rings were basically small slices of onion battered in a donut.  Coconut shrimp were pretty large but also kind of burnt. 1/10 definitely would not recommend or bring anyone here again.

They have the necessities, but you will pay deary for them.  In short, it ain't cheap, but it's in a good location if you live in the area, since Busch's Grove is gone, there really is no other game in town...unless you just drive like a mile to the schnucks down off Ladue.  They have outlasted all their neighbors so they must know something about staying-power...or the own the building and don't have to pay obscene rent.  Either way, you can expect them to be there for a while to come.
Horrible service, dirty silverware and could not get a order correct if there life depended on it.

I have since updated my review after living at the complex for nearly 5 years. This place went downhill to the point that I had to leave. The utilities will cost you upwards of $150 a month for a 1 bedroom apartment even if you leave your thermostat at 60 degrees in the winter. I'm not exaggerating, this is what we as tenants were told to do by the front office and by the utility company because the front office refused to listen to their tenants and switch to a fair billing system. Avoid at all costs

I have friend shrimp po-boy.   The fries that accompanied the po-boy were average.  But the sandwich itself was quite good, with a generous amount os shrimp.  I stopped by around 2 in the afternoon, so perhaps not their busiest time.  Still, I was seated right away, and had attentive service.

This place is a MUST go to!! Love this place, staff is amazing!! The cheesy fries were awesome and the Super Italian was out of this world good!! Will make this a regular stop when passing through!! Wow good!! Thanks for the awesome service also..

Went here on Saturday.

Service was a bit slow.  I had the bucatini which was fantastic.  Fresh, handmade pasta, definitely fresh.  Would go back.

A loved one was recently in ICU at UMC. The doctors and nurses were absolutely terrible about keeping in touch with us about their condition. With the virus going on we were unable to visit and our loved one was not conscious enough to call us. We relied on the medical staff to keep us informed and they failed miserably. We didn't even know about a broken bone until we got our loved one home. No one discussed with the family about after care for when we were able to take our loved one home. They should be ASHAMED of themselves.

Probably two and a half stars maybe.  This place has always been very hit or miss for me.  The happy hour menu is no deal.    You're a Spanish tapas bar.  When you trot out Ms Plank's Heaps O'Calamari Rings and it is rubbery, bland and awful...  Now I want to walk the plank.?  And the "marinara" (I use that term loosely here) tasted canned.  What makes you think I am going to have confidence in the rest of the menu?  I really feel as though the menu is approached with the attitude that the more you pay, the better the quality.  So rather than enticing me to try more with flavorful and creative happy hour entrees, I feel I'm being punished instead.  Another reviewer commented that the sangrias are cheap... and they are not very good.  That about sums up Barcelona.  Spain has a rich tradition of serving the highest quality food and hospitality.  They couldn't even remember I ordered a beer here. Twice.  The atmosphere is kickin and the decor and vibe tempting.  But be forewarned. It's siren's call.  You would do better to keep walking... maybe all the way to the real Barcelona.
this brunch is fantastic- pretty consistent with all the different things I've ordered. go with the mocha and the burrito if it's on special. omg i want to go back now and get it!
Food was really good. Just don't go between 3 and 4 pm if you want a full meal. Wait staff is slow and asleep on couches.

The place smelled delicious right when I walked in the door which was a great sign.  I had a traditional Cuban pressed sandwich which was the best I have had in years.  I also had the black beans and rice on the side which was done just right as well.

Make sure and try the hot sauce they make themselves, a little kick and a unique "sweet" flavor as well.  I actually bought a bottle of it to take home with me.

If you like Cuban food don't miss this place.

We had a 7:45 reservation on Saturday 2/8 and arrived at the restaurant 10 min. before that. I checked in and was told that we would be seated in about 15 min. By 8:00 we were still waiting. I asked the hostess how much longer we would be waiting. She said that several people had checked in ahead of me and she could only estimate that the wait would be 15 min. longer. We watched as guests who checked in after us with 8:00 reservations were seated. At 8:15 my husband went to the hostess to ask why our party had not been seated despite checking in at 7:35 for a 7:45 reservation and others with an 8:00 reservation had been seated ahead of us. As the hostess tried to put us off for another 15 min. an older woman approached and asked what the issue was. My husband explained our situation and miraculously we were seated immediately. Our waiter approached our table soon after we sat down filled our water glasses and took our drink orders. He came back quickly to take our dinner orders. We ordered the garlic pepper chicken wings. The wings were delicious. Dinner took over 30 min. to get to our table from the time the appetizers arrived.  My daughter had the 7 cheese mac and cheese and my husband, son and I had steak and fries. The mac and cheese arrived cold and was not a cheese sauce but a butter sauce. There was a small puddle of cheese at the bottom of the bowl that tasted like a slice of American cheese. Our steaks were overcooked and the fries were cold. We tried to get the attention of our waiter, but he never returned to ask how everything was or if we needed anything. When we caught his attention, we told him that my daughter could not eat the pasta and why. He offered to get her another one but we declined because who knows how long that would take. We told him about the steak and fries. He offered to take the pasta off of the bill. We did not order dessert as we were anxious to just leave. We had brunch reservations for Sunday at FF, but decided to go elsewhere. We had really high hopes for this restaurant. I would not recommend this restaurant to anyone.

Overall the visit was fine. 

The problem is: this location isn't covered (and this insurance plan coverers a lot!). I strongly suggest you check they take your insurance before you go back as they won't tell you until you get the bill in the mail.

We visited Sopapilla's with high expectations and left feeling "blah" about the experience. The what's wrong list from our experience is quite long: the salsa was so-so, chips were stale, queso took 30 minutes to bring out and by then it was cold and tasteless, the "house special" margaritas were overly sweet (like the kind you buy in a plastic bucket) and the main courses were bland in taste and visually boring. It's too bad they are expanding next door, I don't think they will need the space after they are open for a longer time.

After visiting Rick Bayless' new restaurant Red-O in LA the previous week, a superb experience of the ultimate Mexican/southwestern food experience, this was a definite let-down.

Also, they only take reservations for parties of 8 or larger.

So, I went today to the new location of The Sweet Life Bakery, and ordered a Petit Four. I started eating it there and it was super dry. I tell the attendant that it was dry, so I asked for the trash can. She offered to throw it away for me. Then apologized because the Petit Fours have been there since Friday, and today is Wednesday. I waited for her to offer me something else, but she didn't. She just kept apologizing. 
Wonder why they are selling 5 days old petit four? She did mentioned that the cup cake I purchased for my daughter should be ok because it was baked yesterday. It is obvious this employee hasn't been trained in customer service, or she didn't feel empowered to make the decision to offer me something else or give my money back. It's not her fault. She was always kind and well spoken. Please train your employees better or empower them to make decisions that will make the customer happy and keep them coming back.

Had the best experience tonight! Went here for the first time for date night.  We sat at the bar.  Met the owner and had the best time!  Great draft beer selection.  The roasted fingerling potato appetizer was incredible!  Also loved the gnocchi!  Overall excellent atmosphere, food and drink selection.  Stop in!

I've had this place bookmarked for months and glad i was finally able to make it there. You order at the cash wrap, they give you an order number to place on your table, and the server brings out the food. I wish they had a menu board placed on the wall so we can decided what we want while waiting in the cash wrap line. Instead I felt like we were rushed to order because you don't see the hand out menu til you reach the register and there are other people behind you waiting in line. That's the only downfall. Oh and the street parking was kind of crowded. The staff were really nice though. 

I order the french toast which is a thick cut of brioche with scramble eggs and bacon. It was all delicious. The eggs were fluffy and scramble perfect. Bacon was cook perfect with that nice crispiness and the french toast was made just right. Not too thick and wasn't soggy like some other places i've had.  My boyfriend order the create your own bowl of grits. The grits were cooked perfectly and i'm not really a fan of grits but these were so yummy! 
 
Oh and there's bottomless coffee and hot tea!

Burgerhaus was alright.  The Aussie burger I had was dry but I guess that happens sometimes if the chef slightly overcooks a "medium well" burger.  Overall the beer selection was great.  The service A-1 and the view of the canal unbeatable!

Well they get points for service. Well atleast our server this particular Thursday night at 10 p.m does. Wittney or Wendy, took care of us. Whatever spiral-cut potato thing with cheese dip I ordered was atrocious, but my blackened steak pasta was pretty good cooked medium well. My friend ordered some healthy chicken thing but half way in the chicken sprouted a hair and that was it for him. When our server came to check on us again we told her of the incident and she really was traumatized and assured us this never happens and was very apologetic and gave my friend a free meal coupon for his next visit. I'm not a frequenter to this establishment bit he is so he was grateful. She ever offered him a free dessert on this trip but he was done for the night lol. But will gladly return for the half price appetizers after 9. Will keep an eye out for foreign objects in out meals next time though.

I've gotten used to the smoking ban in Indianapolis so I was surprised to see folks smoking in this Carmel area bar.  Menu was kinda average to crappy.  Nothing sounded appealing and I'm glad I had dinner before I went there.  I've also gotten used to better bars in Indianapolis with much tastier menu options for foodies and more local and craft beers.  We won't be going back because there are plenty of better options not too far from their.  As a place to drink beer, smoke, and party with friends it probably would fit the bill, but not the scene I'm into.
Great deal for happy hrs drink but sushi isent too fresh too much rice on the roll I eat sushi all the time so I feel like I know what I'm talking about

I walked over to O'Fallon Brewery from my hotel because I am always up for trying a new brewery.  It was about 4pm, so all the workers at the companies around the brewery hadn't been released yet.  It was very quiet, so I sat at the bar.  I ordered an IPA, which had an ABV below 7%, but it was still served to me in a snifter.  Double IPA's are consistently served in snifters, but I've never seen an IPA served in one.  I found that quite odd seeing as the ABV was near other beers that were served in pint glasses.  I ordered a chicken quesadilla to go along with it.  I had to do a little searching to find the chicken in the quesadilla, but it tasted very good.  I don't think hoppy or high alcohol content beers are O'Fallon's strong suit.  The IPA I had was pretty average.  I did try the red ale after that, which was actually very good.  They were hosting a mixer for one of the companies near the brewery as I was finishing my meal.  It got super crowded, but the bartenders never forgot about me.  I will come back to try more of their lighter bodied beers.

Multiple phone calls that do not get returned, overpriced items, unknowledgeable employees.  After a visit to the store and visits to the house, we will not be using them again.  The last straw was worker coming over to figure out why the filter wasn't running correctly, he said he would just turn the pump on 24/7.  He was asked not to do that unless he wants to pay the power bill!!  So he set the program to run 9:00am to 8:59am, maybe being off for one minute qualified in his mind??  Never again.  DO NOT RECOMMEND.  Take your money elsewhere!!

My mom was looking at the menu here about 10 minutes before they opened and a lady came out and said "we aren't open yet," which was fine. At 11:58 my mom went back up to the counter to order, the lady seemed kind of impatient and aggravated (bad day? I dunno, this is also fine, no big deal, stuff happens). Then my mom went to the bathroom and gave me her credit card to pay. When she was out of earshot the lady goes "wow she must be hungry." It was so rude I have no choice to respect it. I have no ill will towards this place or this person, I just wanted to remember this happened when I'm scrolling Yelp on my death bed. 5 stars.

It's okay as a motel. Typical Motel 6. No surprises; no great disappointments. There was, however, no need for the desk clerk to ask me, a single male, if I needed a second key "for the wife." I was the only one in the office so there was no reason to assume I had a little wifey out in the car. Unless, of course, one assumes that every adult male has a wife - which might be the case in Nigeria, India or Kansas. But this is coastal California where I expect something better than Kansas stereotypes. At any other motel the question "One or two keys," does the job.

Pretty decent. I ordered the California Club with steamed veggies and the partner got the Portobello Wrapini with chips. The sandwich was a little bigger than I expected, but it was delicious! The wrap was good as well. 

Total price with drinks came out to about $16+

As another Yelper said, the staff is very friendly. There are some that could use a crash course on best public health practices (i.e., cover your mouth when you cough near the salad bar). 

Other than that, the place it pretty good. It's usually a last choice for us when thinking of places to go though because I don't crave sandwiches that often.

Came for some dessert after several hours in the hot sun with the kiddos. They're blasts came out perfect for the most part. My shake came up completely melted. The kids wanted a cup of water, and we asked multiple times but they never brought any out for them. Needless to say, I won't be going back to this location.

Unfriendly and unwelcoming staff the day we went in. The "sweet tea" was some strange concoction I've never tasted before and never want to again. The unsweet tea tasted kind of like poison!  Food was ok, guacamole was good. I'm not sure if we could've gotten more tortilla chips or not because they give them to you from behind the counter by the register. Salsa to me isn't chopped up tomatoes but that's what they offered me. I asked for "the liquid kind"!!!  and I got some that was pretty good. Won't go again. We have decided Crazy Burrito is the best in Dunedin.

I've been coming to wilkie for 9 years for service.  I gave them a chance on my new car and Wesley Pinnock dropped my monthly payments and beat Prestige Lexus, BMW of west german and MB of Fort Washington. Great dealer!
Big fan!  Been here a small handful of times and everything I've had here is pretty good.
YUMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMM! not super fast, but they are nice... environment meh.  BUT THE BURGERS ARE TASTY!

The acoustics inside are not very good, especially when there's live music, so it's not the best place for dinner conversation. The new outdoor patio has a great atmosphere, but the service is about the worst I've ever seen. We ordered drinks, and they arrived 45 minutes later. It's not that they forgot, since we asked 3 different servers. We finally had to say something to the owner, who sent a someone belligerent manager to our table. In all, 5 people were involved in our drink order and it still didn't seem to matter. Regardless of the food quality, and in this case it was mediocre, we probably won't return unless we hear that they've done something to improve the service.
This Pho is the best and most consistently good Pho in the Philly area. Broth is tasty and neither beef or chicken is over or undercooked. Service is quick and you can't beat the price.

Just as I'd hoped/planned/expected, the wonderful Royal Tavern has quickly become my go-to neighborhood watering hole. Their tap list is short but well selected, and I'm undertaking the glorious task of trying each of the names on their rather extensive list of bottles, which has thus far yielded some pleasant surprises.

I tried their burger - freakin amazing. We popped in for brunch, too - not as amazing as the burger but an excellent meal nonetheless. All of our servers have been great, and I've never had a problem with the crowd, or even with finding a seat.

Love this place. If I'm not at home or at work, check here next.

I hadn't been to a true barber shop since I was a little kid and I wasn't much of a fan. Growing up I found a hairdresser and stayed with her for most of my life but I grew tired of the drive and it wasn't as convenient anymore. After asking a friend where she goes I ended up at a salon on the north side. The pampering was very nice, but the haircuts were not. Because, as Brandon pointed out in my very first visit, I wasn't getting a haircut.

All of that is said to give context because now I get a haircut and every damn time I leave the shop I'm happy. I walk a little taller and I feel better and neither of those things are all that common in my life. Sure, call that hyperbole if you like but it is absolutely true. Getting a proper haircut by someone that knows what the hell they're doing is an awesome feeling. Brandon is great to work with and I cannot recommend his shop enough.

Went in to ask about combining some family jewelry into one ring and was treated SO well.  (nice piano playing upstairs and champagne at the entrance).  Although it is a very upscale store, and their jewelry is exquisite, I was treated very well, quite different than the other comment on here.  Bob Bryant  takes GREAT pride in producing beautiful pieces.  And get this, they were the ONLY jeweler that made me a wax piece BEFORE making the final product.  As many times as necessary (at no cost) until the wax piece felt and looked perfect.  They are amazing, and kind and take great pride.  Bob, the owner, is an amazing philanthropist in town too.  This is the way a small "mom and pop" store should be run.  It's no wonder they've been in business for over 50 years.  It shows with how they treat every customer, regardless of what you are spending $$.

I never leave reviews but I would feel morally irresponsible if I did not in this instance. BEWARE of this company. During the recent cold spell, our furnace went out in the middle of the night when it was 3 degrees outside. We woke up to a frigid house and my wife called around to get a technician out. We unfortunately called Service Plus who sent a technician that told us our 3 year old furnace needed entirely replaced. Service plus then sent a second individual out to the house who also looked at our furnace- he quoted us $11,000 for the job and told us that our pipes would freeze and burst if we did not act soon. He was pushy and told my wife we needed to sign up for the service before noon (it was 11:30 in the morning on a Friday) or all of their slots for Saturday would fill up. My wife works in sales and identified this sales tactic- which made her feel uneasy, pressured, and uncomfortable.

Mind you, it is now 6 degrees outside. the inside of our house was now 49 degrees and dropping quickly- and we have a one year old. Neither technician did ANYTHING AT ALL to get us any heat. They simply wanted us to sign. 

We knew at that point we had to get a second opinion. Luckily, we found a technician from a different company who came out quickly. He looked at our furnace (which he also mentioned is still under warranty since it is three years old- something that Service Plus failed to mention) and within minutes reset an open roll out limit on the left burner box, and wouldn't you know it our furnace kicked on and has worked perfectly for the past 48+ hours with no signs of issue. THIS TECHNICIAN DID NOT EVEN NEED A SINGLE PART TO REPAIR OUR FURNACE. 

I am absolutely blown away that Service Plus, a company who brings an entire folder of paperwork talking about how they believe in the golden rule, making an ethical profit, etc. etc. treats people like this. They attempted their best to take advantage of us when we were in an extremely vulnerable position. 

Shame on you all for this abhorrent behavior.

I got taken again! $50 person for dinner and I left hungry. The food tasted great but $10 for three forks of cauliflower. I could have gone to China town and had food that was as tasty and half the price. The only reason they get two stars was the service and the look of the place.

I called because I had a oil leak which I didn't know I had and I was from out of town about 250 miles away, they said bring it in and will take a look if it's just the pan plug they could probably fix it which they did thank God and that's what it was. When I got ready to pay they said there was no charge go oh no I want to pay. They put a half a quart of oil which had leaked out in my Subaru Forrester and also filled the window wash. At any rate they treated us very good and I would've been willing to pay to have what they did knowing that my car was repaired driving 250 and some miles home. And we made it fine so thankful for their help.

Mind bogglingly medicore! I could not get over the prices for the most banquet-y food ever. Presentation awful, quality average and prices through the roof. Save yourself the $ and go to a diner where the food is better. 

Atmosphere: Our table was actually a 1" thick plywood (i'm assuming) circle tipsly perched atop a base. No one wanted to rest on the table for fear it would flip. And it clearly was in the way because all wait staff had to awkwardly walk around it to get to other tables. It is as if they were just trying to squeeze as many tables into the space as possible so threw this impromptu table assembly together. Very strange. 

Service: Very attentive nice waiter though he did forget my husband's drink, forgot to put his dinner order in (so he got his plate of burnt food 20 minutes after the rest of us) and then totally missed his glass pouring water so poured it all over the table cloth and never said anything. Just walked away. Very strange night. Also very hard to hear him because the place is SO loud. 

Food: All I can say is WOW- in a BAD way! The food is like the before in one of those restaurant makeover shows. 

Apps/Exotice Meats:  Reason we were there. For a friend who likes that sort of thing. So the table got the kangaroo sliders and alligator kebabs. Growing up in Fla I've eaten alligator many times and I have to say it was not that bad here but just so mediocre in presentation and creativity. Overly oregano-ed alligator pieces on small skewers with a side of what we think was supposed to be fried eggplant but not sure cuz the pieces were like an inch long and coated in so much orange, thick greasy batter we were not sure what it was. The kangaroo sliders were rubbery patties of what tasted like rubbery sausage served on mini rolls with NOTHING else. Even the plate was empty. No garnish, no condiments, Nada! 

Entrees: Went downhill from there. The swordfish that 2 of my dining companions got (20 minutes apart) was the thinnest slice of swordfish imaginable and both were BURNT! The lemon risotto supposed to come w/ was in fact a rice, quinoa (we think) medley. Not risotto at all. My "Fig and Port Short Ribs -Six hour slow-cooked short ribs simmered in a fig and port wine sauce, accompanied by roasted butternut squash mashed potatoes and fire roasted Brussels sprouts for $28" sounded amazing. Instead I got a big grey hunk of meat with little flavor, sitting in brown gravy with grey semi burned mushy brussel sprouts! It was so sloppily plated that the sauce has sloshed all over leaving a big brown ring of gunk. SO unappetizing. I've had better food on planes!!

Drinks & dessert: The only good thing about the meal was a very good martini and dessert was not bad. I tried the Crème brûlée and it was the only well executed, well done dish of the night!

Not sure how they pack them in there with this food when there are so many good restaurants nearby.
Great experience! Our waitress was friendly and knowledgeable. The food was amazing. Will visit the other location, closer to home. Compare & contrast. Stay tune...

Met my friend for dinner after having not been to this place for quite some time the only thing I have to say is the white sangria is very tasty the food however leaves a lot to be desired this place has gone downhill the hot and sour soup was lousy not what it used to be The calamari appetizer was lousy and completely not edible because it was so hot and I like hot peppers very disappointed my friends chicken dish was very tough as well as the beef in my dish was extremely over cooked and what started off the night bad was that the appetizer came out before the cocktailsit was a horribly rushed night and PF Changs Marlton has seen the last of us

They have specials on food and give you half the portion! So really, you aren't getting a special! The service wasn't great and the manager on duty was sitting at the bar with her friends. We weren't checked on after receiving our food and were waited on by two different people! I'm very disappointed local!

Warning! If you don't see people grilling the pollo asada outside on the grill do NOT go inside the restaurant, asking for it. That was a big mistake. We paid for a whole grilled chicken and a half rack of ribs. To our surprise we received half a chicken in our order. I don't recommend the ribs they are not the hype so save your money. We payed $42.79 for half a chicken and half rack of ribs, it was gross. The food was tasteless straight out disappointing and will never come back to this place... amen.

The only reason this joint is getting any stars is because I can see where it would be a cool atmosphere at night. We went on a Sunday afternoon for lunch. There was no one at the hostess station so we just walked in and sat ourselves. Found a table and waited about 10 mins while the waitress of the section walked around and took care of all the other tables. She grabbed her tip off the table without bussing it and we never saw her again. Screw it. We're going to the bar. The bartender wasn't very beer savvy and wasn't clear as to what was on tap. She just asked if we wanted to try it. The food isn't anything to write home about and the service sucked.
A bar crossed with an art gallery??? It works quite well.  They have a good selection of beers, nice staff, and some really cool art.  Nice place to grab a drink.
Maybe the best breakfast I've ever had!  Only disappointment is, we are heading back home on Monday and they are closed. Was hoping to eat there again before the drive home.  That cornbread though!!

This is my first time in New Orleans and of all things, I was craving pizza.  So a friend of mine recommended I go here.  The location is great and I enjoyed sitting outside on the patio.  It was so nice.  I had the fried shrimp skewers for my starter.  Those were GREAT!  I wish I would have gotten 2 orders of those.  Then I had the 4 cheese pizza for my main meal.  It was just average.  One of my slices, was more bread than it was cheese.  It was ok, nothing special.  For my drink, they had this Appleton Mojito, which was not good at all.  So my waitress was kind of enough to switch it for another drink, the Bahada Island.  It was a sweeter drink, but still just ok.  Above all, my waitress was fabulous! She gave awesome service and constantly checked on me, which I really appreciated.  Many of the other people that worked there were also really nice.  They stay open really late, which is a plus.  If I were to go again, I would definitely get the shrimp skewers!

I've live in Philly for 9 years and it is still my favorite place in the city!!! Having grown up working at a family run ice cream parlor, I'm an ice cream snob. This place BY FAR tops them all! Not only is the atmosphere AMAZING! Everything is homemade! If you like peanut butter get their homemade peanut butter sauce, A.MAZ.ING!!!! 10 STARS! 
Also..they're open til 12AM, so if you're looking for a late night treat they're open!!

The only down fall is that the staff is not very friendly. Being that there's always a long line I understand that it's hard to always put on a smile. However, the last time I went was during the week there was no line and only one other customer but the staff seemed very annoyed that me and my friend walked in and it wasn't like they were going to be closing in 5 minutes. This isn't the first time the staff seemed inconvenienced. But the ice cream is good so I'll keep going regardless.

3/5 plain food, good service.

so the service here is friendly and prompt. i just got something pretty basic for a late dinner, but then the food came out like it was from yesterday. the potatoes were like little rocks (not exaggerating), and couldn't be pierced by either fork or knife. i had ordered a wrap and when i opened my to-go box found they made it a sandwich instead, which okay wouldn't be a big deal if the bread was just a little less stale and the meat a little less dry. 

overall i got the glimpse of what may be great lunch options, but i wouldn't reattempt in the evenings.

This place doesn't even deserve one star. I can't speak for all their locations, but this location was horrible! I never received updates about the status of my car, my car was not completed when promised, I had to bring my car back for additional repairs, my key was returned to me disabled, therefore I could not use it. I did not receive any paperwork on the "work" that was done to my car, I had to retrieve my own car upon pickup, haste decisions were made about repair coverage without approval from my insurance company. My point of contact was the general manager, Lincey. THE GENERAL MANAGER! The manager should set the tone and example for the shop. All customers should leave with a pleasant experience since the situation at hand is not a favorable one (crashed cars). I will never use them again and you shouldn't either.

Before reading the rest of this review take this statement into account....We did not order pizza! 
We were in the mood for chicken wings and ordered 1 order of each on the menu. They arrived (delivery) fairly quickly but they were so ridiculously over-cooked we gave up trying to eat them. It was late enough at that point not to call and complain. There was no moisture left in them and they were dry as a bone. There was not much meat on them to begin with and the BBQ sauce was the worst I've ever had. Lesson learned.....DON'T BUY CHICKEN WINGS FROM FORTELL'S
Good lunch in a small restaurant in an interesting area. My shrimp and bacon quesadilla was good but small. My wife's salad was fresh and good.

They just opened and my co-workers and I were curious to try it out.  We ordered the bento box and could not believe what a deal it was!  You get so much food for such a low price.  We went into the restaurant to pick up our food and the place looked very nice inside.  We were impressed because most of the restaurants nearby did not look as nice as this one.  Service to pick up was normal.  Once we got back to the office, we all tried the food and everyone was very pleased.  It was a winner for us.  The food was delicious, filling, and a good price.  We will be back try more bento boxes. Welcome to the neighborhood!

I have had pizza from Europe to Brooklyn's Famous Julianna's,I must say that ricks is a must try. The sauce is just right, the cheese is delicious, most importantly 
the crust. When you pick up a slice its NY style thin that folds down and is cheesy and greasy!Just the damn way I love it. If you hate franchise generic brand pizzas like I do, want to try something more genuine, Try Ricks. I eat a lot of pizza and already made all the mistakes of ordering from the wrong places. Also the pizza showed up HOT!

Service was great, our server was the best. The food was ok. The owner not so much. We were trying to finally enjoy a night out after the nightmare of Covid and got chased off our table. If you are looking for a relaxing evening look elsewhere. I lived in the city for 25 years and dined at every restaurant in the city, never once got pushed off a table. I need to lower my expectations for the Conshy crew. My advice to the owner, people have waited a long time to enjoy themselves, act like you understand that.

Any place that uses pre-process Yucca that calls itself a Cuban restaurant should get 2 stars.  Mashed Yucca is totally different taste and texture and whole Yucca pieces.  The rest of the food is OK but does not bring my rating up

Burgers are much better than a standard fast food meal and you do pay a premium for it.  We have only ever done take out.  When you walk in the staff is very welcoming and triple checks as they take the order.

Excellent pizza. I just got a slice but it was very good. I will be returning for more. As a consumer I expect to be greeted in a gracious manner with smiling faces. That wasn't the case and that's why they only get 4 stars. They were t rude but I did t feel like they appreciated my business. Treat every consumer as if you want them to come back no matter how large or minute there order is. Even if they come to buy a drink. It's about creating the ultimate customer experience.

A so-so Jack experience. The best thing: it was quick, though there was no one ahead of me in the breakfast drive-thru lane. 

The Croissant Supreme order was correct. The presentation was sloppy with none of the ham, egg patty, and buns matched to each other.

Really bad service, waitress ignored us for 20 minutes to take our order and then we asked for water and then we never got the water, she was more focused on the the other tables, no condiments where offered and the food was ok, we ate it because we were starving. We never had such bad experience we did not really enjoy our food. Then when we asked for the check we got that right away !!!
Pho is okay, but the grill meat over rice dishes are good.

Attended a concert today, my first time in the Fillmore and if I did not purchase tickets for another concert months in advance I would not be returning. A few of the ladies that work here were awesome, but everyone else, I mean EVERYONE were complete douche-bags. Concert was General Admission standing room only, so I picked a place on the balcony. I was told after a while I was in the way and had to move by security along with everyone else who was in this area. I waited in a long line to have a good space and then I was told I was 'in the way'. Like, are you serious? They ruined the performers set that I wanted to see so I just decided to leave. I found a exit and tried  to leave and was told by THREE people that I couldnt 'These doors are closed". Wtf? I asked how the F bomb could I get out, and was directed to a guy in a suit who said I couldnt leave, then said if I did I couldnt come back in. Awesome!!

Horrible place!

Ordered from grubhub. Ordered two pounds of King crab legs. 

Less than 1 lbs total was delivered.  And cold.  Restaurant is about 3 blocks away.

Called to get it straightened out and was told basically that I don't know how much crab legs weigh.  Very rude and dismissive customer service.

Pics of what was delivered below.  Weighed on scale.

Really disappointing.

The only thing that wasn't disappointing was GrubHub's customer service.  They were understanding and issued a refund. 

But beware of the boiling pot. You're not going to get what you ordered.

I hesitate to give five stars because I never do. Just generally in life, there's always room for improvement right? So I never give a 10 out of 10. But considering the really nice establishment, friendly service, delicious hot flavorful sandwich, I mean... What else can I ask for? They're only selling sandwiches and coffee and tea drinks. I love when an establishment has a very limited menu just to do the limited things very well. I only had a sandwich, but I look forward to trying the bubble teas & Thai iced teas and coffees, etc. 

I was surprised at the sticker price, it's definitely the most expensive banh mi ever. I was also surprised at the nontraditional choices. Off the top of my head, I believe there was fried chicken and fried shrimp, stuff like that. Reminded me of a po' boy. 

Since I am very picky, I did not want to order a traditional filling, like pork with payé & if it was bad I was going to be really annoyed at spending 10 or $12 for it. So I went with bulgogi. Not traditional filling, not even the right country, LOL, but at least it was Asian!

There is not a single negative comment on the sandwich, and I've been eating these all over the city. Chinatown, North East, west Philly, I would drive all over the place just to get the sandwich and go home. This is just a few minutes from me and I am already thinking I'll be back this week. I went there two weeks ago for the first time.

The bread fresh crisp crust on the outside warm and perfect on the inside, meat was hot seasoned perfectly, so delicious. The fresh raw veggies- yummy yum. I'll be back for lemongrass pork & a drink for sure!

We found this place via Yelp! The donuts were fresh made to order so the wait is a little long. Don't come in your first time and expect to be out soon. There is a process. 

Let me repeat. EVERYTHING IS MADE TO ORDER! So you can't pick donuts off a rack you have to take your time and read the menu. 

We loved the peanut butter and raspberry jam the best! We ordered a dozen donuts and it was too many. The next day they aren't as delicious so order what you will eat the same day!
The concept is great but the meat is always dry. I am giving them 4 stars because they cut the meat fresh for me today. But it was still chewy...

I hate having to do this, but this is my second time ordering from them (Takeout) and this is not it. The queso is insanely over salted, the salsa is not salsa you'd imagine but pico de Gallo. The Baja sauce is definitely delicious, a nice creamy but spicy topping I'd recommend. I think for the price they charge for a burrito and the size you get/meat isn't worth it. The shrimp in their bowl was undercooked, maybe they factored in sitting in heat during delivery? I wasn't impressed the first time, so I gave it another go and I still feel the same. If it's your first time, go for yourself so you can make a decision/support local! Just giving an honest review.

The carrot butterfly was really a nice touch. Now maybe in between carving vegetables learn how to cook. It's not bad like terrible, or what I mean to say is the fish isn't out of date stinky. It's not at all bad. But, interplanetary distances would need to be used to describe the gap between good and what this place is. 

I would never recommend this place under any circumstances....Wait wait if it was a hot summer day you had a heart condition and it began to rain and your fiancés foot had just been operated on, it would be ok to go inside to wait for Lyft or an ambulance.

Service: 
It's on par with chipotle service but at quadruple the cost and worse food. 

Food:
Soup is past the point of calling it salty it's irresponsibly ruin the flavor salty. 

Spicy salmon is so bland it's some how less present than of it came out of a can.
Sehr kleiner Laden mit sehr begrenztem Angebot. Hinterlässt den Anschein, dass hier nur die Reste abverkauft werden.

I made a reservation in advance for a car. When I showed up to pick up the car I was told there weren't any cars to rent out. I had a 3pm appt. The female ahead of me showed up early for a 3:30 appt. They gave her the last available vehicle. I had to sit around and wait for a person to return a vehicle. The definition of Reservation: an arrangement to have something held for your use at a later time. The whole reason I made a reservation was to avoid any issues like this.

This review is strictly for the margaritas and happy hour. Superior is my favorite place to go after work on a Friday. Half off ritas are amazing and super strong. I always order a second, but really shouldn't.  I don't eat there much because the food is only okay and not very vegetarian friendly.
The unlimited chips and salsa are yummy and the servers are always friendly and they never try to rush you out of there. 
Also, be warned that when you leave, you will smell disgusting. The fried food smell lingers in your clothing forever. If you have a few margaritas, though, you may not care at all how you smell.
In short, great happy hour, awesome drinks, free snacks. 
See ya Friday at 4!

I bought a very nice used car last month and had a terrific salesman but can't say anything good about the management there.... other than horrible!  took almost 3 hours to get the paperwork initiated. then I had to come back the next day to sign.... but it still wasn't completed...... and got an outrageous int rate at 7%....    I would never recommend them unless you were desperate to get a car. !

I walked into this store, fully prepared to spend $150 on walking shoes. Unfortunately, I didn't bring socks to try them on. The less than friendly sales women said that I could buy a $14 pair of socks, but it was not hygienic to try on shoes without them. I don't disagree, but having less expensive pairs for purchase would be helpful. Also, I suppose that anyone who comes in to try on new shoes with dirty, sweaty socks is acceptable. I'll spend my money elsewhere.

Bardenay Eagle is one of my favorite places to enjoy brunch on a patio. If only the Boise and Eagle offerings were the same, I'd be the happiest camper around. As it's only offered in Eagle on Sundays, we often aren't able to make it.

Food: I've never been disappointed with my choices. The satay's (chicken, beef and shrimp) are my favorite happy hour item. I can order one of each and they fill me and my husband up! If we're going for brunch, which is often why we go, the French toast and Bardenay Breakfast are our two top picks. I've also had the Basque Scramble (SO GOOD!) and Eggs Benedict--all were divine! What I also appreciate about Bardenay is the ala carte brunch ordering. Typically if my husband orders a meal, I'll order a side of egg whites and fruit, making it a great place to enjoy a great atmosphere as well as a healthy meal. 

Drinks: We haven't had much to drink at Bardenay!

Happy Hour Offerings: M-F 4-6 Happy Hour (late night happy hour, too!) with food and drink specials. Get the satay's... they're great!

Atmosphere: The only patio that beats Bardenay's is the Ram in Boise, which is why I give it such a high rating. It's lovely out there! The sound of the fountain on the patio, the summer heat and very little traffic noise make this place hard to beat.

Service: As Bardenay is almost always very busy and it's a seat-yourself arrangement, it can be hard to get prompt service. 

Healthy Hack: Order a la carte. I typically don't order off the menu but instead get a side of egg whites and fruit!

TIP: It's seat yourself and if it's busy people typically don't mind sharing the big, long tables.

We traveled to 4 different places.  1 of which was another Vision Works,,this location is the absolute BEST ....aka TOP NOTCH when it comes to  Selection, Price points, knowledge of product and CUSTOMER SERVICE,  

Deaf. Friendly!  Respectful transactions on the sales floor!

About a year ago, I went here and wasn't impressed.  I had to give it a chance at redemption.  Again, I wasn't impressed.  I won't be back.

This time I got the Thai Basil.  It was spicier than last time, so they get some credit for that.  But otherwise it was just average.  The wait staff was disorganized, and the soup had macaroni in it.  My entree was alright; if I had to go back, I'd order it over the ginger meal, which I got last time.  Still, like before, the vegetables didn't taste very fresh.  Also, I tried the pad see ew, and it wasn't very good.  The sauce tasted jarred.  The only reason I was able to try it was that my friend, who ordered the entree mild, couldn't eat very much of it because it was so spicy.

Update:

In response to my review below, Ben texted me that he would be sueing unless I remove this review. Which lends a question to the accuracy of his rating here and my review below, which you can take to your own. Rather than taking my word for it, you can make the decision based on the screenshots-- if you need the conversation, I'm happy to share so you can see how Ben really conducts his business 

----

I originally felt really good about Ben as he had good reviews and I had good over the phone interaction with him. However it ended up an incredibly disappointing and unprofessional experience with Ben that left me without a mover the business day before the date. I originally contacted him early For a quote. It did take a while and I had to call back several times but eventually got a quote. I asked to confirm and he said yes. I even asked him for anything he needed and never got anything. The day before, after not hearing back from him, I texted to confirm. He asked for 2 articles to confirm-- none of which he asked for before. So, I'm supposed to move Monday and as of Friday, I have no mover

Rude employee at the register. Never going again.
 Food is mediocre anyway.

That whole shopping center is tacky and these people fit right in.

Good breakfast place. Not exactly a diner (for people who know diners, like NY or NJ folks), but it's a diner breakfast. Nothing fancy, but that's reflected in the cheap prices. Cash only! We had great service. Good sized portions. French Toast was great. Arrive early like us because this place fills up with the silver haired regulars. We'd go again.

Went to St. Louis for a visit and decided on Katie's because it had always been great.  Unfortunately I didn't realize that Katie is no longer associated with the place.  The service was horrible.  We had to go to the bar several times to get our own drinks.  Waitresses walked right by us and even heard them complaining at one point.  The food was still good, but they really need to get the service under control or this place will not be around long.

Someone from Starbucks suggested this place to us, and we came here to check it out. This science center is actually pretty huge, and there are activities for the kids to engage and interact with - seems to be better than the Museum of Science in Boston... we did not get to finish exploring the museum as we only had an hour. The kids had a great time there, for the short time we were there. The outside has these two big dishes, and we definitely spent a good 10 minutes playing and testing the sounds. Overall, had a great time with the kids here.

As much as we love Cafe Du Monde (the super popular beignet place), we wanted to give other places a chance too to prove that their beignets are just as good. We tried the beignets at Cafe Beignet and found that we liked Cafe Du Monde a lot better. While it wasn't bad, the beignets were really thin and crispy and I like them more light and fluffy.

See blog for Cafe du Monde vs Cafe Beignet comparison: http://sophaddicted.com/new-orleans-la-what-to-eat/

We walked into this place and I thought to myself...wow.  This is somewhere to be seen.  I felt like I needed to brag about my wine cellar on my speed boat.

The bread was really good, served with an olive oil mix, also good.  I thought we were in for a winner.

I ordered the olivia with tilapia.  My mother ordered a shrimp Cesar and mussels in "not sure" sauce.  

The shrimp were over cooked.  Salad was mediocre at best.

The mussels came
Out with "extra crispy" sausage.  The sauce was a weird orange color.  It tasted awful.  The mussels themselves were fine.  

My dish came
Out and it looked like progresso, septic, and Tyler Durden got together for a gang bang.

Mostly I was shocked.  

I took one bite and was not interested.  

The server was a sweet heart otherwise this is a 1 star.

After our first experience, we went to High Hat maybe another handful of times.  And the food was always OK to good to pretty good, until the last time.  The special of the day was shrimp creole, Yum! right? And the sauce was pretty good, but the shrimp which should have been mighty tasty were inedible.  Why? Because they hadn't been cleaned properly.  Since they hadn't been purged or deveined, they were so gritty as to ruin the dish.

And, I guess, that's why we won't be back anytime soon.  The attention to detail just doesn't seem to be there.  From past their prime veggies, to inconsistent seasoning, to not properly cleaning their seafood, I just don't trust their product.

Strangely enough, there is a large wine selection of many horrible varieties.  The bar is fully stocked, but the drinks are not well made, such as the case while muddling an entire lime slice, including the rind, in the mojito (the effect, causing a bitter drink, instead of a refreshing one).  I have to give credit that the drinks were fast.  I did not like the lack of late night table service, even with a patio area and such high prices.  The Malbec that was paired with my meal was stale with fast legs, therefore, the stale, high alcohol content of the wine was less than expected.  The food was cold and the steak bruschetta was not good.  Cold and roast beef taste!  Never again.

I was excited to come to this place. We had tried to come before but there was always a huge wait. I had the Cochon signature Benedict, and my husband the Blackstone. They were served promptly, but mine wasn't as warm as I would've liked it. Good flavors, but huge. We both couldn't finish them. We ordered the pancake special, pecan and bacon pancake. The pancake was very good. Overall this place was good, but it didn't have the wow flavor factor for me. I  liked Horn's more. So, go give it a try and judge for yourself.

I've been to many Whole Foods' around the country and this is the weakest store  i've ever been too.  Its small, dated, not nearly the amenities of it's sister stores.  They should call this location Half Foods Market.

My very favorite place in NOLA for drinks and dinner.  I love the feel of the place, the tables outside, and everything on the menu.  Really great happy hour and great salads (the rum house salad is the best "house salad" I've ever had), apps, and tacos!

Let me tell you...I have heard quite a bit about this place. The first time I wanted to go, there was a line leading out the door. 

So, per my current life (AKA stay inside and order food to eat between study breaks), I ordered some food via delivery app. I ordered the stuffed redfish, spicy potatoes, and steamed green beans. 

I was not disappointed at all. The entire order was simple but delicious. The green beans had a nice, mild flavor. The potatoes were nicely fried and actually had a spicy kick. 

The stuffed fish THOUGH...really, really nice job on this. I was not expecting it to taste as well as it did because I assumed their efforts mainly went toward boiled seafood. However, I was really satisfied. The redfish was SLIGHTLY dry, but not enough to ruin the fish or the entree itself. It was seasoned really well--not spicy, but seasoned well. The stuffing is what sold me though. It's sweet with a mild kick that has a trace of crabmeat (at least to my taste palate). The consistency is thick. 

I'm excited to try more food from this place, but I honestly want the same meal the next time. Lol.

Vincent Van Doughnuts in the Grove has a gorgeous dining room.  3 or 4 small tables and a wall full of bar-height seats against the window.  As you wait in line, you can see the beautiful donuts lined up waiting to be taken home.  The kitchen is open so you can see the baker making some fresh doughnuts if you're lucky.  If you follow them on social media, you will know when they have special treats: vegan doughnuts, a breakfast biscuit sandwich, or BOGO specials.

I order with my eyes, so I chose the Samoa doughnut topped with chocolate drizzle and coconut shavings.  One bite proved that the coconut and the doughnut were saturated in grease.  Not sure if that is how all the doughnuts are, or if the coconut flakes were greasy enough to saturate the dough.  The doughnuts are Instagram-worthy and huge, but they lack the advertised flavors.

I love a cinnamon sugar doughnut hole... They sell them here 6 to an order.  But my most recent trip they were sold out.  They give them away for free to little children, so none left for me :(

We decided to go here for dinner tonight since we had enjoyed our last visit here last spring.
Our server took our drink orders and we also ordered conch fritters and onion rings--yelp freebie!--along with a broiled combo to share.
Our server delivered our drinks and said that the apps would be out shortly, which they were.
Barely had time to try them and our dinner was brought to us by someone other than our server.
He knew it was soon after the apps were brought because he started apologizing--we hadn't made a comment at that point.
Soon after, someone else approached our table and asked how everything was and we said it would have been much better if we didn't have all our food at once.
Well, she offered to take it back and keep it under the warmer. My wife, having worked in restaurants, said no, the reason being it would get dried out.
Needless to say, our experience tonight was not the best and certainly could have been handled better.
Not sure where the blame lies here, our server was very pleasant, but unfortunately our dinner was disappointing.

Great location- right on the water next to our hotel. The staffs were friendly. I got here close to 8pm. Not sure if it's kinda late, everything i ordered came out luke warm. The lobster bisque seemed to have been sitting there for a while, although it was creamy, there didn't seem to be much lobster in it. The crab cake tasted good and actually had decent amount of crap meat in it. The food came with bread (not sure which dish) which was kinda nice.

All of the good stuff about this place is true.  I was in town for a volleyball tournament with my son and he wanted me to do his laundry!  So this place to the rescue - the wash and fold service is perfect - you drop it off and they call you when it's all done!  I was able to Go back and see the tournament and come back later that night to get it.  Thank you!

My dishwasher stopped working so I called AZ Appliance Repair. After paying the service call bill of $49.95, I was told that the motherboard needed to be replaced; however, this repair company couldn't order the part, and if I ordered the part then AZ Appliance Repair could install the new part. I ordered the part which arrived in 4 days. The repairman put the new part in, but it still didn't work. I was charged $129.00 for the labor that did not fix the machine. I was not able to return the new part, because it was an electronic part which could not be returned. That part had cost me the Grand total of $152.00 . I still have a broken dishwasher after having spent just over $330. Did this company know electronic parts could not be returned? If so, shame on them for encouraging me to make this purchase, adding to the service call plus the labor that repaired nothing.

Pho or Kuy Teav Noodle Shack is the best and only restaurant that serves authentic and delicious Cambodian and Vietnamese food in Edmonton and probably all of Alberta. It's located in a tough looking neighborhood, but we regular customers knows the owners are genuine and hard-working. They're not a chain but family-owned. The food stands out without help but the business is worth supporting during COVID-19 times or any times really. If you need a home-cooked meal or a tasty treat, this restaurant got everywhere else beat. Highly recommend.

SO yummy. So glad that there is finally one in conshy! All of the workers are so friendly and helpful, and the food is freaking delicious. I have yet to try the salads there, but the pizza is amazing, and isn't that expensive either!

Was inline for at least 30mins to be seated. 

Service in general isn't that great; luckily our server was nice. 

The raw oyster taste that great; but the chargrilled oysters was really good. We ended up getting another order of them

I brought my car in to have the AC repaired.  Staff was super helpful and informative.  I got a fair price, car was fixed in a timely manner & everything is once again in working condition.  Thanks so much!

Lindsey
The room was nicely appointed with good climate control.  Customer service was excellent.

These announcements are very expensive but the quality of the letters themselves seem decent. I got the most basic shipping option and they arrived in about 3 weeks. I honestly don't understand how they're charging $30+ for expedited shipping. I received my package today and the letters were wrapped in paper to stay protected. The envelopes however were not and a lot of them are dirty. What do you expect if you just throw them inside a dirty box? Also I got a certificate that you give to a parent and it was just thrown in the box and the edge was bent. You guys need to package your items better if you want to charge so much.

I am up here at Dat dog on Frenchmen an my wife just had the crawfish dog and I had the Alligator sausage and I must say that Linsey was the most on point bartender ever such a friendly vibe and as sweet as can be me and my wife shall  return soon real soon thanks to Bill and his staff love you guys for the hospitality  much love

I have been going here for about two years because I live so close and I'm too lazy to walk further. I have not had anything tailored but my fiance brings his work shirts there to be dry cleaned and ironed. Over the past two years their ironing has gotten sloppier and sloppier, to the point where I have to completely re-iron his things before work. I'm talking HUGE creases in every 3 out of 5 shirts. Also they've burnt several shirts, which they've reimbursed us for... but why do they keep making the mistake in the first place? I'm finally going somewhere else.
Pretty nice vibe. Owner is very passionate and enthusiastic about making the customer feel comfortable. It's also nice to see a diverse crowd at the coffee shop. Def will come back again!

A kid friendly spot to grab some tasty drinks and Mexican food.  Outdoor dining and indoor dining available . Water was set out for patrons to help themselves.  Set in the cute 4S neighborhood, so save some time to wander afterward!

I've always really liked Oregano's, although this is always a significant wait for weekend dinners so plan accordingly. I always get the Pablo Picasso salad. It's absolutely amazing, but it's HUGE. The "half" portion can feed two people. I've also had the thin crust pizza, which was good. But being from Chicago, I wasn't impressed. The pasta dishes look yummy, particularly the Lady is a Scampi. Again, the portions are HUGE so prepare to take home leftovers

What is left to say about the Starz Center or as I have known it, the Performing center, like everything  else in Tampa, they like to change the name of everything.  It is centrally located in Northeastern  downtown Tampa.
Let me start by saying when the Performing  Arts opened, many of us thought it was the beginning  of some real culture for Tampa, & personally  I don't think we were wrong.
The Center itself comes with some inherent problems, that at this point in time I see no solution for.  Let's  start with parking or lack there of.  Yes there  is  a  parking garage & yes there is valet. The problem with valet is the long lines upon arrival & even longer lines on departure.
Next the bathrooms are all located below the first floor. The women's  room at intermission  is impossible! Any time you must go, you miss more of the performance  than you would like. They are packed & overflowing, always!
There are too few bars and few places to sit or even stand without getting jostled down to a quarter of the drink you overpaid for, in plastic. Who wants to be in formal wear involuntarily  spilling a drink in a plastic cup?
Now the posative  definitely outweigh  the negatives.   Quality performances from symphonies  to satires & everything in between. Yes, we have CULTURE, alive and well in Tampa, Florida.
Seating, staff, ease of purchase & volunteers  are all exceptional  and stellar.
Pricing is fair. Seats are good, wherever you set, but I am partial to the orchestra  section myself & pricing goes along with seat selection and whether  it is a Mata née  or evening, weekend  preformance.
Overall I am thrilled with the Starz & proud to be a patron. Tampa needed something Similar  to Starz, but Starz really does do it all & they do the very best they can!
What's  a  girl  to  do?  It is so nice to get all dressed up, do a pre fixe dinner & enjoy a masterful operah.

This is my go to place for piercing. Every piercing I've had here was perfect. I've had my nose, tongue, 2 tragus and horizontal eyebrow. I've had all the guys who pierce there and they all are friendly, clean and efficient. I was extremely nervous to get an oral piercing (tongue) and they were reassuring and quick. I will return here for all my piercing needs.

I was apprehensive because of its location, I admit it.  But this was some delicious Greek food!  Pricing was reasonable.  I sat at the bar and had a glass of wine while I waited for my friend.  When we got a table, it was cozy and fun, like being in someone's basement.

The entrees were delicious.  Good salads, stuffed grape leaves.  I was pleasantly surprised.
Does my update add on to my original review? 4 stars since the staff has always been friendly. Sushi and sashimi was on point. My takoyaki was delicious. Hope to come back for "omakase."

The AYCE Sushi wave that's run through the area is really falling flat. It's basically fake crab and salmon and shrimp tempura put together in 25 ways. Not as interesting or inventive as other places.

I miss real sushi.

Loved everything we had  
38 Luck luck*

44 Smokey tempura roll*

Spicy girl


49 Kevin's signature *

And a few others...

Yep. Perfect wait service! Idaho nachos divine! Steak cut not amazing, bit bland. But no means terrible! Shrimp wrap sweet and well textured, nice offset of blackbean soup. Again blackbean soup, more FLAVOR please... Perhaps add pepper? Call pepper blackbean soup. Reccomended wine, excellent. Portions were bring it home to mommy, and your baby! Atmosphere quiet to medium noise, lighting, soft to medium, sports screens available to view. A must stop if in Greenwood! Bathrooms could be more well stock, and interior walkpaths swept more... But it's also a greenhouse ya know? So no judging here. Non alcoholic beverage selections also minimal. Nitty gritty: waitress 10, atmosphere 8.5, food 8.2. Js...
I must admit that I found the Chicken-Stuffed Spud to be incredibly tasty. Definitely worth a try if ever you visit the place in the future.
This Starbucks is the SLOWEST Starbucks known to man! Jesus! I come here often in the mornings because the drive through is so convenient in this area but IT TAKES FOREVER.

Wooo Weee! Yes, the place to come for a seafood boil. Nice blend of Cajun spices in a bag, perfect for a gathering of friends and the diagrams helped this seafood cracking dummy get through it without injury.
