My friends and I ordered beer to be delivered to the restaurant and took lots of pictures with our Foodie group, Philly Foodies! 

It's a winner in my book! Can't wait to go back!

With a name like Repeal 18, you'd think it would play with the speakeasy theme. I was slightly disappointed that it does not. It's not a bar with the facade of a laundromat, or anything like that. I guess I was just looking forward to the secrecy of a traditional speakeasy to live up to the name.

However, it still is a nice chill place with a pool table, dart board, and board games. The bartender was very friendly, which added to the chill ambiance. It was as if she's saying "make yourself at home!" 

Apparently the Old Fashioned's are the thing here, but I don't drink whiskey so I can't speak for that. I did order an Aviator, which is a good "girl's drink."

I've been to many casinos before and this one sucks. It seems like you gotta be a senior citizen to enjoy this place, no offense. But Casinos are usually fun and most tend to throw out to the younger scene but this one really has nothing going for it. The service at the bar is mediocre and the restaurant they have in there isnt so great either. Just steer clear of this place and find yourself something else.

I ate at Stanley after passing it up initially and walking around hopelessly trying to find the right place. I ended up back at Stanley, and I was very glad I did. I had the shrimp and oyster poor boy (not listed as po on the menu). It was served with coleslaw on the sandwich, remoulade sauce, and cocktail sauce. This was the most flavorful po-boy (poor boy?) I've ever had. The batter on the shrimp and oysters was corn mill, which was amazing. The amount of shrimp and oysters given on the sandwich was far more than expected yet fair for the price. 
The downfalls for this place was no side provided with the sandwich, which I suppose is okay because the sandwich is huge. Also, Stanley does not seem to know how to staff appropriately. The shorthanded staff provided what service they could, but it was slow and not what you'd expect in the square. 
I must come back and try breakfast. Pancakes and French toast served with ice cream? Yes please!

The smell is gone!

No longer does this place emulate the Red Wedding episode from Game of Thrones!

The drinks are now as good or better than neighbor 308, (super strong) and the food is delicious. 

I'll be back. Even though I'm ruining the wall around the dartboard.

A friend picked me up as we drive around trying to figure out what we will have for lunch, did a yelp search on the nearest location and Green Eggs Cafe came up.

Went inside it was not very crowded? Server came right away took our drink orders.

The food was delicious I must say

If I could give this place no stars I would. Management was HORRIBLE they threaten me and charged me an extra $200 saying they called the cops for a smoke detector but I could not find a police report and I don't even smoke. I stayed there for two nights and no one knocked or said anything about any smoke detector when I checked out. When I called to see what happened the lady told me I could "come down there and see her" if I wasn't happy.  When I reported this to HR all they said was sorry and they couldn't even me my money back. There were hookers and parties on every floor. It's a very unsafe and unsanitary hotel. I would not recommend to my worst enemy
Overall, the restaurant was alright. The lunch specials are nice but it's not worth the price. If I were to go here again I would just stick to the soups

I used to love Panera which I don't know why. The only thing I constantly order from Panera is their medeteranian egg white breakfast sandwich but a couple of months ago, they changed the recipe. It used to have sundried tomatoes. Now, it has seasoned chopped tomatoes with maybe sundried tomato paste. Either way, Panera isn't all that.

It's super expensive for what you get. Sure, it might be a healthier alternative than others but there are so many other alternatives like Newks which serves the same type of food. Better tasting and better price for the amount of food that you get.

This location specifically is right next to Vanderbilt so it is constantly busy especially during lunch. The workers work fast and hard though so you really don't have to wait that much long. Somehow, even with that many people, there are also always tables available so it's not that bad.

Julie

So I've been here a couple of times for dinner and it's always packed and the food is decent with lots of variety.  Drinks are pretty steep on prices but ur paying for atmosphere.   

I came here to avoid the lines at all the little quaint brunch spots around town that serves awesome french toast but makes you wait on line over an hour like Sam's Morning Glory.....   And just as i thought, the Continental was empty for Brunch.  And it was a great experience.    

I went with the Tofu skillet.  It's soft tofu in a light curry sauce mixed with zucchini and some other veggies.  Small slices of pita surrounds the bowl and the portions are big enough for a large dude.  I thought it was just delicious and exactly what i was looking for.  Ordered some
Turkey sausage on the side but will pass on those in the future.  The sausage skin became chewy in my mouth and the taste was awkward and dry.

My mate had the Eggs Benedict with smoked salmon.  Very good and no fishy taste to the salmon.  Sides of potatoes were a good compliment.  

The service was great and the coffee cups were always topped.  It was such a pleasant surprise and I would definitely consider coming back to try other offerings on the menu.

This was my second time here. I love the energy and the bands they tend to have. If you sit outside try to get a spot away from the entrance because the smoke from cigarettes can liger strongly. They sat us at a small table which was surprising because we were a larger group. I was hoping for some raw clams but it seems most are cooked. I don't know the price of clams but 6 for the price we paid seemed a little high. The food was good but the music was better!

My AC fails right when the temperature reaches double digits. I call horizon to schedule an appt. They say they can come out in about a day and a half...fine, i can live with that. When they get to my house, the technician says they don't service rooftop AC units. Nothing like sitting around for a company that has limitations in what kind of jobs it will take. Would it have been so hard to ask, "Mr. Martin, can you tell me where in your house your AC unit is located?"..? I'll take my business to a company that uses common sense, and isn't afraid of a ladder.

Four of us were in for hair cuts and while everyone was very friendly and all four of us had no complaints over the cuts, it's was over priced for what it actually was.  The aesthetic of the place leaves a bit to be desired as well.  I won't recommend the place based on price.
The place seemed out of sorts. Staff was confused & food was not on point like the original location.

I've been eating here for about 25 years.  Says something about any restaurant to be around that long.  They have had their ups and downs and I believe right now they are up.  
Food is fresh. Prices reasonable. Portions generous. 
Crab Rangoon is a must order. Delicious and you can see bits of crab inside. My favorite right now is Empress Beef or moo shu pork (no pancakes/wraps). Skip the hot braised chicken as it's over breaded and over sweet. 
Clean restaurant.  Fast service...but definitely not fast food.

Sushi is great!! Servers are strange,, not very friendly.  Dine here 2 times a week to get my sushi fix and you'd think I was a stranger .  Guess that's ok, but nice to be acknowledged. Oh well.. sushi's good.

After driving our camper van from California, it began having some serious coolant issues, so we brought it to this location for repairs.  I had so much anxiety about what the potential problem would be, this van is my baby & at the time, my main residence.  

I honestly cannot praise the people we dealt with here enough.  Angela was incredibly kind & informative about the matters at hand.  Jason went above & beyond to make sure we were taken care of, and even helped us out with some tips for apartment hunting in the area.  Anna was very personable, making the whole process a smooth one.  

The repairs took about a day & a half, considering what a nightmare it was under the hood when we brought it in it was surprisingly quick.  And it's been running great ever since, 5 months later.  They definitely made me feel like they put a lot of care into their work & made sure it was in good shape before releasing it.  

The whole experience made me grateful to have gotten a AAA membership & made me wish I had found a place to live closer so I could come back to this specific location anytime I needed to.

We are happy. Sampled potato latke, sour cream  biscuit,  local zucchini and green bean salad, sourdough pearl sugar waffle and the Milktooth double. All were wonderful and I would have all of them again!  My husband learned that just because you can't pronounce it doesn't mean you won't like it.

They were quick to serve, and receiving the car was easy as pie with a bright and accommodating representative. Returning was a little less smooth, with an unfocused counter person keeping me waiting, but my mood completely turned around with the shuttle driver. She was a retired school bus driver, pleasantly conversational, and dropped me at the Marriott Hotel (I was staying for an EARLY flight out)! Such nice service from her at the end of a long day.

Calling this place the "Last Hurrah (hoorah)". 
I literally felt like I was going to enjoy staying in a hospital after major surgery after staying here the first night. Feeling homesick literally is an understatement. Had to stay for 3 nights due to a volleyball tournament. 

The Ugly Truth:
Walked in the room and it stunk like a convalescent home. 
Had to wear flip flops at all times in this room.
Popcorn ceilings.
Paper thin walls, could hear both rooms next to us complete conversation. Not fun at 1am and you're getting up at 5am. But payback is a you know what! 
Elevators had smashed in and or missing floor buttons. 
Carpets in hallways were so dirty, your imagination starts to scare you thinking about what the stains could be.
The bathroom. The tub/shower had a permanent stain covering where you stand. Porcelain tub chipped and looks like it was repaired with Liquid Paper. The toilet would shoot a geyser at your head if you forgot to close the lib before flushing. I know......EWWWWWWE! 
It gets worse, I remembered I was paying them to live like this for 3 nights/days. They should be paying people to stay there! 
My room overlooked a gorgeous view of a marijuana dispensary place and a shady alley way where Im pretty sure I witnessed several drug deals take place and bums urinating on the wall. 
Pretty sure I have PTSD.
Oh and if you think you're going to walk around this hotels surrounding area at night safely.....you're not. Not a good area at all.

Positive Things:
Close to the river walk.
Has running water in each room.
There is light.
A/C worked well.
White sheets.

If you're reading this and you're smart then you'll do yourself a favor by staying at the Atlantis, Peppermill or Grand Sierra Resort or actually better yet, stay away from Reno. Thanks Harrah's put the worst taste possible in my mouth.

Costed more than double than what was estimated, the people in the office are rude and unprofessional, and they damaged furniture in storage. Of course, they have a scam: They mark your furniture as damaged (with codes that you don't notice) before they store. So now, I'm trying to claim that a desk bought this year in perfect condition was damaged, but they had previously marked it as damaged. I saw they marked a new carpet as "soiled." Ummm, but it isn't. Eff them. Seriously. My fault. Didn't do my research. We were in a crunch. Never again.

A 45+ minute wait for food, when the items you're waiting for are soups, appetizers and a main dish that requires no cooking, bespeaks trouble. My dad managed a nap while I learned about the entire history of the Legal Sea Foods business and my conversation with my mom eventually lapsed into silence and emphatic blinking. 

The pacing here needs work. The modern decor makes for ambient dining. Food on the whole was tasty + fresh, and service is professional and borderline friendly depending on who you get.

Summary of dishes:
They comped us for the long wait with a plate of clams--well seasoned, hearty base, probably my favorite of the night. 
The crab cakes were too dry and a bit chewy. 
The new england clam chowder--pretty excellent.
The Maryland crab soup--too fishy and over-flavored.
The treasures of the reef--fresh and good with interesting side condiments.

Consistently delicious!! Amazing Sunday brunch. Lobster avocado toast insanely tasty! Love this place. Always yummy!!

Dinner tonight was the Halibut. YUMMMMMM! It's always consistent. I love it.

We went on a brewery tour and the only downfall was the guy who ran the tour wasn't personable! It was very clear that he didn't want to be there, he asked us when we bought our tickets for the tour because he wasn't expecting anyone for a 4:00 PM tour. It was apparent he was caught off guard and he was very quick with the tour!!!

Didn't surprise me.  For a $18 burger.  I was expecting a juicy patty with the cooked way I asked for. But my patty was dry and not in quality at all.   The meat tastes like some frozen patty from a market.  I asked for Pink,  but my patty was almost over cooked.   Burger doesn't have much flavors.  

Have two stars for the fries.  fries were good!

I'm not a hyper-critic, and generally don't like giving weak reviews (I'd rather just keep my mouth shut in those cases). But I was profoundly disappointed by my experience here--maybe because I wanted it to be a really good experience--it it fell way short of the mark.

The good news first: Very lovely and accommodating staff. 

The bad news: The food was terrible. Pinefish was promoting "LobsterFest" so we decided to go with the flow and try that. The dishes (we ordered two different kinds: lobster tail and grilled whole lobster) were mealy, tough, and felt as if they were dragged out of the deep freezer from last year. The only redeeming part of the plate: very tasty grilled corn.  We also ordered duckfat fries as a side. Thank goodness for that. They were delicious. 

We paid a lot for a meal for two. On Walnut, Passyunk, or in Fishtown, the price we paid would have produced a diner's delight. We got indigestion. 

Sorry to rag on this place. Maybe they were having an off night, Or maybe we should have stuck to their traditional menu.

Completely understand they are understaffed I assume due to the aftermath of COVID-19. Seeing an employee vaping in the bathroom and taking their time was annoying. It took 35 minutes to be seated, over an hour to get our food. Then when we were ready to leave. It took another 20 minutes to get our check and pay. They were not at all packed or full so this was very disappointing.

I have gotten services there before.  However, recently I was disappointed with my haircut experience.  I chose a cut and style with someone in their downstairs salon.  She had a sign posted that she will be getting a promotion soon and her rates will increase.  I don't know if I agree.

I had planned to have my hair cut and possibly donate it.  However, I needed my hair washed and dried before the cut and thought since I am already paying for going to the salon, they would do it.  Then she could just wet the hair enough to style it.  However, my stylist said she would not have time to do all that and then style it.

Not only that, we seemed to be having communication problems.  Once, she showed me how much is 8 inches.  So, I told her to cut 11 inches off, but I did not verify her planned length before she cut it.  I measured and took pictures the ponytails later at home.  The ponytails were mostly 12 inches and one 13 inch.  WHAT!!

Again not only that, she told me it will cost me an extra $12 to style so I am thinking I would have to pay about $40 for this job.  I told her I was quoted a price of $28 total.

So I learned two lessons from this visit.  Wash and dry my hair before a donation and a cut at a salon.  Also, say you want less hair cut off and verify the length before the actual cut.
Everything was great I had the braised duck and loved it I just wish they would've given me chopsticks for my leftovers

I'll start at the very beginning of my transaction with this company. I went online to book an appt. the service was awesome because I could multi task while scheduling a cleaning. That's about the only good thing about this company I can tell you. So I booked my appt and the next day I get an email saying sorry we can't schedule you, can we squeeze you in the following week. So I called the company as I specified in our interaction that this cleaning was for a party and I'd be out of town the following week. I spoke with Jane she informed me that one of her staffers grandparent died and the entire office would be out ... This struck me as odd since my appt wasn't for 3 weeks. So I asked that she have the supervisor call me. I waited about two hours and called back. I was upset at this point because I had bought the yipit deal and was concerned about my loss of money. I spoke with Jane again I'm sure I wasn't at all pleasant. I informed her that I wanted a refund ASAP and that I had never heard of a funeral taking place nearly a month after a death and that I was extremely upset. She paused and told me there had been a mistake on their end and she thought my appt was for the next day. She assured me that I would be able to keep my appt. I asked to have the supervisor call me. About  3 hours later I got a call from Chris he said he was the owner of the establishment. He apologized for the mix up and once again assured me they would " take care of me". Fast forward 3 weeks . It's 8 am the time of my appt. I called the office because I wanted to make sure they were on their way as I had to take my dog to the kennel at a certain time. Jane was once again her pleasant self( sarcasm) and informed me they had a half hour window and she would call them. Less than a minute later they showed up. My optimism was restored. The women were polite and asked me if I wanted anything focused on.  I informed them that the cobwebs, dusting and baseboards were most important. I left the house. About 1.5 hours later I returned . They were finishing up. The house looked decent. Not spectacular not awful but decent. They had spilled hot wax from a candle on my side table which they informed me about. So I paid them and they left. I started decorations for my party and that's when I started noticing they hadn't dusted at all! They hadn't cleaned the cobwebs. They did do a good job on the baseboards. I took pictures and sent this text to Chris "Your girls just left. Had I looked at this before they walked out the door I wouldn't have paid them. They asked if I wanted anything focused on I told them dusting, cobwebs and the baseboards. My review will go back up. " ( he asked me to take my prior review down because he didn't want it to negatively affect a new business)  he then sent me this text "Look ma'am I understand you're looking to give us a negative review, so it was inevitable you were going to find something wrong. I'm asking you to just leave it be if you're unhappy with the cleaning we can do a reclean for you in the future. Overall we do a great job and its human error missing small spots. No one was late and I know the team that went to your home is a great team. So I'm just asking you to not leave any review and if you want to use another service in the future that's fine " ummm not at all what I wanted to hear. So I told him I was leaving the review and that I really didn't want his crew back in my home. So all in all a pretty awful experience . I'm currently I'm currently cleaning the places they missed. I won't recommend  them  and I won't be using them again.



Update:: after posting this review , I received 5 very long texts from the owner Chris. The texts implied that I was trying to ruin his business and my review was unfair because " I was looking for something wrong" I informed him that yelp is for consumers not business owners and that my review wasn't personal it was based on a paid for service. Chris continued to send me harassing texts and i continued to ask him to stop. i finally I had to pull up the yelp contract business agree to. Quote a by law regarding owners interactions with consumers and contact yelp for further assistance. Then finally the texts stopped. I'm writing this update because this guy seems to take serious issue with anyone who he thinks is trying to. Hurt his business . He needs to realize that texting harassing messages to a woman when you have all of her personal information and have just been in her home crosses so many lines and in general is just bad business. 

RUN dont walk in the opposite direction of Chris and his crew at check maid !!!

This venue is absolutely magical! We had our reception here and Liz and LaurieAnne could not be any sweeter! They were so welcoming of us and being a gay couple that is sometimes hard to find. We had excellent service throughout the entire process and then day of could not have been better! Our guests loved everything! We had a courtyard ceremony, cocktail hour in the carriage house, served brunch in the house, then dancing in the carriage house. I was worried about having guest spread out but everyone really loved it! Our guests enjoyed the beautiful architecture of the house and the staff from MBP made all of our guests feel like MVP! We had a larger wedding for the venue but we loved it it made everything so much more personal and romantic. I would for sure recommend this venue for any event but it does make for a BEAUTIFUL wedding and you won't find better care anywhere else!!

The beer is good - look for it but that is not why I am writing here today folks, I am reviewing the tour. 

Upon driving into the brewery you get a sense that you are in the wrong place and this is a one-man operation. It is the only agriculture-based brewery in (nation, state, you get the point) and is located in a beautiful setting. You begin in the upstairs of a house that has a porch where you can do the tasting. They provide local honey, crackers, and cheese for you to eat also. There was a big crowd for this tour and we were excited....

After a little over an hour my party decided to leave early. Yes, after an hour we were leaving early. I can't say how long it would have gone over that but the man just wouldn't stop talking. Don't get me wrong, I liked that the founder was giving us information and being detailed but there is a difference between detail and just rambling. He repeated himself over and over and even answered his phone during the tour. The beer kept flowing, which kept people's interests but we were hungry and  ditched the rest for some Pizza by Elizabeth's. We weren't the only ones losing interest.

Overall, go at your own risk. I can't say whether or not it is always like that because no one else has reviewed. For $22 I can't say whether or not I got my money's worth. I was the non-drinker of the group and when we asked for a discounted price she knocked it down to $10. It was nice to see and if he would have just kept to the main points it would have been great. It was hands-on - we got to taste the grain, they were examining the hops when we left, and it was intimate.
My first time there and the food, service and free margarita's were fabulous.  Liked it so much we are taking a bunch of friends this weekend.

Walked in for dinner with sissy && hubbub. Hostess was just mahh.. A bit rude.
This Little Tokyo is pretty spacious && very nicely built. The environment seems pretty fun for large groups. They have a very looooooong sushi bar as well. 
&& the menu... Oh my goodness....! The size of a newspaper! Plenty of options for vegans / vegetarians.  
Server: attentive, no complaints there.

FOOD;

$9 ~ Seafood yakiSOBA~ a seafood stir fry! good to share between 2-3 people. You get the option of choosing yakisoba, which is the thinner noodles && the yakiudon, the fatter noodles. Seafood Yakisoba came with salmon, shrimp, & veggies. Plenty of food, but way over salted! I will probably get the yakiudon next time.

$8 ~ Squid Steak ~ I absolutely LOVE the squid steak from Sushi Brothers && the flavor at Little Tokyo was again overly salty, but the size of the squid steak here definitely defeats Sushi Brother's by A LOT . Quite worth the buck size wise! ask for a side of rice to calm the saltiness down && a side of the eel sauce!!

~$5.25 ~ Spicy Edamame~ very different from the other spicy edamame I've had that were always tossed in sriracha.. the edamame here is tossed in what seems to taste like a SWEET chili sauce. [ask for sriracha for a kick]

~$6.75 Spicy Crawfish Taco ~ there's a picture on the menu of this.. It's pretty much 2 huge balls of crab meat & crawfish topped w/ a pickled okra & placed on top of a crunchy sort of chip. There was nothing spicy about it...,but was pretty tasty && can be shared with 3 people! 

[ starting to think the spices are off at this point ]

~$4.95 Vegetable Spring Rolls ~ 5 little FRIED eggrolls served w/ a side of sweet chili sauce over a bed of lettuce...Super super SUPER crunchy little things..! Warning..Very Chewy&Crunchy. [ cautious ]

[ FINALE; This Little Tokyo whips up the meanest sushi! ]

~$14 Po-Boy Roll ~ so delish! A roll filled w/ fried soft shell crab, crawfish, shrimp, lettuce, && some other items I can't remember... Packed tight in some sort of delish soy wrapping .. Way too busy stuffing my face...JUST ORDER IT! ..

~ Crunchy Roll ~ ordered this classic roll & was surely not disappointed! Declared the best crunchy roll I ever had! Everything was super fresh... For sure high quality ingredients. [get it made with Brown rice!]

- All in all.. had a great time..private lot w/ pretty limited parking . Very very great for big groups... Pretty cheap for the quality && portions served! I definitely definitely recommend coming here for the sushi though.. I find the other Little Tokyo's are more pro w/ the hot dishes! Yes, credit cards accepted. Yes, handicap accessible! Yes, large groups are welcomed. && family/kid friendly.

I give them 3 stars because their place is nice, but they don't know Asian food.  I ordered this Korean bbq dish with ribs that came with Kim Chee.  Not only did they forget it on the dish, I had to remind them, as it was written on their menu, but when they finally brought it out it was microwaved.  Kim Chee is served chilled unless it's stir fried into something. Disappointing!  Also I'm Asian and offended.
This is the 2nd set of dentures I've had made by this company both  Sets cracked now they want  $80 to repair . My advice shop elsewhere

I will never forget this walmart experience because they found and returned my wallet when I called and asked them if they had a wallet in their lost and found. Lately I've been feeling all over the place so I'm super glad that they have great employees who turned in my wallet. It honestly could have been lost or someone stole some money or something but my wallet was returned with everything in it. Thank you guys so much!

While my 20-something significant other and I are nowhere near elderly, we certainly came out curmudgeon-y after staying here.

I suppose when you pay $80 a night, you shouldn't be expecting too much. And you're probably right if that's what you're thinking. Normally, I'd be inclined to agree with you.

However, as someone who has worked in the hospitality industry (as a housekeeper for a pro-football team, at that)--I'm disappointed in this Ramada. The room is large enough, I suppose, but three strikes you're out, Ramada:

1. The shower: I get it. Florida has a lot of elderly tourists. I know the jokes and all, but why make a shower so difficult to work with and difficult to adjust the temperature? The water is scalding and it's almost impossible to make it otherwise unless you only want a small jet of water.

2. The television. I don't think there's an excuse for having old, non-working television sets anymore. TVs are generally pretty affordable nowadays. The fact that ours couldn't display red or greens without malfunctioning tells me the owners are lazy about checking the rooms and whether certain things work or not for their customers' benefit.

3. Here's where I know they're lazy about checking rooms: the towels AND sheets both had a significant amount of dust on them. This would be unacceptable at any other hotel and it's unacceptable for customers. Had we done something like this where I worked, we wouldn't have had the football team's future business.

And so, with that, Ramada lost my business. Laziness is not rewarded in the hospitality industry.

P.S. If you want to claim you're trying to be environmentally friendly, how about you work on sealing the doors a little better so the A/C doesn't have to run so much?

Awesome nail salon! Highly recommended! Thai and Hanna, the owners are just so nice and very very good! They have the greatest variety of dipping colors in town, and they always are super accommodating, even when they have wedding parties or large groups coming in. They know how to treat their long time customers with care and respect! Kenny is the best with pedicures! I love getting pampered here! A+++

Pleasantly surprised by their delicious food. Heralding from Texas, I'm especially picky when it comes to Mexican food, and Casa Romero delivers great food at a great price. Everything seems made from scratch and fresh. Definitely a must visit place for those craving that Mexican flare, in the Waterloo area. I'll be back!

The service was prompt and attentive and we were surprised at how quickly the lineup to get in was processed. We sat upstairs and the setup allowed sufficient room at the table was quiet enough for conversations.  The food was quite good- not the best we had in New Orleans but very tasty and not a single bad dish ordered between the four of us.

Burgers are awesome! But wow - I was literally done eating my burger before my beer came out. There were only 3 other tables & I specifically went to the counter to ask for them. Management doesn't care. I want my 20% tip back SMH

I ordered the meat pie and the Bao.  Both were tasty and very fresh.  

The prices were reasonable for how much food is served.

I would be willing to come back and try more items on their menu.

To be clear, this is a great club. Clean, nice facilities, and I found most employees to be courteous, helpful, etc. However, I had a bad CONTRACT experience here and I want to make sure it's heard.

When I signed my 2 year contract, I wasn't even thinking about whether or not the club had a pool. As it turned out, it didn't. And in fact, it's missing a few other accommodations that I've come to appreciate (this is where it's important to know exactly what interests you & is going to make you successful). After a couple of months, I realized that missing the pool was a big factor for me. I stopped going to CHHRC and started going to another club. 

After 5 months, I called up in good faith, attempting to cancel my contract. I knew the mistake was my own, and I knew I'd get hit with *some* kind of fee. I politely asked how much. Maybe 1 month? 2 months? 3? No... They wanted me to pay for the next 7 months (1 full year) in order to terminate the contract. Of course, I could use the club for the next 7 months, but that wasn't of much help to me. So to be clear, they intended to make $50/mo off of me, for 12 months straight, even though I had only used the facilities for the first 1-2 months. They called this a 12 month discount (letting me out of my contract 12 months early). They called it their legal right. I guess it is a "discount". And I suppose it's legal. Whether or not it's good customer service & practice... you be the judge.

So unfortunately, rather than leaving on a positive note, recommending the club to friends, etc, I am leaving on a bitter note. The club has basically communicated that they have no sympathy for somebody who makes mistakes (unless of course you consider my "generous 50% discount" a sympathetic gesture).

Bottom line for me... DO NOT sign a contract here unless you are 100% sure it's the club for you. DO NOT be fooled into thinking they are family & customer friendly on the CONTRACT side. They will not let you out of your contract easy.

Dan David Hernandez Jr. Bethlehem Pa.
Look up this case! I paid John Mc Mahon $15,000 To Represent My Husband in an Attempted Homicide Trial... He Did NOTHING at All, But take All Our Money and Build A Relationship with The Northampton County Prosecutor Patricia Mulqueen. He Pretty Much Used My Husband as a Bargaining Chip to Get In Good with the county. We are going through a direct appeal to try to fix what this bozo did (didn't) Do. The lawyer On My Husbands Case Now says, Never in his Career as an attorney has he seen an attorney do as little as hes done, for a client..things that should have been preserved to help my husband in a direct appeal he didn't do, so that his appeal would be unsuccessful!! I hate this man for ruining our lives and taking all of our money. DO NOT Trust THIS FIRM!!!! look up my husbands case before you have this crook represent you or your loved one!! God Doesn't  like ugly John!! I Pray he deals with you for cheating us out of thousands!!

Should be called the Unfortunate Palace.
We ordered take out last night 30$ dollars worth of Garbage.
"Call the food police I've been robbed!"

Maybe they havearnew owners? I Had been looking forward to eating here after the reviews I read. We ordered fried prawns, pot stickers, veggie egg foo yung, and pork fried rice.

PRAWNS: these were panko breaded small shrimps crispy but overcooked and rubbery texture on the shrimp I'm sure they were prepackaged frozen product.
Then I opened the......

 VEG FOO YUNG.   "Oh my god I sighed" ...are you kidding me? 
they were perfectly round 1/2 inch thick discs and I mean round I'm not sure they were even made with eggs and  there were some soggy bean sprouts inside. Apparently, all you need to do is microwave the frozen patty and add some frozen veggies to your basic bland thickened chicken stock and "voila" You've got veg foo yung  Oh PLEASE! you couldn't even beat an egg?
Does Mc Donald's make egg foo yung molds now

POTSTICKERS:  Definitely not the best frozen pre-packaged stickers I've ever had, 6 of these beauty's. We ate two each, by the time we got to the 3rd one we were chewing on sponge. Had to toss the last two. The dog wouldn't eat them. 
The dipping sauce could have been sweeter. Tasted only vinegar & soy. 

PORK FRIED RICE:  Overcooked, mushy rice, typical frozen peas and carrots, not enough egg (SURPRISE!) and bland. 
There was a good supply of pork and that part was actually OK.

I'm not surprised there was nobody was in the place I would be embarrassed  to be seen in here now I know why the people at the bar were wearing masks.
This place is way overpriced for the quality oops Crap they serve. I can microwave a better meal with the stuff from the freezer at my local Asian grocery. Go to Panda even theirs is better.
We normally get our Chinese at CJ or shanghai  both Very mediocre at best, said I was done with them but now I have to go back since at least they cook their food from scratch.

OMG. The noise level is ridiculous. Food is pub fare standard. A little pricy. Not sure why but parents who allow their kids to scream, yell and throw things seem to love this place. Still don't understand why the restaurant doesn't address parents behaving badly. No need to go back. Once is enough.

I was in the mood for breakfast and was on a different side of town,  decided to try something new. The menu was varied and had plenty of unique yet healthy items to choose from.   I had the day glow juice which was light and delicious. And the the avacado, bacon and tomato hash with my eggs over easy.  Food was fresh,  flavorful and presented well. It came quickly and the service was really good. I'll be back for sure.

Stopped by with 4 adults and a toddler Saturday night and were seated quickly. I think I've been here before but did not recognize the interior, so maybe not.

Tried the carnitas plate myself and was satisfied. The portion size was good and the meat was juicy. Rice was surprisingly less dry than I had anticipated. Beans were beans.

Wife had the appetizer combo plate. Most was stock catered style Mexican snack fare, but the little fried tamale balls (masa + meat + chile + fry) were interesting. They did not hold up very well as leftovers, however.

Kid had a bean burrito and grapes. It was a bean burrito and grapes.

All told, we were satisfied. The service was good, the food was good. The price was a bit higher than the quality warranted, but not onerously so.

The good: nice outdoor patio, good scones
Mediocre: okay coffee
Bad: flat, heavy beignets; paper cups even when saying "for here," expensive workout the quality to back it up

I ate half a beignet and gave the other two to a homeless man on my way back to hotel. If you want beignets, much better to go to Cafe du Monde. Otherwise, an okay breakfast option for scones. Omelets looked fine.

Delivery. Straightforward, tummy-stuffing chinese counter. Other reviews' pricing not accurate for dinner delivery. Paid $20 for full order of general's chicken with crab rangoon with delivery tip. comes with steamed rice; TONS OF FOOD. typical communication difficulties with phone order; food came when promised and was hot! perfect to satisfy the i'm-not-leaving-who-is-fine-seeing-me-in-my-PJs-with-pinot-grigio-in-my-hand-craving that I only get once a year or so.

First time here and had to wait 30 mins for our food! We tried calling ahead but the line was busy. When we got there, we mentioned their phone being busy. They told us that they took it off the hook on purpose! (Not sure why they would do that). 
My fiancé had the seafood cheesesteak, and I had the dungeness & shrimp platter with veggies. The "veggies" consisted of frozen broccoli & corn! Definitely not worth the $27 they charge! The crab was so-so. 
We won't be back.

I have used this salon on various occasion for many years so I have seen the good, the bad & the ugly!

My regular stylist, Christine E., is wonderful, friendly and very professional. I have been using her for 3+ years and never not liked a color or cut (ok, only once but she fixed it to my satisfaction w/out any hesitation).  I have had my makeup done for numerous weddings by Melissa(more natural) and Robert (dramatic and wow!) and updo's by Angel (awesome & completely in synch w/the latest trends).  I regularly get waxed by Gallena who is also fast (especially w/those bikini waxes) & professional.  The spa is hit or miss, but when you get Tanya for a deep tissue massage, you won't regret it. Appreciate Spa week in fall & spring allowing me to take advantage of some services I otherwise wouldn't.  Unfortunately, the salons flaw seems to be with the front desk that is every changing, not organized and doesn't communicate well with the staff.  Pricing could be alittle more reasonable but for this area, it is competitive.  

Thanks to those who do their work w/pride and expertise!

Can't wait for this place to get shut down.. puppies should be socialized not locked up in tiny glass boxes without any toys. And that's only from what we see and we have no idea what goes on in the back. Owners are horrible people and only see these dogs as a profit. Don't get scammed by these over priced sick pups and do not give these people your money. Sister store has already been shut down and this place will be next. 

Also.. they don't let you take pictures.. I wonder why?? Could it be because these poor pups are left in 4x2ft glass cases sitting in their own poo and pee? No toys or anything??? Hm.

I love the location of this small and local coffee shop/quick breakfast spot. If you are craving something sweet and somewhat heavy to start your day, This is the place way to go. The Chicken and Waffles were so great and delicious. Huevos Rancheros at the same time. I cannot say this enough but it is deliciously perfect! Sabrina's Café definitely serves a good portion to fill you up. I highly recommend and if you're ever looking for somewhere to eat to start your day.

My boyfriend and I love pizza.  Its not something we take lightly.  Having said that, this place has the potential to be the best in town. 

We LOVE their pizza.  You want fresh pizza made with care and an eye for ingredients?  You really cant do better in Reno/Sparks.  The dough is fluffy and perfect.  The sauces are amazing.  The veggies are fresh and crisp, and all of the ingredients, married up with their sauces, work perfectly.

That being said, this place needs......tweaking.  Three words:  UNIFORMS, SERVICE, PROFESSIONALISM. Most employees are walking around like they just left class from the high school down the street.  There is no differenciating who an employee is or who a customer is.  Uniformity would really add to the cohesiveness.  This place isn't a small joint on Victorian anymore.  Its a chain, and recognized as one.  Start showing the professionalism...

Next:  SERVICE, SERVICE, SERVICE.  We've never been okay with sitting at a bar and waiting 20 minutes with an empty couple of glasses in front of us.  If you are in the area, scope out your bar.  Notice.  Its not that hard on a Monday when its totally dead. Be PROFESSIONAL.   If you work here, let the other employees serve you.  Don't show up, off shift, looking like a gang banger and walk behind the bar and fill your own drinks.  It looks VERY tacky and incredibly unrefined.   We like dive bars in this city, but we don't expect this place to be one of them. 

This place has MOUNDS of potential.  The food is literally incredible.  Imagine how amazing the PLACE would be if it compared....

I reached out to your customer service team already earlier but I wanted to express my disappointment again with this company. I decided to return the coffee table I ordered and I was told over the phone that unlike what it says on the return policy, no one would pick up the item I purchased. The customer service rep also said I'd be charged over $200 for returning the item, I'd have to take the item myself to UPS AND that UPS might charge an additional cost. I realize I'd have to pay shipping for the heavy item but to not even know the total I'd have to pay is ridiculous and to be expected to take the item to UPS myself when it explicitly says in the return policy that it would be picked up... I don't even know why it's written especially after reading more reviews on Goedekers and seeing that this happens often. I read the terms and conditions that I agreed to as well as the return policy and the terms don't even say anything about returns! I realize that on the product detail page i'm now reading that this item can't be returned. This information cannot be buried and also it says nothing of final sales on the terms or return policy. That is not right. I had to dig to find the terms and conditions and privacy policy isn't even on the footer of your site which is also not right! You're leaving me with no choice but to keep a coffee table that is actually bigger than the measurements specify. This site is NOT PCI compliant nor is it even abiding simple ecommerce standards. So much information that should be written and obvious and it isn't. I cannot express enough my disappointment with this company and I do take responsibility as there are many poor reviews that I should've read before but that is no excuse for not being clear with your customers.

Well, I was wavering.  You see, I like diners ... Places where almost any combination of foods especially if fried can end up on a plate in front of you

Every diner has purchased the same water glasses.  Every diner has a long list of lunch or dinner specials.  And, every diner has food items you can't find at any reputable restaurant like stewed tomatoes and beets just beets.

So I found myself unexpectedly in front of the Harrison House, a diner whose sign boldly proclaims "best of" so like a piece of cheap tin pulled in by a powerful magnet I was seated before I knew it.

Lots of choices ... Lots of specials and that all too familiar glassware.

Navy bean soup, a diner staple, was my option choice to go along with a crabcake sandwich with fries and coleslaw.

The soup did not disappoint.  It tasted like a decent version of diner navy bean.  The beans had a good texture and the broth was flavorful.

Before I could finish the soup my lunch was delivered.  Fries HOT - Crabcake HOT; coleslaw in a little plastic cup & tartar sauce runny.

The fries were crisp on the outside and hot fluffiness inside.  My crabcake had a nice crisp exterior and the interior of this overly breaded "crab"cake met diner expectations.  Actually an overly breaded crab cake is what diners make; never expect the taste of crab to grab you.  The tartar sauce was surprisingly tasty and the slaw .... Well it was served diner-style in a plastic cup would you really expect a lot from it.

The diner is clean but it does smell like a diner and if you have eaten in your share of diners you will recognize the scent (Perhaps that's what happens when your kitchen presents such a variety of foods.).

I decided to hang around a little because there was one unique phenomenon at the Harrison House that I picked up on immediately and remained consistent during my entire time in my booth.  All of the employees lips are permanently turned downwards.  Not a single person working here smiled except when greeting a customer (if then) but the smiles vanish when they again contemplate their place of employment.  A no smile reality is not a good sign of what happens behind the scenes

So if you are in the neighborhood wanting diner food this place is ok but definitely not worth a long trip down the highway.

And, why no rice pudding with my special??

Been going here for 2-3 years. Always been good service since day one. Have referred Atleast 10 people that have come and gotten systems put in. But went in today and got treated like complete dogshit by a guy named Mikey. Simply asked to look at a loose wire that was all put in by yall and got completely disrespected and cussed out simply because he didn't like the stereo deck I had chosen to have in the car. Sad to say but that might be the last time I'll be there.

I at times would stop in here after being at beauty brands. Never have they disappointed me. But today. Nothing was right.  They were not busy. No excuses.  Potato soup tasted like they used sewer water. Bacon turkey bravo had some sweet sandwich spread and maple flavored bacon. Tuna tasteless.  Hopefully all their energy isn't going to Belleville Crossing. First downtown. Now greenmount.  How about product consistency

Cute place with ideal location to Tradewinds Resort.  I ordered the Brussels sprouts and Risotto Oceana.  I could have easily stopped after the Brussels sprouts and salad before the main entree came.

Brussels sprouts topped with the fried crunchy onions were amazing!!  Nice mix of tangy and salty with the crispy onions.  The salad had this unique salad dressing - a mix between Caesar and Ranch dressing.  Not horrible, but definitely different.  They also pair it with crispy flat bread and roasted red pepper hummus...  I didn't find them exactly ideal pairs, but the crispy bread was tasty.

By the time the entree came, I was definitely slowing down.  Overall the risotto was okay.  It seemed to be lacking seasoning - the shrimp and scallops were good, but the risotto seemed heavy and missing a punch of flavor.

Overall, a decent place if you're looking for a place off resort property.  Not sure if it quite worth the price.

Honest Mechanics do exist in this world and this is one of them!

I've been bringing my car here for a few years and always feel that I'm getting fair, trustworthy, and reliable service. Ed (the owner) is so honest that even after diagnosing an engine problem with my car over a 2 day period he still told me that the repair may be covered under a manufacturer recall. I was so blown away by that type of selfless behavior that I gave him the work regardless. 

All car repairs are going to cost money, no way to get around that, at least here you can have piece of mind that you're not getting the wool pulled over your eyes.

This is a gorgeous space.  If you're looking for somewhere to host your several thousand dollar dream wedding or something like that, then definitely give this place a shot.

I came here for a wine and spirits tasting convention type thing and it was a really lovely place to get a little tipsy.

We recently went here for brunch celebrating a special birthday.    The birthday person, celebrating her milestone, liked it so much when we went in October, that she decided to return.   Certainly a downhill spire since our first visit.  While our server was very nice--in fact she was the highlight of our visit--she had too many tables and it was difficult for her to follow up with us.  Food was fair at best--not one of 9 people had anything nice to say.  Both people who order the pasta only ate half--it was so peppery; sausage and home fries were terrible;  burger ordered med well, and it was rare--not med rare, plain rare--I guess they didn't see that when they cut it half before bringing it to the table; coffee was very weak.  Oatmeal and pastries were good.  4 people ordered ice cream for dessert--3 came and the 4th person was served when we were almost finished our ice cream.  When I sent management a note about how terrible it was, I was given an apology and asked to give them a second chance and to call them so they could help us get a reservation.  The way they are going, reservations will be very easy to come by.  Get a new chef then ask me to try them again.  In speaking with several others, this is the same complaint--great a few months ago, terrible now.

Live down the street from this bdubs. If you plan on ordering for pick up always check. They have screwed up my order almost every time. Take the time to double check before you leave. Can't tell you how many times I have to come back and pick up the rest or correction of my order.
When in Nashville, this is a must. The sandwich was so amazing! The comeback sauce and French fries  were phenomenal!! I can't say enough good things about this place and can't wait to go back!

There wasn't enough space to add all of my review so I selected a few key pieces and compressed them: 

I texted Chrissy the day before we were to arrive home from vacation to make sure everything was ready for our arrival.It was then that she told me the project was not finished and she didn't want to tell me because she didn't want to ruin our vacation.Chrissy assured me that our floors looked gorgeous and that we would love them. We were now prepared for the sight of an incomplete, yet gorgeous floor. When we arrived home, we had a sofa in our kitchen.Our front stair case, which Ted continually referred to as "custom", was in a state of disrepair with a quarter-sized hole in our wall near the first step.The first step was not rounded properly and looked as though someone pressed too hard when shaping the round edge.The first riser was not covered (as expected) yet was a splintered, ripped up mess.The doorway between our main dining room and the kitchen was also quite damaged. Ted let me know that the original trim wrapped around the door jam and he "didn't like that" so he removed it.This left the wood of the door jam, and the edges around it, torn up.They attempted to spackle the damage, but it looked rushed. Ted let me know that they "are not painters" as I walked him and Chrissy through the damage to our house.I was also informed that neither one of them came to see the finished product. Our floors had so many gaps you could see the subfloor. There were nails pointing through to the inside cabinetry where they added bead-boarding. For the repairs, Chrissy told me that they were having the cartons of wood shipped "climate-controlled" and could go directly into our floors upon arrival. (Later, I watched from my office window as Ted drove up with the cartons in the back of his truck.) Ultimately, because of so many flooring issues, I let Ted know that I wanted to file a claim with the manufacturer. He strongly advised against it as it can take a "very, very long time to resolve. IF it gets resolved." I still requested that we start the process. He told me that he would file the paper work but that I needed to be patient because of how long it could take to even hear from the manufacturer. He told me that it could take months. So, I waited. After a month went by with no word, I visited the manufacturer's website where I read that it is their goal to resolve all claims within 30 days. I called them directly and was told that there was no claim on file. With that, I took matters in my own hands, and contacted an inspector that routinely visits homes to review work performed and can determine if the issue is the product or the installation or both.Through this expert review, I learned it was not a product issue, but a workmanship issue.Ted read the report and agreed to come out and look at the kitchen and other areas that needed to be addressed. I showed him the gap in our custom stair case between the riser and first landing. I showed him the repair job he did previously on another gap between a riser and a landing which he admitted did not look good. And, that he must have been "kidding himself" when he thought it was acceptable. He ordered new cartons, had them delivered in advance, and allowed them to acclimate to our home. We agreed on a date and time for his crew to come out and completely re-floor the kitchen (for the third time) and address areas that were still outstanding. This is now February. The job started in August. His crew arrived and began to work. Ted was here initially, but once he left, he never returned to inspect the final results. My wife indicated that a new gentleman was performing the installation, rather than Rob (who performed our first kitchen repair). His work looked amazing. Ted did not address the first gap in the staircase, as he had originally committed. As it stands today, there are still splinters on boards they chose to not replace. Their solution was just to sand and cover with putty or stain. The front staircase is still incomplete and damaged, and the back staircase is still carpeted. During this process, I learned that another customer of Magestic had issues with them. As Ted was leaving one of his visits, he shared again that he's never had this issue and never had a claim. I had finally grown tired of hearing this repeated sales pitch and let him know that though he didn't have claims, he did have unhappy customers. He seemed surprised and said that most complaints are due to customers causing their own problems. We didn't cause our problems, no matter how much Ted wanted to blame us. 

This was an incredibly frustrating six-month long relationship, during which, the once-friendly Chrissy disappeared entirely. People need to be aware of what can happen if they choose to do business with Magestic. 

As a note, this story isn't complete. I just ran out of space.
First experience here. Sushi was delicious and the people making the sushi were personable and accommodating. All good at Yume.

I ordered the Tiki French toast and it was fantastic. The food was really good and the atmosphere was clean and inviting. I came in at 10am on a Monday, expecting good service because the place was empty, unfortunately that didn't happen. I was immediately sat and was offered a cup of coffee which was great. My server sure acted like she didn't want to be there or didn't like her job. No smile, as little conversation as possible and she basically sounded like she barked at me" more coffee,what would you like, a box for that" No thank you what so ever. Even though the food was good the guest service was terrible. Would rather spend my money at a place where my business was appreciated. Will go to First Watch or KeKes next time. Smiles go a long way, I left 20% because that is my minimum.

As other people have said, no chicken is worth waiting over an hour for. We came here late at night hoping it would be better and it was terrible.

Bolton's, Pepperfire and Hattie B's have great hit chicken and won't make you waste your night for it.

Been here a couple of times and really enjoyed it every time.  You can't beat the fried mac n cheese or country fried bacon as apps and the Monon burger was absolutely awesome!!! Definitely worth a try.

I went before to their location the one located in media . But never got chance to go to this one . So I called first and ask if they have buffet in dinner . They said no only in lunch I was little disappointed . But still me and my husband decided to go . We went their dinner time . Food was good not so impressive . Customer service was good . But I wish they have buffet in dinner too so i would have chance to try different dishes . Overall it was good experience .

WORST company ever!!!!  They recently took away all of the realtors lock boxes that were hung on the fence without telling anybody.  Then made it a huge hassle to get them back.  They could have just called the number on the lockbox and asked for them to be removed.  
Any dealings I have had with them they have been rude and unprofessional.  They think they are doing you a favor if you ask them anything when that is what they are being paid for.

I had a repair that needed to be done which was their responsibility to fix and it took a year to get done.  Absolutely ridiculous!!!  
 
I would never hire them if I had the choice!!

I had a good experience at this nail shop. One girl did my manicure and another did my pedicure simultaneously, so I was in and out in no time.  They both talked to me while working and were very personable.  I asked them for their opinion on the color since I was trying something new and they were adament that I go with the purple that had a shimmer to it.  I have gotten a lot of compliments so far on the color.

This is my favorite church I've ever been a part of! It is so easy to feel Gods love and to get involved and start building relationships here. I have made so many friends and grown immensely thanks to this place and its people.

Bread - excellent and fresh as it should be 5/5

Salad - basic 4/5

Fried string beans - this is a pretty basic dish but the addition of the red sauce was pretty good and worked well 5/5

The salmon - the salmon itself was pretty good, tasted fresh. I didn't order the vegetables, this was the choice of somebody else I was dining with.  4/5

Beef short rib over risotto topped with shoestring onion rings- honestly this dish was just overly complicated... It was like two separate dishes were combined into one. The beef short ribs were great (fall off the bone) but then the risotto was overpowering... And then an additional overpowering onion taste from the shoestring onions... 
The items individually were great... Eating this meal I just felt like I didn't want to eat anymore, it was just everything slopped together, which actually ruined the flavor of the individual components.
The dish itself 1/5 

Steak with shrimp on top - the dish just felt like it was slathered and sauce with shrimp on top... And then there was spaghetti mixed into it. Again really not sure what was going on here.
The spaghetti with all the sauce and shrimp could have made a perfect separate dish.
I don't know if I should be upset with myself for ordering a steak at an Italian restaurant in South Jersey, or confused at the multiple dishes pressed into  one... Overall this dish just felt sloppy and unappetizing - 1/5

Manicotti - good shells, good sauce, good ricotta. Very few places actually make manicotti but this was pretty good. 4/5


Cheesecake - not much to say about this it was good 4/5

Cream puffs - very good, pretty sure they make them on premises 4/5

I really want to give this place a 3 but unfortunately those two specials really ruined the visit for me.
I think I need to come back and order classic dishes and reevaluate. 2 stars for now

I have to start out by saying I enjoyed my night at Tria immensely... but this was primarily due to the company and the rediscovery of a great Belgium beer I used to drink in my college days. I'd agree the wine and beer list probably can't be beat, but the small plates left me uninspired. The bruschetta wasn't as flavorful as I would have liked (goat cheese, garlic, and pesto) and I would say the same of the sliced Italian meat plate (Italian meats are usually quite flavorful, but I found this selection bland). The Tuscan bean spread was pretty good, but the texture was odd... it seemed to have cheese in it and was somewhat heavy and greasy, something I don't expect in a bean spread. From the rave reviews, I wonder if I chose the wrong dishes? And I actually think my boyfriend may have liked the food better than I did... anyhow, I'd recommend this place for a drink (especially with groups, large or small) given the selection and the atmosphere (upscale hip inside, small outdoor seating area), but can't recommend it for food given what I tried.
Pearl is a great, family owned, delicious restaurant with a wide variety of food and drinks! I highly recommend the pineapple fried rice and the sweet rice and custard! Yum!

Truly, who would come here twice?  The rude waitstaff and horrible, expensive food are astonishing!  Give your money to a restaurant that wants it.  Autograt on a 2 top when she never came back to the table? Insanity.
Nothing is ever ready on time. It's hard to get help from employees. They're doordash system is horrible

Having been born and raised in Tampa Bern's was always a staple for a great steak, celebrations, and dining with friends.

Just our opinion; success and growth have taken the "edge" off of what we recalled from the "old days". Still good but seemed rushed and, understandable, because of the numbers of people, somewhat pre-fabricated?
Top notch service, fast and affordable ...second time I used this company.

We were greeted by the host and we knew this was going to be a night of amazing service. He was so nice. We made a reservation on opentable.com and everything was ready the minute we arrived. Our server had things to say about the food and gave helpful recommendations. We started with the red pepper soup (with smoked gouda), which could have used a little more salt, but it was good. We then had the gnocchi and the salmon special. Both entrees were delicious. We were amazed with the amount of food that was served! We topped off the night with the warm chocolate torte which was to die for! It was a great meal and we are so happy we came here. It had a great atmosphere and i would definitely recommend this place to anyone visiting!

I love this place. You feel like you're sitting in someone's kitchen or dinning room. Its so warm and inviting. I haven't been here in a while... but I'm dying to go back! perhaps on a date I have coming up. Who knows! But the food is delicious with different touches from around the world... Italian pastas... French Sauces... so much more. However delicious, its not pretentious at all which I love! I feel like i could walk in dressed up or down and not feel uncomfortable. The servers are fun, casual, but polite. Food just amazing. I can't wait to go back!

Mercato is a small restaurant located in center city serving Italian inspired cuisine. The restaurant is closely packed, open kitchen, warm and inviting. It is hard to get a table for a big party but they were very accommodating. I want to go back right now and eat there. The food was delicious, the service was perfect (except for the one who told us off for eating too much butter) and even though the restaurant is tiny and crowded, the ambiance is fun. Read the full review here http://www.phillyhourlunch.com/2015/04/mercato-byob.html

Via my friend Charles: 
"Me n gf ordered a large buffalo chicken pizza from a south philly spot two days ago. Please behold the horror."

Also:
"i would call it a soggy yet burnt chimera a culinary abortion of horror"

The gluten free one btw. What the heck is this monstrosity! Coulda made something more impressive in the microwave.

This is a good sized store for the chain and well stocked with a lot of varying merchandise. Focused on women's fitness they strive for both comfort and support in their clothing designs. 

They have a good mix of pieces for a range of work out styles, such as, yoga, running, biking, etc. They have a really large fitting room which is set in the back and offers great privacy. 

Staff was friendly and quick with tips and tricks, as well as, advise on clothing depending on needs. I am on the task side, so finding pants that fit length wise I'd a struggle. They offer a tall option here and it's really helpful. 

Overall: A
Prices are high but clothing is well made and staff is knowledgeable!

I don't eat beef or pork.  So, I'm always looking for places that offer options.  I tried the turkey burger.  It was fantastic.  My friend had some kind of salad with grilled chicken that looked good.  I did not ask to try it as I was afraid they might want to try my sandwich that I did not want to share.  I will definitely eat here again.

I have been shopping here for the past 6 years. Prices have gone up exponentially!
Its highway robbery to charge the same amount for a thermos mug (used and dinged up) that Walmart is selling brand new. That purchase was last summer.

This weekend though I was furious..... This saturday 11-15-14 i went to check out with my 20+ items (i do this for a living... seriously I buy soo much stuff) and they REFUSED to sell me some of the items! One dress had no tag! I was IN LOVE with it! I found it on the rack with NO TAG and the manager pretty much said that I RIPPED IT OFF AND EVEN GAVE ME A DEMO ON HOW THEY NEVER FALL OFF..... Savers please don't treat your customers like CRIMINALS! Tags do fall off!

Issue #2 
I purchased $14 boots with mouse feces on the inside and totally chewed up and ruined from a mouse making a nest out of the soft wool footpad soles. 
If your going to overcharge your customers at least make sure they dont step in mouse crap when they buy your shoes.

Compared to most thrift stores the turnaround is fast and its pretty clean (except the bathrooms here are always pretty nasty and smelly) I get that they need to charge like any normal company to make money (even though they get it for FREE) So if the items are quality and not ripped or damaged or missing buttons its awesome and i get why they charge more because they are going over items more thoroughly and being choosier.

We can not say enough good things about our stay. We were very excited about our trip to St. Pete as it was going to be our first time. We decided to try staying at an Airbnb. What a disaster. It was out of town and had not been cleaned. As we sat in a park contemplating what to do for our last night, we just could not picture staying another night at this airbnb. So we checked online for a last minute hotel and this little gem had one room left and in our budget. It was as if it was meant to be. We booked it right away and that was the turning point for our vacation. As soon as we arrived, we were surprised by how beautiful the hotel was. Randy checked us in and we felt right at home. Randy is the sweetest and most helpful person we have ever have help us at a hotel. 

We got comfortable in our room. Yes, it is small but the same size room in San Francisco would run you three times as much. All we wanted was a clean and comfortable bed and we got that and more. Randy told us that the amenities at the next door sister hotel was available for use. Our 4 year old son, was so excited. We got ready to go to the pool and wine and cheese and snacks awaited us at the lobby. Can this get any better? we thought. It sure did. We walked over to the next door hotel and the pool was perfect. There was a party going on and there was a DJ and a band! There was also poolside service. The pool had a large area that was ankle deep for our son with fountains so he was able to play in the pool. We had such  a good time that we stayed at the pool for dinner. We went back to our hotel showered and then the shuttle service driven by Dwayne, another sweetheart, dropped us off onto the main strip to walk around. We ended the night with an ice cream.

We slept so great in the bed. It was very comfortable. The next morning, we decided to check out what the breakfast voucher was for and we expected bagels and fruit but no! It was a full breakfast! Can we move in?! 

Our only regret is not staying longer. We are already planning a longer stay.I have told all my friends and one couple is already planning a weekend getaway coming up. 

We fell in love with St. Pete, Randy and the Avalon. We can't wait to return!

If you want to impress someone, this is the place to go! The location is amazing! This restaurant sits on the beach. 

All around, this place is great! From service, to the food, to cleanliness. I did not give 5 stars because there are a couple of things that could use improvement like the coconut shrimp and lobster. Do NOT get me wrong, they were not bad but they weren't amazing. The coconut shrimp could use a better sauce or glaze. The lobster could use a bit of a seasoning to enhance the grilled flavor. 

Unfortunately I cannot remember our waiter's name but he and the bus boys provided outstanding service. 

You must try the blood orange margarita!! 

I'm so happy I found this restaurant and I will definitely be back again to try breakfast/brunch.

This charming Havertown tap house offers up a plethora of great pub fare, including mussels, Brick & Brew Pretzel, fried pickles, wings, burgers, fried chicken, smoked brisket, pulled pork, and more. 

In various visits, I've sampled their Whipped Ricotta (with local honey and garlic toast), the Fried Pickles, the Wedge salad, the Short Rib Grilled Cheese, the flatbreads, and more. A shout-out for quite possibly the best Wedge salad I've ever had. And the Whipped Ricotta was fabulous and I'm here to tell you that B&B does NOT skimp on the portions. I don't believe that the Caramel Beignets are REAL beignets (fried pizza dough perhaps), but it hardly made a difference -- they were fabulous and quickly devoured. 

Too noisy for great conversation but the food and great service more than makes up for it.

This place is fantastic. Bacon-wrapped figs, shishito peppers, and countless other dishes exceeded our expectations for a laid back Saturday lunch. Equally fantastic was the service. All staff were knowledgeable, attentive, and personable. We will definitely be back on our next trip to Philly.

We wont be back !  We watched one of the staff, redhead with braids, drag a German Shepard across the parking lot like a rag doll, it was disgusting. We saw the owner trying to tell her he would help getting the dog to the door but the staff member was so angry she just kept dragging this dog across the parking lot.
This place is not a good fit for our family and our pets anymore they just dont care. We have been clients here for years and the doctors have changed and not all for the better. They overbook, charge 75.00 for a visit if you do not have an appointment which most dont unless you have booked MONTHS in advance and people with appointments are put in the same order as those that dont. Went from caring about pets to being greedy and flat out disrespectful.
After waiting in our car for hours along with a whole parking lot full of ticked off people with thirsty dogs we were told that this business could careless whether any of us return or not. Why is that, because they dont really care if their long term clients stay because they have a parking lot full of people without appointments paying 75.00 dollars just to sit in the parking lot sometimes for up to 5 hours but 2 and wait. No longer a trustworthy business in our view. Dr Kern, if you cant do your job and treat people with respect, find a different profession not working with animals or the public. Dr Musick, people love their pets, have some compassion when in a room and people have to put their dogs down, be prepared the process is difficult enough, we should have left the practice when we started noticing the decline now it is just ridiculous and shameful. Keep the surgeon she is one of the only decent people left. The rest, I no longer trust with a hangnail.

I love going to this close-knit vegetarian/vegan eatery for brunch. I've been several times now, each time growing more satisfied with the dishes and the service. 

If a contest was held in Reno for the title of best salad in town, Great Full Gardens would be in the top 5 easily.

This was my first time to eat at this oyster house, which located in University City. They offered amazing seafood, and their environment was quite comfortable, but I would not give full five stars for some dishes taste so fishy.

For food we had lobster rolls, lobster bisque, Korean BBQ skirt, shrimp cocktail and some raw oysters. Generally, their food was good, but not delicious enough to gain five stars. I pretty much agree with Yohanna's comment that "They have a wide selection of locally sourced oysters" (Yohanna, Yelp.com). The raw oysters were fresh, but the sauces taste just so strange. There were three kinds of sauces in plate, but none of them taste good. The hot sauce was too sour but not spicy at all. The oysters had fresh and clean taste that I loved but some of them had a lightly salty feeling that kept stimulating my throat, which made me feel I wanted to cough. The lobster rolls taste not bad but just too big to eat. It's hard to send such a big roll into my mouth, thus the lobsters always fall out everywhere and some lettuce sticky on my face. That's really awkward while eating with a lot of friends. One of my friends ordered Korean BBQ skirt, which tastes good, though the sides' potato was a little bit strange. The shrimp Cocktail was awful. They do offered fresh shrimp, but the sauce really left a bad smell in my mouth. Lobster bisque might be the worst one I've ever tried. It's too salty that seems like salt is for free. In brief, I don't like this restaurant so much. As I am not a crazy seafood lover, this restaurant does not attract me so much. However, someone may love this kind of taste, it might not be the best place to go, but may be an OK place for seafood fan.

To be honest, their service was pretty good. Our servers were very enthusiastic and helpful, and they smiled gently. Because we had about ten persons, there were about three waiters served us to ensure that they could take care everyone on the table considerately. They asked everyone about if we had any allergies. Jenna C said, "Our waiter was very friendly and seemed knowledgeable about the drink and food menu"(Jenna C, Yelp.com). I totally had the same feeling, they asked our preference and gave advice about their menu. When we were eating, our waiters were waiting outside the door (we booked a VIP room). Whenever we asked for service, they came to us immediately.

The environment was good and clean. Their decoration is kind of vintage style. Lights were made of the shell of oysters, and they hang a lot of vintage maps on the wall. Their table and chairs are also in the old fashion style. The lights are smooth enough to create a comfortable atmosphere to have dinner. We really enjoyed the long dining table with candles in the VIP room. Anyway, their vintage and smooth ambience is what I like best about the restaurant.

Got superior service when I visited this shop the other day for an oil and filter change. I've been to other JL's in different states. This one stands out. I was 'offered' other services like air filters, but I did need one. No pushing items that weren't necessary. Thank you to the team. They also left a handwritten thank you note in my car! Try them out. You can always find something cheaper, but this is a local shop with good people.

Savory Leaf Cafe if you have not gone it's a must.  Not just good food but great.   I am not a Vegan... and I love it.   Service is friendly with a smile.    Try the Reuben Sandwich.  Soups are so good.  You will not be disappointed.

They have poor management in regards to lease agreements and payments. Do yourself a favor and call them month to month to assure all charges are paid,  otherwise they will let it accrue and not even notify you.

Cool place to go and eat - full bar with Dixie on draft (in addition to a few others as well). Loved the outdoor area. Clean bathrooms!

Got the surf and turf po'boy: 3 stars, the bread (5 stars) was awesome for this poboy, but I think I needed to keep things separate. The individual flavors got muddled from all the gravy. My bad

Would definitely come back to try the shrimp poboy separate from the roast beef po boy. And grab a cold beer

Ordered the wrong thing but will not give a bad review for ordering the wrong thing! Atmosphere was awesome(live music stops at 10 pm) great outside seating and our server was very nice. Garlic knots are awesome! My girl friend got one of the stacks and loved it.

My fiancé and I just moved back to Tampa, and decided to try out some small places in the new neighborhood.  I love good coffee, and anywhere that brews Buddy Brew Coffee is one I want to check out. The place was busy when we got there at about 11:15am, but there were still a couple of tables available. We didn't wait in line to order more than a few minutes. They don't have an extensive menu, but what they have is versatile enough.  The atmosphere was good, cartoons on for the kids that were there, not too loud, and games and books available to pass the time. I ordered a breakfast sandwich with eggs, cheese & ham on pressed Cuban bread and a large Cafè Ybor coffee. The sandwich was really good, but the coffee was amazing!  I'll definitely be back soon!

Prices a bit expensive, but then these outlets are all name brands.  If name brands are important to you this is the place otherwise try the Wal-Mart on Cortero.  Disappointed in the number of restaurants in the mall, but I imagine there will be more selection outside the mall in the next few construction phases on the property.  Heard there may be a box store.  Was hoping Macy's, but rumor has it perhaps a home improvement store such as Lowes or Home Depot which is good too.  The setting is beautiful.  Will be anxious to see the rest of the development and what is in store to enjoy.  Perhaps then my rating will improve.

We arrived very early in the morning at around 2am and we were promptly checked in. Our room was 721. The room was very elegant with dim lighting which set a very nice mood in the room.
