Our room had no real view.  You looked across the street at the building next to the hotel.  This was not an issue for us.  The hotel had a very nice ambiance.  We really enjoyed staying there.

Room size:  Adequate
Bathroom: Small but updated nicely.  Not much counter space.
Bed:  Very comfy.  Pillows were extra high loft, non-allergenic, polyester fiberfill.  I asked for soft feather pillows and they brought them to us within minutes of our request.
Hotel restaurant:  Did not eat there
Hotel bar: Very nice.  Has at least one nice chess set, if you, like to play
Hotel terrace:  Small dipping pool, hot tub and about six lounge chairs around the pool.  Very small area.  There is also a couple of cabanas with lounge chairs off to side away from pool and a few two top tables.  It is a very relaxing area.  We went up on a Friday night at 11pm and the terrace was all ours!  Amazing!
Noise level: Our room was across from ice maker closet and the guest in the adjoining room were very load (shouting, etc.) the first night.  We turned down the air conditioner temp and turned up the fan and it seemed to drown out most of the noise.
Location:  We were there for the final four games and I think this hotel had one of the best locations!  It was an easy walk to the Superdome and, trust me, traffic was hell even if you were trying to drive 4 blocks to the game.  Being able to walk was a huge plus.  The hotel is also only about a 5 minute walk to the edge of the French Quarter.
Night time snack: PB&J sandwiches and hot cocoa severed at 10 pm in the lobby are a nice touch.
Service: Staff was not overly friendly. This may bother some people but it did not bother us.

If you are in New Orleans to party in the French Quarter this may not be the hotel for you but for our purpose it was great!

This is my third time here. I like unique of the restaurant with music venue. The restaurant is separated from the music venue. The music venue sometime is free but most of the time requires a small cover fees.  However, you can eat at the restaurant without attending the music venue and not charge with the fees. 

The restaurant has a lot non typical food from the swamp or sea. They have the smoke mullet, fried gator tail, popcorn crawfish, beer boil shrimp, smoke fish dip.  The service is quick and so does the food come out.  The ambient is very interesting that the restaurant is constructed from a shack. The full menu offer a lot of seafood dish found in many restaurant. In season, they also have the stone crab. 

Please check at their music calendar that is playing and the required cover fees. They also have special event held here call skipper dome for the entire music venue. They open late at night if you want a bite.

My man & I hopped into this spot because it was close and convenient on New Years Day, I can't say I enjoyed the food or service that day so we were disappointed.. I did however attend their happy hour & was a pleasant surprise, get the small pork dish with fresh grapefruit & vodka- Yum. 
 Perhaps a third try will be a winner..

Not very accommodating in the gifting department during Christmas.  I had planned to gift a $400 gift to my in-laws but it was last minute (this past Tuesday) & when I called the restaurant they informed me the only way to bypass the 7 day delivery of a custom gift card was to actually come pick up a card. ‍ not happening! In the future they should probably provide something other than a true card & allot for quick delivery via Internet. So unfortunate.

I was with a party of five girls renting a room here for our stay in Reno to celebrate a soon to be married member. When we arrived the room was not what we reserved, usually availability is the main factor for this, as the front desk staff had assured us was the case. But this struck me as odd because they book by reservations online only and they refused to compensate for this mistake because the reservation was not directly through their site but instead through a third party site. The front desk staff did apologize saying she has complained and got complaints before for "them" to update the internet reservations information. But the trouble didn't end with that, we got up to the room and the thermostat was locked on heat and it was 80+ degrees in there, we were baking alive opening all the windows trying to call the front desk to get ahold of maintenance to shut off the heat, they explained after turning the heat setting off that the AC unit was shut down for the season and we would just have to wait for the heat to go out the windows otherwise there'd be no other way to cool the room, the front room cable had temperamental service to the TV, the shower head would squeal when you turned on the water, the front room phone wouldn't call out even to the front desk, the iron stocked in that room did not work at all and lastly at lesser fault to them the church bell across the street went off what seemed like every fifteen minutes, we were trying to fit five girls in a one bed one bath tiny room tiny kitchen with a double bed and two very uncomfortable pullout couches (and we had to call down multiple times for toilet tissue), and even though we stayed out in town late when we came back to sleep the upstairs neighbors kept partying and stomping around and conversing plenty loud enough for us to hear. We definitely did not have a good stay here and would not come back and definitely not recommend this place for your stay.

3,000 miles into a 4,000 mile road trip from east to west coast, we needed an oil change that didn't take all day and didn't require us to stray too far from our car that was packed since we were moving. Not to mention for an old 2000 Camry. After searching for a bit, Yelp led us to Einstein's Oilery. 

I was intrigued with the whole idea of staying in your car while they work, cameras showing what they're doing under the hood and under your car too. And yes, they give you your drink of choice when you pull up as well as offering you a newspaper (or Kindle Fire if you are so technically inclined) while you browse your options. They'll suggest different types of oil (standard vs. synthetic) and make sure your car is in working order outside of just your oil. They checked our lights before you pulled into the garage and our other fluids as well. We decided to go with also changing our transmission fluid since it was time and they suggested it. Certainly not pushy with their offers of extra service but do know your car and what you actually need! 

Wanted to shoutout Sara & Alex for making our experience extra special. You can see Sara's skills and expertise shine while she assisted with changing our transmission fluid. Alex was friendly and kept our wait short by chatting with us while he worked. 

We wished we lived in Idaho because I would come here all the time!

There's thin crust and then there's pizza on a cracker; Jules is the latter. They do have some inventive topping combinations, and the proximity to our home makes it appealing.

But someone explain to me how a pizza place can shut down at 8 pm? Seems every time we call, the surly person who answers the phone (and they are ALL surly) informs us that we have about 8 minutes to get there because they are "closing soon".

The work ethic at Jules is as thin as the crust. And the attitude is terrible. For overpriced pizza, I would take my business elsewhere.

Great experience here with a pedicure. The technician was friendly, and did an excellent job! She asked me twice, if the water was a comfortable temperature, and even though they were a little busy, didn't rush. Most of the customers seemed to be regulars, and the atmosphere is very pleasant. 
The place is very clean, the price is almost half as much as other local spas, and I will definitely return!

When you get in for your service, they are wonderful. I have never had a bad facial, or bad massage from here. However, I do not recommend this location. My husband and I pay X amount of money to have monthly massages. 2 massages ago they were booked out far so we missed a month. That transferred that missed months 'credit' to the following. We decided to use the extra towards facials. Day before service they called saying I couldn't do a facial because the person was out sick. So after our massages we scheduled our next services. Again, a massage and making up those facials. The full month of Nov was already booked so they booked us into December. (So note that's the second time in a row we couldn't get in each month even tho that's what we pay for.) I got a text message this past Friday that again, my facial needs to be rescheduled. I expressed I was upset about this and asked for an earlier date. They said they could do both mine and my husbands on Monday (today.) I verified it was this Monday. Get there today and we aren't even on the schedule. The guy at the front desk was talking to the manager, (or supervisor?) & I could hear them. Chuckling, & not seeming to really care. She came out & did apologize, said it was their mistake, & with the facials that we are actually scheduled for in December, we will get a free enhancement. At this point Im not holding my breathe. Very upset with this establishment. Mind you, I am appreciative of the free enhancement, but at this point its been a rotating door. Showing messages to show this is just an ongoing issue.
Don't bother.  Once you get seated, plan on an hour or two wait for your food!  Not worth it whatsoever!

Nutshell:  casual, friendly, great kolache beginner experience.  Definitely worth a stop in for breakfast or lunch.

Upon recommendation of a teammate of mine, I had to stop in.  This was my first kolache experience...and it will most certainly not be my last!  I rolled in late morning on a Sunday and there was a steady stream of folks.  I decided on savories:  sausage, cheese, jalapeño and their monthly special, country breakfast.  Both very tasty.  Two was the perfect amount for breakfast.  My only issue, was I expected them to be a bit toastier, but that was it.  They have a load of coffees that you fix up yourself.  Very good flavor on the two I tried, pecan and hazelnut.  My bill was about $8 for everything.  Not too shabby.  I look forward to heading back for a sweet one.

The park I shard to rate higher after going to Aquatica. Small wave pool and hard to find your way around. Had to choose a lawn chair off the beaten path that wasted even near a water ride or tube, just birds that kept fighting. Free water refills is nice and it was overcast so not crowded. They should charge less and would be worth coming.
As for the rides, bad connectors, we kept getting scratched on the tubes. Good thing was were able to bring in coolers with food...Chao for now amigos.

I am not a donut fan at all, but I am obsessed with the vegan donuts at Seedz! I've tried the vanilla chai, raspberry, and birthday cake flavors and all have exceeded my expectations. They're super moist and coated with a thick layer of frosting. The donuts are still a bit too sweet for me to finish by myself. However, you can either split with someone else or save them for later as they taste just as good the next day. 

Each donut costs about ~$4 which can get pricey, but it's worth it! I think the main reason I like them is because they're more like mini cake and not quite as dense as true donuts. I've also only gone to the little coffee shop and not the actual restaurant. I keep browsing the brunch/dinner menus though so will need to try the food sooner or later.

This is only a review of their baked goods, I have not tasted their sandwiches, but overall it left me unmoved.  Normally mediocrity isn't really the end of the world but for dessert I want to feel like it was worth it and for me their cakes, pastries, and general sugar based creations are not.  Their bread was fine.  Really it was all fine. 

*yawn*

The people were very nice though and I like the atmosphere.

Went out with the family, my sister in law was buying. We were seated fairly quickly which was nice the party of 7, 3 of which were kids.
We were doing all you can eat sushi for everybody. I ordered our appetizers which consisted of muscles and miso soup. The muscles came out really quickly and it was sad to see that they were Infested with mayonnaise. You can smell the mayonnaise when they put the plate on the table.
We dug in as we were hungry, but unfortunately, all you could taste was mayonnaise. I brought this to our waiters attention that all you could taste was the mayonnaise because there was an abundant amount of it on each muscle. He said no problem and would bring me back another plate of muscles, unfortunately, the next plate was just like the first one. Way too much mayonnaise, so I pulled one of the managers aside and asked if they could just do a dab of mayonnaise instead of a tablespoon on each muscle. They brought out our rolls before they could bring out the other plate of muscles, when they brought out the other plate of muscles with just a little bit of mayonnaise it seemed to work, although the muscles seemed undercooked in my opinion. The sushi was OK very mediocre. I have to admit and say that I was not entirely impressed with the service nor the food. We arrive there about 6:30 PM and did not leave until 9 PM because of how slow the service was. The worst thing about this whole experience was that I discovered that I had food poisoning from this place when I got home that night. I was up all night with diarrhea and nausea. I think what makes it worse is when I think about how much mayonnaise was on each muscle. There are a lot of good sushi places here in Reno, but this I would say is not one of them. Don't waste your time or your money go somewhere else! Don't get sick like I did!

I've been going here for a couple of years and really keep coming because I really like a couple ladies that work here. Also the prices are reasonable and I appreciate when they ask if I am still a student. 
However, I would say half the time I am satisfied with my eyebrows and the other half I have to finish shaping at home. 
Someone mentioned this already, but whenever I request the shape I want, there's always a "I can try but here's why I shouldn't." It wasn't until I went to a different place for the heck of it and didn't get the same response.

Decent food but service is extremely rude.

I brought a bottle of wine and asked them if they are BYOB like the Old City location.  The reply was "you can bring the bottle, but just don't open it" in a sarcastic and demeaning tone.  Will not eat at a place that talks down to its paying customers ever again.

Came here for lunch on Friday at 1140.  We have waited for this place to open for a longtime.  Our party of 8 was quickly seated by a friendly hostess.  The inside was not as busy as we thought it would be and it seemed like a large staff at the time.  Our waiter came by to take our drink order and  came back after several minutes to take our food order.  Most of us got a sandwich and we had one salad.  After waiting for 45 mins, three of the dishes arrived.  We waited 40 more mins for two more dishes to arrive and then just asked to cancel the other two dishes.  The waitress was very apologetic and she knew the kitchen had really fouled up.  The manager came over and comped our whole table which was the right thing to do and we made sure to leave a nice tip.  After exactly 95 mins, our final two dishes came out in Togo containers.  

I'm not sure what in the heck happened in the kitchen, but because the manager compensated us for our wait, we might come back with our fingers crossed.

I've been a customer here for 3 years and even my partner likes to come here for car troubles too now. John and his team are always so polite, friendly, helpful and honest. They are consistently there to help for any emergencies, are quick, communicative, and above all, do honest and quality work. They always ask if I need a ride anywhere after dropping my car off and offer hand sanitizer and water. Lastly, they like explain exactly what needed to be repaired in an easy (but not condescending) way, this is so greatly appreciated. Thank you EZ Automotive!

I would discourage you from ordering here!

3 co-workers and myself ordered delivery here at 11:40 am. We ordered a couple sandwiches/wraps and wings. 

Delivery took 1 hour an 15 mins! Rediculous.

Meatball Parm had almost no sauce on it.

Wings were the size of a small pinky finger. 

The other food was rated as mediocre. Doubt I would go back.

There used to be this water-ice place down in the bowels of South Philly that had actual chunks of fruit in its icy product.  That spoiled me against any other water ice.  Rita's is not awful but it lacks the fresh-fruitiness that I have grown to love.  I just know it's chock full of high fructose corn syrup, artificial color and flavor.  I know now that water ice doesn't have to be like that and it tastes much better when it's not.

It's a Philly institution like cheesesteaks and pretzels but that doesn't mean that it's good.

Cheesy, overly nice, plastic wait staff training wannabe wait staff.  Service sucked.  Food was tasteless.  How can you screw up a sirloin steak....  Couldn't get a drink refill here even if you were on fire.  

Go to Steak and Shake or hell even Boston Market instead.

Best Steak and Shake I have ever been to. John greeted us promptly and had us seated immediately. He actively moved us to a booth with more table space when we ordered a few dishes that could have overwhelmed our current table. Kudos to the kitchen! The food came in record time! Nothing was missed. It was a great experience overall. John refilled drinks without having to ask. He even remembered not to add ice as requested. I came in expecting a typical late night restaurant service but received an eye opening experience instead. John you're the man!

Elegant bar and restaurant in Ybor, the interior is beautifully decorated as well as the bar. Aquaduct was a great cocktail. We ordered the Raclette, so delicious! Cheese poured tableside.

I do hope they bring on a plant based entree and appetizer in line with their mission soon, currently there is not a vegetarian entree.

First off, the food here is absolutely amazing. We came as a group and tried a little of everything. The lobster rolls (both) were 5 stars. The lobster quesadilla was fantastic (definitely getting that again) and we tried both the clam chowder & lobster bisque soups - both 5 star. We also tried the tots & the fish n chips which were also amazing. Lastly the Whoopi pies were fresh, thick and delicious. We nearly needed a wheelbarrow to leave the place. Well be back and bringing everyone we know! Becky at Cousins was amazing at suggesting the top items to us. She served us tentatively, made sure our table was clean and fully sanitized, and kept our glasses full of water. She's a gem. When you go, ask for her and ask her for her suggestions - she won't steer you wrong! Thanks Becky and thank you to the whole staff at Cousins Maine Lobster. Can't wait to come back!

For a more local experience, head north of downtown near Fall Creek (an actual creek) to a neighborhood called Fall Creek Place. There's a small strip of businesses including this local market. Here's what's to love:

1) Space: It's an actual market known for its deli. You can also buy a few other locally made items and the there's a beer/wine cellar (with seating) in the basement that you can't miss. Upstairs, there's outdoor seating and window seating for guests. There's a bathroom in the back.

2) Food: It all is amazing, but I recommend the batali sandwich for lunch. It's packed with meats and cheeses and is a well down sandwich. It's not massive, but does fill you up. 

If you want a more local experience, head here!

Gold's has all you are looking for when it comes to equipment- both free weights and machines. Plenty of treadmills, ellipticals, stationary bikes, and stair climbers. In all my visits, and there have been plenty based on my check ins, I've never had an issue with having to wait on using a piece of equipment. 

Gold's does need to focus on cleanliness. There's no way in the world I'd use the men's bathroom other than to wash my hands. At times, there's no paper towels or disinfectant to clean the machines. 

I can't speak to any of the classes or trainers as I don't use those services. 

As for cost, well, just depends on how well you negotiate. There are other gyms at a lower cost if you need just the equipment to use.

My husband and I did a skeptics ghost tour of Nola. I'm not much of a believer in spirits and ghosts and my husband is. I do however love a good scare of hard facts and so I was looking forward to this tour. I read online that Miss Loretta was awesome and I was so happy that she was our tour guide!  She was absolutely awesome!!! She texted me about 2 hours before to confirm the location and kept us updated of where she was. She had a warm/spunky personality that was the perfect fit to be a tour guide. She hit all the spots I had heard of and then some and debunked some of the ones that I was a true skeptic of. I think I even heard a little quiver in her voice while saying the stories of the LaLaurie mansion were true. 

All in all, I really enjoyed this tour and LOVED Miss Loretta I'd recommend this tour and tell everyone to request her for their tour guide!!!

Possibly the worst dining experience of my life from start to finish. Late Sunday afternoon arrival, hostesses were snobs and acted like we invaded their social time. Also it was a 45 min wait to be seated when the restaurant was only 1/2 capacity. NBD, though, we love the concept of the place so we decided to get a drink. It took 15 mins for bartender to acknowledge us. Again, not a busy time of day. Happy with our drinks though! Once seated, our server didn't even come say "hi, I'll be with you in a minute!" - waited 15 mins with no water and eventually got the attention of Trevor, another server, who was AWESOME. He helped us get through to our server. She took our order and it took 45 mins to get our food. Didn't bring silverware. Acted defensive when we asked nicely for some. Food orders were all wrong except kids meals! Including under cooked meat. Eventually got the manager who took away our terrible entrees and said he'd refund us half the ticket - when none of us had even gotten to eat our food! Unbelievable. If the food was good but service poor or Vice versa I'd return, but with food and service both pretty terrible we won't go again. We tipped Trevor, who was not our server but the best part of our experience other than drinks  lol

This is truly the worst Walgreen in America. Just read the reviews. I've never been in a major metropolitan area when stores are closed on Sundays. Another reason why Nashville will never be a good town, its got a lot of growing up to do.  Another thing, what's up with that weird deformed dude that they let sit at the checkout counter every morning reeking of pee and poverty. Why can't he work Sunday's? No one else in there clearly wants to. Just last week during the CMA they were open on Sunday. Every employee was salty about it when I asked if they were going to start opening on Sundays. It's really easy to steal from this store. All the employees are really too busy chit chatting about their bullshit boyfriends, or talking to the guy who works there that looks like a fat little Richard with bad teeth.

I wish there were more Hardee's around. Hardee's​ is not your usual fast food place especially breakfast items. Hardee's has a great selection on menu items that are not on regular fast food chains menus. But this place needs an upgrade. Outside is not to bad but the inside looks like something from the 70s. Customer service was ok the cashier was new but did a decent job. I recommend that you ask for your food to be made fresh b/c they pre-make sandwiches and leave them under the heat lamp which we all know dries up the food.

AWESOME place to get your nails done!  Great service, pleasant people!  My hands and feet looked and felt AMAZING when I left!  Zack the owner is soooooo cool his designs are so cute and creative!  The prices are reasonable so don't  pass go just stop at the lil nail boutique in Swarthmore!
Terrible service. Food is just as good as the broad ripple location, so go to that one instead. There are only a handful of tables at this location and they still can't seem to serve them adequately.

First of all...Excellent instructors and in-class tech support (5 Stars to them).

.... but if you fall for TechSherpas "offers" aka "UTP" you will be carrying your training hours all year around. Few of our coworkers did the UTP that got us 10 days of training at a very good price. The catch is, you can ONLY enroll in classes that are guaranteed to run(GTR) so you better be signing up for their popular classes because if you're interested in the PMP's, CISSP, CompTIA, CEH and some MS's you'll be checking their website weekly and adjusting your schedule at their convenience since you will be at their mercy.

Honestly is a shady way of doing business there was no mention of this when we signed up. I guess buyers beware ....and thanks to Yelp now companies beware.
I called in the order and it was made on time the staff gave me excellent service. The pizza was okay and the chicken wings we're good.

I have had the worst service I've ever experienced at this Tire Kingdom. The bad service was not just from one individual either. The first manager I dealt with was a very cranky old man. I used to work in the automotive industry and know that the guys in the shop are not hired for their social skills. That's why they have a manager that can handle people with good communication. This place got missed. I do not recommend going there. 

I had an alignment done on my truck and waited for 3 hours while they handled other people that came in after me because their jobs were quicker. When complaining to the manager, he just got cranky. Two weeks later I went for another alignment because my truck was pulling HEAVILY to the right. I check with the manager to find out when they can put my truck up for an alignment and he said come back in 40 minutes and he would get it done. Well when I came back, he had me wait for another HOUR before he even had his guys put my truck up. The moment I mentioned anything he quickly justified it by saying he was busy. I counted the cars in the shop. 3 cars (my truck not included), 4 techs, 3 of them doing nothing. After another HOUR, my truck was finally done. I do not recommend this location.

The times that I mention in this review are accurate as I actually started timing my wait.

It appears that express scripts is in bed with the firms making blood glucose meters. It seems that express script drops previously covered meters every few months. This results in them having to provide replacement meters at the Governments expense. This is criminal and should be stopped. This is why the government is running out of money. Write Congress expressing your concern.
Eaten here for more than a decade. We first came here when visiting Tucson, and have eaten here since we moved here in 2007. Love the vegetarian options.

Waited over an hour for our food and the food WAS NO WHERE NEAR WORTH THE PRICE. Our waiter was also the bar tender so he was too busy the whole time and barely came to our table. Which is not his fault at the end of the day. The biggest disappointment about this place is that the food is way over priced for what you are getting.
Food usually good but down hill since they tried to be a full bar. What's up with the queso lately? Super watery.
Food Was Just Plain noting to Write Home to Mom about I have had Much Better

Terrible customer service...
Very disappointed. 
$27 for a pizza that was not what we ordered, twice.  We asked for extra sauce & light cheese. 
As you can see in the photo, almost no sauce & heavy cheese. 
When asked to speak with management, we were told, the manager was too busy. 
We will not be back.  
Does anyone know who the owners are?

The food was excellent! Jambalaya needed just a few more minutes of cook time as the rice was still crunchy. Service was good, however, you could tell our waitress was a little overwhelmed. A large group of us were able to be seated together. The bathrooms could use an update.

Gluten free menu...dubious. You have to tell your server repeatedly. Plates brought over, waiter looks down and explains, "oh, he wasn't supposed to put the sauce on, it's not gluten free!"  Seriously?  Back it goes. He then comes back with the same plate, asks if cornstarch is ok, and was told it depends on the ingredients. Five minutes later, the same plate comes back, and we are told the manager said it was ok. My daughter tried to stay away from the sauce on the plate, but was violently ill later due to a gluten reaction. We were comped for the meal, b/c she didn't eat most of it, but seriously, they don't care about a patron's health?  If you have a gluten issue, I'd advise you to stay away. 
Bathrooms:  disgusting. My daughter came out asking for hand sanitizer after washing  her hands.

The only reason why I am giving this practice two stars is because of Drs. McCullen & McCrosson. Dr. Richman said that I would never go into labor on my own as a first time mother. He also said that I was "too fat" to have a baby vaginally. He wanted me to schedule a c-section. I had no indications for a c-section. I was due around Christmas time, Gwen wanted me to schedule an induction. Again, I refused to schedule just for convenience. She also said I would not go into labor on my own. She also said that my baby would be too big to push out vaginally. He was measuring 7.8 lbs at week 38. I actually did go into labor on my due date. Labor was taking a bit longer than Gwen liked. She offered me a choice of pitocin or IV fluids. I had made it very clear I wanted a medication free delivery. I chose IV fluids. She forced me to push the baby out before I was fully dialated at 9cm. As a result I tore front to back (it took a year to heal) and my son almost died when he was born because of this. (I have my records.) At my 6 week checkup she said I should have been sectioned! There was no medical reason to section me!!! 

I also had issues with Terry another midwife. I had gotten Mirena and had serious complications from it. She told me they were normal. (I had severe bleeding, bruising, and mood swings.) I bled so much I was anemic. According to the side effects, this was not normal! She refused to take it out. I had to beg for her to. 

Please do NOT go to this practice!!

I love this place. It is one of the few places I tell people are a must. One of the waitresses and I have had a falling out before about the Gyros. I wanted lamb instead of beef and we used to argue over it and she would always give me the dinner plate and overcharge me and it was frustrating. I had written Chef Maurice about this issue and stayed away for a year (no response). I was pleased as punch that he had added a lamb gyro lunch plate (just the gyro and it is put together rather than having all the sides and not being put together in the dinner plate). So what I am saying here guys is WAY TO GO! You obviously listened to me and made a change and for that I will keep returning. 

The food is out of this world and we can never figure out what we want so we just order everything and go crazy. The gyro lamb is done in Indian seasonings and the veggie and tzatziki on their fresh naan is something you can't get elsewhere...flippin amazing! They have different types of baklava and that pistachio saffron ice cream is perfect. Mostly the service is awesome and the music is hypnotic Greek style stuff that takes your stresses away. I particularly like Chef Maurice and his personal connection he makes with his guests. 

Try it!

I stopped in and ordered the chicken Marsala. The meal came in a styrofoam plate with plasticware. Ok. Ok except that it was quite a battle to cut into this very tough chicken breast. And the sauce had the same flavor of a Salisbury steak tv dinner. Bleh. I gave up half way through.it, The waitress apologized and gave me my full bill. Skip this place, go on down to the fast food. Take the express ealkwa toward the other terminals- it's three minutes and there are great choices, like vino volo.

Coffee bar is super hip and the hot spot to be. We get it. But their lattes are mediocre. The espresso isn't my fav.. but I still go here 3 times a week before work Cause their pastries are amazing. As is their quinoa bowl. So I come back.

Was in the mood for some Jambalaya and heard about this place. 

I had Jambalaya dinner with rice and my husband had the gizzard dinner w rice and beans. Both were very tasty and tasted delicious. Big portions

My husband wished the gizzard were sautéed instead of battered and fried but it was tasty 

Will definitely return

I've been once and I liked it. It's close to work and I can pop in there on my lunch break.  

However, the last 2 times I've tried to go, it's been closed! Hours state they are open 10am-6pm; I'm going at lunch time.  

I asked Great Clips next door if they knew about it closing or not. They said they saw "him" this morning, but he pops in and out.  

Sorry, dude, trying to give you some business.

I'm giving four stars because from the minute we walked in we were treated so delightfully. The gentleman that was the host instantly brought a smile to our faces. I'm recognizing this more during these awkward pandemic times. Eating out requires a bit more planning and accommodation and the hostess was wonderful. This continued to every level of service... our server, our food runners and everything in between. We ate outdoors in the private, partitioned, heated booths. This is the way to go when eating outdoors during this unfriendly winter. 
Now... the food. The star of the night was the whole roasted cauliflower. It was delicious. I could have dined on that alone. The seasoning, the butter, the perfect roast that made it soft enough to cut into but firm enough for a fresh taste. The beet salad that we shared was also good. I ordered the chicken and for me, it was a no go. The fries were cold and the chicken was over cooked. (It was dry) I explained it to my dining partners as way too "chickeny ". And there was absolutely no jus. None. My friend really liked her chicken pot pie and the hangar steak ordered by another friend was decent. We were celebrating my good friend's birthday and we haven't been out together since my friend's last birthday a year ago, so this was a special night (thanks to vaccinations). The staff was so good at recognizing our special occasion and that made a memorable night even more memorable. Even though I wasn't taken with my dish, there was enough for me to be satisfied and delighted about. But that chicken really needs some seasoning and some jus.

NEW OWNERS/NEW MENU.....DID NOT HELP!!!
We went today and they touted all newness and great food. What a HUGE DISAPPOINTMENT. If this is under "new management"  then the WOW FACTOR and the idea of hitting it out the park with the food DID NOT HIT the mark! 
Ordered their daily special of catfish and when you live in Louisiana it should be crisp on the outside, hot on the inside and not taste milky. Their catfish was soft and oily on the batter and the fish was cold. I am sure since this was the daily special, this fish was sitting under a heat lamp and was cooked for a lunch crowd, hoping many people would order to keep it moving, but no doubt it sat there.
When you order onion rings you are expecting a crispy goodness on the outside with a tasty hot vegetable on the inside. This was the total opposite. Greasy on the outside, batter was not done at all and the inside was white and not fully cooked.
My wife ordered the meatloaf, another special of the day, and what a disappointment that was. They must have a smoker on the premises, but when the meat is overwhelmed with the taste of the wood and not the flavor of the meat and the seasonings you, have nothing but a single taste flavor cooking process.
If I had to base it on food, only I would not have given two stars. My stars come from the wait staff who are trying hard and training new people. But if the food does not represent highly, than the staff is overworking themselves and making false promises at the table.
This place really needs someone to oversee the chef, the menu production and the quality of what's going to the table.

The lady at the desk was extremely rude. The other reviews echo this same problem. They need better staff.

I should also add I've been waiting an hour past the apt time and not been seen.

Overall: Go here for happy hour

Food: Bland Indian food. There is nothing great about it. Everything seemed to taste the same. There are better places to go in St Louis.

Drinks: A bit over-priced. There are maybe 15 bars on this street who will do a better job in serving alcohol. Get the Lassi if you like sweet non-alcoholic beverage.

Service: They will come ask you how everything is several times. So the service is top notch. I wish I could tell them to take the food back because its terrible.

Price: Overpriced for quality.

Started with oysters which were good but I had to clean pieces of shell off of them. Ordered the half poboy and a side. It was actually a quarter of a poboy and a cup of my side item. For $15 that combo was not worth it. The flavor of everything was fine but nothing to write home about. We also felt like we were being rushed the entire time.

Recently moved to the area and exploring places to go and eat. Happens to see this place driving through the parking lot.
Right now it's a breakfast/lunch menu with dinner service starting a let time comeback. Went at 11:30 for lunch and it was crowded. The menu had a decent selection of items to cover your breakfast or lunch desires. We both had wraps (Caesar grilled chicken and crispy chicken) both were done really well with fries and coleslaw.
Service was good and our server was pleasant and attentive even though she was running at 100 miles per hour. A testament to working through staffing shortages and longer wait times.
If your in the area you should give them a try.

We went to Applebee's only because we were given a gift certificate.
The staff is very friendly.  The food left something to be desired.  Ordered from the 2 for $24.00.I had the 1/2 rack of ribs, wow they were the smallest portion I've ever seen.  I talked to the manager and he offered to bring out chicken strips to make up for the portion.  I was eating ribs.  Husband had steak, is was OK, he didn't finish it, only 4 oz.
If I'm given another gift certificate, I'll give it away.
I don't get the hype. The food is ok, pretty standard beer list, service has always been kind of rude to me. Sort of feels like I get judged when I walk in - not a fan
Went with a large group and they were very accommodating. Decent food and service. 2 stars due to the $20 whiskeys. Really? $20 for a double Bulleit? I know you're a tequila joint but c'mon.
This place is on point. GREAT 'za. Nice peeps workin there too. Very quick.

Good God this was terrible.  I was there for about an hour and 50 minutes.  To get a beer, a salad, and an overcooked, under seasoned, tough, gristly NY strip.  I had a bad feeling when the waitress kept calling me "babe" and "sweetie" and found it necessary to rub my forearm when delivering the menu.  She asked me if I wanted a wine list.  I said no, but how about a beer list.  She gave me a beer list and a "wine list," which was actually someone else's bill and credit card.  I ordered a beer.  And sat there for 20 minutes until someone figured out that their bill was sitting on my table.  Then my server came back and asked me if I needed anything.  "how about that beer I ordered?"  She couldn't remember what beer that was.  She felt it necessary to rub me again, this time on the shoulder. 

So then I got the beer and ordered my food.  40 minutes go by before I received my salad.  Then another 15 or so.  She came up and asked me what I had ordered - clearly she had not put it in.  She apologized, saying it had been "hectic."  At that point I was one of 9 customers.  Ok.  Another 15 minutes go by and she brings me my roll.  Which had been grilled, so it smelled like charcoal, not in a good way.  Then another 5 minutes and she comes by again to assure my my food is coming right up.  And to give me another salad, which I didn't want.  I tell her to box it up because by this point i can't wait to get out of there.  Steak comes out, cooked medium well (I had ordered it medium rare).  A cheap piece of meat, full of gristle.  But hey, they comped my beer.  She told me it normally takes a long time to get food from the kitchen.  That's all I need to hear - they don't have to worry about me coming back, ever.

These people have no business attempting to run a restaurant.
Terrible place very disrespectful lady upstairs doing the warrantys. big shit show there!
Great food. Totally tasty! Good service and neat environment. Ideal place for brunch. Order The fried chicken. Worth to try.

Tried to contact SNS and their site contact us is not working. Figures, right? Any-who... went there on 7/28/17 and ordered side of cottage cheese which had a pineapple on top. Pineapple had mold on it. So, how did that happen? Did it sit out of the can for days? How did the waiter not see it on it's way to the table? It was quite evident. Asked for manager who was conveniently unavailable. So, I guess mold is Ok cause they made me pay for everything. My future decisions to dine at this location are not going to waiver quite a bit between healthy and not getting food poisoning. I know some mold is good, but... I don't think I should chance it.

Mehhhhh ..... figured we'd try this place out since my friend saw good reviews on yelp. It was hard to find because the doors to the place were open and the name "Capdeville" was on the doors.  We arrive and the place isn't as trendy as we thought.  It's not a dive bar... but just a small restaurant and/or bar. 

I thought my gin drink was good, I dont remember the exact name, but it had the word lady in it... 

So I was a little disappointed with over all service, not many people in the place so I'm not sure why the service was slow, but I was even more disappointed when I asked about the oyster Rockefeller on the menu and the waitress told me it was a great choice, later to find out they were out of oysters.  Yup, on a Tuesday night in NOLA... out of oysters.   We both ordered fish tacos just cooked differently.  They were okay.  Nothing to rant and rave about.  It was made with drum fish which I've never tried before... like I said, they were okay.  Tried the banana pudding for dessert and it tasted more like roasted marsh mellows then bananas, as the outside crust was burnt/toasted... Not sure if that's how its supposed to be as this was my first time trying it.

Probably wouldn't go back as there would be many other places in the area to try.

Burgers were good, fries were awesome. I especially liked all of the sauces and salts. Try the blueberry ketchup, you won't be disappointed. 

Prices seemed a little high. The restaurant area was pretty messy, but the waitresses were all nice and helpful when we ordered.
Love love love coffee and coconuts! Awesome atmosphere, great coffee and the pastries are to die for! My go to coffee shop as I do not support or buy from Starbucks! Come visit for sure!

I tried to adopt a kitten through this rescue and they would not let me adopt a kitten simply because I was looking for a single kitten. There is an abundance of kittens that do amazingly as solo cats. By restricting kitten adoptions to paired kittens only, not only negatively affects pet owners but also affects the kittens' lives. There are so many pet owners who can provide an amazing life to solo cats. Additionally, there are so many cats/kittens that prefer to be solo. It's very disheartening and disappointing that this rescue would rather leave kittens in small shelter cages than adopt out solo cats.

I don't understand the craze about this place. Don't sweat outside waiting in long lines for dinner here because their food is just OKAY. I would  go for oysters at Oceana, Royal House and Desiree over this restaurant anytime.

I was not very happy about the quality of its oysters because they are small and quite sandy inside. Compared to the half shell oysters I've had at other restaurants in the French Quarter, the half shell oysters here seemed to have the most "dirt" inside with black sediments around their shells. 

Gumbo rice with seafood would definitely be a safer choice here if you are holding a menu now. Adding some signature New Orleans spicy sauce to your food would also make your Gumbo dish more flavourful!

I'm within walking distance of this place and went to meet a friend to watch a hockey game on a Wed night.  Got off to a bad start when I was stopped at the door by a 20 something doorman/bouncer.  I brought my credit card and cash...but no ID.  Well young cocky door guy had to make a stand and not let me in...I must look like I'm on the bubble of 21.  You know...I'm only a 51 yr old chubby bald guy!!!  Must have been a tough call.  I wasted 30 minutes going back to my condo to get the ID.  The place has plenty of TV's and a pretty good atmosphere.  Our server was friendly...the two times I saw her in 2.5 hours.  Dreadfully slow.  Bad first impression.

This place is really cute!! Settle in a busy area but felt hidden!! I am here now and I love it!! The restaurant has an amazing chef!! The food was so flavorful and delicious!! I had lambs chops as an app. Chilean Sea bass and hubby got the seafood with pasta as a main! Good potions too. Reasonably priced - Fast and friendly services. The rooms are clean, big and very cute!! It was very peaceful and very relaxing! A great place for a couple who needs a place to get away from the busy town -that's hidden,  but yet close to everything!

Terrible attitudes. We were excited and ready to wait, we even expected to sit around for a while! But after spending $50 on ice cream and waiting 30 minutes we were stunned when we tried to correct our incorrect order we were met with eye rolls and disdain. Ice cream was fine but the service made it so we won't be returning.

This pizza was sooo good! We ordered delivery the other night and we will definitely be ordering from there again! We decided to order two medium size pizzas which was the perfect amount (left overs for sure - two of us). We ordered the white pizza and added bacon (omg, yum) and we also ordered the pesto pizza (added mushrooms and switched to chicken cutlets instead of grilled chicken). The crust on the pizza is really good too and usually I don't eat the crust!

This place was great! Like the atmosphere and all the details in the decorations. 
It would be an understatement to say we inhaled our food. Holy guacamole, the nachos grande were delicious and huge. Finished by 3 people in under 5 minutes. The triple cheese Mac and cheese was so good and swimming in a delicious cheese. The only improvement would be if it was served with a spoon so I could get all of the cheese sauce that came with it.  The national wings, spicy buffalo style, were delicious. Their spiciness was a perfect heat, just enough to clear your sinuses. 
Overall great place. I'd like to come back to try their drink specialities.

Woohoo! It's so good! Excellent and quality wine. Fantastic and pleasant environment. Great business hours deal too. I will definitely back with my folks! BTW, "Tria" is actually one of my HS teacher's name (an excellent teacher too). LOL

Won't be headed back there soon.  While the server was awesome, the appetizer took 30 minutes to appear.  The "fully loaded potato" arrived uncut, unloaded, blah.  My partner's "special," a "crusted Mahi," was obviously pre-frozen and tasted off--with kind of unpleasant results after we got home.  The prime rib (ordered medium/rare) showed up dripping with blood and fatty.... Picturesque place, but atmosphere doesn't make up for mediocre food.

I've eaten here before and it's been ok but this last time has left a foul taste.  I ordered an egg bagel and after 3 different attempts and tons of attitude my order still wasn't right.  I finally gave up because my flight was leaving.  The wrong order didn't bother me as much as the attitude when I apologized and asked for my correct order.  I'll stick with another place for now on.

Worst service ever. The gal working the desk was nice, but the doctor wouldn't even see me. Would not come into the exam room and even listen to my symptoms and offer any help. They just sent me away. Hopefully I die, so my wife can sue and pay for our baby's college tuition with the proceeds. Can I give negative stars?
Bollocks

We love the boil seafood house and have visited twice now when in the area. The food just doesn't disappoint as Cajun sauce just doesn't get much better than this. The platters are great to share and the crawfish beignets... so good! Their cocktails are also a home run. I didn't give it a perfect score due to the service. We made a reservation a few days before, via open table and requested a table on the first floor. Once we got there the host was guiding us upstairs and so I asked if there was seating on the first floor (as requested). He shortly pointed at a table right at the entrance where the people coming to check in are literally touching you. He didn't offer that we wait for a table that would be ready soon. We decided to take the table and order but my partner had to come sit on the banquette next to me because the chair was just too close to the door. All the food came out at the same time and there was barely space to eat or put the shells of the crawfish and shrimp and the waiter nicely asked if we were ok. I didn't want to complain so I said yes... but come on, do you not see? We made the best of the experience and we'll be back but would appreciate a good table as requested and a little more intuitive service on the part of the staff.
Healthy food. Some good choices for vegetarians.

Used to love this spot.

Great concept, but dropping the ball on delivering that concept.

Love the beer selection, but they refuse to give you a taster.   They tell me that is because they keep their beer prices low.  First of all, their beer prices are not low.  Second of all, a two ounce pour once in a while from a keg that  is going to generate over $1,000 of profit is not going to change the margin that significantly.  Third of all, I don't feel like paying $7 for a beer to find out that I hate it.

Food is decent, but it is hard to get a table, so often I have left and ventured elsewhere. 

Noise level is way too high and needs to be addressed.

There are too many places nearby that offer better service, better food and better atmosphere

Food atmosphere is always great

Try the squash dumplings. Always good.

Desserts are always tasty

No waiting, they got me in and out in about 15 minutes.  Very affordable, and friendly.   I could watch them on a TV from the waiting room.  The employees checked off all the boxes for me, and didn't try to up sell anything, which I appreciate. They are close to home, and they have my continued business.

Commtow or shall I say "Con Tow". You illegally towed or should I say STOLE my car. I was not parked in a handicapped spot, nor was I parked in a fire line, double parked, on the line, or in a way that disturbs traffic flow. I was simply parked in a spot that my apartment complex provided for me. You claim that it is illegally parked because there is no parking block in front of my car... well then you should have stolen 25% of the cars in the apartment complex! There was no sign that said you cannot park there. This company has tow truck drivers that come prowling in the middle of the night to tow cars- ILLEGALLY. ON TOP OF ALL OF THIS GARBAGE, you ruined my car while it was in your possession. I have a cracked wind shield, chipped paint, and bird poop all over my car (after I just got it cleaned). AND TO PUT THE CHERRY ON TOP, I turned on my car and my check engine light is on... which is absolutely absurd because I own a BRAND NEW MERCEDES C300 that I service religiously. Of course they tow your car in the middle of the night while you're sleeping in your apartment complex because they know they are doing WRONG and are straight up scared to have confrontation. I missed my appointments, I missed my work, & I wasted quite frankly half of my day. Please contact me If you'd like to fix this personally.

Heard that Two Chicks was the breakfast place to go in Midtown.  I absolutely love that they use organic, local, fresh products for their food, which is why I gave them 3 stars.  When I went with one other person, it was completely insane there. The staff all seemed so stressed and just too busy to help us.  We ended up sitting at the bar.  It took another 20 minutes to get acknowledgement from someone... then we finally ordered.  The food also took a while, and it was very rich.  It wasn't your traditional restaurant, which I really liked.  I just think they couldn't accommodate all their customers.  It was a very long wait time, I think the total dining experience was over two hours for two people. I might give them another shot... to be continued.

With very ample parking I can see why plow and anchor had to close down. Kind of sad cause I always wanted to try it. I literally live down the street here right off St. Clair and got here around 1130 am on a Monday and there looked like some construction going on near the taco shop. Luckily we took a chance where construction was and scored a meter parking spot. Upon arrival it was very empty. Was hoping for a lunch rush of some sort with all the working downtowners. There was guy working the counter at the moment so he was cashier and cook. He was very helpful giving me a run down of there menu. It's simple which I love because I'm one decisive girl. I ordered 2 chicken, 2 pork, and 2 Asada tacos to take for take out. While waiting I was able to scope out the graffiti art and plenty of seating. Love the touch of art cause it reminds me of tacos places back in CA. Looks like they will have daily specials but that looks to be pending. My 6 tacos took only 5 mins which was definitely not a wait at all. And while I was waiting was able to see they make all there own tortillas in house. It came in a huge take out bag. Love that they put one of each taco in each box without me having to tell them. I have to say I love the Tortola. It's not the typical Mexican kind but it's soft and when eating a taco it keeps everything together. First time I ate a taco and didn't make a mess. We started with the chicken and it wasn't so flavorful. Not sure why some people said it's salty. It needed more of something else. We both like the pork tacos and felt the carne asada tacos should not have been shredded but more grilled and chopped. This definitely was not Mexican street tacos but it was still good. It was around $19 for 6 tacos which was pricy but I get it it's downtown so everything tends to be more. I would definitely be back to try the daily specials! I saw torta on the menu and really hoping to try that. I think they should lessen the tacos to $2.50 I think more people would come because honestly the tacos are kind of small. Heck they should have a Taco Tuesday special!

I had the pleasure of having Sarah wax my brows.  We started off the appointment with her coming up with a detailed plan for my brows, which made me feel very confident in her hands. I've had eyebrow horror stories before, so it was especially nice to have that open communication and know what to expect.  It is a tad more pricy to have your brows done here than any run of the mill place, but you definitely get a higher quality outcome. - also was very thoughtful of Sarah to send me a personalized thank you card. 10/10 will return!

The reviews on here were pretty much right on.  Its a bit loud inside, but not unbearable.  The prices are high, esp beers, but I guess that's to be expected in CH.

That said, the short rib pizza was great and a nice change of pace and the cheesesteak spring rolls were delicious too.

EDIT
My 2nd trip here was just so so.  Got the same menu items.  I really think this place is overpriced for what you get.  Cheesesteak springrolls are great, but tiny for $10 or whatever the cost.

My sandwich was okay. The roasted red peppers, portobello and goat cheese is one of my favorite combos but the sandwich was cold and the baguette had a bit of a freezer burn taste. I think if the sandwich had been toasted after being assembled, it would have been much better. The fries were fresh  and hot but a bit undercooked but not too bad. The waiter seemed rushed even though the place wasn't busy and got my drink order wrong but did come by to check on me throughout the meal. I think dinner would be much entree here. For $8 though, a pretty good amount of food.
As an update my car is in for repair. Stew has apologized and was very upfront. So hopefully the repair goes well and car sense comes through so I can raise my review to give stars.

It has been a while since I visited this place. I didn't find their food to be anything special despite good reviews. Service was great but you pay for it because they are not affordable. I dined out once and haven't gone back since then.

We came on Saturday afternoon for a tasting and it was very crowded, loud and uninviting. Took us at least 20 minutes to get someone's attention, then get their attention every time we were ready to move to tasting the next wine. Not a very good experience.

The service was bad I never got a server had to find one . Then I never once got asked for a refill Watched a table come in before me order and get their food  and was told by the waiter since the big table ordered before us they have to get their food first . That's horrible customer service when we was already at the table for 20 minutes before they even came in .I don't care about a discount I care about good service .I sat at the table for an hour and fifteen minutes before I said can I just get it to go so all in out without a packed out restaurant it took me 2 hours to get my food . I'll never eat here again. They didn't care about making us happy . It was more so it is what it is " I didn't see y'all" So since you didn't see us It's not your fault ? And only took 6 dollars off ? Don't eat here

Awesome home made ice cream. The real deal.  Friendly staff too and great selection.  If you want incredible home made stuff, this is the place to go. You will be happy you came here rather than some of the national chains.

This taco bell sucks a## there was 3 of us and all the food was cold. I mean all of it was cold.  This was not perfectenschlog. Then to make it even better 2 husky women just destroyed the rest room smelled like homeless mixed with shit threw out the whole place was just bad and I love taco bell :(

Tiffany would be horrified if she witnessed the incompetence of her staff on the morning of 3/25.  I got so fed fed up watching every order before mine get screwed up and then re-made.  I finally walked out even though I paid.  Never coming back to this store.
I love this place.  It's a really authentic feel with the cushions on the floor that you sit on, the decor, music, wait staff, food, etc.  It's a really good place to go on a date or with a group.

It was good.  Not as good as I'd hoped, but not as bad as I expected.  Food was solid and plentiful but the coffee left much to be desired.  Coffee is important in my world, especially in the mornings after a night of too many cocktails.  I had the Southern Fried Chicken Salad which was delicious.  The chicken was crispy and well seasoned, but I'm not sure I appreciated the black eyed peas on it.  It gave the salad a strange sweetness (there was sweet cornbread croutons and sweetened pecans too) that made the salad  a bit unbalanced imho.  My husband had the traditional eggs Benedict and his only complaint were the potatoes which I believe were seasoned with rosemary and not much else.  Our server was a bit distant but not so much that we had to send out a search party.  Overall, if I were in Reno, I'd visit them again, but maybe for lunch or dinner.
This spot is great! Delicious, fresh seafood. I got the snapper and it was flaky and cooked perfectly. For a new operation, the service was spot on. I'll be coming back.

This is place is not professional at all we been living there for 4 months and it was hell I don't ever want people to experience what we have experienced right now we have a eviction on us because we've been trying to get out the apartment I don't understand how people that's suppose to be professional actually have you sign a 60 day notice to vacate and then In 2 weeks you already have a eviction notice the day we put in our 60 day notice is the day they filled the eviction on us and we are being sued we were never late on rent accept til May we suffered so much living in this apartment we also let the people know about the roaches i had to move a stove and get behind there and put acid on the floor our first day there we woke up to a cockroach on our dresser and we're clean people it takes them a whole month to do pest control all they do is put a piece of paper up underneath the stove and charge $15 dollars the windows aren't sealed correctly so all our air going outside The sliding doors don't stay closed it was a hazard to stay in that apartment we became sick for 2 weeks due to something going on with the air we even told the people their were bees getting in the apartment from up under our stove which wasn't even connected to the wall correctly every night we came home to cockroaches in our kitchen coming up from under our stove after I put down the acid we didn't see anything no more till recently with the need I can't wait to get this to the news and social media to have them look into this complex basically messing up people's lives to where they can't even rent a apartment or house trying to get away from hell we went in to see how much we owed to the complex everything jumped to 2k they didn't even know our total they just wrote something on a sticky note and gave it to us nothing was even written in paper I'm only 21 years old now with a eviction on my record just starting life over this mess we're struggling to find somewhere to actually live

Really love their pulled pork, ribs and brisket.  I found their chicken to be a bit bland.  This is nothing a little salt and pepper can't fix though, especially when it's falling off the bone.  I also wonder if it's bc I had their special sauce vs their original sauce.

Sides can be a bit hit and miss.  The mac and cheese is AWESOME as are the yams.  The potatoes,  mixed veggies and cornbread all leave something to be desired.  I'd pass on those.

All in all, really yummy food and fast delivery.   I'll keep their number on hand.

I came to thins place because I wanted to go to a new place for wine and cheese that was not your ordinary place.  I searched in yelp for such a place and found Bacchanal.  After reading a couple of reviews I told a friend that I had found a new place for us to discover.  

As we were parking the car I wasn't sure i was in the right place.  The outside looks nothing like the wine and cheese place you would expect.  But don't get thrown off by the outside looking, you will regret not coming in!

You first enter this weird old looking store, where you will find a wide variety of wine.  You can choose whatever wine you want, there is also beer and other options, and then in a small fridge there are also fantastic options of delicious cheese.  

i love the fact that you can choose whatever cheeses you want and then just go to the register and pay! i highly recommend getting the cheeses with the bread and the olives!  Once you have payed they will ask you were you will be sitting outside.  When we went there we didn't know we had to chose a table outside before buying the wine and cheese, so i suggest finding a table first and then going back in the store

The outside is beautiful and unique.  All the tables and chairs are different.  The lights and the torches give the place a nice touch.  And the live jazz music is great!  A server will find you and you can enjoy your cheese! it is truly great!!!  There is also a menu of food you can choose from, which we didn't try but looked delicious.  

I will most definitely be back to try the ceviche and other menu dishes that looked amazing!

I would say this salon is just ok. I bought a groupon that was for a massage, facial, and mani/pedi. I have to say the girl who did the facial was excellent. She also massaged wonderfully. I believe her name is Sarah. The massage was terrible compared to the many I have experienced. The girl, Desiree, was extremely light handed which is unusual for a massage therapist. She also talks to you excessively throught out the session. She even stepped away from me to look outside the door(for no apparent reason). Unlike any other therapist and is very nosy with some of the questions she asks you. The owner did my manicure which was sloppy I was surprised considering it was the owner. The person who did my pedicure did a fine job though. They are reasonably priced I would recommend the facial if you go. I gave this three stars but only because the facial was soo good otherwise it would be 1 star.

I love the downtown location, so we figured give this one a try. First off way closer and better parking so thats nice! We had a large table and they were excellent in giving us two servers. (I think with mandatory 18% tip any place should) HOWEVER they broke us into 2 tables which honestly sucks. At a big table you have a hard time talking to all your friends at 2 tables it is impossible  But like I said the service was good and the food was great! IMO you cannot go wrong with Cajun. The habanero sauce is awesome, but be careful it is hot! The place is very nice inside. You can only hear the bayou music if you use the washroom, but it is a nice touch. The prices are a tad steep but mostly worth it. I would def go again!
Great place! Very clean and professional. Great food, lots of different choices. Especially for the vegetarian folks  and FREE ice cream  I will be going back!

The Ridge was recently at a Taste of West Nashville festival, and without going to that we wouldn't even know this place was there. It's tucked just a bit down 54th, but not too visible from  Charlotte Ave.

Stopped in for dinner with the better half, my parents and a friend. Had the Prime Rib Sandwich and Tater Tots.  Both were pretty good and were ready pretty quickly.  Though at that time there was only one other person in the restaurant. 

I didn't have any of the Barbecue but I did have some of the BBQ sauce with my Prime Rib and it was pretty good. 

Some of the potential cons about the location are the parking and the location. The lot is very small, and making a left onto Charlotte during many times of the day could be a near suicidal move. 

I certainly plan on returning to try some of the other dishes.

Soooooo delicious! Their burgers are to die for! I always order the bacon cheesy with fried onions. And the milkshakes... Let me tell you about the milkshakes. I am not a milkshake kinda gal but i get one from here everytime! Strawberry is my fav but I've never been disappointed with any of them that I've tried.

It's interior is modern and sleek. Different fron most burger places.

Get ready to wait a long time and hope your food is accurate. 

Tonight I came to founding farmers for my 5:15 reservation. We were very excited to try FF for dinner. When we arrived we were greeted by the manager who explained how the place works and gave us water and told us the waitress will be with us soon. 10 minutes later the waitress came to take our drink order. We ordered drinks and apps and about 15-20 minutes went by and we had not gotten either or seen our waitress. We finally got our apps before our drinks. The drinks came out about 5 minutes later and they forgot one of our members drinks which we asked if we could receive it. It never showed after 10 min and it was a simple soda water. We asked for it and I asked for another cocktail, another 5-10 min go by and the soda water came out but not my cocktail. The entrees then came out and I ordered the prime rib and replaced the veggies with mac n cheese. They ended up replacing my mashed potatoes with mac n cheese and gave me the veggies anyway and also forgot my au jus. I asked for my mash and au jus and they said it was on the way. It didnt show up for 10+ minutes. My food was pretty cool at this point but I ate it anyway and it was delicious luckily. We finished and ordered dessert and paid. I never did get my cocktail after mentioning it a couple more times.

All in all, the food is the only thing that saved this because the mess ups and service was horrendous.

The only reason I am giving a 3-star is because I came here for brunch last week which was excellent cause go figure...I served myself at the buffet.

Save yourself some stress and wait a couple months before trying, the food is great and the drink I had was also good.

I normally love Steak n' Shake but this one is just awful. If you dine in they ignore you, if you go through the drive-through it will take at least 15 min on a good day, and you won't get what you ordered. Went today hoping it would be different but I sat for 17 minutes, and half of my order was wrong. When I went back through to get it fixed I sat for another 15 min, my food got stale, and I ended up just leaving because I didn't have time to wait on what I ordered in the first place. Don't recommend it at all. I live down the street and would rather drive to Carmel....

If there was a way to give zero stars, I would. Sat out on the balcony with my wine group. Definite lack of shade so if you sit there, bring a hat, umbrella, cabana boy with a large fan, go nude! All I could smell was the popcorn from the cinema below. Staff seemed apathetic, low energy. Almost had to do cartwheels to get my order taken. I could've easily walked out without paying. Extreme disappointment. I'll never return.

After a surly greeting at the door by Jeff, the owner, we were disappointed to learn that they were not serving brunch on Mother's Day.  When we elected to leave in search of Brunch, Jeff and a defensive team were accusatory instead of apologetic.  They began with "we called everyone to tell them."  When that proved false they informed me I made the reservation today...also not true.  Jeff then schooled me that they serve dinner only on all holidays.  Thank you for an education I will not soon need. All it would have taken was a sincere apology for having inconvenienced previous guests on a holiday meal.  

We are blessed to have many choices of where to spend our time and money, sad to feel compelled to take a favorite off the list...
Came in on sunday 5/22/16 was going to have dinner but service sucked so bad decided to go elsewhere. Was not even busy.  Now i remember why i dont go to applebees!!

We have been to the Atlantic Kitchen a few times now.  The fish and chips, with dressing and gravy is so good, although the last visit saw that the fish was pretty greasy.  Then again, its definitely not what you'd eat if you were dieting.  It was still delicious.  Nice that they have Newfie cold plate available for take out (or dine in) and Jiggs dinner, though I do think they are a bit steep in their pricing.  I know how much it costs to make a whole dinner. 
The staff is always pleasant though they tend to be short staffed during peak times, and the 'seat yourself' thing is kind of awkward.  If it is busy, you're left to stand at the door and wait.  No differentiation between the 'lobby' and the restaurant and no benches to wait on.  Would serve them better to have a hostess or a waiting area at least.  
The parking lot is difficult to access if you are travelling north on Hwy 2.  You have to circle back to get in, though that is through no fault of the restaurant.  The parking lot is also a bit of a disaster.
They have a very nice selection of Newfoundland treats and grocery items that you can't get in stores out here.  The decor is welcoming and the Newfoundland music is nice and not too loud. 
When we are looking for an nice, authentic, east coast meal.  This is the next best thing to cooking your own.

I've been a ride of die with Condado since 2014 when they opened their first location in Columbus. While they have pivoted their menu to be more signature taco offerings than build your own..I'm not complaining one bit. 
This is my go-to lunch spot when I am craving tacos. 
The service for lunch is always super fast, although I usually sit at the bar and few people do so that could be a factor. 

Any of the dips (queso, guac, salsa) are all ready to go so they come out super quickly when you order them. 

My new favorite taco suggestion: the Blue Dream which comes with ground beef as the protein and features a double cool ranch Doritos shell!

So disappointed.  I wanted it to be good - I really did.  We tried every beer they had and the only one that was any good was the stout.  Bartenders weren't very warm to us, but seemed to be chummy with the regulars.  Bonus plan, we saw the cops chase someone into the brewery and arrest him while we were there.

Love coming to the Adobe Cafe!

Seems a bit steep as far as price goes, but I've been here several times with a Groupon. Never been disappointed. The food is amazing, the servers are always a delight (with a small exception to their PR/CSR lady who comes around during meals asking you to fill out a survey). The Margarita's are great and you get free chips and salsa.

The meal portions are big; I've yet to finish a full meal there without needing a doggy bag. The duck taco's are my personal favorite, but everyone who's eaten with me there has gotten something amazing.

The atmosphere is nice, and the restaurant is surprisingly big on the inside. Never had to wait in line to get a seat.

I'd definitely recommend Adobe Cafe to anyone that liked Mexican.
...the day's caprese omletee, a "yes" (fresh mozzarella, a nice hearty touch)....the bacon,  -banging-...service, very cool and attentive...shall return...

This was my go-to for a while and I really loved it here! However the last time I went I had one of the men do my nails (apparently it was his first at this particular salon) and I just wasnt in love with like I usually am with my nails afterwards. I get dip powder and he made them way too thick. He also would get up and leave for 10-20 minutes at a time?? Not sure what he was doing. I spent almost 3 hours here. And in the end he could not remember what type of nails he gave me and almost overpriced me for it.

Solid food but not by any means spectacular. Not even close to these 5 star reviews. 

Bowl had way too much rice as compared to meat/egg. Otherwise good and empanadas were solid.

Workers were nice, but constantly chatting amongst themselves without even checking up on me once. This was when the place was practically empty too. Always had to initiate contact for takeout bag, check, etc.
One of the best happy hours around. Plenty of half-price apps and pizzas to choose from. Great specials on beer and wine. Service always on point for me. Great for a date or hanging out with friends.

Super Tasty food, but it takes so ass long that it may not be even worth the wait if you want to eat within 20 minutes. 

I ordered the lamb roll and it was gigantic, delicious, and well priced, but I literally waited 30-40 minutes before it came out. It wasn't like they were super busy, but apparently the kitchen can not prepare more than one order at once. Maybe it's a sign of freshness that they cooked my lamb when ordered, but if i wanted to wait that long, I wouldn't have gone out to eat.

Well worth it!   I used to go to the one in Camden.  The one in Medford is a much nicer location.  So much better than Pats or Genos.  You need to get the pepper sauce to go with.  Come in on a Sunday and you get fresh cut rose.  And they have a frequent buyer card.  I am in love all over again!

I came here on a Sunday before leaving Santa Barbara and even though they did outdoor dining and to go orders it was BUSY! I got theState   Street Bagel my myself  and the 222 for my husband. 

The state street bagel was yummy! The bagel was LARGE with cream cheese, tomatoes, onions , lemon pepper and pickles. The 222 is two eggs any style, two links of sausage, bacon or two slices of ham and choice of two pancakes, two pieces of toast, french toast or any bagel. My husband did get the French toast! The food was so good! The food came in huge portions. I would totally recommend going! The only reason why this place lost a star is because I waited nearly 45 minutes for my food. I saw so many people come after me and order and leave way before me. The order was no complicated so I wasn't sure why mine took so long. We all had to wait outside the front of the restaurant for our orders so I did notice it because we were all in the same area waiting for our food other than that, the actual food was delicious!
The food was amazing and our service was outstanding our server Ruben was very attentive, and fast paced we will definitely will be coming back and requesting him every time. Awesome job!

I'm not going to give this place one star because I didn't eat anything. But here's my reason for the low rating:

We walked in and the place was a disaster. Empty boxes (I think) everywhere. Floor looked filthy. Table setup was disorganized too. 
Worst of all, we went to the restroom to wash our hands and there was no soap. A restaurant with no soap in the restrooms? So... How do the employees wash their hands?
Should be one star, but with all the good reviews about the food I'll go ahead and give them the benefit of the doubt.

Our daughter is in Kindergarten/pre-1st and we are very very happy with the education she is receiving. It is definitely a bit more academic than I was expecting but our daughter loves it. She is learning Spanish, French and Mandarin as well as the violin. We got her a violin for Christmas (used from another parent) and she does not want to stop playing it. We couldn't ask for more.

Tatar sauce scores a 9, Fish taco a 6. The batter  covered Fish and oil were fresh but bland. This was a food truck Covid-19 experience; and they did ok. Cashless didn't mean i didn't have to touch there devices. They had restaurant food serves gloves to use on there order kiosks, but no sanitizer available. The Unsalted & Unpeppered batter over cod is a 3 star performance in my book. I need some spice either on the fish or in the batter

I really want to like The Nook, I really do! But as many have mentioned their food is "okay", but not for the price point. I've been here a handful of times and have experienced their crab cakes, pork tacos, brat and even a few of their specials such as the jalapeno poppers and cod with quesadilla.
