I'll only be back here for the great beer at Lama Dog but sadly encourage others to BYOS (bring your own snacks). I highly recommend some sort of food happy hour or improving the quality of food. I have high hopes for you Nook!

The service was great and the food was good. It was noisy but we could have a conversation. The thing that makes it a won't return for all
Of us was the male and female shared bathroom. Definitely an  uncomfortable experience for all of us.

Great little takeout/delivery joint ran by some old ladies. They wear hair nets while cooking, which offers no value to this review but it appealed to my paranoid germaphobic self. They have daily specials written on a white board, there is also a fine print on the white board stating that since May 2010 all prices have been increased by $1. Even with the price increase, the portions are generous and the price is still fair. I called a couple times and no one picked up, then about 5 minutes after I gave up, my phone rang. It was the Thai 2 Go lady explaining that she had many calls and asked if I would like to place an order. (I don't know if them calling me back was a good thing or not. Prank callers, beware!) I called ahead for takeout and ordered the drunken noodles, spring roll and beef with Thai basil. It took about half an hour for my order to finish; I got there early and sat at one of the two patio tables outside. The drunken noodles were excellent and full of flavor; the beef Thai basil was skimpy on the beef but full of sauce and the spring rolls were average.  I'd definitely order from them again and next time I'll be sure to check out the daily specials first.

We recently bought a place in Clearwater Beach and were out exploring and rode the Jolly Trolley to Tarpon.  We wandered into this tavern and sat at the bar.  We had a wonderful, attentive server, Alissa, who explained the many beers (30) on tap and provided a little taste so we could decide which one.   We are new to the area and Alissa gave us so much information about everything around.  She told us about a wine tasting in July, and we are booking flights to come back.  

The dang dang shrimp was amazing and the grouper Reuben was excellent.  We will definitely come back to the Tarpon Tavern.

I visited this place for the 1st time last night. I was underwhelmed. I arrived at 8:30pm but was warned there was an hour wait. I was seated just a little after 9:30pm. My entire wait I looked at reviews and planned what I wanted to order later to find that the menu they were using was the "late night" menu or something similar with NOTHING I wanted and mainly sandwiches/burgers. So I paid $77 for 5-6 wings, 2 burgers and 1 jerk chicken sandwich that I only ate half of, 1 alcohol drink and 2 non-alcoholic drinks:/ The atmosphere was pleasant as well as the staff but I'd never return at night again for a sandwich when I want a seafood Caribbean dish.

The people that are giving this place 4 and 5 stars are either employees, liars, retarded, or all three. We ate there at 5pm on a Friday night and it was dead save for three hairy drunk gentlemen at the bar and 5 employees shooting the shit. I know, I know, we're dumb. Place is filthy. The floors, counters and kitchen area have visible filth on every surface. We ordered before we noticed it. We got a catfish po'boy and jambalaya. The cook didn't know what jambalaya was until the other cook pointed it out in a pot that was sitting on the stove over heat for god-knows-how long. He threw my fries into cold oil then turned on the heat. The fish was WAY undercooked. It was still cold in the middle despite having sat under a heat lamp for 8 minutes while the jambalaya was reheated. The jambalaya was reheated to an almost-luke warm and served with two pieces of untoasted, plain white bread. I sent my fish back to be cooked and my date poked at her mediocre jambalaya. When my sandwich came back it was cooked through but the bun was now chewy in a microwaved sort of way and the fries were ice cold. I  took a few bites because, frankly, we felt sorry for the staff and then paid and left. My stomach was out-of-sorts for the rest of the day. Avoid at all cost. BAD BAD BAD news all around.

Yep, it's Pancho's!  Let's see, you start by getting a tray.  Then you have the flautas, the chili rejenos, the tacqitos.  Then you are asked if you want beans and rice.  You move down the line to the 3 types of enchiladas.  Next up, there are tacos.  Finally, you get a cup for your drink and you pay.  At the table, you are brought chips and salsa, and if you need anything else, you turn on a lamp.  At the end, you are brought a basket of sopapillas.  Tell me it's not Panchos.
Excellent service! Staff is very good! I recommend trying the Oyster T.L.T. Glad I stopped by to try this place out! I will definitely be back!

Typical bar food (burgers and such). Came here on a Monday night to watch some football. Beer selection was decent but pricing wasn't on the cheap side. 

Nothing really stands out about this place imo. Nothing good on food that is. The sign for the restaurant does stand out though.

As Yelp already nicely puts for 2 stars..."Meh. I've experienced better."

The restaurant has potential, but they need to work on a few things. 

1. Update your menu on the web & in the restaurant. The Bridge Street menu still comes up. Also, some things were available that weren't on the menu. Have a chalkboard that lists "Today's specials..."

2. Please put a light in the vestibule. I'm not familiar with the area and leaving through a dark doorway made me feel unsafe. 

3. We placed three take out orders and waited well over an hour & had to wait at the bar. It wasn't crowded. Why tell your customers to wait there? Have chairs in the take out area. What if some customers have children with them?

4. The pictures on Instagram should have prices or at least say "this is a double order of..." Let people know if it's a real order.

5. The food is good. I do see potential in the place but would like to see more a la carte options. A few things for kids would be nice too.

I would go back if I didn't have to wait so long and if there were a few more options. Overall I'm not sure if it's a sports bar or lounge (a lot of tvs but loud music playing).

This was the worst $90 I have ever spent. The service was abysmal. Our server not only NEVER refilled our waters throughout the entire meal, but also never brought us the bread and butter that every other table received. Now, this was not a busy night, the dining room was essentially empty and when we arrived, our waitress' two other tables had already been served their meals. I hate to make assumptions, but we were the youngest there and were clearly not being treated as well as her other guests. I completely regret leaving her a 19% tip. I'm not even going to go deep into their food, which is horrible. Just a heads up, to this place, a few drops of mozzarella qualifies as cheese on a pizza. It was basically just burnt dough with a light drizzle of sauce. At $18 for an 11" pie, I'd expect a lot more. If you want to try good gourmet pizza, head to Slice or Birra which are both with in walking distance, have better service, and are much more adequately priced. Personally, I'd suggest anyone considering going here try Birra instead. They are 2 blocks away and have a much better menu.

Bridget is amazing- she did did an eye lash lift and tint and dermaplane for me and I couldn't be happier with the results. She explained the whole process and each step in detail, understood my concerns, and did a fantastic job. I look more refreshed and my make up goes on so smoothly now. Couldn't recommend her more!

This beef samosa appetizer was $8.99... you decide if they are worth that based off the pic... 

Also gave me the wrong item...

The other item in the order was a salad, no plastic fork or knives provided, let alone napkins...

For a take out order I would think that would come with a salad... co worker currently eating with toothpicks we found in the lunchroom

Went here for a cleaning because of convenience and he is in network.
First impressions, old dirty equipment, depressing atmosphere.
The dentist did the cleaning, and he was uncomfortable, aggressive, and could care less about the patients comfort. I was surprised he didn't wear a face mask, and his breath was appalling. Worst experience I've had at a dentist. I will never go back or recommend him.

I do not like the coffee. It leaves a bad aftertaste in my mouth which leaves me to believe the beans are old. 

The staff is super nice tho.

I'm going to leave a short review for this place because I just really like it! There are a few of them around the local area, and I've hit them up before. They do a decent job of making a semi-authentic taco. Love the horchata also. It's a little over-priced, in my opinion. One of my big pet peeves is the real taco trucks that sell tacos for around a $1 to $1.50, and other places have to sell them for like $3 or $4.
I will say, they are good, and larger than your usual taco truck taco. I also like the selection of protein, and they have nice vegetarian options as well. My favorite part might be the out door seating on a beautiful day. I would suggest coming here if you haven't! I think it's a very reliable place to have a good and reasonably authentic lunch.

Ayers is the worst place to take your vehicle the owner is really snotty and will have a huge repair list of what the car needs although dealership reciepts show I've kept up on maintence they will try and bang every dollar outb of you and give you a fake reason on why yo should change a part. Beware of this place and defiantly read the disclosure before you sign the line . Once was enough for me to avoid stick with tge dealership they don't have any idea how to properly fix a car.

The problems started with a (confirmed!) scheduled delivery for 8:30 the day before our wedding in Tahoe. After an hour of waiting, I called and the office and they couldn't tell me anything! Initially, she couldn't even find my order! The driver showed up at 10:30 without an apology or the dinner plates. (Not to mention the most clueless men I've ever met!) After calling the office, the owner had the nerve to ask if we could travel to Reno and pick up the plates. In the end, the plates didn't arrive in time for the rehearsal dinner.

The day after the wedding, everything was stacked and situated for the drivers to pick up. Not thinking they needed supervision, I told the gentlemen what to take and where it was. After the drivers left I noticed items they did not take. Before leaving for our honeymoon, we had to deliver the items to Reno- (completely unnecessary when you pay for delivery)! 

To top it off, the company had the nerve to only return $10 of my deposit. This company will never get my business again! Nor will I ever suggest this company to anyone!

Pleasantly surprised!  The previous restaurant in this spot was not good, so when it sold and got a make over, I was not expecting too much.  However, this new place has excellent pizza and good pasta dishes.  High quality ingredients are used for the pizza toppings, such as the sausage that is sliced in fairly large sized chucks, which I love.  The pizza has a terrific sauce and nice crust as well.  I do like a little Italian seasoning on top of my pizza, so I sprinkle that on at home when getting a delivery, which makes it even better.  The white pizza is great.  Super cheesy!  They have the best pizza out of all the places that deliver to my house in Pottstown.  The fettuccine alfredo is really good.  The sauteed spinach side veggie dish is very tasty with lots of garlic.  Delivery time has always been what they quoted, and the delivery people have all been friendly and courteous.  Overall, really good!

I went in with high expectations for this salon. I asked for a very short hair cut(almost a pixie). The hairdresser gave a great shampoo and conditioning and spent the next hour on my hair. The front looked good but I was left with a mullet in the back that I later discovered at home. Towards the end of the haircut another client walked in and I feel like that's why he rushed through to finish the job. He didn't even offer to dry or straighten my hair and only used products to style. I ended up walking out the door with semi wet hair in the early chilly morning. 
As it was the first time I had gone this short I was very excited to see the results, but all I could see at home was the ugly mullet that I had to cut with my own scissors. I've been growing my hair out ever since. The cropped haircut that I asked for could have been done much better.

This is the WORST Trash removal service I have ever encountered.  They miss 2-3 out of 4 pick ups a month. A bunch of deerhunter friends who decided to start a trash removal sercice. They need to go back into the deep woods where they belong!. Left out two small lampshades next to the trash and they never picked them up. Yesterday, while outside I literally watched them just use their pick up machine to pick up the can and left 2 leaf bags there. It was too cold for them to get out of their warm truck!
Disgraceful!!
NO CUSTOMER SERVICE!!!! THEY RARELY ANSWER THEIR PHONE!
I have left multiple messages over the past month and they never call you back. I called another trash company and the woman said we get calls and new customers from that company all the time complaining about their horrible service!
 They reel you in with a low price, trying to get as many customers as they can and forget about you! 
They only pick up the can and nothing extra. Do yourself a Huge favor and look for someone else!! 
When I told them I was dumping them and found a reliable and reputable company because they suck, he had the audacity to tell me they have won best cutomer service and other awards for the past five years! I had to stop myself from hysterically laughing! They only way they won an award was in their dreams cause in reality they are a nightmare ~!

In the MT Laurel area of NJ, I was impressed with little hole in the wall Italian place. Went for lunch and had a good salad and a Chicken dish that was prepared well. Well worth the visit and will go again when in the area.

New favorite seafood place. Friends recommended it and it was fantastic. The Chargrilled Oysters are out of this world! I could've had 10 more. Hands down best chargrilled oyster I've had. For 2.99, the Oyster Shooter is a no-brainer: freshly shucked, vodka and seasonings.
Service was fast, but we had to pry answers out of the waiter to get an idea of the local beers they served. The place was slammed on a Tuesday at 6p. When we left, the line to get in was down the block. Place was amazingly good!

Kim's nails are the best place in town to get a quick mani/pedi that is not so overpriced. I've been to many places in town and always come back to Kim's. Everyone is enjoyable and not too nosy like some places. 

It's best to make an appointment. Nonetheless they'll do their best if I just show-up. 

What more can I say then they're a friendly comfortable nail salon that does a good job for a reasonable price.

Unacceptable Customer Service. I purchased a pair of pants from this company earlier this week. However, before making the purchase I asked Tom B. (the sales clerk) what is your return policy? His response was this "as long as it is within 7 days, has original tags, and the receipt". To my shock when I returned the never used item this morning Dinette (todays sales clerk) said i was only to receive store credit. This was not made clear to me at the point of purchase. If it was, I would not have purchased the item. Moreover, Dinette continued to insult me as a customer, question my aptitude of memory, and demean me in conversation. In addition Dinette said "it says this on the receipt". How exactly am I supposed to read whats on a receipt before ai purchase a garment? Ultimately, This is absolutely unacceptable customer service and I urge everyone to stay clear of this repugnant establishment.

Although there isn't a store front, Lahpet does a pop up in markets or cafes in New Orleans. 

I can't get over the deliciousness I just consumed. I had the coconut chicken noodles (ono khao swe) for $11 which had delicious bits of soft chicken in a creamy coconut sauce garnished with garlic chips and a duck egg. This dish inspired me to do a Southeast Asia trip. Not kidding.
good selection of beer but lots of anti mask and otherwise disrespectful rhetoric overheard from customers and employees alike - should be called bold chauvinist instead.....

HORRIBLE EXPERIENCE!!!  Do not book through them. We later learned from others that this company  who is owned by James who lives out of state has a horrible reputation. What you see on their website and promised upon booking is not what you get.  All 11 of us who barely fit in our 14 passenger rental were picked up late for a wine tasting in a dirty old piece of crap Ford van which was UNSAFE. As soon as we got in it was clear this automobile had not seen an inspection and tune up  in years. It was shaking and bumpy but we chocked it up to the AGE of this jalopy and left optimistically anticipating our private 11 am wine tasting at Riverbench winery. Ten minutes later the van was careening all over the road until the driver finally realized we had a flat tire. By this time the tire was smoking and lunging toward the ditch before she pulled over. We then sat by the side of the 101 North for over 2 hours in the rain waiting for the "15 minute" relief driver who never showed and car to fix the tire.  The office and driver were ill equipped to handle this type of situation. They did not have any other vehicles available and did not call anyone to help us out. We had to call UBER and arrange OUR own rides back to our own vehicle and the winery where we had another limo company pick us up. This caused a break up in the group who was celebrating a 50th birthday so we ended up splitting up and having to meet up way later in the day.  We scrambled and booked another limo service with Jeff from SurfsUpLimo in Santa Barbara who saved the day and was a total professional gentleman!  Buyer Beware with Rock Star!  Maybe some of the 5 star reviews got the newer vehicles but any company who leaves 11 women stranded on the side of the road for over 2 hours in the rain with no back up plan probably doesn't follow a safety maintenance program so why risk it?  was very dangerous and beyond unprofessional on so many levels.

I got their shoyu ramen with pork shoulder. I was satisfied with my order. It tasted like regular shoyu ramen I order from any other restaurant. They give a lot of pork shoulder so for those of you who are always unsatisfied with 2 slices of pork belly and want something more filling, choose the pork shoulder for your option of meat. My friend got their ramen with tofu, and the inside of the tofu was still cold. When it came to paying the bill, they would not accept a $100 bill without the blue ribbon. In my opinion that was a bit ridiculous because they can just mark it up with one of those pens to identify if it's a real or fake bill. Other than the $100 bill experience, customer service was immaculate. Our servers were very nice and always checked up on us. Pretty average ramen that won't disappoint & good customer service.

This is my new favorite restaurant in Harahan. We got the coconut shrimp, pad see ew (with rice noodles instead of flat noodles), and honey basil chicken. Literally each dish wasn't just good, each dish was amazing. 

The shrimp was cooked perfectly and melts in your mouth. The pad see ew I loved because it wasn't too salty and wasn't heavy. I tend to stay away from noodle dishes since I typically find them to be too heavy for my taste, but that wasn't the case with the pad see ew at all. As for the honey basil chicken, it was hands down the best chicken ever and probably my favorite part of the meal (even though it's hard to choose because everything was delicious).

We took our order to go. The interior is on the smaller side but it's cozy. The employees were really friendly as well! Lastly, I also thought the pricing was really reasonable. For all three dishes, we paid just $37.

So like I side earlier, this is my new favorite restaurant in Harahan and I'm really excited about it!

I have used the King Dry cleaners for years now.  The owner and all of the employees are always very nice and respectful to me.  My clothes always come out perfect, on time, and I have never had anything damaged by them.  I can't make that same statement for other dry cleaners I have tried in the past in Reno.  Their prices are very reasonable, and their customer service is great.  I would highly recommend them if you want to have a great experience in fulfilling your dry cleaning needs!  Warren
Pricey menu for average tasting food. Service is decent, ambiance is very nice.
Really good pizza! I had their Goodfella's Style Hawaiian pizza and it was phenomenal. Waitress was super nice and food came out in a timely manor!
These were the best damn tacos!!!! It is now my life's mission to try all the tacos/ quesadillas on the menu. The flavors are absolutely amazing and I'll be back

Top notch.  Took service and food back to a time forgotten.   I wish many of the restaurants out there took a page from their book.  Our waiter Matt was best of the best.   Will make the trip back here form the northeast.

I don't see why this place is highly rated. I really didn't like it.
I checked it out one day because it is close to where I live. 

The customer service wasn't very good. The clerk seemed rather anti-social and not very talkative. I had to tell him 2 times what I wanted to eat before he heard me.

I ordered the burger which had hickory sauce and onions. Without the hickory sauce the burger would have lacked any flavor what so ever. I think that is why they doused it in sauce. The texture of the patties also threw me off. The fries were nothing special either. All the food here is frozen and NOT fresh.

This place had some entertainment value They had some video poker machines and a few arcade games. However the jukebox only had southern mullet music on it. I am not really that big of a fan of The Doobie brothers, Molly Hatchet, or Ram Jam.

In short there are way better burger joints in town!

I picked up a print of one of my paintings, I don't know their process, I'm usually going with a giclee art print, but it was done through a friend of mine in New Orleans, the colors were washed out, and nothing was done to make sure the colors were close to the original,  I'm in the process of a refund.  I wouldn't recommend anyone using this service for anything resembling art,  maybe some black and white flyers.  Never again.

Been here a few times and today, decided to get take out for the first time. The service is quick and friendly but prices have gone up recently and didn't realize it until I picked up my food. I love their queso though, that's why I like to go here. It's spiceier than most and that's why I like it so much. 

I got the usual entrees that I get and one side of beans. Usually these entrees are $8.99 a piece and have gone up to $10.99!!! My total was almost $30 and usually, I can get away Eating here pretty affordably. This was kind of a shock! They didn't skimp on chips but definitely skimped on the salsa. One tiny cup of salsa for two giant bags of chips. 

Hopefully next time is better.

This auto repair shop is the worst. I took a car there to have my air conditioning fixed because it intermittently switched to blowing out the slots used by the front window defrost instead of vents for passengers. They charged for diagnostic, worked on it, and it worked right for one day. 

Their tech told me that if the repair that they did the first time didn't fix it, they would have to take apart the dash to get at the air conditioning system and the repair would be $1500. I didn't bother taking it back to them. 

I did take it to Donovan's in Lutz. They found and fixed a bad solenoid, saved the bad part, showed me exactly where the problem was, and I was on my way for $200. 

This negative review  of Superior Auto in Wesley Chapel probably never would have been written except that we took my wife's car to the Honda dealership yesterday. My wife drives a CRV that Goodyear replaced a leaky radiator on. The Honda dealership reported to us that the bottom of her radiator was coated with epoxy. Just a coincidence, I'm sure. 

I would not go to this shop if they were the last place on Earth to get a car repaired.
Awesome place, clean excellent service ! Food outstanding ! If you are in Plant City you need to eat here!

We came here on Father's Day for brunch.  What a great place.  I made reservations online for 14 we ended up being a party of 12 and our waitress Caroline was amazing!  She really knew how to work a larger party.  She was very attentive, she made great suggestions and was very knowledgeable when we asked menu questions.  I really liked the interior.  We had a big table in front of the booths and it didn't feel cramped.  Not one of our party had any complaints about the food.  I tried the breakfast pizza which seemed to be going like hot cakes and the two eggs meal.  Both were so good no salt or pepper needed.  We got the bottomless mimosas I tried their syrups they were ok but I will stick with the oj while I'm there.  By syrups I mean you could get the flavored mimosas ie pomegranate or strawberry syrup that you pour into the champagne.  The one issue I had was that when I came in to check in at least 2 hostess passed me several times fixing tables and I think I stood there for close to 10 minutes before my name was taken.  Other than that the experience was great I will definitely be returning.

I really liked the food and coffee here. The staff was very knowledgeable and friendly and there was a variety of seating available. Unfortunately, the smoke from the smoke room spreads into the nonsmoking area of the cafe. I had to leave because I couldn't handle the constant smell of smoke and began to experience some problems because I have asthma. Other than that, this place was great!

Great place to take folks from out of town, a good Jewish deli experience, with the locals painted on the wall and the pickle bar in the back( which is just great!), and deli counter full if desserts to take home as well. They aren't always the best with being sent over to my gluten allergy, though generally they keep things straight.

This was the first restaurant I ate at while on my trip to Nashville. I was not prepared for the amazingness that happened! Since we were a party of 10 we decided to go with two pitchers of mimosas. We ordered the original and also the pineapple mimosa. I preferred the pineapple mimosa just because it was a little sweeter and I tend to like sweet drinks. If you enjoy Bloody Mary's, you will love their version which is made with sake instead of vodka. 

We were warned before ordering that our food would not come at the same time since we had such a big party. Honestly, that was not a major issue. The staff was very accommodating and the wait time in between when they brought our orders was not long enough for us to notice.

I ordered the Germantown West Benny. This was eggs Benedict topped with salmon, spinach, and hollandaise sauce. It also came with a side of potatoes. A friend at my table ordered the Banana Foster Pancakes which were to die for. I'm not a fan of pancakes but even I could not resist these. Someone else at our table ordered the blackened fish with grits. The fish was flavored well and even had a slight kick from the spice. 

We all loved the food so much that we passed our plates around because we just had to try everything. Overall, this was a pleasant experience since we received phenomenal service and ate delicious food! If I'm ever visiting Nashville again I know where to come for a great meal.

Food was good. I had heard a lot of rave reviews, and I was a little disappointed. The chicken was very average, nothing special, I could have made it myself. The filet mignon was excellent. The veggie sides were great too. My biggest complaint was that the whole restaurant smelled of sewage. It was so strong that I kept expecting someone to acknowledge it "oh sorry about that smell, it's not normally like this, we are having a plumbing issue...". I would give it another try.

The brisket here is by far some of the best I've ever tasted. It's super tender and you get to sauce it up however you please.

The brisket cheesesteak might have put me over the edge. It's a hoagie roll lathered in butter, toasted and then brisket, green peppers, onions and nacho cheese is added. Super healthy ;) I luckily we had to walk a few blocks after this lunch to make me not full. 

Truly great place to go for lunch! 

TIP: They change their sides daily so you can't get the tots and Mac and cheese every day - I went two days in a row and most of the side menu had changed.

Before I begin this was my personal experience and I gotta be honest when it comes to my review. I recently had my wedding reception here at Livyo's and it was perfect for the intimate micro wedding that we had. The food I must say was really good especially the shrimp and grits and the curry chicken and her pineapple sweet tea, yes you read right pineapple sweet tea it was soooo tasty and refreshing and was a huge hit with our guests. The buffet layout was great I loved the donut wall but expected homemade donuts instead of store brand(but whatever we still made it work) and the staff was very attentive as well was the owner Lennise. There was even an area in the rear side of the building that allowed for us to have a photo booth. As I shared this is a very nice spot/hidden gem if I must say and great  if you're looking to have something intimate which we were. The reason for the 3 star is because of the heat. The a/c didn't work properly and it was super hot inside. I must say the owner did attempt to add fans but it only circulated more heat as well as one of my guest ran to the store to purchase 2 additional fans as an attempt to cool it and just to make the best of the current situation. Unfortunately we ended the reception early because of the heat. I'm sure that this isn't an everyday issue but just a word of advice I felt as though maybe a greater attempt should have been made to correct the a/c issue such as calling someone to repair it because we were there the night before decorating and had advised the young lady that it was extremely hot and to find out the next day it was just as hot was disappointing. I'm quite certain any other bride may not have been as nice and understanding as I, but hey there are other major things in the world at this time going on but I am only sharing my experience and other than that it was nice and I would recommend for catering only.

We ordered to-go. I have to give you guys one star. Why you ask? Well, I ordered bun cha gio which is vermicelli with eggrolls. Please tell me why my eggroll had no meat ? Go to ANY Vietnamese spot and order "bun cha gio" I guarantee you it will have meat in the eggroll, if it's called an eggroll. I can confidently say I didn't order vegetarian eggrolls. Now, my hubby ordered a BBQ rice dish and he was like MEH. Yep, won't be back. BTW... we are Vietnamese. I know my Vietnamese food.

Soup was $9 for water and 2 pieces of rubber chicken and $40 for 2 not even half filled containers
Very disappointed and very poor quality of food
Stay away from this overpriced low quantity restaurant
I guess I won't have minded so much if the food was at least Good.
BUT NO FAKE THAI FOOD

Man these steaks are monstrous.  My wife and I split a large and then lugged a large italian grinder back to Denver.  Awesome hot sweet peppers, tons of chip selection and a friendly staff.  Fairly close to the airport if you're on your way to or from West Chester.  They'll wrap it for travel for a small fee.

The service is so slow. Tables sit open while there is a wait out the door. The kitchen sends things out incorrectly. They deliver food piece meal to tables. 30 minutes after we ordered our waiter thanked us for our patience and told us it would at least be another 10 minutes. The same thing happened the first time we came here but we decided to try it again, we won't be back to eat. The positives are the coffee bar is awesome--the lavender London fog is my favorite. Our waiter, Eric was also very nice.

Finally had the chance to make a visit! The food lived up to the hype, the service, unfortunately - did not. My daughter placed an order and the description on the menu didn't match what she was given. I politely asked to swap out the appetizer. When the bill came, both items were there. I asked that they remove the initial order and was met with resistance it. Eventually it was taken off but I can't remember the last time we've dined out and needed to make the case for something not being charged. Gratuity is also added for 5 or more people. 3/5 were children. Again, while the food was good, I was so very disappointed with the service. I'm willing to try again down the road but not in the near future.

Prior to what I'm about to say, I've eaten here once before and had lunch. The crawfish bread was amazing. This time I came here on a dessert date and what we ordered was pretty mediocre in taste considering how much everything cost. The cookies tasted like regular, smegular, cookies and the dessert at the bottom was pretty much tasteless. They have other spectacular bakeries all over NOLA to try out than this overpriced place.

Front desk staff was great. The lobby is very appealing. Most of the top travel sites rate it as 2.5 stars....That's a stretch....I stayed for 5 nights....Didn't have any issues yet with my truck in the parking lot yet there is always local traffic and random cars in and out of the parking lot...People have left old furniture in the dark areas of the property..Local dumping ground I suppose. ..There are security gates that are always open and can not be closed.  the landscaping is very basic....The outdoor areas are basic and had alot of people outside enjoying a cocktail every night people were out until 1 and 2 in the morning having conversation and enjoying cocktails...
This Hotel seems to be a Day Vacation for locals....Ice machines were few and far between and I would here people getting Ice at all hours of the night.....However everyone was cool....  The property could actually be really cool with the layout. The pool is decent and is used frequently......The rooms need alot of upgrading...Beds were decent and the cleaning staff did a great job. I have stayed at alot worse places for 65 bucks.....Yet if I was going to rate it I would give it 2 stars.....If I was in a pinch I would stay here again but it wouldn't be my first choice.
Caesar salad was gross and fries were like rubber bread was hard. Would have been better if I was high.

We are not even hungry but our kids wants to try this place. Skeptic at first that this will be the same chinese resto "taste the same" (stereo typing). Well guess what ??? It not!!! The food really taste so good. We have been looking for ramen place and their ramen taste good, pork dumplings are good, veggie spring and kung fu lomien hhhhmmmm yummmy. People are friendly which is always nice in a restaurant.
Yum! Great atmosphere and location. Very unique experience. You won't be disappointed

Small, no-frills place with plenty of seats and cheap eats.  The people running it seem very nice, as well.

I got the coconut pork and salmon (I cannot remember the names) with rice.  The food has a homemade feel to it that I enjoy, but I'll admit it's not the best I've had.  A lot of sea food options for pescetarians.  Portions were plenty--combo was enough for two meals.

They sell bakery sweet buns!  I took a pandan bun to go on my way out.
I'll be back as I take classes nearby.

*accept credit card!

The meal was delicious, and the service was very fast even with the long line.  The cheddar bacon hot dog was a little undersized, but had great flavor.  The chilli cheese fries and Chicago combo were spot on and just amazing.  I wish that it was closer, as it is a little bit of a hike from Lakeland.  Can't wait to come back.

Saw the episode on Diner's Drive-Ins and Dives featuring this place. Was in Philly overnight and came here for an early dinner instead of doing the Philly Cheesesteak...and I'm glad I did. 
Their menu is unique for a bar.  The chicken pie is different from any pot pie you'll ever have. Lord Chesterfield Ale, made by Yeungling is always on tap out of a converted old refrigerator, along with a nice variety of craft beers.
Parking can be a challenge, I recommend public transportation (UBER). The place is clean with a nice dark wood finish throughout. The staff super nice and they know the menu and the beer selection well.
This place can make you forget all about the Philly Cheesesteak.

Freshest, creamiest ice cream you will find in the South Jersey area at some very good prices, along with some neat music. They have plenty of flavors as well as gelato, which you can enjoy at the tables outside. Be sure to bring cash since I don't think they accept cards. I will be returning very soon to sample more flavors!

Grateful Gardens always has very satisfying food for me. They satisfy my need to not have gluten. In addition they have vegan as well as paleo. They really go out of their way to be able to satisfy any dietary need. I almost never vary from what I order. Their grilled cheese sandwich has an amazing flavor. I add onion because melted cheese and onion just taste good. This time I decided to have the ham sandwich. It was wonderful. I also added a cup of the totally tomato soup. This isn't the grade school tomato soup that I learned to detest. This is flavorful with big chunks of tomato.

This is a review for a Friday night. Maybe the weekdays are better...but I doubt it.  Let me preface this by saying that I have been working in the restaurant biz for 15years in Philly.  High volume, corner bars and fine dining you name it, I have worked there.  
  I met up with six friends on a Friday for my friends 31st birthday.  Brauhaus was busy, I expected as much.  We wanted to sit down and have dinner before we continued on with our bar crawl, but could not be accommodated, which was fine.  So we crowded around a tiny high top.  I ordered the parsnip soup, which was bland and tasted like hot heavy cream.  Also it took 30min to get to me, after I had to remind them of the order by tracking down a group of servers standing around the computer stations.  I would love to have told someone how much I didn't enjoy the soup, but not once was I asked. So fuck it. We also ordered a  charcuterie and cheese board.  Only the meat was explained.  Luckily I was able to explain most of the cheese to the table. The charcuterie was delicious as was the cheese. 
    We had been at the restaurant for two hours while at least two tables sat empty.  That was when someone from our party went up to the hostess to inquire if we could have one of the empty tables.  She explained to him that there were reservations coming in soon and an average party of seven takes 2 1/2 hours to eat dinner.   If that's your turnover rate for a Friday night, you must be bleeding money, and your servers aren't doing their job! 
  Luckily the high top next to us finished and paid. But when I asked a server if I could pull the high top over to accommodate our party, she quickly told me that the three top two tables over was still using it and rushed off.  They weren't.  I feel bad for the establishment.  They definitely missed out on at least a $300 tab that would have been in and out in under an hour.  I left to go to work hungry and the rest of the party moved on to tatood moms where I'm sure they had a blast.

Tonight was my first and last time here. Aside from the owners somewhat witty responses to negative reviews, all negative reviews hold validity. This place sets a great example to why the game of billiards will die generationally. We are all in our 20s and were met with an obvious attitude bias towards our age. The older man working there (who seems to be the owner) has a short bitter attitude that creates an unwelcoming atmosphere. First words when we came through the door was "no outside food or drink. No hoodies. And ID". I am used to bitter old men. But what is a bother is watching as he proceeds with more concern, attention and genuine customer service towards elder customers whilst continuing to be shallow with any new younger customer. May sound petty but it is something you have to see to believe. You may have been in business for 25 years, but your customer base is changing to younger generations. You WILL NOT continue a successful trajectory unless you can drop the ego, and be more welcoming and warming to the customers that are supporting you. I'd say this to you in person if I had the chance. However you seemed more concerned with finishing the tab and continuing conversation with the 2 ratchet drunk girls at the bar. Maybe you got lucky. Or maybe they leaned on a pool table and spoiled the night for you.

Losing stars only because tr place is getting very very DIRTY. As we were leaving today, all employees behind the bar were chillin' on their phones. Time to lean time to clean.

Aside from the need to disinfect your seat and area the seats are the best in Tampa, food good, not as good as the original restaurant but acceptable, drinks good. Usual movie goodies seem to be cheaper here too which is a bonus -

But for goodness sake CLEAN this PLACE!

My partner is a recent transplant from out of town and told me he has yet to explore Tucson's core and south side.  Sensing the ample opportunity, I decided to introduce him to what Tucson is particularly known for, her Mexican food.  And what better place to visit first than a place that has never let me down, El Minuto Cafe.

Tucked away in a local barrio, this gem is known as a tourist spot, given it's location across the street from the convention centre.  Naturally it can get very crowded and very loud; last nite it had winded down, which was to my delight as I wasn't in too much of a mood to deal with loud crowds.  Our host was considerate enough to seat us in a secluded corner of the room, with a wall to my back, something that I ask for after seeing so many mob movies.  My partner commented on the decor, saying it reminded him of Rocky Point and it's festive atmosphere.

Not even a minute after we were seated did our server receive our drink orders, iced tea; and her busser brought the standard chips and salsa.  Chips are crisp and not too thick, salsa was spicy but not overpowering to where the spice numbs your pallet, rendering your meal as a painful experience.  On the contrary, it finished very nicely, very clean after tasting.  I ordered three beef tacos with rice and beans, he ordered shredded beef enchiladas with rice and beans.  

About ten mins later, our food arrives hot and pleasing.  My tacos are fried to a crisp, I ordered patty tacos, my preference, but they will make a shredded beef taco if you want.  They're towering with lettuce and unmelted cheese, allowing me to distribute cheese as I see fit before it finally melted completely.  Add a little of the finely finished salsa and I'm in culinary heaven.  Beans is topped with melted jack cheese, rice is fluffy but not dry.  

Our added bonus, our server left us a full pitcher of iced tea for refills, something I appreciate as I get annoyed if a server intrudes on my meal too often.  In my opinion, an excellent server can walk the fine line between bothering too much and ignoring their charge.  Score one for El Minuto for having such a professional server.

My partner was very pleased with his meal, but seeing how he has his own Yelp account, I'll let him write his own review.  

I would like to thank El Minuto Cafe for serving my partner his first local Mexican meal that isn't from a food truck or fast food joint.  He was pleased and so was I, once again you did not disappoint.  Maybe that's why everytime I dine out with friends or family, the first place on my mind to take them is El Minuto, or as I lovingly call it "the minute cafe".

This place is a bit worn out. The furniture is old. It has the feeling as if someone left their living room unkept for many months. The coffee is decent and it definitely has a cool neighborhood vibe. With a little updating, I think this could be a great coffee house

I researched a bunch of different companies in SJ and got multiple quotes and Ajecks was by FAR the most reasonable price. Andrew was very accommodating and squeezed us in so we could get new home owners insurance. His foreman Charles and the rest of the crew were super friendly and did a wonderful job! They even took pictures of the rotted wood so we could see what plywood they replaced. I would absolutely recommend Ajecks to anyone looking for great work at a great price!

Sat on the patio Friday night and had horrible service. The waitress even knew she was screwing up because she kept apologizing. Food was good. We MIGHT return but we would definitely ask for a different server. Good thing we didn't sit inside because the music was insanely loud.
bangkok cafe is the bomb.  love this place love the curry and thai ice tea

Your typical tourist attraction without the real "attraction". I was looking for gifts to take home so I did not purchase any of the perishable items (there is fruit and veggies as well as hot food being sold there). With that said, I did find some nice local artwork. Mission accomplished.

At Peddler's village.  Decided on this place. And was not impressed but I was not completely disappointed. 
For starters the wings. Very good. Nice size wings and i had mild and it was just right. Season and the skin was a golden crisp. 
The pot pie was very good. Different from the traditional pot pie inside a crust. But it was really good. The ribs. Sad to say was not there.  The sauce is horrible but the ribs were actually good without the sauce. I'd suggest you get the ribs with sauce on the side. And judge it that way.  

Over all a pleasant visit. Passed on desert to have at another shop in this Peddler's village

I've come to this car wash a handful of times and I've always been kind of disappointed. The last two times I've been here, no one even indicated when my car was finished and just parked it in a lot around the corner where it wasn't visible. I ended up waiting almost 30 minutes for my car when it was just waiting in the parking lot the whole time. 
As I got home, I noticed that they barely even wiped down the interior (which I paid for). The cup holders were dirty, the front console was still dusty and there was still trash in the side of my door. It's disappointing to pay $30 to get my car cleaned when I have to go home and finish cleaning it myself. I probably won't be returning.

Had the perogies with sausage, just average .
The hamburger was a frozen patty, no taste, for juice. Sent it back, they took it off the bill. Won't come back here

I brought my dog here today because there was a possible infection in his leg. The staff was very courteous on the phone. Then when I got there they were incredibly efficient. I was in and out of there in 45 minutes. Everyone explained things clearly. The cost was reasonable for a Sunday /After hours visit. The care was good, and they treated my dog very well.

It would be a 5 star brick oven pizza place, if you're rating just the pizza, the place is dirty, if you go to the clean plates website they failed due to mouse and bug droppings along with a dozen other dangerous violations. I'm not going back till they clean up their act!

This is a super cute store! I think they are just getting started, so there isn't a huge amount of product available, but what they have is displayed really nicely and the prices are AMAZING. I'm talking like cute shower curtains for $2, small area rugs for $10, electric blankets for $35, things like that. Lots of couches, chairs, barstools and other things for the home. Really quality and well-curated items for pennies on the dollar.

If you are on a budget and you're looking for home goods, I would definitely recommend visiting Revibed. They offer open-box new items, like shelving and cabinets from Target's Threshold brand. They also offer unique items that have been thrifted or acquired and refurbished. 

It was a little difficult for me to find the location, as they are located within an industrial complex, and you can't really see the building from the street. If you see a mattress warehouse, you're in the right place! Revibed is in that area. You can park in the same parking lot as the mattress warehouse.

I've  been here twice now and I'm so happy that this location is finally open! No more driving into Tampa! Everyone that works is here is very friendly and helpful, especially Samantha M.
Always in and out when I order online!

Food was very good.  We waited over 45 minutes just for our drinks and the place was not that busy.  Then the waitress came out and said they were out of an item that 4 of us ordered. We did like what we ordered but they really need to improve their service.

My husband and I did the Garden tour and we have Mr.Bird as our guide. He was fabulous! The information and added time he took with us since it was just 4 of us was great! We have been on a lot of the ghost tours but this was our first in this area and it was great! We were so glad that we did the tour and found our great history of the area. He was a great tour guide and definitely recommend this tour.

Weak!!
Drive a couple more blocks & go to Chipotle.

- Moe's lacked flavor.
- Moe's ingredients were inferior to Chipotle. 
- This location seems dirty. Not filthy, just not clean. Booth was wobbley too.
Great jazz venue. Will make u happy. Go support jazz in philly today, I'll be back, hear?

Mediocre food.  Decent happy hour menu, but never going back due to a strictly enforced rule that they're unable to refill an alcoholic beverage before you're completely finished with the one you have.  The server's tone and adherence to this rule made our group feel like juvenile delinquents rather than paying customers.  It's not really leisure time if each person at the table is trying to aggressively flag down the server as their drink dries up or is left to sit out happy hour with an empty glass.

We have eaten here every year for the last 5 years. Until now the food was good and worth the stop for being a quick easy restaurant near the French quarter. Today I ordered a seafood salad which was advertised with spring lettuce, crab meat and grilled shrimp. I changed the grilled shrimp to fried shrimp. 
The salad came with iceberg lettuce. The crab meat was awful. There were 4 fried shrimp. Not the best I've had at Felix's in years prior and I was charged an additional $8.95 for having them fried (no mention by the waitress of the additional charge). The total cost of an iceberg lettuce salad, 4 fried shrimp and nasty crab meat was close to $28. 
I could have ordered an entire platter for that cost. The waitress would not adjust the ticket. 

Other main meal ordered was etoufee. Being a native Louisianan and also eating Cajun food my entire life, this meal was far from what Cajun cooking is like. 

 We will not be back. What a shame - this place has become a real tourist trap!!

Just a side note, I watched a waitress fix dip for the oysters lick her fingers and then serve the dip. And also the oyster platters were stacked on top of each other so if you are having a platter, check for the counter crumbs on your oysters.

Yummy ! Our kind of breakfast! Delicious, presentation, portions and of course seafood options. Clear sky Cafe made us feel welcomed and safe visible practices were followed for the current pandemic. Outdoor seating during late August in Florida is definitely a challenge, but this establishment has ample fans which helped tremendously. Friendly staff and knowledgeable waitress( Alexa) eased any reservations about the menu. 
What is good here? Everything we had. Java french toast yummy as the others reviewed, chocolate pancakes for the little one delicious, seafood omelette hits the spot, lobster and crab Benedict ; WoW! Over loaded with more that you can fit in your mouth! Oj and Apple juice to wash it all down.. 
Definitely worth returning.

It was okay. I am not a breakfast person, but when I am I like your standard breakfast foods. This place has very interesting dishes to say the least. I was also not a fan of their alcoholic beverages.

Great place for happy hour and have not tried eating there at other times. The valet parking gets annoying but I understand that it would fill up really quick if cars were not parked as tight as they get them.
Best chocolate I have ever had. I had merlot and chardonnay caramels as well as raspberry and butter cream milk chocolates. Absolutely phenomenal. Wonderful customer service. Very friendly and funny.

Went here together with my family to have a dining experience of NOLA's oldest restaurant. Simple and classy set up. Not much a big fan of their food, not really impressed with the taste and presentation (didn't get the HSW factor). Probably my expectations were too high (specially I'm from food industry). I would suggest for them to work on their recipe and presentation a little more. Their presentation needs a little wow factor.

Positives: Reliably quick with friendly service, quick delivery, and good hours.   Their baked mozzarella fries are good if you're looking for a side dish & don't mind that its probably clogging at least one artery (but lets be honest, if you're ordering from a local pizza shop your not worrying about that)

Cons:  Pizza is decent but not great.  It is more of a greek style which isn't my style of choice so keep that in mind (maybe that is more your flavor).
The poke taco and fries were really great but the gangnam meat was too chewy and not close to korean style.  banh mi was disappointing as well

We went to celebrate two of our kids birthdays. We had never been before. They first offered us free meals for their birthday and then said no because we didn't have proof. Who carries around their children's birth certificates or passports? We don't for their safety and future. The only positive was our waiter was very attentive and enthusiastic. The chef was horrible. He provided no entertainment, which is sad because places like these can be alot of fun. He would merely mumble to himself. There was no interaction with us. The decor was boring and the walls needed repaired and painted. The bathrooms needed attention. They were dirty and smelly.  The food was just okay. Nothing special. Our family will not be coming back or recommending this establishment to others.

The emergency room is filthy.  In a place where I'm about to have to get medical treatment,  you would think it would be clean.  There is food,  hair and stains all over the floor in every room.  Absolutely disgusting.

The food and pastries are amazing. 
The service is horrendous. Long waits, and angry servers who clearly do not like the owner/chef.

Apparently, this is the place to be on a Friday night.

When you think of bowling, do you think of old people bowling leagues and children? Fine, this place won't destroy any stereotypes, but the old people and children love these lanes! I often scour Groupon for date night ideas and stumbled across bowling. I like doing activities, so I bought it and figured my husband would rather deal with bowling than waste the money I just spent. Luckily, I was right and we found ourselves driving up to Countryside on a Friday night.

I would classify this as your run of the mill bowling alley. It isn't swanky and hip, but it isn't dirty/fear for your life. It is right in the middle as slightly run down, but generally clean. If you think being average will prevent people from coming, think again. Almost every bowling lane was occupied! There was some contest going on, so that might have contributed to it. Also a birthday party was in full swing (again you see your old and your young). I don't think this is uncommon, however, so expect it to be full of life. I definitely prefer this to an anemic bowling alley, so I enjoyed the atmosphere. Besides, I honestly don't think it will ever get so busy that you'll have to wait for a lane.

If you have younger kids, they have a super cute dinosaur shaped bowling ball helper. Basically, you place the ball on the head of the dinosaur and it rolls down the back to give it force to make it down the lane. Little kids can bowl too!

They have an arcade area and a food counter. The arcade area is larger than I expected. We didn't check it out, but I saw some kids running in and out. The food counter offers the basics - pizza, popcorn, etc. 

This place is the epitome of A-Ok in my book.

The margarita was good but we were served some type of shark or fill in fish instead of the grouper we ordered. (We are both native Floridians and know fish!) That would be one thing if it was still good but it was tough and tasteless. Our server uncorked a $142 bottle of wine when my husband clearly pointed and had more than one verbal exchange with him about the $64 bottle of wine he wanted. We ended up paying only the $64 but that was only after the manager got involved and argued a great deal with us. We are used to the impeccable food and service at the Pensacola Beach Hilton, as well as other Hiltons we've experienced, and were quite disappointed with this property.

We ordered tuna sandwiches and cole slaws. The sandwiches were a little bit greasy and not enough lettuce, tomatoes, and no bagel was offered. At a nice deli I would expect the sandwich to offer a bagel in lieu of plain bread-not done. This was not like any deli I recall eating at-meagor at best. Also, the cole slaw was swimming in grease, and not edible. I told the checkout person this but she said it was mayonnaise based-duh. Obviously it is mayonnaise based, but it did not need to be so greasy. We were very disappointed.

I honestly wish I could give a negative 10 stars!! I went to this place to purchase something to eat. I asked the guy taking the order if the plain egg rolls had pork in them. He responded "yes". So, I ordered the shrimp egg rolls(same price). After receiving them I got in my car and being the true greedy gut that I am lol I started to eat one. I noticed the pork pieces in them and spit it out. I drove back and told the same guy of the dilemma, and right off the bat, he started yelling at me saying I ordered the ones with pork in them. I told him that didn't make sense because I'm allergic to pork and haven't eaten it in over 10 years! So, I proceeded to ask for a refund and he said no and continued to demean and yell at me. He also wouldn't make me a new order! I explained to him that if I got sick from the one bite I took, I would have my attorney contact him. He didn't care and continued to yell. I WILL NEVER IN MY LIFE ORDER FROM THAT DIRTY HOLE IN THE WALL AGAIN!!!!

Francine there is really truly talented at color, by the first time I went to her her highlights were fine and subtle and look so natural and she was genuinely happy and excited for the outcome of my hair. The second time they came out bleach blonde and she refused to tone them and I am unhappy with how streaky and stark they look.

My appointment was later in the day and left my iPhone charger there, by the time I had gotten home and realized they were close, and called the very next day to pick it up and it was nowhere to be found. Shady. EDIT: found and returned! Good on them :)

Unfortunately not a good experience.  1st trip cancelled due to winds and swells, he said, but I worked in the business and checked myself and did not get the same info.  I re-booked for 3 days later and was cancelled again due to a cracked motor.  If he was on the water and this happened, he either limped in or called Sea Tow to be pulled in.  Instead of advising us when it happened he waited until the morning and we could have booked with someone else or made other plans.  Unfortunately that was not his plan.  We've had 5 people in town for vacation and now no fishing.  I should have trusted my gut after the first cancellation.

The food was delicious, but after we were seated we waited around 20 minutes before anyone acknowledged we were there. They were out of the wine we ordered and then recommended a similar alternate, which was nice. However, it wasn't on the wine list and they didn't mention it was a higher price bottle. At other establishments when this has occurred, the recommended bottle is either less expensive or discounted to the price of the bottle ordered. I would have at least liked a warning that it was more costly since it wasn't a listed menu item.
Overall, we were disappointed. We had higher expectations based on the great reviews. The ambiance is nice and once the server came over, they did a good job with the exception of the wine $ issue.
I think the problems we experienced were probably due to poor management. But be prepared for a little more wait time on 1/2 wine night and verify the price of any substitutes offered!
We had the calamari appetizer, scallops, NY strip, ravioli and beef short ribs. All of these were very good. My compliments to the chef!

Very unimpressed by the food. Crab cakes had a lot of breading. No chunks of crab. Soup was good but not great. Baked clams were raw. And not that good. The ceviche was plain. It was rated so high, I expected much better food. 
The view was great. We sat outside. But it was cold and people waiting wanted to be near the heaters. So they stood in front of our table talking on there phones. And blocked the view.  
Our server was good.
Wow the food is amazing and is always great.  Love the chips, and everything I've tried was wonderful very authentic.  A must place to try

I can here based on yelp reviews and was underwhelmed. We sat at the table for a while before anyone even greeted us. The gumbo was terrible and for a place called gumbo shop, that's disappointing. However we got their combination plate and it wasn't bad but didn't stand our compared to everywhere else we ate!

In town for the concert down the way and wanted a drink beforehand. Stopped in here as we saw it was 'new' to the area. Well, not the building but a new owner as of 2017. There are clovers on the walls, darts, jukebox, and a disco ball hanging from the ceiling. Very eclectic mix of things. There is an area roped off outside if you would rather chill out in a chair out there. We opted for 2 Goose Islands and both were served in a glass. A REAL glass, not a plastic glass, thank you!!! It was COLD and delish. 

The owner made his way in with his dog Bruno. Clearly Bruno runs the place because he was checking out everyone as soon as he arrived. :)  Love that it is dog friendly. 

Music was ok while there. They did not play it too loud and it was a mix of genres. 

Staff was friendly. As soon as my beer was low she asked if I would like another one. Attentive, I like that, but not pushy. The one thing I am not sure about is the tank top staff was wearing. I believe it was business issued but it really brought the vibe down. I get the tank top thing, more power to you ladies who can pull it off, but keep it classy. That is about the only thing that I saw could use some work. Other than our beer not being happy hour price at 5:30 PM. THAT could also be worked on. 

Clean and nice inside. I would return.

Dr Perotti and Dr. Ti are absolutely incredible.  I am new to chiropractic and they made me feel safe and comfortable.  They have taken such great care of my family and me and the front office staff is very friendly and helpful.  I highly recommend Advanced Health Chiropractic.
Great color & cut - service on the other hand is horrible, we live in Blue Bell not Los Angels - some of the employees at the salon are really full of themselves. Get over yourselves!

The salads are excellent crisp and large. The pizza however is always undercooked and soggy at the center. The toppings are incredibly abundant a result of too much sauce and if ordering anything with vegetable topping the same issue is even worse. Be prepared to pop the pizza on to your own preheated oven pizza stone and bake a bit more which helps. Or perhaps recook each slice in a non stick pan. Regrettably this is the problem which is a shame. Never ordered anything else beyond salad and pizza. In person service is hyper to order and takes a normal time to bring out. The and the staff are loud and live in their own world with annoying music and shenanigans. They are oblivious to how weird they come across.

The service was terrible. The lady at the restaurant that was taking my order did not want to spare four chopsticks, and hung up the phone on me when I got home and found to my dismay that the  order was wrong. There was almost no chicken in my kong-pow chicken. Only nuts!!

Had the Club sandwich & Lattice Fries in the Grill area on the 2nd floor.  Always enjoy this Potato Prep, but the Club was dry & heavy on the breading.  Others in our party had individual slices of pizza, didn't finish them...not to their liking.
I was not sure what to expect but I would recommend Sports Clips to anyone.  Great service and an excellent haircut.

This was my second time coming here, and I gave it a another chance to review. Our first experience they he been revamping the menu so I understand that some options were not available but if you are out of something inform your guests. Twice in the same meal this happened to us. I ended up customizing a chicken sandwich but was up charged $2 for each item and the base price was the specialty chicken sandwich price and I did not receive those items listed that I paid for... but it was a good chicken sandwich. The Pizza was fresh but not a signature item worth coming back for. 

Last night was a joke... the ahi sliders pictured were pathetic. With tip they $15 and it comes with 3, which is $5 for the saddest slider I have ever seen. The bun was clearly from a bag and wasn't warmed up or toasted. The bread overpowered the sandwich but it may have been bearable had there been some tuna. It was the most miserly portion of tuna I have ever seen topped of with a think chewy chunk of maple flavored bacon. I essentially picked the tuna and lettuce out and left hungry. 

The Grilled Cheese was very greasy but it well advertised on the menu which is why my boyfriend ordered it. The only saving grace for him was the tomato soup. Personally it tasted like spaghetti sauce. 

My last comment is do your homework at least with the establishment that was there before this one could get a show.

Go hungry,  and expect excellent food.  We've been here numerous times, and always get the fish & chips.  This time we went for other "traditional" English meals.  My wife had the Steak and Guiness pie, I had the steak and kidney pie.  She ordered a couple of the premium sides, and I stayed with the traditional.  The meal was "washed down" with an English cider. The food arrived, and we were amazed at the size of the pie dish.  It could easily serve 2, and maybe a third.  Both were outstanding, both extremely flavorful.  The only reason I'm not giving a 5* is, the puff pastry was a bit on the tough side.... almost as if it had been microwaved before being put under the broiler. Most definitely, we will be returning.

Had a good meal at Birra. We went for the Veggie Stromboli which was very good. The roasted veggies inside were great and the dough was very tasty. The beer selection is very good. The wait staff was good. The only bad thing I have to say about the experience was that our table was really close to the bar and my wife had to experience people brushing by her trying to get by.

The food here is great. The only reason I give it 3 stars is that it made me and my girlfriend sick after eating it. Sad because I truly enjoyed coming here, but now I have a bitter taste in my mouth.

VETERANS , DONT LET THIS COMPANY IN YOUR HOUSE!!!! We had a flood overnight and our property management company called them, not our choice.  From the moment they showed up very unprofessional.   Could never communicate with us on updates and when moving our items out to repair the house, our personnel items were damaged.   New Dryer had the top damaged from putting heavy boxes on it.  Missing items, etc. The price for the repairs seem to double when the sound out the insurance company that would cover this matter.  When asked about the damage to the new dryer,  they sent the same unprofessional person out to take pictures and access the damage,  he proceed to suggest it was our fault.   Never have heard from them about this matter, to fix or compensate us.
So I strongly suggest not to call this company.  This company's  commercial that runs on the radio is exactly what they say they aren't.  If this is how they treat Veterans,  I would hate to see how they treat other. Only giving them a star because they won't post this without it.

Kam Buffet sits deep in the heart of Trenton off Clinton Avenue.  It is a fairly large space and seems to have an active lunch trade.  Service is minimal as in most buffets, with the two women in black directing seating and delivering drinks.  They are friendly enough.

The food spread is a combination of hybrid Chinese American, Latin and variations of fried chicken for everyone.  I sampled a cross section of their offerings, including, General's Chicken, Sesame Chicken, Egg Foo Young, String Beans, Pepper Beef, Boneless BBQ Ribs, and Dumplings.  All were average to below, and some were cool from too much time out of the sun.  Edible fare, but far from inspiring.  

The biggest problem is price.  When such low quality victuals cost $10, there is something wrong with the proposition.  Even Flaming Grill out on White Horse offers much more for substantially less.  And, they use bigger plates without the notice everywhere that if you leave food on your plate there will be a 15% extra charge for waste. Plus, an extra buck for hot tea!  Not a fan of buffet police. 

Hard to recommend Kam unless you are in the immediate area without means of getting to another Chez Chinois.  Almost every China shop around here has lunch specials for $6 to $8, where the quality is better, and the portions ample.  

Kam Buffet. Once was enough.
I can't rate the food because they refused to seat us. We waited by the host stand and the bartender stared us down but nobody even tried to talk to us. It was a Friday night at 6pm.

Good haircut and nice people but had to wait 35 minutes past my appointment.  Saw the reviews about possible long wait for walk ins so I made an appointment but still ended up waiting.   Got the impression this is normal and appointments are "flexible" times. 

Would recommended but need to have a causal attitude towards time.

We were in search of a restaurant that could accommodate 4 of us on a Friday night and Tacklebox (located across the street from our hotel) was available. 

Appetizer: Crawfish Beignet bites (you can't go wrong with fried dough and a crawfish sauce, delicious)

Entrees: we chose the redfish with asparagus and mushrooms, the seafood platter and the chicken sandwich. (Not very memorable but it was fine for a dinner to catch up with friends. The food was tasty but not great.)

Dessert: Apple cobbler to share, served warm with ice cream. (Good but the portion is big so you will definitely want to share) 

Our server was very helpful in navigating the menu and drink options. He also was very attentive but not overbearing.

Service was ok sat at the bar ordered the bone in rib eye med rare steak was just tough and stringy didn't cut like butter like most steaks I've had really had to fight with it like a rocky movie would i go again maybe would probably order something else just to give em a second chance but thats a big maybe

I found Grove Electric online via Google search. I read the reviews on Yelp and then placed a call. Chris personally answered the call, listened to what my need was, explained how they work, fee structure, etc. A few hours later an electrician from Grove was at my door (on-time) and was friendly, efficient, knowledgable and professional. They built trust and proved to me they do what they say they will. And, the price was very affordable which I really appreciate. I only wish I would have found them sooner as I've not had the same positive experience with all the other electrical companies I've used in the past. The best news, thanks to Chris, John, and Jason, I now have a trusted electrical company for any future electrical needs. Thanks Grove Electric!

The restaurant is really in the middle of nowhere but let me tell you, it's a good spot to try if you like Italian. The restaurant itself is very cool looking, cool decor, great looking bar, cool menus, and plenty of seats and they are not all bunched up together (the last few Italian restaurants I've gone to I've felt like I was eating on top of other people). Our server was OK, she did her job but I did not feel like she went the extra mile in any way to explain their top dishes or beer selections or anything really but she was fast in taking our orders and checking up on us for drinks and stuff. So, I'm OK with that. The food was pretty good, I loved our appetizers (stuffed something) including the rolls/bread that you get, and my steak and pasta....all good. My only concern and my biggest reason for giving it 3 stars were the mashed potatoes. I love mashed potatoes and something was off about them, not sure what but they tasted...how can I say... like oranges, potatoes, and lime juice mixed together...for real and I was not the only one to think so...so I hope it was just that batch but either way beware and my suggestion is to not risk it and order another side. The other sides and I tried a few were all pretty good.

We had reservations somewhere else which was a distance from where we were staying and decided to try Alma de Cuba last minute. Coincidentally (or is it?) - both restaurants were run by Starr Group. 
Alma is another restaurant in an old building and a long walk up a flight of stairs. We could have stayed downstairs in the bar but we opted for a real table. Very cute place. Very good service. Pretty good food. My friend had the fried rice with duck & a sunny side up egg.  I had the scallops on a quinoa "cake". We shared. Both of us noticed that the fried rice was VERY salty. That is our only negative. Probably heavy-handed on the soy sauce. Presentations were lovely and the sangria was delicious. This was  a Friday night (we dined early) but it got crowded with couples & groups. The vibe is sexy and romantic. The music was terrific. I would definitely recommend and go back if in the neighborhood.

Blue Moon is one of Nashville's hidden gems!!! Tourist always ask, "Where do the locals go?" Duh... Blue Moon!!! Their drinks are AMAZING!!! It doesn't matter if your docking after a long day on the water or you drive over, it will be well worth it!!! The service is ALWAYS top notch, food is on point and you can't beat the beautiful waterfront atmosphere.

I always get the same thing... Taco salad with beans, cheese, rice, guacamole, tomato, but no meat. It's always excellent, but I can't say the same for the strawberry daiquiri. It seems like it's never consistent. One time I get it and it's really great, then another time they don't have the whip cream to go on top or the next time it tastes really sour and I have to add sugar to it. Why is it so difficult to make a good strawberry daiquiri? 
There are a couple of women that work in there that are really personable, but there's a guy that wears glasses, who is probably in his 40s that's always grumpy with an attitude. I don't know why it's so difficult to be pleasant, especially when you are earning your tips. I would tip a lot more for friendly service, especially if they put a little more effort into those strawberry daiquiris! On second thought... I think I'm going to try their other restaurant on Main Street. Good daiquiris and friendly service would earn them five stars instead of four!

Tonight was terrible! Angela was our server and I would run out the restaurant if I ever had her again. She's a liar and a blatantly rude server.

My boyfriend and I ordered the raw bar sampler and a few appetizers. Any other time I have been here, we are served the soup and hot appetizers first. I've had the calamari and grilled octopus before and they both come before the raw bar sampler. But tonight, we got out sampler first... Not a big deal. They closed at ten and started frantically cleaning around us and made it uncomfortable. We finished our sampler, which isn't a quick dish to finish... And we still haven't gotten our appetizers. Since they were closing, we decided to forget our hot food and go somewhere afterwards and get food there....(whoever will serve us after ten.) 

The server avoided us the while time. Never checked on us. Never refilled beverages. We decided not to call her out and just asked for the check. She then said..."you guys didn't want your other course?" ...uhh, hello. Why is soup our second course after a larger meal? I told her we thought we'd get our appetizers first and she said "oh, we usually serve the cold food first." COME ON. Lying? Seriously? I've been here plenty times before and my boyfriend also and we certainly are not stupid, even if we didn't have prior experience of how Oyster House courses their meal. 

Get rid of Angela. She's no good to your establishment, Oyster House. She didn't even deserve the 15% tip we felt obligated to leave (yes, we usually leave 20%+) - just because we are experienced in the restaurant industry also. 

Ugh. This is more of a rant. Oyster House has usually been good. But that girl needs to be trained better or leave.

I frequent this blow dry bar and cannot brag enough about how amazing it is!! I have blowouts anywhere I visit while traveling and hands down as one of my top pics! Even over NYC!! 
AND Kyle is the BEST STYLIST I've ever had! I have difficult hair and he styles  it effortlessly and I love my hair every time!! Plus it stays for several days! They have an incredible selection of quality products to purchase as well! I assure you this is THE place to go for your next blow out!

Wow, isn't it crazy how poor service and attitude can ruin a new customer's opinion? Met one of the employees at a local brewery who recommended this place. Decided to drive over and give it a try. Sunday bartenderess acted like she was SO busy with a sleepy bar and already served 8 laidback patrons. ....no problem. Asked the procedure for service for sitting outside and received a curt reply. ...No problem. Was shoved a menu and beer list, as if it was a burden. ...no problem. Patiently waited to order while she batted, about slamming cooler doors as if someone had forced her to get paid for working. ....no problem. Asked to try a couple of the unusual beers (unusual for someone local who has traveled to 49 states trying beers...), got an eye roll in return. Done. Review time. (Attitude continued, btw)
