Conversely, at Mitchell's Deli they WERE busy and I was offered a taste of any of the beers which interested me. Got a sandwich at Mitchell's and through more my fault than their's I got the wrong order. When discovered-by them, I was offered a new sandwich. I was perfectly happy, so no issue with me. On the way out, I stopped to tell the super sweet server that I didn't end up trying the sandwich she recommended, due to the mix up. She apologized needlessly and demanded to make me another sandwich. I declined but the first was SO good and she was SO persuasive, I capitulated but only if she gave me a hug! So, I left stuffed, hugged, and happy AND with a replacement sandwich. Excellent food AND service was at Mitchell's Deli, not Rudie's. 
Life is too short to put up with surly barkeeps.

We had a party of 10 with a reservation and got seated immediately, great service, friendly attention but not overbearing. The food was excellent whether grouper special and sandwich or salmon dishes. It is a bit pricey and stuffy but a nice formal dining experience that won't amaze but isn't likely to disappoint.

After trying to purchase an electric range from Home Depot with no luck, I called hh gregg. They told me they had wanted. I went to the store and got a better price than Home Depot with delivery the next day. Great service in all respects.

We liked the food. When ordering I asked for water in a cup. I was told that the only water available was a "Box of Water"  for $2.50. I asked why they didn't have cups with free water from the faucet which was in plain view. The reply was, "It's better for the environment." Uh. Ok? The wax cardboard water container is better for the environment than a paper cup? I'll get over it but felt like after spending $45 for lunch a free cup of water wasn't too much to give us. The food was pricey; especially for a place where you ordered at the window and ate off of paper plates.

Store was clean, well stocked and brightly lit.   Cashier was very pleasant gentleman.   This was my first time there and I was very impressed.  Wanted to try their "famous" chicken so I got a chicken club sandwich and a small order of western fries.   BOY WAS I DISAPPOINTED!!!    the fries were horrible -- tasted like they were microwaved....hot but chewy in spots and hard in others.   Okay, no problem - they are "famous" for the chicken so let's try that.    A very nice looking sandwich!!!    I enjoyed it (very good white meat) until I found myself chewing on a chicken bone.   I watched the woman pull apart the chicken and this bone was sizable so I don't know how she missed it.    Unfortunately, that did it for me -- no more chicken sandwiches or fries from there.    I'll go back to Wendy's or Chic-Fil-A.    For other food items, yea, I'll give them a shot.

This is a condemnation of St. Luke's Hospital.  They may have talented physicians, but the "caring" part that goes with taking care of patients is sorely lacking.My husband was admitted to the hospital for a heart attack.  I was not allowed to be by his side.  I needed to give him his medicine, and advanced directives for the hospital.  I was not allowed again.  In my 45 minute wait for someone to pick up those pills and papers I counted no less than 60 people coming in for Dr.s appointments, deliveries, and other "visits". I know Methodist Hospital will let spouse be there to support their loved one (just can't leave the room), but "Hide behind Covid 19 St. Luke's" will not. I had heard their customer service is lacking...THAT is an understatement! AND to date not one person has called to give me an update on my husband.  There are no patient advocates there!
It's ok ...they just always mess up my order... one day i ordered a pie...i open it and it was uncut.. it's not all that it's hyped about..just regular pizza and wings

I really enjoyed this place. Very lay back, nice ambiance. I ordered the Mushroom Ramen with citrus grilled shrimp and bamboo shoots. It was lukewarm which made me sad because I like my food hot. However, even with that, it was very tasty.

I had an excellent experience here! Brought a pair of boots with a missing heel cap to have the cap replaced. Not only did he replace both, and they look great, but also recommended filling the hollow heels to reduce noise. The fill didn't add any weight, and they're so much quieter to walk in now - no more clomping around! I really appreciated the suggestion, they were done quickly, and the price was beyond reasonable.

I was in the mood for Chinese and haven't had any since moving to STL so i had to start somewhere.  Decided to give this place a whirl after grocery shopping.  Will not go back as it was one star above inedible.  This is the greasiest Chinese food I have ever had---in fact nothing I ever purchased on a stick from the state fair was even this greasy.  I bit into a crab rangoon and grease dripped down my chin.  I had no problem placing my order or the communication as some of the other yelpers--so no problem with service and they did seem to be very very busy--and handled it with professionalism and ease.

I got an order of sweet and sour chicken, general tsos, and crab rangoon.  My sweet and sour chicken was a little on the limp side and there was not one big chunk of pinneapple, green pepper, or onion in it.  The fried rice was an odd color very brown and none of it had much flavor.  My bill was 20.00 as most combo's are around the 7.00 range. 

The ladies bathroom was not the cleanest I have been in and it had two scraps of toilet paper only.  

wont be going here again....
Great grilled cheese! Great games. I mean, what an awesome concept. I almost wish they could somehow work a couch and a Nintendo with Super Mario Bros 3 into this space.

My husband and I went here for the first time and got Dan Dan noodles for take out. It was the worst Dan Dan noodles we have ever eaten. The noodles had absolutely no flavor, tasted almost like spaghetti. There was barely any pork and it was again, almost like we were eating Italian food with a hint of chili oil. Also, the Dan Dan noodles had shredded lettuce in it.... truly bizarre for this dish. Overall, it tasted like leftover scraps were pulled together from the kitchen for a quick meal. Can't speak to the other dishes but this dish is definitely a no go. I would have given this place a 1 star but the staff was really nice.

Came here last night for dinner with a friend and was absolutely blown away by the quality and the value. The restaurant is beautifully decorated and tranquil. (It would make a great date spot.)

The staff were friendly and capable. And the sushi! We ordered the sushi-sashimi platter for two. The fish was perfectly fresh, firm, and delicious. The pieces are cut generously and when I was done eating my sashimi I felt perfectly satisfied but not over-full.

The restaurant is BYOB so next time I will bring a bottle of sake to accompany the meal. As much as I love Center City, it's nice to be able to get great-quality sushi a bit closer to home.

Scond time here..this time dinner on sunday pm.apparently a coupon frenzy? The place is still new and not ready for big crowds yet..out of ice..out of toppings at bar.maybe short handed? I hate to rate them yet..but help is still needed here til the kinks are worked out. My roll was dry and my chicken dry and overcooked.i love pollo tropical so i know they can do better...will try again in a month or so..may21 @6:30
Dogs are out of control. Our dog got attacked and the owner didn't care. I would not recommend!!!

Not bad, not great but just alright. 

The Location: This place is located in a plaza with a La Colombe nearby and some other shops that might entice you to walk around after eating. However, the parking lot is a bit tight so it is best to get there at a time that won't be too busy. 

The Food: Turning Point is good at some things and not so much others. I do like the variety of the food. However, the quality of your selected dish depends on whose cooking. A few times I came here and everything was spot on but other times...Let's just say I went to McDonald's right after. I think the coffee here is easily burned so I tend to get a tea option. 

The Atmosphere: It is rather bright inside due to the numerous windows in the main dining area. Despite that, the staff can be sometimes rude or ignore diners which detracts from the dining experience.

Go somewhere else!!!!Beyond disappointed with my experience with Whisk Bakery. I sent several photos to Chanda asking if the designs were something she could recreate. She said, "absolutely". Well, when the cake was delivered, it had the appearance of a cake off of the TV show "NAILED IT". Sloppy and poor detailed work pretty much sums up the cake. I will say this though, she was able to accommodate the dietary restrictions that I requested and the cake did taste good. So overall, disappointed that I did not have a positive wow factor for my husband's 40th party.
The service at this restaurant is the worst.  I love walking in and being ignored (enter sarcasm)If only management would read these reviews.  I wonder if they actually care about making a profit.

Very friendly from the minute I walked in the door to the minute I left with a new tattoo. Great waiting room area with features of Black13 in various magazines on the wall to reassure you you're in the right place. I didn't even have an appointment, but not only did they work with my schedule, they got me in the same day.

My only complaint: the backroom where the tattooing takes place is just that-- one room, with many people being inked at the same time in the same area. As a lady, and with my tattoo placement, my shirt had to be lifted over my head, revealing much more of my stomach, chest and back than I would have liked to be displayed to a room full of dudes. I was not warned of this, nor was I asked if I felt comfortable.

Our favorite Mexican restaurant. Fresh, tasty food - friendly and engaging service - great outdoor seating on the esplanade. Ooh fancy word!

Tonight's chimichanga was stocked deep with chicken. No excess filler or distractions. Great sauce too.

2.5/5

Do you know what has been my #1 peeve about hotel stays? Thin walls and rude neighbors. The first night I stayed here I could hear every single song my neighbor was playing, every annoying commercial in between songs, every single conversation Mr. I LIKE TO YELL EVERYTHING I SAY was having on the phone and with others in the room, you might as well have no walls at all that's what it sounded like lol. I felt like I was living in my sh!tty apartment complex back in the day when I was in college. I could also hear every door slam from the entrance doors and occasional Zumba elephants (Phrase courtesy of Christie S) stomping down the halls. On the bright side(?), the loud A/C unit and bathroom air helped cover up some of the noise, and I had to turn on the TV to additionally cover up the noise next door so I could sleep. 

Luckily, the second night was so much quieter and I barely heard anything from our room. I read some reviews of people hearing airplanes overhead due to the proximity to the airport but strangely enough I didn't hear them at all while we stayed here.

The front desk attendant was friendly and speedy at checking us in, but was a bit too speedy because we didn't receive the free breakfast vouchers we were supposed to get. I didn't even know my hotel stay came with free breakfast had my friends staying in another room not mentioned it. It's continental style, served from 6-10 and includes make your own waffles, yogurt, oatmeal, hard boiled eggs, various carbs (Bread, bagels), juice, milk and coffee. They do have soymilk in addition to regular milk but it's next to the oatmeal rather than at the coffee station for those that want milk alternatives in their coffee. There's a hot breakfast upgrade for $5 which looked like it had some sort of cooked eggs (Probably scrambled), bacon and sausage to name a few items.

The room we got itself came with a fridge, microwave, and small coffee maker. There's a small balcony on the 2nd floor rooms. Ours was overlooking the lawn and pool. The room is tiny and the bathroom even tinier. You can barely walk in and close the door to the bathroom. The bed was comfy at least.

The bathroom had quartz shower walls and countertops with good water pressure and the tub stopper had a few spots of rust but the actual tub was very clean.

There's not a lot of shopping or food options in the surrounding area due to its location. 

We chose this hotel mainly because we were headed further south the next day and the airport hotels were the closest places to stay that were in a major city. Due to the thin walls I'm not sure if I'd stay here again.

I forgot how much I enjoy this place! I love the simple decor and the wood paneling gives this place a warm and earthy feel like you are having an indoor picnic. They do not do table service over the lunch hour but they do in the evening when it transforms into a wine bar. The ladies at the counter were friendly and answered any questions we had about the menu. The menu is listed on their wall in colorful chalk and there is a nice selection to choose from for reasonable prices. My friend chose the fish soup and  I decided to have the soup and salad combo for $13. You have 3 types of soup to choose from - meat, fish or vegetarian. Each are made with different ingredients.  I decided to go with the fish soup that had salmon, lentils and curry spices. It was excellent. They only offer one type of salad but it will not disappoint you if you are looking for something fresh, simple and satiating. The salad had big leafy greens, almonds, cranberries and cheese mixed in their own savory dressing. I was quite impressed with the portions that you get for each. It is definitely worth the money!  The place really starts to get busy around noon so I would advise that you go earlier or for a later lunch to be able to get a table.

Not great options for food. Everything is grey and drab. Air conditioning is over powering in terms of air flow hitting you no matter where you sit. Wide aisles for walking, but there are better airports out there.

Food was really good.  Had a shrimp dish that was exquisite - prosciutto wrapped shrimp with basil leaves/ grits and a Philip cheese stake.  Both were great.

Service was dandy.
Price was expensive
Location, including the "beach bar" not so good.
Real conundrum - food was really good, location and price detract...

After living in the area for 20 years, this is by far one of the best spots in town. They have good cheese steaks, great pizza, great stromboli, and great calzone. Their sauce is home made and beats any other local joint; we always ask for extra sauce to dip, because it's that good.

The owners have been in the community for probably 30+ years in the same prime location in a busy and ever changing college town. I think all of Chester County court house goes there for lunch...  It's well worth the quality and atmosphere.

My boyfriend had previously had Jack's Bistro and recommended it for breakfast this morning. While we live in Goleta, and it was a bit of a drive it was well worth it. The line moved very quickly, we found a nice cozy table, the food was brought out in an efficient manner and it was delicious. I had the chorizo Bagel and I loved it so much that I forgot to take a pic! Very friendly place, with lots of regulars, clean and small. My only disappointment was that they ran out of French vanilla creamer and even then life can't always be vanilla! So highly recommend checking it out!

Just seconding what most have said. The Awesome Awesome was in fact awesome rather than awful awful. I suggest ordering some chili to put on the fries and sharing the burger and fries with a friend. Otherwise a huge amount of fries will go uneaten.
We got there about 8PM on a Friday. The place was packed and trashed. Took us a while to find a seat for four of us. A messy dive for sure but great food and it is a must revisit for me!

I've never really enjoyed this place. My boyfriend was obsessed with it for a while so I have given it many tries. At first it was ok food was decent and service was great. It wasn't until recently when they expanded to the other side that I was just over it. Service was terrible and food was just bland. Never got a refill on my drink even after asking.

Food is excellent! Always fresh and delicious. The spinach pie, grilled chicken on pita, greek salad, etc are all to die for. If my rating were based on food quality alone, it would be five stars. We order from here frequently and we keep coming back because of how fantastic the food is, but the customer service from Sandra and inaccuracy of the majority of our orders has us second guessing if we will be back. If I add any extras to a meal, they are usually missing. This happens so frequently that I call the restaurant after I place an order on UberEATS to confirm the extras and politely ask if they can double check the bag. In the past when there were mistakes, I spoke with really nice people on the phone who apologize immediately and understand the importance of the mistake. Most importantly, all I want is to just receive the correct order the first time. Today, I called to double check my order with the restaurant after placing it on UberEATS and it still arrived without the side of feta and side of Greek peppers that I paid for. I understand that mistakes happen and I think I've been more than forgiving given how many times this has happened. Upon calling the restaurant to let them know what happened, Sandra treated me in a way I seriously wouldn't have expected from this place. She was completely unapologetic, bothered, and told me that she would have the two sides that I already paid for waiting for me for another time. Ok, so my meal was incorrect and I will have to drive out of my way just to pick up exactly what was missing from the first time? I figured it would have been appropriate for her to replace my salad or something of the sort if I had to come entirely out of my way not for the first, second, or even third time, but she felt otherwise. She was extremely rude, unprofessional, and sarcastic. All I have to say is that it's all in the details. I'm shocked that they even employ someone like this because it will seriously drive customers away from a nice establishment.

This is the third time ordering from this place and the second time they messed up something on my order. The first time they replaced the meal they made a mistake on and even delivered it but everyone had already eaten by the time my food came. This time we placed an order and we ordered enough to get the free Crab Rangoon but were told it wasn't enough and we had to add something. We chose not to add anything since we added fried dumplings already. When I got home I found that they never added the dumplings. So I didn't get the dumplings or the ragoons which was the main reason we picked Mayflower. To top it off, the meal I ordered came in three small containers instead of the big carry out that the combos normally come in which allows one meal to be stretched into two. Granted, I am picky and usually special order but the tiny portions I received in addition to the mess up that didnt include the dumplings or the free Crab Rangoon has annoyed me so much that I am done with this place, which is a shame because when done right the food is awesome.

Yes, they sold out. Yes, the other locations are not as good as this one no matter how much more they sell there (including prepared foods, which this one does not sell), but they are the original. They are the best in the Italian Market.

My family has been coming here for at least 30 years, probably more. I've tried their counterpart (you know who I mean, the ones who Yelpers with lower standards claim are "not pretentious like DiBrunos"), who feel more like a Jewish deli, who are not as knowledgeable or as friendly as DiBrunos. They're definitely cheaper, but you get what you pay for. DiBrunos has higher quality meats and cheeses ("the other one's" house provolone has NOTHING on DiBrunos Auricchio Stravecchio). 

DiBrunos is where you will have the best experience at the Italian Market. The "cheese mongers" are engaging, will make the best pairing suggestions, and help you find the cheese that's right for you. 

The best part? They feed you like an Italian grandma and let you try EVERYTHING (even the $120/lb. jambon iberico, which by the way, is amazing. I bought a few slices once).

Pro tip: the black lava cashews are incredible.

Ordered from here for the first time a few days ago and I was pleasantly surprised! I was looking for a solid Chinese delivery place and I have found one. The combination meals are a fantastic deal. You can choose any of the meat options and you get an egg roll and crab rangoon on the side as well as your choice of soup. I got orange chicken and egg drop soup and enjoyed both for such a great price. The delivery was quick and I will definitely be a returning customer. This place offers the typical "Chinese-American" hybrid of food so if you're looking for authenticity, this might not be the place for you but if you're looking for yummy food for great prices, you're in luck.
My dog loved the steak bites! It was perfect for smaller dogs...nice dog friendly place :)

What can I say, it Denny's an American instatution. The food is good. The portions are also good. This particual location is newly refurbished and it's nice and it's clean. I give it a 3 star not becuase theybare bad, but becuase its is as expected, and average but quality resturant. 

I do have one critique, it seems that no matter how many times I ask for toast dry no matter where I go, I get it slathered with butter. I hate butter on my toast. WHY?

Mediocre bland food, beer was not so great. Place was empty for a Wednesday night. Felt like a place that was well past it's heyday. The bar girl was on her cellphone, obviously bored out of her mind. This place needs some major changes if it's going to survive. We only ate here because it was close to our hotel and I was tired of driving. I wish them well.

Great spot for a cozy biz lunch with excellent food and service.....just make sure you have at least an hour!

Had a bidness lunch with some guys from Wells.....nice short walk over and arrived around 11:30 am.  

First thing I noticed was the smell of smoke (when is Mizz-o-ri ever going to ban smoking in restaurants?)....then the nice/formal setting.  We had a party of five and received great service from Garret.

Only issue is that one of our customers had to leave by 12:30 and the service of the food was SLOW....and the place was about 1/4 full.

FOOD ROCKS.....it is EXCELLENT.  We ordered the toasted ravs for the table (they were GREAT- and there were enough for each of us to have at least 3 pieces) and 3 of us had the tenderloin sand - it is HUGE, made of many medallions, is served on an excellent toasted roll with a tasty melted cheese and a mountain of fries.  LOTS of FOOD at a VERY REASONABLE price.

In reviewing the bill it TICKS ME OFF that they charge per Arnold Palmer (they really should tell you that before refilling the glass).

1 Sierra Mist     3.25
1 Diet Pepsi   3.25
1 Pepsi     3.25
2 Arnold Palmer   8.50
1 Toasted Rav    10.00
3 Tenderloin sand    39.00
1 Turkey Burger   8.00
1 Fish Tacos     9.00

Total - $90.68***   very reasonable for 5 at a biz lunch!
Mediocre people (all ugly) and ugly decor. Not my scene. And that game out there in the sand is rigged. Get it together.

This store is great! I went for business attire and business casual attire, and they had amazing slacks, blouses, dresses, etc. They did not have any skirts, though, which was frustrating. I ended up buying some of the best fitted joggers, and they seem to have endless racks of clothes to go through. I ended up buying more than what I originally came for.

I've been going there for several years. Noticed the last time or 2  that the rolls are half the size that they used to be. Also sushimi isn't as high quality as it used to be. We talked to waitress about it and at first she tried to tell us it was all the same. We let her know that we've been going there for years then she said changes were because of new owner. Food still good enough but too expensive for what you get.

The quality of the food depends on what you order. Some dishes are just OK. Others are above average. Nothing is spectacular. My favorite thing to order is the House Fried Rice and the Salt & Pepper stuff. The lo mein is terrible. So is the wonton soup. Anyone who's had Chinese food in California, New York or Boston will think this place serves dog food. But this is Tampa, where we need to be content with the few average Asian restaurants we do get.

My major problem with this joint (and with the Yummy House empire in general) is they refuse to work with Uber Eats or other delivery services. Which means you have to place a takeout order yourself and pick it up yourself. There are many problems with this. First of all sometimes they don't answer the phone and you have to call ten times. Second of all there's often no place to park. Third of all they make you go to the bar to pick up your takeout and the whole scene is a disaster, as you try to figure out who is processing takeout orders.

You can tell they really despise takeout orders. That gets them two measly stars from me.

deep fried chicken wings.... and sauce .... interesting to hear all the wait staff complaining and bitching about something. i get that at my job, so I don't need to hear it when i get 30 minutes away for dinner.

Such a disgusting excuse for pasta. This place is horrible. The calzone my wife bought was terrible too. I'm gonna make it a mission to make this business go out of business. That's how disgusting the food is.

The place is cute, but inefficiently ran. Opentable has nothing opened at 5:45pm, however my group of 4 walked in at that time and there were plenty of tables open. Service was great and our waiter was extremely friendly and informative. We ordered the first round of tapas which included the spanish octopus, fried pork belly, lobster and crab mac & cheese, seafood flatbread, and spicy chicken pulled empanada. I would definitely order the mac&cheese again, that was delicious and not super heavy actually. Our first round came out so quickly, we got the food before we got the drink. Everything was aromatic and delicious, but I didn't really care for the octopus. They were mushy and the olives overpowered it with a bitter taste. 

Second round included serrano ham croquette, weybridge from the cheese section, and chorizo picante from the cured meat section. This took awhile even though there wasn't much cooking involved. The cheese is from a cow, however, it tasted strongly from a goat. I think my face was in a scour look all night from the first bite. Serrano ham croquette was interesting, but I didn't really taste any serrano ham. It was just stuffed with cheese. Also, didn't care much about the chorizo picante platter.

Overall, it was ok, but not a restaurant I would go back to.

Let's see... My wife's bagel sandwich was completely cold and my $14 "Montecito" lox bagel was a total rip off. I'm sorry, but for $14, there better be a good amount of lox. This thing had less than an ounce. IV Bagel charges less than $10 for the same dish, only gives you more lox! Definitely not worth the money. Also, anyone who owns a bagel shop should know that you always put the capers ON THE CREAM CHEESE! OTHERWISE THEY JUST FALL OFF!

They have tasty, original burgers, and I always appreciate a locally owned restaurant. The beer list is ok, basically your standard fare plus a few craft beers. The location is pretty prime too, on the corner of Mass Ave - so it's prime for people watching in the summer time. However, the staff can be extremely pretentious. We were meeting a few friends one night, and upon arrival we were told there was about an hour and a half wait. When we told the hostess we thought our party might already be there, she responded with "Well, I've seen a lot of people tonight, we're really busy. I don't know if your friends are here." Oooook, we just wanted to peek inside and see if they were already there. We took our business elsewhere that night!

The food was pretty good, but would definitely be better at the restaurant versus delivery I'm sure. The one thing that I didn't like was the price for the amount of food I got. Again, that could be because it was a take out order that I had delivered.

I liked the fact that there aren't a ton of restaurants with the same menu items. Diversity is always good.
It's about time a higher-end Indian restaurant opened in Nashville.  Great vibe!  Love, love this place.

I have no complaints. Here for a late lunch. I like a medium rare burger. It was perfect. Our server Ashley was very attentive with drinks and food. She asked us about ourselves and was happy to be working here. My son had shrimp and fries. He loved it. This was a great experience.

I always call Key Pizza "the best bad pizza" in the city. If I'm low on cash and craving pizza, I'll go to Key. It's very doughy and very cheesy and very greasy and very delicious and usually makes me sick hours later or the next day, but I still think it's worth it and will continue to go there.

I had an appointment today at 3pm for a massage. I live roughly 40 minutes away from the hotel and was planning on getting to the spa 30 minutes early, as they suggest at booking and on their website. At 2:05pm, while I was already on my way to Nashville, I got a voicemail telling me my appointment was cancelled as someone called in sick. Their cancellation policy clearly states that if you cancel within four hours of your appointment, you will be charged in full for the services. I did not cancel, rather they canceled on me within AN HOUR of my appointment and I received no consideration for their poor customer service. I am incredibly disgusted by this lack of customer service and will never attempt another appointment at this place again.

Just got home from this hookah bar. I loved the basement atmosphere and the music was great not too loud. However, it was cold the entire time. There was definitely a draft. We were greeted by a yelling manager. He was not hospitable at all. He wouldn't let us arrange furniture or do much of anything. It was overpriced for sure. I have been to over 4 different hookah bars in St. Louis and this by far was the worst. The little stools they give you are for 5 year olds. Never going back

Used to like Minato a lot.  New location sucks.  I wanted to like it but the staff is terrible.  Bad service and incompetent ones at that.  They did not know the menu very well.  They were not very busy so there were no excuses.  Half of our order did not come.  Had to wait and wait and wonder what happened.  Ended up reordering.  Beware of the bill.  They had the bill all wrong.  I didn't argue the bill and just left afterwards.  I would not recommend.

My wife and I visited on Saturday night Feb 15  @ 6:00pm before going to the movies. Service was very slow and it wasnt crowded. We received our drink order in about 15 minutes. The staff was hustling and seemed unorganized. The place started filling up and the new patrons were getting restless. We received our food in 40 minutes. Wifes shrimp tempura she said was good. I ordered the simple sishimi plate of 12 pieces. 3 varieties of four each. Really! Super white tuna, shrimp and red snapper. I was dissapointed. service continued down hill. We left at 7:20pm and glad we did. We felt bad for everyone there waiting for food and to be seated. They had no idea what was in store for there evening.Will stop back for second chance to check out the lunch menu.

We ordered two pulled pork sandwiches, which were fantastic, they were moist and very flavorful. Loved the sweet sauce. My hubs got the brisket and was disappointed, tough and not much flavor at all. Mac and cheese was decent, not the best I've ever had and not the worst. Beans were good. Glad we tried this place, but won't return due to the brisket, maybe it was just a "bad" day for brisket.

30 min waiting in line, 45 min waiting for food, overcooked poached eggs, cold potatoes, sent back for undercooked eggs and flavorless Benedict. You should never feel that you had flavorless food in New Orleans. Extremely disappointed. Too many other options to even think about returning.

Had a groupon to use for laser hair removal. Waited nearly one hour until I was taken in for my scheduled appointment; very unprofessional and the owner Shantell was present as well.  Was not offered a tea or anything while I waited, and I finally got up and asked at the desk if I could reschedule.  Owner does not seem to care whether or not she has/maintains client relationships; she was very rough with my face and didn't wash her hands before or after touching my face.  Not impressed but had a groupon of 6 treatments; only returned for three more treatments as I travelled quite a way to this studio from where I live, and it just wasn't worth it. Would definitely NOT recommend this place to anyone I liked!

ZERO STARS!!!!!
Lets' break it down in order of events for our first and ONLY visit:
- Hostess - zero personality and rude - spent a lot of time trying to pickup customers = Lack of training = MGMT issue
- Waiter - do you have happy hour?  I don't know = waiter was not properly trained = MGMT issue
- Time to order - 30 mins + = waiter not trained = MGMT issue
- Food quality, we order expensive steaks - no flavor and overcooked.  One member in our party ordered meatloaf (I would never order-lol) it looked and tasted like dogfood.  = Chef's not trained = MGMT issue
- Follow up from waiter = none = MGMT issue
- Multiple waiters, no communication between them, and on and on = MGMT issue
- last but not least, conflicting dress code - dress code says no shorts.  What they don't say that this only applies to men.  Women in casual or ripped shorts or kids in shorts are allowed.... hmmm do we see a problem here??

I'm not wasting any more time on this review, as they are not deserving of it. 
 People - this is not a steak place, nor is it a good restaurant to begin with - go to Berns, Charleys, Ruths, Capital - where the food is good and the staff is PROPERLY TRAINED!!  ANYWHERE BUT HERE
Another Chain Restaurant, Menu is pretty good. Depends on who's cooking.

Somebody in the kitchen had a heavy hand with the salt today.
Guacamole---------SALTY!
Carnitas----------------------SALTY!

Food today was MEH!!!

The two dead palm plants in the foyer do not
add to that authentic Mexican theme!

When I was in the area, the group I was with decided to stop into the store. I feel bad for the animals that they keep in store, especially the rabbits who never have a proper supply of hay, toys, or space to live. The employee I spoke with at my last visit was very kind, but seemed really uneducated about rabbits and rabbit care. This bunny was in the store, and lm pretty sure he's got a syphilis infection all over his face. It is completely curable, but only with the correct treatment. The employee told me they thought it was ringworm and gave the rabbit ringworm medication and that the bunnies are only taken to the vet as a last resort. He also tried to tell me that "the albinos just have weaker immune systems than the other breeds" which is flat out false. Sick animals deserve proper care and it breaks my heart to think that they are being taken care of by people who don't know how to take care of them. I really hope that Pet Supermarket joins PetSmart and Tractor Supply in stopping the sale of rabbits in their stores!

I was soooo looking forward to this restaurant. Food was just okay. Potato salad was unusual and the coleslaw had Tabasco sauce in it. Egg cream was flat. 
I'm so sorry.

WORST SERVICE EVER.  I've been here a number of times and its always the same old bs. Bartenders are unattentive and show absolutely no interest in customers unless their on a first name basis with you. Being in the restaurant and bar industry myself, i always leave a generous tip and not once has 1 single bartender has ever said thank you. Came today to have a drink. Was sitting by myself and tried to order a drink from a server who told me she couldn't help me because it wasn't her station and told me to stop another server and ask ror help.  (Instead of telling her coworker herself that I needed some assistance). I stopped another server who said the same thing, so I then asked a 3rd server who just walked. Five minutes later the bartender herself brought me my drink. When I was ready for a another drink, I stopped a server and told her "since no one know who's my bartend can she please ask ANYONE to get me another drink." She hurriedly walked away. While I was waiting for another drink from God knows who, one of the managers overheard me making a comment about the service. He asked about what was happening and I told him. He didn't even blink an eye or apologize for his staff's crappy service, he just simply walked away. Then, another bartender came and gave me my drink after 10 minutes and asked if I wanted a menu. I replied, " No thank you, I just wanted to know who my server was." He then went on to tell me that since i was sitting with another person that moved to the bar, that they didn't know we weren't together. Come on now.. I've been sitting by myself for the last hour and a half and you still thought i was with someone?? Really?? Come here because it's close to home, obviously NOT FOR THE SERVICE.

You want an EXPERIENCE .... You want to try the BEST ... cocktails around.  Do yourself a favor and come visit this venue where every ingredient and every inch of the bar was thought out.  

In this day and age where no one is held accountable for actions and words Le brings standards to Philadelphia nightlife, so act accordingly and follow the rules and you will have no problems.

I've been to Audrey Claire a couple of times now, and unfortunately, I was not impressed either time. The food was just ok as a vegetarian, though my friends who ordered meat did seem to really enjoy their dishes, especially the lamb chops. For an entree I ordered the portobello pappadelle which we heard was very good; it was fine, but again just not impressive. Same deal with the butternut squash risotto and bosc pear & gorgonzola flatbread appetizers. 

The service was also subpar on both occasions. Most recently, the server wasn't blatantly rude but he did make us feel rushed and was pretty curt. 

So overall, not a great experience but not an awful one either. A solid three-star experience for me.

First time today, I was greeted by everyone when I walked in. Thu was my nail technician, I will only go to her from now on!  She know exactly what color I was looking for and she did such a wonderful job! I can't wait for my next visit! :D

Meh! I can't give it anymore than 3 stars because I'm a fan of the Olde City location and in appearance this one doesn't even seem like it's should be called Han Dynasty. The Dan Dan Noodles (minus the meat) was the reason that we stopped in. I love Han Dynasty's Dan Dan noodles! While they tasted the same, the feeling just wasn't the same. There's no waiting area & people are bumping one another as they enter/exit through the front door which is pretty much in the dining area. I doubt I'd go back, just because there are too many better ones in the city. But if you want a quick bite (esp the Dan Dan noodles) & are in the area, then go for it.

Not a friendly vibe from the get-go. The Doc spent all of 5-minutes with me at every appointment. It was purely turn-and-burn style medicine.  I went to at least 5 appointments. I had severe knee pain and I'm pretty sure the Doc didn't even do a physical exam of the knee. Just had me do Xrays, and told me I had osteoarthritis and it was due to being overweight and if I wanted it to get any better I had to lose weight. I don't dispute that I'm overweight and need to lose weight but there seemed to be more going on than something as simple as that. I'm not severely overweight but would definitely benefit from losing 40-50#s. I asked about physical therapy and I was told "no, just lose weight". I asked about wearing a knee brace and I was told "no, just lose weight".  I'm very active with hiking, kayaking, camping, traveling, etc. I was offered a steroid shot and that only gave me about 25% relief. I was never offered an MRI for my knee to further investigate the problem. Then I was offered Hyaluronic Acid shots (a series of 3) and I accepted and I was in so much pain that I could barely walk for weeks and was never told there would be severe side effects, and I had almost all. The only thing they made me sign and acknowledge was that the cost was $1600 and I would be responsible for paying if my insurance didn't. After 6 weeks, there wasn't much improvement so I asked my Primary Care Provider for a second opinion. I was sent to ROC. What a far better experience from getting the appointment, to checking in to meeting with the PA and the Doc. After getting the MRI, my new team determined I had cartilage damage behind my patella, often called chondromalacia, and a very large baker's cyst which I have suffered from since my late 20's, I'm now in my late 40's. Oh, and the osteoarthritis I have is so minimal they said on a scale of 1-10 it's barely a 2.  We went through all the options. We are starting with a fitted knee brace, physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee, and another steroid injection into the proper location. They used an ultrasound with the injection to ensure they were getting the medicine exactly where it needed to be. I'm not kidding when I said I had instantaneous pain relief. Literally within 2-minutes, 95% of my pain was gone. I definitely will not be recommending GBO and I will let my insurance know what has happened too. I am now a huge fan of ROC and will be sure to let everyone know. It's not just b/c the pain is gone, but I was treated with dignity, respect and they actually listened to me and spent time on me and you could tell from the top to the bottom that that is their Organizational culture. It was exactly the opposite at Great Basin Ortho. The indifference at GBO towards the patients is alarming.

Everything we got was above all my expectations!

I got the Salmon and Avocado Toast with the Creamy Vanilla Ice Coffee and my boyfriend got the Trifecta Benedict!

The service was also extremely fast and friendly. The vibe was overall great!

Definitely a place to visit for sure!

TIA gets the job done, but it's nothing exceptional. Yes, it's easy to get around... Because it is super small! Only 15 cities are served out of 20 gates... 

Pray that your flight is not delayed as you may die from boredom... The eating and shopping options are pretty limited. 

The people working at TIA seem to be in better moods then those working at other airports, which is refreshing. A-OK is the best way to sum up TIA.
A short hike (walk) to a beautiful visit. Well worth the side trip. Make sure to bring water and use a bathroom before you get out there.

Solid service. Pretty good buffet. Good value for money. Will be back. Some options were...a little surprising lol....but hey it's like 8$ for a buffet. It's not gonna be 10/10. For what it is, it's pretty dang tasty. Might try a margarita next time lol

This place needs some fine-tuning, but it's not related to the quality of food or passion of the owner.  the owner actually waited on us and everything was really good.  Seafood (Fruti di Mar) Ravioli, Sausage & Peppers appetizer (think salsiccia patty, sauteed red / yellow peppers, and wonderful red sauce), salads...all the portions were large and the food was delicious.  Decent but abbreviated and  inexpensive wine list.  I found the service to be a lil slow, but not overly.  I'll be back for sure.

Stopped in tonight after an Artist reception at the Liberty Fine Art Gallery.  It is freezing outside and ordered hot tea that was a light blend of floral delight.  Started with spring rolls stuffed full of fresh vegetables and my husband has his with shrimp. The peanut dipping sauce was like dessert with the scrumptious spring rolls. 
We both went with the two star heat for the salmon curry and the seafood curry. Woe, just the right heat for us and the Thai flavored curry dishes were fresh and yummy. We are so glad we stopped in on a cold winters night for a wonderful dinner. Excellent friendly service too.

I'm writing this as I stand in a line that is steadily  wrapping around the store. There are two workers at the cash register working in slow motion. The people that workers here make shopping here the worst. They always have such an attitude and they act as if everything you do is an inconvenience to them. I'm watching the other workers walking around the store doing nothing when they should be jumping behind the cash register to help get this line moving. I hate this forever 21 so much. hire new people that don't hate their lives and people in general

My first time here tonight and I absolutely enjoyed it. I had the green curry with chicken. The broth was thick and creamy and sweet and spicy. My boyfriend had the shrimp pad Thai and the shrimp were a good size and the flavor was on point. I'm glad to find good eats here in the northeast. I will definitely come back again.

My husband had a job out this way and we weren't really familiar with the area. This is the closest hardware store to where we were so I have it a shot. 
I walked in with my kids without masks. (They are actually following the mandate and only requiring people to social distance)
I was quickly met by a nice man who asked if he could help me find something and he brought me right to it! He brought it up to the register and had it run up before me and my slow poke kids got there and we were in and out! I gave 4 stars because the price on when I went for was a little high but like I said, we weren't familiar with the area so I couldn't shop around. 
I would definitely recommend them for helpfulness and not judging or kicking us out for not wearing masks!

Place is clean and staff was always nice when I stopped by. It is still fast food- but it's definitely a healthier option. The fries are baked- not fried, and the meat is all hormone & antibiotic free. I'm willing to pay a little more for better quality! A burger with cheese, air fries and a drink will run you about $10

I cannot say enough good things about this office. For starters, it is convenient, you are able to book appointments online. The staff is friendly and attentive. The office is beautiful and puts you at ease with its style and comfort. After my first appointment the office sent me a hand written letter. I would recommend this office to anyone and everyone.

The Good- Our money was returned.
The Bad- The cheesesteaks that we ordered were burned and inedible. I wanted to avoid making dinner tonight and it didn't happen.
Won't be returning.

Best kept secret in St Louis. Period. Upon walking into the Taj, one is greeted by the aromas and colors of delicious and affordable Indian cuisine. You can't beat the lunch buffet, which includes the essentials: naan, rice, chicken tikka masala, paneer, curry, and much much more. The next time you're in the loop, swing by the Taj Mahal for a culinary thrill ride.
This restaurant was great, I loved it. The Cesar salad was amazing, the burgers were even better. They have CINNAMON ice cream. It's amazing. Everything was grest

This is my favorite late-night food spot in Mid-City. I love their fried shrimp! You order at the bar, and the food is usually fast. They have a ton of booths that no one is ever using, so there's always plenty of seating. They also have pool tables in the back and a bunch of huge TVs.

After lunch we were looking for a place to get some dessert and this place popped up on yelp. They don't just do donuts as they offer other foods and drinks as well. So I would give this place 3.5 stars just based on the donut. We got the Doberge donut which was a chocolate and lemon glaze donut with chocolate glaze, lemon curd and chocolate buttercream. It might sound like a lot for a donut but all the flavors work well together. The lemon side is just tart enough but not overtly tart. The chocolate side is sweet enough but surprising not overly sweet. When both sides are eaten together it offers a good contrast. So if you're looking for a good dessert place to go to, this is a good place to start.
The better of the strip clubs in NOLA.  I just hung out with our group in the back.  Didn't really watch much of the dancers.

badly in need of a facelift.  with added fees for travel companions, i expect more.  the mens room is old with mirrors rusting out from water damage.  food was same old stuff. bartender not at her post at 10 am.   

save your 29 if you have a guest and use the airport bar if you want a drink.

Horrible place to eat, what a waste of money. Went in here and paid over $100 for a dinner that wasn't even fresh. Ordered chicken that was served ice cold. The rolls they served us still had freezer frost on them. If you're gonna serve frozen food, at least make sure it is defrosted before serving it. The wait staff was horrible, and the manager/owner did absolutely nothing to make it right. Don't waste your time or money on a nice dinner here, there are plenty of nice places in Newtown to eat at such as the Temperance House or Black Horse Tavern.

I went and got a pedicure today. I left at 2:00 and by 3:30 part of the nail polish had gotten smuggled.  When I do my nails at home it doesn't get smuggled. Waist of $$. Know I will have to go to to store and pay more $ to try and get it fixed.

Your typical national chain AMC theatre. Weekday shows are a decent price and usually free of annoying kids even though this location is in a mall. Concessions are the usual, overpriced but still tasty. One bonus: bring your significant other and you can raise the arm rest between your seats to snuggle close during the scary parts of the movie.

This is not my usual Walgreens.  This one, however, is open 24 hours and not too many are anymore.  It's located at the southwest corner of Main St. and Keene Rd. in Dunedin.

This location is huge, clean and tidy.  I was immediately greeted and then pointed in the right direction.  They seemed to carry a lot more inventory than the Walgreens than I usually go to.

I can definitely recommend this location especially if you need to access prescriptions 24 hours a day.

Good good food . Fried chicken skins? Good. Were they $15 good? Nope. Steak tartar- awesome. Pork buns? Awesome. Steak (bone in) was a little tough but well cooked. For $85 I've had better so not worth the $ at all. Pork shoulder was highlight of meals... Really smokey and great flavor. Cocktails were great and creative. All in all a good meal. Plenty of food to pass around. Not certain $100/person is worth this place but it is good, just not $100/person good. 4 for food and service, -1 for lack of value.
One of the worse meals I ever had at a palm. They served me a lobster raw and expected me to eat it . Save tour money go to McDonalds you will get better service and quality of f

Hello... What happened. You were so nice over the phone. So understanding of the situation. I honestly believe that you were concerned and expected a refund as you promised. It has been 7 weeks. What happened. Please contact me.

Disgusting!!!! Ordered pizza with extra cheese and mushroom . It was wet cardboard with dried out cheese and old mushrooms . Chef boyardee pizza kit was better. The pizza has been a hit and a miss this was its 3d strike  out. We will not be ordering every again.

Ate here twice while we are on vacation, so not bad.  

What I liked about shells is that it was exactly what I expected.  The first night we ordered a Cesar salad with shrimp, fried calamari, and the crab cake sliders.  The kids got chicken fingers and fries.  The salad was good and came with a ton of shrimp which was marinaded with a teriyaki sauce and grilled to perfection.  The fried goodies were fried goodies, hard to go wrong.

The french fries were delicious!

Second night we went for Lobster Tuesday, sooo happy we went back. The lobsters were cooked to perfection and we ate every last bit.  The accompanying sides of red potatoes and cole slaw were surprisingly good.

We also tried the key lime pie which hit the spot.

The service overall was average, nothing good, nothing bad.  The decor was what you'd expect, wooden tables with wooden benches.  It had a very nice vibe.  If you're in the area on a Tuesday, definitely go for the Lobster!

This is the takeout Chinese place we always go to.  I have enjoyed everything i've ever had here.  My whole family loves the Cold Noodle with Sesame, and the Steamed Dumplings.  That's a meal right there.  My favorite is the Garlic Chicken, which comes with a good amount of vegetables.  The fried food, like General Tso's and the Sesame Chicken, along with the egg rolls, is nice a crispy.  They also have pretty generous lunch specials.  The service is also good, and fast.  And the prices are pretty low. 

A lot of Chinese take out is so-so, but this is the best I have ever eaten from.

I've driven by this restaurant a million times. It's never busy. Tonight I gave it a try. I ordered a single cheeseburger and a vanilla shake. The burger was small in my opinion, though I do prefer a larger bun to burger ratio when I eat a cheeseburger. Good flavor. No crispy edges that I love. It actually tasted like the burgers I make at home with steak seasoning. No condiments, neither did I request any. I guess you only get what you ask for. I was tempted to try the shoestring fries, but went with a milkshake instead. The shake tasted like a run-of-the-mill vanilla shake. I was the only customer besides the two boys in front of me who obviously knew the cashier. I might return for lunch sometime. The two items were $12.40 and Im still hungry. No wonder business is slow. 

Ps there is a Tesla docking station in the parking lot.

Love the place. However, the bartender gave my debit card to another customer on New Years Eve. They seemed to know who it was they gave it to after charging his order to my card. Then the individual was able to use my card at other locations before I discovered it. A real pain in the butt to start my year.  I recommend paying cash, or cashing out with each order. Don't give them your card as they do not have a process in place as previously stated in a similar complaint.

Some of the best sushi I have ever eaten.  My wife and I love this place and we were so happy to find it.  It's kind of hidden away from the rest of Hockessin, unless you're heading up to PA.  The service is always excellent and the food is even better.  If you enjoy sushi rolls with lots of "stuff", you must try the Kailon roll.  It's a house special roll and it's well worth the price.

My kids love this stuff so we keep going back. We usually pre-order and hot, fresh pizza is waiting for us in under 20 mins. Toppings are generous, and crust is good enough that we polish it off as well. Reasonably priced.

After visiting the French Market this is a great place to visit. It's free and interesting. I work at a bank so visiting an old US Mint was definitely on my agenda. They have old bank equipment, rare coins like half dimes and an old dollar coin counterfeiting press. Upstairs they have a jazz museum and a photo exhibit. The jazz museum has a lot of cool memorabilia and rare photos of Louis Armstrong. They have pictures of his New York home and that's now a museum. I've been there so it was nice to read up on it. I loved the images in their exhibit room. The covered everything from Black Lives Matter to Arranged marriage. This is a museum you can get through in an hour so I highly recommend it and will definitely go back when I visit New Orleans.

THis is my second time coming here since 2006. Uh third time must be the charm be cause this is just okay. I mean it still good but I'm not running to tell anybody about this place.

I will however tell you to take yo azz a block down the street to Lil Dizzy's Cafe and let them put a smile on yo freakin face!!!!

Anyway this place has its ups and down and their Famous Ferdi special with debris isn't that bad!

It's a ok spot on tasting your way through the city.

This place is a mess! 1st try on the pizza...wrong pizza. Second try on the pizza...HAIR! In my pizza. I see the waiters not wearing a hat or hair net. I now assume that the cooks do not either. My group and I also got a calzone it was amazing, but not worth the health risk or eating here.

I really enjoyed the crust on this pizza... slightly chewy and a well-rounded flavor. The part of the crust that sucked was that the wood-firing left black chargrilled marks that were less than tasty.

Overall, I think they could do better.... not enough flavor or exciting menu ideas. The whole place was just boring. Meh.

This used to be one of the best restaurants. It used to be very authentic unfortunately today I waited 1 hour and 45 minutes for boneless spare rib combo and a pizza roll. They took my address incorrectly luckily the driver called ahead once e realized it was the wrong address . Turns out the original owners sold to some Cambodians there's nothing Chinese about this restaurant. The quality of food has diminished the boneless spare ribs are no longer the same recipe stick to Emperor's garden.  I called the young man on the phone couldn't even give a straight answer as to why it took that long for the food nor could he give something in return for cold food Sincerely a disgruntled customer

Stumbled into this quaint restaurant on a whim and was pleasantly surprised. Very calming atmosphere, simplistic interior with food that offers a wonderful punch of flavor. I ordered the Vegetable Soup, which includes vegetable dumplings, tofu, and shiitake mushrooms. The effervescent broth was full of savory flavor, without weighing you down after eating. The portion was huge, and definitely worth every penny. Accompanied with a hot green tea, I felt I was able to tackle the day ahead of me with vitality! Wonderful service. Would definitely recommend for a quick lunch or a dinner date. Will be back! :)

Went there for dinner for the family.. We got the garlic pamesan tenders , cajun bbq tenders,  hot garlic tenders, buffallo wings , ribs , onion rings and fries... Everything was really good. The garlic parm tenders were delicious.. The hot garlic tenders were nothing more than buffallo chicken with some garlic.. The ribs were ok, my wife loved them.. All in all will go back again.

Very rude. My brother is visually impaired, so to see things more than a few feet away he uses a small telescope. Upon seeing this the cashier told my brother he could not use a camera in the store (it doesn't look like a camera). We explained to the cashier that it was not a camera and that my brother needed it to see things, to which he loudly responded "I don't like it, OKAY!". I then told the cashier if that was the case we wouldn't not be purchasing anything in the store, his response to which was "Good, get out!".

Dennis Automotive has outstanding Customer Service, industrial knowledgeable technicians, and fast and accurate service, while constantly updating you on the repair status of your vehicle. They even have an web App to help you keep up with the required service of your vehicle. Only place to bring your vehicle for maintenance.

Me and my husband stayed here recently and we probably won't stay here again. We did enjoy the close proximity to Cracker Barrel. The room was okay looking.  Here's what we didn't like though, one of the bathroom towels had a large stain on it. There was a bunch of empty spots in the vending machine. The bathtub plug didn't work. We did speak to the manager about this and they said that they have had issues with bathtubs over flowing so they purposely leave a gap around the plug so you can't fill the tub which, is kind of unusual. We also noticed that they don't make your bed if you leave for the day. Which, at other hotels we've been to, that's what they do. We were pleased though that they did fold our towels and take the trash out but we were really expecting the bed to be made upon our return to the hotel in the evening. The free breakfast was disappointing the first day but better the next day. The bed was pretty firm which was uncomfortable.
Please... Do Not go here.... It's the coolest  bar in NOLA and I want to be able to get a seat!!   The fried chicken is way too good for you to eat...  save it for me!  I beg you!!

Place was great. 2nd time here and enjoyed the food. Had the pei mussels which was great. Pork Oso buco was tender but the rest of the dish was bland. However the Bison Short Ribs was good. Tender and flavorful. But I would say the star of the menu are the cocktails. 'Be Holden' being my favorite.

I must be honest. No way near the quality of a NY bagel. Not even close. 3 stars b/c the service was kind, quick and reasonably priced. Salt bagels by request only? Not sure what that's about. Very dense bagels. Very limited varieties and kept in plastic tubs. A dozen or so varieties of cookies offered too. 30 minutes from home makes this a one and done for me. Bummer.

I prefer this place to el camino for a couple of reasons. I like the atmosphere more. It's darker, not at loud, a cooler not so "commercial vibe." The staff is really excellent and I've had good service each time I've come. I also like their food way more. As a vegan, they have a soup option that's great as well as seitian tacos that are amazing! They also don't charge you a dollar for chips and salsa which is just plain stingy. Their salsa is amazing. Ive never been here during the day, but I would think it would be nice to sit outside at lunch.

Caught them in the middle of a shift change late afternoon on  Saturday.  Service and the food was average. Beer is a little pricey as is the food. 
If only the plastic laminated menus had been clean it would have been a little better experience.
The baked Chicken is delicious. Black beans are flavorful who know beans could taste so good.
My gel manicure was really sloppy. Two fingernails were all smudged and two more peeled off within 48 hours. I normally really like Le Nails but not this location!

Once upon a time, this place was cool. Trendy. Then, I don't know...it just kind of fell flat. Could be a change in management. Who knows. I just always labeled it a Back Up Plan if Manayunk Brewery was too packed. Sometimes you just want a chill spot where you can hear yourself think.

The atmosphere is chill for sure, but single guys, don't plan on walking out with anyone but a nice size bill for your drinks.

We have been here a number of times, both for dine in and take out. I'll start by saying the staff is extremely friendly and accommodating always, which is a huge plus.

The food is incredible quality for the price. Sashimi is 3 pieces for $6, and our favorite signature roll is $8- if you're looking for high quality sushi on a budget, this is your spot! 

Our only complaints would be the hours Can seem pretty inconsistent, as we've walked over during their advertised hours before and they've been closed. 

Other than that, we're big fans - try the Mr Kaiser roll!
Stopped in here for lunch, in town for business.  The burger was good and they have Irish beers on tap.  Bartender was friendly.  Good choice.

Shockingly good. Anyone who is deterred by the location or lack of ambiance is probably very busy living a sad and monotonous excuse for a life right now. No judgements  This place is special. Fresh, flavorful, and delicious!
Great food and service. Drinks are also very good but the menu was fairly small. The atmosphere was very uptight less relaxed. Probably first date location.

Overrated. I really don't understand these 5-star reviews. Let me give you the real deal. Disclaimer: I took advantage of a Groupon. 

When the cashier handed me the bag I thought this bag sure is light. I'm so glad I had a Groupon. The dish looked great, looked like restaurant quality. However, I make better mac and cheese. The mac was a step above Kraft. The chicken was fried perfectly. It was very tender and juicy, but something was missing. The crawfish sauce was good, but they don't give you a lot, or at least I didn't get enough sauce to combat the dry mac and enhance the chicken. The potato salad was very good, but they only gave me two tablespoons. What the? I bake so I know what two tbsps look like. Lastly, the punch was horrible. I was expecting something akin to the ghetto punch from Wing Snack, but this stuff tasted like water. Not watered-down Kool Aid, WATER. And why it's called Dat Sh** on the menu boggles my mind. I know it's in the hood, but shouldn't we be lifting the neighborhood up? The drink I had today should be called Dat Crap. Actually, Dat Sh** works too. I don't know if this was an off day, but they need to step up their game. Thankfully I have another voucher to give'em another shot. 

Chicken thigh, mac and cheese, potato salad with some crawfish sprinkling. I've participated in enough church/family suppers/fundraisers to know that these are not expensive ingredients and certainly not worth $14.

I love Danielle and I have never gotten such good treatment when getting my hair done! She is very fare with her prices and also goes out of her way to make sure I always leave 100% satisfied!!! Love love LOVE This salon!!!!

Awesome place.
Highly recommended. Excellent food, good atmosphere. Excellent food! Good prices. Laid back place. Cool battenders.

They close between 3 - 4:30 p.m. for their staff to take a break.  The closed sign did not stop me.  They left their door unlocked. Ha!  Big mistake.  I walked in around 4 p.m. to order food to go.  The young lady was very polite and explained to me that she can take my order but the chef won't prepare my meal until 4:30 p.m.

The chef was kind enough to interrupt his break and prepare my food while I was waiting. I ordered Seafood Pad Thai ($12.95) and Seafood Thai Fried Rice ($12.95).  Both entrees had lots of seafood chunks.  Something missing though.  They were not as tasty as from Bangkok Cuisine; therefore, I give them three stars only.  The decor is more of a fast food place rather than a nice seat down restaurant.  Somewhat surprising especially they are located in Caughlin Ranch. They have a sticker on their door that says "Yelp loves us!" What the heck?  Which reviews are they referring? Bangkok Cuisine is the best!

Wanted some breakfast food and didn't feel like leaving the house. There wasn't much to order from around here so decided to try 2 in One out. Great decision! I got the 2 eggs, toast, meat, and home fries combo which was only 6 bucks. Delivery time said it was going to take more than an hour but they came in less than 30 minutes which was incredible. The food and delivery time was so fantastic, I will definitely order from them again.

Grilled oysters: Order, yum!
Gumbo, Bisque and Side Salad: Mehhhhh
Crawfish cornbread: NO
Asparagus - outstanding
Grilled shrimp pasta: mediocre 
Blackened catfish: Order!
Scallops: Pretty good
Service: Annoyingly slow 
Manager: Nice guy!
Ambiance: Nice restaurant but very loud

Worth a trip but I was underwhelmed compared to my expectations from the reviews...you just have to order the right things and a little better service would have bumped it up.

Good place to get fresh food at a fairly reasonable price. The "Salmon Spot" as us locals affectionately call it is very popular and usually identified by the lines wrapped around the corner. It goes fast though because everyone typically orders the same thing so they make them really fast. They also have produce stores there (Iovine) that sell fruits and veg for good prices, plenty of specialty stores and culture specific boutiques and markets to keep you busy. Great place to walk around too.

This my neighberhood store. I like the selection and the floor people are okay. However, I will not shop in a store that sells Yeti Products or products of any other anti-gun organization. Sorry Sportsman's. I know that no one on the local level has any say in this matter, but I have a say and the answer is Thank You but NO THANK YOU. GOODBYE!

I thought I'd give victory another attempt after recovering from bad service the first time around. I enjoyed the food and atmosphere but the level of service is below what is normally expected. She got our appetizer wrong the first time. We weren't able to tell her that because she never came back around. I in both occasions asked for a second drink and never received them. There was no follow up period. No one asked us if we needed anything... if our food was good.. needed refills... nothing. I would like to go back but I can only now expect bad service.
Consistently yummy food and very friendly employees .. always makes you feel you're welcome and wanted.  A neighborhood favorite for many of us.  Great salads!!!

We had the best cheese pizza we've ever had in Philly by far.  The crust was crunchy yet soft in all the places, cheese was strangely crispy on the outside but nice and gooey on the inside, perfect amount of sauce, the basil brought in some crisp and freshness to the pizza.  Textures and tastes were amazing across the board.  

There is something about taking simplicity and making it so impressive and they did that 

If you can make a plain cheese slice with such perfection, I'm just tryna figure out what other moves you got ‍ 

Oh yea...customer service was also phenomenal.  They get you in and out with the goods efficiently.  Couldn't be happier with this place opening back up.  What a blessing!

FYI they only take cash but there is an ATM nearby so no worries there.  We had to call in about 67 times just to get through (it kept ringing busy)...but we eventually got through.  DON'T give up!!! It's totally worth it!

For a fast food concept it takes forever to get your sandwich. I've been 3 times and every time has taken too long. The last time, they lost my order and it took even longer. No apology or offer for a free drink or come back coupon. The owner was even working the register. Even though I like their egg salad sandwich, I'll just make my own.

Although the approach to the resort is spectacular, its opulence seems a bit over the top and drowns out the natural surroundings. 

Underneath the facade, it's a bit run down.  The carpet in the main building is dirty, and the buffet breakfast served in the marble stepped open dining area was rubbery and tasteless. Although my large room was satisfactory, the a/c did not adequately cool the space; I was sweaty and opening up the sliding door cooled the place down. 

While the resort boasts of a fitness complex with multiple swimming pools and as well as a gym, the gym seems to be where they cut costs; it is very small and looks like "work-out city" in an anonymous strip mall. Finding it alone is quite a feat because, after a long hike, it looks like green tool shed attached to the competitive swimming pool.  

The tiny particle-board locker room is too cramped, old and smelly and not worthy of what Westin is trying to achieve.  It also was not clear how to gain entry to the locker room because although they have a swipe for the room key, it doesn't work; a goofy bolted-on keypad also didn't work, and I found this frustrating. 

The crowded work-out area itself is grey and dreary with aging life-cycle equipment.  I got the impression that the real emphasis is on selling expensive tennis lessons at the nearby sprawling tennis center; I observed the tennis pro trying to talk up anyone who approached the front desk including me, and it felt like a hustle.

To add insult to injury, I called for an appointment with a fitness instructor, Nancy Manning, who never showed up nor returned my calls, so the Health Club lacks professionalism. On my next trip to Tucson, I want to stay at a place that is less pretentious and more authentic.

The wings here are very good, large, cooked in a variety of ways.   It is what it is.  Classic sports bar.  Don't under any circumstances purchase a mixed drink here.  You may as well just order the mix.  They have a long standing policy of under pooring.  You can't taste the liquor.  I assumed this was just a one time mistake but it has to be a policy because its be going on for at least several years.

Just recently got back from a summer road trip I took with my partner of six years. My plan was to stop at many of the Harrah's properties. The first property I stayed at was the Harrah's Biloxi my next stop was the Harrah's New Orleans. We arrived to the Harrah's New Orleans at around 3:30 am. I was hoping to get a room. When we arrived a lady and what looked like a security guard were at the door and asked for our ID's. The lady, an employee of Harrah's, claimed me and my partners ID's did not look real.
I am a 34 year old male and my partner is 27. My partner also showed his passport. The employee asked him to smile and starting sassing at him, "show me your dimples!" We were then told to get lost by the security guard. So we went out the door to the main entrance hoping to speak with management.
At the main entrance we had the same problem with being denied entry after the woman, Omega, was radioed and told not to let us in. At this point I requested to speak with management just before another security guard came out to tell us to "get off my property".
Waiting outside management finally came but not to address the situation. Instead we were told to "not even step foot into the building" by Leroy and Tina, who were acting as management. After describing our situation and providing them our multiple forms of identification they claimed they were unsure of the validity of our California issued Driver's License. Leroy even began to make subtle threats of calling the police. I'm as unsure as to why just as unsure as to why we were denied entry. Given the late hour of nearly 4 in the morning we decided to give up and stay else where.
As someone who frequents Las Vegas multiple times per month during the summer at least once a month during the year. We play/stay across multiple resorts; I've never experienced this before.  Never has this been an issue in Las Vegas or even in Biloxi or anywhere while we were in New Orleans except for at Harrah's. If you are gay stay far away from this property unless you want to be denied entry.

I was so excited to try this den of Cupcake Wars declared deliciousness, and it saddens me to say I'm not a huge fan. 

The cupcakes are big and beautiful, yes! However I was disappointed with the texture of the icing, which was too fluffy and sweet for my taste-it reminded me of my great aunt's old recipe that used Oleo (ya know, Crisco). I was uninspired by the cake too which was just, eh. I couldn't finish eating the ones I bought-just not to my liking-sorry.

I'm glad they were Season 3 winner of Cupcake Wars though, that's awesome! Maybe they'll get lots of business besides mine. Sadly I won't be back.

I visited this diner several times in the past. It's hit or miss. I gave it a break for about 6 months. Their sides for lunch and dinner aren't always the best. Somebody should tell them everybody doesn't like vegetables drowned in marinara sauce. Tried it again this morning for a take-out breakfast. I was told it would be ready in 20 minutes. I got there in 25 minutes. My order wasn't ready when I got there. No one apologized that I had to wait an additional 15 minutes.  The very young, thin host brought my order out and just handed it to me. No eye contact. Never said thank you for your business. Never said thank you for waiting. But then again.... I've never seen him ever smile at a customer or thank them for the business. But he definitely makes sure that your money is right when you're at the register paying for your food. Today was my last day at this particular diner. They need to work on the customer service.

Food is good, beer is tasty, staff is friendly, and decor is cool...only one complaint.  The music is way too loud, hard to have a conversation with the people at your table and difficult to even focus when eating.  We asked if I could turn it down a bit, even stating we didn't want to ruin the vibe, but just a little bit to reduce the obnoxious-ness.  We were told according to their manual it must remain at that volume.

Stayed one week. Hot tub was not working upon arrival. Guests or long term tenants stated it hasn't work in a while.Temperature was too low and the jets came on but quickly turned off.  I asked about it at the front desk and was told there was nothing they could do, they tried, but would have to dig up lines. The dark haired gal at the front desk was rude, the other gals were nice. She had an attitude, heard her on the phone and experienced it in person - not a customer friendly type.  They also need to trim trees prior to arrivals as they scratched the top of our RV.  Not all sites offer cable TV and their wi-fi was poor. Okay place if you really need full hook-ups, but not a destination location.

From the first email requesting a quote, I was impressed with the response and professionalism of M&J Professional Cleaning. They had the best rates of all the companies I contacted, and I was able to reach Michael immediately to schedule a time to have our carpets cleaned. He was friendly and worked well with our schedule and time frame! A day or two prior to our scheduled cleaning we realized we needed the hardwood and tile cleaned, too. This was not a problem at all for him!  What a relief!  Michael showed up on the day and time of the appointment and began working. He was super friendly and I felt comfortable with him at the house. He was great at communicating throughout the cleaning, and even worked with us when both my husband and I had to leave him at the house by himself!  Payment was easily done through PayPal (which was great since he finished while we were still gone)!  He was respectful of our home and wanted to ensure we arrived prior to him leaving so that he did not leave our home unlocked and unattended. I highly recommend M&J Cleaning for all of your floor needs!!!  Great job!!!!!

Our microwave stopped working after a potato caught on fire in it. We called Salim, and he came out the same day. He fixed it, and now it works great. He was very knowledgeable and professional. I would definitely contact him again for any future repairs!

I've been to see Dr. Weiss for fillers twice now and both times I've been absolutely thrilled! I was recommended by a friend after having a very underwhelming experience at another office, with results that barely lasted me two weeks. But Dr. Weiss and his staff made me feel completely comfortable and confident in their abilities and they did not disappoint! Dr. Weiss takes the time to sit down with you and get a clear understanding of what you want and will check back in with you throughout the process to make sure you still love the results, while also giving you his professional opinion so that you're both happy with the outcome. And I'm very happy! Exactly what I wanted. Can't wait to come back!
