Blaaaa! Cesar salad was brown. Bread wedge served with it was stale and cold. Calzone was cardboard stuffed with "sausage" that looked like sliced roast beef. Wanted jalapeños and got what looked like pickled beets. It was served with a side of sauce that was ice cold. I would definitely NOT recommend this place for anything. We didn't have any chicken but any time someone came in to order some they kept saying they only had 1 quarter piece left. They pushed for us to order something they already had made. Took them half an hour to make the calzone.  Too expensive and not good.

I thought this was an okay place. The matzo ball soup was good and warm. I tried the Jewish roll and I thought - apart from the abundance of bread, that is was good and tasteful. Service was excellent and done with a smile. The only shock to me was the price. We paid almost $20 each for lunch sandwiches and extras. I thought that was a way off for what we ordered. Everything else I thought was okay.
Terrible staff with what seems to be inconsistent service and a very unprofessional manager who i believe her name is Claudia

I just bought a 1966 for mustang  and they been very nice initially. So no problem, I pay and I got my car! 
Omg DISASTER.. BIG LEMON!!! 
I really suggest you yo be sure what you getting for, what they advertise is not what I got! My car is seating waiting a New engine  ! I had to spend 15k .
This was not told to me what they sold to me was Very good car!!! Apparently was not!!!! 

Terrible terrible I will never never buy a car from this people, they sold a car with a trashed engine... And told several time was in perfect condition 
Of course I reached out and I got email back saying that they don't take nY responsibility!!!

food was the standard Fazoli's food, the dinning area was dirty and the restroom was gross. The place was crazy busy, and the management was not allowing people to find a table until they had ordered food.

The only times that I tend to come into Burger 21 is when I receive some sort of email blast consisting of a promotion that is actually worthwhile. Like Five Guys, Burger 21 doesn't offer any type of combo meal that includes a side of fries and a drink. So, when you purchase a burger, fries and a drink all at a la carte prices, it can add up pretty quickly. 

The burgers and fries here are good, but they are just not worth the price to me. My burgers have always been cooked well and have come out in a timely manner. The menu selection is nice and rather large compared to most burger restaurants. They have a few unique sauces that are simple and good that most places would find a way to charge you extra for. I applaud them for offering these at no additional charge. 

I'm not knocking their food, I just think for the money there are plenty of other places that serve up a good burger for the same price with better quality.

A+ service!  Big E had Jarrod pick us up from the airport, also provided ride for our daughter the following day, then took us back on departure day.  On time, nice big Towncar, delightful drivers.
 
Highly recommended!!

Every time I am satisfied and overjoyed. I took my foodie friends there and they loved it all. They did feel like the Rico pork was a bit sweet. They loved the Brazilian cheese balls. The kottu was great the vegan Rico is fabulous. The broken noodle salad is amazing.
really really good food. i really liked all the dishes. my favorite was the fries. the cocktails were on the sweeter side.... didn't exactly love them, hence the -1 star

I never even knew this park was hidden away up here in the Summit Ridge area until today.  It's easy to never go up this way if you don't live up there.  I'm glad I found it, though, because this is really a nice park.  It's quite big for a residential-area park, with lots of grassy open areas and some walking trails.  The playground here is REALLY nice with equipment for both younger and older kids.  In the summer the kids can cool off in the sprinkler-like water-play section.  There is a great pavilion near the playground with nice, big concrete tables.  In other areas of the park are basketball courts, horseshoe pits, and exercise equipment.  The one detraction (and the reason for the loss of the star) is the weather up here.  The wind howls over the ridge fiercely, making this a really unpleasant place to be on all but fair-weather days.

The WORST customer service EVER.  DO NOT use these people. You can buy the same appliances from many other stores. They have come 4 times to install our appliances and leave every time with the job incomplete saying it's another department. They also delivered one of the wrong appliances. These people don't care about whether your stuff works and they don't care about doing their job correctly.  They are TERRIBLE.

Worst nail spa experience. Was made fun of by several members of the staff for picking a service on the menu that was handed to me. The nail tech was so aggressive that I ended up with cuticle infections on both big toes.  This has never happened before and I will never go back there.

Overall I think it was okay, the seasoning was a tad salty and my crab was way overdone. I enjoyed my sides more than the entree. IF I return I think I'll give the shrimp a try. It was unbearably hot inside of the establishment and I waited 20 minutes for my food although I placed an online order. I absolutely hated every minute that I had to wait in there. Especially when I was told they don't start making the order until you arrive and pay(so what is the point of ordering online).

Pretty sure humans have canines for ONE simple reason: to eat La Boca's steaks.

Seriously though, my 14 oz. NY Strip (the Bife de Chorizo) left me speechless. I honestly sat there, wide-eyed in amazement for a few seconds before I spoke, savoring the amazing marinade that the tender meat has soaked up.

Holy cow - no pun intended. I'm not kidding you, La Boca makes a mean steak.

Sorry I'm getting ahead of myself so let's backtrack a little. 

We got here at 11 pm. We were running late for our appointment but the staff was ever so kind and attentive. Our server Ron was very amiable and extremely helpful especially when it came to wine recommendation. I had a glass of 2005 Santos Beck Gran Merlot. (I'm not 100% sure though, just ask for the one called The Blend). It's great for people who want to have a red but don't like anything too "spicy" and dry.

We got the Provoleta while we were waiting. If you love cheese...you will love this dish. I pretty much hoarded the bread basket and just enjoyed slathering it with sizzling, semi-melted cheese. 

You know what else I really liked? The sauces. I am a BIG fan of sauces. I kind of sit there like a kid with paint pots. Honestly though, I was gleefully dipping the bread into those sauces as well. There's the traditional Argentina steak sauce (green one), a Dijon mustard type sauce (yellow), and a roasted red pepper sauce (red). I felt like the combination of the Provoleta with roasted red pepper on my bread made it seem like I'm eating pizza...

Back to the steak! (I know my review is heading in all sorts of directions but I honestly am so thrilled and excited sharing this that I am just writing whatever comes to my mind..)

I devoured it. Actually, that's a lie, I expanded my territory to Paul's Bife de Lomo con Hueso, the 12 oz. Bone-In Filet Mignon..AFTER finishing mine (Again, this indicates how content and pleased I am with La Boca's cooking). The Filet Mignon is more flavorful than my NY Strip but mine was perfect in terms of tenderness which was attained from cooking it to a medium state.

I personally enjoyed the traditional and the Dijon mustard sauce the most on my NY Strip and the traditional and roasted red pepper on my boyfriend's order of the Filet Mignon. But truthfully, you don't need any additional sauce for flavors. I did it because it was just experimental and fun to try it out, see which one compliments it etc. 

I am also a biiiiig fan of bread pudding (or anything that either has corn or just sounds carb-y really). So I asked for the Baked Corn Pudding. Wow. I was full by the end of my meal but I still gleaned the pan for every last crumb. Loved it!

Since it was about midnight by the time we were finished, we didn't get to try the dessert but man, I am definitely going to try and make Paul bring me back here :) 

Yes. It's THAT good.

We've been coming to Grimey's quite often since moving to Nashville. They have the best record collection, as well as some pretty rad live shows. However, I miss the 8th Ave location so much! The East Nashville location doesn't even compare. If only they had stayed on this side of town.

Great friendly service, incredible ice cream and top notch food. We vacationed from Chicago and they made us feel like we were home. Great hospitality and even better food. The owners made it fun. They obviously have a passion for this, They take great pride in how they roast their own pork and make most of the deli items themselves. 
Good times, family orientated place. We visited every day we were there!

Probably the best Thai food you'll find in Santa Barbara. The service can be hit or miss, but you'll get over that once you get your food. The pineapple fried rice, tom yum soup, and pad thai are great, as well as their Thai iced tea.

I think I've heard of this place a long time ago but it just escaped my mind to ever visit. I'm always up for food so I ventured there recently.

The menu wasn't what I expected. I made sure to check it out online first because it takes me forever and a day to figure out what to eat.

My plan was to get the hummus and pita. I love that. My party ended up skipping appetizers and just going straight to the entrees. 

I got the tilapia +spinach+rich. The other got the chicken and waffles (I yet to try that combo anywhere) and the meatloaf.
Since I vowed to cut down my alcohol I got the peach non-alcoholic fizzy drink. 

The tilapia was encrusted with pistachio crumbs. I enjoyed it but towards the end, something about it became  nauseating. Maybe it was pistachios overkill..maybe the semisweet butter sauce surrounding the spinach and rice. Either way, towards the end I lost interest and ate the rest as fast as I could just so I can say I finished it.

For dessert we shared the brownie and ice cream (meant for 2-4 people). It was quite large  and it was good but not great.  I wouldn't get it again. 

The restaurant as a whole was okay. Nothing special I'd say.

3 1/2 stars.   

Place was packed even for a Monday night dinner, but we were already forewarned that it would be packed.

I let friend do most of all the ordering:

-Deviled eggs - ok $4
-Butter lettuce, with bacon & bleu cheese salad - ok $7
-Ceviche - refreshing, it's been a while since I had a fresh tasting ceviche and this hit the spot for me. $8
-Steak Tartare with potato chips---a must.  I really loved this $11
-Smoke salmon on sweet potato latke - good not outstanding

Overall my favorites were the steak tartare and ceviche.  Unforgettable.

Parking is off street, on the neutral ground parking area, and there is a small parking lot that is shared along with the other strip mall retailers.

Great for groups, kid  friendly menu and after work noshing.  Credit and cash is accepted.  Dress is casual anything goes.

Amazing!   The service was amazing, they were  nice and  helpful without being annoying.   It is one of those restaurants you order and then they bring you your food, but I never felt rushed while picking my food.  They made sure you were comfortable in their restaurant from the moment you walk in, which was awesome.

I got a steak sandwich deal with a fruit cup as a side.  This was the best sandwich or meal I've had in Nashville thus far!   I loved it.  Super flavorful and the perfect size as well.  I didn't feel hungry afterwards, but at the same time I also didn't feel I had eaten so much they would have to wheel me out.   

I also had their strawberry lemonade which was super yummy!

I can't recommend this place high enough - you have to try it!

We came to discover Sakana through an article my husband had read about a chef alumnus of Masa in Manhattan, who was serving quality sushi at a budget price, so we had to go check it out. We didn't feel like stuffing ourselves that night, so we opted for the 12-course omakase dinner. This was a BYOB establishment and we forgot to bring any wine, but I'm kinda glad we didn't bring any since it allowed us to fully enjoy the sushi. At $58/person, we were first served with an oyster sitting in ponzu sauce, which was delicious and a great palate cleanser. This reminded me of a smaller version of what we have had before in a Japanese restaurant in New York that we loved. Some of the highlites of the omakase included wagyu beef topped with uni, fatty tuna, scallop and spot prawn sushi, but all 12 were amazingly good. Sakana is what Philadephia lacks and needs, and at $58/person, you really can't beat the price for the quality you are getting. If you're very hungry, I would recommend going for the premier omakase, you won't be shortchanged.

Easy to forget this place if you live closer to Mazzarro's.  If you don't, this is an authentic small Italian deli that may not have a huge inventory but is a great little find. Great olive oil, and really nice sandwiches. The owner is very gracious and happy to help.  Kudos.
This is a local neighborhood place for us. They don't pretend to be anything fancy, and I appreciate their honesty. I had a very solid breakfast and it was a good price. Overall I'm happy.

Grooming- I cannot give them a real rating because I never got to see what my dogs looked like AFTER their appointments. I was told to bring my dogs shot records, which I did, the vets office scanned them to me. They were UNACCEPTABLE  because the vet did not sign them, but they were sent on the vet's letterhead. Oh, and the notations were handwritten, (also UNACCEPTABLE) I guess that's how my vet's office does it - on the outside of the file and didn't sign the file?? And apparently, the best part, my vet did not send the sticker from the outside of the  medicine bottles on the file. 
So would I like to reschedule?  Um. . . No- and I truly hope none of you schedule one or two as I did. Go somewhere reasonable. 
Good luck and looking for a bather/ groomer for three big dogs if anyone has suggestions

Ordered pizza and some fettuccine Alfredo and everyone who ate it is now throwing up. Stay away from this restaurant. I just read a review finding out that 4 days ago a rat was smashed in the dough machine and workers were vomiting and people were screaming. This place is awful. The food wasn't even mediocre. It was just bad.

If you want information and friendly service, ask for "Juan!" He is the bomb! Kind, friendly, and super attentive! Check out the HUGE onion rings! 1.5 inches wide! I had the double cheese burger- so tasty! Of course, we will be back!

Clam chowder is wonderful and full of clams as promised.  Caesar salad was large and crisp with lite lemon and tasty shreds of Parmesan cheese.  Can't wait for the Chicken Primates and the Grilled Seafood platter with skewers of shrimp.  Yum.  Great food and Tom gave us the best service,  making sure we were well taken care of.  Thanks Tom!!

Terrible customer service!  This was the second bad cs experience of today!  I was tagging along with my 2 friends I hadn't seen in years and their kids running errands in between and we stopped at this little knick knack psuedo dollar store.  Most items were not a dollar.  Items that were a one dollar the owenrs charged double at the cash register. of course the kids wanted things and for those prices we decided we would go to the real dollar store.  So my friend picked up 5 packs of planting seeds that were listed 5 for $1.  When she went to pay it totaled $3.12!  She questioned the cashier and the cashier said tax!  I thought PA sales tax was 6%?  She purchased the seeds only after the cashier/owner said she'll charge $1 and no tax.  My friend gave her $1.10, said keep the change and she'll never come to that store again! Terrible customer service, store is over crowded, owners follow you around the store, merchandise is a mixture of outdated items and items purchased from the real dollar store marked up three times the price.  It's sad we couldn't browse while we waited for an order for bulk items we were picking up near Swarthmore College.

Went there a Wed night and sat right down.
I had read about the egg ravioli and reviews and coming from NYC was interested to try.
Service was very good , food was excellent !!
I thought the egg ravioli was a very different and very good.

Here is the surprise I had gone during a business trip and need to use my corporate card for the meal.
Low and behold when it came time to pay it seem I had forgotten my card in the hotel room.  
Upon talking to the Mgr he allow me to leave unpaid and to call in from my room with the credit card.  
Not something I believe would happen in NYC!!

Checked out this place this past Saturday for pickup & it didn't disappoint. Ordered the honey habanero wings & the chicken fajita salad. Everything was delicious & I can't wait to get back into the bar once the quarantine is lifted. The bars recent renovations transformed this place into a really upscale bar can't wait to get there on a nice day to utilize the patio.
I have been here twice. The first time was okay. Last time I went will be the last time I will go. My toes and my hands were rush. Multiple nails were crooked, the girl hurt me more than once.

This place was gross. It was really disgusting, actually. My burger wasn't even cooked, at all. I sent it back and asked to have it cooked more and it still wasn't cooked. The service was poor, but the patio was great. It was also ridiculously over priced.

Guess I am just picky but the unpaved and very dusty driveways and parking areas cost me a car wash. Because of this I won't be back.

Didn't see anything that can't be found at Mesquite Growers or elsewhere.

Yard House was an A-OK experience.  The restaurant is quite large and echo-y inside, so when it is full it's pretty loud.  Decor is kind of industrial but chic.  The bar in the center is quite large so it'd be a great place to grab a quick lunch if you don't want to do table service.

The whole feel of the restaurant, from what they offered from their specialty Cocktails to their food, really reminded me of BJ's Brewhouse.  They offer a wide selection of beer on tap.  The food tends to be a little more "modern/upscale" in it's offerings than BJ's, so I was excited to try a little salad with Ahi tuna on it.

We started with the Calamari which was A-OK.  One thing I thought was interesting was that they deep fried thin lemon slices as well and that added a really cool flavor/texture component with the marinara dipping sauce.

My salad was solid - you could tell the produce was fresh and the Ahi Tuna was seared Rare.  It wasn't a memorable vinaigrette used but it was still tasty.

My husband got the fish and chips which were ok.  The fries were a little cold and greasy - he had ordered them "garlic" something.  The idea was good, the execution of it was a little off.  I think they would have been better had we received them hot out of the fryer.  The fish was a nice light piece, lightly battered, and came with tartar sauce.  

I got a mini creme-brulee at the end of the meal.  I love that they offer the options of the mini desserts, because nowadays desserts are intended to share, making the portion sizes huuuuuuge.  The creme brulee had the little caramelized bananas and it was tasty, and just the right portion size for one person.

Yard House is somewhere I'd go back to, but only at someone else's suggestion as it's across town for me and there are too many other local options that offer better food.

This place was a very fun interactive experience. We were the last few to arrive to the table for dinner and were quickly caught up to speed on the "rules". You sit with other diners at one large dining room table. They start you off with salads and biscuits and gravy, pass to your left and keep passing as the server refills when needed. The gravy was not very flavorful. Next comes the sides; green beans, fried okra, collards, potatoes, hushpuppies(dry and not the type of hushpuppies i am use to) and more. And then the main event; fried chicken, ribs, fried trout and lasagna. It was rather odd to have the Lasagna. The fried chicken was super crispy but dry on the inside. The ribs were my favorite!  Dessert is preset on the table; banana pudding.  Not my favorite, so I passed. Wish they had two desserts. It all happens so fast and it's unfortunate that you aren't able to enjoy the meal.  Great experience, but I don't think I would go back for dinner
Love our product and the team they have working for them. Got a large job done in about 2.5 days. Very accommodating- Highly recommend!

Ugh. Used a LivingSocial deal to order Penne with Chicken (note, the person who rang me up did not hit redeem on my coupon, I went ahead and hit redeem myself! Probably shouldn't have...I feel like an idiot for paying for what they gave me). Opened it up to find disgusting, rubbery looking slabs of chicken. I don't want to speculate what that piece of chicken looked like before it was cooked. Took a bite and it was as bad as it looked. I thought I would eat my way around the pasta and just go for the penne. Somehow, even with something as simple as pasta, was just bad, bad, bad. Two hours later and I feel like throwing up. This was the second time I've gotten dinner here, the first time I ordered  a sandwich and--maybe I should have learned my lesson the first time--that was icky as well. 

I come here now and again for a slice of pizza at lunch because it is so close to where I work, and the pizza is actually edible. It's not very good, but it's a slice of (a bit too chewy) pizza. 

It really sucks, but it's so clear what this place should be--a mediocre relaxed, no-nonsense Italian joint. They have a great location, and the inside is really nice, but the food, my gosh...

Riding the rails out of Reno we learned that the parent company of Harrah's, where we had just stayed, declared bankruptcy for its largest division earlier in the day.  Indeed, our hotel, a component, was deader than usual this winter. 

The Eldorado casino floor was busier, although not bustling, making one wonder if the casino biz is similar to movie theaters:  At peak times they can be annoyingly hectic, but off-peak business appears insufficient to pay the snack-bar staff.

Eldorado Resort Casino is diagonally across the street from Harrah's, and is where we four spend the second-most time in Reno.  We also change it up sporadically and stay there when rates are attractive.

Rooms given to bargain hunters may be cramped and spartan, but thoroughly adequate if used for sleeping and bathing.  Restaurants are plentiful, and vary in concept and quality.  Briefly, only the buffet and Brew Brothers brewpub are worthy choices for this ripened, prudent (read:  old, cheap) guest.

The main entrance is an ideal place to start your three-block, freezing-winter indoor hike, but the walk past its boundary through the two connected neighboring hotels, Silver Legacy and Circus Circus, is most useful for people-watching; the best selection of whatever you're looking for is within:  bars, casino and sports book.

Furthermore, the cocktail ladies who capably cover the casino are Downtown's finest, dressed in black satin corset teddies, sheer pantyhose, and pumps.  Most Harrah's comp-bev servers now wear throwback, gold lamé change-maker vests with black slacks, and for more than a year some men have assumed those duties.

Don't hate the sexist who nevertheless tips the men equally, but wonders not how such corporate decision-making, after a fashion, could induce bankruptcy.

Two stars only for the customer service I got from the girl that took my order on a late Tuesday night. I got some wings, which were small, over priced, and had no flavor. You can go up the street only a block away to Noble Pie and get delicious wings and pizza! Decided to give this place a try. I will stick with Noble Pie Parlor.

I'm not even sure why I feel like I need to review Raising Canes, except Yelp keeps bugging me on my welcome page to write it.

Raising Canes does one thing.  Chicken fingers.  And they do them well.  You can pick the size of your box, they are served with crinkle cut fries, Texas toast and dipping sauce.  I suspect the dipping sauce is made of mayo, ketchup and crack.  It's pretty yummy.
They also have good sweet tea.  

Once, I pulled in to order at the drive thru and the girl says over the intercom in a hilarious voice "chicken, chicken chicken!".  That made me smile.  

If you like chicken fingers, this is your place.

Great place to go.. My boyfriend and I strolled along one Tuesday afternoon. . We got to relax in one of the hammocks . The view of the river is beautiful . Not too many people around . The food trucks were amazing some a little pricier than others. At night is a whole other atmosphere is totally different . With the beautiful lights & all the people. Beer is like $8. . Better to go on like a Wednesday night it's less crowded than the weekends & parking is free.

I am so glad we found this little gem. Its part of Cool Springs only eat, shop, live concepts. There are cute little condos and apartments around the restaurant. As well as other shops and things. This place has a really nice patio area with cute lights and the indoor decor is also nice. They played the most random music the night I was there. It would go from Ariana Grande, to Billy Currington to Kanye. I loved it. 

We got a pitcher of cucumber margaritas. They were very refreshing and had chunks of cucumbers floating around in them. At first I thought that was a little odd, but it worked strangely. The waiter said that the cucumber marg was their most popular drink after a regular marg. The pitcher isn't on the menu, and it was about $40. There were four of us and we each had about 1.5 margs each.

The chips and salsa are good. TIP - check in on yelp and you'll get free queso! I had the steak street tacos and they were delicious. The serving sizes were a little small, but overall it was a great place!

OH! They also give everyone at the table free sopapillas with honey! Sweet little treat!

I'm not sure what I was expecting, but B's didn't hit the mark for me. I came in during Final Four weekend and ordered a half spicy sausage po' boy and slaw to go. Paying $7 for a half-size, I guess I was expecting something more substantial. What I got was a 4" sandwich (if I'm being generous) with a thin sausage patty that had been cut in half to fit the bread. For taste, it was fine. It had a good spice to it, but really, it tasted basically like the extra spicy breakfast sausage you can get at the store. The slaw, however, was portioned appropriately and priced fairly, in my opinion. It was very tasty with a nice Cajun seasoning.

I don't like doing one and done visits, so I plan to go back to try something else when I get the chance and revisit this review. I just feel like the sandwich I received on this day was not worth the price I paid for it.

There comes a time when you need your daily allowance of saturated fats in one sitting. What better place to accomplish said goal than at wholesome Mickey D's? 

Ever since the notorious movie Super-size Me, portions have become, lets just say, less generous. To compensate for the meager servings, I now have to eat 1 Big Mac, 1 Fish, french fries, 1 wrap, and a soda. I will pay in spades in a few years.

Northwest confuses me.

I've been there a couple times before and had some pretty okay, three-star kind of espresso.

But then there have been times when the baristas clearly have no idea what they're doing.

Like today: I asked if they could make a traditional 5-6 oz cappuccino. Confused, they said that the smallest they had was 8 oz. I clarified, "you know, the classic 1:1:1..." The cashier and barista asked an older staff member, who clarified what a real cappuccino is.

But he didn't offer to make it. Instead, the barista--who'd clearly never made this drink before--did, and it came out probably as well as I deserved for pushing the point when they clearly didn't know what I wanted.

But besides that, there were more fundamental problems: the barista pulled the shot, then left the espresso sitting there oxidizing for almost a minute as he poured and steamed the milk. I asked for the drink "for here," but the only ceramic they had was espresso cups (good), coffee mugs, and giant latte bowls. Nothing for any of the classic drinks. They said they'd have to just put it in a mug.

I guess the thing that really gets to me about this place is that it's got the vibe of a real coffee place, it's got the Marzocco of a real coffee place, and it acts like a real coffee place, but then they don't even have the *cups* they'd need to serve the completely standard drinks of a real coffee place. It's like a... poser real coffee place or something.

Is Kaldi's really the best St. Louis has? If so, that's a real bummer.

If you are looking for a cool welcoming vibe don't come here.  Upon being seated for our  7pm reservation we were coldly told you have 2 hours to spend at this table as it is booked for another reservation. I could of lived with that, but the staff (3 different people) insisted on reminding us every 20 minutes of this policy while we struggled to enjoy ourselves. I ordered Tommy's Margarita and I guess Tommy never had a good margarita because this one was lousy.  Perhaps I would not have noticed if it actually had some liquor in it.  Overall a pretty miserable expensive evening and I will never return.
Not bad. Ordered the pulled pork, ribs and pork sandwich. Side portions were pretty small and their collard Greens were bomb. The food was fast and the service was decent. Would order again.

Had a great time! We did the Wild West room and got to try the new Christmas themed room. Both were super fun, and different! Clever too. Jace and Nick were both helpful in setting us up and were great hosts!

Won't go back, we sat at the bar asked for water, waited for sometime. We were about to leave because it took so long . The waitress took our order than proceeded to complain about us to another server. The both were looking at us as they were talking. We left
I was not impressed when I needed a quote. Keep looking and save yourself time, money and pain by finding another termite company.
Great place! Friendly staff.  Good food!!!!  They heard about my bad experience at another restaurant and brought out key lime pie for my birthday.  Please visit they're awesome!!!
I have been coming here for years and never had a bad meal. The pan fried spicy won tons are the best. Cozy inside seating or to go.

A one star rating for my last stay at this hotel is appropriate. The rooms are dated. The bathrooms are way to small. The bathroom fan must be 80 years old and sounds like a circus. The rooms are large, but the office chair and desk setup is horrible. The wifi is so bad that you cannot use if you want to work from the room and use the internet. Bring your own wifi. I'll put up with a lot to have a quiet room, but there are limits.  

It rained on the last day of my stay. The crappy elevator to the third floor had standing water in it the whole day. Plus, the elevator is at a point that is the farthest away from where you park, so if you travel with luggage, good luck climbing the stairs and if you need an elevator, be prepared to walk. The only redeeming factor is a half-way decent breakfast and nice kitchen staff.

The hotel is basically an upgraded 1970s motel. If you like that kind of thing, then take a risk. The price of the rooms is too high in relation to the quality of the offering.

My specific beef this time is that I called down the night before to tell front desk I would checking out a day early, but when I arrive at the front desk the next morning to check out, they insisted on charging me for another night. This was after assuring me the night before that I would not be charged. WTF?

I am a business traveler. This is unheard of and a poor business practice. As a result, I've forbidden my team from using this hotel. Together, we've put in over 125 nights at this place in the last year, all of which will move to another hotel and possibly another chain.

Bruh. This place is basically in a college dorm, but it's got a mix of freshman and plain folks like us. Brunch specials named after poets and sh*t. Wife had some Maya Angelou dish... Off the charts with creativity and deliciousness. Well worth it.

This is ridiculous. 1.5 stars (as of this review)?! 

This is a five star McDonald's. I mean, it's McDonald's, so common sense and context need to come into play. This isn't a five star experience, but it certainly is a five star McDonald's.

The people here are friendly, the food is always hot, the wait is never long. I mean, like, what else can you ask for from a McDonald's?

The crowd I usually see at a McDonald's wouldn't look out of place shuffling through the DVD discount bin at a Walmart, so the fact that I'm greeted with a smile by employees who have probably spent the day dealing with hordes of angry customers coping with meth withdrawal is really all I ask for.  

Good work, friends!

Paradise Cafe is a toss up between two and three stars. As a rule I like to give the benefit of the doubt though, so three it is.

I find myself at Paradise Cafe once every 18 months, and each time I return I always wonder why I don't go there more often? Its location is convenient, downtown but slightly off the beaten path. It looks great indoors and out. Clean pleasant indoor decor, and one of the best patios downtown, period.

The menu's nice too. Simple Americana. Burgers, fish, chicken, steaks. Wine list seems reasonable. Well stocked, nothing crazy. If you're looking for a safe nothing-fancy place to eat -- and there's nothing wrong with that at all -- then Paradise Cafe may be just the thing.

Unfortunately, each time I do eat there, I remember again why there are 18 month gaps between visits. The service is well meaning, but poor. Well intentioned high school or college kids may be friendly, but good waiters they make not. The ritual of greeting, menus, drinks, orders, food, checking, dessert, coffee, check seems to be lost on them. 

The waiters are unsure, seemingly hoping you'll fill in the blanks for them. Food comes before drinks, drinks come out one at a time, and apps are moved aside to make room for entrees. Too bad really, cause it seems the waiters simply haven't been told what to do. They look bright enough, just never trained. Corners are being cut.

So, service is subpar. The food was a mixed bag. Sad veggies, messy plating, and indifferent starches. The guys on the line are cooking by numbers. No love. One bright spot was provided by whomever was on grill. The burgers, fish, and steaks were all spot on, thereby saving the day.

Still when all is said and done, mediocre is not what you go out to dinner for. Especially nowadays, credit crunch and all. When you eat out, it should be more than just meh.

And so, the Paradise Cafe is certainly not paradise itself, it's just located there. A fact it takes full advantage of with its superb location and patio. If you have plenty of time, and could care less about veggies, then Paradise Cafe may be the place for you.

Me? I think I'll wait another eighteen months.

I had the pleasure of taking my family to this restaurant the day before my graduation. I will say that my parents were impressed. My only other time at Square One was during Mac Mondays.... (Its $3 for mac and cheese. Definitely on budget for poor students) 

My parents loved the atmosphere. We sat next to the two large "beer making machines" (Don't know the correct name for that.. obviously not that big of a beer fan.. but my dad is).  I had the Pulled Pork Sandwich that was cooked in their special ale. My dad had the smoked Salmon BLT, my mother had the Cuban Sandwich, and my brother had the fried chicken sandwich served on sourdough. The food was good and I loved that they incorporated the beer in many of the dishes especially the fries! YUM.

 The service was also exceptional.  I recommend this place for many families with of age children. I am sure they will enjoy it.

I love, love, love the Rev! Even though their Brandon location was a bit of a drive for me, I still frequented them because they have the most amazing flavors you'll find in the Tampa Bay area. I was so excited for this new location close to home, and a wee bit nervous that the new location just wouldn't be the same. I've visited this location a few times now and it's definitely my go to ice cream spot! The flavors and creamy texture are perfect and just what you'd expect if you frequented their original Brandon location. Definitely try the Fatty Rolls or Porkys delight - they're my favorite and amazing!

The staff over here are friendly and the decor is cute, with a kinda hipster vibe. It's a small setup but the vibe is super relaxed. Next time you go out for ice cream, definitely head over the Rev!

I've been to First Watch locations before and was disappointed in this location. It's not too big. 

Perhaps I forgot, but the menu was smaller than I remembered. Not a lot of options but I settled on the tri fecta which included waffle, 2 eggs and bacon. The waffle was good. I ordered my eggs over easy and they can over medium which was annoying. My husband convince me not to mention it. The bacon was okay but nothing to get excited about. 

My husband got the Acapolca omelet and  loved it! He was very happy with his choice!

You have to pay yourself at the cash register when you leave. That's fine except there was only 1 staff member at the register trying to get customers bills paid and get incoming customers checked in. This made for a long line in a small area and wasn't a good way to end the experience. 

If I was in Indy again I would not return. There has to be better places to get breakfast!

Great beer selections, but I wouldn't eat here. The food is definitely nothing special. There are a lot better places in the area for a good meal.  If you are looking for a place to hang out and have a few craft beers, this is a great place.

Before going on our trip to FL, I wanted to get a mani/pedi to freshen up and get ready for the great weather. After looking at reviews (and location), I decided to call Heaven & Earth Salon to try their services. Now, I don't treat myself to this very often, and was excited to get a little pampering. The reviews led me to believe that this was the place. I called, and after finally reaching the front desk, the woman rudely told me that the ladies were leaving (or weren't there anymore, kind of hard to believe at 2 in the afternoon) and that I couldn't get in that afternoon. Before I could ask if there were appointments for the next day (though I was questioning if I still wanted to  go here) the woman hung up. I don't know if this was because of the weekend, or because the staff is rude. I will think twice before attempting to use this salon again, and ended up going with another local nail salon who were more than happy to get me in.

Please don't plan your entire visit to Philly around the cheesesteak debate.  You will be sadly disappointed when you sink your teeth into one of these sandwiches.  The Cheese Whiz addition was pretty fun, but the sandwich is basic and the customer service is nonexistent.
We ended up here when another restaurant we wanted to visit was closed. They're open late, have great customer service, and an eclectic menu! Fun! The food was good, what's not to like?

No granola. No strawberries. Basically they are out of half the menu without those ingredients.

This is the second time they've been out of a key ingredient. Absolutely terrible management.

It was pretty good. Highlights: the onion soup that my companion had, as opposed to the miso soup that I chose (which was totally fine) was outstanding and the perfect amount of salty that I want in this phase of the bento box dining situation. I'd have liked for the salad, which was good, to come out in a separate bowl like the soup, instead of in the box, but meh. Splitting hairs. The california roll in the box was also great, as was the gyoza. But both of our proteins (katsu chicken and steak) needed salt. and pepper. and more salt. or something. They were both bare meat. I'm generally not one to pick up the S&P shaker at a restaurant, but it was needed. I got the feeling that the server didn't really want to join our fan club (just a party of 2 out for lunch), but that could totally be me projecting. It was fine, but nothing that would make me shout a recommendation from a rooftop.

It's a cute little place with great outdoor seating. However, I think I got the wrong things. I ordered a Thai tea. It was nothing like Thai tea at thai places. It tasted like regular black tea with milk and orange food coloring. It was very watery. I also had the chicken quesadilla. It was decent. The prices are really affordable. I should have tried some other things because the menu looks good. I went in to get a pastry. All they had was oatmeal cookies. So I ordered the brownie bowl off the menu and she said they were out. I settled on the what I ordered and it fell short. I might try again some time for dessert but never ordering Thai tea again.

We discovered Rivers Spence's shop on Magazine St, purchased a rug we loved for our LA home and decided that since we loved Rivers' taste, she would be a good choice to redecorate our New Orleans home. She is a very talented interior designer.

Fantastic Chinese spot around Temple U. Staff is always friendly and the food tastes excellent! Miles above other local Chinese spots in the North Philly area. I'd definitely recommend the General Tso and Chicken Dumplings!!!

The lady at the front desk may know what she is talking about when asking her about the packages they offer but she takes about 15 minutes to explain and by the time she's done you still are trying to figure out pricing and packages. That's not the worst part though her personality is not fitting for a desk position she would be better just cleaning the beds and not talking to new customer or current clients. The personality type could not not be more cold, and almost seems on edge. She's easily recognizable with the tattoos on her wrists. I do not know her on a personal level but she's not pleasant and welcoming in making you feel she's has your best interest in mind while also just providing good customer service. In final review the place was clean, had many lotions to choose from, and different bed tiers that were available at a fair price with no contracts which is a plus.

This place is near my home, so I pass by it all of the time, but never gave it a try until today. I was in the mood for Thai, but too lazy to drive to my favorite place in Philly, so I decided to give this place a try. BIG MISTAKE. 

I ordered the house fried rice, with shrimp, to go. After speaking with the waitress about veggies included in the recipe, I specifically requested that my rice only include broccoli, peppers and onions...I repeated it about two times to remind her of what I specifically wanted in my dish. She placed my order, I paid and it was up in no time. 

Finally go home, and ready to eat. I noticed that my rice had not been prepared as requested. It has mixed veggies(corn, peas,carrots and green beans), in which I repeatedly asked not to be in my dish. There was broccoli and PLENTY of onions, but no peppers, and only 4 medium sized shrimp. The rice was so dry, I almost choked on the first spoonful. I added soy sauce and chili paste to moisten it up, however that did not help. It was as if the rice was days old.

I am HIGHLY disappointed, and probably will never go back and eat here again. I usually try to give places a second chance, but this was horrible and a waste of money.
I had a craving for Chicken 'n' Dumplings so we headed to the local Cracker Barrel. The service was great and the food was decent.

Thought a visit to Nashville would not be complete without a concert at this iconic spot. But I was wrong.

This isn't the original site of the Grand Ole Opry and besides the pew type bench seats (which were surprisingly comfortable), this is really like any music venue.

We saw an afternoon performance and it was only about half full and just OK. The singer was clearly just rehashing his former, 20 year ago hits and the crowd almost looked asleep. Really--no energy at all

So unless you are going to hear a beloved performer, skip this and go to a little venue instead. The ambiance and vibe will be better.

My girlfriend and I were looking for a new coffee shop to study at and she had heard good things about Coat Check Coffee--so we decided to check it out. As students at Butler University, we are always looking for good spaces to study and this place definitely is a new favorite of ours! It was so modern and cozy and there were SO many areas to sit, drink coffee, and study!

The coffee and food was a bit expensive, but so is everything for two college students! My girlfriend got a butterscotch latte which not only looked amazing but tasted even better! I ordered a coffee of the day; Utopian blend, which I had never had before and very much enjoyed! We also both got a ham and cheese croissant which wasn't our favorite, but you never know until you try--we're eager to try something new next time!

We both agreed that we would want to go back again (mostly to see if we could find a space in the warmly-lit hallway just to the left of the ordering area!

Seriously, folks...it's a diner.  No cloth napkins.  No waiter and runner and busboy.  No silver plated utensils.  So let's keep the expectations real.

Food is okay.  Not memorably good, nor memorably bad.  Just okay.  I do remember that breakfast was hot.  Oh yeah...I should mention that.  I had the breakfast, because I LOOOVE breakfast, and restaurants *usually* can't screw up eggs or pancakes.

Our coffee cups went empty.  That's bad.  Really, really really really bad.  
Oops..and when coffee mugs were first put on the table,  one of them had lipstick on it.  No, really.  I am NOT making that up.  
(Not a normal color, neither, but like some "late night drag queen hot pink")

Food was hot but took longer than it should have.  I'm used to diners, because I LOVE diners and diner food.  Gimme a diner breakfast or diner meatloaf and mashed potatoes any day!  

But I dunno...this place just seemed like a "miss" on so many levels.

First time ordering from this restaurant.  The food was super delicious and the staff was super friendly.  Will definitely be ordering again soon.  Highly recommend if you have not yet tried this place.
I still prefer Whole Foods Market. This place is not well lit. Brighter sides: very friendly staff and fresh veggie and fruits that live up to the its name.

Fine enough, but definitely not the best I've ever had. A friend and I got a turkey hoagie, hot ranchero wrap (chicken fingers, bacon, ranch, lettuce tomato), and fries to split. I am a HUGE fan of hoagies and turkey is my go to - this turkey was a little dry for my taste and I was a bit disappointed. The wrap was decent, as were the fries. If I ordered again I would want to try the buffalo chicken cheesesteak, which a friend has gotten and recommended.

Another fun spot on Broadway with live music!  Dogs are allowed inside so keep that in mind.  The cheesesteak calzone was very good although the peppers could have been cooked slightly longer.  The Caesar salad was also surprisingly good.  I can imagine this would be an awesome last stop on your Broadway bar crawl if you are looking for somewhere to sop up the alcohol from the night!

Great breakfast! We were amazed at the big, warm smiles on the staffs' faces. Lots of energy and entusiasm. Very welcoming for kids...a kids bookshelf by the door to reduce boredom while waiting. The Original Pancake House features all of the breakfast basics you expect...especially the namesake pancakes.
Not enforcing masks. Will not be going here anymore. Please start enforcing masks to protect the health and safety of our community!
MY FAVORITE SPOT EVER. Best sesame balls I've had in Philadelphia. I come here at least once a week and stock up for the rest of the week.

Today was my first visit to Frutta Bowls. The decor is super cute and I felt like I was in a beach town. I love how they decided to decorate and utilize fresh and ceramic pineapples. You place your order at the counter and they call your name when it's ready. I ordered the Frutta bowl and added bananas. It was beautiful and was finished quickly. All of the fruit was perfectly ripe. My only complaint was the amount of granola. I wish there was more of the açaí purée and less granola. The bowl was super filling. I also feel that the store should open earlier than 9 if they can. That's not a realistic opening time if you'd like to swing by before work. Next time I would like to try a smoothie to get more of the fruit. The açaí bowl didn't feel overly healthy with all the granola.

When I am in Tucson on business, I drive by this restaurant many times and this time I decided to stop for dinner. I called first to see I they have gluten-free options and yes they do. I venture in shortly after my phone call. I walked into the restaurant to see the hostess and she asked me, Can I help you? I found to be a bit odd since I was in a restaurant to eat. I am usually greeted with a, "How many for dinner?" question. So I was a bit puzzled but okay.  As I am sitting reviewing the menu, I was a excited to see the first section marked with GF; however, as I turned to the main course pages, no GF marked on any of the menu items. So I inquired with the server and she brought me a GF menu. As soon as the GF menu was dropped, the main menu was picked up. I looked at the "GF menu" and it was a list of items. No descriptions or prices, just a list of names. I had to stop the server to ask what certain items were. I only asked for the description of one item and ordered that, Guac Taco It would have taken too long to ask for more. I was not the only one in the restaurant so I made the quick decision. When my meal came, it was two guacamole tacos and beans, since I do not eat rice. The tortillas had the consistency of flour tortillas so I asked the server to make sure that they in fact were GF tortillas and she stated, yes. Since I have Celiac and I have to abide by a strict GF diet, my body reacts to gluten immediately. Not even 10 minutes from leaving the restaurant, my stomach started cramping. And I was probably 25 minutes from my hotel, not that anything would aide in my cramping situation. For a destination that I was so looking forward to eating, I was disappointed in my experience. Was my food GF...I can only take the word of the server. Unfortunately, I will not be back. I can not risk it.

A very average burger ( not at all like the pictures ) and modest size fries for $ 6.99 is disappointing, but a glass of iced tea for $2.69 is outrageous.! Two burgers, two fries and one iced tea for $ 18.17 is a lesson learned. Never again.

This mall. Has it all!  From the most high end designers to the simplest store. 

This is an excellent mall. To do some good shopping. 

They have Tiffany's,  Louis Vuitton etc great spot for eats and much more.  

Parking garage is huge so parking here won't be an issue. 

Now if you are looking for a deal I will not recommend it.  This mall is full price only.  Not the best for deals. 

Restrooms are nice and clean. 

Sundays are open at 11.

Was recommended this place for breakfast. Stopped on a Sunday morning at 8am (just opened) I was the only patron. i wanted to sit outside on a small square table yet they were setting up for a huge party and took all the tables.   i was offered a huge 5-6 person table yet felt that would have been ride to take up for myself.   Only after i had ate half of my food inside, they took small square tables from the back and brought them out front ... hmmm Guess they couldn't do that for me.    

The bartender and another employee sat at the bar and gossiped the whole time about who knows what.   Plus one of them sat eating their breakfast.  Personally i don't believe any employee should be lounging around "in public view".   

My server was awesome and super accommodating yet o didn't catch her name. 

The food.... The shrimp was great!  The grits could not stand up on their own without the salsa that was placed over it.  Grits were bland.  I was very surprised to get low grade bacon. From the looks and price, i expected Maple or thick cut bacon instead of the thinly sliced Sysco style bacon that was just lumped together and had no flavor.  

A+ to my server yet subpar for the food.

The food is mediocre at best. Go to Bru burger across the street or even Tavern at the Point next door for tasty food! They are called World of Beer but have a terrible limited selection of brews. Take your money elsewhere folks!

I've always been on the fence with Five Guys.  I've eaten there a handful of times in the past and always thought that while it wasn't a bad burger, it was not meeting anywhere near my expectations of what an overhyped burger joint this place has turned into.  The burger is flavorless and grossly unseasoned.  I think Five Guys tries to mask this by letting you choose your own toppings and condiments but it's not enough.  And the American cheese they use is not cutting it.  I will say the fries for the most part are consistently good except on my last visit they were undercooked.   The regular fries order is enough for 2 people. The service is always fast and friendly.   Five Guys, not the worst I've had but not very good either.

We love the food. However I usually order thru their app to pick up on my way home from work. Their system is HORRIBLE!!! Today my order wasn't ready even though I had my confirmation email from 1 1/2 hours earlier & I was 5 minutes later than time it was to be ready. Mgr said somehow alarm notifying them was off & offered nothing but quick apology. Last time ordered 4 salads w/o bread. Got extra bread & dressing for only 2 salads. I called & mgr refunded cost. Of 4 times prior to this, only once was it completely correct. I'm not sure if I'll try again.

Since I was by myself, I sat at the sushi bar. Then I had to go back to the hostess stand to get a menu from the hostess who said she would bring it to me. Back to the sushi bar and waited.. and waited... 

Finally the sushi chef, who could tell no one had come over, was like - what would you like? I gave him my order for the lunch sushi/sashimi bento box.

Another minute goes by, then a waitress came up to me and was like, what can I get for you? So I gave her my order for the lunch sushi/sashimi bento box.  She leaves, and moments later, yet another waitress comes over and was like, what can I get for you?  So I gave her my order for the lunch sushi/sashimi bento box.  True story!  

The bento box actually took no time at all to arrive, and while I really did like the flavor of the sashimi, the actual contents of the box was pretty sad.  3 pieces of 'sushi' (egg, shrimp, and krab), 3 thin slices of sashimi, cucumber salad, and 4 pieces of a roll.  The bento boxes are supposed to come with salad first, which I got after flagging one of my many waitresses down, after I received my meal.  The salad was drenched in dressing, and the dressing was very garlicky and not very gingery.

I would go back to try some of their specialty rolls, but that bento box would not draw me back for lunch.

First thing we noticed was the harsh lighting, very "office casual", glaring off the plastic table covers. There were only three tables occupied, but we didn't have many choices of seats as a number of the tables had not yet been bussed, and old food sat out for the duration of our meal.

Service was excruciatingly slow. The food is not Spanish from Spain, but rather a selection of dishes from central and south America. That's totally fine, just be aware. The food was pretty tasty, but not stellar enough to warrant going back to a lackluster environment we ended spending way too much time in.

I used to come here quite often. I wish I had reviewed it then, for the food this visit was not nearly as good as I remembered. I ordered the meat cravers pizza on thin crust. The pizza despite its appetizing looks taste very bland and was a huge let down. For the price I could have gotten a larger better tasting pizza from a local mom and pop pizzeria. I remembered the pizza's being so flavorful at this CPK. Overall the service was good. I'm hoping maybe they were just having an off day.

Went for dinner at 6pm on a weeknight so please take my review with a grain of salt. Had actually read about the place in Lonely Planet NOLA (they are usually reliable!) so was hoping to be pleasantly surprised. Food was descent but the restaurant was all but vacant!! What difficult to look past this to appreciate the good service and ?authentic atmosphere, unfortunately... will not be back. Also, too pricey for what they delivered.

I usually have a great experience at this location. It is relaxing and enjoyable. The staff is very friendly and make good recommendations. I never feel like they are trying to oversell me. 

My favorite experience happened yesterday with Ashlyn. Not only was she friendly, she went out of her way to make me feel comfortable and to adapt to meet my needs. I will be coming back to her moving forward bc of what a great impression she made.

Took my girlfriends car to get a thermostat changed on a 2009 mazda 6. The process of the thermostat on a mazda 6 (every model) is the same from 2004 to 2009. The  guy Mike, after getting all the permanent vehicle information,  put us on hold to find out exactly what the cost would be. He came back on the phone (while on speaker phone) told my girlfriend what the cost of the part labor and tax was going to be. His exact words were "with the parts labor and tax we'll have you out the door for $125". 
Of course, after dropping off the car, since she's a woman, he called her an hour later to tell her that 
-the job is different because of the model. Which is not true. 
-that the problem getting to the back of the engine would make it more difficult. Not true (the thermostat is in the front)
-and then said he never gave her the out the door price for parts labor and tax. Which is also not true. He 100% did and we wrote it all down while talking to him about it, and that the work would be upwards of $300. For a thermostat change that the part cost $32.00.
This is the kind of shady tactics that mechanics and garages use to get over on people, especially women,  that don't know much about auto repair.  
This is so dirty and crooked. Before you go there know specifically what you need. And go with someone you know.
Crooked shady establisment.

I went to Under the Moon for a birthday brunch for my sister in law, and most recently for my mother's bday.  It's a very eclectic, retro kind of place, where just about every table, chair, cup and saucer are different.  Makes for an interesting environment, though some of the seating is very uncomfortable, as the table bases are old sewing machine tables, meaning it's nearly impossible to slide in and out of the table/banquette w/o smashing your knees.  

The menu is pretty diverse, and the lunch/brunch menu included Spanish tapas, which is great....but...you can only get tapas in a flight/tasting style, so if you want to order breakfast and a couple of tapas, you're not allowed to do that.  Odd, since we probably would have chosen two or three from the list they had...but no.  

Service was OK, and food was meh. Over-cooked eggs in the Benedict, overcooked potatoes.  They brought my hot tea in this very cool looking, but rather non-functional square teapot, that you couldn't use w/o burning your fingers and spilling as you poured.  

They have quite a large selection of desserts, and a bakery section in the front of the cafe.  The bread pudding was interesting...a solid block of bread pudding, very unusual.  It was OK, but a bit dense.  We also got a chocolate brownie cake type of dessert, which was very good.  

Overall...lots of style and ambiance, and a unique space, but the substance and service were a bit underwhelming.  The fact they had individual tapas dishes on the menu that we were, literally, not allowed to order, was disappointing.

COMPLETELY DISAPPOINTED 

**DISCLAIMER** I've stayed here many times before and it was great, great price and great amenities (pool, breakfast COFFEE, and Sesame Street shuttle) amd
clean. 

I understand covid precautions...I really do...but upon check in we were told that due to "safety" precautions the shuttle isn't running, no pool, no coffee, "grab and go" breakfast was a substitute for the previously great breakfast, and there was no coffee in the lobby (which you NEED after a day at Sesame with a bunch of tired toddlers)  
These are literally all of the reasons we chose this hotel at like $100 more than a hotel only a few miles away and there was nothing on the website about the lack of services/amenities. The discount offered was insulting when I asked the front desk...barely paid for the $25 parking we now had to pay at sesame. Not only was that all surprising and frustrating but the hotel itself was filthy. The lobby bathroom was out of paper towels, toilet paper, overflowing garbage etc..2 days in a row. I wouldn't stay here again. Moving forward we'll spend a few more dollars and stay at spring hill and walk.

I love the vibe here. It's super spacious and great for sitting down and getting work done if that's what you're looking for. They have free WiFi. They also have a small stage with acoustics which I presume would be for live music and acts later in the afternoon/night. 
The chai latte is delicious and reasonably priced. I think I'll try one of their breakfast items when I get hungry in a bit.

Update: got the avocado toast. Most places charge $12+ but this one came with two toasts for under $9. Add some salt and pepper on top!
Great food, the setting is very bar-like, it's good for a large group of people.

My wife and I came here on one of our trips to STL to see a baseball game. This place was not all that bad, its just you had to pay for your drinks even if you were playing. Don't get me wrong we still drank. The casino is close to the Landing and a short walk to another casino which I will do a separate review for. We did eat at the buffet and it was not too bad. We ate the "Beer and BBQ" buffet and it came with all you can drink beer. NICE. The casino floor was not all that big and the wait for new people who need cards is extremely long. All the people that worked there were really nice and we didn't have any issues. I'm sure if the drinks were free while we were playing I would have gave another star. Oh and getting a cab is CRAZY...I think we waited for like 30min and then we just went to the hotel next door and the bell hop there got us a cab in a few minutes. 

We probably would go back when we come back, if we didn't have anything else to do. 

MZ

Ok, I want to rate this high. However, I'm struck by three flaws at @ramen even though it has provided me a ramen fix on occasion.
1. Skimps on the ramen! Man, it's gone after three mouthfuls. The noodles are few and little. Though the broth is full of flavor.
2. Expensive! Jeesh $14 dollars for a bowl of skimpy noodles is outta line. Considering the food options around Main Street Manayunk and specifically that there's another ramen place one block down and two Pho place two blocks up.
3. Lack of choices. With 4 ramens to choose from. The excitement goes after one or two tastes.

Yeah, it's ok. I would hope the management steps up the game or reduce their prices.

Thank you so much for taking such wonderful care of us. You and your staff were so very accommodating. The food at El Charro was delicious! We  had a wonderful time 
Once again THANK YOU SO MUCH for everything! We had a great night out and you and your staff helped to make it that way! We will be back tomorrow night and Everytime we visit Tucson. Thanks again for all your hard work and consideration, shout out to Jesse for giving us a tour of the home and facilties as well as pointing out all the photos and history.

This review is more based on the interaction I had with one of the owners then it is actually about the Mac and cheese I had today. I'm hoping one of the owners will read this review and will reconsider their position as they open the new brick-and-mortar store. This week I found myself checking out their food truck near the state capital and I just got the regular mac & cheese which was perfect for the cold crisp fall day. Their regular mac & cheese that they have is quite good and it always hits the spot. They recently added a taco Mac to their menu which is very delicious. They recently announced that they're going to open a brick-and-mortar store near the Edwards on Overland. I run a meet up group that sees movies quite regularly and we go out to eat after the movie. I have several people in the group that cannot eat eat gluten. I had asked them  before about gluten-free options and I understand it's a struggle to find gluten-free pasta that works with their Mac and cheese concept. I know there is another successful food truck that utilizes all gluten-free menu so maybe they could discuss with them some ideas. On this day I asked I believe the female owner about gluten-free options at their new brick and mortar location. She replied that they haven't been able to find a gluten-free pasta and when I suggested maybe some salads since they already using chicken she said salads don't sell. My perception of the conversation was that she was arguing with me rather than trying to find different ways to grow her business. I was offering to bring sometimes groups of 10 or 15 to her business but she refuses to have any gluten-free options available on their menu so that means our group can't go there. I hope that they expand their menu as they get into their new location and consider other people such as gluten-free diets and be more accommodating to their diet. I'm excited to try the new brick-and-mortar store since I live close and I really enjoy their mac & cheese. Normally I would give this restaurant five stars based on the food but just my interaction with them today was a major disappointment and that they're not willing to accommodate those that cannot eat gluten. I think through networking and adding different food options they would be able to come up with something rather than just dismissing it and limiting what types of customers will frequent their business. Hopefully that will change and they will have some options available for those needing a gluten-free option. If you're looking for some delicious mac & cheese and that you and and you're not on a gluten-free diet this is definitely a place to check out especially on these crisp fall days. Their mac & cheese is some of the best in Boise will definitely warm you!

Believe the reviews. HORRIBLE. 

Trying to figure who gave less of a damn (AND IT'S GOING TO BE HARD): 

* the employee who at 4:20 told me the bartender doesn't start until 3:30 (when I told her it was 4:20, shrugged and walked away). 

* the concession guy who I was informed via sign to get my ticket at the concession stand from, and then after a 10-minute wait, pointed me at the ticket taker. 

* the ticket taker who didn't know that my 3-D movie (had to prove it to him on my phone) needed glasses. 

* the irritable manager who kicked over a box of glasses to the ticket taker after I asked for the glasses I'll need to view my movie. 

$17.24 for this? You have GOT. TO. BE. F'in. KIDDING. ME. 

Why even bother being open if you'll treat people like this from top to bottom? Horrible experience. Plenty of other theaters in Nashville without having to deal with this. Any movie theatre execs whining about having to close places need only look at this crappy treatment. I'm a 100-movie-a-year-in-theaters guy. 

Looks like it's time to upgrade my theater at home. 

$17.24. 

SMFH.

We had been to Brickwall a few times and really enjoyed it. The atmosphere was great and the food was decent with a good beer selection. Unfortunately this time we encountered the new menu. We are not a fan as some of our favorites are gone.

Kids got a cheesesteak and a chicken sandwich. For $15, you would expect top quality beef, but it was tough and came with no fries!! Are you kidding me, so for $5 more you get a tiny cup of fries. Now it was a $20 sandwich. To top off the bad meal, my other daughter's chicken sandwich was raw. The server was, sorry, we will take it off the bill. They weren't appalled or even really apologetic about it.

The server tried to make amends when my daughter didn't want anything else. He asked her if he can bring her a big brownie Sundae on them. She is pumped now. A few minutes later, he shows up with a wrapped up brownie and lets us know the chef said to throw it in the microwave with a cup of water for 30 seconds and it will be soft and gooey. I was flabbergasted and just paid the check and left.

They have lost a repeat customer.

Been here a couple of times. Service has been friendly and welcoming. Enjoyed the coffee. They have some great pour-overs as well as brewed coffee. Nice vibe. Seems like a place where folks like to hang out. It's in historic St. Charles, which is a cool place itself. Worth the trip.

I hadn't been here in a while and now I remember why. We were seated promptly (they weren't super busy on a Thursday night) and were promised their famous bread, which was never mentioned again. We had at least 4 different employees come to the table and none ever asked if we wanted bread.

The Rattlesnake eggs were good for what they were but we had ordered Ranch dipping sauce, which didn't come with the app and by the time we physically got up to find someone to get us the forgotten Ranch the app was room temp and the cheese was coagulated. 

My bfriend's side salad was served maybe 2 minutes before our meals so by eating his salad while it was cold his meals proceeded to cool down. 

I was only drinking water and while our server said she'd be right back to refill my empty glass she didn't return with the pitcher until she came to clear out plates.
I guess my real objection was to the service because if the food had been served with the requested sauces in a proper sequence with adequate spacing this would have been a four star review. 

As it stands the food was fine but we didn't get to enjoy it because the service was so off. The one truly redeeming thing was that my bfriend's Dallas filet was cooked perfectly. So that was good. 

I might try a different Texas Roadhouse someday, but I won't be back to this one.

Saw a great show but the VIP is a complete waste.  The "buffet" was rice and beans, salad, chicken with veggies and pita bread which they called Mediterranean  food.  Mediocre and not enough food either.  Only plus was we had great seats because we got in an hour early but next time we will come when the doors open @730 for the riff raff  and either  grab a seat at the bar or a table a bit farther back.

What a fantastic job the staff performed at this location! The team that work here are friendly, professional and thorough.  I will continue to return here everytime I need our vehicles serviced.  This location is definitely recommended for any eastsider's needs. Keep up the great work crew & management! Thank you very much!
First time here, last time here. Please do not order a stromboli here. Its just dough and sauce. No steak in it. Very bad and tasteless food.

Went there today for a late lunch (2PM). Ordered house Chardonnay and wife ordered a coke. Service was good. She ordered luncheon menu shrimp quesadilla and I ordered the luncheon shrimp cod combo.
We enjoyed our experience except for two things. The fried cod was not cooked all the way, but still edible and when we received our check I was charged $9.99 for the house Chardonnay. This seemed a bit pricey. After coming home I checked the menu online and found the house was $6.99.
We will come back and try it again. Hopefully a little bit better experience.

Spizzico DID have the best food in town, but NOT anymore! Ever since the original staff (the cooks, the manager, and even the person on a phone) left, the food from this place became literally un-edible! We did give them 3chances to get it right, but todays order was it - the pizza was not cooked through, it had absolutely no taste, and the chicken wings (which we requested to be Mild) had HOT sauce poured all over them (not the way you make them, anyway), and there were no Blue Cheese and celery. We will stay away from this joint from now on, and so should you. ACME frozen pizzas are 10 times better than theirs.

Ease of making a rezzy: 
The time I wanted wasn't available, but 15 minutes later I received a text indicating that my requested time was now open!.....5 stars (way to go Yelp reservations!)

Service: 
Great. Attentive and relaxed. No obvious signs of upselling. 5 stars. 

Food:
Chicken wing appetizer: ridiculously good. I would come back just for those.  Perfectly fried with amazing flavor.  My last bite, though, must have had an unusually large chunk of ginger in it because it overwhelmed me.  I could see someone who didn't love robust flavors not agreeing with the rave reviews. For me, 5 stars!

Entrees: Black rice seafood paella. 2.5 stars. The black rice seemed to be crunchy and a bit undercooked (not sure if that's the way the chef intended). Bits of seafood atop about one cup of rice for $30 seemed a bit pricey. Presentation was nice in a cast iron dish.  

Clams with annatto bread sticks. 4 stars.  This dish was very flavorful with the perfect amount of spice.  There was broth that amounted to a cup of soup left in the dish that I wanted to slurp right up! Excellent clams, but not a huge fan of the lamb bacon accompaniment. It almost seemed raw to me, but my husband loved it. 

Overall, I like this place.  The ambiance was cool and I could easily overhear other patrons praise their dishes (you do sit that close to others). My biggest pet peeve: I have a hard time dropping $100 on dinner when I have to rip off paper towels from the end of the table for a napkin like I'm at Hooters.  Or, reach across the table to pull out a fork and knife from a wax paper sleeve. Bad call.  Yes the food is good, but at that price point there are many other options that provide a more connected experience. When my entree is $30 bucks, I expect a real freakin napkin. But those wings might make me forget that.

While the food is decent and the service appears to be good the servers "forget" to tell you about unexpected charges to your bill at the end of the meal then add up the items wrong ... being a food server is tough, I've worked as one, but tell the customers up front about all the dining costs, don't put on extra charges, and don't make up the final bill amount in your head.  (Also, refill drinks more frequently, and I don't mean alcohol!)

I have been here a few times and I would have to say this place is just okay. The service is always hit or miss. They do have a lot of seating options because the place is pretty big. They have the elaborately carved booths and the flashy murals. It's pretty inexpensive here. They have some starters that aren't really Mexican, but more American. So if you have someone in your party who doesn't totally love Mexican food, that's a nice option for them. The arroz con pollo is pretty good here. Nothing in particular has really stood out to me. I personally think there are better Mexican restaurants in the area with better specials, food, and friendlier staff.
This is my second visit. Everything I've had is great. I love the choices, soup, salad or noodle dish. The staff is very friendly and it is a comfortable seating area.

My family decided to renovate the basement when we came across a mold problem. We were recommended Hera Tech from another friend who's family had a similar situation but had such wonderful results. Since they had a great experience we decided to give them a shot. Plus they were local! We got to meet Nikhil and his team who were all very professional from the moment we called; up until the job was done. The entire team was very patient and thorough answering all of our questions, explaining their jobs & process step by step, and providing outstanding customer service. 
Their services were a bit pricey from other ones we looked into, but we invested in them knowing how local they were and hearing how quickly they could get the job done in a timely manner. Overall, it was worth the money knowing we got the best bang for our bucks: local service, phenomenal customer service from start to finish, and really feeling like they genuinely cared about us and our home!

This establishment is wonderful! I came here for brunch when I had some family in town, and we had the loveliest time. We loved the family style concept and ended up sitting with a friendly family from Nashville. The breakfast spread had all the classic southern breakfast items: biscuits, gravy, pancakes, cinnamon rolls, eggs, sausage, bacon, ham, and fried chicken. This was the best fried chicken I have ever had. I don't even like fried chicken, and I helped myself to several servings! My one critique is that I am not a big eater. This is an experience but is expensive if you aren't able to "pack it away." Also, it gets really crowded. We arrived at 9 am on a Sunday and still waited for twenty minutes. You need to come early!

I just moved to St. Pete yesterday and came here for food. I had never been to a Publix before and was pleasantly surprised. At least 4 staff members very kindly asked me if I needed help while I was browsing and 2 others showed me to specific items and had great conversations with me about the city. The check-out staff were very kind and funny too! I've never been to a grocery store with such great a culture/atmosphere and will be coming back!

One of the best choices I have ever made. Food was incredible, I had the Rooster Slinger it was amazingly good. The only thing keeping them from a 5th star is the service was slow. Overall my fiancé and I will be returning.

I love this place. Very sad at the cleanliness.  From the front patio, to the table.  Dirty.  The walls of our booth---dirt!! I am from Miami.....Cubans are immaculate!  Restaurants are like their homes....clean! The health felt reviews are terrible!

The most horrendous service I've ever had in my 38 years of living.  Waited 45 minutes for food and then finally my guest flagged down one of the waitresses and asked ask "where is our food" she responded by saying "what are you doing here?"  45 minutes!  Waitresses walked by numerous times and NEVER asked about us.   I would give a negative but this site won't allow it.  I've been treated better in foreign countries where English isn't the spoken language.
