Beautiful place, well decorated and very clean. Menu had lots of vegan options. I had a matcha latte and a gingerbread cookie today cost was $9- something overpriced imo. They had numerous options for nut and oat milk. My tea was luke warm not hot as I personally would have preferred. Cookie was ok. My friend had a matcha latte also hers was also luke warm not hot. We decided to order some food from the menus on the table when we went to order we where told that menu is only available from 5pm on (it was 2pm) the other options didn't excite us so we didn't get food. I don't know how it works but why have a menu on tables if people's can't order from them? 

Apparently they play live music. They also had beautiful art for sale on the walls. I'd give this place another chance didn't love it didn't hate it.

Hella good! That is all!
We had cat fish, gumbo, gator bites(fried), hush puppies and crab cakes. Excellent

My very first visit to Brick and Brew- you could tell they were just getting into the swing of things. Service was a bit slow, there was some confusion amongst the staff (but very well masked, I feel like I may have noticed only having been a server myself). Regardless, the food was very good. 

I have frequented the location again a few times- their margarita on the specialty cocktail list was not something that I enjoyed to my particular taste, but everything else has been phenomenal! 

The mussels... OH the mussels.. they are what dreams are made of! The prosciutto fries were also amazing. I've dolloped in their pizzas, which also have not disappointed. It's also become a new stop on my "casual drink" trail. Love sitting down outside to just have a glass of wine! 

Try and catch a seat outside on a nice day or least right by the window! So glad to see a place like this around Havertown aside from the relentless Irish Pubs/Sports Bars that plague the area (don't get me wrong, I enjoy them.. but there's A LOT). Overall, I am definitely a fan!

STAY. AWAY. Worst service ever. I cannot imagine how someone would purchase a car here.  TREAT YOU LIKE GARBAGE.  Liars. I just can't understand how they are in business. Do yourself a favor and stay far away  I wish I could give it a zero.   They really should not be allowed to work with people.

Went for Sunday Brunch but could only eat a croisant. Every available option was really breakfast and I don't eat much breakfast food. Really disappointed because I was excited to taste the food here. Menu did not reflect available brunch options

The venue itself is a ten! I would like to say that your security officers could use a lesson in hospitality. The pudgy, mid forties, brown haired, guy you had working outside this evening was less than desirable and treated the group of people I was with terribly. Instead of asking us to not take photos he flashed his "ego" light vigorously at us with no explanation and upon us asking why it was not allowed he became very hostile and shouted at us. All he had to do was say, "ladies, photography is not allowed". Then again, he's a forty year old security guard- probably used his "aggression" to compensate for his lack of character.
This place is great! Wish they'd kick the customer service up a bit though.

The pizza is okay....crust is too hard, it needs more flavor in the sauce and only about 4 pieces of pepperoni on a large pizza! lol The place is a mess, if they don't get better staff it will be gone within the year. Food all over the floor, dirty and not one customer in the place with three girls just standing behind the register doing nothing but twirling their hair. It's a shame, I think with a little work it could have been something.

This place is pretty good! I decided to visit here on my daughters recommendation. We have been seeing this birria and consome combo all over social media and had to try it. I ordered 3 tacos which come with beans and rice and the consome soup/dip and an order of 3 chicken flautas. OMG! The taco meal was petty big, and im a big guy. The tacos themselves were thick, but i wish there was more cheese on them (i ordered with cheese). The consome was good too, i wish it had more flavor really, it was a little on the mild side. The flautas were the hit! Fried perfectly, and served with a chunky guacamole with a little too much tomatoes for my tastes, but it was still pretty good. Order ahead and save your self wait time! Wait staff were sweet and amazing!

Great brunch spot with good food! Me and my friends came here on a weekday for brunch and waited about 10-15 minutes. We got some coffee while waiting since they had a section for coffee and bread. We ordered the house smoked salmon Benedict, crab cake Benedict, and the waffle with fried chicken. Really enjoyed the mustard crab cake Benedict a lot! All the dishes were good and very filling. Servers were very friendly and attentive! They also have their own house made hot sauce for the chicken and waffle. The Benedicts came with potatoes and salad.

I would originally give this place 5 stars... The interior is pretty cool and the girl who waited on me was very sweet. 

However it was my first time here and I didn't have a stellar experience in the end. I ordered an iced coffee and the Hudson bagel. Iced coffee was pretty good.. Nothing I would go outside of my way to make a trip for but good. 

Then, the bagel. Got the Hudson bagel, no bacon (to go) got in my car and checked it out to make sure the cook got it right and... There was bacon on there. 
Okay, so I work in a bar. And I could have easily picked it off myself. I'm sure I have consumed some things non-vegetarian in the past unknowingly. But... I figured I was there and would rather have a new sandwich since I checked it before getting home. 

The girl took it without a problem and said she understood as she also doesn't eat meat.. When I got it back she told me he was in the process of making another order for someone else and gave me that one (after I questioned the timing) got to my car.. And yep. The cook just picked the bacon off.. With pieces of bacon still left behind. I didn't go back in because I didn't want to make a fuss and be all hardcore about it. However it left me with a bad taste in my mouth about One shot. To me, that is just pure laziness at it's best. 

I did eat the sandwich and survive. I'm pretty understanding. It's just a matter of the point, I asked for it not to be picked off. And it was after being assured it wasn't. Ah well.. Hope one shot reads and takes a note. And vegetarians/kosher customers beware.

Food - 5 stars.   Service and process - 1 star.   We went here with a large group of 19 people.   You can't actually make a reservation for that many - after 15, they insist you do a catering menu or something.  We didn't want to do that, so we made two separate reservations for 10 and asked that they seat us near each other.   We asked again when we got to the restaurant, but ended up seated clear across the restaurant from each other.  There was plenty of space to accommodate us together, but OK...whatever.  

The server assigned to our table was super slow and clearly overwhelmed.  He gave us all Happy Hour menus and took a round of drink orders.  The happy hour rocks - $2 tacos, a selection of apps, and half price drinks - I'm sold.  The problem was that I'd looked at the menu online and really wanted some of the street corn.  We asked for the regular menu, but were told that the happy hour menu was our only option until 6pm.   It was 5:15.   Ok fine.  We chowed down on the complimentary plantain chips and salsa - yum.  Apps were some queso fundido and the chicken flautas.  The flautas were amazing.   We also sampled each of the $2 tacos - all were enjoyable.  

At the end of the meal, we get the check.  One check for nine people.  He had warned us when we started ordering, but the warning really should have come with the reservation.  And frankly, come on...just split it already.  It's easy and I know it.  He's lucky it was the beginning of the trip and we all still had cash.  Because they forced us to order only happy hour food, it was actually very reasonable.  Rather than try and do the math for nine people, we divided evenly.  Not ideal, but we made it work.   

We meet again with the other half of our party outside and the first thing I hear about is how amazing the corn was.  Wait...how did they get corn?   Overall, I liked the food and the happy hour, but the place was ruined for me by bad service and process.

This place has horrible service. The water took 15 mins and the food over 40 mins to be completed for the take out order. I was there on a Monday  morning at 1am. 

The spicy sausage poboy was really good. Really good actually. The pork chop was ok and not stand out. The fried green tomato was bland and not cooked well. I like the tomato crisp but not crunchy or raw. The gumbo was standard. 

If I was starving early in the morning and nothing else was open yes - go there. If not, pass.

I did banking here for a U of A club, I am not a fan of vantage west. They charged fees for everything, the tellers were slow and not very helpful. This location is a pain to get into and out of. 

I'd take my money somewhere else

You need a reservation, please call ahead of time.  We didn't have one and was told we needed it next time.  Totally apologize, you must be the best Korean bbq in town.  Not.

I understand it's AYCE town here in Reno with the numerous sushi restaurants and buffets but have some passion in your execution before you demand a reservation thinking you're any hot shit.

Your bathroom doesn't have soap or paper towels.  86 from the beginning of the meal and afterwards it still wasn't refilled.

We from San Francisco and Los Angeles.  We know coming here we didn't expect anything, please Nowhere can beat Los Angeles for Korean, not even Korea in some opinions.  

Ban Chan, 4 of the most pathetic and horrific I have ever seen.  You mind as well just skip that or give us a salad.

Rice, how old is it?  Did you not measure the water level, it would be nice to use it as a face scrub.

AYCE, wouldn't you think to fill your customers up with banchan and edible rice which cost nothing because it's cheap stuff you're using anyways?  Even the worst grade select meat will cost more.  

The meat, mediocre at best, pork belly was best, 5 out of 10, the rest under 5. 

Go visit park bbq or genwa in Los Angeles.  Go to a BCD chain, $20 you get kalbi and soondubu, unlimited badass ban Chan , grilled fish, and top tier steaming hot soft and airy rice.  

Go to San Francisco tofu house, and try their rice out of a clay pot.  One of the best.  

None of these spots demand a reservation, probably don't even take any, just wait for your table.  It's that good.  

Not a good value for $30pp, if it included all you can drink beer, maybe.  Not.

One extra star for Jacky because he was a good server, not his fault hostess and food was not up to par.
This place is great! Love the hometown atmosphere always has good live music classic rock/blues it welcomes everybody!
I love Pho Grand!!! I service is quick, the food is delicious. Also, the wait staff is fairly nice.

Tried to purchase 2021 Subaru ascent, was dealing with Anthony, got everything set in on a Sunday evening before closing time, they ran our credit, we settled on numbers, Anthony said he didn't work on Monday but he would come in anyway to meet us there at 5ish. He said he'd slap a sold sticker on it and put it in the back for us to pick it up Monday. Then Monday comes around and we call him and he says yeah he will get it ready for us. We're pretty excited all day and clear our agenda for the day so we can do this...so an hour before we're supposed to go pick up the car and sign, he says he doesn't have the deal anymore and his finance guy is working on stuff and will get back to us. Dude never got back to us. Complete let down and a waste of my time, not to mention a waste of inquiries on my credit. Don't waste your time here, go somewhere where they have professionals, not time wasting clowns.
Good beer. Good food. After spending time in Germany, Frankford Hall is definitely a close substitute. Come early because the lines get long after 7pm.
The service was excellent. My country fried steak was ok, not very flaky. Hubby got the Mexican crepes they were spicy but good.

Vinoteca was a staggering disappointment - a "renowned" tapas chef, friendly wait staff, and a great location was no match for the overly-oily, overly-cheesed dishes, underwhemlingly theme of most of the tapas, and the utter lack of proper execution in serving wine, water, and food. 

Unfortunately, the lack of variety in Wilmington might necessitate my return, but I shall rue that day.

I was excited that this place Finally opened! The space itself is beautiful. My husband and I each ordered a truland burger, and they were disgusting! So much grease all over the burger and bread, dripping everywhere. The meat was over cooked and everything covered in tons of grease. We decided not to get sides (no fries included with burger). The Nutella shake was good. We do not plan on going back, for the high price there are many more places in Tucson with a much better burger.  If you are in the mood to eat grease with a side of burger, this is your place.
Great Thai food! Best in West County area for sure. The ladies that do all the work there and get the orders ready are awesome too! Been going here since I was in high school 20 years ago.

I was talking to them about a car they had for sale(not knowing it was already sold a couple weeks before). So I make an appointment, drive all the way out there just to find out they sold the car already. Yeah, I wouldn't suggest buying from them.

Spitale's Deli is a simple place that while it may be short on looks, is great for its food.  I lean toward the po-boys, with fresh crisp french bread and an interesting choice of toppings.  Today it was the grilled turkey pastrami.  The po-boy came dressed with swiss cheese and a generous portion of sliced turkey and pastrami.  You get your choice of small (6"), medium (9") or large (12").

Plate lunches are worth trying as well.  

Don't let the looks fool you, it's a restaurant not a body shop.

Caladesi Island was a spontaneous and last minute thought for us on a four day trip, but I am SO glad we chose something outdoorsy for the last day. We drove to Honeymoon Island, checked out the area but it was a bit crowded. We then bought ferry tickets and took the ferry to Caladesi. The ferry crew were so welcoming and friendly, they felt like family! Caladesi itself is absolutely amazing. It is an "untouched" island where you're bound to see some wildlife! We saw a beautiful tortoise as soon as we got on land! We visited the shop, walked along the beach, and then headed for the kayak rentals. I HIGHLY recommend to EVERYONE that you should kayak the mangroves of Caladesi if you're in the tampa area and have a day to spare. The experience was like no other! I took a first time kayaker with me and he loved it! There is a 3 hour option and a 1 hour option. Either will have breathtaking views and probably manatees! I will definitely be back to this beautiful beach asap!

Just tried this place out and will definitely be back. The front desk staff are so sweet, I forgot my water and they gave me one at no charge. The class was fun or as fun as a workout can be and went by quickly thanks to an energetic instructor, good music and cold air. I like that they dim the lights or turn them off  so you're just focusing on the workout. The chilled eucalyptus towel at the end is a welcomed treat.

It's a pretty darn good deal for AYCE sushi. $21.00 on a weekday night.

Though service is slow, just order enough to last you until the next time your order comes out. Whenever the waitress/server delivers the food or comes in your general vicinity ask for a new menu. Also, the appetizers like shu mai and gyoza tend to come out a little faster, so maybe order some of that to bridge between the time that the sushi comes out.

Pork katsu was pretty good (they used a tempura breading which is fine), as were most of the rolls. Fish is fresh enough for me and they allow you to order just sashimi so I will come on back for this great deal

Lots more items on the AYCE menu that I haven't mentioned including fried bananas, green tea ice creams, soups/salads, tempura, etc.

The building on the intersection of 15th and walnut has seen quite a number of dwellers through the trails of time from the National Bank to the Butcher & Singer Brokerage to the famed Striped bass, but It now houses a red meat joint namesake to the brokerage firm. What hasn't changed is the aura of the interiors which still look  reminiscent of the beautiful old times with a mammoth chandelier the crowning glory of this place. 
Now to more important things pertaining the palate. bunch of us planned to attack  this place for lunch for the 6$ burger. We landed at this place around 12ish and the place was still pretty empty so dint find a problem to seat the 9 of us swiftly by our pleasant hostess . 
Looking at the menu the place is swinging towards a pricey side but does have some interesting selections like the alaskan king crab legs , and the idaho trout and the Australian twin lobster tails, but burgers is what we came for and burgers what we got. 

The service was impeccable , our waitress was sweet and prompt with our drinks and food. She even was raving bout how most ppl would drop by just for burgers and they almost grill ~200 burgers for lunch a day. I can totally see why too. the burger is made of dry aged prime beef topped with english chedder and charred onions. There was also a smokey sauce romanticizing between layers of lettuce , tomato and the pickle. The burger was soft but at the same time had been toasted with butter which added to the flavor. the burger was quite a mouthful and becomes messy as ur blaze ur way through this scrumptious burger, and that's exactly how its supposed to be in my mind, i certainly got more than i expected at this place. 

I am not that big a 'star' of the starr restaurants primarily because i generally am not satisfied with their service, but this place has me thinking again and has the right recipes to be a successful  Meat place to feature in the ever expanding stellar establishments in philadelphia.

Meet a friend often for breakfast. We decided to try Our Place today and we totally enjoyed ourselves. It is a large comfortable diner type restaurant. I saw a private room with a huge table which would be great if you had a group.

The decaf coffee was hot and tasty. I order the Triple Decker Club. It was stacked high with fresh roasted turkey, smoked ham, bacon, Lettuce tomato and mayonnaise. My choice of bread was lightly toasted wheat. The sandwich was made to my liking and large enough to take half home. The side I chose was breakfast potatoes which I don't recommend.

My friend decided to have  scrambled eggs, bacon and hash browns with toast. She said it was made exactly as she ordered it. She wanted peanut butter for her toast but declined as it was $.95 and it didn't seem worth it.

Before our breakfasts were served my guy called and asked to join us. I ordered him softly scrambled eggs, bacon, hash browns and wheat toast. When he sat down, the server asked what he would like. I explained the second order I placed was for him. She mentioned that she thought it was a to go order but that it was ready. The portions were significantly smaller than the one that my friend was served (that was interesting)

The restaurant is clean, staff is friendly and proficient. The food is well prepared and fairly priced. Oh, there is a large free parking lot too. Definitely a place to go to again.

The new A La Mousse location is a light, bright, and welcoming space. I ran in just to grab a couple of desserts, but I could definitely see myself returning to enjoy a dessert and tea later. 

The dessert case still had plenty of choices in it late in the evening. I had the matcha, earl gray, raspberry cake and my husband got the chocolate caramel cake. Both were packaged up nicely for our trip home. They traveled well and tasted nice and fresh. The matcha earl gray cake was mild and not very sweet. I enjoyed it, but personally, I would have liked stronger earl grey and matcha flavors. The stand out was the chocolate caramel cake. It was not too sweet and the chocolate was nice and dark. It also had a pleasing contrast of textures from the chocolate glaze outside and soft cake, creamy mousse, caramel, and crispy chocolate pieces inside. The cake prices are the same as the original location, so it's a bit steep. Not sure about the tea one, but the chocolate one was worth it though. 

The main negative is the parking can be hard to find, but that is the same issue as with the Chinatown location. I'd recommend to take public transport if you can.
I could probably eat a pencil eraser less rubbery and awful as this stuff. Felt like chewing on leather. Wasn't even that good tasting either.

I like Dollar Tree (DT) in general and I tend to go to different stores in the area because you never know what great deal they may have in different stores. The reason I started doing this is my husband used to bring the kids to a DT when they were really little and he would say "you can have anything you want in the store" as a treat. :) I usually would just tag along and look around, get some aluminum foil or whatever, but one day that DT had a ton of Boost 6 packs! A 6-pack of Boost for a dollar and they didn't expire for another year! We checked other DT's and no other store had Boost. When that store ran out we couldn't bear to buy boost for regular price.

So, I pop into these every blue moon to see what they have.

This one really impressed me because I found an item that I verified with the clerk that will not be back once it's gone...so I got all of them. I asked for a box and the clerk found me 2 boxes, packed everything in the boxes for me, then pushed my cart to my SUV, and loaded everything for me. I've never experienced this type of service at any DT. So kudos to the employees at this location for customer service!

The only reason this place doesn't get a 5 stars is because it was difficult to find a cart.

Can't recommend these boys enough! 
Went in for an oil change and quick checkup and was made aware of problems a previous mechanic swindled me over. These guys definitely saved me a totaled car and a hell of a lot of money. Will keep coming back!

I really wanted to like this place. They have a great setup and ambience, and a great location. The food is super pricey and the wait times are insanely long. I waited over 30 minutes for them to bring out two scoops of vanilla ice cream, and they messed up the order (one scoop of chocolate and one scoop of strawberry - literally the opposite of what I ordered) - keep in mind there was almost no one in the cafe, so it's not like it was rush hour. Coffee isn't very good, not worth the price. And the avocado toast was a major regret; it was $14, took them 20 minutes to bring it out and the whole thing was soggy and cold. The vegan burger was actually very good, and their fries are pretty good too - the one part of the meal that went well.

Great location and lovely staff. 

took away one star for parking prices, gross mixology at the gin bar and small size of pool. 

Food is great at their restaurants and all the rooms are VERY tasteful.

Went to The Docket for a rainy Saturday morning brunch. It is located on the first floor corner of the SLU law building but inside it's a modern diner. The breakfast menu is extensive and includes breakfast cocktails. We settled on the breakfast fried rice and chocolate chip pancakes and left with very full bellies. The service was great. We were immediately greeted when we walked in and the waitress was attentive without feeling intrusive. For a no frills, cheap breakfast that will leave you satisfied I would recommend stopping by The Docket.

AutoZone, you're the bomb. 

I've gotten my battery tested here and a blinker bulb so it's about time they get a review. It may not be the prettiest of places but it gets the job done. I was having car battery issues over the summer and they test batteries for free and give advice on what to do next. And no, they didn't suggest buying a new battery from them. That's what you call classy. It was a separate issue and was so nice to have the honest complimentary help. 

My blinker went out last week so I stopped in and they looked up the model of my car and grabbed the correct bulb right off the shelf. I've heard they'll put it in your car for you but I didn't ask. I went on my way and did it myself. 

Long story short, they kill it in the customer service department. Keep up the great work, AutoZone!

Got their specialty Hot Chicken. Made the mistake of eating the crispy crust and skin off of the half order of chicken. The taste was great, but then the heat hit me like a great ball of fire! The sweet tea did not help put out the fire, and eating the chicken meat did not help either.  Next time I am eating the chicken meat and crust at the same time. I was the only one in my party of 8 that had this issue.  Will eat here again for sure!
My car got a flat tire at 8am. These guys opened up and saved my morning. Very friendly! Go Select Auto

This review is from July 2013. 
Came here with daughter and son in law for fourth of july week.
Appetizers were very good. we had the lobster dumplings, the potstickers, and crab cakes. All worth ordering again. Entrees weren't as good I tried the drum as I had had such a great drum at nola but unfortunately it was dry and sauce was non-existent. I cant remember what my daughter  had but she was not impressed. Son in law had steak which is not good idea at seafood specialty restaurant. 
Service was just ok.

Aunthenic? No , Americanized? Very much. This didn't have the authentic taste and the only thing that was somewhat good to me was the cabbage which was way to buttery to be Jamaican, the oxtail? Tasted more like they added nutmeg or something but I can gaurentee that's definitely not authentic Jamaican taste ,Was so tough and I only got three pieces? For 14$? The rice and beans were bland Yeah no I really shoulda went out my way and made it at home but here I go wanting to try local places. Also got the festival was more doughy than anything and way to much cinnamon

Came here for a very special occasion. Our waiter was wonderful! The Manager came over twice, and also was caring, and checked up on us.
My kids (16, and 22) had great meals, my wife's shrimp was quite undercooked, so sent back for correction, and my 12 oz fillet was pretty flavorless. I cringe at the thought that I had a much better tasting Filet at the Texas Roadhouse. 
The tenderloin taco appetizer (I had one from my kids) was amazing - the best part of the whole meal for me.
My shrimp appetizer was slimy, quite nasty, so sent back, and replaced.
I got the chocolate cake with molten center, which was way, way overcooked to the point of being bone dry.
The kitchen scored a big zero from me..........
At least my kids had good meals. The prices here are completely outrageous!! 

This place used to be my favorite steak house!!! No longer. 

$64 for a 2 lb. Lobster??? REALLY??? 

I've been here in the past for very special occasions, but at this point, I'm done.

Their prices are absurd, and the food didn't nearly measure up to the price charged. If it was so great that it knocked my socks off, then fine, it is what it is. But it wasn't!

Morton's used to be great..........

Since I didn't see a email address on the main website I will post my complaint here.  Tonight we went to DD for dinner since my wife wanted seafood.  This is not our first time there since we like the food and the atmosphere.  We ordered and food was great.  Service was a slow for a Tuesday with nobody in the restaurant, however that is not my complaint.  The complaint is for the group of people that wait staff sat on the patio next to the main door leading into the restaurant (outside seating area).  We got sat at the 4th table down from where this group was.  Through our whole meal we listened to this loud table swearing and carrying on like teenage kids.  Actually teenagers acted better than they did.  During the whole time the wait staff was catering to this table and they could hear was being said.  The manager was also tending to this table.  I let it go until the end of our meal.  Also, another couple was sat next to us which would be table 3 from the group.  Shortly after sitting down and listening to how the other table was carrying on they asked to be moved.  We paid and as we passed their table I told them how inconsiderate they were for the way they carried on.  The reply I got from the guy standing next to the table was, "Don't come back if you don't like it!".  So my wife asked the wait staff for the manager.  My wife voice her issues with the manager and I think it kind of went on deaf ears since we found out they are regulars to the restaurant.  Not once did any of the wait staff ask the table to settle down.  Also, I think they should have been sat someplace else away from the other tables.  We eat there every 3-4 weeks but may be awhile before we go back.   So hopefully this gets back to the owner or another manager for DD.
Our experience tonight was horrific! From the kitchen moving like pond water to the wait staff that was below par I WILL NOT BE BACK!!

This is the best Sushi place and fried rice place in Nashville. The service is a bit slow, but the food is always hot and fresh. Tastes very upscale with reasonable prices. Love love love this place. I'm constantly recommending to everyone I know. I'm giving four stars because the service is quite subpar. My servers have been found to be quite awkward and can sometimes come off as a bit stingy. For example, if you pay in cash, they will give you the bills back, but never your actual coins. It just rubs me the wrong way. However, I go here all the time, and i'm not exaggerating. 10/10 Recommend!

I find myself coming to the Galleria when I need to hit up several stores in one stop. There are several large department stores here such as Dillard's, Macy's, and Nordstrom's, which is new! There are a large variety of other stores as well. There's something for everyone! The mall is pretty nice IMO. I love how it is decorated during the holidays. In addition to a large selection of stores, there are many food options to choose from including a food court in the basement where the cinema is but there are also restaurants. These include five guys, cheesecake factory, panera and California pizza kitchen. 

The only downside about this place is that parking can be a big pain. Get here early because it gets pretty crowded!

Called them with a pretty detailed message but I never did hear back from anyone. I just can't understand why a business would give you the option to leave a message  but not call you back. I was really hoping to get some service from them based on the yelp reviews but we weren't so lucky.

I really don't know why I bother with Big Lots. Back in the day (15+ years ago) you could find "close out" prices of different items. Nowadays it seems like their prices are the same if not more than Target/Walmart. 

We stopped in to look for some cheap tomato cages. We needed about 12 of them & didn't want to spend too much. We looked around & found some 33 inch tomato cages for 3 bucks a piece. Since Lowes is literally in the same parking lot we looked over there and the SAME tomato cages at Lowes are $1.88!! We bought the cages at Lowes.

Place has sort of slid down hill imho.  We had two things on the table that were under cooked, apple fritters were full of raw dough

Staff was great as usual as well as atmosphere. 

Maybe I'm old school but if I'm having coffee with brunch keep the cup full. I shouldn't have to ask.

I'm all about the food, cool/popular factor doesn't matter to me.

I usually go to a hairstylist... This was my first time in a barbershop, my stylist had been busy and my hair was getting out of control, luckily a friend recommended me this place, Barber Ito specifically, I was impressed, the place is clean, people are friendly and Ito gave me a nice clean cut! My only complaint is the lowd foul music they play.

I'd been wanting to try this place forever and I finally got my chance this past Sunday. I met a friend at Indeblue for brunch and since I was early I took a seat at the bar and grabbed a drink. The Rose Mimosa was nothing to write home to momma about (it was just champagne and grenadine), but the price of the drink ($6) and the attentiveness of the hostess/bartender were pluses. For brunch I went with the French toast and a side of bacon. I didn't pick the most adventurous thing on the menu but overall the food was decent (especially the side of super savory bacon...it was a VERY healthy portion and probably could've been shared). PROS: fast service, they honored my reservation time, the staff was super friendly, the restaurant/bathrooms were clean, portion sizes were perfect, and the prices were on par for the location.  CONS: There isn't a variety of options on the menu, the cocktails could've been a bit more daring. 

I'm definitely visiting again because there are some other items on the menu that I'd love to give a try. My friend had the chicken and waffles and she gave it an 8 out of 10.

This place makes up prices depending on if they recognize you, or not, it would seem. $10 for Makers Mark, in a dive bar? Believe it or not, this is not top of the line. There is no top shelf when there is only three choices. I could go to a place where all the patrons were NOT just lonely misfits who want attention at karaoke night, but, on Christmas Eve I chose to stay in my neighborhood. Not the best idea. In fact, every time I go to this place, it is not the best idea. But then again, Santa Barbara never really understands reality, not even the underbelly of Santa Barbara understands reality.

Is giving less than 1 star an option? It should be!

Our intent was to have a nice and romantic dinner on the day prior to Valentine's Day to avoid the rush of restaurant crowds, so on the evening of Tuesday, February 13, my partner and I arrived at the restaurant at 7:30PM per prior reservations made by phone. When we arrived, we were promptly seated by the attendant at the reception counter. Upon being seated, we waited for approximately 15 minutes before our server, Alanna, came over to introduce herself and to take our drink orders. She was very pleasant and friendly; however, after taking our drink orders, we waiting for another 15 minutes or so for our coffee to arrive. Alanna then returned to take our appetizer and food orders, again another 20-25 minutes until our appetizers (Caesar Salad and Lobster Bisque soup) and bread arrived at our table. My date ordered stuffed flounder/Yukon Gold garlic mashed potatoes; I ordered shrimp and pasta. In the meantime, another party arrived at the table next to us; we could clearly hear complaints from that party to Alanna on the delayed service they were receiving, as well. She sincerely apologized to our table and to the other party about the long wait. When asked why there was such a delay, she mentioned that there was a rush of customers that didn't have prior reservations and because of this; the restaurant was short-staffed and didn't have enough servers to accommodate the extra, unexpected customers. We were a little annoyed with the response; however, we realized that this was not within her/restaurant's control. When my partner's Caesar Salad arrived, it was very wilted and drenched in salad dressing. We didn't complain, but he was only able to eat half as it was not very appetizing; however, my soup was very good. After finishing our appetizers and approximately 30 minutes later, Manager, John Park, stopped by our table to tell my date that the cook "wasn't feeling very good about how the flounder turned out." Huh? He then proceeded to tell my date that he could order any other fish which would be prepared in any way that he would like. My date is extremely particular about his food selections, so we were quite annoyed that it took over 45 minutes to find out that his food choice was not available, with a very insincere and nonchalant apology about what was happening. Mr. Park never offered to adjust our bill until we asked because at this point, the whole situation was unacceptable. When asked if he would provide any type of adjustment to our bill, his response was, "I'll take a look at it." Our guess is that the kitchen's supply of flounder was depleted. And even if this was the case, it should not have taken over 45 minutes to be informed that a food selection was no longer available - how is this good customer service? Again another 20 minutes to wait for his order (salmon/mashed potatoes) to be cooked, which of course delayed my food order. By this time, we'd been at the restaurant for over 2 hours. When our food was finally brought to our table by John, we were not very hungry due to filling up on coffee refills and appetizers and mostly due to the fact that our night was ruined by the experience at this establishment. He never stopped back to ask how the food was or if there was anything else he could do. We had after dinner plans that of course had to be cancelled due to this unfortunate occurrence. We thought about just leaving after the appetizers arrived, but had been impressed with prior visits to the restaurant and never imagined that something like this could occur at a high-end establishment such as the Chart House. When we received our bill, $18.50 had been deducted from the bill, listed as "Guest Relations". At the very least and to show good faith to valued customers, we feel that my date's entire meal should have been comped, not just appetizers! Needless to say, we will not be returning to Chart House, nor will we recommend family/friends to patronize this establishment. We will also inform the public of our experience via social media. We feel that communication and sensitivity training is in order for Manager, John Park, as his customer service skills are questionable and severely lacking.

Went with the 2 for 20... Got the cheese curds for the starter which were deep fried bricks. I chose  the Kentucky bourbon wings and grilled chicken breast with broccoli/rice for the entrees.. The wings were crunchy but they had barely to any sauce on them, I guess times are tough right now.. The grilled chicken was delicious with a solid portion size..  but next time I'll just get two of the grilled chicken meals..

Disappointed!!  I ordered a to go order for my family and it wasn't cheap.  First, we had a ridiculous wait time to pick up the order but, I thought it was because of staffing shortages.  The first container I opened was the Mongolian Beef.  I said a curse word out loud... it was a half-order and was dry.  The Honey Chicken looked like it was a mix of an old order and a new order that was sitting.  The breading was falling off and it was also dry.  The extra sauce that I ordered was gelatin like... never seen that before.  The Lo Mein wasn't even good.  I called and spoke with Amy (Manager).  I opted for the food to be remade because I had hungry teenagers here.   The food is being picked up and I pray it's good.   PLEASE remember that the quality of food is super important especially at your prices.  Please get it together!!  The food I received this evening gets a half-star.  But, because of Amy's professionalism and calming nature, I am giving two stars. 
I appreciate the way she handled it.

This is maybe the 4th chance I've given this place solely because it's affordable and the staff seems nice, but they are just so apathetic. Each time we call it's like the first time, there's never any record of what we've had done before and we're always starting over. When I dropped off my (reactive) on-leash dog there was a loose dog that walked right up to her face and not one of the three staff members standing there seemed alarmed or moved quickly to remedy what could have been a dangerous situation for both dogs.  I had to step in the middle and pull my dog away while a staff member took her time coming around to grab the other dog. She apologized and nothing more happened, so I felt fine to leave my dog anyway. I Just picked up my dog and realized once we were home she has blood on her foot from her quick being cut. Not sure how I didn't notice before we left, but mostly how they didn't? I can't imagine she didn't cry when it happened? Or they didn't see blood on the floor at her feet because it's all over my house. I called to let them know and got another apathetic "oh that'll happen" response, so I won't be returning. I hope they can get their act together or at least pretend to care for their other customers.
Have ordered here over 10 times and not once has it missed the mark. Best BBQ anywhere around.

Very sad to say that it is the worse Cracker Barrel we have ever been to. Starting with the waiter all the way to the manager. Took an hour for us to get our meal and when we got it, it was cold. McDonald's would have been better and quicker.

Server, Kelle was great. Cold beer and nice decor. Food bland and somewhat tasteless. Three of us shared this opinion. Shrimp and octopus in a ceviche cocktail chewy and rubbery. Enchiladas cold and tasteless. Carnitas burrito was totally bland and flavorless.

It was Valentine's Day, somewhat busy in the late afternoon. Hopefully just an off day, will try them again.

This place has a nice whiskey selection and the cocktails are good, a little on the pricier side though.  I wasn't impressed with the BBQ or any of their sauces, but the side dishes are great.  The mac and cheese with rib jam is where it's at

Modern ambiance with high ceilings and noisy.  utensils and glassware were perfectly polished.  Menu is limited.  I believe it was 4 of each course.  Service was slow.  Wine was good but expensive.  Whistlepig Rye Manhattan was perfect.  Meatball (3) appetizer were very light and airy and no Italian flavor.  Bread was served with dipping oil, no butter.  Boneless beef ribs (2) with two minature root vegetables was very good and cooked perfectly.  Filet of Beef with small portion of spinach and 3 slices of small potatoe.  Crust on meat was hard, but otherwise tender and perfectly cooked.  Apple pie was stewed apples and ice cream.  Not worth the price.  Overall, dinner was expensive for the portions.  Dress is casual.  Large bar area.  $155 for 2 (before tip).

We decided to book our stay here after reading good yelp reviews. We booked two rooms for one night. It was a bit strange because as we arrived at one of the rooms the door was just wide open. We thought that was kind of strange but walked in. Within the next few minutes a maintenance worker came in without really saying much and started working on fixing the door which we didn't know was broken because we didn't need the key to get in. After 20 minutes of him working on it and us just awkwardly trying to nap, he finally left. All seemed ok until we heard running around constantly upstairs for hours. We gave them the benefit of the doubt and just went about our plans of dinner and drinks. We were staying in two rooms on the second floor and BOTH rooms we were woken up early around 5:30AM from running upstairs. We called the front desk and asked that they ask the tenants if they could keep the noise down, in which their response was rude and not helpful: "We can't tell a family to tell their kids to not run around. Sorry." 


Awful experience. The rooms were decent, customer service lacked. Will not be staying here again. We spent hundreds to basically not sleep lol

Food as usual is great . But having ppl put on hold for 10 minutes while they can hear you helping other customers is crazy. 10 minutes?? I love the food here but annoyed by our to go order! It's not hard to get on a phone and say sorry your still on hold.

Plain mediocre! Staff is rude.  Pizza tasted like pizza.  The menu is all over the place. They have Gyro, Spaghetti,  and Edy's ice cream.. needs to be simplified so chefs can be great at a few things.

Nothing different really, from the other reviews. The food was hit or miss...I had a calamari appetizer and spinach salad. Neither was amazing nor terrible. I would stick with the more pub-like fare (meat loaf/fish and chips/burger) and be a little more pragmatic with their higher end food offerings.

We ordered flights of pretty much every beer on the menu and the selection was full of more hits than misses. The raspberry mead was particularly good as was the Blonde. Not a huge fan of stouts, porters or, really, any dark beer, I still found their Chocolate Porter impressive. Yes, definitely go for the beer and be careful with the food.

It's definitely dead on weeknights...or at least Wednesdays. And, I hear, you can still smoke at the bar!

AYOOO! Barber @ 24th (& Lombard)...where you been hiding at my whole life?! AKA my few years in Philthadelphia -- regardless, Stevie J. would like to give the utmost props to my HIGHER POWER, who was looking down from above, or for some of yous - the Lord/Jesus/God/Savior,etc. -- who is also monitoring your (recommended) bi-weekly maintenance cut.

I WAS a loyal client, or "cuttee," at a competitor's shop in the neighborhood for long time...but by golly, when I MOST needed my "guy," my go-to resource failed me and was overbooked. This was a VERY important Saturday morning -- and Stevie needed a fade that was FRESH TO DEATH in order to photograph beautifully, court some stunning bridesmaids, shake some elders' hands, kiss some babies' foreheads, and basically impress every single human I would be encountering in the coming days. That afternoon included wedding pictures and festivities for which I needed to look as close to Brad Pitt as humanly possible. Trust me when I say -- it was NOT Stevie. J'.s wedding, as I currently remain a single and mingling Irish-Itlian-Hybrid 
 STALLION -- and intend on staying this STUD for the coming years, with absolutely no intentions of slowing it down. 

All self confidence aside, in a frantic and panicked state, I scrambled the interweb trying to find a reputable Barber that could successfully recreate and implant Clarke Kent's face and hair combo onto my average looking face and over-sized dome.

Behold, BARBER ON 24th! No ifs, ans or buts about it -- go to this place! Stevie J. has been there twice -- not 1 time, but 2 TIMES -- and I sincerely recommend that any local Philadelphian do the same. Barber on 24th offers a variety of services (more than your average barber), at a reasonable price - $35 for a basic men's haircut. 

Staying open until 8pm on Weekdays is key for us hardworking and studious folk who struggle to find the time to book these manscaping appointments. On a Saturday morning, they had 5 active barbers -- a mix of men and women -- all willing and able to procure any and all cuts one would need! 

I have now had my hairs cut by both Previn and Israel -- and I was more than pleased with both! Need I say more? 

1. VIBEZ = GREAT (solid playlist covering various genres)
2. SERVICE = FANTASTIC (quick, easy, flexible, chatty and helpful)
3. PRICE = STANDARD!

~STEVIE J. $$ WE OUTCHEA
This has to be the worst place to go . First , the food is horrible and the service really need to be revamped.

The food was good, not great. I shared a Seafood Platter, the fried zucchini sticks and split the bread pudding. I also shared a crab cake. We share I our family, do everyone gets a taste. The bread pudding was the highlight, served warm with whipped cream. Homemade and very good! The service was horribly slow. It was a holiday weekend and the kitchen couldn't keep up with the demand. It was a very slow lunch.

I ABSOLUTELY love this place. They have the best BBQ I've had in a long time. Everytime I'm on Tucson, I have to stop at Brushfire. It's a small little place on a very busy road and they're usually very packed. Their fries are amazing, love their sweet tea, and they use their own house BBQ sauce. I usually either get their brisket or pulls pork.

Local favorite. Great "diner-style" breakfast. Very popular on weekends for breakfast. Famous bloody Mary is the "Jake's". Nice covered/heated patio with morning sun. Helpful and friendly waitstaff. Average price per entree $7 - $12.

On what would have been my mom's 80th birthday I celebrated in a library themed restaurant for my librarian mother. Every little detail was stunning, from the glassware to the chandeliers to the plates and on and on. All those little details added to the charm. We sat at the bar and Nathan was incredibly knowledgeable and friendly. Our food was delish, tomahawk Ribeye, spinach & potato wedges. The icing on the cake was that I found a pearl in our very yummy oysters. Yes, a pearl. Definitely made a difficult day a little easier and the pearl from above was like a hug from my mom when I needed it most.

Four stars because their coffee is the best I've tasted in Tampa. Otherwise, the breakfast is average. The potatoes may be a bit over seasoned and the service could improve. They are great at bringing you your plate, but not following up to see if you need anything else such as a refill.

You have to go. One of the few places on Bourbon we visit. Absolutely adore the courtyard, and the hurricane, of course. For as large and busy of an establishment, the service is surprisingly good. Turn in your glasses as you leave for cash back.

The service wasn't bad when you saw you waitress every 35. They were under staffed on a holiday weekend which is unacceptable to me. We ordered drinks from the cocktail waitress before getting a table and she told us to go to the bar. When we ordered drinks at our table the drinks took 15 minutes to get. Most drinks are champagne or wine based. The bar layout was weird and there was a painting of a cockroach dissection on the wall. I would just stay on bourbon in the future.
Great hotel, really enjoyed it. Trendy, friendly staff, extremely comfy rooms and very clean!

Haven't been here in years.  The kids still enjoy this place. I can do without it. They have some variation of dessert like pancakes and French toast now. I should've tried it. I just stuck with my go to order from years ago: veggie omelet and pancakes. Service was ok, nothing major.
Guacamole is good and and the crispy pork taco. Service was slow for it not being busy. Moscow Mule was pretty good too. Hopefully next time they will have more help.

I would say Sitar is a solid Indian buffet. It's all-you-can-eat chicken tikka masala, naan, samosas, and lamb vindaloo! Like everyone else has already said, flash your student ID and you'll get a 10% discount. Pay in cash and you'll get another 5% off.

We always enjoy very special fine dining at Cafe Ponte.  Our six-course tasting menu started with delicious, velvety cauliflower bisque. Next, a piquantly seasoned salad of shredded brussel sprouts; then savory cavatelli in tomato-lamb ragu.  Our selected entrees included  NY Strip steak (spouse) and Faroe Island salmon (me).  Both were lovingly prepared to perfection, creatively presented alongside exciting and unique sides.  5th course was a superb selection of cheeses plated w apple butter, honey-almonds, and cherry-crostini.  Dessert was heavenly flourless chocolate torte.  Service (by Scott) was impeccable and attentive.  Several complimentary extras were provided including an amuse-bouche of beet puree, an apricot and passionfruit palate cleanser, and the cafe's wonderful choices of breads.   We purchased a lovely Riesling to accompany our dinner.

I wanted to have a nice steak dinner with the family while on vacation. My in-laws rarely go to fancy dinners but I insisted. We had a great time and an excellent experience. We each had a different cut of steak and everyone was pleased. We ordered a few different sides for everyone to share and they were all excellent. My creme brûlée for dessert was delicious. We also had our 12 year old daughter and she loved everything served. Very happy with our dining experience.

Good vegetarian food if you don't mind waiting a REALLY long time. If there are greater than 5 customers, expect at least a 20 min wait. Kind of long for a place that only sells sandwiches and soup. And a bit pricey for what you get.

King of Donair. KOD. The much anticipated donair shop to ever hit Edmonton in decades. It is the OG donair spot in Canada hailing from Halifax where the donair was invented? Idk. Whatever. There's a lot of bragging rights this donair shop has but for it's first shop in Edmonton it has failed to hit the mark. I've been reading some reviews/blogs/ig about how "good" the donair is here and after trying their regular donair here. I'm pretty unimpressed. With slightly seasoned meat, a little sweeter sweet sauce, mediocre veggies and a cold pita. I don't know if it's worth coming back. Had a sample of garlic fingers and big whoop, just cheese on bread with sweet sauce. Nothing special. Maybe it was the months long hype on facebook or the fact that I love donairs so much but this has got to be one of the most disappointing donairs i've ever had. A part of me feels bad for the owners as it seems like they don't know or have tried Swiss, High Voltage, Homestyle, Top ....etc, list goes on. If you've tried any of these Edmonton donair shops and truly think KOD is above the rest....you are definitely wrong. Even on the shawarma side, Sunbake makes their own warm pita. You can't be serving us cold pitas and passing this off as the "King" of Donairs.

Located on Whyte ave, this place is going to be busy...for a month. I'll cut them some slack that it was the first weekend of opening but damn such a long wait. You're serving a donair with two toppings of just tomato and onions, while not advertising that you have other toppings. For a place that don't even toast the pita, speed it up. Cool shirts though.


Sal says: Just Skip It (2.5/5)  
Geoff says: meat was moist and well seasoned but donair was more like a salad wrapped in aluminum foil, not rolled up at all, sauce was a bit runny and made a mess before even opening the donair and couldn't understand what could possibly have taken them so long to make it

I had the opportunity to visit to Sonesta for a business trip.  The hotel is about to undergo a remodeling, so this review might get outdated really quick.  It is a typical hotel.  It's not fancy, but for a business traveler it worked just fine.  The room was quiet and spacious.  I enjoyed the gym, though the elliptical broke in my hand (whoops!).  There is a restaurant in the hotel, though it is an English pub.  I would have preferred something a bit more neutral.  What I liked is that it centrally located to my employer.  That worked well.

Was in the area and decided to give this place a try based on Yelp reviews. I've been in Delaware my whole life and have never been to the plaza this restaurant is located in. The parking lot looks small but ended up having plenty of available parking. Our waitress was super nice and you could tell the restaurant is very popular from first walking in. My fiancé and I both got tacos, I got chicken and he got steak. Our taco platters were delicious and I personally loved that they give you two different salsas to put on top.

I decided that this would be the first upscale restaurant on my Philly burger tour so I made a lunch reservation and came in.As it usually is with these places the service was exceptional and everyone was really nice and the place was mixed with both well dressed and casually dressed people here's the breakdown

Bun-- the bun was good and nicely toasted
Meat--very high quality but not seasonEd
Toppings--the fancy cheese and gourmet onions were adequate 

good but not outstanding

Had a wonderful experience here with a client. Great food, good drinks, excellent service.  The menu is on an iPad- and you can request your server, more drinks or order food from it- it's the way all restaurants should be.  The edamame hummus was delicious!

will never go back here again
manager not wearing a mask
prices mismarked
way more expensive than shop rite
 called ahead to see if they had something
got disconnected. clerk told me they had the item.  when i got to the store which is 30 minutes from my house advised it would take 10 minutes to prepare item.  took 30 minutes!!

i love their bottle beer selection. great prices on all the beers. great specials too. food for the most part is good.  great live bands play here including full throttle from the magazine full throttle. and if you're in the mood to play corn hole or volleyball, they got it.
Incredible staff and owners!!! A must visit shop for unique everyday wear and costumes

I absolutely love the concept and the architecture of this place!  However, I've had polar opposite experiences during my two visits to Bailey's Range.  The first go around was near perfect- friendly & timely service, great beer recommendations, and delicious food.  I had such a great meal here that I told all of my family and friends about Bailey's Range and had to bring them back with me for my next visit.  Unfortunately, the second go around was pure chaos- it took forever to get the server's attention, to order our food, and to get refills on beers.  We had to give up on dessert and go elsewhere for our milkshakes because it was just taking way too long.  The other downfall was how loud it was- I went home that night with very little voice left after having to yell across the table at my dining companions.  When we left that evening, David Bailey himself was working near the front door and was a complete wall flower- no "thanks for coming", "have a good evening". . .nothing.  What's with this guy?

In terms of food, Bailey's Range has some of the best pretzels I've ever had, and some very interesting burgers.  Unfortunately, the kitchen hasn't gotten their burger temperatures down yet, so it's a roll of the dice what color your burger will be when it lands on your table.  The beer menu is one of the best I've seen in town, focusing 99% on local micro brew offerings, with one out-of-town guest brew.  The rest of the drink menu is just as exciting with numerous craft soda offerings and homemade milkshakes. 
 
All of this aside, the menu is so enticing that I'll probably return, but never again will I go on a Friday evening.

Sales associate took the time to help me find the correct tires for my car.  He gave me all the info I needed.  Bought four Falken and couldn't be happier.  They have the BEST prices and got me in and out in an hour.

Love the salads they taste amazing but not sure they are worth all the money we pay for them!!!! he used to have a event truck and i loved them at the fair but i feel like the prices are about the same as they are when you see them at the fair!!! also he is only open tell 3 and i work pretty late so he is never open when i really need not even dinner ... the owner is pretty rude and i always have to pay an arm and leg for ranch .... they have other food witch is amazing but same thing a little pricy for what your getting... when calling to order out i have to call at least 4 times before he picks up and my orders always come to over 40$$$

They screw up every single online order you'll ever do. They literally can't read... and I'm a regular. And god forbid you use a delivery service. The food is good. Prices decent. The service for the most part, is absolute trash when it comes to receiving correct items. ALWAYS check before you leave that you've got exactly what you ordered. Or it will be wrong. Period.

I would rate this two and a half stars. Needed vitamins, so popped in here. Although staff were busy, and a bit brusque, they answered my questions. To be honest, not so sure if I made the right choice, and the staff is hit or miss, some know what they are talking about, other don't and I had to search my phone for the answers I was looking for which is why I give it the 2 1/2 star rating. Also, that Eminence skin care line is super expensive and doesn't work. Don't buy it!!!
Fries were not even half full. Burger was overcooked and dry. Chicken sandwiches were also overcooked and very dry. Not going back, I wouldn't bother.

We were on our way to Haru, but they are closed on Monday's , so we drove down Longley... and found this place.

I love chicken Parmesan, so that is what I ordered.
Hubby had the eggplant Parmesan, along with the house special salad.

It was different eating upstairs, with one server.. running around and up and down. He managed well as more people started to show up.

The atmosphere seemed to be with a lot of regulars.. so I am sure they enjoy visiting here.

My dish was a fair portion... chicken was tender and breaded nicely, but a little too much cheese on top.

I'll continue my search for the ultimate chicken Parmesan ...
Great tasting authentic Thai food but was a little more expensive than we expected. We will absolutely go back when we are in Sherwood park

Mi Casa is just an okay Mexican restaurant at best. There is not a whole lot of flavor. If I want really good Mexican food I go to Abuelo's. For awesome Tex-Mex Miguels  in Tampa, and yes it is worth the drive from Plant City. 

Me Casa has gone down hill over the years And it seems like nobody really cares inside the restaurant. Services is just OK.

If you want really good Mexican food you're going to have to drive 15 to 30 minutes.

The food from Holy Tomato Pies never leaves me disappointed. Honestly... this is the best pizza in South Jersey. The crust is always paper thin and super crispy. The sauce is bursting with flavor. My favorites are the Parma (I never get extra toppings on this because the flavor is unbelievable on its own) and the white pie with chopped clams and parsley. I also always get the Famous House Salad and Pepper Shooters as well. Trust me... you will not be disappointed with your order. Service is always friendly and with a smile. I look forward to many more orders in the future!

Been recommending this place to friends and even told my boyfriend we should go back  again for him to try himself. Solid 4 stars!  Almost directly across from Spinners restaurant out by St Pete Beach. Definitely make a reservation and especially if you have a large party. Seating is very limited for parties larger than 4. We went  on a Friday night  a little after 8 and Because  we had a  party of 5  instead of 4, (most tables seat 4), we waited over an hour. But we had gotten some wine  and  bread was able to hang out and sit in the outdoor sitting so we were able to pass the time.  We didn't opt for outdoor seating because the only available tables were not covered and it was drizzling lightly at one point on and off.  

The Sauvignon blanc i tried, (but I don't recall which one) was really good. Once we were seated, for apps we ordered the bacon wrapped scallops (3  large for 17 dollars) and truffle fries (10 dollars), and fried goat cheese also 10 dollars. Fries were so addicting especially with the siping sauce. Scallops melt in your mouth soft and fresh. Fried goat cheese... need I say anymore?

Typically I don't  order steak entree when I dine out. I'll eat  a steak but it's definitely  one of my last options to order as a protein.  Simply put I like seasoned super flavorful food and steakhouses tend to just let the meat do the talking and keep their offerings  traditional/ standard  usually the flavors are pretty mild and not too much   in the creative direction. But 1200 CHophouse flavors did impress. Of course I sampled everyone steak but opted for something else, the Chilean sea bass: lobster beuree blanc sauce, arugula, balsamic glaze, mash potatoes  (35 dollars). The sauce is a rich silky buttery  sauce. Totally impressed how fresh, flakly, moist and flavorful the fish was  and it was a pretty hefty size! My freshness test is the Fishy smell test and it passed.  Mashed potatoes was creamy delicious and just the way I love them with pieces of red skin mixed in.  

The steak was very good quality.  Only complaint  was that one of my friends who got there "au poivre"- black pepper/ peppercorn reduction was too overly black pepper tasting. The most dominant flavor and all you could taste was black pepper. Like someone had a heavy had with the black pepper. Less is more would have been ideal here. My friends got it rare plus (between rare and medium) and it was cooked beautifully. 

Our server was wonderful and checked on us frequently and even tho our meal took us past  closing, we didn't feel rushed. My friend wanted coffee like an espresso but the waitress informed us the coffee was from a keurig...Oh no! She wanted something strong so she did not get it.  Overall if you're looking for a steakhouse restaurant not over the top pricey like Berns but   much nicer  and steps above in quality to your typical chain steakhouse restaurants like Texas cattle company , Roadhouse Manny's  etc..., 1200 chophouse is a great choice.

I came here with my boyfriend and a couple of friends. The service is fabulous here. They are very attentive and friendly. 

The portions they serve are huge. So  you could probably share the steak with someone. The steak also comes with veggies and a baked potato or mashed potato. I must say that the steak isn't the best but for the price it's not bad and since the portions are huge it's well worth it. The Caesar salad is sooo good and the clam chowder is pretty delicious too. Oh and at the end for dessert they also give u chocolate covered strawberries to end your meal. Overall it was a great dining experience but I recommend sharing because their portions are huge.
Great food. Nice decor. The odd, floating head outside from the Grounds For Sculpture kinda throws you a little though.

Good experience buying a used Subaru.

They were supposed to make a repair on it before I picked it up and didn't. I live in CA so couldn't come back. Luckily I figured out how to do it myself. 

They forgot to give me the spare key and mailed it. 

Smooth process of taking car to CA to get registered and licensed.

Not bad, but in my humble opinion, not exactly NY Pizza. I'm from NYC and the pizza I had as a kid was large, greasy fold over goodness with grease running down your arm,  mozzarella so gooey and stringy and wonderfully good.

This pizza is an attempt to duplicate NY pizza and that is a hard thing to do.

my past experiences with this dim sum place have been relatively positive and i am disappointed to say that my most recent experience was very poor. the service was mediocre at best. we felt neglected and had to wait around 20 min for our orders to arrive. food quality was not as great as other dim sum places and many of the popular dishes were unavailable. waiters were also rude and loud and failed to show they cared about their customers. definitely will not be returning in a long long time, if ever.

The beer list is good, that's the one nice thing to say. 

I had dinner here with a group of friends. The waitress was clearly confused as to which kegs had kicked and which hadn't - and kept coming back to ask me what I wanted instead then to inform me that "never mind" my original order was actually still available. Okay. 

4/6 people ordered the cheeseburger- the waitress never asked how we wanted them cooked. We realized this after she walked away and assumed she would come back to rectify the mistake. Nope. All the burgers came out well done. Gross.

The reason for the low score is primarily based on the poor management and treatment I received as a guest. I booked a long weekend in Tucson from LA to golf and take in some sunny weather. I called one full week ahead of time and prior to booking my stay to request a certain room in the complex that had a balcony and great view. I was told it would be 'blocked' for me so I went ahead and booked my stay. Upon arrival, I was told my room was given away without essentially not the slightest degree of care. Further, they didn't 'block' another room anywhere near the room I requested. In fact, they did absolutely nothing. My weekend was ruined yet nothing was offered to compensate me for my loss (not even breakfast). After writing and complaining to hotel management the 'manager' finally responded simply hoping my next trip would be better with a hollow apology.  As you can imagine, I'd never stay at this property again, although its quite apparent that the manager could care less. I have contacted the SPG program management to make them aware of my ruined trip. Be advised, the SPG program is nothing like the Marriott rewards or Hilton programs which treat guests wonderfully and would've take care of this issue immediately.

Our trip to the Victoria Freehouse came courtesy of a Groupon where we could get 1 app and 2 entrees for $24.  So not too shabby right off the bat.  We went on a Friday night around 5.  Here is where it gets weird and slightly annoying.  As we walk in we are greeted by not the restaurant staff, but by a table with people dressed in European period clothing talking in awful British accents.  They loudly and obnoxiously greeted everyone who came in the same way.  Now I'm not quite sure whether these people come here for kicks to do this? Is it some sort of club thing they do? To me it was bizarre.  I don't mind it at all, but when there are is practically no one in the restaurant, it's quite intimidating and off-putting to be semi-harassed by a bunch of drunk not-funny dorks as you walk in.  The restaurant staff apologized for their behavior, but why not do anything about it?

Parking along Front St. is fairly easy and we parked not even a block away on a meter for approximately 1hr 20 min, thinking this would be enough time to eat and get back to the car.  Think again.  The waitress, while very nice, was not very attentive or very good at all.  She took our drink orders right away and the bartender made them fairly quickly.  The drinks then waited on the bar for 10 minutes in an empty restaurant.  The bartender proceeded to be annoyed by this and delivered the drinks herself.  We were in the restaurant for about 20 minutes before she eventually asked us if we wanted food.  Bad form.

We ordered the Tikki pizza, which was good and comes in 4 slices.  Nothing mind blowing, but it was good pub fare.  My girlfriend and I split large fish and chips and the roast beef sandwich.  The roast beef sandwich was our favorite as it had excellent flavor and the meat wasn't dry (which happens to a lot of roast beef sandwiches).  This came with chips, but never said so on the menu.  The large fish and chips was outrageously big.  Next time we would definitely get the small.  The fish was good, but not great.  It was missing some flavor, but I'm not sure what.  The chips however were excellent and would come back here and snack on those any night.

Somehow this all took an 1 hour and 20 minutes and had me running to the car as the girlfriend paid for the bill.  This is all in an empty restaurant.  All in all, the service here leaves a lot to be desired.  The owners should also do something about the annoying British acting troupe to stop scaring away customers.  I'd give this 3 stars normally, but the service aspect and experience just put me off.  I'll likely try again, but probably not for awhile.

rented from the Baltimore / Washington airport. Prices are good but the car i rented was unsafe. The change oil light came on 10 minutes after we drove away, 7% oil life left. No window scrapers were in the vehicle and we were stuck in a snow storm In Delaware. 
the wiper fluid was completely empty which made it even harder to see on the bad roads. I kept having to stop off just to throw snow on the windows to help clean them. 
when we first got in the car, is reeked bad of marijuana and the smell lasted for 3 days. I feel like this particular location does not care about the safety of their customer.

The pizza is great and enormous! The beer is tall and cold! I love the patio and that you can sit outside because of COVID. Unfortunately the service is meh. It not bad but it doesn't wow you. The biggest issue is that most of the staff were not wearing their masks correctly. This was an issue for me as you have to go inside to order and then a staff member (with their mask not covering their nose)  brings out food to you....

This place is a hoot. Like any owl, it's all about the wings, and like any owl, your eyes will pop out. Because like any sexist establishment, Hooters only hires ridiculously good looking women, usually with massive natural breasts or preferably fake ones. They all wear low cut tops to show off said assets, and tiny little spandex booty shorts that make them basically look like Playboy Bunnies mark II, or strippers, both of which look fantastic and are awesome, but are also completely derogatory towards women and set us back about half a century with regards to equal rights.

I came here against my will. It's true. My last will and testament explicitly states: "Do not drag my corpse to Hooters." Now you know. The people that brought me here (Jacob and his friend that does not want to be named), who live in TN, will give it a 6 out of 5 every time for the drinks, the wings, and the girls. One of them also claims they have the best burgers in America. I could not disagree more - the burgers are boring and bland. The wings are good, not great. The girls are spectacular... Jacob was too busy staring at the meat. On the burger, I mean. Typical Jacob though. I think he had the best case of beer goggles (for burgers) in America. 

My opinion of the food was very different from my friends. The wings, I liked. I've been to maybe 10 Hooters in my life and the wings are always pretty good, and don't taste microwaved. However, I got to try a bite of the burger, and it was horribly overcooked. The only saving grace of my visit here other than the nice wings, was our server. She was so lovely and adorable. But the Hooters girls almost always are. However, the bill was insane. And I think generally you tip Hooters waitresses twice as much as you'd usually tip, because they're wearing all that stuff (or that lack of stuff) for you, so it ends up costing way more than you'd expect. Drinks aren't cheap either.

So, given that it is a corporate chain, it has to score 1, 2 or 3. Given that it has amazing beautiful women who are so nice, it scores 3. But given that it is so sexist and slightly overpriced, Hooters is back down to a 2.

I was friends with several Hooters girls in college. I found out that working there is sort of like being a Cheerleader. You can consider it along similar lines, because the employees do. I have to say this particular Hooters has some really amazing women. And like every other Hooters it has good wings, so it's worth a visit. Except that it is an evil chain that is taking over food and putting all the families out of business.

Which means it scores a lowly 2 and you should steer well clear. But if any of the girls from this location are interested, I'm not doing anyone, I mean, thing, next Tuesday night.

I came to this theatre for the first time today to view a dance act that's part of Indy Fringe festival. It's not meant to be grand and ornate, as cities don't usually make relics out of small, intimate venues like this one. But I've been in small theatres that make a little more elegant use of their space than this one.

Small, maroon four-seater tables and chairs are the theatre's hotel convention room-inspired accommodations. A dark-colored, English-looking painting hangs on the left wall just outside the theatre, furthering the oldish theme. Thank goodness for a beautiful stained glass window that conveys the antique feel in a more enlightened manner.

This wouldn't be my first intimate space theatre choice, but it gets the job done.

Cool place for healthy food. Eat Clean has many choices for all tastes. It has many options for vegans. Açaí bowls are popular items. They make everything with fresh fruits and vegetables. 

Has a large menu of juices and smoothies, which seem to be the main thing here. It also has bowls if you're looking for a meal. The lunch menu includes salads and sandwiches. Get a bowl at breakfast. They have a few choices of these. I like the Açaí Smoothie Bowl. It's about $9.25 for a good amount and there's many ways to customize it. This comes with a choice of fruit and seeds. 

It makes a good breakfast. Some time I have to go here for lunch. The rest of the menu makes me want to find out what else is good there.

Love it. Absolutely the best in town. Wonderful combination of textures and tastes. Fresh ingredients, slight crispiness to the meat, soft pita bread, tang of yogurt sauce, hot sauce if you want.  The side of hummus and pita wedges with two incredible olives on top (my favorite) is almost a meal itself. Other side choices include salad or tzaziki (sp?).
Don't get the oatmeal.   Its sugary milk w little oatmeal.  Damn should of checked it before I left with it to go.   Ugh!!!!

The ambiance of this place is classy and modern. My husband and I really enjoyed the calamari appetizer although we both agreed it was a bit greasy. The Caesar salad was well dressed but I was unaware it would have red pepper flakes in it (I might of missed this in the menu description when I ordered it) but it kind of overpowered the traditional flavors I was looking for. The Parmesan crisp on top was delicious. My filet was charred to perfection but not the medium I ordered. I didn't even really notice or care much because it was delicious but our waiter and a manager came over and asked if it was cooked to my liking. I said it was OK but they immediately took it and cooked it for a few more minutes and brought it back out looking fantastic. They finished out our meal with a piece of chocolate cake for our anniversary. Would I come back here? Absolutely. Is it the best steakhouse meal I've had in Indianapolis? Probably not on this trip. Maybe next time it will be!

Took an extra month to get my order in. Every time I called they referred me to some sales person who didn't return calls. When I went to pick it up, they couldn't find it for some time, the. They brought me a box that had been opened!After some research, I found the same exact lighting elsewhere for hundreds less and they had it in stock. I was ripped off.

This is the original location. My friend said we could not head to the beach until we ate here. It's close to Veteran's, so why not? OMG! Do yourself a favor and come hungry. The ribeye tacos are fantastic and come with equally wonderful sauces. Someone in our group went back to order more! This place is busy, so place your order and sit inside or out. If you can stand the heat, sit outside, it's tiny, but be assured they're cranking out some serious eats! Can't wait to come back!

Since my son is due for a haircut...actually overdue, we are at Extravagance. It is a great barber shop.  The staff is always friendly and often funny.  You can wait for a certain barber or take whichever barber frees up first.  They have a large waiting area and a TV to watch while you wait.  Our barber of choice is Sam.  He does an awesome job on my boys hair!
