Pho is awesome! More people should come here! Awesome service and the food is always wonderful.

Disappointing. Really...

I've driven by this multiple times as it was being built and have wanted to try it ever since. Well, I finally got to and unfortunately was only a subpar experience. It was about 5:30pm on a Wednesday so I was really surprised to see the amount of people seated with their treats. There were a few patrons standing in line either waiting for their order or browsing the menu and only one other car in the drive thru by the time I entered the queue. Now, I should tell you that before I go to new places I tend to study the menu ahead of time so I know exactly what I want without the pressure of someone in the drive thru telling me to take my time and then 30 seconds later prompting me to order OR my boyfriend  and his lack of patience. But I digress. I knew exactly what I wanted.... A banana split for myself, brownie boat for the man, and on a whim a hot dog with mustard because I have not eaten very much at work. 

As soon as I make my way to the window the young lady was wrapping up an order from the car behind me told me the total and took my card. Then she had my card for what felt like 5 whole minutes before she told me that she's accidentally hit cash rather than credit. A little hiccup, fine, just please inform me ASAP. I don't know about you, but I get involuntarily anxious when someone holds my cards out of view for well over the anticipated transaction time. Especially, if I'm in a drive thru. 
Anyways, finally everything was fixed and I got my card back and checked the receipt to make sure everything was correct. A few seconds later I received the banana split in a styrofoam hotdog type container and I saw that it was leaking a bit, perhaps from being overfilled? I hoped so! Then a young gentleman mistakenly brought over a strawberry come with sprinkles. Not mine sir, sorry. Then the brownie boat came out. Unlike the banana split it came out opened with a spoon (which are normally individually wrapped) already in it. You can imagine my wince as I was the only one in the car and I have tan fabric seats. Luckily, and don't ask why, I had a poncho in my back seat so I grabbed it and put the two desserts on top of it in my front seat.  The woman said thank you for coming and I told her I still had a hot dog. She said "oh" and that they were waiting for the machine to warm up.. I'll remind you it's 530p on a Wednesday. Then she proceeded to ask if I could wait in front and I politely said sure. Big mistake. There's no front to wait in. I went and parked by Panda Express (entering in the wrong way) but figured she wouldn't see my car amongst all the panda patrons and then pulled up literally right in front of Twistee Treat. Blocking traffic if there were any. A couple minutes later the same woman who handed over my boyfriends uncovered brownie boat with the spoon stuck in it found me and hurdled over the mulch filled divider, clearly not meant to be hurdled over and handed me the good through my passenger window. I said thank you as she made her way back over. Of course I was hungry so before I pulled away I grabbed the hot dog and took a bite. Cold. Luke warm at BEST. About at warm as putting a cold hot dog in the microwave for 10 seconds. Literally. I was hungry so I ate it in my way home. Now, I only live roughly 3 minutes from this place. When I got home and grabbed the desserts the banana split had oozed out all over my (thankfully) poncho covered seat.  I grabbed the banana split to look at the damage and it was like releasing the flood gates. Melted ice cream EVERYWHERE. The whipped cream was still intact. So I placed it on the roof of my white car tried to cover it back up only to have more ice cream ooze out. What a mess. I grabbed the brownie boat and banana split the best I could and made it up the stairs to our home. I had to kick at the door for Danny(my bf) to opened it and help me as ice cream dropped down my hands. I should also mention that the brownie  boat wasn't nearly as filled as 
My banana split but it was equally as melty.  After  we made it safely in with cream covered hands I opened the containers and only sad remnants of beautiful ice cream remained. I took a photo (banana split only since Danny had already dug into his) which I'll post as well. 

Taste wise it was good, nothing spectacular, but creamy and not grainy like you might get from other places. The chocolate ice cream was speckled with bits of chocolate? But the texture was just as creamy.  I'm not sure we'll go back. Maybe after awhile when they've had time to run more smoothly. I love food and this was a sad experience for me.

It's been several years since I last ate here. My previous experience wasn't great- the food was less than mediocre and the beverages were not good at all. 

I went last week with reservations given my last experience, but ended up having a pretty good meal! My husband had the fried chicken sandwich and it was very tasty. I had the alligator sausage sandwich and it was also good. 

This, by far, isn't my favorite restaurant downtown, but a decent place for a fairly reasonable meal.

Easy place to get gas off of I 74 but a bit pricey because it's right off the interstate. Very clean station and friendly people working here. And they usually have Kazooles my favorite Willy Wonka candy. Closest place from my house to get beer in a pinch.

Went to the hostess stand was greeted by a very nice young lady.  We let her know that it would be two for lunch.  As she began to seat us we asked if we could have a table instead of a booth and I think she was a bit confused but was able to seat us at a table.  We received water right away and I asked the man who gave us the water if we could walk around and look at everything...he said your paying to be here aren't you.  oh my, I guess he has a point...  later we were told about the hall to see behind the aquarium--which by the way is VERY COOL.  We ordered the crab bisque--ABSOLUTELY FANTASTIC.  But had to wait a while before we got someone's attention to bring a spoon.  Ordered Margarita no salt...got salt (the server took it off our bill--Thank you).  Our lunch was very tasty... I had the burger and my hubs had the Fried chix and bacon chop salad...very good but had to wait a while to get someone's attention again for a salt and pepper shaker.  Over all the food is GREAT!  The atmosphere is AWESOME!  The service was eeeh.  I will be back though because the food was just that good!  :)

Most Indian restaurants don't serve fresh ingredients.  Tiffin is the only one that comes close. I order it all the time.  The guy who picks up the phone doesn't speak English all that well. Ordering is pain in the ass. He asks for your info over and over.   They don't pay attention to loyal customers. Bit oblivious about making special requests like ... "I love the complimentary soup, can I order that as main dish"... The guy will simply say .. No. We don't have it... Wake up! Here is your chance to add items to menu that taste great.  PS.  This is the only Indian place where tandoori chicken is da bomb.  And yes I'm Indian.
Grabbed lunch and a few drinks there today. Our server was Riley and she was so nice and very attentive. Definitely will stop by again before I leave town! Nice ambiance and daily specials.

French onion soup rings and lobster mac were both amazing. Our waitress was Shawna and she did a great job at answering all our questions and was very friendly. May go back for brunch tomorrow, awesome place!
Food was good. Restaurant was clean. There wasn't enough staff to be able to fill the needs of all the customers. Otherwise, a pleasant experience.

Would never come back to these guys.

I had hear about The Mechanic through a friend at work being somewhat new to Nashville. My fiance's AC had stopped working and it's been getting hot out. I called and asked if they would be able to take care of it if I brought in the next morning at 7am. The woman on the phone told me that that worked and to bring it in. I had even offered to bring it in later in the week but she said that the next day worked.

So I brought it in the next day. I woke up early so that I could bring my fiance's car in while we worked and i could take her to work. After not hearing anything and it being close to 4pm I called and they said that they still hadn't figured it out yet and they would call us to let us know. Well closing time came for them and no one called us back. No one answered our calls. The next day they said the same thing and no one called us. The woman there told us she had tried to call my fiancé yet she had no missed calls or messages and they still didn't know. Finally after not getting back to us and closing time come and gone, the next day the woman called us later in the day to tell us it would be a 1,300 dollar fix for an A/C belt. We picked up the car so that we could figure out if we could make it work. My fiance decided it was worth it and had them do the work. Same thing. They said they'd have it done that day and of course no one called and the car sat there overnight once again. After calling them a few times the next day they called to let us know it would be done at 4:30. We got there at 4:30 and sat there for awhile while they finished it. We saw them take it for a test drive and the guy parked it out front. We asked him if it was blowing cold air in which he nodded his head seeming annoyed and walked inside. We paid them and left. While riding home we noticed that the air was not cold. We were hoping that maybe it needed some time and brushed it off. The next day when it got hot and my fiancé was on the way home she told me that it was still blowing hot air! A week and 1,300 dollars later. We called and they said to bring it in and that they would take care of it. I brought it in when we both had time off and made sure it worked for them as we could not have the car overnight again. I checked in periodically over the day with the woman sounding more annoyed each call with closing time rapidly approaching. Finally they said they had figured it out and would have it done although it would cost more. Then they called back saying the had not fixed it and if I didnt want it their overnight and through Sunday with them not being open then i should come pick it up. I went and picked it up after closing hours and they called periodically to check and see where I was as it was closing time. I had to drop what I was doing at the gym and take an uber over so it took a little while as my fiancé was at work. I dropped it off a few days later. We didnt have to pay anything and they finally fixed it but I can thoroughly say that it was the worst experience I've had with a mechanic. It took 1,300, a few carless days, and a week and a half total to take care of. Sorry for the long post.

Can't say enough great things about iCreate Cafe.  My wife and I love going there for lunch and have never been disappointed with anything on their menu. The hummus and babaganoush are a staple favorite.  The atmosphere is cozy and very comfortable. Price is very reasonable for the taste and quality.

I got accused of smoking by the house maid. I don't even smoke cigarettes and she said my room smells like smoke, she argued with me and got in my face and said she was going to tell on me. I immediately called the front desk and asked for management. The house keeping management came in and said there was a slight smell of smoke, mind you there are no windows in my room, the curtains are smoked stained along with the ceiling, the room hadn't been renovated in at least 15-20 years. There is a thick film of old dust n whatever else. The room hadn't been wiped in a while, there were other guest socks under the tv n some other nasty things, the pull out bed is broken and had wires sticking out. The room smells like it's had too many years of use and not enough attention. There is a musty smell maybe of stale smoke as if someone used to smoke in there for years, but I just thought that's how it smelled, the entire hallway smells the same. My bed linens and towels still smelled fresh. Above all, the maid ACCUSED me of smoking!!!! That was humiliating and a defamation of my character, never in my life have I had trouble like this in any of my travels. I heard the maid receives 20% of $200 from the smoking fee, con artist work here!!! I booked for a lil over a week, I can't trust anyone here.

YAY!! Sierra, Darin and everyone at Jump on the School Bus are awesome. We are really happy we chose them for our wedding. They were SUPER easy to work with. We made changes literally up to the day of and they handled everything beautifully. The bus was very cute with custom lettering and colorful lights to get the party started. Our driver Brian was friendly and chatted it up with us and our guests. Super accommodating and professional but down to earth. Very much worth the cost to support such a well run, local company.

Old-fashioned hamburgers without the old fashion service.

First time going through this small town and loved the look of Ambler. It was around lunchtime and I was hungry so I figured I would stop at one of the small establishments and support them. I passed this burger joint but then flipped around and thought I would give it a try. What a big mistake to make my first memory in ambler. The food was decent but the service was horrible. When I came in there were tables and a young girl sitting by the cash register just starring and typing on her phone. She just sat there playing with her phone. I finally asked if I should sit down and order or just order from here. She said I could order here. After looking at their menu on the wall and see the specialty burgers I asked her which one are they famous for. She just looked at the menu and said we have these burgers. She seemed like she was really put out and I was distracting her from her phone. I finally ordered the Kevin Bacon but without cheese, fries, and a milkshake. 

It came out but without any Bacon on it. I had to go up to her and interrupt her again from her phone and explain that the burger had no bacon on it. She gave be a blank stare like as if she was saying what do you want me to do about it. I had to tell her again that I ordered the Kevin bacon burger and there was no bacon on it. She told me that she put it into the computer correctly and to not have cheese. She went and told the cook and told be that they will bring me out a side of bacon. They did bring out the bacon and I had to put it on myself. 

They did give me a generous portion of french fries but the Ketchup bottle on my table was completely empty. I looked around at the other tables and there was only one other table with a bottle of Ketchup that was almost as empty but went and grabbed it. I did not want to try and ask for more ketchup and distract the girl on her phone.

The burger, fries, and milkshake were decent but the service as horrible. I really wish I skipped this place and try out one of the other small eating establishments in the area. I also regret the two dollar tip I gave before I got my food and the lack of service.
100% what i hoped it would be for my firat philly cheese steak experience. a must try! dont forget the chili cheese fries! game changer!
Recommended by a local. First half of the meal was good, until I saw a mouse run across the floor behind the counter...

i am Not sure what these people are talking about.  We just been there for my daughter's birthday, and beside us, there's only 2 other party there, and they all done already when we sit down.  the service is slow, and the price is fair, but the hibachi chef is not good at all.  he kind of burn the egg, and make the fried rice taste bitter and salty.  we have 6 people, so we order chicken, shrimp, lobster, Chilean sea bass, steak, and fillet magnon.  The only thing that come out good is the steak and fillet.  The lobster has no tasty, and undercook, and fish has no tasty, and chicken is little overcook.  the shrimp is okay.  As for show, h

Of course when I walked into this crazy shop I couldn't help but hum... 
What baby? baby with the power 
What power? power of VOODOO 
Who do? you do 
Do what? remind me of the baby 
Dance Magic, Dance 

This store is all about the Voodoo, you almost get a chill when you walk in. Anything you what or need they have, spells, charms, dolls, masks, I think I even saw a chickens foot. Yes it's full of tourists but they actually keep some authentic mdse. Then even do palm reading and tarot cards, that is IF you can handle it. Freaky as hell but a must go.

I have been to Bertolini's twice. The place is just okay to me. It is good though. I expected better though. Twice I got the mediterrian salad, and enjoyed it! One time I had a virgin strawberry daqiuri, and somehow I had a fly in my drink. I'm usually don't make a big deal over anything, so I just let it go, and left the drink there. I did end up going again a second time. However, besides the salad, I enjoyed the soup very much. It was a special soup, a creamy clam chowder. The desserts there are good there as well.

My son and I decided to go to 4th Avenue on our day off. We had never been in Sky Bar before and thought it would be nice to have a beer and sit and chat outside. It was a beautiful day. However, the bartender profiled my son. It was more than obvious what he was doing.  My son has facial tattoos because we are Niitsitapi (Blackfoot) and that tattoo is the letters of our tribe (Siksika) written in our language.  The man was fixated on my sons tattoo and I thought he wasn't going to serve us. I asked him if he had a problem and he responded, "no, no problem" and looked at his card again and handed it to my son. I started to hand him my card and said, "No, I won't give you my business" and told him what he could do with his beer and walked out. Yes, my son was embarrassed but I was SO upset because we have spent a LOT of time on 4th, especially my son, and have never had any issues with anyone there. As a matter of fact, my son is known by many of the store owners there because he used to live close to 4th and walk there often. My son works 50-60 hours a week, has been in management for several years, has a college education, a nice car, etc. But, you know what? It doesn't matter, no one should be treated like that, ever. I will not be back.

Finally found a hotel that has great service, good location, great breakfast and very clean!  We found it to be quiet even though located right off the road.  There is a Starbucks and CVS walking distance.  Located near many restaurants and La Encantada Mall with upscale shops.
Every employee greeted us with a smile and the breakfast staff went above and beyond to make sure everyone was pleased.  Made to order omelets and eggs, bacon and sausage, pancakes, cereal, fruit, etc.  You won't go hungry here.

When we entered we were offered a table by the door and we declined because it was so cold out and the door didn't close all of the way.  Well it didn't matter because it was just as cold everywhere in the restaurant.  I hate eating with my coat on.

I ordered a large hot chocolate and my husband got coffee.  My husband's coffee was really good!  We highly recommend the coffee.  The hot chocolate looked awesome but after you got past everything on top it was a let down.

I ordered the south of the border omelet and my husband got pancakes, bacon, potatoes, and an egg on the side.  My omelet was edible.  I love omelets so my expectations are probably really high, but they just put green peppers and sausage in the omelet and then two slices of american cheese on top.  My husband's pancakes were big and ok, but he received hardly any syrup so ask for extra syrup or you'll be eating dry pancakes.  

Nothing was too bad and I probably sound like a complainer but I've been to truly great breakfast places and I hold all restaurants to that standard.  If this was just a local diner that was not trying so hard at being trendy and different, than my thoughts would be different.  If your in the area, drive up to Rooster instead.

Met up here for drinks with one of my friends a week or so ago and decided its the perfect place in my new hood to have drinks. The cocktail list offers something for everyone and there are a few adventurous options that combine a few lethal potions together.The reason for 3 stars is the price point Most cocktails ive had on this side of broad have been in the 9.00 /10.00 range ( with the exception of supper whos cocktails are crafted by FM) for the size of the served up cocktails i cant agree with the 12-13 price tag. The wine list is ok and i did have a really good white blend from france but again the size portions just didnt add up to the cost. 
The food options seemed intriguing and i would probably come back to try out the menu options in the future.

They do not enforce guests wearing masks inside in common areas despite their being signs saying it's required. The first two employees I saw were wearing masks, but as I was checking out I saw a housekeeper without a mask. 

If you're looking for a safe stay, this probably isn't it. 

Also, the decor is ugly and outdated (but personally this was a non-issue for me).

Loved it! Everything tasted fresh and homemade. They came out piping hot and even still tasted good a few hours later. I can't even say that any one flavor was my favorite because I liked all the ones I tried. I will definitely be returning!

While we were roaming the streets of NOLA, we saw the happy hour sign at Evangeline and decide to go in here and grab some small bites and drinks! 

The place itself is very tastefully decorated but can't say the same about their food and drinks! They did not have a very interesting happy hour menu so I went with a safe mule and it was OK! I was really excited that they had fried okra, but when it got to my table all the excitement dropped down as it looked very unappetizing and tasted pretty bland and chewy! My friend got the fried alligator and she said she'd definitely had better alligator at a much cheaper price! 

This place is OK for a quick drink, but nothing more!

I saw a fun ad for this take-out place on YouTube and saw the good reviews here, so I decided to check it out. I tried two rolls and the mahi mahi hibachi special. First off, the sushi was just not good. The "crab" was imitation crab stick, which was not disclosed on the menu. That lack of candor never a good sign and often forebodes other corner-cutting measures. The fish tasted frozen. The cooked eel was all right. Perhaps the deep-fried sushi makes their ingredients taste better, but I didn't sample any of those. As for the hibachi, the vegetables tasted bland and frozen, which is a shame given the bountiful veggie harvests all throughout Nashville right now. I did not like the syrupy soy type sauce everything was swimming in, and the fried rice tasted old and gummy. The mahi mahi was properly cooked, I'll give them that. My experience was definitely a letdown, and for the price paid ($24), it was not a good value.

I came here during the Eagles parade to dine with my friends. Upon arrival, the server had the worst attitude. She didn't greet us or anything and her negative energy was annoying me very much.

Anyways my friends and I ordered our food. The server didn't bother checking on us or anything. About 15 minutes after we got our food, she dropped a check when we didn't ask for one. We ended up ordering more food, which I guess annoyed her. 

The food was okay. I guess this place is just convenient if you need a quick cheap take out when you're running late but definitely not a brunch place to sit and dine with family. 2 stars for the food.

This is my first time eating here and was recommended by friends. The crab with house made cilantro and basil noodles was delicious! My wife said the toasted coconut creme brûlée was the best she has ever had.
The pizza is amazing.  Cannot find NY style pizza in the Lou often.  A true east coast girl and this feels like home. Lynn the bartender is fantastic, warm and friendly.  Would definitely recommend.

One Night Stand is every country guy and gal's bar. Complete with a mechanical bar, if you drink enough you may just feel like you're back in Texas. They obviously play all the country hits, but if you like r&b, you'll hear that here as well. This bar is always a hot spot on the weekend. Get there early if you plan on sitting down, because there's only a few booths available. The floor is usually sticky from spilled beer, so don't wear your nicest shoes. I'd say it's a pretty basic bar with just that additional country touch to it, making it a tad more fun than the other hot spots on Central Ave. You'll still have your typical college kids there who don't care to say excuse me when walking through the crowd, because they could care less about you, unless you're buying them drinks. But, hey, the drink prices aren't bad, so that's another bonus.

They seriously are the worst, and feel like scammers.  I don't know how they even got my package.  They said it was delivered, but it was not.  I had it delivered to my office, and they said they got a signature, but it was a Saturday, when no staff is on premises.  I emailed them and they told me to contact Amazon.  IT IS NOT EVEN FROM AMAZON.  I called them out for being scammers, but have yet to hear back.  Now I have lost a very sentimental Christmas gift, that I have already waited weeks for.  The reviews blow my mind; how is this company still in business?  If I had a choice, I would NEVER use them.  Ugh, beware people!  

Honestly wish I could give them negative stars :(

I LOVE this place. the food is a bit on the pricey side compared to the "neighborhood" Mexican food I usually get and most of it tastes the same but some things are definitely superior to my hood staples.
Their grilled burritos are to die for... As is the nacho supreme! 
just about all of their listed menu prices everywhere I've seen them are wrong so your order will be more than you expect.... So be prepared for that if the order taker doesn't give you your price on the phone ;)
Pretty tasty. They seem expensive. Hopefully soon they will have some plant-based or vegan options. If you're vegan, expect no cookies (so far).

I love supporting small business so i was excited to try this place.  I ordered a half Buffalo Nickel .  From the little bit of chicken she out in my sandwich I bet if it was made wuu it the better proportions it would of been yummy but since I didn't have much chicken I was left to eat the panini bread.
Excellent! I LOVE the food, appetizers, and service! We order two platters and it fed six people with leftovers to send home. Definitely will return again soon!!

Pretty bummed after eating here. I was drawn in by their heirloom caprese salad special, which they were out of. After reading about their special pizza oven that cooks a pizza in 90 seconds at 900 degrees I was still optimistic. It was 10:30 at night and there was hardly anyone in there- so much so that I asked the staff if it was too late to order food, their sign said they close at 11.  They said no problem, but after my pals ordered hot wings, the waitress returns and tells us some crazy line about how the kitchen closes at 10, but we could still get pizza. I'm still confused about the details of this- so we settled for 1 pizza and 1 calzone. The worst part was that order took a half an hour. The pizza was both unimpressive and cold.  Why would you advertise a 90 second pizza oven that "takes years to master" only to deliver something slightly better than Tombstone that takes 3 times longer to produce? From the top to the bottom, I recommend you take everything everyone says and/or has printed on the menu with a grain of salt.

I'm 50/50 on this due to an advisor rather than the whole dealership.  I've been a Honda customer for almost 8 years now & I don't expect the red carpet service but good service.  There is a difference in consistency with service but no one is perfect. I've had issues but no matter what there has been one advisor who has gone over & beyond. Kalef Torres has been amazing. I use to take my car to Wesley Chapel location and I live in Riverview because that's where he was.  Never tried to throw in service I didn't need but rather the facts.  I appreciate that more than anything because his honestly earns my loyalty.  There's also a young lady with a tattoo sleeve who use to be an advisor but I think has been promoted.  Both of there service keeps me coming back to Brandon Honda.  Don't get me wrong I've had moments where frustration has gotten the best of my chartered with some service but with these two great people there I'll continue brining my car for service. 

Management please don't take this wrong but I hope you value and properly compensate employees like the two I mentioned. Customer service & the golden rule of treating people how we all want to be treated is rare to find these days!

I have nothing but wonderful things to say about this store. The selection is awesome and it's not picked over. Karen and the other ladies were helpful and friendly. All of the ladies working today made me feel welcomed as I am new to the area from VA. I will definitely be going back.

We had a solid dinner tonight.  A few fried seafood dishes.  A couple pasta dinners.  I had oysters and hush puppies for an appetizer.  Surface was fast, waitress was at tentative, and food was above average. I think maybe a little over priced, but good food, and I'll take that any day.

Look I get it... It's not easy righting a mistake when the food needs to be delivered. All the more reason not to mess up so big in the first place. 

I ordered online just after 5pm and requested a 6:15 delivery as their site allows. I wanted to delay it a little so it didn't sit here before my husband got home. Now he was all excited for their veggie burger because, despite my star rating, they have a really good veggie burger. I, on the other hand, wanted their Mediterranean salad and their hummus; normally a really solid dinner. Well 6:15 passes... 6:20... 6:30... I called around 6:35 and was told that I ordered too late and they were slammed and I needed to be patient but the driver was on his way. That was the tip of what is now our last experience with this place.

The food arrives and my salad is warm, my hummus is no longer crisp and cool and even worse, my husband's veggie burger is an angus burger.

Now I'm annoyed because we are at an hour and a half into waiting for this food to come. I called and the same girl on the phone just "okay"s me to death until I asked for a manager. No manager is around so she puts some other girl on the phone who "sorry"s me to death and "what do you want?"s and then says she can give me a credit for next time. I figured I would try to right their wrong for them with asking how long it would take to get the right burger (not bothering to mention MY food). She tells me the chef will start on it right away and it won't be long because they aren't busy. Thanks for the lie girl #1!

40 minutes later I call and my favorite girl answers the phone. "He's leaving now" and a hangup. 17 minutes later (mind you we are lazy and know it's literally a 5 minute drive up the street) I'm waiting on hold for the pretend manager as the poor driver pulls up knowing we're mad. As my husband assures him to not apologize because this isn't his fault, the "manager" tells me that they only have one driver and she had to wait for him to get back not bothering to acknowledge my original inquiry about how long this would take. Then she offers a credit for next time. Next time?! No thanks and please refund my card. Bu-bye delicious veggie burger! You're not worth the hassle...

Freaking AWESOME!  We are 58 and 59 years old and played like kids!  There were all kinds of corners and crannies and secret places, some only maneuverable by very small children.  We went over/under/through and found several slides that ended you-didn't-know-where.   One slide was 10 stories down, spiral and in the dark (I probably won't go down that one again).  

They sell cheap knee pads that made crawling easy but you don't have to crawl,  you can sit comfortably nearly anywhere.   There are several places where you can buy refreshments and even a beer.   Lots of old school pinball so bring quarters if that's your thing (I wish I had brought some).   Also, if walking in dark tunnels bother you, bring a small flashlight.

In addition to fun exploration, there are artifacts to peruse, fabulous architectural art,  and amazing uses of industrial equipment.   My hubby kept pointing out what the stairs were made of,  what that machine over there used to be used for in the manufacturing world, and all kinds of stuff that I couldn't have even guessed about before they were commandeered for this amazing museum.

Don't think about it,  just go!

It was all pretty sad.  Tried the Prime Rib special.  Looked NOTHING like the picture.  The rice side was nearly inedible, almost like they failed to cook it.  The broccoli was a bunch of cold stalks.  The starter (loaded potato skins ) was delivered to the wrong table.  They sat there until we were served dinner then our waitress brought them to our table.  Our waitress tried her best, bless her heart, but it was an uphill battle for her. Shame since it used to be a nice place to eat.

Visited New Orleans for a wedding and came here for hair styling. It was a very humid and windy day and not only did my hair look great but also stayed intact throughout the evening (and even the second line!) Lashawn was my hair dresser and did a fantastic job! Highly recommend her!

Was an showdown at Jim N Nick(JNN).   With dinosaur BBQ fully processed in our tummy from last week, and taste still fresh in memory, we thought we be judge of TN vs NY BBQ.  JNN had Good BBQ,  meat was falling of the bone good.  Now.. Does rise above Dino's?  Some of you might cry foul bias.. But I had a dude from Atlanta with me.. And we both agreed... Dino's take the trophy.

Beef brisket, original pulled pork, ribs, collard greens, and potatoes salad and wings 

Without too many words, I think we agreed it must be the sauce.  During out discussion, An observation I made settled the decision... At JNN, I was constantly adding sauce on my plate.   At Dino I didn't need to, and finish the plate.

Meat characteristics.. Both falling of the bone good.  I found JNN bit dry on the ribs.  Pull pork was good, beef brisket was excellent.

Great food, great waitress... And because it was out first time, they were nice enough to offer one free dessert.

Ashys burger is not worth your time. I was short on time during a lunch break and thought I would give it a shot.
 I ordered a bacon cheese burger and fries. Not only was I unhappy with paying $ 10.50, but I also got burnt bacon on my burger. The fries had no salt on them and the burger had no flavor.  
I suggest driving an extra 10 min down the street to five guys if your in the area and crave a burger.

Stop here to have lunch. Servers and staff ver nice however the menu is no NON spicy friendly. I am highly sensitive to spicy food. If I would of know that even the getting with our sauce all the food has a big kick including the mild. After receiving the check we noticed a charge for sour cream. This is a fine Mexican restaurant who charges a sour cream condiment that goes with the tradition of the food. Not right. However as I said staff is very friendly but menu is very limited and they charge for condiments with a burrito. Needless to say not my favorite of flavors when it comes to so called fine Mexican food.

I happened to be visiting St Louis for a Cardinals game but was not sure what to do in the time between. Then a commercial for he zoo cam on the TV and I was curious if maybe I should see it. Upon asking my friend and other people the zoo had only good reviews, so I decided to go visit. For a free zoo this place is beyond belief, I was awestruck by the sheer size and the quality. This zoo is much better than some of the paid zoos I have visited. The staff was helpful and exited to educate on animals and their habitat. Three hours just flew by and I enjoyed every second of it. If you are visiting St Louis and have time, go visit the zoo. It is definitely worth it.

I feel like Hunan has gotten a lot better since it remodeled/re-opened this summer.  We've been going there for dine-in and take-out regularly over the past few months and haven't hit a bad dish.  My go-to option is the spicy 3-pepper chicken.  Kid-friendly and a nice neighborhood atmosphere.  Best chinese we've found on the main line... by far.

Kim Cameron, Valencia, CA
I'm surprised Giovanni's only got 3/5 stars. I think it's worth at least a 4/5!! It's good pizza & sandwiches, but I like Pizza Mizza's pizza a little bit more... No matter, it's good food, & good quality!!

Let me start by saying, I'm a burger snob, and Goodburger made it just the way I like it!  I give the burger 5 very bright stars, however, the service and speed were lacking.  Also; the decor, it looked like they couldn't decide on a theme.

Went there today  Their selection of house plants was excellent the prices were very good their customer service was awful not one person that work there said one word to me it was as if I was invisible that is why I gave them 1 star. The lady that was working in the plant section walked past me twice stood there behind the  register while I walked around looking at the plants but not a word that is not customer service I didn't feel welcome there at all. I had to go get someone to ring up my plants because she didn't come back I could see her standing there talking to the other employee but that was all she did. I wanted to ask some questions but she didn't make me feel that I was could.

I'm usually very forgiving of business faux pas or lack of common courtesy at times. However, this salon has left such an indelible bitter taste that I'm taking to review them several months after the incident. 

I was a walk-in customer and it didn't seem like they were too happy to see someone who didn't have a prior appointment. I was introduced to a grumpy hairstylist, who barely smiled once during my entire session or offered any pleasantries, except to bark instructions to follow her to have my hair washed. All my small talk was immediately struck down either with a curt reply or a head shake. I do not recall her name, but she had plenty of tattoos and absolute disinterest in engaging with the customer. The haircut was so terrible that it was a glaring testament to the stylist's indifference and the fact that she did not hear any of my request or preferences. 

And just when I thought things can't get any worse, I went to the front desk to pay my bill and I was hinted to leave a tip. I bit my tongue and decided to look past everything and asked them to add it to my total, so I can put it on my credit card - But much to my surprise - it was CASH ONLY to tip! Instead of accommodating a customer who did not have cash to tip, they had the nerve to point me to their ATM machine to withdraw cash in order to TIP the HORRIBLE service I had just endured!!! And to top it - their cash machine charges unreasonable amount of fees to withdraw! How greedy can one be!

What a bunch of insensitive and pretentious snobs who are only out there to fleece you!

I work for a local business with over 2000 employees and our company used to send out weekly email bulletin promoting this salon to all their employees. Well, not anymore since I've advised our HR/PR folks at our office to replace these disconnected elitist with far more deserving salon services in the area.

Never again.

This was our second visit to this place. Lats time we tired Indo-Chinese and didn't like it. yhe food was very Oily n Greasy. This time we went for traditional veg snacks. Orderd vada pav and dabgeli. Both were strictly ok. Nothing to rave abt. We also ordered missal pav which came 20mins late. Also, missal didnt have any salt or spice in it. After giving 2 attempts, i would not recommend this place.

The female, Caucasian, middle aged sales clerk at the Calvin Klein outlet store had an attitude problem toward Asian customers.  I visited the store on my way to a scheduled doctor's appointment.  I was the only customer waiting in line and she was deliberately taking her time with this other Caucasian customer at the checkout.  They chatted for no less than 10 minutes while I was anxiously waiting there.  They both saw me but maliciously chose to ignore the fact another customer was waiting in line while her family gathered around and waited.  The sales clerk did not bother to wrap up her lengthy chat until another Caucasian customer started to queue up behind me.  This is beyond bad customer service.  I will not shop there again.

Rock Bottom used to be a favorite place to go for my boyfriend and I, as well as for some of our friends. We had been coming here for about a year and a half since we live close by, and we always had great food and service, but recently both have been lacking. We wanted to keep giving Rock Bottom a chance since we really liked the food and atmosphere here, but we now cannot get past the changes in food that are not for the better, and the service that has been unacceptable. Last time we were here the food was not great..we ordered one of the new tostito apps and we only got 4 tortilla chips, and we ordered burgers and they were very small and we were still hungry when we left. The two times before that it took unacceptably long for us to get service to even get a beer at a table in the cocktail section, and every beer after that took forever to order to the point where we might as well have just ordered our next beer when we got served one. Some changes definitely need to be made to get the food and service back to what it used to be.
I had better oysters in Seattle, but everything else here is beautiful. Chargrilled oysters were beautiful as well as the pecan pie

Yumm, I love the old school ice cream shop they have going on. Lived here my whole life and finally stopped and got the banana split. To die for! I must say on my behalf, you need at least on partner to tackle this job! It is huge. Love enjoying this place now.

I'm sad that I'll probably never be able to eat a Cuban sandwich anywhere again.  Thanks Bodega.  Although I do have to say that it's probably a good thing that Bodega is 1,000 miles + from where I live because I'm sure I would be packing on the pounds by eating these delicious sandwiches on a weekly basis if it were located close to my house.  Really cool spot with some amazing sandwiches and drinks.  Couldn't believe how inexpensive it was, and as others before have mentioned in reviews, don't be deterred by the long line.  It moves fast and the food comes out pretty quickly.

I feel like our experience might be an outlier, as this is a nice restaurant (very beautiful interior). The hostess was friendly and engaging! However, I'm a little torn because while our male waiter was professional, our female waitress didn't seem happy to serve us. It felt like she didn't want to be there, and was just annoyed or in a bad mood. I believe this led to a chain of events that included mistakes in our order, and then eventually charging us for food we did not order (which I didn't realize until way later).


We ordered the following:

2 mocktails - Passion fruit spritz and strawberry palmer. They were both bland. I wouldn't recommend, but it was a reasonable price.

2 cocktails - Honey child and zolo malbec I didn't drink it, so I have no idea. Pretty presentation though!

Turtle soup - They added sherry to it. It was good! I wasn't sure if I'll be comfortable trying it, but everyone on yelp said try it so I had to.

Crawfish goat cheese crepes - Very yummy! I really enjoyed it. 

Shrimp grits - A bit salty which took away from the meal, but was decent and would have been super good other wise.

Blackened redfish - Though it was the most expensive a la carte dish, it was totally worth it.

Chocolate cake - It was too sweet for me, but that's a personal preference problem. It looked really nice.


I remember observing something quirky and odd about this place. They serve food in extremely large dishes. So large that it was hard to fit all the food we ordered on our table. It was like playing tetris. It was sort of annoying but also kind of funny. We figured it out eventually.

Now the two issues we ended up having was the following:

1) We were charged for a dish we did not order, even though we had already corrected the grumpy female waitress (found out after returning home from vacation) 

2) We were told we could go to the balcony and enjoy drinks. It was repeated multiple times which built up our excitement. Then we were told there was a private party so we couldn't. Then we were told we could in a little bit, so we waited after finishing dinner for 30+ minutes. Then we were told we couldn't anymore because they were taking longer. Then we were told they were clearing out and that we could in a bit. We ordered another drink for this purpose. Then we were stopped and told by another waiter that the balcony was closed off, and we were forced to exit. The whole experience was frustrating and a bit upsetting.

I feel there was much potential, but we were stuck with a waitress having a bad day. I don't think there was an excuse for being overcharged though. As for the whole balcony situation, I get that it happens, but they could have handled it better.

I was impressed by this Jeweler immediately upon walking in the door; I didn't realize that they did custom pieces, and they took the time to explain why it didn't make sense for me and my fiance to walk in looking for a ring. We were able to meet with Abbott Taylor himself briefly, and we set up an appointment for a week out when we'd shopped around and had a solid game plan. 
We came back to the appointment; (after showing up once to find that Abbott had called out sick and then having to re-schedule) and had a fine time picking out four small diamonds and designing the ring and talking about the settings. We had a budget that, judging by his face, was way lower than he was used to, but he made a lot of efforts to adjust. Thinking back, it made me up this review another star; it was that good. So, he said two weeks we'd get a call to come look at the computer rendering for final tweaks, then two weeks after that we'd have our ring. Fantastic!
Only it wasn't. Four weeks after that, I called, trying to figure out what was going on. I had to leave a message, which I got returned 3 days later; They were busy, and wouldn't have a rendering for anther two weeks. Ok, fine, it was February, lots of orders? 
Three weeks after that, again, I had to call, and I talked to a very friendly jeweler, Jerimiah (not positive) who I'd never spoken to before. 
"I'm really glad you called; we put in some of the diamonds, and weren't sure where you wanted the rest."
What?!?!
They had cast the ring without us ever seeing the design. We went in to look at what they had, and it was close, but they were willing to tweak the stone settings for us and re-cast the ring. There weren't any charges, which was fine.
It's a beautiful ring, and I'd still give this place a full heart-ed five stars, with the caveat to keep on top of them with phone calls if you actually want to be involved...
BUT
One day after picking up the ring, we discovered a huge gash in one of the diamonds. Whether this was part of the polishing process or what I'm not sure. They promised a replacement stone in... you guessed it, two weeks. If there is a charge associated, the review will remain at four. No charge, possibly five stars? It's a fantastic ring, but very frustrating service after the initial contact.

Wanted BBQ for lunch while in Belleville on business so went to Hicks based on Yelp reviews. So glad we did! I ordered the slider sampler (pulled pork, brisket, pulled chicken sliders) with garlic potato chips and baked beans (it was a LOT of food!). All of the meats were moist and delicious. The potato chips are THE BOMB! The beans were OK -- a little more fire than I like. The other two in our group ordered the pulled chicken sandwich with green beans and cole slaw and the rib sandwich with green beans and fries. Both liked their sandwiches very much, although the one with the rib sandwich said it had a little bit of gristle that needed to be picked out. We sat inside because the outside was full. It was 1-ish when we were there so the peak lunch time was over. Order at the counter and they bring you the food -- service was quick and friendly. We've passed this spot many times and are SO glad we gave it a try! We'll definitely go back.

Let's start with the goods:  The food is hot, chicken is juicy, breading is well-seasoned, the fries are pretty good (for fast food), and I prefer their popcorn shrimp to other drive-thru deep fried shrimp.  

However, even on slow days the food is often not ready quickly, asking you to pull up and wait 4-6 minutes on chicken to come out.  The biscuits are bland but are served (and appropriately eaten) with honey.  I've oftentimes had problems getting my condiment requests filled, pulling away to find myself without cocktail sauce or honey, only to drive back around.  

Let's be honest, I'll still being coming this Louisiana kitchen for my chicken and shrimp fixings, but I've got my eye out for something better.

On a quick overnight trip to Santa Barbara, my husband, teenage daughter and I had dinner at Brophy Bros.  It was a Friday night and we stopped in fairly early (5:30-ish) and were told that there was a 45 minute wait.  No big deal, as it was early.  We walked back to our nearby hotel to change, and as soon as we walked in the door, our pager started going off alerting us that our table was ready.  In most cases, having less of a wait than anticipated would be great, but not in this instance since we were back at our hotel.  My husband called and was told that when we returned, they would reset our pager and we would be given the next table.  Worked for us :)

When we returned shortly thereafter, we grabbed drinks at the bar and enjoyed them on the deck with an incredible view of the harbor, boats, etc.  The bartender was kind and offered to take a photo for us, which we gladly accepted.  We were seated at our dining table within about 15 minutes.  The restaurant was packed and lively.  A basket of french bread & butter was on our table within 30 seconds!  We ordered a cold platter to start (2 oysters, 2 clams, crab, peel & eat shrimp & ceviche).  The fish was very fresh and flavorful.  For dinner, we ordered fish & chips (very good!), a shrimp & crab salad with regular louis dressing instead of spicy (delicious!), and a bowl of cioppino (very enjoyable!).  The service was very good and we had absolutely no complaints :)  

I lived in Santa Barbara 29 years ago and used to eat at Brophy Bros. frequently.  I was excited to find it still thriving in SB and was really looking forward to dining there again.  Brophy Bros. did not disappoint!

Worst experience ever! My husband and I went to this place to check the upgrading options of our KIA 2013. We weren't looking for actually buying, just checking. The sales people AND manager almost surrounded us to take home a new care that matched our envisioned price. We didn't want to, because they had not done the paperwork. But, the manager came and insisted that they have carefully checked everything and they are sure it WILL work fine. So, we made and appointment to come next day and just sign the paperwork. WELL, as people say, never take a care home without signing document. We called them before going and asked if any additional document is needed. They asked for something which we brought. Long story short, we took leave of our work, went and waited there fore almost FOUR hours and the process didn't even begin. The reason: the finance company needed a lot of documents which we were not told to bring. Some, we had on dropbox and worked. But, they wanted our social security card which you normally don't carry! They kept holding us forever, eventually we gave up and asked for our car. The sales supervisor, Rich, was telling us that it is not their fault. They were not even admitting that we lost two precious day because they are so unprofessional. And, then the highlight of the day was this conversation.
Me: This is so unprofessional, you should know what documents needed and do your homework and tell the customer to come prepared.
Rich: Well, how should I have known that you guys are not citizens (Well, we are not and any two-year kid can tell from a mile). If I asked you, I would have been acting racist!
Me: This is even worst. How can you give away a brand new car, almost forcefully, to a customer you don't even their residency status?
Rich: I THOUGHT you were trustworthy!

And, boy, my credit history rocks. I can get any finance I want, and I didn't even needed a car right away. And, guess what, we had to wait another 15 minute for them to call our insurance company and return our own car back on our insurance account so that we could go back.

NEVER trust anything they say.

Update: They told me they are going to finance the car with KIA. While they were telling me they are waiting for approval from KIA finance, apparently, they had started to contacting many other financing companies. I keep receiving notes from various places in mail about a recent request by KIA Desimone!! I disapprove them even more.

I've left New Orleans but on a trip just re-visiting, I had to stop by this place again.  I last saw them early 2020 and I was afraid that the time and the pandemic would have diminished their quality.  Nope!  The chicken is still my number 1 of all time and all places.  We got some onion rings this time, it was ok.  I think that is my formal statement on their sides, they're ok.  Also their bubble tea, is just not good.  I think last time I said the bubbles were undercooked, well this time they were overcooked.  I wouldn't waste your money on the bubble tea and not waste too much money on the sides.  But spend all your money on their chicken!!!

Great neighborhood coffeeshop with plenty of options and friendly staff. I love that they have simple syrup for those of us who want our iced coffee. They also offer a rewards card which is hole punched for their frequent customers. I love a place which rewards their frequents!

If you want to get a great blow out for $40 don't come here. I have been here several times because of the proximity to my home but have been uh unhappy every time. The blow out doesn't last for lore.than a day because.the stylist.load up on product.and also don't wash your hair more of a rinse and 15 second application of conditioner. I would recommend the Blo out bar in Philly. Hands down the best. Further drive but worth it.when you.see how.great your hair looks

Disclaimer: This is a review of the Wendy's Pub fries I purchased from this location, not the restaurant itself. Although, this location isn't all that either. 

So, I had a hankering for some hot and salty fries from my favorite fry joint...no, not Wendy's, McDonald's...of course. However, the app wasn't working correctly. It could've been the lack of cellular service in the area, but, nevertheless, out of shear desperation, I headed to Wendy's. I just needed a lil something to tie me over till dinner. So, they had a coupon for free pub fries in the app with a purchase, and that's what I ordered. Pub fries and a chocolate chip cookie. Because when something is free, I try to stay as close to free as possible. I would normally get the apple chunks, but that side dish has been disappointing lately. 

Now, about these pub fries. That was one of THE most disgusting things I've ever eaten. I don't know what was in the sauce, but it tasted the way stinky feet and a wet mop smell. WTH! How did they pass testing? Thank goodness they were free. It didn't help that the fries were cold. Why are the fries cold?! What's up with the heat lamps? On the other hand, the chocolate chip cookie was the bomb, as it normally is. The chocolate chip cookie is one of the few things they get right. It tastes every bit of its 330 calories. As someone who counts calories, it was worth every one. 330 calories of chocolaty, chewy, sweet perfection. 

So, why did I rate them a two? Well, I'm ashamed to say that I actually finished the fries. I was hangry by that point and just needed to get that belly full. Don't judge me.
Terribly run office. They do not stick to their hours and leave their patients hanging

My son decided to rent an apartment at The Legends this weekend. The front clerk, McKenzie, was very knowledgeable, professional and bright. We had a pleasant experience learning about the complex and available apartments. It looks like a lovely place to live, close to great shopping and restaurants... a beautiful area. I expect my son will be very happy here.

We went here for SIPs because it seemed to have one of the best varieties of SIPs offerings- drinks and food-wise- out there. We got the pulled pork sliders, truffle fries, and grilled flat bread which were all pretty good and even better at half price. The flat bread dough was a little odd for an Italian place- more like a tortilla/ pita mix then pizza.

We ate dinner there because we got the SIPs discount. The bread came with a sundried tomato dipping sauce that seemed promising, but was bland and oily. I got the half-portion of the bucatini with mushrooms because bucatini is one of my favorite pastas. The mushroom sauce was really tasty, but unfortunately, the pasta was underdone. My boyfriend got the gnocchi with truffle cream sauce which was flavorful and light. I was expecting more of an alfredo sauce, but we were both pleasantly surprised that the gnocchi were pillowy and the sauce just rich enough. And the picture of the gnocchi dish on this site looks a lot heavier than it actually is, so don't think you'll be rolling out of the place if you order it.

The server was friendly, and the atmosphere is more comfortable and private than at La Viola (read: more space between the tables). I also like how they offer a lot of their pastas in half portions.

This place is great! They visited at my business facility and their food has never disappointed us. Super yummy! I will say one thing though, on the menu you've used the wrong form of "accept" you have "except." Other than that you guys are amazing! :)

Trying on the dress was fun, but paying for it was horrible. They help the people behind you before finishing your transaction.   Everyone who walked in was greeted and helped while I waited. 
Consultant said that even though I wear a size four I need to order a size ten dress. They are making sure the people next sorry have dresses to alter. Holy cow it's 7 inches to big.  Scam scam scam. 
Dress is wonderful they people who work there are trash.

Worst calzone we ever had. It was under cooked in the center and the bottom was not edible, it was burnt. I don't see how little Italy let this one get by. Their pizza and food is always good. This happened to us twice in a row thought the first time was a mistake but not so. We have been eating here since they opened , that's a long time. Sorry but I had to share this maybe it will be corrected.

We never made it to the original Olga's and knew this wasn't the same people but were still very eager to try out a new diner. We made it a week into the grand opening and the results were just a meh. The place itself looks fantastic and is very spacious. The tuscan omelette started out good but damn this thing was cheeeeesy. The sausage actually tasted quite good but after the first couple of bites all I could taste was the cheese and nothing else. The waitress was very nice (even though she forgot my toast dammit!) but def seemed overwhelmed and new to this. 
  This isn't a place we are eager to go back to but not a place we would avoid either. Sure this isn't a ringing endorsement and I'm sure we will go back when we are nearby....and starving.

Recently stopped by Pecks Chicken for some take-out. Their lunch special is only $5.75 for chicken and two sides which is definitely a good deal. My friends and I shared an assortment of the sides including potato salad, fried okra, french fries, cole slaw, mac and cheese, rice, and black beans. Each order came with a pita bread.

The chicken itself was flavorful - certainly on the bar-b-que side of things. Not the most moist chicken I have ever had, but satisfactory. What I really enjoyed were the sides though. The mac and cheese was excellent and creamy. The cole slaw was to my level of sweetness. Surprisingly I even liked the okra which is something I tend to shy away from.

The lady at the register was friendly, although I don't think I would actually eat inside the place because it has a very diner-like feel to it. Parking was a bit of a mess, especially getting out of the place, but it was accessible nevertheless.

Pecks is certainly worth the price, especially for lunch. I heard their ribs are delicious and they recently sent me a tweet with a photo of their fried ribs which I will have ot leave for a future visit.

Love picnicking with perfect sunset awesome band and great neighbors.  Outdoor venue without extra gouge for parking.  Tractor took us right to the door or you can tip the Boy Scouts to Sherpa your bounty.

Love taking walks with my dog to Lake Park. There is a large lawn and a path that goes all around the small lake. There are geese that are pretty friendly to people because they are constantly being fed. The park also has a playground area for children to play. There are benches placed close to the water to sit at. Beware, the water is not too clean looking. I am surprised the city has not installed some type of pump system to keep the water flow moving. I would not recommend attempting to swim in the lake or have my dog in the water. It's silty and dirty!

The food is pretty good I've had better Pad Thai in Nyc. But it's a good compromise. I've ordered delivery a few times it's a bit expensive considering the amount of food you're actually getting.  The sandwiches are very flavorful and tasty as are the rice bowls. I'd still highly recommend because they delivery quickly and food arrives hot.

I went on the cemetery tour with my friends and their kids.  My friends and I are from New Orleans and we thought we already knew a lot of information about the cemeteries.  Our tour guide was Thorn, and we learned so much from him!  He was so relaxed and easy to listen to! As locals, we had a lot of questions, and Thorn knew all the answers!  We had a great time, and the two hours flew by!  As a native New Orleanian, I enjoyed the tour and all the extra information that Thorn gave us.  Highly recommend taking this tour with Thorn!!
Love them, they're right around the corner and it's hard not to stop each time I walk by.  A great addition to the neighborhood.

I usually have all my maintenance done at the VW dealership because my brother-in-law works there and makes sure I don't get played/overcharged...but upon noticing my inspection had expired last month (that goes to show how much I've been driving lately), I decided to just take my car to John Carney's this year. I was a little nervous I'd be told there were 100 things wrong with it, or it wouldn't pass inspection because of X, Y, or Z, but the convenience of them being located in walking distance of my house overrode my paranoia that I'd be taken for a fool.

Despite my general distrust, I could not be more pleased! JC charged me $116 for a full inspection and an oil change. They didn't try to upsell me one bit. I have mad respect for them and will absolutely be returning for more mechanical/routine work in the future. Their prices are hella reasonable and they seem like genuinely nice people. Plus I'd rather support a local business than the VW machine!

We stopped here on our way down the coast when we went to 5 CrossFits in one week! Must say, this was my fav! There was a great community feeling about this place; so many of the people had been at this gym for so long and you could tell they really loved this box! The free wod on Saturday morning was really cool, and I loved how they created a partner wod on the spot to pair all the newbies with someone who had been crossfitting for a longer period. That's what crossfitting is all about! We are planning another trip to SB soon and will most definitely be here as much as we can during the trip.

A-OK is a good description, Yelp. 

The friend chicken IS good. The rest tastes mass produced and / or canned. 

I will say the environment is charming, and I enjoy the family style, no cell phone allowed setting. 

However, for what it is, $19 for dinner is OUTRAGEOUS. Seems like a lot of other people fully enjoy it, so I don't think my "meh" feelings will do them any harm :) .

Wanting to try a new breakfast spot downtown, my friend and I decided to give Patachou a try. We both wanted to like Patachou and we gave it a fair shot, but both of us left feeling like "mehh, we've had better and probably won't be back." Which is the reason for 2 stars. 

The restaurant is located on the first floor of the Simon building. We were seated almost immediately upon arrival, but then waited ~10 minutes before being acknowledged by the wait staff. When the waiter arrived with the waters, he apologized and told us that the waitress who was supposed to have our table actually forgot that it was hers, so he took it over after noticing we had been waiting for a while. Odd start.

The menu has both breakfast and lunch options, many of which I would describe as "boujee". That said, they have several options for those that are vegan or gluten free. Unfortunately, I am neither and had a difficult time finding something I thought I would like.

I ended up getting the Cuban breakfast which has a fried egg over seasoned black beans, rice, white cheddar, avocado, sour cream and spinach-jalapeño pesto. I also added ham, at the suggestion of our waiter. My friend got the California dreamer omelette, which comes with avocado, white cheddar, sour cream and jalapeños. 

The Cuban had way more cheese on it than I was expecting, and completely covered the egg, which masked the taste. However, I did enjoy the jalapeño pesto sauce, which added some flavor and a kick. My friend's omelette was pretty bland, but she did enjoy the side of toast and jam.

Overall, our bill was $29 before tip, for two people who only got water to drink. Of note, the coffee is self-serve at a coffee bar for $3.95. This seems a bit high unless you plan on having 3+ cups, but everyone has their own opinion on prices so I won't dock them for that.
Dry rub ribs were tasty. The choices of sauces were yummy. We enjoyed the sides of Mac and cheese and baked beans. Sides were a little small but good.  I would go again.

The staff is friendly but the older woman that did my nails was slow. Took two hours to do gel acrylic refill. It never takes that long. I repeatedly had to tell her that she needs to put the acrylic closer to my cuticles because it was going to look like I would need my nails done again in less than a week. My thum is also peeling on the side because she missed the whole corner of my nails. I wouldn't say it was the worst experience but it was really agitating. If I go there again I would make sure not to go to her.

First time NOLA. Used to great beer selections in the West and Midwest, I was excited to see a NOLA bar offering "The Best Beer Selection in New Orleans".  Ordered 1st beer: "Sorry, we don't have that."  2nd beer: "No, we're all out of that."  3rd selection : "Yeah, that's my favorite, but we're all out of that one."  Found some joy after the 4th try.  Beer was a good stout. My friend asked for several simple cocktails, but they were out of three basic liquors as well. Bartender was friendly enough.  Our hotel bar may not have had as many beers on tap, but at least they were all in stock.
We had lunch. Cesar Salad, soup, chicken wings,we're good. Entrees chicken parm below avg. Sauce tasted like ragu. Vodka rigatoni very good. Service wasn't good. Looks,like pizza is their big seller.

Hodak's really is your place for delicious chicken dinners. Not to mention that the menu items are so cheap, it's hard not to double check your bill at the end of your meal! The staff is superbly friendly and attentive, even with a full house. What I love most about Hodak's is the option to sit at the bar and grab a drink, even though it's a family restaurant. Don't expect fine dining, your meal comes on plastic plates and your drinks in plastic cups, but the atmosphere makes it a great after-church stop or casual weekday dinner dine. 

My favorite menu items are the classic chicken dinner with fries and the turkey club! Also, they have some of the best coffee you can sip in the area. Portion sizes are great, and pleasantly surprising for their affordability. I would give Hodak's five stars if only their menu options were a bit more vast. However, they do what they know and they do it well!

I was volunteering for this a little bit in the office. I don't recommend it, it's no fun dealing with office politics going on especially when a boss leaves, so I do what I can in staying out of it. Avoid it as much as possible, I recommend that for those with autism who are volunteering as feedback. It's like who is better then who, and who gets a job permeation and what not.

I usually enjoy going in this store just to grab a drink and one or two other things and now that they have expanded their options, I like it even more but this past weekend it was so uncomfortable being in there with one of the cashiers and whoever the shift leader was. The way they interacted with each other with so many customers RIGHT THERE was atrocious!!! Not only that but the boobs that were falling out of the shift leads top was awful!!! The cashier needed more training, the shift lead needed A LOT more professionalism. I get it, it's The Dollar Tree but c'mon talk to each other respectfully and when someone is asking for help, give them help and let your customers know they matter!!! It was such an odd experience!!!!

We dine here frequently. The food is delicious...prime rib, mashed potatoes, snow crab legs, corn, etc. Let's not forget the delicious desserts...chocolate marble cheesecake! And the hot chocolate...a must!! My son loves the Mongolian BBQ station. 

But on top of the great food, we received EXCELLENT service from Timothy. He's extremely attentive even when his section is completely filled. He's quick to refill our beverages, always smiling, very pleasant to be around, and definitely goes above and beyond for his customers. The hostess only had a booth available and since my 15 month old son was hungry, my wife and I decided to just take the booth. Tim saw that we were struggling with feeding our son in the booth and asked us if we would prefer a table. Once a table became available in his section, he set up the table for us with fresh drinks, silverware and a high chair. All we had to do was bring our plates over. 

We wanted a banana for dessert for our son but the buffet was out of bananas. Tim went to the other restaurant and asked them if they had a banana, and brought it back to us! 

So if you plan on dining here, ask to be seated in Tim's section. You will not be disappointed!

Terrible !!!! Don't buy here!!! Got my "famous designer" desk shipped, and is broken. See the picture I posted.
When I placed the order, an item was sold out and they never notified me. I had to call and ask why I was not getting the item, for them to tell me that they canceled the order!!!

Impressive menu that runs the gambit of Asian cuisine.  From all of the plates in my party, I tried the Chicken pad Thai, Shrimp pad Thai, Broccoli with garlic sauce, and Pork mei fun.  Everything was delicious.  

Restaurant isn't anything fancy and the open floor plan is a little too... open? But I'd definitely put this on my list of fave Asian spots on the DE/PA line.

Cozy environment and friendly staff. Food wasn't very flavorful, there is no where to stand when you're waiting-- and it gets busy so the wait can be kind of long-- it can also be kind of pricey for the amount of food served. Overall decent restaurant!

I have only been bringing my dogs to the Batt brothers for 6 months but 10 minutes after I first started talking to Dr. David Batt on the phone about my English Bulldog having a C-Section I felt that he was the perfect vet for me and my pets. I instantly trusted him and that's so hard to come by these days.  All 9 of the pups were fine by the way. If you want the most caring and knowledgeable vets for your animals bring them to Dr. David, Dr. Brendan and their staff. These guys actually care and you can feel it. I've sent people to their office and the feedback I get has always been great ones. They really do what's best for the dogs without draining you out of every last cent you have. I can't say enough good things about them. To know I have two wonderful vets taking care of my dogs makes my life so stress free.

This place has a cool atmosphere but my partner had the chicken dish which came raw and too salty! Expect to pay $12 for a mixed drinks..wish I knew this before but there are no prices on the drinks menu! Doubt that I will return!

I love Fred & Ricky's commitment to plant-based foods, and everything I have purchased there has been tasty (especially the chocolate pudding!)  However, because of its "grab-and-go" business model, I do not go very often...I just can't get around the use of so many plastic containers. We need to reduce our use of plastic, particularly single-use convenience-based plastic, and I'm doing best to remove convenience-based plastic from my life. I realize that F&R's containers are recyclable, but that doesn't take into account the energy expended in manufacturing/shipping them in the first place or whether the market is there for that recycled material. If Fred & Ricky's introduces a model in which I can bring my own containers or one that adopts a more traditional sit-down restaurant model with reusable dishes/flatware, I will become a far more frequent customer (please do!). Right now, I already have more F&R containers in my "reuse" stack than I can handle!

Stopped here for a quick iced coffee to go on an early Saturday morning.  It's located in a strip mall off Tchoupitoulas and if you come on a busy day, good luck parking!

Service was friendly and quick. And my iced coffee was good.  Only thing I found a little odd was all the random nicknacks for sale which I thought came off a little cheesy.  Costume jewelry and candles?   Totally takes away from the brand.  I think they should focus more on selling items with the PJ's Coffee logo on it. The random items just cheapen the location.

Aside from that observation, I would come back.

I ordered the Spicy Tonkotsu Ramen. BY FAR the worst Ramen I've ever had. Tonkotsu is normally very flavorful but this one was as bland as can be. No flavor at all. Not spicy at all so idk why it's labeled that. Very disappointing. I'm used to pork broth having a good amount of oil but this was excessive. It had a huge layer of oil. The only reason I gave 2 stars is because the passion fruit green tea punch was good.

Our waiter is the only thing that made this restaurant earn this one star. I understand that restaurants are busy on New Year's Eve, however the point of a reservation is to make sure your customers aren't standing outside 55 min past their set time. It is kind of contradicting that they take your card so they can bill you if you are late but they did not even have the courtesy to call you to let us know they were running behind. Once inside the manager promised to make it up to us but never even came to check on our table. We also called in advance to let them know we had a vegetarian guest coming with us and they said they would accommodate to her. She received a plate full of raw vegetables in replace of the normal options (filet steak or grouper). Not to mention the lack of any type of seasoning (not even salt or pepper) on the vegetables. I had ordered the filet for my main course. They ask how you would like it cooked and I said medium. They proceeded to give me 2 raw filets and then I asked for them to just put the 2nd filet back on for a second and then it was brought out burnt. ( I would have eaten it well done but it was to a crisp). At this point we were done and wanted to leave and the check still came out to almost $800. (So nothing was taken "care of" like the manger had promised). All in all food was good when they put effort in. At some point management needs to do their job and accommodate. This is probably a Great restaurant when it is not busy and they have another manager on duty. We had all worked in the service industry before so we know lack of effort when we see it.

Went over on Taco Tuesday with a group of friends who have begun making this their Tuesday night hangout. Definitely more of a locals place (fine with me since I qualify). Our server was friendly, extremely knowledgeable about brews and availability as well as the menu, and was fairly attentive, considering how busy the place got. He was a little slow at times, but it was easily corrected by getting his attention.

The Mahi tacos were very good. I'm not fish taco aficianado, not yet anyway, but these were interesting and I was hungry. They went down well. The presence of pineapple was a bit interesting, but all in all it didn't get in the way of my enjoyment of the meal. Also had a half dozen of the steamed oysters. They could have been more well done, but I didn't ask for it and, quite frankly, was hungry - they went down even faster than the tacos. They were fresh, served quickly, and with the appropriate sides (horseradish and shrimp sauce). 

I enjoyed the food but think the service could have been a bit better. Since this was my first time here I really would prefer to visit a few more times before I update to any higher star rating. I felt they deserved a 3.5, but since that's unavailable, and I wasn't quite comfy with a 4 star at this time, I gave a 3. But that's not to their detriment! I just need to visit again to get a good baseline. :)

Drinkers is the kind of place that is what exactly you expect from a small, local bar--Cheap food, cheap beer, and a loud atmosphere.  The oddest part about it is that it's right in the middle of the City, and not in some shady area.

There really isn't much you need to know, the cheap beer and food should be enough to get you in the door as it is.  The tacos are $1 a piece and are actually quite good for bar-style tacos.  In fact, all of the food that I've tried here is pretty decent bar food.  The tv's always have sports on, the music is loud, and there are plenty of twenty-somethings to mingle with.

A night that usually starts out with some cheap drinks, catching up on sports, and good bar food can easily turn into a night of drunken debauchery.

C'mon, what more do I need to say.
This is a top notch repair service. Fair, reliable and comes in a reasonable time to determine repair needs. Highly recommend!
