Pretty delicous, good price and casual, I give them a solid 4.5 stars. We went twice, once for lunch and the second for dinner. For lunch we ordered the Jumbalya with seafood, gumbo straight up and a bloody mary. Drink was excellent, gumbo was ok (prob would recammend to get it with the seafood) and the Jumbalya, that was awesome! The only problen was it was a little cold. We tried them for dinner a few days later. Got the Jumbalya again and it was perfect this time. Then decided to try the cajin seafood pasta and it was so tasty. Basically a spicy alfredo with a good mix of seafood.
Started out well but but the staff became increasingly rude and waiting for drinks in a mostly empty night took way to long.

4.5 stars

Our meal at Zahav was easily in my top three meals of 2014, beat out only by Rose's Luxury in DC and Ink in LA. 

The food was beyond excellent. I just spent six months living in the Middle East and ate more than my fair share hummus; Zahav's blows all other hummuses (hummi?) out of the water. I'd never had warm hummus until our meal at Zahav but I highly, highly recommend giving it a shot. It brings out flavors that would otherwise be overlooked by the palate. 

The timing of the meal was the one element that was lacking. I wouldn't have even minded having somewhat extended waits between courses except that the temperature felt like an arctic cold had swept in and, given it was August, I hadn't brought a down parka with me. Had we had the beverage we had ordered to distract ourselves from the cold it may not have been so bad but between the wait and just how painfully cold the restaurant was, I have to dock it half a star.

After our initial and promptly served round of cocktails (I had the Desert Rose with gin, hibiscus, grapefruit, and cucumber), we went for a pitcher of the very summery Lemonnana (Jim Bean, muddled mint, fresh lemon, verbena) and waited for it to arrive. Our food courses came and went and still we waited. It doesn't take that long to mix four ingredients yet we waited some more. We were close to the end of the savory portion of the meal by the time it arrived and, since there were only two of us, we had to drink faster than preferred to remain on course with the meal.

We began with the Masbacha - the warm chickpea hummus. Unknown to us, this came accompanied by six small veggie side dishes. Seeing the spread of food before us, we knew that we probably shouldn't have ordered that in addition to the "taste of Zahav" menu for each of us. Regardless, it was delightful and the veggies were a nice treat with everything tasting very fresh.

Zahav allows you to create your own tasting menu (two mezzes, one grill, one dessert for $42) which was perfect since we were able to try a sizable portion of the menu. Our four mezzes were the crispy grape leaves, fried cauliflower, Yemenite beef stew and konafi (cheese cubes). The first three were fabulous but we both found the konafi to be unpalatable. Justin ate a couple bites but I was done after one and only reluctantly swallowed that down. The GM inquired about the nearly-full plate and we said that we were glad to have tried it - it was certainly unique - but neither one of us cared for it. He insisted that he would bring us another dish of our choosing. We politely declined, afterall we were the ones who chose it, but he insisted and brought us the watermelon, feta and black olive salad. This ended up being the best of the five mezzes we had and we were very thankful for the generosity of the GM. 

For the grills we had duck and kofte, the latter being my favorite. And for dessert Justin chose the black forest mousse and I had the white chocolate custard with watermelon and hazelnuts. I generally dislike fruit in dessert but this dish blew me away. This was the best dessert I had all year and it was beautifully plated. 

Some of the menu here is seasonable but there are staple items that remain all year around. Regardless of what is on the menu when you visit you're sure to find a bunch of winning items...just remember to bring a sweater despite the temperature outside.

I'm only giving it two stars because the restroom did not have toilet paper. Imagine how frustrating it is to be done and have nothing to wipe with. I wanted to scream  for someone to bring me toilet paper like I do at home lol. 

I only go there for their butter pecan ice cream.
Great food, great menu, great ambiance.  A little noisy and need a reservation for sure at night. But overall a great experience for lunch or dinner.

I noticed that Olga's diner finally opened at their new location on route 73 after the old Olga's was torn down when the circle was reorganized.

T new Olga's is much smaller than the original, with no bakery.  Also, the menu is much smaller but the prices are more expensive.

Fist time I drove by the new place the parking lot was packed, today (12-20-19), ALMOST EMPTY, at 8am)

Décor is modern, clean,and service was excellent.

I probably will not be a regular,

Friends and I were visiting from out of town and this seemed to be the place that fit our appetites. We were looking for some place to do a light dinner and be able to share plates.

This restaurant turned out to be much nicer than I thought so I felt a little scummy with my hooded jacket for the cool evening, but we were still treated well by the staff. Here's what we ate:

Cheddar Hush Puppies: I don't remember tasting much cheddar in these hush puppies, but the chipotle honey accompanying it made it taste delicious. So much so that my friends even used the honey for dipping the fries.

Garlic and Herb Fries: This was actually my choice because I really wanted some fries. The aioli really made these fries tasty. How often is it that you get aioli with fries?

Caramelized Cauliflower: This was actually a variety of different kinds of cauliflower (purple, orange, white and green) served on a heated skillet. Definitely get this if you're a fan of roasted vegetables. 

Roasted Beet Salad: Quite possibly the most beautiful dish of the evening. I wish I had taken a picture of this, but so much of this spoke "spring" to me with the greens contrasted against the red/yellow of the beets as it was a combination of regular and golden beets. 

Scallops: I can't remember if these scallops were roasted or not, but this was a recommended dish by our waiter. It's so easy to overcook scallops, but these were at the right consistency that I expect: still soft and tender. While scallops don't have as much of a taste on their own, it was delicious with the other items on the plate: generous halves/slices of avocado, interesting citrus with the grapefruit and corn salsa. If I were to return, I'd probably get these as a main dish to myself.

Service was attentive and terrific. The only thing I didn't like so much, and it might be because I'm not from Santa Barbara, is that you have to ask for water because of water restrictions. I wouldn't hold that against the restaurant, but I'm glad our waiter let us know that they can't offer water unless we ask.

Good and healthy food. Fair service. But, when it came time for our check, they COMPLETELY fouled up our bill.  It took FOREVER to fix. AND, there were only 3 TABLES filled?! We were late for our event. When something gets fouled up like this and inconveniences the customer, something should be offered. But, nothing was. Will certainly NOT be going back.

I used to love this place and would frequent it often since I live so close by. I'm bummed that their lunch menu has changed a lot and now there is only one item that I can eat because everything else is so spicy (even by my husbands standards and he can take much more spice than I can). The cupcakes are still amazing, though, and I would love to be able to support the local business more. Bring back the lentil soup please!

Very unimpressed. This is the driest past that I have ever encountered in my life, I was excited to taste the delicious New Orleans sauce but there's literally no sauce in this. I can't even describe what it tastes like because it just tastes like plain boiled pasta. And it's so inexplicably dry. The chicken also, super dry. I don't understand charging this much money for this. Just...disappointing. At least the portions are generous?

Ordered catering from here. The food was great.:.its not cheap. Everyone raved about the food but it was a STRUGGLE getting them to send us the invoice. We called, emailed, and even went to the store and a month later we finally got a receipt for a $300 order. Wouldn't do catering again just due to this hassle but would return for brunch.

The locals call this the "Nazi range". 

The one and only time I went, the RO belittled a father and his son a few stalls away from me, for the RO's own mistake. Let me explain. 

The RO started saying " ok folks we're gonna have a" *bang* he's interrupted by young boy popping off one more .22 round. The RO and staff went nuts on these poor people. " OH my God , cease fire cease fire!!!". Staff comes running over and kicks them out!

Later I tried to complain and was told to go away. I complained directly to the MDC and was ignored. 

They failed to issue a proper range command, and everyone there suffers for it. 

Do yourself a favor and find another range!

Great burgers! This is my go to place when I want a good burger and I'm never disappointed. My only complaint is the smoking inside. Unfortunately, it's usually the workers and I prefer not to have someone smoking next to me while I eat. As annoying as it is, the burgers make it easier to overlook.
The carne asada tacos were on point I'd come back for those. The beans and rice were awful, they tasted like they came out of a can. The horchata wasn't that great either not even fresh brewed.

TLDR: super long wait, no parking, food/drink tastes bad. 

Had a pretty frustrating experience when I recently visited the Blind Tiger in Seminole Heights. As with many businesses on Florida Ave, the parking situation is a headache. With no spots left in their designated lot, I had to park across the street and walk over. 

Upon entering, you'll notice a ton of cool merchandise, people working on their laptops, and a great selection of coffee and food. I placed my order at the counter without issue and waited for it to be ready. 

I ordered a seasonal coffee, club sandwich, and a cookie. I immediately received the coffee and cookie since they were already prepared or quick to prepare. I then waited for 20 minutes for my sandwich. 

As I waited, I watched about 7 employees run around unloading inventory, drinking coffee, and talking to each other. I had to ask about my sandwich and was told I had another 5 minute wait. 

When it was finally ready, it was left on the counter. Not even handed to me LOL I grabbed it and left, frustrated at the wait time and indifference from the staff. 

I was starving and decided to dig into the cookie on my drive back home... what a flop. This "gluten free chocolate chip" cookie tasted like sand with chocolate flavor mixed in. It was awful. In an attempt to wash it down, I drank my coffee with the seasonal flavor added. It was horrible. Barely sweet, which was meh, but I got zero hint of pumpkin either. 

When I finally got home, I opened my to-go box to find a sandwich with very toasted bread, but totally cold ingredients. I had to wonder if toasting the bread alone is what took 25 minutes to make this sandwich. I was so disappointed. 

I got home and tossed the coffee and cookie that were both terrible in the garbage and toasted the sandwich myself to heat it up and melt the cheese. It was very dry and plain but came as described on the menu. Had to add some pizazz of my own with condiments. 

Overall, this was a bad experience. I like to support small businesses, and I like to support local, but when the experience is negative start to finish, it makes me not want to return. 

Regardless of how trendy your business is, nothing beats solid customer service and a great product. Sad that Blind Tiger fell extremely short on both fronts.

2 stars instead of one because of the amazing history. But otherwise to me this place seems totally overrated as a music venue. Very uncomfortable seating for a longer show. Better places to see music in Nashville IMO but the history is indeed amazing.

Went to Burger Up in Franklin, Sunday May 24, 2020. None of the staff wore masks, they did not social distance their patrons, and the food was horrible. Tried the fried pickles, the breading was very thin and just not that great. I got the burger with the avocado and herb mayo. The mayonnaise sauce was not good. The fries were greasy little stick fries HORRIBLE!! We paid $44.00 for this crap and definitely cannot believe this waiter kept standing right by me with no mask on!!  I do not recommend this place.

Came in for a gel manicure and was so disappointed by the quality as it chipped within 24 hours! The tech was rushed, painted over my cuticles and they didn't offer me anything to drink when other customers were given water or wine. I will not be returning. A waste of my $ and time.

The elderly lady who runs this shop is VERY nice and professional. Unfortunately, she has bad luck with employees.
I've kept a UPS box there for over a year now, and only in the past 4-5 months have started to have awful problems, including a package sent from France, addressed correctly, which was returned to sender!
They also (lately) do not call me promptly when I have packages, and I've had to argue that a certain package was, actually, delivered. 
Do not use this location to RECEIVE packages if you value receiving them. Kind of takes away the use of having a mailing address, since I originally got a box so that my packages wouldn't be left outdoors on my home's front step all day.

There's one plus about this salon. It is beautiful inside and gives off a serene vibe. However,this place is ridiculously overpriced. I caused my nail to peel after a morning of heavy shoveling to dig my car out of the snow a/b a week and a half ago. It drives me absolutely insane to have chipped nail polish, so I was in a frenzy to find someplace to fix the small peel on my nail. I work right around the corner from here, so this was the logical stop. I wear gel manicures, which are always more expensive than regular manis, but $5 to repaint ONE nail is absolutely insane.The tech saw the disbelief on my face when she told me the price (which she did beforehand), but I was in such a rush that I allowed her to repair the polish anyhow. She then proceeded to talk about how a lot of the clients comment about how much higher the prices are around here than they are at other salons. Tuh! Ya think? To top it off, she unevenly filed my nail, which I didn't observe until the nail finished drying. By that time, I was so perturbed that I kindly collected my things, tipped her & moved along on my way. My advice? Skip over this place unless you're in the tightest of binds.

We stopped at Rusty's on the way home from the beach. I'm really wishing we hadn't. The place is disgustingly dirty! There is graffiti etched into the tables and the pizza was marginal at best. Do yourself a favor and go somewhere else. The kids didn't even like the pizza.

Don't go here. It's a rip off and the ladies aren't very nice. No customer service. I wanted a regular pedicure with regular polish and they didn't ask me and gave me gel and then charged $38 for one pedicure. Fucking ridiculous.

This review is for Copper Moon in Terminal B.
The coffee brings me, nostalgically, to a greasy spoon. Except instead of being served by a waitress who calls me "Hon" with a smoker's cough and pours it in front of me with press-on nails, you've got twenty-something girls in pressed uniforms pulling a handle behind a counter into a paper cup. But it's the same coffee. That's not a good thing...

We did our first party with Level Up yesterday and had roughly 36 kids so about 6 more than capacity (which I wouldn't recommend from a host standpoint) but it was crazy fun and the kids had an absolute blast!! All got a chance to play what they wanted to play and you definitely get a lot of bang for your buck.


Jeff and Sharon were wonderful especially with our request to have them in the lot next to our house instead of the curb which I know was a little difficult to navigate for them but still worked out beautifully. The only rough part was the angle of the sun which couldn't have been helped with their accomodating our request for their location but it corrected as the sun went down and the kids didn't seem to mind. :)

It is a wonderful party option with fantastic owners who absolutely went above and beyond in accommodating our needs and making this one of our daughter's most memorable birthdays. 

I would absolutely recommend them for your parties. Pricing is great and it is run by wonderful LOCAL people who work hard to give your kids a great experience. Thank you both for an amazing time!!!

What a fun spot! Great decor, great place to hang out with a group of friends, super spacious, lots of seating, fun long island iced tea flavors, karaoke, fire pits, great music playing in the background, pool table, and pretty decent food. So why only 3 stars? The service we received was absolutely awful. It took over an hour for a couple of us to get our drink! Not cool. If course at the end, they apologized, and gave out discounts, but our group of maybe 10 people, was way too much for our waitress to handle, which was super disappointing. If I ever make it back, I'm hoping the staff would be a bit more attentive!

This is a huge Target store, the kind with groceries.   It is convenient to stop in on the way home from work.  I live their bakery as it allows me to get bread that is fresh.
For the most part this store is well stocked and clean.
The biggest drawback is their people.  Not friendly at all!  Tonight as we were checking out, the cashier carried on a side conversation with a coworker while she checked out the customer ahead of us and continued that conversation completely through our transaction.

The swordfish sandwich was absolutely delicious. Combination of ingredients on the fabulous milk bun was perfect with every bite. Small shop with attention to detail and quality and a nice addition to our neighborhood. Please support them, we need places like this to survive the pandemic.

So good, probably the best falafel I've had. This place is pretty tight though so I wouldn't plan on eating in. Line moved quickly for lunch rush but it was worth the wait. Can't wait to go back and try one of their milkshakes!

If you're looking for authentic Central American and Caribbean cuisine THIS IS IT RIGHT HERE! As a Salvadoran woman myself I can say that these are one of the BEST Pupusas I've had in the states. A visitor to New Orleans this was by far one of the best meals I had in the city. The restaurant is beautiful and feels just like home. Manolo the owner took care of us and is extremely kind and welcoming. If you're looking for amazing Central American/ Caribbean food with even better service, this is your spot!!
Food is excellent and authentic.   It is a no frills atmosphere.  Very reasonably priced.   BYO is a plus.  Definitely returning here again.

Went there for breakfast and was quite disappointed as I was expecting some great baked goods such as donuts and muffins. They had none of these - just some cupcakes and cakes. The only. Breakfast offerings they had were on the board and consisted of a breakfast burrito, a bagel and one other item I can't remember. We ended up just getting two lattes. The lattes were good but small for the price. The interior is charming and very inviting. They have Two rooms. They also have a porch area and it looks like the "garage windows" open so in the summer it could be a fun place. Great location overlooking the park. I looked at the lunch menu which consisted of several salads and sandwiches but it looked pretty pricy as everything seemed to start at $11 or $12.  Cake slices start at $8.95.  As I said very charming and could be a fun place to go after dinner for dessert and a glass of wine.

Do you love tasty pizza that's not too greasy and has a myriad of crazy toppings like mac and cheese or a greek salad?
Then go to one of the other Peace A Pizza locations, cause this one's terrible.  Chalk it up to the high schoolers that operate it and the manager that I've only seen in caricatures hanging up behind the counter.  The dining area is usually marginally clean and the kids clearly have no interest in helping customers or replenishing slices during peak times.  No cheese pizza around dinner time on a Friday night and no interest in making more?  Are you kidding me?

If there was a "Good for:" checkbox down below on this form that was labeled "Nothing," I'd check it.

The cookies were super good and they are BIG. You can easily share. They need to work on the curbside delivery. My husband waited outside for 20 mins and then finally went in and they had lost our order.

I wish I could give this place two separate reviews. The gas station would get a 1 star and the convenience store would get 3 stars. As far as the gas station this is one of the slowest places I've been to. The attendant seemed nice enough but even though there were two guys "working" it seemed only one of them was actually working. I likely wont get gas here again due to how long I had to wait. It was close to five minutes. I nearly got out and pumped the gas myself. 

The inside seemed clean and I didnt have any issues. I agree with other reviews I did notice a slight smell of rancid oil but I still got some fried chicken anyway. The fried chicken was decent. Was it the best fast food chicken I've ever eaten, no, but it wasnt bad either.  I'd likely get their chicken again. They had the usual assortment of drinks and other snacks which you expect at a convenience store.

I guess I forget to hit submit at the bottom to get the reviews posted.  This is a great addition to the slim pickin's of restaurants in Land O' Lakes, Florida.   I felt like I was in a trendy place in Portland, Oregon instead of Land O' Lakes.  Food is fresh and somewhat innovative and we like going here.  Neighbors also like it when I take them here and have become repeat customers.  They have now opened a second location across from the outlet mall in Wesley Chapel.

Please do not go!  This is not healthy - look up the deaths associated with "The Sweat Lodge" or just use common sense.  Sitting in a room ~100 degrees Fahrenheit and trying to workout can cause heart arrhythmias and (more commonly) fainting.  Look up hot yoga and you'll see that many people faint in these classes.

Besides all this, although the owner was very nice, the staff person who helped us was incredibly unhelpful.  We were new and she all but harassed us!

Seriously, though, they do not ask if you have a heart condition or screen in any way - this is heat stroke inducing and NOT HEALTHY.

I had such high hopes for this restaurant. Ordered the catfish basket. It was cold and tasteless. I'm giving it 3 stars because the fries were good and and service was speedy. That's said, my food was cold so maybe that's why it was so speedy? I am going to give them another try but they are going to have to really exceed my wildest expectations.

It's a nice place to take the kids. To have a good time. Many games to choose from. Have a salad bar, pizza, wings, drinks and few other foods to choose from.. Look online for deals. They always got something going on... 

The only thing I dont like is you buy game card but you still get tickets when you win. Do away with the tickets and add them to the card. To make it easier. 

It does get packed on the weekends and when the kids are out if school for the summer.

We ordered takeout and everything was sooo good.

The truffle flatbread was a favorite for everyone! The vodka sauce was also delicious!  There was a generous portion of chicken in the chicken parm which was seasoned wonderfully...the piece was a bit thick for all the flavors to come through. 

The mushroom ravioli was also great.  The deserts were very rich! 

We will def be ordering from here again!

We checked this place out for the vegan challenge. I am plant based, I eat dark chocolate even if it's not vegan, but what's in a name, I like good food! It was dinner time when we arrived, it was windy and cold, the small parking lot in front had like 5 spaces, all taken. The courtyard was cute, I'm imagining coming again during the day when it's hot and cold margaritas flowing. So back to our experience, the chips, salsa and guac were good, we were hungry and devoured it. I didn't know the salsa was made with chicken broth, is that true? I ate it anyway and never think to ask if the salsa I eat out is vegan... My agua fresca tamarindo tasted like water with tamarind placed inside, she gave me  jamaica as a replacement. The potato floutas were crispy albeit bland, I enjoyed it with the salsa and extra cilantro that were heaped onto my street tacos. I ordered the soyrizo and tofu. Both were also just okay, the tofu one had a sweet sundried flavor but too much onion, no matter i just scraped it off. The soyrizo also was just okay, I make soyrizo scramble with cut tiny sweet potato, cilantro, red onion and kale, mmmm. I realize maybe they were new to the vegan challenge? Not sure, but with feedback and 'back to the old drawing board" tests, I think it'll be great. Service was nice, the waitress and maybe the owner? kept coming back to check in with us. My son had the fish tacos, but thought it was too breaded, he peeled off what he didn't like and ate it. We'd come back for lunch and a sunny patio experience. I hope they continue to develop their vegan flavors, especially those potato floutas!

Fun historic hole in the wall, just be ready to wait. Our wait was about an hour. The server was fun and candid in a pleasant way, and the food is good and drinks are boozy! Good place to check out if you have the time. Also, the bathroom is the creepiest I have ever seen but that makes it somewhat interesting.

My wife and I went and found it impossible to eat our meal together..1st the green beans and the rice came. Bartender asks" something wrong with the beans" No.. just waiting for the rest of our food 15 minutes later my wife's food comes so I ask the bartender where my food was and was promptly told "the food will NEVER come out together so 15 to 20 minutes later my food comes.

Ok hookah.. the place looks very beat up and old. The staff doesn't pay attention  and doesn't recommend much of anything for the flavors. They don't have food. It's. Ambiance isn't so great and too loud to hold a conversation.

While trying to help a friend with her phone (she wanted to mount and SD card.. little did I know her phone was not MEANT for an SD card.  I pulled out the SIM card, but had a problem putting it back in) I caused a small problem and we needed HELP.  We knew going to the Verizon store by the mall was not a great option, so I hit up Yelp for an alternative.
This GoWireless location is just a couple of blocks from my apartment, super convenient.  It's in the same 'stip mall' as Cloud Nine Brewery,  right near the Carl's Jr. on 17th & State.  
We went in expecting the usual hard sell that you see at Verizon, and perhaps a little 'oh what morons' attitude. but we were in for a treat.  Enter "Smiling Benny", a cheerful and helpful fellow who was most happy to listen to the tale of our debacle and help us out of our fix!   I had gotten the SIM card lodged in it's slot, but NOT in the little tray that is meant to hold it, and it was wedged in there deep.  It took Benny about 10 minutes using his smallest tools to get the card out (it was rather like playing the game Operation).  He then showed us the correct way to re-install it, and told my friend she'd been bushwacked at (insert name of big box store who SOLD her an SD card and told her oh yes it was definitely the right one for her Droid Maxx).  
Benny gave us a great first impression of GoWireless.  He even explained their partnership with Verizon. In essence, unless it's for international calling issues, GoWireless can help you with ANYTHING the Verizon store can.  
I will certainly be going back when I need help with anything related to my Verizon account.  Thank you Benny!

We subscribed for 1 year. When the paper was not delivered/late, we called and their automated service said we'd be "credited". 20 days before the end of the subscription, we renewed over the phone, using a credit card. But the delivery got so spotty, and they failed to suspend when we went on a short trip, that I called to cancel. The customer service call center sounds like the Philippines, so it was hard to understand the rep, who tried to hard-sell me into staying. I insisted, and she said "a refund check will arrive within 5-7 business days". Well, nothing arrived, and I called, and the rep said that my payment "failed" and was never processed. I checked my bank statement, and it cleared, and my bank rep and I both called the outsourced customer service line (that's all Idaho Statesman offers with billing issues!). The rep said that she could not send our bank any e-mail confirmation of the payment failure, but it was referred to the "financial department" which will take 7-10 business days. My bank rep and I got off the phone and we submitted a Visa dispute form. On top of this, the original e-mail I sent to customer service (at the request of the overseas rep) included a screenshot of my bank statement charge, and "Janet" (no last name) replied that "the payment failed" and that I in fact OWED  $7.91 since the subscription expires Jan 20!  If anything, Idaho Statesman owes ME that amount, since I had not received the paper since Jan 20, and my original prepaid subscription ran until Jan 27. So not only did they NOT credit my account for all those missing papers, but they are saying I OWE them money, when the reverse is true, and they are confusing my cancellation date (Jan 19) with the expiration date of my original subscription! 

The Idaho Statesman has made a serious mistake farming out their billing department like this; the overseas reps, though nice, do not have accurate information, they hard-sell renewals, and have no power or authority to resolve disputes, but refer them to some mysterious "financial department" we cannot contact directly. They confuse the cancellation date with the date of expiration, say you owe money when they owe you, and say they didn't receive a payment, when my bank says it cleared!
I even went on their website contact list, and they do not have anybody over there locally in charge of the billing department; you are told to contact their overseas call center. Terrible, esp. when they keep making mistakes.

Finally, one of the main reasons we cancelled was the lack of news value. They never fail to include a full 4 pages of sports, while lately, they are doing away with important "letters to the editor", and a disappearing Saturday paper.  I've written to them about their spotty inclusion of Carolyn Hax, and how online they don't even have her articles archived up to the month (with zero response).  Also, for some reason, they constantly print the divisive and inflammatory op-eds of Eugene Robinson and Leonard Pitts Jr., both living in very urban liberal areas in America, in an almost blatant propaganda attempt to undo the values which make Boise a nice place to live.  Also, huge puff pieces on the BSU president (and nothing about BSU researchers/professors) and the hagiography of education bureaucrats (over any critical analysis of how we can get more teachers instead of throwing money at them) is disappointing. I have written to a couple writers over there, with again, zero response. 

Stay away from this toxic paper. There are some good reporters, but it's not worth the billing hassle and the rest of the fluff and propaganda.

I called on a Friday afternoon for an appointment to get my septum pierced and they got me in within a couple hours. Process was fast, clean, comfortable, and well worth the money. The piercing cost $35 and my jewelry, a 16g silver captive ball hoop, cost $12. There was some swelling for the first couple days but no real complications, and it healed quickly. I'd definitely return for more piercings and I've already recommended Imperial to friends.

Hill Top was a solid well-done mini golf course. It was packed into a tiny space no bigger than a parking lot, but didn't feel too short at all. Many of the holes involved putting uphill, which was a great challenge. Lots of historic monuments and sites make an appearance here including a Sphinx, The Great Wall of China, and Mount Rushmore. I just can't help loving a course that includes monuments. There were also water features. Looks like it was renovated in the past year or two. Gameplay went along just fine on a weekend in August.

Creamwich (in the same plaza) was good for some post-game ice cream.

I'm not impressed with this practice. We just moved here and I have twins and they were due for their 9 month check up. My smaller twin has been having seizures since her 4 month vaccines so I wanted a place that would do delayed vaccines for her, but her twin sister is fine. Well, the appointment took almost 2 hours and the actual time seeing the dr was about 15 min. I just sat in a room with my twins for about an hour and a half by myself. Then they kept pushing me to give my younger twin the hep b vaccine and even though I said no she kept pushing it saying oh vaccines don't do that... you're a DOCTOR! You should know that can be a side effect, it even says so on the CDC website! I felt like I wasn't going to be allowed to leave until I gave it to her and after being there for 2 hours I finally said fine because it was one shot. Well, less than a week later we're in the hospital because my daughters seizures are off the charts. We've been here 5 days now. I will be finding a new pediatrician who listens to me and my concerns.

Almost the textbook definition of a mediocre upscale restaurant, and sadly the go-to "elegant" spot in Boise. 

The wine list was good, with a bunch of options in the $20-$40 range, the mac and cheese was fantastic, and the appetizer sampler was tasty, but the entrées were almost uniformly underwhelming, bordering on actually bad. My totally unimaginative pork chop was served on a bed of white beans that probably had another 10 minutes of boiling before they were done. In my world, undercooking the beans is a big problem for a restaurant of any kind, least of all one like Berryhill, where the entrées are upwards of $20. The tomato bisque was totally flat (not enough cream?), and the desserts were functional at best. At least they try to locally source their meat.

Add on top of everything else some live piano muzak (never a good idea) and iffy service, and there's not much to recommend -- call ahead at the Red Feather or Le Coq Rouge instead if you're looking for a nice meal.

I gave this place 3 stars only for service. My food was amazing!!!! But the service was awful! The young lady who was our server kept forgetting about us. Mind you the restaurant is pretty small.

About the food, we came here from a recommendation from a friend and the food did not disappoint.  I had the Sunrise Wrap...it was a  Pita with egg, ham, bacon chedder tomato spinach red/green peppers, garlic aioli with homefries and  tomato jam. I did not think I was going to enjoy it....but Baby!!! I topped it off with their Bhoti Chia drink. My tummy never felt so good!!!
The only reason I ever came here was for proximity. However I will not be returning any time soon as I found a registry that is way friendlier, quicker, and efficient.

"What is the difference between a pick pocket and a peeping Tom?"
"One snatches watches, and one watches snatches." 

As touristy, annoying, and overpriced as this place is, the guys who shuck oysters at the end of the bar are hilarious.

I managed to tolerate it better than a lot of other places on Decatur. 

The food was good and fresh. Smallish portions, but better than a lot of the food I found around here. They have a small selection of beers that are supposedly theirs (debatable) for around 7.25 each. Ouch. 

I'd go back though.

Every time we come here, we play my least favorite game: "Will they get my order right?" Unless you watched them pack your order, check it before you leave.

I ordered via the DD app one time. When I arrived, they had no idea what I was talking about. Not willing to even try that again. 

Pros: The restaurant is clean enough. They offer DD and BR products in the same place. If you go late in the day, sometimes they throw in bonus donuts.

Cons: Most employees treat customers like an inconvenience and are very hard to understand. Better (or at least faster) service is offered at the drive-thru than at the counter.

As close as it gets to a New York style Italian-American Trattoria. Almost gave a 5 star review but there were a couple little things I had to hold back one star because of. To start with the service was great, very polite and very attentive floor staff. I ordered the gnocchi alla sorrentina and my parents both ordered lasagna which they had the last time they went there and loved it. The gnocchi was wonderful, the texture was light and the sauce was flavorful although they could've held back on the amount of sauce they serve because my gnocchi's were swimming. As for my parents, the sauce used to finish the lasagna was a little bit on the salty side, not inedible by any means but for their sensitive palettes it was a bit too much. The servings were quite large and if you're overly ambitious and try to finish your plate you just might not have room for dessert. I know I might be nitpicking A little bit but to be honest at this price point it's about as good as this type of restaurant can get. I'm definitely coming back to try the Oso Buco because I've heard amazing things about it.hopefully next time I'll save room for dessert.

I went here with a group of friends late on a Saturday night and had a pretty good time. It kind of turned into a club with people dancing and having fun. We did not eat any food so I can't rate the place based on that. They had TVs everywhere and music videos playing. It was an overall good night out atmosphere.

PROS

-Nice atmosphere for a late night outing
-Cheap prices
-Clean bathrooms, especially for a Saturday night
-Nice staff
-No entrance fee

CONS

-Needs a bigger dancing area
-Seemed mainly 30 and over

We came by to try a new meat market. We got the BBQ bundle (chicken leg Quarters, pork chops, two steaks, ground beef). The meat has been ok. The store is ok, the gentleman who helped us was very difficult to understand. We will continue to look around for a butcher.

I've had a mailbox at the store for going on 20 years. They've out grown this space and it appears they have way to many customers and packages to handle everything. I can not recommend using this location.

Daughter's been taking Uber to the train station but will not be doing so from now on. Same story - never showed up, probably couldn't figure out how to get through the construction, didn't contact her, charged her $4 and she missed her train. Ad says "reliable pickups" ... NOT!

I did breakfast there today so review is based on that, I'm local and was looking to expand my breakfast place repertoire. 

Ordered the ribeye and eggs and a coffee. Steak and eggs is my go to, and figured the $13 version here would surpass the $10 version elsewhere, but I was wrong. I got a thin 5-6oz ribeye, which was only different from the sirloin by having a ring of fat around the outside, but on such a small steak it's pointless.  Normal kind of greasy home fries and toast I had to butter myself. 

Top it off with a $3 regular coffee ? Idk what that's about, but for $20 after tip I won't be eating breakfast here again.

We went in on a Friday night and were seated right away for a table for two.
We had beer, wine and appetizers.
The Tex Mex Spring Rolls and Ancho Bbq Ribs were perfect and delicious. 
The waitress was efficient and friendly. 
It isn't a restaurant for intimate conversation because of the enjoyable 80's music that was playing this night. 
My only complaint is, we were  seated near the front door and outdoor patio doors. Since the weather is cooling down we kept getting chilled from the opening doors. 
Next time we are in town we will be going again and will recommend Matador to our friends.

I have been here a million times how have I not reviewed the place. First off this place used to be amazing. I will say that we came on Thanksgiving and the place was packed. I mean filled to the brim w people. 

The salad bar is my favorite pet and there were many areas that were empty and stayed that way for quit some time. They had someone up there actually cutting kiwi which was awkward. The lox also sat empty for sometime as did the sweet potatoes. 

I of course thought it might have been due to staffing issues so I did not make a big deal. 

The meat situation however happened last time as well. They do not come around nearly as much as they used to. I feel there used to be a specific path they took but no more. We had to ask for our sides as well as the bread. 

My biggest pet peeve of the day was the rest room cleanliness. There were no paper towels, one stall was broken, the water in the sink was coming out at a trickle and there was no soap.

I was raised on two Natural History museums, the one in New York and the Santa Barbara location. I haven't been back to the NY one in 20 years but went back to Santa Barbara this weekend. Guess what it looks exactly the way I remember it 30 years ago when I was a kid. This museum is frozen in time 90% of the exhibits look exactly the way that I remember it. The planetarium show even seemed dimly familiar. They have created more outside spaces and have the butterfly exhibit which wasn't there in my time but that was about it. 

The museum as an adult seems even smaller then I remember it. You go through it very fast and even with a planetarium show, you should be done in two hours. They had a special travelling nanotechnology exhibit which really made the day. Without that it would have been a bit of a disappointment. Best for kids under 12, older then that and they'll be bored pretty quickly. I wonder where the fees go to for this place it must be maintenance and education.

I booked via 3rd party and the price dropped twice since the 1st time. When I arrived. the check-in process was incredibly easy... they found me and my reservation via the drivers license scanning on the kiosk. 

I got to pick any compact car on the lot, but there were only 2. A Versa and a Kia Soul. The mileage on each car was 26k and 18k respectively. I picked the 18k mileage Kia Soul. Neither had the smoking stench that I experienced with Enterprise. 

The return was equally as easy... in and out with a receipt... now if I only remember to sign up with their rewards system to earn some points

Healthy, Organic Lebanese fare in a clean, friendly establishment near home. Liked the Green Coffee and Hibiscus Tea options, really liked the Baba Ganoush, and being able to see some of the offerings thru the cooler window. They offer grass fed beef, and locally farmed chicken which is all great. The bad part for ,me is, I don't care about any of that. The Shwarma wrap was good but after blowing 20.00 on lunch for two, I wished I had hit up some nasty buffet and gorged my face hole instead. This place is probably too good for me, and we're breaking up now. It's not you, it's me, Fresh Bites. I can't tell the difference with unprocessed organically grown expensive foods, and I'm more about quantity than quality. Stay healthy my friend, I'll call you after my first bypass.

I like this movie theater the best out of all the ones in my area.  It has a great selection of movies, the theater and screen sizes are impressive, and the Regal Club card has rewarded me plenty.  The only complaint I have is the arctic temperatures they keep the theaters.  Don't forget to take the afghan or you'll be sorry!!!

I got a 30 minute massage and 30 minute foot massage and charged me 95.00. It was amazing but was not expecting to pay 95.00. I had a language barrier so they acted like they couldn't understand me. I wont go back to many other places out there.

Not the place to go if your hungry. Although the food was good. The slap of baby back ribs I got were one of the smallest I have ever seen. Also the sides were very small. The baked beans were in a small cup that was half full of liquid. I guess this is one of the reasons that at 12:30 on a Saturday afternoon no one was in the place.

I've never seen such a pale bagel in my life. 
First I asked for a jalapeño bagel but I received an everything bagel. 
Then the bagel tasted like a 3 week old bagel you buy at the grocery store. Luckily the pork roll was decent but it's hard to get that wrong. 
I'm new in the area and looking for a go to bagel place and I have to say this place did not make a good first impression

The burger meat reminded me of a bad grade school lunch. The roll and veggies were great, it was just the rubbery burger meat that left me disappointed. The burger did come with potato salad that was ok.
The deli was also hard to find as it's in the bottom of a corporate building, luckily they posted a sandwich board outside. The service was friendly and the were willing to bake fresh cookies if the patrons were willing to come back in 20 mins.

Clean shop, wonderful staff, and nice waiting area.  With appointment, oil change took over an hour, price was competitive.  When I arrived I requested they reset the diagnosis reminder on my onboard system, they said they would but of course did not.  With just a bit more attention to detail this could be a 5 star shop, but for me now it is just average.

It's 1:40 p.m. & I've just come back from PJ's. I went in for their delicious parfait which  they didn't have. I asked the cashier about when they would have them& she said every other day. She asked if I would like her to make them- that it would only take 5 minutes. No problem ! I waited & waited in between customers which was about 15 - 20 minutes. No problem! I continued to wait about 10 more minutes thinking she could finish the parfaits, heard her on the phone & then she went out on the patio. By this time my patience had run out so I left. As I was walking by the patio I saw her lighting a cigarette. ARE YOU KIDDING ME ??She just looked at me with a deadpan expression as I shook my head in complete disbelief. So whoever her manager is you might want to talk to her about her priorities & her customer service or lack of it. They just lost me as a customer.

I love dining in Midtown and this part of town has been lacking a restaurant with a solid reputation for tacos. Not anymore! NADA is amazing! Super cool vibe and decor inside. The service was top notch, the drinks were strong and delicious, and the food was fantastic! You can't go wrong with ordering any of their tacos, but the steak fajitas are a real treat! Beautiful presentation and delightfully different than your average fajita plate. Will definitely be going back soon to try more food and drinks on the menu.

For a taco truck, this is as good as it gets. I ordered 2(3 Tacos for $5), a burrito, 2 quesadilla and a coke for $30. The carne asada meat tastes more like ground beef however their marinade is exceptional. They make their tortilla fresh on the spot so it's quite authentic. Ate at the fold out table and  plastic chairs but after 3hr hike it's a nice pit stop for some food. I definitely recommend this place, especially if you're not looking for anything other than good Mexican food fast.

Maxine's Chicken and Waffles is solid and I look forward to trying again. Chicken and waffles is one of my favorite meals and a staple on many menus in my former home city- Memphis. But guess what, I didn't try Maxine's...you have to allow 45 minutes and I had to get back to work...there will be a next time I'm sure.
The service is attentive and nice. The boyfriend said the bathroom was really clean. The restaurant could use better lighting and a little updating.
The food was good. I had the catfish basket and the boyfriend had Neice's Pancakes which came with 3 pancakes, 2 eggs your way, and sausage (beef or chicken).
For a southern girl like me the no pork option could take a while to get used to. Who am I kidding...I will never get used to it so just hold the sausage on my order.
This place is A-OK.

Many things are relative. So is this place: there is a dim sum/Cantonese restaurant place next door, and these appear to be the "authentic two" in Northeast Philly on Bustleton Avenue. However, I highly prefer and recommend the place next door called Ruby Garden over this place.

Long wait on a weekend for dim sum - with inefficient service and low selection (comparing to next door). Questionable sanitary practices, especially in the summer when they leave the door open. They are more eager to seat you than serving you real food.

In the beginning when this place opened this was an iffy okay-ish place. But now - stay away for dim sum. Dinner time is somewhat okay-ish, but for the prices, taste and service - go next door or risk being frustrated. Why settle for "meh" when you can have okay to good next door? Gambling may be exciting, but not on food.

This place was amazing!  I had the smoked chicken, cornbread, macaroni and grit casserole.  I would've given it a 5 star rating, but I didn't care for the grits and didn't love the macaroni.  However, the margaritas were on point and that is the BEST cornbread I have EVER had.  Sooooooo good!

An hour + later. Look at you trying to make your business sound better. I was wondering where my food was 30 min from the time it said it was out for delivery. The poor customer service is telling me and grubhub I would be charged the full price to have the mozzarella sticks and a small white pizza delivered again.  You have a poor excuse for customer service.  Your business that hard up that you would charge $25 again for a meal that cost you less then $5.00.  Accept your idea of customer service deserves less than one star.
Great late night "elegant takeout" In town for surgery and this was a great find.  Absolutely amazing appetizers.. definitely a must try!

I was in there wanting to look at a new cellphone. 4 employees standing around and not one of them come up and ask if I needed help. I walked to another part of the store and walked back a few minutes later. They were still talking with each other and not interested and seeing if I needed help.

This is much, much, much better than others in the area. Not only is the service prompt, friendly, and overall fantastic but also the food is off the hook great! The ramen broth is full of flavor, and there is generous use of fresh veggies. The menu is quite diverse and there's something tasty for everyone.  This is not a fancy ambiance setting so casual dress is perfectly fine.  The location is a block from State Street and easy to find, so if you're downtown doing the tourist thing it is well worth the short walk for a super meal!

We came here for drinks and creme brulee, they have a sampler you can get.  Here is the problem, my girlfriend is allergic to bananas and one of the creme brulee dishes contained them.  We wanted to have one of them substituted and they would not do this.  I was pretty shocked at this and was surprised that they would not substitute one of the three for another of the sampler.  In fact, there solution was to just order the sampler and not get the banana dish.  SO, buy two for the price of three?  

This place was pretty loud as well, the acoustics are not that good.

This is my second visit to this place and it was ok. Seating is limited with more bar stool seating than an actual table. The pastrami for my dining companions stated they thought it was good with a generous amount of fries that were ordered separate as a combo. I ordered the philly cheesesteak with marinara sauce. I probably would have rated this better, but they forgot my order and didn't realize it until I went up the counter to ask the guy about it. No apology and not compensation was given. Food was good, but the experience was less than stellar. So, second time around the experience was not so good. Eat here at your own risk and pay attention because they might forget you

One of my favorite places to get wine and a snack! Great atmosphere and the staff is very friendly, plus accommodating! I recently had a meat and cheese board, which I really enjoyed. You can pick all your own options on the meat and cheese board. There are lots of different options of wine as well and the prices are reasonable. I do wish there were more options for small plates. I still need to try some of the other food options, but I do really enjoy coming here.
The bartenders who can't make basic drinks , and clearly have zero experience also steal your change because they know they've done nothing to earn a tip. Waaack.

Where is this place ? Apparently no longer on belcher road address on yelp 

Please update address

I really wanted to love this place. It's been on my list of places to try since they opened. Sadly, I could've continued to miss this opportunity. 
For starters, we made a reservation for Sunday, when we arrived there was a sign on the door stating they had a mechanical problem.  No call, just a sign. We called the next day to see if they were back up and running, the manager tried to argue that he called to notify everyone. I guess in an attempt to prove his case, he looked up the name, and realized he had not, in fact, called to notify us. 
Thinking it was just a fluke, we gave them another shot. We ordered crab beignets, wings, fries, a burger, and lobster risotto. I'm not sure the beignets actually had crab in them. The inside was runny, like a tuna salad with breading on the outside. Everything else was served lukewarm. We asked for a warm batch of fries, the second batch came out just cold on the top. The risotto was by far the most disappointing. I was expecting a creamy rice and chunks of lobster, what I got was more like crunchy rice in water with chunks of lobster. I took a couple bites and left the rest. With the growing popularity of seafood restaurants, there's a competitor within one mile in any direction with better execution.

The absolute WORST service! If you'd like to be able to use the wifi that you pay monthly for, then this most definitely isn't the service provider for you. AND, on top of not being able to use my wifi, anytime I call customer service (weekly, sometimes daily, basis) I am ALWAYS somehow "disconnected". Really meaning their way of dealing with issues is to hang up on their customers.

For the price of the rooms and food, it's a luxury casino. I would have given more stars, except that their room (particularly the room I stayed in) is infested with bed bugs. I do not recommend staying more than a night there.
Highest quality meat on a burger you will find. Not as inventive with the toppings as some other upscale burger places, but the quality of the burger itself carries it.

I went here on Valentine's Day with my date and they took us in regardless of no reservation, and it was a busy day for them. Place is very nice, perfect for a special occasion since the ambience is not completely causal, just classy but not extremely formal. The options are mostly Japanese and a small section of Thai foods. The food was amazing! I ordered 2 rolls and a Japanese steak. Our waiter was very patient and super nice! There is also a small bar to sit at. The place is relatively small so parties larger than 6 would be overwhelming. I would definitely come here again!!! A+ service!

I love Terra. Although it is a bit pricey, the food is excellent. Their Jerk Chicken Wrap special on Wednesdays and Chicken Kabob special on Thursdays keep us coming back for more! It's pretty small, so get there before the lunchtime rush hour to get a seat!

Nick's is a block down from my house and I'm very glad about that.  It's pretty simple really--meat cooked on a charcoal grill.  This alone makes it different from most places around.  Their wings and ribs are fantastic, I usually just get them.  I've tried a few sandwiches and have been pleased.  But if it's your first time, just get some wings and the ribs.  It's worth it.

They do not comply with the rules of the health department, they prepare the hamburgers with the painted ones, without gloves, they sweep and continue cooking without washing their hands, we talk to the manager and he does not care, Monday at 10:40 of the
night.

Not sure why this place has such little stars! 
We stumbled across this place for lunch after our original lunch place was closed for new years. I'm glad we decided to try this place! 
The pizza place is kinda like Mod Pizza or chipotle where you choose your own ingredients for the pizza and then it gets fired up with their 1000 degree oven. For my first time I decided to go with one of their signature pizzas - sausage and peppers (Roman style - oval shaped pizza with a thicker crust). The pizza came with mariana sauce, italian sausage, peppers and onions. My friend went with the white pizza which is a classic white pizza (Neapolitan style - regular round, thin crust pizza) with garlic sauce, mushrooms and she added spinach + half balsamic glaze. 
It took about 15 mins for them to fire both our pizzas up and they brought it over to our table.
For a Saturday afternoon there wasn't many people in the restaurant and we felt safe dining in. Both pizzas came out super hot and were both so delicious! Fresh tasting ingredients with a crunchy flaky crust what more can you want? I love how you also just pay for the size of your pizza ($10.95 for each style) and you can load up as many ingredients for no extra charge (including meats!) 
Would def. Come back next time and design my own pizza!

Such good food; too bad the service sucked.  Hash & eggs were delicious.  Greeted, coffee and order taken quick enough, but nothing after the order arrived.  How can my coffee be "bottomless" if no one ever refilled it?  Never got the hot sauce I asked for.  Check was delivered without asking if I wanted anything else.  I live in Soulard.  I love Soulard.  I chose to support SCG instead of Uncle Bill's.  Guess I know where I'm going next time...
Wonderful people that make you feel at home! Props to Andy who hooked it up on the grill and made my tastebuds weep tears of joy!

When I ran into this program  online it sparked some curiosity and i decided to  explore what it had to offer. I applied and didn't expect  to hear back.. When I did I decided to go forward with the challenge and prepared myself mentally as much as I could.  This approach  is new and refreshing.  The trainers are attentive to your needs and will test all your boundaries  like nothing else had.  It is exactly what I have needed all these years. The extra motivation  was there from the beginning.  I am so grateful  for this experience.  I have  a different  outlook on life and fell like a new woman overall.  Thank you Psychosomatic  for all you guys do. Leti Spellmeyer

Took an hour to get entree. Had the crab claws as an appetizer. Not only were there very few crab claws, some has no meat. Good flavor to the disg. Ordered shrimp and grits. Good flavor. Did not eat shrimp and grits because shrimp were not deveined. Husband had blackened shrimp jambalaya.  Shrimp were not deveined. The dish was "ok". Child had chicken tenders. They were overcooked. Manager offered to remake something, but we didn't have the time. She did take the entrees off of our order. Hurricanes were homemade and delicious. I'll ask from now on whether shrimp are deveined.

Fast take out, had caprese sandwich which was just ok. Done a long roll, lettuce, tomato, mozz, olive oil and olives. Had better to be honest. Garlic knots tasted pretty good, crunchy on top soft in the middle. Calamari was a bit chewy, not the best in the area, at least a little crispy. Friendly service from take out.

The  place is nice,  amotsphere is nice. The coke machine is cute,  it's the one that's computerized with a ton of different flavors.  

You definitely get for what you pay.  $6 for a burger and you get a small but that has the name branded into the bun. The patties are dry and they're small. I got the burgefi burger and it came with like ice berg lettuce and a tomato slice that is bigger than one of my patties. You think it has a thick patty but it's really just two thin dry patties stacked on top of the other. 

The fries are average.  I think the best thing I had were the onion rings which are HUGE. You get about five but I don't know how they get the onion rings cut that big. They probably cut them like into five slices. They were good.

It's been a while since I've been here.  I've been here a couple times and I just don't get it.  Maybe its the whole vegan vibe they have or the earthy clientele, but when I think of going out to dinner this is the last place i consider.  The first time I went here I had salmon, and it was barely seasoned.  The server was outside smoking the whole time and we had terrible service.  The last time was for dinner after the 4th ave street fair.  A group of us went there, because my friend knows the owner.  I ordered the chicken and it came out raw.  that's right, chock full of salmonella.  I tossed it, finished my salad and decided it was best to get out without getting sick.

Slooooooooowwwwww

Pros: food is decent, good price nice portion 

Cons: unreal slow service, messed up the order, gave us a dirty coffee cup which took 15 minutes to replace. Asked us three times if this was on the same check. Took 10 minutes to get the check.

tldr: I feel that the $150 charge to come again is a scam and I was pressured to make a decision even though pricing and designer information was not transparent. If pricing and designer info was more upfront then I probably would've bought the dress from here. Instead I bought it elsewhere.

I'll start with the positive: Marie Gabriel has a very extensive collection of designers and collections that they carry in store. They do carry several designers that are not available at other Indianapolis bridal boutiques. I will say Kate did pretty well in selecting dresses for me to try on.

The rest:
- The $150 charge to come again:
It's not normal for a bridal boutique to charge $150 for another visit to the store to try on any dresses you are interested in. I visited a boutique in Carmel and a boutique just west on 82nd street twice without a similar charge and they let me know I can come in as frequently as I would like to try on the dress. My sister bought her dress at the Pronovias designer store in NYC and they let her come in and try on the dress several times before saying "yes". They understood that some brides need time to make a big decision and it can't be done within a 90 minutes appointment.

- Pressured to make a decision:
At the end of my appointment when I had my favorite dress, I really felt pressured to say "yes" by Kate. I had two of my friends/bridesmaids visiting from out of state and we had several other appointments to go to while they were here. I was told "finding the dress is not about thinking but about a feeling" and that I "wouldn't be able to remember this feeling later on". We mentioned that my mom wasn't able to come with me so I would like to show her the pictures later. Kate suggested that I facetime my mom right there in order to make the decision. I didn't appreciate this pressure.

- Misled on the designer:
When I tried on my favorite dress, I was told that it was a Pronovias dress. My sister was facetiming in and asked for the dress name so she could see it in my detail since she was not in person. Kate withheld that information and said she had that information on my file when I decided to come back. We could not find this dress ANYWHERE on the Pronovias website. I called a few days later and the person who answered told me that it's a St. Patrick dress but she didn't know what other dress information she could tell me to find it online since this dress is "exclusive" to their store. (which is not true... and actually really absurd to make your bride feel that this boutique is SO exclusive that I can't find it anywhere else). I acknowledge that St. Patrick is part of the Pronovias group, but it is actually wrong to call this a Pronovias dress. You wouldn't call a Vera Wang Bride dress a Pronovias dress even though it is under the Pronovias Group. (Actually, I looked on the Marie Gabriel website and the dress names are actually labeled when you hover the image so not sure why I was told otherwise.)

- Not upfront about pricing:
I asked how much the dress was and I was told that with tax it would be around $2200-2300. Due to my naivete I did not press. After I went to several other appointments, other boutiques were very forthcoming with the price to the CENT. When Kate followed up a few weeks later over the phone I asked how much the dress was and she responded the same and that they don't disclose exact pricing until I decide to purchase with them. This makes zero sense to me. It wasn't until I complained a bit that I was told the exact pricing before tax. After I calculated tax on it, it was actually over $2300.

- Miscellaneous:
For a bit, we were the only customers in the store so Young was around quite a bit. However, I found that her customer interaction skills could use improvement as she noted I was "too skinny" for certain styles/dresses and this didn't make me feel great. I found Kate's customer interaction skills on this side to be much more engaging without making me feel bad about my body like Young did.

Judge for yourself since everyone has different levels of tolerance but it seemed like there was more noise transfer from the upstairs apartment than I've experienced elsewhere.  I was surprised by the number of dogs in the building and the owners that didn't pick up after their dogs.  The first time I received a package I was surprised I'd have to pay 20 to get access to package concierge lockers where my package was left. The fire alarms seemed to go off every few months usually in the evening.  Maybe all apartment buildings this large have these issues?  It was an ok apartment, but I wish I had picked a different apartment complex.  
When I moved out they charged me with a pet treatment fee on the carpet.....only I don't have a pet nor was there ever a pet in my apartment while I lived there.  When I explained this to them they didn't agree to remove the charge.  This didn't make sense to me...
Not the best facility in the world, but great for location. Balls are OK, tee boxes are fine, drinks and snacks as well. Wish the Mini-Golf was still open, was one of the nicer ones around.

I've stayed in many JW's before and this was somewhat subpar; except for the executive lounge.  My furniture were old and scuffed up, not to the standard I accustomed for a JW.  This JW seems very dated.  I stayed there for a week and only asked for maid service once and she didn't even bother to clean my bathroom floor which was dirty.  I ran out of towels and it took 2 hours to get some.  They still charge for internet (I don't have to pay cause I'm platinum but I thought you should know).  Also, the parking is about $35 a night....not worth it if you ask me.  I'm surprised that a Marriott JW is in such bad shape.....

Someone brought a cellophane baggie of Blue Frog's Cajun Chocolate Bites to work.

After the first bite, I was hooked.

The chocolate bites are larger than an M&M and more rounded than a Smarties.

These bites are spicy on impact, but don't leave you watery-eyed. The spiciness helps bring out the amazing flavor of the dark chocolate. These aren't the type of chocolate that only burns as it goes down the throat, making you cough. These are smooth and delicious.

Palmface moment: I can't believe I was in NOLA over new year's and didn't know 1) Blue Frog was there, or that 2) it had been selected as best chocolate shop in NOLA for several years running.

AWFUL service. We order the crab cake pasta and the roast debris both were terrible. We sent both back they still charged us for the crab cakes. The roast debris tasted like dog food. The manger was extremely rude and Disrespectful!!!

I've always been afraid of the dentist and have avoided them as much as possible but It was finally time for me to face my fears and go. I came to See Me Smile for the first time and was greeted by really friendly people. Mona, the dental assistant, was incredibly friendly. She kept me distracted and made the time go by fast. Dr. Doane was amazing and took the time to explain what was going on and giving me good advice on how to keep my teeth clean and healthy. He's gentle, nice, and really cares about his patients. Overall I would definitely recommend See me Smile to anyone who hasn't already gone.

If I coud give less stars I would. 

I decided to give a local company some business for something I could buy online. They told me I could only by the straps for a CPAP with the mask, and that I would need a prescription.  I verified with my doctor and she told me I could get them legally without a prescription. 

I found the straps and the mask sold separately in several locations.

That wouldn't bother me so much if the sales clerk wasn't so rude. 


If you need medical supplies, don't waste your time there. You can get them delivered in a few days with Amazon without the negative attitude. 

I thought all the Google reviews on this shop were low ( a few stars). Now I understand why.
Emma, the owner, is a DOLL and she has done a great job with this place.  Classy and delicious :)

$$ -- We tried Peno, the new neighborhood restaurant for lunch today.  The service was great.  It seems to be a family owned restaurant and everyone was super friendly.  I ordered a lunch special of salad and pasta.  My husband and son both ordered pizzas.  The salad was really nice with a lovely lemon vinaigrette dressing.  It came with cheese sesame seed bread which was very tasty.  The pasta was superb--corkscrew style noodles with an excellent meaty and flavorful sauce.  I tried a piece of both pizzas.  Each pizza was very different in sauces, cheeses and spices--both unique and equally delicious.  After our meal, we ordered espressos which came with small lemon rinds in the espressos!  This added so much flavor to the espressos--it was a really nice touch.  My only complaint about this place is that the decor is a bit drab.  The caliber of the food exceeded the caliber of the decor.  Maybe tablecloths, table centerpieces, and lighting could give the restaurant some mood.  

The dinner menu looked great, so I'm sure we will be back for dinner soon.  I'm hoping this place is here to stay.  Seemed like a great crew and great food.

I feel like I only write reviews for places I love, but I love Wildflower! LOVE Wildflower. As a local, everyone I know goes here and it's a hidden gem. This is the place you bring your grandmother or your brother. I'm not sure how kid friendly it is because it's not a super loud restaurant and it's not that big. 

The wait staff are so sweet, all the women there are very helpful and usually a little overwhelmed on the weekends because it gets so packed! I usually order the waffles, the eggs benedict or french toast. I just had the french toast and eggs benedict (i was hungry) last weekend and I forgot how decadent the french toast is! It's really enough for one person and just make sure you have time for a nap after! 

The food here is rich and they have great portions. I suggest grabbing a loaf of Museli Bread before you go and they have yummy looking cupcakes (but I'm usually here for breakfast). Apparently you can order their cupcakes ahead of time for parties. 

A few tips, you can call ahead to get a table, or at least I did once. Usually the wait is about 30-45 minutes at peak hours. Order tea. I prefer the peach tea with out as much sweetener. The bike trail is across the street, a great place to stop!

Reno has needed something like this for a while and they really nailed it. It's a pretty big space so there is a lot of room to sit and hang out and there is comic book and general nerd culture decor everywhere. They did a fantastic job decorating and the atmosphere is fun, welcoming, and feels like a great spot to hang out in. All of the staff was very kind and happy to be there which is always a plus. The menu is surprisingly big and has some unique things you can't find anywhere else like Green Tea Chai latte (which is delicious!) But they still have all the regulars you'd expect. bagels, donuts, and pastries in the display case by the counter if you wanted a snack, several iced teas, all the coffee you could ever want. Fantastic spot that I can't wait to go back to.

I have mixed feelings about this place.  The food was absolutely excellent.  Fantastic, creative selections that were well prepared and presented.  We had the fried soft shell crab appetizer, duck and 2 different salmon dishes.  The portions were large compared to other restaurants serving high end food.  The quality was fabulous.  However, the service was not up to par.  When I arrived there was no one at the hostess desk resulting in a line of people literally out the door.  All who appeared to have reservations.   The restaurant was packed and loud.   We were seated in their enclosed patio that was quieter and completely lacking in atmosphere.   Our waiter while pleasant seemed more suited to work at Applebees then fine dining. The staff acted as though they worked at a small bar and grill.  They restaurant offers a multitude of specials that are written on a giant chalk board as you come in and rattled off by the waiter.  All items are complex and detailed making it difficult to retain.  I don't understand why they don't just print out a sheet for each table.  We had to ask for several to be explained again to which our waitress responded she could not recall the details of one.  Of course she can't.   To sum up, go for the food but doing expect much of the dining experience.

When places have over 400 reviews, I don't tend to add mine, cuz, why bother?  BUT, making an exception here for wonderful weird little Khyber Pass - don't pass it by when you're strolling in Old City.  Yes, it's dark, yes, there are lots of locals - BUT, step inside and you'll find some truly great local beers and some mighty tasty food.  Founders Rubaeus, yum!  The happy hour brings good value, including on the night I went, some very very delicious hush puppies with sauces for dipping.  The bar area is good for drinks and appetizers, but sit in the restaurant for more quiet and a real meal.  Will go again...

Thought overall food & beer experience was a solid 3 stars but the incompetence at the hostess stand brought City Works down to a 2.  

Brief Summary:
-If you go on a weekend night, consider making a reservation
-Check in with hostess if you are waiting more than 1 hr. We never received a text that our table was ready
-Be prepared to flag down bartenders if seated at bar, they will not automatically come by to check on you
-Enjoy the food it's tasty, but if you get the Thai Chicken wrap you may end up with soggy fries

Complete Review:
The free burger coupon we received in the mail helped make City Works the destination for a Saturday Date Night. My wife & I arrived around 5:15pm-ish to find a packed house. I echo another reviewers comments about the "thunderous noise" upon entry & the fact  that you stand at the hostess stand for a good 2 minutes before anyone recognizes your presence. After giving our name and phone number we were told we would receive a text when our table was ready. Well, at the time of writing this review (Sunday, February 5th,10:47am EST) we are still waiting for that text 17.5 hrs later. 

Fortunately we were able to score a lone seat at the bar so we had access to the bartenders who were slammed and did their best at keeping up.  However, an empty glass in front of you was not necessarily an international sign for the bartenders to check in on you to see if you would like another drink here. You really needed to bring your flagging down bartender "A game" here on a Saturday evening.  Which I get.  Just be warned.  

After waiting over an hour for the text that never came, we decided we weren't getting a table & made our home at the bar. Fortunately another large group at the bar had gotten their text that their table was ready & opened up an additional spot for me.

Setting up shop at the bar was probably the best thing that could happen to us. Our food order took approximately 13 minutes (we timed it) to come out, which despite how packed the place was I thought that was impressive.  My wife said her burger was fantastic & my Thai Chicken wrap was good except, the wraps were a tad on the wet side and leaked all over my plate resulting in soggy some soggy fries.  The non soggy fries were definitely a homerun. We highly recommend the FRIED PICKLE appetizer, the breading was rediculously good.  

As far as value, food prices are decent however, fries portion was a tad small in my opinion. Beer prices were a bit steep, paid $12 for a La Trappe Quaddrupel which may have been worth it just to get it on draft.  We lived in the Netherlands & this was by far my favorite of the Trappists. 

Overall place was decent, we have two more coupons so we may try City Works out again, but we'll make a reservation. When we notified the hostess stand that we never got a text about a table they said our name had never been put in the system.

Debated between a 3 and a 4 but ended up with a 3 because my whoopie pie was just sooooooo underwhelming. I felt like it wasn't worth the 3 bucks. On the other hand, my husband absolutely loved his coconut almond crunch cookie so maybe it depends on what you get? There's probably better places to get baked goods downtown but if you're already in Reading Market (esp. on Sundays when many places are closed) they have good service and the prices are reasonable.

Hype fail. This place has great Yelp reviews but I left here dissapointed. 

Crispy pig ears - overcooked to the point they were hard. The waitress said they were supposed to be crispy. Hard is not the same.

Chicken liver pate - supposed to be rich, was watered down to the point it lost its appeal.

Pizzas - mediocre

Wheres all the hype coming from? Come on Nashville, dissapointed

Yeah this place was pretty much  a BIG BIG BIG waste of money. I was supposedly a perfect candidate for this procedure: Light skin with very dark hair. I am of Greek descent so naturally I have a bunch of hair. 

I had a bunch of treatments done with no results. I had many more than 6 treatments in each area I had done. I cannot stress how useless this procedure was. Yes the hair fell out after treatments BUT it all CAME BACK. All you have to do is wait and you will see the results are not permanent. They used the GentleLASE Plus - Alexanderite Laser By Candela.

So I tried other methods...electrolysis: Flash, Blend, and Galvanic.
