The best method was Galvanic Electrolysis ....the oldest method of permanent hair removal. I used one of those home units (the ones with the probe/needle that delivers galvanic electrolysis) and saved myself a TON of $$$$$. DO YOURSELF A FAVOR and do it yourself or find an experienced knowledgeable electrologist. If you do it yourself research prior to jumping in.

Getting laser performed is basically paying someone a bunch of money to shave your body/face for you... if you like that sort of thing laser may be for you : p

Came here on.a Friday night with a few girlfriends for dinner, drinks and to listen to acoustic singer.

We were seated and ordered drinks. It took more than 35 minutes to get two beers and two vodka and club. The waitress was just so overwhelmed as they clearly didn't have enough help. The waitress was especially rude to us which made our visit less enjoyable.

We ordered our dinners which took one hour until they arrived. I ordered the Lump Crab Meat Salad with asparagus, tomatoes for $19.00. What came out was a big bowl of Spring mix, 3 asparagus spears, grape tomatoes and about one tablespoon of crabmeat. Honestly if i would have known they were going to give me so little, i wouldve just ordered the house salad.

My girlfriend also had to send her salad with salmon since she requested dressing on the side and it was on her salad. They made her a new salad but brought back the same salmon with dressing on it and it was cold by then.

This looks like a decent place to sit at bar and drink.

Do not come to this center. It is a scam / potentially involved in fraudulent billing practices. 

The business is not in operation to help patients but to maximize billing and income. Seek treatment elsewhere if possible.

I loved this place, it's a shame I didn't have the best experience. The woman behind the counter took my order and was so sweet, but the skinny, tall brunette with curly hair had was rude when giving me my bagel. I was on the phone (which I understand isn't the most polite thing to do, but was on the phone with my sick sister whom lives in Mass) when I didn't hear her call out my order and she had to repeat it a second time l, I apologized and thanked her, but she rolled her eyes and walked away. If you can't be kind to people then you shouldn't be working in the customer service industry.

My family chose to go here to celebrate my Dad's 60th birthday. We had not eaten at Ruth's Chris in over 5 years, and we were excited to give the new location a try. Overall, the experience wasn't very memorable. I will start off by saying the service was excellent, and our waiter Sean was at our table practically the moment we sat down full of suggestions and very attentive throughout the meal. Our cocktails were great...I ordered a Moscow Mule and it came in the proper bronze mug which was cute! Really the only issue is the food quality...the steaks were both not cooked to the proper degree that both my mom and sister had requested. This resulted in my mom and sister both switching their steaks from each other's plates twice because the steak seemed to be cooked differently on each side. I ordered the Crab Cakes and, while they were tasty and had delicious lumpy crab meat, it seemed they were sitting in a pool of butter which was somewhat unappetizing. The meal had a pleasant ending with delicious dessert and great conversation while finishing a nice bottle of Plumpjack. If this new location of Ruth's Chris is able to step their game up and perfect their cooking methods and dish presentation, I do believe they will be up to par with the status the restaurant had in Indianapolis a few years ago.
Great place for milk and eggs.  Halo has been here forever.

I have been to this restaurant at least 6 times since it has opened, mostly for brunch.  I feel like my experiences keep getting worse and worse.  It is near impossible to get a second cup of coffee, and the service is slow.  I don't mind the food taking a while to come out, I understand it is fresh, but getting your check and then getting someone to take your money takes near forever.  

One of the last times I went, I was on my own for lunch on a Friday and ordered a skillet.  It was not busy at all, still took forever to take my order and get my coffee.  I was sitting at the counter and the chef gave me my food, asked me if I needed anything, and had to tell the waitress I was done because she had not come over since taking my order.

My last experience, I had a cup of coffee that had leftover food stuck to the side of it, and when I said something just had my cup replaced with out any sort of apology.  Their coffee cups never look super cleaned out, but to have leftover food stuck to the side was just the last straw.

I am very disappointed because I really enjoy the food there, and it is super close to my house, but I just can't stomach the thought of going back anymore.

Horrible service ordered endless app recieved only 2 refills took over an hr to get my entree the manager had every excuse in the book we ended up getting the app rmoved from the bill and was told we were ungrateful bevause the kitchen was doing the best they can. 2 total hours there and only 2 refills on endless app if thats their best they need to find new jobs

I was not super impressed by this place. It's pretty expensive for one. The food was made with fresh, basic, few ingredients. The Mexican was very watered down California-vegan-hippie Mexican, which was fine but didn't taste like Mexican food. If my hypothetical friend Suzie who, say, has white girl "dreads" went to Whole Foods and made her son Apple dinner, this might be what she'd come up with. I was a little taken aback by the Buddha statue decor. I guess I'm not used to Santa Barbara yet. 
My partner got overcooked fish tacos with disintegrating tortillas, but he very much enjoyed his smoothie (my milkshake was par for the course).

La Calaca Feliz (Navidad!) That's what it felt like - a Mexican Christmas fiesta! The only complaint here is that the wait was a bit long, but it was a weekend night and I didn't even make a rezzy! What should I expect?! La Calaca has a great bar right when you walk in, and they were serving up primo margaritas (of which Stevie obviously consumed a few)! The dining area is past the bar in a back room, which makes for a more a modern and intimate dinner experience. Definitely a casual and original spot that you should be sure to checkout if you're in Fairmount!

We were apprehensive staying here due to reviews and unbelievable prices. Checked in and kadeem at the front desk was amazing. We had a couple of small requests and he met all of them. This hotel should be happy to have him.

I really was not going to write any review of them either way, since there were a couple of missteps such as an unreturned call and a lack of info that was promised me -- but i didnt think that was enough for a negative review. Oh, and a saleswoman who was totally clueless, or lied, about a warranty. Still not enough for a bad review, relative to the other trash that's out there. Then i phoned last week to go pick up my back pillow, which i had left there during a test drive and they had been holding for me. Mr. Internet Dept. ("Ryan"?) aka, the person at every car dealership who is assigned the job of lying to you on the phone before you walk in the door -- doesnt want to tell me if they still have the pillow, since I am finally in the area and want to stop by. When i ask him to inquire about it, he finally puts me on hold for about 2 seconds, then comes back and says that no one knows anything about it and he cant find it anywhere. He then tells me that the salesperson i dealt with went home for the night, hours ago, and i he does not want me to come by to try to get my pillow (which is no longer sold in stores). I go there anyway, and not only is my salesperson right there, in this very small showroom/dealership, but the pillow is immediately behind the front desk, and everyone at the desk knew it was there for weeks. One of the "suits" shuts down the receptionist who is trying to explain what happened, and instead invents some garbage story about how Mr Internet, who had been sitting right at the front desk, could not possibly have known my salesperson was still there.Then he tries telling me he wants to help me -- but he will not talk to Mr Internet in front of me. Of course not, because apparently if you're not coming in to buy a car that night -- lies about warranties included -- they train their staff to let you know that you are not welcome in their store. WHAT KIND OF DEALERSHIP NEEDS TO LIE ABOUT A PILLOW??

Cute coffee shop we stopped in while checking out magazine street. I asked for heavy cream and was told yea they have half and half. Kind of bummed to not have heavy cream or sugar free flavored syrups. Coffee wasn't terrible though. The barista was knowledgeable and gave me history of chickory.

While Chandlers is known for their steaks, which was delicious, albeit a touch small, what was really the highlight of the meal was the goat cheese cheesecake. I was pretty on the edge about ordering this because goat cheese is usually pretty savory (think Mexican restaurant queso), but this was really the perfect balance of sweet and savory. Top that off with the creamiest cheesecake you can imagine, and you have the best dessert you could ever imagine. I came for the steak, but I'll keep coming back for the dessert.

This morning, in the drive-thru, about 8:50, the twit had the balls to call me rude, and throw attitude because she couldn't seem to do HER job properly. 

I ordered, and I quote, "A venti AND a grande, white chocolate mocha." She said, "Ok, that's a grande w.c.m. venti cup, see you up here." I guess I didn't pay attention, as I thought she was competent to do her job, but my husband asked of she got both or if she really said something along the lines of a smaller size coffee, in a bigger sized cup. I didn't know but I would ask when I got up to the window. Lol well, when we got there, I asked her if she got both and she said just the one. I told her it was two coffees, different sizes.

She looked at me and said with a shit attitude, "Oh, we only heard the one, it's not our fault", almost like I didn't speak english when I ordered and how dare I. There was even another twit hiding behind the window, saying how rude it was, when we were driving away. What a little puss you are girl 

Lol some advice for you assholes, it's not rude to fix MY order, you messed up. Maybe you didn't like the fact that I had that look of "WTF" on my face, as I didn't think your job was hard, but I'm the one paying for them, so I have the RIGHT to get what I wanted, to make a correction if something is wrong and shouldn't have to deal with shit heads like you. 

Maybe try instead, "Oh, I'm sorry,  I will fix that, I didn't hear you correctly", with a smile on your face because me, the customer, didn't make the mistake. If this was a local, smaller business, I don't think the owners or managers would think your customer service was up to par or approve, AT ALL. Grow up if you're going to be working with customers. You make the entire place look bad, not just yourselves!

This place was recommended by many people, but it was bad. I'm sure the food is great when it's fresh, but all four of ours came out cold, the eggs which were supposed to over easy turned hard, and our waitress didn't seem to care. Overall this place has promise, but not with that terrible service.

I was not at all impressed by the food. Don't get me wrong, the place is nice! There's a nice sized seating area that's separated from the bar by a glass wall. There's a plethora of TVs and the beer list was good. But the food was so bland!
The kids Mac and cheese was below average. It came with a side of tots that was huge and actually kinda tasty. The Barnyard Burger lacked flavor. I am not the type to add additional salt and pepper but, boy, did the burger need it! My egg on the burger was over cooked with a hard yolk, not over easy! (Probably the worst restaurant burger I've had.) My husband's chicken sandwich was mediocre and the fries were just ok. Everything we ordered was room temperature...I'm not sure why because beside our table, there was only two other occupied tables. Not sure when we will be back in the downtown Indy area, but we will not be returning here for food.

Pretty nice place but WOW is it expensive.  What I paid $30 for in SoCal, a checkup and a cleanup, costs $180 here.  This is my fault as I should have asked up front what the costs were, but this is clearly a case of price gouging for insurance companies.  If you don't have dental insurance, this place is totally not for you.

This used to be our favorite place to get chicken, although it has been a few years since I was a Nashville resident. While my extended family does not eat spicy food I love it so had to get some extra ghost pepper dipping sauce. The quality is still very good but the service was a bit off for a catering order we had placed well in advance.

This is so many times I pick pieces of meat out of my vegetarian order, but this time not one or two missed pieces.. a handful of bacons over my vegetarian pasta :( I put the whole dish out for raccoons. See the pic and judge yourself. No, not accidentally marked bacons while ordering; and yes, I did tip well. Very disappointed in the service. Two stars because the delivery drivers work hard do the job properly.

I have to say that I am a Boston Pizza connoisseur, call it tacky or whatever, I love BPs. 

I don't get the opportunity to go to this BPs very often but when I do it is on Sunday evenings. It's pretty much completely dead, and its basically like sitting out on your own patio with waiter service. The patio is nice and warm and the food is that homey BPs food that we all love.

This location is definitely one of my favourites. I've been feeling lost since I had to change BPs locations from the west side. It's nice to switch it up. 

If you can't tell I love BPs haha.

VEGAN REVIEW: We came down for Parents weekend at UT and this place was a request of non-vegan family members. Full disclosure we did review the menu beforehand and saw there was only 2 possible items that could have been veganized: Hummus appetizer and a veggie pizza. 
Great ambiance with that Caribbean outback vibe. Pulling up... there is a long valet line and long waits for tables. If you want to skip the restaurant, the "beach" is first come first serve. More about that later. 
We opted for outdoor seating on the water. Pretty views and the weather was perfect for outdoor dining. Our waiter was friendly and accommodating. Fun drink menu with zany names and concoctions. We ordered the hummus plate as an appetizer without the tsaztsiki.  It was actually the best hummus plate I've had aside from Greek restaurants...came with olives, marinated cherry tomatoes, banana peppers, pita and cucumbers. We asked our waiter if the chef could prepare something special for us considering they had pasta and veggies. He made a linguine with brushetta, olive oil and mushrooms. I requested a side of the mango salsa and mixed it in there. It was decent for a meal but I wonder why they couldn't have a black bean burger on their menu. I find this so often and news flash: veganism isn't a new fad...it's been around for 70+ years as an American lifestyle and diet and much longer in other cultures. I understand a local whiskey seafood shanty isn't going to have garlic roasted tofu with a balsamic reduction over steamed quinoa but is a black bean burger with avocado and mango salsa too much to ask for? 
And the beach...we decided to go check it out. Definitely a fun place to hang. Firepits, tiki torches, live music, cocktails, sand...what's not to love? Good times and definitely a place I'd love to return to as soon as they get a couple more vegan options.

I would like to start by saying that the servers are wonderful and friendly and the brewery has a lot of potential.  My husband and I went here on a Saturday afternoon and the brewery was technically not supposed to be open yet.  The reason I am giving 3 stars is for 2 reasons.  It was incredibly hot so that my husband and I had to leave earlier than we wanted because we were basically sweating.  Air conditioning or a big fan would do a lot of good.  The second reason is that I am pregnant, wish I could be drinking beer (but obviously cannot) and they did not have any water or non alcoholic drinks available.  I am sure this is something they will get, but seriously do it ASAP.  The beer itself was pretty good. I tried a sip of my husbands and the IPA had good flavor, but it was a bit too light on the hops.  I liked the location of the brewery and the interior decor as well.  I think once they have their grand opening they will be very successful.

We have been here three or four times. They seemed to have the kinks worked out more and more each time but this trip today was the worst. We had a reservation at 2:30. We were told that it would be 20-25 minutes. We checked with the front desk four times every 20-30 minutes and keep being told it would be 30-45 minutes very rudely by the girl working the counter. She obviously didn't give two shits about our tee time. Finally after tracking down someone with a brain it was sorted out and we were taken care of.

I have gone to Show Hand Nails several times in the past and have seen a trend of my gel manicures chipping off sooner from here than other salons I have tried. On my most recent visit, one of my nails completely peeled off less than 24 hours after I got them done!! Two stars only because my mom really enjoyed the gentleman that did her manicure and pedicure.

This place almost killed me. 
I called for take out and explained that I have a severe allergy - asking about the ingredients in my order. The guy checked and said that all the food would be made to order. Got my spring roll, it had onions in it. I went into anaphylactic shock. I was out of service for DAYS recovering. Called them to tell them about it. ¨Oh, sorry.¨ was the response. 

Another time I called and was put on hold for 3 minutes. I hung up and called back. Girl answered. I asked her about a menu item, she put me on hold and took care of another customer with me on the phone just waiting. After about 3 more minutes I hung up. 
 
There are lots of Chinese food joints. Why go to one like this?
yummy and FUN! LOVE THIS PLACE! Great food and service...fun vibe and fantastic location! This is an EXPERIENCE not just a restaurant.

Went to Mr. B's for their Sunday Jazz Brunch. Everything we ate was delicious - Gumbo Ya Ya, Bacon Wrapped Shrimp and Grits, the Breakfast Bowl, and warm Bread Pudding for dessert. The Bread pudding is served with a warm whiskey cream sauce and was the best bread pudding I've ever had!

The restaurant is really neat - it feels like you've gone back in time to the 50's and 60's.

I took off one star because the service was not the best. They use a "team" approach to serving and it led to them being on the slower end.

All that said, I can't wait to go back to Mr. B's next time I'm in town.
Ordered the clam chowder.  Not good.  Ordered stuffed grouper.  could have been better.  Beautiful restaurant with great view.

I'm not sure when they finally opened a Tucson location, but it seemed like the doughnut lovers of southern AZ were chomping at the bit in anticipation of this wonderful little shop coming to our town.  
I finally made it to the shop located on west Ft Lowell near Stone.  It was in the building previously occupied by a Luke's Italian Beef.  Thankfully there are several other Luke's locations so this one wasn't too terribly missed.  
They really changed the place a lot.  It was very nicely decorated and pleasantly air conditioned.  You could smell the sugary, sweet dough from the parking lot and there must have been 20 or so people on the tiny building at 9:30 Tuesday morning.  Everyone was raving about the place and the excitement seemed to be shared by the staff!  They were really filling box after box of doughnuts... mostly for to go orders.
When I made my way up to the counter, I got a chance to see what all the fuss was about. The doughnuts were just regular, glazed doughnuts...but what makes this place famous is the toppings!!!
Dozens and dozens of round, log shaped and twisted pastries all beautifully topped with different colored icings and then sprinkled with crushed up candies!!!i ended up getting a couple  Peanut M&M, Reese's Pieces and Twix!  When I got back to share with my employees they were pleased as punch!  Everything looked so gorgeous and though the doughnuts themselves weren't any different than your average coffee shop, the toppings really made the difference.  
The only reason I only awarded the shop with 3 stars is that the prices were FAR too high.  The thing about food like this is that it's supposed to be cheap...really cheap!!!! After all it is just fried flower and even the candy topping wouldn't be too incredibly costly if ordered in bulk.  The doughnuts are $2.19 EACH!!!  That's newly $30 dollars a dozen!!!!  My favorite doughnut shop offers far better doughnuts at around $6 a dozen. (Nearly 5  times the price just to have a tablespoon of candy dust on top).  
At any rate, I love what they've done and wish them all the best at their shop.  I probably won't be returning.  To be fair, I don't really eat much sweet stuff as I am rather up in age and I weigh more than I'd like.

This was a fun place to go with a group of friends.  Had good rolls and good service!  Lots of variety in the rolls!  Some of the rolls were a little "interesting" which I stay away from.  Like things with fruit in them and such.  I expected it to be a little more "trendy" inside considering the name.  It wasn't super amazing, just regular average sushi.  Also had edemame for our group, which needed some salt.  I'll go back to try it again!  BYO!

Thought we'd give Nacho Mamas one final chance. Big fail on their part again. Asked two times for plastic knife and fork. Did I get them in bag? No.  Asked for two salsas (twice!), did I get them in bag? No. Again. Like almost every time. The drive thru service here is horrendous. Consistently horrendous. Tonight I believe it was one of the owners working the drive thru - still can't get it right. Then she had the gall to tell me they are working their butts off and it's a family business...how about putting what I asked for, twice, in the bag? How about not slamming the drive thru window shut before acknowledging what your customer is asking for? ...twice! Have spoken to several others that we know who've gone to nacho mamas, they are having the same experiences. You guys have got to get it together. You are losing customers. If you are considering eating at Nacho Mamas, particularly through the drive thru, take my advice and DON'T.

I patronize this pizza shop frequently.  Tonight was the worst experience I've ever had.  When I called I was put on hold and then hung up on.  I called back and placed my order.  I was told 30-35 minutes.  I showed up 45 minutes later.  They were seriously unorganized.  I gave the woman my number and she acted like I didn't place an order.  After 5 minutes of searching the computer, she found my order and took my money.  She said it will be up shortly; mind you I showed up 15 minutes past the time stated on the phone.  I had to wait 15 minutes before I received my order.  When I got home, my hoagie had onions on it, which I didn't order and the roll was soaked in so much oil, I threw away the roll.  My cheesesteak didn't have the fried onions I order and they put ketchup on it.  I didn't order ketchup either.  My pizza was ice cold.  Waiting almost an hour to get my food and to get what I didn't order and it's cold is not acceptable.  I looked at the pick ticket.  It was correct; however, I don't think anyone checked it.  I don't think I will be returning here anytime soon.  They just don't have pride in ownership, nor do the give good customer service.

Where is the good Chinese food in the St. Louis area?  It's not here!  I live in the Chicagoland area and there are several places with great Chinese food near my house.  I haven't lived in St. Louis for over 20 years, but still go back to visit family and finding good Chinese food has been impossible.  

We decided to try this place for carryout after reading other reviews and were disappointed.  First, this place is really expensive compared to what I've had and it's not worth it.  The egg rolls were baked, not fried like other places.  The "spicy" dishes were lacking.  To me, a good spicy Chinese meal makes your nose run enough for about 4 kleenexs, well this stuff didn't even require 1 kleenex!

The portions were about the same as I have experienced at other places except the Crab Rangoon.  We only got 3 for the price where at home, we would get 6 for the same price.

We did carryout and when it came time to pay, there was no explanation to our bill, just lots of numbers on a calculator and a total.  We tried to use online menus to order, but there were quite a few available, all with different prices and options, so we just stuck with tried and true entrees.

Another failed attempt to find good Chinese food near my parents!

Should have known something was up when the salad showed up looking like it was made around lunch time. But still gave it a chance, big mistake. Ordered steak med. came out med well. Could have got a better steak at Outback.

jeff's auto is a lifesaver! we were referred to him by a friend who's been going to jeff for years and after taking multiple cars to him i can see why. 

i'm not very car savy so i'm always nervous about getting fleeced by mechanics, but my friend said that jeff was super honest. he brought his car in to jeff and after checking it out, jeff told me friend he could fix it, but it would probably cost more than the car was worth so he'd recommend he look for a new car. what mechanic does that? jeff does.

i had to get a new clutch put in my mazada 3. i got a quote from a mechanic i went to before finding jeff for $1,800. then my friend told me about jeff and his quote was $1,000. needless to say i was excited to see that significantly lower number. even at the price jeff uses factory parts. 

he found a few other repairs, but it was still well below what i was quoted at the other place. jeff even stayed late to keep working on my car and get it back to me as soon as possible. when we brought in our minivan we had the same experience. high-quality work at very reasonable prices and great customer service. i can't recommend jeff enough!

My family and I went to De Dutch many years ago regularly in Vancouver and so it hit a nice spot with me when they finally opened one in Edmonton.

I'm not sure if it's because my tastes have changed, but I was less than enthused with this location.  I even tried it twice hoping it would be different. Nope.

Maybe the franchise has changed their standards, but the food was nothing to write home about, and they didn't have bacon. I'm talking real, greasy, strip bacon.  They had back bacon, but that's just a fancy name for ham.  And people who like ham cannot be trusted.

I kid.

The service was not the greatest, and we were sitting for quite some time after having finished and had to flag down a waitress just to pay for our meals.

Definitely will not be trying this place out a 3rd time, and there's plenty more brunch places with free parking that I'd be more inclined to go to.

I do at least 80% of my grocery shopping at this ALDI. I have saved so much $$$ since I started shopping here. Organic and non-GMO products are a top priority for me and I am blown away by the ever increasing organic options ALDI offers. LOVE this place!!

I have had the pleasure of eating some of Jug's great food at a handful of family events.  Most recently they handled my sister's wedding reception.  The food is fresh, flavorful, and hot right when you need it.  The rolls are to die for, and the comfort food is wonderful.  The staff have always been friendly, helpful, and diligent.  If you are in Indy and need something catered, these are the people you call...period.  Keep up the great work Jugs!!

i have to admit i have a fetish for sneakers.  as a professional i should probably be drooling over cole haans but that's just me.  

ubiq is in a mall in downtown philly called the gallery.  lets just say there are some sketchy looking patrons and more than a few closed stores.  the mall just might make some women feel unsafe.  a bearded man shamelessly leered at one of my companions breasts and said, "Well hello there".   not to compare but this has Never happened in Canada.

however, my advice would be to live dangerously and check this place out.

as a torontonian, 905 poser to be honest, ubiq does not compare to get outside.  the product line at ubiq is much more main stream:  nike, addidas, saucony, new balance.  but honestly, the product is VERY reasonably priced and they have some definite gems.  i picked up a sick pair of saucony's for $49.99.  I was torn by some asics tigers that were all white and gorgeous for the smae price.  Don't forget there is no sales tax in PA.  

my adice would be bolt into the galley head to ubiq.  it's on the third floor.  exit as soon as possible and get out of that part of Philly.

First time at hotel. The river walk/greenbelt is nice even at flood stage, however they should let guests know they are under renovation.  The entire wing that is facing the river is closed.  The beds are comfortable and the room is clean. Needs updated and the bathroom is rather dark. On to the breakfast.  It is small and if you are looking for some granola to go with your yogurt they don't have any. If you are a vegetarian or vegan good luck. It either has cheese, egg, and or meat. Unless you just want oatmeal which has milk in it. Other than the few things listed its pretty decent, not my favorite but at least it's clean. Till next time Boise.

I recently purchased a new Giant Hybrid from Ed at Bike King and could not of been a better experience. I am the first to say I have a tendency to over analyze everything but Ed put me at ease with his knowledge and more importantly his patience. He listened to what I was looking for and directed me to what he thought would best fit my needs. At no time did I feel pressured or rushed in the purchase process. I would highly recommend this shop and expect become a regular customer as I get further into cycling.

This is not my car insurance. Recently I was rear ended & this is the guys insurance company, I am having to deal with.
People are impossible to reach, don't answer your questions & leave you feeling lost & alone. It's like they are begging you to get an attorney, so you can get some straight answers.
Trying to keep just dealing with them, but it is exhausting!
What's a girl to do? Maybe I do need to get an attorney.

This place is stupid overpriced for what it is. The food here is pretty good, however it is not worth the price they make you pay. I recommend dropping in and getting either the Kalua Pork (really good) or the Loco Moco (also really good) with their Macaroni Salad as a side. You won't like the price you're paying, but atleast you'll enjoy the food. The establishment is on the grimes side, and delivery takes five years.

My significant other and I went here for for the first time tonight. My significant other is gluten free and the staff did an AMAZING job making sure that we knew what foods were safe and which weren't and were very apologetic that they didn't have more options (even though they had more than most places). The food was good but it was the service that really made our experience great!
Absolutely the worst fill in I've ever gotten. Biggest waste of time and money... granted, I feel like he tried but i feel like a 3 year old could have done a better job.

This place hit the spot! Line was worth the wait. Bulgogi bowl on point with an egg to top it off. Bibimbap with the hotpot,  so tasty all under $10, can't beat it. Again so far from where I live, but worth it...walked off the meal on the popular delmar loop. Parking can be tricky but park around the residential area and it should be a breeze. I will be on lookout for there truck, hopeful it's a lot closer to me next time.

I stopped in here on a Wednesday night after the Front Desk at my hotel recommended it and gave me a free craft beer card with the purchase of food. I wanted to walk around St. Louis a little bit after driving for 9 hours from Cleveland instead of staying in the hotel and just going to the lobby restaurant but I probably should've just stayed in...

First off, this place is on the corner of a really cool intersection that is laid with original Cobblestone roads. The atmosphere is really cool with exposed brick walls and views of the brewing process through glass windows behind the bar. Unfortunately, that couldn't make up for my experience.

I arrived at around quarter to 9 and was one of the 5 people in the ENTIRE restaurant. I emphasize entire because this place is ridiculously large, taking up nearly a full block or two. Granted it was a Wednesday night but I was expecting a bit of a larger crowd. 

I sat at the bar and ordered their "Award Winning" Golden Pilsner which was less than average in my opinion. It had some flavor but I agree with a previous review that stated it tasted watered down. And quite frankly it looked that way too with barely any head after being poured straight from the tap. 

I asked if the kitchen was still open and the bartender gave me "the look" and told me she would "run and check" which is never a good sign but I didn't really have any other options at this point. She came back with a food menu and told me they would "be happy to make me something" but knew they weren't happy about it having worked in restaurants & her initial reaction. Because of this, I decided to take the easy route and got a Side Salad with the Soft Pretzel appetizer and Beer Cheese. Both were easily some of the worst things I've ever eaten...

The salad was piled sky high on a classic clear glass leaf plate. It started off with "mixed greens" which consisted of almost all iceberg lettuce with some purple cabbage, then came a heaping pile of cheese, and finished off with raw red onions. I got the balsamic dressing which came loaded already on it. Every bite of the salad was cringeworthy and I couldn't pick around it because it simply made a huge mess falling off of the tiny "side" plate. 

As for the Soft Pretzels, they were the ones you can get at your local grocery store in the freezer section/the MINI ones you get at a baseball stadium. They served three of them and they were loaded with way too much salt and were really just plain bad. I guess I can say the beer cheese was alright until I saw that it was an EXTRA $2, bringing the 3 boxed soft pretzels and beer cheese to around $8. I've had some great Bavarian pretzels with beer cheese at plenty of Breweries for the same price and these were just plain embarrassing in comparison. 

I'm being generous with the two stars because the service was good as the bartender and other employees standing around chatted with me while I sat at the bar by myself. The bartender also offered me a sample of their newest beer that was yet to be released for no charge which was nice (unfortunately the beer just wasn't that good). 

If I'm ever back in STL, I won't come back to Morgan Street Brewery. The food was downright terrible and the beer was below average especially for a "BREWERY" that has (somehow) been there for more than 20 years. Stick with the hotel restaurant I suppose or maybe go deeper into town to find something that's really worth it.
The food is alway great here. Stopped in to get lunch for me and the in laws. Everyone was happy.

I don't know why this place is still open since their ramen is over price and the quality was not worth it. I do recommended the chef work on the ramen broth. If anyone craving for ramen I recommended go to Raijin Ramen or Yoshimatsu.

Yeah. The chicken sandwich has a perfectly crisp, juicy, tasty bird in an dreamy soft bun. Though totally unnecessary, I did enjoy the dipping sauce (I got pepper honey) with the sandwich.
I don't like the pickles--too asazuke.

Our waiter Todd was awesome! Catered to every question or need our table had. The service was excellent all around... I had two Manhattan 46 martini's which where definitely absolute after a long holiday day along with our appetizer (mozzarella and tomato's). I ended up ordering the KC strip mid rare which ended up being no big deal but the overall flavor of the steak was slim to non. A KC strip priced at $47 you could assume I was a little disappointed. But a friend of mine has the bone in ribeye when was melt in your mouth good with a tone of flavor along with another ordering the filet which was just okay. This place does a decent job at fine dining but still fast pace. I've had much better steaks and drinks for a cheaper price at plenty of places in the stl area. Not to mention the tacky plastic logo sticks in my $14/$15 martini. I don't see me going here again for a nice dinner out but maybe I'll swing by to give it another go during there happy hour? Service A++, Food A- to B average.

Dude. The fried chicken is the bomb.com. The restaurant is Big, spacious and beautiful (except it was too cold) and the food and drinks were awesome. Room for improvement on the sides but I'd definitely be back!

Hershel's service is speedy even in the middle of the lunch rush. Their corned beef Reuben was one of the best I've ever had. My rating is because it's a bit pricy. I paid $14.75 for a Reuben sandwich and small soda. Table seating is also first come, first served at Reading Terminal Market. At the price I paid, I'd expect at least a comfortable reserved seat to enjoy my food.

Never going back. Wanted some pho on my lunch break. New to the area and decided to give it a try. Person appeared to be the owner asked for my drink order and I told her sweet tea (it was an option in the menu). She brought me non... I can get over that. But... I had to hunt someone down to get the right thing. Then... I asked for chicken pho ( she said they didn't have any today) I almost left but had already wasted 22 mins of my hour break.  I order the verm noodles. After seeing another table walk out and another gentleman wait for his order while his husband had already been given his meal, I saw his order delivered. I thought it looked familiar and asked "what did you order"? He replied, CHICKEN PHO. My meal was delivered, not very appetizing, noodles were very dry and starchy. I passed on most of it and drank my sweet tea instead. Oh, and at the very end I had to hunt someone down to get my check.  Very disappointing. Great location for a lunch rush, however if they don't get their act together quick they won't be open for long.

It's Mexican fast food.

if you are expecting a feast, take a step back and level yourself.  It's not gourmet, it's not outstanding, and it's not innovative.  But for fast food, it's quite the treat.

Greasy chips and surprisingly fantastic salsa will be brought to your table every few minutes.  The reason for the quick turnaround is that the chips really are addictive.  Overpriced margaritas are available for the willing, and the fajitas are also quite nice.  Big portions, funky decor, happy service, can't complain.

If you go there looking for a simple Mexican-style meal, you'll be thrilled.  But for those looking for authentic goodness?  If you've settled on Don Pablo's, perhaps you haven't looked hard enough.  But you won't be disappointed, regardless.   There's plenty to choose from and it's all pretty good!

This place was extremely disappointing. My friend "won" a happy hour that was basically an hour of open bar. This open bar was to include all draft beer and well drinks. However, when we got there at 7pm, they informed us that the draft beer system was down because someone had messed with the gas. We all ordered well drinks instead. Then they let us know that the tonic water was messed up, followed by letting us know that there would be no carbonation in any of the drinks. All drinks were served in small plastic cups and we were told to bring our cups up anytime we wanted another drink. All in all, first impression was that this place is pretty terrible and unprepared for a scheduled happy hour. Giving two stars for assuming management didn't prepare the staff for the whole situation and the bartender being somewhat accommodating.

Hostess had a major attitude and we waited 5 minutes just for her to come up and greet us. During the meal we got checked on twice and the food was okay. The fajitas looked better than they were. No seasoning to them. It's also unsettling that their chips sit on tables for hours on end and if you finish yours they just give you ones from another table. Gone here a couple times but this one was not a great experience at all.

Kim is such a fantastic groomer! She gives my dog (Elliott) the best haircuts he's ever gotten in his 7 years of going here! I can tell he just loves her and everyone else there! He is spoiled rotten when he goes in for his haircut so he runs in as fast as he can! The store is adorable too and we have gotten some fantastic things over the years! All of the toys and collars etc are very sturdy and last forever! I wouldn't go anywhere else!

We have gone here for several years but noticed this time around that the people were reusing gloves on different people as they worked.  I'm not sure if this has always been but I'd rather have someone's washed hands do my feet than a used gloved that has not been cleaned. I hope they fix this....

This was my first time ever going to this Chain restaurant. We stopped by after a early movie for lunch. The atmosphere is beautiful. It is in a neat location.  We arrived there around 12:50. We were sat right away.  This is where the problems started. We sat at our table close to 10 minutes before anyone even asked us for our beverages. We almost had to insist he take our meal order as well...
He did bring over a few drink replacements after bringing out our drinks. It took 55 minutes for our food to reach our table. The food was great. I enjoyed my food.

If it would not have taken almost a hour to receive our food I would have given it a higher score but, let's face it, the food can be great but if the cooks and server is not on it, it really isn't worth the wait...

Came to Brewster's for my first time, the ambiance is pretty chill, the food is great and the beer is very crafty. However. Upon arrival it took several minutes to even get acknowledged. Once a waiter finally came around he said our server would be right out but he would get drinks started. I ordered the blueberry beer and my companions ordered a Jamison's on the rocks and a taste tester of the mosaic. Once receiving our drinks no one ever came back to get my companion another beverage after the taste tester. I started a timer out of aggravation ..only because upon first taking our seats we now had less than half hour left on the happy hour appetizer menu. But after still not seeing our "so-called waitress" the timer began. After 12 whole minutes !!!! (now I know the timer is being a little extra but honestly sitting here for more than 15 minutes all in. with no service. while starving and with now  less 15 minutes left to order off the happy hour menu was more than frustrating!) FINALLLLYYYY someone came to our table and got my companion a beverage. Still not taking our food orders we now had 5 minutes left to order off of the happy hour appetizer menu. Once she brought the raspberry beer back she finally asked for our food orders. Now I can understand if the restaurant was even a little bit busy there would be a reasonable excuse. However there was maybe 3 people in the entire building! Once getting our food it was fantastic!!! I have absolutely no complaints about food or beverage!! It was all so delightful. Once scarfing down my meal (**starving after waiting for service)  still no server was around to ask me how my food was. I was already finished by the time someone came to ask me how my food was. Mildly disappointed .. what if my food was disgusting. And I just powered through it because no service was offered what's so ever and of course being starving wouldn't have helped that entire situation. After being finished I did tip my server over 20% because I was also a server once and I still value good food and beverage. However if my tip alone was based on service it would have been an entirely different story. 

I do recommend the bison burger with the tots ** outstanding!! As well as the bacon Mac and cheese **insert drooly face** 

* however if you're hungry right now!! I do not recommend sitting here!! The wait is on and you WILL be here a while*

Although I made reservations, there was no wait and plenty of seating available when my friend and I went at 11:30 AM on a Sunday for brunch. I didn't even remember to tell them we had reservations. 
I had a 3 egg omelette with hash browns. The food was good, but nothing to really write home about. I mean it was not spectacular or unique in any way. However, I did not try any of the German fare. 
The service was good and the food came out at a quick pace.

As avid fans of Gemelli in Narberth, we had to see the new digs in Manayunk.  

We started with the caprese and asparagus salads.  The roasted tomatoes with the caprese salad was a nice touch and there was an interesting dressing on the asparagus salad.  We then ordered the salmon and fettuccine for our main courses.  The fettuccine in particular was nice in that there was an even vegetable to pasta balance - not heavy.  Although an honest misunderstanding led them to mess up our order, the wait staff was accommodating and polite in fixing it and offered to comp dessert.  The food was tasty and we would probably go back.  

There are caveats, however, and this might be simply new opening pains.  While the food was good, it fell short of memorable.  Maybe it was the ambiance that left that general impression.  Certainly, the vibe at the old Gemelli simply cannot be beat, and we missed the intimate setting tremendously.  We laughed (visibly to all around us) when the waiter, from his straw basket, used a utensil typically employed for plating piping hot, delicate, soft, oven-fresh rolls to give us our bread.  What we got instead was cold and seemed suspiciously supermarket-bought.  And for the wine drinkers, this might not be your best bet.  My husband was ready for his 2nd glass of wine while I was only 1/2 way through my 1st.  When the waitress poured him his second glass, hilariously, it was less than my 1/2 drank glass.  To say they pour partial glasses would be a generous overstatement.  We ended up paying $120 for 2 people and that seemed high.

We recently visited The Block and it was a good solid restaurant.  I wasn't over the moon over anything.  The service was reliable and basic.  I ordered a salad that I liked, but was very happy that I ordered chicken with my salad.  The greens were very tart and the salad was best when eaten with the chicken and the other things in the salad.

Fantastic place. There are so many coffee places nowadays, that many of them don't stand out. This place is different. The owner is extremely friendly, and obviously loves her job and meeting new people. If you're looking for a nice respite from the drudgery of a commute to/from work or school, she'll provide it.  Prices are reasonable, and coffee & a bagel certainly doesn't cost $7.00.

The skin on my garlic Parmesan wings were barely seasoned and they were not crispy at all. The three waitresses I came in contact with were very brief with me which came off as them being rude, and I was only given about 5 French fries to go with my meal. You're better off ordering from Buffalo Wild Wings around the corner or going to a different Beef 'O' Brady's.

Painting was incomplete on nail, cut down nails without asking any preferences, shape, length, crooked filing, residue left all over skin, took over an hour to get attended to for gel manicure. Going back in tomorrow to see if they will fix it.

Update: added one star because they did fix them, after about 5 minutes back and forth of "there's nothing else we can do" even when I asked if I could simply just get them repainted... I kept being told "there's nothing wrong with them" to the point where it was just rude. I ended up saying "I am just not satisfied with my manicure, and the two women I came in with yesterday are, so I'd just like a redo." The woman who redid them for me was so incredibly kind and very apologetic. She said she had no problem with redoing them, and just wanted to know what I wanted. 

While my manicure is much better now, I would not recommend this salon. I would recommend the younger technician, she sits in the last chair at the back (I wish I had gotten her name). The damage was still done (I didn't get any say in the shape, length, anything because of the first technician) but at least now the polish is clean and even.

I read a bunch of Yelp reviews prior to dining at Monarch, so I showed up expecting a super classy, way expensive experience.  When I arrived, I noticed a big sports bar TV in the center of the bar tops, which dominates your view when you first walk in.  Not quite the epitome of classy.  

However, that's not to say that Monarch isn't a nice restaurant.  It's very well decorated and has low lights to suggest a sophisticated ambiance.  The table tops have white linens and the wait staff is complex:  waiters, drink servers, valets, managers, etc. were all walking around to wait on my party.  Our server, Chris, was very friendly and nice.  We felt taken care of, for sure.  Monarch reminded me of The Cheesecake Factory, if The Cheesecake Factory wasn't so loud, crowded, fast-paced and in a mall.

The menu isn't very large and a bit unfamiliar if you aren't used to New Orleans style cuisine.  I ordered BBQ Shrimp and Grits, which was very good.  For $19 though, I would have liked more shrimp.  Apparently they are famous for their fried chicken, which someone in my party ordered.  It takes a long 25 minutes to cook though so be prepared to wait a while if that's what you decide on.  The dish came with a lot of chicken, so seems worth it!

I tried the Boudoin (sp?) Balls appetizer (meatball type things with rice and sausage), which weren't very flavorful.  All I tasted was the grease--not recommended.  But, the bread baskets they brought to our table more than satisfied our appetizer wants.  The baskets come with rosemary bread and little corn muffins.  Delicious!  I recommend you skip the appetizers and enjoy the free bread.

Overall, the food was good but I doubt I'll remember it as worthy of a return visit.

Fantastic "local hole in the wall" find! Great prices! Flavor-able food. Friendly service. Try their Grilled Peanut Butter sandwich (The Elvis) for a small snack. Their pork chop (tenderloin) that is served with their breakfast menu is pretty amazing. 

You will drive right by this place if you're not looking for it and probably have many times in the past. Make it a point to seek it out.
sucks stayed home all day for nothing claimed they came but i was here all day customer service always bullshit you

Best baked potato I have had in my life! Hands down, they are magical. My waitress was great, never felt rushed. The burgers are some of the best in Louisiana. Drinks were powerful. Be careful if you don't drink often. Can't say enough good things about this place. Worth the walk from your French Quarter hotel. I will be back again and again.

I'm in town for a few days to visit my dad, who moved to Wesley Chapel last week. He's big into seafood, so we decided to go to Pinchers. I didn't read reviews until we were seated, and I'm glad I didn't read them
Until then, else I probably wouldn't have given this place a chance. I'm very picky about my seafood, so believe me when I say that this place is amazing! 

The atmosphere is very similar to Joe's Crab Shack--lots of colorful, mounted fish, frames pictures with crabs and tropical fish meant to give the impression of a large Key West fish shack. There's a cooler case with all sorts of fish, clams, crabs, and shrimp that greet you along with the hostess, who will tell you that anything in the cooler is fresh and delicious. The atmosphere is casual and I saw a lot of families dining.

Dad ordered a bowl of the crab and corn chowder--it was so good that I couldn't help but steal a spoonful or two. The crab was sweet and the both had just enough spice in it to enhance the sweetness of the crab. I definitely regret not getting my own bowl!

For dinner we split a half proud of the grilled shrimp. They were cooked to perfection, seasoned with dill and lemon pepper seasoning. The shrimp were so fresh--no shrimpy aftertaste here!--and they came out quickly after we ordered.

Don't let the negative reviews scare you away from Pinchers. I can't wait to come back on my next visit to Florida--the crab pincher appetizer and fish and chips platter definitely caught my eye. Can't wait to try them!

Barbacoa with jackfruit and amazing spicing made this my favorite dish of the week. Vegan dishes have a bad reputation of being bland. Well? There's nothing bland in this amazing little jewel. I'm so glad to have found it and will become a regular. The menu changes daily and is always "colorful even on the palate." That's the best I can do to describe the amazing flavors and textures of this food. And they utilize local produce to make everything fresh. Down to the bowl of soup du jour that is "gratis" and your first taste of the unique spice blends and freshness that will explode into your senses. I look forward to my next visit.

Overpriced for mediocre quality meats. The bibimbap lacked vegetables which is an important part of the dish. The experience of cooking your own food is fun and the restaurant is decorated nicely but the quality of the food is not worth the high price of he restaurant. It's just another trendy place with no substance.

Great BBQ for the northwest side. Service was fast and friendly. Only had one order to-go; but it was ready on time and still hot and fresh.  Nice inside and outside dining with a full service bar.  I'll be back for a more in depth eating experience.

Needed to have a last minute wax and this place had good reviews and it was close and reasonably priced. I was able to make an appointment online same day which was huge for me because I am unable to talk on my phone during work. Everyone was nice and friendly. The lady that waxed me was really nice and made me feel comfortable. I was a little disappointed though that when I got home I realized I still had some hair left behind
Didn't meet expectations. Called the manager. Rocky said that he would refund the cost of the 2 steak taco orders. The next morning only one was refunded.

Be Warned: This hospital supports the legal kidnapping of children. Please inquire into "Dr" Sally Smith, as well as the entire CPS system in St Petersburg. Please seek to disbar and legally charge Dr Sally Smith and her support staff.

This is a great place to pick delicious, huge blueberries. The family was very nice! I read some negative reviews and found none of them to be true! They don't allow children at this time because their farm was vandalized. The website and their Facebook page state the rules. If you want delicious blueberries give them a try!

I was hoping this would be a great experience to celebrate a great event.  I was sorely disappointed in many ways. I have to say the food was very good, a bit pricey tho'.
The service left much to be desired and 'someone seemed to have a snippy attitude'. It took forever to be seated, to place our orders and to get our food. If their staff cannot handle larger groups, they need more help. Our server did the best he could and I'm sure he would have been better if not so over worked.

Word travels fast and it would be beneficial to focus on Customer Service and courtesy, not to mention 'Service with a Smile'.

Decent food, bad service.

The people working the counter never want to make any of the speciality drinks and often claim ignorance like all they are there to do is claim money for the buffet (if they are even there). The buffet is decent but you have to put up with horrible service. It's like a dive bar for curry.

They get two stars for appearance only.  $11 for
10 oz Bloody Mary that was sub par at best - suppose to have got what they called Bloody Buddy (additional $5) with it but waiter forgot it.  We didn't even stay to order food - won't return to Janes Hideaway
They had the key, prompt service, etc. but the young man that waited on me did not wear a mask. Didn't feel real comfortable in a small store with that situation.

I gave this place two chances. I wanted to use them because I live in the same building. Now I walk 6 blocks to my new dry cleaner. Why? I drop off my shirts less to be cleaned and more to be ironed, but here they come back so wrinkled as to be unwearable. Maybe you'll have better luck but I doubt it.
The counter girl ruined my otherwise acceptable burger by making it well done, even through I ordered it medium.

We came here for lunch with our friend and enjoyed it very much! We had the poke nachos, carnitas tacos and a veggie burger. All were delicious! Except for the fish and chips our friend ordered, everything was great. The drinks were nice too and! I ordered a black orchid martini. Would definitely recommend to anyone wanting to try a new place.

This is the first time I stopped in here. It seems like a fun dollar store kind of place. Like a place for stocking stuffers for kids.  The main things I saw were toys, cosmetics and candy. There were also cheap phone cases and earbuds. 

I don't see a need to go back here because I do not have any teenagers in my life, but if you do, it would be a fun place to shop with or for them.

GREAT SERVICE, such a cool room, which used to be some wealthy families living soooo cool!

But, let's review the food as this place is a restaurant....

Ok it is average, if you want enchiladas with very large pieces of filet that they should of asked you how you wanted it cook, rare, medium or well... Really takes away from the taste

I ordered the taco truck  and wish I had it from the taco truck I think it would have been better

BUT, the service was GREAT

I ate at this McDonald's today at 3:30 with my daughter. She ordered the mushroom and Swiss cheese burger w/ fries.  She was feeling good and we were enjoying the warm weather. Halfway through the burger, she started feeling full and uncomfortable, and then a little nauseous.  Three hours later and she feels awful. She thinks she may have food poisoning. I tried to call the location to warn them, but no answer. Please be careful of eating there.

Incredibly bad customer service. I was warned not to buy a Browning shotgun by the guys at my gun club. Bought a Citori CX anyhow, and it is a nice shooting gun. However, when the bluing began to fail after one short year of light use, I sent it in for repair. The response: It was my fault, I was told, either my "body chemistry" (this is a true story) reacted badly with their bluing or I had spilled corrosive chemicals on the bluing. None of my other steel guns seem to dislike my chemistry and I certainly don't spill corrosive chemicals on my firearms, which i treat better than my children!
So I spent $100 or more on postage and insurance and got back a defective gun. I've heard others tell me they waited seven months for their guns to be returned from Browning.
I regret buying the Citori and wished I would have listened to my colleagues.

When I hear the words "yellow" and "porch" i think sunny, warm, and ice tea.  Was really surprised how dark the Yellow Porch's dining room is during lunch service.  It reminded me of a dark, cheesy lounge...such a bummer because it was a nice, sunny day and a little bit of natural light would have been so nice.  They have tons of windows but all the shades were drawn..strange.

So if you can get over eating lunch in a dimly lit dining environment then this place is ok for simple sandwiches and salads for lunch.  My turkey club sandwich ($8.95) was tasty and the potato salad side sufficed (1 star).  Service was fast but not overly friendly (1 star).

I purchased this tier cake for a 1st birthday party and it fell out from the bottom causing the top to fall over.  It was very crumbly. I couldn't even cut slices.  When I confronted Sweetsboro with the pictures there was no responsibility taken. I agree with the reviews that the counter girl needs an attitude adjustment. But to treat customers this way even with proof that they were in the wrong is disheartening.  After years of going to them out of convenience, I'll be taking my business elsewhere.

A group of coworkers and I stopped in for lunch on a Tuesday.  We arrived after 12:30 and the place was empty.  Not the best sign.  It was so empty that it was "seat yourself" on the first floor.  

Orders around the table varied -- some hot chicken salads, a burger, the hot chicken ramen, chicken salad plate, some appetizers.  Our food came out relatively quickly, although the restaurant was empty, so I would have been mad if we had waited a long time. 

I had the hot chicken salad at a "hot" spiciness level.  First few bites--not hot.  Then out of nowhere the heat kicked in.  Not so hot that you could not eat the food but definitely hot.  The chicken on the salad is chicken tenders.  I must have touched one with my finger and then touched my face because the skin on my face started to burn.  Won't do that again.

Feedback from others at the table--the hot chicken ramen was more like a bowl of noodles.  Not much broth.  The coworker who had the burger said it was "disgusting" and did not finish her food.  The side of macaroni and cheese was "really good" I am told.  The sides are really small I will note.  The person who ordered the boiled peanuts said the peanuts were cold.  That's just gross.  

My salad was fine.  It was a salad.  I thought the chicken was good.  I would order it again--just maybe the medium heat level.  The service was fine.  All-in-all, a solid three-star Yelp experience.
I love Bowl. Been there many times and will continue to go. Food is not the same old stuff made for the typical American pallet. Stretches out and tries new flavors.

Word is this is the best live music venue in Santa Barbara. I experienced it for myself this past saturday at the Halloween/Punk Rock Karaoke/Costume Contest/Nerf Herder show. Very fun vibe, lively costumes, and engaging staff. $7.50 for a grey goose chocolate martini plus $10 cover is pretty reasonable for a night out with so much to offer. 

The space is really big considering it's hidden in Victoria Court - there's two main rooms and an outdoor patio plus more I didn't see. Extra thoughtful items on their part  - big screens in the back of the bar allow you to watch what's going on onstage while you're getting a drink, chilling at a table, or just taking a break from the crowd. Also, the bathroom is centrally located & right by the entrance

3.5 stars, rounded down.  Great specialty cocktail called The Hemingway. Good but not great food in the French quarter.  Our waiter was a hoot.  We had live music, which was a nice surprise, although it was a bit too loud.  We chose it because it was close to the other music lounge we were going to afterwards.

We bought a beautiful Torque Toy Hauler in March of 2021.  The locks to the generator and outdoor storage quit working almost immediately.  We went in to Bish's Service Dept to order the parts.  They told us it would be December before we could get the part.  I have called several times, most of the time you can't even get somewhere to leave a message.  I have left several messages for Tim asking for a return call.  Nothing has happened!
The thing that is really upsetting for me is my dear husband passed away unexpectedly in July.  They know he passed away and they still are not helping in anyway.  
They are good when it comes to their sales - but their service lacks in every aspect.  As I am talking to others, I hear that their service is terrible.  I totally agree.  It would be nice if they would take care of their customers after the sale.  We have a warranty on it as well. 
If you buy from them don't expect them to take care of you after the sale, they won't.

We have been eating at Duffy's for a  couple times.  Every time we enjoyed their good food and good services.   I had a  tuna Poke bowl this time . I changed the rice to  noodle. With the pickle cucumber , the whole bowl was a satisfied meal. Our favorites side dishes here are the parmesan French fry and sweet potato fry .  They were very good. The ribs and Seafood items here were also pretty tasty.  This is a sport bar ,  a lot of TV were hanging around every wall and corner.  They runs different happy hour throughout the days of   week and also rewarding point system program.  If customers plan ahead and come here  to eat and drink at the price discounted time  , ones could grasp some good values of a delicious meal.

So fun! The whole family loves this place. Less crowded than some of the other trampoline places that we've been to and the staff is always very helpful and friendly. This is the kids favorite place to go!

My company used to do business with them. They had a lady named Chris working for them. She always did a great job and communicating and getting jobs assigned in a timely manor. Once she left, the company went down hill fast!! We couldn't get our work taken care of. I emailed and called several times for jobs and never heard back. We waited almost 3 months for a refund that we were told was mailed off repeatedly! We will never use them again!!

Popped into Walgreens to pick up an Arizona Iced tea, which was offered on their signage at 2 for $1.00. This was an easy buy. The store was ... well it's a bit dirty and the staff was a bit MEH in the terms of customer service, but all in all it was a decent visit and I got what I wanted without too much hassle.

As I walked in the host staff stared at me as though I had the Polish eagle standing on my head. I did not feel very welcome. I went to the bar and again the bar staff did not make me feel welcome. The Chopin vodka martini was prepared well and the food was good. As I ate at the bar the bartender finally spoke to me in a friendly demeanor. Staff - one dupa. Martini - five dupas. Food - four dupas.

I really hate to leave this review. The reason being I do not want the world to learn about this treasure and have it get to the point where I can't even get a table without a long wait anymore. From 3:00 to 7:00 they have happy hour. Their Margaritas are $2.99 each and $16.99 for a picture. They say it's a house Margarita but, as a quasi margarita expert, I will tell you there is good is any top shelf Margarita I've ever had anywhere. You get your choice of Frozen or on the rocks The service is fast the food is delicious the decor is special and they need to raise their prices. The employees are simply delightful and all work together to be sure your party is well taken care of start to finish. I actually drove by 5 agave 50 times before I ever even stopped. And now I have dined / invibed Margaritas here at least a dozen times.  Captain Bill, "Lady Di".

The drive thru is absolutely ridiculous. I keep coming back because it's in my neighborhood but I'm done. If you are looking for quick service.....keep looking. The last 3 times I have been here I have waited in line no less than 13 minutes with only 2-3 cars ahead of me. The worst was last month when the 2 cars ahead of me got sick of waiting and just drove off. By the time I drove up to the window, the poor teenage girl was in tears and screaming at the other employees that everyone was just driving away. There is never a "sorry about the wait" or any remotely logical customer service. This is clearly an ongoing Owner/Manager issue who couldn't care less. Worst Port of Subs in town. There's a reason their overall rating is so low.

Okay let me just start this off by saying, this food was absolutely delicious. I would have left the food 5 stars but I did have to kick one off due to their drinks. I'm not usually one to complain about a strong beverage, but our white sangria really just tasted like white wine and cinnamon. We had to ask for extra pineapple juice and Sprite so it was drinkable. We had two pork tacos which were so tasty. We also had the great American taco which was good, but not out of this world. The lava drops and the tempura Royal cali(I think) which were so damn delicious. I wish I ordered more lava drops as you only get four. Needless to say I will definitely be back until I've eaten my way through this menu. Our server was great and attentive and I'm sure I'll see him again.

I discovered this dessert vendor at a food festival last year and look for them EVERY year!  They were recently placed as the best strawberry dessert in the 2017 Manyunk food fest -- their sweet lavender cupcake was moist, tasty and we ended up getting half a dozen!!  The crème brulee is divine and my utter weakness! We often order ahead and bring Sweet Lavender desserts as our offering to potluck parties - it is well worth every penny!  The owners are friendly and not pretentious in the least, and always remember repeat customers!

Well, I am back 5 years later and if I could have given them a no star rating, I would have. I'm going to blame it on Covid and hope that this restaurant regains its footing. We ate here five years ago for my husband's 60th and as you see from my reading below it was one of the best dining experiences and food experiences we have ever had. So while in Nashville again for my 65th, we decided to go again. We sat at the bar to have a drink before going to our table. The bartender never looked at us and seemed overwhelmed and was rushing around to serve no more than five other people that were in the entire bar! It took him about seven minutes to even acknowledge us. No smile and when we asked him to take our picture he grumbled something under his breath. Very friendly hostess then seated us at our table and we noticed even though it was only 7:15PM, there were only six or eight other diners in the entire restaurant. Our Waitress greeted us with a nice smile and told us she was brand new! Yikes! We ordered another round of drinks and told her we wanted to take this dinner slow. We only ordered one appetizer which was the scallops wrapped in bacon. They were good but a little overcooked. The dinner entrées were the sirloin steak, the fillet mignon oscar, and the cold water lobster tails bAfter what seemed like a very long time, our entrées were served. As the waitress was serving us she asked us what we wanted for dessert? Really? Another waitress came to the table to serve my daughter her lobster tail and proceeded to drop the lobster tail on the floor! Really? My daughters whole entrée had to be cooked again. the spinach side tasted terribly sweet. My husbands sirloin steak was cooked perfectly and very tasty. I took a bite of my fillet mignon Oscar and noticed the asparagus and crab meat were cold and the steak was barely warm, and there was no hollandaise sauce! My daughters lobster tail was barely warm , tough and not very flavorful. I sent my entrée back and proceeded to eat my baked potato! By the time our  meals were re-served, my husband had completely eaten his, and I had finished my baked potato! The meat came out for the second time looking burnt and with no hollandaise  sauce Again! We asked to speak to the manager who came to our table and offered us a free drink! Really? I told him that it was my 65th birthday and this was the restaurant we chose because in the past it had been outstanding but this whole dining experience was a huge disappointment and I would never come back. He never said he was sorry and basically turned around and walked away. At this point we were so aggravated I didn't eat or want my no sauce filet. The place was empty at 9:30 PM.
     We left  and I never ate dinner or even had dessert from my birthday! I will never eat at this establishment again. It was a huge huge disappointment and the staff seemed inexperienced, unprepared. I cannot imagine what was going on in the kitchen. The manager had no idea about customer service.

Uh oh ... it's a "beef, chicken or pork" Mexican place ... but the pricing was alright

Super Nachos w/ Meat: 6.25, which isn't bad, except they nickle and dime you on the guac and sour cream at 25 cents extra each ... and the guac is really pretty bad. I got chicken and it seemed like canned chicken rolled in seasoning ... even worse than Taco Bell chicken

Fish Tacos: 2 for 5 bucks but not even worth that. Dry and terrible. Tough little pieces of what seemed like overdone Gorton's frozen fish up in. Want some guac and sour cream to lubricate them? 50 cents extra. 

Also ... iceberg lettuce instead of cabbage in a baja taco? What?

Final impression is that it's a super-gringofied place that isn't trying very hard. Only saving grace is that it's cheap for the riverwalk area, but the food isn't even worth the low price. 

I'm gonna start calling these places "BCP joints" I think ... another bad warning sign is when they're playing Phil Collins or something instead of that oompa oompa tuba and accordion Mexican pop music. You hear "Invisible Touch" and see "beef, chicken or pork", just turn your ass around and don't even order anything.

Went with a friend on a busy Friday night, was a long wait for seating, but luckily we were able to find 2 bar stool and stayed put there for the night's dinner. 
Had a great meal, the food was all very tasty. Seems the cooks have really improved in this location. 

Unfortunately though the service was poor to average at best. Even sitting at the bar, with 2+ bar keeps there, our drinks still went dry several times, the appetizers took forever to get to us, and those all so yummy biscuits seemed like they would never arrive quick enough. The other customers sitting at the bar made excellent conversation, as my friend and I were in a social mood that evening, so this helped pass the time, as we all sat waiting and wondering when our next dish or refill would arrive. And it wasn't the busy night that was to blame, as when 2 bartenders are standing in front of you, and you have empty drinks, that is no excuse. 

Overall, excellent food that night, but poor service, and long wait times.

I went to Palm for lunch and think that this is just an OK place. I had the lunch special where you get a salad, entre and a desert for something like $20. The salad was just OK. I picked the filet mignon entre which was not that impressive and is just what you could find anywhere else. The desert was huge and enough for two. Overall, this lunch menu offers a good value given the amount of food that you get but don't expect anything extraordinary. 

The reason I am giving this place 3 starts is because of the service. Our waiter was very rude and mixed up our orders.
Honest mechanics and always do a great job! I recommend for both oil change and auto repair work!

Awesome sushi for S. IL! We loved the wasabi shumi - something very different and wasabi hot! Delish. We came for the sushi because of the reviews and will be back again. Service was on spot and we had the place to ourselves but yopi can taste the authenticity in their rolls and the freshness of their wasabi and fish.

Come check them out! Enjoy the authentic cuisine. They have been here 6 years and will be here many more. Cheers!

Place is amazing, the history, the scale and all built in 36 months!! Middle of no where and could use a small cafe. But probably would lose money..
The only reason I list 3 stars is the fella who took us round. Chuck has a 5000 hour badge. He used to work there in 1970's. He has done tour so many times that he rattles it off so its difficult to hear. Lord help you if you ask a question he may, or may not have addressed in his tour. He is pretty acerbic so just beware, I just gave up asking. There is another Chuck in the superbly lucid video your are shown before the tour. I met him also on the tour. "what?" I asked, 'is the succession plan when all you guys who worked here have retired from this?' (they all retired from active service years ago). I got short shrift and he moved off without a comment.
Not warm guys here really..and in 20 years I suppose that we will have lost the experts and just have second hand stories from kids filling in term time. Shame these guys with such knowledge and history can't be more accessible.

She's good, and the only reason I am giving 3 stars is that it just wasn't my style.  

I prefer a fixed course massage with some focus on any specific area I have problem with, but it seems like Kate is more focused on starting from any area you have problem.  I hope this makes sense...other than that, Kate is great.  The massage place is really cute and Kate is very professional.  Pricing isn't bad either.  

I would say go to this place if you know what you would like to be done and have any specific problematic area in your body.  But if you are looking for something general, this may not be a place for you.  Overall a good experience, but I'll probably go to somewhere else.

Never again. I was a 2-4 time a week guess in this establishment. No more. 
  It always takes a long time for food to come out of this kitchen but 31 minutes for 2 easy eggs and kids pancakes is a bit excessive. My 3 year old daughter and I wasted over 30 minutes waiting on food this past week. 
  The waitress blamed it on a party of 10 that was ahead of us. No excuse. As I was leaving without any food for my 3 year old or myself, the table behind us who ordered a good 10 mins after us was getting their food. 
  She only said I'm sorry, nothing else. Then pointed around. 

Unacceptable. 

Great food if you can ever get it. get your act together if you want to be a real player with the locals. Who when the snow birds go home, pay your bills. Each and every one of you.
Great spumoni ice cream, really liked the canolli too, only down side is they are a bit off the beaten path.

We ate here for the first time after moving into the area. My Kung Pao Chicken was good. My wife said her Sweet and Sour Pork was too crunchy. The Steamed Dumplings were good, but the Hoison sauce was rather flavorless. I will eat here again

The employees at this location are tops in their fields! Always professional, and always more than willing to help.

I just bought a gallon of custom-color paint last weekend to touch up interior walls that were painted 15 years ago. I wasn't expecting a perfect match, but was hoping to get close. I'm super excited to say that the match was perfect and I couldn't be happier. 

I honestly don't believe I would have had similar results with HD, Lowe's, or Ace. Dunn-Edwards is the place to go for all things paint!
