Another sad experience in the 'high pressure sales department' chapter: I asked them to come out for a proposal to replace our doors and windows with high impact hurricane windows/doors. We did not even get there. The rep who called me said: Your wife's name is "name"? I said, 'maybe', wondering why he would ask me that (I did not give my wife's name to them when I called initially...he must have done a bit of research on line on the tax collector's website). Then I asked him why he would ask me that? He said: "well I want her present when we discuss the proposal". I said: "This is none of your business. I want to get a price for replacing windows and doors in a house. Can you do that?". He continued insisting that he needs to know when she would be back from work so he could come by at that time...When I said, I would not give him this information, he cancelled the appointment and hung up. And I am glad he did. Such behavior and attitude is probably not a good harbinger for a successful contractor experience...on to the next appointment...stay tuned...;-)

Wow, these minis are delicious! They were still warm when I arrived at my mother's house. We shared a box of 8. Eight is definitely not enough. I was wanting more of these little treats. Their Maple Bacon donut is even better than the ones I've had in Tampa. The cake part of the donuts were soft & fresh. This is my new favorite mini donut place! I also loved the Churro (which actually tastes like a churro) and the Peanut Butter Chocolate minis. It had a rich chocolate icing with chopped up Reese's cups and a peanut butter drizzle. It's totally addicting. If I had to say anything negative, it would be that the Guava donut needed more guava than cream cheese icing. The guava flavor was kind of lost. It was still a very good donut. I also liked the Nitro Cold Brew (made with Blind Tiger Coffee). I'm looking forward to trying more flavors and other coffee preparations. 

This place is already becoming very popular. There were quite a few people there at 4:00 today. They did a great job of getting the line moving. The service was quick & friendly. What more can I say?

Spent 3 nights at Capital hotel with friends. We arrived very early not expecting to check in just drop bags. They got in our rooms and invited us to enjoy breakfast buffet. Very very accommodating. It's a great location to visit Nashville. Room is comfortable and clean. Bathroom spacious. Bed was comfortable. Room had mini fridge and breakfast buffet choices were perfect. Price was reasonable for location in Nashville.

OK, the food is very reasonable in price and fair to middlin' in taste and quality.  The menu includes some nice home-style Italian faves.  It's the type of place you grab a bottle from your wine rack at home, jump in the car, and go enjoy an impromptu night at this Mom and Pop operation.

Pop is in the kitchen throwing together mass quantities of food and Mom is out among the Friday night half-filled restaurant looking like she is hating every minute being there.   Her miserable puss exudes a bad vibe throughout the restaurant.

One time I was there,  Mom was having a shouting match with one of the servers in front of the customers, to which the server exited on the spot.  It was a most uncomfortable evening.  So I waited awhile to go back.  

The Shrimp Fra Diavolo grabbed my attention and the nice spice hit the spot.  Dinner Rolls buried in garlic and oil are just leaving my taste buds 3 days later...overkill there.  The salad which was included might as well not be... iceberg lettuce and a dab of Italian dressing sitting in a tiny puddle in the middle of the salad doesn't do it for me.

Service is OK.  The girls are working their butts off.  I guess they don't want to face the wrath of Mom.

Cool place for comics but I came for Funko Pops since Yelp told me this was the Funko Pop place in Tucson to go. Was disappointed in the selection. So if you want comics- YES, go! But if you want Funko Pops don't bother.
I love this place it was great to see al the different assortments of succulents and fruit varieties.

My favorite upper scale Asian restaurant in town. Everything is delicious and they have wonderful live music. You can't go wrong with anything on the menu whether you want apps, entrees, or anything else.

This is our absolute favorite breakfast spot! If you go on the weekend be prepared for a little wait. No worries, the staff will offer you some delicious mimosa's while you wait, and WOW is it worth it. My husband loves the stuffed French toast and my go to is the Brioche French Toast - Banana Foster style. The food taste so fresh and the flavors are incredible. This is a MUST for us every time we go to NOLA. We prefer the location that is linked with the Pelham Hotel.

Attentive, efficient service.  Great house salad.  Wonderful interior design, and a worthy addition to the Wash Ave streetscape.

But the pizza is pricey and mediocre.  So far; I'll give them more chances as they mature.

This is the worst place I've ever stayed. The online ads and photos on here are completely false. We booked a room with double beds because there are two of us.. we checked in and were told there are no doubles and we needed to share a room or leave. We get to the room and there are ZERO outlets, the phone doesn't even work because it wasn't plugged up. We didn't have a light they had a Walmart lamp in the corner of the room and the two other lamps didn't turn on because there was no outlets. There was a dirty tooth brush on the floor next to the bed, and two small rags only in the bathroom. The toilet flooded when we flushed for the first time in the middle of the night, phone ain't work so couldn't call anyone. When we were sleeping we could hear noises in the walls all night long. Honestly a terrible experience was the saddest hotel I've been in. When we went to the office the next morning to notify them of this before we even said anything they knew something was wrong as well as other people in the office were there to complain.

Jules is tucked away behind the Chili's and Barnes & Noble so you have to drive around the back (Noble Train Station).  The crust is thin (super thin and crunchy but not burnt).  The toppings and sauce are fresh and organic.  They have great, rare, original combinations.  It all taste awesome.  They also have plain cheese or the Brooklyn thats a slight variation of plain, but so good.  They also offer Gluten Free pizza if you pre-order it and offer gluten free goodies too.    The service is fast, polite and friendly every time.    They sell slices so you can try their different creations.  I highly recommend it!

Bought a beautiful car from this place. The experience is where things went bad. I was sold GAP insurance that was never purchased by them for me thus they stole $400 from me. I called numerous times and no one would return my call nor resolve the issue. We now know why "used car dealerships" have such a bad rep. I would never purchase another car from this place nor recommend anyone to take a chance with them. Super shady and dishonest!

Nice sized menu, but food is just mediocre and service has been slow all three times we have been there. It's the only sit down place in the immediate area which, I think, is the only reason it is still in business.

If you want soggy chips, a literal tree trunk in your bowl, and some mystery mush that they pass as quinoa then this is your place. I don't fault our server, as she was polite and seemed to be new... but when half of the food is dissected and laying on the table shouldn't a server ask what's up? I don't know? What I do know is that I'll never come back here again. VILE!!!!!!!

First time eating Indian cuisine, and my family and I ordered delivery from Uber Eats. We wanted to try an array of dishes, so we went with 2 appetizers, 2 chicken dishes, 2 lamb dishes and 2 vegetable dishes, which included chicken tandoori and chicken tikka apps, butter chicken, chicken korma, lamb curry, lamb saag, dal palak and baigan bhartha. We also ordered a side of Raita, which we all loved! While we loved everything we ordered, we narrowed it down to our favorite dishes, which included chicken tandoori appetizer, chicken korma, lamb saag and dal palak. We will definitely be ordering from Flames again soon! Wish I would've taken some pictures, but it smelled so delightful that we all just dug in!! Highly recommend Flames! The owner is super nice and helpful too!

Came here and was told one thing... Key Lime Pie is a must!


That's all I ordered  and it was well worth it!  I like sour candies, but never really had a sour dessert.. it's tart and doesn't have a fake texture like Jello-type key lime pie.. it was more of a smooth cream cheese texture.

Zak, our waiter was quick on drink refills and service was fantastic!!


The valet service was very nice too!

The food was wonderful. I had a bone in pork chop with asparagus and it was cooked to perfection. Their whiskey and bourbon selection is pretty solid. The interior is a little funky but overall not a bad place at all!

I'll start with the one positive part of this entire very expensive experience. The glass is truly beautiful. 

All the problems stem back to a sloppy owner who doesn't really give a sh@*!

The job was late 
Excuses 
The job was installed wrong 
Excuses
The job was short cut
Excuses 
Called for service 
No answer 
Called for service 
No answer 
Called for service 
No answer 
Called for service 
No answer

I used to walk past here all the time and never stopped in...until last week. It was nothing I expected - it took me back to college days, but with better booze. However, I was sadly disappointed by the disco lemonade. I was hoping it packed a bigger punch. I'll have to come back and spend some quality time with the pooch and pals.

I dont understand customer services these days. One of my pet peeves are stingy restaurants. For example, today I stopped by Sawatdee for a thai iced tea. I do not understand how "not too much ice please" translated to "don't fill the drink to the top". Seriously, how much can you save by being stingy to your customers. So I left with my drink, filled 3/4 of the way (picture attached), since I requested less ice. I even made a comment regarding to how much was filled, and they merely just added more ice. If you eat here, don't order your drink to go because you will be left feeling jipped. Also, their thai ice tea is terribly sweet!

Had a group of 10 here the other night. The food and service were both great. It has become a regular stop for our yearly trade show trip to Indy.  The steaks, scallops, and the salmon were all excellent choices according to the group we had there for dinner.
Chris at Kea plumbing is the best plumber in town. Always on time, professional and trustworthy. Thank you Chris for always getting the job done right the first time.

The wife and I took the daughter here for brunch on our way home. I ordered the blueberry pancake breakfast w/ bacon, it was good plus for whatever reason ( maybe cuz the syrup spilled on them?) the waiter brought me two more eggs which was also good. The wife ordered the dungeoness crab omelette which she liked even though it smelled like death. The daughter ordered a apple French toast thing which she initially liked until she found a couple of hairs in it more than one no fun!!!!! The waiter gave her a 25$ gift card when she left but she plans on giving it away cuz hair ewww soo I cant say I've been back to places that got me like that.

Absolutely amazing!!  American-Asian Fusion spot.

The ambiance is so different at the main ground floor level from the rooftop level. Ground floor has an old school, but classy vibe. Rooftop has a more laid back and fun vibe. My friend and I chose to go to the rooftop, don't regret it!

The bar tender/server was very attentive and gave me good recommendation on the French onion soup and the crab pad Thai. The French onion soup dumplings are brought in a bowl with open slots designed for them and are very hot (have to wait a minute or two!) The Pad Thai had mostly tofu and egg as the protein, an OK amount of crab, would have liked a bit more. Overall not too greasy and didn't feel too heavy. It is a big portion of noodles, so be prepared to leave some behind or take with you!

The only thing that was awkward was that he said his shift is over and asked if we would mind if our tab was closed (assuming he wanted the tip, which is fair. He deserved it, but awkward). We politely asked him to leave it open because we weren't finished ordering drinks!

Currently standing at the front desk with my dog for an appointment that was made for ten minutes ago. Customer service is nonexistent thus far. Went and got an associate from the store who said the groomer was being picky (unsure what that means) and he would be with me in a minute. Not what I expected.

Went here for lunch on someone's recommendation. They don't have a lunch combo special (like teriyaki, salad, and 2 pieces of nigiri, or whatever) but they do have all AYCE sushi. $20 for lunch. It's almost cheaper to get that than to get two rolls and an app separately, so I did it. I was hoping for more of a cheap lunch combo thing for less food and less money since I wasn't starving, but oh well. The sushi was fine -- not off the charts, but not weird and smelly. Service was nice. If you're starving and feel like stuffing yourself with sush, try here. If you want a more particular experience (classy date night, etc), branch out.

Absolutely horrible service, DO NOT go here.  We tried this place for a second time to give them the benefit of the doubt, they continued to fail.  Server Emily took a long time to greet us, took our drink order (water and a draft beer) then went to another table and took their drink order, food order and chatted it up with them. Five minutes later no water or beer yet the table behind us got their beer.  Also saw the table she gave food to prior to greeting us have to go and ask another server for silverware. 

Will ABSOLUTELY NOT GO THERE again.  

And no, they were not busy. We sat in the bar patio area which had four servers so no excuse for the poor service.

Katina's is very small, locally owned Greek shop in the Sponge Docks area.  Here you can find a tiny assortment of Greek and Italian imported goods e.g. cheese, wine, greek olives and olive oil, hand made soaps, canned grape leaves, etc.

I ventured to the Tapron Springs for the annual Sponge Docks Seafood Festival and wandered into Katina's because I saw a sign that boasted of free wine sampling.  Of course, I had to go check this out.  There were a couple of guys sitting out front as I ventured into the teeny store; saying that it was a 12' x 12' room may be a bit generous.

Finally, the store owner - one of the guys sitting out front -- came in and asked if he could help me find anything. I let him know that I was there for the wine tasting...and the fun began.  I told him that I was only interested in dessert wines so he gave me a sample of:

1.  Sodap Commandaria Saint Barnabas -- a raisin wine
2. Cocoa di Vine (Chocolate & Wine) -- grape wine with chocolate and cream (not sure that this one is imported though)
3.  Luccio Moscato D'Asti

I'm not really a connoisseur but I enjoyed all 3 and bought a bottle of each! The owner was very nice and told me a little more about wine #1. Apparently it is one of those deals where you only drink an ounce or so.  Aside from that he gave me a small discount for buying 3 bottles.

As I approached the restaurant for my 7pm reservation, I didn't see a soul in the dining room even though Open Table said they were booked. Should have been my first clue.  But, there were patrons in the back courtyard. The hostess could not have been more charming.  The waitstaff was equally as kind.  But the food was not as kind.  Or good. I love turtle soup and it was why I chose this restaurant since I saw their online menu. First bite-nothing. Second bite-still nothing. It was completely flavorless.  They offered me the gumbo instead.  Same story.  Watered down. If there are two things a that a NOLA restaurant should get right very easily, it's those two dishes.  I have never done this before, but I cut bait , paid for my wine, and left. I really wanted this to be a great dining experience since this old "Phoenix" has risen from the ashes, but I can't imagine the food quality will sustain them, unfortunately.

This is a small little bar located (and connected to) directly behind the Azalea Grill.  They have a nice side patio that's actually larger than the interior of the bar.  They have crawfish boils every Friday (during the season), ladies night on Wednesday and other specials along with occasional live music.  The patio definitely seems like an awesome hidden away spot to enjoy a nice day.  This is certainly one of the cooler bars on Jeff Hwy and I will certainly be returning to enjoy the patio.

They only have a Facebook page and no direct website so Yelp won't let me enter that for the address so you will just have to use Google to find that page.

I must have had bad luck. Went for lunch with a friend- the waitress should be a tax collector instead of working in the service industry. My raviolis were bland and literally floating in a milky sauce. It was not what I would call a cream sauce. Then it had some weird gross crumbly thing on top. Yuck! I would only go back because I saw so many great reviews. My friend's dish was soupy too...

I really like the Pub at Penn Valley but really wish it was not always so crowded.  There is no place to wait and they don't accept reservations. So, you take your chances on getting a table.  Since I usually go here with my husband, we are always prepared to sit at the bar which is really just fine. 

We usually go for the apps, salads and sandwiches rather than the entrees which are a little pricier. I especially like the "wedge" salad and the pork sandwich. The mac'n'cheese and other specials of the day are also good bets here.   Overall, you get a decent meal for a moderate price. And if you like sports, there are plenty of TVs. Bartenders are friendly and the food always comes fast.

Critiques:  It is a bit noisy and they could have a better  variety of craft beers on tap.

The food was delicious. The Mac and Cheese was very delicious - I definitely cleaned my plate. The ice cream was especially good. You could definitely tell it was homemade and definitely hit the spot on the hot day.  The only downfall we had was the service was a bit slow. The service assistant was amazing and had our drinks refilled before we were even halfway done, but we had to reach out to another server before so we could order ice cream and get our checks. We had been sitting (with our plates removed from the service assistant) for a bit before we decided to get the neighboring servers attention. I would still reccomend going back!

There are only three BBQ restaurants I frequent here in Reno, and Texas Soul is absolutely one of them.  It's all so good that I have a hard time pegging a favorite item, but if pressed I'd have to say the pulled pork and the cornbread.  Jeez, my mouth is watering as I write this.  

The most important thing I need to mention is that the sauce is not only delicious, but also not like any other available in the area, and the prices are very reasonable.  Texas Soul is easy to get to, too.  One block east on Moana, just off the freeway exit in the shopping center on the left at the light.  Totally worth the trip!

My sister invited her close friends and family to have bridal pedis at Kim Nails.  This was my first visit to this salon, and considering there were 6 of us getting our nails done pretty much all at the same time, the staff and technicians did a very nice job with the pedis and getting us done in just a few hours.  I found the stations to be clean and the tools looked to be sanitized and sealed.  Of course, I always like having a massage chair when getting a pedi.  Jonathan, the young man who worked on my feet did an exceptional job and he was friendly and personable.  The cost was $25 per pedi. 

There are just two little suggestions that keep me from giving them 5 stars.  One, I think having 6 pedis booked for a bridal party, a small discount might have been in order or a complementary glass of wine.  Second, after our nails were done, we waited about 15 minutes in the chairs with no instructions.  After a while, we took out our toe dividers and went to pay.  While were were at the cashier, one technician belatedly caught us and sprayed our toes with what should have been a drying or polish setting spray.

My wife's a bit of a "cupcake connoisseur", so when we heard about this local cupcakery/froyo/gift shop we decided to hop into the car and make the Pilgrimage. 

Jilly B's is located in the inside-corner of a very unassuming shopping center, right down the street from Virtua Hospital.  When we arrived, we were surprised to find that they have recently changed their hours (from what was listed on yelp) and that the shop was closed.  Luckily, the gentleman cleaning up inside was very friendly and let me make a quick "after hours purchase".

I asked for 2 vanilla/vanilla cupcakes and watched as he patiently opened and closed several airtight containers, presumably holding fresh-baked cupcakes ready for storage for the next day's business.  Meanwhile I checked the place out - it was very tastefully/whimsically decorated and had some nice/attractive merchandise available in the gift shop. After finally locating our cupcakes of choice, he carefully boxed them up, rung me up, and sent me on my way with a friendly smile.  Excellent customer service!

When we arrived home, we unboxed the cupcakes and dove in.  The cupcakes were VERY fresh, moist and tasty.  My wife mentioned that there was a tad too much icing, which was very sweet and tasted like it had some cream cheese in it.  The icing was very clean, however, not the oily mess that some cupcake places put atop their cakes (I'm looking at you, Brown Betty).  The cupcakes were very soft, though, and fell apart in our hands.  I assume if given a night to firm up, they would have been perfect the next morning.  

All-in-all a great place to grab some tasty cupcakes, we'll be back.  Perhaps not the "cupcake holy grail" we've been after, but certainly better than many of the cupcakes I bring home from work (in Philly).

Oh, and they serve La Colombe coffee - a rarity in South Jersey. Worth the trip alone.

My four year old had her very first pediatric  dental appointment at Dental Dreams on Cottman. We got there 14 minutes late. Three families with children that arrived at least 45 minutes after us, without appointments, were called in before us. We weren't called in until one hour and thirty-one minutes later.  During our first ten minutes in the back room, several staff members walked by us and yet, not a single person said hello. The exam rooms, much like the back staff, were dark, gloomy, dirty, and out of date. After a few more minutes of being ignored in the dungeon, I took my child's hand and walked out. The only good experience was the one nice receptionist out of four that sat upfront. Unfortunately, every receptionist left us to rot in the lobby for almost 2 hours and none were trained dentist or hygienist that could examine my child's teeth.  We scheduled a new appointment with Doc Breslers Cavity Busters the same day and had a much more pleasant experience. Doc Bresler's was five-star-dental royalty compared to the treacherous, motel-ish Dental Dreams office. My advice to parents with young children: avoid Dental Dreams like The Plague and bring your kids to the wonderful, creative, clean, colorful, courteous, and timely Doc Bresler's Cavity Busters instead.

Typical froyo without a place to sit. 
Good selection of froyo and toppings. Typical pricing, but no seating. Had to take it to go.

We arrived early on Saturday, about 10 minutes before they opened. Walked around used the restrooms ( very clean) & waited for ALL the shops to open!  
Food court had only 4 places to choose from, but "on a good note"- 1 of them is Charley's & they have amazing subs!
First visit the Oakley store. They were having a sale 2 for $25 deals and a clearance special with additional savings. Ended up buying two shirts and 4 pairs of socks for less than $30. Sales associates were pleasant.
Went over to the Nike store and to the Under Armour. It has a separate area for all the Youth Sports apparel. We got some great finds here for our kids. Sales associate here too, were wonderful & even got me signed up to receive coupons via email @ no additional cost!
Many sales & clearance bellow store prices and that's what "Outlets" are suppose to offer!
We will "for sure" shop here again!

We lived here for 3 years up until August 2014.

They had a major bug problem that ruined the carpet (we had no pets and are fairly clean people, plus we're never home). They charged us 600 dollars to replace it. We found out later, they just put in hard wood floors and were charging the former tenants to pay for it.

The area is nice, people are okay. Some ghetto assholes can ruin an entire building, but mostly everyone's chill.

I would say other than the BUGS, BUGS, ROACHES AND MORE BUGS, it's a decent place to live.

This Dominos Pizza is completely garbage, ive ordered from this location 2 times. Both experiences were terrible, the first time my pizza was unattractive to look at and did not taste as it should. My second time ordering at this location ive taken a picture of what my pizza looked like as it was delivered. My pizza was also not the pizza that I ordered, justice must be served!

They served complimentary bread and it was warm fresh and delicious, and I was really excited to eat here.. until our entrees came out :( my boyfriend got the lasagna and it was stone cold in the middle, while I had a pasta dish with absolutely zero flavor and an overwhelming lemon zest. They charge to much for the quality that they serve, to bad.

The ice cream was average and the selection was decent but god awful slow service. I 1000000% do not recommend getting any fresh squeezed beverage. It took at least ten minutes to get a lemonade. Tasted fresh but only a few minutes shy of growing your own lemons, they definitely need a way to expedite this option.  There was plenty of staff but flow was lacking severely. If you are just dying for ice cream, there are probably better shops along the way. I cannot speak to the fudge or chocolates which could be amazing, the ice cream was overly average and resembled that of a grocery store purchased carton.

I had lunch here with a buddy. Sat at the bar where I asked the bartender for food recommendations. She rattled off a few items (this is likely one of those places where everything is good). I decided on their wings with both dry rub & buffalo sauce, and I can say that these are easily some of the best wings I've ever had! 
I met the owner & pit master after lunch and gave them my compliments. 
The service, the food, we're all fantastic! Only reason I'm not giving 5 is it wasn't as upscale as I'd like, but that's a personal preference. I definitely recommend if you're looking for good BBQ.
Hey when you list hours of business you should probably be open when I drive 25 minutes to get there Saturday morning 10 a.m. doors are locked so I wouldn't suggest this place on a bet it sucks

Last night we decided to drive thru for some food and snacks. The line wasn't super long, but.... First it took us 40 minutes to get through. We were going to leave but at that point it was a game and we wanted to see how long it would take. Then, an employee came out the door to deliver to a car and she was wearing flip flops and socks. The slide on kind of flops and socks. Pretty sure that's againt code. The food was a mess, the order was all wrong but no way we were getting back in line. And last, why on earth do EMPLOYERS let their employees, IN THE FOOD INDUSTRY, wear super long cheap looking fake nails? Long fake nails of any kind. Come on, that can't be a good policy. Disgusting. And flip flop gal was back working near the window. This used to be a great DQ, I will stick to the Hampton store from now on.

I have been wanting to visit this place and Sunday for brunch seemed great. So I got there about 2 pm and we where sat promptly and the server greatest us in a timely manner. We order drinks and food after a couple minutes.  This all seems good and normal but then it all went down hill. I ordered the breakfast burrito and it took over 40 minutes. I asked the server why it was taking so long and her answer was " We where busier earlier". They were not to busy at this time so I was confused on why eggs and sausage can take 40 minutes to make or what earlier had to do with now. Finally food arrived and the could not complete our order because they ran out of food. After we ate I complained to her how long it took to get our food and again her excuse was they where busy earlier. Geeze I hope she can find better excuses for bad service. With so many brunch options in Nashville I probably will not be back here and truth is I was busier earlier also.

I have been in Nashville for several years and never found a shop I really loved. Some are too pricy, or slow, or in an inconvenient location, while others kind of seem like they just straight up don't know what they are talking about or they are trying to fix things that aren't broken just to get your money. That being said, we brought our van in here for several issues after we saw the good Yelp reviews. We had just gotten an oil change and Midas informed us of some things that needed to be worked on. Then we noticed some coolant leaking issues, so we decided to address all the issues at once. We brought the van to this shop with no notice or appointment at 8 am. They looked at it immediately. We showed them the Midas paper work and they told us that most of that stuff did not need to be addressed at all. I always appreciate this since I don't know much about cars. We left the car there and within a couple of hours, they had informed us of the coolant leak issue and the price to fix it. They got it fixed in no time. We picked the car up that same afternoon. These guys were friendly, knowledgeable, honest, professional and reasonably priced. We will likely make this our go to shop from now on

I've been here a few times now for nails & pedicures.. & they are always efficient & consistently do a good job. The space is generally quite clean & i believe they have tea available but I've never been offered any.  My reasons for 3 stars is the inconsistencies in the pricing.. I've paid 3 different prices for a fill when I was getting basically the same service each time.  When I added on a manicure it was cheaper than when I didn't get it but had one broken nail.. both of which cost me more than their advertised full set price though.  So my advice to you would be ask at the start exactly what they are doing & what the price will be so there are no surprises because they are really good at trying to up sell services! 
 So although I find the pricing issues annoying.. I like how they turn out so I'm going to just be sure to ask ahead & try & guarantee that I don't pay more than I expected in the end

I wanted to give this place 0 stars...I don't know who the manager is in this place but it's dirty as hell!!! I didn't even want to order because the food is kept sloppy and everything was dirty..the are gotta be clean to serve food..the staff were also NOT one bit friendly and they were out of stuff and we gotta stand in Line Waiting for someone in the back cutting meat on her own pace and someone else pouring out something out the back..they were all just standing around with a sad face!!! I would never go here again...Chipotle need to do better..
I felt welcomed and was greeted as soon as I hit the door! The food was delicious and cheap!! Cash only though. I will definitely be back .

It is unfortunate that the customer service is so terrible because this is definitely the place to come if you are party planning. I walked in and asked a couple question about balloons and was looked at like I have was such a nuciance. Heaven forbid you actually have to do work at your job. I then asked if they would allow me to choose the arrangement to which I was thrown a pencil and post it for me to write it down. Rude all around. Doesn't make me want to go back. It is really unfortunate that customer service these days has gotten so bad.

I've lived in Nashville for 22 years and I had never heard of this place until last night when I was invited by some friends. That was a red flag to begin with. It's rundown, the bathrooms aren't clean, the bar is boring. The food was less than mediocre. The chips were old. This just isn't a good restaurant.

I ordered a house salad with grilled chicken.  The menu said romaine with leafy greens.   It came with mainly iceberg lettuce and a couple sprigs of leafy greens.   Disappointed and did not eat salad.  However everyone who ordered seafood enjoyed their meals.  Large portions and nicely prepared.

I can't believe I've never heard of this before today! A friend of mine mentioned it this morning on our run. So, since I got my work out in, headed over. The variety is amazing-had the bananas foster scone and hand pie (egg and chorizo) for breakfast and am saving my cupcake and oatmeal creme pie for later today (if I can control myself).  Will definitely be back.

Been meaning to check this place out. Went to the one on Magazine Street and loved it. This one - I think the population of the folks is what threw me off a bit. 

Service: good. 
Food: good. 
Price:  could be better. But not bad. 
Ambiance: hipster/idk what to call them/a bit ... Just different than what I expected. 

I ordered the beef enchilada with pinto beans and yellow rice (instead of black beans). The enchilada was great. I ordered more jalapeños tho, because they only gave a few. 

It was a good meal; there was nothing bad about the place, service, or food. I believe I was just expecting a different type of clientele. 

I would likely go up the street to taqueria Guerrero next time.
This is a horrible rental agency, which gouges customers! Horrible service. $243 for a single day!

Been here three times and all disappointing. First, walked in at 3:15am and the guy repeatedly yelled at me for being closed(door was open and sign said "Open") and offered no apology. Second, ordered a delivery for work and delivery showed up an hour late. Third, asked the guy 3 times if this was the cookie with the peanut butter cups on top and not the peanut butter flavor, returned home to find the peanut butter flavor. This place shows absolutely no urgency when busy, employees are outside smoking cigarettes and workers are slow inside while people wait impatiently in line. Fantastic cookies, but despicable management and customer service satisfaction.

Nerdiest bar ever! absolutely love the atmosphere & endless board game selection! and the food is INCREDIBLE! Not at all your typical bar food, that stuff is GOURMET!  Great beer & liquor prices! My fav place to shoot'em back! The staff is the best! LOVE, LOVE, LOVE, Bad Wolf!!!! Also, the annual BarCon is a must! Greatest hidden gem in the Westbank!

I am in love with the downtown location of Robust! 

I arrived on a Saturday night with 2 others. We did not make reservations only because we had no idea where we wanted to go for dinner until about 10 minutes before we arrived downtown. We are bad at planning apparently! Well, when we arrived I noticed the place was busy (not surprising for a Saturday night) but it was not packed! This is a good sign. 

We stood at the main entrance for about 2 minutes before anyone greeted us and when we told her we did not have reservations she looked at the seating chart and said that she would be able to seat us right away! Whoa! That is kind of a big deal. No wait at all. 

Once we arrived to our table we were immediately greeted by the warmest and friendliest server, Neiko. She was a true gem and I could tell she was having a great night. I really enjoyed having her as our waitress. She made my night so much better! Neiko took our drink orders and made recommendations when we asked for some idea of what to order. She explained the menus and left us alone to go place the drink orders. When she arrived back to drop off our drinks we were ready to place our food orders. I ordered the Robust salad to go with my glass of blended red wine. I am not sure of what the others ordered since I was too busy enjoying my wine.  Neiko was able to provide information on their specials for the night and she gave a ton of recommendations for what would be a good meal. 

Once our orders were placed we were able to sit and chat. I was checking out the restaurant and I must say that  the atmosphere made me feel really relaxed. For some reason there was a calming presence whenever I looked at all the wine bottles! Before we knew it out food was being delivered and Neiko was checking on us to make sure we were okay. We had everything we needed and so she left us alone to check on her other tables. 

By the time we were done eating we weer all stuffed and ready for our check. Neiko brought it over immediately (but of course she had to offer us dessert)! I resisted and the bill was paid. We were on our way to see a movie across the street so we didn't have much time for lingering. 

Overall, I give this a 5 star just because we were seated right away on a Saturday night, I think we had exceptional service, the food was delivered quickly and it tasted great! I can't wait to go again soon.

It's probably a little silly to pull into a bar and not order brewskis, but we had miles to go, and find driving hard enough when sober.
So, we got iced tea and a dozen oysters for about twelve bucks.

Ollie's is definitely a dive, and would probably be insulted if you called it anything else. It's possible to eat inside, but to do so would be to lump Ollie's with your average watering hole in Scranton or Omaha. So we scooted out to their deck which overlooked a slimy canal within hailing distance of the gulf. The iced tea was just fine, and refilled when requested. The oysters were maybe as good as one can expect in July.

I will not make any invidious comparisons with Deliverance, but Ollie's is not a place where you would want to flaunt your liberal or northern sensibility. On the other hand, Ollie's is a genuine local hangout  within an hour of Clearwater, and if you want to go to Florida to hang out with people just like you, there's always Orlando.

I want to warn anyone looking or currently living at  The Lodge at Lakecrest, a UDR community. To those looking STAY AWAY. To the current residents: take pictures and good luck.  I wish I could say the opposite, but our time with Lodge at Lakecrest and the UDR corporation has ended nothing short of insulting, burdening, and heart breaking. I feel like I was completely taken advantage of by them and will NEVER live at nor recommend a UDR community ever again. 

I was one of three responsible, clean, and friendly adults to rent a 3br/2b for 12 months. During our time there, the maintenance staff was absolutely amazing. They are very helpful and complete tasks quickly and effectively. Although some of the office staff is also equally amazing, others lack compassion and caring for their residents. However, this is not the reason I am writing this review. This is a warning about the costs they will impose on you when you move out. We were charged over $1,500 for a "Carpet and pad replacement" for a carpet which was perfectly clean upon move out and other small fees and charges. I asked for a walk-thru before move out, and was booked with one of the managers, only to have a gentleman from maintenance show up. Normally this would be fine, but his report is clearly not being honored, even though it says "apartment in very good condition". They said this is because it was not the manager's report, even though that is what I requested.  Being naïve, we did not think to take final pictures, as the staff had been very friendly and honest before, and at the time I thought the walk-thru report would be honored. Needless to say, we were hurt and taken aback when we received such a charge for a stain that did not exist when we moved out. The "pictures" they provided to us were not labeled as to which room they were taken, were taken without any of us being present, and looked as though they came from a camera made in the early 2000s. Not to mention these "original photos" were sent to us as computer printouts and had 6 to a page (they were extremely small). This "stain" looked like a shadow from the window, and was towards the middle of the room. A stain that would have been clearly noticed by not only us, but also during the walk-thru. 

We contacted cooperate and thought we had a chance there, but were told "we are supporting the charges that the local office has given". Upon me asking for more information twice, I received no response. 

After they took out our security deposit of $500, we had 21 days to pay $1116 or have it handed to collections and our credit stained. We were offered an opportunity to set up a payment plan, but it still hurts to give money for something you didn't do. Especially being a graduate student who is paying for their school fully on their own. 

I am sad to say I will absolutely never rent from or recommend a UDR community ever again. It is truly sad such a great experience ended so bitterly. The best of luck to anyone who rents from this company.

Darling out door seating area. Extensive menu with everything from Sand Dabs to Lamb Burger. Some Itallian and some Mexican choices also. I'd read reviews and heard so much about this place that it was a huge disappointment to order crepes and have them arrive with whip cream from a SPRAY CAN instead of the real thing.

Upon our visit to this restaurant we had great service from our waiter. We were sitting at our table and wanted to play music so we put on a couple songs and we were listening to our songs when we realized some of them were skipped and our waiter informed us the the bartender Angela did not like our music choices so she skipped them because of her person choice in song. I'm not sure why us picking music and having a good time is such a negative time for her. She had the waiter come up to us and tell us not to pick that music anymore, she has no authority to be doing so because she is just a bartender. Very upset with the hostile environment that was created and we do not feel welcome to this establishment. We also observed her scold the staff in front of the everyone at the restaurant because they were serving tables and not getting to the food right away because they were busy, when she is at the bar serving 3 people drinks and they aren't drinking that quickly for her to be busy at the bar. I feel bad for the staff that works here with her, she should not be hard on these kids that are 17-18 at there first jobs. She should be ashamed of herself. We will not be returning here and we will let everyone we know not to come here as well.

To add to this the restaurant was very hot, we were told it was set at 80 degrees because that's what the bartender wants so we opened the window behind us to cool off and then when we left she told the waiter to make sure the windows are locked.

The 2 classes that I took here were cool, but not cool enough to get me to finish the 3 class trial I purchased.

I always felt extremely challenged during the classes and wonderfully exhausted afterwards, but I'm just not into rowing, I guess.

The coaches are really encouraging and friendly, so if you actually like rowing, it's probably worth visiting. Prices are reasonable, especially for class rates in center city.

I ordered 1 of each type of wings and large pizza 1/2 baked( so I could cook it later)the wings were over cooked burnt to a crisp. One bite and right in the trash
the pizza was more cooked then 1/2 baked but at least it wasn't burnt .  Husband said it wasn't bad, I felt pizza had no flavor .  So I had tomato sauce for dipping that save the dinner. $50 was way too much money for the quality of food I received.  avoid take out

I usually dont write stuff like this but I was super mad wouldn't even give a star to this restaurant...
Hubby and I was there last night hungry AF they took our order gave us nasty water and they serve other people before us that came in after us, this place wasnt even packed at all. The waiter looked at our table and just walk away not even telling us that our food was on the way, we waited 30 mins for (noodles) so we just walk out... hungry AsFuck!!! bad service!!!
No branch manager on site and a lack of personal bankers. If you need brains and something done in a timely manner, don't come here

Came here for breakfast/brunch, and reservations are recommended because they fill up quick. Once it was our turn for a table, stepping inside we were welcomed by gorgeous French inspired decor. There was light piano music playing that took me back to the saloons of Red Dead Redemption. The staff were great, very welcoming, super customer service. 
     Now let's talk about the food. My partner and I started with the turtle soup. Aged sherry was drizzled on top and I think it really did it's job of enhancing the soups flavor. Definitely recommend. 
     Next we got the Eggs Hussarde and Eggs Sardou. I loved them both. Both eggs were perfectly runny and oozy. The Sardou replaced the muffin for the base with a breaded fried artichoke which absolutely worked for the dish. The tower sat on a bed of spinach that were delicious. The sauce was made just right, making the whole dish super yummy. My favorite part of the Hussarde was the sauce. It was packed with flavor and tied the entire dish together. And the mushroom bits, wonderful! ~ 
     When at Brennan's you have to get the Bananas Foster. The dessert is flambéed right in front of you. I am not a sweet tooth at all, but you can never go wrong with roasted banana. You know a dessert is good when your partner who is less of a sweet tooth than you are, scraped his bowl clean and finished before you do. (10/5) recommend.

Stayed two night but was locked out after first day, went to the front desk to let them know my keys were not working he proceeded to tell me I didn't pay for two night which I did. I proceeded to show him my bank statement on the two transactions he then told me I needed to call my bank. I then did that and he told the bank that they were wrong. He then proceeded to fix it and tell me if he still don't see it went through then he will try to do it again but of course I didn't keep anymore money after that so it got declined on the third try. The morning to go check out i then get told to call my bank again which I did again...  after getting on the phone with a bank he proceeded to tell them I had two transactions at two different times. After all that I told them on the paper that was slid under my door this morning says I owe nothing. He then tells me that he sees it but it's not coming through their system. Which they told me I owed nothing after so much trouble with me calling my bank because their system is not working correctly...... the guy was rude as could get the first time. He proceeded to apologize as leaving but in general. The general manager was rude.

Food is awesome. Probably one of the best ramen spots I've tried, including the ones in NYC. Prepare to wait, even though you're staring at empty space and eventually you will be seated at the table you've been staring at for at least 30 min. The hostess is by far the worst at her job I've ever seen! 


*Update*
We went back for lunch yesterday and the dinner hostess wasn't present (or maybe she doesn't work there anymore) and the wait moved as it does at any other restaurant. Otaku was still packed, not an empty table in the place, but as a table got up another table was sat. Might have worked more smoothly because the owner was there....
Philly's best coffee. Cute local coffee shop with nice Italian style serving-ware. Right by Rittenhouse. Limited Hours, No Internet. Otherwise, perfect.

Living in Berlin for 10 yrs and just discovered this place a few months ago. Tried most of the local Mexican joints in the area but Maria ' s just keeps drawing us back.  Don't be fooled by the old style looks of this unassuming place, the food is top notch.  You are greeted warmly by Maria herself who puts her heart into each meal.  EVERYTHING we have had is excellent from the AL PASTOR tacos to the huge GUARACHES.  The fresh chips and GUAC is..you just have to taste it for yourself. Wash it down with freshly made Jamaica water.  Had the POZOLE soup this week with succulent chunks of meat and hominy. Just delicious.    Don't get me started on the CHILE RELLENO!

Very good bar food - burgers, ribs cheese steaks. Great bar crowd and excellent bartenders. Half price wings on Tuesday. Come early. Reasonable prices, both at the bar and the menu. All the staff are beautiful  (predominantly female ).

I love this place! The big windows make for a nice room to sit and enjoy an Americano in the morning. The people who work here are friendly and welcoming. The bottomless coffee is fresh, St. Louis roasted Kaldi's. Definitely affordable, $4 for two eggs, bacon (or sausage), southern fried potatoes and onions, and toast! I'm holding out the last star for when the potatoes get a little seasoning, but until then the salt shaker works just fine. Don't forget to try the raspberry white chocolate scone -ask them to warm it up for you! Yum!!

Vicia is definitely something different. If you are looking for a new perspective on fine dining and food in a broad sense, Vicia will play that role. The space is welcoming and very well designed, especially the patio that features poured concrete and a massive modern hearth at one end. Tables are either 2, 4 or communal.

Our service was fantastic from Nick. He was very knowledgeable about the wine list and nuances about ingredients. His descriptions were helpful and spot on. We definitely learned some new bits from him.

Cocktails were sensuous, each featuring an aromatic: Fennel, Rose, Hop and Citrus. Prices are fair at $9 for a complex cocktail.

Wine list was exceptional, showing interesting varietals from Austria, Greece and Eastern Europe. Bottle prices were also fair, without a significant jump from BTG to full bottles.

Cheese board with 3 interesting iterations of familiar cheeses: aged Gouda, Bleu Cheddar and a washed rind cheese that was memorable. Local honey. Root purree and walnuts.

Speck rollups with greens and weeds, dipped in a tarragon emulsion. Slight touch of tarragon and citrus, then very bitter from the greens. Looked to be dandelion, arugala and more. However, I'm probably wrong.

These two themes would continue: bitter greens and me not really understanding what I was eating.

Celery root carbonara was rich with plenty of bacon. Traditional flavors with a chewy veg noodle. 

Beet tartare was also rich with fantastic focaccia. It was essentially a citrus spiked purree with delicious egg fat. Best dish of the night.

We ordered 2 of the 3 mains. Turnips with beef neck and farro with horseradish vinaigrette was a bowl of braised 'pot roast' flavors. I realize now that I don't like turnips. Too sour and mealy for me.

Grilled King oyster mushrooms with wilted weeds, polenta and charred veg molé was a creative riff on a tamale, or other Mexican flavors. Cumin and coriander evident in the sauce. Greens were far too bitter and medicinal for me. Wife dug them.

Didn't do dessert, but left super full and with a bag of leftovers.

Four stars because this is a challenging concept, and some of the flavors have more intellectual purpose than purely for flavor, so I think we will need to grow into this one.

Appreciate the Union Loafers bread and menu design very much. Looking forward to how this changes with the seasons (or even micro-seasons).

In total, this is a fine experience, but one you definitely have to carefully consider who to invite to join you next time.

The most adorable, chill outdoor cafe here in the French Quarter. During breakfast time the lush gardens provide ample shade. The charm of the French Quarter really shines in this spot. Don't let the long line scare you, it moved fast enough and menus are available up front to grab and review while waiting. One order of beignets is enough for a breakfast meal; they are rather big (bigger than Cafe Du Monde). IMPORTANT ***you have to grab your own napkins*** (Necessary for beignet-eating) The major downside of this place is their cafe au lait sucks. It's pre-mixed and not frothy whatsoever. Really flat and blah. If you want an excellent cafe au lait go to the cafe right inside the Prince Conti Hotel. See my other review.

One of the servers needs serious customer service training. When I ordered food, he looked like "what are you talking about?" He also put my takeout food in front of me without saying anything. Very unfriendly.

The thing that bothered my girlfriend and I the most was the ignorant servers. If your not part of their in crowed then your treated like crap. When you disrespect someone you never know when it may come back to bite you in the PYT.

We gave Marlowes a second chance after miserable dining experience several months ago. This experience was equally as miserable. Terrible service, inability to get a drink that a manager had to eventually retrieve, order came out wrong, and seated next to one of their employees. She cursed and spoke so loudly the entire meal and consistently had staff surrounding her making conversation instead of working. The only pleasant experience of the entire meal is kudos to the kitchen because our food was actually delicious. Unfortunately we will not be going back anytime soon given everything else that occurred.

Unbelievable! Every single time I go in this store I am disappointed. Ross, TJ Max, Burlington coat factory are brand new clothing and are either the same price as Savers or Savers is priced higher for used clothing or shoes. WHY IS SAVERS prices on Men's name brand USED CLOTHING either the exact price or more EXPENSIVE. I find plenty of new clothing at Walmart with the same price, same exact brand and style at Savers used. Savers needs to be ashamed of itself. I will never donate here or shop here till they start pricing their donated items as a 2nd hand store. The Community donates their items to them to support this store and they turn around only to rear end the community. Corporate greed! For the community? Or for their own pockets? You decide.
Consistently delicious food and nice service. I know I can always count on In N Out for a good burger and shake, although the lines are usually long no matter when you go.

Went here with some friends on a weekday night. Had a good time but mainly BC of the company. 

Food was fine so no complaint there but process were kind of higher than expected for your typical bar food. My drink tab came out more than expected as well even though I only ordered 2 drinks. I should have found out how much it cost before ordering.

Bartender was nice enough but catered more towards regulars or her friends.

They do allow smoking and you have to pay for shuffle board to play...

Good food ! Stopped through for some ethnic cuisine and got just that!! Amazing falafel & delicious and large Greek salad !!  Hummus and warm pita bread were also delicious  highly recommended while passing thru college town central .

Used Best Dry Cleaners to hem my daughter's uniform. The hem was out in 3 weeks. I brought it back and explained problem. She redid it but it only lasted another few washes. 
  I brought another daughter's uniform to be shortened. We agreed on a length. We picked it up and it was way too long. I asked her about it. She had my daughter try it on and then I heard her tell my daughter  "your mom" so she tried to tell my daughter that I wanted it longer!  
 Now my daughter won't wear it so I have to take it somewhere else to get it shortened again. I've paid more in alterations than I did for uniform.

This place also came recommended by a local in the area. Stopped in as we were walking around the French quarter and again was not disappointed. The calamari was light and not greasy. We shared a 1/2 seafood platter which was plenty for two of us! The stuffed crab and crawfish were delicious! Not a fan of the broiled oysters but I haven't been able to acquire a taste for them anywhere. The alcoholic drinks were in huge glasses although we didn't try one this time. Would certainly recommend this place if you're looking for great seafood close to bourbon street!!

Fantastic food, great service!

This was my first time eating here and I enjoyed every moment of it. My friend and I sat at a little table and were quickly greeted by our very pleasant server who was more than happy to answer any question we had about he food. Since I'm a gluten-free vegan finding places I can eat is an adventure for me, so you can imagine how delighted I was to find this restaurant.

Once we placed our order, the food was served in a timely fashion and every bite was delightful and bursting of flavor. I was so impressed with the creativity these meals consisted of. And my friend, who is not vegan, enjoyed her meal just as much as I enjoyed mine. 

Highly recommended for any individual who loves great service and amazing meals!

I would love to provide a review but sadly I sat for 15 minutes and was invisible lol.  Greeted friendly and place looked clean other than my table had sugar on it.  Wasn't so busy as to be forgotten in my opinion anyway ;)

I was highly disappointed when I got here and did not find Michael, Tito, or Germaine.  Just kidding. This is a small fenced in area that is located between the Cathedral and Washington Military Battery Park. It spans 2.5 acres and some call it New Orleans most recognizable landmark. it is very clean and extremely well maintained. It has a small fountain, some very nice gardens, a few benches to sit and nice paved walkways. But its main event is its bronze statue of General Andrew Jackson, which depicts him riding a horse and tipping his hat. This statue can be found in many New Orleans travel advertisements. 

The square was originally called Place d'Armes and was designed by architect Louis Pilié. It was redesigned by the Baroness Pontalba in 1914. Much of her design remains today and incudes an iron fence, formal gardens, walkways and benches. The following year, in 1815, after the Battle of New Orleans, the square was renamed "Jackson Square" in honor of the General.

The square now hosts weddings, festivals, Caroling in Jackson Square, and TV and movie sets. 

The park is very picturesque so, it will obviously be a popular destination for photographers and photo shoots. It can also be rented out for weddings.

Fun fact: In 2012, the American Planning Association selected the Square as one of its "Great Places in America" for beauty, accessibility and being a "place where people want to be."

This is a nice stop on a larger sight seeing trip and you can get an awesome photo of it with the Cathedral in the background from Washington Military Battery Park across the street. You can not leave New Orleans without getting that picture. So, regardless of your sightseeing plans, you will definitely end up here because it is centrally located near many of the city's other wonderful attractions.

If you like to have long nightmares, then be an idiot and come and sign hells application!
Tried this place for the first time, and it is very good! It was not greasy but had a lot of favor. Going back for sure, the only negative is I thought it was a bit pricy. $1.20 to add cheese.

Typical meh-to-bleh fast food but Carl's Jr. has the best rotating low-price specials of the fast food burger chains. 

$5 for a small Big Carl combo (be careful not to order a Hot Carl by accident) seems to be ongoing, it's been in place since the summer here at least. It hits the spot when you want a big sloppy double cheeseburger, soda and fries for cheap and don't care too much about quality. 

2 for $3 breakfast biscuits the highlight. They're suprisingly thick and filling, one usually settles me by itself for a quick breakfast. Apparently they bake the biscuits on site too. I think these are an ongoing special too?

This is also a decent location to sit, clean every time I've been and with slightly padded booths and table service it's a little more like a diner than the usual fast food place. Not much traffic inside anytime on a weekday. Bathroom was a disaster zone the one time I tried to use it though. On the whole still way better than those two amazingly ghetto locations off Market in SF though.

We love Sal's for their pizza. Close, quick, and delicious. There are a lot of really good food places in this area and we have a go-to depending on our order. Sal's has great plain pies, nice thin crispy crust, delicious sauce, and great deal for 2 plain pies. 

We also frequently order their BBQ and Buffalo chicken pies. The BBQ chicken pie is our favorite in the area. The Buffalo isn't the best in the area but it's REALLY good and mild enough for the wife and kids to eat, so it gets the overall family vote. 

VEGGIE WHITE PIE. If you like white pizza this is one of the best around. 

Salads aren't bad, we usually go with the Italian. Sandwiches are okay, we usually go elsewhere if we want those things...BUT the Mt. Holly Fries...mmmm mmmm. Not great for the waist line but delicious!

Check them out, great people, been around a long time.

Just the right kick with the sauce, crispy breading & tender inside. We also had the chicken and dumplings... DELICIOUS!!! Oh, & the hot chicken tenders... you won't be disappointed!

Just got another awesome hair cut by Nick Sr. I have been going to Richard Nicholas (to Nick) for maybe 24 years now? Haven't had my hair cut anywhere else in that time. I live in Bucks County now but I'm always ready to go to Philly for my hair!
Honestly everything about the salon works on all levels. Highly recommend!

Our server was nice.  The food was not exceptional.  Our appetizers had a great presentation but were on a paper coated in grease.  very unappealing   nothing special about anything we experienced  my salad came with a "peeled tomato"  it tasted and looked like a tomato out of a can of stewed tomatoes   Needless to say it was a disappointing experience   Hope you all fare better
The food was great, it's too bad the servers were the most rude people we came across in New Orleans. Eye rolls, sarcastic jokes, and snobbiness all in one place!! Save it!!

Blind Pig Score: 16

Burger Quality: 3
Bun: 3
Fries: 3
Extras:  3
Value: 4

2 Sentence Review: High value burger, nothing overly fancy...mildly hipster atmosphere...worth a return trip, which I don't say often. The folks here care like Brad Pitt and what's her face. 
 
------------------------------------------- 
How does Burger Einstein's rating system work? 
- Burgers are rated across 5 categories (see below) 
- Category scores can range from 0 to 5, with 5 being "Outstanding".   
- Total score (out of 25) is determined.  Scores above 20 earn 5 Stars (rare), between 16 and 20 gets 4 stars and so on 
 
Burger Einstein Categories 
1. Burger Quality: Good meat or Bubba Burger? 
2. Bun: Does it compliment the burger or overwhelm it?   
3. Fries: Do I accept the guilt or compare them to my Ex 
4. Extras: Wild card.  Could range from Cheese to bourbon to atmosphere 
5. Value: Burger cost relative to smiles (heavily influenced by how much bourbon I drink)
Good food. Service is decent. Wine is good. I sometimes wish this place was slightly more casual but other than that I like it. It's also good for happy hour for appetizer deals and wine deals.

I bought a Groupon for an alignment and tire rotation. I booked two weeks in advance and dropped my truck off to get worked on. The massless desk guy never greeted me and just handed me a paper to fill out without even saying anything. I fill it out and go to tell him it's pulling right and steering is loose. He is not listening at all and cuts me off saying alignment has nothing to do with steering and ask a mechanic. So I'm like, ok bye, rude much? He said he'd call when it's done. Two hours later, no call, so I call and someone says they'll check, and that its done come get it. I show up and the same desk guy says we could not do the alignment but we rotated and filled your tires. The rust was too bad to do alignment. I'm not thrilled but I understand if they decided to not work on it but they should've known that right away and I never got a call it wasn't being done and did I want to come get it. It just sat there. Then he expected me to pay I believe and I said I wouldn't because no one told me the whole service couldn't be done. So I'll get a refund through Groupon. Fine, waste of time and bad service. Then I get home and see that in fact all my tires are in the same spot and have not been rotated. I know this because my front tire has a repair job and my back tires are snow tires. So they couldn't have been rotated either side to side or front to back. Straight up lie on top of everything. I call to tell a manger and am put on hold because of my phone number I believe he knew it was me. I have my husband call on his phone and he's answered immediately. With reluctance he's transferred to a manager to whom I tell my story. The manager says sorry no one called but my guys didn't lie about rotating tires and he'll go check on it. He gets back on the line and said it was too dangerous for them to do anything and nothing was done. He says front assumed it was done and wasn't lying. Makes no sense. So if you want your time wasted, to be treated rudely, to catch covid, to be lied to, this is your place

I actually come to this place on a regular basis. I even craved it during pregnancy. However, they have gotten rid of 2 of my favorite tacos. The cochinita and, just recently, the bistec. All my sisters and I agree the bistec is probably the best taco they have other than the fish, which I believe they sell a lot of. The got rid of the bistec once before then brought it back. They've recently gotten rid of it again and I have to reason to come back until it returns. I'd love the see the cochinita come back as well, but the bistec is a must. I refuse to be limited to the bland chicken or short rib taco since I don't eat seafood.(the fish and shrimp are my husband's favorite). If they bring it back, I'll change my review. Until then, they get 2 stars from me. Only because I like the queso and salsas. ‍

I'm a sucker for any place where you get free Peanuts and can leave the shell on floor. The atmosphere is super relaxed and the service has always been well above average. The food is always OK, but not great (hence 3 stars). The best part is that they have 2 for 1 happy hour until 7 pm!

Since we came across Victorias by chance one day we have not been able to stay away from there.I think i have just about tasted every dish and side they have to offer My lastest fetish have  been the crab cakes. I cant stay away from them. Yes it is real lump crab meat and is very good. I always have enough for two meals. . My husband always chooses either the bake chicken or the salmon which i have loved every time we purchsed it. The service is  ok and you will be waiting for the food but it is a very busy place especially on the weekends. So i will recommend you to take out and enjoy in the comfort of your own home.So you can unbutton your pants and let it all hang out afterwards.  The food is definitely worth the money.The only gripe that i have with Victoria is that she puts PINEAPPLE OR PINEAPPLE JUICE IN THE YAMS WHICH DOES NOT DO THE YAMS JUSTICE. PLEASE STOP PUTTING THAT IN THE YAMS.Or at least make an option to order them witHout. Not every one likes that taste in their soulfood. But other than that i have no issues. Victoria Please put down the PINEAPPLES.

Ich bin hierher gekommen um Fangear von lokalen Football Team zu kaufen. Der Store hat zwar Fangear aber nicht wirklich eine große vielfältige Auswahl. Lediglich die Standardware, die sich sowieso verkauft, gibt es dort.
Für einen Store mit diesem Namen hätte ich mir schon etwas mehr erwartet.
Excellent fried chicken. Its so crispy and flavourful. There was only 2 employees - cook and cashier. But they were so friendly. I would definitely come back!
Me personally I don't care for this market. They are so ridiculous with the prices and the portions it's pathetic.

Says the drive thru closes at 2am multiple times coming here no later than 1:45am to no service and when I'd get there before 1:45 there would be an obvious attitude from the man taking orders and there was no meat for any "big burgers" whatever that means

Stopped in for some cupcakes for no reason. Lots of employees getting everyone's orders and notice there was not one happy employee in there. Person who assisted me with my order she was not mentally there. You work in a bakery you should be happy! Management?? Give them a cupcake.

Beer is tasty.. ..****  spoke w manager. .  He made everything right..WILL DEFINATELY GIVE THIS ESTABLISHMENT ANOTHER GO.!!!!    service and food are lacking...  my daughter loved her pizza?? Fiancé ordered a chicken sandwich which she could only half eat because of the dry bland flavor. Not at all what we expected from a well known place . I chose the rainbow trout for the first and last time. The asparagus was the tastiest part of my meal ..  well besides the beer . Will not come back .WAY OVERATED.   !!! SEE ABOVE

Everyone that works here is extremely helpful and  friendly. Super clean and beautiful. But the best thing of all is that they really make your stay special. We are currently staying here for my girlfriends birthday and they wrote her a birthday note with chocolates and rose petals on the floor and bed such an amazing place with even better staff

The guys here are always so awesome. Their prices are competitive and they are very military friendly.  They are very competent and knowledgable unlike other vapor stores I've been to. Their juices are delish and I have never been disappointed!

We chose this restaurant because it is in the neighborhood, it's Italian and it's an independent business. Entering with high hopes, we we're disappointed in both the service, just meh, and especially the meal. Our salad was well dressed greens and tasty but expensive for the portion size. The Duo of pizzas which sounded fabulous disappointed on a few levels. First, lack of filling. Lots of dough, very little filling. I also felt the dough could have been cooked more. Very chewy and the cheese was not to our taste. This is a one and done for us. Sad since they are so close.

I called this restaurant for 15 minutes straight and no one picked up. Guess what? They don't answer! I even called El Dorado to ask a simple question and they kept redirecting the call to La Strada and kept ringing on and on. I wasn't willing to drive down there on Hot August Night and find parking for a "fine dining" restaurant that can't even pick up a phone call that has been ringing for the past 15 minutes. At least McDonald's picks up their phone.

The last time I was at Sugarbowl was about 2 months ago with a friend of mind. This is one of the best places in Edmonton to eat. The line up is long and you may have to wait for a while but it's totally worth it!!  The chicken and waffles are too notch....top notch!!!  Great selection of beers and our charcuterie platter was simple yet presented beautifully. Any friends and family that come from out of town that want a fantastic meal I'll always recommend Sugarbowl.
this location is dirty and nasty. ordering service is terrible. could hardly  hear over the loud music  blasting from the radio behind the counter.

Great for some fun family activity.

Shoe rental cost $3.50 for adults $2.75 for under 12. Price per game bowled varies depending on the day/time... but we paid about $2 per person per game. 

They have digital scoring on the overhead tv. (And you can change animations for more fun for the kids). If you have an issue, buzz the screen for assistance, and they are there in not time (even when your kids push the button and shouldn't have. Woops). Bumpers can be raised up so. I gutter balls for the kids.... ok, maybe a little for me, too. And also a rack/ramp assist gadget to use for your littlest of kids. My 2 year old used it! It helps so 1. They don't have to hold the ball (just push it down the ramp) and 2. The ramp gives it a little momentum that they may not be strong enough to do by pushing it themselves. 

Oh yeah, need a ball?  like most alleys, those are around for borrowing, too. A bit bumped up and well used, but they work. 

They have clean bathrooms, arcades (we didn't go to them), and concessions (we also didn't have any) and I'm sure more at this location, too... but we didn't look around a lot. Here to bowl! 
I believe also a putt putt in the summer. 

Wonderful fun for the whole family!!

The food here is just ok.. i've had better, i've had worse. The worst part about this place is the service! The woman who works the register is an absolute witch. She doesn't know anything about customer service, and she makes you feel like paying for your buffet is ruining her entire week. She is VERY rude. You will see what I mean when you go. She rolls her eyes, does not make eye contact, doesn't smile, is very impatient, doesn't thank you for your business, and pretty much acts like she can't stand you. She should be grateful for any business she gets for her mediocre indian cuisine.

We shared mixed appetizer plate (everything good but not memorable), I had a really delicious thick lamb soup, and a lamb platter with a pitcher of the mint tea (nicely served with a simple syrup to sweeten, nice touch). My husband ordered the platter with couscous but it came with a yellow rice pilaf which we just shrugged and dug into. The lamb was very tender and the rice was tasty and saucy - unfortunately though my husband who is extremely sensitive to MSG which is often found in packaged rice mixes within minutes got a MSG headache and throat swelling.  Of course we can only speculate on the source but the timing was suspicious, there were no other potential offenders all day, and in retrospect the rice really did look and taste like a packaged Spanish rice - never had any problems having eaten at dozens of similar middle eastern / falafel places.  Service was friendly but waitress was alone and also taking phone orders.   Given the number of choices in town at which we have equal or better food and no allergic reactions we will not likely take our chances with a return visit.

Tried this relatively new spot last week and was not disappointed. It was Tuesday so we did the Non-commital, 3 course wine dinner. Great pairings of wine with small courses. The house made sausage and polenta as a first course was very tasty and the rose paired with it was superb! Second course paired a Cakebread Chardonnay (which I rarely drink) with a duck raviloi, scrumptious!! The third course was a delicious chocolate torte served with a wonderful Terra d'Oro Zinfindel port! The service was very good and we loved the atmosphere. We will definitely be back soon.
The food was not good at all. I got sick after eating the slimy chicken on my chicken nachos. I'll stick with El Maguey.

Five Guys might need a few more Guys at this location for lunchtime. It is busy! 

Drawbacks include shared parking with the nearby businesses, but there is a lot under the building which has a good chance of having at least one spot open for you. Otherwise, you might have a difficult time finding a spot on the street, or having to pay to park. 

Overall, they deliver to expectation on the burgers and fries.

While i am and always have been an avid Best Buy shopper, it seems to me the store chain is just dying out. it used to be whenever I would go into a Best Buy, it was always busy, and full of shoppers, now it seems like there's an large amount of employees more than anything.

one more thing I have noticed is the pushiness level of the employees is getting to the "leave me alone" level. I have no problem with approaching an employee when help is needed, that's actually how I prefer it to be, what I don't like is being asked   "you finding everything ok?" 6 times in one department, by  different employees.  nor do I want to hear about the cable/ phone specials you are offering at that time, i am there to make a purchase and require no assistance unless I specifically ask you for the help.

I am still however giving a review of 4 stars at least, based on the products alone. I do like the products the store offers as well as the prices being comparable to the other few remaining similar store chains.

First time I was here was great. Food was great, service was great, everything was great. This time however, I orderedthe artisan avocado toast and it was not toasted AT ALL it was just bread although their was a good amount of avocado which made me slightly less annoyed. I also ordered the California flatbread and it would've been so much better if it was hot like it was so cold the bread, the eggs, the chicken sausage WAS COLD. It was still pretty good but it is disappointing knowing how much better it would have been.
This place was a great find. The food was excellent, many choices including ice cream for breakfast. Great place for a lay back Sunday morning brunch. Very reasonable.

Cold Brew was good, but the Honeysuckle Latte was pretty darn good. Breakfast was fine, definitely worth the price. Convenient and quick. Bottomless mimosas? Thought that was an interesting twist for a coffee place.

Spot is philly is cool. Vibes is on point. Staff is cool, even though one of waiters asked a weird question and gave a kinda offensive remark. But it's all good , no harm done.

Food was really good but come on guys, $19 for mantu?? That's kinda pushing it. But otherwise this spot is official.
I was giving my card numbers and as soon as the card went through she straight hung up on me. I wasn't even done with my order. Sloppy and unprofessional. Going back to Papa John's

I was looking for a good Cheesesteak on my recent visit to Philly, but was really disappointed as soon as I touched my sandwich. The bread was too soft, like a hoagie, and the meat was not generously served. When I tasted it, I put it down and decided to go to the next place to find a better cheesesteak. The place looks like a little while in the wall, so I had high hopes, but unfortunately the sandwich disappointed me. On my cheesesteaks, I look for firmer bread, like French bread, packed full of meat and cheese. I was really surprised that this philly was so bland. Don't waste your calories here.

For this day anyway...the pit bosses were absolutely unfriendly and so were the dealers.  Not sure why anyone would come back again!

Beer was also a rip off!  No happy hour!

I went to Cucina Forte last night with some colleagues and my only complaint is that I didn't find this absolute gem sooner.
