The food was incredible, some of the most authentic I've had outside of Italy, to which I've been 3 times, in different regions, the wait staff was friendly and accommodating, and the price was right. I'll definitely be returning, and I recommend very highly!

Well here is the dilito.  The food was amazing. I had chicken and waffles and the meatballs with gravy appetizer.  The meatballs were good but not much different the chicken and waffles was very yummy. Service was great once seated.  However, I had to stand outside 30 minutes in the cold ( when they could have let you sit inside and not freeze) and to make matters worse a table of four got in without any wait!  Because I was single and so was another gentleman we got discriminated by table size.  Unbelievable then, food is cooked to order and was a bit slow...but then friends of the owner came in and I noticed how fast their food came.  So, Yes it was good- a bit pricy I spent $25 all together but I would not stand in cold again, so they lost points for size of party discrimination and then friend discrimination :-( but food was amazing! So pick your battles :-)
** To respond to the response from the restaurant and clarify those tables were all not filled when arrived. There were two different parties of one waiting and the party of four walked right up for the table of four after both of us were waiting over 10 minutes.  There was a third party  (of two) but they got upset and left .  So regardless of term, discrimination or not, that is what occurred.

Avoid at all costs. The worst customer service I've experienced not just in New Orleans along the entire Gulf Coast. From the hostess to the manager, we walked out. This restaurant perpetuates an atmosphere of rudeness, condescending, & aggressive behavior. This place was the worst decision of Mardi Gras 2019. 

Go somewhere where they're friendly & happy to have your business!

Stopped by here with a large group and we were easily accomodated and given a table promptly. Food was delicious, very close to being authentic. Everyone enjoyed their food as well as the service.

The only downside would be the rude manager and they didn't have my fave, bun cha gio. Otherwise, I would have given this place 5 stars, even without bun cha gio. I was just dismayed by the manager's behavior.

I absolutely loved this place. I rarely write reviews unless I'm very impressed. The food was so good and the waiter Hak, was even better. He referred to all of us by name, did a nice new years toast, and played 80's jams and freestyle music for us. What else could you possibly want? Even my sometimes hard to please husband was very happy. Thank you! Will be back for more delicious food and great food experience.

I really wanted to like this place considering almost everyone at my office raves about the cakes & danishes here. I did have a danish & it was delicious;  however, the cakes I'm not impressed with. The cakes we have had at the office were "ok" & I ordered 2 cakes of my own to give them a fair chance & both were dry. The icing on the red velvet cake I ordered was very good. Maybe it was just my luck with my orders but I'm giving this bakery a little break.

We have been coming here since they opened years ago.  Never again.  

First complaint:  We arrived on a Thursday at 5 p.m. and asked to sit on the patio.  We were told there would be a wait - when we have never had to wait for patio seating before which is why we usually choose to sit there.  I asked why we had to wait when we had entered from the patio and it was virtually empty but full of tables that needed to be cleaned.  The hostess looked confused and said they couldn't seat us until the tables were clean and the wait would be 15-20 minutes.

After 20 minutes we returned to the podium and asked how much longer we were going to have to wait.  The same hostess informed us that they just cleared the tables and someone took us back to the patio.  We passed a table on the way to the patio that asked if we were seating on the patio and we told them yes.  They replied that they asked for patio seating and were told they weren't seating on the patio.  

We get to our table only to find every table that was a mess when we arrived were NOT cleaned off - so there was no reason for us to have waited 20 minutes.  I asked to speak to the manager.  Several tables were seated after us and their drink orders were taken and their appetizers were delivered before our drinks arrived.

The manager delivered our meals and was unaware that I asked to speak to him.  I asked him to return after our meal - never saw him again.

Second complaint:  2 weeks after that incident we went back on a beautiful Sunday around 2 p.m.  We again asked for the patio and were told the patio wasn't open despite there being other customers out there.  I asked for a manager and a different gentleman from before arrived.  I began to explain the complaints from our last visit and he cut me short where I didn't get to explain everything to him.

He advised that he had NO servers scheduled on the patio and that we could sit out there but that we would have to wait 45 minutes.  For what?  To order?  To get our food?  This manager was rude and condescending and acting like he was doing US a favor by not seating us on the patio.  

Like the other reviewer below - I advised him that we have been patrons for years and we wouldn't be back.  He told us to have a nice day or something to that effect.  

I contacted their corporate office and was advised that their manager couldn't make us happy and they were sad to see us leave but they respected our decision.  No offer to fix the situation and they didn't address how rude their manager was or why there was no staff sheduled for the patio.  I wrote a reply to their corporate response and have never heard back.
Jenna is awesome for Facials and Laser treatments. She is gently and explains everything she is going to do.
Free Wi-Fi, Hot drinks, friendly staff.  Parking can be a hassle.

Finally!! Trish is on YELP!!! 
Cleaned my house one year for the holidays. She was on time and did a great job. The house looked great!!

I found the cooking and preparation inconsistent. Sometimes it's great and other times its meh like today.

Today I ordered the seafood rice noodle soup. I told them I didn't want the imitation crab meat. The soup still came with it. I told them I wanted extra noodles, and I got a side bowl of cold dried out rice noodles. The side plate of vegetable topping came with bean sprouts that were starting to get that brown color of being not fresh, basil that was drenched in water, looks like it was being washed but not dried.
The broth lacked that nice saltiness and richness I had last time when I was there.

Stopped by here to get some ribs for Desoto Park. The service was good, and the food was great. Where are you going to find good ribs at $14.95 a full rack? We got the sweet potato fry's, the baked beans (excellent) and you have to get the fried okra. (great!) They serve orders with texas toast. 
They have several sauces. I recommend Rib City!

Customer service does not exist at this location . John was rolling his eyes and booking other customers in while totally ignoring us and telling us that our rooms where not ready ! We where physically at the hotel at 8 am confirmed we where here and left till 3pm and didn't get a room till after 4 pm !! Very disappointed with Hilton for allowing John to represent them with his bad customer service...

Pet friendly helps. Check-in staff were nice, but mold in room, blood on sheets, and overall not clean. We even found a small child's shoe under the desk when walking into the room for the first time, which makes me question if the room was cleaned in the first place. Not returning. Not good at all.

Beautiful decor, fantastic service -- there's an area in the back where they let you sit with laptops and hang around after you've eaten. Not the most authentic tasting nor particularly amazing compared to other options for Middle Eastern in Philly. But a wonderful, fresh vibe for a classy experience.

I went to this place a while ago for a birthday. The atmosphere was ok, seems a bit tacky but lots of warm colors and lights. The music in the background is a bit cheesy though, and typical of an average American style Mexican food restaurant. 

The food is average, a cultureless limbo like every other Mexican food stylized to American taste buds. Nothing too special. I had a stuffed burrito which had decent flavor, but it also came with Mexican rice and black beans. Mexican rice is very hard to get right, and the flavor was just nothing special. The black beans had no texture whatsoever.

At least they have margaritas. Alcohol is kinda strong, if you're a diehard tequila fan this is perfect.

I would say come for just drinks, chips, salsa, and little stuff. There was quite a crowd between the managers/waiters/waitresses and some of, I'm assuming their friends, really having a blast at the bar area. I'd say don't spend over $30-$40.

Overall a relaxed below average restaurant. Don't expect anything too special here. Save this place for a hangover or drink craving, not a special occasion.

This is "our place".  The place where we go for birthdays and anniversaries.  The hogfish is the best thing on the menu.  It has a light, delicate flavor topped with seaweed salad.  I love to pair it with a Limoncello martini

From Zach our waiter to Fidel the chef to the manager to the hostess to the great food.   Let me just say wow.   I had the firecracker shrimp and a special order blue burger.  And my wives salad and peal and eat shrimp.  Thank you.   This was our twentieth time at least and it is consistently good.  Never bad meal service or over priced.  This place rocks.  One more very important thing. My wife and I were not referred to as GUYs.   Thank you

Not the same as it was.
This location's owners no longer include fries with their signature Chicago dog. Now it is $1.75 for a small order.  And I do mean small. About a dozen or so in the basket with the dog.
That is, when I finally got my order fifteen minutes later at 11:30 on a weekday. Wasn't crowded.  Should only take a couple minutes.
So, nearly six bucks for a hot dog and fries with no drink. Not much value for the food or the service.
If you like their dogs, try another location; as different family members and franchisees run each a little different...

Great Tuesday Night Small Plate Happy Hour!  My friend and I enjoyed calamari, bacon-wrapped dates, crabby fingerling potatoes and the Judi short-rib pasta along with a couple of drinks. The food was delicious.  Drinks were yummy and the waitstaff were very good!  We will definitely be back for dinner!  Glad we got to sample the menu this way first. You will not be disappointed!!

Currently here for breakfast & the wait is an hour, which I am fine with. An hour pass & I go in to see how much longer the wait will be and the waitress said she has already called my name, which is funny because I've been sitting outside the door the entire time & she came out to call other people's name, but ironically, she hasn't called mine when she stated she did. Now she said that I have to wait until my entire party arrives & 2 of us are here. I'm only waiting on one more person. I will not come back after today.

My daughter had an orthodontist consult scheduled today at 4 we arrived early and the front desk was ok and gave me forms to fill out.  We waited 30 min and I finally went to the front desk to ask how much longer, I was informed it works be at least 20 more minutes. I said that wouldn't work she offered to reschedule I said no thanks. No apology, no explanation, no letting me know before or upon arrival there would be a long wait. I then called to complain to the manager and her response was "huh".  I was hesitant to even come in for a consult given the poor reviews on line but thought I would try as I really liked the payment options. I should have saved my time and listened to the online reviews. Dont waste your time.

I've been there twice now. (Take out) The food was less than impressive both time. Caritas was dry and crunchy. Fish and street tacos weren't good either. All the food was flavorless except the beans. They also for got the guacamole my second time there. Ill stick with Murrietas across the street.
Highly recommend.  Fried mozzarella and chicken skins were amazing starters. Burgers lived up to the hype.  Staff was great

No vegetarian options. No boba in the Thai tea. The kitchen messed up and only brought one order out. I had to wait another 10 minutes for my food. I would not recommend this place. 

No social distancing booths
No Covid precautions with condiments

If you're starving and in search of local flare, but don't want to venture off Bourbon, pop in Felix's.  A great spot for food with bite (no pun intended :P ) such as fried alligator, fried pickles and fried catfish.  The servings were enough for 2-3 people for about $15 pp.  There's also an oyster bar inside, with great local catches.  Try to finish all because we tried to take home leftovers and nothing seemed quite right.  That instant steam bursting through the layer of crunchy Southern batter is a memorable experience that only happens when the plates come straight outta the kitchen baby!

This place was fantastic! Very cool vibe, yummy food, great drinks. Staff very attentive. Will be back again for sure. Loved the "sugar" plate. Very fun finger foods. Who doesn't like fruity pebble peanut brittle and puppy chow! Delicious!

Lunch buffet is ammazzzinngg.  I've gone only on the weekends but my friend told me that their weekday buffet is really similar same except for 1 or 2 items. I've tried the buffets at Rasoi and House of India, but I prefer this place over the other in terms of both quantity and quality of foods. You get a decent selection of curries, meats and sauces to accompany your rice and naan that will satisfy any person's taste (unless you hate Indian food period. Can't help you there). 

I'm no expert in Indian cuisine and don't really know how authentic the food is here, but I can definitely say I'm always satisfied when I come here for their buffet and ALWAYS walk away with a massive food baby.

First timer at the Blowfish. I love the mix of music and sports. I also am a big fan of cleverly named cocktails. I ordered the Cumbersome: Tanqueray, tonic, simple syrup, lime juice and cucumber slices. Get it!? I can recommend the Mushroom "Fries" (Deep fried Panko encrusted sliced portobello mushroom caps then dusted with Parmasean cheese served with homemade spicy chili tomato sauce. )I could have eaten a serious amount of these.  The Mojo Pork Sliders were fantastic. Pulled pork topped with orange jerk caramelized onions and homemade gorgonzola cheese. That cheese sauce was divine. I wanted a bowl of it to dip every food ever into.The only issue of the day was that the food came out with a serious quickness. I had barely touched my salad when those sliders came. I'll be back, I like the vibe of the place and the food was a step above normal bar fare.

I wanted to soooooo much give this place 5/5 The first time we came the service was quick and friendly, the bands are fabulous Stacy Mitchhart and band are amazing the music is 5/5 on the other hand the food and service on 2/18/21 was extremely lackluster and disappointing. Me and my friend waited at least 20 minutes to get a drink mind you they were barely semi full I had to go up to the bar to get a drink for the both of us finally the waitress came up after another 20 mins had lapsed she got our order finally. We waited for another half hour to pay the bill had to go up to the bar and pay as the server never came back,  i'm a firm believer in keeping the restaurant industry going by patronizing places, but when the quality and service Is bad I feel like it's been a waste of time. The food was ok not great my friend got jumbo and it was tasty but such a small portion for the asking price it was not worth it, I got the blacked catfish basket it came with two thin pieces of catfish which were ok but the hush puppies were burnt and Unedible, If anything I would only come back for the music and maybe drinks, food was overcooked Service sucked !!!!!

Absolutely awful customer service and financing.  I have termites back again a couple of months after spending thousands on a contract.  I have tried - unsuccessfully - at least 6 times over the past few weeks to contact anyone in the USA who can speak English. Each time it was over an hour on hold or leaving a voicemail that wouldn't be returned.  
I finally reached a live US based person by stating that I wanted to cancel my contract in their horrible voice response system. It has gone nowhere of course.  They certainly pulled one over on my wife who had no idea what we needed while I was traveling for business. They do not deserve even the single star I was forced to leave.

Backyard Bowls makes the best acai bowls I've ever tasted. I wouldn't even compare this to Jamba Juice; it's on a league of it's own. You can't go wrong with the standard Backyard Bowl, but my favorite is definitely the Cacao Almond Bowl, which is blended acai, banana, cacao powder, and cashew milk, topped with banana, blueberry, raw cocao nibs, almonds, granola and honey for $9.15 or $10.95 (large).  

Some other cool things about this place: they have a reward system with stamps. Eat your bowl there or return your to-go cup for a stamp toward one free bowl. They also serve Pitaya Bowls and what they call the "Behemoth Bowl." It's a giant acai bowl topped on a 12 oz bed of yogurt and it serves "2-4 Normal Humans, or 1 Beast." I would love to see it some day!

I am still at my booth and wanted to review right away! This place is awesome. I had the shrimp fajita quesadilla and it was fabulous. The margaritas were great as well. When I ordered my 3rd, the waitress said that I should have gotten the jumbo. I will on my next visit!! Great food and great service. If you're in the area and in the mood for Mexican food, this is a must visit.

This place is delicious and dependable. I've been here more times than I can count because the quality of the food. The menu has a decent vegetarian/vegan selection and some unique Mediterranean/middle eastern dishes you won't find anywhere else (I like the majaddara platter). I really recommend this place for brunch or coffee. Some of my favorites are the lemon poppy pancakes, the iced coffee, and the burekkas(pastries). The service is good, the price are reasonable, and the atmosphere is clean and comfortable.

I really wanted to like this place the owner and a couple of the staff I chatted with were really pleasent and friendly
  I had the  chicken wings beef brisket corn bread greens mac n cheese and pecan pie.The wings were so small I think they came from quails.The brisket although tasty had so much fat  it would gag a horse if a horse ate brisket.Greens were good as well as mac.& cheese  and the pecan pie had about two cups two much sugar.
   On a return visit I relized the menu has changed but the change was the prices went up ( confirmed by the counter girl ) but the portions were the same if not smaller.This was on a takeout order were they failed to add my beans in the bag but charged me all the same.I do wish the place luck but they 
lost my business for sure..I expect more for my money and  they are way to overpriced  in my opinion.

Grimaldi's makes a solid pizza. I would give it 3.5 stars if the option were available. We ordered a large pie with sundried tomatoes and onion. The pie was $22--a bit steep. Our favorite gourmet pizza joints in South Tampa are more economically friendly, have larger menus, and don't skimp on cheese or sauce. The crust was great--thin, chewy, with a nice crisp. The cheese was lacking and the sauce was as well. The menu is very basic. Pizza and salads. The decor inside is inviting and brings you back to old school NYC. We will probably not be back.

Went to IHOP late one night with a group of friends. It took us 30 minutes for our waitress to bring us our drinks.(All water btw) The manager was wondering around like a chicken with his head cut off, not helping anyone. Didn't take back the menus and just ran away. Waitress doesn't even know the menu and needs to be re-trained. Slow service, incapable staff, and shit food.

As I stated in a previous post, I am on the hunt to find the best Boba in town.  There aren't a ton of options in this city, which is disappointing, but let me help you narrow it down....don't go to this one.  Here's why...

I ordered a Taro smoothie with Boba...I could hardly taste the taro at all, there were huge ice chunks in the smoothie (which I hate) the taro were undercooked, and it was really expensive for the size (which was tiny and the only size they had).  I was really bummed.

The staff were very friendly and the food was very good. My wife, who doesn't care for spicy found her's to be a bit too hot. I can eat Fire and enjoyed the hottest spice level, although I feel they went a bit overboard with the cinnamon. Overall a good meal for a fair price!

There are six of us, and we came here after winetasting. Yesterday, we had amazing Mexican food at another place over in the Funk Zone, and we were hoping for great Mexican food here in the Presidio part of town as well. The ambience is nice. We love that it's in a courtyard and open and airy, but the food was disappointing. We thought the servers were really nice, but not readily available, and the cocktails and the food were just OK. There were peas in the burrito. They should not have been there. The guacamole made tableside was $10, but it was just one avocado, so it was a very small serving. The chili relleno was made with a jalapeño instead of a poblano, and it was way too spicy. The salad was greasy and had too much dressing on it. Failure to follow through with the promise of incredible ambience upon arrival.

worst staffing ever.  I can't go because the lines are so long.  TERRIBLE.  Thank goodness you can order the deli meat at the screen because that's at least 30 minutes.   Have to return bad food all the time.

We went for an overnight, to get out of town and to celebrate my husbands birthday... Really nice, and the decor is modern, gives it a clean a simple touch... The kitchen was stocked with all the cookware, tabletop and cleaning supplies... The hotel looked fabulous, I was very impressed, until I noticed that the kitchen counter was dirty and sticky... I wish I would have taken pictures, and then we discovered that a pillow was dirty and light switch dirty as well... With everything going on with Covid19 you would think that cleanliness would be a top priority... Very disappointing because on hotels.com they had rave reviews and that was the reason why we picked this place... Was not worth the $200 plus dollars we spent...

I was a little disturbed by the size of the cup when I ordered a #1 from the billboard menu. I've seen complimentary water cups bigger than this. My order was to go, so I asked for some ketchup. The manager happily provided 10 packets. I asked why I could have so much ketchup and so little to drink. I wasn't pleased with the response. I was also a little disturbed by the sassy attitude from the girl at the cashier.

I did not see the value in the service or the $7 burger and fries. Will not be returning to this store.

I have to agree with the other reviewers - this was some of the best ice cream I've ever had! It's hard to find an ice cream place in the winter, so I Yelped and found the good reviews for this place, so I tried it out. Wow is it delish!  I had rum raisin and coconut and the flavors were intense and the ice cream hard and perfectly sweet. I also had Jamaica (hibiscus) agua fresca which was sweet and pure. A wonderful gem! So glad my fellow Yelpers helped me quench my craving!
This place is the Truth! We tried them all. The vegan tacos, vegan bowl, and the vegan box! If you haven't been, get there. These guys are feeding the Heart and Soul of New Orleans... Literally!
Love the pizza and salads here. Half of the wait staff is good if your lucky and the hostess' don't seem to have a clue.  The pizza is the only reason to come back.

New ownership now, mercifully, as the original S10 degenerated into total trash under the previous owner.  Note that the original S10 actually moved north to Awataukee, south of Phoenix, where it is now known as Sushi Ken and is as great as ever.  As for the newly reborn Sushi Ten here, nice generous chirashi presentation, but -  I've been here three times since the latest change in ownership -  I'm afraid the quality is going down hill again.

I was completely and fully impressed and enchanted by the eatery right inside of the Missouri Botanical Gardens. It was a hot day in June and I spend about 4 hours enjoying the gardens. I had already read the wonderful reviews and decided to lunch at Sassafras. You walk into a well decorated dining room and order at the desk. Many options are available for both kids and adults. I ordered a grilled chicken salad with Gorgonzola cheese, peppered and sweet bacon, avocado, fresh greens, tomatoes, And an emulsified balsamic dressing was served on the side. I had vitamin water to drink. I was then given a number and asked to be seated and display my number on a stand already placed on the table. About five minutes later a young man delivered my drink, salad, silver, and napkin. Such a nice young fellow who checked back on me from time to time. My salad was fantastic-the avocado melted in my mouth and mixed with the cheese and all the veggies were fresh and crunchy. The chicken was cooked perfectly. The whole meal was less than $10. If you come to the gardens, please remember to stop and eat there. I highly recommend it!
I like the place, it's like chipotle but seems fresher and definitely not huge waits like chipotle. It's close to my house too so I'll be back when I want one of their bowls again.

I learned years ago to bypass Bourbon Street and head to Frenchman Street instead. The Spotted Cat never disappoints. Fantastic music, good drinks and clean bathrooms! Patrons are generally courteous to note when standing in front of someone else--definitely a quality of good southern hospitality. Don't forget to tip the musicians. They are not a tourist attraction!

I have used this storage facility for the past several years and the staff has been excellent.  Linda in the office is extremely personable and professional.  I received several shipments monthly and she signed of the deliveries and had them delivered to my unit.  That was extremely helpful and secure.  I would highly recommend this storage unit to anyone looking for a storage unit in the area.

Went here with a friend for dinner.  We sat at our table for a few minutes before anyone came over to us.  There were quite a few people there for a weeknight and it looked like there was only one waitress/bartender, but she wasn't very attentive.  

We both ordered tot-chos.  They were huge and delicious!  If you don't know what tot-chos are, shame on you, get to yardline and have some!

There were tons of gaming spots, the music was ON POINT, and the food was great!  I imagine it's a super fun place on the weekends or a game night!

Very mediocre at best. Figured I'd try some delivery from here to see what the hype was about as I love pho and feel a cold coming on, wanted something spicy. Ordered spring roles, green onion cake and extra spicy beef satay. The spring roles were alright as well as the green onion cake however nothing special. The soup was not spicy at all And rather tasteless. I ended up spicing with my own sriracha hot sauce at home. Pretty disappointing. Service was friendly and delivery was quick, the food was very hot on arrival which is always a plus but I probably will skip on pho king again!

The web site says, "If you're anything like us, you love a good glass of wine at the end of your day."  That's how the Palomar promotes its wine/social hour, where they serve a "selection" of free wines every night in the lobby.  The problem is that the "selection" consists of one $5 red and one $5 white, and they serve the same two wines all month.  $400/night should garner more than that.

The good news is that the location is great, the staff is very friendly and helpful, and if you get a "Spa Room, the jacuzzi is incredible.  The bad news is that the rooms are small and the beds are awful...they kind of feel like a bunch of blankets piled on top of a board.

Also, watch out for their policy of having to let the hotel know by 6:00 the night before if you'll be checking out early the next day.  If it's the first night, it's before you know that the bed sucks.  However, after a vehement complaint from me, they relented and didn't charge me.

Anyhow, the place is funky and the staff is great, but the bed and $5 wine make it definitely not worth the money.

It's clean and location is fine. Some of the staff (particularly Jasmin at the front desk, and the woman handling the breakfast, whose name I unfortunately did not get), are great.

But.

At 1:00 a.m. I was awakened by someone repeatedly trying to get into my room. I went and looked out the peephole- it was a couple. I heard one of them ask the other what room was written on the card holder, and the other said MY ROOM NUMBER. Fortunately I'd put on the chain lock and deadbolt, and they did not get the door open. Not wanting to signal that I was a woman alone in the room when they HAD A KEY, I said nothing to them and instead called the front desk and explained the situation. The person I spoke to didn't seem particularly concerned, but said they'd handle it. The couple gave up trying and left. I went back to bed.

And then... THE PHONE RANG. It was the front desk again. They had questions. What was my name? Who had checked me in? They then had me stay on the phone while they typed away and muttered to themselves, trying to figure out what went wrong. Yes, it was mistake and mistakes happen. Yes, it's good to get this figured out. But don't CALL A GUEST AT 1 A.M. and keep them on the phone while you do so. Oh, and maybe put safeguards in place to make sure potentially dangerous mistakes like one DON'T happen in the first place!

Hospitality industry. This seems like it should be self-explanatory.

Two bars, so much beer, what's there not to love?!  Make sure you take a tour, sample a flight (include the Madura!), and buy some beer soap on your way out.  If the BBQ truck is there, consider yourself lucky, and get the pulled pork nachos.  You'll thank me later.

Had a pretty decent experience here. I saw Dr. Borromeo for my first pregnancy. She was pretty good but quick during visits and does not spend a lot of time with you. She also told me that she probably would not be delivering my baby unless she was on call that night, so I could just expect to have any doctor delivering my baby (which is exactly what happened.)

Office staff is a little brusk at times and it can be hard to get an appointment. 

However, the nursing staff is nice and know what they are doing.  
The male doctors there I would not recommend. I have seen many of the MDs there and Dr. Glazer is great as well.
Delicious Thai Food, but service not so great. I waited 10 minutes for a waiter to take my order.

Go for the guac- enough said. It's chunky, fresh, and feeds about three people. We also got the queso con chile and it tasted like pizza because the cheese was covered in tomato sauce (actually very delicious).

The margaritas are very strong and you will definitely get your money's worth! I got the mango flavored one, but found out that they also make watermelon flavored ones (with real watermelon) during the summer. Can't wait to try it! My vegetarian quesadilla was really good but was about 90% cheese so would love some more vegetables so fun!

We originally came to sit on the patio which ended up being closed (due to wet paint) but it will be open soon!

Very unprofessional. I submitted a rental application online with my credit card to pay the rental fee. I never received any kind of email confirmation or phone call to confirm receipt of my application, so I assumed that the apartment had already been rented or they didn't receive my application. The next month I found their application fee charge on my credit card statement. I can understand that they chose another tenant, but I can't understand how they could charge me without even contacting me to say that my application was not selected. I called to ask them about this, and I got a rude response as if they have no obligation to contact applicants, even if they charge them. They charged me for nothing.

Such a great location. When I first tried this place, I was unsure of what to order and was too shy to ask, so I ended up with something I didn't enjoy and have stayed away since. Well, recently, I decided to give them a second chance and get some proper recommendations. I really love their selection of food. They have sandwiches, salads, charcuterie boards, and wine! What more could you ask for. I'm especially obsessed with the ploughman's lunch. Such a great spread for the price.

The coffee is fantastic. The donuts taste like donuts..

The hours leave something left to be desired... 
8-2 Monday to Thursday
8-4 Friday to Sunday

I'm not really sure how people get the chance to come and try the fantastic coffee with these hours. If the hours were better it would get 5 stars IMO

During the hottest week of the year, the A/C died. Here's the story of my week-long clown show with CHF.  (Based on actual events, just like in the movies!!!)

* WEDNESDAY, July 17.  CHF is not our first choice.  My A/C problem has already been diagnosed by another vendor and my landlady has a quote for a new unit.  But CHF apparently has a contract with the condo association to handle A/C for the building overall, so she calls them to get a second quote.  They are supposed to arrive first thing in the morning.

In reality, CHF rolls in around 10:15.  Not a promising start.  When the quote comes, it's higher than the first vendor.  We decide not to use CHF.

* THURSDAY, July 18.  Unfortunately, the first vendor is now booked up for the week, so we're stuck with CHF.  They promise the landlady they will be out to install a brand new A/C unit first thing on Friday morning.

* FRIDAY, July 19.  I take the day off work and sit in a puddle of my own sweat until noon, but CHF doesn't show.  I finally call.  "We never said we were coming today.  We aren't coming today."  Later they make a new promise to the landlady:  they will arrive first thing Monday.  We're beginning to think that "first thing" means something different to CHF than it does to regular people.  

* MONDAY morning July 22.  I take yet another vacation day to sit in a sweltering apartment waiting for CFH.  True to form, they don't show.  My landlady, the niceset person in the world, calls me and unleashes a string of f-bombs about them. No one seems able to tell her if they are coming and when, so I sit and wait.  And wait.
 
At noon I call and am informed that they never meant that they would be there "first thing," but they may show up within 90 minutes "if everything goes according to plan."

* MONDAY afternoon, July 22.  A CHF tech arrives--but without the replacement A/C unit.  He tells me he is just there to disassemble, the replacement unit will arrive "soon."  I literally text a friend:  "If these geniuses show up with the wrong A/C unit, I am going to stab them to death with a salad fork."

A couple hours later I find him at the utility closet with another CHF tech.  They seem genuinely mystified.  "We measured the width AND the height" he says (he seems very proud to have remembered two of the three dimensions that define the physical world) "but the new unit won't fit."  CHF apparently doesn't realize that A/C units are three-dimensional.

I wish I were making this up.

To sum up:  CHF VISITED THE SITE, THEY MEASURED THE CLOSET, THEY WORK REGULARLY IN THE BUILDING where all the apartments are the same, AND THEY STILL MANAGED TO ARRIVE LATE AND WITH THE WRONG REPLACEMENT UNIT.

* TUESDAY, July 23.  The heatwave has now broken.  It's gone from 104 to 70 degrees outside -- just in time for CHF to show up with the correct A/C unit.  I have endured a full week of record-breaking temperatures, during which I have taken two vacation days to sit and sweat and wait for CHF to not show up.  

In the end, they do a clean install without too much trouble, but I sure as fuck will never call them again.

---
TO SUM UP:
* More expensive
* Consistently late
* Brought an A/C unit that didn't fit, despite the fact that they themselves measured for it.
* No apology

I would recommend the philly cheesesteak.  It's nice that they have the touch screen to order food, but it takes forever. Service is slow even when they aren't busy.  They charge $5 for a fountain drink (that is flat/diluted).

This place is pretty nice and cozy. It is a little busy. They have a gift shop in the waiting room. You can sit inside or inside patio. 

They have baked goods, breakfast, lunch, dinner options. They have a few vegan and gluten free options as well. The vegan breakfast burrito was very good. Their salsa and guacamole is very fresh and wonderful! 

Prices were on the higher end of average. Staff was friendly. Food came out pretty quickly, within 15 minutes.
Didn't like the flavor of it. I think it would've been better if I was at the restaurant and if they mixed it for me,

Tony Luke's is a fabulous place. After spending a miserable 6 hours flying from CMH to DCA to PHL during the remnants of hurricane Ida, I needed that steak badly.

TL's delivers on the goodness, using an extra sharp provolone that'll knock your socks off. No fatty pieces of meat, perfectly cooked in all aspects.

It was the perfect meal for the miserable weather, I only wish I had time to visit twice due to their extensive menu.

Just ate food from this restaurant with my wife we both ordered fried rice with our meal.Both of them tasted and smelled like eating weevils unbelievably gross. We left half of our food on each of our plates no tip for the server because why would you tip for food that is able to get you sick and we are both disgusted. I warn anybody who goes there smell the rice first and taste it there's a distinctive taste and smell that weevils put out....!
It's a neat place but they really need to grow up as an attraction. Total chaos when it's busy.
A Taste of Heaven is okay.  Its nothing like the Soul Food in the South but it will do.  It is a tad bit pricey ...at least $14-19 for an entrée with two sides (small portions).

As a coffee lover, this is the place to be!  It has everything you are looking for in a local coffee shop.  Everything is done well and all the baristas pay attention to the details when making your drink.

We ate there on Friday and Saturday. The gumbo is outstanding the creole was great however.... The service was some of the worst I've ever experienced. Attitude was bad and they seemed lost. On a positive note the balcony seating has a great view of Bourbon.

Bitter sweet dining experiences...

As I write this I am waiting on a half and half from Yats... 

The "Indy Taco" from a food standpoint is great. The burritos and tacos are authentic and taste great. However, the customer service portion of this establishment is has very little to be desired. This was my second and probably last time visiting Indy Taco. The first time I came here my order was forgotten and I had to wait for it to be put in. Ok cool, things happen right? Imagine my surprise when visiting this place a SECOND time and again, my food order was forgotten and had to be put in... Not this time. I casually asked for a refund and existed the restaurant. I'll spend my patron bucks at Yats. So again the food is good but if you don't have the patience(from ordering to serving takes too long in general) nor the time, I wouldn't recommend unless you're in the mood for a very good burrito. Bitter sweet..

I'm a local, so I had an idea of what to expect from a Brennan's restaurant.  I wanted to love it. After all, it is in an amazing location and good for LePetite theater.  It was awful! We had the three stooges waiting on us...dropping things, breaking things, and introducing themselves multiple times.  Our order was simple, shrimp and grits, some salads, an appetizer.  The salads were small but good.  There were other tables we saw complaining about their salad (looks like they chopped a head of iceberg lettuce and put it on a plate).  I'm glad we didn't order that one.

Then came the long wait. I noticed a table next to us getting an order of shrimp and grits.  That table had their order mixed up too, so they thought it was a gift from the kitchen.  It turned out that was our food....which took another 15 minutes for them to tell us. 

All of this would've been forgiven if the food was good (cause that's what we do here if the food is good).  I hope they get their act together. The service was embarrassing, the food not good and the management stood off to the side watching it all and not stepping in. It was a strange experience.  We nicknamed the place Tab-blah!  It might pass as good to a tourist, but not for a local.
For the most part they do a great job, but I've been to this business multiple times and twice now, I have had items stolen from my car. Will not be going back.
Excellent tour company!!!  Very knowledgeable and helpful.  I took the cemetery no 1 and courtyard tour and learned a good deal of information.  Definitely recommended
Absolutely delicious!!  Their sandwiches are full of awesomeness and delicious.  I highly recommend this delicatessen.

Looking for something to do?. Soo much chili to taste, plenty of beer, and vendor tents to shop around. They're were entertainers singing inside and outside the event. This was definitely a great weekend well spent, especially for the event being free. Unless you wanted the VIP treatment than payment was needed but went to a great cause helping children (I don't remember the charity name). They had a inflatable slide for the kids, funnel cakes, snow cones, beef jerky, what more can you ask for lol. Way to go Yelp and Grand Sierra in hosting the event. I would definitely return if back in Reno.

If I could give it 2 1/2 stars I would.  My wife and I are always looking for new places to try since this was open a few months back we though whuy not give it a try.  I had made the reservation for Saturday night at 8 and thought that being a saturday it would be busy so we would get there ealry and have a drink at the bar. When we got there past 7 they could sit was.  I was surprise that for a Saturday there was a lot of empty tables and no wait.  Altough the staff where attentive to our needs and the restaurant was nice the food lack luster for the price.  There was not one item that we eat and said "oh my god that was great".

The burgers are awesome....VERY filling!!! I order the Diablo Burger every time I'm there and it is incredible!  The only thing I don't like is that burgers don't come with fries...fries are extra. The truffle fries are a great appetizer and they have some really good dipping sauces. Parking can be a pain in the butt during peak hours. The back patio is a great atmosphere with misters for the summer.
I love this place, either stop in for a slice (or two) or buy a whole pie - the food is great, the prices are great ... Guido's is just great.
Came in for dinner and place was packed.  Found a spot at bar and service was excellent.  A first for me anywhere else.  Got the tuna seaweed salad, will be back again:)

I dropped in this conveniently located garden shop to see if they had any late-summer herbs worth buying. There were a few varities of oregano, some onions, and a bit of thyme, but they all ooked only a little better than other wilty offerings I've seen in similar shops.

On a more interesting note, one of the shopkeeps was infected with a case of verbal 'rhea. He saw my Texas license plate and proceeded to use my answer as a gateway to tell me about the wife he left behind in Dallas, who stayed behind to waitress at Cheddar's.

I discovered this place when it was under different ownership, and there may have been 3 owners during the time I have gone there.  Went there yesterday for lunch.  The place is cleaner than I have ever seen it, hence the 2nd star, but the food is strictly blah.  We had pulled pork as a luncheon special, one had baked beans the other coleslaw on the side.  The pork wasn't bad, seemed to be a whole Boston butt cooked on premises, but they ruined it with lousy commercial barbecue sauce, too salty, too nondescript.  Coleslaw, the same, right out of a can or container, too sweet.

I think I can safely say I won't be returning anytime in the near future.

If I pay extra for a car seat during my online reservation, I expect the car seat to already be installed upon arrival.  Or at the very least for a employee to assist with the installation.  Just handing me a seat in a plastic bag and leaving when I have luggage and an infant is not helpful.

I drove 45 minutes to this dealership yesterday since they had the specific Jeep Wrangler I was looking for. I test drove it and loved it! I then came inside and worked with Richard Levine to negotiate a lease payment. I had been offered a good deal by a dealership that was further away, so I came to see if they could give me a better one, hoping to avoid the long drive. Well, after probably an hour or so of negotiating, Richard kept coming back with offers lower than his previous, but still not better than the other dealer. Finally he offered to match the deal, but Richards car didnt have the LED lights/fog lights option, which the other dealerships car did. When I asked if he could do better, he became infuriated. I simply stated it's okay if you can't but why would I lease the car for the same price with less options? When I pointed this out he acted as if I was being completely ridiculous for not buying his car at the same price as the other one over one silly option. In the end, I thanked him for his time and went to shake his hand, but he just shook his head at me, repeatedly said "goodluck" in an rude sarcastic tone, stormed off, and left me sitting at his desk. In the end, I called the other dealership and he made me an even better offer than the one Richard could barely match! Nevertheless, I drove my brand new wrangler home today and I am in love! If you are looking for an exceptional experience buying a car with sales reps who are kind honest respectful patient and well informed, steer clear of this place and check out Savage 61 Chrysler Dodge Jeep and Ram in Reading, PA. TOTALLY worth the trip!! The sales manager Sean is absolutely incredible(and he gave me a great deal!!) and Louis is also extremely kind!!! Happy car shopping:)
I'm from out-of-state, but very glad that I stopped in.  The atmosphere was inviting, and the sales experience was top-notch.  I don't think I've ever been in a friendlier dealership.

My wife and I have been to this location several times. The first time was amazing! Great service and great food. But every time since then has been less than great. We usually sit in the bar and decided not to tonight. The server first of all needed a tissue and kept sniffling when speaking to us, that alone is gross. But then he was unable to located the vinager and oil for our salad, how do you lose them? Brought the salads out anyway with no way to eat it without the dressing. Not to mention the rude response from him when we stopped him to ask where it was after waiting for about 5 minutes. Half way through the salad our drinks from the bar had not come out yet.  We had to ask for them. Then they were out in less then 2 minutes.We ordered the exact same steak, one came out bery rare while the other was cooked fine. Might I add that a manager had to bring the drinks and the steaks. So with that being the 3rd bad experience at this locaton we will be taking our buisness to either greenwood or castleton from here on out.

Last week I went to Ibiza for the first time to see Kara for a tanning appointment. As uncomfortable as spray tanning is, Kara made the experience relaxing and enjoyable. She custom-made the color to fit my complexion and was very thorough and consistent. My tan looked real (a real feat compared to the majority of spray-tans I've received in the past!) and has lasted beautifully. I would (and will) definitely go back to Kara anytime I feel like spraying some summer in my life!

I was so dissappointed in my experience at this resturant. it is one of the nicest resturants in town, yet they made us wait over 30 minutes for our table even though wr had a reservation, and then once we finally got to our table we didnt get any food for almost another hour. this date was supposed to be really special, and the service that the hostess and the manager gave us was extremely disappointing, especially when we are spending so much money. nobody ever even acknowledged how long we waited. wouldnt reccomend.

This historic building was refurbished several years ago when the Plumsteadville Inn reopened. There is a lovely outdoor patio for dining in warmer weather, a bar, 2 large dining rooms (1 casual right off the bar area and the other a bit more formal), and a smaller dining room that would be great for a private party.

They have live music in the bar at night and it is always crowded on Friday nights. The food is decent. I have never stayed in the Inn, so cannot comment on that. 

Overall, great establishment that offers fine and casual dining or a fun night out in a quiet Bucks County town.

Enjoyed the Prasino burger here today- VERY good! Smoked Gudda cheese and a tomato jam sauce that was amazing. Service was a bit slow, BUT, our waitress kindly got me another double espesso on her while we waited. Overall- excellent food that was well worth the wait, and very reasonably priced!

Finally a truly authentic Bavarian German experience as close to the original Hofbrauhaus in Munich as you will find without needing a passport! If you look at my photos you will see my photos of my visits to the original Hofbrauhaus in Munich Germany and I can tell you the one here in St. Petersburg Florida is 100% identical in the beer, experience, hospitality and 95% as close in food.  The Bavarian German food dishes are spot on authentic but to please some picky children or adults there are a few "American" type meals for those who may be visiting for the beers and festivities but might not know what they are missing in not indulging in true Bavarian food.  Make sure you realize visiting The Hofbrauhaus is just as much about enjoying a true German beer and band while making new friends as it is about enjoying authentic Bavarian lunch or dinner so don't plan to just go and eat and think you're leaving after your meal because you're surely going to want to stay much longer while relaxing sipping your stein of HB brew, listening to the true German Band and maybe enjoy dancing with your family, friends and new friends you're definitely going to make when you are at this wonderful experience The Hofbrauhaus of St Petersburg!

Two very nice gentlemen own this restaurant. I came last minute with my "Groupon" (it's the same thing but from Amazon I think). 

While I was waiting for my gyros, I asked the guys about their competition, since there are many gyro restaurants in St. Louis. They were honest and said that some of the other owners were their friends, and they ate at Gyro Company before this place was opened! Gotta love genuine business owners. The guys said they never receive any Bosnian customers and it's mostly European-Americans (basically white people). They say that a restaurant is authentic and good if people of that heritage go there, but this rings false for Soulard Gyro & Deli.

The sandwiches were scrumptious. It came with the customary lettuce, tomatoes, tzakziki sauce, pita bread and meat. I wouldn't say it's the best gyro I've ever had but still great.

Couldn't wait to get to this place when we arrived in Nashville. Had heard a lot about it and mostly favorable. Only worth 2 stars. Band was great- they're worth 5 stars. Waitress- 1 star- took 15 minutes just to get a drink and acted like we were a huge inconvenience to her. Had a coupon for free fried pickles- that took another 45 minutes- (although they were good when they did finally arrive)Didn't get a bill- waitress just rattled off a number of what we owed- had taken her so long to come back around- we just paid it- figured it out later and she charged us wrong- I'm sure it's not the first time- plenty of other places on Broadway that have good food and much better service.

Second time around was not a charm. Totally opposite experience from our first go around with sub par service, sub par food (along with a nice strand of hair embedded in someone's burger). The manager was apologetic of the situation and took care of us, but only after our waitress pointed out that the lady in our party who ordered the burger also had "dark colored hair". What the !@#$!! Are you kidding me? One bright spot was that the waitress from our first visit remembered our whole party as we were walking out. She engaged us in some nice conversation and would probably be the only reason why I would go back there. Thank you Melissa!
Great food and beautiful ambiance.  Service was really good too. Would definitely go again

My husband and I have enjoyed RNS for two years! We used to make it a priority every time we traveled to NOLA, but now that we live here... it's only gotten better! 

We have recently branched out and tried new things and we're so happy we did. 
Tonight the service and food was perfect. I will say that the music can be too loud some nights, but tonight it was okay.

You  must just try the Shrimp wantons!!! Oh, my goodness! 
The crunchy roll is my favorite ( insider tip- ask for a side of sweet mayo)! The Fulton street roll and BBQ eel rolls are roll-n, too. 

Go there tonight! You will thank yourself.

I normally don't review places but I felt the need to do it this time because my experience warrants it. My husband and I spent 5 days in The Westin Nashville right before the 4th of July. We first went to the Oak Steakhouse on July 1st and the service we got from one of the servers was beyond rude and unwarranted. If it wasn't for the decent meal and other great servers who tried to salvage our experience (Mike and Savanna), we would have never considered going back. Anyway, after a long day of exploring Nashville, we decided to order food to-go on the evening of Monday, July 3rd. My husband ordered the rack of lamb and I ordered the pork shoulder. The pork had a bad smell, characteristic of meat that had been spoilt. Unfortunately, I discovered this after getting back to the hotel room; I was already starved and exhausted so going back to the restaurant wasn't even an option. One of the reasons I'm writing this review is to warn consumers. Yes, there are great people who work there. No, not everybody makes you feel like an appreciated customer. Yes, you do have to worry about the quality of food that's served to you. I get that this is a pricey restaurant whose margins aren't that high yet (fairly new and sales are probably still unsatisfactory). Having said that, it's never appropriate to serve spoiled meat to customers. This can have highly undesirable effects to those who consume it. So, I do appreciate people like Mike making us feel welcome; however, do be cognizant of the food that's served to you. It might just be the meal you regret eating for a very long time.

I ordered a large deluxe thin crust pizza, delivery time was good. The pie was crazy high priced and had no toppings for the first 2" around and very light on toppings and cheese on the rest of pie, not going there again. Sad excuse for pizza!!!
I had the taste of the bayou. I liked the red beans and rice, but didn't care for the jambalaya or gumbo. The fried alligator was delicious.

Taller white guy that works there is the definition of a pos. stay away from this dump. I don't care if they serve gold. This guys attitude was horrifying. Feel bad for anyone that has to work with this disgusting human.

Great way to walk around and people watch.  While you are out watching all the people doing all their people doings, you can pick up some fresh produce.  I got some real tasty cheese.  In the market there are a few quick restaurants that you can enjoy a nice lunch at.  One was even featured in an episode of Diners, Drive Ins, And dives.

This hotel is dated but the staff are very nice and helpful. Rooms and furniture is really old and needs a refresh. Morning complimentary breakfast includes hot cook to order items and is great. Free WiFi , nice small (but dated) workout area with good equipment. Free parking and proximity to Vanderbilt make this a good deal.

Stopped in for lunch this Sunday, wonderful place! We ordered the smoked chicken wings, brisket and pork sandwich, all excellent! Macaroni and cheese was delicious. Friendly and helpful staff and locally owned. Highly recommend, we will be back!
Decent food and nothing special that's worth such a crazy wait. Cinnamon break sticks need some sauce or something. Good prices.
Pizza was fantastic. Mine was a little too moist with oil/grease but that's just me having to critique something. Four point 5

This place has some delicious chicken sandwiches! I had one today and was amazed at how good it tasted. Perfect mixture of chicken and sauce on some amazing bread. I had their mandarin oranges mixture side dish which was a great complement. Everything was fresh and it did not take them long to make my meal either. Prices are just right for the quality of the food. I'm glad I learned about this place today through my family and will be back in the future.

I followed people's reviews on yelp and zagat and went to dinner at this place. 
I got the hot and sour soup. It wasn't hot nor sour. Tasted and smelled like eggs. Way too much egg. I guess if you don't use corn starch egg will thicken your soup. It had a bunch of tofu and other veggies in it but I couldn't see or taste them because of all the eggs
For main course I was debating between two dishes. I asked the waiter. He didn't know what's in the dishes. Went to ask another waiter and then came to me and said" brown sauce, with shrimp" I tried to get more info but the guy had no clue. 
I got their version of general tso chicken. 
In this version they slice white meat chicken very thinly and fry it to a crisp in a batter. Then they toss it in a sweet and sour sauce. It is just wrong! No vegetable, dry, sweet, without any spice or personality. Even the rice I got was overlooked and old. 
This place gets a 22 in zagat. I wouldn't give it more than 17. I can name 5 better Chinese restaurants in Philadelphia. And I'm from NY....
Don't waste your time. This is a fast food take out Chinese with fancy restaurant prices

I wasn't so hungry but my boyfriend was starving so he ordered pho & I ate the summer rolls.  The pho was okay -- good enough to get you full but didn't leave you deliciously satisfied.  I tried a bit of his & I wasn't overwhelmed by it.

The summer rolls -- I had the pork and shrimp -- were pretty lackluster.  They were just these floppy rice rolls.  The only reason they tasted good was because I doused each bit with the hoisin sauce they provided.

I really wanted good bubble tea too so I ordered regular milk tea with tapioca.  This was the first time I never finished my bubble tea!  I drank maybe a 1/4 of it.  The tapioca was all stuck together in a giant goop & clogged my straw too!  The tea was mehh.  I just put it to the side & drank my water.

The store front is open and baking delicious hadpies.
had the curry, steak and potato and spicy beef.
Homemade fudge welcomes you as you enter.
Try the tractor soda's with you handpie-coffee and a few sweets to go.

I stopped here for the first time today. Faith did a great job on my hair and my sons. Pro tip... its cash only. I had to run to the bank after my cut. Bringing cash is a hassle but it's worth it! I'll be going here for all my cuts from now on!!
This is truly my favorite BBQ place in metro-east. When I want bbq this place I go. Keep up the good work.
Meh. The dumplings were underdone, pancake super greasy, and tacos drown in sweet sauce. Just pay a little more and order from Giwa.

This restaurant is fantastic! The food was flavorful and varied, the price point makes it an accessible fine dining experience, and the service was top-notch. 5 star review from my fiancé and myself; we will definitely become regular patrons of Gulf Shores.

We were excited to try this place based on the reviews.
The good: the sushi we ordered was tasty and fresh.
The mediocre: the hibachi was ok nothing special.
The bad: Our service was poor. They were not friendly, didn't refill our drinks, and we had to wait forever after our meal for our check.

Ho-hum.

Avoid their emergency room at all cost. Not only is the staff terse and hostile, but there is one doctor, an older, heavy-set white man, who never talks to the patient, palpates your belly, and leaves, never to come back.  He did that to my daughter on two occasions.  The last one, he palpated her belly, and that wasn't where she was having pain.  He examined the right side of her belly, when the pain was in the left side of her back.  None of the nurses interacted with my daughter. People who came in after her were hooked up to IV's and had blood drawn, while she peed in a cup, and waited for over two hours. I picked her up there, and brought her to JFK in Cherry Hill.  While the wait was long, once they took her back, they immediately asked questions, had the doctor come in, who examined the correct part of the body, ordered blood work, an IV drip, a CAT scan, etc.  They were warm and friendly.  (On another occasion, I went to OLLMC ER, where the nurses and doctor were hostile and downright abusive.  Worst experience I ever had in an ER.  That was almost 2 years ago.  I now go to JFK, even though OLLMC is much closer to my home.) This is supposed to be a Catholic Hospital, doing good work for the community.  Jesus would have kicked the people who run OLLMC out of his temple for defiling it.

Let me say this is more than a 5 star sandwich shop.  The place is quaint with about 4 tables.  The owner is friendly and it's in a great location!

Let's start with the onion rings.... oh..my... goodness!  Crispy light battered and great flavor!

The steak and cheese sandwich will be a memory(I'm dieting and this was a cheat day) that will be making my taste buds water!  

You will not be disappointed!

Went to Whiskey Kitchen for the first time this past weekend and it was wonderful!  We got there early for a Saturday (6:00) and had to wait about 40 minutes (we were originally quoted an hour and a half).  

Our server was friendly and knowledgeable about whiskey and had a lot of good food recommendations.  There were 3 of us and we ordered a few appetizers and after a few more drinks, we each ordered an entrees.  All of our food was amazing!  Great taste and presentation.  I will definitely be back to Whiskey Kitchen!

AJ's is the Ritz Carlton of all grocery store chains. They have a large meat department, a delicious bakery, an extensive wine and liquor department, and some of the best and largest produce available. They also carry items that other grocery stores do not offer i.e. pellegrino limonata (which I happen to love). AJ's also offers wine tastings and food samples. If you enjoy good quality food and have the financial means to shop here go right ahead. I know I would.

I like to study in starbucks, so my review reflects what I find conducive / disadvantageous - 

Pros - bright 
         - good location (in terms of sitting outside / walking around)
         - friendly staff
         - closes at 10/11 pm as opposed to 9:30/10 pm (cherry hill location)
         - good drink selection + clover brew

Con - Bathrooms almost always dirty, and usually 1 out of 2 are "out of order". 
        - Floors around seats often have crumbs/garbage around them. 
        - Parking is challenging

I understand this is a very busy starbucks and like the atmosphere, but the fact that the bathrooms / floors are always less than stellar bother me.

We came here 3 times during our short trip to Montecito! If I lived there I would never leave that place alone. Best bagel sandwich I've ever had in all my life!! I've had bagel sandwiches many a time- this is an informed review. The gourmet flavors actually blew my mind! The owner said he might open a shop in Oakland-- I'm SO hoping that's true!!

I am a delivery driver for a third party app. This was my first time here. I must say. This place smelled delicious walking in the door. And they were very busy. Not surprised because it's a Sunday. I walked to the register and I have to be frank. Both ladies that I felt with both were very smug and did not seem happy. Both of which felt the need to try and pass that terrible attitude my way. Not happening. I was not greeted at all either. I had to ask  for assistance. The looks that I was getting as if I was in the way or that it was a hassle to help me. Eventually I had a second lady tell me AFTER I was told by the first one that I should wait to be helped at the register which is where I went. I had placed my delivery bag on the counter and the second lady had grabbed my delivery bag and said for me to not place that one there. She was so rude and just miserable. She went on to tell me that the register area was for PAYING CUSTOMERS only. Uhhhh what's is this third party delivery system then? This is a PREPAID order that I am simply picking up and delivering. Yes, I may not be the person laying but I am representing a customer who ALREADY PAID. Back to this second lady, she proceeds to tell me that there is an area they specified and that it's by the sign. Well this SIGN was actually FULL BLOCKED with a stack of THEIR take or menus so no, I didn't see it. And I let her know this and she had the "oh shit" look on her face because she was being incredibly rude. My point is that I really hope some one checks these ladies because they were incredibly rude and unpleasant and I personally think that it's a bad representation of an establishment that seems to be much more than what these two ladies are capable of offering.

From the Nada chef, comes a gem in Carmel.

The dishes were top notch. I had one of my favorite salmon dishes ever. I could have eaten two of the salads I got. 

The ambiance was impressive and incredibly tasteful. I was even wowed by the bathrooms. 

Will be sampling more of the menu on my future business trips. This now edges out Late Harvest due to the atmosphere. I'd say the dishes are comparable in quality and that's a good thing for both.
Slowest Starbucks I've ever been to. Read the rest of the reviews this is not only once it's all the time.

We sat inside as the outdoor area was full. It took our server(Melissa R.) 15 minutes to come over to get our drink order. No greeting, no hello...just, "Are you ready?" Long wait for the food but that's ok- it was good. Expect to get your hands messy(plastic bibs provided) if you order the seafood combos that are served in bags with your choice of seasoning. Our server was awful but we were helped out by another server(Cindy- kind Asian girl) who was great! 
Overall- food was good, freshly made(not that filling though), service left a lot to be desired.
Great for anything on Canal Street. You can by Jazzy Pass 2,3, and 5 day unlimited rides from Trolley conductor.

When we were getting ready to sell our home, we hired Krown Service to paint the exterior.  John gave us a lot of options to choose from so we could get the house looking good while staying within our budget.  Long story short, he did a fantastic job and I have no doubt it contributed to the successful sale of the home.  

Since we knew we could trust John, we hired Krown again to paint the interior of out new home.  John and his crew painted the entire house (just over 2500 sqft) including all of the window/door trim and baseboards.  John and his team did an amazing job in less time that we expected.  John will be the only painter we use going forward.  I cannot recommend Krown Service LLC highly enough.

I have been going to Nikos for years now, they have one of the best salad bars ever! The decor is very outdated, the wait staff is not very friendly and numerous vists I have had to ask for refills or extra napkins. The prime rib is great if you get their ealry enough and get it served at the rite temperature. I wish they would clean the restaurant its very dark and dingy in there.

I ordered A large pepperoni pizza and hot wings for the family and a cheesesteak for myself. The cheesesteak was disgusting. It also came with French fries which were on the burnt side. I basically put the whole platter in the garbage. The pizza and wings are okay but that's about it. Really disappointed in this place.

Tuesday night. Decent size crowd. But probably mostly due to the slow service. Waiter said they were starting a new system tonight. 

Food was average. Guacamole was good, even without ordering the table side style. 

Margarita was not made with fresh strawberries. Cheese on beans was interesting, it was more of a liquid than melted cheese.

I would have given them a 5 star review because this use to be our favorite sushi place, but since they changed their menu and staff about 6 months ago we really are very disappointed. When do you have to wait to be seated at the sushi bar when there are seats available? The hostess is unfriendly and not very professional. They have raised their prices considerably as well. I ordered beef teriyaki thinking that is was their usual sliced plate, but they bring out this steak that looked like it came from a 5 star restaurant when all I wanted was the original plate. The cost was as if I was at a 5 star restaurant. Not very happy. Now you have to wait up to 20 minutes to be seated when there are obviously tables open. We have returned to the original Sushi Pier on plumb and are very happy again. As the customer service is awesome, they even offer dessert with any meal and the prices have remained the same. No need to look any further, we have returned back Don't fix it if it's not broken!

The best food ever! !! I travel to wherever this food truck is going in Florida to get their foods! !! The rice balls are amazing .. drizzled with their amazing sauce! !The potato balls are so yummy! !! My personal favorite is their cuban ..  it's on fresh cuban bread and so yummy the sauce is incredible and the yucca fries are to die for! !!
Awful experience. Lackluster Santa, bored employees just going thru the motions, and terrible picture quality. Had to edit my digital files at home....ridiculous and a waste of $50.

Maybe I'm just unsophisticated, but I tend to judge place on the food and service and less the look. If it's clean it's fine by me. And Urban Spoon is clean.

I needed to burn some time on a Friday night as I was waiting and I found a seat at the bar. I ordered a burger that had buffalo sauce and blue cheese on it, on texas toast (I think). A burger is a burger is a burger, but this might have been the best one I've had since I moved to Philadelphia. And they had tater tots!

The service was fast, the food was great, and the place was clean. If I'm ever on Fairmount again and need something to eat I'll never hesitate to stop in.

Overall the place is amazing. But I should have take the advice from reviewers when it came to the staff. Luckily I didn't deal with an older man or the young lady. But my issue is the customer service. Note to self, I have only been there twice and that was my final straw. Let me first start with the fact that when I came for a review my mother, my grandmother ,and myself came in and everyone gave us the cold shoulder. It felt like they didn't give us much detail ;they didn't try to reassure us and they just kind of took my dog back there to hurry us out of the business . When I came back to pick up my dog after the evaluation my family had said to maybe go in there alone because maybe we intimidated them ,which we did nothing of the sort. So it kind of felt awkward that no one really wanted to talk to me and my family. What really got me though was when one of the staff came out to ask who they should go get from the back and this girl said this "mutt" thing! I literally thought that was so disrespectful I literally wanted to snap but I kept my composure. I still decided to take my dog the next day because I'm new in town and when I came to pick up my dog I saw that there was a sale so as I was looking I picked up one toy and then she just decided to ring me up I wasn't even finished so they really could've made a big sale but because they rushed me I only got to buy my dog one toy. I also  asked about specials and I was told about a punchcard which was really upsetting because I had already been here one day and that could've gone to the punch card, but no one wanted to tell me previously the day before because no one even wanted to talk to me nor look me in my eyes.  Overall it's just really disappointing and it makes me kind of question if this place is racist and I hate to say that. I hope the new owner really pulls a grip with their staff and makes customers feel welcomed and appreciated. Thankfully I found a new place close to my job that makes me and my dog feel safe and wanted. Such a disappointment.....

Wanting some authentic tacos on a beautiful day! Also this place was a plus because the outside patio for my dog. It was pet friendly. Didn't have any of the staff tell me I couldn't have my dog. The menu consist of tortas and tacos. It all look so very good and neat tortas were name after celebrities. The place has an authentic side as if I stepped into a taco joint into Mexico. I ordered 3 tacos to go and I even asked for pickle carrots/jalapeños which didn't take long at all. I wish I could give them 5 stars, but there card machine was down and she attempted to write my card number down to run for later. No thanks. Good thing I had cash in the car so I walked back to the car to get the cash, and even with that it took a while to process the cash machine because to open the register took forever. So My freshly tacos has been sitting in a bag for a while and when I got home to eat it they were cold. Also, no pickle carrots or jalapeños!! Also the lime was super dry! I could not squeeze any liquid out of it. I had to use my own limes at home. Tacos were not bad. The chorizo was spicy which I liked. Chicken was kind of dry. But I did enjoy the steak. I'm sure if I ate it right when I got it it would had been good. I will definitely give it a try again and elect to actually sit out on the patio.

We've been to Bonefish grill dozens of times and it's always been excellent. Tonight was just...meh. 

The biggest disappointment was the the Imperial Flounder which was listed on the menus as "stuffed with shrimp, bay scallops, crabmeat Mozzarella and Parmesan and finished with a lemon-caper butter"

It wasn't stuffed, and when the manager came by - she said it's not stuffed, but we put our ground up imperial dip. 

They are trying hard to deal with COVID - but service wasn't great after we placed our order. Our server never came by to see how the order came out and we had to flag another server to get the manager to come by and to get silverware. 

I had the Oscar Grouper which was excellent. 

The featured cocktails were tasty. 

Won't be back for a while, but will be back.

I'd like to thank the owner for commenting on the review 6 months later and getting all the details wrong.....so much for attention to details!  The package was shipped from your location to New Orleans and then rerouted back to your location.  I picked it up and resent it again.  The UPS in New Orleans sent your location a credit for reshipping, since it was already in transit back.  So come on man, get the details right and you should stop stomping your feet and insulting your customers!

This hotel wasa good experience.  We love the free parking and free shuttle to the Dali museum and downtown. We walked from the hotel to a restaurant downtown and the streets are safe and it is an easy pleasant stroll.  My main complaint is the third floor halls smell baby if stale cigarettes.  The room was okay.  The price was reasonable and they have free bottled water, coffee, wine, etc.  Beer accommodating staff.

My husband and I tried it last weekend. They had several drinks, appetizers and desserts for 7$ each.  We tried several items and everything we had was really good. Especially the Mac and Cheese fritters.... we ordered a second order for our dessert. Spoke to Rudolph the executive chef... he explained how the restaurant came together ;)  -----we'll definitely go back!

I usually don't write any reviews, but I had to this time to make everyone aware. I ordered 3 burritos and chips, guac and salsa during the prime lunch time hour. Picked up my order and on my way back to the office & realize they didn't include the chips...so I went back again and got the chips but had to wait 10 mins because the guy was on the phone. This isn't even the highlight, I'm more than half way done eating the food and I find out what appears to be a mosquito in my food! Gross...I won't be going back again.

Ahhhh! HOW does Solomonov get his hummus SO DARN CREAMY and DELICIOUS?!?!?! And did he master the art of pickling? I foresee a forthcoming hummus party as the result of experimenting to create Zahav/Dizengoff-style hummus and I could eat the pickled veggies alllll day.

Can't speak to the establishment itself, as today I dined on take-out acquired by another, other than to say that I was sadly disappointed last time I made an attempt to dine-in ('cause they were sold out...sold out sadness!!).

In any case, all four hummuses on the menu today were fabulous. I think the lamb and the hominy were my favorite. The lamb itself was well-cooked and so flavorful: it paired perfectly with the hummus. Loved the blue corn in the hominy hummus: fun texture addition! The beet hummus was solid, too: beets were cooked to the perfect tenderness (not too hard, not too soft), and had a nice, not-too-sweet flavor. And of course no one can go wrong with the traditional hummus, particularly on Dizengoff's fluffy pita bread: more like naan than pita you'd pick up in the supermarket.

Will definitely return, though for elevensies, 'cause apparently by 11:26AM the line was out-the-door!

I just left the store at 10:19 PM after waiting in line for 10+ minutes to checkout because there was only one line in the entire store open. The self checkout lines were lit up and looked to be completely functional but had them roped off even though they weren't closed. Seems like this is the perfect time for the self checkouts to do what target put them there to do but instead every customer has to wait in line unnecessarily.

Their fancy marketing names for each cupcake were confusing as Hell! Just say what the heck it is and save the poor girl behind the counter her voice.
