I bought 1/2 a DOZEN and they were very ordinary, small cake and bad ratio of frosting to cake... I had to toss part of the frosting away.

It tasted like cake mix. Wasn't moist and my dinner guests were not impressed either.

Aesthetically there are some things that need to be addressed with the "branding". The cupcake logo on the building, is different to the logo on the business card and different again to the logo on the box.
The flyer looks like ass too. 
The website is OK but needs to have better click through navigation between images. 
The images of the cupcakes look like they are a mix of home shot and semi-professional, some of the cupcake frosting look like giant turds. Generally bad lighting and not great focus.
Then there are all these cheeze-ball photos of kids faking that they are having a party but the whole thing looks forced.



I give this place a year.

We came here for the ugly sweater Christmas party thrown by flats mafia fishing last night. This was a great event with an insane raffle. They raffled off endless prizes with everything from gift baskets to a boat! This will definitely be an event we come to every year from now on.
As for 81 bay, this place is huge. The food truck they had out front (wicked oak bbq) was amazing. I'm not much of a beer drinker but I did try a sip of two different kinds and liked both. They also had wine, champagne and sake for those of us who did not want beer. The bartenders were friendly and you never had to wait long for a drink. Prices were average with a brewery, about $6 for a beer and $7 for wine. They are dog friendly and kid friendly as well.
Alena Williams-Wallace has been a resident at Kingston Square community for 11 years and really enjoys living here.

Good coffee, horrible WiFi (won't even load my work sites or email but Facebook and twitter work fine) 
Pastries are good too

This is a good Mexican restaurant.
Friendly efficient staff, casual friendly atmosphere.
The food is excellent, good portion sizes, and great quality ingredients.
We had a table of 8, and no complaints from even the pickiest of diners in our group.....
Fajitas, burritos, chimichangas, soft tacos the list goes on. Everyone had good food.
When we come back to St Louis, we will be there again!

Oh, boy...I don't want to be to long winded here.  I paid up front for acoustic pickups two years ago, as Tony said he was on the phone ordering at the time I was in the store, and he claimed he could get me a deal.  I paid more than $200 up front, two years...no pickups.  He also sold a consignment instrument of mine and kept the money. And; there is more to this situation (not good stuff) that I wont share here.  I don't event know what else to say...BEWARE!

Whoever decided what items this small Target should carry made some bad mistakes. They should not be carrying items that every CVS and Walgreens carry (and those stores are located on just about every corner) but should concentrate on valued items that are hard to find elsewhere in this part of the city. I'm thinking of toys as an example. They have a small and awful toy selection, that takes up about 2 shelves. No one else in this part of the city is offering reasonably priced popular toys, but Target has chosen not to fill this niche. I guess I will be continuing to purchase toys for my 4 grandchildren online from Amazon. 
Target should have put some thought into the items that customers can't easily find elsewhere, and concentrated on selling those items, instead of taking up enormous amounts of space selling laundry detergent, shampoo, aspirin, and the myriad other items that can be purchased easily, in every pharmacy in town. Really poorly thought-out selection.

I love going here for Ramen, it is the best one so far. I have try others ones in Chinatown but this one so far beats the rest.
It's a very small place, but worth the wait. But it is up to everyone taste preference.

Didn't work for me even though I did everything they said to do. Just tell me how laying under lights on a table, listening to relaxing mood music and standing on a platform that shakes and jiggles your body could possibly make you loose weight?  How could I have been so stupid? And they tell you to drink alkaline water but give you a bottle of regular water to drink while you are there. I requested at least a discount after finishing the program with no results but since they pressured me onto signing a contract I am stuck. I should have read the reviews before going. This is a rip off and i should have realized it while talking to the overweight man running the company. I highly advise you to try a more conventional method of loosing weight.

Their mussels are legit. When I came into their  establishment I could smell seafood was cooking and I took that as a good sign. The place is dimly lit which threw me off at first but I got used to the ambiance. Would try it out again. Try the meatball appetizer - a good price and great homemade meatballs.

I had a lump above my tooth. They my did a root canal, then a cap. Went back a few months later because I STILL had that lump on my gum. They told me they root canaled the WRONG TOOTH!! So they root canaled   The tooth next to it. Two years later I STILL have the abscess there and now there's PAIN which obviously tells me they STILL did something WRONG.  
I said I'd like to have veneers on my front teeth because of a bonded tooth, I couldn't have them whitened. They wisked me back in the chair. They started working on my teeth and I fell asleep. I woke up and looked in the mirror and they grinded all of my front teeth down to POINTS!!! I started screaming and Dr. Dutten told her assistant to get the Mirror away from me. They put caps on my front teeth ( that had NOTHING WRONG WITH THEM!!). I started crying and her reply was, "I told you we don't do veneers here". $5,000 later....

Very convenient shuttle bus to & from other terminal to terminal F.

Once arrived, there is a food court, 10 different food vendors. Plus more retailer, including an express spa. Perfect time for a quick massage!!!

This terminal is newer & well maintained. Very detail directions signs.

A little far from downtown, but a very nice hotel to stay at. Ample parking, friendly front desk and our room came with a cute little patio. We booked through hotels.com and got a fantastic deal! We will definitely stay here again if we get the chance.

Yeah, the oil change was fairly quick.  But I waited ~45 minutes before it started cause the place is ridiculously understaffed.  I don't blame the workers for that, but still.  Also, I don't know that I was given every oil option.  There was a lowest priced listed that was never mentioned verbally.  I don't know if that was because it wasn't feasible or because they just didn't want to.  Might take my business elsewhere in the future.

I got the broiled shrimp and daughter got the shrimp and scallops broiled. Vey good, came with grilled yellow squash, zucchini and mushrooms as well as rice pilaf. Plus corn fritters (sweet!) and 1 side. I got coleslaw which was very good. Daughter got fries. We both really enjoyed our meals and will definitely return :)

I really enjoyed this BBQ. Waited in line for about 10/15 minutes around 730 pm on a Saturday night. You wait in line to order, then find a seat and they bring your food. They have a good amount of beers on tap and in cans. My boyfriend got the brisket plate and the brisket looked a bit dry to me. It was fine with some sauce though. I got the baked potato with brisket on it which was to die for. The Mac n cheese was alright. 

It's pretty big inside and wasn't hard to find a seat. It definitely gets busy around lunch and dinner time though!

Lodge is good place to meet up with friends for drinks, but I don't recommend going there if you are looking for a great meal. Their "American Sushi" is pretty boring, and is incredibly over priced. Their service is subpar, and I don't think I have ever had a great server there. They have table topper drinks, which make it fun for a group. It turns into more of a club atmosphere at night, so if you are there on a weekend, expect it to get busy if you are eating a late dinner. 

I would recommend going to another one of the Ciccio restaurants if you are looking for a yummy meal. Lodge is just nothing to write home about.

Ordered thru the website on 6/24. Never heard anything back. Called the store and they wouldn't talk to me and hung up. Tried every day for a week. Never did get to ask about my order, they were always too busy to talk to me.  We've ordered from the Spice Corner for over 20 years.  I guess I will have to look for another place.  Sad when a business forgets their long time customers.

Great food, great selection of beer. Really comfortable environment. We come here often after work and have had no issues in over 5 years. Kick ass bar tenders and games always playing. Food is always correct and quick. A must for anyone passing through the Santa Barbara area.

I've been in and out of a decent number of convention centers over the years, but this is probably among the least welcoming and most impersonal.  It's not what I expecting in a city as warm, charming, and inviting as New Orleans.

Let's start with the good -- it's functional.  The massive building is very tidy, with good acoustics and visual set-ups in the conference rooms.  All the escalators were working, which is more than I can say about most convention centers.  So it serves its convention purpose well.  The only downside is the skinny, mile-long layout, which means it can take upwards of 20 minutes just to walk from one meeting room to another.  Give yourself lots of time, and wear comfortable shoes.

But what it shows in functionality, it loses in character.  The interior has all the charm of a prison cell, with lots of battleship greys and lack of natural light.  Also, there's virtually nowhere to sit, chitchat, and recharge your electronic devices in the common areas -- which is a big no-no if you're hosting a large convention in the 21st century,

Concessions are the usual overpriced junk food.  There's a Starbucks with an hour-long line -- it's actually faster to go outside to the Starbucks down the block, sandwiched between the Marriott and Harrah's casino.

All in all, the New Orleans convention center gets a big "meh" from me.

I'm Peruvian, I went to this place a couple times and always it's the same....the pollo a la brasa is always way too dry and never fresh. The green rice is too greasy, I found a lot of oil underneath and it's gross. Other places have so much better food than this. They should check the food before served, like making sure the rice isn't so greasy and that the chicken is served right off the grill, not super dry like it's been sitting around for hours.

I had never been here, but always heard how great it was. Took out of town people here and were so disgusted with the rudeness of the hostess crew. We are low maintenance people, but when the hostess marks your party as "incomplete", causing you to wait an extra 45 unnecessary minutes it is frustrating. Mistakes happen, but when the hostess rolled her eyes in an "oh well" fashion and walked away from me while asking a question it escalates from being frustrated to plain angry. I told her she was being rude and she replied "we are really busy today and we have a lot going on" Hmmmm. Last time I checked, this isn't a freaking ER unit. There is no excise for being an asshole, sorry. Left messages for the manager and never heard back. This place is relying heavily on the popularity of it, but they need to wake up. Over hyped food with awful customer service (except our badass waitress, Sara) is a recipe for disaster.

If you like good tasting coffee, good food, and nice costumer service interaction, this is not the place to go! Unfortunately the place is ran by a rude man who doesn't appreciate loyal customers, and even belittles his employees in front of them. Take your business to another local company, this place is a joke.

It was my first time at a Chuy's. Not bad. I do like that they had 1800 margaritas for only $3 all day, everyday!  (Normal house margaritas run for $2)  Pretty cheap for those of us on a budget, and love our margaritas! Ahem!  =)

The bartender/servers were very friendly!  Never been, so they gave me the rundown on what was good to eat and when was the best time to come back for either happy hour or to hang out.  Pretty cool.

Now, I don't know if this is the normal fare or just during lunch, cause it was my first time there, but the place is basically self-service.  First, you get to seat yourself, then a server comes to give you your menu, however, you must go to the counter/bar area to place your order and pay for your meal when done.  It was fine with me though, cause it's not like the bar is that far away... lol.  

Everything on the menu was pretty cheap too!  Tex-Mex cuisine...Most entrees were under $7, plus they serve breakfast all day too!  I ordered the Whitefish tacos (1Fried & 1 Mesquite Grilled).  Not too bad.  I admit, I've had better, but it was ok.  Not too much flavor to it.  I was expecting a bit more flavor or spice.  Next time I'll try one of the burgers!  They do have a big menu, so I'll opt to try more things and give a better update next time.  =)

All in all it was a nice place...pretty big space actually.  Cool décor and pics on the wall.  Did I mention there was a self-serve salsa & chips bar too!  Anyways, worth coming back again, if not for the food, for the margaritas for sure!  =)

My first time going. Had a full bikini and a full leg done. My wax specialist was a very sweet professional girl. But the room was freezing. I understand that it helps so that the wax performs better. I had several missed areas on my legs. I feel as if they should use a mag light to see the hairs closer. I ended up going home and waxing some spots myself. The full bikini was ok but she had to do several passes on the same most sensitive of areas. Applied way too much wax and tried to pull before it was dry. My skin wasn't being held taught so it was just straight pulling. Needless to say it was extremely uncomfortable. To the point that I told her to leave it and I'd get it myself. It took much longer than they estimated. The tech tried her best but I feel as if some more training would be helpful with the bikini area.

This is more like a 3.5 star... but couldn't really give it 4 because of how long it took to get drinks and food.

Absolutely lovely place, I LOVE the atmosphere, the decor, the different rooms and the outdoor garden-ish area in the front. 

Drinks were very good and VERY strong! Definitely get the coconut rum & lime drink.... deeeeelicious. 

Food was pretty good, I had the mahi mahi sliders and they were a bit overcooked but still tasty. Seasoned fries were yummmmy, and the fish special of the day was very tasty... it was monkfish I think? cooked very well, though the passionfruit sauce it came with was a little too sweet for me. 

The service was very friendly, but it did take a loooooong time to get our drinks and our food. Only after waiting for about half hour for food did they bring out some bread and oil for us to nosh on. I understand that kitchens get backed up, but to make the experience better you should make sure you bring out bread early on so people aren't so starving by the time the food gets there. 

That was the only drawback, but  I still had a great time and would go back again to try it out. It may have just been a slow night

This hotel is shit! Dont stay here if you dont want a pimp trying to look into your room or a junkie comes and knocks on your door asking for drugs or if they can stay in your room. You need to call the police yourself if you want the police to come out and help you.  The management team will NOT DO ANYTHING TO HELP YOU! If you leave your room and give them the impression you left they will go in your room and steal from you. The female one who goes by an alias of Sammy will over charge people to just over charge she says deposit but gives no documentation stating what it is in regards to nor the refund policy. She is racist and will say snide remarks about patrons to other patrons here.  They allow hookers to come in at night and stay free of charge for sex services. The night mgmt which is the husband of the day personnel is conducting this sex exposition and having his minor child be his look out! The city has been notify due to bed bugs a family has been diagnosed with an official doctors note for bed bugs. They hide all these episodes from the city to avoid being fined and or shut down. They tried to offer work to a patron of the hotel and had the nerve to offer the man $16.00 A DAY!!! NOT AN HOUR A DAY!! AND DOING A 12 HOUR SHIFT!!! FOR 16 DOLLARS A DAY!!! They have been reported to the city the state and corporate along with Ice and CUSTOMS.  There was an accident in room 255 a 2 yr old child foot was caught in an ac machine which was not properly closed correctly. The child sustained severe injuries to her foot. Instead of them showing compassion they tried to cover their asses and tried to call CPS on the family the police told the family it is a bogus call. The management never did a full complete clean in the room the room has blood in the carpet in the ac machine in the curtains.  Instead mgmt was more concerned about getting another body in the room. Sanitation is never done correctly in the rooms people will break in the rooms to sleep in them mgmt will not tell house keeping to clean the room again they just repair the break and rent the room out. 
Dont stay at the Vagabond Inn go some where else this place is falling apart half of the outlets dont work they will have electricity shortages they have shitty wifi. They have constant sewer overflows and refuse to call a professional plumber no proper ac no accessibly entry to the 2nd level for handicapped individuals.  Terrible place!!!!

I stopped in for the #renoveganchefchallenge and while the food was good, we got the bbq pulled jackfruit sandwich. It was just not worth the money. They brought us about $3 worth of food for $18. Horrible presentation.

A handful of sweet potato tots, and some jackfruit on what looked like sara lee flat bread. 

The waiter was amazing.

Will not be returning!
Meh. Not great and not cheap. Drive by it all the time after work and don't go. Food is so-so. But the Dairy Queen and Fry's nearby are awesome.

Got dragged in here by wife on search for something...service was excellent, not like most furniture stores that hover around like a hawk.  And for the guys, it has an awesome game room section you can hang out with your kids or on your own while your other half shops till her feet hurt.  Great selection of basement/game room items including accessories for pool tables and such you wouldn't expect when walking in.

Definitely a level above all the other chain pizza joints around.  When you taste the pizza you can tell it is prepped differently; with a focus on quality instead of mass production.  That atmosphere here is a lot of fun as well.  Good beer menu with craft beers and a full bar.  They have craft root beer and cream soda on taps.  You order at the counter and your food is brought to you.  

To call this craft pizza is a stretch.  If you want true craft pizza with unusual and fresh ingredients, then go to Napolese or Pizzology.  For everyday pizza ingredients at a higher level of quality than you are used to, this is a solid choice.

Once a nice mall full of college students and young families it has now turned into a dump, and a rather dangerous one at that.

Maybe if "car stereo loudness wars" or "dodge the inmate in training" become in vogue pastimes it will shine again.

Avoid.

Visited here while in Nashville.  Didn't realize it was all you could eat before we sat down.  Staff was nice and engaging.  We're not big eaters and really didn't get our moneys worth.  If you're hungry, go.  Prices were reasonable.  Family style.  No leftovers or go boxes, not a big deal.  Probably wouldn't come back just because of it being all you can eat.
It was so good the first time but the service the second time wasn't up to par. Good food, decent prices, and on Bourbon St.

I came here needing a breakfast sandwich, and looked past some of the poor reviews. I didn't want a $7 egg sandwich from Honey's with 2 sides - just a decent egg sandwich.

I strive to ignore hyperbole on yelp, so I paid no attention to Mary S.'s comment about the horrible smell of moth balls and bad ice, but I should have listened. The smell is really bad, and weird. Unfortunately I can't leave a place once the nice person behind the counter looks me in the eye, so I ordered a sandwich. I then look back as their making it, and there are 4-5 people crammed in a little space fumbling around doing what 1 could have done more efficiently, when I see them put an egg patty in the microwave. I can't believe I didn't see this on any other reviews. You do not serve breakfast sandwiches if you're not going to cook them. Stick to beer and hoagie take out.

It took them over 10 minutes to microwave an egg sandwich, and they didn't even toast the bagel! If I ever run out of breakfast fixings again, I'll go to Honey's. I really don't want to be harsh because I'd love for a place like this to work in the neighborhood, I just wish they'd listen to some of the reviews.

We had 9 people in our party for meet the parents before we got married. Waiter was useless and rude an super impatient. They bragged about red sea bass being the last one. 1/2 was overcooked and other half barely cooked with zero taste. I personally ordered the veal and I would have had an easier time eating my tennis shoes. Sent both back, got different food to replace it. Inlaws paid the bill. Got to the hotel and they noticed they were charged for the returned food! Make a call on the morning and it was a hassle. Place USED to be great now it's just junk.

Delivered a day late, claiming nobody was home, which was not true.... and the order was nothing close to what I ordered. Just don't do it! You will regret it. Their customer service was nothing short of horrible. They did not offer to replace the flowers, but said they could come get them and credit my card...,the day before Thanksgiving. The  so-called "designers" were gone, so they couldn't replace them. She had no explanation for them, but said my mom liked them! I even sent her the photos. There were no fall colors, a coral and red rose, yellow daisy/mum looking things and a lot of greenery. The candle wasn't even standing up straight.  I honestly don't  know how they are in business.

I've been here twice now and while the food is good, the service is incredibly slow. We have gone for dinner both times. I've heard there is a brunch buffet with live music, which would probably be worth trying out.

If there seems to be any Hyatt Place deserving of a major renovation and facelift this would be it. For starters it was pretty hard to see where it is. There doesn't the be a sign at the entrance while the one on the facade is hard to see from the road as it's obscured by a large tree. Talking about trees, we arrived in the evening and thought the property looked somber. In daylight though, it looks like a private oasis of lush tropical trees. While our room seemed clean, rust around bathroom fixtures, carpet stains and such appear to show the hotel's age. The staff were very friendly and accommodating. The hotel does not seem to have a "lost and found" department despite a proclaimed six months hold policy.

They have this ridiculous phone prompt system that just loops and loops and goes nowhere.   So frustrating.  When you finally get a human they transfer you somewhere and you are lost on hold all over again.  

So I dropped off a prescription in person.  Someone was supposed to call me to make an appointment.  One week later - no call.

I usually stay at a Marriott owned property when I travel if I can. I stayed here for a night and was pleased.  
It's close to everything down town and on the water. 

My room was clean, the staff was courteous, check-in was a breeze and I would stay here again.

I didn't have a rental car, but paying to park would be the only thing I would have a problem with otherwise, I would suggest staying here.

One of the most interesting things about a visit to Tarpon Springs is the presence of the Greek language. It is the largest percentage of Greeks in the US, but I was not prepared to hear the language spoken by people just walking down the street. Of course, Greek is pervasive in the restaurants, so you know the food will be authentic.

It was raining during our visit, so I stopped at the first place I came to. It was a good decision.  The service was excellent, and the Gyro platter with Greek salad and plenty of Tzaziki was superb. The warm bread was an unexpectantly nice touch.

I will make more visits to Tarpon Springs as I did not get to spend as much time as I would have liked, and I will explore more restaurants, but Mykonos will remain a favorite.

This review is for the location in South Reno in the Summit Mall:
Excellent service and very good Mexican food. We enjoyed the Camarones al Ajo (lots of Ajo... :) ) as well as Burritos and other dishes, and deserts. Nice bar with beers, wine and spirits too. The place is quite large and can become a little noisy at times but a great place, if you go with a larger group.

My experience  with this facilities & it's  doctors was dismal. First the building is poorly marked with signage & looks like a warehouse. My GPS & I were literally going in circles.
After the paperwork came an almost 4 hour wait to see the doctor, way excessive in my book. The the doctor breezes in with some trainees with him & is as impersonal, as you can get. I think he was looking for a mirror to check his hair in.
In a less than 10 minute consultation, he informs me I need surgery, no discussion. I attempted to ask questions & was shut down. Go schedule the surgery with his surgical coordinator & he'll see me for surgery.
No way in hell, am I going to have surgery under these circumstances or with this physician. To put it even more plainly, I would not allow him to preform knee surgery on my dog.
Yup, I was out of there & not looking back!
What's a girl to do? Find & utelize doctors you trust, that answer your questions & value you as an individual.

I've been going to this Tokyo Express for years and I love it. The food's so fresh and the service is super fast, even if you walk in and there's a line to the door, it's but minutes before you're at the front of the line-up. The amount of staff during busy periods is well-planned and adequate. They're swift and efficient at the till, I just find the wait to get the food a bit annoying (You have to wait for your number to be called out). But on the pro side, it's always clean here. The soy sauce bottles are well-replenished and fresh. I like how they have a cold water and ice dispenser you can access yourself. I always order the dynamite rolls. They're delicious. I've also tried the Mexican rolls which has a bit of tempura in them that's crunchy and yummy. Mind you, this is Japanese fast food. If you go to a higher-end restaurant, you'll taste the difference in freshness, flavour and presentation.  I used to get the teriyaki chicken bowl without skin here, but started finding the sauce was too sweet and sugary, so it cancelled out the health factor of eating skinless chicken and veggies on rice. They have bento boxes with a little of everything if you want to sample. Coming in for six rolls has always tided me over on the way home when I'm starving. Nothing like sticky rice to pacify road rage.

The burgers are amazing!!!!! Try the Kage Burger, which is a burger, topped with fried chicken, bacon and cheddar. It's out of this world delicious!! Definitely take advantage of the $1 up-charge for the onion rings or sweet potato fries; they're equally tasty!

I like this place a lot. If the weather is good for sitting outside, you'll have a fantastic view of the river. Inside, most of the tables don't really have a river view, but the atmosphere is very pleasant. The food is excellent, and fairly reasonable for New Hope. I get the chili every time I come here--it's great.

Think this would be good for a girls night out. Problem is I'm a man.

This quaint wine bar on beautiful Beach Drive is nestled perfectly in the heart of DTSP. The atmosphere reminded me of an upscale NYC lounge and brick walls and dark cedar wood bar. There was a two man violin and guitar playing at the entrance which entranced the mood more. Perfect for a quick first date or glass of wine. Unfortunately I came here for DINNER with fiancé and mother but ended up eating a 'real' meal when we got home. This is a wine and cheese bar which offers small portions of food; smaller than tapas portions. The cheese, wine, and portions we ordered were good but extremely lacking sustenance.

This place is great to have in the neighborhood. you'd think it would be super expensive but it really isn't at all and they are constantly running specials via Groupon or the coupon books you get in the mail. Mainly all running shoes.

The treadmills stop in the middle of runs.  I can be going at a 6.9 to 7.0 speed and they just STOP.  It nearly knocks you on your butt.  I see this happening to other people all the time too.  I told management but they act like they don't care.  I asked if they had any idea of why it would happen and in a rude way I'm told, "How would I know?  Do I look like a treadmill technician to you?  They never even asked if I was OK.
The parking lot is hard to get around because people park in the middle of the aisles when the close parking spots are taken.  This creates a fire hazard and just makes it hard to get thru.
The rowing machines readout screens are always broke.
The sauna is always broke but this is the least of my concerns at this point.
They do have good free weights and weight machines.  Also the racquetball courts are nice.

We ordered take out breakfast. We asked
For no egg on our sandwich and it was more egg than sausage. We also got a stale bagel.

I ordered at 9:20 on a Saturday, a grinder and fries.  They said 20 minutes.  I got there at 9:45 and it wasn't ready.  Then I noticed the mad scramble because the fries were ready but nobody made the grinder.  10 minutes later I walked out with a grinder and cold, soggy fries.  I'm really disappointed, this used to be a great place but the service and quality have decreased significantly over the past year.  I think all the promotion they've done may have made them busier than the staff can handle.  That's really sad, it used to be the only decent place in the neighborhood and I recommended it to everyone.  Lately I hesitate to order from there.

This is by FAR the worst Taco Bell I've ever gone too, and I've been to a ton.  Unfortunately, this is the closest t bell to my house thus I've been there more times than I'd like to admit.  My main gripe with this location is that if you go after 10pm there is literally about a 60% chance you'll be met at the drive thru with "our computers are down" or some related excuse on why I can't order any food.  No bs, at one point I went on a stretch where like 3 out of 5 times attempting to pull through I couldn't order food for one reason or another.  At this point I just assume this happens because the staff doesn't want to work.  Ive always wondered if the owners aware of this?  You'd think so considering sales after 10, let alone midnight (good luck attempting to get food then), must plummet.  Otherwise, the quality isn't so bad, other than it being Taco Bell food, of course.

Oh god no.  Nope.  What in the heck did you give me ?  I ordered spring rolls...you know, the crispy, nearly translucent wrappers with a fresh vegetable filling?  Not hard, burnt, eggy with brown stuff inside.  Gross.  And, the pad thai was awful.  Why ?  I can make this so much better. It's not even hard.  I pay $30 for 2, because I expect it to be convenient, but also good.  Good, it was not.

First time at Bernie's. Food was good and bartender was attentive. I was a bit shocked when I got the bill since long island iced teas were $12 each. That's a new all time high for me and I eat out 3 days per week for years and at many 5 star expensive restaurants. Never paid that much, especially at a neighborhood type restaurant.

I setup an appointment 2 weeks ago. Upon making the appointment the person who scheduled the appointment looked to see if there were any service recalls. I was notified that there were no recalls. I asked estimated service time, one hour. Move to appointment time 8:00 a.m. bring car in and was told to have a seat my car will be done in no time. Half hour later Service advisor Patrick came to my husband and I said they would need the car an additional half hour because of a recall to update the brakes and transmission through the computer system and he couldn't let me leave with my car!?!? Ok I  understand but we are 2 hours later and I'm still waiting. I go to see how much longer and Patrick has an attitude.  He the had the audacity to ask me where I bought the car. It was there fault I wasn't notified. Then 15 minutes later my car is done and he said he wasn't aware of the recalls until I brought the vehicle in. Ok so why wasn't I offered a loaner. I'm driving a 2019 Cadillac with factory service and warranty???? Poor service. Don't worry Patrick you made me feel like I wasn't wanted as a customer and believe me I won't return.

This is my new favorite place.

I've been here twice with groups of friends for Friday night dinners and both times we had a blast. While it's evident that they are new and still working out the kinks, the staff's friendliness and positive attitudes make up for the less-than-perfect service. It takes awhile to get settled in (they need improvement in getting guests menus, taking orders and getting drinks on the table), but the staff is very nice, the menus offers a wide selection, the food is really good and the prices are awesome (everything on the menu is less than $12)!
It proves that when people are apologetic and friendly, people will come back to dine even if they didn't receive perfect service.

Also, the BEST part about this place....it's a BYO(T)! 
AND...the margarita mix is free!

We recently learned from our server that they also feature different theme nights. For example, there's all you can eat taco night, poker night and a Spanish/English conversation night as well.

So if you're looking to get tipsy on tequila and have a cheap dinner and a fun night, this place is for you.

I agree with Chrissy R.  The food is very, very good and high quality -but it's just not worth the price.  However, they do carry a startling assortment of gourmet chocolates and snacks.  

The salad bar option allows you to try a small bit of the items that appeal to you.

They used to have a slew of designer cupcakes, too.  Not sure if they still do.

I went here for a date on a Sunday night. The atmosphere is excellent. The service was excellent. The food was very good. The margaritas were AWESOME. The only problem is that we spent $60 for two meals and two drinks, and we left feeling hungry. We ordered three tacos, guacamole, and a rice bowl to share between two people. It just wasn't enough food, and for $60, I definitely expect to feel full. If your budget is unlimited, DEFINITELY worth going. If not, I'd say skip it.

I love the accessibility of the monon. I run on it a few times a week along with thousands of other people. I feel like it's a community in itself with passing similar faces each week. The paths are always clean and well maintained no matter what the weather is like. Just be alert and careful at some of the busy road crossings because the cars forget to look for pedestrians. I really enjoy how many restaurants and bars are built along the Monon. Great way to people watch or stop to refuel. 

My only complaint is that I wish it was a little wider with a separate biking and running lanes. It can get crowded on nice days and bikers have to weave in and out of the walkers/runners which is troublesome for everyone.

I've been wanting to try this place since it opened and had extremely high hopes- especially because of the creativity of the menu. I was disappointed on all fronts. 42 dollars for a coke, 2 mediocre pasta dishes and a side of broccoli was cringeworthy. I love spending money on a great meal, this was not that meal. I should have known it was going to be weird when I was told it was a fast-casual ordering system. Not worth the 20 minute drive or 42 dollars spent and it makes me sad to say that.

I generally like shake shack but my experience this time was slightly under my expectations. I order the shroom burger and the cheese fries. The shroom burger was tasty, but the buns were just overly greasy. After eating it, I felt a little bloated and unhealthy. The mushroom itself was delicious though - juicy on the inside and crusty and crunchy on the outside. Just really let down by the greasy buns. I expected the cheese fries to be cheesier. The fries were also dense and mushy. I'll probably come back here again just because it's close to my classes and sometimes I'll forget to pack my lunch.

My second experience with T-Mobile and another disaster.  Because of my bad experience at Tucson Mall, I chose this store.  I recently purchased an I-phone and wanted to port my number over from my other phone with another company.  My I-phone was working, but was just on another number from Seattle.  The salesperson there told me I would have to buy another sim card and it would be $16.  I said that I didn't need one because there was already one in the phone.  He said, "You HAVE to buy a sim card.  What do you expect, you're going to get this stuff for free?"  I texted my daughter, who knows cell phones, and she said I did not need one.  I just asked the salesperson if I could port my number over.  After piddling around with the computer for 15 minutes, the salesperson informed me that the system was too slow, and I would just have to call customer service to get ported over.  I asked for the number; he said, "611, Duh."  I went out to my car and called customer service; I was jacked around to four different people, and ended up with someone in the Philippines who I could not understand.  After a total of 90 minutes on the phone and in this store I ended up contacting my daughter, who, using a REAL T-Mobile connection, got the port under way in under 10 minutes.  I am going to keep this service, but never go to any of the stores.  This one is as bad as the one at Tucson Mall.

Went again a few weeks ago to give it a second chance. Food is still delicious - great spices, depth of flavor. But service is just as unorganized and slow. It's so disappointing when the food is so top notch.

I visited Pink Lime around August of 2012 - I was lured there by a Groupon for a mani/pedi and chose to upgrade to the spa version for an extra $20.  The Mani/Pedi was nice enough.  The girl doing the service was quite young and told me that she'd only been out of school for a couple of months - which might explain a few things.  After the polish dried, once i was home, I noticed that my fingernails were covered in tiny bumps.  It actually looked like somebody had applied polish over gravel on my nails.  I'll give them credit - I called the next day to let them know what happened and they offered me a free polish change.  I couldn't take them up on the offer because of scheduling problems, but i appreciated the offer.  HOWEVER - before making that offer, I almost felt as though he thought I was lying and had intentionally done something to ruin the polish application.  He kept defending the product and saying he couldn't understand how that happened.

I was also less than impressed with the tiny selection of colour that they had.

After the initial service, that's where it started to go downhill.  While I was waiting for my nails to dry in the lobby (there are no dryers so you have to just sit and wait), the man at the desk started talking to me about their VIP club.  They were offering a discounted fee to clients who came in with the groupon and if you purchased the membership you would receive 50% off ALL services for a full year.  I was impressed because they offered a full salon and spa and my regular hair stylist was just about to go on Maternity leave... the timing worked out so i signed up.

I get my eyebrows waxed every 2 weeks, without fail, and typically spend $200+ on my hair every 4 - 5 months.. so i was looking forward to the savings!  The first time I called to book a brow wax, I asked for an appointment 2 days away at 6 pm.  I was asked if i could make it for 5:45 instead - so i left work a little early and headed downtown.  AND HAD TO SIT AND WAIT UNTIL ALMOST 6:15.  I was seeing red by the time i got my service done.  but the girl who did the waxing (I think her name was Justina) was pretty talented.


Too bad i was never able to book with her again.  the next time i called, i found out that she was on vacation for 3 weeks.  The next time, she was all booked up.

Honestly, this place is not worth the money they're charging and despite the fact that I have this "membership" until September - I won't be going back.

Came here for Making Time and had so much fun. Free sailor jerry drinks and PBR until 11. Free hotdogs and buns next to the unisex bathroom. Bartenders and staff were helpful and friendly. Danced the night away. Definitely coming back.

I think of this place from time to time. Had such an amazing experience here. The most amazing bbq shrimp. I was skeptical with the "bbq" title, but shoo wee pa paw...it is divine! Super crowded. The beef tornado dish was fabulous. Truly a Nawlins experience. Can't wait to go back!

Unfortunately, we had a not great experience. The service was fine. I ordered an appetizer and the chicken sandwich. The sandwich was dry and I had to ask for extra aioli. I should have asked for a steak knife. 

We left after lunch and then I realized my appetizer never came out. Such a bummer.

We won't be back for awhile.
Everything I tried was delicious, and it has a lovely area to sit outside in nice weather

This is a great car wash I love their set up. When you first pull in you pay using the kiosks. I'm not sure how the system knows who paid for what but I've only ever gotten the basic wash. You can buy a package as well. It takes cash and credit cards.

I like that there isn't those two metal tracks that you have to drive your tires into. I'm always worried that I'm not going to turn my tires the wrong way and mess up my tires or rims. This is more like a flat rolling track.

My car gets very clean and they get every spot. The best part is the vacuum section. Once you're done with the car wash you can pull into the vacuum area. There's two vacuums for each parking spot. They are very very powerful. Be sure you have anything important out of the way because if you move the hose in the wrong direction you will suction up some thing you may not want to. They also have a place you can clip your mats so that you can vacuum those. They also provide rags if you want to wipe your car down.

I actually bypass another car wash to come to this one.
Love everything about suburban square.  Can't wait to get back there.  Apple sore is helpful and friendly.

Look people, if youz think your gonna get the best cheese steak in Philly at Terminal F, then you deserve to be in Terminal F. 

Just appreciate that if youz was too stoopid to find a good cheesesteak among the million or so places OUTSIDE the airport, you can still pretend.

The studio is cozy, yet the space is very well distributed. The yoga instructors are very knowledgeable and caring. The showers are clean as well as the rest of the yoga studio. I love their hours too, even though it would be nice if they had a earlier Bikram class, but the other classes are pretty good too.

 I look forward to continuing my yoga practice here.

What a terrible experience.

I was in New Orleans with a small group of 6, and we had all heard rave reviews about this place. We made a reservation for 9pm and were happy to see the line for dining snaking out into the street - usually a sign of a great restaurant.

We were told by several friends to get the alligator cheesecake and the fried roast beef po boy. In the interest of health, I ordered the potato-encrusted drum.

The first problem was when our waiter forgot a glass of wine that we had ordered several times. Eventually, he delivered it and said it would be on the house. That was fine, but then he delivered entrees. 

On the menu, it said that entrees came with a starter salad. Since we hadn't received them, we asked, and his exact response was "Well, only two of you ordered entrees, everyone else ordered a combination of starter plates to share. So decided just to cancel the salads." Really?!? Is that your decision to make?

Ten minutes later - LITERALLY ten minutes! - one of our party hadn't received his meal yet. Because our waiter wasn't the one delivering the meal and hadn't walked by our table in ten minutes, it took us that long to alert him. Instead of apologizing, he said "I'll be right back" and disappeared into the kitchen. He stayed in there for 6-7 minutes, and reappeared with the Chicken & Biscuits that we ordered. At that point, it had been almost 20 minutes and our food was cold. The Chicken & Biscuits looked like crap thrown on a plate, and was so gross that Alex didn't eat more than two bites. It was clear that the waiter had completely forgotten that order when he came back 5 minutes later to ask what Alex's sides were. All it would have taken was an apology from the waiter, but instead he ignored the problem and tried to charge us for that meal.

Additionally, my drum was soggy and not worth $25. I would have gladly paid $13 for this meal, but not worth the $25 I was charged.

Honestly, I will not go back to Jacques-Imos. Their advertised "Lousy Service" is true. Mediocre, overpriced food and terrible service. Was by far the worst meal in this city.

I stayed here for one night and I would say it was very mediocre. The plus is that the location is great. It's walking distance from movie theater, shops, resturants, etc. There is a kitchenette which makes it convenient for making food. However, the bathroom had makeup smudges on the counter from whoever stayed prior, which means it wasn't cleaned. The towels and wash cloths were dingy and tattered, the microwave was not cleaned out, and there were stains on one of the pillow cases.

Stopped in to check it out based on the reviews. Was hoping for a better experience. The service was not good. We were at our table almost 10 minutes before someone came buy and offered us water. Did not take a drink order until another 5 minutes, and food order another 5. 
Burger was overcooked, good but overcooked, chicken wings were the worst wings I have ever had, dry, no taste, just crispy dry wings. Fajitas were good but the chicken was dry. Will not be back, doubt this restaurant will make it long term unless management gets out and actually observes the service, and talks to customers.

I have read many of the reviews and I guess people should start going for Lunch.  I love the salmon sandwich, flat bread, salads and salmon with butternut squash risotto.  The owner always takes the time to ask about the food and service.

Their salads are top notch! These babies were not an afterthought when creating the menu. We shared a pizza and the crunchy cooked edges and crisp cheese were good. Old school Nintendo in the attached bar is a fun addition, check it out while you're there.

I absolutely loved my experience here. I went during a sample dress sale and went home with my dream designer dress at an incredibly fair price. Jessica and her team are kind, personable, and helpful. When we bought the dress, she had a cute piece where we took a polaroid photo with the dress bag which says, "she said yes."

There was a great selection, the venue was lovely and clean. Jessica recommended an alteration specialist, who is phenomenal and well priced. Great experience all around.

Noticed this place on our runs to Produce Junction.  Got a Groupon and figured on giving it a try.  Glad we did.  

Started with an order of Kheema Toast which we really enjoyed, would have ordered a second if we didnt put in for meals.  
Main courses were Veggie and Chicken Biryani.  Good flavor with the right amount of heat.  Also had some Naan bread which was ok.

Place is small and filled up as we ate.  They also had a steady take out.  One guy was covering the entire front and did an excellent job.  

Will patronize this establishment in the future

This was once a great place. Superb quality steaks, cooked right and good portions. Service was always top notch. So good we ate there every week for the first 3 years it was open. Not anymore. The steaks kept getting smaller, they were never cooked right and it just wasn't the quality it once was. Shameful state of decline to the point we were getting bad meals more often than good ones. It was time to quit. We'll probably try it again, but we are no longer the fans we once were.

I have ate here couple times. Food is okay. I liked the cumin lamb but everything else is an average, especially the beef noodle soup.

The service is the reason why i gave it three stars only. No one was filling waters for us and the waitress is helpless in recommending dishes. Not mention my horrible experiences with calling them for take out. They showed no patience and are very rude in responding questions and request!

The salsa was fresh chips were good. The taco was fresh but for some reason I prefer the crunchy shell to any tortilla. I had the seafood soup because it looked good on the menu and was different. I should have went with the tride and true. The seafood all taste it started of frozen and then they over boiled it all, The veggies and broth were great but I didnt get the seafood soup for the veggies. I may give it another try and get some enchiladas or taco salad but there are a lot of Mexican places.

We've been coming here since I moved here. 4 years ago. My fiance has lived here his whole life and loves it here. 
In the last year we've noticed some changes that caused a 3 star instead of a 5 star. Customer service is rushed, still nice, but without the smile and awesome customer service we'd expect.  
Our pizza accidentally got burned, they offered an appetizer while we waited, complimentary of course. THANK YOU!
 When the pizza came, and our Alfredo the dark, it was all tasty and delicious...but 2 things though that caused 3 stars instead of 5.
 The Alfredo was not as thick, cheesy, and WOW factor it is. Our amazing bartender David helped out explaining that it was still really hot and that when it cools it will thicken. I understood this because I've made sauces myself. But I noticed that they didn't offer the parmesan cheese on either dish when they arrived and that made a big difference. We didn't recognize anybody that we usually see either, really sad.
David was wonderful though and totally made up for everything and we will be back. Thank you!
Had it not been 80 degrees in the place, I would have givin another star. Just so you know, it was in the 40s outside. Food was pretty good, it was just hotter than hell in the dining room.

Virago is one of the swankiest sushi places in Nashville.  Actually, I went to Virago for my first time this past September because they had a half off deal for all their new sushi rolls.  I must say, I am impressed with the selection of rolls, wow, so many to choose from!  The fish seems really fresh and the servers are some of the best.  I had a roll that was (I think) fried in panko.  It was super delicious and had shrimp and seaweed in it.  I love the loungy feel in the place, it has a great vibe... a great departure from some of the other more mundane sushi places in town.

Stopped in here for dinner last Saturday night with my beautiful wife.  This is a tiny restaurant by most standards - seats about 40, I guess - so be prepared to waid a bit for a table.  It's a cute little place.  I'm guessing parking would be a challenge on a busy night (we walked from the hotel)   

Food:  We weren't such fans of the roasted tomato salsa, but I think that was a matter of personal taste.  I could see where some people might think it's great.   For dinner, we had the tortilla soup, chilaquiles and carne asada tacos.  The soups was fine.  Chilaquiles were quite good, but a bit dry.  They also would benefit from some green chiles to liven up the taste.  The tacos were also dry.  Plenty of meat, though.  I love the taqueria style tacos, but these just didn't compare with some of my regular haunts.  One final comment on the food - the dryness was resolved very nicely by the salsa, and though we weren't crazy about it with chips, it was great on food. 

Service was very very slow. it seemed to take forever for them to refill drinks and take our plates.   I had asked for a soda and a water - both were bone dry for fifteen minutes at least and I was getting very thirsty.  I finally had to start on my wife's water.  

If it was convenient, I'd go back and give them another try under the assumption that it was an off night.  There was some pretty tasty looking food coming out of the kitchen.

Ambiance is really great and fun decor. Chicken biscuit was great and the breakfast sandwich was good but you could get it anywhere. Fun for a drink but there's so much good food in New Orleans, you could go elsewhere if you don't have much time.

Solid Indian (and having spent a year living in India, I'm particular) and being BYOB didn't hurt. We had veggie samosas, lamb korma, chicken tikka and garlic naan and left happy. Service is pretty informal. The interior pics posted here don't look like the restaurant - odd. We will be back.

Went for drinks on a hot day. Sat at the upstairs bar which had a great view. The Red Stallion was pretty good and the gator bites get an A+. The bar, however, was not kept clean and you can tell they haven't cleaned their taps or wiped behind the bar in months. Tiny flies swarmed the taps and our drinks and they all came from the standing water and yeast behind the bar. Disappointing and kind of disgusting. I would return if they cleaned a little.

Fast service. Solid little Mexican joint in Horsham. I really enjoyed the tropical margarita. The venue is a bit small but everyone is very friendly. We went there for dinner but I think it would also make a great lunch place.
Food was excellent   Chicken was golden brown and the smothered cabbage was full of love and flavor.
I came to Caruso's for breakfast while on travel, and it was excellent. The hash and eggs were delicious, and came with a biscuit smothered in sausage gravy on the top.

My first real meal in New Orleans, it was just okay. I got the combo plate which came with seafood gumbo, rabbit and pork jambalaya, shrimp creole, rice and red beans, and fried chicken. The fried chicken was just okay, i wasn't a huge fan of it. The creole and red beans with rice were okay too but I think they're just not really my thing. The jambalaya was great though; I wish I had just ordered that. At $14 pre-tax a plate, it's pretty pricey but I guess that's what you get in the French quarter. Service is pretty nonexistent. An average place, I'd only suggest the jambalaya!

Drunk tourists that are rude? Check

Locals that hate life because they can't get around? Check

Drunk people that try to fight you? Check

Horrible time? Check Check

Mardi Gras has to be one of the worst things you can experience in the city if you actually live here. Sure there are some fun events, but they get old fast. No one respects the fact that most people have things to do or actually live in the city. This is like the night that The Gold Mine Saloon had a line blocking the street and I was trying to cross it to go to my Mom's late at night and they wouldn't let me pass. It's like that, just EVERYWHERE.

Mardi Gras has been taken over by people that have nothing to do with the city and just come here because they think anything goes. I wish they would throw all the idiots in jail or at a minimum beat them in the streets. So this was my not funny review of the month!

In my honest opinion this location is the best customer service, unlike other locations when going through the drive thru turns into a chore, the workers here are always smiling and seem to actually like their job. The prices are really affordable but doesn't effect the freshness or quality of the food.  

My favorite absolute items are cheese burger subs and steak and cheese, the rest of the family thoroughly enjoy the wings and chicken tenders.  You can tell they change their grease on the regular because all the fried items taste fresh and not stale like some other establishments which will remain unnamed.   There taziki sauce is also very good that I always get a side of it to dip my fries in.

I am very glad they have this location because the other location had me seriously thinking about not returning but this one is always great.  The wait time is not very long and every item is made fresh.  Like for example anytime you order fries they are always cooked to order, you will never receive a cold batch here which is an A+ in my book!
Good but nothing to write home about it. Overpowered flavor to the point I cant taste my food. Overpriced. Go to Beast in Belleville, call it a day.

I give Gail Willey Landscaping two stars for at least coming out to our home.  A couple emails from them, one very quick trip they made out to our home, and that was the last we heard from them.  They never gave us a bid so we never found out whether or not they have a Veterans Discount.  They failed to answer all our questions.  They were not timely with their communications.  They failed to give us their warranty.
Pleasant patio and good service. We loved the buffalo cauliflower appetizer. Excellent fish and chips and fish tacos.

Had the veggie ramen and the octopus fritters for dinner. The veggie ramen had too many onions and did not taste great to be honest. The octopus fritters were very similar to takoyaki but more dense and filling - better than the veggie ramen though.
Onion ring batter was bland and thick. Burger was good. Fries are good. Milk shake is good. Service varies based on how busy it is. Waiting area is small for the number of people waiting.

Great Place! Lamb is good, beef is good, Tandoori chicken is good. In fact everything is good. I took a group from work here and for the first time ever, everyone loved it. This is my new favorite St. Louis restaurant. The wait staff was super attentive, and we were even sung to in Farsi (one I'm the party was having a birthday.)

 I liked it so much I went back the next night just to introduce my wife to the experience. This is a must visit!

After driving around for a good half hour, the bestie and I stumbled across the uptown location! As soon as we saw it, we were sold and found parking a split second later. It was as if it was meant to be. 

We walked in and saw we were the only customers in the place. The waitress told us we could pick where ever we wanted to sit. The bestie chose a small 2 person booth by the window. We were said to find the apricot tea from the other location was not provided here. I chose an orange, pineapple, and mango blend with an order of the corn beef hash dish. 

The blend was decent but I had only gotten 1/3 into it when a fly landed on the edge of my cup and fell in. By that time I had already finished picking at my plate and chose to just stick with my cup of water. The corn beef has was okay itself. I enjoyed everything but the beef. The potatoes were better than the last time at the other Surrey's. I'm not a huge boudin person but on this dish it was pretty good.

Overall, it was a decent luncheon and I think we'll stick to the other location for our apricot tea. There's still much from the menu I would love to try!

As fellow Yelpers stated Tacy is a gem! I got a gel manicure and polish change on my toes. She even painted my toes twice because I didn't like the first color. She fixed 2 broken nails and shaped then all perfectly. Definitely will be back!

Today was our first visit to Los Arroyos. In terms of food and service, our experience was second to none! The salsas (quemada) were absolutely delicious, homemade corn tortillas, and wonderful carnitas and fish (del mar) tacos! Our waiter and waitress were a delight and added to our meal via helpful recommendations and pleasant conversations. The owner was there and also treated us to a free appetizer to show off some of their food! We loved it and will certainly be back. 

I regretfully take off a star because the prices are quite high. They're fairly appropriate for dinner, but not so much for lunch. We will be coming back soon and are very much looking forward to it!

I love the food at Voodoo, but they need to step up their service. We waited ten minutes to be greeted. I asked the server for a drink menu to which she said they don't have one. A minute later I look over to the next table and see a lady looking at a DRINK MENU. The glass my boyfriend had was filthy. There was the cooks ticket in my plate. Maybe they were short staffed, but it wasn't packed. I won't be going to this location again anytime soon.

The freshest, juiciest chicken in Nashville is found here at McDougals.  Their strips and wings meet my comfort food cravings every time...especially the hot thin, hand-cut fries.  The strips are available grilled or fried with the availability to be tossed in a signature sauce.  Haven't tried anything else on their menu other than the chicken strips, wings, and fries...and i guess I don't really need to.  I don't come for the soup and salad...
Note that they're added a location in Cool Springs (Franklin) with an even larger outdoor patio.  This location boasts a hot sauce bar to mix and mingle different flavors without paying extra for each individual sauce.  Oh, and an ice cream machine for a milky ice treat to cool your tongue off from invading the sauce bar!

The worst walmart I have ever been to, on my way home and rushing I stopped to get cheese and the cheese had mold on it (this is no joke) i was so grossed out I abandoned my cart and left and kept it moving never will i buy anything that has to go in my mouth from this place

Took our dogs to have their shots at the vetco clinic inside Petco. I thought it might be a cheaper option as we had moved to the area recently and did not want to drive back to the vet. It was not cheaper and in the end, I still have to go to the vet, pay for the visit to get the prescription our dog needs. 

So if you think that this is a good option, think again. Just Don't It!!!

My wife and I went here for the first time.  It is a garage themed mexican style restaurant.  They even had a spoked motorcycle wheel for the sink in the men's and women's restrooms.  The food was good.  The service was a little off, but other then that they were friendly and prompt with our order.  The menu selection was larger then I thought it was going to be, but they have something for everyone. 

I did find it interesting they have a special parking spot for Hot Rods and Motorcycles.

Howdy folks. How are all of my fellow Yelpers doing? Your's truly, Nasty Nathanial here, and it's time for another one of my groovy reviews. I'm gonna dive right into this review by saying that there are two main reasons that I chose to stay at Hilton hotels when I travel. 1) Great customer service. 2) Clean comfortable rooms that are modern and contemporary. Well, the Hilton Garden Inn in Oldsmar was lacking in both areas and I'm going to explain to you all why right now. 

Now my flight arrived in Tampa on the morning of the 25th at around eight thirty in the morning. So after picking up my rental car I drove straight over to the hotel where I was hoping to be able to do an early check in. By the time I reached the hotel it was already after ten. I didn't think I'd be able to check in that early being that check in time was not until three. So I took a nap in the car, which was very difficult to do being how hot it was very hot outside. Finally at around one I called the front desk to request an early check in. 

Please keep in mind that I have requested early check in's at Hilton hotels I've stayed at in the past and I have never been denied an early check in. Actually, Hilton staff have gone out of their way to accommodate me. But this was not the case at the Hilton Garden Inn in Oldsmar. 

So at about one o'clock I call the front desk and request an early check in. I politely explain that I have an important meeting that I need to attend therefore my need to check in early. The lady that answered the phone didn't seem to care one bit. There was no "I'm so sorry sir but...." Instead it was simply that they couldn't and I could call back later if I wanted. So I waited about a half an hour and tried calling again. Same kind of a response.

At a little after two I get out of my rental car and walk into the front lobby. Standing behind the desk is a younger African-American woman with a look on her face that wanted to say, "what the f*** do you want?" Real welcoming right? No smiles or "how are you today sir?" Nothing like that at all. Being that she was not wearing a proper name tag, I'll just refer to her in this review as Ms. Personality. So I tell Ms. Personality that I needed to request an early check in and explained my situation to her. The only response I got was that there were no rooms ready at this time and to check back later. 

As I previously mentioned, I have requested early check in's in the past and Hilton staff have always gone out of their way to accommodate me. For example, when I stayed at the Hampton Inn over in Largo, the nice lady at the front desk wasted no time in calling house keeping to inform them that there was a guest(me) that needed to check in right now and for them to get my room ready ASAP. Why couldn't Ms. Personality at the Hilton Garden Inn in Oldsmar of done that? 

Finally I am able to check into my room. By this point it was almost three and I knew I was going to be late for my meeting. I just accepted it for what it was and went up to my room. The room itself was clean. However, it was a bit smaller than what I've come to expect. But it was just me so no biggie there. However, there was no complementary bottles of water. This irritated me as I was thirsty. I guess I could've asked Ms. Personality down at the front desk for some water. But at the same time I guess I could also ask, why didn't she offer me any bottled water like staff normally do when you check into a Hilton hotel?  

Plus there were no USB outlets in the room. Now you all may be wondering why I am even bringing this up. Well, like I mentioned earlier, one of the things that I really like about Hilton hotels is their rooms are modern and contemporary. I would not call a room with no USB outlets modern or contemporary. I wouldn't call it ghetto. But it is a bit behind the times. Come on hotel management. Update your rooms. 

Upon my arrival I would learn that the hotel's bar was only open Monday thru Wednesday from five to nine in the evening. I found this to be quite disappointing being that part of the reason that I chose this hotel was because they had a full bar on site. What kind of a bar only stays open Monday thru Wednesday? Is there a shortage of bartenders in the Tampa-St. Petersburg metro area? 

So I think I am going to wrap this review up by saying that because of the lack of good service at this hotel, I had to sleep in a hot car causing me to sweat my rear off, and be late for a very important meeting. I hope that if management at the Hilton Garden Inn in Oldsmar is reading this that they take this information that I have provided and make an honest effort to implement some positive changes so that guests will not have to experience the lack of good service that I have come to expect from Hilton. Thank you for your time in reading this. Until next time may the hotel staff be friendly and the rooms have USB outlets. Happy trails. 

NASTY NATHANIAL

This restaurant opened in the original place where the froyo store Sweet Alley used to be and I think it's a great place to go to if you're craving decent fried chicken or want a chill place to hang out with friends after a hard day at work or a long study session! Their fried chicken and yangnyeom chicken is quite good but the prices can be a bit high especially for students. I recommend getting half an order of fried soy sauce garlic chicken and half an order of yangnyeom chicken to try initially then eventually figuring out what you like on their menu :)

This place is horrible . Whole staff incredibly rude. They dont even follow their own operation hours.  They just open and close their kitchen When ever they please . How when you're open till midnight? dont bother placing a to go order because the rude heifa that picks up the phone will hang up on you like she can't be bothered to do her job. I really don't understand how this place is still in business. Dined there before and was going to give it another try because the food wasn't half bad and they're open late . I assumed things were lacking from a lack of staff like everyplace else but this is just a normal occurrence.

The setup inside is very weird, with a bunch of rooms with only one or two sitting places in each. It's a little weird finding a table and a little too intimate with the other people there. The menu is fine, but it took over an hour for the food to come which was completely unacceptable. I ordered a hummus plate which consisted of hummus, veggies, and pita. What about that takes an hour to make? The coffee is fine, but it is definitely not worth going there to wait an hour for. I will not be returning :(

I just got engaged, to the love of my life.  At 31, I feel like I've waited for this man, not settling for less, and I want our budget-conscious, intimate wedding to reflect that.  So I'm looking for a dress.  No, THE dress.  It has to be beach-appropriate, comfortable, beautiful, and affordable.  And I want that wrapped up in a nice shopping experience, if you please.

Not getting it from Town and Country!  I never expected, but bridal gown shopping is hard work!  You need bridesmaids and attendants for a reason: getting in and out of hundreds (thousands) of dollars worth of fabric and boning is no easy feat.  Most bridal salons ask for appointments, which makes sense.  

So after finding a dream dress in a magazine, I did some online research and found that the only local Melissa Sweet retailer was T&C.  Viewing the storefront, I assumed it was not the first place I would've chosen to walk into, but I kept my mind open and set up an appointment.

When I arrived, my friend/coordinator Mel was already there, pulling dresses from the racks.  I was addressed by a sales clerk, who shook my hand and didn't offer her name.  The clerk ("Sally", we'll call her) with whom I booked the appointment was currently with another bride.  This lady ("Jane", we'll say) was going to "get me started".  

I was asked nothing about my wedding, budget or taste. Mel and I pulled about 6 dresses and asked for a fitting room, Mel being the one responsible for schlepping the dresses there, as "Jane" sped about the floor.  I don't know how many espressos she had that day, but her energy and speedy speech made me dizzy and uncomfortable.  

"Jane" told Mel and I that any more dresses wouldn't fit on the dressing room hanger (???) and to go ahead and get started.  We did, and after trying on all 6 dresses with no accommodation, I waited, half nude, in my dressing room for about 10 minutes expecting someone, like the "Sally" I made the appointment with to come and check on me.
Nope.

Mel got the other selected dresses and we proceeded on our own for the duration of my experience.  Towards the end, "Sally" peeked in and said the other bride was a friend of hers that "just popped in."  I guess she was more important than I was. :(

After coming out of the dressing room for the first time, with one of the last dresses, my friend and I spied a sale rack, which was never pointed out to us.  (Of course not!)  We found 3 more dresses and as WE'RE pulling them to try on, "Sally" walks up and says, "Oh, you found some more you'd like to try? We ARE beginning to close..." ???
Seriously!?!?  (I bet if you waited on me like you were supposed to, we'd be done by now!)

That's just rude.  You NEVER tell a customer "we're closing."  Especially a bride, who's planning to spend a significant chunk of money on a very important garment.  

They weren't downright ugly to me, but I left Town & Country feeling low and stressed out.  I definitely would NOT send any of my friends there for any reason.  Find your wedding dress somewhere else!

Went to dinner in the evening on Sunday and we were waited on by the lovely, April. She was so helpful, warm, personable. Everyone has been wonderful, but she stands out. Shout out to the people in the kitchen, too. We ordered the shrimp and fried green tomatoes, fried Brussels sprouts, and for dessert, the bread pudding, all so delicious.

Food: 1-2 stars
Service: 2 stars
Atmosphere: 1
Cleanliness: 00000
Price: 3

Just not that good of food and dirty. Roach infestation (I work for a pest control company)Oh and hair in the food. I would try somewhere else.

Not at all impressed. The bar tender creeped us out, and spoke passive aggressively. It smelled like pee. The drinks were mediocre at best. With all the good food and drinks around in New Orleans, seriously don't waste your time, go somewhere else. Only plus side is that there was alcohol at all, and I was kind of surprised it even met that standard.

I think the food is good.  I like the octopus and the guacamole and can't complain much about the tapas of any kind.  Very flavorful. The drinks are tiny.  Teeny tiny.  Like a cocktail not fit for a man to hold.  The bartenders also lack customer service and are arrogant.

Had dinner here with friends, upstairs....very disorganized and difficult to find seating for more than 4 people.  Highly recommend reservations because the hostess/seating was definitely lacking some organization.

Got lucky, and got a table.  Food was mixed at best.  Half our table ordered $70++ worth of entrees and half were delivered as ordered.  It also took quite awhile for entrees to be delivered.  We ordered light and it was fine although not delivered as (and when we asked it to be). I would try the market again but would skip the very much over priced beer, wine and food up stairs.  For a busy downtown St Pete Saturday night there are a million better choices.

They call and call and call long after the contract has expired. Twice I have requested a copy of this contract they claim it renewed automatically 3 months after it expired. They say that they will put a ticket in to get a copy of the contract? Well, how can you say it self renewed 3 months after original expire date if you don't even have it on file?

This is my second bad experience with them the first time was several years ago when they billed me through auto-bill to the tune of $400 and the company that was using it had to reimburse me.

I love this little restaurant. There are certain restaurants in Nashville that have been here for years and will be here for as long as they want to be. They do a brisk lunch and dinner as well as a lovely Sunday Brunch. 

I have tended to frequent the brunch and lunch over the years so I will speak to some of my favorite menu items offered  there. I really love the bennets(sp?)  that they offer at brunch. I also like the eggs benedict. For lunch I get the grilled portabello plate. It's meatless but the seasoning is oh so good and the meal is hearty enough to fill you. 

They have a full bar and it's a lovely place for a friendly but non meat market place to get a drink.

Beware!!! I have never in this lifetime ever walked into a restaurant and before I could be seated looked around and saw how nasty and filthy the place was and walked out. 
But this one I did it actually turned my stomach to see ground in French fries on the floor were they were trying to seat me but when I pointed it out the waiter said "oh" walked over them again and tried to seat me somewhere else as I looked around the entire place was like this. When she walked to the table I said no thank you I  changed my mind and I walked out the door.

Nomiya noodle bar boasts hand pulled ramen noodles and they do not disappoint. The noodles have a nice soft, chewy texture. I had the black garlic broth, which was rich and flavorful, but a bit too oily for my taste. I would recommend it to someone who wanted to try something different than the typical shoyu or miso broth. Service was friendly. We were sat at the bar at first, but were quickly moved to a table once there was room.

Although it's a one stop shop I get sucked in every time. I came in for two things and I am leaving with 20. Damn you Wal-Mart. LOL the prices are awesome at times. I really only like this Wal-Mart though. They seem to have things together much better than the other locations

Not going to lie. I was a little disappointed.

Okay, so the ambience is spectacular! I think the high ceilings and the old bank feel give this steakhouse a big push. Really feels like you're in a high end steakhouse. So, I was pretty excited about the rest of my experience, but this is where it went flat.

Service was weak. Takes a while to get to you or flag someone down sometimes, because the place is probably so large. Food was too salty. I mean I love steakhouses and how generous they are with salt and butter, but it was one time I was like "Whoa... too much." What made it a perfect storm was that I needed a fresh drink to wash down the salt, but I couldn't flag a waiter down.

I'd say if you're looking for the grand experience and ambience, go to Del Frisco's! If you want more, then... unfortunately I've experienced better (multiple times...).
Great vegan and healthy options. They even have coconut cream for your coffee, but you should know the coconut cream is vanilla flavored, so no sweetener needed. Yum!

I ordered this through Postmates, wanting a cheap, and easy meal. While it was both cheap and easy, it was also mediocre and not very flavorful. There are a ton of Chinese restaurants in South Nashville that are better. For food that is so salty and has so much MSG (I could tell it did because my heart rate was elevated after I ate), I don't know why there isn't more flavor in the food. I got steamed dumplings, vegetable fried rice, chicken teriyaki and hot & sour soup. The Hot and Sour Soup was mostly broth. I was underwhelmed with everything. While it wasn't terrible, it was just okay and I would not go there again.

Ive never been to a more childish establishment, where bartenders and wait staff can throw temper tantrums because they're hot and the management coddles and soothes them when customers have complaints about their service.
