The bartender and wait staff were rude and condescending and once you address their attitude they act like children and run off. I've never had a bartender treat me with so little respect and when addressed about it tell me it's because he's busy(with 4 other people sitting at the bar mind you) and then proceed to blatantly ignore me for the rest of the time! But this just must be how they train their employees because when we asked the food runner for condiments because NONE came with our meal (I was told since I didn't ask for salad dressing it came with none, oh and the tartar sauce that it said it came with on the menu, didn't get that either because he didn't like us maybe? I'm not sure) he told us to ask the bartender because he's too busy and it's not his job! We then asked the bartender and he refused to get them so we had to ask another bartender. And once you ask for a manager, they don't even care to hear what you have to say and act like they can't believe you had the audacity to ask to speak with them. Management essentially blames you for their employees inability to do their job and will actually say "I don't know what you want me to do about it" It's the blind leading the blind in this place. The most hilarious thing about this entire situation is that once we said something to the bartender about his attitude he was giving great service to everyone around us, which was kind of pathetic since that's the kind of service people should expect in the first place. PSA!!! If you're a miserable and nasty person, don't be a bartender. They're lucky the majority of their revenue comes from drunk college kids because treating your patrons like they're second class citizens doesn't work for most adults. I didn't even get a chance to get the bartender who was hot and bothered or the manager who had the "better than thou" attitude because they didn't introduce themselves and I hardly got a chance to speak to either of them.
Last thing, the food was great and Anthony did a great job of taking care of us after our issue with first bartender. This review was just about the service.

I had lamb chops, which were good, but not amazing. They came with instant mashed potatoes and unseasoned, blanched asparagus. Someone else at my table had a pesto pasta dish, which was served in a bowl with excess water standing in the bottom. Apparently, the pasta wasn't well-drained. It was also over-cooked. The food was not what I was expecting for the price. I can think of much better meals I've had for thirty dollars. I don't understand the rave reviews. On the other hand, the raspberry mojito was absolutely delicious. Unfortunately, the server seemed annoyed and was rushing us through our meal, so I only ordered one. 
The restaurant is large and open, so it gets quite noisy.

I don't have anything badd to say about this place! Drinks were reasonable, atmosphere was cool. Yes its small and yes the stage is small and yes there isn't much room for dancing...SO!! its a place to just have a few drinks with a not so big crowd, maybe sing a couple a tunes and then roll out to your designated spot. I like that small crowd feel soooo YEA I'M A FAN!!

I promised my mother that I would review this facility. Here goes:

My mom had a stroke. The social worker at Lourdes told me to pick a facility for after-care. I read reviews, and concluded that CareOne was the best. 

Except one thing- I did not read Yelp! reviews about CareOne. Wish I had.

This place was terrible. My mom was very depressed there and begged to leave. I think she even left early; it was that bad. 

One English nurse was slightly rude. Maybe I could have won her to my side by stating that I had seen "The English Patient". 

The nurses would not come when summoned. I had to call to have my mom's diaper changed. They did it forty minutes later. When I asked a male nurse to bathe her, as she had not been bathed for several days, he stated that he would try. 

The nurses would not ensure that a doctor would sign my brother's forms for federal caregiver leave. They hassled my brother. A doctor did it with ease when asked directly. 

They would not lend out a charger for my mom's phone. At one point, the landline phone in her room did not work. I think that they remedied the problem, but I cannot fully remember.

They told me that she was not eating. No one encouraged her. 

Ironically, I had skimmed Yelp! reviews on PowerBack, where my mom had requested to be transferred to. The reviews were crappy, so I decided against it. My mom loved being at PoweBack. I thought that since CareOne was in Moorestown, which is close to Willingboro and in a "richie" town, that she would receive great care close to home.

Abandon all hope, ye who enter here. 

-Inferno, Canto III, Dante

Well let's start with what we liked:  Sazeracs!  Atmosphere was fun in a funky kind of way; servers were good humored and efficient. Excellent beans and rice. 

Now to what we didn't like: The long line to get in. The disagreeable hostess who summoned us from the line, pointed to a table, then threw down menus, not once saying anything or making eye contact. The rap-hip hop soundtrack. The muddy looking and overcooked gumbo. The flabby skinned and raw-in-the-middle chicken. 

This place was recommended by not one but two locals. Have no idea why.

Went there for lunch.  Very reasonably priced, nice atmosphere, and the sushi was very good.  The other dishes, one udon and Pad Thai, were a bit on the bland side.  Maybe they just don't use much salt.

But I would happily go back.  Good value overall.

Such a pleasant lunch meal we had here. The place is a little tight with so many tables close to each other, but atmosphere is laid back and informal with a nice bar we didn't get to taste.
The small plates are not that small. Try the Hummus plates - soft eggs was our pick, worth it! Falafel was also very well executed and tasty, but our favorites were the babaganuch and the yogurt. The pita is really perfect - they bring it straight from the oven. Lots of boxes to bring home though, portions are not that small, but definitely worth the trip, really tasteful.
They always have fresh produce and a great selection. I like their fresh breads in the bakery and their huge salad bar. It is always clean and the staff is helpful

I use united often and have always been a fan. But I became more appreciative after meeting one of their drivers, mr. David hammer, it was really hot outside and I wanted to go job hunting uptown but only had enough money for a cab to the nearest street car which would have meant I would have to walk and catch more buses. When David showed up he insisted that he take me all the way there to help me out. Along the way he gave me some good tips and was ridiculously amazing to a young girl trying to Pennie pinch. So regardless of the next cab driver being an asshole, I will always be faithful to united because of the kindness showed to me by mr hammer, cab 219. Thanks again.

This place seems to be one of the few choices in the area and the location that QVC and Zoetis house their employees coming from out of town. They are doing a social hour now, with comp cocktails. Starwood properties have Starbucks coffee in room which is a plus. Their restaurant is NOT good. Go across the driveway to potentially the worst outback in America, it's better than the restaurant here. The rooms need a remodel as there are some minor touches that are dated. Walls are thin so I always come here equipped with ear plugs and sleeping mask. To be honest, have not stayed up the street at holiday or hampton inn...but if you have a choice they might be worth a shot because nothing special is going on here. Three stars for the on sight bar which I would guess holiday inn and hampton don't have.

Had so much fun with my two daughters here. We  did The Cabin and it was challenging. The clues and set-up were quite good! The atmosphere is very nice and the place is very clean. Felt welcomed and valued as a customer. Will return!!
Best brunch ever! The server William was so helpful and nice, great place and great service.

Interesting experience.  I ordered from an online delivery place.  They offered a significant discount if I picked up the food , instead of having it delivered.  I decided to try that option.  

I plugged the address into the nav because I'd never been there.  I'd never even ordered from there.  I was disappointed when I realized it was in the mall.  I have been avoiding crowds and I never would've made the order of I knew this in advance.  It was too late though.  

When I arrived, I gave the two staff members in the front my full name.  There was a bag to their right sitting there.   They confirmed that I ordered from the particular delivery service.  Then one of them insisted on seeing some confirmation on my phone.   This was unnecessary.  What if I didn't have my phone with me?What if I sent my daughter to pick it up for us while I waited in the car?  And why prolong this interaction when it was very apparent that the order was mine?  I think I know why they insisted, but you can't exactly get inside someone's head. 

I opened the app but did not immediately see anything similar to what the staff member asked for.  I then just opened up a text message I'd received and showed it to her.  I was then given my food.  

The interaction with the staff was a dealbreaker for me.  I'll never patronize them again. 

As for the food, it was okay.   The portion size was small, particularly for Italian food.  The preparation and presentation could've been better also.  And it was slightly bland. 

With the discount, it represented a decent value, I guess.  But not a great value.  

This was a lesson learned for me.

I had been looking forward to coming here for a long time because this place has such good ratings. Maybe their meat is good, but this vegetarian was super disappointed. 

I ordered the soft tofu with spicy sesame sauce. I wanted to get something spicy since Sichuan cuisine is known for that. Maybe I just chose poorly, but the dish that arrived was one of the worst I have ever had. It looked pretty good--tons of cubes of tofu drizzled with an ominously bold looking sauce. At first glance, I wished there was more sauce, but I figured maybe the small amount indicated exceptionally strong flavor.

I couldn't have been more wrong. I put some on my rice, took a bite...and I had to double check my plate to make sure I had gotten something besides rice on my fork. It tasted like absolutely nothing. No spice, no salt, no flavor whatsoever. I might as well have been eating plain rice with tofu straight out of the box because that's pretty much all it was. And there was no soy sauce or hot sauce or anything on the table that I could use to try and bring out some kind of flavor in the dish.

Maybe I just chose the wrong thing to order, but my husband was pretty disappointed with his fried rice as well, which arrived COLD. Like I'm sorry how do you mess up fried rice?!

I won't go back. I don't want to risk trying something else only to be let down again when I could just go to Old Peking instead.

Man, what a huge coincidence that this place has only one other review from another Jeremy A. 

Sadly, I didnt have the same experience with my cheesesteak. I didnt think it was that great as the beef was super dried out and the cheese was there but it wasnt melted. 

Could have done with some mayo or whiz just to make the cheesesteak a bit more soggy like a good one should be. 

It was huge for 8 bucks I just cant go three stars because I wouldn't come back for another one.

Service food, and drinks were all terrible. Definitely not up to NOLA standards. 

Food: fried shrimp was clearly frozen, not fresh. Fried chicken was extremely dry

Service: waitress took about 10 minutes to bring us waters. Food took about 40 minutes from the time we ordered. Waitress made us bus our own table. Like not even kidding - she had us passing and stacking plates when there was plenty of room for her to go around the table and do it herself. 

Drinks: bottomless mimosas and rose had a bottom after about 2 refills, mainly bc the servers were inattentive. Bloody Mary was basically all store bought mix - so depressing. 

This was my 2nd time here and I will never go back. Sad bc it is super cute inside with a really nice courtyard in a great location.

A bureaucratic mess! They denied one of my 3 maintenance prescriptions saying they refilled it 1 1/2 months later than they did. Despite Walgreens calling and telling them this is a mistake, I had to drive 1400 miles without my blood pressure medication. 4 phone calls 3 messages from me, and countless fax and calls from Wallgreens. This was after they sent me a bill because they changed my insurance info to an insurance company I do not have. It took me 2 hours on the phone to revert to the insurance I originally gave them.
Caramel macchiato frozen is so yummy ! Also the coffee milk tea is a plusss ommg great drink for the day or a snack

I went in for a balayage and a cut. This was my first time here, so the appointment setter on the phone put me with a mid-level stylist. My total came out to just about $200, so no crazy surprises on that front. 
My hair is fine and straight, so it's a super easy cut, as I just want the ends trimmed and the layers kept. I think I was spoiled when I got my hair cut in Paris, as I'm not sure anyone will ever match that artistry they put into a simple haircut. Overall, I am happy with the way my hair turned out. My stylist asked what I wanted and listened.. I am pretty open minded about the overall color, so when she asked if I had photos, I did not (but I appreciate the extra mile). She explained to me the processes that were happening throughout the whole experience. 
A mask is required. They were kind enough to provide me with one as I forgot mine. It is in Ybor, so parking is annoying as per usual with Ybor & I paid $6 to park in the close parking lot. 
I did buy the milkshake leave in spray as it smells devine & works like it should.

Get a new cutting board. It is so dirty and old looking. My bagel with apples were cut on it, right after Lox and Lunch meat were on it. People could get sick. Invest in something less porous.  You can't cut corners in this.

Great environment, great music, great coffee, but the place is just not clean.  The cutting board is the biggest concern from a food safety perspective.

Avoid at all costs (no pun intended) because money is their number one priority.  Horrible staff, no communication with me until the bill was ready - and came out $2000 over their original quote - this is simply a profit center. I've asked around and everyone I've spoken with has had a similarly bad experience.  I just took my dog to a vet outside Stone Harbor in cape may county - he is from South Carolina and has heard all about this place - apparently their bad reputation is well known.. So do yourself a huge favor and go somewhere else.

I really loved this place and enjoyed my time there with my daughter.  Would highly recommend.  It's right there on the beach and there's a great little cafe within walking distance.  Everything else in SB is a short drive - for the price it's totally worth it!

Worst Service Ever! Fowler Ave. Location, we have been waiting since 930 a.m., and we have an appointment. The time is now 1130a.m.  The employees act as though they don't want to be here.  Overall a horrible location to visit. Go to the desk to find out what the heck is going on,  and we're told, "their running behind". Good advice to not falling behind,  stop OVER BOOKING APPOINTMENTS!  Been here so long,  I had to leave to get lunch,  so we didn't fall out.  Very sad for a state ran clinic!

I have been trying to cancel ME membership for my husband and I since October, when we gave them the signed "cancel my membership forms." They are still charging us. It doesn't really matter if its thievery or incompetence -- I'm still arguing with them about this money. Every. Damn. Month.

It is very SAD to see the PA that you see instead of a MD ,and the lack of patient doctor relationship that this clinic lacks.
It is all about money building for them,I was on Vacation and ran out of a hand cream that they prescribed to me .I asked them to call in a refill as I was out of state and they said we haven't seen you on that particular alignment for over 6 months.So they would not fill my request,and told me to go to an urgent care office on vacation REALLY ?????? I would highly suggest finding a REAL doctors office instead of this puppy mill.
After driving this was the fist gas station we saw in the area and stopped to get gas... Thank god as we were on empty.

My friend recommended Desert Hills to me as I was not happy going to another veterinarian. So glad she did! Not only did they treat me and my dog well, but loved on him and made sure he was comfortable with procedures! 
When we had to put our beloved cat to sleep they were gentle and kind and supportive. I will continue to take my family pets to them for their health and well being.
Vicki
Friendly staff. Large area with lots of tables & space. I have only tasted their chai latte and it was awesome. Just a bit pricey. Free wifi.

What's a brunch place without hot chocolate and omelette? Sucks! I had to eat Umpqua oats ( which I can even have in my office every day) and that too I had to eat some random flavor coz my favorite one Kickstart wasnt available. So pissed off!
Sunday Brunch: So they have "bottomless mimosas" until 3pm, but they "ran out" at 2:30pm. Food was ok. Bluegrass was excellent. Overall, wouldn't recommend.

Pros:
1. It's free.*
2. There's lots of variety**
3. Staff can be really friendly***
4. But look- it's a library- appreciate it.
5. Print 200 pages a month for no charge.

Cons:
*  Unless you go to the Schlafly branch by car, because it's nearly impossible to find a parking spot on the street, and there is a charge to use the attached lot; a day late on movies is $1 a piece- lives have been ruined by these steep fines;
**Unless what you want is good, because that stuff is stolen by the third person who gets it.
*** But many aren't.

Good food and good service once again. They had a different menu since the last time I came here. I started with the sausage and lentil, which was served hot the way I like it and with a little spice. I had the chicken carrabba w/fettuccini for my entree. It was good but I would try something else the next time I come.

I must say that my group of friends were very excited about eating here for dinner since the reviews were so good. After waiting about 30 minuets in line, which was not a problem, we were seated under an air vent and it was freezing and there was the smell of garbage lingering. We asked to be moved and our request was granted. However, no one came to take our order for another 20 minuets. Finally our order was taken by a very friendly and attentive waitress. I ordered the seafood nachos, which was delicious and the shrimp and grits which were horrible. I actually sent my shrimp and grits plate back and didn't eat it. As we were leaving, we asked to use bathroom. A young man working at the bar told us "sure! This is the cleanest bathroom in the French Quarter!" However, to our surprise the bathroom was disgusting. The same smell that we smelled upstairs in the dining area was coming from the bathroom and the kitchen. The kitchen, which we could see as we walk to the bathroom was also gross. We also could see the "secret" ingredients used in their cooking, jiffy's cornbread mix and   zatarains gumbo mix. The only reason I gave this restaurant 2 stars was for the kind waitress and the seafood nachos. I wouldn't suggest anyone eating there.

We also were really excited to have a new Cuban restaurant in the neighborhood, and great to have some more BYOB choices. The atmosphere was nicely done with paintings, faux finish walls, soft lighting and there was live music for Friday night. 

Unfortunately we had to wait about 20 minutes to even get menus and well more than an hour for dinner to come. They were out of one of their two specials already. They apologized profusely for the service troubles and took a nice cut off our bill to make up for it. They said it was the busiest night they'd had yet and that they just weren't quite prepared for the crowd. Food was good but not wow. 

We'll give them a bit of time to work out service and such and then give them another chance. I think they have some real potential. Hopefully the 2-star rating will go up in future.

The deck out back looks like a fun place to hang out on a summer night. Next time we may need to bring along mojito makings. :-)

Can reserve a table through www.opentable.com

I don't get the hype. I ordered that pie stuffed cake CAKE for my husbands bday and yuk. It was dry and the icing was completely flavorless and the consistency of butter. I don't even want to think about the $80 spent on it . We've ordered whoopie pies and my son grabbed it and immediately said "I don't want to eat this!" ... I then picked it up myself and the cake was sticky WHY because the freeze their goodies so they 'travel' better. Whatever I call bs, they freeze it because making them fresh is not convenient... We also ordered the butter cake which was the most decent thing we've tried BUT it is BUTTER cake lol add a ship load of butter to anything and it'll taste good. I don't even remember the other stuff we ordered because they blew... Never again.
The building is cute. But holy crap it is noisy. If you are a light sleeper like me, look elsewhere. The walls are super thin and you are right by the highway. The room is average.

Cattani Catering & Kitchen on Parkway Avenue, Ewing, NJ, is an exceptional Italian house with small dining room and five stool counter, turning out creative and delicious dishes for those lucky enough to know it is there. 

I stopped in for a late lunch last week, grabbing a spinner overlooking the kitchen. Only one table was occupied by women in bonnets, although a few people came in to pick up take out orders. Sherry was running the floor, and cheerily brought me the clipboard menu along with a cool copper mule mug of water and lemon. Prices were on the high side, but given the description of the food and Sherry's enthusiasm for each of them, I accepted that this was a "budget be damned" moment. I ordered the chicken milanese sandwich with side salad. The order in, I then saw Ms. Cattani herself putting my meal together. She waved and said hello while cheffing for me. 

Turns out she is basically a self taught uber foodie, who decided to open her open shop after working for other restaurants for several years. She is self taught and obviously a great student of her craft. 

My lunch arrived on a thin wood plank. Minimalist, but strikingly presented. A feast for the eyes, and ultimately, the stomach. The chicken breast was pounded into a thin steak, lightly breaded in Italian crumbs and pan fried, with marinated peperonata, parmesan and aged balsamic drip on a crusty seeded "rustic" torpedo roll. It was delicious. No one off, this. Each element made a contribution to the overall work of art. I like milanese dishes, and even prepare them at home. Still, none are as interesting and tasty as this, and it was just a sandwich! What would it have been as a stand alone fully plated dinner? Don't know, but a week later I am still thinking of when to find out. 

All the while, Sherry was seeing to the incoming phone orders and refilling my water mug during the gaps in the calls. Service was friendly and informative. I was well cared for. 

This was a superior lunch from an unexpected source.

 Surprise was perhaps the tastiest part of the dish.
Don't bother going there the receptionists are more concerned with each other than actually help customers. They kept talking about there weekend and who was going to get more wasted.

I've never been so disappointed in a "fancy" restaurant. My husband loved his steak and creme brulee and my gin fizz was excellent. The good ends there. We ordered a crab and shrimp appetizer and it came on top of a hash that had sausage in it. Only vegetables were listed in the hash on the menu--who puts sausage in a seafood dish without mentioning sausage on the menu? The shrimp was over-lemoned and the crab was so mushy it seemed like it had been pureed--and this was just the appetizer. For dinner, I had gnocchi with shrimp and scallops. The gnocchi was very mushy, and the shrimp was absolutely horrible. Chemically and not fresh. I couldn't eat it. The dessert I had was also disappointing, waaaaay too much ganache for something that was supposed to be a peanut butter tart. I didn't finish it. And this is coming from a peanut butter/chocolate addict. 
The service was good but a little stuffy, and they weren't very knowledgeable of the wine, which is disappointing for a restaurant that claims to specialize in it. Selection of wine by the glass was very limited.

Located in the old Visko's restaurant location, El Mesquite grill had good food for a reasonable price.

A friend and I have made a pact to try new places each week. While it isn't necessarily a blood oath, we did pinky swear. If we fail, the penalties are swift and harsh.

This week we decided to try El Mesquite Grill. The location is significant, as both of us remembered going there as children when it was Visko's.

Inside, the location hasn't changed. The restaurant is large with several different rooms for dining. The walls are covered in dark wood paneling which doesn't necessarily scream Mexican restaurant, but it somehow works.

The menu is very large. Items are numbered and the menu recommends ordering by number. I like this idea because trying to pronounce some of the entrees would make me look dumb.

Our meal started with chips and salsa. The salsa was brought in a small carafe which probably was once used for wine. It was a nice touch since we were able to pour own own bowls of salsa. With our own bowls, double dipping wasn't a problem.

For entrees we had S19 and 52. S19 was a "Westbank Favorite" and was some sort of taquito dish. The taquitos were filled with shredded chicken and were very good. 

52 was a trio of enchiladas with each having a different filling, and sauce. The cheese enchilada didn't do it for me, but the steak and chicken versions were very good. The dish came with rice, beans, and  a guacamole salad.

The portions were very large and neither of us could finish our meals.

This meal was a very good value. The total was $25 before tip, and that included the margarita.

The one bad thing about this meal was the margarita. While not a margarita guy, my friend said it was not good at all.

If you're on the Westbank and want Mexican, I recommend El Mesquite Grill.

Love this location. Staff is always really friendly and service is generally good. Often a wait to be seated during peak hours, which is to be expected. They do call ahead, too. 

Things I love: their filet mignon is seasoned perfectly and the nutritional info online shows that it's not a half bad option if you're on a diet! I also love the seasonal vegetables (which I always request NO butter otherwise you may find them a bit oily), and their blue balsamic wedge salad is awesome. Desserts are surprisingly delicious for a chain restaurant. 

People always seem like they're  in a good mood here. It's one of those places that works as a date night or a night with friends or family. Lighting is a nice medium lighting and the music is audible but not too loud. Wish they had a few more draught beers to chose from. Still, this just might be my favorite chain restaurant.

After being a longtime Tip Top customer, I'm taking my business elsewhere. The quality of the dry cleaning has declined dramatically - stains, dress shirt collars pressed bent funny so you can't wear them, etc. Worse, they've gotten really bad about losing my clothes. Their ticket system is actually set up to lose clothes, I've learned the hard way. The give you a claim ticket that doesn't state the number of items (although it is 5 shirts per ticket). Unfortunately, if they fail to give you a ticket, or simply lose your shirts, you don't have much recourse. Their ladies working the counter are always nice, but overall, Tip Top is NOT Tip Top, so I would recommend that anyone to have decent dry cleaning and get all their clothes back to avoid this place.

Ate there recently after checking out a show in Fountain Square. Friends recommended this place & it was a short walk but it was very late. Well, I've eaten at a lot of greasy spoons in my day, & they all have  character. Firstly, the food is good & the prices are on the low side. But, the place is rather dirty and unkempt (my gf had a real pb with it as she felt her food would be affected). I had a burger & fries & it was fast & delish. But, also noted the downside --  the strange woman staring at us, and the guy who vomited just outside the door. As we left to walk to our car a man sitting outside the place told us to "be careful" walking thru these parts @ 1am...duly noted!

Food is fine just the employees seem to have a attitude and lack experience slow business but waited like 10 minutes when there were like no more than 1  ahead of me and the person serving me typically threw my food at me but to make matters worse there seems to be drug addicts, but everything else is okay

This is my first time at The Hut Bar & Grill. I enjoyed the outside seating with the mist fans. It really helped keeping me cool & my time there enjoyable. 

The service was average & I waited about 20 minutes for my food & felt that my server had a lot of tables to cover. 

While my burger was good & the toasted bun gave it an extra crunch, the produce was not the ripest. The did like that I was able to pick between fries or onion rings. Meals also can with a pickle. 

The restaurant also offers 32 oz drink buckets at $15 each.

Very good but not great. Ceased salad to start and lamb rib chop entree....all nice. We were party of 5 and shared steak and a bunch of side dishes. Mac and cheese was delicious and onion rings were excellent. Steak was cooked a bit rare (ordered medium rare). Service was very nice and friendly.
The food is okay. Just okay. It's super busy all the time. I would definitely say this place is a tourist trap. There are better places to get brunch around here.

Delish and Reasonably priced.  
Great addition to our neighborhood.
No need to travel to Center City

Had a battery backup sump pump installed. Ethan ans Marcus were very courteous, efficient and professional. 
Highly recommend.

The place is very nice and very friendly staff this was my first time here the crab claws Came with Bread that was very hard and steal maybe it was just a bad day I will try to again to make sure but overall I did not enjoy the food but the staff was good

I called many places for quotes, asked some friends who they used for tinting, etc..
I am absolutely loving what Keleef did to my car! 
Used a 15% all around per Keleef's suggestion and wow! What a huge difference! Also loved that he came to my work to apply the tint. This guy is good! He's professional, friendly and I'd recommend him anyday! Support small businesses!  Thanks K!

I have been here a few times with my family, as we ALL enjoy some wings.  The first couple of times I thought they were good but I just wasn't impressed.  The latest time, on New Year's Eve, I decided to change up my normal 'mild' wings and went with a spicy honey mustard.  WINNER!  I am now sold on these wings.  Buffalo Wild Wings' mild sauce holds a special place in my heart, but all of the other flavors I've tried from Wings, Etc. beat them out.  

The location is in a strip mall and the inside isn't anything fancy.  It's even a little dingy, but that's OK.  For wings I just need some water served and some wet wipes to wipe my fingers when I'm done!  Ok... now I'm hungry and want some wings.

This place did not disappoint. I had the chicken nuggets (not spicy) with a side of curly fries and the MaiTai Maple syrup and BBQ sauces. That MaiTai Maple sauce was bomb!!! The chicken nuggets were the absolute perfect texture and breaded wonderfully. The curly fries were perfect. This place is a winner!

The gym itself - The equipment generally is pretty well aged. I think there are only 4 stationary bikes in the gym, but overall it does not get very crowded which is one of the redeeming qualities. 

The main crux of my review: Cancellation. I sent 2 emails to the support team at the gym and heard nothing back. After calling, they told me I would need to contact a Billing company to cancel, this tactic it seems is purely to make it more difficult to cancel (not being able to direct me to a number published somewhere they had to read it to me over the phone). After all, why would I need to call a billing company to cancel if I didn't to sign up? Wouldn't you think that the support team at the gym would deign to take that step themselves? Additionally, this billing company requires 30 days notice of cancellation for no apparent reason (which means you will be billed for the month after you wish to cancel), THEY ALSO DO NOT ACCEPT THE NOTICE OVER THE PHONE.  The company has to read you the email to send the notice to AND your account number over the phone. After all this I send an email to said address, but receive no confirmation that it was received and/or filed. If I am billed for October, I will have to call my credit card company and mark the charge as unauthorized.

What it comes down to: it would be a much more honest way of doing business if there were a simple online form to cancel membership. Its not the 30 day notice per-se that I have issue with, its that it takes 2 phone calls, and an email simply to cancel, which is unnecessarily circuitous.

Arielle' deserves a four star " plus" rating.  It offers high quality food  prepared by a creative kitchen in a charming (but somewhat elegant) atmosphere at a competitive price. There is is an interesting array of appetizers, as well as entrees and desserts.  The garden, as well as the flowers in the dining room, are almost worth a visit in themselves.  I believe the owner was the person who expertly explained the options and their preparation.   She and the young man assisting her offered professional, pleasant service.
If you go:  
      The intimate atmosphere caters well to parties of two or four.  I would not recommend it for larger groups.
      Portions tend to be large.  My pork shank could easily have served three.  Consider sharing an entree or ordering soup, salad, and an appetizer.

Hard to believe I haven't written a review for this place yet (and no one has) as I come here pretty much every week.

The guys (yes, they are all males) who work here are super helpful and don't give you the "(Sigh) You're a 'tard because you don't work at an auto parts store" attitude. They'll give you more than just parts tips and will often give tips on installation, diagnosis, etc. The store is always clean, well-organized, and well-stocked.

Two tips:

1) Get Autozone card. For every $20 purchase you get a point. For every five points you get a $20 credit. If you purchase strategically, you can essentially get 20% off everything.
2) Almost all vehicles sold in the US 1996 or later have an OBD-II port. When your "check engine" light comes on and you don't have an OBD-II reader (only about $30 now), go to Autozone and they will read it free. Write down the code, google the code and your car's make/model/year online, and there are often detailed diganoses of the potential issues related to that code. Depending on your skill level, you can then repair it yourself or take it to a shop. If you choose the latter, you can at least talk like you are not an idiot and will be less likely to be fleeced.

They have hardly any toppings anymore. No fruit only chocolate and sprinkles. Frozen yogurt was all runny. No one asked us if we wanted to taste anything and our total was $16 for two people. Really disappointed, this place has gone down hill. The pricing menu is super misleading showing the largest size at a different price. Ugh I hate wasting money.

This is a great place to go for a late breakfast or brunch. They have many options in their brunch buffet and some taste like they are from a five star restaurant. It is a little pricey which in my opinion knocks it down to four stars, but the taste itself is five. If you end up coming, GET THE MEATLOAF!

10-01 Is a great brunch bar with some serious taste, I highly recommend for birthdays or minor holidays.
I eat at PF Chang's a couple of times a week. Lately, there have been too many mistakes with my order. But receiving burnt food is the worst! So better, please.
The tacos were just ok.  Not a fan of the chips and queso, you could chip a tooth on the chips and queso was watery.

I'm so done with this location and this company in general! I go there every single day sometimes twice a day for years. I am so done it's not even funny... I'm tired of spending $5.40 on a grande to receive the amount of a tall inside a grande cup. I brought this up to employees and even the manager over and over and nothing changes. I only ever get excuses of why it happens! Ive gone as far as contacting corporate they haven't really done anything as I just talk to the district manager and his offer to me was one free coffee. This is a multimillion dollar company I spent hundreds there every month as they can see on my rewards membership how often I go.... and you offer me one cup thanks, you can keep your cup of coffee I will go somewhere else. Congratulations Starbucks you have officially become Mc Donald's your quality is no longer there you're all about the turnaround time and The quality of your product has suffered!!!!

This range is pretty good. The hours are weird but the price is affordable. I believe they only take cash or check so come prepared. I read some reviews on Google about the RSOs being jerks, but I didn't experience anything like that. Most ranges are very binary -- right or wrong -- and there is a reason for that. I did see a couple folks violate rules and were informed which rule the violated and why that rule is important. Don't like it, then don't break the rules! My main issue with the range is it is all open down the line. The pistol side doesn't even have stalls. Usually that isn't really a big deal until you have Slim Shady doing mag dumps with a Glock next to you like I did. I wanted to shoot the rifle side, but there was a waiting list, so I just kept it to 50 as you can shoot rifles on the pistol side as well. I would like to come back during the week to see if it is less crowded and to possible avoid the weekend gunnit crowd.

Well we are here on vacation and this place was suggested to us by our hotel. Well it was an awesome time and the food and service was great!

Thank you!

Pretty standard food quality and service as compared to other Sonic locations. I was a little surprised that they hadn't thought, or perhaps had simply decided not to shovel or plow the snow from their drive through. Got stuck a few times making the loop this last week.

2 stars for the friendly staff and how beautiful the location inside is. Very clean and pretty.

Now...

Went in for laser hair removal. I've had laser hair removal done numerous times elsewhere but decided to come here. For starters, this spot is VERY overpriced. I'm talking $250 for your face, and I think the nurse who did it actually tried to give me a good price lol. She was great and friendly, but man. I've paid less than $85 for facial laser. Not to mention the machine was very bad. I still have a lot of hair patches all over my face, upper lip and forehead. It was totally not worth $250.

As for my boyfriend, who also went in for laser hair removal in his neck area, I can't tell you if it's just his skin type or not but I think its worth people knowing. My experience, I didn't even feel the laser whatsoever, it was very gentle and smooth, I have no burns but again, I have a lot of hair left. My boyfriend left with no hair removed and some pretty bad scabby burns all around his neck. He's done laser for 10 years and that's never happened to him. 

I don't blame the workers, the nurse was super friendly like I said, but their machine really sucks. Maybe their facial stuff is better, like their other services but I don't recommend the laser removal here.

This was my second visit to Appalachian Brewing Company and I was excited to come back because I had a positive experience the first time. However, it seems like their quality of service has deteriorated. I ordered the Water Tap Wheat Ale which tasted more watery than a beer. For food, I got the Hog Wild which was good but the bottom bun was so soggy that I had to eat my burger upside down. The burger itself was delicious and I ordered it with a side of cole slaw. The downsides were that the food quite a long time to come out, despite the fact there weren't that many patrons. The biggest gripe was that the waitress had poor communication; she didn't notify us that substituting the sides for cole slaw or onion rings was extra. That is one of my biggest gripes that I notice waiters doing more often; they'll casually list the sides you can choose from without pointing out that there are extra charges for certain selections. But one could argue that I should have looked at the menu more closely, ok fine. But my friend ordered a side of onion rings which should have been $1.50 extra but on the bill it was $2.50. There are plenty of other places that opened up in this area where I'd rather spend my money.

I ordered my pizza at 8:20 online, busy restaurant and know it'll take about an hour. 9.30 I check the website tracker and it says it was delivered. I call and they say it is almost there but the manager marked it as delivered. Okay whatever, they are trying to keep their times right it is probably in a car right around the corner. No, it wasn't. 10pm call again but they keep answering saying they aren't taking any more orders then hanging up or just straight hanging up on me. Finally get a woman on the phone who says it was messed up and never remade. She promises to remake it and have it to me in 15. It shows up and is missing an ingredient and has random pieces of chicken on it. It also has the stickers on it with the printed times. That is right, this is my messed up pizza quickly reheated an hour after they messed it up the first time. I call and ask for a refund cause at this point I am over this. She tells me I have to come in with the pizza to get a refund. So it has been 2 and a half hours since first ordering, they send an hour old reheated messed up pizza, and won't give me my money back. 

Do not order from here. It isn't worth it.

Sake Cafe on Magazine for happy hour is dope.  My boyfriend and I ate like beasts and each had two rounds of drinks for about $40.  For that very reasonable sum we got a salad, spring roll app, goat cheese app, a crunchy roll, a crawfish roll, their Rock and Roll, two Moontinis (I'd never ordinarily order something with such an incredibly stupid name, but they were sufficiently boozy and delicious) and two rounds of sake.  Our waiter was a guy named Ned and he was terrific.  I'm a server and I know what a drag happy hour is, but we got his full attention and awesome service.  The only reason I'm not giving them five stars is because it is quite a bit pricier for dinner service.  Definitely catch them 3-6 for a good deal.

My husband and I went to Marathon Grille in Palm Harbor for dinner.  There was plenty of parking and the restaurant looked very nice inside.  The booth was comfortable and our server was pleasant.  The biggest issue is the menu has way too many selections and variety of food.  There is no way the cook in the kitchen can have all that food in the freezer/cooler for it to taste fresh and flavorful.  For example, we ordered the saganaki flaming cheese and honestly I could not identify what kind of cheese we were served.  All I know was it smelled like foot odor and I could not eat it.  The bread that was served was sub par at best, like Greek style Wonder Bread.  

I had the avgolemeno chicken lemon rice soup and that was a disappointment.  It tasted like mushy water mixed with rice and flour and their were tiny pieces of chicken that you would need a magnifying glass to identify it.  I was wondering if the cook intentionally makes the soup mushy due to the volume of elderly customers who are likely wearing dentures.  

 I ordered the spinach pie for my entree and we waited almost 45 minutes for our dinner and the place had very few customers.  Our server disappeared and the owner walked up to our table and apologized for the long wait, she said the cook was too busy making food for the "to go orders." Wow, thank you for making us feel significant and worthy for spending our hard working money in your place.

Well, when I finally was served my spinach pie I was shocked to see it was made in a frozen prepackaged pastry shell with frozen spinach and I could barely taste feta cheese or whatever cheese they put in there, possibly cream cheese to save money.    I cut the spinach pie in half and it did not look appetizing at all.  The pastry shell just fell apart and the spinach was frozen and mushy tasting and it had no flavor at all.  The menu states the spinach pie is baked in a flaky filo dough, NO it is not and that is deceiving to the customer!  It is actually baked in some sort of gross tasting prepackaged pastry shell.  

A real authentic greek spinach pie is made with filo dough, feta and ricotta cheese, dill, eggs, olive oil, oregano, salt & pepper and that is it!  Unfortunately not at the Marathon Grille and this was the worse spinach pie I have ever been served.  It tasted so bad that I could not eat it!  They added a couple of potatoes on the plate and I ended up eating a few bites of the potatoes.  That spinach pie was cheaply made with poor quality ingredients and it was by far the worse tasting spinach pie ever!  The menu is too big and the kitchen is too small for fresh, high quality food to come out of that kitchen!  We have not desire to go back to this place.

The Bahn mi's are OK. Not up to par with the real Vietnamese places that serve theirs for $5-6. The Ramen does shine. It has a great flavor and full of different ingredients. Pointer if you are going to serve $4 lemonade don't serve it from a half gallon jug and a small cup.

I'm a member and It's nice to have a theater in this town that shows independent movies but the headache of actually trying to get a parking spot in that tiny convoluted parking lot and end up having to pay metered parking at the lot below that does not always work and then finally getting through the 15 min concession line only to find out that they oversold the movie and there's not a single available seat left is not worth it any more. One or twice, OK but it's like this Every. Single. Weekend. My husband already cancelled his membership. I might have to, too. Not worth the stress.
Been here 2 hours and still don't have food.  HORRIBLE service.  Too bad, because the food is good (or, so I've heard).

Had the eggplants, beef fried rice, and the dim sum sampler. All delicious!
Also the atmosphere is nice and staff are super friendly.

I love BOTH of the Kalamata's restaurants.  There is one in Green Hills on Hillsboro Pike, and one closer to where I work in Brentwood on Franklin Road.  This review is for the Franklin Road location.  It's in a strip mall (kind of) with a bunch of other restaurants:  Chill Spot, Subway, Brick's, Five Guys, Peter's Sushi, etc.  

The staff is friendly, and the food is always fresh!  My favorites are the salads (the dressing is YUMMY), and the Chicken Gyro Pocket.  My husband almost always gets the special of the day, and he loves it.  His favorite lately has been the Salmon Florentine served over a great greek salad.  

I love tea (and I just stopped drinking soda), so I splash a little sweet green tea at the bottom, and then fill the rest with their unsweet.  It's refreshing and delicious.  

Also, before you leave, grab a baklava.  You're welcome.  :)   

If you go during the work week at lunch, it's pretty crowded, but they turn tables very quickly.
I was impressed with the atmosphere (clean, modern, up scale) and with the food choices. Certainly would recommend to others visiting the Gulf Coast.

Another reviewer nailed it: quick, clean & courteous. I'm glad I called ahead, though, cause at 11:30 am on this particular Friday in July the place was MOBBED. Funny, too, they had all kinds of customers, from suburban moms with little girls in tow, to twenty-somethings yakking on cell phones to little old lady types....  

If you do gel polish, they only offer OPI and Shellac brands, and mostly just the pinks/reds/wines -- I didn't see any blue, green or neon shades but I know these brands carry them. 

Great prices, plus, not only do they take V/MC/D/Amex but they also allow you to put your technician's tip on the card, too. Very handy, for those of us who tend to not carry much cash.

I used Slem's Pet Care for the first time last week and had such a great experience!  I was a little "gun shy" about using a pet sitting company as I had just had a HORRIBLE experience with another well known pet sitting company in the St. Louis area.  But, Slem's exceeded my expectations by a long shot.  From the initial meeting, I felt very comfortable with Slem's and could tell my dogs would be in loving hands who truly cared for their well-being. 

Brandy stayed at my house and watched two of my Standard Poodles while I was on vacation for several days.  Donna and Brandy came to my house for a meet & greet before my trip and I was impressed with how much attention and love they gave my dogs from the minute they walked in the door.  It was love at first sight for my dogs!  They appreciated the love, hugs, petting and cookies.  Even my shy girl shouldn't get enough of it!  Donna and Brandy asked lots of questions which made me feel that they truly cared about getting things right.

Brandy sent me lots of texts, photos and even a video of my dogs while I was gone.  I was very impressed with her communication and professionalism.  She spent a lot of time with my dogs during the day.  I had previously used a company where the sitter was only present from 8pm to 8pm and any additional time was $15 for 20 minutes.  It really felt great to know that my dogs were in such great care and I was able to relax on my vacation without worrying, which doesn't happen often! 

I came home to find my house in the exact same condition I left it in.  My dogs were happy and clearly well cared for.  I have to say that I was beyond satisfied with Slem's and I feel that the care they provided was absolutely above and beyond!
casual and accommodating!! service isn't super fast but i think it's because of the many many options on their menu/drinks. sushi is good too!

My friends and I wanted to get out of town for the weekend so we decided on Santa Barbara. While looking at hotels, Avania was the most affordable and it was very close to State St., which was perfect. 

There is a parking lot on either side of the hotel and the entrance is front and center. Customer service isn't that amazing. The woman behind the desk (I think she owns this place) appeared to be having a terrible day, wasn't very prompt or polite when we asked for extra shampoo and towels (3 middle eastern women cannot share one bottle of shampoo). When we decided to partake of the hotel's jacuzzi (they also have a sauna and a pool!), right at 10:00 pm, she walked in to let us know the pool was closed and hung around like we were miscreants. So, that wasn't that great... but the hotel is a decent enough place to stay. Like I said, it's very near State St. and Stearns Wharf, the room was clean and pretty spacious considering there were two queen beds shoved in and they have a tv with cable and wifi (their wifi is awful though). They also serve a complimentary breakfast in the morning which isn't amazing but it's decent enough considering it's free, the highlight is that their coffee is actually very good.

The price is right though so I'd stay here again. Hopefully customer service improves.

Super friendly staff, wonderful toppings selection, and yummy yummy frozen yogurt flavors! Highly recommend a visit if you're in the area and in the mood for a cold treat.

Price depends on how many toppings you choose as you're charged by weight.

Their website says open until 8pm. We drove 20 minutes across town and got there at 7:05 and they were closed. Guess they don't value new customers, we won't be back! Outside of building looks run down
There is now a fence so that dogs can't escape over the stone wall! My jumping corgi no longer escapes

I can dig this place-- some words that come to mind when in bazbeaux: family friendly lodge pizza cozy sharing noms.  Appreciate the vegan option for my main squeeze & our server's attention to non-dairy crust.  The wine selection sucks-- I mean really, don't even think about trying it.  Good beers though.  I'll be back!

Nothing like a giant, blacklight-reflective cup of melon-flavoured antifreeze to get you going on a hot, sunny day in NOLA. Hand Grenades are poison, especially if you're not a big drinker (I'm big, but not a drinker). I had 2 in the span of 15 mins and I was out on the street, puking quietly into a cup of water. I wish I could give these zero stars but the bartender working that day was awesome. She got me a cup of ice chips and called me a cab within minutes of me doing the ol' Technicolor yawn. 

So yeah, if you're not used to this kind of thing, stay away. Learn from my mistakes!

Worked out perfectly for our late arrival into NOLA. Plopped our bags in a room, went to the bar to wind down. Our bartender, Nick, was exceptional. He kept up with all his patrons, as well as taking time to write down some restaurant recommendations, which we took him up on. All were out of this world!
Barbacoa tacos all the way - food is great and it is cheap so can't beat that. Probably my favorite tacos in philly. If you go for breakfast the line will be much shorter.

Did some pandemic takeout from them. Enjoyed the chicken empanadas highly. The mushroom empanadas were decent but I definitely prefer the meat ones more. 

The street corn was ok. I've had better versions of this personally. But not too bad. 

I'd give them a shot again just for those empanadas.

Headed down to Santa Barbara for my bachelorette weekend and was looking forward to spending some time with my girls. Mind you, we weren't looking to get wild and crazy...that's what our 20's were for. Apparently, SB and Hotel Santa Barbara, don't take kindly to the soon to be-married. 

The Good: Prime Location, rooms were nice...albeit small. Complimentary continental breakfast.

The Bad: My sister and cousin drove in later and arrived to the Hotel shortly after midnight. I had left a key for her upfront. They were rudely greeted and told that they were aware they were here for a bachelorette party. My sister explained to them that yes we were, however we had no intentions of partying it up in our hotel room...the hotel room was for sleeping. Seeing that the front desk and security were still giving them wary stares, they offered up the fact that they were both mothers and were not party animals, which made the staff soften a little. But really, who needs to explain this to prove themselves?

The Ugly: The icing on the cake, we had returned from a local club just past midnight (again...wouldn't a wild and crazy party be stumbling in way later?) and were deciding whether or not to take the stairs or elevators. Mind you the lobby echoes and yes, coming from a loud club can make you speak a little louder. The security guard comes bombarding towards us and yells to us in a tone meant for elementary school children. I would understand if we had been continuously loud or if other guests had complained, but to be chastised in that fashion was completely unnecessary and unprofessional. As someone who has worked in the customer service industry for many years, there were other ways to be tactful in his approach.

Overall, would probably recommend this place for couples...couples that are uber-quiet. It's a shame, a place with such a prime location should be used to the fact that they are surrounded by bars and clubs would have more diplomatic approaches to dealing with guests. 

Note: I try not to yelp in anger. I gave myself a couple of days before deciding to write this review.

Aahahahahahahah- Kingdom of Vegetarians is ludicrously bad.  Yet, it made for a meal so hilarious that it may actually have been cooler than if it had been an excellent restaurant, because was so very memorable, more than if we had had something actually good. I usually reserve 1-star reviews for incidents of bodily harm, but I felt crappy from the insane levels of MSG, so I think this qualifies.

We were lured in by the sign on the door- "All you can eat vegetarian dim sum- $12".  What could possibly be wrong with that?

The waiter brought us some tea, and even after we ordered dim sum, insisted on leaving the menu so that we could order more items if we were still hungry.  They were serious about this AYCE business, it seemed.

After a bowl of slightly-below-mediocre cornstarchy soup, our first plate arrived.  Instead of using a cart, they brought it out in courses.  So that was their game!  Bring out the cheapest stuff first to try to fill us up!  If I'd known where it was going from there, I would have been much more grateful for the rock-hard fried sesame mock-chicken.  Incidentally, Kingdom was the first Chinese restaurant that I've been to in years that offered a fork, but no chopsticks.  Not that I'm a snob, just sayin'.  The sauces were standard issue, straight out of the little packets but squirted into bowls. 

The rest of the items were so untasty that we ended up pushing those things to the side.  Next were the dumplings and pork buns.  The buns were still covered with paper that had stuck on  when they were microwaved and the dumplings were made of bread, unlike every other dumpling in the world.   The waiter noticed the piles of unwanted food pushed away and thought we were done, but I politely asked to skip ahead to the seaweed salad.  He argued but finally relented.  After a very long wait, the manager(?) brought the seaweed out, saying "NOW are you happy?"   Temporarily appeased anyway. It was almost entirely unseasoned, save for the 43415613456 cloves of garlic.  It was the best thing we had.

After we ate, I started trying to signal to the waiter that we would like something else.  He literally made eye contact while I waved at him several times, and then returned to folding napkins but when I stood up to go get him, both he and the manager had obviously been watching me because they were instantly at my side.  I stated my case and the manager brought out the fried bananas, telling us "This is your last course".  Uh, AYCE whut?  

Oh my word.  The itsy bitsy little slices of banana were encased in giant puffballs of batter.  I surgically extracted the banana, followed by the fillings of the eggroll, of the be-papered bun, of the dumplings, and now of the banana, leaving neat little dough cases delicately split on my plate.  It was bratty, but dammit, I was still hungry!  By now we were feeling weird and flushed from so much MSG but we weren't ready to give up, so we ordered the only other thing on the admittedly extensive dim sum menu that wasn't fried: coconut triangles. 

 Again, I had to chase them and they gave me some shit, but I reminded them it was AYCE, and actually we were doing them a favor because we had skipped 75% of the menu.  It took a loooong time to get these.  The manager told us they were making them from scratch.  They were the last thing on the menu, and I guess no one had ever made it that far before dying of MSG poisoning or a heart attack.  Finally, she brought out deep fried triangles with pockets of oil literally oozing around between the layers of fried.   Again, I surgically excised the meager filling. It was orange and tasted like sweet potatoes, with perhaps a hint of coconut flavor, courtesy of McCormick. Damn.

I'm confused by all the glowing reviews.  Maybe people enjoy different foods than we do, but the service?  Some of the worst I've had.  I'm polite, friendly, forgiving- was it something I said? Just stay away.

If I could give cox a negative 5 star ratings I would. My mother moved to a new house and I went to the cox store with here and she could not remember here PIN number. She showed Kenny in the Santa Barbara store. He was fine but did not give us the PIN number. We took the mini boxes home and hooked them up to the TV. This is when the problems started. Nothing worked. I called the number and the girl would not help us because we did not have the PIN number... So stupid for the worker at the store to not give us the PIN number. ( Thursday March 5  4:45 PM )
I asked for the supervisor and she was the rudest thing I have ever talked to. No help !!!!!!! at ALL... My mother went back to the store and got the PIN number and came home and I called the number and still no help and still no TV working. What a waist of 3 hours. The support girls only response was we can send out a tech person. We need Comcast in our area so there is some competition. I give COX a negative 5 stars

We have been going to Fresh Bites for years. It's an amazing family owned restaurant. The food is delicious and fresh. The owners are kind and wonderful. I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone!!!

Nice place. Drinks were ok. Server was wearing a face shield with no mask but forced us to order food with our drinks because the City says so. You don't get to pick and choose which regulations you follow ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

Prices are average in comparison to other theatres in the area. My husband and I go here often, and like to go in the afternoon for cheaper ticket prices. Tickets are available for purchase online, and you get to pick your seats. The seats are comfortable and recline and there is plenty of room. My favorite thing about this theatre is the amount of butter you can put on your own poppcorn and the refiills available if you get the popcorn and drink combo. Really have nothing bad to say about this place, it's always been welcoming and clean, and the bathrooms are taken care of as well.
Sometimes the wait is very long. It has fairly good steaks but not a top of the line chain place. It is ok as a group place to meet. Fun concept.

Butter + Salt did four days of catering for my wedding weekend at The HideOut this September and it was nothing short of amazing. I can't recommend them enough. 

I went in with a vague idea of the style of dining I wanted for the weekend and gave Butter + Salt the freedom to design the menus. It was totally stress free and the meals they came up with tasted amazing and had exactly the unique-twist I was looking for. Everything was elevated (just take a look at their sample menus on their website and you'll see what I mean). Our guests raved about the food the whole weekend. 

The execution was also perfect -- imagine getting together three meals a day for almost four days -- and their staff was kind and courteous. If you need a caterer for an event or wedding in the Reno-Tahoe area, look no further than Butter + Salt. Seriously.

Man, this is one of the times that Yelp has let me down. Saw the ratings on here and decided to go here for lunch. Got the chicken Nixon sandwich and it was very very very average. I'm talking about I can make one better at home and I don't even know how to cook. Very bland. Friend got a cheese and egg croissant and it was okay. The fries were the best part of the meal. Maybe it was an off day. The bartender served us and was a nice dude. Don't think I'll come back, but maybe I just had too high expectations of this place.

Mini review after 1st visit. They get it here. Super clean, bright, friendly service and good reasonably priced food for what it is. Will be back for sure. Quick math indicates lower cost than a smoothie and much better atmosphere to eat in after workout.
Service was meh... no one ever checked on me after I was served, and I had to ask for the check.  Clam chowder was excellent - tuna salad was okay.

I went to get my hair cut and I'm the type of person who trust the hairdresser to know what is going to look better than what I think. I asked for change. All she did was thin my hair and than blow dry it for 40 minutes. It didn't look awful after, but it didn't look different either.

Been a patient here for more than 4+ years, and introduce as many people as i possibly can. I honestly say they are the best chiropractic around. Highly knowledgeable, goes at the pace of what your body can do, and makes you feel so good after. Had a herniated disk, but not pain thanks to Dr Kim, Dr Jo (whoseunfortunatelynotthereanymore) and Dr Hwang!

PS if you have weird, or tight schedules, they always try and accommodate your needs first! Cant ask for anything more than that!

So, I have been here a couple of times because it's so close to my home. Tran is fantastic. That's about all I can say that is good at this place.

Most recently, I went to get a manicure. Normally, I get dip powder but that means I have to skip the hand/arm massage and the hot towel etc so this time I wanted to treat myself.

So, I paid $32(not including tip) but a manicure that lasted 17 minutes exactly. My friend was next to me and we were blown away by  how terrible it was. The "massage" didn't even last 15 seconds. Most of that time was spent with me "washing my hands." For $32, I would expect it to be relaxing, excellent customer service and then have beautiful  nails but no. Per her instructions, I waited 20 minutes to dry. Got home that night and reached into a draw and two of my nails were ruined because the polish crinkled up. 

Now, that I'm removing all the polish I have this weird yellow/orange tint on my nails where she painted. I'm reading it could be an infection from unclean tools- I'm so confused. I've never had this happen before. 
All that to say, I would skip this place. Still looking for a great place in Nashville/Donelson. The best I've seen so far is LAGOM.
The food was satisfying, and the service was good too! Recommend to come with a group of people! There's a lot of food. You're  guarantee to leave with a full stomach!

Love this salon! KYRA is absolutely amazing. Listens to everything you have to say and delivers. She is so easy to talk to and is very genuine. This coming Friday will be my third appointment with her and I can't wait! This salon is also absolutely adorable, you feel welcome the minute you walk in! I moved to Reno about 5 years ago and have had an interesting time trying to find someone who does my hair the way I like it. Kyra is the one!! So happy!!!

Boyfriend loved his fried chicken. The mac and cheese was just not that good -- not ooey gooey, but clumpy and odd. Collard greens and mustard greens were nothing special, and pretty bitter.

Our waiter was nice enough to accommodate my special request of just a platter of fried oysters with greens. They were pretty yummy -- I was impressed that somehow, the batter wasn't a ball of grease.

The cornbread was sub par...lacking flavor and moistness. Frankly, I expected more in this department...

I had to change rooms 3 times to find one that didn't reek of SMOKE! Yuck! Then the girl at the desk messed up by not activating my key TWICE! I had to sit in the carpet in front of my room door waiting for my BF to go all the way down to reactive the keys. I'm also disappointed the room didn't have a fridge or a view at all. I normally wouldn't care so much about a room, but when you are spending $200/night for the "Grand Suite" you don't want it smelling like cigarettes.

Middle of the road. Museum was not that hot. Staff was nice and courteous. Art work incredible.  But not the same Lower Marion experience. Way too many people. Could not enjoy art as Barnes wanted us to. Gift shop lame!

Slowest sub counter at any Publix I've ever been to. Inattentive staff that spends way to much time chatting with each other. After waiting in line 15-20 minutes for a sub here, I'll just skip it the next time I'm in the area. Hello Jimmy John's!

8:45 pm Thursday, September 13 experience

This was a painful Panera visit. Typically, Panera is one of our go-to options when it comes to drive-thru with some healthy flair. This particular team seemed very heavily concerned with closing up for the night and not providing a strong experience for their last few customers of the day. We waiting in the drive thru line for 15 minutes after we ordered the food. Upon pulling up to the window to receive our food there were a few more disappointing pieces to the puzzle: the music was jamming inside the kitchen and there was a sloppy vibe to our window staff - I'm opting not to include her name but her hair was quite sloppy for being in the food industry and she kept touching her hair which seemed to be the cherry on top for the sloppy scene we were seeing from the window. I get that we were one of their last customers of the day but service needs to roll through until the night is officially a wrap.

Great food! I have lived in Nashville for almost a decade and have been searching for some quality Chinese- how lucky for me that I moved next to China Panda! We love the dumplings and the lo mein is delicious too! My Chinese food hating husband even loves it!

This used to be one of the best values in town;  there was a change of ownership a while back and it went downhill.  On recommendation from a family member, I tried it again.  And again was really disappointed.  My meat did not taste grilled; it was touch and not cooked the way I asked.  The ribs were COLD--tasted like they came out of the fridge and were not reheated.  For the cost, I expect the meat to be grilled as promised and the ribs to be cooked to order.  They are supposed to close for 10 days to fix the AC--think there is a lot more that needs to be fixed!
Hour wait for table. Empty tables took 30 minutes to seat. Another hour wait after we ordered.  I will give them some slack because their "WIFI was down."

I went in for a haircut because my mom kept complaining that I should get layers. So I go in and I tell him I don't want layers because It makes my ponytail look like a chicken's tail. I told him my mother wanted me to get layers cause I have thick hair and he was like, "oh yes I will make it the way you'll like it!" boy was he wrong :C He layered my hair so close to my scalp that now I have a weird bump in my hair because the ends of my hair tend to frizz and he cut my bangs the way I didn't like it. I told him I had straightish bangs that was wispy and he literally cut it straight across AND also cut it to wide so now it looks like I have a bowl hair cut on top of my layered thin hair ;-; He finished the cut faster than the lady washing my hair! Don't you think something is wrong with that? plus i could tell he was rushing cause it was near closing time. Overall a) I can't tie my hair up without hair falling out near the neck and sticking out b) I have an ugly bowl looking hair cut c) I have short strands that frizz and will take a year to grow out d) I learn to never trust going to a hair salon ever again, I'll cut my own hair e) It's a good thing he didn't even cut my hair short, my mom said he was planning to make it a bowl or pixie cut like what no way right? . . . right?! f) FML at least this horrible hair experience was cheaper than that other horrible hair experience . . .

This was our 2nd dining experience at PYs.  A pricey place which was ok the first time, a few months ago.  This time, early Saturday night, the service was terrible and the meals even worse.  The cooks seemed to be children who failed cooking school.  My rack of lamb was raw, after finally being able to flag down the waitress (we were the only patrons there...) 20 minutes later, they must have stuck it in the microwave to try for medium rare.  It was so tough even the dog would have walked away.  No comp on the bill.  My friend's brisket was also tough and drenched in a1 sauce.  Never again!

ABSOLUTELY HORRIBLE SERVICE! NEVER EVER WILL ORDER HERE AGAIN! i know this review is long, but it'll definitely be helpful! Unless you're ordering for pick up, I definitely DO NOT recommend this place!! 

1. It took an hour for delivery!! I called after 45 minutes to see where my order was, and the guy on the phone nicely explained that it was a busy night and that it was on its way and I understood. When the delivery guy finally got here, I'm standing by the door waiting for him to give me my food and he sits in his car, and yells out the window "Come get it!" then hands me my food through his car window. SERIOUSLY?!?! dude first of all, if i'm ordering Chinese food, i'm most likely saying, i don't want to cook, give me something quick to eat. If i knew i had to wait an hour for my food, I would've just cooked something for myself, a lot healthier too! Also, shouldn't you at LEAST apologize? You know what, I wasn't even expecting an apology, but making me walk out to your car ?! bitch give me back the tip money! if you don't want to work, just quit!!! please don't ruin other people's day/night because you want to sit on your lazy butt. 

2. So I sit down and I'm already STARVING and I noticed that there aren't any  vegetables in my "vegetable" lo-mein! are you kidding me? Just because you place it in the same container as my main dish which has vegetables, doesn't make it a vegetable lo-mein! Then for my main dish, which is supposed to come with, lobster, scallops, and jumbo shrimp, did NOT have any Jumbo shrimp! It didn't even have tiny baby shrimp! there was absolutely no shrimps what so ever ! I mean if you're going to take your good ol' time and eat away others', i mean at LEAST make the order correctly?!

3. Then, the first time I ordered from here, (this is my 2nd) I specifically asked them not to include any meat in my order, because I am a pescatarian and can't have meat and they completely ignored it and put pork in my dish. This time, I asked them to give me sides of hot sauce, and once again they completely ignored it!! How hard is it to toss some hot sauce packets into a freaking bag?! -.-

The WORST customer service ever! seriously, never again.
WAY TOO EXPENSIVE for what it is. 2 kids and 3 adults got chicken fingers and wraps for $70. Shame on me for not walking out the door.

Not worth the 30 minute wait. I was looking forward to a great meal and ordered the breakfast special with a side of fried ham and cheese grits. The ham was obviously not fried, but the waitress insisted it was. This is my first time visiting New Orleans, so maybe my expectations were too high, but meh. My niece had the shrimp creole grits which were ok too.

We have been coming here for breakfast and it is good and the service has been fine. The dinners are very disappointing and will not be back and rather go elsewhere for dinner. I would give it a five star rating for breakfast but will give it a 3 star overall since we have had poor dinners lately!!!  The waitress staff and help is nice !!!

Sat at the bar- had quick and friendly service. Was recommended to order the queso fundido and shrimp tacos (called something else about 3 lines down on taco menu). The queso was so thick- delicious, but I literally felt like I was putting on 5 lbs eating 5 bites. Now, the shrimp tacos..whatever they call them.. are AMAZING. If you do order the queso, go ahead and put some on the tacos. They offer complimentary chips with red and green salsa and a bean dip- the bean dip is truly top notch. Next time around, I'd skip the cheese dip and just order an entree. Shrimp tacos would make the cut again. For sure. I've heard they're starting to close for lunch soon. Solid happy hour and dinner though!

Extremely disappointed with this office. I had a test done that was not done correctly/was inconclusive, and they are now requiring me to pay for another office visit and another test at the super affordable price of $570 (we're talking a simple test). They refused to work with me at all even though it was their fault, and now won't return voicemails or emails. If you're looking for a Dermatologist, I'd look elsewhere.
Ralston's is one of my favorite restaurants in Indy! The chicken House of Dnart Nachos are the best nachos in town. The cheese sauce is amazing.

This place is decent. The food is basic yet interesting enough that I think with most groups everyone will be able to find something they're interested in. Even still, I think a lot of the menu items are a little pricey for what they are (basic pub food). I got the salmon burger (which was *delicious*) but they fries it came with were very thinly sliced and mostly too crispy/burned. I love potatoes and usually one way or another I'll finish a plate of fries but I had less than half of them. That seemed to be the experience for everyone who got fries. 

Honestly I think this is a 3-star kind of place, but the reason I'm taking one star away in my review is that when the group of 7 I was with paid for our meal, everyone put in cash and I paid my portion on my card. I got my receipt, and that was it. The next day, checked my account balance and it had been overdrawn because the full amount of dinner and drinks for the party of 7 was pending on my card. I called Prime Stache, they connected me to someone named Shawn (?) who I assume is some sort of manager, who informed me that although he wasn't there, he looked in the system and saw that the final amount in their system was the $20 that I should have been charged for. He explained that "this has happened before" and what probably happened is that my card had been run for the full amount, then that transaction was voided, and then run for the amount I requested be put on the card. Once the charges finish pending, it should show up for the amount that I should be charged for. He was polite and apologized, but I was somewhat surprised that he/Prime Stache wasn't more apologetic about the mix up. Given the caliber/hipness of the place it seems like they should have tried harder, as most restaurants I think would have. Really I don't know why the person who ran my card that night didn't tell me that he'd had to void a transaction on my card so I could know about that possibility of a charge much larger than I was expecting to be pending on my card and be prepared in case I needed to move money around in my accounts to prevent an overcharge. It wouldn't have been hard to just give me that heads up, so that was a huge oversight on their part. I understand that card systems are whacky, but that Shawn (?) told me that it's happened before just seems like there's some kind of gap in the training of the staff-- and the restaurant was not busy when we were there. It's not like they were all rushing around attending to numerous demanding tables, it was a mostly empty place. Prime Stache is alright, but I think this experience is going to keep me away. I don't feel like I'll be missing out.
