Amazingggg. Seriously hands down best tacos ever. The guys who work are so nice, make amazing food, and they get the food prepared in less theb 10 minutes. This place is my dinner about 2 to 3 times a week.

Koreana's is a great hidden gem of a karaoke bar. They have both English and Korean songbooks and a good selection in each book. The owner recognizes the regulars and has a funny sense of humor with everyone there. Come a little earlier to be sure to get more turns to sing, before it gets busy. There are decent drink specials during the week but not on weekends.

Fun atmosphere, good band, people seemed to enjoy the food.  I left after a drink.  I had to ask for a drink and menu and my change after I was asked for $5 for my beer.  No offer of a tab or my order.  Disappointing.

Jennifer is not just a photographer, she is an artist by true definition. I believe that it is her personable demeanor and inspirational vision that enables her to truly capture her subjects personality and therefore produce a work of art that embodies them as an individual. If you're looking for the stiff portrait with a cheese smile, go to Sears. If you've flipped through the fashion magazines and thought "I wish I could look like that."  Book your session with Karmapants.

It took 20 minutes to find our bottle of wine, because the sommelier wasn't working today. The appetizers were great.  We had the stuffed chicken wings, pork cheek boutin and burata salad.  We also finished a double bottle of wine before our entrees arrived.  When they did, three meals were warm, while two were cold, the veal porterhouse.  The manager did very little to explain what happened, barely even apologized.  The staff became quite inattentive about 25 minutes into our wait between appetizers and dinner. Our server needed to go back to the manager to ask about taking care of some of the bill for their mistakes. Took $90 off of our $500 bill... Still think they could have done more...

Terrible experience. I walked in and there was one guy sitting behind the open wall playing on his phone. He looked up once, saw me, then continued to play on his phone. He didn't acknowledge that I was there or give any indication that somebody would be with me shortly. I waited for a few minutes for either him to help me or somebody else to come and help me, but nothing. I walked out. Not even sure if he noticed.

The service kinda sucks, the one dude didnt even know how to work the kiosk. 

As far as the food- I ordered my usual veggie burrito bowl and when i got it and opened it up it smelt straight FOUL. Ive ordered this quite a few times from another location and it has NEVER smelt anything like how this did. I decided to take a bite even though it smelt like literal vomit (honestly my mistake for eating something that smelled that disgusting). Sure enough, its was disgusting. I gave it a chance and eventually spit my second bite out. it tasted NOTHING like it normally does. Im honestly unsure why is was so different and disgusting (i guess they maybe made it wrong?). 

Few hours later you can assume my stomach didn't take it well (even though i only had a bite) 

I would recommend avoiding this location and definitely don't get the burrito bowl there

PRICEY CHAIN RESTAURANT WITH THE FEEL OF A 
WELL-HEALED, BIG-CITY INDEPENDENT LANDMARK

Maggiano's, located on 86th Street just east of Keystone Avenue on Indy's Northside, is a chain, test restaurant with the feel of a well-healed, big-city independent landmark. 

Great surroundings. Above-average service. Tasty recipes. 

A few negatives to expect: 1) Seating is not their strong suit, even with a reservation. 2) You will pay top dollar for the nice decor, ample staffing and quality food. 3) Not all Maggiano's servers are created equal, so identify one who gives good service and request them. 

$50 w/o tip ($60 total) for two chicken picatta dinners (no soup or salad... just chicken breasts and a small bowl of angel-hair pasta), one drink and one Diet Coke is a bit salty even for the Keystone @ the Crossing area. 

A note to the MANAGER and OUR SERVER.... When your guests leave, and you're chit-chatting with one another, and the guests take the time to thank you and say goodbye, AT LEAST TAKE TIME TO ACKNOWLEDGE YOUR CUSTOMER'S COURTESY. IT IS YOU WHO SHOULD BE THANKING THEM AND SAYING GOODBYE.
My iced coffee tasted burnt and watery at the same time. No one wore masks and the whole place felt grimy and dirty. Won't be back ever again.

Got the Beef Lo Mein - fantastic, the Sesame Chicken combo - very very good, and the Sweet & Sour Chicken - real good and they give you a huge amount of that. First time there and will definitely go back, a bit pricey but I think most Chinese places are nowadays. Very good and highly recommend.

This is the worst Starbucks ever!  No matter what time of day they are extremely slow.  I've visited this store st 6am and was only the 2nd customer and I still had to wait 10-15 minutes for a cup of coffee! 
When I visited 2 days ago I ordered a caramel Frappicino.  It took me about 25 minutes to get my drink.  And when I did it was watery! I told the girl it's not supposed to be like this and she grabbed the drink and said she will put more ice in it! I didn't get a chance to tell her no thank you please remake my drink.  When she did arrive my drink looked like flavored ice.  No coffee.  It was horrible! By the time I left over 30 minutes past and I was left with a $6 nasty drink.

It's a special kind of hell when a movie theater allows people to bring babies into movies with them. Do you know how amazing it was to see Black Panther at 8:15pm (ended at 10:30pm) and listen to a baby crying?! Hint: I'm so annoyed I'm actually typing this review as I'm waiting for the end credit scene. This was literally the worst movie experience I've ever had and will never come back here. I would rather wait for a movie to come out on demand than to see a movie here ever again!

Came here with a friend and got takeout here during COVID. The owner was very nice and gave out some good recommendations.

This was a pretty decent place, the owner certainly knew how to hype it up. We weren't too big of a fan of the bread, it was tough. Didn't seem too fresh. The lamb was pretty good. It was cooked well, tender, nicely seasoned. The rice was well seasoned a very good. The hummus itself was ok. Nothing special about it.

All in all, it was an alright experience. Maybe one day when they open for indoor seating it would be better. Looking forward to that.

Let's just start by saying this right off the bat... if you live in Springfield, thinking of just going out for some pizza, and you say to yourself or anyone you're with, "Why don't we go to Sbarro tonight", then what you should do is either smack yourself in the head or have someone else do it.  

Sbarro is not a "let's go out for pizza" destination, not when you have places nearby like Peace A Pizza or Bella Roma in the area.  Sbarro is a "we're in the mall anyway and too lazy to go anywhere else so this will do" pizza place.  I'm a firm believer of supporting local pizza places whenever possible.  They're just better.  

This all being said, in a recent moment of "we happen to be in the mall, let's just eat here because it is convenient" mode, I will say that their pizza may have improved.  The manager said that they recently upgraded their ingredients, and the pizza actually wasn't that bad.  

Let's put it this way, despite the price point at Sbarro which remains too high for what you get in the end, taste-wise I'd now choose Sbarro over national chain places like Domino's, Pizza Hut, and Papa John's.  But certainly not over a place like Bertucci's.  

It may not be the highest praise, but that's all you get from me.

I brought my relatives from Kentucky to dine here and we experienced poor customer service by our server, Marissa. She not only got my order wrong, but never apologized for it. She also displayed a pissed off look when I told her that my order was wrong. She also forgot several orders that were included with our orders. I reported her to the general manager who advised me she will talk to her. I was embarrassed in front of my out of towners thanks to Marissa. Please this emplo requires immediate training on how to provide good customer service.

$100 worth of diagnostic fees to check 
1) an oil leak  +  2) air has stopped coming out of vents when AC is on.  

Diagnosis:
1) I need an oil change & new filter 
2) they want an extra $55 to figure out why no air is coming out of the vents. 
   
Also got "sold" the $9.99 discounted complete inspection.  Pretty sure you (like me) can count on them finding hundreds of dollars worth of additional "issues" they recommend to be taken care of. 

It's not my daily driver so I just got the  "$150 dollar oil change" & called it a wrap.  

I gotta say, the guys are really nice...
The shop is walking distance for me...
But I'll be looking for a non-corporate "mom & pop" shop.  I'd rather pay the mechanics than the shareholders.

Beautiful spot! I'm new in town and caught wind of this local treasure when getting a pedicure. Rumor has it, Love Circle is where the local kids go to makeout at night ;)

Its a great spot for a nice walk, a picnic, or just to watch the sunset over the city. From one side of the hill, the view is more rural with a beautiful stretch of orange and red leaved trees while the other side of the hill looks out over the lights of downtown. Would be nice if the top of the hill didn't have small fences enclosing electrical units... 

Top of the hill has a beautiful view. You can't miss the spot due to the monstrosity of a home that the star of Big and Rich built, unfortunately. Too bad that some people feel they can steal a local treasure because they have enough money. I always knew I hated country music.

I personally don't like this place.  It really isn't my scene.  Here is the scene:

Frat house, college, mostly white people, tshirts, jeans, sandals (on brave souls that don't mind their feet getting tramped on), 80s music, rap songs that everyone knows and can sing to (Juicy, Next Episode, etc).  Beer is pretty cheap, they use plastic cups.  Cig machine at the bottom where the bathroom is.  

Frankly, I hate coming here.  The bouncers at the door have power issues and will let some of your group in and keep you out.  

One time I was here for one of my best friend's birthdays, so we were there at 9.  Not many people in there, but it was still pretty loud due to the music.  I was told to quiet down.  I wasn't yelling obnoxiously or anything, I promise I wasn't drunk yet.  I know I'm a loud person, but being told to quiet down in a loud bar is odd.  I just don't feel accepted here (I'm Asian, and pretty big), so I would prefer not to come here.

But if you like this type of thing, you'll have a blast.  Some of my friends that like this type of scene love it here.  Just don't expect diversity.

This hotel is gorgeous, convenient to local shops and restaurants, nice pool and rooftop furniture and layout, fabulous gym and friendly staff.  The downside is that the full size bed is not tolerable for those who prefer a soft bed.  My pressure-points were in a lot of pain during our 4 day stay.  When I called the front desk to inquire if there are different mattress brands in other rooms, such as the king beds, and was told that the mattresses are the same brand in all rooms. I explained that the mattress was the worst that I have ever experienced at a Marriott hotel and the reply was that this is the first complaint, because the hotel is new the mattresses need time to break in, and there remedy was to send up one blanket for extra padding.  LOL. The bathroom is not soundproof which allowed for zero privacy while using the "loo."

Bottom line -  I would stay here again since the hotel is one block from my son's apartment as he is stationed in Tucson.  The restaurants, breweries, and coffee shops in the area were original and DELICIOUS.  Also, many shops, historic landmarks and museums in walking distance.  What great memories and family time this 2017 Thanksgiving in lovely Downtown Tucson.
Ok I guess I will add a star because soup and salad for lunch is actually quite nice and a good value.

Another must try for the regulars of V- Street and Vedge. Wiz kid opened up recently right next to V street. If you are not in the mood for a sit and serve style dinner or lunch then Wiz Kid is the place to go to. They are serving vegan sandwiches and salad with some sides and drink options.

The menu is limited with 2 - 3 options for salads and subs, we ordered the  WK steak and the Yucatán salad. The service was quick and both  were good but slightly on the saltier side. 

Interesting use of ingredients and flavors but might be a bit pricy for the casual dining experience.

I want to start off by saying I've been buying contacts from trend eye care for about a year now. My prescription ended and I needed a trial pair because I'm pretty much blind and they wouldn't give one to me until I saw a doctor. I bought a beautiful pair of Tory Burch glasses last year that were pretty expensive and something was wrong them and the girl at the front desk said that there was nothing that they could do about them & they couldn't give me a trial pair of contacts because I needed an eye exam. Yes I know I need an eye exam but now I have broken glasses, 2 children that I'm nannying and I'm supposed to be driving these kids to activities.. thanks for all of your help Trend Eye Care.

The sandwiches here are pieces of art. Just look at the photos, the sandwiches are all masterfully constructed in layers, creating masterpieces. Not only are they beautiful to look at, they are delectable to eat. 

I was moved to come here for lunch by Ian Froeb's STL 100 List. His "must order" was the Smoked Beet Sandwich. Being a lover of beets, my arm did not have to be twisted to try his recommendation. This sandwich is especially a winner for vegetarians. However, being a big meat lover - I'd definitely come back for a repeat.

The Smoked Beet is served on Ciabatta Bread, that of course is baked in house. It's created with  layers of; tasty beets, sauerkraut, emmenthaler cheese, hard egg and Thousand Island dressing. I'll be honest it is a messy sandwich to eat. It slips and slides as you left it up for a bite. I giggled as I looked at my fingers that got a little stained from the beets. Which was easily remedied by finger licking. The combination of beets with sauerkraut and emmenthaler cheese was genius.

The cole slaw that I had as my side is worthy of a Stevie B Award - Best Cole Slaw. I honestly could make a lunch with a double order of cole slaw, a baguette and a glass of Pino Grigio. 

You may wonder why I held back a fifth star if I loved it so much, I'll tell you. Even though the bread was fresh and baked in house, it was too much. The slices were extremely thick, I would have enjoyed a thinner cut. Also, I could not get the attention of a server while I was sitting at the bar to get a knife so I could lift the top portion of bread off and eat the remainder as an open face sandwich. I am sure when I return for a "meaty" sandwich, I'll be bumping it up. 

When: June 15, 2016
Why: Lunch and Ian Froeb's STL 100

Very cute little spot in mount holly close to the courthouse, it has a dirt parking lot across the street and limited street parking
It was crowded so prepare to wait on the weekends but get there early, they close at 2p...... 

The menu is pretty basic for breakfast foods and some lunch and dinner options as well. I ordered stuffed French toast, which was amazing but the other food wasn't anything to come back for. I've had the shrimp and grits and it was just BLAND while my eggs were extremely salty. The eggs came with potatoes which were dry and poorly seasoned and turkey sausage that was just a no for me. 

I'd recommend sticking to pancakes and French toast here

Visited on 8/25, first time, mixed experience. It was 9:45 PM, so the place was pretty much emptied out. Loved the atmosphere, but the AC inside was so cold we opted to sit outside; even though it was a slightly chilly evening it was still way warmer than inside.  The patio area was charming with a lighted awning, umbrella tables, beautiful stone wall with subtle lights, all reminiscent of a grotto.  We got grilled steak/feta salad and spinach mushroom flatbread, both delicious and reasonably priced.  So far excellent, right?  Now the mixed part: the service.   This started out fine - a friendly waitress greeted us, explained that she wasn't our assigned server but was helping out, took our order, and answered questions about beers.  After a reasonable time, our food arrived delivered by the kitchen.  And then...nothing.  A woman came by asking if "everything was tasting delicious" and then we were abandoned.  We never saw our original waitress again.  The woman who had checked with us once seemed to be the only waitress in the patio area (only about 4 tables were occupied besides us) and she never once glanced over.  We finally waved her down for drink refills, after which we were on our own again.  We saw staff inside casually chatting since there weren't many diners inside (no doubt because of the extreme AC!) but despite this, no one seemed to be efficiently covering the patio.  We finally were able to again wave down the woman (not easy since we seemed invisible to her, so this reqired windmill arms, and others were using that method as well) to get our check.  I'm willing to give this place another try and even 4 stars , with the hope that the deficiency can be chalked up to one unfortunate waitress or maybe even a misunderstanding with waitstaff as to whom we were assigned.  But in the end I still have to blame management who should be looking out for this kind of thing.

I stopped here for dinner and was seated in the dining area. I was alone and was seated at a table for six which was weird. I felt like I was waiting for friends that didn't show up or something! Where I sat was cold and drafty and I didn't want to bother asking for a new seat even though there weren't that many patrons at the time.

The service was decent and the salmon salad I got was good. It wasn't amazing but still good. There really wasn't anything special about the salmon and I thought the salad was a tad overpriced for what you get. I don't drink, but maybe if I liked beer and sampled some of their brews it would have been a better experience. Having an entree, vs a salad, may have been more telling, but my salad did have salmon, so...

Every thing is fresh and tasty. Your order comes quick too if you have an event to go to next. The only down side that I saw were the prices, a bit high for Thai food. We had the crab fried rice, pork green curry, vegetable rolls, crab rangoon, chicken rice soup, and Singha beer.  Everything was very good and the service was attentive. We were pleased.

Not sure what all the attraction is about this place.  Over priced for hockey puck biscuits.  Was not impressed at all and shouldn't follow the fad.   Thank God I came on a day that we just walked  in because I would have really been upset if I wait an hour to get in the place.    Save your money and eat somewhere else
This place is a joke and dr Thomas is a quack off his rocker blo hard that has no business treating patients!

First, their website is really informational and they carry so many wonderful dress lines!  It was very easy for me to make a reservation using their online system.  After I make a reservation, in my confirmation email I was asked to complete a survey of my wedding dress style, which was really fun. 

When I went for my appointment, the shop was incredibly cute and decorated so nicely for Christmas. I had the first appointment of the day on Black Friday, and there was no one else in the shop but me which was nice.  My consultant, Megan, took control of the appointment and told me exactly how things were going to go.  She had a great strategy for picking out dresses I liked and kept my budget in mind. 

The fitting rooms were really large, which was awesome (unlike another shop I went to) and the lighting was also great. Their mirrors were fantastic and could be moved in a number of directions so I could really get a good idea of what the dresses looked like from different angles.  Megan took the time to make sure I got the feel of each dress before trying another one on.  

They also have a generous appointment length - 90 minutes.  The other shop I was at was 60 minutes (I'd only overheard them say that) and then at one point my consultant disappeared.  Megan stayed with me the entire time, and was upfront when she had to move onto her next appointment.  Buying a wedding dress is a big decision, and I only felt a little rushed at the end of my appointment when I had to make a decision (I am not the fastest decision maker).

I ended up purchasing my dress that day, and got a 10% discount as part of their black Friday sale, which was great! They gave me earrings as a gift for purchasing a dress, and a really nice folder with lots of helpful information.  They took a photo with my group and posted it on their Facebook page later in the day which was nice.  And I think I saw 3-4 other brides purchase their dress there that day as well, so I think that says a lot about the shop.  

So overall, really great shop, cute part of town, many dress selections, and excellent customer service so far.  Plus it's great to get all of this without having to go into Philly. I'm looking forward to my fittings!

I am not one to write negative reviews, but feel it has come to the point that I now have to, due to the horrendous hair damage I now have to deal with. My head literally has 1/2 inch long chunks of hair all over. 
I have been a loyal customer for almost a year, and will not return. My hair was heavily over processed, and from that, I received chemical burns, and my hair is breaking off. I called to let the salon know of the issue and my call was not returned by my stylist Sarah, as promised. I called back the following week, and again was told by the apprentice that my hair was "most likely over processed, but next time we will use a lower volume of hair lightener". Um ya, there will not be a next time. I valued my relationship with Sarah, as it is hard to find a good stylist nowadays. I would have appreciated a personal call back from her. I am disappointed, and will definitely be letting others know about my latest experience. 

I want others to be aware of my situation, and maybe think twice before booking an appointment.

Debated a long time between Creole Queen and the Natchez. Natchez is a bigger boat, and clearly has more of a following in NOLA. But ultimately we decided on the history tour on the Creole Queen to learn something more about the place we were visiting (not just listen to music, as on the Natchez). 

Dr. Wendell was our tour narrator on the morning history cruise. He's a history professor, and was a great guide. His history lesson kept us engaged throughout the ride to the battlefield, and he took time to indicate points of interest along the way. Before the boat left, he walked around and greeted each passenger by group, immediately welcoming us to the tour. 

At the battlefield, a Park Ranger takes over to talk about the battle. This presentation was less polished, but since it is not controlled by the management of Creole Queen, I won't review it. Let's just say he could learn a thing or two from Wendell.

Back on the boat for the trip to port, there was a living history personality who shared the story of Jean Lafitte and the privateers of NOLA. That's worth listening to as well. Meanwhile, Wendell was making the rounds visiting with those not listening to the pirate talk to answer any questions, etc. 

Great trip. Cool way to see the city and learn more about the history of NOLA.

Tourist trap but they do have a nice selection of beers and other alcoholic drinks. We stopped here on the Happy Thursday Nola Social Ride and it was great. You pay for the location but the quantity and quality matches the price.

Stopped in for drinks. I felt like I had just walked into Jersey. 

Nice place if you are into that sort of thing, but it was not for me.

Nice atmosphere. Went there for a date night and it was good. I had the Caesar salad and the toasted rav. Both were good. The lettuce for the salad was not the freshest since some of the outside edges were starting to brown but it wasn't bad. The rav's were great. My husband had the calamari and the lobster risotto. He must have loved both because they were consumed very quickly. I also ordered a candy apply martini which was not served in a martini glass and the heavy pour made it almost undrinkable but for $8 a glass you bet your a$$ I will drink it. The beer was cheap which was a good thing. When we first sat down we where poured water and given the bread but I was confused on why there was no ice in the water? The table clothes also had a brown paper like you would use for an art project or wrapping a package over the linen table clothe so I was confused by that one as well but it was good. Would return but I don't think I will order another mixed drink there again. Some people like a heavy pour but I like to actually taste the drink. The service was great!

The salad, the iceless water, and the heavy alcohol pour where really the only reasons I did not give 4 stars.
Great place! Really great tamarind margarita, food, and friendly service. Would recommend!
$20 for an hour and a half of bouncers flashing their high beams in my face? Not worth it. Plus the rest of the staff weren't friendly or all that helpful either. Good luck getting a drink

Came here for lunch during the Urbana conference (triennial Christian missions conference held at the Dome / America Center) because it was walking distance from the center.

Full disclosure I probably didn't experience the restaurant at its best because of the huge crowd due to proximity to the venue - so I would say this review is what would happen if there was a large conference at the America Center and people had 1-2 hours for lunch.

Vibes: Not a huge space, probably could fit around 40-50 people, I can see how it would be a really good date place at night, with the bar and nice seating/ decor.

Not the best place for large parties (one large party of 15-20 occupied a long middle table and basically was like the center of the whole restaurant).

Understaffed to handle the big rush, but probably enough staff on a usual weekday lunch/night. Simple menu with cheaper lunch prices which I appreciated, I got the pad see ew with tofu for about $15 including tax & tip.

The food maybe took 20+ mins to come out but I think it was because they were busy doing take out orders as well. Reasons for giving this a 3: despite being really busy, my friend asked for water twice and eventually just walked up to the bar area to ask someone to give him a glass. Also another friend ordered the penang chicken curry and it didn't come with any vegetables, which I thought was weird (it was only chicken and rice). 

Overall though I think my pad see ew was alright, and spice rack is provided on the table. The restaurant was probably unprepared to handle a big rush - I would say it's a solid date place for 2 people but maybe not for large groups.

Big pro though they were down to split our checks into 4.

Went to Pin Chasers last night with friends to work off our dinner. We arrived a little after 10 and chose the 2 hour deal for $ 11 (not including shoes). We could have chosen to bowl until 2 am for $15, but I don't think we would have made it that long.

We were given a lane almost on the far side of the building, but the walk was good for us! We arrived to find ao  birthday party had taken over the area.The manager offered us another lane, but the party cleared out quickly and soon we were up and playing. Our touch screen did not work, so no silly names could be entered,. We were just 1-4. 

Around 11, the lights went out and day-glo and strobes began. Music videos were showing on the screens that  came from the ceiling. Very strange mix of music videos- Yin Yang Twins to Toby Keith, Shakira and a few others. Odd, but still fun. 

We did a lot of people watching while there as well. There were a lot of kids there, I mean under 12 kids there past 11.   It is a great place for kids before 10, but really think that after 11, it should be for older kids and adults. But, who am I to judge.

I never give 5 stars as only wife is  perfect to me ... HOWEVER, what a game changer from the old Tamarack. Food was fantastic, timing was excellent, service from our waiter Mark .... professional and on point. Even saw another waiter named Michael going above and beyond to build quick rapport and trust with his tables.  Ambiance is night and day under the new ownership. Adam and Kimberly have done a phenomenal job of turning this neighborhood steak house into an absolute must for all in Northern Nevada.

I absolutely loved the vibe and decor here! Vintage/hipster.  And the food is amazeballs!!  Practically everything is gluten free.  I got the chopped salad and it was probably the best salad I've ever had. Next time I'll get a burger because the rest of my family got different burgers and they all looked sooooo good.  We were all very satisfied. Yum.

Beautiful venue. Parking nearby is easy. 

Ordered a pulled pork sandwich and french fries for $15 plus a coke for $3.  Pork was room temperature and pretty bland.  The herb butter fries were tasty, but they had been cooked earlier in the day and keep warm. A little dry and old.

My mouth is watering thinking about what to write for this review. I'll make this short and sweet:

Pizza-- AMAZING. They grab their ingredients locally at the Farmer's Market and they happened to have a pizza special with Bartlett Pears, Smoked Gouda, Red Onion, Garlic, Mozzarella, and creme sauce. Soooo soo good! The crust was crispy yet indulgently soft where it needed to be. The creme sauce... omg A1!

Fried Chicken and Coconut Gritz-- Chicken itself was really juicy and tender, breading was crispy n flakey, Coconut grits were outta this world. The honey sauce really complimented the chicken and sweetness of grits.

+ Service was great. The servers were very nice and welcoming. Would definitely come back here again, no questions asked!

When the Pacers Bikeshare launched downtown, I thought it was a good idea but never really thought that I would use it. A few months went by and a friend who had it started using it more and more, so I went ahead and paid the annual fee and joined.

Why the change of mind? Convenience. I can walk somewhere and then grab a bikeshare home, or I can leave my car where it is and run an errand. I don't have to worry about finding a place to park and I also don't have to worry about looking for a place to lock my bike up.

The bikes are well maintained and although some of the racks can fill up on busy nights, they do a fairly decent job of distributing them all over the place. It's easy to see how many bikes are available when you're in search of one, and it's also fairly easy to see how many docks are available when you're ready to return it. No reserving though... it's first come, first serve.

Ordered a taco with chili releno and guac salad. I received a very cold taco with a plate of half beans and half melted cheese. No chili releno in sight. I've been here before and it was ok but this is bad and I doubt I'll be back.

BPV offers the full package. Great place to watch games and have lunch while also the hottest spot for nightlife in STL. The best views of Busch and the coldest beer in town! I really enjoy the food and atmosphere.

not only was the hostess rude upon my arrival...the tables where put so close together. we sat in the back at a round table with hardly any room. one of the people in my party was smashed up against the service station and her side of the table kept flopping down. They fixed it...sort of...and then when we asked from the beginning to comp us the ONE appetizer (for a table of 6) because we had tickets to the theatre, they didn't do so when we got the bill. When you pay as much as  you do for over priced mediocre food, you expect better service. I won't be spending my money there again.

I had to switch rooms, twice, in 2 days due to ants. On my second switch, I was offered popcorn and a movie. I would not recommend this hotel, at all. I woke up with ants on me, in the bed, on the nightstand, and in the bathroom.  If I wasn't staying for a conference at that hotel, I would have left! I feel that since I was polite they disregarded my complaint and the inconvenience this caused me.

Mona Lisa's is a cute Italian place on the far end of the quarter towards esplanade. The atmosphere here is great, they never have you waiting longer then you can stand, and the food is consistently delightful. I really like to have a nice quiet spot that even when full of people is quiet enough that you don't have to shout. Also helps that they only have four or five front of house people so if your a local and go more then once, odds are you'll get a nice smile and a "welcome back" nod.

Just got ripped off at D2. Eighth of Huxton Green Crack $45! Price was too high for sure. But what really pisses me off is that it was harvested 8/31/2020. As of today that's four plus months ago WTF! I called and complained and just like I figured they couldn't care less. That's it no more D2 for me. Prices too high but more than that their flower is terrible. And now I know why. It's OLD!

The deck is such an amazing atmosphere in nice weather but the bar staff is awful. They need to hire new bartenders or train them better. I watched one of them spray wd-40 all over the door to the cooler to make it open easier which got all over the contents of the cooler of course. MAJOR health code violation and just disgusting and lazy... just clean it properly. I also ordered an aperol spritz which they made with club soda instead of Prosecco. I'll probably go back for the environment but I'll be cautious about what I order.

The seafood bisque was packed with seafood and piping hot.  The chicken parmigiana is always a good choice, which my husband throughly enjoyed.  I had the broiled scallops, tender and tasty.  Way more scallops then I had expected.  Great dinner and diner.

Amazing staff that are gems to work with. Scheduling is very easy and the wait is never bad. I have a very timid doodle that comes for grooming and the staff has been great to make him feel comfortable and pamper him. Very pleased with Doogie Doos!

Loved the food, the service left much to be desired.

Came here with the wifey for lunch the other week.  My wife works close to Squealers, and loves to rub it in my face how often she goes.  Finally, I had the opportunity to come check this place out.

AMBIENCE- not fancy by any means, but would you really want to go to a super fancy bbq joint?  The whole place is decked out in wood, and the walls are covered with a variety of blues paraphanelia.  Booths and tables a plenty, this place was packed on Friday for lunch.

SERVICE- Our server was just plain AWFUL.  After we were seated, our server was no where to be found for a good 5-10 minutes.  The hostess ended up taking our order, and another server brought it out to us.  The only time we actually saw "our server" was when we were paying the bill, and I had to ask her to please hurry, because we were in a rush to get back to work.  Other people seated in our area seemed to be irritated with her slow service as well.   My wife has claimed that every other time she has been here, the service has been good, which is part of the reason I'm not putting them on full blast right now.  

MENU- typical bbq joint food.  Ribs, pulled pork, brisket, rib tips, sides, etc...  They did have a potato that was topped with pulled pork, and pulled pork nachos which looked pretty awesome as I stared at another table that ordered them.  They also have a different special every day of the week.  On Friday, it's a pulled pork sandwich with chips.  

FOOD- though it took a while to get to us, the food was pretty damn tasty.  My wife and I both ended up getting the pulled pork, and we were not disappointed.  I got mine "wet" with hot bbq sauce, and she got hers dry with a side of regular.  The sandwich was a good size, and at a fair price.  I wanted to get a basket of fries to split between my wife and I, but they only had loaded fries, or just a side.  Perhaps on another visit.

OVERALL- poor service aside, I really did enjoy the food.  On a subsequent visit, I'm sure I'll change my rating of this place (as long as I don't get the same server), but take out wouldn't be bad either.  Good bbq at a fair price, way to go Indy.

Celebrity Rating- Deidrich Bader

This place is awesome. Ive always heard great things about it and finally made my way over last night. I ordered the chicken tacos which were delicious. The tacos come on two soft shells that are cooked perfectly. My chicken was shredded and tender and full of flavor, i would definitely order it again! but i also really want to try a burrito from here... My friend got the special which was pulled pork tacos and my other friend ordered the steak huaraches. Both said their meals were great! The restaurant its very small but the atmosphere makes up for it. We got there around 7 for dinner and shortly after we sat down the place began filling up with what appeared to be regulars. Since it was our first time we werent sure to go to the counter and order or what, but soon after the nicest guy came out from behind the counter to get our drink orders and help us out. Can't wait to come back here!

Honestly idk what the hell happened. I went for a 2 o'clock appointment and started getting tattooed by 3pm because the owner was maybe high or so full of himself that he couldn't stop talking about conspiracy theories and his personal life. I was uncomfortable from the start. I did my best to play along for as long as I could but as a woman experiencing a weird manic extrovert I just shut down and let him ink. I wish I stood up for myself. I wish I asked why he was tattooing me freehand... I wish I had gone anywhere else. I had to go to a very expensive cover up artist to undo what he did to my hand. My first tattoo was a botched rejection of a tattoo and it has been an embarrassment for me for two years. I hope he has been shut down since Covid. Go anywhere else.
Charged me for an order they messed up. I went back to get a replacement without sugar and instead the woman in the drive-thru said, "Welcome back that'll be $3.31."

Well I was a little disappointed. The wait was very long and we were the only ones waiting lol. The food was ok. I ordered the pork adobe and garlic rice. My daughter ordered the pancit. She did not enjoy it. Not returning sorry

I bought a Groupon online. I arrived at the salon and it is beautiful. I met with Marissa who would be my stylist. I've had horrible experiences recently with stylist. Marissa eased my mind. We talked through what I wanted and she proceeded with full highlights. I love my hair! This place is a hidden gem. Yes I made another appt. to return. Buy the Groupon if still available.

2nd try today...  and it was both better and worse.

I ordered a Brisket sandwich (wet.. or very fat)... and my girlfriend ordered a Brisket sandwich (dry, or very lean). That shouldn't confuse anyone right? lol! Girlfriend got her lean Brisket sandwich, no problem! Me? I got a Pulled Pork sandwich! lol! 

I went the pickup counter and let them know it was not what I ordered... and the guy in back fixed me right up... Brisket sandwich (wet, extra fat). It was not as fat as the sandwiches I get elsewhere... or when I smoke my own brisket. But it was pretty good. I don't think they use a rub or salt the meat or anything, as it is pretty bland. Nice smoke ring, and a decent smokey flavor... but still a little bland. 

Girlfriend said her dry (lean) sandwich was actually dry, as in needing a bit more moisture (which is why I don't order them that way) :) But she ate it.

Sauces are kind of ho-hum too.... the spicy is more tart / vinegary than spicy... the sweet is mostly just sweet, and the hickory sauce is probably the best. A mix of all three seems the best way to go, skewed to preference. I did not try the mustard sauce.

I had the hushpuppy side dish, and they were pretty and crisp on the outside, and mushy and flavorless on the inside. Girlfriend had fries... and they were pretty good.

Service was good... place smells great, is clean and comfortable. I just wish the barbecue was better. Maybe I will try the ribs next time...

This business is not worthy of my money and doesn't deserve any stars. The customer service skills of the black girl (puppet) and Hispanic was below average. My husband and I asked a question and the black girl rolled her eyes and head, looked us up and down, and spoke with an unprofessional tone. She enjoyed being disrespectful and her behavior was welcomed by the other workers / owners. Mind you, we've never been to this store before and wanted to give it a try. We don't know anyone that works there either so the behavior of the workers was unprofessional and unnecessary. We complained to the Hispanic managers / owners and nothing was done. No person, regardless of race, ethnicity, age, class, gender, etc., should be treated indifferently because we don't look like the owners or the residents of that particular community. As far as I understand, U.S. Currency will never lose value no matter where you shop. We will never shop at a store that doesn't have a register and doesn't provide an itemized receipt. First impressions are a lasting one. Give me your money for bad service is bad business and a poor reflection of the owners. Now I understand why everything is priced so cheaply.

My husband and I came late on a Thursday night after an Ascend show. The elevator to the rooftop is in the very back left corner, FYI. It's a small rooftop but I found the staff friendly and attentive. They had a wide variety of cocktails and beers. I enjoyed my cocktail, the views and the friendly staff. The hotel itself looks beautiful.

Ugh. My run-ins with terrible service at CnP is really starting to get to me. At the location in S. Philly, my GF and I received almost no service while the table next to us were tended to like celebrities on a slow Sunday. Hardly anyone was there, but we felt as if we were left out of the party. Fuck you S Philly Chickie's, suck a nut.

And now at the NE place, a few friends had checked in on a busy Friday night waiting to be seated. We witnessed our buddy walk up to the counter and asked for a table. We proceeded to wait for the table to be called for 45 minutes. Forty-fucking-five minutes. All the while, people were being seated that we knew were not there nearly as long as we were. So, we ask. 'Oh, you never told us everyone was here.' 

FFFFFFFUUUUUUUUUUUU

I was ready to grab a bottle of piss [insert any light beer] and throw it at the bitch. We did confirm, in actuality, we did it twice. All for what? Some yummy bar food and beer? No. No more Chickie's for me. Ignore me while being served? Fine, I'll take my food slightly cooled, and leave a shit tip for lack of service. Switch out your blue-cheese dressing to something that tastes like mayo with itty-bitty chunks of mold? Whatevs, I'll bring my own. Completely remove me from the equation and leave me standing in that crowded, festering entrance like an asshole who's really, really hungry for almost an hour? Last straw, I'll traverse to Tony Lukes for my fatty greasy grub.

edit: I wrote this review weeks after the event. I guess I was more pissed than anticipated, because goddamn was that a rant.

Very overpriced for what you are getting. I was not impressed with the shrimp or the chicken. They should offer a sampler meal to atleast get people to want to try new foods. The inside decor is ok, the service was nice, but nothing was even closely impressive for me wanting to return.

If you have a larger party than 5 people you might as well avoid this place. We came specifically for the all you can eat crab legs and we were all pretty dissapointed right away.  When we got sat at our table it took a couple minutes for the servers to even notice us, and when they did they proceeded to argue within ear shot of our table on who was going to be taking care of us. Once we all ordered what we wanted and the crab legs came out we were all happy, until we wanted more. We all sat with EMPTY plates for about 30 minutes while we watched one of the servers bring out fresh crab legs to the same tables every time. after MULTIPLE bowls of crab legs that never made it to our table, the guys at the table in front of us told the server we needed crab legs. That's not something that they should be reminded of. During this time our server completely avoided our table so we couldn't even ask her for more crab legs. Long story short if you're looking to have good service for all you can eat, I suggest going somewhere else. I was completely dissapointed.

Forget looking anywhere else for an amazing hamburger-- MB has become our favorite. For between $9 and $14 you get a seriously gourmet burger and a side of perfectly seasoned fries (or try the sweet potato)!

I got the Montecito and loved every bite (griddled goat cheese/bourbon glazed mushrooms/house onion ring/truffle aioli/arugula/grilled onions). Other favorites are the Funk Zone (sunny side up fried egg/applewood bacon/bourbon glazed mushrooms/bbq sauce/cheddar cheese/roasted tomato) and the Downtown (blue cheese/grilled onions/fried jalapeños/sriracha yogurt sauce).

If you're craving a great burger, want to escape the tourist crowds and hit a spot created by and favored by locals, Mesa Burger is it. 

Added plus: surf-themed interior with friendly service and indoor heaters to keep it toasty!

Staff is very nice and friendly. Assistant office manager Heather was awesome. Spent more time than she had to trying to find the best solutions to my issue. Made me feel very welcomed. Office very clean. There is a jar of cheese balls on the counter for us to guess how many. I think there are 213.
I was very happy with my mani/pedi. The place is very clean, everyone is extremely friendly, and surprisingly, it wasn't crowded at all! I am going back tomorrow for my next manicure!

I was starving and needed to get food and fast. Not wanting to cook after working a 9 hour shift, I decided to stop in here after running a few errands. The man at the counter was not very pleasant at all. He just kept saying "yes.. yes.. yes.." and then while I was ordering, he took a phone order and rang somebody out (...umm.. rude much?!?!)

I ordered the basic combo platter: chicken with broccoli and garlic sauce on the side with fried rice and a portion of steamed dumplings. I was told ten minutes. Ok.. 6 minutes go by and the food comes out. A little fast.. no? I paid ($19 AND SOME CHANGE... WHAT THE WHAT?!?!), checked the bag and while doing so, the guy kept going "THANK youuuuuu... THANK youuuuuuu"... I asked for more sauce (because they are totally skimpy on the sauce, which, I might add is in these little cups with lids and are so watered down. YUCK!!) and drove home.

The food: someone else in their review said they threw it away. I should have as well. This food is GOD awful. And when I say GOD awful, I mean GOOOOOOOOOD awful. The steamed dumplings-- how can you get steamed dumplings wrong? Well, this place does. They were doughy and tough. Tasted like they were bunt (as in freezer variety). I had to put them in a pan and sautee them a little more to get them warm and finished. The whole purpose of buying chinese food was so I didn't have to cook. *groans* The chicken and broccoli -- the chicken tasted funky, like as if the chicken had been sitting way too long. The broccoli had a plastic taste. The rice was far overcooked just by looking at the strands -- it shouldn't look like it's been beaten to death. The egg roll was also doughy and tough to chew on. I received no fried noodles, no sauce and two crummy tasting fortune cookies. Man.. skimp much for the price I paid!?!? 

Do yourself a favor and go to China Inn on Dekalb next to Dekalb Produce. That's where I'm heading now to get an actual DECENT chinese dinner!!!!

Their costumer service is the worst. I had a terrible experience with the Groupon. I purchased and called before it expired to make an appointment, they were booked until after it expired.How convenient right? I asked if it mattered that I was calling in advance? They said "no"! One of the girls actually had the nerve to say that that is a rule listed in the fine print. IT'S NOT! 

  I was going to give it another shot I scheduled an appointment and got quoted a price I was told I could use the groupon for the actual amount I paid. When I came in they said they quoted me a higher price and asked if I wanted the service anyway? I of course said no because I was completely done with them!

  I would prefer to a go to a place that doesn't offer groupon and pay more for quality costumer service. Groupons are meant to generate more business for places in need not make people want to go elsewhere.

Just got takeout. Place was locked down. Only a window. Had to knock. It was a little weird. 

2 orders of pad Thai and 2 egg rolls. 

Got home and it just looked wrong. Chicken was very white. It tasted like boiled chicken. Not good. 

The noodles tasted off. Wife and I didn't want food poisoning so we trashed the food. 

Tucson does not have a very good food scene. It's hard to mesh up Thai street food but I should have known given the "fusion" 

4.5 stars is usually good but....

Maybe it's a Covid thing. Who knows.

Wasn't impressed with the service, for being totally empty it took a while for someone to even welcome me and see what I needed. The older gentleman that helped me wasn't the friendliest and I had to remind him of the soda I had ordered. The pizza ended up being quite greasy, the bottom pulled away from the top, so after the first bite you were getting more topping than crust. Next time I think I'll order from the golf links location, they seem to care about customer service and a quality product.

Visited here while in Philly and was instantly overwhelmed by the sheer size of the offerings of this market. There were so many places to visit yet so little time! My strategy was to walk through the entire market first and then decide on what I wanted to eat. 

I gaped at desserts that were strategically placed at eye level to entice customers, lobsters chilling in their tanks, trinkets and colorful jewelry, and pickled pickles and meats. And these were all in addition to the fresh fruits, vegetables, and meats that were on sale. 

Not realizing all of the available food options here, I had made dinner plans so I opted for some ice cream. I will say though that the Philly cheese steak (with bacon) that was prepared for my colleague looked fantastic! Should I visit again, I'll be sure to grab one. 

I wished there were more samples but settled for some delicious banana pudding. I found a map of the market in the middle of the market and found it to be useful so I'm sharing in here.

Quite a lovely spot to visit when in the area. I'll be sure to pop in again for that cheese steak....
We had the Mild Chicken Tikka Masala, the Goan Shrimp Curry and the Saag Aur Paneer. All of them were good!

I went in to buy a wifi router for my first apartment, knowing nothing about how to set one up. I asked for help to see what my best options were. The sales associate told me he would get me their best guy to help me. He told me I was all set and had what I needed to get my wifi going. I got home to realize I needed an ethernet cord to start it up. Irritated that I had to go back, I went and bought the cord. When I got home it still wasn't connecting because I still needed a modem.. (how was I suppose to know that..) I was so irritated with their awful customer service by that point I just returned it all and went and bought a router and modem from elsewhere. Never going back to that Best Buy again!!

I was really hoping that we would finally get a decent restaurant in Pleasant View.  No such luck. Sidelines Grill is pretty much a nondescript sports bar, with TVs on the walls and very little else for even an attempt at decor.  I didn't go into the bar, so perhaps it's a bit better (?). 

On to the food and service, which is why we go to restaurants in the first place, right? Sadly, the decor was the best part of the experience.  Service was painfully slow, as if the wait staff had never had even as much as an hour of training. When the food finally arrived it was even more disappointing than the slow service. I tried a cheeseburger, and when it arrived it looked like it was made from what looked like a frozen patty thrown on the grill. It was enough to make we wish we'd gone to Wendy's instead. And the fries were cold by the time they arrived. Yuck. Couldn't eat it. 

My wife was unfortunate enough to order the catfish. I didn't want to risk tasting it, so I'll have to take her word for it: "Bad". Her vocabulary is much more polite than mine, and "G" rated. I could have found a more colorful descriptive word to use, but since this is a family web site I'll hold my tongue. 

Sometimes you have to give a restaurant, especially one that just opened up, the benefit of the doubt. I'll give 'em another try in six months (if they're still in business), and report back.

Great food that got to us really fast! Their cream sodas are made on sight and so delicious. I would recommend the cream sickle ice cream float. Lovely atmosphere, outdoor garden, friendly staff. Great place for any time or occasion. Large craft beer selection. I'll definitely be coming here next time I'm in town!

I bought a Groupon for Kada for $39 partial highlight, cut, and blowout.  I didn't expect much being that it was quite inexpensive but I was very pleasantly surprised.  I had Janine as my stylist.  I was in and out within one hour.  That was a plus for me.  I don't really enjoy sitting for hours in a hair salon.  

Janine was quick and precise with the highlights.  She did an amazing job with the blow out.  I went out for dinner with my family for my birthday that night and everyone complimented my hair.  Then people continued to compliment the hair based on pictures on Facebook.  It was kind of funny because people don't normally make that many comments on my hair...ever.  

I'd say the only reason for the lack of star is that it was not a glamourous atmosphere.  I don't know what the prices there are normally but that may reflect in lower prices than some fancier salons.  Which is worth it to me if the result is the same!  

If you buy the Groupon, I would confidently recommend Janine if you are looking for a straight blow out.

This salon is located in the Drexel Line shopping center.

This place specializes in Sichuan cuisine, but that doesn't mean you shouldn't come here if you can't handle a lot of spice. The food here is quite mild and light in peppercorns, but it's probably the best of the Chinese restaurants in the area; you may need to ask for dishes extra spicy if you're used to Sichuan food. Of the dishes I've tried, I think they do vegetables pretty well, and I also really liked the happy fish and the cold shredded chicken with chili sauce app.

Complimentary pickled vegetables - good

Happy fish (沸腾鱼, $19.99) - really great!! The fish itself is kind of mushy but I was still super happy with this; the wood ear, bean sprouts, and bamboo shoots at the bottom are great too. It's fairly mild though - I asked for it extra spicy and while the heat was good, it was still only barely numbing. (My friend thought this was too spicy though.)

Shredded chicken with chili sauce (口水鸡, $8.95) - great, lots of sesame paste. Not very spicy.

Sautéed snow pea tips ($18.15) - really great, probably my favorite version of this in the area.

Sautéed Chinese squash home style ($14.95) - great, still firm and not overcooked or greasy.

Eggplant with garlic sauce ($10.95) - great though a bit heavy in sauce. The eggplants are sweet and tender and delicious.

Sautéed bitter melon ($10.99) - great, a little oily but still nice and bitter and not sopping with sauce!

Chongqing spicy crispy chicken ($14.95) - good. Really thick breading and not very spicy, sort of reminiscent of popcorn chicken.

Ma po tofu with minced pork ($10.95) - tofu is nice and soft, but it's not very spicy and pretty greasy. There are a lot of fermented black beans. I liked this more than the shui zhu yu though.

Braised fish fillet in hot chili oil (水煮鱼片, $18.95) - fish has some type of heavy coating/thick sauce, surprisingly salty, not spicy unless you get a bite with peppercorn. Vegetables on the bottom soak up a lot of chili oil and are spicy though. Really different from what I expected; I'm not a fan. It also came out really really quickly - I wouldn't be surprised if it were precooked and reheated.

Twice cooked sliced pork ($14.95) - this is mostly scallions with relatively little pork, which is very fatty. It's pretty greasy, not spicy, and very very salty.

Tea smoked duck ($18.95) - not good, super salty, meat wasn't great :(

Service is attentive and friendly, and our water glasses were always full. There's also free wifi (password is on the back of the door). I'm slightly concerned by the number of flies buzzing around our table though - this and the noodle that appeared in the pea tips don't make me optimistic about the kitchen. Nonetheless a solid 4/5 for central Jersey.

Good place. Similar atmosphere as brawleys. 

The bacon tomato cheese omelette and hashbrowns were delish! 

My mom enjoyed the chicken sandwhich.

We ordered our pie to go, the insides were hopping! Obviously a popular place to go, which was why I was a little disappointed with our pizza. We got the So-Cal (all ingredients i LOVE, so was crazily anticipating getting my hands on it) the drive home was torture, it smelled so good. Unfortunately, when we dove in...well it was just "okay". I was expecting an explosion of flavor! The toppings were good, but not mind blowing. The biggest disappointment was the crust. It was bland. Again, I wanted to fall in love with this place. My love for pizza is something of an obsession. 

Now with that said, I'm not counting this place out just yet. I'd be willing to give another shot, maybe we caught them on a bad day. Until then Jackamo...

Sub Zero's ice cream is quite delicious and refreshing if you know how to pair your ice cream flavor correctly to the toppings. Luckily they have preset combinations already, that way you don't have to think too hard on it. The experience seeing them make the ice cream in process was insane; felt like I was seeing a dog machine making ice cream.

The reason why I gave them a 3 star is because we found a short piece of hair in our ice cream and also there was a lot of small little flies flying around the facility which really grossed me out. 

The customer service was nice and helpful and parking is not free, heads up!

Okay, so i had my verbal temper tantrum about them refusing my friend service because she forgot her wallet, and now I can go back to trumpeting my original love for the Pol.

5 Things I love about the Irish Pol:

1. It's frequently, and unbelievably, NOT crowded, so your group can sit and talk and laugh all night.
2. If you don't have friends, or they are boring, the signs can keep you entertained all night.
3. 38 (?) beers on tap! go ahead, i dare you-- try and drink them all :)
4. Midnight Happy Hours, awesome specials and overall CHEAPness.
5. It's down the street from the meat markety-ness that is often National Mechanics and that is always Madriver, so it's very, very easy to dip over when your friends are "shopping" and you're just not in the mood.

p.s. don't forget your ID.
I've been coming here since it opened. I just got pho for dinner tonight and the broth had way too much msg. I hope you can read this review take my critique into consideration, I'd like to go back.
Beans and rice were ok. But the sandwich itself is a must! Liked it a lot! Definitely recommend!

Great soul food without the greasiness. Catfish was fresh, juicy, tender with a light crisp batter flash fried to perfection. No oil dripping from the fillet... Not over cooked ...the ribs were awesome.. Slow cooked, tasty, tender, falling off the bone delicious... Just enough bbcue sauce - did not over burden the taste of marinated meat... Very well prepared.... Red beans and rice... Good, just a tad too salty for me... Collards were good too. Would have preferred them a little less cooked - I like my collars still slightly crunchy, but I realize not everyone does.. I did appreciate that the greens were not salty. People tend to over season greens.. Not here. They weren't bland either.... Just enough seasoning to know it's there.  Fried chicken- awesome... Just like the catfish, the batter was light & crispy, leaving the chicken juicy & tender.  And let me not forget the service! Excellent, friendly staff..  Armand was our waiter. He was extremely patient and entertaining, and wasn't the least bit fazed by my boyfriend who can be extremely challenging to wait on! Way to go Armand! Go niners!

I purchased both security cameras and a mini recorder from Privacy. The recorder, while expensive, works great.  I have had a couple issues with the cameras, once was user error and another was because they stapled through the wires and water was able to get in ruining the wiring.  They charge something like $75 for a trip fee to repair which I think is a bit excessive so I now use a local company for any issues.

Food was good but the service was pretty poor.  I was alone so sat at the bar. Asked for my drink twice, side salad came but the silverware didn't arrive for another 5 minutes.   The gentlemen on the left and right of me received their entree and paid before mine arrived - no explanation.  Won't be back

I purchased a Groupon for this place. I also appreciate and support local businesses, but with that, I expect hot food and good service. Dachshunds fell short on both of these expectations. I will give it another shot, just because I understand they had just opened a new business and working out the kinks. But for now, I will wait.
Great food, great service....please support Nicks.  Every time we order it's a fabulous meals.
This is a wonderful Indian restaurant in Manchester. The vegetarian menu is extensive.

As much as I enjoyed the food and the creative menu, I will not be returning to dine at Feast. I was there with 9 other friends to celebrate a birthday. I did not make the arrangements, but the staff seemed to accommodate us with no trouble (we were there late afternoon between lunch and dinner rush on a Thursday). Service was decent, not the best, not the worst, but generally friendly. A couple of us arrived and ordered late but they were able to bring out everyone's food together, which was noticed and appreciated. Lots of inventive and tasty options on the menu, I split goat-cheese stuffed squash blossoms and a ragu of wild mushrooms and fava beans with someone else at the table. A little pricey though, but for quality food, I don't mind paying. HOWEVER, when the checks were delivered, everyone at the table was charged 20% gratuity (fine for a large group) and a $3 PER PERSON CAKE PLATING FEE. Now let me be clear, we brought in cupcakes. The restaurant was notified in advance, and apparently informed the event organizer there would be a fee. But there was no cutting, and there was no "plating". They brought a stack of plates, and we served ourselves. To be perfectly honest, I found it to be outrageous, and I said as much. I have never been asked to pay a fee for bringing a cake/cupcakes to a restaurant, not even at a 5-star steakhouse. And certainly not $3 PER PERSON when most wedding venues charge $2.50 per person to actually cut and serve a cake. This was basically a "You didn't order our desserts so we're punishing you" penalty, and it left a horrid taste in my mouth. Bad, bad, bad, bad customer relations move. You may have gained $30 from the table, but you lost my repeat business for sure.

Sadly the person who ordered these horrific flowers may not have read  any reviews.  The arrangement was less than satisfactory and customer service is/was HORRIBLE.  The so called manager took zero interest in resolving my dissatisfaction although I called and sent photos with a half hour of receiving.  Clearly not interested in customer retention either!

Very disappointed coming here for my birthday to find out that the menu they have online is not the menu at the restaurant it's called bait and switch 
For the prices they should accommodate the customer they lost a few hundred dollars on two people who were there to celebrate my birthday we would've ordered four entrées alone and not one of them was being offered on their new menu.  
They are a bunch of dirty rats no pun intended
Kc Prime here we come

This is my first experience eating here. I stopped in for a quick lunch, and was greeted right away!

I didn't really have time to look at the menu but I ordered something I like which was the Italian sandwich, to see what their take would be on the sandwich.

Initially I thought the serving size was a little small for the money but it was really a good sandwich!

My only advice to them is do something about the table tops, mine was very sticky.

Basically a hotel lobby restaurant with an uninspired menu of basically fried everything. 
Tourists of course but you probably will not see many locals. 

Walk a block up and enjoy a little more of what NOLA is about. And pay half the price. 

But if your a visitor, enjoy!

There are a few self designated sacred spots in a city that you love to haunt and this is one of mine in Nawlins. Unfortunately, the staffs lack of service as two p.m., aka, their closing time neared. 
The gentleman who sat us did so with a scowl and a throwing of a chair towards the near by wall, so I my son could be placed in a high chair; and no that is no exaggeration. I was not only startled but was surprised that the table near by didn't say anything, as we are seated in close quarters. 

A young black butterfly, flitted around between outlets two tables, taking orders, gathering beverages and delivering plates of Trolley Favorites. 

My catfish po-boy was almost bare bones. There wasn't even a drizzle of the Crystal mayonnaise that was promised on the Menu and my sons chicken tenders were delivered sans the honey mustard, and once requested I was told that they didn't have any and asked if I'd like ketchup...as someone who delights in the various ways sauces can titillate the tastebuds, I am well accustomed to what ketchup could do but was anxiously awaiting the variety of their honey mustard. Maybe next trip. 

So, I was taking photos and wanted to see the menu, when the Tasmanian devil struck again grabbing up my menu and snorting some reply when I asked for it back. 

If I tell you we were in New Orleans for just about 30 hours and this is one of the places I wanted to make sure we made it to, you hopefully understand my disdain for the lack of hospitality to someone who didn't come in begging but actually chose your restaurant to come to, dine in, and pay for a meal...meaning, that you are taking my money from my server when you don't want to assist with serving...maybe someone needs to look into that. 

"No Roger. No Re-Run. No rent!"
"No service. No hospitality. No tip!"

I came here for brunch today for my first visit and I have to say I was not at all impressed.  I ordered the crab cake eggs benedict and it was lacking for sure.  The english muffins were stale and hard and the there was not enough hollandaise sauce.  Moreover, the eggs were just basic fried eggs and were not the cooked in the way where the yolk is in the right semi-runny consistency.   You can spot the taste in an eggs benedict by how pleased you are with the appearance of it on arrival.   I can say that I was not impressed and my pre-taste assessment did not disappoint.  The mussels we ordered were and were HUGE.  I had never seen mussels so big and the sauce and bread they came with were fantastic.  

On a side note, this place serves pho.  Not sure why a white-owned venue would serve a Vietnamese dish along with seafood and regular bar food but I would recommend you not order it.  No, I did not taste it but having eaten pho from Asian restaurants I have no doubt you will be disappointed.  And they overcharge.

Great place to meet up with old friends.

Very casual atmosphere. Limited selection of beers, which is expected. We had a great time sitting and chatting for hours.

Portabello fries were good to munch on.

Nice beach restaurant.   Thank you for the  $2 beer drafts!  Food was fresh and excellent.   We got the fish spread and the blackened grouper.  Tartar sauce was runny which is the only thing I would change.   Our companion got fried oysters.   Looked yummy.   Music outside added a nice touch.  Staff seemed stressed and not sure why more people don't work here.   Pretty busy so I'm sure tips are good.  We will definitely be back.   Keep up the good work.

Best gyros in town! I love Greek good and especially gyros and this place is great! The customer service is excellent and the portions are huge!!! Very clean and friendly restaurant. Definitely recommend!
Good beer recommendations by Server Chris- very knowledgeable! Beer cheese best I've had

I love the Whiskey Kitchen but there is always a wait. It is kind of annoying that they don't take reservations but it does make it so that the restaurant always looks packed; which is obviously good for their business. I don't recommend going there if you are a highschooler..they will look at you like you have three eyes. Other than that, try going for lunch on a Tuesday because it is just amazing. THERE IS ALSO PARKING!

Warm and delicious neighborhood pizza. Their personal sized pies are ideal to split between a pair trying to sample more than one. Curbside service was fast and right on time. Cant wait to try the rest of the menu!

forget ted drewes! silky's is much much. it didn't leave me feeling bloated like other creamy custardy desserts would. 
it's a very small shop on the corner of a strip mall.. not much seating here but that didn't matter. no waiting in line, fast service and loads of parking.

I just love the office and the doctor, but bring back the old medical staff! They were so on point about calling you back after leaving voicemails, these newbies can't find time in their day! Called 3 times and still no answer about medication questions :(

This is 1 of 2 sushi places I will eat in Boise.  The all you can eat is a great value. The salmon and yellow tail nigiri is excellent!  It's not too much of a formal setting so it is comfortable for any time you decided to go.
 I would have given more stars, but our server wasn't the best.  She didn't understand English very well and got confused by our questions and requests.  But she was very nice and tried hard.

I give my experience 3 stars  due to a couple of factors. First, the Wiz Fries needed to be cooked in fresh oil, the fries tasted a bit rancid. Second, the Wiz Kid steak was delicious and flavorful, however it was very light on the "steak", with just a thin layer of filling rendering the sandwich mostly bread. For $9 Wiz Kid needs to step it up and put more "steak" on the bread. 

The Philly fries were great, they were covered with wiz, broccoli rabe and long hots so I could not taste the rancid oil on these. I could tell by tasting the plain fries I ordered for my kids that the oil was not fresh.

 Overall great flavors, but the chefs, management or whoever is in charge needs to provide fair portions, and change the cooking oil through out the day. No shortcuts on quality.
The fajitas come on a regular plate.  The steak looks like what you'd get from a supermarket freezer section if you were buying philly cheese steak meat.

Great pizza!

I first had Paulie's at the South Philly Review's Pizza Olympics. Their General Tso's Pizza was unique and delicious! We have since ordered delivery. We had some sort of Cheeseburger Pizza--- pretty much Big Mac pizza with ground beef, pickles, lettuce, cheese and special sauce. They skimped a little on the beef, next time I will definitely ask for extra, but it was another unique pizza idea that turned out to be great!

Give this place a try-- they have a lot of other gourmet pizzas that sound amazing, I can't wait to try more!

I was working nearby and decided to walk across the street for lunch.  The service was fast and the waitress was nice.   I had a gyro,  and it was ok.   It has kalamata olives on it,  and cucumber sliced like an apple as well.   Way to many toppings for me personally and the flavor wasnt all that great.   I probably wouldn't go back to be honest
They let me make an appointment knowing they don't take appointments so when I showed up trying to get my car her spay the told me it's feral only! I paid 25$ to set my spot.

Unable to try this place I had half an hour to kill before our reservations nearby so thought I'd stop in and try some coffee and a treat.  They can only allow one " group" at a time in and one lady was inside waiting for her order for 20 minutes which prevented anyone else from entering the shop. I finally gave up as my reservation time came up and  just left giving my spot to the next person waiting outside to order. Highly recommend that you ask people to wait outside for their orders once they've placed the order vs stalling incoming patrons.. ‍
Good is always amazing. Always get the Pick Two. Always fresh food. Bread bowls and pastries are Amazing. 5 stars.

One of the best places in Nashville! Went for brunch and had a fried egg sandwich and chicken salad. Both were great! Drinks and service were good. Neat atmosphere. I don't eat oysters, but they looked good too. We sat at the bar, and service was fast. Seats were also available on patio too. Wait on table was about average.

This is my favorite restaurant in St. Louis. It used to be that I didn't even know what sous-vide was. Now, when I meet women at cocktail parties, after they ask me what my FICO score is, I ask them if they've ever had pork sous-vide, to which they typically reply, "This is a cashmere sweater." If you're ever in St. Louis, eat here. Try the pork sous-vide. The beet salad is better than most. And the truffle tater tots are ridiculous. I love you guys! Keep on making the world a better place!!!
Great atmosphere, great service, friendly and personable owner/Chef! Lovely meals. Highly recommended!

Yes, it's run-down looking and located next to an "adult" store, but plain and simple this place is the best. I always go with a simple broasted chicken and fries meal. This place is a real throwback to no frills dining. You can rest assured that you're not paying for any extra perks here....just a trip to flavor country. Long live Speck's!

The lot can be accessed from either Ridge Pike or Germantown Pike, as the location is essentially right where the two roads meet

I have been seeing Shilpa for over four years now and she is great! I will continue to see her indefinitely! She does an excellent job with my brows and for waxing too.

The front desk staff (Joy?) could use a little training in customer service and common sense.

I arrive around 4:55 for a 5:00 p.m. appointment. There are two girls sitting in the office. Shilpa comes from the back at 5:02 after finishing a client and inquires who's next. The woman at the desk says "you have two walk-ins who have been waiting" and they are invited back. 

I turn to her and say firmly, but politely it is 5:02, I have a 5:00 appointment - I do not appreciate having to wait for walk-ins. This woman proceeds to argue with me that they were "here first" I say yes, but I have an appointment. Doesn't appointment trump walk-in? She insists they were here first, and I ask "why bother making an appointment if you have to wait behind people who walk-in." 

Instead of taking a second to think about it, she's defensive and says 'If you had come in the same time, you would have went next." WTF? How does that make sense? 

The girl overhears and offers that I go next, I refuse. Shilpa offers to take me next, I refuse, because this girl had already went to the room, but explain to Ms. Clueless and Rude at the front desk that going forward, I do not intend to wait behind walk-ins when I have an appointment and I am on time, regardless of "who came in first."

I've honestly never had a more ridiculous, lacking in common sense conversation.

Being new to Boise looking for a personal grooming location proved to be one of the hardest things we needed to do. We have tried 4 SportClip locations, all were great but this location on Overland Rd was outstanding. The automated check-in and the speed of service are perfect. We have waited less than 5 minutes for a stylist, the cut was clean and exactly like I asked for. Then the wash and vibrating chair in teh wash nearly put me to sleep I was so relaxed.

I highly recomend this location to anyone wanting a haircut that is over the top.

Food here is healthy AND delicious!! Wait staff is always friendly, I definitely love coming here when I'm in Reno. Today I had the Paleo Breakfast Bowl and my husband had the Vegan hash. For drinks I had the Azteca and fresh OJ. Our waitress Lindsey was so awesome and so helpful with helpful helping us decide from so many great options on their menu!

I'll start off as positive as I can - MY food wasn't bad.  Onion rings were possibly the best I have had anywhere.  The thing is, it's dirty and the waitress was TERRIBLE.  I worked in restaurants long enough to know that if the windows are dirty and there are stains on the walls, the kitchen is NOT going to be clean.  My girlfriend ordere her burger medium and it was rare - borderline raw in fact.  It was so undercooked that the bun was red with blood before it was even bitten into.  When sending it back, the waitress told my girlfriend "that's how we do medium here".  I was shocked.  A restaurant having uniqueness and doing things uncommon is sometimes great, but the temperature of cooked meat is the same everywhere!  Like, what was she talking about.  Will not return, especially with Red Robin right there and with much better and cleaner food.

On four separate occasions, today included, I've arrived to find doors locked at the advertised open time. I called when I couldn't get in. No answer. Watched the staff unlock the door 7 minutes late and once inside was greeted with, "You look like you're in a bad mood". I replied, "I don't understand why the doors aren't unlocked on time" to which she answered, "they were unlocked...you don't have to be rude."
Really tasty soup, super friendly staff, what's not to love?

The Oysters were just okay. Honestly wish it was a bit more seasoned. I love acme oysters so this is what I compare these oysters too. I gave it two stars because the service was great it's just the food that I would not recommend.

Holy shit, this is good BBQ.
