12 bucks for a rack of ribs with baked beans, collard greens and a side of pulled pork.  This was the highlight of my trip to Nashville.  The best BBQ I have every had.  The ribs were fresh, tender as hell and not overly drenched in sauce.  Transcendental ... orgasm inducing ...

The set up is cool.  There's a long line that stretches outside and you are served in a buffet style line.  So there's no need to worry about bad or delayed service.

It is easy to see how Jack has earned his reputation.  

If you go to Nashville or anywhere nearby, then go to Jacks.  End of story.
Nice bar area. Smaller parking lot. Clean place. Came here last Saturday for Happy Hour from 1:00 - 3:00, $5 sushi and plates. Was a good deal and food was yummy. No complaints.

Most of the time when I order here the food is good. One time I went inside ( never again) the floors were sticky , it smelled like urine , and a homeless man was in there ( also the door is locked and you have to wait until they push a buzzer to let you in... must be a really bad area) I had no problem buying the homeless man a burger , but he made the whole place smell like piss and chips  .... he should not of been inside... it wasn't a cold winter day either.... Most of the time I ate my food in the car , the drive thru can be slow at times, sometimes it's a long wait if you order more than 1 .... 1! Order... it can take a while. All my food has been hot and fresh tasting but because of my paranoia I check every food item before I devour it. Haven't found any little buggy nasties or hairs yet. I would recommend this place, just not if you're in a rush rush .

Overpriced subpar food. We had the nachos with queso and it was not anything special. I had the fish and chips. Jalapeño tartar sauce was good as well as the breeding for the fish. Fries were basically super salty potato chips. Dessert was carrot cake which was decent as well. But then the check came and it was hilarious. Definitely not worth that for subpar food.

Alcohol & ice cream......yes please! This is the best after dinner and your in the mood for dessert and drinks type a place. Great for a girls night out for sure!

I love that the concept of a adult ice cream shop and Beans & Barlour is just that and on top of that they have coffee because you're not an adult if you don't drink coffee...Right!? 

When you walk in it's kinda like you stepped into a music video with the old school ice cream parlour/ bar atmosphere. There are giant gummy bears hanging from the ceiling and fun chandeliers and giant candy decals. 

All the ice cream is homemade. You have the option to make your dessert of choice with or without alcohol. 

What we got-

Tiramisu Float- this very good. If you like a STRONG espresso taste on your lady fingers this is for you. 

Girl Scout Samoa Milkshake- this is a specialty item. I don't think this is available all the time. This was so tasty! The only thing is you get a lot of coconut flakes when you're drinking it.

Creme Brûlée Cappuccino- I like my coffee sweetened so once you break the sugar at the top I highly recommend stirring the sugar in the cappuccino. It sweetens it perfectly otherwise the cappuccino is a little bitter.

Most disappointing.  Very overprices.  Calzone and plain spaghetti came to over
$30.00.  Spaghetti was not bad, could have used some parmesan which was not offered.  Calzone just not good
Had the new lighter version of lemon thyme chicken for lunch. Food was not as expected. Taste was off, tool salty, cold, etc ... Nevertheless service was great. Server was very attentive.

Unfortunately, I accidentally threw my receipt away, but I was at this Denny's yesterday around 2 in the afternoon.  I had a craving for the pot roast that I used to get a few years ago.  I will never go there again.  From the handle on the front door, which was sticky and filthy, I should have known to turn around and leave.  The place has deteriorated so badly and every aspect of it screams "dirty."  The floors, the tables,the condiments, and the silverware (which I had to "wash" with my water and a napkin), were disgusting.  The food, the pot roast that once used to be so delicious and appealing, was a mound of mush drowned in onions and mushrooms.  Though there is a sign at the hostess desk re: putting your phone on silent mode, the restaurant phone rang throughout the entire time I was there.  I did not finish my meal and left and Purelled my hands.  The waitress was nice, but the hostess took her good time to seat me.  This Denny's used to be so nice.  It's a shame.

It really doesn't get better than Marcello's. I've been to New Orleans several times and have been disappointed with too many dinners so our dinner at Marcello's a few nights ago was a breath of fresh air! Excellent ambiance, service, and food. Our hostess was friendly, our waitress was perfectly attentive, we got to walk around and pick our own bottle of wine off the racks, and the food was fantastic - make sure to try the cannolis! A+ all around!

This pizza is fairly priced and i have not been dissapointed yet. Better than pizza hut, little ceasers and papa johns in my opinion.  Their calzones are huge and  enough for two and really tasty, just get the extra sauce on the side. I mistakingly thought it came with and it didn't.  Also my kids LOVE their pepperoni pizza and the Mediterranean pizza is pretty darn good as well.... sorry i would have a picture but we ate as soon as it came..  not to mention the delivery driver is super awesome and friendly and funny with the kids when they want to help carry stuff in.  He made them feel special and helpful where others may have been annoyed... happh to have found them. Thanks blackjack!
With the new daily specials would like to get red lobster every now and then for a treat get some lobster and shrimp stuff like that

Mar was my savoir on my wedding day.  To say we had extreme circumstances was an understatement.  I was unfortunately pretty ill on my wedding day and had spent the morning in the ER getting an IV with multiple bags of fluids.  I should have taken before and after photos!  Honestly, nobody would have even know that I had spent the morning of my wedding at the ER - Mar and her team turned me into my most beautiful self.  The TEAM helped keep everyone calm and collected throughout the wedding day and I am truly thankful that Mar and her team were there.  I highly recommend Mar!  She is honestly the best!  XO

Awesome, awesome, awesome!  My friends and I had been wanting to try pole dancing for some time.  We found Rock n Body online and decided to give it a try.  Needless, to say we had a blast.  The class was fun and exciting.  We will definitely be going back for more!

It is an outdoor mall and could use some shaded areas - seriously, metal chairs in 106 degree heat with no overhang covering? So know what stores you want to hit when you go and park near them. That being said, I scored HUGE bargains on a Tuesday afternoon - at Sax 5th, Kate Spade, Avenue, and Dress Barn. The Avenue store was larger than any I have seen, with tons of clothes. The only rude place was Brooks Brothers. Went in to look and they didn't even say hello or ask if they could help. I know I was dressed super casual and looked a hot mess - it was 105 degrees- but I am an attorney and could afford their clothes. Anyway, this was about the mall.

Not very impressed with the food or, candidly, the service. The waiter was nice enough, but with very limited menu knowledge. We had the mozzarella and tomato salad to start, which was innocuous and bland. The warm ricotta and bread was decent, but hardly memorable. My girlfriend's grilled fish was also ok,  but the chicken on my pasta was dry and tasteless. What saved this from a one star review was the tagliatelle pasta, which was fresh and well-prepared. That said, Bella's down the road has pasta that's just as good at much more moderate price.

****UPDATE**** There is a new group of greeters, staff and servers, I'm so glad!! We were greeted and seated promptly although the restaurant was totally packed. Everyone one was friendly and professional. We really enjoyed the beautiful decor and 'Marina' view. The Stingers Fries are absolutely delicious I highly recommend you try them and the cheesesteak rolls. Although the description for the fries may scare you off as far as the sodium content don't be afraid. The fries were served with a very nice balance of ingredients the old bay was lightly tossed through the fries. Best of all?? Danielle our server who was friendly, energetic and professional. Great job to Stingers Waterfront it appears you addressed the service issue!

Let me say when I started my wedding planning back in June of 2019 for originally a May 2020 wedding that turned into October 2020, never in my head was the idea of how close I would get to Beth! Seriously Beth and I had to only meet each other a couple times throughout the wedding process but the day of my wedding it was a whole other level! Beth, my wedding couldn't have been what it was without you making my vision come true! It was absolutely perfect! And even though Chris hit your golf cart with his golf cart and then got a beer for it!  you are truly a person that I won't just let go out of my life that easy! You helped me try to bustle my dress!  we dealt with a power outage  but all in all it was the wedding I wanted and I thank you from the bottom of my heart for everything! 
Side note! I got plenty of my items from the store and will be donating some of my items back there soon!

Took my Mother out to dinner tonight and waited almost 75 minutes to get our food while we watched three tables that were seated well after us and ordered well after us receive their food. After demanding to speak to the manager we waited over 15 mins until I made a second and stronger demand to speak to a manger. After another 10 mins one finally appeared with no explanation but a simple apology. At that point no apology was acceptable!! This is my second bad experience at this location in the last two times I have attempted to dine here. Needless to say there won't be a third attempt!

Quaint little Boba shop with a small food menu. They have alot of flavors to choose from and make great teas. I had the chicken fried rice and spring rolls. Everything was fresh tasting and delicious. I ordered a pineapple and a honeydew milk tea with lychee popping boba and they both were amazing. I'd you are looking for a good boba tea and a quick bite to eat at a great price this is your spot.

Been going to this Idaho Pizza for years now for the lunch buffet. I go at least once a week sometimes twice and always meet 1 or 2 others there to eat with. It is usually pretty good but the quality and really gone downhill. They cook the pizza too far ahead of time then keep them in a warmer, not good for the pizza at all. I really noticed when we went to the new Idaho Pizza in Nampa near the car lots. SO GOOD! Pizza is cooked fresh and put on the buffet line right from the oven. They also have dipping sauce for the bread sticks, smart. All Idaho Pizza locations need to take notes from this one. I work in Meridian most of the time so I will be taking the drive to Nampa to this Idaho Pizza from now on.

A great restaurant if you like Italian and wine. They have a huge wine selection. My meal and that of my dining partner was fantastic. The service was also excellent and very knowledgable regarding the wine selection. It gets a big thumbs up.

Great customer service. I walk out feeling confident that my totally bad-ass, hot rod of a Toyota Camry received top-notch care. The price is a little steeper, but when it comes to a two-ton vehicle that you depend on with your life almost every single day, I'm willing to pay for quality and peace of mind. Call ahead for appointments (...though they did squeeze me in for a quick oil change/check-up before a long roadtrip... AND they were speedy about it.)

Amazing. In a city full of delicious holes-in-the-wall? Where each individual bar or restaurant has more character than an entire strip mall does back home? This place STILL stands out.

Keep in mind it's a dive. It's casual, quick, even brusque if they're busy. But the canjun-inspired food? Exquisite. Best cajun pasta I've had. If they have specials (ask!) try them. Really. 

Classic drinks? Classically executed and cheaper than many places in town. Try a Sazerac.

I was very happy to see they survived Covid and Hurricane Ida-- it was just as good in 2021 as it was in 2019. And get this, the same staff. My bartender has been there for 10 years, & she's not the only familiar face. 
You F&B industry people know how unusual that is, and how much it builds up a business to have experienced folks sticking around.

My only complaint -- wish they served an hour or two later. They close early by NOLA standards, at 11pm, and closed entirely two days a week.

There's a courtyard out back, a multi-sided bar, and a bunch of tables. Come hungry!

One star is generous. Filthy. Smelly. Way overpriced. 
The hallways felt as if there was a heater on, well over 100 degrees. 
Dirt piled up on the carpet in the corner of the room.
Just plain BAD. Teriyaki chicken was like chewing on an old shoe. Ginger salad dressing tasted like peanut butter. Miso soup was cloudy and tasted like rotten fish.

Located in the Gaylord Hotel, this is a standard restaurant.  I ordered the sushi sampler and the quality was good.  The sushi was fresh and the presentation was solid.  Others in my party ordered the crab cake sandwich which came with fresh cut fries.  Th crabcake looked to be more filler than crab in my opinion.  

Breakfast buffet is eh and coffee is not included which goes against the idea of a buffet.  With Marriott now owning Gaylord, I would expect things like this to eventually change.

The redesign of lower level where this restaurant is was done post flood and they did a nice job.

My husband and I checked this place out because of all the reviews. Cool atmosphere and really friendly service but the food was only okay. Wasn't super impressed by the burger and it was $11 and their pulled pork was not that great for a BBQ place. For a "diner" atmosphere, food was too expensive.

The food is really good, but definitely pricey. We went for the Sunday Brunch. Miss Jubilee and the Yas Yas Boys were playing, which made the whole experience worth it. 
Our waitress was MIA for most of our meal, and it took forever to get our check. We were seated really quickly, though, which was nice. We will definitely go back, just to see Miss Jubilee.

last time i will EVER GO HERE  was up here past weekend .. 1. asked if we can be helped  gave order ,. then asked if they can help us .. { this took 10 mintues !} then didnt  understand us ?!? had to give order AGAIN !?  then told us to go to 1st window , gave the order AGAIN ! "oh is that what you wanted " then was talking in front of us making snide remarks of my daughter in laws mother ..as we paid , the lady , mention she was sorry ...{ then laugh!}  my daughter in law mention ,. its not polite to talk bout their guest in front / or behind their backs .. 2 , get the ORDER RIGHT .. ORDERED A SAUSAGE ON ENGLISH MUFFIN NOT BACON EGG ON A MCDONALDS ROLL ...if we wasnt in a hurry , would of taken the order into the place & tell them get the order RIGHT .. JUST Horrible service ... NEVER AGAIN ..

This place may be good at night time but in the day time is horrible.
Nothing to see and the streets are dirty.

Stores are small and honestly not much to do in this tourist place.

I don't know why people recommended to visit this place.  

Personally I didn't like it at all.

I am officially addicted!  Wow,  I ordered the chicken and waffles country style and mild.  Also ordered one extra piece of chicken that was the size of a Turkey leg! It was so good. Lots of chicken and not a lot of flour. The chicken was fresh and the waffles were delicious.  Ask for extra crazy d sauce. Our family fought over the last of it!  New favorite chicken place. Don't forget to grab a a peach tea drink, so refreshing.  Next time I stop by, I'm going to try the mango habanero to see if I can handle it.

Went here on a weekday and the place was completely packed. I got a little table in a corner but it was so loud, it was hard to hear anything. The food however was very good. Personally, I have had better mole but if you like sweeter mole, then this is your place.

This is just a store that sells Legos. I was sadly hoping for something more. Activities, building shops, etc. It's a store not an amusement place. Their prices don't seem any better than you would find any other place. What you are going to find here is selection. Lots of kits you won't find any other place than online. The other perk is seeing all the assembled Lego kits.

About 20 years ago I lived here for a month, it's not a hotel, it's an extended stay motel for prostitutes and addicts. Back then they charged 25 bucks a night and an extra 5 bucks if a friend came for a visit. I'll admit it saved my ass when I was in between apartments but the quality and the kind of people who reside here are not for tourists. These days it's too expensive to have an extended stay but the quality hasn't changed according to the reviews. So for all you outta towners? I recommend you keep it movin...

I just experienced the best customer service of the year here. 

I received really terrible news earlier in the day and the last thing I wanted to do was run errands, especially looking for a rare battery. After driving around for over an hour I decided to call Advance and the GM, Mike picked up. He immediately found the battery and offered to hold it for me. 

When I stopped in, another cashier quickly assisted and got the battery. There was some confusion with the price; Mike heard and saw my demeanor going south very fast. He offered his sympathy by jumping in and getting me out the door with a great price and a small but very helpful part I didn't even know I needed for the battery. By the time we were done I was smiling and incredibly grateful for his ability to pick up on a customers uneasiness. That's one of the marks of an amazing manager. Thank you Mike!!!

Work luncheon at its finest, ok service and food. Had a great time with my team and the atmosphere. Food was tasty and their are a ridiculous amount of flavor options for the wings, but it was very difficulty to find a commination tat was gluten free and sauce free. For the cost, I was still hungry and after the lunch I ended up getting another snack to make it through the day. I assume people getting wings drink, and so their more likely to spend the money on the wings, but when you don't drink and actually count the wings, it becomes very expensive very quick. The last challenge I had was the service. When you are serving a larger group, I would assume you would give some attention to their drinks. I ran out of tea fast, and was served my meal with an empty glass on the table. Being their over an hour and having 1 glass of tea doesn't work for me, and I assume the rest of my table felt the same. Maybe next time it will b better, otherwise we might be looking for a different venue to host our work lunches.

I came for breakfast last weekend based on a recommendation from a friend. How awesome is this space?!

We sat in the back garden which was very serene on a crisp Saturday morning. I love mornings outside so I'm sure I'll be back often to this spot.

Service was excellent.

Bathroom situation: there's one downstairs which is unisex. I had one good experience and then one surprise in the toilet when I went in (not the restaurant's fault) BUT ladies, just go upstairs. At the top of the stairs is a women's only bathroom with 2 stalls (2 I think but definitely not 1). It's much faster and you'll be out of the way of the busy hallway where the bathroom is situated.

I ordered the hash and eggs after being torn between that and vegetable omelette with brie on top. I'll go back for the omelette next time. Food was solid and everyone enjoyed their meals.

Great for a unique breakfast experience.

If this market really were a boutique then the prices would be okay. But it's just another grocery store carrying the same goods and brands as First Food or Spruce Market. 

The selection is limited by their size, and I accept that. The produce has varied for me, sometimes it is excellent and fresh, and sometimes it is okay. The fuit is often perfectly almost-ripe, so I know when I buy a peach I'll be able to eat it the next day or two. The veggies tend to be very ripe, though not as local as I'd like (tomatoes from Mexico when New Jersey is just across the river, for example). Prices on herbs went up tremendously recently, and that has been a big disappointment.

The staff are all very nice and the manager, Tito, is always extremely helpful. 

This market is only worth it for extremely convenience, any real shopping or shopping for standard goods like soap or cereal should be done elsewhere.

went twice because I worked down the street. Kinda overpriced and the woman who took my order seemed friendly at first but had a short fuse after I asked a bunch of questions. It seems cute and cozy but after looking around I could tell there was dust and dirt. I won't be going back since the staff seems impatient and irritable.

FIX YOUR AIR CONDITIONER!!!!!

This is Nashville and Summertime.  Employees told us that management has known that it is not working but have resisted repairing it.  It is miserable.

Seriously?

I don't know why people like here! It's the worst korean restaurant I have been to. Everything here is not fresh. I am not picky, the food here sucks, compared with traditional korean restaurant in Los Angeles.

Pretty good burgers. Haven't tried the custard yet. The burgers were well-seasoned and cooked properly. The fries are the smaller thin ones, which were nice and crunchy. We didn't have to wait long in line nor wait long for our food, even though the place was packed. Definitely recommend trying this place.

This place is literally a hole in the wall. Driving by, it wouldn't catch your eye. I am so glad someone recommended this place to us. 

Only difficult thing is parking. Its right off the circle so only options are street parking, pay $10 for one of the close lots, or pay ~$3 for mall parking (just hope they aren't charging for event parking).

This place is a bit on the pricey side but so worth the experience. Expect to pay about $25 a person for just your main dish. 

We came here for mothers day. They take reservations, although we didn't need them because the place wasn't full. 
My mom and I each ordered 1 pound of crab legs. It comes in a bag with an ear of corn and a couple of small potatoes. You have to pay extra if you want more corn or potatoes. I like that you get to choose your seasoning to be mixed with the butter so it gives it lots of flavor.

They provide you with a plastic bib and gloves so you don't get all messy. This isn't a place for first dates lol unless you want butter dripping down your face.

Due to it being in the basement, you have to walk down/up several stairs, so this isn't a place for the elderly or anyone who has troubles walking.

I work in E. St. Louis. I'm sure you can imagine I don't do a lot of errand running in that neighborhood so on a Thursday afternoon when I decided I needed some silver polish for a work project I thought hey, why not run over to Culinaria as it's only 9 blocks over the bridge? So I did. They have an abbreviated selection of everything a Schnucks would have at Culinaria but they did have the silver polish, score! I got it and picked up a few cookies for work and my round trip was about 25 minutes out of work. Yay me! More street parking would be nice!

Tried to order at the take-out window.  I was ignored by every employee that I saw, except one that told me that someone would be with me in "two seconds".  That may be code for something, but no one waited on me.  I went to Jet's Pizza instead.  I like Sonny's, but someone there needs to figure out some service issues it appears.

I really wanted to love this place, I REALLY did. But I sadly do not. I DO love the decor and vibe. However, I was not a fan of the brunch experience. My mother and fiancé  came in on Saturday for brunch early in the morning, and I was expecting we'd get sat right away since it seemed empty. There were many tables left indoors, but yet we were told we could only eat in the lounge area. We ended up eating breakfast on a couch and coffee table.

Anyways, we got to the food part. I ordered the chicken and biscuits, my mom ordered the waffles, and my fiancé ordered the tacos. Out of everything the tacos were most tasty. The waffles and biscuits were cold. The chicken lacked taste.

Food and coffee was expensive. Service wasn't warm and friendly. I may try again for drinks with friends. If so I'll update this review.

Let's talk about the breakfast sandwich. I tried the Prosciutto, egg, and cheese and it was absolutely scrumptious. I enjoyed the new take on this staple. The sandwich had just a bit of heat from the herb spread and crispiness from the fried pancetta. It makes me want to come back and try the other morning options. The mocha was well made as well.  


Just a heads up in case you're planning to work while you grab a bite and morning coffee.  There is no wifi, so make sure your phone is fully charged because you will need to use a hotspot.

This seems like a good place to go to for frozen foods, snack foods and non-food items, but it does not suit my needs very well. I have had issues with most of the spices I've purchased here tasting old. This is not an issue when I purchase spices of the same brand elsewhere.

Also, I recently rented a DVD here, but I won't be doing so in the future. The film I rented was of such poor quality that it wasn't even worth watching. I'm quite positive the DVD was a bootleg; I saw the movie in theaters and know that the film is exquisitely produced. I was very disappointed.

The owners are polite and friendly, but I don't quite trust this store when it comes to buying ingredients for home-cooked meals.

I'm still shaking my head about this visit... Here's a little back story- my sister and I went one night because she had a Groupon that was going to expire soon. She'd been here once before and said it was a really good place and that I would enjoy it (I, of course, had never been there before.) 

So, we get there, we see that it was going to be 30-45 mins for a table (there was a huge convention group there at the same time as we were there.) So, we grabbed a table in the bar area. The waitress comes over, we order our drinks and immediately place out order for the spinach artichoke dip appetizer. We get our drinks and go about recapping our days. 20 minutes pass and we start wondering where our appetizer was. The waitress comes over with a refill on our drinks and explains that the appetizer didn't look up to their standards so they're remaking it and the manager would be over soon to explain. Two seconds later the manager was there explaining that someone left it at the window too long, it didn't look good and they didn't feel comfortable serving it to us so they're remaking it and they were taking it off the bill for us too. We thanked the manager, then countered with, "that was very thoughtful and we appreciate it, but she (my sis) is hypoglycemic and needs to eat soon, so when will it be out?" The manager understood and not five minutes later out appetizer was out. We placed our food orders (I think we did this when the waitress came back with out refills, but I can't remember correctly.) I ordered the tuna burger and salad with sweet potato fries and my sis ordered salmon with rice. Soon after our appetizer came out, we got our food as well. I grabbed a fry, only to find out it was almost soggy, in fact all of the fries were and they didn't taste good either. I pushed them away and decided to eat my burger and salad. I lifted the bun only to did that my tuna burger "patty" was a hunk of tuna one quarter the size of the bun. I was disappointed at the huge difference in bun to burger ratio. So, we called the waitress, she came over, I asked her if this was normal and she replied "they weigh the tuna steaks into four ounce portions, sometimes they don't fit the buns" to which I replied, "I understand about the weighing, but the fact that this is one fourth the size of my bun is absurd. Couldn't they cut it in half and make it fit better or something?" The manager came over a few seconds later and explained they were comping our entire meal and they were sorry that our experience was so bad. My sister and I thanked them and apologized (as if it was our fault there were so many problems. My sis and I don't like to cause problems and we hate sending food back, but this night just seemed to be a bad one.) 

The reasons I gave it two stars is because of: 
1. My sister swears it's a good place and that night was just abnormally bad. (I've come to trust her judgments) 
2. Although it was an abysmal night, the customer service was on point. 
3. We still have the Groupon that expires soon, so we just might be going back soon to try again.

We went to the soft opening at Luna Grill and were very pleasantly surprised!!! This place is a great mix between casual and fancy. The food was delicious! And the staff was friendly.

I tried the chicken and gyros plate as well as the gyro wrap. THE SAUCE WAS MY FAVORITE. Soooo good. Chicken was well seasoned. But the gyros were definitely the best. 

I will absolutely be visiting here again... if for no other reason than baklava ;)

7 days ago, paid $30 for gel manicure. It started to chip the following Wednesday. I went in today to ask about repairing it and Tony the tech who did the work did not apologize for the bad manicure and try to make it right, but instead he inferred somehow I caused this to happen by stating that that lots of people coming in after the holiday with chips and then he grinned about it. I requested either the repair to be made or my money back. The manager sat in her chair and did nothing. This is my first Yelp review and it saddens me that it is negative. The customer's experience should be the first priority. This nail salon has alot to learn about business and customer service. I will never go back. Should have waited for someone at VN Nails.

This place straddles between average to below average. As someone who actually lives in Hong Kong, don't expect this to actually taste like Hong Kong dessert. As another ding to the rating, Hong Kongers read Traditional Chinese, so I'm not sure why the décor are some simplified and some traditional. This is just confusing and reflects poorly on whoever manages this store.

Aside from the confusing language debacle, the service is also mediocre as the cashier pays more attention to her phone than the customer standing at the counter waiting to place an order. Once another girl caught a glimpse of my zombified stare, she nudged the other cashier who said, "Oh, I didn't know you're ready." Yes, please make excuses and blame me for your lack of competency.

For the price you pay averaging $6 a cup, I would expect more than sweetened milk with ice with a bit of fruit toppings, but that is exactly what I got. There is the very fibrous mango that tastes sour topped with store bought whipped cream, the rest of the cup is filled with milk slush that is reminiscent of milkshake's step cousin on a diet. It's watery enough so you'll have to struggle scooping the watery content onto a very shallow plastic spoon to put in your mouth, but not so watery that you can drink it down (you'll choke on the ice like an idiot).

Overall, I'd find somewhere else for dessert.

JCP is the same no matter which store, town or sate you are in to me what makes the difference is  the employees. I went in this store to find a bathing suit (uuuggghh ladies you know this is not your favorite thing to shop for and is dreaded every time) the sales lady made this experience a little better by being so helpful and really friendly. I like this store it is easy access from the parking lot and conveniently located off sr 60 in West-field Mall. I will be back to this store. They seem to have the latest fashion and home goods.
Great place, very clean. Love Christine and Paul, the gel many stay for 3 weeks without chipping. I am definitely coming back! But beware of Sophie!

Doesn't even deserve one star. Where is the manager?! Owner?! Anything. ABSOLUTELY DISGUSTING. I was scared to touch anything. The guy was nice but damn get a hold of your employees. I can only imagine what their homes look like.

Welcome to Moe's!!  I can imagine how annoying that can get!

The portion here is HUGE so I don't have many complaints.

Called in an order to take brunch for my mom. Unfortunately the manager was on vacation, which seemed to leave chaos in the kitchen, and when I got to my mom's place, the sandwiches were literally empty inside! Whitefish salad ordered for both, but none on either one. See photos.

The manager made good on it the next week. She personally made me two huge sandwiches (we could've shared one) and didn't charge for either one. They also had credited my card for the empty sandwiches received a week earlier. So that manager fixed a negative situation.

On the plus side, the whitefish salad is kick ass, and their bagels are light and delicious. I also like that they put toppings on both sides of the bagel, not just on the top. This is definitely my go to place for picking up a bagel when I go to see my mom!
Donuts taste it okay but they were just way too big and way too fancy for my taste I just like plain old plain Donuts. They need to clean the tables in the floors in there

Great place and great ice cream!!! Huge selection too
Come and visit when around Opryland

Just returned from my day in the park. I have not been to Busch Gardens for at least 20 years.
(I have vacationed yearly with my family at Walt Disney world.)  As I had a few days in Tampa I thought I would check out Busch gardens for the day. 
Few thoughts
We ordered tickets online and glad we did for the cost savings.  I Thought admission was on the high end as many kiosks and snack venues were closed and a very limited amount of entertainment available.
Staff-- at the animal exhibits the animal keepers were awesome.  At the tiger exhibit - we had a great experience with Jody. She was so interesting and we learned a great deal .
In the parks most staff appeared less than happy to be there and did not engage the guests. Many were not able to answer questions regarding how to get to a particular attraction. We took the train ride around the park and were less than impressed with our conductor.  Guests on the train did not appreciate the "humor" and would have far more appreciated a narrative of the animals we were seeing.  Election jokes were definitely a taboo topic based on yesterday's results.

I recall seeing more entertainers and a far more exciting atmosphere.  ..... I know it has been many years but overall I was disappointed  - and my family that joined (first timers)were even more so as our day did not live up to their expectations

My wife and I had dinner at Brian's on Friday night. It's a three course fixed price menu, ala carte and associated prices are not offered unless asked for. The wait staff was efficient and relatively knowledgeable about the items on the menu. 

The meal started off with a delicious roll that tasted like a croissant. For our appetizers, my wife chose the Sea Scallops with Cauliflower, Wild Mushrooms and Oregon White Truffle Oil ($10 supplement). I chose the Lemon Crab Risotto. 

The scallops were seared to perfection without with being over cooked on the inside and the mushroom medley complemented it perfectly. The risotto was a wonderfully creamy lemony concoction made with long grain rice, not arborio, containing an ample amount of lump crab. The dish would have been adequate as an entree.

For the entree, my wife chose the signature Jumbo Lump Crab Cake and I the Sea Bass. Neither one of us asked for the ingredients in the crab cake, which had an abundance of red bell pepper that we found to overpower the dish completely. The sea bass was seasoned and sauteed nicely. Both dishes were  served with over cooked snow peas and carrots.

The desserts we chose were Bananas Foster and Chocolate Pot de Creme, both very good.

Overall the meal was a bit pricey but good.
No communication and One bus running for an entire airport! The employee was unable to stop for her restroom break with an overloaded bus, this is Unacceptable!!!

Weeeellllll.... not a fan of final menu.  Out of wings on  Tuesday at 6pm. Not good. Had to rethink meal.  Ordered  Blackened chicken tacos no bueno.  Salsa was yucky at best. That was comped which was greatly appreciated.  Chicken and funnel cake was good.  Best was happy hour beer prices and good service.  I really hope the kitchen gets their act together.  Understand they are trying to go gastro pubish.  Not sure if it will succeed.  
Will try one more time in a few weeks and see how it is progressing.

Everything that I ordered was mediocre. The best thing was a side item, the garlic mashed potato. It was mashed into a purée like it was mentioned. 

The risotto was kind of salty and it wasn't mushy enough to my liking. 

The lobster mac n cheese was nothing special. There was barely any lobster meat, or if any at all. I would also like to suggest on using a different type of pasta instead. Penne/tube-like pasta just didn't taste right. It could not hold the sauce, "meat", and cheese together. 

The porterhouse steak was medium rare, but I find it kind of dried on certain slices. Bigger steak doesn't mean it will be better. I should have just gone with the smaller cut. 

Honestly, for how much I've spent, I would rather go to Doris or another steakhouse.

Edit: So I ate the leftover Mac n cheese and found some pieces of lobsters. Probably still wouldn't go back though.

Had to stay just 'cuz of the name. Built in the early 1950's and hasn't been refurbished since. Nice lady checks you in. First come first served. Cash only. $10 key deposit. Try to imagine it before you go in. Tube TV. Late night drama. Restaurants by the dozen nearby. Lives up to it's name.

I love most Fox Concept restaurants, and really want to love this one as well. And who knows, maybe once they get the kinks out I will. For now, the wait is ridiculous, though once you're in the service is good. One entree (meatloaf) underwhelmed, and the other (fried chicken) was served burnt. We complained, and received a discount, but really--who plates and then serves burnt fried chicken? At their prices, attention to detail should be paramount. My drink--La Vida Verde--was spectacular.

FALSE ADVERTISING!!! Tried to purchase a new $1200 bike for my son today and use the 20% coupon. Came up as "not valid for purchase." Coupon came from Pull magazine as a thank you for them being in business for 30 years. I called and spoke with the Manager Bill and was told there are exclusions with the coupon. Apparently this coupon is not good toward ANY complete bike. The coupon clearly says "20% off regular priced item over $100." He also said they wouldn't honor it because they are offering free shipping and you can't use 2 discounts at once. Nowhere online or on the coupon are exclusions listed. Bill called the owner and was told by him the coupon would not be honored. If bikes are excluded from their coupon this should be clearly visible to the buyer. I was really disappointed with the lack of customer service and willingness to make this right. I was able to purchase the bike from Dan's Comp and received free shipping and a discount on the bike. Your loss J&R.

I bought 6 bags of biscuit mix.  Now I know this mix is nothing more than corn starch and flour., maybe some salt and preservatives, could even have a bit of corn flour.  I haven't read the package ingredients.  HOWEVER, these are pricey at $5.99 each and then there's always a tax.  I spent $39 for these 6 bags and then asked if I could have one lousy 1.5 inch in diameter biscuit to remind me of how great the biscuits are.  The snotty barmaid just gave me a flat no.  I reminded her that I'd just spent nearly $40 and could have bought a couple of sandwiches instead and I would be given a small basket of warm biscuits.  She didn't care and she was not going to budge.  Well it will be the last time I buy anything at Jim n Nicks.  I've also had a case of their biscuit mix shipped to me.  Won't happen again.  My relationship with this restaurant is over. I hope they replace this nasty barmaid and I'm not the only yelper that has made comments about the service.

Possible the worst experience I've ever had eating out. I work in the food industry so I'm pretty understanding, however this was absolutely chaotic. I took my brothers here and two of the burger which were not hard to make (one was plain and the other one was no tomato and no mustard) pretty simple. We got the order the first time that it had everything on both of them. Now usually I'd take off everything and move on but they had smeared mayo and ketchup all over the bun and it was already sunk into the bread and since I did say plain (and they rang it up as a plain bacon burger) I did go back and tell them. They remade the order and yet again the second burger had tomato and mustard literally the only two things we asked not to be on there. Then the man proceeded to ask for the messed up ones so they aren't "extorted" um sir it's covid season and you can see the clear PLAIN on the receipt and see how thick these are they aren't right. He also said they were going to "fix them up" which is against code and during these times you're bringing in food back into the workplace that has already been touched by at least two other people is seriously disgusting. Will definitely never go back there again, sorry to whoever orders the next Sonic cheeseburger bc  they're probably gonna give you a germ filled messed up burger. So incredibly disappointed. If this is how all other Sonics in the area correct their mistakes I will surely never go to sonic again. What a joke.

This is a great place to grab lunch.  Pizza is always fresh and delicious, salads are nice too.  You can get one of their awesome specials (2 slices and a drink, 1 slice with soup or salad) for under $10.  They are always nice and service is great.
Great steak.  Loved the mashed potatoes.  Very laid back.  Awesome service.   Will have dinner here again!
Someone needs to tell those corporate a holes that they need to take down the 24 hour sign in front of the building I was just there earlier this morning to find out you guys are closed

Delishes!! My dad and I were running out of options when trying to find a brunch place. We finally were able to find the sun and it was delicious. My dad got an egg sandwich on an everything bagel and he loved it and I got an egg white omelette bacon on the side, the bacon was absolutely amazing. We will definitely be back soon!

Came here for some hanger racks and was supper happy that they had a few to choose from.  Did not take a closer look through the store because it was an in and out kind of thing. But this place has plenty of parking spaces.

My charbroiled oysters and red beans and rice were delicious, but unfortunately the service and cleanliness of the restaurant left much to be desired. The staff behind the bar were loud and arguing about who does what job right in front of customers. They were also having an extremely loud debate with a friend of theirs over who was the best oyster shucker. The tables around us were full of dirty plates and remained dirty for 20 minutes before someone came to clean them. And to  top it off all of this happened while the manager was near. 

It's not just the food that makes for a great dining experience but the overall service.

30 minute wait for cold, raw burgers and disgusting greasy, limp fries? Gross! I was too hungry to send my food back. My wife sent hers back and got a new burger and fresh fries in 2 minutes. How can that be??
We saw other people sending food back, too. Maybe it wouldn't take 30 minutes if the food was cooked right THE FIRST time? And you wouldn't have to comp so much food.
Most frustrating part is watching other people eating that came in after us! Evidently you have to know about all the "lists" to get on. Maybe that's good for regulars but I'd never go back. We could go to McDonald's for better food and service!

Very expensive for a thrift store. I won't be back. 
Disappointing of their prices. Did have quality clothes which can be found at other thrift stores.

$1 raw bar oysters!  What is not to love?  Went here three times in a two week period.  The oysters were terrific each time.  The rest of the food was pretty decent although nothing special.  If you can sit outside - do so, as the old fashion booth atmosphere inside leaves much to be desired.  The service ranged from pretty damn slow and indifferent to really good.  No rhyme or reason to much here.  But relax, sit outside, get some oysters and a drink or two and you will not regret it.
Great wine bar! It has a wonderful wine list outdoor seating when it's warm. Knowledgable and friendly staff.

The building itself is very cool, music sounded fine, the food we (eventually) received was OK, nothing great, but man the service was HORRRRRIBLE. From inattentive to just plain ol' not around, the draft beer wasn't working and the bartender seemed very disinterested in serving or letting it be known that he wasn't able to pour beer. 

Having to stand in line to order then wait for a place to sit... ugh.  This place will succeed simply due to it's location, but I wouldn't ever recommend that anyone go.

Couldn't decide between the Surf & Turf or the Evil Princess. Wanted something spicy but the  Sizzling Surf a& Turf sounded so good. That's what I ordered and it was delicious!  The manager came over to see how everything was. The waiter was great!
They brought me out a few sauces that I could try (since I had a had time deciding ). The Evil Princess sauce was good and a little spicy ....but the very spicy sizzling sauce was OH SO good. Alright, I'm coming back again. Next time, I'm bringing a friend  so we can share the amazing-looking and sounding desert.

I cannot rave enough about how great Blinds Brothers is. My boyfriend and I had been living in our new house for almost a year and still had paper blinds on our windows and we had a dog who kept ripping the paper blinds to peek outside. We decided it was time to look into window treatments and I had contacted a few places including one of those large commercial blinds companies. I read their reviews and felt super uncomfortable with the idea of using them and found Blinds Brothers and I'm so glad I did. They had someone come out and measure all of our windows (pre-COVID) and do a design consult with us. They gave us a very accurate and fair estimate for all the window treatments that we wanted to get done and gave us a realistic timeline of when they could be installed. This got pushed back because of COVID but they kept us updated and when it was safe for them to install them, they did while still abiding by social distancing guidelines, which my boyfriend and I really appreciated. I would highly recommend them to anyone. They were amazing from start to finish, answered all of our questions, and sometime in the future we will plan on using them again to look at options for our sliding glass door. The quality of the products they have and the installation and the customer service is top-notch and we are so thrilled with what we have. (Asher was also really sweet with our overly excited dog during the consultation which was amazing). I'm posting pictures of our shutters and the honeycomb shades we got which both turned out amazing. We got shutters in our living room (our windows are huge, maybe like 9/10 ft high) and we also got them in our master bathroom. We got honeycomb shades in the rest of our windows and we got room darkening ones in our master bedroom. The options to customize individual rooms was great and we really appreciated the design input they had. We also got the top/bottom honeycomb shade option which has been amazing for letting light in but also maintaining privacy in a south philly rowhome.

I really was not sure what to rate this restaurant.  The food was very good and that part I liked but like so many others who have reviewd this place the service really did bring the rating down.   I sincerely hope the owners read these reviews and and see the theme of what is going on.   Great food is one thing but without great service you are not going to get people to keep coming back.   I must say our food was excellent and the portions were far more than we could finish.  However from the moment we walked in we got a vibe that was less than welcoming.   Then we got a waiter who I honestly hope not to ever have again.   His name was Joe.   He had his good points as he did check on us and did get our items to us and was doing a great job of turning tables.   That however is where his profffesionalism ended for me.  First of all I was with my boyfriend and it became quite obvious to me right away he did not want to talk to me.  He would only look at him whenever a question was asked.  He also served his lobster bisque before mine which I found rather odd.   Normally most people serve the woman first not that I am all that old fashioned.   He never offered us the specials although in all honestly we did say we knew what we wanted but he really did not try very hard and could have offered them up anyway.  He did however upsell some cheese garlic bread so he managed to work that in.   I have to admit at first I thought maybe he was just into men but then I began to realize as I looked around that he was treating different tables differently.  When an older wealthy looking couple came in he was all over them asking if it was a special occassion and rattling off the specials.  At that point I knew what was going on.   I have to admit I began to think this was the same waiter another reviewer had spoken about who basically ignored his wife.  I think now he caters to the men assuming they are paying the bill.   We go out a lot and to some very nice restaurants all over the country and I have to say nothing turns me off faster than servers like him.   To me this is far from proffesional.   We normally tip at least 18 percent but his tip reflected his service.   I have seen this same theme in several reviews when it comes to service.  Even the hostess was nice but we got the vibe we were bothering her and honestly they were busy but not that busy and there was no wait.   So needless to say the experience was less pleasant than it should have been. Now that said the food portion was exceptional.  We both had the lobster bisque and I had the chicken spedini which was wonderful.  He had the seafood rissotto which he said was excellent as well.  We also split the special cheese garlic bread.   The portions were really way too large and quite frankly they could cut them down a bit and lower the prices a little but the quality was excellent.   I will go back but I have to say if I have another experience with a server like this it will make me less likely to return.  I would have given them 5 stars if not for the service but please owners if you read this do something about your staff because in time it will cut into your bottom line.

This business has a sign on the door that states no opening time, & closing times of 5:00 PM, M-F. I have a picture as proof. After driving out here & arriving at 3:45, I was less than pleased to see bars on the door & for the business to be closed for the day. I asked the guy what was at the mechanic shop next door & he said "They close when they want. They're never here past 3."  That's highly unprofessional to not honor the times you've chosen to operate your business & have posted on your door & website. I suggest you change your sign or remove it. I needed a tire today & got one....from somewhere else. I would have been a new customer & own 4 vehicles, so I'm assuming you guys have so much money that you have no use for customers. I won't be returning. Best of luck to you if this is how you choose to do business.

We were not impressed. Service was slow and inattentive, wine was just ok, and both of our pasta dishes were SO SALTY it was hard to finish the meal. And I love salty foods! Atmosphere was great but the meal left a lot to be desired. We will probably not be back.
Food was pretty good. Like the atmosphere, but can't say I would go back. Cheese plate was just ok. Best thing was the veal over salad.

Been coming here for years. The food is decent and the staffs are friendly. During lunch time can be hectic and drink refill can be a problem.  But overall, I come because of the friendly staffs and their willingness to accommodate requests.

Had dinner last evening. Great ambiance and a very friendly staff. I ordered the 8oz filet medium. It was cooked perfectly and added the sautéed onions and shrooms. I'd recommend this place if you're looking for a business dinner or night out with someone special.

Morse Code has something to suit any style, from punk/rock to classic chic. In front they have funky dresses and graphic rock T-shirts, a bit similar to what you might see at Mars & Venus down the street. I thought their bags and wallets were pretty cute, but the bags were a little pricey.

In the back is where they keep all their vintage items. Here, you can find something for any age. It's all colour-coded and organized by size too, so you can browse by colour and your own size. These pieces are much more affordable, and it's easy to find a Diesel skirt or Banana Republic sweater for a bargain price. They also have 80's style jewelry available, if you like to adorn yourself with atypical bling.

Owner Kimberly Rosadiuk also does on-the-spot alteration services, if you're in need of some tailoring. It's pretty convenient if you get something there that is a steal, but it doesn't quite fit right. If you're into the kind of bargain shopping that requires sorting and patience, you should check out Morse Code.

My Jeep broke down and had recalls on parts so it was sent here for service. They called a few days later and sent a courtesy driver to take us to the dealership (a 35 minute drive from our house!) We got there and got our car, and about 3 minutes down the road, the battery light comes on again! We brought it back hoping that it was a glitch and just needed reset. Nope! They put in another "bad alternator!" We were told it had been driven around before we got it back but the battery light almost immediately came on when we turned the car on. Then we waited 45 minutes for the courtesy driver to come to take us home, without our car :( We have NO idea how it is possible to take out a bad alternator and put another bad one in! We were also told the manufacturer is supposed to check the parts before they're sent to the dealership so they "shouldn't have to check" the parts before they're put in cars. Big waste of time!!!! PS--- I didn't catch his name, but the courtesy driver was the only one with good customer service skills! He was very friendly and helpful and understood our frustrations.

Giving this place 4 stars because they have Kao Soi AND it was good! The server was very attentive as well.  Wr will be back and my review will be better! We drove 40 minutes and found this place and will prob be back a few times a month

Literally the best spa I have EVER been to, hands down. My mother and I went for a massage the other day any absolutely loved it. You walk in and are nicely greeted by the front desk and taken to the tranquility room to have a peaceful wait for your massage therapist. The room has fresh ice water, and a lovely, calming, aroma. The massage was AMAZING. I had a Swedish massage and my mom had a deep tissue massage and we were both so happy with the results. After it was done we went out to the little shop in the front and browsed all their cute home decor and clothing products. Overall, super relaxing, super clean, super professional and very reasonably priced for what you get!

This is one of the best Reno has to offer.  When you've been to Vegas multiple times like I have it is really difficult to NOT compare a casino to what is in Vegas.  The hotel is clean, lot of restaurants, and all the gaming.  Peppermill wasn't so good that I would never go anywhere else in Reno.  Besides the casino/hotel Reno felt like a city that has never really modernized, I felt like I was sent back to the 70's.
Best seafood ramen ever!  We went there on a rainy day and had hot & spicy seafood ramen.  It was delicious and price was right!

I grew up going to Dunkin Donuts on the West Coast. I remember visiting with my Dad and getting donuts, thats about it. 

I am not a huge fan of morning sweets. I do like an occasional scone, donut or bagel but my visits to Dunkin typically involve a desire for coffee! 

I really enjoy Dunkin coffee. I know, I know its a huge franchise but I like it and its right on my way into work. The fact that they have a drive thru makes it almost impossible not to pick some up. My Admin at work loves their coffee too, so I often run through to grab us both a cup despite the fact that we have a very cool Keurig machine at the office! 

I will occassionally indulge the office or family with a dozen, but its rare. I like the hashbrowns, and some times a bagel as well but I dont care much for the other breakfast items, all very processed and over priced for the quality. The flatbread melts are good, but $4 for a cheesy flatbread is obnoxious to me, none of it is fresh its all frozen pre-packaged. 

Andrea is my favorite employee here. They have had several over the past year or so they have been there, and she is my fave. She is consistently friendly, welcoming and fast. 

I wont make a trip here on a Saturday, but during the week if I am not in the mood for a Keurig brew and didnt have time to stop at Bongo, I will drive thru here.

I wanted this to be good,  but continuous issues during our visit led to the one-star. We had a larger party,  so I understood things might take longer; however wrong orders brought to out table TWICE, that's after it taking over an hour from arrival to the food being delivered to the table. It's no wonder why the area's Steak N Shake restaurants closed and haven't reopened if management runs it like this.

I purchased a used 2007 FJ Cruiser with a cash purchase. My salesman, Dan Highes was professional, and recommended a used Toyota FJ Cruiser to replace the Jeep I have had for 17 years, and allowed me to test drive it. I lightly negotiated on the already reasonable price, and requested that the car be undercoated and alignment fixed. Both of those requests were met to my satisfaction, in addition to them detailing the vehicle for me. 

Dan was great to point me in the direction of the perfect vehicle. It would have been nice to go over all the features of the vehicle in more detail as I discovered there are a few off-roading buttons that I have no idea how to use, but almost accidentally pressed on my drive home (A-Trak, Differential Lock). However, with the internet, I'll figure out what they do. Not a big deal...but I would recommend that they acclimate the sales people with all buttons and features so that they can de-brief customers on all functionality while at the dealership. 

The only drawback during the purchase process was spending well over an hour sitting, waiting to pay for my vehicle. Since the price was agreed upon on my first visit, I was hoping to be in and out quickly and on my way to get some dinner. I arrived at 7:15 and left just before 9:00. That's very long when all I need to do is look at the vehicle, and write a check. 

On a positive note, their personnel are professional and personable. They met my minor vehicle requests without any issues. The dealership is clean, and modern looking. The music is lame but they have to already know that...I was only there for about 2 hours and the music was bland-rock. I don't even know if that is a music category, but it is the only way I would describe it. Ciocca is a car dealership...it should be fun and exciting to look at cars. Get some tunes to match. Their salespeople and customers will thank them for it. Overall, Ciocca is a very good business with good, ethical people.  I trust them.

i want to love the oakmont pub, i really do...but for now, we're just "in like."

as far as h-town goes, it's my go-to spot for grabbing a beer as they have a decent selection on tap (sierra nevada, yards, dogfish, hoegaarden to name a few). not sure how frequently they change 'em up tho. great place to watch a game on their huge flat screens if you can get a table downstairs or a seat at the bar.

food wise, it's pub fare meets quasi-italian as it's part bar, part family restaurant. being a veg, i have plenty of pasta options (the putanesca is quite good!), however, i'm not exactly feeling a big pasta dinner all the time, esp when going in to drink and watch a game. the caprese salad is decent when tomatoes are fresh and i'm in a salad mood...otherwise it's mozz sticks, poppers and fries - oh, boy! a veggie burger and/or eggplant parm hoagie would make nice additions that would remain inline with their current menu offerings.

the crowd here is diverse - a mix of college kids and senior citizens, h-town lifers and new home owners, families, friends and couples. pretty much anyone and everyone can fit in and feel comfortable at the oakmont.

Not sure why this place is getting decent reviews. I've actually been here for lunch and breakfast and found both times to be subpar. First waitress was not overly friendly, soup was extremely salty and grilled cheese was too greasy. Their Dutch baby on the breakfast menu is better but still very subpar. There are better places out there.

Honky Tonk Central is a great time. There are three big floors of live, local bands. I was truly blown away by the talent. Each band played four hour sets non-stop, and then when they were finished, the next band would set up. It was incredible! My favorite of the weekend was the Harold Allen Band, but there were so many other great ones. I felt spoiled. 

The layout of the venue is awesome, with big, open floor to ceiling windows that have balcony railings which provide for a really cool indoor but yet somewhat-outdoor vibe. 

Service was hit or miss. We scored with John, who took care of us on Saturday. He appeared out of nowhere every time we needed something, and he was as sweet as could be. On Sunday, our service was really spotty. We would have been better off just going to the bar and ordering there. But in all fairness, we only saw one girl working the entire second floor. 

The beer selection is limited but when I session drink I tend to go for the easy stuff anyway (pro tip!). That said, the beers got progressively warmer as our Saturday tenure wore on. I don't know if they couldn't get those domestics on ice fast enough for the busy crowd or what, but by the end of the evening I was icing down my drinks in a cup of ice water. We did order dinner during one of our visits; the buffalo chicken wrap with tots was legit!

Definitely looking forward to return visits on future trips to Nashville!

I went there for lunch but I'm not sure how the food is I never got that far.  I waited 10 minutes in line with one person ahead of me.  The customer wanted a rundown of the entire menu and operation of the company apparently.  To her credit the cashier was very patiently answering her questions.  My problem is there are 2 registers and no one would make eye contact, acknowledge me, or open register.  I counted 5 people back there I could see.  No host, wait staff or anyone checking on the customers.  After 5 minutes of the waitress holding the customers card in her hand with no swipe I'd figured they just didn't notice I was there and left.  
      I have two stars because the food looked really fresh and the facility clean.  That alone in my book is worth 2 stars but none of that is any good if you don't service your customers coming in the door.  People don't have :15+ minutes and the time to prepare the food on their lunch break.  I hope they get this fixed because I love the concept and would really like it to succeed.  I'll try again in a week or two hopefully then I can change my review.

Why do people love this stuff?  The cheese is bad.  BAD.  
And this part of clickC's statement is true:  your cardboard thin stick to the roof of my mouth   

Does that sound appetizing to anyone!!

I am a local and love this place. It is a great place to get a bite to eat, have a drink or watch a game. The place really comes alive in warmer weather but can get crowded in the colder months. The food is excellent, I have many choices to choose from when I order and I normally am the type of person to stick with what I know but I always try new things here.

Summary: EAT HERE
The seafood, the fried Brussels sprouts... it was truly epic. A beautiful brasserie, with fantastic, attentive wait staff!

if your Yelp review ever disappears - re-Yelp! 

this place is one word: Meh.

i went here a few times. the staff never cracked a smile, never seemed like they wanted to offer me service. i remember doing lots of waiting. hopefully by now they have different people working there..

Extremely shady. Looking for a used car for our son, we told them our budget. We made it VERY clear how much we were willing to spend. We were directed to a car which we drove and liked. We started negotiating and suddenly the car was double the price we had said we wanted to spend.

Looking on the internet, the same car was listed 1500 below their sale offer to us.
Descent place, descent service, descent food. Nothing to get fired up about.  Get hush puppies!
Update...the response from Terri in customer service came along almost FOUR YEARS LATER in June of 2018 (initial posting was July of 2014).  Not surprised in the least.
Friendly bartender, music was too low and the cocktails weren't premium in my opinion.
Good food at a good price. The service is great and there is a wide variety of food to choose from. The food and atmosphere have that nice "down home" feel.

I loved this place! It has more of a fast food feel to it than a restaurant feel, but don't let that fool you. The food is fresh and delicious! I had the Szechuan chicken with lo mien noodles and it was great. The veggies are fresh and crisp, not lifeless and soggy like Chinese takeout places. And the food was not drowned in sauce. It was perfectly sauced with just the right amount of spice.  And the best part- with your meal you get 2 beers for $3!! It's not a huge beer selection but we were happy to see Corona, Amberbok, and New Castle! For a tasty, quick, fresh meal, you can't beat this place. My meal and 2 beers was $12!  The staff are friendly and helpful and they bring your food right to your table. You can also watch the Wok chef make your food because the kitchen is open. Next time you're at the mall do yourself a favor and head across the street to this place. I will most definitely be back.

Today was my 3rd time at this place.
I love dinners and the vibes they have. This one in particular it's really cool and old. 
The 2 other times Everything was pretty good and yummy but today... 
So today I went for breakfast, a bit packed but the waitress are always friendly. I ordered the country fried steak with the steer in potatoes and it came with a pancake. Everything was great but then my pancake had a ton of butter which I love but the butter had some green weird stuff in it, I was suspicious and so I asked if it's was regular butter or flavored one, the waitress looked at it pretty disgusted herself and took it back to the kitchen , after like 5 minutes she came back with a new one saying that the butter has some blueberries but it was ok... I mean you can tell it was green , not blue , geeeerrlll!! 
Good thing she gave me a new one and she apologized but I'm a little surprised not even a free coffee lol

Tried using the drive through last night after midnight. Only to be turned away because they are serving Uber Eats and Door Dash only. Not everyone is on drugs or is drunk. Ever hear of shift work. So, you think when someone gets turned away they are going to pay more to order your food or they now going to drive more trying to find another place. Good plan!!!

This is one of my two favorite Indian restaurants in St. Pete. The food takes a while to prepare - usually about 30 minutes, sometimes less if they aren't busy but if there's a large party in the lounge area, be prepared to wait a little longer. The food is worth the wait though. They take the time to make sure it comes out right. Service is excellent too. Their staff is very accommodating to food requests and spice adjustments. 

Since I'm vegetarian, I can only speak on that portion of the menu. But my favorite dishes are the Paneer Tikka Masala, Dal Makhani, Dry Chickpeas (Chana Masala), and Dum Aloo Gobhi. All of their naan breads and appetizers I've tried are delicious, and their Gulab Jamun dessert isn't a bad way to end the meal either. Their basmati rice they serve with the entrees is also worth pointing out because they add cumin seeds for flavor and aroma as well as a touch of oil to give it a nice silky texture and prevent the rice from sticking. It creates a notable difference from the rice served at other Indian restaurants in the area.

The Wyndham Philadelphia hotel - Historic District was my base of operations for my recent stay here during the 2013 Philadelphia marathon. My stay here was for 2 nights on 11/16 & 11/17, departing on 11/18. The Wyndham brand took over this property when it used to be a Holiday Inn and it was subsequently rebranded. I would say that this upgrade was needed and I am happy that the Wyndham brand took it over. My overall experience here at the Wyndham Philadelphia hotel is a positive one. They had your basic in room amenities, as well as the other amenities that some properties charge for, including wifi and business center / the lobby computer terminal for guest use. Its great that the wifi was nice in the room and the signal is strong. Need to ask about wifi password at check in.

The Wyndham Philadelphia hotel is located on 400 Arch Street in center city Philadelphia. It's between 4th and 3rd Street and walkable to most of Philadelphia's top tourist landmarks, including the Liberty bell, independence hall, and Betsy Ross house, among others. The property has a semi fancy lobby and very clean. Spotless in fact. There is also in house restaurant / bar called Corsica, which I haven't eaten in, but  from what I saw, it looks decently cool. The check in process was fast, professional, and positive. I even asked if the room they gave me had a tub, since the last two properties didn't and I wanted a tub. So glad this one gave me a tub. The tub is for marathon related uses only, along with taking a shower. 

The room itself was very clean and comfortable. The double bed room was great and the beds themselves were very comfortable, almost comparable to the Westin's "heavenly beds". There was a large tv and a coffee maker, which was perfect for our needs. There wasn't a refrigerator or a microwave in the room, but we didn't need it. The office desk and chair was a nice touch as well. The room was clean and spotless. Very pleased with my stay here and hope to return again soon here or any Wyndham branded property. During check out, I forgot about my Amtrak rail ticket online, so that business center terminal at 4am really hit the spot! Thanks Wyndham!!

Thanks also to the gentleman at the front desk, whom called the nearby taxi company for me when I needed to get to the 30th Street Amtrak Station as quick as possible, as I had a 5:15am train to catch towards Newark airport that morning. Very professional and positive at that hour of the morning. All around great service! I definitely will recommend this property to those visiting Philadelphia. While it's very much centrally located to all the tourist areas, it's priced right for those who are on a budget too. Happy with my stay here and hope to return again soon.

Support local!

A

We hit up Ponzio's for late breakfast and were surprised at how busy it was for a weekday. We had no issue with seating from the apathetic hostess, mostly because this place is huge with plenty of tables and booths.

Menu selections are plentiful and I decided on a bacon and cheese omelet with home fries, wheat toast and coffee, of course. There was a ton of bacon (mmmm...bacon) mixed throughout with the right amount of cheese. Potatoes were okay with some ketchup.

Service by our waitress was pretty good, considering how busy she was. Obviously, the longevity and history of this place has built its fair share of local and regular patrons. I'd probably stop back if I'm in the area again, but wouldn't necessarily go out of my way for it. Good, but nothing spectacular. 

Maybe a sampling of their ample and appealing baked goods would've helped my first impression.
Had wings, salad and beer once there. Girls friendly. Different outfits than other Hooters I've been to across state. Average on all fronts.

I arrived at my hotel next door (La Quinta) a wee bit early for check in (7 hours early.. lol) but I'll go into that in that review. I felt bad eating the free breakfast when I hadn't checked in yet so I decided to walk next door to Bob Evans.

I've never eaten at a Bob Evans so I didn't know what kind of food they served but I quickly found out it's like Cracker Barrel and Denny's had a baby. Like Dennys, you get your check and pay upfront. It also has the same layout. But like Cracker Barrel their food leans to the southern/comfort food style. They even sell candy at the front like Cracker Barell does.

When you walk in the server seats you and within no time the waitress is there to take your order. I've got to say she was the friendliest,  most enthusiast waitress I've ever had. Our drinks and food came out within no time as well.

My boyfriend ordered the Sunrise skillet, which had eggs, homefries, sausage and a bread item (toast, biscuit, pancakes, etc). He liked it. I got the rise and shine with two eggs, homefries, and bacon. Simple but good. What I loved were the biscuits, made fresh daily. 
Overall, I would recommend this place for your typical American breakfast and comfort foods. Nothing fancy about it, classic, cheap and fast.
the donuts were soooooo good i couldn't help myself but a few hours later me and my girlfriend had the worst explosion where the sun don't shine. i would eat it again.

I was an established patient here. I lost my job and had state insurance for a very brief period. I had an urgent need to be seen for suspected shingles (later confirmed). My NP Emily (who is wonderful) wasn't available, so I saw Dr. Eva Parker. I was in pain and a bit concerned (I had never had shingles), but Dr. Parker was condescending and even stated (in front of her nurse and NP) that had she known I had state insurance, she never would have let them schedule me. I am still a bit perplexed by the whole ordeal as I paid my bill in full upon checkout. I sure hope she doesn't fall on hard times and have someone treat her that way! I have good insurance now but I will be changing derms!

Great beer selection but food has gone way downhill recently.  Used to be one of the best burgers in the city but tonight it was a tasteless generic frozen patty.  I am so disappointed in Perch Pub tonight.  I will probably never go back.

The sushi here is always room temperature or warm even if there is nothing that has been cooked placed in your roll. The rolls are all good though but they don't taste fresh when they are not cool and fresh. It is also ALWAYS way too hot in there and muggy. Also there are always flies flying around which I think is disgusting. I do eat there on occasion but I make sure to do so in the middle of the day when there is no over crowding.

The hospitality at Lili's is like Grandma's kitchen: full plates, routine drink refill, and easy conversation with NO "rush" to leave. 

My favorite lunch is the chicken fajita salad with "perfect" pico de gallo as the dressing. Spicy ... just the way a Mexican salad should be.

I have to say I was a little concerned about this activity. I thought the price was a little steep, and when we arrived, I was unsure about the venue. When the activity started, all worries were calmed. 

This was truly the most fun I've had in a long time. I was there with a group of teachers I worked with, and between the ten of us, we had all of the subjects covered. 

We found nearly all the clues, and we found the magnifying glasses. We solved every puzzle and made it out of the room with 6 mins and 39 seconds to spare. 

I CANNOT wait for Sherlock's game room to open because I will be back!

The Pour House has become a standing favorite amongst my group of friends.  The beer selection is solid, with great variety and styles to choose from and they're pretty generous with the samples if you're having a hard time deciding between brews.  The homemade chips & onion dip is one of the best upgrades to my secret guilty obsession of Herr's chips & jarred onion dip that I've tried and the burgers.  My goodness, THE BURGERS.  I have yet to try one that was not amazingly delicious.  They alone are worth the wait for a table.

Long wait, but understandable after a Cards game. The food was horrible did not eat my plate and waitress took and didn't ask if something was wrong with the food.  Service was bad as well waitress kept forgetting things and other servers had to bring us our drinks and food.  Don't recommend.

This Goodwill store is pretty bland. I didn't search through clothes but the trinket department is wack. There were barely any electronics. It was just blah overall which was surprising. Thrift stores are usually better outside of NY.

It's funny because I did find a pair of Mossimo sneakers that were brand new for $5. In another review for this store, a yelper said this Goodwill had a lot of clearance items from Target & if she wanted Target, she would go to Target. Well I shop at Target and if I can get it cheaper than what Target sells it for, I'm down. 

Thrift stores are always a hit or miss, some just have better quality items than others. Just know this one is not a hit.

Froesel Tire has been one of my to-go personal auto shop since I arrived in Saint Louis. These guys work hard, is honest and is trustworthy. I do recommend Froesel to anyone who is looking for a mechanic to take care of your car.

Keep up with a great work you guys!

I went here for my birthday dinner with about 13 people.

Pros:
-Vegan-friendly food
-Very attentive waitress
-Awesome falafel

Cons:
-Lost my reservation, but I was able to make a new one
-The hookahs we ordered were harsh
-The waitress didn't know how much an appetizer plate would cost if they made it big enough for 13 people.

Overall:
-I liked it and would definitely come back, but maybe for lunch instead of dinner.
