The restaurant in beautiful. The menu is horrible, not a good selection of food. I ended up ordering Guacamole and I ask the waiter could I get a ginger beer w/ lime, she said they only serve it with tequila. She called it a Mexican Mule. The mule was small yall. I ended up pay $30 for an appetizer and a small as drink. My girlfriend got the melted cheese and shrimp with was small, but cost $12. I was so hungry I asked to see the menu again and I ordered a flat bread (pico, cheese and steak) . I than asked waiter could she ask the manager could I order a ginger beer and lime--No Tequila, she walk off and come back and said he said Yes. That ended up costing $3.50 which is what I want the first go around. Me and my girlfriend was talking for about 2 hours the waiter which came every 5 minutes than said to us if we weren't going to order anything thing else they need the table for reservations. Which meant we need to leave. I was applauded I ask for the second check which was $25. I paid my Bill and got up and left. I will never go to El Shit again and would not recommend the place to friends. They are vey high for a Mexican Joint and the food is nasty at least what we had.

Whisk Bakery where the caterers for my wedding. They were amazing! We had an amazing breakfast buffet and even an omelet station. It was all delicious. In addition to that they made my beautiful wedding cake, and it was SOOO GOOD!

Had lunch here the other day while my car was having some work done nearby.  I ordered the Caesar Chicken Wrap.  Usually they will have some caesar dressing on it or something to live up to the name.  This one was just grizzly chicken, lettuce and some kind of weird sauce.  The fries were good and the service was fine, but I will not return.  The food is mediocre at best.  There are lots of other good places in Safety Harbor to grab lunch.

I am absolutely horrified by the treatment I received here! I have been coming here more frequently over the past month due to location convenience and I must say that the service I have received here has been worse with every visit. With the most recent encounter, I had requested a standard manicure and pedicure. I was not aware that there was an extra charge for gel nail take off because I had never had it taken off before, but when I initially requested the services I had told them that I had gel polish on my nails and the person helping me did not mention the extra charge. My boyfriend treated me to the mani pedi and left me with enough cash to tip. After he left and I sat down to start my pedicure. The woman doing my manicure told me there would be an extra 5$ charge for gel nail take off. I only had enough money tipping so I asked them to cancel the manicure. This was very embarrassing for me because I did not bring enough cash for the extra charge. They agreed to the refund but made it very difficult, they made me call my boyfriend and get his credit card info to void the charge in front of everyone. They did not apologize for the inconvenience, but rather treated me as if I had done something wrong. I then went through with the pedicure which was supposed to include a massage and hot stone treatment. My pedicure included just barely that. I had received this pedicure before so I know what it entails, and this treatment was barely meeting what I had received previously. The woman then wrapped a hot towel on my feet so tight that it literally burned them and caused a lot of pain. After the pedicure I went to dry my toes at the drying station where I usually receive a complimentary massage, the masseuse asked everyone but me if I wanted a massage and then walked away. When I left I did not receive a thank you for my business or an apology for the treatment I received. 

   I have spent so much money in this overpriced nail salon over the past month. 4 weeks ago I spent 50$ on a gel French manicure and had to come back two days later after two nails had chipped. This would be understandable with a regular manicure but not a gel. 2 weeks ago I went for another gel manicure and tie polish change, the women doing my gel manicure complainer so much during my manicure because " the polish was poor quality and difficult to apply" that I left apologizing to her! Then I went for a polish change on my toes and they didn't have a matching color for my nails and toes.

Beers from around the world. Seasonal on deck. Friendly staff friendly prices and in a clean rich part of St. Louis 

Come on down and try for yourself

The atmosphere is clean, open, and casual while also not too trendy.  The drinks are solid an on par with (if not occasionally excelling) the ever popular craft cocktail culture of New Orleans.  The service has been decent and reliable, without being intrusive.  The food has been absolutely mouthwatering.  This being said, why oh why do I not go to Maurepas Foods more often, especially since it is down the street from my house?  
I recently racked my brain concerning this, especially when I often pass it on my way to so many other (sometimes decidedly less delicious) Bywater institutions.  Finally a friend who shared this dilemma explained it to me the other day.  
Maurepas serves in essence upscale comfort food, sometimes with a twist, but still comfort food.  The problem with it is that it's in really small portions for the price.  Comfort food, by design, seems weird in small portions.  Booty's serves portions just as small, and sometimes smaller, for somewhat less, and I'm okay with that because it's tapas style street food, not Southern-influenced comfort food.  However, there's not really consistency in this either. Entrees you expect to be served in larger portions are scant (like my fish -- why list side items underneath if you only plan on putting a teaspoonfull at best on the plate?) and ones you think would be smaller come out huge (yes, I'm talking about you, squid and sausage sandwich).  My other half wasn't all that hungry and I was starving.  His meal was -- no joke -- at least three times the size of mine (and at least $5 cheaper).  Unfortunately I wasn't in the mood for squid.
Honestly, I would rather have a decent sized entree at $30 or even $40 then to get a $15 to $19 that is amuse bouche sized.  I felt like I had fallen prey to a con game.  You see one price on the menu at the door, see the casual atmosphere, and get fooled into thinking you can fill up on $20, then it comes out and you realize it's going to cost more like $45.  I have absolutely no problem with dropping $150 a plate if it's quality and I know beforehand what I can expect, but I don't like being the victim of a "bait and switch" or paying $30 a person and still having to grab more food on the way home.

Finally renovations are finished in terminal D! It was always a nuisance departing from that area. 

Security is hit or miss. It can take anywhere from 15 minutes to over 2 hours. 

The main negative is the luggage handling. I've had so many issues with damaged, delayed, and lost luggage. All stemming from Philly's end.
Had brunch here. It is a very classy brunch spot and basic girls will love it. Food was pretty good and service was excellent. I recommend if you want to be fancy.

Waited about two hours and the entire dinning experience was about three and half hours. Great location with a mellow feel and plenty to drink and see while you wait. Went on a weekend at about 8 so that was probably their busiest time. I had the pulled pork in banana leaf and it was delicious. Chef was friendly and took some time to greet everyone outside. Overall good food with a great location. While we waited, we ordered some of their cocktails which were really good, walked around the area, got a cigar from a cigar shop across the way, and appreciated the scenery.

Southern cooking at its  best. Cafeteria style meat and two veg. Each day is a different menu. Tues smothered chicken beef tips over rice. 6-8 different veggies to choose from. Squash casserole blkeyed peas corn best Mac and cheese in the world. Spiced apples. Corn muffin w jalapeño and a fried cornbread pancake that I'm sure my mother in law cooked. It was just that good. 
Off the beaten path not in a tourist area. This is an awesome place for lunch. Definitely worth finding this place!

Made a spontaneous trip to Reno from Sacramento, we booked online the day of, and the pictures looked nice, the rooms looked clean. When we pull into the parking lot there was nowhere to park. We get here to check in and the front desk was having issues with their printer and card maker. We had to go across the street to get a room key. The place is very dirty and does not have a pleasant smell. We get into our room and the floors are sticky, the carpet has not been vacuumed. The toilet barley works, and the top of the toilet is off. Went downstairs to ask where I could find some water and I was told I had to go across the street to purchase water. I would not recommend this hotel to anyone.

Mike took care of us today and made us feel like family. We were celebrating saying YES to the Dress  where my daughter found her wedding dress.  We enjoyed the most wonderful food and fun atmosphere.  Truly grateful for finding this special place. Thanks again to all the staff.

I think we get hung up on our usual places and forget about Renée's. I mean, it's a nice, cozy little place close to home that has great food. I guess the solution is to make it one of our usual places!

My Thai Chicken Curry pizza, with spicy chicken, red curry sauce, sliced zucchini, red peppers, onions and basil was imaginative, spicy and just plain tasty. It'll be hard to order anything else when I return, although I did see plenty on the menu I want to try. The presentation on my wife's Chicken Caesar salad was stunning. The leaves were left whole and the chicken and parmesan sprinkled about. My wife said that could not even tell that the croutons were gluten free. Many others menu items were listed as gluten free, including pizza.  

Not sure why it took us so long to get back to ROO. We'll be back again soon.

We are living in philly for 12 months -originally from Sydney Australia. We were delighted to find max Brenner in philly since there are so many branches of this place back home. 

Went there on a Monday night with my wife and 2 year old at 8pm when the restaurant was literally half empty. Got told there was going to be a 20minute wait. When I questioned the empty tables I was told that they had few servers that they did not want to 'overwhelm' -fair enough so we went for a walk and came back. After 20minutes my daughter had fallen asleep on my shoulder and when we returned was told it would be another 10-15minutes. By this stage another young family was waiting for a table and significant part of the remaining restaurant diners had cleared out. 

After yet another 10-15mins I was asked if my daughter would sit in a high chair. When I pointed out she was asleep and asked if it was okay for her to sleep in her stroller I was told there was no way we could be accommodated for another 15-20mins with the stroller. Despite now that the restaurant was almost empty. When I asked why I couldn't simply sit near a whole set of empty tables I was told we would be in the way and they 'weren't holding me to dine there'. 

Absolutely incredible- have never experienced anything like this before. We were essentially refused a table because we had a little one and even though the restaurant was empty we couldn't sit with our toddler sleeping in her pram. Since I didn't want to wake her up we walked out to dine at the Cheesecake Factory across the road- seated immediately, far extensive menu, impeccable service and far better ambience. I am not sure who these people think they are but there are clearly far better restaurants with better service in the immediate vicinity. Stay away and take your money where you will be treated with respect.

My sister and I stopped in here in our way home from shopping for the first time today. We were greeted with smiles and free garlic knuckles (yay!) our waitress was very friendly and helpful about the menu. I love that they have pizza by the slice we wanted to try them all. I settled on the canal and the Brooklyn-Queens and my sister had the 34th street. The kids had pepperoni. The slices are HUGE. The toppings are pretty good. We had lots of pizza left over to take home. We both got the cannoli for dessert. Scrumptious! 

Overall this is a great place for pizza. I know everyone is crazy about the knuckles but for me there was just way too much butter and I'm a butter lover. They were still good though. We will definitely go back. 

On a side note I LOVE the music they play. It was all 80's on our Sunday afternoon visit. They also have TV's for the guys.

Your typical low budget hotel. I'd highly suggest paying a little extra and staying at the Motel 6 next door. I've been there and it's way better than this place. My room was next to the freeway (loud as hell; I had to turn on the fan to mute the noise) and bed is super springy and uncomfortable. I'd rather bring a sleeping bag+pad and sleep on that instead. It's DEFINITELY not worth $170 for a couple. Take your $ elsewhere. I regret not believing the reviews lol.

I have been going to the Bull to get my line dancing on for years. In fact I went to the "old" bull for those who know where it is. The new bull is amazing and looks like a barn. Of course it comes equipped with the staple country bar mechanical bull, tons of bars that have fast service, and two large dance floors. The first floor main dance floor is for country music and the upstairs dance floor is to shake your bootay on. Either way a mixture of people come from all different places to get their dance on. I learned to dance at the bull and I will never forget the friends or memories that I have made.

Still says soft opening but I think they must be past that point by now.  The place was very clean although I would say it was under lit.  large selection of lots of items and the staff were very friendly.
Much better than the last time we were here  few years ago.  Food was good, excellent service, you can't miss.  Home made salsa was great, no complaints from anyone at the table!

If you are considering Comcast, take my advice and RUN!  I've been with them since 1995...only game in town where I live.  The retention agents will lie to your face in order to keep your business.  The rates for poor service are outrageous and you'll spend literally hours on the phone trying to get your contracted services to work properly.  After signing a new 2-year contract, I find out that voice mail is not included.  The service agent I spoke with never mentioned this and I can't find it in the contract, either.  Agent Valerie told me today, tough beans.  The cost is $3.95 a month.  $3.95 a month and Comcast won't budge.  This is how they treat a 24-year customer.

I was not impressed with the food here. The people are nice, but the food wasn't as good as some claimed it to be, and its over priced. No one in my party was satisfied with her food. I was disappointed. Perhaps it was an off night, but I'm not willing to try it again.

Great service. Great manager. I love that they are sensitive to the fact that getting mechanical work done can be intimidating for women. I always feel respected and satisfied with the services they provide.

I really wanted to like Red Lion.  I dragged my wife here one weekend recently for an early dinner and I was excited based on what I had seen in the yelp reviews.  

First of all, the service was painfully slow but I looked past it because it's a bar.  We started with the fried pickle chips which were great -- good pickle to batter ratio and nice and crispy.  For our meal we went the British route and ordered the shepherd's pie and the national dish of Britain, chicken tikka masala.  The first thing we noticed was that the serving sizes for both meals were tiny for how much they cost, which was $12 and $13, respectively, and neither came with any sides.  The shepherd's pie was decent but it didn't impress me.  The chicken tikka masala was bland and unappetizing and my wife didn't take more than a a few bites before putting down her fork.  I tried a bite and agreed.  Our waitress could tell my wife wasn't satisfied and was nice enough to offer her another dish.

Also of note was the men's restroom, which was filthy and barely functional with no working urinals and one disgusting toilet that was overflowing.  The bathrooms seem to be shared between other establishments so maybe this isn't all Red Lion's fault.

Based on the wealth of great reviews, I'm hoping that this particular visit was an anomaly.  First impressions are powerful things though, and I don't think we'll be finding ourselves back at Red Lion anytime soon.

So let's just start with the fact that Sweet Tomatoes has the freshest and most current produce of any of the restaurants I've been to in a long time.  Not only do they have fresh and current produce that is seasonal, they give a military discount when you show your ID.  The salads are green, the pastas are fresh and the breads always hot.  Being a vegan, it's really hard to find good places to eat out, so when I found Sweet Tomatoes I found my own little secret vegan heaven.  Instead of ordering a soft drink, which is full of calories in sugar, I always opt for the "water cup."  Why do you think I am so excited about the water cup?  Other than it's free, I use the water cup and use the "soda water" along with putting the fresh fruit from the dessert bar inside the soda water to make my own sparkling natural fruit spritzer.  How much better can it get for vegan then a fresh free sparkling water spritzer with fresh fruit?  So whether you're trying to get healthy or you're a vegan and want to find a great place to eat -  Sweet Tomatoes is the place to be.

If I could give zero stars, I'd do exactly that. 

We lived in a house managed by Christone for three years. The owners of the residence came to see the house multiple times during our tenancy and the house was in great condition. 

Upon our departure, they withheld $875 TO PULL WEEDS. Not to mow, but to pull weeds. See attached "invoice."

The address and phone number for this "lawn care invoice" is Christone's address. Not only have they NOT performed the service, but they did not give us an itemized receipt.

We have taken our husky twice here for grooming.  Huskies have a lot of hair and they did a good job removing the loose undercoat.  They use the furminator which is a real plus.   The staff seems to care about dogs and are friendly.    Zakoya came back happy and looking good.  The fact is some places don't do such a good job and she has come back with hair flying all over the place when we pet her.  We will continue using this facility because they seem to do a good job.

They were quick to show up for a diagnostic appointment and were polite and correct with their recommendation.  They didn't charge for their time, but I requested a quote for repair or replacement of the system and never received follow up.

This place is great. Tried a few flavors and they were all good. Met the owners at the Saints vs Cowboys game and lost a bet so had to come as we said we would the next day and Glad we did! Great service. Cool folks. Cool spot. We will be back by next time we are in town.
An OK place for craft beers, not a lot of options for quads though is OK. The place is clean and employees are friendly. The bottled beer options are decent enough for your likings.

This is a hipster place that's worth trying. Super friendly staff. Coffee is really smooth and good. We tried the bravocado toast and the Gigi which were both good. The downside is, of course, that this is a healthy lifestyle eatery so they don't carry soda (duh). Also the toast is a small portion so it's good for portion control if you're on a health regimen!

Thank you Dr. Chaifetz for such an awesome arena! What a great venue for basketball. Great site lines were had at the VCU vs. SLU game. Can't comment on the concert aspect of the arena but look forward to seeing a show here. Nice to see alcohol served here considering this is was a NCAA event.

First, please read my review of Ardmore Acura because that obviously is the better of the two.

BMW of the Main Line is the worst when it comes to their service department.  No one calls you back and it is very difficult making an appointment for a simple oil change. And you guessed it...they are severely overpriced. 

BMW is definitely not the ultimate driving experience when it comes to service...

Bought a used car. They kept the car (that I have been paying already) for a month and a half because they were slacking off from ordering parts and fixing it. They promised me that it will only take 3 days initially. They made me come back and forth to the dealership only to be told that the car isn't ready yet. They were avoiding my calls and texts when I ask for updates. Managers were no help at all, they kept telling me it'll be ready the next day and when I get to the dealership it isnt. Got the car after a month and a half drama; it smelled like someone smoked inside (wasn't like that when i test drove it), tire pressure indicator was lighting up, the car was VERY DIRTY (they promised that they detail all used cars before releasing it to the owners) and that there was an unreasonable high km's added to it.

Read your contract before signing, as when I was in contact with them, I was trying to claim something from the contract that the manager denied was in our contract that I had to screenshot it for his information and prove it is indeed part of the contract. They are very sly, so you have to be very careful.

The food was good and reasonably priced. It was quiet and comfortable. Unfortunately, the service was slow. I don't know if it was the server or the cook staff but my steak was cold and my husbands hearts of mahi was rubbery. The server was as sweet as can be so I'm gonna hope that it was the kitchen staff. I'm also gonna hope it was a fluke. We'll give it another shot and see what happens.

After eyeing this place for few months, I found got around to check the place out today. I fell in love with Lady M crepe cake in NYC and was happy to find a place offering a crepe cake closer to home. As soon as our group walked in we noticed an intense bleach odor and complained to our server. She did not seem to speak English too well and half heartedly suggested that they had just cleaned the bathroom and that's probably where the bleach odor was coming from. I asked the server if they can keep the door open since the odor was strong enough to give me a headache and it was actually a very nice day out. The server made a frown and did not bother to explain why they cannot open door nor did she offer to turn up the AC to circulate the air. After waiting about 10 minutes, same server came around to bring out our order. Again, I asked her if they can please open the door because it was very hot and bleach odor was very intense. She did not bother to explain herself and did not make any efforts to accommodate our needs at all. I also noticed that she was in her raggedy sweat pants and a T shirt. No exactly the professional look you would expect especially considering the high end prices you are being charged. 

As far as our dessert was concerned, our cake was ok although House Black Tea did not have any flavor at all. My son accidentally drank my tea and actually thought it was a plain hot water. 

It a shame that the customer service does not live up the lovely desserts and the atmosphere of this place. I'll probably visit this place in the future since I'd love to try other desserts on the menu but I'll opt for a take out instead.

Wow. I paid $6 for less than a scoop of ice cream and they sprinkled some cereal on top. I will literally never go there again which is a shame because I live a mile from there. People will not want to pay those prices for such little food.
Yuck. Never again. I said that before. Gave it another chance and it was the meal was god awful. Service sucked. Now I know I won't go back ever again..  at least I gave it another shot.

Love the food love the atmosphere love the people. If your thinking of going to a nice Japanese restaurant I recommend trying this place out. Clean tables and booths. Food tastes amazing . Good selection of sushi well made. Friendly staff. I've been here twice and have no complaints definitely coming back again
Closed se 6:45 pm though on line said open until 8pm, I called just to book a 7pm spot and they said they were done!!!

My last night in Nashville.   I had tickets to a Cirque show at the Opry, so I decided to try and grab dinner at the mall.

I saw that Chuy's had green chile.  Green chile in the South?   Sign me up!
I arrived when they were super busy.  I didn't want to wait 30 minutes for a table.   I was lucky to find the last seat at the bar.   
I squeezed in and was ignored for a few minutes, but then a waiter brought me a menu, got my iced tea, and gave me some chips and salsa.   I ordered some green chili NM-style enchiladas.   
The chips were the thinnest I've ever seen.  They were like potato chip thin.   The regular salsa didn't do much for me.   There was also a white, creamy dip.  I am not one for dips, usually, but this one was intriguing.  I kept going back to it.  NO idea what was in it.  Good stuff

My enchiladas were a little dry, but the Hatch green chile made me homesick.   The beans were pretty good.   I asked for no rice, but they brought it any way.   (I hate that.  It is wasteful.  I asked 2 times for no rice.)   

It was super loud in there, but most bars are.   One of the bartenders had her family in there, so she kept coming back to them.   Kinda cute to see her dote on her niece.

(-5 stars) $1000 to move a 1BR apartment 20 miles away. They started at 9 AM and I had just about 20 boxes, a queen bed, dresser, tv stand, and some other very small pieces of furniture (think 2 or 3 other pieces that I could move myself, so they weren't heavy). They took 4 hours to move me out of the 1st apartment and another 2.5 to move me into the next apartment. Mind you, I had reserved elevators at BOTH LOCATIONS. Not to mention, they got the elevator stuck in my new apartment building - and then they had the nerve to tell me they may have to come back Monday. When I said the price/time it took was ridiculous, he said "You don't know moving." I've moved 3x the distance in half the time before.  When he first arrived at my building his comment was, "This is a nice place." They clearly tried to take advantage of the situation.  I wouldn't recommend this company to my worst enemy.

Racist. Wouldn't let me in because i had a scarf on my head (simple white scarf that matched my outfit & would keep my hair from frizzing up due to the humidity) and said it was deemed "ghetto" & wasn't for for a family restaurant & my necklace w/ a small pendant (simple diamond) wasn't allowed ..

Third time there first two were good don't think we will return were seated after a wait table was under a fan which I asked if it could be shut off as it was cold inside was told no so sat there cold the whole time and when our food came it was cold halfway thru eating due to ceiling fan on it the whole time many other restaurants we can go to and not freeze due to A/C turned too low and fans on too high.
Horrible!! Should be a zero!!! Crappy cold food!! And they got our order wrong and couldn't careless....worthless!!!!

I am glad I attended, but I should have just went to a face-to-face school and done like Nursing type of a program. I thought at first, that their Patient Care Technician Program was offered online, but no it was not, so I stuck with the Associate's Degree in Health Sciences for Pharmacy Technicians, because I already held a certificate and a license in that field, but I am also a Certified Nursing Assistant and would much rather further my education in that area, so.. I learned quite a lot and everyone at this school is very helpful and nice, though. It seemed very expensive, though for being a non-profit org., though, but I at least obtained an actual degree and have an Associate's Degree now, so I am not complaining by any means, but I am confused, as to if they are technically a not for profit school, then why are the online fees so much, but I know I live in IL now too. I used to reside in Florida, though, but we live and we learn I guess. Right? I am now going to do what I enjoy doing more and going to go back to school and take classes for nursing pre-reqs., which I should have done in previously, because non of my credits are recognized and I don't understand why a school claims that they are accredited, when in fact they are not or at least regionally. I should have done my research more ahead of time, because some of the private or for profit school institutions will accept my credits and of course leaves me to pay like over $8,000 out of my own pocket. Who has that kind of money, because I know I don't. Lol!
Always looking for a new Vietnamese food spot. Special Pho lives up to its name. All the food we ordered was hella good. Friendly and fun service. A top eats for me!

All I can say is delicious.  Spinach salad was different and very tasty.  All the flavors were infused in such a manner, the taste just rolled on the tongue.  (I think it was the bacon in the dressing). . .Yes, I said bacon.  To me it was subtle.

So, Hungarian Goulash, I was expecting a stew or soup. . .oh no, like a pancake folded omelette with all the deliciousness of goulash.  I thought the sour cream on top was going to take away from the flavor, but it actually intensified it.  I've eaten many different Hungarian Goulash stew before in countries all over Europe, but this was unique and very delectable.  Savory, and it compliments all the ingredients.

The decor was lacking. . .didn't understand the art gallery tip, unless they are talking about the paintings and display case on the 1st floor.  Nice view of the courtyard area of the small strip mall shopping are of the main dining room.  

Peter was moving with him and another waiter were the only ones on staff for 30 plus people waiting to be served.  I was impressed.  Peter was on point.  Moving with a purpose.

The hallway is small, so large people would not be able to fit.  I know that there is an upstairs and downstairs bathroom.  I think there is more seating upstairs, but did not wander to explore.

This place is only getting 2 stars purely because it's the ONLY "nice" theater around. Cinemark just opened up at Christiana and I'm sure that'll be the one we frequent. Penn cinema has had awful customer service since we found them. The ticket machines outside are always broken and no sign is ever posted. So, you waste 5-10 minutes figuring this out for yourself before walking inside to be told it's out of service. Second, the concession stand is incredibly small, undertrained, and understaffed. The employees have no sense of urgency and this keeps lines LONG. I've only ever seen 3 employees behind the counter at once. 
The theaters themselves are nice.. Still small though. The seats are comfortable and everything appears to be clean. 
I feel like the frustration that comes with going to this theater is not worth the ticket prices and the slow service. You risk missing your movies opening scene regardless of how early you arrive. Every. Single. Time.

Hands down the best fried chicken in St. Louis! It is a super fun spot with a fun staff. I recommend trying the pickles, if you know you know. But I would say the restaurant is small so there isn't a lot of seating, good outdoor seating so summer time is best but they do offer a drive through if u want to grab and go.

Inattentive waitress, bland food, fuddy-duddy atmosphere. Only highlight was the knowledgeable bartender whom I visited due to absence of our server to ask about a few craft beers. Get your act together, Stanley's.

We were in Tampa for a business trip last year and decided to make a pit stop at Ballyhoo Grill while driving back to Orlando. Since it was during the lunch hour we chose to casually sit at the bar area. We ordered craft beers and parmesan encrusted oysters which I'd highly recommend! Nothing too extraordinary about the restaurant itself and service was not over the top. However, when my boss left her laptop at the restaurant on accident the manager was kind enough to ship it back to her. Thank you for going above and beyond!

Awesome shop that has an "old days" feel. Turned the chairs facing the middle of the room for conversations  instead of staring at myself in the mirror. Ritchie did a great job on my cut, will definitely be returning.

I also wanted to counter some of the negative reviews.

I used a Gift Box.  I turned it in (in person, since I'm near their office) on March 11, 2013, got access to the medium-res pictures online on April 19, and got the box with my originals and the DVD on May 1, just under their eight-week estimate.  The scans are good -- these are about 500 35mm slides from the early 1970s that required some color correction.  I'm not a professional photographer, but they look good to me.

I'm a little sad that the DVD didn't include all of the medium-res (~750 x 710) images alongside the full-res (~3000 x 2800) images, and that services like "please include unretouched TIFF" cost so much.

The front office staff (Emma) was very kind and professional. My wife was not treated kindly and respectfully by the person doing her X-ray and consultation. We did not meet with Dr. Fallender so we don't have a comment but he did come highly recommended by our dentist.

Awesome place! Very laid back but good food! Cash only!!!! I had the Americano omelet which is huge! It comes with a home made biscuit that's perfect.  I also got a side of grits! Needed a little salt but other wise perfection! Nice people and good food I will be back!

Easily the best breakfast food in Indianapolis. It's really busy on the weekends but they get you in super fast. I order one of the skillets every time and it's never been bad. Even on the busiest days, the service is great

Look at those churros!!! Three of us shared the Dolce de Leche churro and it was the perfect amount. My only disappointment was that the churro had the oil fryer aftertaste. I will definitely be back to try the other flavors.
Yummy! Excellent potato salad and the atmosphere was very enjoyable! Service was impeccable!  Highly recommend!

Do yourself a favor and go here for brunch! 150% worth. the. wait. 

I had the ATB omelet with egg whites. It came with home fries topped with a perfectly balanced pico de gallo, a gorgeous little fresh fruit cup and a slice of toast. The toast was unremarkable but everything else
on the plate was WOW... So yum. 

Hubby had the biggest man meal of his life- the pancake stuffed French toast with eggs, bacon and cheese. We should have taken a picture but we were hungry and dove right in. I tried a bite and the pancake was so fluffy with a perfect butter crisped edge. I loved the cinnamon batter and subtle crunch of sugar on the outside of the French toast. Nice touch. The bacon was thick and the eggs were fluffy and plentiful.

We will seriously be thinking about this meal
for a while. I say again, yum!

Showed up at around noon on Labour Day. They were out of fried chicken for the next 25 minutes but I was there for the doughnuts! Some of the fancy doughnuts were sold out but there were still plenty of options. I bought strawberry shortcake, chocolate eclair, and vanilla spice which is a hot doughnut not a fancy doughnut, so it sells out slower and they serve it hot.

All were good, even a day later. A bit small, since back in NY we have shops like Doughnut Plant with huge doughnuts, but the flavors at Federal are very rich so you don't really need more. Chocolate eclair is especially divine. Vanilla spice was a bit salty but I didn't mind it. Beware, all of these doughnuts are a bit messy to eat, lots of crumbs.

Excellent slyly located coffee shop on my way back to work from my lunchtime walk. I cannot ever resist a vegan cookie (theirs are so good! please always have lemon sugar!) and the tea is dandy as well. Haven't gone for the food before, but I definitely have it in my back pocket for when I need a quick lunch.
Roger came and did a wonderful job which allowed to get my car out and he was professional and safe with mask. I highly recommend Roger and A1!

About a month ago I did a shout out to yelpers to find some interesting places to visit and restaurants to dine at while on a trip to nashville, and several folks recommended Margot's.  My husband and I were not disappointed. In fact we liked it so much the first evening that we then made reservations for ourselves and another guest for the next night.  She too was impressed.
Our servers on both nights were helpful and knowledgable, and offered tastings of the wines we were thinking of ordering so we could be sure we liked them.  
Meals were prepared to our orders--wonderfully rare grilled lamb and a steak of some kind for hubby. The handmade chips and aoili are crazy delicious and plentiful, enough to share.  I had sweet potato bisque with tangy goat cheese and pecans (sweet and salty are great together but hard to get just right, and this was perfect); hubby had conger eel (!) with kalamata olives and I was a bit afraid to try a bite, but he insisted and it was remarkable and delish.  With us having 5 meals on two nights with appetizers and main courses (no desserts unfortunately) we were very impressed by the selection and presentation and freshness and creativity.   
The place is funky and quirky and I like that and also appreciated having this choice among a million ribs and barbecue and buffet places.
As you Nashville folks say, if I ever get back to the area, I'm fixin' to go back to Margot's.

Be aware of this extremely dishonest business. It has being the worse rental experience I had have. They canceled my pre-online rental agreement because my plane was delayed. Their policies said "no cancellations". I never did, they did it, and charged me the full amount.
With no other option, I rented a small Toyota Yaris for $29/day! I told them "no insurance" but they still charged insurance.
In short, never use this company.

This is strictly a review for our Teppenyaki dinner. Let me start by saying the food was good, maybe even great. The service was great as well. Problem is when you go to Teppenyaki you go for the show, and this show, frankly, was a bit disturbing.

 I was there with a party of three and there was one other couple at our table, all adults.  Our chef was young and energetic so I expected a fun and exciting show. Instead I got a show full of 3rd grade humor and insults. 

The first odd turn the meal took was when our food was cooking and our chef pulled out a blue plastic train whistle, blowing on that while banging his spatula up and down saying "choo choo choo here comes the train" . 
Later he pulled out a plastic squeeze bottle full of sake and wanted to squirt it in my mouth, I said no thank you, he then said " oh come on....man up"  seriously, Man Up. This was not the only time that happened either. Later in the meal he asked me to blow out his torches. Well I couldn't get them all the way out and again he told me to "man up".
 Next he pulled out another little plastic man, pulled down his plastic pants and proceeded to pee water all over my friend, saying "don't worry it's just water it will dry"  I'm a bit speechless.....more like biting my tongue.

So now onto the food! Let's start with the appetizer.....or not. Of course an appetizer comes with all the meals, you get a choice of shrimp, calamari or tuna.

 We all put in our orders and the food starts cooking. First the fried rice and it gets served around, next is the vegies. Steaks and chicken are next, at this point I mention to my friends that we never got our appetizer, we wait.

 Finally after the rice, vegies, steaks & chicken he pulls out the tray of seafood to cook. our meal is close to finished.

 He starts cooking the shrimp, calamari and tuna. Now I ordered a combo meal that included shrimp, and the shrimp appetizer. There were 9 shrimp on the grill with everything else, all cooking the same way. The chef then put six of them on my dinner plate and three on my lonely little cold app plate that's been sitting empty the whole meal while saying "here's your shrimp appetizer". I chuckle. How the heck do you cook 9 shrimp all the same way, 3/4 of the way through the meal, then put three on a separate plate and call it an appetizer? 

I think some better chef training is in order, and maybe some age appropriate comedy training for when there are no children at the table!

Not impressed.

If you have the time and money to kill, go for it. The food was very tasty, but took a long time and cost more than I'd normally spend on breakfast. I got two acai bowls - the Indulge and Power bowl - and a muffin to go, and they all tasted great. But they took about 15-20 minutes and I was the only person in the place. I liked the atmosphere though, and I'll probably return to try one of their juices - they looked good. But the next time I want an acai bowl, I'll just make it at home.

This was my first time visiting this establishment, and I would label this one a so-so experience. My favorite part was the atmosphere and the music in the front room by the entrance. It kind of had the appeal of a sophisticated night club in an urban area in a major city. On the other hand, the service needs some major work. While they were nice, we felt rushed from our first few minutes there. The food was okay but did not have much of a wow factor. I ordered soup and a curry dish...the sauces were great, but there was something unsettling about the texture of the meat. Overall, this was an average experience.

so i was really torn between 3 and 4 stars...
so lets start with the cons....server had zero personality.  being a server myself, i know a servers position is CRUCIAL to a good meal (if you are in a fancy pants montecito restaurant especially), and even more so when you are the only customers in the whole place! and she never refilled our waters :( 
also, you have to pay for chips and salsa, and really, they aren't worth it. (from reading reviews, sounds like any good server would try to sell you on the guac, it is amazing, but it does come with most meals.  be patient)

ok, now to the good...my burrito mojado (prob spelled that wrong...wet burrito) was so good.  i got it with shredded chicken as opposed to grilled.  every part of that burrito i LOVED.  needless to say, mexican food is one of those things that you get right if you try...thats why the 3 instead of 4 stars.

but...if i am ever craving a fancy mexican place, for date night or what have you, i will be back!

I have been searching for a new car wash close to home.  Mike's was my go to but now I don't work near one.  I thought that Soft Touch was an answer to my problem.  I went there yesterday, truck looked clean ran to gas station.... when I got out to pump gas I still had soap on the truck.  No issue I'll just drive back over there and they will make it right.  Wrong,  guy said "we can run it thru again but it won't help"  I thought I'd give you a chance to make it right but they weren't interested.  Their motto is let us take pride in your ride.  you would think for $15-18 they could rinse off all the soap.  And if it missed run it thru again.  Pretty cool they have enough business they don't need mine.

Tacos Jalisco has quickly become one of my favorite places in town. It's close to where I live and the food is AMAZING! The food is authentic and the atmosphere is warm and inviting. The service is wonderful and everything about this place is worth the experience.
This restaurant is closed. The glass in many windows are smashed. Not much else to say about this place except that it is closed.

Food was very good, but the service was horrendous. We had to wait for longer than normal to order, and then our food took more than an hour to arrive after we ordered it, including a 30 or 40 minuted period when we never saw our waiter. Our waiter also did not seem particularly concerned with how long we had waited, and became defensive saying that he could not make the kitchen cook the food any more quickly. While we understood this, we would have appreciated at very least an update from him to let us know what was going on. Squeal was not exceptionally crowded when we were there, so I am not sure why everything took so long. There were also other tables that we saw wait a very long time for their food.

Great Brunch Place in St. Louis!
I can't give it the best in STL though, I'm still on the hunt (although Michael T claims Drury Inn-Union Station)

My friends and I went around 12:30 and there was a 25 minute wait for 3 people, but we hadn't all seen each other in months so we passed the time catching up in the tables outdoors. The wait staff was superbly friendly and the inside decor- CUTE!
 It looked anthropologie-esque without the attached $200 price tag and with a delicious and variable menu.  

Between the three of us we ordered the autumn hash (a seasonal special), brioche french toast and the Cali omelette (hey, gotta stay true to my roots) and an order of the donuts.

The donuts came first, oh my goodness, oh my goodness they are SO good. They were crispy on the outside, but moist and soft on the inside and rolled in a cinnamon sugar crust with a hint of citrus. They were pretty much perfect churros with a nice surprise for your tastebuds everytime you get a hint of citrus flavor. 

Brioche French toast was delicious, but I think too expensive for the price and quantity. I would still rate the french toast at Brassiere higher. The Cali Omelette was amazing and the fresh guac on top-absolutely delicious. Autumn hash was delicious with eggs and butternut squash and potatoes all mixed together in fall-y goodness.

They also have drip coffee that rotates and it was well made. 

Why is not 5 stars you ask? I can see how the wait would be annoying if it was cold outside or you had a large group. The staff were very kind and refilled our coffee and water often, but it did take quite a while for our food to come. This kind of place isn't where you could come and eat breakfast in an hour and be done. Half and Half is at least a 2 hour commitment that is worth it to try, but not a place you can frequent often for that very same reason.

Costco is a great place to come when you do not have any problems in your life so you need to create one. Your problem will be that you bought too much cereal at once, or maybe big muffins. Also you might accidentally buy a 32-pack of individual hummus cups. And then you will eat all of the hummus in 3 days because it is there and you cannot be expected to practice self-control with such a pleasant and flavorful chickpea dip. This Costco is great because it is full of white people who pretend to be in a hurry. They pretend to be in a hurry because that is what everybody else is doing. There are also free samples here. They are mostly handed out by very large women who have to sit on stools that are just regular-sized. They have sadness inside of their eyes, I think. This makes life flash before my eyes real quick because I wonder why they have to do that job. I wonder if I will ever have to do that. I start to feel sad. I wonder how many of the samples they get to eat. A lot, I hope. This Costco gives me very bad social anxiety. Mostly because it is so crowded and everybody is pushing around those giant carts. I am afraid of being in the way of one and getting accidentally smashed to death. That would not be an ideal way to go. This Costco has great bathrooms. There are lots of stalls, so you can still use the bathroom even if someone is in there doing a lot of poop all at once. Sometimes my mom buys a big churro on the way out of Costco. She eats it in the car on the way home, and asks me if I want some even though she is just eating it like a big corn dog. I say no thank you. We don't have any more conversations after that.

This place is super fun!  I'm more of a fan of it in lesser crowded times of the day (Have I mentioned that I am an old lady that can't handle the S. Tampa crowds anymore), but I always have fun.  Here are the reasons:

1)  Fantastic beer selection and very knowledgeable staff.
2)  Life size Jenga - I mean come on, really.
3)  Juke box with every song ever.
4)  Couch to nap on (don't judge).

Really though, I like Yard of Ale.  I like it, a lot.  I'm sure I will enjoy many brews in the future here.

My negative review is regarding the experience. We came tonight with the specific intention of sitting at the bar to eat dinner, have a few drinks, and watch the Nova game. The only open seats where next to a family, with kids ranging in age from 4-15. Yes, there were children sitting at the bar when it was crowded. If you are an adult and are sitting at a bar, the last thing you want to is sit next to children. Their 4 year old was coughing non stop and then when seats opened up next is, we moved down. (This isn't friendly's, it's a steakhouse) The bar tender asked why we moved and I told her the 4 year old was endlessly coughing next to us while we were trying to eat. Once we moved, the parents then put the girl in our old seats, essentially moving even closer to us. This isn't the 1980's, isn't it an ABC violation to have children at the bar?

Note: We have 3 kids, but I would never, ever seat them at a crowded bar. 

Also... We ordered both steaks medium. My rib eye came out medium well and her filet came out rare. Hers had to be sent back, so we basically ate in shifts. Food was still good, but the experience was terrible! But don't worry, I still left a 25% tip. 

$113 for dinner for 2 at a bar and this is the experience you give us? Not cool.

Dr Chiarello is a most exceptional doctor and not just because he correctly diagnosed my foot issue when three other doctors got it wrong, but more importantly he lacks any arrogance or ego you usually encounter with most doctors. He explains things so well and makes himself available whenever you might need him. As I told him myself . I would be very happy if he could be my only doctor for every condition... He will make you feel you are his friend not a patient because that's his nature and sure you will agree...

What a cute little cafe. The staff was very friendly and even accommodated my indecision on sides by letting me do half and half. It was a very relaxed ambiance and cafeteria style service (order through a line and get your tray at the end). I had the mushroom veggie burger (so good) and the arugula veggie burger (plain compared to the other). I got the broccoli salad (excellent) and the kale salad (just okay) as sides. Would definitely go back.

Excellent service by waiter Steve.  Food was both unique and delicious!  Atmosphere was joyful, open and functional.  Wide range of food options should keep everyone happy.  Price of food for quality was supperb.

This review is for food/outdoor dining experience.

The first time we went, we got burgers, crab cake sandwiches, cocktails, and beers. The burgers/crab cakes were delicious, and the fries were hot and generous. If you're looking for a good burger or crab cake, this is your spot. We were excited to come back and try more off the menu.

We recently went back and two people at our table ordered the ribs entree. One asked for a substitute of fries, and the other stuck with the potato salad. I kid you not, for $18, TWO (and a half on one) ribs came out on each plate. Two! That's not even a 1/4 rack. The rest of the plate was filled with either cold fries or a small portion of potato salad and a cup of collard greens. While the taste was OK, the food was barely warm and the two people who ordered ribs went next door for a cheesesteak after our meal. To top it off, one person at our table ordered a Diet Coke and it was flat and tasteless.

The pandemic has certainly changed dining, so I won't even comment on service other than saying everyone was nice and doing the best they could! My suggestion to patrons - if you plan to dine out here, definitely stick to the sandwiches. They are good and you will enjoy them. As for the restaurant - I understand that times are tough, but if you can't deliver a quality product with a reasonable portion, just shorten the menu until you can!

I've been coming here since I started wearing contacts in the beginning of high school.  Then I left for college, and I still came here whenever I visited my parents on holidays.  Then I left for graduate school, and I still visited whenever I visited my parents on holidays.  Now that I'm a permanent New Yorker, I still come here every time I visit my parents on holiday.  I've been a regular at this store for 10+ years... (gosh I feel OLD now).

The optometrist is personable.  We've exchanged chitchat about random topics, such as his life in DC (he's a Redskins fan, people!), and my plan to get Lasik done within the next two years.

I would advise that you get the $99, 3-year eye care plan if you wear contact lenses.  You're required to get an eye checkup every year anyways, so you might as well subscribe to this plan, which comes out to $33 per eye exam.  On top of that, you can see the optometrist for eye complications, such as pinkeye.  

The contact product plan is not the best price you can get for your contacts, but you could always shop around online since you already have an active and valid prescription at America's Best.

My only gripe about this place? Your appointments NEVER start on time.  I usually wait, on average, a good 10-15 minutes before the optometrist sees me.

We stayed here a few days during Mardi Gras and while it wasn't one of the better Marriotts we've stayed in, it was in a great location and everyone was very friendly. 

Our checkin and checkout was easy and the executive lounge is conveniently located right off the lobby. We mostly just used it for water and coffee which was great to have, but the usual snacks we enjoy at other properties were never seemed to be out and they did not provide the customery wine/beer which was disappointing. 

Our room got upgraded at checkin and was a really nice corner king with a luxurious shower, comfy bed, and a coffeemaker and mini fridge. The room was also surprisingly quiet and even if it wasn't they had complimentary earplugs which was thoughtful. 

We ordered from the bar one night and I enjoyed their chicken caesar salad and the husband's wrap and fries were pretty good. We had trouble getting room service later that night though with part of our order not making it to our room. Though we had waited over an hour for the first delivery, it would take another hour to get the missing part of the meal..so we declined. We enjoyed the executive breakfast one morning with gf labeling and a wide variety of hot and cold items along with juice, tea, and coffee. We tried to have breakfast another morning but it closed earlier than it was scheduled to so we didn't get any noms.

Overall it's a nice hotel and we thought they did a great job managing the Mardi Gras crowd. Just in the realm of Marriots it's a bit below the bar.

I'm really happy to see the old neighborhood making a comeback and people taking a chance opening up new restaurants. That being said I don't think Bread and Earth will be around for a forth year.

I went based on recommendations form other "foodies" and probably will not be back. 

The beer selection was very good, service was OK, the rye taost with butter I made when I got home had more flavor then the veggie flat bread. 

Think they benefit from the over flow from McMenimen's.

Reno location. Here we go again. Wait staff and manager were awesome. 2 of three burgers OVERCOOKED AGAIN, WTF IS IT WITH THE COOKS. On top of the fact that one of the OVERCOOKED BURGERS WAS COMPLETELY WRONG. We ordered a new Jamin Bacon burger. See the pics for the REGULAR BACON BURGER.  We received a snotty little girl seating us who didn't listen to us for our seating requirements. Again, Manager Mike and waitress Liz were awesome!!!

Yet another disappointing meal. Never ordering from this place again; unsure if the owners changed, or the idiot cashier is an asshole. I order a cheesesteak  hoagie, no steak- just the cheese and lettuce, tomatoes and cheese and onions. Keep away, clearly your order does not matter, and you'll get what you get.

DO. OT GET YOUR INSPECTION HERE. They wanted thousands of dollars to fix my car in order to pass the inspection. Took to another shop. They went over the receipt from Midas and said they would take a look at my car. After the 2nd opinion they physically showed me everything Midas said needed replacing, and confirmed it was NOT TRUE.

Great place if you are looking for Mediterranean food.  I love being able to eat outside on nice days.  We generally order a variety of things for the table so that everyone can share.  The servers are friendly and the service was good.

Fast service for the crowds that come here. 
Cute outdoor seating.
Good cafe drinks and delicious fresh beignets.
The rest of the food here is...just food... I would come here for a nice beignet breakfast/snack and enjoy the outdoor seating.

I'm very disappointed in the etiquette of this hotel... I'm in search of a venue for a wedding and was disappointed to receive a message telling me to tell our guests to bring their own kosher meal to a wedding at a 5 star hotel! This is appalling and I will NEVER step foot on this property or give my business here and make sure none of our friends who spend top dollar at resorts from Bacara, Ritz Carlton and Four Seasons who allow you to bring your own kosher caterer for a wedding costing minimum $40k just for the venue fees !! Shame on you.

A nice restaurant for those that like a lot of different flavors from the South & Caribbean.  Varied menu and there is something for everyone.  The restaurant itself is cavernous and noisy.  Outdoor seating available but you are chancing hot weather & humidity in the summer months.  Reservations are recommended but make sure they do it correctly....such as getting your email address for confirmation.  Kids that answer the phone are not that competent, so beware.
Food is good but, not great.  Main reason for me is because it is highly salted and it ruins the flavor of seafood.  Not recommended for a date night, quiet time together, etc.
Small, quiet, quaint place serving tasteful food. look forward to going back.

The sampler was good, the mashed potatoes were good, the chicken fried steak not so much too much breading and the chicken looked and tasted rough. I came for fried chicken on Tuesday's and Sundays (I heard) but after ordering the waitress came back to say only for dinner on Tuesday I got there at 2PM. I used the stall bathroom and my feet began to slide out from under me as if I was standing on grease. Online menu stretched and blurry, kind of pricey... Our waitress seemed afraid...

The place is good, clean and friendly service. The only drawback is that there are always people in the parking lot asking for money. I think the manager should keep there customers safe inside and out!
Sir Pizza is great. We eat in or take out at least once a week!  The crust is delicious.   We love that it's located in a former pizza joint from the 80's too!

20 minutes for a toasted bagel and cream cheese and a coke.  This place is a mess. 5hey need to figure something out.  It's supposed to be an easy lunch place,  it's not like it's a sit down dinner.  For God's sakes  there isn't enough room to stand and it's always loud near the cooking area.  They yell out a person's name when it's done and hope you hear it over the way to loud noise. Even the employees say that the tickets are a mess and confusing.  I won't be back again.

Went for lunch on Mother's Day. They only have a brunch menu till 4, which is mainly breakfast food not many of lunch options. My boyfriend asked for a Budweiser and the waitress assumed they weren't out and offered bud light. She went and verified with the bar after that and they did have Budweiser which cost $6.50. We ordered a calamari appetizer which had a kick that was enjoyable. I had the avocado toast and side of fruit. It was okay nothing spectacular. My boyfriend had the burger and truffle fries. The burger wasn't bad but again nothing spectacular and the truffle fries just tasted like regular fries. For a $4 up charge you should definitely be able to taste the truffle oil so that was pretty disappointing. 

The view is very pretty and they also had live music that was great to listen too! It was too loud and was enjoyable to listen too! Wasn't overly crowded. Server was nice but lacked knowledge about their beers.

I suggested this place to a friend and got on their website ahead of time to check out dine-in issues related to COVID. But because their site isn't particularly mobile friendly (the screen gets dark and jumps around a lot), I kind of gave up too soon. What I didn't see is that they prefer call-ahead reservations in an effort to keep the restaurant's waiting room clear. They ask that you wait outside and then they give call-backs for seating. That's all fine and good when this policy went into effect last May, but it was in the 20's when we were there last weekend. I walked in, gave my name for the first time and was asked to wait in my car. My car? That was a little off-putting. Luckily my friend arrived within 2 minutes and we decided to skirt the system by grabbing coffees at the bar.
Simple enough, right? But there's no sitting or even standing at the bar currently. Uggh. I hadn't brunched in Indy since we could do so outside and just wanted to relax. Oh how the two of us health workers wished we could just flash our vaccination cards carried in our purses. With their ceramic-mugged coffees in hand as leverage, we found a spot with two chairs near the waiting area and hoped we wouldn't get asked to leave.
They do as good as any place I've seen maintaining social distancing, keeping every other table or so free. Between that and the tables that were reserved for folks who had yet to answer/arrive from their call-backs, it seemed like they were always well under 50% capacity at any one time. 
I had a specialty egg dish, the lobster and Brie omelette. The lobster and Brie sauce is layered on top with cream cheese filling the actual inside of the omelette. That's a bit over-the-top rich, and I might have ordered something else had I read the menu thoroughly. But we were there to catch up not scour the menu and though busy gabbing, we weren't rushed by our server. She noticed I mentioned a desire to be gluten free when I ordered grits as my side, so she took the liberty of adding GF toast instead of the standard English muffin. That was appreciated. We asked for something just about every time she checked on us, and she always met it with a smile. 
This is a very solid brunch place, with some specialty dishes you won't find at too many other places and a great location just off of 465-exactly what we needed.

We were definitely not blown away by this place. We ordered through Uber Eats so maybe if we had eaten there it would have been a lot better. It came quickly and was packaged nicely.

I had the hot chicken and it was so overly spicy that it was hard to enjoy. Don't get me wrong, I love spicy food and hot chicken is one of my favorite foods but I wouldn't exactly classify this as "Nashville style hot chicken." I really enjoyed it coming on sourdough bread though. That was a nice touch!  The fries weren't great, could have used some seasoning. But once again it was delivered so I'm sure they're better at the restaurant.

My hubby got the ugly chick sandwich and didn't care for it. He was expecting a piece of fried or grilled chicken instead it was almost like a chicken salad with bacon and goat cheese. He said he would have loved the sandwich had it been an actual piece of chicken. 

We split the honey chipotle tenders and they were okay. They were definitely heavy on the sweet with hardly any hint of spice. 

The best thing that we had were the fried onions. I would eat a whole bowl of them! The breading was perfect and they were perfectly crispy! 

My mom said her regular chicken tenders were "super-duper" yummy. So, you get an A+ from mom and my 1 year old. It may be enough to warrant a return trip from us. Hubby and I will just pick different menu items.

The service was as horrible as the food was AMAZING. the waitress would just dissappear...where the hell did she go? was she running a flea market in the back?!

menus? wait 15 minutes
beer? wait 15 minutes
lunch? wait 45 minutes
dessert? wait 15 minutes.

We were really happy when the food finally came tho. The lamb kebob's were perfect - crusty on the outside, juicy and tender on the inside. and the baklava was dense & gooey.

We'll definitely be back, just at a different table.
5 min/car in drive through and drive through online is not fast food. I doubt I will ever be back to the Washington street location.

I would actually like to give this place a 2.5 but I'm in a generous mood tonight. 

I went here for iftaar, which is the meal you eat after you break fast during Ramadan--the point being I was freakin' starving. 

I ordered the vegetable appetizer which included a mix of 4 different appetizers. The only thing that was edible was the pakora. Everything else was not good. I was very disappointed because it was the first thing I had to eat in like 20 hours. 

For the next course I ordered the House of India biriyani which was horrendous. It tasted awful and had so many raisins and nuts in it. Not a big fan. The chicken tikka masala was all right, but nothing I would go back for. The naan was also decent, but you can't really mess up naan. 

Overall, the service was good, but the food sucked. Never going back.
I went there because of the great reviews and locality.  Very expensive and was insolent.  Quess she doesn't care how she treats customers anymore.

Been here twice.  Both times wings were great and came fast.  Steak fries are very good.  Could be a little cheaper but tolerable.  Friendly wait staff and management. Manager took great care of us.

It is a good place, but I like the Clearwater one a little  better, except for...Paula.

On a side note: Paula is a godddess.  She is absolutely beautiful and should be a model.
They always have plenty of fresh pies to choose slices from, and they're cheesesteaks are great. Can't go wrong here...

My boyfriend and I went several times always hoping for a better experience as we want to support local business. The coffee house is attractive and the coffees and chais are quite good but the PRICES.... unbelievable-- in the stratosphere. Especially since we moved here from the SF Bay Area and are used to ridiculous prices, He had a dirty chai and it was delish but was $7.50!!. A little cookie is $4.25.  We will not be back until they offer more bang for the hard- earned buck.
Clean place, friendly staff.  Food is a little bland but ok.  Looking for the best breakfast place.  Still looking.

If I could give zero stars I would - just awful service. Sat down at the bar bartender asked if I could hang on a minute said sure - but he didn't give me a menu to look at either for either good or drink while I waited a few minutes  another patron sat down while the bartender went down the bar and talked to the wait staff, a couple were at the bar too, and tried to catch his attention for a good few minutes, meanwhile the other bartender was more interested in pottering around doing inventory than serving, even though there was folk looking to be served - continued to wait, bartender came back still ignored the couple looking for service and then me and went to the other patron sat there told him if he could hang on a minute then went back the the other end of the bar ignored all of us and talked to the wait staff again, eventually he came back to the couple, and could not after three attempts get them what they wanted, the other patron at this point gets up and leaves, the bartender then gets to me I order a beer and ask for a menu the one I get has a drink spilled on it and is still wet and to top it off the beer is flat & he's back talking to the wait staff - so I'm done and close out and the couple are still looking to get what they need. 

So to sum up you'll wait, wait some more the staff appear busy doing nothing you'll still be waiting & you'll eventually realize you are not getting this time back and that Vitoscope Hall would not be in business if it wasn't part of the hotel

I love this store. Where other groceries have a tiny section of leafy greens this one has huge assortment of many kinds. Where okra is found in tiny amounts in other stores this one has a table full. The same with ginger root found in abundance. The people are so nice and friendly not only the ones who work there but the customers. I ask for help with the Indian names of items and everyone is so nice about explaining things to me. I am older and wanting to eat healthy and that is the place to go. I just wish they had a sheet to translate the Indian name to the English name on items in the freezer. If you buy large eggs that look like small balls every where else buy eggs here. They are really large and sell for less too. I love love love this store and all the people in it.
I went there for lunch. The presentation looks good for every dish. I liked the mussels but not the gumbo...they put potato salad in gumbo and I think that's weird...

It's a cute gift shop with a tea room. I go here often. 
The gift shop might be their main focus and secondary is the food. 
Most of the time I  go with the special of the day. I have not been disappointed yet. There croissants are amazing and fresh. 
I really enjoy their Tuna on tomato or chicken salad on tomato. Their quiche is good when it's fresh and not overlooked. Just make sure it's "today's" I have  ask and was told sometimes it was yesterday's quiche. I am not a fan of leftovers at a restaurant. 
Their salads aren't anything spectacular. They are the typical simple salads. 
It's not a "high end" tea. It's an average tea house that does charge a little too much for what you get.  
I walk out hungry sometimes, my fault. I don't get desert. I know I would not be hungry if I did. 
I do snag some chocolate at the check out for later. It is worth the splurge. 
The Service is usually petty good. They are kind and quick to serve. 
ONLY Once - One time only I had bad service. I was by myself and it took me over 30 min to take my order - 45 min to get my drink and 1:15 min to get my food. I don't mind slow at a tea house but that was too slow. And no he only had two other tables. It just had opened and there was only a few tables. You shouldn't judge a place by one visit. EVERYONE and Every restaurant has a bad day.

Service and food was very good. This place offers to empty the steam bag into a serving dish for you, whichI prefer. And the gloves they provide are actually useful. I really enjoy this prep style for seafood. Didn't try any fried options, but the steam bag options were very good.

If I could give it less than one star I would.  Rented and paid for a car a month in advance.  went to pick it up and immediately when we walked in the door the person behind the counter said " We don't have any cars."  There were already two people in the store.  We stood there in disbelief. Then he said, "Do you have a reservation?" When we said yes he tried to say we made it thru a third party.  When we proved him wrong he said, "Well I don't have any cars."  Then proceeded to give two people that were there cars.  These people didn't have reservations.  He said he would call the airport store to see if they had any and we asked for the same rate.  Airport didn't have any and if they did wouldn't honor the rate.  Needless to say, since we were leaving the next morning,  we had to hunt around for a rental car and pay a premium rate because it was a last minute rental.  Never, I repeat, never use this company.  The staff was extremely rude and not helpful at all.

We rented kayaks from Paddle Sports Center and had a great time paddling around the marina. The staff was really helpful and friendly. They showed us where to go, how to get back safely, and they even gave us cool stickers. 

Kayaking was a great activity to enjoy on a sunny day - highly recommend. 

They also have a little shower in the back in case you need to rinse off after your adventure.
Fish tacos too salty:( Thoroughly disappointed in excuses of certain menu items not available. No desire to return

Amazing dinner and drinks! We went to Autograph for a special occasion and everything surpassed our expectations. The food - especially the special Chilean sea bass - was so delicious; cooked perfectly & probably the best fish I've ever had. The atmosphere is eclectic and fun but still intimate enough for good conversation. Can't wait until our next visit there!

Great server, great location, but no lobster and no bread bowls for the clam chowder-- two things I would have liked. Had I known they didn't have these on the menu, I would not have gone. But great fish and chips, good spot on the harbor.

This is truly the most gorgeous department store and a must-visit on a trip to Philly for the history and grandeur! Unfortunately they're doing some renovations and everything I touched on the ground floor had a layer of white chalky dust on it - the pricey designer bags and accessories and all. I understand it must be hard to conduct maintenance and keep a store running, but maybe you need extra staff to keep the store and its wares clean during this time. I wasn't inclined to buy because items were dirty and already seemed used. Ignored by staff despite it being a quiet Sunday and during the St Patrick's Day parade...and sadly this wasn't because they were busy cleaning :(  

Looking forward to a better experience next time!
We like the wings from here. Like mild wings but can never find the "right" mild? When you order tell the waitress you want the mildest mild. Sounds weird but it seems to magic!!

They are great, what more can I say.  Each item gets its own bag, high quality work, high quality hangers...they are good!

My recommendation; don't use them for your everyday items as they are pricey.  I am not saying they are not worth the price but if it is just a regular everyday shirt, slacks or dress you may want to consider another cleaners.

For my formal wear, outwear and delicates they are the best choice!

If I could give zero stars I would.  Mark sr gave an estimate to do our driveway and also an estimate to our neighbor for his driveway. We were suppose to get both driveways done on the same day. After 3 times of scheduling our driveways and never showing up, we have decided that this job was not something he wanted to do. No calls to say he was not showing up. Very unprofessional. After reading recent reviews about the quality of the work, I feel relieved to not have to deal with them.
Didn't arrive for first appointment, followed by their rescheduled appointment. Lol, what a joke.

Service was quick, the staff friendly, and the coupons you can print from their website made it really cheap. Went for an oil change, paid about $18 and it took just short of 45 minutes (I had made an appointment online)
There's not much of a waiting room, but there's LaCumbre plaza to hang out at

The lentil soup was AMAZING!!! Super filling and hearty for lentils. The location is pretty unique and has plenty of indoor and outdoor options for seating. The coffee selections were very good. I have a peppermint mocha latte which was quite delicious. I highly recommend this place.

Was excited to try this restaurant though I had never heard of Fabio Viviani the "celebrity" chef who opened this restaurant. I did hear every waiter/waitress bring it up, which was so odd. Having moved from Vegas in May, you don't have every server welcome you to Bobby Flay, or Giada de Laurentiis restaurant. It was silly.

The atmosphere was cool. There were plenty of TV's with sports on, but had more of a restaurant  bar/grill vibe than a straight up sports bar. Place was well decorated easy to navigate. The staff was great from the time we walked in, to getting drinks at the bar to our table service. Even though they were slammed (30 minute wait) everything came out quickly and correctly. 

The menu was an interesting mix of Pasta and Burgers with some American classics mixed in. Some items coming out looked really good. Specifically the smoked mozzarella and the pastas. We decided fairly early on that the pretzels looked DELICIOUS as we saw them fly out of the kitchen, but a lot of the appetizers looked good.

The pretzel was very good. Has the nice crunchy exterior with a soft inside. You could smell the yeasty fresh by bread. Salted just right and Texture was perfect. Beer cheese and mustard also complementary. 

I was going to give the place 4 stars, but I'm so irked by the menu that I can't do it. I ordered the "salmon risotto". While the salmon was great, the risotto was 100% not Arborio or other risotto rice. It was barley. If I knew that I wouldn't have ordered it. The grain was dense and tough. I understand the need for a stronger grain to support a huge filet of salmon and that aborio wouldn't bold up, but that is so disingenuous to call what was on my plate risotto. Especially from a "celebrity chef". 

This was only compounded by the fact he called his cottage pie a shepherds pie. Point out a shepherds pie should be lamb, and cottage pie is beef. Also the cheddar crust is probably cheese- its, which is fine but calling it a cheddar cheese crust again is a misrepresentation. The whole menu presentation coupled with everyone talking about chef Viviani just sets an underlying ambiance of pretension that I was not a fan of. 

As far as tase and flavor profile, the salmon on the barley was good. There was a touch of sweetness but not too much. The char was nice and the fish was cooked perfectly. The tomatoes were a nice touch and sauce/broth the barley was cooked in was pleasant. I do feel like the dish lacked any real unique flavors. Pretty standard. Not what I expect from a "celebrity chef" having been to many of those establishments back home in Las Vegas.

The cottage pie was also good. A little bland on the meet part. Needed a touch more salt at a stage or two of the preparation. The cheddar jalepeno mashed potatoes were good, though the jalepeno flavor didn't come through at all. 

This was a solid 4 stars if you get rid of all the pretentious garbage that Fabiano built into his menu and staff. But if you're going to rub your celebrity in everyone's face, at least deliver something special.
This place has forgotten part of my order 3 times in a row...this is the last time it happens to me....Do yourselves a favor...Don't go to this place...It's a joke.

Perfect place for locals and tourists to grab a drink and a bite to eat smack dab on the beach! You can't get any better than that.
