I've been to bongos twice, once with a party of 8+ and once with a coworker from out of town. Both times I've had great service and great seafood. The last time I ordered the fish tacos and would have loved to have fresh guacamole instead of prepackaged. But the fish is so fresh and delicious - nothing like fish on the beach.

Since you are on the beach your going to pay beach prices - for a two drinks and two entrees you can easily rack up a $50 tab. But for that view, especially at sunset - it's worth.

I was excited to try this restaurant since I love Indian food. I was so disappointed by this place. The papadum appetizer was stale. They also give us a very small portion of rice despite ordering two entrées. We were taking leftovers home and the server gave an attitude when I asked for additional rice since there was none left. Overall, not a good experience and would not come back

Erin Rose is legendary and for good reason. Their bar tending service is unmatched - solid drinks and incredible hospitality. Carlos and Jacob will take great care of you and make you feel at home. They're also really great at checking vaccination cards so you can drink, at ease. I can't wait to come back !

I went here today before work around 9:45. I was waiting for the line to move up as I was next to the microphone where we order. As I was waiting, the man employee on the microphone who took my order forgot to mute himself. So I could here his conversation which will prevent me from visiting this location again. He begins to tell his coworker that the water he's using for the coffee looks like toilet water. The coworker begins laughing and he says "no really like from a porta potty it looks so gross". They both continue laughing and I started recording at this point just as he noticed he still had his microphone on. I wish I could have gotten this whole exchange. Am never coming to this location again, especially when they find that funny. Don't go here. The employees do not care about the quality of their food (I mean it's fast food but I still expected better than for them to joke about how gross the water is!). Along with all of this, I got 3 items. They forgot 1 of them. Even after the lady repeated my order to me she forgot 1 of the things she even said. Don't go here. Just, don't.

We purchased two pizzas, a Porky Fig and a Socrates Revenge, and took them straight to our hotel next to Dewey's. Because we did not eat at Dewey's, I cannot speak to the quality of service. 

We couldn't get thin crusts. 

Neither pizza had a crisp crust. 

The Porky Fig pizza was so sweet it should have been labeled a desert pizza. The pork was much too salty, so after two bites, I picked the pork off my slices. 

The Socrates Revenge pizza had toppings I liked but the sauce was ho-hum. Because the crust was thick and it wasn't crispy, essentially we were eating good toppings on wads of doughy bread.  

The upshot is, I wasn't as impressed as most reviewers. I was intrigued by the names and  descriptions of the pizzas, so I was disappointed by finding them lacking

On a positive note, the food is very good, competitive prices, and friendly wait staff. So why the low mark? This place has two things wrong with it: appearance and ambience. The first thing noted is all the cigarette butts, trash, unmaintained landscape, and door mats hanging from the handrails at the entry doors. 

The second thing noted is the apparent policy of this restaurant to wet mop floors with bleach in the entryways; wash and hang out floor mats by the entry doors and leave mop, bucket and bleach out so that customers have to walk around to get to a table. All of this during normal business hours. We've been told that this is the practice with another Taco Giro location but "nobody complains". We've asked the manager of this location these questions and complained a number of times with the same response: they keep doing it.

Well, we've complained and its done no good so it's time to find another Mexican restaurant.

Went to honeys recently and not sure we will go back. Staff is not only pretty caught up in being hip, but our particular server seemed almost annoyed by having to wait on us, and only came over after I asked hostess if anyone would be serving our table (after about 10 minutes of sitting with no one even coming to get us coffee). Food is good - taters and biscuits are especially yummy - tho $4 for 2 pieces of bacon (literally) is way too much. (If you are going to charge $4 for a side, at least give the customer $4 worth of it). All in all as much as I would love to love honeys, the loves not there and we will have breakfast elsewhere in the future.

The food is decent. The potato cakes are the best thing on the menu! We have been there several times and that's the reason I come back. The biggest problem is each time we've been there the hostess and staff that crowd around her are unprofessional talking about personal matters. I watched the hostess tell people including us there would be a wait and the restaurant had numerous tables open. If I was the owner I would be livid! The number of people that walk away because of this is enormous. I just don't understand it.

I'd actually give it 3 and a half stars if I could.
We've eaten there before (last time was a year ago) and it blew us away most of the time. Not 100% consistent food though.

Got a take-out order there the other day and was told 10 minutes. 30 minutes later, I got my stuff. Ok...I can deal with that. it's NOLA after all.

The pulled pork sammach was almost as good as usual though a bit drier than I remembered. Luckily, I asked for extra sauce and cole slaw on top.

The potato salad side was ok and my girlfriend who loved Mac and Cheese wasn't too impressed with that side.

It's still one of the better places in NOLA for bbq but there are times when it's just not as good as usual.
I'll go back, of course and probably have better meals again. This time...just not that wonderful.

Pricilla visited our home which we just moved into, so it was quite bare.  She informed me her services were "pricey but worth it".  From the looks of our home, and the way I was dressed -in scrubs after an overnight call (I am a doctor), she probably thought we were poor and couldn't afford her services.  She stated she would get back to me with estimates but never did.  I sent her two email reminders but never heard back from her.  Well, we ended up getting another decorator who did an excellent job!  I'm sure she does high end homes, but no need to be snobby!

Went to SoDo for dinner tonight and three of the four dinners were excellent.  A little off-putting that they don't give you bread with your dinner (or at least offer it)--just seems a little cheap for such a nice place.  We'll give it another try though.

If you want the most violent case of food poisoning this is your place! I ate here on Friday 8/11/17 around 4:00. I will never eat at a McDonalds again after how sick I was. I got chicken nuggets, and clearly they weren't kept at the proper temperature. Eat at your own risk here. I tried called all day on Saturday to let them know so they can try to be safer in the future and never picked up the phone all day. I finally got through to a manager named Sarah on Sunday at 6pm and she was extremely rude and unapologetic, completely unprofessional. For the sake of everyone out there, avoid it!

This place sucks! Out waitress was so rude! She forbade me from ordering another roll of sushi until I had finished my previous order. We ordered the all you can eat sushi and it happened that my husband didn't like the salmon sushi so we wanted to order something else instead. After she refused to let us order, she finally she came back and let us order more. At the end of the meal she said she would have to charge us for NOT FINISHING! Are you kidding me??? Where the hell do you hear of such a thing. If they don't want to throw away sushi don't have an all you can eat buffet!!!! It was awful don't go there!!!!

Never a difficult drive to this place. Good selection of Goods, best deals on eggs, cottage cheese, chicken, berries, avocadoes, and Gluten free products like chips. Lines are quick and staff is always helpful and friendly...Do not care for the vendors blocking the isles on weekends however...I'm in a hurry people.

Fantastic location.  Overall Solid service (if not a bit, Overzealous).  Over-hyped, Overpriced, chain-style food. Simply Over it.

White tablecloths, tables jammed in the space, there's nothing particularly original (not surprising, of course), special or different about this place.  Big salads, nicely sized pizzas, all at a hefty price tag and the end result is food that tastes like half of what it costs. Blah.

Just what we need.  Another 'chain' type restaurant that pawns itself off as something original. I'm sorry, but that just won't do for this town. 

On a related note, as the amazing and delicious Noble Cookery closes, a Serafina opens.  What's wrong with this picture?

I am a regular at Ruth Chris on Fulton. Went in tonight and the server was rude. It looked as if he was upset we were seated! He came slamming dishes on the table without greeting us. He didn't even tell us his name. We was sat at 6:47 and didn't receive an appetizer until 7:49. I asked for the manager THREE times!! Just to simply ask for a new server. The manger ignored us 3 times. Once he came he basically said No! It's nothing he can do about anything. Didn't apologize for our poor service. No sympathy or anything. We asked his name and he said Mike. We asked his last name and he said "you only need to know Mike. I'm the only manager name Mike!" Extremely rude and heartless to not care about our treatment at the restaurant. I eat at this location more than 10 times a month and this is how I was treated. I am infuriated and I'll never be back!

The food was ok, but we all lost our appetite when the slim bartender with straight long hair came on shift and brushed her hair right in front of us and preceded  to pull out her deodorant and apply it. That ruined it for all of us!

We went for our anniversary. Wow!! Best steak we ever had! We had the ribeye with crabmeat and bearnaise. Perfectly cooked and seasoned! Service was impeccable, busy Saturday night but functioned like a machine! Anthony, our amazing server recommended we get different side dishes because they are large enough to share. He was spot on! We shared mushrooms and onion rings. I didn't care for the onion rings. I guess I thought they would be the thick rings. They were stringy. My husband enjoyed them. We also got the famous St. Elmo shrimp. Sooooo good! Building was beautiful, barhrooms were clean. Great time! Can't wait until we go again. I was to try the prime rib pizza.

It's really not all that. Especially for what they are charging for the quality and for the PORTIONS! I can get just as good of a cheesesteak at Subway for half the price and a larger sandwich at that. I could tell my fries were cooked hours ago and reheated back in the deep fryer. $10.85 for a regular old cheeseteak and a small cup of reheated fries was a complete waste of $ and I will not be back.

Was recommended to Ms. Tootsies by a local during a business trip to Phila.  At first was a little apprehensive to give it a try due to most soul food restaurant when you're out of town are overrated and underseasoned.  Boy was I wrong!!!! Ms. Tootsies did not disappoint, the staff were attentive, and food was amazing.  From the time I walked in the tone was set with the ambiance and RnB was playing in the background. Tunes from the early 90s to Beyonce's latest had me grooving subtly in my seat.  I order the fried chicken with rice/gravy and string beans. Everything was seasoned, hot , and met my expectations. I would definitely recommend this spot to anyone who's looking for a soul food spot in the area.

Waffle house is the ultimate destination after a night of imbibing! Not many places open all night with decent greasy food to soak up the alcohol, save Taco Bell! That isn't to say it can only be enjoyed while drinking. But be aware it can draw in some seedy customers from time to time.

Their menu is simple. It's not gonna wow you when you come in, and the food while good, is hardly anything to write home about. To be honest, I only come for two things in particular. 1) Their hash browns. They give you the option to load them up with all sorts of toppings like ham chunks,  veggies, cheese, onions, etc... They can make a boring breakfast or lunch plate a little more exciting. 2) Two words: pecan waffle! It's awesome. I've made many trips and just ordered a couple of these. They are that good. It's a waffle loaded with crushed pecans and delivered nice and crispy. If these two items didn't exist on their menu, there would be little reason to visit unless greasy food was just calling your name late at night. All being said, they are average at best, but often hit the spot.
I just had a pedicure at Ladue this past week and was quite disappointed. It felt rushed and just wasn't great overall. I'm not sure I would come back for a pedicure.

The only thing glorified about this place is the wait, which I was told would be 45 minutes but actually turned out to be enough time standing in the cold for a set of 7.5 inch icicles to grow from my mustache, which was not on my face when I began the wait. Once the staff of this establishment deemed my wait sufficient to prove myself worthy of their service and food I was seated with a menu for approximately three seconds and it was made clear if I didn't know what I wanted after such a wait I would no longer be welcome. 

A suggestion to improve this is to provide one menu per group while we wait in the cold.  Don't worry we'll have more than enough time to mull things over, surf social media, and obtain a college degree before we even need to consider what it is we would like in the time you allow us to wait for a table. 

In fairness the food is decent, which is great because if it was bad I would have blown a vessel when I received the bill which seemed to be confused for what I can only assume is the amount NASA spends on fuel per space flight. I don't remember ordering seventeen drinks with my breakfast but the bill easy covers all alcohol for any frat party on any weekend. 

If the business model for this place to serve overrated food at larger than are reasonable prices, keeping people interested with a line out the door around the block and into the next closest breakfast place, they are a solid A plus. Unfortunately for them, I like quick service, good food, and not making my appearance in the prehistoric time to have a spot in the front of the line.

Tried The Eagle last night and I was less than impressed. The wait was long so I was placed on a waiting list. Once I received a text, I came to the hostess for my seat. I was greeted by a rude hostess who was dressed like a Las Vegas cocktail waitress. I'm still confused on why she had everything hanging out of her shorts. Her appearance isn't one I would want customers to see prior to a meal. 

Next we were seated at a penitentiary style table. Yes, I felt like I was in prison. I was seated next to people I don't know. We decided to move to the outdoor bar due to the LOUD music. The music was awfully loud. The outside bar was much nicer. We sat down and were immediately helped. We ordered our drinks and looked at the menu. I ordered fries and my friend ordered chicken. Well 2 mins later, cold chicken arrived at our table.  About 20 mins after the chicken arrived, I was served my fries. Fries were excellent!!

Finally we asked for her bill. Well the bartender was already given our credit cards to open our tab. So I was surprised when the bartender asked for our credit cards again due to her being "confused." Well our bills came with my friends name on both. She ended up paying for both meals since it was such a mess. Of course to big surprise, when we looked at the receipt to sign- it had a total strangers name on it.  Ughh- we sat there for a while helping the bartender do her job. 

Hope I never come back!

I was insulted by the staff after being in the bar for about 2 minutes. While waiting to order drinks and waiting for a friend to finish in the restroom, the bartender announced over the microphone to the entire bar, "hey, if youre going to be a perv and take pictures of the girls on your phone, you can at least buy a drink." We were standing amongst ourselves minding our own business, phones pointed at the floor and texting.It was embarrassing and incredibly insulting to be called out like that in front of the entire bar. I would never come to this bar again. The bar was dirty, smelled bad, and very poorly maintained on top of it. Don't waste your time.

Underwhelmed by flavor (we shared the Korean, carnitas, hot chicken, and tequila lime tacos) either they were too dry or just badly cooked. Their Korean taco was the most disappointing it didn't have much flavor. It was like if you used half the kool aid packet it was just kinda "watered down" flavor.  Tourists visiting THIS ISN'T WORTH YOUR TIME. We didn't even finish our food it was that bad.

I feel kind of sad for this place! 2nd time visiting when coming to town. I remember it being sooooo good. Unfortunately the service was so terrible it ruined the meal. There was a 20-25 min wait for 2 (around 7pm). Once we got our table, I'm glad we had baby wipes because the table had bird poop on it. Such a simple fix that could have been taken care of before we sat down. There were at least 4-5 occasions the busser and server could have taken our dirty plates from the appetizer. They sat the whole meal in the way. When I asked the server for more water he looked at the cup to see how much I had drank first. Full disclaimer I understand the need to be patient during these covid times but this was not a reflection of a shortage or overworked staff. Next time we'll try a different spot in town. Bummer.

He was pleasant and easy to deal with, returns calls He got us through some really ruff times !  I would recommend at least talking to him if you are having financial issues. It might be easier than you think to save your home ect.  He helped me save mine. Even though there  were some unusual issues with my case he saw it through.

This place caught our eye while driving by one evening so we came back during the day and sat on the third floor. I had high hopes based on the cool location and the live music. But I was disillusioned and sorely disappoined with the food and mediocre service. I didn't realize this was a touristy chain type restaurant. It was pricey for what we had and the food lacked flavour. I had the grilled grouper sandwich with fries. There was a choice of blackened or broiled so perhaps those options would have been a better choice. The fries were the best part of the meal. The husband had the fish and chips and also found the fish "very plain", but also agreed that the fries were good. One piece of advice I wish I had known before coming is that you can purchase parking for 2 hours at the hostess desk instead of paying $6 for 2 hours in public parking. If you are looking to have drinks and enjoy the beautiful view this place is great. If you're looking for a nice meal I'd try elsewhere less touristy.

Stopped in last night for dinner at CoreLife Eatery. Was looking for something different to try and this fit the bill.  Their mission is to serve clean, healthy meals. We reviewed the menu and I chose the Spicy Thai Chicken & Rice Noodles. My mom chose the Broccoli Cheddar soup. 

The noodle dish had spicy thai antibiotic-free chicken, spicy thai rice noodles, napa cabbage, sesame sriracha sprouts, scallions, peppers, cilantro, and thai chili sauce. All along the way they made sure I wanted the ingredients they were adding. This was a pretty spicy dish and I thoroughly enjoyed it! The noodles were cooked al dente with just the right bite and I thought the napa cabbage gave it a bit of freshness. If you like a little heat I recommend this dish! There was also so much to eat that I had enough to take home. 

They had different types of lemonades th hat I indulged in but found I liked the beet lemonade the best.

Would give this place 6 stars if I could. Don't really remember this place too much (drunk) but I do remember drunk crying and making a scene, the server gave me free ice cream to make me feel better and although I continued to cry, the next day remembering what had happened I was really pleased to know someone had given my drunk crying ass some free ice cream. Really happy I came here and I think it was really tasty from what I remember. Ps. Im fine, I don't know why I was crying, life is pretty great.

I ordered this door dash. I'm really hungry. This was $8.50. A normal waffle is like $9 so I thought this would be similar in size. They don't have any pictures uploaded. To my disappointment, I got two smashed pieces of waffles. Now I know things are packaged weird because of delivery, but they need to be descriptive on their site. This is what you are getting for the price of a waffle that would be a meal. This is not enough to feed an adult. The higher price for this small amount of food was misleading. 

I don't know how to reply to the owner specifically. They messaged me saying that I ordered from the Murfreesboro location. I ordered the food to a Nashville residence so I don't see how it's possible that I could've gotten the order from Murfreesboro. I know where I ordered my food from.
Friendly staff and very good food at the grille. Too much residential housing, lack of interesting holes and condition of the course as a whole.

We ordered this for lunch today. Went on line placed the order. Everything was good. Then we got our food. (We are a couple blocks down the road. So it should be hot right?) It was cold, fries were over cooked and cold. The chilli was in inedible. It had so much cheese you could not taste anything but cheese. Salad was sad looking barely covered the bottom of the container. Was not a good experience. Will not make that mistake twice. One guy had to go and get Wendy's.
Heidi has been going to see them for the last 10 years. They are amazing with their furry patients!
Robin is attentive to my car and I always feel confident he will clean the interior of my car like it was his own car.
Wife and I stopped here after walking Tumamoc Hill and what an amazing spot.  Bacon breakfast burritos and some outstanding drinks! Highly recommend the signature tea. A must visit

Food has zero flavor and was soggy. No salt, barely any sauce and noodle dishes were dryZ got a caprese salad and they only gave me two tomato slices and 2 cheese. For 10$ I expected way more. Do not recommend at all.
This store is open. Couldn't get anyone to help me.  Not much of a CD or movie selection.

I was working on a last minute event for my office, and we were in a time crunch to get some croissants and other treats. This location jumped into action and popped croissants into the oven for us and packaged everything up in beautiful boxes for our clients on very short notice. We appreciate them saving the day! And everything was DELICIOUS!

If I could give 0.25 of star I would.  My server was very nice.  The food was terrible.  The wait was long.  The alcoholic beverage that I ordered tasted like it had no alcohol in it.  Will not return.

I'm going to be honest- this hotel has all the makings of a great hotel- it has great amenities for the price- it had been decorated nicely, has large televisions, free wifi, pool, hot tub, fitness room, it was efficient and even comfortable... but sadly they aren't staying on top of needed maintenance and repairs- that should be an ongoing continuous process in a large hotel but it just isn't happening...  and if it doesn't start getting taken care of this hotel is going to completely fall apart around them..  most of the negative reviews are based on this overall problem- as for my experience I generally stay at the Grand Sierra when visiting Reno but the rooms I had wanted were booked- this hotel is located very close to the resort so I felt it was a great compromise- there's even an hourly shuttle provided... another great advantage- and it was about 80 dollars a night cheaper too- we had a few bumps in the road- we were missing the bathroom plug part but was provided with one quickly- nothing major- but then again I travel frequently so my expectations are pretty realistic and because of that I'm surprised at the other reviews.. it isn't bad and the service I received was great- they went out of their way for me- overall a good value

Another one of my go-to casual restaurants when it comes to Italian. The food and service was good. We came for the never ending pasta and couldn't even get past the first plate. Those damn endless breadsticks I tell ya.... they get me every time!

This review is for the bar only - not the hotel.

Being just a few years removed from college, I was turned on to this place from a few friends who claimed it catered to my age and demographic in addition to it's exc.  I've been quite a few times and have had, for the most part, good experiences with a few exceptions.  

For being such a nice establishment, I was very surprised with how reasonable the happy hour specials were.  Such a nice place to come after work on Friday and enjoy a drink on the patio with a few friends or coworkers if the weather permits.  During Mardi Gras, it's a great place to grab a bloody mary and take a short break from the parades, but it can become a mad house once the parades end.  

My only knock on the bar is with the bartenders.  From my experiences, they tend to be obnoxious college frat boys who seem to only cater to their college (possibly underage) friends.  From what I have gathered from some of my colleagues and friends is that they have also had similar experiences.  I have on several occasions been treated rudely by the bartenders who act like they are doing me a favor when I order a bloody mary.  On a separate occasion, I even noticed them being boisterous and starting fights with customers for no apparent reason, which is very unattractive considering it's reputation for being such a classy venue.  The egos and false sense of superiority I witnessed from some of these bartenders was repulsive.

Consequently, I usually just relax at a table on the outside patio or order from a server inside.  Overall, great nostalgia, good food, and great location to meet some friends for a drink during Mardi Gras or in the afternoon.

Coming from LA it's really hard finding decent boba/tea spots in the central coast area but this place is amazing. I tried their rose green tea and it was delicious and could taste the quality of their ingredients. Definitely will be coming again.

Creative and user-friendly menu with several small plate options.  Quality and presentation on our choices were excellent (Greek salad; bison burger sliders; Cuban crostinis).  Service (by John) was friendly and attentive.  Music by Josh Higgins was a great surprise; this guy is terrific.  As they say on Open Table, this is a 'Neighborhood Gem.'  We'll be back, especially when Josh is playing. Only quibble: wine list was slender with only ho-hum choices.
Highly recommend this restaurant! The food is amazing. The service can be slow sometimes but the food makes up for it!
That cute blond girl with the curly hair was super nice and the pizza was exceptional, she should get a raise.

I had reserved Easter Brunch reservations months in advance. Our plans changed and we needed to add one more individual to our party. I was told that they were unable to accommodate for ONE extra person to join us on Easter morning. Not to mention, the employee that I spoke with was rude and offered no empathy, telling me my only option was to check open table repeatedly...now our Easter plans are ruined and we will not be dining at this establishment ever again. Disappointed is an understatement...

Great lunch menu and even better baked goods.
The service is really great here.  The coffee is roasted fresh (you can see their roaster working behind the counter).  Just a really great all around neighborhood bakery and lunch shop.
Best pizza. The eggplant and white pizza are the best. The garlic knots are perfect. The owners are super friendly and make random foods of the day. So good.

Come thru here when visiting from home. Great place, kind folks, great coffee, tasty crossiant sammiches. Clean place with fun decor. Definitely my go to place and i look forward to my morning drives to get here. Couldnt recommend it enough
Really bad. Paid 55 dollars for tasteless chicken and broccoli paired with the wrong sauce. Crazy how bad this food is seeing they'd charge so much.

I happened to be a block away and dropped in. I enjoyed it. As other have said, you will see some stuff from the show but they seem to be selling mostly t-shirts and mugs. It is what it is - a tie in to the show. 

The building is beautiful and the area is being reclaimed. Will be very nice someday.

Beware: Parking is a bear.

This is the best pizza ever.And also the best steak zeps are the best. Been in Florida for 6 years and I miss this food so much. My 2nd favorite is Corpollese tomato pie which I can now have delivered in a kit just have to bake it. Goldbelly's is who does the delivery. I would be the happiest girl in Florida when you figure out how to do this delivery with your pzza PLEASE!!
Go to place for a quick casual meal. The pizza's crust is cooked to perfection and great beer selection. They occasionally have soup and it is always exceptional.

Remember if property management isnt fulfilling their leases use the govt agencies & health regulations, small claims courts. 
Landlord & Tenant Act, Alberta Health Regulations re stove, fridge, water, pests, etc. Not doing repairs, then taking damage deposits from undone repairs is not permitted under laws. You have a lease & agencies to back you up..USE THEM! 
Management companies are bastards.
Very good food! Highly recommend the cheddar kale meatloaf. Also love that it is a byob.
I'm here more than I should be ....excellent sushi even better service.   $21.95 dinner all you can eat plus a glass of sake can't beat that deal especially if you're hungry.
Ok fare. Reliable and consistent. Better than pizzeria beddia bugs because I don't have to wait three hours for my pie.
Cute spot in Dunedin. Standard bar options are far as food goes but great choice of fruity flavored beers including basil pinapple, apricot, cayenne, etc

We reserved a specific space for a birthday dinner for 8 a week in advance.  When we arrived, they had given it to another group.  They put us in the loud bar and it took 45 minutes for the waitress to take our drink order!  Another 30 minutes before they took our food order!  When the bills came, the waitress struggled with handling the bills and providing change.  We've lived in this community for 30 years and have often chosen Nickel Plate for the high quality food, service and local atmosphere.  I am so disappointed in the service we experienced, I will not go back any time soon.

I think I was predisposed to love the Copper Bistro.  First I call at Noon on a Saturday for a 7:00 PM that night.  Done.  Then we pull up as I'm bitchin' about how impossible it is to park on Second Street in NoLib. Bingo, directly in front, my new best friend is pulling his Miata out.  Zip in.  Incredulous. How can the food be good?  Can't be.  Walk in.  Warm, inviting, beautifully lit.  Can we wait a few minutes? Of course.  Now if we can have that elevated table in the window. Funny how piggish I get so soon after a few gifts.   This way sir. He's  not aiming for that table in the window?  He can't be?  He can and was.  What's going on?  I didn't know and the three of us didn't care.  Food was very well prepared and service was casual but excellent.  Lighting and noise level perfect.  As perfect a low budget restaurant visit as I've ever had.  Nice people and they can cook.  My problem is, I will expect that same parking space and that same table next time or feel a bit let down.  Humans are so demanding and unreasonable , or is it just me?
'

Ehh I won't be ordering from here again. Very clean and inviting establishment but the food is subpar.  

I called and placed a lunch order for general tso's with pork fried rice and a small wonton soup. My order was completed in a timely manner and service was friendly but the food was subpar. 

The pork fried rice was just awful like it was old and leftover or something. I ended up taking two bites and throwing it away. The chicken was okay but the portion could have been larger for the price tag of almost $9.50.

No thanks

Good food. Got the chicken n cheddar sandwich and my friend got the tenders. The sandwich was pretty good, the only ding I give it is the cheddar. It's a really strong cheese. It made me think if other chicken sandwiches have cheese on it and I realized they either don't have cheese or use provolone or swiss. I think those options are much better choices, however it was still very solid. The tenders were crispy and good flavor, the fries were great. It's definitely worth stopping in if your flying out of BNA

Great authentic Mexican food with nice staff.  My husband and I have been here multiple times trying different items and are usually happy with the food.  This was the first time I tried the tostadas but was a little sad the the tortilla were soggy on bottom.

We had many blinds in our home (not from this company) fall apart as if on cue. They are a couple of decades old, so it's not surprising that they might fall apart, but the fact that they started disintegrating in such a tight time span was pretty impressive.

So with this immediate onset of hot sun baking our house and all semblance of privacy removed in our COVID-solace bubble house, I set out to find a good place to get replacement blinds. Upon reviewing the reviews, I called this place to get an estimate and hopefully to get started on the job ASAP.

I had heard they were continuing to do business during the stay-at-home recommendations, so I was very excited at the prospect of getting the window coverings replaced so that our work-at-home might feel less like being in a Showtime rotisserie.

Despite calling them a number of times and leaving messages several times, I have not heard back from them. Their phone system is still taking messages and the greeting doesn't indicate they are shut down, but they are nonresponsive, nevertheless.

I'm imagining the best for this company, that perhaps they are indeed closed for the time being, and were unable to update the phone system to reflect this. But in any event, the net result has been I call, leave a message, they don't call back.

I've been meaning to come here for months, but I finally made it today, and I am so pleased! Everything is vegan and gluten free -- as someone with celiac disease, this is glorious. I had a meatball hoagie, something I haven't had in years, and it was so tasty! My non-celiac older son loved the cookie and smoothie he got. They also have a turmeric chai, which is something I've been looking for since The Post East closed! We will DEFINITELY be back -- I'm dying to try the mac and cheese and a breakfast sandwich!

Beware...Initially I was satisfied after getting a detailed report I noticed there were some discrepancies and had them corrected. After purchasing the house I found items that weren't working I contacted them and told the inspector would call that day. After over a week and  persistent attempts to contact I was told that the water softener and the water sprinkling control wasn't in the inspection and only done sometimes as a courtesy. After paying more than other inspections, over $500 I was disappointed that they wouldn't report obvious issues that will now cost me a lot to replace, that's what you have an inspection done for. 
I've seen other's claim of not standing behind their work, I need to agree, just get lip service.

Service was quick and great. Love the way they checked and service everything. I went in for oil change but received top notch service. Wind shield wipers replaced, fluids topped off tires checked.  Best part no all day waiting

And everyone that touched my car was super nice and customer service focused.   Honk my horn so much that it blew away street traffic

My friend and I went to dinner here on a Saturday night.
We hovered until we got a table outside.
We ordered the asparagus and strawberry salad.  The asparagus was chopped into the greens.  The strawberries were on the side of the plate.
I had the pepper encrusted ahi.  The server said it was 'amazing'.  I thought it was just reg'lar ole ahi slivers with some big pieces of pepper.  My friend had a chicken breast stuffed with goat cheese and some other stuff.  They took out the prosciutto for her.  Hers, of course, was great.  This is the last time I take a server's suggestion.  Our taste buds are different.
We had, of course, the LaFond Pinot - GREAT! 

6 stars for the wine+5 stars for the 2nd night my picky friend loved her meal - 4 stars for my reg'lar meal when I expected something 'amazing' = 3 stars

Rafters seems to be a home decor/import store. One half of the store contains a lot of cute home decor items. I was ecstatic when I saw that they carried Yankee Candle! OMG. I miss shopping Yankee Candle. I bought three votive candles and I am content at least for now. The other half of the store has a lot of super hero/Star Wars type of stuff- magnets, mugs, bobbleheads, etc. You can also find an assortment of candies and beverages not found in Canada. Overpriced for sure but worth it if you haven't had a Milky Way Midnight or pineapple soda in so long.

I used to deliver foods to this chain of restaurants, so I have a pretty familiar idea of what's to offer. 

The server we had at the south location didn't seem entirely present mentally during our visit, and this caused quite a few issues to put it lightly. 

I've never been to a restaurant where I ordered a beer, and the server brings a bottle to your table and then leaves...with the bottle still capped and I'm in need of a bottle opener. Incredibly awkward and off putting to start. 

I then ordered the jackfruit BBQ sandwich, and was brought a sandwich with PORK not jackfruit. I was unaware of this until the first bite, which was an unpleasant surprise to say the least! I'm mostly plant-based, and it was irritating that meat had been wasted in the situation and that I was subjected to eating it. 

The server admitted it was her fault for entering the order as a normal BBQ sandwich...Have you ever been having a conversation with an associate and you can clearly tell they're not understanding what you're saying, but they agree anyways? Well that behavior is what caused this entire ordeal, and there's absolutely no excuse. 

If you don't understand what someone is saying directly to your face, ASK THEM WHAT THEY MEAN. Don't continue as normal and then proceed to ruin a portion of that life. 

Another staff member approached me about the incident to try and lighten the mood. Overall, people need to know this happened. Maybe the food was comp'd, but this has absolutely ZERO impact on the issue and why I'm plant based in the first place. Won't be returning.

I frequently visit this location and my family and I ate there on Sunday. I will say this is by far the worse service that I have received since coming to this location. Typically, we don't have a wait. This day, we waited to be sat because the greeter was not at the front and we stood there looking for them. After she approached, we were told that we could not sit on the left side of the restaurant because there was no waiter servicing that side. Cool. We sat on the opposite side. We sat and looked at our menu and our waiter was still no where in our sight. A male waiter frequently approach the table adjacent to ours but never approached ours. This went on for 15 minutes. We were not approached about our drinks or a courtesy "One moment please". I pushed the call light to get his attention after he went to the same table for the 10th time (no exaggeration here) and he still took about 3-5 minutes to approach. 

He approached and apologized for the time and asked for our drink order. At this time, we were ready to order because we had reviewed the menu for a while by that time. So I told him that we could actually order and my fiance stated that they were moving slowly that day which I agreed. He appeared bothered by my fiance's comment but it was the truth. We ordered and my daughter ordered an appetizer, the app came out with our food opposed to before our dinner. We did not receive any refills and had to ask for those too. I was very bothered by our service received that day. Although I did leave a tip, I did not want to because the waiter was not showing good customer service at all. 

The one thing he did do great was correct the mistake on the receipt. I saw that my kids ordered from the 2/20 menu and he charged them individually. I brought it to his attention and he cleared their meals from the ticket. 

This is not the service we are used to at this location at all and I hope he was just having a bad day.

I gotta say, I was fairly skeptical, but also STARVING, and inexplicably intoxicated after only 3 beers. The place is in a small area I call "hooker highway", because of the low-rate strip clubs and 'massage' parlors that dot the section of Iberville. 

However, I was dying for something new, and the sign saying "VEGAN FOOD" sold me. The place is no-frills, but clean. A kind man behind the counter helped us decide what we wanted, and we sat, drank the free (!!!) water, and watched an Indian cricket match on TV. 

Our food came out, and it was delicious. I couldn't believe how much food there was for so little money, and how good it tasted. The other places in town are horrendous compared to what I got. The owner was very proud of his vegetarian offerings, and suggested I order some for delivery soon. Don't you worry, Sir. I will!

PIzza here is great, if you are a fan of thin style pizza. As commented on by others, the slices are huge. If you are not super hungry, I'd recommend splitting it. They have a huge beer selection. However, I wish they would have more ciders: I'm a pansy when it comes to darker beers like stouts, etc, which is what they primarily serve. The Woodchuck Ale Cider I had was incredible! Easily the best cider I've ever tried. 
My other complaint is that service tends to be slow. We usually go there hungry and are about ready to gnaw off an arm by the time food arrives. 
Overall, great food and beer. Fun place to hang out with friends.
Hate the salsa - it's too peppery, but the chicken chimichanga is filled overflowing with chicken and is HUGE.  Best thing on the menu by far.

I am really picky with my Mexican food being that I grew up in the border, and taco Giro has decent food. Prices are reasonable, and quality as well. I usually take friends and family and I never had any complaints or bad service except for Friday September 26, 2015. I have easily gone to Taco Giro more than 20 times, and I usually ask for the red chilaquiles, a fabulous coffee and no sour cream. This time the server that I had was not on her A game and the cook was sloppy. I get my plate with dry chilaquiles that look a day old, some of the tortilla from the chilaquiles is not even covered in red salsa, it looked like they gave me the scraps!! I work a culinary kitchen and know different tricks that the chef might do. Their cook defiantly tried to get the last of some salsa, mix it with the red, add water but it backfired because it was dry, with chili seeds that was making my stomach upset. I then asked the waitress if she could bring me some red salsa in a bowl that way I can try to fix the taste of it, she then asked me if I had asked her for red or green salsa and she said she ordered red, which was fine. I never got that salsa I was patiently waiting and my eggs where getting cold, I never got a refill for my coffee, and she took my water cup to refill but never brought it back. By the time the rest of my friends where done eating, I could not eat because I was about to throw up! She then saw my plate unfinished on the side and asked surprised if the cook or someone ever brought me the red salsa. I told her no, then she asked me if I wanted it and told her it was too late, the food was cold, and my stomach was hurting. She felt bad and asked if I wanted anything else, I responded by saying no. She then asked me for coffee as well but I said it was too late for that too. Then when I got my check everyone in my table was shocked that she still charged me for the food that was disgusting. I would at least pay for the coffee, but really!! after being her fault and the cook she decided to charge me! That was just the cherry on top of my bad experience in Taco Giro. Then she said sorry and that the next time she would make everything perfect, but who knows if there will be a next time in this location with the waitress named CARMEN. Overall, a bad experience, stomach was upset for the entire day, and they charged me for terrible service. My recommendations if you eat there: check your food quality first, don't like it have them re-make it since they will still charge you no matter what, and get one of the older and more experienced waitress.

Lions, Tigers, and Bears(oh my!) Philadelphia's Zoo is a fun place because its got some grit to it. Unlike other Disneyfied zoos around the country, there's still an element of wildness here that's hard to find anywhere else. They have a random selection of reptiles and birds that seem to rotate depending on what they can keep alive. Come here if you want a nice day date or place to people watch.

The drinks were a little pricey (a $12.50 Hurricane) but the place was excellent.  The music was great. We saw three different bands and they were all awesome.  The staff was very friendly and pretty sexy.

Love love love this place. Very clean restaurant. It's also well designed the atmosphere serves a Purpose. (To take you on a vacation in Indiana) 

I opted to get 3 of the street tacos. One being steak and two being chicken. They were all extremely delicious. 

The chips came frequently and were warm. I also loved the salsa. It was great quality. 

My absolute favorite part was the margarita flight. The margarita flight was amazing and not over watered with alcohol. I found this to be very refreshing since a lot of other restaurants dilute their margarita. 

All in all they have a great food selection with amazing service.  Loved the drinks and food. I've also utilized them for catering for work and the price point and quality for catering can't be beat!
Love the pastries and other baked goods. Have yet to try their lunch menu. Their prices have gone up and I know it's because of the economy but it's stopped me from coming back as much as I used to.

Di Bruno Bros has a great variety of gourmet items from cured meats, cheeses, fresh produce, meats, and seafood to baked items and prepared items.  The prices are definitely more expensive than your usual place, so if you can find items at another nearby store, you probably will pay a lower price at the other place.  However, Di Bruno Bros also has a great collection of prosciutto that you cannot find in Philadelphia and the free samples of cheese are also an added bonus.

The sandwiches on the panini press are also delicious and are at a fair price.

Enjoy!

It breaks my heart to write a bad review being that these donuts are the best and the only ones I will eat. 
We drove 40 mins out of our way at 8:30pm on 1/5 and was told by an employee that they were "fresh out of everything" - if that's the case then close the store and don't say your OPEN UNTIL 11PM. This is false advertising. Not the mention that we wanted to buy dozens of donuts not just 3. 
Ridiculous considering that these stores are few and far between. I'll definitely be calling before I drive out of my way - if I decide to ever go back. 
Get it together...

This is a great addition to 104th st in the former Ric's grill space. The room is now much more open and brighter with a lot of homey, warm wood featured in the decor. Build your own burgers are the specialty here with a huge selection of combinations of different buns, patties, condiments and add-ons on a form you check off yourself. I admit I did not have high hopes for the vegan burger but was very pleasantly surprised. The patty and bun were fresh and homemade and I added yummy toppings like house-made pickles and onion relish. The potato chips are crispy and are a generous portion. The fries are also a generous size. All the food came out in a timely way. Our server was friendly and enthusiastic. They were out of a few wine choices but this would be nothing out of the ordinary for a week old restaurant getting the feel of what sells best. The one thing I wish they would reconsider is the self-service seating. It's confusing since there is still a hostess station and I felt it led to a longish wait before the server came by at first. Once the server started with us, everything was timely, though. 
Verdict: vegan burger is definitely worth going back for.
Very nice hotel and service. Large rooms with very comfortable bed. Hotel staff brought us bikes to ride around the beach. Large tv that swiveled towards the bed.

I was really looking forward to my Drybar experience but overall it came up incredibly short. My appointment was scheduled to start at 10:45 and I was not taken back to get washed until after 11:00. The receptionist at the front desk was incredibly nice and friendly and she was the one who washed my hair- no problems there. The stylist who did my blowout was not overwhelmingly friendly and seemed to have little interest in being there and working on my hair. I felt I made it very clear what I wanted when explaining to her how I wanted my hair. I expressed not wanting a full head of curls- just a lot of volume up top and the ends curled under with a round brush and maybe some curls with a curling iron at the bottom if needed. I was unable to really see what the stylist was doing since I had a TV in front of me, which was totally fine. When she was finished and turned me around, my hair was (sort of) curled up to the top of my hair and the top of my hair was completely flat and my part was not where I wanted it to be. Because of timing and needing to get to a wedding, I left and when I got in my car, I found a number of pieces of hair throughout that were completely straight. Overall I was incredibly unhappy with how it looked and had to quickly head home to fix it to my liking. I wish I could have had a better experience but unfortunately I will not be returning.

I'm honestly not crazy about their actual pizzas, but everything else on their menu? Ridiculously good. Their cheese grits, calzones, and mac and cheese (which tastes like smoked gouda) in particular are highlights.

Small place 
Only a few tables
Birds flying in and out of the restaurant 
Beignets were okay 
Cafe au lait were good.

My wife had a friend in town that has a pretty strict diet. They found this place and my wife fell in love. She's taken me a couple of times and I have to admit their bowls are pretty tasty. Plenty of options too for most people. The bowls are just slightly on the spendy side, but really, what isn't these days.

I was really excited to eat here because I love Italian. And I was so excited when I saw they had gnocchi. I found the layout a bit confusing initially but quickly figured it out. Service was friendly. However I found my food rather flavorless and on the mushy side. Maybe there's better options to try but with some of the great other options on the area I'm in no hurry to return here.

I have always really enjoyed going to Giovanni's, but I will never go there again. Yesterday we arrived to eat at their outdoor seating area. Upon arrival, we were told we would not be able to eat there after we already ordered as they had rented out the entire patio for a large birthday party. All customers that had been eating on the patio were told that they had to leave after they finished eating. Even with the Covid pandemic, there were about 30+ people mingling outside, most of which were not even wearing masks. We were forced to wait for our pizza in the parking lot. There were also people part of this large group who got service while NOT wearing a mask, when there is a clear sign that stated "No mask, no service." Our pizza took forever and when we went inside to ask about it, they ignored us. Overall terrible experience, it is evident that they don't care about their customers, just the money they are making.

Went with colleagues for lunch here. Ordered the famous Philly steak and it wasn't that famous.  The bread was too soft and the beef needed a bit more seasoning like salt and pepper would have help a little.

You might ask - why such a low rating, Steph? Did you have a bad day? Did you have your snack today? Are the stars out of alignment? My answer would be no - I'm having a great day, I just ate lunch and the stars are delightfully in alignment. Why then such a terribly low rating for Mugshots? By now you should know that when it comes to Steph H. and coffee shops - it all comes down to internet. When the mark-up of your average cup of coffee from a coffee shop is something like 500%, I REALLY don't think it's necessary to CHARGE FOR INTERNET. It's all part of the coffee shop experience, a nice cup o' joe, and free internet flowing through my wireless card. When those two things don't happen. I GET ANGRY. No, I don't get angry, I just write about it on yelp :)
Any professional hockey game is going to be a blast in my book. But being a Blackhawks fan, this barely even compares to the United Center in Chicago. Still, this is high-quality entertainment.

This is my breakfast stop every morning while in NOLA. The crabs Benedict is fabulous. Hubby loves the grits. Service is a bit slow, but just enjoy your coffee (no chicory!) and watch the people walk by.

Been here couple of times for drinks on weekend nights.  To get a drink at the bar took a minor divine intervention.  The bar doesn't look crowded, but somehow I waited for what it seems hours for a drink.  The place is kinda snazzy.  I don't mind pretentious, but I do mind over-priced drinks with little amount of liquor.
Great food and service. They pride themselves on ensuring folks have a great time while in Clearwater.

Not our preferred Mexicqn Restaurant. 3 out of 5 because we dealt with at of annoying fruit flies. Overpriced tortilla with chicken that was supposed to be a quesadilla - for $9 ....Could have gotten a better one at Qudoba for half this price. Basically the food was just OK. The chips and salsa - well we had better in this area too.
Drinks were very good and the Highlight - this is the only reason we would consider returning here. Very good selection of drinks at a reasonable cost.

I recently hired Ramsey's for a move, the short version is: I would not recommend them. "Good" customer service means nothing when there are no issues, real customer service is about how well a business deals with a problem when they occur. It is here that Ramsey's failed, even after given the opportunity to succeed. 
Initially everything was wonderful, the owner came by to inspect all the items to be moved and provide a much more accurate quote. The quote came out to $1575 using 4 men for 6.5 hours, materials, $60 of mattress bags, and an hour of drive time. While large items were our primary concern the only things that we were not having them move in the quote were some small décor items, and things they were not allowed to: propane, etc. Before hiring them, we asked about any discount that might be able to be applied, it never hurts to ask! They said the best they could offer was 3% on the labor, but they normally over quote jobs and would probably be done quicker. The quote included mattresses, 60+ boxes, several shelving units, a Christmas tree, and many other items which in the end were NOT moved by Ramsey's. We rented a 6x12 trailer and moved over 3 FULL loads ourselves before Ramsey's. WE moved 90% of the boxes, we moved the mattresses, the Christmas tree, and so many other things. Our thought was that we needed help moving big items, and if we moved over several smaller "last minute" items that always take up a lot of time during a move, then we could save a fair amount of money and make the move very quick and easy.
Unfortunately, this was not the case. It started off well, they arrived a bit over a half hour early. However, 5 men showed up, this was a change to the job that was not communicated to us. One was clearly the supervisor so I wrongly assumed that there were 4 workers (that came with the job) plus 1 supervisor. I didn't assume that Ramsey's changed my bid to charge me more money (186 vs 224/hr). The pay clock started, but the work was slow and poorly organized. When hiring a moving company like this with good reviews I expected careful, motivated, hard-working guys. Instead only 2 out of 5 of the guys were truly this, 1 was alright, and the other two were taking breaks or on their phone for a large part of the time. Time I ended up paying for. To further explain how easy this move was for them. When we got to the unloading half of the job 80% of the truck was simply unloaded into the garage.  We moved most items into the home ourselves. We did this because the carpets were just cleaned the day before and the movers were messy at the home we left. We didn't want to deal with making sure dirt wasn't tracked into our new, clean home, so we had nearly everything put in the garage.
Is it our job to tell the workers to work harder or is that the supervisors job? I didn't complain about them taking breaks, taking one small item at a time, walking slow, or being on their phones because I didn't think I'd be charged for it. I figured either the supervisor would know to cut that time out of the job (which he didn't because he was inside wrapping furniture), or Ramsey's management would take care of it. Which they did not.

They felt that because the move was completed ahead of schedule and under-budget that everything was great, the problem is they only moved 60% of the items that were originally in the quote. That quote was for 26 man hours, it somehow took 22.5, that's only 14% less time for 40% less stuff! Clearly something was amiss, but Ramsey's thinks everything was peachy. Our concerns fell on deaf ears at Ramsey's, I hope this review helps you have a better moving experience with a different company.

11/19/19 Edit* review unedited, response to Ramsey's comment. I could post all conversation between Ramsey's and I, and you would see they just don't get it. They mention they over estimate quotes...but items I left were the difficult ones? A little harder than boxes sure, but all the furniture was also TAKEN APART, that was supposed to be included as well..You see, it's simple, they will do whatever they can to squeeze the xtra money out of you. If their guys don't work hard, who cares? Both them and the company make more money! We did not receive $273 back, we received $30.67 back, for a 3% discount on labor agreed to before the job. Them not charging us the full quote is not the same as receiving money back.
The form states...ok, everybody reads every word of those right? If I had declined that portion I wouldn't have been able to move that day, if a company puts these things in their contracts its so they can scam you out of more money. 
They are surprised I didn't work as on site manager during their job. I stated in email that I didn't care that workers were on their cell phones or working slow, I just didn't think they would have the audacity to charge me for it. Reached out minutes after job/bill. Other mv comp is broinlaw in othr state. Revw was not threat. Hit chratr lim.

I don't know what it is but I just never quite feel like the layout and atmosphere is right when I go. I know partly because the food and drinks just always seem a little high price for the type of place it is. And forget going there if there is a big game on during the week. The bar area gets to crowded and there is no place to walk up for a drink or even stand after you get the drink. If you go for a simple lunch get-together earlier in the day or even an early dinner it is ok, but that's about it.

For $50 I got a perfectly mediocre fish taco, a decent (but how can you screw it up) enchilada and a very weak large margarita.

You do not get what you pay for here.  The food is very bland and the drinks over priced and weak.  

The ambiance is what drew me in, however there was nothing more than decor to this place.

We all ordered the strip steak. The service was great, the steaks were merely marginal, at best. We had a friend in town and this was to have been a treat but it was embarrassingly poor quality food with the exception of the fried green tomatoes. This
experience causes us to place this restaurant on the "do not call list."
The ambiance was a beautiful old-style  steakhouse with great ambiance, but for the price, we expected much better quality meat.
My goto place in Philadelphia for great Mexican Cuisine. Absolutely love the bar. Totally recommend the blood orange Margaritas and the mahi mahi tacos. Remember to book in advance.
The pastor nachos or tacos are AMAZING! The service and food quality is spot on every time. Did I mention, the prices are very reasonable. Must try if you're looking for a new Mexican spot.
Hazelnut encrusted black grouper in a coconut sauce, with jasmine cilantro rice, broccolini and warm bread. Rye boulevardier with a single rock. Perfect.

Really good coffee. I love when I order a fancy coffee and I don't have to add extras. Crema is the right word, perfectly creamy. I don't like foam in my coffee, shoot me now, but I don't pay for air, fill the cup with the real juice I need please... and they did! :) Just the right amount of foam to make it soft. Hearts to you barista!

I also grabbed one of their egg pastries and I wasn't sold on it. It had a weird taste that I could not moved from so I didn't finish it. 

The place is nicely located and has a nice decor, relaxing and calming in the middle of the caos to come and drink some coffee and read a book, have a quiet conversation or do some work. They have outside seating that is nice on a day with shade, it doesn't have a roof so I imagine it could get hot on a summer day. 

Overall, I like this place and definitely will be stopping by for some good coffee, come check them out.

Read the reviews and thought ok gotta check this out.  This little place is where memories are made. I am from Nebraska and brought my sister...who lives 9 miles away for her first visit!  Delicious garlic butter everywhere! The Alex Bake was plentiful and as delicious as promised.  I will return because this hard working group is serving some amazing food!! Thanks guys it was amaazing!
It's okay. Tried the toasted ravioli of St. Louis. Waitress was great and patient as I decided what to order.

First, the good.   We ate outside which was nice.   The food we ordered was very good.   The bad.   You are given a tiny menu with food that states its to share and prices for sharing.   We ordered the special which was a dinner (THERE ARE NO DINNER PRICES ON THE MENU) and there is a reason for this.   I had sea bass to the tune of $38 my date had Veal $42.   The portions were very small.   We are not big people.   Do not go here if you are hungry or on a budget.   This restaurant is way, way, over priced.   You can do alot better elsewhere.   My biggest complaint though is they are not forthcoming with the prices.   If you order dinner you better ask what it will cost or risk heart failure when you get the bill.

this place is a disgrace ...they dont owe up to their wrong doings. I was driving down the street when one of their employees was moving and her mower shot debris at my car causing $1300 in damages. I pull over to speak to the employee to express my frustrations and she never even turned to mower off and ignored me.  I couldnt even speak to her about it. So I had called to speak to a manager and they hang up. I had to get my insurance involved and they wont even pick up the phone for them !! WHAT KIND OF. BUSINESS IS THIS??
Very good service Megan was extremely helpful and polite very quick serve would highly recommend

For those of us that like Tacos De Lengua, don't even. First time and it was a terrible first impression. Hard, burnt yet rubbery texture. I couldn't taste anything but burnt cilantro. I will not come here again for those. However their fish tacos weren't bad... gives me a complex.

Our house burned down in January, 2015.  Our insurance company ordered asbestos testing and was supposed to pay the bill.  Over THREE YEARS later, in May, 2018, I received a letter demanding payment along with the invoice which was addressed to my insurance company.  We had never received an invoice before.  I called my adjuster and he hadn't spoken to anyone, so I sent him the letter and invoice.  I also called this company and the lady was RUDE.  She told me if I was going to act that way, she will just send me to collections and told me that it wasn't her fault I don't pay my bills.  She also said they send the invoice to the insurance company as a "courtesy" to us.  WE DIDN'T ORDER THE TEST!  This was the insurance company's bill, not ours.   Then, I got rude.  She then tried telling me she spoke to my adjuster but she actually didn't, because he didn't know about this or who she was.  I sent her an e-mail with a cc: to my adjuster.  Within 45 minutes, he replied and told her they were paying it.  This lady would not listen to me!  I had NO contract with them, we never ordered the testing and we never received an invoice from them.  In fact, we had never even heard of them.  I work with insurance companies too, and I will be sure to emphasize NEVER to use this business.  Not only does this lady not do her job, she's absolutely RUDE.  P.S.  I PAY MY BILLS!!!!!

The Continental offers a great new take a number of my favorite dishes and some I'd never had. I went with a  group and we ordered multiple appetizers to share. A few of the group favorites included the French onion dumplings, lobster Mac-n-cheese, and shoestring fries. Everything down to the dessert was absolutely fantastic! They did struggle a little to get all of our dishes out at once during their peak dinner rush so it took us a little longer as a bigger group. I'll be going back!!

The ambiance is lovely with linen tablecloths, faux vines overhead lit with tiny white lights and fresh touches for Christmas. The five of us had a quiet booth at the back as requested so my elderly mother could hear our conversations. The booth was perfect. 

Two of us had calamari piccata, two had linguine with white claim sauce while the fifth enjoyed the fish piccata. The food was pretty good once it came. My only negative is that the service was beyond sluggish. 

Jack, the new owner, greeted us as we departed asking how everything was. I told him the above. 
I will return but will have wine and kick my heels up then so I can enjoy the long afternoon more and not wonder what on earth is taking so long.

What a great find!  Another reviewer described it as an oasis and I really agree with that assessment.  We stopped in on a Saturday morning bike ride.

The coffee is well prepared.  I chose an Americano and K went with a hot tea as she is not drinking as much coffee as she used to.  Brunch was great.  There is a good selection of breakfast fare.  The ABE sandwich was delicious.

The prices are a bit higher than you'd pay at the big S, but I rather support a local business.  There is a minimum charge of $5 on credit cards so be prepared in the event you want a second cup (learned this the hard way) with your breakfast/brunch.

Overall, One Shot is a really enjoyable experience.

Sedona was a pleasant surprise! We went and watched part of the Nashville Sounds baseball game and decided to leave early to go grab a bite and a beverage from Sedona. So glad we did! Quick walk from the stadium and we were there.
I ordered the filet mignon. Mostly because the price point was the lowest I've ever seen at a restaurant ($29.99). I think they need to raise the price! Perfectly grilled and seasoned. My mouth is watering just thinking of it.
The beverages were delicious. I think I had their rendition of an old fashioned. Can't go wrong with one of those.
The ambiance outside was perfect. They have a huge gas fire pit that must be 6' across surrounded by a concrete bench. Take a neat group pic with your friends around the pit.
Check out Sedona and you will leave happy.

This place is super arrow but still a great venue. The stage is weirdly in the center facing a wall with a bar on one side and tables on the other  so if you wanted to get a good look at the band you'd have to stand right in front which is exactly what I did. It was a semi-busy night when I went but I must say the staff was extremely friendly and nice and the drinks were decently strong and not overpriced, wow. You should check it out.

My bf and I went on a trip down the coast earlier this year and I've finally had the chance to start reviewing.  We spent 2 fabolous days in Santa Barbara and absolutely loved it.

The Natural Cafe was the first lunch joint.  It received great reviews on Yelp so we decided to give it a try.  The food was delish.  My bf got a turkey burger and I got a regular burger.  Being a meat eater, I could tell the difference from the two, but the turkey burger was quite good.  The soup was also great.  I can't recall which one we got, but it came with a huge piece of bread (which I loved!).  Dipping bread in soup is the perfect mix.

The food was healthy, reasonably priced, and the location was fabolous.

Soho used to be so good before they switched the owner. When the new owner came in, the wings became 18 for $20, it was more expensive. I don't really mind it but for the fact that the wings are only flavorful on the outside but it's bland on the inside. Other than that, their noodle & other dishes are still quite good.

Ordered the Chicken Tikka and the Lamb Rogan Josh on a Saturday night via Skip. Both dishes didn't have the spice level of authentic Indian food that we were craving, and the Garlic Naan tasted under-seasoned. Kind of disappointed being that it was the highest-rated Indian restaurant north of the river. Maybe it was a one off, but with other Indian restaurants being close by, I'm not sure I'll order their takeout again.

They must have been sold again and changed the name to Silver Spring. Thumbs down. The food was tasteless. I wrote rave reviews of the restaurant when it was run by Michael from Yangming - the food was fantastic, BYO, great service and a neighborhood gem. It's gone progressively downhill since then, and with the new ownership, I've given up. Back to PF Changs for me.

I can get the stuff I want, that is not the problem. The clientele is so ratchet and shady. People cut in line, steal, lie and start fights. I saw a woman on her scooter cut in line and then proceeded to pilfer snacks from the stands in the cashiers aisle! She stuffed them in a plastic bag. I also believe that the couple in front of her were helping her to steal; which makes sense since they were stealing too. I asked her why she cut in line and she said that she was already lime and that the couple in front us had saved her space! Not true. It was their word against mine, but I know what I saw.

Arigato is definitely good sushi with a variety of inventive rolls.  But for the tiny portions at that price, I expected much more.  I love Japanese food and have been to a lot of very expensive Japanese restaurants that I have felt deserved the money but Arigato is not one of them.  It may be the best Japanese restaurant in Santa Barbara, but if it was in San Francisco or LA or even Oakland, I have a hard time believing people would go back.

Went here over the weekend with a group of 6 people.  I was the first to get there so I put my name on the list and asked how long the wait would be.  I was told it would be about 1 hour, so we decided to wait it out and get a coffee and pastry while we waited.  They had a great variety of coffee to choose from and had 3 different sizes.  I got a latte and an brownie and both were excellent, but a little hard to eat because the waiting area was super crowded and there was no where to put anything down.  My friends tried other items and I was told they were all delicious.  

After a while of waiting we started to notice that the crowd in the waiting area had died down and that people that came after us were being seated.  I figured it might be because we were a big party but decided to ask about the wait and if it may be beneficial to split up our party for faster seating.  I was discouraged from splitting, with them telling us that we would have to go to the end of the list, even though we had already been there for 1 hour.  When I asked how much longer, they told me it would be 45 minutes.  Since it had already been an hour, we spoke to the manager and were informed that there were 9 parties ahead of us due to the no wait app and that they had misquoted us on how long the wait would be.  It still didn't make sense to me why people who were not yet at the restaurant would get preference ahead of us, particularly because we had a large party and the restaurants had limited tables that could seat us.  We even noticed a table that was big enough for our group open up and sit empty and were told it was for people who had registered on the no wait app and were not there yet, which honestly didn't make any sense to us.  At this point half of our party decided that they had waited long enough and left.

A few minutes after they left, they were finally willing to seat us.  Initially we squeezed into a smaller table but they gave us the table next to us when that party left.  Things def improved from here as the manager checked on us several times to make sure everything was ok and we were comfortable.  The food also came out very fast, I think it took about 10 minutes.  I will say all the food here was incredible.  I had a chicken hand pie and it was very flavorful, the crust was flakey and moist.  The side salad with it was delicious as well.  

To make up for the issues we had, the manager discounted our meal 20%, which was very nice of him.  So after a rough start, the restaurant did begin to redeem itself.  I would def go again, I am hoping this is just a kink they are working out.  I will however def use the no wait app next time.

I used to go to this place all of the time for their burgers but they aren't as good anymore. The burger was over cooked and took 40 minutes to get it to me when the place was not close to full. Then plus sides are that the people there are very nice and that their catfish is top notch. Not a place to watch the game there though unless you are sitting at the bar because there are only a couple TVs.

Really slow service for a restaurant that should know alive after 5 is a busy night!  It took 20 minutes to get water. The drinks were ok but it took forever.  We ordered a fast app the buffalo dip with chips, it was super greasy and kind of gross texture. My wife ordered fish & chips & I ordered the taphouse burger. Well let's just say we should have "tapped out" before the entree. The fish was really greasy and the burger was charcoal. We ate fries for dinner. We told our waiter who then called a manager.  The manager blamed the horrible food on the "new" wait staff . I don't know who gave this place 4 stars but we will never go back.

The atmosphere is great. Thet spent a lot of effort on the decor and it shows.  The food was just a little hit and miss.  However the service was very weak.  Our waitress was friendly but not experienced.  She poured 3 waters and but failed to fill the 4th water glass because she ran out of water.  She didn't return to fill it and after 10 minutes had to ask a manager to help.  Delivered a bottle of wine but required another manager to open the bottle, i guess she was either underage or inexperienced.  The clam pizza was underwhelming to be honest, did like the green beans

Waiter disappeared and was served by whoever we could flag down. Portions small,  wait for food nuts and no one ever checked to see how we were doing during meal. As highly recommended as this tourist trap was talked about it was a waste of time and dollars.  You have plenty restaurants to chose from don't waste it here! Sorry I wish I'd read more reviews prior to coming. Boo hoo

It was our first time here and the service was sub par. They were rude and had an attitude that we wanted potatoes. The food was good but service was terrible. The staff were arguing in front of us and we're completely unprofessional. Don't think I'll be coming back.

So I was here the first week of November and was tired of fine dining and wanted something simple. This was my first time at port of sub and I was totally looking forward to trying one of there tasty subs. My order was taken by a lady by the name of Tia, and she was very customer friendly AT ALL which was a huge turnoff, then they only had white or wheat bread. So after my negative experience with her another young lady by the name of Ann took over to finish preparing my food and ringing me up. I must say Ann was very pleasant, and from beginning to end kept a smile on her face a very customer friendly attitude, and the sandwich was pretty good. I would've given this place 5 starts if Tia would've had a pleasant attitude, and if they had more breads to choose from. I deducted a  for each.

Extremely disappointed with the quality of my order and the customer service of this store. Received my delivery and the pizza is totally over cooked, basically burnt. The wings are rubbery and inedible. I called the location to request a refund or new pizza that is edible and the store manager argued with me. He yelled over the phone and refused to even entertain my complaint saying that he knows the pizza is fine because he "made it". I asked to speak to someone above him and he refused. I then asked for the corporate complaint line number and he told me to Google it. I will never spend my money at Dominos again.

Well this review is for the customer service to book our room over the phone.  I called yesterday to book a room for a quick couple days getaway in November.  The agent on the phone had an attitude I asked for 2 Queens and she abruptly said I can't guarantee anything, so do you want to book this room? When I hesitated and eventually decided to go ahead and book the room she gave a big sigh liked I had inconvenient her and she had to start over.  This is our first time going to stay at the GSR I sure hope the stay will be better than the customer service.
Had a bad experience here.! The price is expensive and they take more customer than they can cater! I got bitten by bugs waiting for the food and the check!
