I had ordered 2 yards of soil mix for my raised bed. They told me they are pretty busy but later confirmed would deliver it on the same day but no time was mentioned. So they show up within an hour and unfortunately nobody was at home. I gave them my credit card details in about 45 mins and they charged $25 extra then what was agreed but they never bothered to inform me. The invoice which I had received also mentioned $84 instead of $111 they charged me. I was looking for my credit card statement and found that they charged more they what was agreed.
When I called them, I was told they went back and came back again and thats the reason I was charged extrra and hey can't do anything about it. Totally unfair and unethical business practices. Really disappointed with them and would never go back to them for anything.

Dive bar full of characters at night. .. lots of stories here.
I gone here for music several times.
It is very tiny and the locals are very local ..... not the sedate stuffy kind up the mountain.
I have not gone to the night when the ladies dance. So I can't really comment on that.
Ample parking in the front.
Relatively clean. Nice looking bar with bar maids when I have been there.
Kind of a cool old roadhouse when a local band is slamming the air waves.

I am Lebanese so I know what Lebanese food is suppose to be like. The food here is good , not great. For anyone who has never eaten arabic food before it'll probably taste better to them. They did have really awesome hummus though you can tell it was made from scratch . They didnt have Arabic pickles there which was a crazy concept to me and they don't serve fries either. Which I'm sure I'm not the only one who likes that with their falafel sandwiches or shawerma. Other than that it was ok. The service is great and they were really nice and attentive. But I wouldn't visit the restaurant again myself

Old airport, need to make some improvements. One of them being that there are signs to show where pick up area 14 to 16 are and to not have to pay parking when picking up people in those area...what the hell? $5 to stay less than 5min in the parking lot to pick up people?! Weird! Even the parking cashier apologized.

I recently got married and had Kurt entertain for the cocktail hour. He was a huge hit with our family and wedding guest. To watch the reactions of guests was priceless. I highly recommend Kurt if you are having a party, he is very very good at what he does.

If you loved the old Contigo on River Road (like myself), don't expect the same experience at it's new location, the Westin La Paloma. On the plus side, the menu is mostly the same, the Arepas are still delicious (the pork with the mango salsa is by far the best one). 

This new location has zero of the old charm of the previous spot. The romantic, secluded ambiance has been replaced by the bustling and generic feel of the resort restaurant that it now is. 

It was very busy, as expected for a Saturday night, but the wait for everything was a little much. They must have been short staffed because we didn't get our appetizers(!) until an hour after ordering. It wasn't just us because at one point I looked around the restaurant and realized that very few other tables had food either. 

My favorite dish, the Arroz con Pollo has been replaced with an embarrassingly massive piece of pounded, breaded and deep fried chicken. Served with fries and mixed greens, this dish belongs in a diner, not this restaurant. A huge disappointment.

We were in this restaurant for over 2 hours from the time we were seated to the time we finally got our check (and not by choice). I would not recommend the  chicken entree but my husband loved the lamb chops. Overall, the food is fine.  It's a decent enough spot for tourists and visitors. But this is not the same charming place it used to be. These locals will not be going back. Shame.

We tried these cupcakes at a birthday party, absolutely loved them, wanted to try other flavors. The web showed an array of flavors, seriously, 90 and more, unbelievable!! So far we've tried only 7 flavors, still long way to go! Would love to try as many as possible, so definitely coming back. The favs for now are: birthday cake, chocolate chocolate, butter popcorn, white chocolate and raspberry. Also tried orange creamsicle, vanilla, s'mores, all are good too, just not as much. The cupcakes really are scrumptious, moist and flavorful, and just enough icing. 4 stars for the size - too small for me!!! 1.25$ per cupcake, I'd love to see a little more of it! Also, to my taste, the flavors didn't match the title on some of the cupcakes. 
The bakery is pretty nice, clean and cozy, staff is very friendly and helpful. I love that they make new flavors every day, doesn't get fresher than that!! 
Their website is great, has a lot of wonderful information and details on how to order for parties. They also have a Facebook page, like them, you'd get instant updates on new flavors and more!

Wow.  Our dining experience at Cellar Door was the best we've experienced during our trip.  John, the manager, was super accommodative in getting our table ready while we enjoyed pre-dinner cocktails at the cozy bar.  Emily, our server, greeted us with a friendly demeanor that persisted throughout our meal.  Our appetizers were highlighted with amazing brussel sprouts and tuna tostada.  Our entrees were tasty and presented well with red snapper as the fish of the day, scallops with a corn salad, and the burger all exceeding expectations.  Emily recommended that we try the beignets in praline sauce, which were served piping hot and delicious.  The meal, paired with an equally high level of service, was memorable one.  We will definitely return to the Cellar Door on our next trip to the Big Easy.  Thank you John and Emily!

Good Service. BYO!  The free bread they give you is not the best, but the tomato pesto that comes with it is GREAT!!! Great pasta specials. Gnocchi is okay. Duck pasta dish is decent, but oily. I had a pasta special recently with shrimp, beans, tomatos, and etc, which was delicious. Clean flavors. Cute decor.

We've had a long history with this place. It's hit or miss, but if you find the right dish it's fantastic! On our current visit we had a mix with the various dishes we had. 

I'll start with the bread. They offer you a tomato pie bread and a roll. The tomato pie was good, but was a little burnt. I added balsamic and cheese and it masked the burned taste. The roll was just fair. The salad that came with our meals were unimpressive. -A little bit of lettuce, a chopped up, discolored green pepper, and a tomato. I ordered the grilled salmon and it was fantastic. I was a little disappointed that they left out the lettuce, but the dish really didn't need it. My fiancée ordered the gnocchi. It was VERY cheesy and the gnocchi didn't seem fresh.  Overall, we loved our meal and will be back soon!

The cakes and other pastries in the front case look good, but nothing we ate actually was. The salad bar is lackluster, and the dinner rolls in the nearby case cold as stone. The late-day breakfast foods others were eating looked good, but the dinner items we had -- meatloaf, chicken croquettes -- were subpar. Mashed potatoes were tasteless, mac and cheese one-note and average at best. Soup wasn't very good, either. Service is friendly and quick, though. Overall, low-end diner food.
Qualitech took my phone call early in the morning and had technicians on site same day by noon.  Lucky enough to get away with a fuse replacement and back on my feet.  Thank you guys.

Service is a little disjointed but the food is delicious and plentiful.  Have been twice once sola and once with a group and had a really good meal.  The free appetizer is such a nice gesture and when servers aren't huddling they are nice.  

The hummus and salads are fresh and the meat Kebabs were flavorful.  Would recommend the lamb for sure.  

I hope that the shop will be successful and I hope that they continue to expand the menu.
the facility is amazing. The food is decent. The performers are AMAZING! The service and operations are still "work in progress".

This was my first time at Seoul Taco. I ordered the Gogi bowl with chicken, paid extra for brown rice. I liked it, but I don't think I'd order it again. I've enjoyed other protein/grain/veggie bowls elsewhere that I like better. I can't really say what I didn't care for! I'd probably try tacos next time. Because the basketball game was in full use, it was very loud, and nearly impossible to carry on a conversation. I know I'm in the minority with my review, but this was my experience.

A really mixed experience. I think this may be the third or fourth Indian restaurant at this location. I skipped the few in the middle, but used to frequent the original before the quality tanked. Upon entering, recognized a worn out version of the original with the tables showing cracks and wear. There were strange wall stickers of Mickey Mouse among others. The food was ok with the sauces being a nicely creamy thick but a bit flavorless. The samosas were good. The service was friendly but seemed understaffed with our server seeming to be performing several jobs. Overall, it wasn't bad but the place could use some freshening up.
Cashiers don't listen and managers aren't accommodating. Cashier messed up our order multiple times and the manager didn't make any moves to fix the issue. Highly disappointed in the service.
Went there for lunch buffet and found it be only a few items. Very limited selections. Food is good though but wished there was more. Give 3 stars for limited items.

Even with the 20 min wait for seating, this place is worth a visit! 
Food was delicious and so were the mimosas! 
Everything is in extremely large portions! 
I had the Eggs Coublon, and was I glad I did! The catfish was a perfect compliment to the egg and the vegetables! 
I thoroughly enjoyed the meal!
Pretty good froyo place! Would totally come again! A lot of families come here so fun atmosphere!

oh green eggs where oh where do i begin.  

i wont talk about how loud and obnoxious most of the people who eat here are. 
i wont go into how this place is cash only and it drives me nuts b/c i always am stuck hitting up the atm. 
i wont mention how ANNOYING it is that the servers are always kind of bad. 
i wont even bother to complain about how when i arrive at 8 am and say my mom will be 15 mins later - that they seriously TRY to tell me to wait for her to be seated.  (i could see if it was noon and crowded) 

i will just mention that as a vegan i am upset w/ the labeling of the menu and clarity over what is vegan and what is not. 

1) they make the tofu scramble w/ actual BUTTER. 
2) the soy sausage is NOT vegan 
3) the potatoes are NOT vegan 

which makes the "tofu scramble" dish on the menu- marked with a clean green V - not actually VEGAN -  you have to tweak it here. 

i wish the servers were more up on things and more informed of what is vegan and what isnt.  its *always* my pet peeve when the staff doesnt actually know whats in the meals they are serving. 

either way the portions are HUGE, the food is tasty, the prices are affordable, and the location is great.

The Chipotle chicken & waffle absolutely sucked. Daughter got the same and felt the same. The chicken had literally no taste. Not even a hint of Chipotle. No salt. The waffle was pathetic, like it sat out on someone's table all day. It was try and tasted like it came from a frozen box.

The wings (not shown) were pretty good. Most of everyone's elses food in my group look pretty good. Shepherds pie looked so so..

Drinks were good. Service was good.

Was pressed for time, otherwise I would've sent my food back and got something else.

The atmosphere is less to be desired, the restaurant itself could use a makeover. The wait staff is no where near enthusiastic. 
The food is good, that's the only saving this place.

Great coffee. Great grilled cheese flats. And of course donuts!! A great cup of fresh coffee is always available. Not as good as the DD on Us 19 just south of Hwy 52, but in a pinch, if you don't want to drive to the other one, it's good.

Not a destination.  It's a last resort type when all your faves are packed.  Their hash looked like it came out of a can/ flavorless.  The sausage gravy was missing....sausage.  If you like average at best food with great service by all means do go.  It's bright and airy and noisy.  Too bad.

Apparently they're not very interested in selling used cars.  I sent in an email inquiry concerning a used X3 (which had been listed for at least a month.. you'd think they'd be excited over interest in it... ) The emailed response was to restate the mileage and price, both were already contained in the listing.  I called to schedule a test drive and ask about any other comparable X3's on the lot.  Ralph was too busy to talk to me and promised he'd call back.  He never did, so I guess I'll be going elsewhere!

Very nice place, great menu and some of the best Pad Thai I've ever had.   The staff are super-friendly and the atmosphere very inviting.  I'm glad to have tried this place and I'll definitely be back.

My girlfriend and I go here at least once a week. Their food is always good but staff seems to be getting slower and slower. Also, when you order take out I think they charge more than menu prices and they are unwilling to give you extra salsa without squeezing more money out of you (and this is from the owner). For being cheap, you lost our weekly ($30) business since it was too hard to provide an extra salsa cup. Poor customer service.

Went in and was greeted by an employee and immediately helped knowing our service was carryout because we were on our last day in Florida having visited from Minnesota and wanted to spend the rest of the night at home and watch movies, but ended up waiting over 30minutes for our food and another 10 minutes in, still haven't gotten my food. During this time, over 2-3 tables had come and gotten their food way even when they arrived AFTER us. By then Nicole the grill girl whose attitude sucked from the start and even seen given attitudes to her coworkers cane over to ask if we were ready and what our order was. It was apparent now that Nicole didn't even bother with our carryout ticket and more on the waited tables. I should say by now we were a group of six so you can imagine how frustrated we were at waiting at 10pm just wanting to get our food quickly and go home. I suggested to another employee who helped us that they should fire Nicole because to be honest, she sucked at her job and her attitude stinks. Will visit again but will never visit again until Nicole is gone from this great food chain I've greatly raved about from my other Steak 'n Shake visits in Georgia. FIRE NICOLE! Thanks! No customer wants to be given an attitude over their order when it should've been known what our orders were when they were taken from the register. After getting back home after a WHOLE hour, my fries were hard and very unenjoyable from being over fried and my fries I melt had no cheese!! First and last trip to this location.

I give it a 3 because i've only had frozen yogurt TWICE!!!

this was my second time. it was my first self-serve. i felt like a little kid at a candy store... literally. they had tons of sweet treat toppings! mmm-mm!

i got strawberry, cookies n cream or oreo (i dont remember) some recee's cereal and sprinkles... then i discovered the fresh fruit! so i threw some strawberries in there.. i paid around $4 and some change.

not bad, it was filling and delicious! its convenient location is nice since i go to drexel.. and west philly doesnt have ritas... 

cant really say much about the staff... can we try to smile and pretend we're happy. please? tis all...

We were disappointed. The interior is nicely decorated and the menu is simple. The staff didn't seemed to be well organized yet, so we waited a while for meat from the flat top, but the freshly cooked tortillas sat and ended up cold. The set up is typical taqueria style with warming trays of ingredients in view, while you pick out your choices. The protein choices looked dry and somewhat sad. The beans were not remarkable, we did not try the rice. The filings in the taco were somewhat slim. The pico de gallo was tasty, the guacamole (price extra) was salty and inedible. Too bad, Clayton needs good, affordable restaurants.

I've always been the type of person afraid of change and I tend to be fiercely loyal when it comes to keeping a stylist around. Nevertheless, I HAD to get my hair done because I attempted to color my hair myself at home during the pandemic and it backfired in the worst way. I had grays growing in that were bothering me (of course everyone else swears they couldn't see them), and had been watching youtube videos for weeks about color theory which gave me way too much confidence. What youtube doesn't tell you is that when you're pregnant, your hormones can completely change how your hair reacts to color. After being frustrated that a darker color wouldn't take on my roots, I bleached my whole head and everything from the ears up turned into an orange nightmare. Thankfully, Delaney was able to save the day for me. My baby shower is Sunday and I wasn't about to finally step out of the house with bad hair. It took a lot but she did it. My husband said my hair is super sexy and I'm getting a lot of compliments. Most importantly, she was able to fix it without damaging it. I like that the salon is a smaller salon as I can feel overwhelmed in big salons. They did a great job implementing and following new pandemic protocols and made sure that everything was sanitized between customers. I left feeling relaxed, confident, and believe that the price to fix my nightmare was more than fair, and rebooked Delaney for a few months from now. I will never ever attempt to try to do my own hair again, nor complain about salon costs. This was worth every penny to be done right. At the end of the day, there are some things you should never a attempt to do yourself: Plumbing, Electrical, Changing your Oil, Or coloring your hair. These situations necessitate a professional! Thank you so so so much for saving me from myself Delaney! Now I have the confidence to rock my baby bump at my shower and get my maternity photos taken. I'll be back!
We went there tonight for trivia which was fun but the food was disgusting! Nachos, gravy fries, wings, and turkey club all were awful. I'm not sure how this place is still open??

We went here our first time in Nashville and loved it. Good craft beer selection (and excellent recommendations by our server.)  Prices were reasonable and so were the portions. My fiancé and I ordered the pickle plate as an app, and it was pretty good, even though we both thought we were getting a fried pickle sample plate.  It was very possible we just didn't read carefully though. I had the hot chicken. Not Hattie B's but very moist and delicious, and we both LOVED the corn cake it was served on.  Fiancé got the DELICIOUS short rib pot roast and a side of yummy crisp green beans.  Overall, we would definitely go back, as the price, service and portions were good.
Great authentic Mexican food and not to mention price is very reasonable! Service was excellent as well. Definitely a place to come with family and enjoy!
Food was sub par bar food. Just "ok". I hate wasting calories on "ok" food! They've got a great location, windows were open, but there is nothing to bring me back.

OH MY GOSH, this place is a shopping mecca from Heaven. I looooove this Schnuck's, and to my surprise, I felt like I was big ballin' in the place.

So maybe "big ballin'" is a bit exaggerated, but in all truth, this place is extremely clean, organized, and possibly the nicest Schnuck's I have ever stepped foot into in the greater Saint Louis metro area. I was thoroughly impressed.

First off, the deli department people are so so so nice. I ordered 3 different items from them, and before they would go to slice the cheese or meat, they offer you a tasting to make sure it is what you want - slice size and cut. I didn't request it which I usually do elsewhere. While I waited there, they repeated this with every customer and they did it with a smile on their face. Talk about true quality customer service. 

The produce department is exceptional. It was definitely bigger than the one on at the South Grand location that I use as my primary Schnuck's. I think my only question is -- why is it 6 cents more for bananas per pound at this Schnuck's than the South Grand one? I did notice that. Maybe they do pricing based on location? Or just maybe pricing on bananas went up in the last couple of days since I last visited? That would make more sense. 

Comparing this Schnuck's to the South Grand Schnuck's is like comparing an apple with a zucchini. It just can't be done. They are two worlds apart even though they're in the same city. Clayton Road definitely serves a different demographic. When I went was around 7pm in the evening, everyone in there was either sporting white collar work attire or exercise gear fresh out of the gym.  South Grand, well.... to put it blatantly, it's ghetto. Not the same million dollar feeling I felt at Clayton Road. 

I like it. I indeed will be back in the near future. In the mean time though, I think I will stick to convenience and go to South Grand.
If I lived around that area it would be OK, the wings are good, could use an upgrade inside

Love this place! Great food and the staff is friendly. I have dinner here every week. The carnitas are delicious and my boyfriend is hooked on the burrito al carbon. I highly recommend this restaurant!

Located in an up and coming family friendly area, this place should attract quite a few regulars from the area.  
Decorated in a sort of rustic / Etsy style, its warm and welcoming.  

I wanted food and a coffee, i figured this place would fit the bill.  Menu is on a chalk board behind the till, its easy to read, and it lists soups, sandwiches and flatbread options.   

I chose a BLT.  Unfortunately, the sandwiches are from a fridge.  They are pre made....and also $9.

Tasty?  Sure.  But $9 for a cold pre made BLT?  No thank you.  

Latte was decent, as was the lemon tart.
Great Phó in Reno! Service was great and the food was amazing! Serving size is great for the price!

Pizza Factory? More like crap factory...

Can't rate the service as we called in an order for pickup. Over the phone we asked about breadsticks and they told us they had twisted breadsticks (FYI - the only thing twisted about them was the flavor and the fact that they were the hardest breadsticks I've ever had).

We ordered the pesto, garlic and sun-dried tomato pizza. Sounds great right? Wrong... Way, way, way too much cheese.

Would not recommend going and I'll never be back. Which really sucks as they are right down the street.  :(

1 star is even being generous.

Where do I begin. I have been visiting dogs and cats at this SPCA for years. I must say that I have seen beautiful purebred dogs here which is very uncommon to see it in the Philadelphia SPCA which house mostly pitbulls. It seems that any dog I've ever been interested in at this Montgomery County SPCA required applications to be filled out and decisions selecting an adopter always in the future and it has never worked out for me. I consider myself an animal lover with many years of experience. I own my own home I walk my dogs 2 to 3 times a day. I buy them only the best foods and treats. I never cage my pets, and I never go on vacation because I don't trust anyone with my . I have excellent vet references and still I can never manage to get a dog from the Montgomery County SPCA. There are two very troubling things that come to mind as I write this critique and the most recent has to do with me finding a dog that I fell in love with, who was turned in as a stray, was described as a young male, and if you were interested you had to put an application in and you were constantly told that there were no guarantees and nobody would get back to you unless you were the person that was chosen so it was your responsibility to call as often as you wanted to see if the dog was still available. I made several calls and up to this point three visits to play with the dog and the young man that brought him out explain to me that this puppy being so young would have to be neutered before anyone took him home. I asked him to check and see if the dog was neutered at this time and it was only then that we realized that this male puppy was actually a female. This pup was in the care of the Montgomery County SPCA for 2 weeks and no one knew that it was a female it was advertised as male on Petfinders and also listed on the card on the kennel and listed in their computer system as a male. How can this happen? Was this dog not ever examined. Of course I was heartbroken to learn this because my situation will not allow me to bring a female into the house. Now another thing that really concerns me is the fact that for many years, when I have visited the Kennels, three-quarters of the kennels are always empty, see the attached photos. How can this possibly be. Rescues are begging for Foster's because there's no place to put these dogs yet the Montgomery County SPCA is 75% empty, and I mean every time that I've been there. Although I have seen some Staffordshire terriers, there we're basically no pitbulls. I don't know for sure why there no dogs at this site to speak of, and I fear that a lot of dogs are being euthanized there. I don't know for sure but being an SPCA why aren't they helping the other SPCAs that are overcrowded. In summation this SPCA has few animals and it's very difficult to adopt from them. The people at the reception desks have always been nice and professional. They're actually the best part of this location. I am grateful to them for any dogs that manage to find their way to a new good loving forever home and that I thank you for Montgomery County SPCA but you need to open your doors for some of the many dogs that are need of a shelter. And finally who's ever making the decision on the best possible adopter get off your butt and make the damn decision for the good of the animals.
My favorite poke chain in Santa Barbara. Lots of creativity and thought put into the menu. Kind workers and good food

What a great place! Service is personable and excellent, they are timely with their turnaround and, best of all, my clothes always look great and never smell like some weird chemical (as I have experienced elsewhere). 

Once, I came to drop off and found the front desk guy in the parking lot doing his level best to help a woman who had locked herself out of her car. He politely excused himself, took care of my need, and went back to her aid. It was so nice and refreshing to see someone going out of their way to help someone else. Those are the kind of people I want to give my business to and this is one of those rare businesses where I'm likely to make the drive to use them even if I move out of the neighborhood.

What a find....my work order lunch from there last week omg it is awesome. I went there for dinner and got the pastrami cheesesteak  delicious. If you are in the area stop in you will not be disappointed

If this one wasn't the closest to me. I would not go here. One of the employees was mopping the floor. Then decided to start fixing my banana split. I had to ask her to please wash your hands first. Every time I go in there no one is using gloves.

In all fairness, I ordered take out via Grubhub.  All of my orders came with meat, but I specified no pork or beef products, which common sense would tell you to replace with turkey or call to clarify.  They place my order with no meat and forgot to add my kids orange juice which I did specify.  The semi rude employee tried to justify the mistakes and told me I could come back for the kids juice if I wanted to...ummm... thanks but no thanks.  What happened to great customer service...

A mix of hipster and homey, this place has delicious iced lattes. I love how they roast their coffee beans at this very location. Service is impeccable, however, I was disappointed to learn after I got my drink that the worker charged me a medium sized latte when I ordered a small size. Also, normally 8oz iced lattes run betweem $3.65-3.85. This place charges the full $4.00. Speaking trying to rake up prices and being profitable. However, I cannot deny the delicious lattes here and would come back.

Very not bad! The thing i like about sushi is that it is hard to F it up. Since there are only about 3 ingredients, the only real dynamic is the amount of food and the price. $11.07 with tax for 3 rolls of sushi is pretty damn good.  And these aren't just Calfornia rolls ladies and gentlemen, but Spicy Yellow Tail or Spicy Salmon or 10 other varieties. The roll size isn't the largest, but I don't feel like I am getting ripped off either. They are an okay size. I have eaten here twice and I wouldn't stand for the micro-roll chicanery.
Service was ehh.
I think my apartment living room is bigger than the size of the restaurant, so keep that in mind.

Oddly, this place doesn't appear to be open on weekends.

Food Quality/Taste = 4 Stars
Portion Size Given Price = 3 Stars
Price = 4 Stars
Service = 3 Stars
Experience with Staff = 3 Stars
Decor = 2 Star

Finally, a comfy coffee shop in St. Louis! The staff was friendly, the prices were good, and the atmosphere is perfect. It's like Central Perk meets book store. 

Two minor criticisms: the chai latte was better than Starbucks and Kaldi's Firepot blend, but it still doesn't compete with Blueprint Coffee chai blend. And why can't we have a late night coffeehouse in St. Louis. I get that it is better to have a coffee house close at 7 than 2 like most cafes around here, but why not open until 9 or 10 a couple nights a week? Again...minor complaints. 

Love this place already!

Truly wonderful, attentive service. The atmosphere was a little touristy, but fun. My friends and I all really enjoyed the food. A little overpriced for the amount and quality, especially the poboys. My friend had the Mardi Gras pasta, which they said was really tasty, but more sauce than pasta.

Overall: Decent food, good service, fun atmosphere, pretty pricey.

Patio Pools has excellent field staff who do excellent work once on the job.  One gets the feeling, though, that the management is more interested in bookings and backlog than they are in creating satisfied customers.  When working with them, I always get the feeling their personal convenience is more important than getting my job completed.  As an example, at one point during the project I called to get a status and it is clear that our job had dropped off their board.  It's disappointing because the folks who do the actual work are great.  
I can recommend Patio Pools if you don't care when the work gets done, how much it costs, and how much you will be inconvenienced by their lack of scheduling prowess.
Been here since it first started. The owner is very nice and friendly. They have good authentic Taiwanese food and add the boba drinks too. Will always come back here. (Will edit with pictures soon)

I brought my kitten here for a cut on her head. They did a phenomenal job. Dr Summers and her vet tech are outstanding! I would absolutely recommend this place to anyone looking for care of their animals.

I am not ashamed! I come by the drive through JUST for a vanilla ice cream cone. I am convinced though that they have changed their formula because it is now more of a frozen yogurt consistent and color. Where as before it was more creamy and white vanilla. Maybe it's just me... but i need answers.

Everything was great, the older gentlemen in the restaurant area was giving excellent service, but there is one young lady that walked like she was dragging her sat, poor service, no courtesy, hardly said hi when you walked in and never said goodbye, she definitely gave a big ZERO as far as service goes! She worked the breakfast on February 6 & 7th 2019. She definitely should not be working in the Hospitality nor service industry!!!!!
Disappointed!! Was ok when they first opened. Hardly no fish on the tacos. Super skimpy. Probably won't be going back.

Every time...

They're never serving blast in the evening bc the machine overheats. 


Every time.

the price is good and you get the bang for your buck that you deserve, the wait staff is attentive and cares for what you need. the food came out fairly fast and hot. good family environment, not too loud, could just improvements on the recipes start tasting alittle bland. overall review: try it out it's a quality breakfast spot!

This place is so-so. Different and unique food which is a positive. My breakfast wrap was bland and boring. The salsa was good. The hushpuppies were deece. I think the crab cake breakfast dish was the best at the table. Good service.

I hate only giving this place three stars because Chef Murphy does a mean breakfast that'll kick a hangover's ass. The atmosphere is really nice and I always find something new and interesting to look at. 

Unfortunately, the service is abysmal. I mean, it's really really bad. I went there this morning for breakfast with a friend. The restaurant was less than half full, however there were several people waiting just inside the door, so the two of us headed to the bar. It took almost 15 minutes before anyone approached us to even hand us menus or take drink orders. The bartender was nowhere to be found. At one point, Chef Murphy poked his head out of the kitchen and looked around for...someone? Anyone? I'm a server too so I tend to be more forgiving than most, but the few people who were waiting tables seemed to be in no rush, wandering about aimlessly. Finally a girl (a hostess, I think) took our orders. Even then it took another ten minutes to get coffee. While we were waiting, another guest who was clearly irritated asked the girl for toast, butter, jelly, and several other necessities for their meal since they hadn't seen their server in a long time. 

The Irish House has great potential, but they need to kick their serving staff into high gear to really live up to it. I honestly wish I could write more about the great food, but the poor service overrode most of my positive feelings.

This place is super cute! It's in a house that has a lot of fun spaces for chatting and drinking coffee. It also has a lot of fun options for syrups and specialty drinks. Unfortunately, the coffee is just not good. I had an iced latte that I couldn't even finish. The coffee was overly strong (and that's coming from someone who typically likes strong coffee). I was not expecting that from a latte! I had salted caramel flavor added to mine that was just overpowering and did not mesh with the equally overpowering coffee.

I would sooner risk going to CHIPOTLE and a bout of food poisoning than EVER going back to ANY of the ZUPAS restaurant. If I could leave a zero star rating I would.
I ordered a chicken pesto sandwich that looked and tasted like a dirty sock!  It was nothing more than a cheap chicken salad with a pinch of pesto mixed into the mayo sause that was oozing out of the bread. The menu is very misleading from what you actually get. In SMALL print its did say "chicken pesto salad".
The chicken pesto sandwich looked nothing like its representation on the menu. It was more liken to a chicken pesto roadkill as it was flat as a pancake with its guts squished out. I had it replaced with a ham sandwich, it too was a roadkill sandwich. One slice of salty ham, turkey and what looked to be a tomato, and again an OILSLICK of a mayo goo.

As for the staff. We were greeted promtly and courteously and that is where it all ended. The KIDS behind the count are more concerned with conversations between each other than to what is actually going on.
.

Absolutely THE BEST happy hour in St. Petersburg for those of us that are wine connoisseurs! The selection of wines by the glass and by the bottle is outstanding.

From 4-6 pm Monday - Friday all glasses of wine are 50% off. Yes! You read correctly. So, stop reading and start driving (or walking) to Sauvignon!

The server was nice. However, she served us the wrong pizza, and had to wait for the correct one. The prices are good. Don't get the side salad. It's super tiny for the price. The pizza is good. Nothing was outstanding or memorable.
Terrible service  -  they don't stand behind their work. We have been dealing with them for over 2 months to reimburse us and they are showing ZERO accountability.  Do. Not.  Use.  Them.   Ever!!!!
Another welcome addition to the area.  I like the cafe/coffee-shop setup.  They have a pretty simple menu with quality ingredients.  I look forward to going back.

This place has good po boys, entrees, and daily specials at decent prices.  They are only open for lunch and cash only which makes it very inconvenient.  They seem to have a deal with a quite a few tour operators as many tour groups seem to end up inside here so if you time it wrong you might end up in a LONG line.  Overall it is a decent place to grab lunch when there isn't a tour group ahead of you and the building has a nice old school ambiance with lots of seating.  I like the fact that the owner seems to always be there and has a hands on approach to running his business.  They would make a whole lot more money here if they stayed open later and took credit cards and I know I would eat here often in the evenings if they were open so maybe one day they will get better hours and finally take credit cards.

Went to Kabuto's for Valentine's dinner after having not been for a long time. While the prices are very reasonable, I agree with others that the place is in desperate need of cleaning/updating and the wait staff is very rude.  Argued several times with our waitress who basically threw everything at us and did not speak good English.  She was very unfriendly and unpleasant.  The chef was friendly but you could tell he was kind of tired.  I know it was a busy night for them but doubt if I will be back.
Disappointment...  Wife had the shrimp and I had the sea food platter.  Both order were left in the deep fryer to long.   The seafood platter was skimpy.

From bad to worse...

We visited Bonefish Montgomeryville, PA.  We arrived and waited to be seated, and had requested a booth if possible.  The hostess spent a bit of time looking at her screen, and finally went to seat us RIGHT NEXT TO THE KITCHEN.  We kindly asked if we might switch to one of the other quieter availble booths and there were plenty.  The restaurant was only about 1/4 full.  The hostess acted as if this was a huge inconvenience, but reluctantly accommodated our request.  She then told us to scan the QR code for the menu.  When we told her we didn't have a phone in the restaurant, she acted again like this was another huge inconvenience to her, but she stated she would return with a menu.  She never came back, but instead spent her time chatting with coworkers and sipping a beverage.  

When a waitress finally showed up 10 minutes or more later she asked what we wanted to order and we explained we were still waiting for the hostess to bring the menu.  

Once we finally received the menus we placed our orders and the food came out a reasonable time later.  We ordered the shrimp and scallops skewers and blackened fish tacos.  The skewers had a distinctive taste much like lighter fluid, I don't know if the grill wasn't cleaned well or what.  
The fish taco was extremely fishy, I couldnt get more than one of them down.  It wasn't long before I wasn't feeling well.  Pretty sure that fish was bad, sitting here sick now.
Waitress wasnt very attentive and noticed I had left 2 of the 3 tacos untouched but didnt seem to care.

Food and service has gone way down hill.  We were so disappointed overall.  I don't know how it could be so bad. The restaurant wasn't that busy, and there was plenty of staff.

Though these guys were more expensive than the other three bids I received I decided to go with them anyway because the owner, Bill, seemed very detail oriented and professional. I was not disappointed. I got exactly what I paid for: painting perfection. His polite team of painters painted the exterior of our home with the precision most use only for crown molding on an inside job. Not a drop of paint anywhere. They re-caulked and replaced wood as needed. Whenever a problem arose, Bill would text me a photo of the situation and ask me my opinion of what to do. I highly recommend these guys.

One of the original tenants in the shopping center. Nostalgic, good, fairly priced and plethora of options for a deli. It's a 5 star deli / diner, not a 5 star restaurant. You want quality food and don't want to pay a fortune? Good option if you have an hour break. I've come to appreciate if more, now that I've moved away from Delco.
We ordered fries and taters. Messed up my cheese fry order twice. Apparently our order wasnt the only one screwed up. The bartender was cool though, seemed hella stressed out by the kitchen staff.

Well we're to start. This place needs a to shut its doors. The service is the worst. Four of us went in to eat. Sat down order coffee and water. Got the coffee but no water or condiments for the coffee. Had to ask twice. Then we ordered. I had the (dry) pulled pork poutine. And a over dressed Ceasar salad. The brisket was like eating a over cooked leather shoe. The baked potato looked like it was left in the steamer over night and reheated. The bar bq sandwich was dry and over cooked. Plus one order of onion rings to them is five pieces only. So if your down for the worst eating experience of your life go here. We asked them what can you do for us Concidering we didn't not eat our food we still had to pay full price. And the mad look on the person's face when we did not leave a tip was shocking. So I say to you. Shut down. Do yourself a favor close the doors.

Delicious noodles for an unbeatable price!

Been here twice already it sorta became a Togo place fo my boyfriend and I after hiking. We always get the Vietnamese coffee, spring rolls, rice paper rolls, and pho, and some other Thai and Lao inspired noodle soups. 

Everything was delicious and very fairly priced. Only thing about the ice coffee was that I wish it was stronger and less sweet. 

But other than that a huge bang for your buck place! And would definitely come back!

S L O W service, meh food and a bit pricey.

It seems no matter what time I go the service is slow. And not because they are super busy either. 

It was especially bad a week ago when I stood in line for 15 minutes behind 5 other individuals placing orders only to be told that my order would take 25 more minutes. Really? at 1:30PM in a "fast food" joint? You expect me to wait a total of 40 minutes? 

Um, no. I left. 

It's not like they were short-handed as they had no less than 5 people making the food and many more back in the kitchen. 

I could see if the food was really good but the meat is chewy and overcooked and you're lucky if you get a teaspoon-sized portion in your burrito. 

The salsa is really bland and flavorless and the queso should be called salto. And then expect to pay over $10 if you want a drink and chips. 

Being from California I can only laugh at this place. They have no clue what constitutes a real burrito.

Hooray for more cupcakes in University City!  I visited the grand opening today and tried a vanilla cupcake with strawberry frosting.  The strawberry frosting was delicious, but alas, there was very little of it -- a very thin layer that barely covered the top of the cake.  The cake was dense and surprisingly chewy, as cake goes.  The cafe itself is very cute, and the employees are friendly -- I'll definitely be back to try more.

I went and got a haircut in April for a cruise I was going on the next day! I told her I wanted long layers. She gave me a horrible haircut. She gave me bangs, which I never said I wanted. In the time that I got my hair washed, cut and dried, my friend got her hair washed and blow dried straight. That lady fried my friends hair. It was crispy. I will never go to this place EVER again!

Idk. Any joy I get from visiting this Starbucks diminishes at the annoying light that takes forever wish I could chose a different way out, but not so.  The store is nice and quick, an friendly, everything I enjoy about the brand, its just I don't want to visit because of the hassle to get out of there.

What this intersection needs is a pretty round about. That way everyone keeps moving and doesn't have to wait forever at that damn light.

Maybe I'll go again and try to get out another way. Ughhh. Im getting old !!!

Great spot but I have to be honest the service SUCKS. On my most recent visit my server mumbled maybe 10 words to me the whole time I was there. Seems there isn't enough staff to pull the load. Again great hang out pub but don't expect stellar service

This place is super old and smells as if it has a septic issue downstairs in the gym area. It reminds me of an old rundown apartment they have fixed up just enough to get some customers. The gym is a treadmill, a bike and elliptical and some dumbbells that's it. My key card messed up multiple times on the elevator and room. I got locked out of both.  My toilet runs all night and the shower and toilet drain super slow. The elevator is rickety and requires a key card but you can take the stairs and no key required to access the floor on stairwell.
They had some weird Argentinian ballroom dancing on Sundays in the bar area which threw me for a loop in Music city. 
The waitress at breakfast was super nice and breakfast was not that bad but it's all a la carte menu so they try to be upscale and high priced on food.

Please do not waste your time with Schicker Ford. We made an appointment Friday to look at a car on Saturday.  We drove 30 min to the dealership just to find out they sold the car without even having the courtesy to call and tell us.  The salesman was very unprofessional and did not even apologize. In addition, they raised the price of the car $2,000 online Friday night before our appointment with no explanation. There were many negative reviews of this dealership on Yelp and I made the mistake giving them a chance. Please learn from my mistake along with many others and do not waste your time with this shady dealership.

If I could leave less than one star I would. I went on a date Saturday with my significant other and the service was absolutely horrendous. I tried to give it a shot but honestly, I wish I hadn't. 

The host seemed confused from the moment we walked in and that should've been our first warning sign. Then, our server wasn't attentive at all. He came over in 40 minute increments and after waiting for almost an hour for our food, he told us it should be coming up any moment. When the wait is that long, I'd expect some sort of more specific answer because obviously a problem needs to be addressed. The food finally came out and it was ice cold. The moment I looked forward to most the whole night was leaving. 

I would not recommend this place until they get their staff managed and trained because I can't blame our server and host for the service. It's a direct reflection of poor management.
The ribs have an AMAZING smoke flavor and the beans are just delish-with quite a bit of kick to it. Definitely going back.

Moron cook thought it would be funny to hide cheese in an omelet ordered with no cheese. Then joked with the wait staff, "you said extra cheese right?" Not funny to a person with a dairy allergy.  Fire that liability.

A bunch of idiots run this place. never have I ever been to a club that required more than one set of i.D. This place is a shame to every club in the tri-state area. Not friendly, nor are they even remotely what one would consider a quality establishment. Go to philly instead. Much better atmosphere than this shithole.

Doesn't CVS have any rules about dogs in their store?! Thousands if not millions of children are bitten in the face every year because dogs are at their level. So you can't even go into a CVS and turn a corner without worrying about your child being bitten the face! If I ever see this again I will never ever shop in any CVS anywhere in the country. The Ridiculousness needs to stop. If people can't go outside their homes without some animal to comfort them and hold their hand, then maybe they should just stay home. What about the people who are allergic to dogs and their hair? Since when in any common-sense Arena do you put animals before human beings?

After having a pretty lousy customer service experience at another restaurant we tried eating at for lunch, we wandered into Fido with high hopes, given that it seemed like a pretty nice and popular spot.  The woman that took our order was pretty helpful and friendly so it was instantly off to a better start than where we came from (Arnold's Country Kitchen).

I ordered a flank steak and egg scramble with potatoes that I was pretty happy with.  My friend ordered chocolate chip pancakes, a yogurt/fruit/granola parfait and side orders of bacon and french fries.  Really, the only complaint he had with his food is that the pancakes were a little overcooked to the point that the chips in them tasted too much like burnt chocolate.

The thing that tipped the scale in our decision to go there were the rave reviews of the hot chocolate.  However, we weren't that impressed.  When I think of deluxe hot chocolate I think of a thick, flavorful drink that fills a huge mug, but ours was pretty watery and lukewarm, like the kind of stuff you could make at home or in the hotel we're staying at without spending $3.50 for an regular-sized cup.

Even the marshmallows couldn't give the appropriate texture and taste to my bev that I was looking for.

Other than that, it was pretty good and reasonably priced.  Most importantly, we left with full, happy bellies.
our first delivery attempt and the pizza arrived stone COLD!!!!

Fried mush for breakfast means Im back home again in Indiana!! It couldnt have been better--crispy on the outside and covered with butter and syrup. Pure Perfection! They also serve broasted chicken but we were too early to order it. Whats wrong with chicken for breakfast?? Coffee was very good. Hubby got oatmeal which came with bananas, raisins, brown sugar and milk. Service was great even though they were shorthanded. Now thats what breakfast should be!

Not really a review but a comment I hope someone at the shop reads..,7pm Saturday night and for the last 20 minutes NOBODY is picking up the phone.  BAD BUSINESS!!!
We order frequently...such a shame!

My husband, two kids and I came in on a cold weekday night hoping to get some hot soup. We walked in an stood around for a couple minutes waiting to be acknowledged... no hello or anything. We figured we'd sit ourselves down since this place is so tiny we were blocking the doorway. We sat at the booth next to the door and I hate to say it but the set up was crappy. Anytime the door opened, we caught a super strong cold current. The establishment itself was already cold enough. It was really uncomfortable. My husband said. "Our food will be cold within a few minutes if we sit here." Anyway, we were starving so we stayed and waited, and waited, looked around and waited some more. We looked at the menu, looked up and still no one. I saw the owner or employee at another booth chit chatting with a family. We still waited a bit longer and then decided to get up and leave. Their service was ridiculous. On the way out the same lady who was chit chatting with the other family looked up and gave my husband a confused look like, was I supposed to help you? When we drove by on the way to another place we noticed she was now sitting with the family who was having dinner with her back to the entry door. What kind of service is that! It's too bad! I really wanted to try this place. Maybe in the summer when it's not so cold.

As a sushi slut, I'd give this place a 3/5.  A decent menu with lots of options for an affordable price.  Didn't care for the beef bowl, but the rolls and nigiri were decent.  
I can see regular sushi citizens enjoying this place without any complaints.  The seating is a bit odd as some of the tables are on uneven concrete, but whatevs.
Spent about $40 for two and both left still a bit hungry.

The best Korean food you'll find in Cherry Hill. I've eaten here countless times throughout my childhood and into my adulthood and it will also be my favorite Dolsot. Even after moving to New York, I still believe this place has one of the best Dolsots. 

I am also a big fan of their dumpling soup and also their tofu stew. Mostly anything you get here will be amazing. 

The sides here are fun, and they'll always give you more if you ask! 

In recent years this place has gotten so crowded. I came for lunch, and I waited an hour for my food. However, I still love this place and will always return.

Have to admit, was pretty nervous from the time I drove up to this building, up until we got our food, then I was pleasantly surprised. Weird little plaza mall location but the sushi was good and the service was great. Didn't like the chairs around the sushi bar seating area with wheels on them, and those little lamps on the tables have got to go! Our table was kind of sticky too, otherwise I was pleasantly surprised and would most definitely come visit again for the hibachi tables. 3 stars for awesome service and good food. Check them out :-)

We really enjoy this place. Whether it's sitting in the bar, or sitting in the dining area. The food is always delicious, I have never had a bad meal. My daughters favorite burger is the Billy burger  is hands-down one of the best. The burgers are always cooked just right, never chewy.   Very TASTY!! The fries are awesome. 

The Caesar salad is DELICIOUS, and man it's big as well. Wing sauce is killer.  

Staff is always great. Very clean, great menu selection.

Second time stopping by here for a casual dinner with some friends...got the potato soup since it was a black board special and it was FANTASTIC!! The star dish of dinner was definitely the chicken and waffles my friend received, definitely can see why it's so well known and deserved. Overall the atmosphere is what you would expect a diner to be, dress casual, and very lowkey. The service was nothing but short of what's expected, nothing over the top, and always polite!

Tried this spot while visiting Santa Barbara. Had high hopes but was let down. Raviolis were 10/10. Mozzarella sticks were cold so 3/10. Cheese fries were soggy 5/10 we were not expecting sausage on them. Wings were 10/10 we had mango habanero and garlic Parmesan. Hot cheeto corn cup was 1/10. The portion was big but there there was no mayo and little cheese. Burger was delicious 6/10

I've only eaten at a Popeyes once,  dined it, and thought it was ok. But it seems that Popeyes has a Corporate hiring and/or training problem. One looked sultry Yelp ratings of at least 10 or 12 different locations. Never good ratings with most being about 2.  Same criticism for all of them - long waits, wrong orders, bad service,  terrible Customer service, poor attitudes, etc etc. this is a Corporate problem so come on Popeyes, look at the commonality of the reviews and remediate!!!!!!

I have had a great experience with Mackenzie from beginning to now. I was just about to fly out to Vegas to get my eyebrows micro bladed when I came across her work on Facebook. I'm glad I found someone in my backyard who does such a great job. My right eyebrow is completely scarred and she did a fantastic job covering it up and matching them to my hair color. Everyone thinks they are my real eyebrows! I would recommend to everyone
Customer service was meh...hence the 3 stars. We ordered their signature black tea w/the tapioca-bubbles were soft and fresh. The drink was good nothing to really write about.

This restaurant was beautifully decorated. We were there at Tuesday night. To avoid waiting for table. We sat at the bar. I had a breaded steak. When I had to choose sides, I was told some items involved up charge. I ended up getting yellow rice and sweet plantain. We waited for about 20 minutes till food came out. The presentation was nice. The food tasted good. The service was attentive. It was a OK experience.
I've tried many Cuban sandwiches before and this one hits #1 in my list. My friends who ordered differently enjoyed their sandwiches as well. I need to come back again and try something new.
Cheese fries good...burgers ok...beers wayyy to expensive.  Especially for New Orleans

Great service and really good food. Never had this type of food before and I liked it. I ordered plain with no spice and it was made just as I ordered it.  

Other family members ordered spicy dishes and they were all pleased with their selections. 
No one was there at first except my party and as the evening progressed, the restaurant started to get filled.  

They must be doing something right!

Interesting business model where anyone can come in a get a growler filled from one of thirty taps. Good selection of beers from local IPAs to a Belgium Tripel (Gulden Draak.) minimal environment but excellent beers by the pint to a growler. (Parking can be tight though.) Friendly Service! Give these guys a visit!

These guys break the stigma of 'Mechanics are out to get you'. Quality work at an affordable price, locally owned, and above all timely and honest in their assessments and work. I've taken multiple cars here for years, and will continue to do so. Glad these guys still have their corner.

Pros: Genuinely delicious food. Good coffee. Amazing atmosphere, whether you sit indoor or outdoor. Books. Couches. 

Cons: Waitstaff always seems a little annoyed. Shit is PRICEY! Seriously, an egg sandwich, a bagel, and 2 coffees ran me like 20 bucks. It's insane. 

I go for One Shot for the experience and the menu is good, it's just clearly overpriced. If you can get down with that I'd say it's worth going.

I went here for Chinese food when working the area. I ordered the beef on a stick and house special fried rice. The beef on the stick was edible but not really that good (also pricey). The fried rice was as bland as ever and the meats in it were not very tasty. Even drowned in soy sauce I couldn't eat it. They may have good sushi but their Chinese is disappointing.

Came here on a Saturday night for date night.  We walked in and were seated immediately.  The staff all had proper mask and other PPE gear that made us feel safe during this pandemic.

We ordered Martini and margaritas, filet minon, ny strip, mac & cheese and asparagus.  Food was delicious and very juicy.  I was very happy with my selection.  Portions could be bigger, but it was filling.

I visited from NYC and I was pleasantly surprised for this PA steakhouse.  I would visit again.

Not really worth the price or your time. Took the combined Laura/Oak Alley plantation tour and was gone from 11:45am until after 7pm. That time made so much longer by the fact that we were stuck forever doing drop offs in the French quarter. Those staying there who were lucky enough to jump off the bus and walk after sitting in traffic and not moving were luckier than those of us in the garden district. As the first ones picked up in the morning it would have been nice to not then be dropped off last. The tour at Laura was good, but the tour at Oak Alley was really lacking. And the bus exhaust was so bad during our drive that you could barely stand it. Not worth how much we paid and not worth an entire day/evening if you ask me.
Woot! Drag show, dancing, no cover, and free shots. Very loud. Sort of grungy. Adequate seating. Could use more bathrooms. Nice open air patio. VERY LOUD.

This store has a great variety of firearms and Fred was very helpful and knowledgeable. The process for purchasing a rifle was smooth and went fairly quickly! Would definitely recommend checking this store out for guns and accessories!

Cafe Square One is a great local business on 3rd Street. The service is a little slow, but the food and the coffee are pretty tasty. It's not the greatest - I'd give this place 3.5 stars, if I could - but good enough for a quick bite.

I was very surprised when I didn't see a line stretching around the block. We went in and were seated at the bar immediately. We had first row seats to the OPENING OF THE OYSTERS!!

That's an official event in NOLA if you weren't aware.

The food, the service, the atmosphere is something I will always come back for. 

Even if you don't like oysters you have to try one...even if it's covered in garlic/cheese/butter goodness

With this place you get what you pay for I guess. The salad dressing was super watery. We ordered extra and both only got half a serving extra like one of the tins were split for us. Also did not get fresh cracked pepper. I had to use table pepper. Ended up opting to take it home and add my thicker dressing at home. 

The steak had no flavor what so ever and was not that high in quality. Mine was a tad more cooked than the medium I'd ordered. 
The asparagus was so flimsy and ill prepared. The server did make sure we had our beverages filled and I could tell it's a family establishment.
Hands down Irene is the best colorist I have been too in this area!  Her hair cuts never disappoint.  Professional and great quality.  COVID-19 friendly.
Sushi is horrible. Fish isn't fresh. Stick with the grill menu, its much better. On the bright side, they're open through the afternoon. Most sushi places aren't.
My cheese enchilada was a plate of microwaved cheese. My friends burrito was "spicy, but fine".

Loved my experience here....this bar has everything you would want.....the bartender Chris makes a fabulous cocktail and he is super friendly and welcoming........will return here again and again and again.  I will review again once I go and get my steak on.....
I've been going to fruscos For the last 15 years I don't know that the owner is doing with the place the steak might me the worse cheese steak I ever had if I could give 0 stars I would

Has this place closed?  I can't review the actual food (and I'm sorry to miss it, based on the other reviews) because I spent a good 30 minutes trying to get them on the phone to place a takeout order yesterday.  No one answered after multiple calls, and there was no outbound recording enabling me to leave a message.  Just ringing into oblivion.  No bueno.

UPDATE:  A fellow Yelper just advised that following them on Facebook is key and did indeed hold the answer.  So I will try them again.  May I also gently suggest that they purchase an answering machine?  Hugs to all, Jane

I visited briefly w/ 3 friends on 1/5.  I advised said friends that the worst 2 mistakes you can make is visiting the attached eatery to the hotel you're staying at, and to go in a place w/o a posted menu.

Well, even though I've visited NOLA before and knew you couldn't throw a rock in the French Qtr. w/o hitting a restaurant, I was overruled for "quickness" sake of looking.

So we sit down and I open the menu and see a $21 fish & chips and $14 cheeseburger.  Then we have a nice waiter spend about 5 minutes explaining the menu, even the burger!  Well, that just ties it for me, and I excuse myself and walk out.  So the rest of the report is comments from my friends...

One said, "expensive but quite good."  One other did not rave, but noted the expense.  The third ordered the cheeseburger w/o cheese, and was immediately warned that the chef "couldn't/wouldn't" make it that way.  WTF???!!!  A chef that's "insulted" by "changing" a dish by simply leaving off the cheese for a lactose intolerant person???

We've all had friends that worked food service, and they know it's a HUGE mistake to upset the kitchen staff.  NOTE TO MANAGEMENT, get a hold of your chef and tell him to give the customers what THEY NEED as well as what they want!!!

1 star from me, 3 from my friends.

I'm going to take the different approach on what I think about Aura World Fusion Cuisine. In my opinion, it's not one of those places that I would choose to eat, but a place I would most definitely chill out, have a few appetizers and  a couple of glasses of wine! The servers are really nice (shoutouts to Cindy)! They know what they're talking about and the give great descriptions about the different things they have to offer. The restaurant is very clean and very chill! If you're in the Midtown area and you're looking for a chill out spot for you and your friends (or a lady friend of yours), I highly recommend checking it out!

Some of the reviews here seem to be focusing on some bad luck things happening (elevator getting stuck, pipe bursting) and I didn't see anything that the others are referring to.  And yes, it isn't that close to downtown Philly, but look at a map before you book!

The hotel isn't anything special, but it is a typical Crowne Plaza Hotel, and i found it to be clean, friendly and and welcoming.  The check-in process was very  friendly and easy for me.  My room was quite nice, if unexceptional, with two complimentary bottles of water in the room.  Wifi was free and had a good signal, and the HVAC was up to snuff.  When traveling on business, a clean room, good wifi and comfortable air is two thirds of the battle.  

Also, they had a ton of electrical outlets, including some in the base of the lamps on each nightstand.  They get major points from me for that.  Good job guys! 

As a Gold Level Priority Club member, I got a complimentary breakfast buffet and it was small, but good (great sausages) and had an omlet station that I didn't take advantage of. 

The hotel sits among a small group of retail stores, restaurants and office buildings.  I stayed there because I was going to a focus group down the street.  Nearby there is a Starbucks, Panera, an Asian bistro and a Potbelly's, all next to some banks and a Target. 

So, no, it isn't the Ritz Carlton, but you probably knew that already if you're looking at a Crowne Plaza.  But I thought the hotel was friendly, clean, modern, and convenient.

If what you're known for is sushi burritos and it's plastered all around the internet that that's what you're popular for, announce it that you don't do it anymore. I'm really disappointed that there are so many pictures of sushi burritos and there are no messages anywhere saying that they don't do it anymore.

Not very great experience!
On a weekday ( I think Wednesday) we were told the wait was 20 minutes to get a table! When I said we are leaving, the guy says, okay the table will be ready in 2 minutes!

Food: the appam was cold, the curry quantity is always less. The biryani order was completely forgotten and we had to remind them that the dish is not for "to go". Water was served at the beginning just once and the waiters don't even care to look at you or check if anything is needed. 

I had gone to this place at the beginning, when initially opened. I thought there will be improvement, well they proved me wrong!
there are better walmarts around , this one is okay- but avoid on the weekends, this place is  a zoo over the weekend- and the employees are not very helpful

Delicious! This place has the best deep dish pizza in all of Clearwater, and the portions are huge! A normal deep dish can easily feed four, and it's really easy to pop back in the oven and reheat.  The staff is really nice and it takes about 25m to make a pizza to go.

Probably my favorite spot in Reno. The soup is awesome, as the name suggests, but their sandwiches and salads are just as good. The staff is always super friendly and patient. If going for lunch, I recommend getting there around 11:45 because the parking and lunch rush is no joke. 

My go-to's usually the Club or the Coconut Curry.

This Whole Foods is much smaller than most with a bit less selection and no prepared hot food bar area.  The unique location and reused space do give it some nice unique qualities.  You can often find grass fed beef here which you usually can't find in the Metairie location.  Whole Foods also has some of the best BBQ beef brisket in the area along with a good mix of local, organic, and non-GMO produce.  They also offer lots of high carb and high sugar baked goods that most "vegetarians" and "vegans" thrive on.  I look forward to one day meeting a "real" vegetarian or vegan that actually eats veggies instead of basically living off desserts and muffins :) 

Parking is usually a painful experience here and it is DEFINITELY better to go in off hours as people act crazy about parking here.  After Katrina I lived next to Whole Foods flagship location in Austin so this is certainly a much less dynamic store but it usually serves its purpose.  Unfortunately during my last visit I'm not sure what was going on but there were the least amount of fresh fruits I've EVER seen at a Whole Foods.  I was going to get some grapes but there were none to be had???

One last review of ONE Star for all the LAZY people that leave their carts strewn all over the place in the parking lot!  If your going to eat healthy maybe take a few extra seconds of exercise to put your cart back and OUT of my way you rude, pathetic excuses for civilized creatures.  It is the utmost in poor manners and you are probably the same douche bags that zoom in and cut people off to steal the parking space that someone else was patiently waiting for.

Cheap delicious Lanzhou noodles! The zhajiang mian (pork sauce noodles) was a bit too soggy and not quite like the real thing, but the beef noodles were perfect.  Enjoyed the daoxiao style- thick noodles that are knife cut.  Also loved the beef appetizer- very tasty and authentic.  For $5-6 a bowl, it's hard to go wrong here!

You want breakfast? They got that. You want burgers n fries? They got that. You want steak? They got that too. I had the Colorado Scramble, excellent. I've had the Chicken n Waffles. Excellent as well... Love the 50's Diner look.

My disclaimer for this review is I'm not a sushi expert. Couldn't tell you authentic from imposter. I can barely tell you the difference between sashimi and nigiri (does one have rice?). But what I can tell you is I love spending a weekend afternoon sitting on the patio at Ra Sushi. I find that the rolls are well put together and the fish tastes good. Their service is quick and they have some great drinks to try on the menu. Ra has a very cool vibe and has all of the great views and ambiance that La Encantada brings with it. 

We have a toddler who can crush California rolls (literally and figuratively) and they have never given us grief about the mess. However, as we have a toddler there are times that I find Ra's music to be too loud as they transition to their nighttime atmosphere. That being said, great place to hang out, grab drinks, and spend a lazy afternoon.

Amazing fast service from start to finish. Got my tire fixed in no time and the Lobby was clean and everyone was wearing a mask. The Manager Nathanael was a pleasure to work with and like I said amazing customer service.

I've been to this place more than once and I've had some good times there but the location and the decor leaves something to be desired.  The food is actually pretty good (not great but pretty good).  I also think the beer selection could be improved.

So, this morning I had to come out and deal with this company saying that my car was "abandoned." And it looks like this guy was just going through the street and marking some of them as abandoned. Where we live in the neighborhoods outside of downtown, such as Old Northside and Herron Morton have to rely on the free street parking, because this is where we live. Never in my two years of living there have I ever dealt with this.

Also, my car was moved several times and it was parked there for like 12 hours at the most. 

The plates are up to date and there are no flat tires, so I was beyond confused to what was going on and if it is someone's idea of a prank it's not amusing in the least little bit. It almost seems as if the company is trying to scrape money or something as if there aren't plenty of other cars in the city of Indianapolis to tow.

Now if I were parked like a complete imbecile or somewhere illegally, I'd understand, but I wasn't.

So I call the customer service and I asked why on earth has my car been labeled as "abandoned" for no apparent reason, all she said was "oh, just pull around the block and you will be fine." 

First of all, if they have this registered with your license and what not in their system that does not solve the problem in the least little bit. What if I did just pull around the block? My car would still be listed as abandoned.

Second of all, the fact that nothing was clearly answered about what happens to this ticket is unnerving. How am I supposed to feel ok about parking where I live in the FREE PUBLIC PARALLEL PARKING LANES if this information is just floating out there?

Third of all, the tone of the phone conversation just made it seemed like I was just a complete idiot just messing around and trying to cover up as if I did something wrong. The person was just condescending about the subject. However, I'd understand if she's annoyed if multiple people called in and asking why their car has been labeled as "abandoned," but don't let your employers do that to begin with.

I just find this company incredibly untrustworthy at this point.

It was clean in the restaurant area and the bathrooms clean too. But, the service was not great and other employees were more important than customers. The food was OK, the chief could have tried just harder on the food taste. This was our first time and it is the last time.

Poor customer service. The wait staff and general manager have no clue how to treat their customers. Hopefully it's just growing pains and they figure it out. Otherwise it will be just another restaurant that has gone out of business in this location.

So first off I'll tell you that this place is in no way, shape, or form a "fine dining" establishment. The bottom line is this; it's cheap, it's good, and it's filling. If you leave unsatisfied or hungry, it's basically your own fault. You get to choose your own ingredients all the way around, so if you didn't like your food then you chose poorly on ingredients. This place is old, basic, and not impressive by any means. So if you are looking to impress that new girlfriend or first date, this isn't where you take them. It's a hole in the wall place with a bunch of Asian guys running it who speak little or broken "Engrish", who just sit around most of the time playing on their cell phones. They'll refill your drink when you need it, but don't expect to make any idle chat or anything.....they're too busy with their cellphones. All of this is really okay, as long as you don't go into this place expecting more than what it really is. Basically it's a fast food Asian joint, where you can get good, filling, cheap, messy food that leaves you feeling better than if you went and ate at McDonalds. I can say that the other location in Nampa is exactly the same way, now that I've eaten in both places. If this sounds good to you, by all means give it a try. If you're looking for something more high end, fancy, or impressive then I'm afraid you're going to need to keep on looking.

I ordered three things. Monte cristo crepes - good but for me way too much meat to taste the crepe and raspberry jam. 

Aspen fruit blintz- I think this was the only thing I truly liked.


Eggs Benedict- this was surprisingly bad. On the first bite it felt like the English muffin was stale or really old. The meat was too rough to chew through and the hollandaise was really gritty. I love eggs Benedict and I've had it from many places. Usually you cut you a bite and everything kind of just goes together. This eggs Benedict was just so weird because it felt like the only way to eat this was just focus on one thing at a time and just eat it separately. Basically the meat and the muffin were way too tough to enjoy this in one bite. I might have caught this place on a off day. 
Will try to go again one day
A great fine dinning place on Broadway, food is delicious and the staff are very pleasant. If you have never tried it , you must, you will love it.

Don't go here to get your car washed. The workers almost burned up my clutch pulling the car out of the car wash. When I left I realized the car wasn't clean inside (which I paid for) and returned to the car wash. Upon returning I realized the wheels weren't even clean. The manager would not give me a refund, only a voucher for a return visit, for fear that they will ruin my car. If you have a decently nice vehicle do not come to this wash.

Highly dissatisfied with the customer service. Met them at the bridal show and put down a deposit which I told would be refundable. Went it to tentatively pick out attire, and was never told the deposit would not be refundable. When I called to say we were going with another company I was told my deposit was still refundable because it was within 6 months of the wedding and to call a manager the following day and she'd take care of it. When I spoke to the manager she said the deposit was not refundable. Would not recommend as they're not up front and you get different answers when you speak to different people.

Ordered a part from their website for a B & D Lithium Edger and Trimmer back on 7/9/15. ( The site said the part was in stock. HMMM! Where is it then?) Have not received any type of acknowledgement/confirmation from the company as of yet. Have called, left my name, phone number and a short message as instructed on the recording. No results what so ever. Sounds rather fishy to me. Should have read the reviews ahead of time.  Will keep trying to get through. I advise not to go here.

Literally using the cheapest ingredients possible.  A very pretty looking spot, with the worst quality food.  Very disappointing.  

Mediterranean flat brad smelled like a public bathroom, unbearable to eat.

Stale bread on peanut butter and jelly...  This is a shame.

Who Knew!!! Steak & Shake serves breakfast! Mo waiting. Maybe because no one knows they SERVE BREAKFAST. Prices are more than reasonable. Service is great, Britney, our server was quite pleasant and competent. We had the Classic Breakfast, myself two eggs over easy, wheat toast, sausage. The eggs were perfect, the toast a little limp, sausage was ok. Cup of coffee was Good. Then it got busy.... and service started to fall apart.  All in all I would stop again for a good basic breakfast, only real complaint, no oatmeal!

Have been taking vehicles to them for years and never had any issues until recently.  A few weeks back took the car a for a leaking radiator, a hose, something that left antifreeze in the street.  Picked it up after it was fixed and a few days later it did the same thing.  Took it back, it seems like they don't listen to what the problem is, for it to be fixed again.  The following day they called to ask for the original key to the car, the one with a chip in it. I never had a key with a chip in it for the car.  It's fixed again, I go to pick up the car, it will not start,  or if it starts it will not stay on.  I cannot drive it out of the lot.
