I took it in for the leaking issue and now they tell my ignition needs to be fixed,  I was told about 186 bucks worth of fixing.  I was told the labor would be free since I was kind of aggravated.  I get there to pick up the car and they charged 300 bucks,  89 bucks of it for labor.    No one offered any reasons as to why the labor charge was added when I was told it would be.  I get the car home and the next day, again I have leaking antifreeze in the street.  so several hundreds of dollars, and I still have a leaking car.    So much for quality work and customer service.

I know nothing about Ethiopian food. I actually know so little that the only reason I knew how to eat my meal was because someone else was eating the same (or a similar) thing before my plate came to me. It may sound ridiculous but when I looked at the menu I felt some anxiety.

The food wasn't great, and I couldn't even tell you what I ordered. It was good enough that I'd like to check out some more Ethiopian food in the future (especially since I know how to eat it now).

We were in Nashville for 1 night, on the coldest day of the year. People were begging those of us on the streets to come in to their establishments. I've never seen such an empty city! We turned down most offers because we were cold, and trying to get back to the hotel. Lonnie's was right by our hotel, and my husband really wanted to go in... he loves karaoke. The manager was outside asking those of us passing to come in, and bonus, no cover. We were literally the only 2 people in the bar. Waitress was friendly, the karaoke hostess was nice, and they were accommodating to song requests. My husband was able to karaoke 5 or 6 times... I lost count. We were joined by 4 regulars, and the occasional passerby. One disadvantage of this bar is the lack of liquor or wine. They have a small beer menu - the American staples (Miller, coors, Budweiser), 1 cider choice, 1 IPA, and an assortment of Mike's Hard and Smirnoff Ice's. They did have Fat Tire, so that was my drink of choice. I think this is marketed to a college crowd due to the drink choices and the signs (voted #10 college karaoke bar... or something of the sort). Overall, a good time. I'd go back.

The Brandon office is awesome so glad I made the decision to take control of my healthcare. The DNA advance metabolic profile, the Bio-identical hormones, the weight loss program, all have made a huge difference in 3 mos I have lost 25 pounds and feel 10 years younger.
My sleep has been so much better feeling rested when I wake up has not happened in 10 years. They have helped me feel like a new woman.

I do love their beer, but one really bad experience there has, sadly, but a bad taste in my mouth for this place. 

Our food on said occasion was absolutely terrible and should never have been sent out to be served. 

I do, however, love the pazookies and shall bestow the 3 stars for the delicious dessert. I also recommend the cream soda... it is something to relish!

My husband and I dined at this restaurant after seeing a show at Media Theatre.  This is the second time I have been there. The food is delicious and the service is excellent. Definitely a great place to dine. Please make sure you bring your appetite with you, the portions are very large. I give this restaurant five stars.

Went here for lunch on a Sunday. Ordered an appetizer sampler, which ended up just being a bunch of fried foods, my fault for not reading. Boyfriend ordered the rarebit, he seemed to like it. He also got a scotch egg, which I wasn't impressed with, but again, he seemed to think it was all right. I got a simple grilled ham and cheese, very good! I noticed near the end of my meal that there were small ants on my plate...we tracked them to the wall next to us. Called over the waitress and discreetly mentioned it so as not to make a scene. She thanked us for letting her know, but didn't offer anything off the bill at all, which I realize they aren't required to do, but as someone who works for a small business, we would definitely be doing something to apologize. Anyway, as we left they sent someone over with Windex to wipe it down and the adjacent tables, hopefully they will do more to remedy the situation in off hours.

For what we got, I would say it was overpriced. $50 for mediocre food during lunch.

Sadly we probably will cross this one off our CRG list - uneven service uneven food - talked to mgr and even after he concurred that the 2 sides we ordered were coming off the menu (tasteless broccoli & mushrooms which came AFTER we finished our main dishes he didn't remove from bill?) - and if they are not good have server "waive u off"  we didn't ask too but that's what u do - heirloom tomato salad was literally a hard green tomato (not ripe or cooked) - who's the expediter main dishes ok but rice cakes in mapo were mushy mango sorbet was odd - for me this was a Cunningham miss - and not cheap - oh and please remember when u clear after appetizers to bring guests clean utensils - we just grabbed from another table - first complaint I've ever lodged about a CRG restaurant and I've been to nearly all of them - sorta sad - oh and server split bill wrong - but at that point we were just ready to go

My husband and I ordered two completely different cheesesteaks and they both had a yummy flavor  but the consistency was a little questionable and mushy. The service was wonderful, which is why we will probably give them a second chance and maybe try their pizza and fries! Their prices are also really on point, so it's an average corner joint that had potential. We just have to try more if their food.

The waiter was nice but really seemed new to his job. Food was okay but we waited for an hour and half for it and the place was not busy at all.  We had ordered a few drinks and again the wait time didn't match the crowd.  Overall this place is a hot mess of mixed decor and food and it all just was underwhelming and expensive.  I won't be going back.

Generally a cute place with a cute vibe. It is pretty crowded, so if that bothers you, I do not recommend you go. The staff was pleasant, but not very helpful. My mom didn't know what she wanted and asked the waiter for help; the waiter was not very good at explaining the menu options to her-- google truly would have done a better job. We ordered based on whims and my mom's meal came 20 minutes before mine, and our bill wasn't correct at the end (by $25). The menu also seems pretty expensive for what you get at the end.
Love the pizza here! Great place to sit down and eat too. It's byob which is nice because there's a small liquor shop right next door.

For $27/person you get yourself a relatively cheaper way to limo your persons around the city to the totally must see tourist spots.

The 24 hour hop on/hop off deal at 20 different locales around the city is pretty sweet allowing you to explore or eat or drink at your own pace and the people on the microphone do a decent job to keep the ride light and sometimes informative.

Great for families as kids usually knock out after the first 12 minutes leaving you with a good 90 minutes of silence and a chance to breathe and relax.

I've been to this location twice now. The first time we were sat quickly, but food took a very long time and service was mediocre - our waitress continuously asked my fiancé if he would like another drink, but ignored my empty cocktail glass. The second time, we sat at the bar, and it took 15 minutes for someone to notice that we had our credit card out to be able to close out our tab and leave...with multiple bartenders behind bar, and even a manager standing in front of us. Hopefully these are just kinks of being a new restaurant, maybe I'll try again in a few weeks.

Ordered multiple Breakfast Sandwiches to go from Brian's Place. Bacon, Egg, & Cheese on Cuban Bread. 

1) Stale Cuban Bread (From Sam's Club?) You are 40 minutes from Tampa - That is Lazy!!!

2) When a Customer Orders a Sandwich with Cheese there should be resembles cheese on the sandwich, not just enough to trick the person that pick up the to go order into thinking there is cheese on the sandwich.

Horrible food!!! If people in Hernando Beach would stop eating at these horrible restaurants maybe someone will open something with edible food!!!!!
Mike's is the go-to place downtown for good ice cream and excellent home-made waffle cones.

Justin was an excellent, excellent, excellent, hotel manager. Price was affordable. Location was close enough to everything, yet far enough to avoid the drama of it all when you were done. The hotel itself is: cozy, antique, old. I myself would stay here again.

Bakersfield is a classic mass ave restaurant. You can't walk past on a summer day without seeing people pooling out of the restaurant with a margarita in hand. Among the classic margaritas you'll find a fantastic list of hand crafted tacos and some delicious gooey skillet queso! Their back patio is also a must for any summer bucket list with friends.

I am hesitant to write a review since every single one is good and I'm wondering if they're paying yelp to take the bad ones off or does everyone actually like this type of cuisine. I also really don't want to speak negatively of the people who work there, because they were all great. But I feel I should warn some people about the cuisine. I had heard amazing things about this place. We were so excited to go. First off, it is a cool concept eating around the kitchen. It felt like i was a judge on an episode of top chef or some other food network show where every dish was plated with such precision, truly a work of art. The drinks were amazing, but by the third round we were so lit because the plates are small. Anyway, the two things that stick out to me are the abalone?? Some sort of sea urchin that lives in a shell. I love all seafood, oysters, mussels, etc. This did not even resemble seafood. It tasted like a tough salty ham. I had to work at getting a few bites down. I realize the red eyed gravy probably gave it that flavor, but it was so exotic it was hard to enjoy. Then the next dish truly will never be erased from my mind. We saw something right off the bat cooking in a pan that resembled a  big frog leg. I have to admit this made me nervous, but we were there to be adventurous. Well, that was not a frog leg, it was a pigeon. I was mortified. I forced a few bites. I just couldn't get past the feet and legs on the plate, plus it was very gamey to me. By this time, people who came in a good 30-45 min after us were already caught up to our course, so we spent a lot of time waiting...especially for the grass fed beef, supposedly mid rare, but it resembled a carpaccio. The smell of the pigeon was so strong. When the people next to us got theirs, I seriously had to breathe out of my mouth. and $334 for this meal was ridiculous. I do not like chain restaurants and have eaten many places from nyc  to LA and have no issue paying big bucks for a fabulous meal. I love fresh, organic, local, etc. Seriously I love all types of food.  This is not my cup of tea. I'm sure some expert foodies will think I'm not a continental diner and I speak blasphemy, but from someone who eats nearly everything, I just don't get the appeal. I'd much rather go to kayne or cafe  Margot over this place. I do not  understand why they can't just make excellent cuisine without having to try to be so damn exotic. I understand restaurants of this caliber are known for their delicacies, but dear god, don't try and reinvent the wheel. This was definitely  a one time experience we will never forget. One I wish I could take back. Been there, done that, got the shirt. Obviously we won't  be back. :(

This Theatre was by far the most disgusting filthy place if I hadn't pre purchased my tickets I would have opted to leave. The women's restrooms were a mess with toilet paper and feminine products all over the floors. The concessions soda machines were full of ice and even had piles of ice on the floor causing them to be slick. I will not be coming back to any future showings.

Highly recommend... It was my moms and I first time being at this salon and let me tell you the experience and our hair is lovely. The owner Ms. baly is very nice, she communicates and answered every time i called or had questions never getting annoyed or being rude. She also gave us suggestions to ensure we were getting exactly what we wanted. Everyone was very kind, there were no attitudes and the shop was filled with laughs and good energy the entire time (which believe it or not can be rare) Both of ours hair turned out beautifully amazing and they cared and took their time  we were only there for about 4-5 hours ( with getting small individuals) . Very well established place and me and my mom will be coming back to you when time for a touch up and future hairstyles. We Thank you ladies and lovely work you guys did. 
- KB and Kim

Truly one of the worst meals I have ever had. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Miserable employees. They screwed up rice and beans don't even get me started on the meat. Please close down you port excuse for a restaurant.

As the other reviewers mention, this is an old fashioned men's barber shop. I left work a little early because usually those types of places are pretty slow, but I was in and out here very quickly. Less than half an hour all in. There are four or five chairs, and each was staffed when I got there about 3:15 on a Tuesday. I think I've found my Eastbank barber shop.

Take a number when you walk in. They're on the wall to the left. Cash only. $16 for a basic cut.

Day one impression (Mon 6:00am)-Is this a gym or the club? I mean it's a nice place to go if you want to meet someone but I came to workout.  I don't want to talk, take selfies or hook up.  Also there were napkins and juice bottles left in the machines.  The first treadmill I got on wasn't functioning correctly and it feels tight.  I'm giving it two stars because of the monitors on the equipment and the stair climber.  Missing PF already because I had space, there was plenty of air conditioning and everyone there was about their workout.  Let's see how this turns out.
Fresh, clean, and friendly fast service. Went there today for lunch for the first time. I would recommend for lunch to everyone who enjoys sushi.

I don't like leaving bad reviews but ... worst experience ever. Service was terrible and slow. I understand things happen but it does not excuse bad service. We ordered our food and one of the plates never made it. We had to ordered it to go and had to stand to make sure they would give it to me. I wanted soooo bad to like it and drove 1 hr to just eat here. Never again I hope they can fix their issues and be bettter!

This place has potentials, the building layout inside is good, except the food they serve is mediocre at its best.  Selection isn't all that, and the food isn't very tasty, service was below average (we had a female server, she came by once).
Friendly, Family Oriented, spacious, Quick and easy service, food and service is consistently good, prices are fair and always enjoy my meals here.

Does not take walk ins when there are all of three people in the place and as rude as all the other reviews said.  Don't bother. Go somewhere else.  Queen nails around the corner was happy to have the business
The best molten chocolate cake in the whole world! Nothing beats that.

Appointments are never granted. Stayed waiting for well over my appointment time, and they just switched me to another chair to wait for someone else. 
This place always does this. It's ridiculous. It would be one thing if they did an amazing job every time but they are always rushed and the work shows. Not coming here again.
Service was great, but the wines left something to be desired.  Several seemed salty to me.
Excellent food, great prices, and a short wait! Visiting from out of town and we LOVED Jack's!
Very good indo Chinese food... Remote location .. Decent ambiance...decent service ... I didn't like the options in lunch buffet...

Amazingggggg!!! Came in for a small lunch expecting it to be good but it was even better then we could have imagined. We got the falafel sandwich and a side of hummus and bread. Both were so tasty! One of the better falafel sandwiches Ive ever had. The bread they use is fresh and the falafel crispy and don't even get me started on the garlic aioli!

I have been to Boogaloo once before on a ridiculously crowded Saturday night when everybody and their brother kept bumping into the back of my chair and the service was unimpressive. My visit last Thursday was the complete opposite - the place was barely half full and the service was impeccable. Big kudos to Kelly who was great at waiting on my friend and I, ask for Kelly on your next visit to Boogaloo. 

Yes, I used the Groupon I purchased back in September, it was a great deal and because I had made a reservation on OpenTable, there was no problem using it. 

- Cuban Caesar Salad: This salad was served with 2 anchovies on top (not listed on the menu) and while tasty, didn't diverge much from a typical Caesar. The quality of the greens could have been better. 

- Shrimp po'boy: I've ordered this before but hey, it's good and what's better than a shrimp po'boy? Served on a nice toasty chunk of bread with lettuce, tomato, plenty of pickles and a remoulade that while a little more thin than I care for, was a lovely condiment to dip the sandwich in. There were about 5 good-sized shrimp on the sandwich, which were not too heavily breaded and had great flavor.

- Fries: Theirs were thin, evenly salted and served at just the right temperature. I wonder if some kind of creole seasoning would be good on them... 

- Brownie with vanilla ice cream: Warm brownie, creamy melty ice cream with fudge drizzled on top; there's no way to mess this one up. It's what you'd expect and satisfies the way chocolate and ice cream should. 

- Drinks: I ordered a hurricane and loved Boogaloo's version so much that I even had a second one. It was sweet and not too syrupy, served with cherry and orange. This was the best hurricane I've had in the Midwest, for sure. 

My friend ordered the jerk chicken sandwich, which looked delicious and I should have tried a bite. I did snag a bite of her creme brulee dessert and that won a big thumbs up.

I left Boogaloo with left overs and a full tummy, I'll be back.

Chicken and Waffles.
Haven't had a better combo anywhere else in Alberta.  A really hip place that's open late. The overall environment is great, very casual, laid back, and relaxing (though I went late at night) which is perfect for having a conversation over drinks or dinner. The decor of the place gives off a vintage vibe, really something to walk in to and look at as you wait for your order.
Over-priced for average food, and small-ish portions.  We were really disappointed. It's worth driving a little further downtown to PF Changs, and skipping this place.
Stunning cathedral with great history.  Beautiful paintings on the ceilings that reminded me of Europe. Really enjoyed looking around and seeing all the intricate statues and paintings.

I had been looking for a while for a not-overpriced salon since I moved to Indy and Sirens is very reasonably priced. I made an online appt with Jamie online and she was great!  Very nice and easy to talk to.  She's very eager to help you find the kind of cut you're looking for--i used to go to a trendy japanese guy when I lived in Boston and i loved the cuts in Japanese hair magazines, so she whipped out her iPad and tried to look some up, but she knew exactly what I was talking about!  The next time I saw her, she had gotten an online subscription! I dropped in for bang trims several times, whenever I saw her bike parked outside. The salon is very clean and I love the Mass Ave location.

Carry out. 2 Gyros traditional style, 1 fried calamari & 2 Baklava. Fast prep. Very friendly service. And Delicious !!!!!!!  Didn't give 5 stars because we didn't try all they have to offer. We're definitely going back to try their other foods. Really like that you can pick your toppings.

I have been wanting to experience this eatery before Covid, but the wait is usually long and I'm bad with booking reservation. My guy and i went Thursday 8/13/20 hoping not to have a long wait and we didn't. The hostess and all the staff was very pleasant. We started our meal with their amazing waffle. I failed to take a pic because we ate it as soon as it hit the table. We shared the waffle as our appetizer. The waffle was 5  by itself it was covered with fresh fruit and a fluffy home made whip cream that i wanted a tub of. I don't like to let pretty things i eat distract me. So i moved all of the pretty stuff to taste the waffle and i was amazed at how good it taste.  I had the egg Atchafalaya, it was good nothing amazing just good. My guy had the Kansas steak and eggs, the grits was extremely thick and had a lot of cheese. He's not crazy about cheese so it was a miss for him. The steak was fine nothing was wrong with it.  I really have no complaints, everything was good but not amazing if The bill is almost $80 for 2 ppl for breakfast i would like better then good for my meal. The atmosphere was very nice and the staff was as well.

I wish I can write a very detailed review on them but I go for happy hour and their hot wings. I strongly recommend their korean hot wings if you are ever craving wings. They're to die for and can be split between two people or just indulge in it yourself as an appetizer. Their beers are just extremely delicious and they are extremely knowledgeable in what they have on tap. I definitely recommend this place for beer, unfortunately, not having their food quite often makes it impossible to write a review on it.

They also have PLENTY of large TVs that make watching games that much better. They have indoor and outdoor seating but expect it to be extremely busy during early dinner hours. Also, they have friendly staff that will make sure you aren't waiting too long for your order. 

It's a family friendly restaurant and they definitely take pride in their beers. Parking isn't too much of a hassle and seating usually gets taken care of right away even on busy days.

Hope you enjoy their beers as much as I did! I definitely will be going in and out of this place for happy hour.

Will NEVER eat here! Part 1. Wife arr ahead of me in separate car. Said she was waiting for husband. I got there and waited 20 more mins, no svc. I approached waitress who was chatting, she advised me that table does not want service, waiting for husband. I, the husband, stood staring at her giving her time to put it together. Gave up. Had to explain that the husband has been at the table over 20 mins. She looked at the table, and seeing only my wife said to me, "No he isn't." Part 2. Now 50-something waitress is angry because I explained it to her. I was polite. She took order to 30-something cook in back who stuck his head out of kitchen, looked hard at me them looked over to waitress and gave an "I'll take care of it" nod to waitress. Would you eat the food that was served?

The movie theater is a nice place. It's clean, well kept and in general does a good job doing what it does - show movies. 

The service though, is where it kind of lacks. Today, getting tickets for the "blue hair" showing of The Great Gatsby, I noticed that one sales woman had a line of about 5 people, there was another woman, holding a conversation in the window with one of her other co-workers, merrily chatting away, not bothering herself to help those who're backed up at the first woman's window. 

The snack bar is a typical snack bar, and this is where movie theaters get you, the over priced half gallon sodas and tubs of pop corn that you end up dumping on the floor.  Though if you're lucky there's a promotion going on and you might get a bag of candy for only a dollar more.

For opening weekend, Gatsby was in one of the smaller theaters, and I can't say I was upset by that. There seemed to be plenty of seating. Only one party had to be broken up, and that was only because they wanted to sit in the stadium seating vs down on the floor. 

With AMC, make sure you're a loyalty card customer, it earns you points towards admissions and free concessions.
Have tried this place many times of the years and have always been disappointed. No one leaves satisfied, the ribs are just bad. My moms burger was so watery it fell apart.

First experience at Eddie V's tonight. We were celebrating a special occasion and we were looking for a new food experience we had not had. We arrived at our table with drinks in hand from the bar so we were patient. The server was apologetic it took longer to great us. She offered app or drink. We said we are ok. She sent the Mgr over and he too was apologetic and offered drinks. We said when ready thank you.   We received our meals which were outstanding. The filet Oscar style was melt in your mouth delicious. My just hit the filet with a side of blue cheese fondu... also yummy. The truffle Mac n cheese was the best we'd ever had. We didn't finish because full and asked for a box. As was the beginning the end the server dropped the ball.. we left without our leftover despite asking where it was. The 'complimentary drinks were still on the bill. They were removed once mentioned but I almost feel like thought we wouldn't notice. The place was very busy at time we sat down. Could overlook wait. The ending the place quieted down but our server was just off. We will return for the food no doubt but know some of fine details may slip.

Stopped in for a few drinks with my boyfriend while on vacation to see him in St. Pete. Really enjoyed the view of the bay and the Skyway! Ordered a couple piña coladas, and sat back and enjoyed the beautiful weather and great atmosphere out on the deck! Much bigger than your usual beach tiki hut, so there was plenty of seating and it didn't feel too crowded. 

Will definitely be back when I come down for vacation next month, great place to stop in for some drinks and food if you need a bite to eat. Best kept secret of St. Pete as far as quaint beach bars go! 

This is of course just my opinion, based on my experience of being there for just about an hour. The bartender was really friendly and she made our visit fun and enjoyable. Can't wait to come back!

Everything a college bar should be, this bar took me back to a good place. All you can drink happy hour on Friday from 6-8 - no need to go on after making that crystal clear. Great outdoor patio and multiple bars to enjoy the Florida sun.
My favorite spot to eat in Tucson! Try their carne asada  I always bring friends who are visiting to BKs and every single person has fallen in love with it too!

I visited here before there big refresh. The layout of the restaurant made you feel like you were some where in the Middle East. The furniture looked cool but was very uncomfortable. I only ended up having humus and tabouli. I found them to be below average in flavor and didn't want to stay and find out if the same goes for the entrees.

Came in on a Saturday for lunch with a buddy of mine. Place wasn't too busy which was nice. The chips and salsa that came out were good; the green salsa was really hot which was great. I asked the waitress on her suggestion on what the locals get. She recommended the skirt steak and the goat (which is a weekend only item). Both items were great, although the goat was a little on the small side. For $15, I'd expect a little bit more meat given that the meat was on the bone but the flavor was definitely there. On the plates, there were some nice additions including a large amount of guacamole and some grilled peppers and green onion. Service is definitely slow as many people mentioned. Waters were only filled once despite my mouth being on fire. I was looking for the waitress but she was almost always in the back. I definitely need to come back and try their tacos which are more reasonably priced.

On the "needs to be improved" side, I received a Yaris with 29,000 miles, and within 2 hrs. in the Tucson heat, it was dead -- won't open the door or start.   Enterprise did send roadside service, and an hour later in 102 degree heat I learned the battery needed to be replaced.

On the compliments side, I accidentally left my prescription sunglasses in the car whe  I retruend it on a Friday evening, and a nice lady phoned me on Monday afternoon, told me she had the sunglasses and would FedEx therm to me.  She sent them overnight on Tuesday, and I had them back (very gratefully) by Wednesday!

this company was all over Groupon for coupons.
so I bought one.
when I went to redeem it 
the $11 for $20 worth of food 
they refused it.
Groupon told me that is still have $11 to spend there.

Jet's wanted me to buy a $25 pepperoni which was on SALE for $9
to use my Groupon.  
I just don't understand their LOGIC or their Crookedness.

FYI
Jet's you can continue spending loads of money on advertising to get the 
people through your front door 
but the key is KEEPING your Customers !!

Passed by this place a number of times during my trips to Philly, so decided to check it out. Decor is pretty plain. Apparently it's a family restaurant because there was a table with children and crying baby next to us. If it IS a family restaurant, it's too dark.

There's a stand up bar but I don't see how it fits into the sit-down-and-eat motif. The Philly Pale Ale is pretty damned good. Waiter brought my first beer quickly, had to be reminded twice about the second one. Sister ordered a diet coke, and we didn't know refills were free until the waiter noticed her empty cup as we were uh...paying for the bill.

Me: Fish and Chips. Fish was GREAT, chips were terrible.

Sis: Short rib. Portions were pretty small and the majority of it was mashed potato. Beef was good, not great.

Mom: Roasted Beet salad. Fairly good. Not enough cheese (seriously, there was one tiny hunk of it), WAY too much red onion (had to help my mom pick it out). Also, the salad was made too early and it sat on a wait station for at least 10 minutes. How did we know? We were seated next to the wait station. And the crying baby table. And the slow on the drinks area. Also, the staff was moving boxes around, restocking the bar. Couldn't this be done at another time? Unless Friday night is a usually a slow night for you guys.

Food is good but overall execution needs work.

A sign at the front door during times when you don't have a host saying, "please be seated" or "seat yourself" would come in really handy. Peaked in the almost empty bar to see if my mom and I could seat ourselves and having the server who was rolling silver at the opposite end just barely acknowledge that I even standing there was also not welcoming.  Actually walking over and talking to us would have been a much better first impression. But was just told "yes."
Had to grab our own silverware and menus and found a seat. 
Food was good. Server was below average.  Made classic service mistakes,  like NEVER refilling my water and dropping then check before offering anything else.  I was going to get a piece of coffee cake, but guess not since he decided we were done and needed our bill.
And maybe the broom that was left in the center of the dining room (at the next table and the whole of our visit) was for us to clean up after ourselves,  sorry we didn't do the dishes.

Food was average but the service was really slow and not that great.   She brought me someone else's food than it took another 20-30 mins to get my food.  I would probably return but with a different server.

I love the atmosphere of this place! It's a beautiful hip place.  Went for lunch on a Friday and it was pretty packed but overall if you know what you want to order quickly you can be in and out in about 45 min.  I had their riggate and it was super tasty with quite a bit of spice. We also had the cashew appetizer and those were so good. We couldn't stop eating them!  Glad to find this campus gem. I however do not recommend bringing children. It's not really a place for kiddos.

Gah I wish yelp had .5 starring! ( more like a 2.5) 
This is literally like your normal Indian grocery store: mismatched labels, non-existent expiry dates, random pricing (sometimes), barely fresh veggies, and a limited selection of certain items. It is definitely a good go to spot for spices, BUT they don't carry famous brands like Maya, and for Swad, I feel like they only carry a portion of the spices offered. There is a lack of indian vegetables and fruits ( NO MANGO?! BABY BANANAS??!?!?! Where are the string beans and sour snake gourd?!). But if you need garam masala in a jiffy like me ( and can't find it at Fresh Grocer), swing by here. 
The jaggery though ( hard molasses) is sort of light in color, and not very sweet. 

The lady at the register is super nice. Unusual thing tho: they actually itemize the receipt! ( never have I seen that before at the smaller, non-chain indian groceries!!)

Ordered two Construction Burritos and a Biscuits and Gravy To-Go. 

To-Go Pros:
Wait time was quick, about 10 minutes, so there is one star. 
The staff was friendly and positive, so here is another star. 
The two burritos were SUNRISING AWESOME; fresh, tasty, and filling! One more star! (Enough food in one burrito for breakfast and lunch)
Finally, the salsa they sent with the burritos was insanely delicious!!!  So, for that reason they get one golden star!  Yes, a star just for the salsa. Oh! And the Pico de Gallo was very fresh and refreshing too!

Slight Cons: 
The Biscuits and Gravy was different. The biscuits tasted like margarine. NOT terrible... just a wee bit too much.  The gravy was very spicy (as in it had a lot of spices).  I was expecting the traditional pepper and sausage gravy.  Bias based on my expectation.  NOT terrible... just different. 
Lastly, we ordered ham, bacon, and green Chile in our construct a burritos and we were almost certain there was no bacon or green Chile... and if there was it was minute.  But, the burrito itself was woo-hoo good! 

Overall:
For breakfast take-out/to-go this was as good as it gets! Not greasy, so you do not have to go to work feeling like you started your day with a mistake.  In fact, you will arrive boasting this place! :0) I cannot wait until I have a chance to have a dine-in experience!

As a non-avid hiker, this was a pretty challenging hike. I've only done inspiration point in town, so I was excited to try a new trail. Parking wasn't too bad on a Saturday late afternoon in the winter. Most of the parking is parallel on the street along a drop off with no curb so be prepared for that. The trail was pretty easy to find and most of it runs along the creek and under the tree cover so the shade was nice. I appreciated the peaceful sounds of the creek along the way. 

The incline itself was substantially steep. Lots of rocks and uneven footing. I would recommend this hike for someone with at least an average level of physical fitness. It took about an hour to get to the meadow with the fork, and then you can choose to turn back, go left for Tunnel Trail, or right for Gibraltar. Since we heard of the stunning views at the top, we decided to go right. This took about another hour to get to the top and it was much steeper than the first part of the hike. Not to mention the end of this trail connects to an actual road so you COULD drive up if you just wanted the view (we considered taking a Lyft back down since the sun was setting).

The view at the top is gorgeous, so worth it for sure, and heading down was much faster. Be careful if you're heading down around sunset. Once it gets dark, it's easy to get lost off the trail under the tree cover. 

I might do this hike again once I've tried some other local ones.
Yelp says they are open till 12, got there at 10:30.... Closed. STEP YOUR GAME UP YELP. Second time yall screwed me over with incorrect times. Yall are slipping.

Giving two stars because food and sushi were good, don't recommend the fried rice tho. Went there since were on vacation and we go back to our home state and realize they put a 10$ tip on my card without my permission and when I called he walked around giving me my money back but didn't actually offer it. Total was 50$ but my card was charged 60$ even though I put no tip on the receipt in-house. When I checked my bank account I realized they wrote in their own tip. If we ever come back we will not be returning here.

A bunch of 925 everywhere.... I used to love coming to this shop as a Gaucho... I think I still have some stuff that I bought here from back in the day somewhere... but then again I may have lost it like I lost my memory every other night in IV.  Good times here for sure and you feel like you are hunting for treasure... with just a little patience you will find a real winner.  Walked by the other week with my sisters and we all got a little nostalgic.

WORST SERVICE EVER! Never again will I come to this chili's. The waiters seems disinterested in serving customers! I waited 30 minutes before someone came to serve me, after telling the receptionist 2 times that my party had not been waited on. Don't come to this chili's, you'll instantly regret it.

My wife and I have been customers for 8 years. The first few years they provided good customer service. Now they suck ! My representative won't respond to emails or return phone calls. I talked to his boss who said he'd check into the problems and get back to me. It's been a week and nothing. We can't recommend this company. Once they get your money and their bonus and commissions are paid to them they are worthless. I'm now forced to contact the annunity companies to see if I can change representatives. YOU'VE BEEN WARNED !

UPDATE: After posting this review they got back to me and took care of the issue.

I use to go to Baxter's all the time, but recently we've had so many issues with all take out orders (over the past year), that we are done. The customer service is not good, we typically get someone fighting with us even though our order was repeated back to us right but came out wrong. One time, the person on the phone raised their voice and actually hung up (we did not yell or say anything to trigger that). The food is good and there is a pretty large menu, I think it seems a little overpriced. However, the terrible customer service outweighs everything.

The food receives 4 out of five stars. The reason I am giving them a two star rating is because you have to wear a mask EVERY single time the server comes to your table - when they bring your water, when you order (have to order everything at one time), when they tell you they don't have the wine you ordered in stock, when you ask for bread, when they refill your water, when they ask if you need anything, when they bring to go boxes, when they bring you the bill, when you pay the bill, when they bring it back to you and I haven't even mentioned when you enter, go to the bathroom and leave. Also, when they told us we would have to wear the mask when the server approached us, the guy asked if we are ok with that. I said I guess we have to be. He said no, you can go somewhere else to eat. We won't be back until we don't have to wear a mask while we are eating!

Unquestionably, the best fried chicken I've ever had. Maybe even the best chicken period I've had.  Be prepared to wait, as Hattie B's is not only well known, but already an institution. I walked 30 minutes and waited 30 minutes more just to try it.  

I got the half bird with half hot and half damn hot sauce.  The hot was perfect heat for me and truly amazing. It was a dry heat with good balance and complemented the perfectly juicy chicken.  The damn hot was a wet sauce that was VERY hot. The flavor was even better than the lesser hot version.  I've never had anything like it and the pain was sooo worth it.  I got 3 sides...pimento Mac n cheese, greens, and coleslaw.  None of them blew my mind but the slaw was tasty and nice change of pace from the heat of the chicken.  The greens were also solid when you put a good amount of the pepper vinegar supplied at each table.  Mac n cheese had nice creaminess and flavor.   

This is a bucket list type of place and you really should go out of your way to taste this perfect chicken.

We went last night for dinner.  It's the first time in years. The food was surprisingly really good. Service was not really good that's the reason for 4 stars. Our server was not necessarily bad but mediocre at best. I ordered a wings platter with 3 different sauces & one sauce was supposed to be a dragon glaze but I got buffalo sauce. She came to check on us & I asked about the sauce & she said the must've ran out of dragon sauce so gave me buffalo. Did I want her to bring a side of another sauce? Not different wings. So I gave those 5 wings to my son for his lunch because I'm not a fan of buffalo sauce Instead of having some for my lunch today.   Then after that she kind of avoided our table. But did bring me a glass of water & our bill.

I Have used this company for the past ten years to service my home and business A/C units. Called them on Monday because my home system was not working and was told No one could make a service call until Thursday! Wow Thanks for nothing GCA!
I went to see if Turkish food was the best in the world.  Turns out it's not.  It's just OK.  Overall not a bad experience.  Attentive service and decent food.  I was just expecting more.

Visited NOLA for a girls wknd! Overall the stay at this hotel was great. It's pretty close to everything and even walking distance to a bunch of great restaurants. I booked through Expedia for a certain rate and upon arrival I was told by the clerk that the bill would be authorized at check out, when made sense at the time. Actually the entire bill was taken from my acct at the time of check in, again fine. On the 3rd day there was another large charge from my acct, when questioning it (4 different ppl) I received 4 different answers about the charges and everyone was blaming it on the lady who checked me in. Again there was no solid answer as to why they even debited by account for this money. When asking when it would be refunded I was told it would take 5-7 business days. Excuse me, why? You just took money that wasn't supposed to be taken and u tell me it's going to take a week to refund?! Makes no sense. So I'll suggest that if you book here make sure you look at every charge and check your accounts. Also, parking is not included and it's about $30 a day, also not mentioned at check out.  I honestly would not stay here again.

By far the worst customer service I've had at any dining establishment. Whoever the young lady who's working as the clerk has an extremely bad attitude and needs to be trained on how to properly greet customers and take their order. I was so taken back by the way I was handled I just walked out without even ordering anything. The food might be the best in town but I will never know because of the rudeness of the staff

There must be a mistake because this restaurant is on Tampa's Top 50 - a very unserving place.

Atmosphere        75        It is the roaring 70"s
Service                 90        Excellent , caring and very helpful
Food                     70        Not to good
Value                    75        Fairly priced if it was good

What can I say. I would not go out of the way nor would I return for this dated restaurant with some bad recipes and a heavy hand of salt..

Incredibly fun place. However, because the whole place is mixed age, you have to walk back to the main bar to order drinks and if you order for someone, they have to walk them out. Frustrating and taxing, but tolerable. 

Overall a fun place. Our 4yr old loved it too! Great way to make an afternoon with TopGolf then a few games and cocktails. 

Drinks: They do have a happy hour and Miller drafts are $2.50. We liked the Zombie served in a cool tiki glass head. Strong and worth it!

Parking: Plentiful with no problem.

This is definitely not a "4-star" hotel, on a typical hotel rating system. It is, though, a 4-star value. However, my overall actual experience was more like a 2 or 3. We had an early flight in the morning, and were looking to stay in an affordable room that wasn't frequented by hookers. 

We paid $69 for a King room. It was small, but clean and didn't smell funny. The wifi was free. The bathroom was small, but everything worked.  The hotel offered hourly shuttles to the airport.  The pillows were terribly small and flat.  The tub was a bit slick and dangerous. The room was very noisy as it was right by the lobby. It was also pretty close to the interstate, and I could hear cars all night. Here must have been a street light outside my window because it was as right as daylight on the other side of the curtain. All in all, the price made SOME of those things very forgivable.  Not all of them. 

I would maybe stay here again, if I need a place close to the airport.  It's not somewhere I would stay if I was a tourist looking to spend a lot of time in the quarter, as it is a pretty long ways away. And I wouldn't want to stay here for more than a couple days. But for an airport hotel, this isn't too bad on value.  I did sleep pretty crapy due to the noise and pillows, though. 

Off to Salt Lake City tomorrow for some more heavy Yelping.

To be fair I am a bit of a sushi snob... Really love high quality well prepared raw dishes. I'm new to the area and had a co-worker recommend this spot to me .. I honestly walked in looked at the menu for about 10 min and then walked straight out ... This place is like the Golden Corral of sushi, all you can eat and everything is drenched in mayo... the decor is also really tacky, I decided to not even waste my time, or money.
This location is a literal dungeon and I am shocked that 24 even allows it to continue running like this. It's damp and gross and there are creepy men. No thanks.

I'm assuming the gentleman that is always working whenever I go in is the owner (just get that vibe) but he is absolutely awesome and so so nice and super informative . Also- the store was just brand newly redone inside and it looks amazing 
....From Tampa to  Clearwater hands down best prices in town !! definitely five stars :)

Service was excellent, but then again I was there during the late lunch hours so it was pretty quiet inside and they had nothing else to do but serve me.

Prices are typical for a eatery in the CWE.

Decor was okay. One of those trendy modern places. Very minimalist, but I'm sure it looks better with the dimmed lights you find in the evening.

I ordered the wok seared citrus salmon. Overall the dish was good. I had a coupleissues though.

1) The heat from the peppers wasn't distributed evenly throughout the dish. Some bites were spicier than others.

2) It was a little too saucy. I know that sounds odd, but when the noodles and veggies are smothered in sauce, and then a browned piece of salmon is put on top, it led to a presentation I did not like. I would have liked a slightly lighter sauce.

Overall, I liked the place, and will give it one more go. The salmon was cooked perfectly, and the service was top notch. However, the presentation and overall heaviness oft the dish turned me off a bit.

Every time we visit Clearwater we always make a stop at the French Bakery. The cappuccino and lattes are very well made but the star of the show is the food. The bacon, egg and cheese croissant is mind blowing. The ham, egg and cheese crepe is amazing - carmelized onions take it to the next level. The quiches are delicious and every pastry I have tried has been awesome. I can't say enough about this gem! If you are anywhere near this place out it on your list of must dos.

Three stars for fond memories, -2 for an uncertain future... In the past I have loved pretty much everything from this truck, falafel, biryani, etc. but now I am uncertain if I will go back. It just won't be the same without the smiling proprietors!

This is my girlfriend and I's favorite Greek restaurant. Very authentic taste, the shawarma is amazing. The hummus and pita are clearly made in house! My girlfriend loved the falafel. Highly recommend coming here

Here is my experience to this place! .. Let me tell you that they only had one waitress on staff well it seemed that way because she was the only one at every table! ... So we waited for her at least 5 to 7 minutes before she even came by and get out drinks (which no one even gave us drinks menu). She came by and realized no drinks menu and went to go get them and she was like I will be right back. That took another 5 or so minutes. At this point I just wanted to order the food. 
She came by and took all the orders at one time. We ordered 2 of their thin pizza and two drinks. 
Food arrived warm and it was well cooked, no complain but nothing special either. The bill came I guess we read the menu wrong but one pizza cost us $25 and this was not even the deep dish! I was like wow. nothing special about these pizza, I mean I had order extra Sause because the pizza barely had any! ... 
Overall it was okay, I would never come back for an expensive pizza and the service was just so slow. 
The waitress was nice and very sweet so not her fault she was the only one working so the two stars are really for her!

So I really wish I could give a better rating bc staff was wonderful, location is great, vibe is fun, but food was very bad. The grilled shrimp and fried fish were the only thing things edible. We ordered stuffed mushrooms that looked like a pile of mush with a couple of flat slimy mushrooms underneath it. It tasted as bad as it looked. The blackened fish was very unappetizing. Poor appearance, texture and flavor. Tasted like 2 day old leftover fish. The fried popcorn shrimp were also very disappointing, and could not eat. The clam chowder tasted as if it had been watered down to make it stretch. The fried fish was ok. And the grilled shrimp on skewer on combo platter were actually very good. The drinks at bar were great.  Hope this is helpful!

Pretty decent restaurant, reminds me of chipotle. It has a very "under the sea" feeling. The water feature inside also gave me the feeling of being in the Atlantis resort. Very beautiful interior. I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for some fresh food.
Food was good. Standard fare. Took a long time though for a restaurant attached to a hotel.

My husband purchased a visor for $35! Overpriced.  Also, you have to pay for any returns! 
And it has to be within 30 days.  Bad customer service.  Will never buy from them again.

If I could give this review negative stars I would. 

Twisted is what this woman was. 
I don't remember her name but she was the owner.

I was running about 10 minutes behind on my appointment due to traffic and went in very apologetically, asking her if it was too late and offering her a very generous tip. She said no it wasn't too late but informed me that I cut time off of our consultation by being tardy. I even asked her if she had another appointment to follow mine or if it was the end of her day. She said she didn't have any other appointments. I do understand the value of an individuals time. I offered her money for her time and she declined.

Our interaction was awkward and forced. She went forward with the consultation donning a smug and condescending attitude.  She was argumentative and headstrong and I never got the impression that she actually wanted to help me. I cautiously moved forward with the following part of our appointment being as polite as humanly possible while she maintained her smug attitude. I was attempting to explain to her what I wanted done, she looked over me with a flat expression and dead eyes, telling me from time to time that what I wanted wasn't possible. And would touch my hair as though it was filthy and foul, saying things like "this hair is so damaged it would fall out if I put any color in it." and "what you want is unrealistic" I know this isn't true, a couple of my best friends are stylists and I ended up leaving and getting my hair to become exactly what I was looking for. WITHOUT FALLING OUT! Actually it's quite healthy. The whole situation pretty much ended with me confronting her about her attitude as politely as possible, saying, "I'm sorry but it's obvious to me that you really don't want my business, and you don't want to work on my hair, that you accepted the consultation even though you obviously feel resentful about my tardiness or something"

The establishment was tiny, unfriendly and dark.  It felt to me that she really wanted an easy in and out, and then to collect the cash. 

I've never had an experience so unsavory in a salon.

I don't particularly like going to the dentist, but this place makes it very smooth. Every time I come in, I don't have to wait more than 5 minutes before I'm taken to a room for my cleaning. Despite being on a busy part of magazine st, they have a private parking lot which is very convenient. The staff are all super friendly, and I've never had an issue. I've never had more than a cleaning done, so I can't comment on other procedures. I highly recommend them.

Stopped in on whim. The guy behind the counter was nice and pleasant. I ordered the turkey necks, potatoes, boiled shrimps and an ewwey gooey cake. The potatoes were too hot. I love spicy food but I don't like it when it's too hot that you can't taste the flavor. The shrimps were big and ok. The turkey necks were ok. Just not enough seasoning in my opinion. The cake was delicious. I'll go back to try other items on the menu to give it another try.

So the outside of the hotel is what made me me think to try it. It looks respectable there. However the rooms are super run down. We are talking about  circa 1981 at the least.  The doors are missing paint and cracked, with gaps and holes in the middle. The bathroom is disgusting and the floors are vomit green. We decided to get a suite and ended up being very disappointed... on a plus side the bed was comfortable and the staff was super nice.

I wouldn't recommend  staying here unless you are a felon hiding from the law.
Great wine! My mother just joined and I had a chance to share her member tasting. Enjoyed the reds very much. Great patio and you can grab a cheese plate next door to go with your tasting.

I hate to sound cliche, but this place really is a hidden gem.  I've driven up the coast to Santa Barbara hundreds of times and never once exited at Santa Clause Lane.  I was headed up to Santa Barbara for work and my coworker suggested we stop here for lunch.  It was love at first sight for me.  The property is beautiful.  You can sit inside at tables, picnic benches, or a couch, or go outside and find a table in the grass.  There's a large sandbox to occupy the kiddos.  There are "adult only" areas (THANK YOU!).  You can sit and watch the dolphins play in the surf.  Between the dining area and the ocean is the train tracks.  When the trains go by it feels like you could reach out and touch them.  DON'T do that.  lol.  The food is really good.  The fish is fresh.  The only reason I didn't give 5 stars is because there's a tiny little bit of attitude from some of the cashiers, and you can't bring your dog to sit with you outside.  That's not cool.  I don't want my dog to wait for me in the hot car.

Another wonderful weekend at the Peppermill with a few more fabulous meals at the Cafe Milano. Love, love, love the remodel here at Cafe Milano. It's ultra modern, light and bright. All of the favorites are still on the menu. From huge portions of breakfasts to really good sandwiches and burgers, to some of the best Chinese food.
This trip we tried the Prime Rib French dip. OMG! For one, it's like six inches tall with a generous amount of prime rib sliced oh so thin. On a nice handmade roll that some how is sturdy enough to stand up to all that meat. Served with a creamy, spicy horseradish sauce. Super good.
Another great fish we tried was the General Chicken and Fried Rice. I was worried that it might be too breaded but the huge chunks of all white breast meat was lightly battered and very tender. The sauce had just the right amount of sweet and spicy. 
The other great thing about Cafe Milano is the wonderful wait staff. They are always so accommodating and friendly. We truly enjoy our meals every time. Great food and great service staff.

I received the worst haircut of my life here.  I am a male.  It was done so carelessly and with such haste that I felt violated.  I explained for several minutes beforehand what I desired in the cut... But she either was unable or unwilling to comply in making me look normal.  How the fuck am I suppose to run a business looking like a hipster child.  LEARN YOUR TRADE!!! I could have done this at home with a mirror and a razor. FUCKING TERRIBLE.  And to make it worse, afterwards, she chuckled, "It will grow back". DO YOUR FUCKING JOB RIGHT. I was so insulted.  Less than zero stars.

I really wanted to love this place because it was recommended by a few foodie friends as a great date night location, but there were 3 things that really made the experience bad for me and my husband...and we are not picky people. 

Our experience was for a Friday night dinner. We had made a reservation for 8pm. 

I'll start off with the positive. Our server was kind and very attentive and his tip reflected our gratitude. My filet mignon was cooked perfectly and the truffle butter was a perfect addition to the steak and the truffle Mac and cheese was also delicious. Our goat cheese sformato appetizer was also very delicious...I just wish it came with more crostini to eat the cheese with. 

On to things we didn't like:
1. Parking is terrible. They are very crowded and your only option is valet if you want a spot and I was not comfortable handing off my vehicle to valet, so we parked across the street and walked over. 

2. It's very loud and crowded and definitely not a romantic dinner experience. To top it all off, we were seated next to the servers ordering monitors and where they stop to pick up utensils and we had to yell across our tiny table just to hear each other. There really shouldn't be a table here. 

3. We didn't enjoy the squid ink pasta dish because the pasta was chewy and stuck to each other. I don't know if that's how it was supposed to be because it was our first time trying it, but I would guess not. 

Unfortunately, we didn't stay for dessert because i really couldn't handle how loud it was, so I can't speak for their desserts. 

Dishes were beautiful, but unfortunately we weren't sold on the environment.
This is my go to breakfast place from now on... right off the trail in Downtown Tarpon Springs, have breakfast and afterwards go for walk or a bike run... a perfect way to start a morning.

Stayed in the "new" building. Clean room but small, and in old building. No movies on tv. Cable wasn't working. The hallway was 100 degrees and muggy. Overall, pretty disappointed. Parking lot is tiny and nearly impossible for an SUV to park.
Been in the practice since 1999. Always fast, timely, reliable and friendly. Needed an urgent visit and got an appt very quickly. Rapid diagnosis, pain relief, and referral. Highly recommend.

After dining at this beautiful resturant my credit card was charged fraudulently thru a different business in Santa Barbara. I loved the decor, it was amazing, The food was authentic and filled with flavor, we loved it. That is the worst part. India House was our only stop in Santa Barbara, and I didn't use my credit card anywhere else. But the host did tell me that they also had another Indian imports shop up the street, hmmm.

I got crab cakes, brushed off the bread crumbs, too dry. My husband got gumbo. He said the shrimp were over cooked and like rubber. My brother got shrimp po boy. He said the bread wasn't crispy. 
Great service though.

I am from Michigan and located the car online.  In the photos online and in discussions by email and phone with the salesman all looked fine. I indicated I would be flying in on Thursday to complete the deal and take the car home.
When I arrived on Thursday, and they knew when I was going to arrive, the car was not even ready to be looked at.  20 minutes later when they found the car and brought it out it was not even properly cleaned.  The wheels were filthy, the interior was not cleaned, and the rear bumper has obviously been hit numerous times, rough with touch up paint, scrapes on the left side, marks on the right side, and no one there acted like they had ever noticed this, and this was a supposed CPO car.  While I at first enjoyed working with Curt over the phone, it was obvious once I arrived that no one prepped the car for my visit. Upon purchasing the car I was only provided one key and no owner's manual, but was told both would be shipped to me in 3 business days.  I purchased the car on June 25th and as of today, July 13, still have no second key or an owner's manual and I've tried to reach the dealership through email and text messages but no one will respond.  In addition the brakes apparently need to be redone as there is a squeal coming from the rear brakes after one week of driving!  CPO, seriously?   How you sell a car as CPO with damage to the rear bumper, not cleaned, needing a brake job, only one key, no owner's manual, etc....just sets a very poor tone for Audi and its CPO program.  At this point I would not recommend the dealership or any Audi CPO to potential buyers.  I feel very let down and will be hard pressed to every buy another Audi, though I love the cars, but customer service is huge, and just because I walked out the door with the car doesn't mean the relationship is over.  Following through on what you said you would provide is essential.

I don't get this. It shows as one dollar sign $ but I felt what we got was quite expensive for what it was:
- A plate of mole, without very much rice nor beans
- A sope de nopal, granted it was double sized, but nothing else, no sides.
- A small papaya water
$29. $29!!! And many plates were $14. And you order at a counter. And it's a total hole in the wall (nice enough but not deluxe). Jeez, maybe I'm too used to L.A. prices? 
My friend said the sope was good. The mole was OK, I've had better, the rice somewhat dry, the beans typical refried probably with lard and nothing special. The food was quick, we went there due to running close to concert time and long lines at other places. It wasn't bad but no reason to go back especially at those prices.

The fish counter here is disgusting. It smells so fishy and tastes just as bad. I purchased the rainbow trout against my better judgement. I took one bite and had to throw the whole filet away. If you want decent fish go to Dierbergs.

THE Shittiest business I have ever walked into. The guy who runs the place is MISERABLE and obviously hates his pathetic life, so he takes it out on anyone who walks in the door. We walked in briefly and he rudely demanded: "leave your drinks on the counter", which is totally understandable but the way he said it was way over the top. My friend and I decided to finish our drinks outside and come back, but then as we were walking out he said: "....or you can fucking leave; I really don't give a fuck." Who the hell talks that way to their customers??? And this is not the first time he's been a prick to me. Sorry dude....no more chances. My friends and I will spend our money elsewhere. Maybe you should move to NYC where that kind of attitude is tolerated.
Very vintage and eclectic feel. Food was very tasty and the people were fun, knowledgeable so I'm sayin yeah when I visit philly I'd go back again

Nope! Definitely a miss. Soggy house special with absolutely no flavor. Thank God I also got the kong pao chicken but that was barely edible. I always get two items from the menu option when I'm trying out a place for the first time. This was definitely not worth the money spent. Never again.

Went here with the family day after Christmas. Small, friendly place. The fried pickles weren't the best in Doylestown, we'd have to give that honor to Nats and Pocos. I ordered the hummus appetizer as my meal, very good, nice ratio of olives, feta cheese, pita bread, hummus and greens. My daughter had a very large caesar salad and enjoyed it. My husband had a large hamburger and fries, he enjoyed. Our son loved his kid's chicken tenders.  Affordable, friendly and clean. We will be back!

To start off, we came for a birthday party and were told it was $1 PER SLICE OF OUR CAKE! The steak market price was $27.00 and when I received it, 40% was just fat. Unseasoned. The "seasoned vegetables" were only maybe 9 pieces of undercooked zucchini and three broccoli florets. The drinks were overwhelmingly watered down and tasted cheap which is super ironic considering it was $12. This was by far the WORST place I've eaten this year. My girlfriend's steak was also unseasoned and bland. This steak had nearly half with just fat. Not even seasoned fat, unappetizing fat. The cheese on anything you order will NOT be melted. These people had the audacity to tell that my seasoned vegetables, which were only flavored with melted butter, was made with love. The shrimp were rubbery and plastic-y tasting and cheap. Red beans were nasty and hard, and lacked flavor. Don't get me wrong, the staff is really nice and polite but that's it. Lol I wish I could leave a -3 star review because this has been the worst experience. Everyone at the table still had nearly all of their food on their plate at the end not because they were full but due to the supreme dissatisfaction with everything. Definitely will NOT be returned or recommending. Save yourself $100 and go to Wendy's or something.

My mom who is 86 years young and I were looking for a restaurant in Safety Harbor where the servers were wearing masks.  Thank you Rachel and your Gigglewater's staff for your caring spirit and making us feel safe during the pandemic.  The servers including the owner Rachel wore masks, tables were adequately spaced and cleaned.  AND the food was delicious!!!!!!!!!!  They even accommodated my no meat order and we loved the tater tots.  

Show your support to this unique dining establishment!!!!!!!!!!!!

I went for a Daquiri but my husband wanted cheese pizza. With left with one. We asked if they were making anymore cheese since it was only one slice sitting there. He responds this is what we got! So we went across the street and got a slice with a 3 minute wait!
Chicken Sandwich and Chili Cheese fries were delicious after a late flight into New Orleans. It hit the spot and the staff was very friendly.

This place was amazing! So many great dishes on the menu it was hard to decide, but I went with the brisket sandwich, loaded potato and the bag of donuts for dessert! Everything was truly tasty as it sounded. I will definitely be ordering from this restaurant again. Would highly recommend.

Although it was a last minute breakfast choice I was excited to eat here but it was not what I expected based on the pictures we saw. They were out of a few things which is understandable. I couldn't understand was the hash browns like McDonald's a why they laid a slice of cheese on my scrambled eggs. The service was great and the place was clean and cute. But I don't think I'd eat here again.

Good food, but the worst service that I have ever had. Will never come back. Waitress did not introduce herself. It took forever to take our order. Waitress did not check on us during our meal. After we finished, I had to flag down another waiter to even get our check and then no one came to pick up our check. I had to go up to the bar to get the payment done so that we could leave.

Here on the 4th of July. Not as packed as I thought. Been here a few times before. Food is yummy but nothing spectacular. Prices are pretty reasonable. Our server was sweet but not really around to refill stuff. Music is always playing and it's a nice selection. The ambience is cool. All in all this place is just eh. 


If your gonna try the beer, I recommend the red and ipa. The rest are mediocre and cheap tasting.

This is a must visit place in Philadelphia. I loved this place and the food,deserts,cheese, philly cheesesteak delicious, honey, etc. They have it all. They also have the nations oldest ice-cream place. Don't eat too much so you can try their ice cream.

Just so you know I DO NOT give out 5 stars to just anyone but this place earns every one.  I have been a number of times and it always delivers.  The ambience is great, very traditional upscale steak place.  It makes you feel important just being there.  The staff is very attentive, very knowledgable.  The food it great, dessert room is great, wine list is HUGE and I don't even drink wine (fyi...my wife does).  You can take a tour of the kitchen and wine cellar if you have the time.  Even though it is fairly expensive it is well worth the money if you want a great dining experience.  BEST STEAK HOUSE IN TAMPA PERIOD!!!
Let's cut to the chase. I have never not finished a cheeseburger until today. A day that will live in infamy.
Not prepared for anything! Charley get a clue! Who manages this location? You need help pal! Good luck, I'm done

Pretty straight forward - this place does grilled cheese and they do it very well. Killer bread and great ingredients. Greasy but damn good. Wish you got fries instead of chips but it's probably for the best...

Prior to arriving at their beach front restaurant for lunch, drinks, and sunshine I emailed the address provided on their eventbrite advertisement. I simply asked "if entry fee needed to be paid if we arrive prior to 3:00p.m." (the ad stated $10 entry for an event at 3:00p.m.) The employee responded "no ma'am, wrist bands could be picked up upon entry" Upon arrival I was told if we didn't pay $25 each we wouldn't be allowed to stay for the event and to enter it would be $10  to stay until 6:00p.m. even though it was 1:15p.m. I explained the situation about my email correspondence and the employee (Devone) stated if we had gotten there at 1:00p.m. we could have gained free entry. Confused and wanting to obtain a correct answer I went inside to speak to a manager. Kenneth at the front desk met us with an immediate rude demeanor. When I explained why id like to speak to a manger he snatched my phone with the emailed on it stated "The manager won't want to come out to see this email I need to take it back to "her." He returned stating the person who emailed me assumed I was staying the night at the hotel and not just a customer of the restaurant. I reaffirmed and pointed out in the email I never stating that I was staying the night at the hotel and would like to speak to manager about this issue since we are paying customers of restaurant and have visited this establishment many times. Kenneth stated entry is NEVER free at ANY TIME and that I was lying about the previous time I gained free entry. He refused to ask the manager to come out and speak to me because determined my issue insignificant to their business. If proper information was given we would have gotten there before 1, or paid the $40 in advance, but based on misinformation we were charged $100 with not so much as a sorry for the misundertsanding. Plenty of other beach bars in Florida who honor their words and treat all customers equally. I'll spend my vacation else where next time.

Must try this one since we don't have this in our area.  The ambiance was good.... coffee shop looks great and It must be good to some but I think it was too sweet.  I couldn't taste the coffee - I tasted the chocolate milk more than the coffee.  The store says coffee but where's the coffee?  I want my Starbucks

My experience here was not as great as the one I had at the south side location. The service was friendly and reasonably fast, but the food was not as good. I ordered a stack of blueberry pancakes. While they were large and fluffy, they were also quite dry - not enough English cream for me. The blueberries were very small and not plump and juicy. 

My lunch mate's raspberry crepe looked great, but he also said it was so-so. He ordered a side of roasted potatoes and sausage (and got bacon instead, but he didn't mind). He said everything was good, but nothing he would go out of the way for.

While I definitely will return to either location of Chez Cora to which I have already been, I will stick with the more elaborate fruit dishes, as that seems to be their strong point.

The great Pat's vs. Geno's debate.

That's what a co-worker and I hoped to resolve when we made the quickest pit stop ever en route to catching our train out of the city.  Pat's.  Geno's.  Quite simply, a LOT of beef, cheese whiz, and bread to put down--all in 20 minutes.  (Luckily, there were no lines this Thursday evening.)

First was Pat's.  $8.25 for a cheesesteak with whiz, no onions/pepeprs/etc.  Soft, yet crisp, fresh bread--nice.  Pleasantly surprised at how well the whiz worked.  But the meat, served in rough chunks, was a little tough and not that flavorful.  Good, but onto Geno's...

Geno's.  I was wary of this place's reputation for in-your-face politics.  The "Take Back Our Country" signs were a little eyebrow-raising, but beyond that, nothing crazy.  Plus, this steak was damn good.  The meat here was neatly sliced, properly cooked, and well seasoned.  My co-worker raved about the hot sauce he loaded onto his.

I understand that Pat's & Geno's are considered tourist traps by Philly natives, but nonetheless, are definitely worth checking out for the history.  Pat's vs. Geno's?  One man's opinion: advantage, Geno's.

Was here twice first time was good but the second time was horrible,  the recipes must have changed as they were very different and the service was really  bad nothing came out as ordered, I would never go back

This establishment was a nice night out for dinner.  The menu is not very extensive and the food is slightly above average and somewhat pricy for what you get  but the atmosphere and the service are excellent.

The food was good and the server was nice and attentive. We love the fried calamari and chocolate brownie dessert, yummy. I always get the loaded sweet potato with my steak, its a must whenever I go to a Longhorn. This one is a little older looking and smaller than the Brentwood location but still nice.

Like all good In-N-Outs, this place is fast, clean, and delicious.  The restroom stalls are clean and very large which is a major plus since this restaurant is in convenient pitstop area for travelers.

This review is also on google:
I like this place for my basic needs. As for food, I would not recommend unless you check the expiration date. I've experience  two times when I went in there to buy the garden pasta in the fridge and some other Asian food only to get home and realize it was had expired for a long time! One product was 2 weeks and the other was a few months. Of course the owner was kind enough to accept my return but his first question after I told him the product was expire was, "did you taste it?". As if to resell again? So unless one wants to get food poisoning go for it and eat up!

Narrow Gauge's beer absolutely stands out against the backdrop of Saint Louis' growing craft beer industry. You can't just make beer and do well, you need to make great beer, interesting beer... Narrow Gauge is killing it, y'all.

Last night I took the hike up to Florissant to see what all the fuss is about. I wasn't expecting that they share a space with Cugino's, which is an Italian restaurant, but I also wasn't mad. You feel me? 

I had a draft Hoppy Meal, one of their dry hopped APAs. It was so right. It comes in at 6.6% + 30 IBUs. Simcoe and Mosaic hops in this beer made for the perfect combination of flavorful, but not overwhelmingly bitter. Fantastic brew, guys!

Julia R is not much of a beer person as you guys may be onto by now, but what a trooper for coming with! She enjoyed their House Margarita. I didn't expect a $5 happy hour Marg. at a brewery inside of an Italian restaurant to be so freaking delicious, but hey, totally cool with being surprised by goodness. They used Espolon tequila on a happy hour drink... All day, baby!

We weren't there for dinner, but it felt wrong not to try something off of Cugino's menu, so I went with Brian M's suggestion: the Stuffed Meatballs. Fried Meatballs, stuffed with Provel, covered in Marinara, sprinkled with parmesan. It may go without saying, but they were VERY good. The only thing that wasn't stand out impressive was the service. We sat once we were finished with empty drinks and stacked plates for too long for how many folks were standing behind the bar. I may not have noticed if we didn't have somewhere to be.

Overall, this place is totally awesome and worth the trip if you live in the city. I'll just leave this right here, in case you'd like to try this place for yourself...

Happy Hour Menu: (M-F, 3-6)
$1 off select draft 
$5 House Marg 
$16 domestic buckets 
$2.5 well drinks
$9 pizza

Dorado Design worked with my contractor to create a beautiful kitchen in my Chicago area home.  Our home is over 80 years old and there were difficult situations along the way. Elizabeth is very detail oriented and solved problems from a distance. We are very happy with our new kitchen!

Every year on/around Memorial Day I take a day trip to Fast Eddie's with a group of friends. And every year we have a great time! Today was no exception, either.

Going on an actual holiday was a smart move, because it was way less crowded than it normally is on a Saturday or Sunday. There were plenty of open seats and the line to order food was minimal. The people who were there were pretty relaxed, too, so it wasn't crazy party time... which was A-OK for all of us. 

AND it appears as if they put a roof on the outdoor patio. It offers a little bit of shade, but if you can't handle the heat and humidity, there's always the indoor part of the restaurant.

As long as you know what you're getting yourself into with Fast Eddie's, it's a great summertime hangout!

I went here for the first time yesterday, after finding it on Yelp. The restaurant and staff were nice but there was definitely something strange that I can't quite put my finger on. I wasn't sure what to get so I went with of their specialty sandwich The Old Broadway, and I wasn't impressed. The sandwich wasn't terrible, it just wasn't my taste. I will be back to try something else and hopefully I will be able to give it a few more stars

this is what you'd expect.  Its got a sweet spot to stop and smoke while on frenchman.  The best nights are the regular Hip Hop nights with DJ Quickie Mart, as if absolutely nothing else the beats will make it all seem better than it is.

While on the Philadelphia leg of our trip, we stayed with a friend in Media.  In order to get to downtown Philadelphia, we took the Media/Elwyn Line.  The trip was long, about 40 minutes (I always had a book with me), but fairly comfortable (thank goodness we didn't have to bus to Philadelphia).  My big complaint is that the train ran only once an hour (with increased frequency during peak times), so we really had to plan our day to ensure that we didn't miss a train.  (I really hate not having flexibility when commuting.)  Despite the less than ideal train schedule, we were able to get around without any problems, the train was reliable, and it ran fairly late.
