If I lived in Media, the Media/Elwyn Line would not suit my needs, but for a three day stay, it was fine. 

As I only took the Media/Elwyn Line and a couple of short  bus trips within downtown Philadelphia, I can't comment on the rest of the system.  But from my brief experience with SEPTA, my conclusion is that it's not the best transit system, but not the worst.  Better than WMATA.

One thing to note: not all stations are accessible.  As I am able bodied, this did not affect me, but how the hell do people in wheelchairs or with strollers get around?

[Yelp collections: East Coast 2016; Planes, Trains, and Automobiles]
Food was great. Server was outstanding support your local businesses! Sangria was delicious. Shrimp tacos are highly recommended

Until now, I haven't been excited about any sushi restaurants in Nashville. This place was amazing! The Panko salmon roll was unique and delicious. The volcano roll was also great. Try the aloe cocktail also! Can't wait to go back and try more.
Really really good white garlic pizza..like, I occasionally dream about it..also their Stromboli are very good..but i def recommend the white with garlic and basil. Way to go guys!

3.5-4 stars for Mother's

Considering how long the line was (and it was Mardi Gras Eve), the line moved pretty quickly. I tried the Ferd Special (ham, debris, and gravy) and liked it a lot! Normally I'm not a fan of soggy sandwiches (which was from the gravy), but the combo of everything was very good.

It's a very casual place - jeans & sneakers are appropriate. I'm glad we stopped by (I intially wasn't planning on it)

Sat outside under trees with dappled december sunshine resting on the linen table cloth. A single rose and a homemade marmalade wait on every table. It's not the excellent breakfast of the array of homemade pastries that makes The Andersens deserve 5 stars, it s the little touches in presentation and service, and the location.

The park has only 4 good roller coasters. It's a good place to go with little kids but not teenagers. I think it is ridiculous that the park closes at six. The best time to go there would be early in the morning. Really hard to find a park map even when you ask for help.  Hate the time, but you can have fun.

In celebration of Latinx Heritage Month, my sweet tooth couldn't help itself but to share quite the hidden treasure in Santa Barbara:
Fernando's Churros.
Off a Westside side street...
behind Rosale's Mexican Restaurant and Auto Zone...
in a vacant parking lot...
sits the bright orange Fernando's Churros truck. 

Bring those bills, henny!! 
Once you get a whiff of the fresh churro dough blended with cinnamon and sugar, you'll be crunching it like it's hot!

Fernando is super sweet and easy going. Once I didn't have cash and his card reader wasn't working--he said to just come back another day to pay him. Doesn't get more trustworthy and local than that! 

Their truck slogan reads: "PRUEBA--DISFRUTA--REPITE! (Try...Enjoy...Repeat!)". Just like that, you'll be an addict and regular like myself! 
Oh, heck, he knew I'd be back for my fix. 

Fernando also does catering for large events (quinceañeras, weddings, etc) with the toppings (chocolate and caramel sauce), plus offers local delivery!
Note: Keep in mind that they're there only in Santa Barbara Mondays and Thursdays but travel to Lompoc, Vandenberg and Lompoc the remaining days of the week.

Since I'm such a Churro fan, check out where else in Santa Barbara County to get your churro on--though none can triumph Fernando's!
https://www.yelp.com/collection/Y3ZRTDE_Iu9CUfDN3Dq4FA?utm_content=Collections&utm_source=ishare

To be fair, I must mention that I was completely drunk by the time I made it to Beer Fest. It's located on Bourbon Street, which is generally a bad sign... but as I remember it is a tad nicer that the rest of the places around there, some of which can be quite dismal. It's a beer bar: they have a good selection of microbrews on tap, which was nice. It's not particularly cheap, but still reasonable. Order in a plastic cup (which come in several sizes... we got the larger one and weren't able to finish it...), and walk around the neighborhood.

Worst service for brunch I've had in Philly. Food came out at separate times and had to wait 10 minutes for it all to be here. Was excited to then dig in and realized there was a hair in my eggs and the steak was cold.

No apologies for the wait or rough service from anyone...

If your looking for a good brunch spot in No Libs, would recommend never coming to Kings Oak.

Had a choice of Eddie V's or Colombia in Ybor city..We messed up and turned out to be a ridiculous mistake. 
Sat at the bar excited to order the world famous 1905 salad and some steak. 
Finally decided on some good Veno and enjoyed till our glasses ran dry. I was curious to see if Barry or Gi-Gi would notice. After a little more than 15 min I asked if it would be a bother to receive a another glass and Barry gave me a sarcastic remark trying to get me to laugh at his embarrassing humor. 

I'm done with this place and will make a better decision  next time guys...

The drain valve on my water heater started dripping so after reading these mostly positive reviews, I decided to give em a try. To be fair, Jack did good work and replaced the valve with a brass unit and flushed the water heater. It was after I paid him that I noticed he overcharged me by $10. I called him right away to let him know. Said he'd send me a check. A week later, no check so I called again.  Said he'd been busy and will send it out right away. Week later, nothing. So I decided to use the form on the website. No response. Also on the site it states first time customers get a special rate of $78/hr instead of $118/hr. Too bad I was never offered the special rate. A business that will not refund a customer for overcharging has no integrity, not to mention not offering the first time rate stated on their site. It's only $10, but it's about the integrity of the company. Won't be using them again.

Two Thai teas for the price of one and sushi roll specials with an incredible selection! Im so happy this is right around the corner from my spot in Tampa. Thank you for existing! ^_^ Also, the service is excellent! The waitresses , including Katrina, are very attentive. Looking forward to returning to try many more rolls for solo dates and with my loved ones

One and done. They've created a menu to make this a $30.00 lunch for two kind of place. There is one size pizza...no small, medium, or large...just one size. There is a strategy in this. If you add another, or add an appetizer and soft drinks you have $25.00 + tip to make it $30.00 (ish) for two people. We eat out everyday, and for us Brixx didn't even come close to earning a $30.00 lunch spot in our restaurant rotation! Of course, that's just our opinion.

We went here on Friday night, 6/3, and I believe they opened for business that Wednesday. The parking lot was full at about 7:15 p.m., so we figured there was a good crowd inside.

We were told the wait for 2 people was at least a half an hour, so we waited at the bar. The bar was full - presumably from residual happy hour crowd. A draft bear and a club soda cost almost $10 - kind of surprised at that price.

The bar is comfortable, bright and inviting. The kind of place you'd want to hang out at. We got the feeling from the bar that we were really going to like this place. We had high hopes for the dinner portion. 

While we were waiting, we were offered two tables so we didn't have to wait for long. However, both times the offer came with "but, no one else wanted them, so..." Needless to say, we passed on both offers.

After about 45 minutes we were seated in the dining room. Shockingly, it was mostly empty. We can only assume that they either a) didn't have enough wait staff or b) wouldn't seat 2 people at a 4-person table. I'm inclined to believe "b" to be true, as it appeared as though there were plenty of waitstaff around.

The dining room is very cramped. The tables are too close together, and the dining area has none of the warmth and charm that the bar has. 

The menu is nothing spectacular, and mostly filled with sandwiches. Very few entrees. Our waitress didn't offer us any specials. We decided to go with a short rib sandwich for him, and a hangar steak sandwich for me. We ordered the fried mozzarella for an appetizer. 

We waited, and waited, and waited for our appetizer. During the wait we were forced to hear the conversation of the other couple next to us (the table were inches apart). Then, they were served fried mozzarella - and we were left to wonder where in the world ours was (we ordered about 10 minutes prior to them). So I visited the ladies' room - where, to my surprise, there was no cold water. Washing my hands was an exercise in avoiding third-degree burns. Even though there was a sign apologizing for the lack of cold water, that didn't really help.

I was hoping that the appetizer would be there when I got back to the table (it had been about 40 minutes since we ordered), but it was not. Five minutes later, our appetizer was delivered, along with our entrees. We also received an apology - something about how our table was normally a table of four, but they separated it for us and the couple next to us, so their ordering system isn't set up for that - I tuned out after that. I was really hungry and didn't care about their kitchen problems. She said she would comp our appetizer.

Both of our sandwiches were fine, but not spectacular. The home-made fries are really good. (The fried mozzarella was a disappointment - so was the tomato sauce, which actually resembled tomato water). 

Incidentally, the couple next to us sent back their appetizer and one of the entrees, and refused to pay for either (told you we were sitting close to them).

Our appetizer, in the long run, wasn't comped - but my bf's beers (there were 2) were. I guess they cost more than the appetizer, so that was nice.

All in all - there are definitely some things to be worked out here all around. Figure out how to seat your guests comfortably, tweak your menu, work on serving times and you might have something. For now, though - we're not in any hurry to return.

Overpriced, snobby, rude and unprofessional staff...oh and all of these free beverages, neck warmers and things people are mentioning, our party was not offered. I was with 6 other people who were ALL getting their manis/pedis done at the same time while I waited (with no explanation or a single word about when I would be next) for an HOUR while FOUR people "working" there stood at the front desk chit chatting. 4 other people walked in as I was waiting and were seated almost immediately, and offered drinks, which I was not. When it was finally my turn, after every single person I was with was DONE, I was not told "sorry for your wait", I got asked how I was doing...Obviously I was livid by this point. I ended paying $40 for the same exact service I would have gotten at a place with friendlier people for $25. Not looking forward to it peeling off in two days as I have read. Way to keep up the snobby Carmel reputation, Little Nail Shop. Enjoy the money you made off of us, because you won't be getting any more and hopefully none from anyone who reads this. Your decorations aren't enough to mask your ignorance. If I had a choice of 0 stars, that's what I would choose, do not go here!!

They did a good job tinting my windows and was able to fit me in quickly. I ended up not liking the tint percentage i chose (Far too light for my taste) but that's def not Tint Studio's fault as the tint looked fine. Pricing was fair and super friendly service. I would highly recommend

Upon first arrival i was greeted by a person that asked if i have been there before. After a quick chat he showed me how to order. You self order then pay and food comes out. Easy right....well the establishment is a hip place with popular radio music. Not bad. Now i specifically camebhere as im a noodle fan and yelped this place and drove here from Clearwater. I ordered the tonkatsu ramen with extra noodle. Now the presentation was amazing. The broth i tasted as thats the major part of this ramen. Its had hardly any flavor as traditional tonkatsu has. Very very oily and honestly isnt that tasty. I will probly more than likely not come back for the tonkatsu but will give it another try on other foods they offer.

I love that it's drive thru and has a place to sit outside.  I wanted custard but didn't want to get out of my comfy clothes... So we went thru the drive thru.  The custard was great.  The only thing that I was turned off by was the person taking are order thru the drive thru sounded so depressing...  If you aren't happy with your job, maybe you should find another.  You work at an amazing ice cream place... Act like it!

The ribs tips aren't rib tips it's just big bulky pieces of meat, the macaroni and cheese was delicious, but the baked beans were too soupy. Overall this place just wasn't my cup of tea in terms of the bbq I'm used to eating.

Ugh. The last few times I've left this place, I've left annoyed. The hostesses and the bartenders are rude. Really terrible attitudes consistently. I will not go back. 
Can get the same fries and mussels at their other location in fairmount minus the attitudes.

They really deserve no stars. Took almost 20 minutes to get our drinks. After 30 minutes and a group of people that came in after us getting their food before us was the straw that broke the camel's back. We ending up leaving without getting our food. My 7 year old was tired of waiting. Our waitress seemed overwhelmed and it was not busy for a Saturday evening. Definitely won't be coming back here, which is sad because I like the food.

Nothing close to real Middle Eastern food. Shawarmas aren't supposed to be cold and the garlic sauce doesn't taste at all like Middle Eastern garlic sauce. The shawarma did not even taste like shawarma. Terrible, extremely white version of "Middle Eastern" food.

Love Mimi!!! Extremely knowledgable and so easy to talk to about  
any of the treatments she offers. Very professional and takes time 
to make sure she knows what you want.

Love the vibe here, food was excellent, service was spot on, food delivered as ordered. Well managed, the place was cranking on Sunday about 11AM but still only 10 minute wait, got a table outside, gorgeous weather! Have been here several times, this one is the best I have been to of their other locations, well managed, keep up the good work people. 

No cons that I can come up with, food is fresh, comes out hot and servers are friendly, you are greeted promptly. I am now a regular after that visit!

We came here for dinner last night and it was absolutely AMAZEBALLS!  We ordered the Fried Green Tomatoes and Alligator cheesecake to start.  Both must orders!  For entrées we ordered Fried chicken and crawfish etouffeé, both off the charts!  Great food, service and ambiance.  Perfect place for anyone that wants a true taste of NOLA cuisine.  We'll be back sooner than later

The bright spot on our tour of Nashville, we hit Las Paletas on a stunningly beautiful day. Each pop is $2.50 which seems to be the going rate for gourmet pops all over the country. I chose the creamy lime and was quite pleased as it was equally parts creamy and lime: just what I wanted. 

There's a wonderful park across the street where you can go and walk as the melting pops make your fingers all sticky. Worth a stop if you're in town, especially if you don't have a spot like this in your own city.

I find myself returning to Beautiful World Syndicate's South Philly location every weekend to buy more vinyl. They have a great selection, and each week their "Just Arrived" bins contain different records. Of course, it's no Princeton Record Exchange, but it's the best record store I've found yet in Philadelphia. Sometimes I find their prices to be a bit high, but the albums are mostly always in great condition, so they're worth the extra dollar or two. 

If you don't mind kneeling down to check out the treasures on the floor, you'll love it too. The guys who run the place are nice and helpful and really seem to enjoy working there, which makes the atmosphere more comfortable. I'd highly recommend this place to all of you analog-lovers out there. And it's right down the road from my new favorite bar, the P.O.P.E.! So after all that searching, you can get a pint of great beer. What's better than that?

In early September, my TPS sensor went off as I was driving. As it happened, I was very close to this Firestone and drove there in 2 minutes flat. Joyce was AWESOME. I was ready to replace two tires before the TPS sensor went off and that's what ended up happening. My original Michelin tires had dry rot after 60K miles. I'm religious about maintenance on my car! Had two tires put on while I was at work and an alignment. It was painless (except for the wallet) and the shuttle was very efficient. Can't say enough good about Firestone in general, but Joyce really got it done for me on this particular day.

This place has great great brunch , definitely worth a visit !!! It's a byob which is awesome , I only gave 4 stars because every time I have been here I have had to wait forever to be seated but it's kinda worth it !!
Awful course with terrible drainage. Don't waste your money. Any of the municipal courses are better as is The Claw. All are cheaper too. Very disappointed.
First time eating here.....and it blew my mind!!!! This is going on my regular rotation schedule from now on!!!!! Great job!!!
The cannoli was the highlight of the meal.  Very delicious! The gyro was just so so and the ravioli was far over rated.  I was expecting a lot more after reading reviews and was not very impressed.

Hotel needs an update. Furnishings and facilities need refreshing. Things look worn and a bit tired here. 

Gym is spacious with decent equipment. I would rate it as better than most. 

Beds are comfy- that's what counts right?!

Location is super close to airport. And yes you hear the planes landing. 

Parking is $10 a night. 

Overall this hotel is serviceable but not plush or renovated.

I've been here three times - my last two visits I left after 10 minutes because the line moved about 5 feet or roughly 6 patrons. No doubt in my mind I would've waited another 20 as there were about 12 people in front of me.

There were four workers and they seemed overwhelmed. The ice cream was okay from what I remember from my first time but this place needs to get an extra hand or two involved to help speed up the process.

We are visiting Idaho for the first time on a short vacation.  We're staying right next to Tucanos, so decided to give them a try.  After first reading the reviews on Yelp, we were somewhat apprehensive, but it was convenient, and the place looked nice enough.

We made reservations, which were recommended, and went on a Thursday night at 6:30PM.  There were still tables available at that time, so there was no wait to be seated.  The greeters were courteous and greeted us very graciously.  They were interested in what brought us there, and we chatted a bit about being from California.  So things got off to a good start from the very beginning.

The waiter came quickly and made sure we again felt welcomed and understood how the whole Brazilian thing works.  Throughout the evening he came often enough as needed to make sure we had drink refills, etc.  We felt well attended to.  I'd give it a five star rating for what this restaurant is.

We visited the salad bar, which we found to be plentiful, well presented, clean, and with a good bit of variety.  I've hit better salad bars before, but a salad bar is presumably not why you are going to a Brazilian restaurant.  So, we found the salad bar was plenty good for our purposes.  I would give it a five star rating for what it is where it is.

What you typically expect from a Brazilian restaurant is a good selection of good meats.  As long as we gave the signal (green), the meat servers came frequently with a good selection of meats.  Some here have complained about the baked pineapple being served like a meat, but we found it delicious and a good mix along with all the other items being served.  So, I just don't get the complaint.  We were probably offered about eight or ten types of meat styles, and found all of them to be pretty good.  We would give the meat offerings a four star rating.  

The meat serving service we would give a four star rating, as they tended to rush.  Frankly, we were being served too much too quickly, and would recommend that Tucanos slow things down just a tad bit.  It's like they've listened to those of you who complained about the meat servings being too slow, and have over corrected.

The dinner was priced at $20 per person for the salad bar and meat servings, which we found an incredible value.  So, value get's a five star.

The environment is a bit noisy, but not unlike other restaurants of it's caliber.  The service, as I said, was a bit rushed.  While you were not forced to rush, everything around you was rushed.  

Tucanos is not attempting to be a trendy or even old-style five star restaurant.  Dress style is casual, and there were whole families there, including children.  So, don't go expecting elegance. or a nice romantic setting.  You won't find it there.    

On the other hand, if you're looking for good food, with good service, at a great price, then Tucanos won't disappoint.

Tamarind is owned by the same people who own Woodlands Indian, which has quickly become one of my favorite places to eat. When we went for a work lunch at Tamarind to enjoy their Indian buffet I knew it would be a guaranteed sure thing, and it was!

Although Tamarind's lunch buffet is quite a bit smaller than the one at Woodlands it still satisfied my Indian hankering. They still have the same fill your water cup ever two seconds type of service and the buffet is always fresh and filled. I think the difference between Woodlands and Tamarind is not just that Tamarind serves meat (including goat curry) but that Woodlands often has the room for more unique Indian dishes in their buffet. But that's not to say that you shouldn't ever go to Tamarind. It's not the best version of chicken tikka masala I've ever had but like I said, it was good. Plus it's a buffet, it can be as fast or slow as you want and it's around $10. What more do you want out of a lunch spot!?

Free parking across the street folks!

**Restaurant Week Review**
Came here last night. Seated at a high top with my group. First impressions this place is pretty trendy with the copper menu holders and such. Waitress came over to go over the menu, they were doing and all or nobody thing with the restaurant week menu meaning if one person didn't want the special then nobody could get it. There reason was because of how they fired the ticket and it would be delivered to the table. Never had a place do this and well that excuse is why they got 2 stars instead of 4.Ill explain in a minute. We got a round of the first and second courses. The fries were good and had what i think was taco mean on them. The calamari was standard, nothing special about it. The shrimp Cesar salad was basic again nothing special. The crab balls were great and had good flavor. For round two we had the cheese plate, again nothing extra special about it and same with the meat plate. The mussels were good and the brussle sprouts were edible. For entree we had 2 pizzas, 2 steaks and the scallops. Well the reason for the excuse was poor because our food did not arrive at the same time. The pizzas came first, followed by the steaks about 5 minutes later. The scallops were last to arrive and came about 7 minutes after the steaks did. The pizzas looked good, i didn't try one. My steak was cooked great, but wish the pesto on top had been warmed up a bit. My wife had the scallops which were over cooked and served with a cold cous cous that lacked flavor. All in all the apps were good, the entrees were ok, the service was great, but the lack of wiggle room in ordering and the lack of follow through i cant say we will be back here anytime soon.

Not a great first impression at all. Being seated immediately was the only great thing about our first visit. We were seated and while my husband was immediately brought his decaf coffee, I didn't get my regular coffee until the breakfast was placed on the table. When we were seated, we weren't even provided silverware and I had to ask for it 3 times. My husband ended up using his plastic straw to stir the creamer in his coffee. The waitress said they were being rolled, but I saw plenty of silverware being distributed to everyone else. I even had to ask twice for my coffee. The food was brought out and while the omelet was luke warm everything else was cold. The waffle was so cold that the butter never even melted the whole time I was eating it. I'm just really disappointed about this place as I've heard such great things. We probably won't be back.

I went here one day to grab my kids a pizza on my way home from work.  I didn't care for the pizza, but my kids seemed to like it.  The next day, I took my kids back and ordered two bacon burger combos, a shrimp meal and I ordered fish & chips.  We ordered our food, and was told it would be about 15 minutes until everything was ready.  So we left and went to the store a few doors down, came back 20 minutes later, but the nice gentleman who took our order was walking out with a delivery, I assume, so the only visible worker left, was the cook.  
I expected the fish to be cod filets or some other similar white fish, but it was one big piece of tilapia on a bun.  Not my usual idea of fish and chips, but whatever.  
The food was just okay, so I don't have any big complaints about it, but the guy cooking the food seemed like he didn't want to be there at all!  He didn't even tell us when our food was ready.  He just put it on the counter and walked away.  Good customer service can go a long way.  Somebody should tell that cook that, because this place could possibly lose more business, just because of one very unenthusiastic worker!

Obviously it's chain reasturant food but the staff here is above par. The managers are very pleasent, always walking the floor, and asking about your experience. The staff is attentive as well, always adhering to the 'have you been here before' policy. I've only had one problem with the food, lettuce was soggy, and the GM readily took care of it.

Do not go to this place for a lash extensions. I go here for my eyebrows and I really like the place so I figured to check out if their eyelash extensions were worth it. They were $35 and took 20 minutes to put on and my eyes were glued shut and I couldn't open my eyes easily for five hours after I got them. The lashes looked fake and all though they are, there are other places that make it look more natural. I should've known with the outcome would've been like since they were priced so cheaply, but since I go there for my eyebrows I figured I'd go there for my eyelashes to give them a chance since it was my first time getting them done.

Just had a meal for two here for Father's Day and we were both very disappointed in the food.  Talk about over-fried...my husband's cod and my onion rings were both very hard and didn't have much taste.  I had a Reuben that again had something missing...didn't taste like a real Reuben.  The salt shakers had salt that was clumped together due to moisture accumulation.  Some of the booths had seats that needed reupholstering badly, as you could see the stuffing/padding beneath.  We will definitely NOT be returning here anytime soon...not sure what happened here as food used to be good.

JosABank offers free steaming for their suits.  I purchased a $1200+ suit from Jos A Bank and came to get it steam pressed the night before a HUGE interview.  I recently separated from the military, was unemployed for a few months, and this was my big civilian interview as I made the difficult transition during an economy that was not so accommodating.  

The manager told me he turned off the steamer and wouldn't turn it on again to steam my suit.  Regardless of the fact that I was a loyal customer and a big spender there, and despite the fact that I had a very important (life changing) interview the next morning.

He didn't uphold the JosABank guarantee when I really needed it!

I will NEVER go to this location at the International Mall in Tampa ever again.  They abandoned me when I needed them most.

I'll update this post with that manager's name when I find his business card, but it slips my mind at the moment.  But don't waste your time at this store. If you really want JosABank and  you live in Tampa, go try it on at the store then order it from the catalog.

If you want an organic green juice, in a hurry, this is the spot!

KEY TAKEAWAYS:

- All produce is organic. 

- Juices are made and bottled on site. Expiration dates are on each juice which are typically 4 days out from when they were made. On the expiration date, juices are 1/2 price!  My favorite is the Daily Mean Greens, lots of veggies packed in, with just enough fruit for sweetness. 

- They also have great "medicinal" type shots, depending on your issue. I often get the immunity shot (cayenne, lemon and ginger) when I feel like I might be coming down with something or before I get on an airplane.  

- You can also get green smoothies made with coconut water to order. I've only had the full Metal, and enjoyed it but I'm more of a juice person. 

They also have a few juice cleanse packages, which has been on my to do list to try for a while now!  

The 22nd street location is equally fantastic!

Let me start off my saying that the food and atmosphere are always great. A buddy and I sat outside and had a few beers along with a roast pork sandwich and the swordfish wrap (more like a burrito). However our waitress (Nicole) really put a damper on the experience. She rarely came to the table to see how things were and most of the times she couldn't be found for awhile. We finished eating and waited about 25 minutes for her to even approach the table. We had our payments ready we a he dropped the check and without question, she walked away without even acknowledging the credit card. 

Good food, good atmosphere, just hope to have a different server than Nicole...

This was my first time visiting the place in years and it was not as I remembered,  wanted to order the stuffed shrimp, but the table next to me ordered and when I saw it looked like a child's plate for $18.99 I changed my mind...got the seafood platter $22.99 instead...I'm not cheap when it comes to food but don't play with my portions cuz my stomach will get angry (I ain't big, thicker than a snicker or two tho) anywho...the stuffed crab is filled with breadcrumbs  nuff crab to floss tour teeth...don't think I'll be going back any time soon, not that my lil $50 made a difference just saying‍ oh but the gumbo was good like $6 for a cup and THE PUNCH!! BEST PART!

This is my local WFs and I find myself here at least once a week. Though I will never understand why anyone would do ALL of their food shopping here simply because of the money involved, I certain wish I had the income to do so. I mainly use WFs for staple items I can't find anywhere else or for some items from their own 365 label that are not only decently priced but damn tasty (ie, their natural peanut butter!)

The only down side is the parking lot. It always seems loaded with arrogance and hostility.

PS: The salad bar is the bomb.

I've been to this location many times when I lived in the area and although it is a chain and not exactly sexy, it does the job on many occasions.   If you need an oil change, this place is ok, if you need a state inspection this place does it.   The key is getting there when they are not busy or else you will wait.   

Again, nothing fancy here a pep boys is a pep boys but all in all decent stuff.

The room and the ambiance alone are worth the trip.
Very romantic. Great vibe. Great music. Very intimate.

Every course was very very good. 
The Tortellini pie was my favorite, by far.
Excellent wine list, and service that was aimed to make sure that not a single part of your night was "off" in any way shape or form.
(We were in the blast zone of the AC, and they were kind enough to move us to another available table).

The pastas, breads and everything made from grain had excellent flavor, but turns out they source their own grains and work with farms directly, etc.

Cool vibe. BUT I am so disappointed in this place. My husband and I were in town for one night celebrating our anniversary and chose this restaurant for our only dinner. 

Our main issue was our SERVER!!!! Apparently each table has 2 servers. A front line and a back line. It was our "front line" guy, Shawn (sp?). Our "back line" guy Peter was fine but his job is mainly to take plates away. 

This front line person can really make it or break it!! He broke it. He didn't seem like he cared AT ALL! He seemed like he didn't wand do be there and if he was having a bad night (which I get it, people do) then send him home to recoup and try again another day. 

I have a diary allergy and don't eat meat. I do eat seafood. My husband usually gets his food dairy free as well so I can taste his (great husband!). We told our server this. We asked for his recommendation and he proceeded to recommend steak and a chicken dishes!! We steered him back to the fish options and he quickly walked away saying he'd check with the kitchen. 

I ordered the cobia with grits and kale. Now. I KNOW grits can be made diary free BUT I get it.....these are probably pre made and they weren't going to make me a special batch so they offered to give me more kale. 

I'm a kale loving girl. I seriously have thought about buying one of those "KALE" t-shirts to support it! BUT this kale was so chewy and tough I couldn't break it down and felt like I was going to choke swallowing it. We relayed this to our server when we could wave him down. He quickly announced he'd have more kale made and ran away. Same situation on round 2 of the kale, round 2 was actually even tougher. We never saw our server again to tell him. 

My husband ordered the drum. The table next to us ordered the same think but we noticed hers came with hush puppies and she said they were the best part. My husband can eat those.  He just wanted his fish dairy free so I could eat if. We never saw our server to be able to ask him to bring these. 

We also ordered the Brussel sprouts as a side dish. They were good. Not the best we've had but pretty good. 

The basic fish dishes we had were also good but nothing that I'm sending people back for. Maybe it would be different if they were filled with butter but a "scratch" restaurant should be able to accommodate this and make that same experience for someone with a food allergy. Especially for a fish dish! It's not like I ordered Alfredo. 

OMG after writing this for a while I want to lower my rating!! I'm getting worked up over the terrible service. 

To wrap it up. I initially started with a 3 rating bc the food was fine but after consulting with my hubby since he took part in this we moved it down to a solid 2 but the server, Shawn, deserved a negative rating! 

Won't be recommending this place based on our experience. Obviously!! LOL

Chicken was good, but expensive for what it was.  Front counter was under-estimating waits by a good half an hour, and just wandered off and ignored me the first time I asked if my order was ready instead of saying  they were backed up.
Ok.  I figured out the system.  Go to their office on Prototype Drive and talk to a real person.  They are the ones who can get you the decent rates and be helpful.  Better.

Of the 3 Asian Markets I hit today, this one looks the roughest on the outside, but had the best selection of "stuff" inside. The only store where I could find the red/yellow labeled canned fish. They carried many of the better brand sauces and a wide selection of noodles. Today did not see much fresh produce or baked goods. Previous reviewers seem to think Friday was the incoming products day, so maybe I'll see.

The market seemed to have a bit of a more lean towards Chinese cooking. Also the best selection of dishes and cookware. Only shop I saw metal Woks and tea pots. Lots of rice, of course.

Store was organized and well stocked. Clean and relatively uncluttered.

The parking lot is sort of a disaster waiting to happen. Entry is down a sloped driveway and from the west bound lanes of Fairview, be prepared to avoid oncoming traffic. While paved, the lot holds water after a rain. Access is probably OK for wheelchairs. Can't recall if I saw a blue signed ADA space in the lot.

Since this store is closer to Meridian, I would shop it again. To complete my survey, I will have to hit the Asian Grocery Outlet and Diana's. When I have another day to spend foolishly...

This has to be one of the strangest Greek/Mediterranean restaurants I have ever been too. From the moment the misguided waitress ushered us in it went downhill from there. I'm sure the food would have been better if not served by such a bitter and unhappy person. 
I had the mixed grill was flavorful but they must have put  a ton of salt in and on everything.
I've never really been served cold pita with an app platter and it really had no appeal to the eye at all. 
The establishment was busy and most people seemed happy.

I visited Rubio's for the first time and it will most likely be my last. Yes, the place is very clean and the service is acceptable. But the food is not what I expected.  I ordered the shrimp taco plate which included two shrimp tacos, some plain tortilla chios and refried beans. The shrimp had no seasoning, was small and didn't fill the soft, overly doughy taco shell. Inside the taco there was a few slices of avocado, the tasteless shrimp and a bland salsa. You tasted the undercooked soft taco shell more than anything else. The tortilla chips had no salt and were stale. I didn't care for the liquidy refried beans either. My son had the mahi tacos, which he liked. The mahi tacos had more fillings and seem to fill the soft taco shell more so than the shrimp ones. My daughter had the chicken nachos. Again, no seasoning at all on the meat and hardly any cheese. There was a small dollop of sour cream and guacamole. Definitely not a place I would recommend.

mmm hoagies

i love primos. they're fresh, delicious, incredible...

but it's like torture since whenever i crave some, they're closed! they're not open after work and most disappointing of all, they're not open on sundays which during football season is the most hoagie-tastic day of the week! sigh...

Wasn't sure what to expect.  I had heard that this was a burger joint/sports bar.  Yes, lots of TVs showing sports but a very good place to grab a bite of very good quality food.  The appetizers were not only delicious, but also very reasonable priced.  Drinks were also very well priced.  
Our waitress, Stephanie, was TERRIFIC!  She was attentive, knowledgeable and very thoughtful - what more could you ask for!

sears overall has very  talented individuals working for them. I have now met 5 of them, including James, Robert, Jessie, MIN and one other that I cannot remember. Sometimes, we make mistakes when we hurry and don't double check out work, and that is what happened with my repairs, causing much frustration and loss of productive time. I would use them again, but due to the prior service visits that did not remedy the problem, they only get 3 stars for this instance. I hope I can give them 5 stars on the next visit.

This is my 1st review.  I signed up because of the flatbread offer.

While the waitress was nice, service was very poor.  We tried to be patient but our flatbread was brought out and we still didn't have silverware  Had to ask for silverware and napkins.  The kid's meals came out on time but everything else took forever.  We had to ask for the check and then had to hunt her down to give it to her.  Plus she forgot to give the discount for the flatbread.

I was expecting this to be like seasons 52 but it fell very short.  My stirfry was good but the crabcake was mushy and burnt.  Hubby had fish tacos and they were just ok.  He didn't finish them.  The worst was the tomato bruschetta.  It was $10 for cut up tomatoes and 3 pieces of torn bread.  No flavor, no seasonings.  I was very disappointed as I love heirloom tomatoes and they did nothing with it.  Also, the desert was pretty gross.  Well at least key lime and banana was gross.  They tasted like they were spoiled.  Also we sat outside and there were frogs jumping around.  Lol that was actually kind of funny.  My hubby ordered the Cabernet and that was excellent.  The mushroom flatbread was also excellent.  Other than that very subpar for a dinner for 2 adult and 2 kids (kids meals) that cost over $100.  Next time I'll just go to King Of Prussia and eat at Season's 52.

Fantastic! Fresh, friendly and delicious!
Subway - never again!
Cali Turkey on Sourdough with guacamole!! 
It is the best. Give it try.
My guys love the tri-tip steak.
And they are growing, they will be in your neighborhood next.

I have chosen to give Directv 1 Star.  If possible it would have received zero. Having been a loyal customer for 20 years I no longer and will never again utilize their services.  We live in a gated community and have no choice in our service provider. So for many years at additional expense we chose to use Directv for our tv service. Due to software updates their service no longer will connect with our internet provide. Have had several high level techs attempt to solve the problem and they were unable. We had no choice but to request Directv to disconnect our service.  They informed me that it was fine but then went on the tell me it would cost us $500 as the contract was still in effect. I spoke with several customer service managers and they informed me that they would not waive the $500. Obviously play loyalty with this company only goes one way up. Beware!!!

I have come here a few times and on 2 occasion now they have gotten my drink order wrong.
Both times they fixed it right away though.

One time they gave me regular when I asked for decaf and I could feel it right away - wow.  What a jolt!

The 'Bux in Paoli is way better.

Yum! My risotto last weekend was one of the best risottos I have had. The wait staff was great, atmosphere was perfect for an enjoyable, full conversation with friends and the beer selection was local mixed with unique imports - just the way I like it! The dessert we ordered to share, lemon sponge cake with fresh blueberries, was the size of a Hostess Donette and had about the same disappointing taste for $8. For how magnificent the risotto was I can overlook the dessert epic fail!

Went here for a quick dinner with a friend.  Pleasant, quiet atmosphere, with friendly service.  I tried the shrimp/pork summer roll and the seafood noodle bowl; both were very good.  Prices were low, too!

Only have ordered a couple of times so far for delivery.  Over all the food is tasty, but I could use a bit more heat.  That being said, this is the only Indian restaurant that has ever delivered to my house, so that's an automatic plus. 

The Palak Paneer is good, the cheese is a little firmer than some other places that I have had and I did miss some of that sponginess.  The Palak was thicker than some other places, which I appreciate, more chunky than liquid.  Again, I could use a good deal more heat in this dish, even after stressing that I wanted it spicy over the phone.  

The Chicken Makhani that my better half ordered was good too.  The sauce was a bit thinner than I am used to from other places, but the flavor was good.  Again needed more heat for my taste.  

The Garlic Naan had a good chew to it, a little overly burnt in some spots, but very tasty.  

I like the extensiveness of the vegetarian menu.  Indian is one cuisine that I think is better when vegetarian.  I am looking forward to trying more of the menu.

Delivery was fairly quick, and arrived at the time they reported.

Got an order from Grub Hub and it's was over an hour late to be delivered. I understand that these are chaotic times times and if we had been given notice it would take this long there would not have been an issue. However, after multiple phone calls to ask the status, we were told they would call the driver and get back to us but never did. The customer service was less than desired. The food was very good but the time it took to get it was a lot

This place is alright. I ordered a california bowl a couple of weeks ago and it was decent. Just a bunch of slop in a bowl, really. Chicken, rice, beans, salsa, sour cream, cheese (cheddar I think, maybe monty jack) and guac if my memory serves correct.
It all seemed fine and not particularly fresh. Especially the salsa. The salsa was straight from a can. Reminded me of those Pace picante sauce commercials. New Yawk City. Yeah, that one. 

I got my bowl to go and it came out super fast. Like amazingly fast. Like I ordered and paid. And then I looked at the menu again while I waited. I got half way through the tacos and BAM my bowl was hot, ready and packed up nicely to go. And it stayed hot all the way back to the office. 

Interested in coming back at some point and exploring the rest of the menu - especially if I don't have time to kill!

I review with mixed feelings! I chose Woodbourne Animal Clinic based on Yelp reviews, and it all seemed okay at first. We had a good first visit, where my cat was weighed and vaccinated. Dr. Patel then scanned her for her microchip, which I had implanted in 2009, and couldn't find it! He used 2 different scanners. I was astounded. Turns out it just stopped working.The cost of the check-up was reasonable, and he told me that if the chip company sent him another chip, he would implant it for free.

There was an apparent lack of communication between Dr. Patel's staff and himself. When I returned, he knew nothing about the chip company sending it, and so I had to pay for it. Understandable, yes. But after investigating, the chip company did indeed send it and communicated it to his office. (It was on the way, though he didn't know it). So, I'm out of the money for the chip now, and he is getting a free chip. The chip company tried to get him to refund me, and he won't. I understand he has no chip, and why he wouldn't refund now, but he will receive it, and could refund me then! So, he won't be getting any more of my money. I feel it's pretty dishonorable. I was embarrassed to show up for my free replacement microchip and find he knew nothing about it, nor would honor his word.

Also, he I realized after both visits that he never commented or asked about the bare belly of my cat, which is obvious. I believe she has some type of allergy, and has for a few years, but an observant vet always points it out to me and tries to suggest solutions. I feel that the scanning for the microchip he did at the first visit made me feel like he was thorough, but in retrospect, the first visit was very quick and missing the bare belly was not thorough.

Sorry to write a negative review about this clinic, as it seems others had good experiences, but I feel really irritated about this. I had to do a lot of work to get this replacement chip and get my money back, not to mention stress out my cat by taking her to the vet twice in 2 weeks. And Home Again microchips may also not last the lifetime of your pet, it seems!

I was surprised by the degree of service I received here. The wait staff were really attentive when we visited during Downtown Dining Week for lunch. What I didn't like was the fact that staff seemed a little odd in that they pulled out the chair for my female friend but not for me, another female restaurant patron. I mean, we're not a couple or anything, but making such assumptions is odd at the best of times. If one isn't able to pull out both chairs for the restaurant patrons, why pull the chairs out for one but not another?

As I said, service was otherwise pretty awesome. It was nice that they asked if we wanted the bread with the meal, especially as so many people have gluten intolerances these days (we ate and enjoyed the bread and the olive oil dip!). Then we shared both of the entrees they had for Downtown Dining week: chicken and risotto before enjoying dessert. The food was outstanding. Before, I had always just thought of Sorrentino's as a place for pasta and now, well I was wowed by their non-pasta dishes!

Service was prompt and we were able to eat and leave in approximately the hour we had for lunch on a workday. The atmosphere was great: the room was really well appointed. I don't often splurge on such a fancy workday lunch, but Downtown Dining Week provided the perfect excuse. 

Perhaps sometime I'll visit again for dinner?

This Burger King I have come to since I was 12 years old so I made it a habit to try and bring my kids and I'm tell you this place has changed so much but for q good way a lot.. the supervisors and manger in this location are great getting there jobs done they are on top of everything and everyone..:the only thing this place needs is to maintain clean a bit more other then that staff is nice, they get a mess up here and there but it's nothing to bad but other then that this bk is great

It's just alright restaurant. The grouper didn't feel fresh. They advertise they serve best mahi mahi tacos in Clearwater but it also felt stale. If you are really looking for good seafood this is not a place for sure. Thought service is good. Staff was very friendly but that's all good about it.

Have been looking for good restaurants near my home, and was delighted to find Bistro Albertino. Went there for lunch yesterday--had a perfect chicken parmesan--pounded well (paillard), fabulous flavor. I asked for extra tomato sauce and was given more than enough (some places are so chintzy). I also asked for roasted potatoes as a side dish instead of pasta and was graciously accommodated. The potatoes were also cooked perfectly. My husband had a panini that he said was good.

While the outside is unprepossessing, the inside ambiance is lovely and the owner a truly gracious host. I plan to become a regular.

We celebrated a birthday here/ party of four. The margaritas were strong and tasty. The service was running behind as the spot is still "hot and new" and I think they had trouble keeping up with crowds. The food was ++++ and before we left they sang happy birthday and offered a desert. I think they realized there are still some hiccups to work out, but we will for sure be back!

Heard from everyone this place was the place t be...  worst service ever!!!!  Checked in for dinner party of 9...  one under 21...at about 7p...they said 45 minutes..  2 hours later we asked how long...  they pointed at a party table, saying when they leave, u get their table..  seated smaller parties one after the other../joke of a place!  No place for a family!  Or a big group...  can't rate the food...  never got any....won't come back!!!  Being back the firehouse!

Nice brunch spot, we sat outside and enjoyed the vibe.  It's an old school steak house with a nice weekend brunch.  I had the Louie Salad, seafood was fresh, it was good but expensive.  We also had an omelet, again good but small and expensive.  

I'd come here again - it seems to be a local hotspot.

I don't mind this store. Convenient for all basic groceries. They have a fairly large produce section which is nice, though, to be honest, the produce quality can be a little underwhelming. But they have everything you need on top of that. They have a decent liquor selection too ranging from low- to mid-quality all at good prices. I've rarely had problems with anyone here, but there have been a few occasions where I've harassed by people out front who are aggressive with panhandling. Nothing major, as there is security posted 24/7. But hey, it's a Schnucks. You're going for convenience and price, not high end. If you want that move to west county.
This location is closed! The sign on the door says they have moved to downtown Nashville, but we can't find them. I tried calling but their mailbox is full.
Great burgers but will always give you cold old fries, even if you ask for fresh fries. It sucks because burgers are really tasty.

Coffee was good, I was just trying to get a coffee it's early in the morning and the lady hits me with hi how are you doing I said good thank you. She said I'm doing good too thanks for asking and I never asked. I'm not sure if that  was to be dick head funny or she was serious either way I'm just trying to get coffee I don't need all that extra stuff or I'll just go to another location

My what big fish you have....
okay day two I found myself back at the truck in University City this time for the Fish (tilapia) Sharwarma ...this time I had to break out the little fork they gave me delcious Wish I knew how to add a picture...
again delicious just like they said it would be

Love this place! It has a comfortable hip vibe unlike any other place in SB. So far everything I've tried has been delicious. Favs are the calamari fries & Hawaiian Plate. Super cool, friendly staff and it's family owned and operated which makes it even more great to support.

We purchased $200 worth of stone crab and lobster tail. Didn't notice during cooking, however, the lobster reeked of ammonia. We paid $80 for those tails. Hopefully they will honor our purchase and give us our money back.

Just want to say how Pleased I am with The Complete Makeover on my old floral arrangement.  
We couldn't be Happier! 
Highly Recommend the team of Ken & Julia.

From the quaint decor to the traditional food, we love coming here. At six on a Saturday there were only 4 tables filled, but one was a rambunctious party enjoying themselves so much it filled the room (in a good way). While I'd been in before, it had been quite a while. I really appreciated he menu improvements including gluten free indicators and more in depth item descriptions. 

Love the beer and wine selection. I had a Pinot I couldn't pronounce and my companion had a traditional Polish beer. I started with the pickle soup- total perfection in a cup. 

I then moved on the the beef stew- mine was over fried to be gf, and my partner had it in a potato pancake. We both were groaning with how good it was. 

Sadly, they were out of the cheesecake my companion loves, but it was still a great dinner.

I was hoping this place would be amazing, but it was just kinda meh. I had the Ukrainian burger for my first try, and while it was good, the burger was bland and lacking flavour. The macaroni bites were the same way. 
I built my burger the second time around, which was a much better choice as I picked chicken instead of beef. 
The shakes and floats are great though, and I liked the thin deep fried pickles.
This place is an amazing small place byob. Will is an amazing waiter totally interactive with the table and made us feel like family. Love this place. Definitely a place to check out.

DON'T BE FOOLED... 

Being a new home owner not wanting to go to a big box store we decided to go to Gerhard's appliances and our first interaction in the show room was great and that's when we fell for it. After that first experience it has been a nightmare working with this company. 1st an ice maker we ordered was not installed prior to delivery as promised and paid for. It took two more trips for their service team to come to our house to tell us the sales rep had ordered the wrong part. Then was another few weeks to be reimbursed. 

The Stove we purchased had a strong gas smell that we thought was just from being new unfortunately we were wrong there too. After about a month of no chance we called to have it service turns out they installed a stove meant for natural gas on propane without the proper adjustments, and guess what that will be another day to have them come fix it since they had to order the parts. 

How does an appliance company go about installing something they know is incorrect and not say anything. I reached out to the company multiple times prior to this review and have received no response from them. Don't be like us and don't be fooled go find a reputable business to spend your money. Clearly Gerhard's does not care about their customer after the initial sale.

A really solid and fun place, totally different than the typical Philly scene. There's a large indoor portion, as well as a huge outdoor courtyard with an army of heat lamps and fire pits. Even on a chilly night, you can sit outside relatively comfortably, especially once you have a liter of craft beer in your hand. 

They also feature ping pong/foosball tables, smores to roast over the fire ($1 each), and games like jenga and uno. It's a really festive atmosphere usually, everyone there is looking to have a good time. The food is also great! I had a sausage there which was everything you'd expect. They have these crazy fluffy pretzels which make for great beer food.

The only downsides: $14 can be a lot, even for a full liter of beer. Also, despite how huge the place was, we had to wait outside for 5 minutes (of course we were the only ones in line) on a saturday night. Depends on the weather. I imagine this place might get impossibly crowded in the spring and summer.

Oh, and if you sit outside, be prepared to smell like fire/smoke. It's going to happen.

Trying it out for the first time tonight.  Tried the Turkey Avocado. The sandwich was ok but not amazing.  The bread is better at Jimmy Johns. Honestly I'll likely just get JJ's next time I want a sub.  The drive through option is nice, however.
The front desk ladies were not friendly at all. They dont look sincere when they said they are here to make your stay more enjoyable.

09.15.13 - One of my fave Rock'n'Roll series events to date. More deets to come!
.

09.19.13 - Of all the Rock'n'Roll Series runs I've participated in, this is unequivocally my favorite.

We found staying in a hotel in Rittenhouse Square provided us within walking distance to & from the Health & Fitness EXPO (registration pick-up) at Philly Convention Center on Saturday & to the START line on Sunday.

Participants make their way in front of Philadelphia Museum of Arts (think Rocky at the top of the steps after his run).

Elite runners promptly start at the 8 a.m. mark while the rest of us find way to our corrals for the "wave start". 

Ready, Set, Go!
We head South around Logan Square towards The Gallery @ Market which makes a Northward turn then the route takes you West on Arch Street past the Convention Center towards JFK Park (LOVE park) for a 4 mile loop meeting again at Logan Square. The Route continues North past the Museum of Art on Kelly Drive & continues along the Eastside of the Schuylkill River for miles 5 thru 8. Runners cross the Falls Bridge and head Southbound on Dr. MLK Drive nearing mile marker 9 & continues to the end of the nearly flat 13.1 mile run back in front of the Museum.

TIP: Quickly find someone who runs at your pace & stick to them during your run. 
I was switching from the cute pony-tailed Asian girl in the white visor, grey tank & black shorts running a 9:30/50 pace (a tad too fast for me) to a couple of guys keeping a slow steady 12 min/mi to the "Black Girls DO Run" team who were running a 10:35 clip. I finally found my running mate. The stunning blonde in braided pig-tails, neon green headband, wearing a pink tank, black shorts with crazy multi-colored zig-zag stripes, neon green compression calf sleeves keeping a nice steady pace at an even 11 minute miles. I found her at just past the 4 mile marker & continued to match stride for stride with her till she made a "pit-stop" at the 8 mile mark. That's where I lost her forever. I was either too fast or too slow for anyone else around me...so it was downhill from there. I had to keep cadence repeating the same 5 numbers in my head no matter what the beat of music was pumping thru my earbuds. Finally, at the 10 mile mark I saw the sign "COLD BEER is WAITING" & the sweet backside of strawberry blonde that I followed to the finish. By the way ladies, this works for you too: I saw a sign along the way that said, "LADIES: run like there's a hot guy in front of you or like a creepy guy is behind you!" (I think I might've been that creepy guy...)

Not my best time for a  1/2 marathon, but to my credit, I stopped to take a few pix along the way, followed a couple guys for 3 miles running 12 m/m & I walked about half a mile cuz of a side ache. While I'm on the subject of walking - PARTICIPANTS: please remember, if you plan to partially walk the route...move your ass to far right. It's like the slow lane on the highway. There is nothing worse than having to maneuver around walkers in the middle of the thoroughfare while in full stride. Thank you in advance.

We were so impressed with the organization (both the expo and the day of race), the volunteers, the live bands along the route, the running course, the awesome participants the overall spirit of the event & of course, the beautiful city views of Philadelphia.

RnR Philly just announced their 2014 date: 09.21.14. We're in, are you?
http://runrocknroll.competitor.com/philadelphia

My second visit but first for dinner. Quite the happening place. Staff were all very accommodating. Bronzino was excellent. Sheppard pie was a bit salty. And I had to return my undercooked mussles. Manager was very nice about it and in addition to taking it off the bill he gave us a complimentary desert.  The oysters were great and we loved the bread and butter but the Welsh rarebit was not what I remembered from my childhood.

I think the awesome thing about this place is the patio. They have some misters which is nice when it's hot out, and on good days it's great for people watching and chilling outside. I ate here years and years ago before I was on Yelp and always enjoyed the pizza. I went here with a friend the other night and choose a couple of slices of mushroom cheese pizza (doesn't all pizza have cheese? It's redundant for me to say cheese. Although I had a cheese-less pizza once and it was, oddly, tasty). They take the slices out of the warming tray and heat 'em up very fast for you and we were eating them before we had time to fill up our soda pops. Delicious to the last and good crust. I think the pizza and two drinks came out to under $10 or so. I like that you can fill your own drinks.

I want to ask if they have any anchovies for the pizza. But I don't want them to throw me a nasty look. I actually like anchovies on a pizza, but obviously, this is not the place to go for them. Truth in advertising.
We booked using the app and it was great. They showed right to our table and the waiter made a few special suggestions that were fantastic: shrimp with a lobster stuffing. To die for!! Love Bonefish

Blenders has great smoothies!! There is a good variety on the menu to find something you like and keep you coming back to try more. The tropical guava was tasty and I enjoyed the little chunks of fruit. 

What is really awesome here is the complementary add on!! I was able to get some whey protein. Plus, I think it is reasonable priced for what you get. It is also decently healthy too!

I have tried a multitude of smoothies and smoothie institutions and Blenders is definitely up there with the best (if not the best).

Great service and decent food.  The wait can be really long when the weather is nice but I try to visit it a few times a year.  Favorite entrée so far was the smoked/grilled salmon they had during the summer.

i went to see x-men apocalypse this past saturday and i loved the movie, but the staff as rude. it was my first time here and the theater being in a hotel, that had a wedding going on was a bit overwhelming at first, but kind of fun overall. but there were 3 people working concessions when we came in for our 8:30. they weren't very responsive and the young lady had an attitude when i asked for my side of cheese, that i had paid for. 

about an hour into the movie my husband and i came out at the same time to use the rest room. his was closed for cleaning so he had to use one outside the theater and we wanted a refill on our soda, but the machines were shut down! kind of crazy cause there was a little more than an hour left in the show and concession was already shut down?! i worked for amc before and we didn't shut down concession until about a half hour before the last movie was over. staff was rude and inconsiderate. probably won't be going back.

Beautiful facility inside & out! Over 2,000,000 sf of total space, but laid out so you don't feel like you're walking miles.  Don't get me wrong, you'll have no problem getting your 10,000 steps in, but you'll be entertained by the architecture, views and county music memorabilia while getting your aerobic workout.  Another cool feature is the grass growing on the roof of the massive building which filters rain water allowing it to be used to flush the hundreds of toilets and irrigation for the landscaping.

My husband and I visited Speedway Indoor Karting. We had a decent time. My kart on the oval track felt like the wheel was going to fall off. The slick track was fun to slide on, but too short. The road track was fun. Just a little bit of instruction on the road course would have been nice. The place has potential, but I wish the staff had better communication. I would not waste money on the oval course or slick track again. I think the road course would be cool after the first time since you have to figure it out yourself. (I let the staff know the kart had an issue and she said that's just how it is.) It was $100 for 17 minutes and 10 of those were a complete waste.

Disappointed   I don't like complaining at all but it wasn't what I would want for a first time there experience.   I came with three other people tonight for dinner and we were quoted a 15 min wait time. It was like 30...which I can understand because they seemed short staffed. We finally got seated and the server didn't appear until about 10 mins later. Again I can understand because it seemed like one server was doing the whole dining room. She was running and rocking it. 
We put in our order for apps and rolls. The apps came out at a good rate but the rolls and nigiri took a long time. Apps were good   They were run of the mill but we were hungry and so it was perfect.  The rolls we got were the mountain, truckee, washoe, sweetheart, and one that was kinda forgettable. The washoe and mountain rolls were great the others left something to be desired. 
It was hard to track down the server (again not her fault she was busting ass trying to help everyone). We were brought things that we didn't order and didn't eat to the table which was unfortunate.  It seemed like they were understaffed and overrun with people.  I didn't mind waiting at all for the food it was that some of it was not up to par while other rolls were awesome.  It was a mix between good and bad. 
Despite being overwhelmed with people our server did a great job (I regret that I didn't catch her name). The food was hit or miss. It seemed like if you knew the right rolls to order it would be awesome. If not you were out of luck.   I would definitely get their washoe and mountain roll again for sure. Overall I want to come back but am unsure.

Love the option of stopping when we wanted. However, couple of the guides were rude, and several stops closed. Lafayette cemetary scheduled to close at 4p on Sat, though the guides said 3p to us....gate was literally locked in front of us at 3:35pm. Not impressed after 20+ min walk to find it due to stop being closed!

This is more of a 3.5.
  
Stopped in here for some nourishment after trekking around Olde City and doing all the toursty stuff in this area of Philly.  I basically came to this restaurant because I was STARVING and needed something to eat fast.  

I ordered a slice of their brick oven pizza.  It was some sort of chicken ranch concoction.  It was a huge slice with a generous helping of chicken and ranch sauce drizzled on top.  I was so hungry I had it gone within a few minutes.  It was a pretty tasty slice, but nothing mind blowing or life changing about it.  Pretty much your basic slice of pizza.  

The service was incredibly fast, very friendly and efficient.  If you are in need of some fast sustenance but hate to eat at the fast food chains I'd say pop into SOHO.  It did the trick for me.

Cantina las Tres Hombres has stepped up their game. 

The server was much more attentive this go around--and was charming, to boot. 

The food was better and fresher. 

This was a very enjoyable visit.

This is one of the worst company I ever deal with. Their contractor doesn't seems like they knows what he is doing. I am having a problem with my toilet clogged for more than a weeks and I had called and talk with them for more than 5 times and still having a issue. stay away  with this company because they will rip you off. They won't fix ur problems right away. They have emergency in home plumbing but they won't treat anything emergency. I wish I can give them zero star

I'm so over business's that don't update their hours. I checked google, their website and yelp. They all confirmed the hours. Sure enough get here and closed. I understand there's a virus but it takes 1 min to update hours that will save a customer from an hour of wasted drive time.
Have been here a few times and quite like the food ! Love the sausage and they have great salads. The wait can sometimes be a bit long but worth it.
Extra slow service, soggy doughnuts, cold blonde brew, dirty bathrooms and dirty tables. Will not come back. Also, the Internet would not connect.

Don't go unless you've got 3 hours to wait!

Told 45 minutes when we ordered at 6 pm.  An hour and a half later -- "its coming out now."  9 pm and now plating our food.  Unless you want your evening ruined by slow and unprofessional service, skip this one.  And to add insult to injury, over priced with 30% -50% increase in prices compared to online menu.  Skip! Skip! Skip!

Despite the only okay reviews this place has mostly gotten on here, I was still really impressed with it. The atmosphere was really cool, and the food was great! The shrimp quesadilla was fabulous, as was the tuna risotto. And I would highly recommend the guava iced tea (not on the menu, but can still be made). It wasn't too busy when we arrived, but picked up while we ate. The staff were nice and attentive, as well. The menu layout looked great, but the organization was a little off; a lot of the appetizers were mixed in with the regular meals, since the idea of the menu was to share the dishes around the table. A good idea, but probably should be indicated ON the menu to clarify things a bit.

Ok I've been visiting Luxe nails recently and the first two visits were awesome. They even provided me with a discount. The last two experiences I had have been pretty shabby. I went on a Thursday and was seated almost immediately. I told them I'd like dip powder. The woman that started my nails seemed very apprehensive but she continued. Over the course of the appointment the tech kept having to restart and sand down the coats she was putting on my nails. She finally finished after over an hour and asked me to inspect them and I was very unhappy. They were very splotchy and one didn't even have the shiny top coat over it. I told her my concerns and her manager came and asked me if he could redo them and I told him I didn't have time. They told me to come back and I did on Saturday. On Saturday I told them what happened and the manager/owner said he would comp them on Thursday but that wasn't relayed for the Saturday staff. Rosie did my nails and she was very proficient in the dipping powder. After my nails were finished I went to the counter to pay and they had a check in offer but when I pulled it out to pay they stated that they weren't accepting them...
I was really trying to give them a chance but I now seem pretty turned off to them. Hope you all have a better experience!

Without doubt, this is the most poorly managed Pep Boys in the franchise.  Because they're somewhat close and can accommodate weekend appointments, they're my go-to for the regular oil change. 
I went online & grabbed a Sunday morning 9AM slot. I arrived at 8:45.  Someone opened the door promptly at 9, then informed us that no one had arrived. We had to wait half an hour for staff to arrive and another 15 before taking keys. 
POOR SERVICE!
Labor Day Weekend Update
Again, I arrived on time for a 9AM oil change. It's nearly 10:30 and my car is still in the parking lot untouched. The service attendants seem to not like their jobs which is clearly a First World problem.  They are fortunate to have decent jobs!!  I would give no stars but not an option. 
I'll stick to the Glenolden location from here on out.

This place is great and compared to some spas out there is not way over the top in price.  Not cheap - but not ridiculous.  They do hair, nails, and there is a spa where they do the massages and facials and peels and all that good stuff.  Once or so a year they have a gathering where they have appetizers and drinks while they show you whats new there.  But any time you go for services, they will offer you something to drink which can range from water to lemonade to wine.  You try a glass of wine while having your tootsies soak in a nice warm bubbly bath and see how relaxed you get!

I recommend my girl Tori for hair or nails.  She did my hair once so good I looked at it in the mirror and wondered where did it come from? Whose hair was on my head?  Soft, silky, shiny!  I wanted to do one of those slow motion hair toss commercial routines it looked so amazing.  And they have all the products there to sell you to keep your hair that way.

Another great small town breakfast and lunch joint, very similar to the Frenchtown Café just up the river.  A great place to finish after a bike ride along the canal path too!  A variety of delicious breakfast options and specials complimented by decent pricing.  I was there on a Saturday morning and we hardly had to wait either, which was nice.

The Owner is just as rude & crazy as the manager.  He refuses to listen to employee's & customer complaints.  I'd stay FAR away from this place.  Even the Spring Hill owner said people call his store complaining about the manager at Little Road.  Other employees I spoke with said she's awful to work for and people complain about her all of the time.  They fired a waitress for not being able to come in on her OFF day due to the flooded roads.  She WASN'T scheduled that day.  I'm calling the news stations,BBB & the Labor Board since the owner won't listen either.  I hope people hear this story and boycott your business!  You would've saved yourself grief if you would've just listened Steve!  Already have posted to FB and many friends said they will no longer eat there.

I have been going to this salon for over 20 years the spa has always been relaxing.  The front desk has an ego problem.  My last apt was confirmed through text message and I replied to cancel and I guess it was never received and they charged my the full price of a haircut when the policy states only half of the appointment.  If you go here they require your card info to book an apt and will charge 100% of your apt. The policy states they will charge 50% but they charged 100%

This place was a bit of a blur because it was the last stop of the night and everyone in my group wanted to stop here to try the charbroiled oysters. We'd been sampling various restaurants all evening and this was the last stop. I'm not able to eat the charbroiled oysters but everyone seemed to either love it (if they were on my side of the long table) or complained about it (those on the other end). They ended up giving us a new plate of them because the ones on the other end of the table were somehow cold by the time they reached us.

I didn't eat any of these because I'm allergic to oysters.

I did however eat the the BBQ Shrimp which was definitely not great, especially when compared to the ones we ate earlier at Mister B's. There were other things on the table which I am not able to recall now, but that usually is because it wasn't good enough either visually or it wasn't memorable for me to remember.

All I did remember was that the restaurant reminded me of a cheap Vegas buffet venue (it isn't a buffet by any means) -- huge space, no decor, bad service.
Such amazing food ! When they say spicy they mean spicy!! We love the cauliflower appetizer so delish!

We have now ordered here a handful of times. The food is good but most of the time it takes over an hour for delivery. I'm still waiting for the order I placed at 5:55. It's now 7:30 and still no food. We called and they just said he was on his way. We have now decided we will never order here again.

Pretty sure my friends and I visited this place every night during our stay in NOLA. Just off the beaten path and more reasonably priced than places on Bourbon Street. Bartenders and patrons are all very friendly. I recommend the jello shots. They're fun to do with friends!
Awesome dog grooming services.    They had a reservation with short notice and their cost was reasonable.

This is re: the location in concourse A at the airport. I had the fish and chips, and found them to be good. Service was good... staff was pleasant and attentive. Unlike the experience of other reviewers my silverware was metal, not plastic. I'm wondering if the plastic was used at some point due to TSA regulations considering this IS behind the security checkpoint at the concourse. Anyway, I found it to be decent for airport food.

I LOVE a great food hall, and this was one. So many vendors and some much to choose from. I can't get enough of this place. There is a very large amishcommunity with fresh goods. I suggest you walk the whole things before deciding what to eat.

I hit this place up out of necessity. When I needed an emergency roll of x-mas wrapping paper, I walked 2 blocks and 5 minutes later home and back to wrapping. The lady at the register was in a pleasant mood, told a funny story and wished us a Merry Christmas. While standing in the checkout line I discovered an amazing thing. Breyer's ice cream in the freezer!  Screw you Buds Supermarket!

I ordered this service for my boyfriend, thinking it would be an amazing gift/surprise each month!  It was not all what we anticipated.  The deliveries were a disaster.  They were always  later than we expected and I often times spent way too much time on the phone with customer service reps trying to track my package down.  The main issue was the fact that they only delivered during the week between 8-5 which is totally ridiculous as my boyfriend and I both work full time (and someone needs to be there to sign for the package).  When asked if they could adjust this, their answer was 'no sorry'.  I then requested Friday deliveries as I sometimes work from home on Fridays.  I adjusted my schedule and the deliveries STILL never came on a Friday.  Oftentimes, the package appeared in my lobby (funny since it was supposed to be signed for??)
