Again, after spending time on the phone with customer service, i finally requested that they send me emails when the delivery was being shipped (surprise, surprise) still haven't received any emails of shipping - they suggested I go online and check my account for a status update- terrible, terrible, terrible answer.  During the last month of our service, we moved and so I went into my account and changed my address.  My boyfriend also called to ensure he spoke with someone that could verify the address was changed.  I logged on to my account (still no emails) and saw the shipment was in transit ... to the OLD ADDRESS.  Once again, I had to talk to customer service. They have an awful process and they need to find a new shipping vendor because it is disgraceful. We did enjoy the beers but we are MORE than HAPPY that we don't have to deal with this anymore as we will be receiving our last (very last) shipment this month and have no plans to use them again.

Lunch today.
Nice spot.
Decided on their basic pasta option. Had shells with meat sauce, and a bottle of water. ~$12. 
Pretty good value. 
Small seating area.
Modest decor.
I must admit. Pasta wasn't stellar. But it was good. After reading other revies, I look forward to returning and sampling both their sandwiches, and their pizza.

Had a dinner with my wife and a friend recently. It was a Sunday night and there was about a 15 minute wait.  Wine came pretty quick.  My wife ordered the mango cashew encrusted mahi-mahi and my Buddy and I ordered the fried fish assortment of shrimp, scallops, crabcake and a fish filet.  All of these were very tasty.  Their salad dressing is a cross between ranch and Caesar. Enjoyed it on my salad and on my fries. Overall a good experience.
Definitely a great restaurant to try in the French Quarter. I enjoyed the crawfish etouffee.

Cute pet store with a nice variety of natural pet foods. My cats' wet food is 10 cents less a can here compared to Petco. Happy to have just moved within walking distance, will definitely be my new place for pet supplies.

For a Monday lunch break from work I was very disappointed in how long our food took to be served. There were hardly any people in the restaurant. We all three ordered soups and salads. This was not complex. The lunch prices however were great. I had a Cesar salad and tomato basil soup combo for only $7.95. And they were both very nicely flavored. Maybe they have better service in the evening??

This place is so great. Tried it a few weeks ago and it's quickly becoming our go to spot.  The pizzas are more on the gourmet side, which is something we were lacking in Donelson.  

The Pepperoni and pepperchini pizza is usually our go to, but today when we stopped in their special was Mexican Pizza.  That was something special. My hometown and a great Taco Pizza, but this was better.  I hope it makes the regular menu.

Good place if you plan on getting a degree from there. If you want to transfer or just get gen eds here I would not recommend it. Everything is student run and not very informed. My 4 year degree will take 6 minimum now and I will have to pay for 40 credit hours that they said I needed that I didn't. Oh and according to financial aid there is no such thing as a student loan. To get a student loan you need to go to your personal bank and take out a personal loan. There words not mine.

It's not my favorite park, but it's close to home...so it works when I'm in a pinch for time. A very kid and pet friendly place. There's a huge playground, volley ball net, bathrooms, swimming pool (that is open during certain hours, 8 bucks for the day), lots of picnic tables, as well as grilling capabilities. I often see a lot of birthday parties happening when I come here for a walk. The paved path area isn't very extensive, but if you go in the wooded area, that could keep you occupied for a little while. It's a pretty clean place.

Just what this neighborhood need. Laid back with reasonable prices. Great place to have a casual dinner. Great Vietnamese food not as authentic as some other place but still delicious. (I think their fish sauce is a little too watered down)

New location for this chain pizzeria, located next to Bisbee Breakfast Club west of St. Joe's Carondelete Hospital.  Medium size dining room with big picture windows that let in a lot of natural light.  Counter service setup although they do bring your food out to you.  A few big screen T.V.s.  Beer and wine available.  We tried the mozzarella sticks for an appetizer, They quickly arrived piping hot, served with a side of marinara sauce, which was a little sweeter than I like a marinara to be, but overall not bad.  We had a small medium crust onion, mushroom and extra cheese pizza along with the cheese ravioli for our entrees.  The raviolis were plumb and cooked slightly al dente.  The pizza crust was firm but yet nicely chewy.  The mushrooms appeared to be fresh instead of canned, and the sauce had a nice flavor to it.  We saw several families with small children there, so if your looking for a place for dinner with your family this place fits the bill

I suffer from a common disease known as Oenophilia. Although there is no cure that I'd care to know about, I can take comfort in the fact that Moore Brothers provides me with the necessary medications. 

They have Malbec for the gritty, earthy days, Port for the hard nights, Nebbiolo for the Italian BYO excursions, Beaujolais for the warm, summery getaways, and everything in between. There's something for every flare up that comes along with this ailment.

The only problem with Moore Brothers is that they turn over their wines quickly. You may purchase a bottle here that you love but it might be gone for good by the next time you return.

I expected a better breakfast. They ran out of cream for coffee and served the egg sandwich on an english muffin that wasn't toasted. Yes, they said we can do it over, but shouldn't they already know how to make an egg sandwich???

It's a nice place especially if you like to take walks or get some exercise jogging/walking. 

One issue is that people don't clean up after their pets and the employees are absent or oblivious to things around them.

Nice overall but could be better.

Cheap cheap dry cleaners. Have been coming here thru their multiple name changes and price increases.  At the end of the day its only 2.50 to dry clean. Never had an issue so I  will continue to patronize!

Elderly couple (owners of the shop, I think) were pleasant and greeted me immediately when I entered.  Watch battery replacement for my Relic watch (relatively inexpensive dress watch with screw type back) was $15, done as I waited in the store, took only about 5-10 min.  Neat to see all the cuckoo and grandfather clocks.  Would definitely come back here in the future for watch batteries.  Plus, the Kolache Factory is across the street.

Caught up with this truck today at Pierce St. Market with my mom. Out of all the places there we decided to try the street surfer truck. I ordered the bowl with chicken and my mom ordered the pork bowl. The food came out very quickly and we were excited to try it. This was the most bland, tasteless meal I have ever eaten in my life. Everything tasted like it was previously frozen then thrown in water to thaw and served in the bowl. We took about two bites then bagged our food up and ate somewhere else.
The worst McDonald's yet it was like I was eating somewhere else they over charge for the coffe they wasted 2 cups of coffe and all I wanted was black coffe the place was filthy

Significant progress since first visits 2 and a half years ago. Well made beers and friendly staff. Only ate here once - Montreal sandwich and bar pretzel, and they were good. Their hazy beers are outstanding.

Girls working need to learn how to make coffee drinks. Paid $4.25 for a latte with OVER HALF FOAM. Felt light and looked in my cup, sure enough less than half actual coffee... This was after I had to go up and remind them to put in my drink order as it was not being made... Super disappointed and waste of money, especially with intelligentsia coffee. Would have showed them and asked for a replacement but had to rush off to be somewhere and didn't realize until I was already leaving.

The John stays cold. They pour strong. It smells like a bathroom (the stuff you use to clean it). They have ping-pong and an arcade machine. They've got a jukebox and slots as well. Great spot. Great crowd.

Closes at 9:00. We (party of 2) show up at 8:10. The hostess says they are no longer seating people on the patio. We ask if we can sit in the main dining area with the chairs that have backs. We are told no, actually the only place we can sit is the bar. Then she informs us that they are closing in 15 minutes. What?! 

1 star for a fairly decent beer & wine list.

My wife and I ate here this morning for likely the last time. In fairness, our previous experiences, 3 for the two of us, and an additional time for me left much to be desired, and this time showed improvement especially in the preparation of the food. I ordered steak and eggs. Medium rare on the meat. Poached eggs. Both were cooked correctly. My wife had two eggs over easy with bacon and a biscuit which arrived barely warm. My poached eggs were inadequately drained creating a very watery plate when I placed them onto the steak.

We received the food without charge because the owner/manager sent us gift certificates and begged us to come back. 

The food was better. The service,  though better, remained weak. So, when you look at the crowds of people who might still be waiting in the parking lot and wondering if it's worth the wait, I'd say no.

French restaurant before the beach at Fort Desoto. If you come at Sunday Brunch, please make reservations, they are fully booked most of the time. I have been here twice. I think I can give them a fair review. Their restaurant is at exclusive area and need toll if you from I-275. 

My friend and me visited them during job assignment near the beach. We got daily special Hachis Parmentier. This is a special dish in French cook with ground beef and potato. Parmentier is French know as potatoes man. He promoted the potatoes in the local. This dish also very similar to shepherd pie in England. 

The second dish La Vosgienne. This is a French crepe wrap with Prosciutto ham and top with sunny side up egg. It is brunch item. It quite good. I like it to be savory. I like this dish the most. The last dish is the Sandwich Parisen. This just a assembly item. No creativity. That is why they 4 stars. The bread is tough to bite.

We love sushi and eat it often. Saw good reviews and over 4 stars and thought we would give it a try. Ended up there during happy hour which meant sushi was half price. No biggie and potentially awesome because we've had great luck in the past at other places. Ultimately, we found it rather disappointing. The fish was room temperature, everything was mostly rice, there was barely any shrimp tempura in the shrimp tempura roll, and they were under-staffed which made service relatively slow. We probably won't go back.

We went with a big group and reserved a big table.  We were seated promptly. I ordered the Taste Of The Bayou with four items. They brought me a plate with four small cups with each item in them. The etouffee sauce was bland and lacked meat as was the jambalaya with dried out rice.  The red beans and rice was ok and the gumbo was average.  I was still hungry and out $21. We also tried the grilled oysters and they were actually good.  Tried a bite of the bread pudding and it was the highlight of the meal. The service was good, too, But this place is located at the beginning of Bourbon street so I guess you could call it a tourist trap.  Much better food elsewhere.

Everything here is pretty nice. I went here because the prices were really good and its a new spot in town. I ordered a few rolls and they were rolled very well, but not the best tasting. This isn't a bad three stars just that it was alright. I would recommend. The appetizers the offer were delicious.

I was an out-of-towner that had KKBQ here. It was a busy night. Food was average.

30 minute seating wait and an even longer wait for our meat.

Worst service ever. Period.

Nothing really special here. Not bad but not great. Maybe I was expecting too much considering the 183 reviews with a 4 1/2 star total. If they dropped the first word in the name it would be pretty accurate description of what to expect.

May 18th, Sunday, Tony and I decided to Urban Hike today again in Nashville. So, after we made a couple stops and we ended up here...Loser's Bar and Grill on the West Side off Broadway near Vanderbilt University.

It is a Sunday and about 11am..so its not really crowded...
In fact, we were the first people there. Our bartender, Peyton, introduced herself. She is really lovely and so friendly. 

Tony ordered a PBR and I got a soda ...only $3 for both with taxes...great bargain!!! We sat at the very nice wooden bar that is shiny gloss poly covering..the bar is really nice. The bar is a little dark but I really  like that.
The music was 80's rock, which we loved so very much..we are a fan of that venue of music.  

This place is larger outside than inside. It has a large patio area that is enclosed but the bar inside is much smaller, but that is okay. The outside is really nice area. I would definitely come here again. Peyton never offered us any food so, I guess they don't cook here, but again OKAY with me..if I came here it would be to drink only anyway and enjoy some music.

I really liked this spot and I would go here again..The prices were right, staff friendly and she is in a band. We asked if she was playing anywhere currently and she said she wasn't ...but she is such a beautiful lady with a great personality and speaking voice. She sings Rock so Tony and I would definitely like to hear her perform..We made a new friend today,...thanks Peyton for making us feel like we were back home...

We both highly recommend this little bar to enjoy yourself alone or with some friends.

Nice little venue.  A little south of the busiest parts of downtown.  Really nice, gentle, lighting, but still wonderfully colorful. Some surrealist wall decorations add some character.  The bartender was a little inattentive.  It wasn't all that busy, but it took some time for him to see me and ask if I wanted something.

On Wednesday when I was there I saw a great jazz band.  They really went off and did some interesting things, quite a variety in tone, pace and style.

The wall art is pretty impressive right now.  There's this giant painting of a moundy Red Rock behind the bar.  It has all of these diagrams that are like Da Vinci blueprints or architectural diagrams to make some strange, hypothetical, artificial mountain.  

Just for fun, to get to the bathroom, you have to sneak past the band, trying carefully not to bump them or knock anything over.

The lighting was also very pleasant, a bit dim, but it highlights the wall art well along with the bright, tasteful colors of the bar itself.

The drink selection was interesting.  Some unusual flavored vodkas that I haven't ever seen along with some other eccentric top shelf type things to go along with old favorites and a typical range of beers, Pabst to Guiness and Sierra.

The space is small, kind of one narrow, but deep room with a long bar on the left side, but if feels about right.  When there's no music performance they have pool as well.

I wasn't a huge fan of the bartender/presumed owner, but the place had so many other charms and good ambience that I still really enjoyed the place.
Very quick and great customer service! I'm not a big chocolate shake fan but theirs are great!!! Great food!

The best thing about this place is the location. Lots of outdoor seating and the doors on the side open up so you don't feel cut off from the rest of the bar. The beer and wine selection is decent, if a little pricey. (Still cheaper than a lot of other places in the area.) I haven't eaten much there, but what I've had wasn't that exciting. It seemed like they were trying to dress up basic bar food by just making it richer. The crab fries were good, but super creamy and the mix of cheeses they used in it made it really salty.

I love this place so hard! First and foremost, their pizza is delicious (hand tossed, cooked in a wood burning stove).  This is a b-day dinner hot spot for my friends and I.  I've been here several times and have never had a negative vibe about anything.  Other great things: the staff is always very nice/helpful/funny, the atmosphere is great (inside or out on the patio), the bathroom is clean, the prices are good, and they have local craft brews available.  

I suggest checking their facebook or calling before you go because it is a small family-owned-operated business and they close down time to time for family stuff.

Best Nail Salon in Tampa! 
It were pretty busy to be a Wednesday, which means they're good! I didn't feel like she was rushing, she took her time with me they give wonderful massages I got a Deluxe Pedi and it's amazing! I love the fact that the person who did my pedi also did my shellac mani! I felt pampered!

This place is not Tex Mex.  With the bright lights and furniture it has all the ambiance of a Dennys.  The food was not hot or fresh when served.  The chips seem like they came out of a bag and the salsa out of a jar.  The service is not very attentive as the dishes and glasses just pile up.

I dropped my sorel boots off on a Monday morning. The guy at the desk said if Thursday for pick up would be okay and I said that wouldn't work because I would be working. I told him I could pick them up on the following Monday and we agreed on that. The next fault was my own. I went in on the following Monday morning and my boots were still not ready. I didn't read my ticket that said after 3pm pickup. That's on me but the older man got an attitude with me.  It wasn't what he said but how he said it. It's an independent, local business and I went in there with a very kind attitude but this guy rubbed me the wrong way. I left in a huff, called him on my way out and apologized but I still didn't feel like the issue was taken care of. I picked up my boots and they looked great. I'm not one to complain over BS if the work done looks good. The following Tuesday, put them on to wear and there's no insole in my left boot. They were there before. Tried calling several times to no avail.

This place use to be super gross years ago.. but now they have a cleaning system and you can really tell things are cleaner.  My only complaint is, I wish the employee would do a better job monitoring the 3 year old and under play areas.  Too many times my 1.5 year old would be playing and next thing you know a 5 year old comes bulldozing in knocking her over, ruining her fun.

I've visited this restaurant many times and I really like the food, today I didn't even get seated before deciding to leave. Upon entering we were greeted by the hostess and asked where we wanted to sit. I replied; "at a table please". The restaurant was mostly empty 2 out of 3, four tops were occupied, 1 of the 4 six tops had 3 people at it. My party had three people. The hostess replies, "well I'll have to clean off the table then". She proceeded to make us wait for the one dirty 4 top squeezed between the other two. Why didn't my 300lb ass get seated at a 6 top? Why prioritize customers you don't have over customers who are ready to dine? Before she had cleared the 4 top i excused myself to the restroom.  When I came out, the other members of my party were seated at the four top squeezed between the others. The space left was not adequate for me to sit and eat comfortably, so I didn't. If you are going to have your hostess ask people where they want to sit, she should not act inconvenienced when she gets an answer.
Visiting from NY and LOVED our takeout meal. Seafood in a basket, Duck, egg rolls ALL of it was superb. Wished we lived closer to from here more often. Said 45 minute wait and it was more like 20.
I had the beef and lamb gyro. I don't even eat lamb, but this was good. Not only that, they were quick. And since it was the lunchtime rush, that is an important quality!

Popped in because, well, I got a little lost and I was STARVING. Didn't get my mojito (yes I wanted one) because they had just used up all the mint. Went with my standard instead, good ole gin and tonic. Opted for the bowl of gumbo and I loved finding the crab claws within it. Entertainment consisted me watching the interactions between the crew and a dance outbreak in the limited floor space. Fun stumble-upon

Paris there was very helpful in pricing the road base for our driveway and when I told him the money I had to work with he helped me get the amount for what I had. Delivery was the very next day and I am very glad. Thank you Paris!

Warrior piercing is the best place to get your piercings done! I have gotten 3/6 of my piercings there and I absolutely would get more! All of the employees that have been there when I was are super great, quirky, calming, and very nice! Also warriors is very professional and clean. Love, love, love!

I wanted everything on the menu! But ended up with the BBQ seitan, which was really tasty and it was a generous portion.  The best part wad the coconut collards.  Ever since I've given up meat,  it had been hard to find meat free collards and they were delicious.  I wanted to order some to take home,  but since I already ordered enough food for 3 people, I decided not to.   It was amazing and delicious and we can't wait to go back.
Trivia Tuesdays along with all you can eat wings (bones in) for $14.99. Monday's is unlimited boneless wings special. Nice clean restaurant with great wings and great service. There's a bar also.

As we were walking with locals and talking about how much we loved Erin Rose and their frozen coffee, we were enlightened to the fact that this place was the original iced coffee in the area. The frozen coffee from Erin Rose was probably my favorite drink over the weekend, so I had to see how this one compared. 

The staff here was definitely not as friendly as Erin Rose. It was the first thing I noticed. You weren't warmly greeted inside here as with the competition. The frozen coffee itself was very different as well. This one was definitely more creamy and had the feel of being more of a milkshake consistency. It was good, but definitely a drink you couldn't have multiple of. We all felt like it was more of a dessert than one you could walk around and enjoy regularly. Almost too filling. I'm glad I got to try the original frozen Irish coffee, but I'll definitely be going back to Erin Rose for them. 

If you're looking for a dessert drink option, try theirs here. Although the ambiance and friendliness of Erin Rose will have you going there instead,
Awesome burgers. Huge, juicy, quality beef. Fries are DELICIOUS. Solid waitstaff. Great beer choices. Music can be loud at times, avoid if you aren't into that. Overall great choice.

Overrated and over priced for what you get. Meat is good quality but if you have expectations of the best you will be disappointed. To be fair, you may have a better experience if you shop in store and see what you are buying before paying for it. I placed my order online. The product pictures on their website are far from the product I received. Again, the meat is good quality and certainly better than what you buy in the average supermarket. But almost any butcher shop or specialty market will have similar quality and selection at a much better price.

Needed someone else to make my dinner instead of boiling up something at my dark and cold campsite.  I couldn't have done better than  this restaurant.  The carne asada quesadilla was great.
It seems that Mexican coffee gringo style, tequila, Kahlua and coffee with whipped cream is not well known in the Tucson Southwest. I taught the very cooperative and sweet server/bartender Claudia, to make me one.  Her first was a great success.
Am happy with this impulsive decision made from the parking lot.

We enjoy the pig.  We almost love the pig.

If I'm craving a good ol' burger - then I love the pig!

You head down a staircase to a dark little landing and think to yourself, "What if I drink too much and fall down?" - but I never have.  I often wonder how in this day and age a restaurant could be so handicap-inaccessible, but I guess they've always been there.  I can remember eating at The Pig in 1991 for Shepherd's Pie with a bunch of hot air balloon pilots.  That's before I had tried the burger.

You feel like you're in an English pub, but they also have four TVs hung around the main dining room so if you're in the mood to watch the Olympics on a Saturday afternoon... you're in luck.  It's not huge, and by the time we left every booth and table except for one high top was full at like 1 pm on a hot Saturday.  A few insane people were eating at the patio tables up top - OK, I'm sorry.  It was hot.  If I was a server there, I'd have to just tell me no, can't do that.

A few times we have been here when there was live music, and sometimes it seemed too loud and too close, others it was fine. 

I can't speak to the full menu, and we don't play pool, but if you want a great bacon cheeseburger for $8.95 any day of the week, head on down.  You won't be disappointed.

Take this one with a grain of salt because I am a tea person. Sump has some tea options but I just did not find them memorable. I thought menu items were a little overpriced.

That said, my S.O. who likes coffee LOVES Sump and says they are the second best coffee in STL. He'll write his own review.

Do not move here! I've lived here for 5 years, the new management are extortionist!!! The amenities previously included in the rent are now additional costs. The club house, they turned into their office, the pool is now $50, there is no lounge area, the playground is only for children under 80lbs,the computers in the business center are most of the time broken, the only thing free is the gym. The gym is in poor condition and the machines are never serviced. My daughter and I lived in a 3 bedroom unit. We could never shower at the same time because someone wasn't getting hot water. I asked maintenance to fix the hot water pressure due to the "trickle" I was getting compared to the full force of cold water. Their remedy, change the facet to 1 handle! I had to let the water run for 3 mins before getting hot water! My water bill was over $50 every month! They adjusted the water heater so I'd get more cold than hot. They pay a vendor to bill tenants for water, sewage and trash removal. That bill is sent separately however it's included with the rent. If you're late paying the bill, they charge 10% of your rent as a late fee - that charge exceeds the actual bill! YOU WILL GO BROKE TRYING TO PAY FOR ALL THE CHARGES! Not worth it!!

Both stars for this rating are for the wings (THEY were great), and the very FULL glass of house wine that was only $4!!!  As for the rest of the meal, unfortunately it deserved no more stars.  Had the garlic rolls, not that buttery garlic taste they could or should be.  Had 2 veal dishes - Saltimbocca and Sorrentino, veal was ok but not great.  For the Sorrentino, the highlight was the prosciutto and the marinara.  For the Saltimbocca, the highlight was the was the spinach and the prosciutto.  They were large portions as reviews have stated; the side dish of pasta was unnecessary and wasted.  Caesar salad had way too much dressing to be enjoyable and ALL of the croutons were stale.  The service left a LOT to be desired... Waitress said she was new, but that does not explain why we received our salads way before the wings we ordered much earlier.  Nor did it explain why she took our refill of tea to another table or didn't remove the dirty plates after multiple visits to the table.  If we were ever to return, we might try the pizza since that seemed to be a lot of the take out.

I went to this dog park three times over the weekend and all three visits were great. Sure, the neighborhood isn't the nicest, but the park itself is nice. The park is big. I like the agility stuff they have, though my dog was definitely not interested in it. I've had some negative experiences with other dog parks in the past, mostly with dogs being aggressive, but my three visits here were good. The owners were all attentive and paying attention to what their dogs were doing. I didn't hear a single growl. All the dogs seemed to get along. The park is big enough that you can play fetch with your dog while the other dogs play elsewhere. So far I really love this park and look forward to future visits.

Not really familiar with the Reno area. Decided we need some water and snacks in the hotel room and yelp found us this Safeway, It's your basic Safeway, it's relatively quiet in there with short lines. (Maybe I beat the dinner-time rush)

I've had Ron Entwistle inspect 2 houses I looked at buying in St. Louis area over the last couple of years. I've bought 10 houses in 12 years and of all the inspectors I've hired, Ron is the most accommodating but above all, the most thorough and detailed. Some of the clowns I've hired in the past have missed obvious signs of woodrot, termite infestation, roof age, structural defects in the attic and doors not opening all the way nor even fitting into the door jam while closing. Therefore not closing. Heck the last guy, in Texas, missed that there were 2 windows that had been sheet rocked over!

Ron is awesome. Do yourself a favor though, be present when he does the inspection because the words used face to face, are so different to what you read in the report. Probably for legal reasons. In St. Louis, I refuse to use anyone but Ron.

Fun and stylish speakeasy with a proper backdoor entry. The staff were dapperly dressed and ready with a humorous comment and a nicely crafted cocktail. 
When we first entered, the bartender was showing a magic trick to the guests already seated at the bar. We took our seats in one of the booths which was roomy and comfortable and had a privacy curtain (we referred to it as a snug).
The gentleman behind the counter made a nice drink and was quite entertaining and witty throughout our time there. The decor and ambience had a nice prohibition feel with bookcases with classic books, a gramophone, and other nice little touches. 
We stayed a bit longer and a jazz band took the stage. They sounded great and the whole visit and ambience was really enjoyable. I would go back for the music, ambience, and cocktails, no doubt!

Just returned from dinner with 6 of my CWB friends and some family from NY. 
Hands down the Worst meal I've had since I moved here 7 years ago. 
I don't know what happened to this place, I haven't been in about a year, but the food, quality and preparation was awful....across the board on everyone's dinner. 
Very very disappointed!!!!

There were tons of people here by the time we got here since it was Father's Day and brunch. We opted for first available seating whether it was outside or inside. I think the hostess has issues or should be replaced. We stood there for a good 25 minutes and tHere were 3 tables that were empty for party of 2 yet she just stood there. It wasn't until a little later she said party of 2 that wouldn't mind outside. Uh... So what was the point of asking seating preference in the beginning? Look at your list! We went up and she seated us at one of those tables. I looked at her list and I saw that there were at least 2 parties of 2 that did not have a preference where to sit. Honestly... She doesn't have a hard job. It's not like she was the waitress or she cleaned tables. 

The waitress was great and came by whenever our water was low in the heat. The food however wasn't great. The hollandaise sauce was sub par. It wasn't thick enough in consistency and you can see chunks of cooked egg yolk. Epic fail.

WORST AIRLINE EVER! If you value your time and money, buy somewhere else!!! They sent our luggage (as well as several others') to the wrong destination and had us waiting at the carousel for 2 HOURS (at midnight) to tell us! People missed the last trains in order to wait for luggage that was never going to arrive. The company's lack of communication cost us 2 hours and a $70 taxi ride! They had 70 customers standing around the luggage carousel booing at them! BUYER BEWARE!
I'm sorry to put a negative review here for a local business- I have to be honest though. I found the curry extremely sweet. It reminds me of walnut shrimp from Panda Express. Some sides are ok.

My husband attempted to speak Thai to the waitress and although she was speaking Thai to everyone else, she wouldn't speak to him, which pissed him off.  But about the food.  I usually get Me Gerang (or something like that) and it is very spicey and tasty, but after trying many other dishes here, that is the only one I really like.  The Pad Thai has a funny flavor to me and the shrimp dishes come with about 4 pieces of shrimp for a dish that costs 16 bucks.  I feel ripped off here. So that's that.

You're hungry. You're sweaty.  Your feet hurt.  You want something easy, filing and affordable, whilst still being touristy and on vacation.  We thought this would be a good idea, too.  Don't do it!  Bubba Gump Shrimp Co. is NOT this place.  

...You'll be much happier if you go anywhere else.  We sat upstairs while visiting the French Quarter on a sunny bustling Sunday and were not super satisfied. Service was pretty bad (appetizers arrived after the main course and the drink orders that made it in took a while), and food was unexciting (and of a small portion size).  Despite the 20+ minute wait many of the tables near us were empty (an indication that they're understaffed and based on our servers attitudes I understand why!). It cost too much for what it was, also.  

Stop in at the nearby peppersauce store and sample some chips to tide you over as you wander farther towards the French Market for something better.

We decided to eat at Tex Mex Connection the other night because we had heard such great things about it! However, I don't think we will be going back again. The food was pretty good but the service was horrible!!!! The waitress, marissa or monica i believe her name was, was so rude and ignorant. She talked to us like we were wasting her time and lwhile she was knowledgeable about the menu she was just plain rude. Not only was she rude but she didnt write our order down and screwed up what I had ordered. I would have said something but by that point i just wanted to eat and get out of there. We would give it another shot as long as we dont have to someone like that wait on us again! It was almost like she was above doing this if you dont like waitressing do something else!!!!

We have ordered a couple of times here, and it was delicious.  Tonight I called in to go "Taco Tuesday " 6 tacos loaded.  I reintegrated "Taco Tuesday " .My husband picks it up, get home for dinner and we find chips with stuff on it.  I called them to let them know, they offered free replacements.  We wanted dinner 6 tacos. I want to be fair, but they may need a person on the phone who understands English more

This place is just O.K.  It seems like it's very hyped up by a lot of the locals and I'm really not sure why.  Maybe they're like Apple and they pay people to go around town getting people excited to eat at Casa Tina.  The service is usually pretty slow.  There's always some dried up food that didn't get washed off stuck to one of our plates when it comes out, which we never complain about.  The food is very mediocre and kind of bland, and the prices are way too high.   The atmosphere is nice for a Mexican restaurant and the location is prime.  Those are really the only two things that I see keeping this place in business, but for some reason or another the place is always packed.   Last night was our third and last time eating here.  We're all about giving multiple chances in hopes that our experience will get better each time, but there are plenty of great Mexican restaurants within a short drives distance.
My burger was dripping in grease and it was burnt and tasted disgusting. And the food didn't taste very good. I would not recommend.
Yelp and Google both claim they have a retail store but it does not exist.  Wasted a morning chasing a ghost in downtown Boise.

I am a total beginner to yoga but decided to try Studio 34 because it is very convenient to where I live. In short, reasonable prices, great beginner classes, and super friendly staff and patrons. 

I use yoga as a way to relax, so I can not speak to it as a physical workout, but Studio 34 has so many options I'm sure you could find something you like. 

My favorite class is the Restorative class, super relaxing and I always leave feeling great. 

The Kundalini and the Hatha 101 were more based in yogic principles. Chakras and the like, which isn't necessarily my thing, but nice that it is available. 

If you are looking for a studio definitely check this out.

Drove 7 hours to be here for a friends 21st and the bar service was amazing while we waited for a table. And initially the service from our waiter was good, however throughout the night she was too busy being on some guys shoulders pouring shots into people's mouths to attend to us? But never came to her own table to give our birthday girl some attention :-( . Most of the food was delicious, although our short ribs came out extremely cold. To make the night even better, as she returned our checks to the table, our server (Lauren H I think it was) made sure to condescendingly wish our friend a happy 21st as she left. I'm not sure if she thought we were underage or what but I'm extremely disappointed in the service our waitress gave us.

Choosing All My Sons Moving and Storage was an absolute nightmare. Their quotes are not accurate, they are slow, unorganized, and they lie about everything dealing with your move. I'm hoping this review will help someone else. They may sound like the cheapest option, they will reassure you in the beginning, and keep you posted every step of the way...and then you walk into hell. We took off work for two days and then they called and said they wouldn't be making it and asked to push our move to the next day. I had to take another day off work and they still didn't show up on time--an hour and a half late! They said they would adjust the pricing and I sat there and argued with them for 15 minutes about it. I could go on and on about all the things we went through with this nightmare of a move because of this company but I'll spare you the details and just say, do yourself a favor and pick ANY other moving company.

First impression when my party walked in.....looked around and stood there for a good 2 to 3 minutes while a lady was around only a few feet from us doing something on the computer looked at us and went about continuing doing what she was doing .  She did not greet us or say hi or inform us if it was self serve or need to wait for someone to seat us .

A few more minutes went by then a waitress approaches us and asked us how many people in our party .

She sat us and left .  Did not even say hi or any kind of greeting .

We ordered ...No one came to us to ask if we needed anything else so we flagged down someone to ask for more sauce for the teriyaki chicken, but never recieved the sauce or our water or drinks refillled .

The waitress came over and assumed we were finished and proceeded to stack our bowls to take away .  I looked at her and she said" oh were you finished"?  With and her fingers in my bowl I said yeah now I am finished, wich really I was not , I just set it aside to to check my phone .

I would like to think you would ask someone if there are finished before you start taking there plates and bowls away .

When we asked for the check, wich we had split into two separate checks and we did inform the the lady in the beginning who took our order that it would be 2 seperate checks, the lady placed the tablet infront of the person who is going to pay for one of the check and proceed to just stand over him looming, proceeded to rush him by taking the tablet from him and proceed to say i'll print you out a receipt .  The gentlemn was older and was not as quik as to navigate the custom tip option as he was looking to do .

She then presented me with the tablet and did the same exact thing and when I went to input my email to have my receipt emailed to me she took the tablet while I was typing and she seemed upset and said quote " oh my god" that I took longer than she would like .  She just went storming off and gave me a pyshical recipet . 

By the way food was bland and pretty ordinary on top of the bad service or lack of .


There are plenty of of other places that do one thing well and not try to please a wide variety of taste and do it mediocre . 

Would not recommend this place .
The food was great and the service even better. What a beautiful place to start your day.

This place is amazing! There is such a homey vibe and our waitress, Randi, was great! I had the Eggs 9th and my fiancée had the berry banana and pecan French toast  we both had a side of hash browns. All of which were delicious! Glad we found this place

I took my son to see Dr Basler after our pediatrician recommended him get ear tubes. My son is 2.5 years old and has chronic ear infections for months. Our pediatrician said it was urgent for him to get ear tubes as he was in pain from the fluid in his ears. Dr Basler dismissed him twice. So we took him to All Children's instead where they actually cared for him immediately. I would not recommend Dr Basler for children.

Came here on a Saturday night and it was packed. Decor was nice, nothing extremely special. Lights were dimmed for a romantic ambiance. I'd order slightly more food than you think - the tapas portion here are smaller than the average tapa plates I've had. 

For three, we ordered:

Clams & chorizo - DELICIOUS SAUCE! Favorite dish of the night. But not enough clams :(

Octopus - also delicious. Served spicy with potatoes. 

Wagyu beef - cooked medium rare served on 3 skewers. Pretty good. 

Ham salad - spinach salad wrapped with LOTS of prosciutto with gorgonzola and figs. Must get!

Mussels - off the shell, served almost like a ceviche style, with slices of bread. The mussels were soo tender. Must get too!

Scallops -  meh. Pretty flavorless. 

All in all, food is delicious!
We went here for my uncles birthday, a party of 11 and they were able to accommodate all of us. Nice spot and nice atmosphere. No complaints on the food. Everything was wonderful.
I love everything about moons! The food, the shakes and atmosphere! The staff is always very friendly. The manager is a sweet guy. Highly recommend a visit when you are downtown.

Horrible experience. I've never wrote a review for anything before this. It was so bad I feel it necessary to warn others.  The rudeness of the staff was so bad my wife and I just got up and walked out before we could even order. Long short one server was yelling profanities at a customer as families ate. Worst host ever confused guest waiting for tables and snapped at us for not waiting in the invisible area we apparently were suppose to know to wait to be seated. Then worst host ever got mad at us for not seating ourselves when a table opened. Walked a few blocks went to cafe soule and loved it.
These people trying to do you hair with cheap stuff and after you had your hair done is all mess uppppp....

Having been to the Victor Cafe many times, always at the request of my wife, I have come to enjoy it. The food is always pretty good, the singing is incredible, but this recent visit was the last time they will see me in their restaurant. It's my wife's 50th birthday. I reserved(as she specifically requested her favorite place) and noted it was her birthday(they sing a birthday aria when requested). I followed up a few days later with a phone call reminding them it's her birthday can they please sing for her? In response I get an annoyed "we are already aware of this sir you wrote it on the reservation, we know". Dinner tonite, order dessert, and guess what, no song. Now this may not seem like a big deal to some, however, after the phone call and the fact I had a special gift to present her after the song, it ruined my good mood. The server said they would get someone over to talk to me. No less than 30 minutes later the hostess comes over gives a half hearted apology and says the desserts are on the house. Having been here numerous times I know if it's your birthday and you don't order a dessert they bring you out a cannoli. Tonite I ordered two cannoli to "set-up" the happy birthday. So I was being comped for one $6.00 cannoli? This was the biggest insult of all. Please do not try to throw a six dollars at me to make things right. I'd rather just an explanation and apology. Really awful. The whole experience. The indifference and laid back attitude about it only made it worse. Or perhaps it was the fact that the hostess sang happy birthday at no less than three other tables while I waited for her to come over and talk to us. Clueless and lacking priorities.

I was so sad when the east bay location closed and was initially hesitant about going to this location. I must say that I have enjoyed the Ulmerton location even more. The food seems to come out hotter and fresher and one of my favorite servers transferred from the other store. Although not authentic they know how to do tex mex. Love  the cilantro infused salsa as well as the hot salsa. I empty the contents on my chile rellenos and wash it down with the house Rita. I don't know what I would do without this place.

We were looking for a place for lunch and found this little gem. I had the grouper sandwich, it was excellent. My wife had the fish tacos, she thought they were just good. Service was great and attentive.  We will be back.

Totally not impressed. Food was mediocre and service was rushed.  Should have gone across the street to a fantastic restaurant but our friends wanted to try this one out since they heard it was great.  Sorry, noop!!

Typical Applebee's decor.  Service here is quicker than most.  Food is good.  However, Applebee's needs a menu makeover.  It seems like I always resign myself to the same thing when I go there.  Their menu doesn't excite me like Ruby Tuesday or Friday's.
The food was good. Time to make four pastas, two salads and the garlic bread was very quick. Glad we found this food establishment while here.

Women's selection has a lot of Old Navy and Gap. Prices are ok, nothing amazing. Shoe selections are a little dated. Lots of winter boots. 
Men's jackets and suits are well organized. Men's shirts are slim pickings; about one aisle at this time.

You know this is a pretty cool place and they work efficiently.  If the line gets long an employee comes out and takes orders from the line via remote tablet like at In and Out. 

I've had drinks here twice now, one was a black coffee and the other was an Americano. My reason for only two stars is that they serve their drinks kinda weak.  Make them stronger and it's a 5-star place.
Great food and awesome artwork. Last time I was here the construction sucked so it was hard to access the parking lot. My food came out great and seeing the ambience made me want to stay for a beer.

After waiting all day for a mattress delivery I called Macy's customer service at 4:00 (and was passed around amongst operators for almost an hour)  to find out where the delivery was, that was supposed to be delivered by 1:45 at the latest. The person on the helpline said that there was a voicemail left for me last night that notified me that the delivery would be delayed almost a week. That was a total lie!!!! The only voicemail I received last night was the one telling me delivery would be between 11:45 and 1:45 today. 
I asked to speak  to a supervisor who said "sorry we dropped the ball".
I cancelled the order and will buy a new materess from a dependable retailer

This is a fairly new restaurant. It is a great German restaurant, but a bit pricey. This was my first visit with my family. We enjoyed it very much. The whole staff was very friendly, the atmosphere was nice especially with the German music playing overhead. 
The food was very good and filled the plate. My wife had Pork Schnitzel (Fresh Canadian Pork Loin Cutlet. Hand Cut, Hand Tenderized, Hand Breaded. Sautéed Golden.) Her two sides were German Potato Salad, and House Salad. She liked everything, but she couldn't finish it all. I had Jagerschnitzel (Chef Ron's Sautéed Pork Cutlet with Mushroom White Wine Cream Sauce.) My two sides were German Potato Salad, and Sauerkraut. I liked and enjoyed everything. I wish I could've tried their German Chocolate Cake, but they didn't have it. Since they're still new they're working out some kinks. 
Our waitress, Tina, was excellent! She was very nice and on top of things. She makes a very good Cherry Coke, too!
Overall, we enjoyed this visit and will definitely go back again. Especially so I can try out their German Chocolate Cake.

I've had 2 out of 3 awesome experiences. I would highly recommend Andy. He was very thorough and kind. He saw and heard my ideas and made a masterpiece! I didn't feel rushed, but very relaxed. They reduced my price the third time because of my bad experience with Darlene the second. I took 6 of my bridesmaids with me this last time, and everyone had a great experience except one of the girls got Darlene (she didn't know I'd had a bad experience, but mentioned D really rushed her and had mistakes on her mani and pedi). Sadly, I wouldn't recommend D - sweet lady, not thorough and 2/2 not good experiences.
This place is one of the nicest settings for a nail salon!

Do you like the dentist? I didn't before I found Mesa Dental. After my first visit I decided I LOVE the dentist and it's all because of Mesa Dental. Let me explain why; from the moment I walked into the office I felt welcomed and valued by everyone I talked to. With large smiles I was greeted, swiftly checked in and asked politely to wait for my turn. Waiting is easy when the office is clean and the employees are kind. I have never waited more then a few minutes before I was seen, which is also a bonus. One of the only doctors appointments where I can say they are never late! My teeth are taken care of by one of the most fun, clear spoken, gentle and efficient dental hygienists named Secilia Spellman. She always sees myself and my wife back to back and makes great conversation all while taking care of our teeth. I can tell that the staff is taken care of and treated with respect all across the board and that's something important to help with a great work environment. Dr. Duke is also extremely kind, quick to answer questions and give a second look if needed. He is always on time and is smart enough to answer the questions I wasn't smart enough to think about. Now I go to the dentist with a smile on my face and leave with a clean smile on my face! I have and would recommend Mesa Dental every time to everyone. 
Thank you!

Short review: Took a dress in that had synthetic leather trim. When I picked up the dress noticed the trim was dehydrated and ruined. It was hard, dry, cracked and the fabric around it was tight like it felt shrunken. Basically the dress was ruined. I've had this dress dried cleaned before without any issues. The owner was very rude and condescending and did not refund me my money. Long story short: Avoid this place! It's better to go to a true professional dry cleaners that know what they are doing and pay more than have your clothes ruined. Poor customer service in general.

So this is my local Sushi joint and I came here because it was convenient and I had OK service here in the past that being said I'm sitting here tonight drinking sake out of a water glass there is nobody using a sake glass and they continue to tell me that there are no sake glasses available this may be one of the most ridiculous situations I've ever dealt with in my life how can you be a sushi restaurant and not have sake glasses available. Additionally this is the second time the waitress just came up to me and said she's 86'd on two of the items that we have ordered how do you not know what you're 86'd 


before you take someone's order
The happy hour had about 8 drinks, majority were whisky. The atmosphere is pretty cool. It's a great date spot.
Thin crust has no taste. Didn't want to wait 40 min for the deep dish. Cute place but don't go for the food

Hiller installed a new HVAC unit in my home almost 3 weeks ago - and I am still waiting for issues to be resolved.  Due to a lack of planning on the part of the project manager that provided the estimate, it was not discovered that my new unit would not fit up my attic stairs until after my old unit was removed.  This set in motion the need to rip my attic stairs out.  This resulted in delays, frustration, and continued problems with the attic door/stairs.  While the "contractor" (if that is who actually did the work) did "repair" and reinstall the stairs/door, he broke a piece of the trim, left many cosmetic issues, and left a huge crack in the door itself.  I had been told that someone from Hiller would contact me to schedule any finishing work on the area/door - which never occurred.  I finally contacted the original project manager who apparently had no idea that there were still issues. I was told to send pictures of the issues - which I did - and that he would get back to me - which he has not.  I am not holding my breath.  
Would I recommend Hiller?  Not ever.  

"Happy you'll be or service is free" - try "Unhappy you'll be with Hiller you'll see."

For something different we decided to hit up this location as apposed to our normal spot inside the Greenwood Park Mall. When we walked in we were greeting and seated immediately (we opted to sit in the bar area to get a table faster). Our server came up pretty promptly and took our drink order.

Upon returning we told him that we were still decided on what to eat, which he of course responded with "take your time"...which he did give us plenty of time. Applebee's has gone the way of Chili's and has one of those little kiosks sitting on the table. It was neat, as it would randomly pop up and ask if we needed a refill. Though it's neat, I feel that allows the server to be less attentive to our needs as the kiosk thingy is doing his job. It also allows you to order drinks from the bar, appetizers, and dessert; again something that our server should be doing for us.

I ended up ordering the sirloin and my dining partner ordered a salad. When our food came out it appeared to be delicious. Overall the food was good, but it could have been better. The broccoli was overcooked so it was like soggy and had a weird texture. My steak, though cooked correctly, could have had more seasoning for a little stronger/better flavor. The only thing that I actually felt was perfect was the potatoes.

Towards the end of the meal our server dropped off the check. Never asked about dessert or if we needed anything else. This was also the last time we saw him. Though the self order/pay kiosk is nice, it clearly has created an environment of not as quality service as I have experienced in the past.

This place was worth the drive. After going through what seemed like never ending roundabouts we finally arrived at Divvy. Our server was awesome tending to both community tables that we occupied. As someone who loves variety, but doesn't like large portions, this place was perfect. The food was perfectly seasoned and everything we ordered (even the quinoa tacos) was delicious.  If you love eclectic food and tapas, this is a must try restaurant.

I came here with a group of friends from work and we had a blast.  We decided to come back two weeks later with the same group and they had a cover band that night.  The band was really good, drinks were amazing, food (for bar food) wasnt that bad.  The only problem we had with it was the place was really packed out and they didnt have enough waitresses. Every time we wanted to order more drinks, we either had to hunt our waitress down of goto the bar.  Overall, its really a great place to hang out with friends.

I've given Mama's a few try's. The food isn't great nor is it bad. Kinda in between on this one. You choose your main course like chicken, beef, pork etc and you choose sides like salad, rice, macaroni salad etc. The price is a little high since half your plate is white rice. I know people who love it and hate it so it depends on the person.

Came to Florida on vacation from California and was looking for a place to eat. Luckily, we found this cute little Italian shop on our drive. This place has fantastic food overall, whether it be the appetizers, the pizzas, or the gelato! MY GOODNESS THE GELATO. We had calamari as appetizer which was lightly breaded and a perfect amount of crisp and texture. We also ordered wings, which were lightly seasoned as well as spaghetti carbonara which was AMAZING, try it with light sauce and super crisp bacon! The pizzas were amazing, we got the margherita and margherita di lusso both with pepperoni, sausage, onions as additional toppings which was a party in my mouth! After out meals we got samples of gelato flavors which immediately made me crave to buy my own portion afterwards. The nutella as well as toasted almond was fantastic it its own right! Overall, great customer service, great food, and great atmosphere! Can't wait to come back again, already thinking about the food right now makes me salivate!
Excellent slaw on the shrimp po boy and the frozen Irish coffee was exactly what I needed to keep me going in NoLa's August heat.

I was looking for a nice place for brunch on the way back to the Bay Area after picking up my daughter from UCLA.  I read the Yelp reviews and decided that the view here would be worth going through the potential service issues, especially since others said the food was good. We got just about exactly what we expected.  They have the view, so they don't have to really care about making their customers feel welcome. 

When we were called to be seated, the hostess waved us to the ONLY table on the patio that had no shade from the sea of umbrellas.  It was very not, so I asked her if there wasn't a way to get more shade and she said there were no other tables available.  My husband asked if we could at least have an umbrella moved closer to our table and she turned and walked away muttering something.  We weren't sure if she was going to check or what, so we stood by the table waiting for her to come back (it was right next to the hostess stand).  She saw us standing, looked surprised and came back over.  We asked if the umbrella from the next table could be moved closer.  She seemed really annoyed with us. She went to get a bus boy and said "can you just do SOMETHING about this?" he moved the umbrella about a foot over from the next table and it made all the difference.  How hard is that?

Our drink order got lost on top of that - and my blood orange margarita was pretty uninteresting.  BUT, the waiter was fine, and the food really was good.  yes, they were busy... But it's their JOB, and my guess is that they are always busy on summer weekends so they should figure out how to manage it.  

If you go, you might as well sit inside where you'll actually have a better view than on the patio, unless you're lucky enough to get a table right by the ocean.  I do recommend the boathouse signature breakfast, poached eggs over crab cakes and potatoes.

Worst chicken ever. If you like old, cold greasy chicken this is the place for you. They would not let me use Popeyes coupons told me due to management change. Well let's just say they need to change again. I was upcharged for white meat and given thighs. I should have looked in the bag before I left. They had no lemonade, no honey. This is on a Sunday. Never go here again.

We've tried the location in Sarasota and really enjoyed it and were excited to come across it one day. They are very family-friendly with nice deep booths perfect for toddlers to get some energy out while they wait. We went on a Friday night, and they were slammed. We did have an awesome waiter who was super friendly to our little one and helped to accommodate her to make it as easy he could given the amount of work he had to do. 

If it were service alone, I'd give at least 4 stars. The food did take quite long as we ended up spending 2 hours here. However, I want to give them another try when they're not quite as busy. I'm not sure if it's typical of this location or if it was just because they were busy, but the food was a little disappointing.

The cinnamon butter is always great, and the rolls were great as well. I don't think that's something they would normally mess up. The buffalo chicken sandwich - if I didn't know I ordered buffalo, I wouldn't have known it was a buffalo sandwich. The flavor was so faint. The veggies were crisp and fresh, but the chicken and coating was very dry. The sweet potato on the inside was good, but I do like the skin which was charred beyond being edible. 

Again, I want to give them another shot, but if this is typical of their food quality - I wouldn't recommend this location.

If you're going for pizza only great choice. If you're looking for service try some where else. I love all the pizza places in Indy but sometimes I like good ol fashioned Donatos. We usually order delivery and regret it every time because it takes so long. I love cheese dip with my pizza and bread sticks. We always order 2 and at least HALF the time I'm missing one that I've already paid for. This last time they did offer to bring it to me but no point in that because once they get here I won't need/want it anymore. The drivers are typically nice. It's just slow slow service. You're much better picking up the pizza if you can. We will continue eating here but just know what to expect.

I've never walked out of a massage before but this place I had too. I asked for have a deep tissue massage and to get out the knots in my back and neck. The woman just kept on lightly rub up and down. I stopped her and said I'm in pain right now and need you to really go deep in the knots and get them released. She kept on telling me she was. To the point I told her to stop cause I'm wasting my money. She was nice enough that I didn't have to pay for the 15mins.

I like Creole Creamery, but not nearly as much as it appears every one else does. Any place that lets me sample as many flavors as I want before ordering scores major points in my books, and I like the classic ice cream shop feel, but while the ice cream is certainly good I don't think it's the best I've ever had. It is the best I've found so far in New Orleans, though, so that counts for something. Sometimes parking is a little tricky in the neighborhood, but a long walk to and from my car only justifies that extra scoop I inevitably always order, right?

This location has done a 180! Looks like they let everyone from the old crew go and started fresh. Quality is consistent s d service is fast now. I've gone at least 6-7 times now and had fresh, hot food each time. I see such an improvement and am very happy now.

I first want to say what incredible wait staff Stella Mare's has... They were so amazing and really went above and beyond. The management was awesome too. My only concern from the get go was the planner we worked with. While she seemed very put together and organized, she seemed to get confused and often forgot about conversations we had. Being in the wedding industry, I understand that brides can be demanding and that big parties can get stressful but being organized and on top of things is important. Because we live in Los Angeles, it was important to schedule meetings ahead of time so we could involve our wedding coordinator. More then once we arrived to sit down and discuss details only to hear that she had to leave in 15 minutes to get to another event. From the interrupted meetings, confusion with the seating chart, confusion with the meal options and then to the confusion with where we were going to stand during our ceremony, it was all so disorganized and unclear. Thank god we brought in our own personal planner to help or things would really have in a negative direction. 

Three days before the wedding I called to verify that we could still use their speakers to play low key music during the reception. She said she did not remember saying we could and that she would never say we could because it was not allowed. Now, saying this to a bride three days before her wedding and offering no help to correct the situation or comfort in the fact that I was frustrated and concerned angered me greatly. Us brides stress out and worry that things will not go as planned. I understood that it was no longer possible but getting angry and frustrated with me who spent well over $4000 dollars there was unprofessional and rude. A few hours later we received a call from our wedding coordinator saying that Bonnie had called and was concerned by my frustration. Our coordinator calming explained that while it may have been an miscommunication on her part, she was present and heard her say that the using their sound system would not be a problem. 

Come the day of the wedding, I did not see Bonnie but two times. She did not come down and check on things with us. The wait staff made sure everything was 100% and even offered to help breakdown the room after, what a incredible service. Bonnie is not someone I would recommend working with. While she was a sweet person and I am sure is nice personally, but in the event planning industry (especially pertaining to events over 20), I would recommend not using her services and perhaps ask if there is someone else who does event planning at Stella Mares.

The food was incredible, ambience was amazing and as I mentioned above, the wait staff was just awesome. I hope this review does not hurt Bonnie but perhaps helping her to her job to the best of her ability.

My wife and I went here because because the menu looked delicious and we read great reviews. We love the variety of great local Nashville restaurants and were especially excited to have a new one to try. We started with the fried green tomatoes and the kale salad. They were not as good as some of the ones we've had at other Nashville restaurants, but I'll say that they were very good and are things I would order again if I went back. For the entrees, I ordered the three little pigs (their most popular dish) and my wife ordered the salmon. I'll first say that I rarely critique food, I'm someone who typically cleans both my plate and my wife's plate, and that I have a hard time wasting food. With that said, this was one of the worst meals we've ever had. Both of us could only eat a few bites of our entrees and we promptly left the restaurant feeling sick. The three little pigs sounds great on the menu, but was beyond terrible in real life. The brussels sprouts are served ice cold (too cold to have been sitting out), the apple stir fry was awful, the quality of the meat was poor (and undercooked), and the entire dish was way too salty. The mix of flavors was on the menu sounded good in theory, but was poorly executed. I will also say that I fully expected the dish to be salty because it was 3 variations of pork, but it was  much saltier than it should have been. The orange ginger salmon tasted nothing like orange or ginger and had a sauce over the rice pilaf that tasted like an expired concoction of vanilla and caramel. The salmon was so fishy that we could smell it before tasting it. I thought this restaurant had great potential, especially after good appetizers, but I can promise you that we will never be going back. It's possible that we both received bad batches of our dishes, but I think the quality was even too bad for that.

I almost hate to say how great this place is, because it's too busy already. Unusual that they could be good at both Thai and Sushi, but they are great. Not sure why they never seem to have hot sake or hot tea, but maybe they want to keep the line moving!

The one good thing about this place is that it is exceptionally clean and aesthetically pleasing. 

I am 21. I wanted to bring my mother in with me because she has had breast surgery before and she knows the kinds of questions to ask. I still need my moms guidance through things, and I was not allowed to bring her in with me during my consultation despite us both being fully vaccinated and wearing masks. However, the doctor was allowed to bring in 2 extra people (receptionists) during my consultation, and neither of which wore a mask during their time in the room, voiding the COVID-19 excuse.  

Not to mention, the doctor himself was not hearing my concerns about the way I wanted my breasts to look. I told him I wanted volume up top and I found a photo to show for reference of what I wanted them to look like, or as close as we could get. The doctor then told me that that is not realistic and to go find a photo of someone elsewhere with my breasts and that will be the result I get. He told me to get a lift but when I went back and researched it, a lift by itself would not give me the results I want. He should have known that. He did not offer different options to someone who clearly didn't know how these things work, and he clearly didn't listen to my concerns as the patient who is having the procedure. 

The doctor I went to in Sarasota immediately understood what I wanted and was able to include that in their opinion of what should be done for surgery. She also allowed my mom in the room and didn't bring in any unnecessary personnel. In my opinion, I do not think receptionists belong in the room during a consultation when they're not going to be in the operating room. I am so happy I did not do just a lift as suggested by the doctor at New Tampa because I would not have gotten the result that I wanted, and would have paid over $10,000 to not be happy with the result. 

So overall, this plastic surgery center did not meet my expectations and I will not be back.

Pizza is always solid here and the people are nice. Bad experience today. Ordered 2 pizzas on 2 separate orders online. Even though I called to confirm, Someone assumed it was a duplicate order and only made 2/4 pizzas so had to wait for them to make 2 more pizzas while the others (and I) sat there for 15 min.

Anyway, they tried to make it right by upgrading the last 2 to 14" from 12", it was a good effort I guess. 

Pizzas were delicious if late to the birthday party. Phosgo is always my favorite, with olives, green peppers and meat, their red sauce is always tangy and rich, very consistent. Will always return but come on Flying Pie!

This is the worst grease hamburger I have ever had. I would rather a Burger King burger over this.  The peanuts are a nice touch and the fries are great but to say they have good burgers is a mistake.

First of all it takes two of the burgers to make a real sandwich then you have to add al the topping to get any flavor. The patties are smashed on a grill top and cooked way to much to the point they are crispy. They need to refocus on the meat and to steal a phrase where's the BEEF and flavor?

Cute place.  The room was very spacious and the beds and pillows were comfy.  The staff were super nice, our room was very spacious, and the view was very nice.  I wish they would have had barriers between the balconies as people on the next balcony over could look right into our room so there was very little privacy.  Our only other complaint was that the room was very dark with minimal lighting.  Otherwise, it was clean, comfortable, the fridge, microwave, and cabinets were nice, and we were pleased with our stay here.

I went to Frog & Firkin with a group of 10 people for a staff dinner.  The waitress came up and told us, "You guys know we don't do individual checks for parties over 6, right?"  What?  How often do you honestly get a request for individual checks from parties of only 6 people?  A lot less often than from parties larger than 6, I'm sure.  This was a bit of an indication as to how the service was going to be all night.  The waitress did manage to get the orders (mostly) correct, but it was painfully slow for only a Wednesday night.

On the upside, the food was actually quite good - actually good enough to make me consider trying it again.  Just with a smaller group next time.

Came on a Sunday and had to wait awhile for food. The staff did tell us there was a wait and having been here before thought it would be well worth it. I got the biscuits and gravy and was very disappointed. The biscuits were dry and the gravy was not that great. I ended up barley eating. My sister got the French toast and it was burned. We had been here before and loved it but this visit on 9/29/2019 was a let down. The only good thing we had was our lattes-they were delicious! Hoping this was just an off day for them with food because before it was really good.

Nice ambiance, very clean, simple, classy, modern. Got the abbey burger, probably the best burger I've had in Tucson. They give you really good homemade sweet cheddar popcorn, and homemade pumpernickel bread with cream butter and kosher salt on the side. The burger was high quality beef with a bacon jam crust and a light layer of cheddar cheese. The only short coming is the skin on fries were over seasoned and greasy, a large steak fry would have been better choice. Overall excellent with great attentive service. Needs wifi.

Didn't feel like cooking so I decided to try Andy's our on my way home.  I first just want to say they are the absolute nicest people ever.   From the moment I walked I'm I was greeted warmly.  While I pondered my selection I was extended the courtesy that I could ask questions if I needed to do so.

I settled on the Sweet Chilli chicken with buttery rice as a side.  I also got some fries on the side.  The first then I tried were the fries.  They were melt in your mouth delicious.  My only complaint here was that at $3.50 the portion size was underwhelming.  Not enough to share with a friend without wanting to smack their hand.  The buttery rice was okay.

The chicken was delicious with the standard crunch.  The sweet chili sauce was the perfect accompaniment.  I will definitely visit again as I want to try their bulgogi.

This bar is OK. I went there tonight and it was alright I guess. The bartenders were very nice but the other people were not OK.  I like to go to other bars better but I also don't mind it when I'm blacked out. This bar is ok in my book but only if you're not looking for the best time of your life. I went to the strip club after... If that tells you anything. XOXO- Gossip Girl

I give it 5 stars for some things and 1 or less for a couple of others.  5 stars: the bright, crisp, lime and white decor.  It's the best looking spot in Wayne now.  The outdoor couches, tables and umbrellas make this the numero uno restaurant for dining al fresco, when you're lucky enough to snag a seat out there.  Love the guac, melted cheese app, duck and enchilladas.  The wide choice of drinks makes ordering one fun.  Bartenders are very good at making recommendations if you're overwhelmed, which I was.      
      Now the bad, the 1 star:  the lack of professionalism on the part of some workers here is astounding.   We actually witnessed a waiter cursing out another employee in the middle of the dining room.   Diners nearby were made extremely uncomfortable.....not the way to enjoy an expensive meal.

Horrible. I made an appointment with Paul, the owner, to have floors and chairs cleaned. I called 4 times to confirm the appointment and did not get a response. They did not show up at all and I never heard from them.

We were visiting Nashville for the NRA convention.  We had dinner here on a Thursday night.  The place was not busy at all, but it took forever to get food and the check.  The food itself wasn't great.  My chicken burrito had more peppers in it than chicken; definitely a minus for me.  The salsa didn't have much flavor and wasn't hot at all.  It was just okay, run-of-the-mill Mexican food.
Nothing too special. Portion was good but too expensive for what it is. They do not exactly have gourmet burgers.

Had been with this practice for quite a few years. They have gone down hill in that time.
Constantly losing dentists. Screwed up my implant.
Difficult to get records and after many years of being a patient, front office staff was rude. When you have a conversation with anyone in the phone it is proper to say goodbye, not just slam the phone down because you're annoyed that I'm not returning.
Have found a fantastic dentist in the area.
My advise...don't do it!

I visited CareSTAT needing an injured thumb to be seen before a weekend holiday. They accepted my insurance, and although the copay was a bit higher than I expected, it was still reasonable. I was seen promptly, and the whole procedure was pretty much what one would expect at a normal doctor's office (ie.. took my vital signs, weight, etc.). The exam rooms given to patients are not soundproofed, and the traffic on West Chester Pike is very audible and at times distracting. The doctor was polite, able to diagnose my problem and called in a prescription to my pharmacy. Overall, I was satisfied with the quality of care I received and would go back in the future if I needed to.

Last week my friends and I scored some free tickets to the Cardinal's game. As we talked about where to eat before the game, my friend Brian mentioned a sports bar in downtown St. Louis called Jack Patrick's. Apparently, it was close to the stadium and he raved about the food; so, off we went.

When we arrived at Jack Patrick's, I was a little taken aback. I'm not totally sure what I expected, but the place was basically just a sports bar. You know the scene. Dim, fluorescent lighting, bar games, and the smell of grease wafting in the air. The tables were a bit sticky, the patrons were a bit disheveled, and the "kitchen" consisted of one young guy who took your order and then cooked it for you on the one-man grill.

Interesting.

I'll admit, I was a little apprehensive.

After perusing the menu (which was full of fried and greasy foods), I landed on the BBQ Burger. Why? Because it was slathered in barbecue and topped with.... coleslaw. Oh baby.

I sauntered up to the window and placed my order with the cook. He wasn't super friendly, but all of that faded away when he told me the price of this meal. Drumroll please... this burger and fries was only $7.50. SEVEN FIFTY. Cha-ching.

When the burger arrived after a relatively short wait, I started getting more excited than ever. Despite the rough appearance of the bar, the burger seemed promising. Here's what I saw... the sweet barbecue sauce dripped down the sides of the burger and soaked the bun. The coleslaw followed suit and flowed freely from the top of the burger. It looked utterly sloppy, yet totally satisfying.

After cutting the burger into more manageable pieces, I picked up a half and watched the condiments slip and slide down my fingers. One bite in... and I was in heaven.
