I am not sure if it was the crisp, savory, and tangy combination of BBQ and coleslaw, the toasty bun, or the fact that I hadn't had a really good burger for several months--but, this little delicacy went down the hatch like butter.

By the end of the meal I was stuffed to the brim and covered in sticky barbecue goodness; and it was the best feeling.

So, here's the final grade for Jack Patrick's:

Food: A
Atmosphere: C
Price: A
Service: C

As you've hopefully gathered, Jack Patrick's is definitely not the place to go for a nice sit down dinner (or a first date! ha!). However, it is the perfect location to drop by before the big game. You might not find top notch or friendly service, but the food (particularly the BBQ burger) is absolutely perfect when you're looking for a fattening, filling, grease-filled meal.

Dig in, folks. You won't regret it!

Words can't express how much I love Prytania Vet.  They are always great at getting us in, and they always treat my dog with gentleness and care. I think he actually likes going there because he gets all of the love (and treats!).  My family has been bringing our pets there since they opened and wouldn't dream of going anywhere else.

Good food but bad service is what I have to say for this place! I came in at 9.40 and the restaurant closes at 10, I was told I could only get a take out. This is something new to me as any other restaurant in philly takes their last order at the closing time and let's you sit in a while longer if you are not finished. We were ready to place our order as soon as we were in but that did not mean getting to sit and eat. Since it was raining and I had to settle for the take out. I requested not adding the rice to my order and then on receiving the package I was told it was on the house. It made no sense because a portion of rice comes with every entree.

So I have never ever written a negative review because I know that this industry isn't always easy, but... my fiancé and I thought we'd give this place one more try since the first time we went, the service and food was terrible but we threw that up to maybe it being in its first few weeks. No... we went in tonight and sat in the bar area seating. Our server was less than enthusiastic which is whatever, but we ordered appetizers and salads. The gap between our appetizers and our very simple salads was odd considering how slow it was so we assume the server didn't time it correctly. When we did get our salads/main course, the server never returned to ask how things were going. You know, the 2 minute rule that every server/bartender is aware of and taught. Turns out my super salad was super dry and gross. It looked like a mixed bag salad after it was sitting in your fridge for two weeks. I couldn't even eat it. So I pushed it forward and maybe another 15 minutes after I did that and we were waiting for the check, our server finally comes over and sees that I barely touched my salad and asks if I wanted a box. I then told her that I didn't like it and she said,  "oh I'm sorry" and goes to get the check. Didn't take it off the bill or make any other comments about it. My fiancé and I are both bartenders/servers and understand how this all works. But would never have let someone pay for something they couldn't even eat! That was the shittiest service I've seen in years and we will not be returning for a third time. Sucks because the strawberry lemonade was amazing!!!

I pickup from this place a decent bit but they tend to sacrifice quality when they're super busy :/ I paid for my fries to be cheese fries and there was maybe a tablespoon of cheese on the huge order of fries, covering about 15% of them. Bummer

This company has terrible community relations. This evening my family including 1 year old and 3 year old went to eat at Taqueria Del Sol with my sister and parents.  Tatyana's is located right next to Taqueria del Sol. They have parking in the back that has an entrance  into the Taqueria Del Sol parking lot with a sign as you enter that makes it seem like like you can park there after office hours. It was 6:00 and the company was closed. There was no additional signage saying who could or could not park there and nothing saying they would boot and fine. 

According to chapter 6.81 of the Metro Code, a company can not boot and fine without clearly marking their parking spaces with signage saying not to park and that booting will be enforced. There was no such sign anywhere. 

On our way to the restaurant we passed a man who did not say a word to us and it was not clear he was the parking patrol. When we got back to our cars after dinner there was a sign and a boot on our cars. The man we'd pass was the one that booted our car. It would have been kind of him to let us know we could not park there since he saw us leaving, but he didn't because he was hired by Tatyana's and only makes money by booting our car.

At this point my kids are crying and we are going to be late for our event. Between the two cars, we had to shell out $100 and stand in the cold while we tried to figure out if this was a legitimate and not some sort of scam and had the boot take off our car. 

I understand that Tatyana 's might not want people to park in their parking lot and would have happily found some where else to park if that had been clear. If anything it seemed like we were permitted to park there and there was an agreement between Taqueria based on the signage and shared entry way. 

I live in 12 South and am a regular customer at almost every local spot and am used to being treated with kindness, fairness and  respect by the local establishments. I left tonight feeling conned and disrespected. After reviewing the laws it appears it is in fact illegal for them to have booted and fined us for parking in their lot without proper signage. This is not how we treat our community members. This is not how we should treat potential customers. Shame on you guys for taking advantage of a family with small children when all you needed to do is put up proper signage.

Went here tonight for Valentine's Day with the husband. I was so excited to go to a nice, beautiful place for dinner. We were taken to our table right away, as we had a reservation. I appreciated the quick sit down.  

We ordered cocktails when we first sat down. My husband's old fashioned was strong and delicious. I ordered a vodka tonic, but to my dismay, it was weak and watered down. (This is my "go to" drink - with Titos vodka - I know they could do better. Especially for a $9 drink that could easily a $6 drink.)

We ordered pretty quickly as we glanced the menu before we came. He ordered the beef stroganoff, and I ordered the crab stuffed shrimp with truffle fries. 

As we waited for our meal, we were served the most delicious bread and warmed butter. (A major reason for a star!)

We waited. And waited. Saw two couples who came in after us get their meals. And still waited. One hour and 20 minutes later, the gates opened and the angels sang and our meals appeared!!

My husband's meal was really good. I wish I ordered it. My crab stuffed shrimp was lackluster. You get four shrimp which is fine because I was pretty full after shrimp #3. However, the crab was warm, while the shrimp was cold. So weird. I don't know if that's what they were going for, but I wasn't too into it. The parmesan truffle fries - I did not like at all. Maybe I'm just not fancy, but the smell of the truffle oil was stomach churning. 

My apologies for the lengthy review. For the amount of food you get, the price is pretty on point. However, the drink costs are outlandish for what you get ( I understand it's a fancy place - just maybe make a better drink.) Lastly, I know it wasn't our waiter's fault that it took forever, as he was even antsy for our food to come out. (I even felt bad for him!)

PS Three cheers for the guy who kept refilling our water while we waited our epic long wait for our meal. You rock.

Went to eat at chipotle and came out with a 40 fine on my car. It is not well posted and not being familiar with downtown Nashville  didn't know you had to pay to park.  I am disputing my ticket. In looking at reviews and the better business bureau not good reviews.  Our story....why we're different...  beware and look out for the little smiling parking attendant sign or you'll be their next victim.

I come here multiple times a year. I like to come in the spring to get flowers to plant in my yard. They have a good selection of flowers available and there's some plants or colors that you don't normally see everywhere. If you wait too late in the spring it gets picked over however. I also love their ice cream, we usually get a gallon to take home with us whenever we come. 

We come every year for their fall festivities as well. I love their giant corn maze and do it every year. It takes about 45 minutes to complete and there's 8 different stations throughout the maze to stop at with a question to answer. This is the first year we did the moonlight maze rather than the daytime maze. It was cool to do at night with a flashlight, we might do it at night from now on. I also like doing the apple slingshot as well and seeing the place decorated for Halloween.

Horrible service, they judge you by how you look and serve you as such. Had proof my turbo was cracked and under warranty, they ran some test and instead of fixing it they told me it was fine but I needed an additional $1200 worth of work. Next day had my mechanic bring it up there and still didn't believe him till three days later after more tests. After two weeks I finally pick up my car fixed and it smelled like cigarettes (I don't smoke). I wish I could give 0 stars because one star is still being generous
Great food. Highly recommend.  Mergherita pizza is great.

I've lived in 4 cities and traveled to dozens more (Canada, US, europe, and Central America). ETS is the worst transit system of all. No matter where you start or where you are going, plan for it to take 1 hour. the system would be more efficient if the bus paths were drawn up by throwing spagetti on a map of Edmonton. Bus schedule has never been even close to accurate. many roads have several buses on them (practically none are express). often the buses all come at the same time (sometimes even 2 buses with the same number). conversely, there are many places in town where you have to take 3 buses and still walk 15 minutes... it should not take 40 minutes to Bus 3km!! According to google, it's almost always faster for me to walk to my destinations. So here I go, at -30°C to walk for 40 minutes because I don't want to take LRT + 2 buses+15 minute walk to get to same destination (fastest rout according to google).

Everyone in our family was satisfied.  

Steak sandwich was good. My 8 year old said the chicken fingers were good, but they were small.  I had the BLT with avocado.  It was just right.  Tried the breaded cauliflower-they were great-not too much breading, so you could actually enjoy the cauliflower.

Typical strip mall bar/restaurant ambiance.

Tasty and filling overall.

Great service.
The cauliflower soup is to die for, the goulash is amazing and it is so reasonably priced!

Disappointing service and food. I went asking for a chili cheese hotdog and was told they didn't have any chili at the time, which was okay. I thought I'd just get a plain one instead. When I got my food about 20 minutes later, the hot dog was cold and extremely greasy and just gross. Not impressed.

I'm going with Yvett, more merchandise! I totally get the smallish approach and I'm thrilled that they are finding ways to be successful even amidst the craziness that is 4th ave right now, but I wish they offered more, options are just a bit slim. Certainly worth popping in though if you aren't on the hunt for something specific. Not certain there is anything else like it in Tucson, excited to see how they grow/develop.
love the louis roll and ginger salad dressing (not spicy). yum

Worst poke bowl ever  Was craving one after 1 month of lock down, decided to try this. Limited sauce, mostly dry rice and raw onions. Yuck Poke bowls at Albertson on Fairview & Eagle are far superior. Only reason I gave 2 stars is because ingredients were fresh.

The service was great; the waiter seemed high but he was the friendliest. The hot sake was only 7$. We had the salmon skin roll and sashimi platter. Everything was standard and delicious, but it was just a little pricy. The ambience inside was great, but I don't agree about paying more for the ambience if the food is not extra special. I usually crave Asian food, so I would probably come back since there are not many options for this in this part of town.
We stopped in here for a pastry. The best part was the decor. The food was only okay. Not sure it was worth the calories.

I really liked this cleaner for a while, even though it seemed to take a long time to get my regular laundry back. I started for the promotion on laundered shirts, but found out snaps are more, as are ladies shirts.  
  I recommended them to my girlfriend for cleaning and she had several jeans ruined, and only a credit was offered for additional cleaning, without the return of her clothes. We both were deeply disappointed. I have given two stars since up until then, I was okay with the service. The treatment to us after the ruined cleaning incident caused us both to find new cleaners. Sorry!

Planned reservations for 6 at Sage for my husbands birthday... restaurant was really crowded and our waiter quickly told us they didn't have enough staff to support the crowd.  We tried to be patient, finally ordered and after almost an hour appetizers arrived.  Took awhile for someone to bring us ice for drinks and water as we were waiting.  Server brought meals 1 at a time, without even knowing what we ordered.  And then only put in 4 of the 6 meals!  2 people had no dinner!  We asked to speak with a manager who offered nothing- a simple apology or offering to remedy the situation would have been sufficient.  Instead the owner blamed the server and offered to comp the meal.  We really didn't even care about comping the meal- just discuss the situation and make us feel better!  Of the 4 meals we did receive my friend thought mashed potatoes were included and asked if they were forgotten- instead of being nice, she brought the menu to prove her wrong- really not necessary.

In the end We didn't pay, just tipped the poor waiter- but the owners lost 6 customers forever.

Great barbecue, interesting setup sitting down at long tables connected together, reminds me of middle school. Wish they could have found some slightly more comfortable chairs or stools. The food is served quick and most importantly it tastes awesome V:

Marche is the first meal out I had in Nashville, it set the bar high for my other experience here. I always come here and get exactly what I was in the mood for, even when I had no clue exactly what that was going into it. The longest wait I ever had was 20 min, which is nothing.  You can stare at their pastry counter, it'll take a solid 15 minutes to get your gluttoned jaw peeled off the floor. When you look at the menu, you could go in blindfolded and come out on top.  The prices are very reasonable, so treat your friend!

Over priced and not good food. The grits are horrible. Asked for soft bacon and the server brought me crunchy and said "that's how they make them". Had to ask for hot coffee. Pancakes had zero flavor. My poached eggs were in a bowl of water. If your known for your Benedict's you should know how to use a slotted spoon. 
If I have to say anything positive the mimosas were good.

What a great store this is! It is deceiving from the outside how big it actually is. If you have any type of pet/animal, then make sure you stop and check out the wide selection of what they carry. There is also a pharmacist on site that can help any medical supplies that you need as well. 

If you're looking for dog or cat toys all the way to horse feed, you'll find what you need here, with reasonable prices as well. It is basically your one stop shop for all things animal. It's nice to see such a huge selection under one roof and not having to go to another store for something. The staff is also super friendly and knowledgeable as well.

There is a tonne of free parking out front, but just a note, you can only access it directly if you are driving westbound on the sherwood park fwy. If you are trying to access it driving east, you'll have to go to the set of lights and come back as there is a meridian that you can't cross.

Great place to do business. Have had both watch repairs done and recently bought a beautiful Omega watch here. The sales experience was great (Thank you Ernie) and they do not pressure you to buy. They are very knowledgeable on their products and are happy to answer any questions. Highly recommend.

Worst service we have ever had!! Our waiter took our drink order and then walked around our table 2 to 3 times and never once took our order. We finally got our waiters attention and said something to them. Never sit in (Nora's) section!!
Sales manager was incredibly rude. He treated me as if I was wasting his time. Went to Carmax and found a better deal. This place is not recommended.

I went here today for the first time with my boyfriend and we went for brunch! The food was excellent, but the service was very slow and it was very loud in the restaurant. It probably didn't help that there was a party of 20 or so sitting right by us. We both got the Pollo Pinto. I can't wait to go back and try something else! The plates around us looked good too!

This seems to be the new place in town that everyone is talking about. Maybe it was because we went for lunch, but after all the hype I was a bit disappointed. There are definitely better places in town for this type of cuisine. 

It's a really neat space indoors with a brick-oven stove, and patio seating outdoors (I sat outside). The service was very friendly, but was slow and seemed a bit hurried when they were at the table. 

I ordered one of their whiskey cocktails to start, and it was delicious, but definitely a pricey cocktail ($10) for lunch, but that was my choice so I can't complain. They bring out fresh baked pita and olive oil with spices to start. The pita is very fluffy and has the brick-oven taste, so quite good. I wasn't super impressed with the olive oil dip, but our shared plates came out shortly after. We had the baba ganoush, labneh (a yogurt dip), lutenitsa (pepper dip), and the curried fried cauliflower hummus. Of those, the only stand out for me was the labneh, which had a really great fresh flavor. The hummus was very bland, but I had the tahini version as a garnish on my entree plate and it was much better. The dip servings are fairly small, but would be fine to share for a few people - we definitely ended up ordering too much for the three of us, with all the shared entree dishes we had!

I tried the lamb kebab, which was ground lamb in a sausage form. It was ok, but having it ground missed the mark on the lamb flavor for me. We also tried the grape leaves, which I enjoyed - a nice rich flavor, falafel - tasty, and the french fries with a special dipping sauce. I'd say skip the french fries. They were good and had a fun seasoning on them, but it was similar to the type of fries you'd get at a Checkers. 

I wouldn't make a point to go back again when I am in town, but it could be the lunch service isn't as on par as dinner, so would be willing to give it another try.
First time at Wolfgang. Crowded, dirty floors, poor service, it has it all. Only redeeming factor was the excellent crisp Chardonnay.

Had the pleasure of visiting the Nutty Duchess this afternoon with a few of the ladies in my family for a birthday lunch for my aunt. It's nestled right in Collingswood on an adorable strip with a few other restaurants, record shops and boutiques. When you walk in you can doll yourself up and get into character with fun hats, jewelry, tiaras and gloves (no not just for little kids everyone should do it!) The decor and the experience made the whole thing and was what I enjoyed. I did not at any time feel rushed and the employees were kind and made sure that we were satisfied. The food was alright but I loved the pumpkin chai! The china was all fun and mismatched and I felt like I was at an Alice in Wonderland unbirthday. Great way to diverge from the typical weekend brunch plans.

Stopped in for destination lunch after reading good reviews. Staff friendly, liked the decor, but seating not that comfortable. Ordered from the small plate part of menu. We had pork and shrimp meatballs and found the coating quite oily. Duck bao buns disappointing, with a good sized bone in one of them. Waitress said she let chef know about it, and he apologized. Bulgogi skewer mediocre.

Last year we made reservations and was glad we did. The food was outstanding and the service was wonderful. Everyone was attentive and accommodating. 

Fast forward to today. Valentine's Day. I made reservations months in advance so we could experience another remarkable dining experience.

It wasn't to be. Seated outside to be forgotten. Had to ask for everything. Chair needed to be wiped off. Waited 10 minutes to be asked what we wanted to drink. Had to request a wine menu. Waited another 10 minutes to order wine. Then once wine arrives we were requested to order both appetizers and main courses at the same time. 

Had to ask for water. Had to ask for dishes to be removed. Had to ask for desert. Had to ask for the check. The meat head that waited on us should be fired immediately. There was a table of four who waited for like 15 minutes who finally decided to get up and leave. I told them I don't blame you. I've had to ask for everything. At least they were getting out unlike us. 

There is no way this place would stay in business a week in Chicago. Maybe we're spoiled with so many great Italian restaurants that serve wonderful food and provide outstanding service. We're use to fine dining and being treated with respect. I can't recommend this place to anyone. 

I so wanted to not tip the meat head waiter. But my wife told me to give him 20% and never come back. We will make sure to tell 10 people who will tell 10 people who will tell 10 people how much the service sucks.

This is one of my favorite sushi spots in goleta/sb

their rolls are huge and delicious, their tempura veggies are delicious and not greasy. Pricewise they are good, and service is very nice. Not much atmosphere, but they do have some flat screen's for sports.

they usually have coupons and stuff offering some pretty decent deals, so keep an eye out.

I have tried the other "Felizes" - the one in Fairmount and one in Manayunk - a couple of times and had good experiences there but that was several years ago. I recently got a job in Ambler so when I was looking for Mexican, I saw that this "Feliz" was nearby in Fort Washington.
     I had made reservations for dinner last night since we would be there on a Friday evening and I knew it would probably be crowded. I arrived there about 10 minutes before my boyfried arrived, but the hosts had no problem seating me before he arrived. I ordered the Bacon & Fig Guacamole and a Paloma while I waited. The service was efficient and both were excellent. Once my friend arrived, the waiter came back immediately and took our orders.
The food was outstanding. My friend and I both ordered enchiladas and even though my friend had a special request for the salsa, the waiter took note and got him what he wanted.
     But, the best part was yet to come. I had a friend who was in the neighborhood and wanted to meet us there. I asked if we could add a chair, but since that wasn't ideal for the traffic flow, the waiter came back with two of the staff who told us they could move us to a larger table at the bar. We just had to get up and follow them and they brought everything to the table. Our other friend arrived, was seated with us and then ordered as well.
     Not only were all the orders correct and came out quickly, the food was delicious and the staff could not have been nicer. A special shout out to Drew our waiter. He was excellent. 
     Absolutely the best overall experience I've had in a restaurant for awhile. Will absolutely be back!

R.I.P-O.F.F.!!!!
They took my  car in and kept it for 3 hours fixing a tire. The thing is, they never fixed the tire. It still had the screw in it that caused the original flat the next morning when I went to work. Bad service yes, but here's the clincher; only a dishonest place looking for a rip-off would write the correct tire on the hand written receipt when taking the info, and when the vehicle was returned the printed receipt had a different tire location, i.e; left rear when it was the front right. 
I really believe they are dishonest, intentional taking advantage of customers. They also called me to tell me I needed a new air filter. . . I had changed it the week before myself.

Great food. Had the carne asada taco plate, excellent! The rice was some of the best I've had, light and fluffy. The meat was perfect. Definitely home-made food. Will definitely go back next time we're in Tucson. The tortillas were tasty.Was past the Oracle/Grant corners on Grant. Building was painted purple, so once we spotted it, it was easy to go around the block to get into the parking lot. Don't try to take a short-cut thru the alley, go around the block if you over-shoot the small parking lot.
I will be back for sure!
Loved our server she was attentive and sweet. We enjoyed the eggs roll appetizer. Nothing fancy but it was good. Husband had fish which he liked. I had the Monte Crispo which was crazy delicious.

The Salad was excellent with a nicely balanced, not overwhelming, dressing. 

The butternut squash was sooo good!


The Chef's special crab soup was excellent with nice chunks of crab and crisp celery. You could taste all the individual ingredients done as perfect combinations!!!
Not too salty and not too heavy, Wonderful! 

The Chicken sandwich was excellent too! Perfectly seasoned chicken with nice fired grill taste!  Most chicken sandwiches are too thick and under seasoned. This chicken is one of the best chicken sandwiches I have ever had! 

The Cheesecake was very good too. Not overly sweet and perfect with the roasted sugar pecans.
Chargrilled oysters!!! Honestly, I think that may taste better than acme's. Something special about how they make them here. Delicious!

Our Golden Doodle hurt his leg recently and was limping, we knew it wasn't broken as he still had full range of motion and we examined his pads, but his limp got worse over the weekend and then he just would not move and was panting very heavily. He looked terrible and I called TBVS, I spoke to Sean who advised me of the wait time of up to 2 hours but thought although not ideal, we headed in and advised we were on our way. We were checked in at 8.23pm by Sean, shortly after lovely lady Amber asked us to heard to an examination room, she checked his vitals, you can tell a dog person, she took her time to explain and talk to our Doodle. This was at 8.38, then the vet came to see us at 8.50! Dr K Meadows, absolutely lovely manner not only with me, but more importantly with our Doodle, she checked him and answered my questions and suggested with my authorization the best and most cost effective course. That is a first for me, not only exceptional care but understanding from an owners position financially. Was taken to front desk to speak to Sean again and then Amber explained the medication, we paid and by 9.10pm were back in the car. AMAZING! I know a sick pet is as worse as a sick child it can and was a stressful situation, I drove away feeling elated that our Doodle had not only received fantastic care, but as an owner I was looked after too. I personally thank you all for what you helped me with that night and our Doodle is recovering very well now due to your amazing attitude, care and professional conduct.

I had the beef bulgogi.  The meat was stringy and extremely gristly.  
The atmosphere is ok...definitely strip mall.
The service was good.

Bleh. Stopped in here cause it looked cute and I had some time to kill before a hair appointment. I guess their claim to fame is some frozen coffee drink. I was in the mood for an iced chai. The guy at the counter looked like he would rather burn himself with lit cigarette butts than make my drink. Not friendly at all, not a word spoken. I always tip my baristas, like dude, it's not that hard to say your welcome or have a nice day. The chai was the kind from a box... A bit too sweet. Whatever, not the end of the world. Hire friendlier baristas and I'll give it another go...

I like you Burger 21; I like you a lot. 

This location can get busy during lunch time, but you will soon see why it so popular. 

I usually opt for their "limited time" burgers and shakes, but their regular menu has some great options too. I personally like the seafood burgers the best because you can't find those anywhere else (Shrimp Thai is my fave, but they also have Ahi Tuna). Beef burgers I usually skip because I feel like I could get those anywhere.

Their shakes are mighty tasty too, but I usually can only choose a shake or fries because I'm so full towards the end of the meal. Speaking of fries, I think they are a little overpriced. $3 as a side or $6 for a shareable portion seems a bit high. They should consider offering a combo meal type deal...burger and fries, or a burger, fries, and drink pricing.

Anyway, I say go for their specials, or try a shake or seafood burger. If you are a fry-lover, try the sweet potato, and check out their condiment bar!

THEY NEVER ANSWER THE PHONE. It's almost impossible to get in touch with a real person.............so I walked there because I don't live far, I figure I'll order to-go in person. I stood at the bar for 20minutes and no one said anything to me. I called out to someone working behind the bar, "can I place a to-go order with someone please?". I was given a paused stare and was told to hold on. No apologies, no acknowledgement, so I walked out. Whenever I've been able to get food in the past it was good, I'm BEYOND disappointed and ashamed that the service is so lacking.

We ordered four main dishes and two type of naan three days ago for take-out.  We ordered inside the restaurant, left for ten minutes, and then picked up our food.  We felt very safe at all times because of the social distancing and the availability of hand sanitizer.  We enjoyed everything we ordered and had plenty left for a second meal the following day.  We especially liked that we could order the spice level that we preferred--from 1 to 5.  The rice was fluffy, delicious, and plentiful.  Hopefully after COVID is conquered, we can return to enjoy their buffet.

Order delivery, 1st the delivery guy went to a completely different address took the food almost 2 hours to arrive. Then when it gets here the mango lassi drink is spilled over the entire order and half the order is wrong. Call the restaurant they say I can't eat any of the food and have to wait (that's fine I get that) takes another hour for the food to get here at this point my girlfriends already finished and getting ready for bed finally it gets here wrong again. I call them apparently, and they'll send it out again another hour goes. Y and the food gets here.... Wrong again are you kidding me? I call them apparently they don't even what I order..... If the food was at least good i wouldn't have minded but this place would not even pace for mediocre. They didn't refund me any of the money apologize or anything. Don't noot ever order take out. I don't know about eat in but delivery  the 2nd worst delivery experience ever .
What a refreshing and delicious place!  Healthy, fresh food at a reasonable cost. Delightful staff. Great atmosphere. Highly recommended.
I have one word for this place, "Sucked". Eater Beware ... take an extra half an hour for lunch because you'll be on the toilet...
Very good meal.  Food was fresh, server was attentive.  Prices were reasonable, we will for sure be going back.  Every aspect was above average.
Pretty decent dim sum, especially in University City. I would recommend visiting this place with a group of friends,'ordering a bunch of plates, and eating family style!

Delicious food, friendly service, but be prepared to wait a while on your food. It's because the food is so fresh! So if you're going there during your lunch hour, order ahead so it's ready when you get here.

I've been visiting Villaggio's for over 10 years now. It's the go-to family dining spot for a nice night out typically when I come and visit. This is old-school Italian with most of the staff from Italy. The outside doesn't look like much, but once you're in the restaurant, it's like a different place with a live harp player and soft romantic lighting. The food is top notch. I ordered the spaghetti with meatballs, which comes with a salad and garlic knots. And those garlic knots -- let's just say, I kept our wait staff busy refilling that basket. You get endless refills (which I took full advantage of!). They regularly offer specials, and if you don't know what to order, just ask the waiter. Our group ordered many of the recommendations that we were given -- and by far, those were the best items we had for the night. On my most recent visit, I ordered something that our waitress didn't really recommend (the caprese salad vs the buffalo mozzarella). I mean, how different could it be? Turns out - very different. The caprese salad was good, but there was no comparison to that mozzarella. It was incredible. And let's just say, I hate to admit it when I'm wrong! In the end, it all is very good. But I have a weakness for their pasta and lasagna. After all, I don't get to eat carbs in LA!

Very nice and attentive staff. The garlic bread called twisters were awesome with lots of garlic butter and Parmesan cheese! Another great bread option is the stuffed breadsticks with ur choice of filling with pepperoni, sausage or cheese. These breadsticks are so big it's crazy. They come in a group of 4 I believe for 7 dollars and they r like having 4 calzones are so large. My son ordered the regular baked ziti and it was also very good and a huge portion which came with a breadstick. Last we ordered the large pizza. We had all that plus the boys had soft drinks with the bill 43. dollars very cheap.  We had a ton of left overs. Personally had they called it GodZilla Zoeys I would have ordered less!!! Will post my pics as well.

Zea's, I really do like this restaurant, but I think the management needs to "re-train" some of the waitress'. Also might want to also have the kitchen check the cook temp of the rotisserie chicken. Our meal was pretty good, the food was great with the exception of the very under cooked rotisserie chicken. The Thai ribs were to die for, the Asian Almond Shrimp was out of this world. The sides with our dinners were also great to. Those are the pros. Now the cons. The "Rotisserie Ribeye" is just a very poor cut of rib-eye for $32.50. We inquired on the Ribeye if it was "rotisserie" cooked. That peaked our interest. The waitress pretty much read the menu description. Didn't even bother to answer how steak was prepared. My hubby wanted the "Mashed Sweet Potatoes", but alas we were told they were out of season. So of course I asked if they are of season everywhere, or just at this location. Well I hope you aren't counting sweet potatoes for Thanksgiving or Christmas. Because the waitress said they are out season everywhere.  

All in all, I would still recommend Zea's as a good spot to eat.  Just be ready to return the rotisserie chicken for being under cooked.

Update fall/winter 2011. I'm living within delivery distance again, and have found myself succumbing to the need for delivery. What do I do? Who do I call? Well, unfortunately, there just aren't too many options (especially late at night). But thanks to Verti Marte for delivering my hot food and cold beer! Now, don't get me wrong, I'm still not a huge fan. I've eaten better food more times than not elsewhere, but beggars can't be choosers ;( and verti will have to suffice when I'm in a bind..
Decent, edible food. Delivers all night - including alcohol. Overpriced.

The worst sushi in nola. Stay away. Serving spoiled fish horrible food. The owner is annoying and seems like hes lying  about everything. The employees lack talent and the food is overpriced garbage that i wouldnt serve to my worst enemy. If u love yourself then i recommend going anywhere else but here.

Hoshun is a perfect example of why I should listen to Yelp reviews.

This was my second trip to Hoshun. On my first try, I couldn't get in because a parade was passing in front. I was determined to try it again, and on my most recent attempt, I got in.

Prior to my visit, I read several Yelp reviews. The reviews suggested the special rolls, but I didn't listen since I am occasionally hard-headed. 

I decided i would try the three roll lunch special which was $11.50. That seemed to be a pretty good bargain and there were around ten different rolls to choose from. My choices were the spicy crawfish, spicy tuna and spicy salmon rolls. The rolls were good, but not great. I am not sure what makes a roll better than another, but I have had better at other local restaurants.

Service was very good. Our server was attentive and friendly. He even made recommendations for future visits.

I will return to Hoshun for another try, but next time I will listen to my fellow yelpers and stick with the specialty rolls.

Stopped by last Friday night (27 June) and did the usual Valet parking. I highly suggest this. Parking in this area is not so easy, just let their valet's do this for you. I was bringing some friends down this night. It's not my first time here so I was kind of the tourist guide on this night. I have to say, that as usual the food was awesome, and the staff did a heck of a great job. My only disappointment of the evening was the fact that they dropped the Lamb Burger from their menu. I decided to go with their Cuban Burger and I think I just found my new favorite. My friends had the Black and Blue Burger and the Portobello Cap Burger, they were both equally impressed. Beer and Bourbon selection as always is impressive. The drink specials and live music are a plus. If you do get the chance, the rooftop seating is a great way to go especially when the weather is nice. The only drawback is that some food and drink items are not available in this seating area, so ask what's available before ordering.

I am staying at the silver legacy and have never had a more negative experience since I have been here since thurs 7-26-12 I have found glass in my drink, I have had someone try to steal my wallet and my fiancé was assaulted in the casino some guy decided to walk up to her and grab her chest he was arrested and charged with lude and gross conduct we were stuck in the security holding center until 6 am they finally arrested the guy. My fiancé is humiliated,embarrassed and obviously not happy about the situation we feel like this place is a very unsafe environment you get out of town to relax and today is my birthday. We are not the type of people to complain I am a go with the flow kind of guy. I went to the management to try and calm down the situation just asked them If there was anything they could do for her didn't even ask for me and there responce was basically tough shit deal with it. They said they need to investigate the situation I guess when she was grooped they need to know how much or how long the scum bag was squeezing her tits... I don't know about you but that just seems a little ridicules to me!!!  We will never stay at the silver legacy avian and the management and the security director need to take some classes on Customer service.

This mall is the "spot" but overall its an amazing and clean mall and has everything you need!! Parking is pretty decent and its a decent size. The foot locker is amazing all of the employees are sooo nice and friendly!!!

Went to Brick and Brew on a tuesday with my husband and were very pleased with everything this restaurant has to offer. The food was great, service PHENOMENAL, and drinks were on point. I will be coming back again, and if you're lucky sit at the pizza bar. It's the best spot to watch them work the oven.

I went to check out this new bagel place. I ordered whitefish bagel sandwich for 9 plus dollars. I got home and was disappointed that my sandwich contained hardly any whitefish salad at all. It's mostly lettuce and tomatoes.

Second time going. Food is decent but the service is awful.  We went yesterday for brunch/lunch. The place wasn't busy but our server, Ian was as inattentive as possible.  Took forever to even acknowledge we were there.  Then it was a bother to have to constantly ask for refills. Terrible service.  Food was ok- warm but not hot. Service was cold. 
There are better choices in the area.

Really good restaurant i love their food and it is very local amazing waiters aways making sure you are ok and you don't need anything very nice outdoor patio just they bring you fresh utensils which is a +

This is the best optical and eye glasses company in Arizona!!  They have been taking care of my son and his special perscription and optical needs for over 5 years.  We dirve 2 hours to get here because Alvernon is worth the trip.  The quality of their service and the lenses they hand craft in their on site lab are exceptional!! Wether you have a complex or basic perscription Alvernon is the best for meeting all of your optical needs!

Ok, today I'm going to whine a wee bit. I love Bruegger's. I loved Bruegger's for about 7 years when I lived in Franklin - I would get myself a ham and cheese bagel for lunch, and a plain bagel for my youngest to gnaw on/teethe on, haha. I missed Bruegger's oh so much when we lived in Tampa for 5 years and never found a suitable replacement for it. It was Bruegger's or nothing. So, we've been in Brentwood 5 years now, and I'm loving them again.
     My whine is the limited hours. I realize bagels tend to be a breakfast item, but my stomach dictates when I want a bagel and cream cheese. I am not a morning person AT ALL! And, i don't do breakfast? in fact, many times i don't eat anything until mid-day and its practically closing time for y'all by then!! I can't tell you how many times my family and I have wanted to nosh there, but we are too late. Bagels are a great food for athletes to eat, and my boys would be downing quite a few if you stayed open later. Even staying open til just like 7 PM would be a huge help and enable us to ingest more of your scrumptious morsels. We just want more of you, Bruegger's.

We had heard that BrushFire BBQ was rated the best in Tucson. If that is the case, then most people have not had good BBQ! The pork ribs were old, the baby backs were just okay, the garlic bread is a waste of time. The only good thing were the beans, but it takes a lot to ruin baked beans. Our draft beers came out flat and a bartender would notice that sort of thing and when mentioned, offered another draft beer without changing the CO2...really could care less. The wait staff were out to lunch somewhere else. This is not good BBQ. Our quest continues...

Arena seems outdated and could use a nice renovation but it seems that there is a good fan base that supports the young team. Food prices aren't crazy expensive like other arenas and employees were nice.

My son was introduced to YellowBird music before he could even sit up on his own.  Even back then with me holding him and us rocking out to the music, I could tell he loved it!  I waited a few more months to enroll him and he has been a groupie ever since.  Not only does my son sing the songs at home, but he has a huge crush on the awesome teacher, Alex.  I am serious.  He barely pays any attention to me during class and always tries to get Alex to snuggle with him during rest time...if only Alex lived with us!  Actually I invoke the power of Alex often at home and ask my almost 3 year old who is prone to temper tantrums, "What would Miss Alex think of that behavior?" or "Would Miss Alex allow you to treat your instruments...toys...(insert whatever you need here...) like that?"  I also sing the 'it's fun to help clean up' song multiple times a day...I usually try to enroll him in the 10:30am classes so that he falls asleep on the way home since I put the YB CD lullaby songs on repeat and I am THAT mom cruising around SB not going the speed limit so my kid will fall asleep and maybe I can take one too!  I can't say enough great things about Alex.  I just love her and so does my almost 3 year old.  My 13 week old is already a regular and I will absolutely enroll him too, once he can sit up!

Slow and poorly managed.  Can't handle a friday/saturday night dinner crowd.  plan on spending 45 minutes waiting for them to deep fry your wings.  Wait staff is slow and have little drive to bust ass and management doesn't either.

Mostly pretty meh here. Fish and chips were bland. Frosé pour was microscopic. Staff is either unenthusiastic or too enthusiastic. 

Craft beer selection is excellent. So many options I had never seen before. And the chicken salad is killer - one of the best I ever had!

However, if I were to base my review off of the pretzel bites and beer cheese alone, this place would get a 1. Completely flavorless and stale. 

I guess this averages to about a 3. Nonetheless, I'll definitely be back for that chicken salad and tasty beers (and am willing to try more)!

Overall, we weren't impressed whatsoever. We placed our order online and we're hopeful for large portions given our extremely high ticket price. We got a few choices of meats and 5 different sides. 

The meat...pulled pork was incredibly dry. Like was this left over from the weekend? The beef brisket... it came in a round container which concerned me..when I opened it, imagine pulled pot roast. Zero smoke flavor and dry beyond our preference. My wife was looking forward to good brisket only to be very disappointed. The smoked sausage, best thing we tried but small portioned for what we were charged. 

The sides...oh the sides. Why is everyone afraid of seasoning? Literally where is the salt and pepper at least? Mac and cheese, boxed flavor. Green beans, bland. Slaw, blah. Baked beans, tasted like beans with a bottle of bbq sauce squeezed into them. Brunswick stew was like eating leftover stew that someone tried to spice up with more bbq sauce. 

Will we be back? Maybe. 
Recommend to friends, no. 
Worth the price you pay, absolutely not.

personally like the flavor of the pizzas at this store. This is the only Howie's I've ever ordered from. My husband begged me to try it and I finally caved In. I'm glad I I've had zero issues there and we order often. I use the Hungry Howies app to order and see all their promotions. I love how I can add or take away ingredients super easy on the app. We always order the garlic butter crust on our pizzas. My husband is the one who picks up all the time so the location itself and sanitation I'm not sure. My husband knows my requirements for places where I order from. As of now I've not found anything that shouldn't be in my food and I pick threw my food before consuming it. I'm in no mood to eat anyone's hair or bandaids. 


Sooo last week we bought three pizzas and weren't able to eat but half the slice because they got to carried away with the garlic seasoning as you can clearly see. We didn't take it back because we had guest over and didn't have time to return it.

This place confused the hell out of me. Why do you have an 18 pack of jumbo crayons but no scotch tape? Rows of coolers with funky tea drinks but no Gatorade. One woman asked me where the band-aids were, she couldn't find them anywhere, and I said I had no idea, but did she think they had any dry shampoo? Inexplicably, they also have pizza and a nice selection of those over-sized saint candles. 

It's like someone's kooky aunt Bea and her rambunctious nephew are in charge of the inventory. Convenient location and hours though, so I guess all in all it's sort of endearing.

I saw Karen for the first time today and had a wonderful visit from start to finish! While I do consider acupuncture to be a medical expense, I left feeling completely relaxed and as if I had just stepped out of a spa. Not only does she have such a warm and calming demeanor, but the ambiance in the room (dim lighting, comfortable bed, peaceful music) definitely adds to the relaxing experience! 

In addition to acupuncture, she also did NAET testing to determine allergies and balance my body. She was very informative and knowledgeable regarding every aspect of her practice. You can tell she cares very much about what she does as well as her patients. 

We are new to the area, but I will recommend Karen to anyone who is looking for a holistic approach to medicine. Her rates are very reasonable and neither she nor Steve (at the front desk) push anything on you. I'm so glad I was introduced to acupuncture a couple years ago and so glad I found my new lady in my new town. I look forward to seeing her again!
The brisket was excellent, the ribs were excellent and the service was excellent. I don't remember too much else about the place except thinking I got to get back there to get some more.

I've lived in st petersburg for years and this place is a pleasant surprise to beach food. Super fresh oysters and the best fish tacos I've had. Blackened mahi mahi with mango salsa. $6 cocktails for Happy hour, I recommend the jalapeno cucumber margarita. Chef chris checked on us and was very welcoming. Definitely recommend stopping in for $1 oysters, ask for Gilligan!

This Is a solid pub in South Philly,  with its steampunk aesthetic ad friendly staff it's a great place to hang for a few beers or grab a bite. Top marks go to their burgers and root fries.  

The menu isn't huge but they do add a few more offerings for weekend brunch and unlike other pubs whose "champagne" choice can be dubious, they use Barefoot Bubbly which makes for a nicer mimosa. 

I also have to mention the roasted cauliflower with feta dip. Although a bit pricey at $14, it does come loaded with feta as well as a gets dip and could easily feed four as an appetizer or two as a meal.

I've gone a few times now and its it very crowded but with a decent beer selection and Quizo, it should be.

Just when I think that I have way too many titles or roles within my own personal time, Rocketown shows me up.

It's a skating park, event space, music venue, coffee bar, and more? 
Hmmm. 

I am attending today for the Nashville Technology Council's Techville conference and have utilized the spacious "main building" that can accommodate up to 1,500 (standing room) area along with the more intimate "white building" which also has a built in stage/sound system and can fit up to 500 standing room. The overall feel of both spaces is industrial.

I'd like to attend another event or two to really digest and figure out the landscape here.

Well I have been terrible about taking pictures so I've slowed on the reviews.  We tried Jamaican for the first time on May 14th.  Now I'm not a fan of chicken, but the jerk chicken in for real! The flavors penetrate deep into the meat so it is flavorful all the way through.  The curry goat was the second dish and it was very flavorful but not spicy.  The rice and beans have a different spice than we're used to in the southwest and complimented either dish perfectly.  Then there is the sweet bread called festival... served at room temperature and crunchy outside and bread like inside slightly sweet and complimented the rest of the flavors perfectly.  Great food, good service but skip the sodas and try water or a house speciality.
Natali is awesome! I trust Natali with my lashes and have been going to her for months. She does an outstanding job and I have had many compliments on them. They are soft and full and last for weeks.

The worst customer service ever. Waited in line for 15 mins and nobody acknowledged anyone in line. Not even an " I will be right with you". When the lady finally took my Togo order she was so rude and very short. And my order came out wrong and cold. I will never dine here again and I wouldn't recommend.

My friend brought some furnitures here, and the store said "We gonna give you a 2 hours window on the deliver day." When it's the deliver day, the "2 hour window" changed to 5 hours window! 

The whole delivering process is just downright ridiculous and nerve wracking, we felt like there's a huge pressure on us to be home ALL DAY and can't do anything but wait for the delivery, and yet, they keep changing the delivery time as well as the chaotic situation of didn't call us before they arrive.

They were suppose to call us BEFORE they come over, but they DID NOT call us, they just showed up without calling first. Had them called us we'll be home for sure since all we need is 5 mins to get home.

Do not even bother to make a plan with them so you can stay home at the time they said they gonna come over, because they won't.  BE READY TO STAY HOME ALL DAY to wait for them. The 2 hour window thing is just a myth..... 

As a store, not only you have to make sure the experience in the store is good, you also have to make sure the 3rd party delivering serve you provide is good as well, otherwise you just gonna end up like this: Customer had good experience in the store, but the delivery company screws it all up, and now I am writing this review with resentment and giving you a 1 star rating. Do you think it's worth it?

Freeeebirds!! One of the things I miss most about Goleta!

As grad students in UCSB, we tried not to go to Freebirds too often because we feared, it would stop being a "special" place. In retrospect, we shouldn't have been so worried!

Their nachos.. ohmigosh.. so loaded, so filled with cheese; you can't finish one by yourself unless you are famished. So I would recommend going with a friend. Their burritos are really good too, but the nachos are definitely the rockstar! 

I've tried their chicken and vegetarian burritos and they are really yummy. Overall, a lot of food for the price you pay! They are open pretty much all the time (I think they close for a 3 or 4 hours a day), so a great place to go to satisfy that late night craving (or when you have forgotten to have dinner because you were working on an assignment).

And on the 7th day, God created Romanti-Ezer.

Happened upon this unassuming place while wandering Santa Barbara on a bachelorette weekend.  Long story short, we were in SB for four days and ate there 6 times.

If you get there before 8pm, the corn tortillas are hand made to order.  Never have I had a tortilla as amazing as theirs.

The horchata was spicy and garnished with freshly chopped almonds.

I had my first pupusa there and, holy moly, why have I never tried these before?!?!?

The service was a bit slow, but the food was amazing.  After the pupusas, my favorite dish was the eggs chile verde, which we had Monday morning on our way out of town.

I would drive 5 hours just to eat here.

I really didn't think this place kicked butt in the cheesesteak category.  The meat was sliced, not chopped with little to no flavor and the bread was chewy. 

I would say go accross the street to Pats..there you will find a much beefier sandwich that is traditionally chopped with cheese.

This was a top notch steak house and was worth every penny. Be prepared to spend some money, but the service and steaks were well worth it.

The steaks were prepared in a high temp wood fired grill that gave them a unique taste like something I have never had. The way the steaks were aged also added to the taste. 

This is not a a place I would go to every week (even though I would love to!), but will save it for special occasions with the wife or when friends/family come from out of town. I will definitely be back. Can't wait!

I saw Dr.Michael Horwitz. He helped me tremendously!! I had plantar fascitis in both feet.  I was in extreme pain for many years. I was treated by 3 different doctors before finding him. I tried physical therapy. Cortisone injections helped for 6 Mos. They would only give me total of 3. so helped for couple years. Purchased expensive orthotic pad inserts. Then shoes.  Helped a little at first. Tried  accupuncture. Pain mgmt. The list goes on. An associate referred me to Dr Horwitz. He literally  changed my life!! First I had Ossitron therapy in left foot (because it was the worst) wore boot for couple weeks. Promised to ONLY wear very flexible souled shoes to work.  Like Nike flex. To keep tendons flexible. I'm a full time florist, and busy mom of 4. Had No more pain!! Immediately scheduled other foot!! I couldn't be happier w results!!! It's was 7yrs ago and ABSULUTELY NO PAIN!! I highly recommend him.

I am updating my latest review an increasing there score from a 1 to a 3. I am doing this because though it was not a pleasant experience dealing with this company after the roof was installed, the crew dos a good job and their salesman Robert was fantastic and sold me on this company. My last review did cause some commotion and I am happy to say the repairs to my home were finally completed after 2 years. John Hogan came to my home and personally apologized and claimed he never knew I was calling me the office. He is a good man and I feel he tried to run an honest company. The woman however who answer the phones are horrific and certainly one of the main causes to my 1 star review. I feel a change needs to be made in their customer service department, continuing education on basic people skills  would be a good start. I am happy this process is finally complete.

So I went here after my wierd-o experience at Duffy's..see my review there. 
The interior of the joint is really nice. Service was really good. I was seated fast and the servers were good enough.
BUT..alas...as it always is..it's all about the food and the food here was screaming...SHOULD HAVE GONE TO EL ADOBE CAFE!!!

The menu had all the usual fare. I saw a 2 taco special that came with rice and beans or a ceasar salad. Ceasar salad in a mexican food joint?? Noootttt too sure about that but I was beaned out so I ordered it.

The food came and the "shrimp tacos" had I kid you not, 3 shrimp each with about 4 pieces of dinky cabbage and some sauce that looked like thousand island dressing. Not very appetizing.

My salad was crisp but the dressing tasted like it was out of a jar and very blah. The two just didn't go together. Should have got the beans.

My mango margarita tasted like a mango smoothie. Here's a clue barkeep, next time..... Use ice!! My smoothie was cool..not cold and refreshing.   I left hungry.

My whole time at the new eateries at this ballpark left me with a bad taste in my mouth and an empty belly.
The only thing the eateries at this ballpark have are strikeouts!!!!

This place has your typical styling and vibe of your usual Mexican joint. But the tables were nice and big to accommodate the multiple plates Mexican food often calls for. 

Food was pretty average, queso was a little thinner than usual but still pretty damn good. Ate the Zarapa because it sounded awesome. Turned out to basically be a fajita that didn't come on a sizzling plate. 

Price was on par with most other chain Mexican joints but food not as good. Considering we were there on a Friday night and half the tables were empty, others must think the same. 

I will say our server was awesome though. Thorough with menu and specials and attentive. Will probably give an entire star just for him.

I ordered a pair of jeans from Roaman's.com on October 3, 2021. I received an email on November 29, 2021 that my order was placed on back order and wouldn't ship until December 29, 2021. To my surprise they shipped the item supposedly on December 21, 2021. However, the item has not moved in over a week. As of December 30, 2021 it is still sitting in Greenwood, Indiana. I could drive down to Indiana and get it faster than waiting for them to deliver it. This is so unacceptable. They have also lost me as a customer and I did find where I was able to unsubscribe to their catalog. I doubt that will do much good. I will just continue to throw their catalogs away as soon as they arrive.

I am pretty particular about my fish and chips.  I mean, I've had fish and chips in Ireland, England and even at Ivar's, a Seattle institution.  I woke up craving fish and chips and thought this may be the place to satisfy that hankering, but I was quite unimpressed.  I should have known better--it's a chain, but the reviews were mostly good and anything that is not a chain is scarce on the way back into Indy from Greencastle.  The fish was blan, the breading was tasteless and lacked the crispy authenticity of an Irish pub meal and the fries were seasoned steak fries--not bad, but I could have just done with regular potatoes.  I mean come on, Irish and good potatoes need to be a priority, not a mere side dish for your pre-battered frozen fish.  Twenty dollars later (including the stale iced tea, tax and tip), I felt a bit taken advantage of. Next time, I'll just save my money and get a flight to Ireland...I mean if I put my twenty bucks in savings every time I get a wild hair for some fish and chips I just may make it back in a couple of years...

Sheer lack of communication by the owners. They've been randomly closed twice now without any notice on their website. I understand the reason they are closed, but at least update your website. Don't just put a paper on the door.

Not only that, but multiple times we've gone there and their menu has changed from what is listed online. Specifically went there for 2 dishes that they had listed online, but apparently stopped serving many months ago. The food was fine and the beer okay, but these issues have just come up time and time again. If you're going to close or change your menu or whatever then at least update your website so we don't drive there only to have to immediately turn around.

Feb 8, 2014 We started with fried calamari, had good flavor but was overcooked and dry, Wedge was good with just enough Blue cheese dressing. The mussels and clams were very good, and the broth was great for dunking the bread in. If not for the mussels and clams, i would have given only 2 stars. I had the NY Strip steak, tender beef with good flavor, cooked med rare, perfect, BUT they forgot the caramelized red onions, with such a limited menu, I don't understand how they can forget a key important item???? The potatoes were dry with NO flavor, where was the goat cheese, I guess they forgot that too. The salmon was fresh and cooked perfectly, the duck was undercooked and the scallops were very fresh and cooked perfectly, the portion was small for the price.
The service was fair to poor, we had to ask for bread and the waiter forgot the coffee we ordered with dessert plus he never checked to inquire how the meal was. The Lemongello was excellent but the Nutella filled cannoli was AWFUL...worst cannoli I ever had.
Overall, I would not recommend this restaurant. There are too many great places in Philly to choose from. The total bill for 4 was $145.00 ;did not include tip. Another note is we ordered 2 pre-fixed meals, which included the small order of calamari and the desserts for $35.00 a person. If you ordered the Duck it was $40.00
One of our favorite places to go for drinks! Great variety and the bartenders are super knowledgeable...
wow this place is so slow, i have never seen a staff that was so slow and did nothing to help people out.

They've ruined it for me. We order and pick up from Bertucci frequently, but no more. Pastas and lasagna don't have a lot of flavor, the pickup time is merely an indication to get in your car. Food is never ready on time (not even close), and on top you'll be dealing with rude kids at the pickup counter that you've already tipped when you ordered online. So, we're done here. They should not be in business.

MOTEL Milo.
I haven't written any review in many many  years but after a stay here felt compelled to do so.  I'm extremely frustrated when a hotel posts pictures that are deceiving to customers, and this is what this hotel has done. The Ocean View room looked much larger online and much nicer. 

Firstly, this is NOT a hotel. It is a MOTEL. Meaning you have to access your room from the outside.  We paid over $300 for an "ocean view room" which is a joke. (And then an additional $30/for parking). The room is very small (#201) and other than a little table with 2 chairs there's no place to sit other than the bed. There's literally no closet, other than a very tiny wardrobe that holds a couple of house coats. It's an older hotel so the walls are paper thin and you can hear the noise and people coming and going from their units. This is an old motel that they've put lipstick on and are milking it because of it's location across from the beach. This is what many of the beachfront hotels are in Santa Barbara. 

PROS
1) Across from the beach and good access to funk zone.
2) About a mile walk to get to the heart of State Street
3) Our room was clean (albeit not the door handles) 
4) Bikes are free to use and readily available.

CONS
1) This is a MOTEL as stated above, Have to access your room from outside 
2) Nowhere to put your suitcases. Would be nice to have luggage racks so you don't have to bend down.
3) No storage
4) No place to put toiletries in the bathroom. The sinktop is very tiny. IT WOULD BENEFIT THE ROOM To HAVE A SMALL NARROW TABLE BESIDE THE SINK where one could put toiletries and you could have extra towels. 
5) The shower door kept popping open
6) Being an old motel, there's no central heating or a/c - it's a wall mounted unit
7) Like many hotels, cleaning a room involves making a bed, cleaning a bathroom and  sweeping the floor. However,  how many of then actually wipe down the door handles and doors, wipe the TV remote, the bathroom handles and the actual toilet flush handle. (Yes I'm picky) Given the Covid sham scare you'd think this would be one of the most important things they'd do to prevent germs. 
8) No elevators given it's a motel so you have to walk up stairs to your room.

The fire pits are nice albeit  it was too cool for us to use them. Again, it would benefit the hotel to offer Smores to make up for the lack of other amenities. 

I would NOT stay here again. Next time we come I'd try Hotel Californian or an Airbnb.

I enjoy coming to this market once a month. They have some one of a kind finds but lots of the stuff can be overpriced and not be in good condition.  We got an entry way table that was perfect for our home.  You have to really search for things, lots of hidden treasures.  I look forward to coming every month.

Can't say I'd like to return. Food was average, nothing spectacular. And pricey. For burgers that cost like $15 bucks it should come with a side of fries at least! The waitress we had kept getting our beers wrong, whether it be the size we order or the type of beer we ordered. Then on top of that, they brought the check out to us in one check. Okay, that's understandable since we didn't specify to do separate checks, our bad. But it took them 15-20 minutes just to split the check two ways! It was absolutely ridiculous. Also, I definitely believe that the customer has right of way in an aisle. Like, okay if you're carrying food that's a different thing, but just walking by and cutting off customers walking in the aisle is rude. A couple different waitresses did that tonight. I will end this with, the Mac n cheese being phenomenal. That's about all they had going for them tonight.

Don't panic, Jersey. Our diners have nothing on your diners. But, hungry and in a panic for hashbrowns and eggs benedict at 10a on a Saturday (and too drunk to make it back down to the city), you'll do just fine. 

The food is hit or miss, but the service is diner-pleasant. Just skip Sunday mornings - this spot is right next to a church and if there is one thing of which my 8 years of Catholic upbringing taught me, there's no good to come of standing between a church goer and her eggs.

I recently went here for a friend's birthday party. I didn't realize it was all outdoor seating, but luckily it wasn't that hot of a night. We had a nice corner of the restaurant. This place has a great casual, almost divey kind of vibe. Complete with live music from a guy with long hair and aviators.

The menu is pretty straight forward, but there were some unique items that you don't always see on menus. A bunch of different apps were ordered for the party...fried pickles, fried green tomatos and corn fritters. I had a bite of each and they were all great. Not too fried!

For an entree I ordered the crab and shrimp burger with sweet potato fries. The fries were awesome...I think it's hard to screw up sweet potato fries. But the burger was underwhelming. It was served with a mango salsa and an aioli. The aioli was great, but the mango salsa would have been great with bigger hunks of mango. Also the burger tasted a bit off, but I ate it anyway. I got sick later that night...not sure if it was the food or something else (no, I was not heavily drinking).

Our waitress for the night was top notch. She was friendly and funny and kept our big group happy. Props to her!

Would I come back? Yes, but I wouldn't make a special trip out. It seems like a great place for a casual group hangout.

This is a nice hotel--would be perfect for anybody either from St. Louis, and especially someone who is not from St. Louis and is looking for a nice upscale but not pretentious place to stay, with all the Delmar Loop attractions within walking distance. The staff was very friendly, and they have private-lot valet parking for $17. The rooftop lounge is a really neat bonus--a place to hang out with a date or with friends. The rooms were also clean, modern, and comfortable. Not what I would call a bargain price-wise (plan on about $200 for a single room) but everything was well worth it. Would recommend.

Excellent service tonight via takeout.  They were quite busy, but everything went smoothly.  We thoroughly enjoyed our food.  The woman working at the front was wonderful.

Would recommend the Duck Larb, Snow Peas/veg/tofu stirfry, and the basil wings.

Hotel review: The rooms were spacious and clean, but do not stay on the top floors unless you don't want AC. The air conditioning system is garbage. We had ours set to 50 and the room was 80 all night, preventing us from sleeping. You'd think they'd have some phone outlets around the room as well, but nope. None near the bed at all, and only 1 across the room which was extremely annoying. Breakfast is awful, avoid it (and the Diner) at all costs. If you want awesome food, definitely check out Wok near the casino. Overall, for the price wed stay here again, they just have a bunch of little things that need to be fixed.

Update: Do not bring any leftovers left from any restaurant, because they don't have microwaves. Not just in the rooms, but we spoke with the desk and they do not have microwaves anywhere in the entire hotel.. You've got to be kidding me. You have thousands of people paying for your hotel, and you don't have a single microwave for anyone to use? Unacceptable. 

Oh and the refrigerator doesn't work and all our food we've been keeping in there is spoiling. It's one thing after another with this damn hotel..

We stopped in here after the Big Ten Tourney game and were immediately discouraged by the line to even get to the front desk to put our name in, but were pleasantly surprised to be seated right away. Our waiter was friendly and fast. I got the pineapple cider as we waited. It was really sweet, but good. They had the largest selection of ciders in Indy that I've seen so far. After My boyfriend ordered a beer on tap that they were out of he ordered another german beer, franziskaner hefe-weisse, a recommendation from our waiter which she said was popular. It was fruity and not a traditional stout German beer like he ordered originally. Since they are known for having over 100 beers on tap we were slightly disappointed in our drink selection and lack of description of the beers on tap. The food on the other hand was great! I got a carnitas taco, which was small but really good. I wasn't very hungry, so I would have ordered 2 for a full meal. My boyfriend got the bbq bacon burger and sweet potato fries. For a $10 burger meal it was a big burger and great quality! We will definitely be returning, but with a little more research on which beer/cider to order.

It's Friday evening and we visit Franklin's Sweet CeCe's. First of all, It's disappointing to see how few flavors there are. I give one star since one of the three young employees were kind enough to help me with getting Heath candy instead of the Butterfinger I accidentally chose.  The chocolate candy flavors were not labeled. What's happening here?  This is the second time that our group noticed a serious decline in the quality, service, and environment and no one seems to care.  Check out our photo. As we left, we looked back to notice one of the three employees sitting with her legs crossed and shoes on the countertop - really?  Gross! Needless to say, we won't be back but we hope the health inspectors will!

Food was great. Beer/Cider selection was great.  Seating is a bit uncomfortable and live music can make conversation difficult. Appeals to some but not all.  We will be back just for the food for sure!

I signed up for yelp just to review this place.
I agree with the previous post about the NYE party. The advertised fire-jugglers and aerial acrobatics were nonexistent. That was the sole reason we choose this party over the other ones. The music was ok at best and the advertised food smelled so bad that we did not eat. That and the fact that there was no soap in the ladies room all night, even after we informed the bartender several times. But I could get over ALL of that if it weren't for the fact that there were only 2 chairs (not sofas, literally chairs) that weren't marked reserved. And most of the "reserved" sofas stayed empty all night. I guess if they didn't get reserved then no one could sit in them at all. On top of that, at 1:15 everything shut down. The only reason why I am giving them 2 stars is because the drink specials were decent BUT I will not be going back.

Terrible purchase experience.  Not sure if it was because I was a first time scooter buyer but the sales man (Sean T.) was extremely condescending and rude.  Even made a racist remark trying to be funny about one of my questions.  Not funny.  Just ignorant.

If there was any other option even remotely close to Philly for Genuine Scooters I would have gone there.  Unfortunately there isn't and I suspect that is the root of the problem with this business.

I was looking forward to trying this place but was a little disappointed in the quality. The chicken salad was enjoyable and the portion was generous. Where the place really fell down was the salad that went with it. Most of the lettuce was brown and slimy and should not have been given to a customer. Some quality control would not go amiss.

hmm, I sure hope I am not witnessing a slow death of a business that I love. Have gone to Sybergs a few times this winter, for either weeknight family dinner or social gatherings with friends,or drinks with my husband, and so many times we have experienced lack luster service. I mean, this past experience really left us talking about why our waitress was even working there since she obviously did not want to be there. I would have sent her home if I were managing her. We were seated in the bar area and it was pretty much empty except for maybe 2 other tables with people and then a few men and off work employees hanging around the bar.  
  Our dinners were good not great; and we left just sort of thinking it might be time for another "go to place".   I really really hope they turn things around and make it more inviting and fun, like it used to be. I really like Sybergs a lot.

This is honestly one of the best waxing places in Philadelphia. Karina and Vanessa are absolutely amazing, and I had a fairly pain-free waxing experience, since they use small strips for waxing. I have a sensitive skin, and I have tried hard wax. But, this is better. You will not regret going here. The ambiance is cheery and jovial, and they are very clean. I highly recommend this place!

Due to pathetic service I will never return to Capedeville and I have a feeling many of the other diners at this restaurant felt same.

We  arrived on time for our 9 pm reservation. The restaurant was not particularly busy, there were some empty tables when we arrived ( including one that could fit us) yet we did not get seated until about 9:30. We got a drink while waiting, which also took forever and were greeted with what appeared to be a half glass of wine- which we were later told is their standard 5 oz. pour- weak for a $9 glass of Malbec that probably cost them $9 for the whole bottle. Once we were finally seated we waited another 10 minutes to get a waiter and then what seemed like 15 minutes for a drink. Waters took about 30 minutes. Half the food came out cold and the side dishes came out when we were pretty much done eating. The waiter was smug the entire meal until he presented us with the check and suddenly became friendly, hmmm, I wonder why? 

The restaurant appeared to have no hostess and no manager and it truly showed with the abysmal service received. The place was not very busy, so I can't even really think of an excuse, other than incompetent, lazy employees.

NEVER AGAIN!!

What a great place!  I have been in the Tampa Bay area for a year and have not been completely happy with my hair.  Mostly the color.  One trip to Kat and she picked the exact shade of blonde for me.  Not brassy, not goldy, not ashey, just a soft natural looking highlighted blonde.  Can't beat her.
Super Nice! Lupi was the best! Noce staff and the place is wonderful too. I am a fan and recommend.

Oh the joy!! THIS IS PIZZA! Not that disgusting, cheap, processed crap America loves because it's fast and convenient. This is what pizza is supposed to be. Fresh, perfectly paired ingredients that transform a handmade dough ball into something artfully crafted and delicious beyond words. If you've been to Italy, one bite may make you feel like you're there all over again. PERFECTION.

Beware........I am out state and needed a local storage place for one year to store extra furniture from my rental property.  They bait you with a low rate, January 2021 it was $90.53 for a 7X10 locker, April 2021 it went up to $108.83.  Today December 2021 I got a notice that it's going up to $193.83!  I have never been late with a payment as they have my credit card number on file.  They have more than doubled my rent in less than a year and I can't easily go down there to move out.
I love this place. Casual, welcoming, all wines under 15$ and weekly tastings. The tastings are $10 for 6 wines, with some cheeses & crackers.  So nice to try before you buy. Great patio, too.
