I was really disappointed with my visit to Kitchen 519. My co-worker raved about this restaurant for a year straight and finally I found myself in the area today for lunch. Service was very, very slow for there being 2 servers on with less than 10 tables. It seems the staff is more concerned about their own conversations than tending to business. The red pepper hummus and chicken tacos were flavorless and the BLAT was only okay. It was definitely a waste of money.

Went after work for happy hour:

PROs: solid menu and vibe, extremely solid fries,  delicious oysters and good burger,  chicken sandwich and fish tacos. 

CONs: bao steamed buns where hurting (some may like it but I don't find that tomatoes and taziki and the meat worked.)   service is slow,  drinks and food take a while (and I would have been more understanding if the staff cared, but I just found them to be indifferent) 

Overall, I may give it another shot,  but I can't help but feel that this place would be so much better if they just tried a but more.
This used to be a good place to go. Now, the focus is on getting the food out cheaply. There's lots better, I'll never go back. The coffee was good tho.

Intimate, dark, and the perfect place for a well made cocktail. A great addition to the Adelman Building. Super friendly service. The menu is a little unwieldy, and the space has limited seating, but the drinks are on point. Have your pre-dinner cocktail here. They have beer too!

It's January 7th, 2015 and Goedeker's has done nothing other than to tell my credit card company that they have offered to help me when they clearly have not. This is the worst kind of business there is. Buyer beware, this company will sell you a product and not stand behind their warranty. I paid for the extra long warranty and it was worthless.

This is an awesome Mexican restaurant. Of you love Mexican food this is the place to be. The tacos were amazing, the corn was so flavorful and the macho nachos were delicious. I'm definitely coming back here to try what else they have.

Ya'll just go! The food is delicious! During a a visit we visited like three times. Customer service was excellent and food was fye! Can't wait to get back to NOLA to eat! Make sure you order oysters! Oh and forget your diet when you're there!

I was really excited about Festiva. I love Mexican food and divey looking places. The porch is plastic wrapped and quaint, which is where we sat. I'll start by saying we really, really enjoyed our margaritas. That being said, our server was a little pushy as we decided on less food rather than more, and was a little bit of a know it all when we ordered one more item, saying, "I told you you'd need more." Okay, fine. 

I thought the pork would be amazing, but I've definitely had better. We also ordered the poblano, which was good but not legendary. That being said, we had a plate of tacos, an appetizer, and a few rounds of drinks that landed me a $96 bill for two people. Just didn't seem worth it to me. Probably won't be back unless someone else is buying.
I regret trying this place out. I had to wait over an hour for a "take off". People who came after me were attended to first. Save yourself the stess and dont come here.

GREAT Food, Atmosphere and Service!! We celebrated our Anniversary at Georgia Reese's. Our server Jaime was great!! We had fried chicken, mac & cheese and red beans & rice. I had chicken & waffles, greens and rice & beans. Good portions and food was served hot (not lukewarm - which no one likes). And tonight there was a band. We will definitely be back!!!

We came here for dinner because there wasn't really any other option for us; there isn't a lot in the way of nice restaurants within walking distance to World Cafe Live. The happy hour specials are good - lots of $5 apps, of which we got three. The hummus platter was great, but I wish they would give you more olives and forget the diced tomatoes. The pita bread was really delicious. The spinach and artichoke dip was good, but you could tell from one bite that it was loaded with mayo and cream cheese or heavy cream or just generally fatty stuff. I mean, it was thick, thick, thick. Rich. Intense. Borderline overwhelming.

The rest of the food was just OK. My chicken tenders were pretty blah and the French fries were edible but nothing to get excited about.

I wouldn't make any effort to go back, but if you're in a pinch and need somewhere to eat within walking distance to WCL, it's a decent choice.
Nice bar on busy tourist stretch.  Lots of beers on tap and friendly service. Clean restrooms.

I will write two separate reviews- one for the bar at The Farmer's Daughter and one for the restaurant.  I will preface the reviews by saying that my husband and I were very excited to go to back to Normandy Farm this past weekend to celebrate our 1st wedding anniversary, as we were married there October 2012.  

We went to the bar a bit before our dinner reservation.  The bartenders were very friendly and attentive.  We ordered the hot pretzels and they were delicious.  The sweet potato fries were good too, but the pretzels were sensational.  

When we went to dinner, the food was decent, not as good as it was for our wedding, even though it was out of the same kitchen.  What I was even more upset by was the fact that when I made the reservation, I mentioned it was our anniversay and that we had been married there and were excited to come back to celebrate our first wedding anniversary at the place that we got married.  Not that I was expecting anything free at all, it just would have been nice for the hostess or our server to say "Happy Anniversary" or "Thank you for coming back."  That was a major disappointment.

We had SO much fun on this tour! We did the self-guided tour (narrated by Steve Buscemi!!) and it took most of the day; even without listening to the additional recordings or taking part in any of the short guided tours. The only element that I wasn't crazy about was some of the bizarre art installations. I usually love art that pops up in unexpected places, but in this instance I felt like it distracted from the overall experience.

Yum. This is definitely different bread than a normal cheesesteak however I like it. It's a stronger breaker where you really have to rip the bread off, sturdy. I got the cheesesteak with onion. The line was out the door but it went by pretty quick, limited seating. Parking is pretty easy because there is a lot of street parking around and a shopping center right across the street. Plan ahead because they are closed sundays and mondays. I was planning on going on Sunday to try the roast pork sandwich but they were closed.

This place is fine for those who don't know any better. For those who do, two words: Frozen food. If you're 21 and want to impress your date with your ability to use chopsticks while not breaking the bank, perfect. P.S. - they have a pair of pants stapled to the wall near the kitchen. Yes, stapled. No, I do not know why.

I'm always looking for good bakeries, and I think I found my go-to place! It's too bad that they close so dang early (3 pm on weekdays and 2 pm on weekends!). What about when I want a muffin after dinner? Oh well. I guess I'll take what I can get. 

I got the key lime cheesecake bar, because I couldn't think about anything else when I saw it. It didn't disappoint! It was tart but also sweet (but not too sweet), and it was smooth throughout. It was topped with whipped cream and a candied slice of lime. I wanted another piece immediately after I finished, and I still want another piece now that I'm writing about it and remembering the deliciousness.

My children were with me and chose a giant M&M cookie to split. Kids just naturally gravitate to candy cookies, I've found.

I would love to try a meal at Rosie's sometime, but I never made it past the bakery display case. I will be back, though!

Really disappointing. Starter salad was red lettuce leaves doused in a cheesy creamy dressing - and my son's caesar salad was also served with a starter salad. All the main dishes were oversauced or had strange combos of flavors,except the aforementioned caesar which had little flavor.  All was served to the thumping beat of 80s oldies. Service was friendly but inexperienced. One of our boys was feeling poorly right after we ordered and was laying down in the car throughout our meal. Though we informed the waitress he wouldn't be able to eat she put his order through anyway. We didn't push the issue but given that he never returned, thought a more professional staff would have offered to hold the order. And then there was the hostess who was waiting around with her hands in her pants. Uck.

I've been going here for years. Excellent sandwiches that are consistently good. Their Italian grinder has always been amazing. The "responsible" has also been an excellent choice now that I've been trying to eat vegetarian. Prices are decent for what you get, too.

I feel like the service has been going downhill lately, though. The last two times I've gone in (one of which was to introduce a friend to the place) - about 2 months apart - the staff has been kind of distant and unhelpful. They kind of just stare at you when you walk up to the counter, which can be offputting. This wasn't always the case, so I hope it was just a fluke on the times I went.

This is an old world Chinese restaurant. The owner goes from table to table making sure everything is ok and thanks you for not cooking at home. The food was very good from the soup to the egg rolls and shrimp with lobster sauce.

You had me at "This isn't a Zumba class." This gym is filled with incredibly knowledgeable trainers (that obviously practice what they preach!), half smiling-half pained sweaty faces, and all the equipment needed to get your strong on. 
My husband and I have a pretty decent garage gym and are both Crossfit lvl 1 cert'd but we were looking to get some more guidance on our olympic and power lifting technique. Chris and Danny are great  at instructing (so are all the other amazing trainers here). I feel like every class I am able to take away something new whether it be a tip for maintaining form, technique, new mobility movement, or type of exercise
They call themselves the Tucson School of Strength and I feel it is well suited. They focus on getting strong not skinny. They recently underwent some renovations (by recently I mean yesterday!) in order to offer more space within the gym. There is talk about installing showers and changing area in the future which I am pretty excited about. 
All in all if you are looking for a place to develop your strength programming, lose weight and get healthy, or just want a community to go with your gym, then Evolution is ideal :)

Get there early for lunch Because they fill up fast. I had a quinoa salad that had chicken, dried cherries, feta cheese and slivered almonds.  It was very good. A friend had a BLTE (Bacon lettuce and tomato plus an egg.) It came with a side and she chose the hashbrowns they were awesome. I would be a regular if I lived here! The service was awesome.

Food was very flavorful. Small plates, so order at least 5 or 6 for two people. My favorite was the garlic shrimp, croquettes and bacon wrapped dates. There are two or three full plates on the menu. Good Spanish wine list. Service was very good.

This place has a great selection of beer. The food is mediocre. The service wasn't great. The beer wasn't very cold. It's very dark and crowded. The parking lot is horrible. They don't have many parking spots to start with and the ones they do have are messed up. Be careful you don't mess up your car. Pretty much the only plus for me is the huge beer selection.

I have always been a fan of Steak 'n Shake but the only time I visited this store I got a milkshake to-go. When I got home, I realized that the milk in the shake was sour. I tries calling the restaurant to ask them to check their milk expiration dates but no one would answer the phone even though I tried, 3 separate times and let the phone ring for 3 or 4 minutes. A very poorly run store.

Hard to believe good Ice cream places are so hard to come by in Florida. We love this place so much, HUGE serving sizes, price is reasonable for, twirl ins come with two toppings which is uncommon. Delicious. Visit every time we are around.

Sugarmom's used to be my favorite bar in Olde City. As has been noted, it's literally underground, and was doing the retro thing before retro was so nowtro.

They still have one of the better jukeboxes you'll find anywhere, and they get major points for having an old school bubble hockey game (which could only be better if it was the cold war era USA vs. USSR version instead of USA vs. Canada).

A good selection of beer and decent bar food are also worth noting, in addition to the awesomeness of just being a basement bar with brick pillars.

The major problem I have with Sugarmom's is that they either ignore the smoking ban or are allowed some kind of exception. The funny thing is that I'm a smoker, but, I am used to not being able to smoke in bars and restaurants, and the second hand smoke was so bad the last time I was here I had to leave.

Also, the vibe felt a little strange the last time I was there as well. Maybe it's just because I'm old now. Who knows? Either way, it's still better than 95% of the rest of the crap in Olde City.
Interesting old fashion theme restaurant. I appreciated the distancing between customers and the food was very good.

I went online to get the best rental car for the best price for our vacation this year. We spend 2 weeks every year in Tampa and rent a car for the whole trip. We normally rent from Dollar. The lines to pick up your rental car are always long at Tampa Int'l Airport. It doesn't matter what rental company you use.  All of the rental companies waste a lot of time trying to get you to purchases add ons. When you tell them that you aren't interested in any of them they just keep pushing. Every car company does this its not just Hertz. When I went online to rent the car hertz spent time to help me find the best rate. We are a family of four with lots of luggage and the standard car isn't big enough. I ended up renting a mini van. When we picked it up the rental turned out to be a Nissan Quest which was much roomier than a minivan. Our rental car cost less this year than in years past even tho we rented a bigger vehicle. We used to get a mid size car. Thank you Hertz.

Lame place, bad line management.  Wanted blues and spent time waiting for a 90s cover band playing TLC's waterfall.  If you are in Nashville this would be towards the middle to bottom of my list, there are much better places with better music nearby.

The Good:
I've been a customer of Garland of Letters for years. I typically come for books, crystals and incense - they have a wide selection of them all - but I often get tricked into buying something I didn't come for, because some of their products are just downright beautiful.

The Bad:
I have to knock off the 1st star because their inventory seems mostly limited to Eastern or New Age spirituality. It'd be nice to see a little more inclusion and variety to suit more people's spiritual needs, but perhaps they prefer it that way (see below).

The Ugly:
The next 2 stars are knocked off because of the staff. They can be hit or miss, and are more and more the latter. You'd expect that when you walk into a spiritual store you feel welcomed and comfortable (aesthetically, Garland does a GREAT job at this). Unfortunately, my experience here echoes some of the reviews I've seen from other people of color, where you're followed throughout the store and treated noticeably different than other customers. It's not entirely surprising, but no less disappointing each time it happens.

Just today, I walked into Garland and found two staff members at the sales counter chatting it up with a customer. Though this was in stark contrast to me not being greeted at all upon entering, I just brushed it off... UNTIL the non-Black customer who walked in four seconds after me received a warm greeting from the staff member who had just ignored me. I honestly considered not buying anything, but I had made the trip there specifically to re-up on my incense. However, after yet another experience like this, this may have been my last visit. I'm also wary about bringing products home that have been subjected to that kind of negative energy anyway. Between the internet and the other spiritual shops (a great one being just a few blocks north on South St.) and botanicas throughout the city, I can find all of what I need without subjecting myself to biased, sub-par treatment. If you're Black too, you might want to do yourself a favor and just skip this store altogether.
Food was good, service slow noise level was intolerable. Spent $275 for family and will not be back.

This place is a must try...it will be your new go to po'boy/seafood platter place in this area! This place is doing it right and thank God we needed a place like this over here. The owners are so friendly and welcoming. Their po boy bread is baked in the Vietnamese fashion by one of the family members and it is out of this world, the oysters were huge and fried perfectly. Every aspect of this place was awesome. I encourage you to definitely stop in and give this place a try you will definitely be going back.

Budget minded room. Clean. Nice pool area. On the down side: tiny thin towels, 
bare floors, small room. But the worst was the bed. Just a mattress on a base. The mattress kept sliding off the base, and we were just sleeping. The bed was a double and had twin sheets on it!  Ick! I found myself sleeping on the bare mattress - or at least trying to sleep.  Shouldn't the bed be the most important thing in the room?
Long night at this place.

24 oct 2018 ***

My wife had tacos, one steak, one chicken in a blue tortilla with fries. Fries were seasoned and tasted excellent, tacos were okay. She enjoyed the porter beer.

I had the pulled pork sandwich with house made chips. The bun/bread was wimpy with no flavor. I was told to fold the alabaster white bun to hold the pork. It broke right away which made the pork squeeze or drop out. The bun was doughy. Pork flavor was okay but the pork was a little tough. The portion was small for price to value. The house chips were okay but needed salt. I went elsewhere for an additional bowl of lobster bisque to be satisfied.

The Snoogins IPA I had was recommended. It was not balanced and was too bitter, especially with a pork sandwich.

The bathrooms were clean, service and atmosphere pleasant. The bar looked great and was made from a rip cut of a tree. the bartender told us that a guy in Gilbertsville did the work.
I just have to say that Glenn was so attentive, courteous and professional. He was such a pleasure to have helped us!

My reviews are very fair, As to my personal experiences. 
Beautiful store with beautiful clothing/suits... i've bought from there in the past and I've loved everything I've ever bought.
My husband and I visited the store yesterday, we bought two swimsuits out of the floor that I tried on, totaling $445. When my husband went to pay for it with his bank card their system was taking a very long time, the lady tried to do it again and it seemed their system was malfunctioning. My husband offered to run his debit card as credit to see if that would work. No luck. The sales lady (Who was very helpful in finding my bathing suit's) said she was going to get her manager to look at it to see what was wrong, the system was saying (timedout).  To us we knew it was on their end because we had just had lunch at the mall and had purchased clothing from Nordstrom and didn't have a problem. 
Where my issue comes in is the lack of professionalism from the manager, she walked up and said very rudely ' it's got to be your card, I've never seen this on our system' she made us both feel like she was looking at us like she did not think that we had the money (we were dressed very nicely so it was confusing)   She made the transaction very awkward and uncomfortable, my husband decided to pull cash out of the ATM using the same card (and it allowed us to) to pay for the suits. So it was obviously The machine malfunctioning in everything but water.   This won't keep us from shopping there, but we certainly both agreed that the manager needs to take classes on how to handle those situations appropriately and professionally. .. very disappointed and embarrassed on how we were treated.

Having been a loyal Mercedes Benz of Nashville customer for the past decade, I do not like rating the service two stars. However, I can never get in touch with my service advisor despite having been a client of his for 10+ years. Additionally, upon dropping off your car for servicing, the staff never seems to know how to deal with clients so that one may leave in an efficient manner for those of us who need to get to other places, such as our jobs. 

With all complaints aside, the service work is of very high quality and completed in a relatively quick time. Also, the complimentary cleaning of your vehicle is above and beyond standard expectations.

All was okay until we got the bill.  Two stupid upcharges that equaled to $2.50 that made the restaurant look like a bunch of morons.

1.  There was a $1.50 upcharge for subbing a side salad for fries.  Fine, but let us know when we ask!  We were not told of the upcharge.  After seeing our bill, we asked about it and the waitress said that it was stated on the menu.  It was NOT.  Just be upfront, come on.  

2.  A $1.00 upcharge for ordering rum "on the rocks."  The $1.00 charge is for the "increased amount of rum that you get when you order on the rocks."  Yes, I have heard of this before.  Of the 100 times that I have ordered rum on the rocks in the Nashville area, I have been charged the "on the rocks," upcharge one other time.  Guess what, I never went back to that place.  Tin Roof 2, you are a run of the mill bar...and I ordered Captain Morgan...a run of the mill rum....and you want to charge me extra money for a "bigger" pour.  Good luck with that.

Some of the best BBQ I have eaten in the Philly area, I usually get the inferno pulled pork sandwich with Mac and cheese and corn bread.  I can"t seem to find BBQ anywhere near the quality.  The speciality sandwiches are also great like the nortourious p.i.g are also quality.

Pulled pork sandwich was great, cajun cuban was great, fried mac and cheese balls were great, and I really like the sauce that came with them. I don't really like going when McCools is packed and crazy, but on a slow day its an awesome place to eat and hang out

This is a great little spot in South Philly and has been a "go to" of mine when I want to stay local. We have been there about a half dozen times since they opened and have tried just about everything on the menu.
We have had their sandwiches which are delish. And while people have complained that fries do not come with the meal, you can order a bowl of fries (enough to split) for under $5. The pizza is awesome and the Stromboli is huge!  I would have to say though that the rice balls are my fav on the apps and my bf raves about the pork sandwich. 
We have visited a few times for drinks and they have a fantastic drink menu with creative cocktails. The Angel Martini is a MUST TRY!
The restaurant has a cute design and there is a garage door window that opens so you have the indoor/outdoor feel. 
The owners are always present and we have been greeted and checked on every time we have visited. The owners and staff are so personable that it really feels like cozy neighborhood spot! Def would recommend especially if you are local. You won't be disappointed.

Two fewer stars after I caught our server picking her teeth with her finger nails at the bar. I'm pretty sure whatever she picked out didn't make it in to my West Tampa Bowl. 

Atmosphere is great; TV's adorn the walls; Drink was delicious; Mini bowling alley tops it off for an overall nice experience.

Really enjoyed our stay at this hotel! When checking in, we chose to save some money and self park-the parking ran is just right across the street. The bell hops are incredibly friendly and helpful! The standard rooms are a nice size and include a mini fridge, but not a microwave. Lots of amenities in the bathroom, including makeup remover wipes and a nice full sized hair dryer. Make sure you sign up for Marriot rewards to get free wifi! Stayed for 4 days and my only complaint is with the terrible bar service. The first night of our stay, the bar closed very early even though the place was packed! Very disappointing... on all of our visits to the bar the service was very slow and staff was not very friendly. Prices are also very high, but that's typical for a hotel bar. Overall, I would stay here again! I will just venture out to one of the fun near by local bars for drinks!

What the heck!!! I just want to say that the docotr did a great job and the service was good.  However, on a visit to New Orleans i fill ill and passed out one morning walking to get breakfast.  My friend drove me to the ER.  The doctor ran a couple test and gave me two bags of fluids via I.V and that was the extent of.  Nothing serious was diagnosed and i was out of there in less then 4 hours.  5 months later i get a notification that my insurance hasn't paid Tulanes claim.  Turns out they had paid the doctors claim of $2,109 but they haven't paid the hospitals claim of $5646.14. Wow, I know medical services are expensive these day but a basic ER visit costing almost $8,000, well that is just insane. There has to be a mistake somewhere.  Right now I am trying to figure out the issue of my insurance claim and hopefully my insurance will pay it but i feel that they might contest it considering it is the highest ER bill for what turned out to be just exhaustion.  i have gone to the 3 other times in  my life and the bills were between $1,500-,$2,500.  That is understandable and i could even understand $8,000 if lots of services were involved but this was not the case.  Hopefully, i can get an explanation.

David Hines
account number 98773838

Positives:  We had the gumbo and fresh gulf fish--excellent.  The veggies were perfectly cooked (my wife still can't stop talking about the brussel sprouts).

Negatives: The waitress was in a bad mood and my seat was right under the A/C vent.  I got so cold, I sat on the bench seat by my wife and another diner.

Food:  5 stars
Service: 1 star

Food = 4 stars. Atmosphere = 5 stars. Service = 1 star. Rounding down to 3 because our experience left a bad taste in my mouth. But, I'll say that with the caveat that we had a very large group, so I can't say the service would be the same for a smaller party.

We made reservations for 20, and in the initial call to the restaurant, let them know that it was going to be a happy hour of sorts--people coming and going at different times, some people eating, some people just drinking, etc. Copia was totally fine with that. When we arrived, we saw that they actually put us at a huge sit-down table instead of in a better place for such a party. Hm. ok. We let our server know the deal, and she was emphatic that it "wouldn't be a problem!" and that they could keep the bills separate. Awesome. 

Let's talk about the good:

The atmosphere: Absolutely lovely. I had no idea a place this large existed in downtown STL, and the patio (enclosed at the time) is a wonderful place for groups. Seriously, it was incredibly charming and just perfect. 

The food: We ordered a selection of apps. For $15, the cheese plate was a great value for a large group. The calamari was some of the best I've had--lightly fried, and not overly oily. Bacon-wrapped shrimp = divine. The salads and pastas and entrees everyone ordered were quite tasty (or so my group told me)

The drinks: As other reviewers have mentioned, the whole "buy a bottle from our wine shop and add a $8 corkage fee" is a great idea, but in reality, it's really not THAT much cheaper than bottles at a regular restaurant. The cheapest cab sauv was $38 bucks, which is a bit steep for the lower end. Our group settled on several bottles of a great $25 petite syrah. If you want something other than wine, like a beer, it's a bit odd. No beer list? 

...and the bad:

The service: it started out fine. Really, it did. Apps and drinks came out quickly. Then the restaurant got crowded and several other large parties came. We ordered entrees. We waited an hour and a half. Nothing came. Other people sat down, ordered, and got their entrees within 15-20 minutes (we watched). I asked our waitress about timing. Her reply? "Well, it's busy in here tonight." Um, yes, but you are SET UP to deal with large crowds like this. That shouldn't be an excuse. 

But we finally got our food even though I was getting evil eyes from our waitress. Commence the bill. We were now informed that they could NOT split the bill, as we were originally informed. 

I get it, I really do. It's a large, complicated party. But the point is that we were promised one thing at the beginning, ordered in reliance on that, and then screwed over at the end. It left us scrambling trying to decipher a bill 4 feet long that we weren't prepared to deal with. Not cool, Copia.

So I'd recommend, but watch out if you are with a large group.

I realized my initial impressions of Yats almost 8 yrs ago wasn't all that good, but after a few additional attempts, I've found my food there. I like my cajun food spicy and saucy so I've stopped ordering the dishes without sauces. Try the Drunken Chicken, Maque Choux or Crawfish Étouffée. Better yet - get half and half so you can have two meals in one. And don't forget their bread - it's to die for. Portions are humongous. 

Bit greasy overall but the flavor's worth that hassle. Taking carryout can be tricky because the sauce almost always drips all over the bag - another annoyance but not enough to stop going.

I really enjoyed the food, and quick service....nice outdoor patio with cool view of older buildings nearby...the ribs were very tender, I went with the dry because if sauce is on the ribs, I prefer it carmelized and not wet...the dry rub was a little excessive and I would have preferred it put on during the smoking process.....but that's not a deal breaker, I would get them again....good sides and well seasoned. I will go back for sure.

I signed up for the gym and the daycare. And tried personal trainer for free and signed up for sessions with one to start. I never got my paid personal trainer not even a call till 2 months after the fact. I tried talking to five people in charge down their with no help. The manager David I talked to in person and still wasn't resolved. The daycare is a joke. My young daughter got beat up 3 times by someone much older than her when David finally said something to the staff. The staff in the daycare didn't even tell me about my daughter, and when my daughter was telling me, they were like no that didn't happen. My child doesn't lie, if they spent more time taken care of the kids coming in than their own kids, or less time talking to their friends maybe the kids would be taken care of better.  I am very disappointed with lack of leadership and the lack of not trying to help resolve the issues I had with them. I would not go back or recommend to anyone.

Seriously good stock with knowledgeable staff, but they strongly discriminate against anyone even remotely scruffy-looking.  A little bit of patience on my part usually gets around this, but seriously.  You're a local business that I'd like to support, and you've seen a lot of money from my wallet, so stop being jerks.

Yes, I cook.  No, I don't like being talked down to.  Don't look at me funny when I ask where to buy clear flour.  Don't try to sell me single-use gadgets I don't need.  Please stay open past 1 on Sundays, as some of us live weird lives, and we need our damn knives sharpened, too!

I really happy with service the Tucson Expo Center provided .
I had rented South Hall for BD rock concert. 
Management  was very helpful and very quick to respond to any questions in which i had. They have good stage and lighting system. I would even defiantly consider using the expo center for my other events in which I may need it for.Special thanks to Scot who made every detail perfect. Anyone looking for large event space should defiantly use this facility. It is great for any size of event.
Yum.  Crossroads is delicious-- I love the chile colorado.

I really like the Fresco at Five points, but this one is just not the same... food portions are *tiny*... I almost feel like I should order 2 meals!
Service is the BEST part, atmosphere, so-so... and the chairs are SO UNCOMFORTABLE! ...and the prices have gone up at least 50%!?!?!  
What gives Fresco?? 
Goleta is the Good Land, not the Greedy Land!
I just expected more ...

Poor customer service from American staff. Great from foreign staff
Poor communication.
Final assessment  Nice cruiseSome information very muddledRoom set up not as ordered.Room attendant very helpfulRhemy from Haiti. First classCruise director nice but over the topAsst cruise director a downerElleana from Ohio is everywhere at all times Female lead singer greatMale lead weak.Karaoke was entertainingDoug Funk comedy. awesomeProduction shows...ok...not spectacularDancers very good.Need more time in CaymanShuttle bus driver to and from very helpful.People who us at airport start and finish very helpful and nice.Buffet should have a better salad bar and more fresh fruit.Mongolian very popular--maybe a better system for faster service.Foreign employees very cordial and helpful..Americans not always..Shops should be open more when people on ship.Poker pro is a hit. Maybe more than one table?Cash crane a hit as wellPhotographers very skilled

I needed to utilize their services for an injury. This was about as awesome of an experience as it could possibly be. The staff was extremely nice and helpful, the lobby is nicer than any physicians office or hospital I have ever been in, and I never waited more than five minutes for anything and I needed x-rays and medicine (which they provided to me on site). I went here because it was the closest one to home. I am moving shortly, and if I need  urgent care again I will drive to this place.

They recently closed temporarily due to health code violations. Turns out, they have mice. Beware! I've seen one in front of the salad bar as well. I'm glad someone finally caught on. When I told the manager, he apologized and said he would look into it. Now they are closed for what they say is "electrical issues" but after speaking with the owners, I found out it's due to mice infestation.

I'm from out of town, so when I saw there was a Sam's Gyros here I had to try. At the time they did not have 'Greek fries' on the menus, but it looks like the owner took my suggestion and put something like it there.
The service here is great and the food is 'ok', but the last time I was here was in 3-29-17. It is worth going to at least once for a try, but it does not compare to the Grand Rapids version.

This place was the only bad experience we had in 4 days in Nashville.  Went there Sunday afternoon to watch NFL football.  TV's were adequate, food was poor, and the service was very slow and at times non-existent.  Also was not told nor aware that this was a "smoking bar" until after we were seated, ordered food and the place started to fill up. Worse yet 6 wait staff sat next to us when the got off their shift and were all heavy smokers.  We asked them to stop or at least move to another table.  They refused and the one waiter said smoking was allowed and he would smoke any where he pleased.  The all laughed and told us to "have a nice day" and all lit another cigarette in defiance.  My bad for not researching this place and understanding this backward practice/law that still allows smoking in restaurants more thoroughly.  However you'd think employees and servers would offer to make a reasonable accommodations from Paying customers.  Obviously they could care less about us as customers.  We left....and certainly won't be back.

Food is great. Wish I could give a better review. I work in Nashville and I am looking for a good, convenient lunch spot. I arrived here before noon but of course no parking at the building. They offer free valet, nice. I didn't have cash to tip, so I had to pay to park. I was in well ahead of the lunch crowd. My friend and I sat at the bar to save more time as we were on our lunch break. We ordered and waited. And waited. And waited. Our food came out around 12:40. Ok, maybe it was 12:38. I wonder how long it would have taken if we hadn't arrived before the crowd. We had chicken tenders and fries. Nothing complicated or unusual. We asked for a to go box, as we only had time to eat one of our four tenders. We were given a small sandwich sized box which wouldn't hold all of our food. We received a text that our one hour paid parking was expiring. if we didn't get out we'd have to pay an additional $6 for another hour. Trying to rush along, we asked for our check. Our server nodded yet turned to ask the customers beside of us, who already had their food and beer, if they wanted water. Yes, they did. Our server poured their water before getting our checks. The parking meter is still counting down, and we are well over our lunch break. He finally gets our check. Yes, check, single. He didn't split it, didn't even ask. I paid for it because we didn't have time to correct it.  Now I have to work over because I was late returning from lunch. Come on Party Fowl. It's the little things that become big things...

Went twice to give it a fair chance...big fail compared to the one in Nebraska! Love, love that one! How can they be sooo diff? Tossed food twice from Franklin. Whats with the chips and avocado? Who breaks up tostada tortillas to make the chips?!  Dull taste. Omg the tamales needs a new name...should be cornmeal blob! No more for me! Will drive the extra miles!

Best.

I have nothing bad to say about these guys, they're doing Mexican Jesus' work and showering Fishtown with tacos as God intended.

I've tried most of their dishes and enjoyed the living fuck out of each one.

Drunken midnight nachos and Margaritas is a guilty pleasure.

Viva la flatulence!!

Need to update menu for plant based food.
Impossible burger or Beyond Meat burger would have me And many others choosing Ale House over others that have moved to new menu items already.

I love this place. They have excellent breakfast options, and their sandwiches are some of the best in the city.

It's in the Bywater so it has vibe of that area, and there's outdoor seating.

They are pet friendly, people typically have their pets outside while eating.

I have given Toyota of SB every opportunity to prove me wrong or change my mind about their dealership. I've tried to buy vehicles there and get my vehicles maintained and every single time I am left disappointed because of the lack of knowledge. I am convinced at this point their mechanics are unqualified to maintain and repair vehicles or just simply don't value "a job well done." I am a mechanic myself and only let others work on my vehicles out of convenience. Any time I bring it to Toyota of SB I am greeted with mistakes and sloppy ness when they call the job "finished." I leave this review in hopes I prevent one more customer from being lied to or misled by a team pretending to be qualified. Not everyone has the tools and skills to fix their "fixes" like I do.

Come here if you're a fly.  Or a person who likes flies.  This Starbucks is conveniently located and usually seems to have tables open.  The employees are friendly and efficient.  But both times I went there to do work, I was grossed out by the flies buzzing around.  It's good to take out the garbage sometimes.  Just a thought.

Amazing food, beautiful view, and a friendly staff. This defiantly a "must check out" place... especially if from out of town.
We had the Crabcakes, calamari, Seabass, and then linguine and clams! Oh and did I mention the garlic bread!? Yum!

We've been waiting to try this new neighborhood spot for what seems like FOREVER!

It finally opened and we attended the Grand Opening during lunch. I was very excited!

Pros:
- Beautiful Atmosphere - very clean, modern, with a traditional feel
- Everyone was friendly!
- Spinach Artichoke Dip was DELICIOUS!
- Menu and wine list were nice + Happy Hour from 3-5ish (May be off on Times)
- Food was FRESH
- Love their check method with the chest

Cons:
- Kale Caesar had like 3 pieces of kale and a bunch of arugula and romaine. It was so tiny that after 3-4 bites I felt like it was gone. Caesar dressing was definitely homemade but bland. Not worth the price.
- Beautiful building, but it felt so LARGE with very little tables and the music was low. It felt very somber, but could just be that it was the grand opening.
- Food took a long time to come out, but again it was the grand opening.
- Pizza was decent, but I probably would opt for a simple Margherita or skip the pizza and opt for an entree instead. Didn't taste like anything special.
- Add signs for the restrooms!

The staff was very nice, but frazzled due to the opening. We will definitely be back to check out dinner and hopefully we will notice some kinks worked out. Very excited for the opening and cannot wait to see how they modify over the next couple of months to satisfy customers.

Glad they are bringing new places to the Arlington Gardens Mall!

I tried the seafood pho here and it was underwhelming. the calamari  was rubbery the shrimp was cooked perfectly scallops weren't edible to me. I've had phenomenal  pho back home. Also not sure why she tried to insist that I may want the seafood  fried rice instead of the seafood pho but didn't insist my man get the beef fried rice instead of his beef pho but I'll keep the reason I think why that happened to just me well ill keep it to myself. I'll probably come back and try something else on the menu.

The bar area is super cute and they have a few cocktails made with kombucha, they also serve bottled kombucha on its own. The service was good and our waiter was friendly though he didn't seem like he knew the ingredients very well.  I got the shrimp taco and to me it seemed a bit over priced ($12) and not very tasty. My sister got the fried chicken and was happy to find out it was cooked in almond flour. I do like that they are catering to a food group (paleo) that is not very popular esp here in Indy, my only suggestion would be to have more items for the pescetarian like myself.

This place gave me the first pedicure I have ever had in my life, and I'm 26. I consider myself a nail polish aficionado and am quite good at doing my nails, though admittedly, I'm not as good at doing my toes. 

My best friend and I like to meet halfway between our apartments to catch up. This time, we did pedis here. We had originally planned to go to French Nails, which had wonderful reviews on Yelp...however I could not go there for my first pedi, due to the state of the salon, but I'll save that for another review.

So we decided to go to the nail place next to the Starbucks we've met at before, Providence Spa & Nails. The color selection was decent (coming from an extreme collector of OPI, Essie, Zoya, Cirque, and Butter). It's not all pinks, neutrals, and reds, which was my fear. I was looking for a nice navy, I do love navy on toes, but there wasn't a true navy available there. I picked out OPI Every Month is Oktoberfest from OPI's Germany collection, which I did not own at the time (my pedi here later inspired its purchase).

The salon is clean, comfortable, and new looking. We did not have an appointment, but did not have to wait very long for two pedicure stations next to each other. My bestie asked for a candy corn design on her big toes and orange polish on the rest. Her pedi came out nice! My pedi did too. I'll come back here if I'm in the area and ever want to pay for a pedi again.

Service was good, I have only ever been to two other nail salons in my life and the service was about the same. The hot stone massage was wonderful and has inspired my bestie and I to do hot stone massages next time we meet halfway!
I was not wowed by the food and the waitress was rude. Order your eggs well done or they come come out gooey.
Service was great.  Food is consistent every time we go.  French onion soup and muffuletta make a great lunch.

We were just looking for a quick bite and decided to stop in.  When we were seated the table we got was incredibly sticky; like old frat house basement sticky. We had someone's order brought to our table that wasn't ours. The burger was ok. Not anything special and it took 50 minutes to come out. We also got the pickle platter which was 5$ and not worth it; the pickled carrots on the platter were gross and soggy. The service was good and our waiter was nice but I would probably not go back for the food.

This was a fun trail and park to be in. The dog park was nice to see and it just seemed like everyone either running or walking was truly enjoying themselves. That was a great view of the far bridge and seemed like lots to just see and take in. If you are in the area after stuffing your face at the nearby BBQ place called The Joint, this is a fun place to walk to digest a little for sure!

We picked TJ's because we wanted a place that delivers. We were not disappointed. We both ordered Alfredo, mine with shrimp, my husband's with chicken. It was delicious, although a little high priced for pasta. The cheesy garlic bread was amazing. If you like garlic, you will love this bread. We would order from here again.

Great outfit! We had a wonderful time on the Crescent tour. Clean, well-maintained, comfortable bikes and knowledgeable guide. Very friendly and welcoming staff. Easy check in process. Left on time. Would do it again!

This review is exclusively for the poor management and lack of communication for the cooking classes. I purchased in-store cooking class as an anniversary present for me and my husband, and was charged $158 on my credit card. We reached the Carmel location for the class in heavy rain only to find that the store was locked but there were some other people waiting for the cooking class, so we thought maybe the instructor is running a bit late due to the rain. At 9:10 AM, an employee came. She told us that she cannot let us in but basically, there is no cooking class. She mentioned that the cooking class was canceled a week ago due to unavailability of the instructor and after checking the roster, she told us that someone may have tried calling me and would have left a voicemail. 
First, I didn't have any missed call or voicemail in the past 10 days that I did not know about. Second, I was not refunded. This should've been the first thing they should've done if cooking class was canceled a week ago. Third, the person should have tried calling the alternate phone number (which I checked was there on the roster) or tried sending me an email to make sure the message was conveyed. 
It's been 4 days and I have still not received the refund. I have followed up with the store over email and have not heard back. I finally called their customer service today and a gentleman told me that in the system, he does not see that the cooking class was cancelled, so he is unable to issue a refund but he is escalating this to a team so they can review my case and follow up, which I appreciate.
I am not sure if there is disconnect between the corporate and store management, but a client should not have to pay for this nonsense. You wasted our time on the day of the cooking class and are still withholding our refund for your lack of professionalism.

As always for fast food, my stars are relative to other locations of this chain.  This location is reasonably clean inside, service is fast, and food is fresh.  Drive thru service is also good.  Servers seem more engaged and professional at this location than most.

This place came highly recommended but I wasn't impressed.  I definitely wasn't expecting the format and ended up feeling rushed because there was a VERY long line of customers behind us waiting to order.  The woman who took our order at the register was pretty disconnected, blank stare and no smiles as my family franticly tried to decide on what to order.  Then disconnection turned to an expression that screamed "will you hurry the hell up and order".
 
So we rushed through annoyed, left wondering if we made the best choices, and shocked by the bill. This place is really expensive in my opinion, for what we actually got in terms of service, food quality and ambiance.  Anyway, we got a table in the back by the kitchen (never a good thing) because it was so packed, then food came out, quickly, but that is to be expected as all they had to do is scoop it out of the warming pans at the counter.
   
So, a couple of the kids got the Famous Ferdi Special and they both loved it.  I sampled the gumbo, crawfish etouffee, red beans and rice with ham chunks and they were all A-OK, none of those dishes made me hum and close my eyes in enjoyment, so I was disappointed. I guess my expectations were high based on the reviews and recommendations.  I was most disappointed by the fried oysters, which unlike another reviewer's experience, mine were very small and mushy, and I couldn't really tell that they were fried.
  
On the bright side, the best thing I had was the bread pudding which honestly, based on the presentation; I was not rushing to try it.  It sat in the bowl looking like a mushy unrecognizable mess (so sorry to say that but it did) but OH it was YumOLicious!
 
So, I would definitely run in for a bowl of that delicious mushy bread pudding the next time I am in NOLA  but that's about it for me, OR maybe I would give them another try just because it HAD to be an off night, based on the fact that so many LOVE this place.
I thought it would be a long way but it wasn't bad this time. The food was great like always. The drinks were even better. I love the music. The atmosphere and the food. All around a good time

Great New concept that operates out of Ynez Mexican restaurant right now. If you've never had West African or more specifically Ghanan food it's absolutely worth a try. Good spice and very filling without feeling sluggish after your meal.

On your way from Philly down to the shore is the Rastelli Market on Route 73.  My wife and I were looking for a place to buy some appetizers and wine.  We stumbled upon Rastelli Market.  It was out lucky day.  Rastelli is a fantastic boutique market with out of this world customer service.  We opted for several prepared appetizers, homemade salsa, guacamole and Buffalo chicken dip.  I chose several new beer varieties and my wife two bottles of wine.  One red one white.  At every stop the employees offered to help.  They were friendly and knowledgeable, not overbearing. On the way home the next day we stopped again to buy steaks $7 strip steaks and $8 filet mignons.  They were fantastic.  They are the perfect size and flavorful.  Rastelli is now my regular stop when I'm on 73.
Horrible service, they change our tech about 3 times. My daughter nails look awful, you can see all the acrylic bumps underneath her gel! I don't recommend this place to anyone.

The owner, Don, went above and beyond to help me finance my car. He's a nice family man. They had a great selection of vehicles in my price range. I originally had given them 5 stars, but now that i've driven my car for a few months I can see how they blatantly lied to me about specific things that I asked about. I've been a salesman and have fallen into those traps, so i guess that's karma. I wouldn't discourage people from going there, I'd say just be aware that it is a used car dealership and you get everything that comes with it so do your due diligence.

if you think these were "the best tacos id ever had" its cause you were in Ybor and drunk. if you thought the staff was rude to you its cause you were in Ybor and drunk. the bath room is outside around the corner by the dumpster..... for drunks. so if your sliding sideways down 7th street way after everybodies bed time go get some greasy tacos and a beer. it costs more than drive-thru window food but it tastes better. not much better but better, plus they got beer. you drunk

After reading all of the reviews I thought this would be a great place at a great price. I thought it was okay, but those who haven't been here before should be aware that you place your order at the register then sit and wait in a crowded in appealing room. Although we got there early, it seemed to take forever to get our food. As a visitor to New Orleans, I felt that other restaurants in the area have much more to offer at comparable prices.

I usually try to avoid going into Sprint stores because their whole game is to upsell you on accessories and add-ons, but I needed to activate a phone and doing so online wasn't working. It didn't help that the chat icon on the Sprint website was deactivated all morning due to high traffic (so they tell me). So I was forced to make an appointment to have a real live human activate my phone for me.

As soon as I walked in, I was greeted and helped by the only free representative. Made me think that if he had already been helping a customer, my appointment would have been for nil. In any case, the service was pretty friendly and the rep who helped me was personable. I did get the usual spiel about buying a nice case and insurance for my nice new phone to protect it, but after I said no, he left it at that and didn't insist. (Much appreciated!)

My account was kind of complicated (on a legacy plan), so he had to make a couple of calls to get the new phone activated. The Sprint rep on the other line tried to sell me insurance too, but after I said no, they didn't insist further.

From start to finish, it took about 45 minutes.

Perhaps the top 3 all time worst customer service experiences I've had in my life.  Almost like Punk'd as if a camera crew was going to pop out the kitchen and tell me it's a joke.
Do NOT go here if you have a flight inside of 2-3hrs. Waited nearly an hour for an omelette.  Staff(bartender) was very rude and didn't seem to care at all despite messing up orders and taking forever.

Rigo's is a constant safe bet. Good is always good, plating is impressive, and the service is always with a smile. The homemade chips and salsa are the star of the show for me. They are what I crave when I know we are heading to Rigo's. The place is clean and has a nice feel for a strip mall location.  The only thing holding it back from a 5 star is the prices are a tad high for what you get. Don't let that stop you though, it is good food and very consistent.

Great Food (5 Star) , Great Drinks (5 Star). Best Mexican food in the area, the county and perhaps the state. Tequila selection is incredible. Likely over 100 bottles to choose from Shots from a few dollars to close to $100. Yes per shot.
I go frequently and love the place. Only problem I have or have ever had is the service. It's OK. Not even plain good let alone great. Mos of the staff is family to the owner and the place does not have much personality when it comes to the staff. Very workman like. Which is quite OK as long as you expect it going in.Love the chips and salsa. If you are looking for the typical Mexican version you will not get it. Salsa is mainly vegetables and no real "spice". They leave that for the 3 sauces they serve with it. Hot, Hotter and Hottest. They are yummy. Guacamole is great too. I've never had a bad meal here. What should you get. Anything. It's very authentic. Plus not your typical spoon full of beans and rice as sides. More gourmet in that sense.
Highly recommend it and tell all my friends. I just temper it all with the service expectation.
You should try it amigo. Your taste buds will love you for it.
PS If you go during peak dining hours be prepared to A) have to scout out a parking spot and B) to wait. It is worth it though.

The worst customer service I have ever gotten in my life! We were strolling around Frenchmen St looking for somewhere to eat and hopefully listen to some good music. However, we basically got racial discrimination. People looked at us as if they had never seen Asian people before and the waitresses just ignored us. I had to directly ask one waitress if we could sit anywhere we want and she said we could, so we waited at the table. After a while she came to our table and said she would be back in 4 minuets. She didn't come back after 10 minuets. We started to feel uneasy and looked around. All of the other tables were already getting drinks. Then I saw one Caucasian couple came after us was being asked if they already ordered. At that point I had no patience anymore and decided to leave. 
They saw us walking away, but still didn't say anything. 
This place is definitely not for Asian tourists. That's all I can say.

I rarely give 1 star reviews (mostly because I frequently seek Yelp's advice and avoid places that have such low ratings, oops), but this place had me irritated.  I've been here for car washes in the past, this place is going downhill- while the price is going uphill.  Seriously if you're raising your prices, maintain your business!  

So I drove up and asked for the middle priced car wash because the it came with some Armorall wipes so I could clean the inside of the car too.  So then they guys says they don't have any Armorall (though that's what the sign said), and offered me one of those little tree car scent things.  I also noticed while I was paying that they had signs all over saying that they no longer accept their stamp/punch cards.  Really?  How grumpy would I be if I had one of those almost full and they told me "tough luck!".  And who decides to stop offering an option that ensures customer loyalty!  Sounds like they don't want to give out anything free, even to the loyal.   Seemed scrimpy.  

So we went through the car wash- that was fair.  They installed those red and blue lights that make the soap look like lava and such, which my kids enjoyed.  The wash seemed a little rough, the washers kept knocking my side-view mirrors back and forth.  I wouldn't take a car with a fancy paint job here!  Luckily I have so many dings and such that I wouldn't notice if it did any bonus damage.  

Alright- car is washed... on to the vacuums (really the reason I came here- the inside needed help just as much as the outside).  First, not all the vacuums worked, so you had to be creative to get the one that worked to reach all spots of the car without pulling out and re-parking.  Second- the part that made me almost lose my mind with irritation- the vacuum turns off every minute- literally only 60 seconds.  So just as soon as you climb into that back seat and do gymnastics to get those kid-crumbs, it shuts off... Shimmy back out of the car, push the button to turn it back on, and climb back in as fast as possible to maximize the brief time it stays on before you have to get out again.  ARGHH. 

Then- there's like 1 trash can available, across the parking lot from where I was.  OK, maybe they are trying to be frugal about everything else, but spare a few extra trash cans?  The place was littered with junk that other people left on the cement blocks, obviously as irritated as I was (but alas, I did make the trek to the trash can with my handfuls of bits of this and that).  They used to have racks of wet rags you could use to wipe things down.  This time, there was one rack of nearly dry rags, also way over by the trash can.  And when I used them to wipe down the inside of my windows, it left my windows with a residue of funk.  

So my overall opinion- what used to be a good place has turned into a penny-pinching, poorly maintained place that is run by someone who doesn't care about customer satisfaction.  Won't be back, I suppose I'll make the the drive all the way down to that fantastic car wash down by rattlesnake mountain (Longley and McCarran), rather than come back here.

Like many of us, dental work freaks me out. On top of that, finding a dentist that males me feel safe is just as stressful. I felt like a lost cause in fixing my teeth. I'm happy to say that Traders Pointe Dental didn't just fix my teeth, but fixed my confidence. Their services made me feel comfort and wellness. Well, after I regained feeling in my face.

I've always had serious seasonal allergies - itchy noise, eyes, throat, and pallette. Yeah, the roof of my mouth itched for 3 months of every year. Horrible feeling. Cough drops were the only thing that gave me comfort. Unfortunately, 20 years of eating sugary cough drops have cause some yucky dental problems. Then I decided to stay in college, with no money or insurance, hence making the problem even worse. Finally, I was married, graduated and fully insured. Now what? Rob referred me to his dentist, saying that they are very good. So I crossed me fingers and made the appointment. 

Every time I visit their office, I feel very welcomed. The receptionist greets me by name and is genuinely friendly. The waiting room is a small yet modern, 4-seater lounge with two computer hubs and a drink station. Water, soda and Keurig coffee. Wait time is never longer that 10 minutes. The dental assistants are lovely people. They set you up in your room with the TVs on the channel you prefer. Oh, and there's a TV on the ceiling so you can watch something while there is a drill in your face. Good times.

Dr. Sang Kwon is always on time with his appointments. He moves fluidly between patients, very down-to-earth guy with a comforting demeanor. He communicates very well and makes sure his patients are as comfortable as possible. My work included fillings, crowns, extractions, including my bottom two wisdom teeth. And I didn't feel pressured into doing something I didn't want to do. The decision is all mine, as it should be. If you have the means, check this dentist out. It is a bit more money, but it is SO WORTH IT.

This place is very cute! It's s nice hangout spot with family or friends. There are board games available to play. They have a lot of different flavors for frappes. I got the Vanilla one, and it tastes great. Not too sweet

Don't usually come here during the middle of the week but didn't feel like cooking. Glad I did it was lit up in there bright enough to see everyone and everything. Wait staff greets people when they walk and say goodnight when you walk out. Very attentive, our waitress Tiffany was very nice, they all were helpful and brought plenty of napkins. Of course we got the chicken and it was GOOD, rice was good vegetable, should have gotten the mango smoothie. Yum yum yum yum

I had requested a mid-size sedan through Costco, but when I arrived at 8:30 pm I was told to choose between the large SUV or the smaller one. Huh?! I ended up with a Jeep that didn't handle well for a 60+ smaller woman. Also, something sounded off with the tires and my son agreed. Back it goes tomorrow.

Three of us ordered street tacos. After an hour of waiting the tacos never arrived. We left. Good salsa but an hour wait and no food is ridiculous. They served two large groups who arrived after us instead of serving us. What a shame.

I feel totally ripped off. I thought I could trust this guy. Instead, Carchedi breached the contract, but not once acknowledged his failure to deliver, let alone take any responsibility for his lack of effort. When we agreed to terms 7/18/2020, he did not bother to tell me he'd be on vacation for most of the month of August. There was supposed to be on-line advertising (due to COVID) through August 2020, but it wasn't posted on his website. I checked daily. There was also "Possible live September sale." Well, maybe he included one or two pieces with other families' valuables. My Uncle's Estate, in its entirety was not advertised. In short, Carchedi did not deliver. So basically, I PAID $4000 to move ALL Uncle's antiques, furniture, paintings, silver, original artwork, wall-to-wall hardbacks, and mid-century bedroom set to Carchedi's warehouse!! Three months later, I'm still waiting. No check. No inventory. No matter. We're going to Small Claims Court. DO NOT TRUST THIS PERSON.

Strange pizza, glad I tried it... over priced.

The sauce was fairly bland, the cracker crust was kinda limp and the cheese was sparse.
The worst.   Rude over worked staff.  Always concerned they are gonna make mistake.   Usually go to Walgreens up the road and today was reminded why I do.

The empanadas here come fresh and piping hot out of the oven! They were very light and not heavy at all. 

I ordered the combo plate of 2 empanadas with a salad and my boyfriend ordered the combo of 3 empanadas with chimi papas. Loved being able to get a combo plate. Between the two of us, we tried the pollo, serrano, and humita (seasonal). They were all pretty great, with the serrano standing out in particular. The dough was the best empanada dough I have had, but I think the flavors could be rounded out a bit more. In particular, the pollo could have been more flavorful. 

The outdoor seating was also lovely with a great atmosphere. Would recommend.

The food was awesome!  I was looking forward to Hefeweizen but since I was refused service at mastry's I am glad we came here.  Blue moon is a great beer!  The service and food selection was plentiful, this hidden gem will become regular place for this Army Veteran.

Great prices, clean facility, and friendly staff! Only subtracted a star because you can't tip on a credit card (must do cash) and overall, the place, while clean, is very bland. However, they spent more time on the massaging/buffing/cleaning part of my pedicure than any other place, even those that charge 2-3x as much. My spa pedicure was so relaxing and only cost $25 (plus tip). I will definitely be going to Zyng for my pedicures from now on!

Corner Dentistry is clean, stylish and has all the new technology. The office wasn't uptight and put me at ease. Everyone in the office was very friendly and welcoming. An assistant took  my x-rays first. Unfortunately, I don't remember her name, but she was great. After the x-rays, Dr. Concepcion she provided me a comprehensive dental exam/cleaning and filling replacement. She was thorough and went over everything in detail. It was calming because she explained everything she was doing.  The visit was the most painless dentist appointment I've ever had and it included getting a filling replaced. I'm extremely happy to have found Corner Dentistry and I highly recommend them for your dental needs!

Not a good hotel. Choose a different hotel. I had the unfortunate experience of staying at this hotel. Upon checkin I noticed that the water in the sink was only lately working and scolding hot. I called to front desk no answer. I sent a message over their text messaging in app about the issue. No response I followed up on the in-app messaging again in the morning. Someone finally responded and I set a time for them to come up at 8.  Waited for 45 mins and no one came up. Finally after going down and speaking with the front desk they sent someone up as I was checking out. Outside of this issue. The tub doesn't drain either. Looks like a poorly maintained hotel and a staff that doesn't care. Avoid this hotel there are better in the area.

One of the most unreliable internet providers I have ever had. I wish I had options but in my case I don't have a whole lot. Was going to check into other options so if anyone has any ideas please let me know. Have been using for over two years.  Services slowed down all the time. Frequently it just stops working for minutes if your lucky because mostly it stops working for hours at a time. Customer service is fair but it cannot compensate for the notoriously unreliable internet service that sometimes May or may not be provided.  Look elsewhere this is a waste of money. And please let me know if you have a reliable alternative.  Maybe all wireless internet is bad.  But I don't think so.

One of the only places I shop in Philadelphia.  You can find amazing small designers that have beautiful pieces as well as great vintage finds, everything is mixed in!  All of the clothes here are very reasonably priced for a boutique.  I am a personal fan of the urban, boho, bohemian, simply made clothing.  I have been turned on to many clothing and jewelry designers from shopping at Vagabond.  There is always a local artists show up in the store and I do not knit, but if i did, it would only make this shop even better.  The staff is super friendly and helpful and in the winter time, if you ask, you can have a sweater made by their own line.

Stay away , interns learning and getting paid  for experiencing on your teeth . 

A friend of mine recommended this office . So I called and made an appointment . 
When I got there the nurse took me for X-rays . To my surprise the doctor was a young female . Than I asked what happened to the main doctor ( my friend told me the was and older very experienced man )  . I was told he was working at a different office . Said to myself this doctor must be good to be in charge .
I WAS WRONG , she couldn't tell me exactly where the pain coming from , she used a lot of maybe , probably , think so . You can just tell when somebody not certain.  
She said my wisdom tooth was bad anyway and better take it out and see if the pain is solved . I was like go ahead take it out . She said we don't take them out in this office ,  I'll call the main doctor if he can squeeze u in at the other office . 20 minutes later the nurse told the other doctor is booked . I waited a week with a lot of toothaches for this appointment . Now I have to restart again . 
I had to pay the 99 bucks and nothing was resolved . Gave me the X-rays .
I managed to make an appointment next day with another dentist , show him x-rays, 
He said thesemX-rays not clear , they didn't take it from the right angles . With confidence he told me the problem was the wisdom tooth . He said it was a simple extraction . He pulled it right away .  
I feel better now , no pains .

I thoroughly enjoyed this meal! I came with my friend for lunch and they had such a great lunch menu! Everything was eight dollars and you could choose between stir fry, noodles, or curry. We got the pad Thai with chicken and the red Thai curry with tofu. Both were excellent and such big portions! We got soup or a salad and two spring rolls as an appetizer as well. The red Thai curry was delectable and the tofu soaked up the sauce so nicely. The pad Thai noodles were really good too. I can't pick which one I liked better! The service was a little slow since we were sitting outside but otherwise the workers were friendly and helpful. I highly recommend for good Thai food!

Last week went there just for the arcade the prices were fair we bought a wrist band for the kids. The kids had alot of fun on the rodeo rider maybe a lil too much. 
My only complaint is while my kids were on the bounce house another kid vomited getting all two of three of my kids extremely ill my son ended up in the hospital he had vomited over 30 times in one day oral zofran wouldnt work for him he had to have iv hooked up to him because he was so sick it made his blood pressure drop and him have rappid heart rate. And my daughter caught it too she vomited a handfull of times the first time and it was never ending she vomited for fives days straight her liver ended up inflamed from this nasty virus they caught here. My only advice is bring hand sanitizer and Lysol with you.

Ok, I had to walk out of this place once but it (sort of) isn't their fault.  They have the hostess do double duty and she leaves the podium.  If you do not catch the sign and seat yourself, the waitresses at the place, much like worker ants, will not pick up that you are there without that assignment from the hostess.  It's rather a blind spot on how the place is organized.

Anyway, the food is good and the service is usually good as well.  Have not been back sense I was blown off for 20 minutes and walked out of the place.  Inclined to give it another chance.

What would William Carlos W. Yelp?

The white stones

So much depends
upon
pale white
stones
crouched in
prayer
beneath the darkening
skies.
