http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/st-louis-cemetery-no-1-new-orleans#MzYTAzrdY6bYGumdSwBIKA
http://www.yelp.com/biz_photos/st-louis-cemetery-no-1-new-orleans#JNOnJq7_teBi0Xezt5qE6g

cutest gelateria/bakeshop ever - could spend all day everyday here. perfect pastels - impressive selection of flawless macaroons, eclairs, cupcakes, etc. Chocolate spice gelato was out-of-this-world, riotous flavor. coconut basil was as refreshing as it sounds. hazelnut crunch was better than any hazelnut gelato I've had.

I bought a 6 session Groupon coupon. Always wanted to try laser hair removal and couldn't pass up the deal. I also notice the high rating from everyone thus, I want to give satori laser a try.

Been a week since I did underarm laser hair removal. So far I have yet to see any changes, the underarm hair is still growing at its normal rate. I haven't notice any decreasing hair growth.

I did book another appointment in another 4 weeks. Will update the review if the hair growth changes.

We came here for dinner as we were in town for a conference. After reading some reviews, we decided to give it a try! The mile trek from our Downtown Indy hotel as definitely worth it! We started with chips and dip- homemade French onion dip!!! Yum! The chips were so light and tasty! I ordered the chicken breast and it was cooked to perfection. The boy ordered the ribeye- also very good. The ribeye was atop a soup of sorts- a little different, but tasty. Both meat dishes were 8oz. The only reason I rated one less star, is the prices are a smidge high for the portions- just by a few dollars. I get that the quality is there, and everything was delicious, but $28 for 8oz of chicken is a tad too high (IMHO). We finished off with the chess pie and a red wine chocolate crema. Careful, the menu changes nightly. But the constant-on-the-menu Chess pie was BOMB!!! We decided to try to recreate it and serve it at our wedding. And we may get another slice before we leave town, to you know, make sure we can recreate it. If you order only one thing, make it the tiny ass, $9 slice of bomb chess pie.

I thought I would give Silky's a try after a pasta meal and I wasn't disappointed. I had a pineapple concrete with marshmallow. The custard was tasty and the toppings were fresh.

The layout is kind of wonky. There is an entrance on one side and you order on the other side of the store. It may make sense if there is a huge line but not if the store is almost empty. Also there are no public restrooms and no place to sit.  

The staff was friendly and helpful and the ice cream was good. I'd go again.

The best thing to me about Circus Circus is that it smells really nice.  It's like a mixture of popcorn and cotton candy and you can smell it all the way through the property and outside on the street.  It's surprisingly pleasant.

I find the interior mildly depressing.  It's old and it seems like the machines are old as well, particularly on the ground floor.  They are spaced unusually far apart compared to all the other local casinos.

Promotions are generous.  I did an initial sign up years ago.  I received a flier for "Resident" that they allowed me to use as well.  It provided 10 dollars freeplay (plus 5 for providing my e-mail), 10 dollars in food and 10 dollars in play in the carnie area.  Not bad at all.

On the second floor is a kid's area with numerous carnival games with plush toy prizes and many coin machines that provide tickets that you can trade for other prizes (at a particularly cheap ratio).  They have regular free circus shows in the early evenings all days of the week in the same area.  I've watched a few of these shows with clients and they vary in quality with a variety of gymnastic, animal and contortionist type shows.  Pretty cool that they're free.  The JR Jones animal shows and  the Chinese acrobat shows were both quite good.  All in the all the floor is a colossal rip-off and I'd just stay away.  In particular the ring toss onto glass bottles is one in which I've never seen a single person win.  Just burn your money.

Upstairs there are a few restaurants, a cafe and additional machines, more in line with the feel of other casinos.  I really liked Smokin' Geckos when I went and will do a separate review.  The cafe/ice cream shop has fairly good food at a good value, but is slower than molasses with seemingly uncaring employees.  Prepare to wait.

There's a tram that takes you around to parking that might be appealing to those with disabilities.  

Compared to the other casinos, Circus Circus just feels flimsy and cheap.  I wouldn't recommend them since there are so many better alternatives.

Word is that the buffet is a very good value.

This is the second time my husband, son and I have dined here.  The food is just okay.  I got the grilled filet with whipped mashed potatoes.  There was nothing whipped about them.  They were dry, more liked smashed potatoes-I did not finish them.  The filet was just ok- a little dry and they grilled the 2 asparagus spears to death making them indelible.  My husband got the grilled kabobs and the BBQ sauce is overly sweet and the chicken was dry.  My son enjoyed his pizza from the children's menu.  Watch out for soda prices-way overpriced at $3 each.  Unfortunately we did not really enjoy either dining experience.   Might go back for sandwiches and pizza but will never waste good money on a dinner again.

I'm really glad the male waiters and bartenders are not in lederhosen, because this place would have lost a star for that.

This is a pretty solid spot to get some good beers.  Definitely helps that I visited during "Kick the Keg", an event they hold the night before their Oktoberfest celebration, where if you order the last beer in a keg, you get it for free, as well as a goodybag of beer related items.

The bar itself looks like how I imagine a beer garden in Munich to look like.  High ceilings with exposed wooden beams, long wooden tables with wooden benches (I believe one of them was actually transported here from a church in Germany) and the ubiquitous soccer (excuse me, football) game playing in the background.  

Of the food options, I only tried the pretzel.  Which comes with these amazing homemade mustards.  I'm not usually a huge pretzel fan (besides those hot buttery ones with the cinnamon and sugar at the mall... drool).  The cold, wet Philadelphia pretzels make me want to hurl.  But Brauhaus' pretzels are incredible.  And they help soak up the beer. 

 The rest of the menu is pretty large, with the majority of it being platters of assorted sausages and carb-laded sides.  Now, I've never actually eaten German food, BUT it doesn't really seem that appealing to me.  I think this chick is going to keep it as a drinking-only type of a place. 

Speaking of beer....The beer selection, in my opinion, is awesome.  But if you are an only-drinks-Miller-Lite kind of a person, you probably should go elsewhere.  I really love wheat beers.  So one of the few words I know in German is "Weissen" which loosely translates to something like wheat beer.  When I visited, they had two wheat beers- a Dunkleweissen and a Hefeweissen.  What was the difference?  Hell if I know.  But I had the Dunkle and it was banging.  Just to note- you can get each beer in the 0.5, 1 or 2 liter size.  The 2 liter comes in das boot. Awesome.

Overall, I really like this bar.  Loses a star because I am a little afraid to try the food.

This place use to be wonderful. Homemade pasta and sauces were so wonderful, but the last three times have been a disappointment and we will not go back. The tortellini is hard and dense now, not the melt in your mouth like they use to be. The rolls are your typical store bought, but the butter is really good. The last two times we had gone for lunch, we were the only people in the restaurant for the entire hour. Maybe it's because after a soft drink and a lunch pasta, it will cost you over 20.00.

Love this place...its exactly what you want, when you want it. I would go here over and over. 

The garlic and herb roasted chicken in perfect. The corn grits are a guilty pleasure....so good.

So this company is back at it. I got another call today and was offered the coleman cooler. I was a fool once a few years ago and like the others in this chain i was over charged, more bulbs than needed were shipped and of course the cooler never came. I tried for about a month to get the bulbs returned and finally had to dispute the charges with my CC company. Finally i was given a return label and shipped all of the product back.
I gave the sales rep an earful and he chuckled a bit and admitted that he is hearing allot of the same. 
Good luck if you have already been duped.

This course is in bad condition. I remembered playing this course in high school, things were a lot better then. Besides, they're now charging for range balls. Fairways, bunkers, and tee boxes need A LOT of work.

However, the views are still very iconic. Scenery is beautiful. Staff is very polite and accommodating. The complimentary chocolate chip cookies are a nice touch.

Soooo my beloved family of 4 went to see "The Butler"yesterday. The cost for 4 tickets and "snacks"? Approximately $100. Yup. $48 for 4 tickets, and the rest went to the snack bar. Wow. So for our $100 movie-night experience our movie was playing in theatre 11. A very crowded theater 11. So crowded that we had the honor of sitting on the front row. So while we're sitting there craning our necks to see the screen, lo and behold out of the corner of my eye, I see a mouse run out from the area of the screen to, you guessed it, right around the area of our seats. Instantly, my mine and my daughter's legs went up like a doctor had been checking our reflexes. My husband and son? Oblivious, of course. After a few minutes, we see, let's call him "Jerry", skitter back towards the screen area. After an appropriate amount of time has passed, we put our feet down, but remained on edge, dreading Jerry's return. Yeah, $100. Saving grace? "The Butler" is an excellent movie!

We stopped in for a drink after a recommendation by a bartender at Pesche.  The hotel is beautiful and feels like it's out of a Wes Anderson film.  That is where the charm ends.  The bartenders and staff were inattentive, loud, and more interested in talking at each other versus speaking to the guests.  It was a free for all!  Incredibly disappointing.

Are you a fan of It's Always Sunny? Of course you are! You wouldn't eat mediocre over priced food at this place if you weren't!!!

Considering this place is owned by most of the cast from It's Always Sunny the decor was pretty cool. Lot's of prop things and photos.

The food was alright and the beers were over priced.

Very nice people running the place. It was empty when we went for lunch at noon on a Wednesday, and only one other customer came in during the time we were there. 

The good : the hot sauce was hot and smoky. The food came out fairly quickly. 

The bad : the service was somewhat inattentive.  They didn't check on us until we were finished, we could've used more salsa once the tacos and tamales arrived. My friend said the tamales were on the doughy side. The chicken tacos were nothing exciting.  When I asked for guacamole, why did they throw a few slices of avocado on the tacos instead of bringing me guacamole like they do everywhere else in the Americas? Plus they charged me for "guacamole".. 

If my car broke down in front of this place and I were hungry I'd be comfortable knowing that the food is decent and I wouldn't have to eat my shoe.  Otherwise, there are too many more appealing options for tacos within 10 minutes of here for me to return.

The service is fantastic here.  The nice lady behind the counter took he time to explain how the ice cream eas made and gave us sples of anything we wanted to try.

The store is clean , has interesting pictures & I here were even "cow" stools (as one child there referred to them).

The ice cream, however,  wasn't to our liking.  It was not creamy....it was more icy.  I am not sure why,  but it was all the flavors we tried.  Simply not the creamy texture I associate with good quality ice cream.

The were definitely some different flavors.

Worst experience at Clearwater beach. The boat shown on the website is broke and we traveled on a smaller boat. Did not go out to sea and made a lap around the bay. As expected didn't see a dolphin. We were told we are in luck we can ride again for free if done within 30 days. Not a problem if we live in the area, and the larger boat is fixed.  A Rip Off for us tourist.

It is a real hole in the wall, even by NOLA standards. The food is ok. The service is good the servers and bartenders are snarky, in a good way. I like going here and will go again for late night NOLA. It is not however a best thing I ever ate place. 

Rabbit and sausage jambalaya and the Crabmeat stuffed jalapeños are good.
Just ok and overpriced. The garlic bread with sauce is the best part of the meal

We arrived at 7:00 pm and they served us food at 9:15 to 2 of our friends did not bring the food to us. We called the manager to solve the problem and he became very conceited and told us that he was going to cancel the food and drinks.  They never brought the waitress to bring us the drinks and a terrible service from the waitresses and the manager.  We will never return here.

I went to Crave Beauty Academy over the summer, so this is a belated review. 

I was impressed that they would schedule me at a time I preferred. Most beauty schools won't schedule you after 3 or 3:30 p.m. and before 5 or 5:15 p.m. because that falls between the day and night student shifts. My appointment was for a facial, but none of their esthetician students were around. They made my appointment with a hair student. The instructor told her what she needed to do and let her know when she needed to get the instructor to complete certain aspects of the facial. The facial was okay but it took some extra time and it was not so relaxing for the hair student to be receiving instructions at several points in the process. The student and instructor were both nice, though. I'll consider going back but will probably request an esthetician. For the time being, I prefer the Salon Professional Academy.

Had a code through Grubhub and ordered some late night food... requested a chicken cheesesteak with extra meat and ended up getting steak (read:steakum) instead. Otherwise, the Stromboli I ordered was pretty good. I'd understand if they ran out of chicken or something but in addition to the online order (which I checked after seeing that I received chicken rather than steak to make sure I didn't screw something up), but I didn't receive a phone call and the hand-written order slip (I'm guessing they don't have a printer handy?) has "cheesesteak" written with no mention of "chicken. I get it, everybody makes mistakes. But it's late and of the 5 or 6 options I had to choose from, this was the closest one so I wanted to support a local shop. In the future, I'll go elsewhere in the hopes that they won't screw it up.

Edit: The original review was based on my one and only poor experience with the place. Their pizza may be solid. The stromboli was really good... I'd encourage you to read others' experiences before making a decision on whether to check this place out or not.

These guys are awful, but they sort of have you over a barrel. I need maintenance medication that is quite pricey. It probably saves a couple of thousand dollars a year to use these guys, over my local pharmacy, but every time there is a refill, there is some problem and I have to call into get it take care of. This time it was 3 phone calls.   It's also amazing how long it takes them to process an order. A refill is ordered on March 15th, and I'm told it will ship on the 26th? They're nuts. Express-Scripts is simply awful.

Crowded,Noisy always a wait-but not long!
Food is always consistently great.
Servers have an attitude.
The Sushi & Sashimi are always fresh. Dali served hot is my favorite.
Nice place for a meet up. Quick lunch.Busier at night.
Great store. clean and well organized but wish it had a Starbucks like some of the other Targets.

I was excited to finally try Chima during restaurant week and overall I had a great experience. I was wasn't blown away by all of the food but I still enjoyed it. The flank steak was my favorite. You definitely need to come on an empty stomach.

My favorite part was the atmosphere. The interior has a romantic vibe and I liked how all the tables were spaced apart just the right amount (although that's probably because of COVID). 

There's not much of a dress code. The patrons were wearing a variety of things from jeans to dresses to more causal outfits. The servers who came around with the means were all very friendly!

I like to see if the bathrooms match the quality of the restaurant and these bathrooms were not the cleanest. 

Overall, I enjoyed dining at Chima and would go back.

I went to Fez on a Thursday night.  The vibe and the environment was cozy.  The service was decent but the food was very mediocre, the portions were small and the price was expensive for the food served.  I would have given them 2 1/2 stars but the Yelp app won't allow me todo half stars.  Don't waste your money.

Not a fan. 

I had a couple free coupons from a Yelp event; therefore, I made a date to try it out. Staff were nowhere to be found when we arrived. When they showed up we presented the coupons for two free teas. 

Sadly, we found out that they no longer accept the coupons even know they have no expiry date. They switched ownership and the new owner will not accept the coupons. We were informed that it has been posted on social media; therefore, we should have supposedly known even though it was our first visit.

We debated staying, but grabbed a drink just to try it out. They don't have prices on their menu which makes it inconvenient to ask about all the prices. Good thing we did though, as smoothies were $8. Yikes. I tried their one and only ice tea for the day which was nothing special.

The indoor decorations are adorable with their island and tiki vibe. However, the customer service is too laid back and they should have honoured the coupons or add expiry dates. I would rather hit up other places on Whyte.

Ehhh happy hour... completely deserted.  They offer ONE beer, one red wine and one white one but they do have many $6 cocktails for those that want that.  The drinks outside of happy hour in general seem very overpriced for what you get.  This place calls itself a bar but is much more of a finer dining/fancy craft cocktail establishment.  They have some happy hour small plate snack food and then dinner offers a very small selection of items which are decently priced for some with a bit of an overprice for others.  I guess the main selling point is that there is not anything else really like it in this part of Nashville.  The ambiance and interior of the building is very nice along with their outside patio so this could certainly make for a good dinner date spot.  The staff here were just not very happy looking or very friendly during my happy hour visit.  For happy hour I just can't recommend it unless you want a really quiet time and I really had a whole lot more fun nearby at the Pharmacy beer garden.

This is my fav restaurant in New Orleans. You can't really go wrong with anything but I do have a fav: Blackened redfish over fried grit cakes. 

On Sundays there's only the brunch menu. Mimosas are only $4 each so treat yourself! I'll be back. I'll always be back!

When looking for a tree, where should you go? A Forest, of course and we went to Sherwood Forest. They have a certified Arborist on staff who can assist you in picking the perfect tree for yard. 

Working with a small space, we knew we would need to focus on dwarf trees. The two we selected were a Walkingstick and a Crimson Queen Japanese Maple.  For 60% of the retail price they will plant your new tree for you and give you a two year warranty.  If you plant it yourself, it is only a 1 year warranty. 

Our Walkingstick was so tall we had to have them deliver it,  so why not have them plant it too. Their truck was so cool. It has a robot arm that lifts your tree off the truck, extends itself and lowers it to its' new home. It was so cool to watch and it made their job so much easier.  We could of done the Crimson Queen ourselves, but had them do it as well. 

They do have other greenhouses, but our focus was trees and we are happy customers. I just wish I could have a tree big enough for a tree-house.

I tried the fish cake and there were un needed ingredients when I took a bite of it it fell apart chefs don't wear hairnets some of them were hats and one of them just wears a bandanna I tried there cornbread and it didn't have that tank it just tasted like bread I was disappointed in that takes a long time to get The tomahawk pork chop I have yet to taste it I finally could taste it it was the part I took a bite of one of them and got a mouth full of fat one of them I couldn't even bite

I've had my car a little over a year now and I love it. Buuuuut when I went for my most recent oil change and service I was told there was a nail in one of my tires and that it was non repairable and unsafe to drive on. I was offered placement of a new tire for $150. I declined the offer went to discount tire shortly after and was told there was no nail in my tire nor any valid reason for me to get a new tire. I went to a second discount tire location and I was told the same thing. Nissan wasted my time and lost my trust in the same day. Stay sharp and don't get scammed.
Wasn't really a fan. Small and cramped on the inside. Server was very nice. Food was just ok. Left more than i ate. Probably will go elsewhere.

one of the worst experiences i've ever had at a bar. the staff at the door were some of the rudest, most obnoxious, and disrespectful i have ever encountered as an of age, paying customer. the main bouncer called me a b*****, taunted my friends, and even took id's away from others trying to get in who were 23 years old. there are no redeeming qualities about this bar. DO NOT GO HERE.

This is the place my family goes to celebrate.  Birthdays, anniversary's, my sons visiting. This is where we eat family style.  I have been going here for years.  The food is always good.  We each have our favorites.  My husband loves the lasagne. I love the salmon lemon and herb.  The deserts are amazing.   Appetizers are also good.  The Creme brûlée is excellent.  The chocolate cake so good.  The lemon cookies.  So good.i went with my husband for an early birthday treat.  Just my husband and I. My experience was not as good because my waiter was on his second day.  He gave me free birthday lemon cookies. But the service was bad. I tried to understand,but I don't think he ever waited tables before.  The experience was less because the service was poor.  The food was good.
Sat at sushi bar and the service was very slow, but the food was fine. Naruto roll was very good, though.

Fun little spot for a good street style taco. Great tasting, and full menu of tacos, burritos, tostados, etc. but the 3 stars are due to items they were sold out of. We arrived at 8pm and they were out it beef empanadas, steak and the mexican corn. But we would definitely go again!

Their "nachos" are pretty terrible. Cold, shredded cheese covering the whole thing. I don't need nacho cheese sauce, but is it too much to expect at least warm melted cheese on nachos? Also, they are now participating in some program where they charge you an additional 3.5% over their advertised prices if you pay with a card instead of cash. If you guys are passing on the fees to your customers, maybe you should be a cash only establishment. That's just the cost of doing business.

Order online, still took 20 minutes to get my order curbside.  Fries were some how undercooked.  I got home and everything was inedible.  My Big Mac was literally lettuce, tomato, and pickles.  Avoid it like the plague.  This place should not be in business.

Was a little disappointed. The entire place had a fishy smell to it. For this reason we  decided to sit outside. The restaurant is located right across the street from the ocean/ harbor . The server and the busboy were pleasant. They turned the outdoor heaters on for us. The restaurant seemed dirty. The Salt and pepper shakers were half full and dirty on the inside and outside. The bathrooms needed some assistance as well.  It just wasn't what I had expected.

Big Brand always has excellent service when I go in.  I usually visit the Goleta location, but this past weekend I had an appointment at the Santa Barbara location on Milpas and everything went great.  Not only did they perform an oil change, alignment, brake check and tire rotation within the 1 hour time period, but they also changed out my windshield wipers and replaced my tail light. I would highly recommend either of these locations for auto servicing! 

A special thanks to Justin (Goleta), Enrique, Thomas and Juan (all at Milpas)

This place is small but I have never found it crowded or had a long time to wait. I am always in and out very efficiently. And the people who work here are always pleasant and helpful. Really, a very nice place to get shipping done. I've never bought anything there like shipping supplies so I can't comment on that.

Very good coffee, prompt and polite service, and a welcoming, spacious environment.

Beignets were tasty, although a bit denser than I would expect (beignets being more typically light and airy). Being able to have different styles in one order was a pleasant surprise. Huzzah for the dulce de leche sauce.

Kevin's BLT was (and this may seem improbable to people) too abundant with the bacon. I could have dealt with about 3-5 slices less, and perhaps a bit more tomato. Still, well prepared, and the blue cheese aioli was very pungent but not at the risk of overpowering the sandwich.

If I'm ever back in town, I would be delighted to come again.
Best Mexican in Wilmington. And you can actually get spicy food here! Also great service!!!

Not exaggerating... every time we order through grubhub (and even times we've ordered in person) the order is wrong. Luckily when were in person we can have them fix it ... yet that means waiting more. The meat is either mixed up or wrong pasta. Really love this place but getting annoyed lately considering our last five orders or so have been this messed up. Why is this a consistent problem here?

Finally can get my winter melon tea in Louisiana!  I always enjoy Kung Fu tea, and they are pretty consistent over all the locations I have been to!

Service was pretty quick.  Even though they look busy, most of them are just hanging around and chit chatting.

Winter melon tea, 50% less sugar is the way to go. The end.

If I were rating this solely based on the amazing individuals that work at this location then believe me, you'd see the biggest 5 stars possible. These employees get hounded daily by huge lines of obnoxious students. I've seen them be berated, I've seen them when they're flustered, and I've seen the way a lot of jerks treat them. I absolutely love every employee here and hope that everyone else appreciates them. 

Since this location is at the UA, and on campus right by the library, it gets slammed throughout the day. There are times where there are lines that go 20 people out the door and the employees here continue to bust butt to get drink orders out. Unfortunately, the UA has set them up for failure by also having an advanced ordering app (not the regular Starbucks app--they don't do that at this location) where while there are 30 people in line, there are then 30 college students in classes also ordering drinks. So at certain times of the day the "in person" customers are getting angry because "tap and go" customers are getting drinks before the "in person" orders. But imagine how hard it is for these few employees to keep up with the 30 people in line and the 30+ mobile orders happening at the same time. Really, UA has set them up for failure because people get cranky and take it out on these poor baristas who are doing amazing work. Unfortunately, I have to deduct a star for the long, long wait times and for the fact that I think its a horrible business practice to have all of these different ordering systems slamming people. But again, still 5 stars for the employees! 

They don't let you redeem your stars for a free beverage here. That's frustrating. You can, however, still get happy hour deals and other perks here. I mainly earn my stars here and then cash them out at "regular" locations. 

What I --really-- hate about this location is how they nickle and dime you. I switch out the milk for soy or coconut in all of my beverages. The upcharge for this is fine, as I appreciate them offering soy, and I realize that costs them a bit more to make. I have zero issues with this. But what irks me is I used to order drinks with "no ice" and I'd have no issues. The ice makes things too cold for me to drink, but sometimes I want something that isn't hot either. "No ice" is a great way to get something closer to room temp. Again, I've ordered like this several times with no issues (and I've NEVER had an issue at any other location ever). But suddenly I'm told there is a 50 cent upcharge here for "no ice". They're actually trying to charge you to take ice out of your drink. I've just saved you some water, some ice, the power it took to make the ice, etc., and the coffee I'm purchasing is around $6-7 and I usually purchase here 3x a week, 2x a day (that's 6 drinks a week, a loyal customer). And you really, really want to charge someone to take ice out of a drink?!

This "new rule" comes and goes. Sometimes they have it. Other times they don't. But basically, they need to get over all of their extra charges that don't exist anywhere else. Seriously, this doesn't exist at any other "regular" or "franchise" location I've ever been to. At some point, you need to realize you're making plenty of money and you don't need to charge to remove ice. After all, I don't get a discount if I take whipped cream off of my drink...

Employees: 5 stars
Long waits and weird mysterious extra charges: 3 stars
Greed of this location: no stars

Worst nightclub I've ever been to. Terrible service. Terribly ran. Got kicked out of VIP for no reason. Literally. I will never come here again. I usually celebrate it at aura night lounge and that's where I will always go from now on. Save your money. If I could give it no stars, I would.
Very disappointed not open for breakfast. Hard to locate off interstate signs misleading
The pizza and cheese bread we ordered was HORRIBLE!! The food was stale and gross.

This place is so expensive! I had a cold coffee, an open faced sandwich and a small date ball the size of a truffle. It was $17! Yikes! The sandwich was good and I would go back if it didn't cost so much but that price is incredible for a counter service sandwich.
Had some great chicken and side dishes at the place. Kind of pricey. Like the atmosphere. Will likely try again.

The chicken panini is good and the chocolate peanut butter protein shake is amazing! It's definitely overpriced and a lot of the menu is a la carte, but I would recommend stopping in for a protein shake at least.

Been coming to his business since it was "horizons". The grilled Seitan  is out of this world. I have never had a bad experience at Vedge. He staff is always courteous and attentive, without being overbearing. Everything from the dirt list to the desserts are delicious!

Ordered the Italian Stromboli. Fair to poor. Crust was soggy. Absolutely no texture as you'd expect with pizza dough. Sprinkled with cheese and herbs that didn't do anything to enhance the flavor, because there was NO flavor!

Nice bar area while you wait for your table, good wine list. 

Of course, the charbroiled oysters are great and what put them on the map. I like to take people here who claim they hate oysters, once they eat here, their opinion is totally changed. 

Crabmeat Au Gratin dip - large portion, lots of lump crab meat, very good but very very rich! 
Lobster Mac- Very good and again not stingy on the lobster but I thought it was overpriced for what you got. ($15 for a side)
Crabmeat ceasar salad- great and loaded with crab meat. 
Herradura shrimp - my favorite entree, very unique! 
Cheesecake - my favorite dessert

Prices are a little steep on some items and priced right on others. The service has always been great and very accommodating to any special requests. It's definitely a treat to come here.

Totally unexpected, a delightful surprise! About a 10 minute walk from our hotel in downtown Philly, it was the most perfect fall weather in the end of August! We headed down towards Barbuzzo - opted for inside seating, because the outdoor seating was not corralled at all from the busy pedestrians and would be a bit distracting. 

We had the Smoked duck arancini which had this really smokey and creamy sauce. It came with 5 arancinis, so the app is good for sharing. Loved the smokey cheese they used, and the amount of duck that was molded into the filling. I am always afraid that arancinis would be too much rice inside and end up dry - but this had a great consistency of cheese/rice medley! Not too salty either. The restaurant serves medium plates for sharing so we got two entrees, braised lamb parpadelle and the scallops with bucatini. Out of the two - i have to say the bucatini had my vote. HUGE SCALLOPS, fresh, housemade bucatini (advertised that all their pastas are HOUSEMADE, and you can totally taste the outstanding quality). The buttery and lemon light sauce over the pasta was a great highlight to the dish. I love lemon flavor on seafood. This dish is something i will defintiely get again, the flavor was super unique and delicious overall. The parpadelle on the other hand was a bit underwhelming. From the crazy party in my mouth from the bucatini dish, the parpadelle kind of lacked. There was a good amount of lamb but the sauce was bland. It was a tad bit salty too. The parpadelle was super fresh and cooked very well. But I am glad i tried the dish. To finish off our night at the awesome BARBUZZO, we ordered the ricotta almond cake. DI-VINE! I was so skeptical about eating a cake called "ricota cake" (also, im lactose sensitive, but FTW!) - but it was a pleasant surprise, so so so delicious! The candied almonds and ice cream was an awesome combo for the cake. a little sweet and buttery crunch with every bite!!!

The service was GREAT - overall attentive and super friendly. My fiance made reservations well in advanced so we were seated right away. We've read that it's hard to get a reservation here so plan ahead :) It's well worth it!

Terrible party host!! Terrible. Were super rude to my guest who had a special needs son. Kept telling her he couldn't go inside the play area because he had braces on his legs, and they weren't even braces.
Party planners did NOTHING, never set up our beverages, plates, napkins, she kept asking me where I wanted everything and that was clearly HER job, then we were rushed. We were to eat at 4, didn't get in to eat till after 4 then she kept rushing she needed to sing happy birthday, she did NOTHING. I will be contacting the owner on Monday. I feel like we were ripped off BIG time.
